eng: From Paul, called by Gods will to be an apostle of Jesus Christ, and from Sosthenes our brother.
local: An sá man Pɔɔl un Unimbɔti là ki yíiń-mi ḿ nīn sá Yeesu Kriisitoo kpambalee nì ti-nabiki Sɔsteen di jáaḿ-ni.

eng: Now I encourage you, brothers and sisters, in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ: Agree with each other and dont be divided into rival groups. Instead, be restored with the same mind and the same purpose.
local: Nnabiyaamu, nì ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo yindi puee, m gbàam̄-ni, ní nīn pēēmaan kinyɔkɔ, digàntil ń taa nín̄ bí ni-kansikin. Nín̄ pēēmaan kinyɔkɔ mmɔntiimin, ni-kɔkɔ bɔti ń nīn sá tibaantiil, ni-landɔkɔi mun ń nīn pēē.

eng: My brothers and sisters, Chloes people gave me some information about you, that youre fighting with each other.
local: Di sá yii m-nabiyaamu, Kloowee dicaŋkun yab biba dɔ́miń ki tùkū-mi yii kujaau bí ni-kansikin.

eng: What I mean is this: that each one of you says, I belong to Paul, I belong to Apollos, I belong to Cephas, I belong to Christ.
local: Kun pu m lì mimmee di sá m gbìl̄ yii ni-kansikin biba lì yii bíǹ nóó Pɔɔl ní, ní biba mun lì yii bíǹ nóó Apooloosi ní, ní biba mun lì yii bíǹ nóó Peetroo, ní biba mun lì yii bíǹ nóó Kriisitoo ní.

eng: Has Christ been divided? Was Paul crucified for you, or were you baptized in Pauls name?.
local: Mimmee, ni dàkafī yii Kriisitoo yākatì u-ba ajan kupaauaaa? Àá Pɔɔl ní bi kpáá ndɔpuntikaa pu ki tīī-ni ní? Àá Pɔɔl yindi pu ní bi fùl̄-ni Unimbɔti nyim ní?.

eng: Thank God that I didnt baptize any of you, except Crispus and Gaius.
local: M jáaḿ Unimbɔti m nín kaa fùl̄ ni-kansikin báà ubɔ nnyimee, asee Kriisipusi nì Gaayusi baba.

eng: so that nobody can say that you were baptized in my name!.
local: An puee ni-kansikin ubɔ kaa ŋūǹ kí lì yii man yindi pu ní bi fùl̄-u nnyim.

eng: Oh, I baptized the house of Stephanas too. Otherwise, I dont know if I baptized anyone else.
local: Tiŋman! M téetí yii m nìn fùl̄ Steefanasi nì u-dicaŋkun yab Unimbɔti nnyim. M yaa gíti fùl̄ biba muɔɔɔ, maa ti nyí.

eng: Christ didnt send me to baptize but to preach the good news. And Christ didnt send me to preach the good news with clever words so that Christs cross wont be emptied of its meaning.
local: Di sá yii Kriisitoo kaa túnní-mi ḿ nīn fùul̀ binib Unimbɔti nnyim, ama u túnní-mi ḿ sòoǹ u-Bɔmɔntiiti ní. Maa làá dū binib ciiŋyi ní kí sòoǹ an bɔti. M yaa ŋá mimmee, m làá yàntī kun pu Yeesu Kriisitoo kpíì ndɔpuntikaa puee ń ŋá fám dee.

eng: The message of the cross is foolishness to those who are being destroyed. But it is the power of God for those of us who are being saved.
local: Kun puee ti nín sòoǹ yii Kriisitoo kpíì ndɔpuntikaa pu kí fīī binibee, an sá tiwaatibɔti ní ki tīī bin bí diwaŋkil saŋŋu poonee. Ama ní ki sá Unimbɔti pɔŋŋu ki tīī timbi bin bí difiil saŋŋunee.

eng: It is written in scripture: I will destroy the wisdom of the wise, and I will reject the intelligence of the intelligent .
local: kii an nín ŋmàn̄ Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon puee yii: «M làá kúntí biciŋfitiibi ciiŋyi ní kí nàan̄ binyiliibi nyil.».

eng: To Gods church that is in Corinth: To those who have been made holy to God in Christ Jesus, who are called to be Gods people. Together with all those who call upon the name of our Lord Jesus Christ in every place—hes their Lord and ours!.
local: M ŋmàn̄ kugbɔŋu nee ki tīī nimbi Unimbɔti nikitil din bí Kɔranti, Unimbɔti lútì-ni ní nīn sá u-yab Yeesu Kriisitoo puee. U yíiń-ni ní nīn bí circir, ni nì bin kɔkɔ bí báà laakin ki jáaḿ ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo bi-Dindaan un sá ti mun Dindaanee.

eng: Where are the wise? Where are the legal experts? Where are todays debaters? Hasnt God made the wisdom of the world foolish?.
local: Niinee uciŋfiti, àá unyil gíti cáá tilati? Bin bí duulinya poon ki kpākantée bí la? Unimbɔti dàkā yii duulinya yab ciiŋŋu sá kuwaatiiu ní.

eng: In Gods wisdom, he determined that the world wouldnt come to know him through its wisdom. Instead, God was pleased to save those who believe through the foolishness of preaching.
local: Di sá yii duulinya nì u-ciiŋŋuee, waa ŋúń ki béè Unimbɔti nì u-ciiŋŋu. An pu ní an fáatì Unimbɔti ń dī tibɔti tin ti sòoǹ bi cáa yíì tiwaatibɔti nee pu kí fīī bin fōō-u ki kííyee.

eng: Jews ask for signs, and Greeks look for wisdom.
local: An yaa sá Juuda yabee, bíǹ nyàab̀ bí ŋá maamaacib bí ká án nīn sá-bi kudaaŋŋu, ní Greeki yab bíǹ nyàab̀ iciin.

eng: but we preach Christ crucified, which is a scandal to Jews and foolishness to Gentiles.
local: Ama ní timbee, Kriisitoo un bi kpáá ndɔpuntikaa puee bɔti ní ti sòoǹ. An bɔtee di làkantí Juuda yab, ní ki sá bin kaa sá Juuda yabee cee tiwaatibɔti.

eng: But to those who are called—both Jews and Greeks—Christ is Gods power and Gods wisdom.
local: Ama Kriisitoo di sá Unimbɔti pɔŋŋu nì u-ciiŋŋu ki tīī binib bin Unimbɔti yíińee, bin sá Juuda yabee nì bin kaa sée.

eng: This is because the foolishness of God is wiser than human wisdom, and the weakness of God is stronger than human strength.
local: Di sá yii tin tin Unimbɔti ŋá binib cáa yíì tiwaatibɔtee jítiǹ binib ciiŋyi, ní tin u ŋá binib kpèé yii an sá iwɔfee pɔ̄ɔ̄ ki jítiǹ binib.

eng: Look at your situation when you were called, brothers and sisters! By ordinary human standards not many were wise, not many were powerful, not many were from the upper class.
local: Nnabiyaamu, kpèèmaan nimbi dikitil din Unimbɔti yíińee, bin binib yíì biciŋfitiibee, nì biyidambi, nì agaajabil kaa wīikí ni-kansikin.

eng: But God chose what the world considers foolish to shame the wise. God chose what the world considers weak to shame the strong.
local: Ama Unimbɔti lēē kun ní duulinya yíì kuwaatiiuee ní ki dūu kɔ́ǹ biciŋfitiibi icoo, ní ki lēē kun duulinya yíì iwɔsɔfee ní ki dūu kɔ́ǹ bipɔɔbi icoo.

eng: And God chose what the world considers low-class and low-life—what is considered to be nothing—to reduce what is considered to be something to nothing.
local: U lēē tiwan tin bi kpèé ki sìŋkīǹ kaa dūu yíiń tibee, tin kaa pée bí tibanee ní u dūu sīntiǹ tin bi wāĺ yii tì bí tibanee taapu taapu.

eng: So no human being can brag in Gods presence.
local: U ŋá míǹ unil báà ubɔ ń taa gāā u-bindi Unimbɔti nimbiin.

eng: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Ti-Baa Unimbɔti nì ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo ń ŋá-ni tiŋan kí tīī-ni ŋgbansɔŋfi.

eng: It is because of God that you are in Christ Jesus. He became wisdom from God for us. This means that he made us righteous and holy, and he delivered us.
local: Di sá yii úǹ Unimbɔti di yāntì ní ni nì Yeesu Kriisitoo ŋá ubaanti. U yāntì Yeesu kpántì timbi ciiŋŋu. Kriisitoo pu ní ti kpántì deedeedambi Unimbɔti nimbiin ki bí circir, u fōō-ti ki líì.

eng: This is consistent with what was written: The one who brags should brag in the Lord! .
local: An pu ní an ŋmàn̄ Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon yii: «Sii un yaa làá gāā a-bindee ń gāā ti-Dindaan nín ŋá tiŋyee pu.».

eng: I thank my God always for you, because of Gods grace that was given to you in Christ Jesus.
local: M jáaḿ m-Nimbɔtiu múlmul ni-pu, tiŋan tin u ŋá-ni Yeesu Kriisitoo puee.

eng: That is, you were made rich through him in everything: in all your communication and every kind of knowledge.
local: Di sá Kriisitoo pu ní u ŋá-ni tiŋan kɔkɔ, ki yāntì ni gbìl̄ ki béè u-bɔti taapu kɔkɔ ki ŋūǹ kí sòoǹ-tì mbaamɔmin.

eng: in the same way that the testimony about Christ was confirmed with you.
local: Kriisitoo bɔti tin ti cáań ki sòon̄ nì nee, ni fōō-tì ki ŋúb́-tì tiŋan pu.

eng: The result is that you arent missing any spiritual gift while you wait for our Lord Jesus Christ to be revealed.
local: Míǹ pu ní nimbi bin kā ki cí ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo gítintamee, Unimbɔti pú-ni u-pɔ̄tam kɔkɔ, báà miba kaa lóò-ni.

eng: He will also confirm your testimony about Christ until the end so that you will be blameless on the day of our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Unimbɔti gbanti di làá ŋúb́-ni mmɔntiimin kí tin cáá kátí-ku án nín làá ŋá pu ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo yaa láa gítìńee, bí taa láa ká ni-pu ikpiti ibee.

eng: God is faithful, and you were called by him to partnership with his Son, Jesus Christ our Lord.
local: Unimbɔti yaa pōoǹ puee, see ú ŋá míǹ, an di sá yii u yíiń-ni yii ni nì u-Jipɔɔn ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo ń ŋá ubaanti.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I want you to be sure of the fact that our ancestors were all under the cloud and they all went through the sea.
local: Nnabiyaamu, m là ní téetí ditagbandi din nìn còom̄ ki dī ti-naanjab ki ŋmíntí-bee, nì bi-kɔkɔ nín nìn dūtì Tikati mɔɔl puee bɔti.

eng: Lets not grumble, like some of them did, and were killed by the destroyer.
local: Ni mun ń taa gbùkum̀ kii bi-kansikin biba nín nìn gbūkum̀ pu ŋkúm tuuŋŋu dɔ́miń ki kūū ki céetì-bee.

eng: These things happened to them as an example and were written as a warning for us to whom the end of time has come.
local: Ditafadaaldi gbanti nìn jéetì bi-pu kí nīn sá kudaaŋŋu ní. Bi ŋmàn̄ tibɔti gbanti kí kpāafì timbi bin bí ŋyuntijoolkaa nee poon nan ní.

eng: So those who think they are standing need to watch out or else they may fall.
local: An puee sii un dàkafī yii a nín yú pu nee, a yú deedeeyee, á nīn nyí nì a-ba kí taa lítí.

eng: No temptation has seized you that isnt common for people. But God is faithful. He wont allow you to be tempted beyond your abilities. Instead, with the temptation, God will also supply a way out so that you will be able to endure it.
local: Icɔŋki in bàń ni-puee pée ŋmàntī unil pɔŋŋu nyɔkɔm ní, kun puee Unimbɔti yaa pōoǹ puee see ú ŋá míǹ. Waa kíikí tin lákatiǹ ni-pɔŋŋu puee ń bàń-ni, di sá yii ŋyunti ŋun ní icɔŋki yaa bāań-nee, u làá tīī-ni mpɔn ní ŋmā kí ŋmèè, ní kí dàkà-ni nsan ní dī kí nyā.

eng: So then, my dear friends, run away from the worship of false gods!.
local: An puee nnabiyaamu, ní sāǹ awaa.

eng: Im talking to you like you are sensible people. Think about what Im saying.
local: M sòon̄-ni kii bi nín sòoǹ binib bin cáá nlandɔkɔee puee ní. Nimbi ni-ba ń dàkafì tin m sòon̄ nee pu kí kpèè.

eng: Isnt the cup of blessing that we bless a sharing in the blood of Christ? Isnt the loaf of bread that we break a sharing in the body of Christ?.
local: Ndaam min pu ti jáaḿ Unimbɔti ti yaa làá jī ti-Dindaan jikaatee di kaa kpáfí-ti nì Kriisitoo fatikuu ubaantaaa? Kpɔnɔ un ti gífí ki yàkatēe, an di kaa kpáfí ti nì Kriisitoo wunti kí ŋá ubaantaaa?.

eng: Since there is one loaf of bread, we who are many are one body, because we all share the one loaf of bread.
local: Kpɔnɔbambaanti ní an sá, ní bi gífí. Míǹ ní ti mun nín kpáfì ki sá kunipaau nee, ti kpántì tiwumbaantiil, kun puee ti-kɔkɔ ŋmɔ́ kpɔnɔbaanti gbanti.

eng: Look at the people of Israel. Dont those who eat the sacrifices share from the altar?.
local: Kpèèmaan Isirayeel yab bɔti: bin nìn jīǹ tin bi cāabìń ki ŋáań Unimbɔti saraayee, bi nì Unimbɔti un bi ŋáań saraayee ŋá ubaanti dee.

eng: What am I saying then? That food sacrificed to a false god is anything, or that a false god is anything?.
local: M nín lì pu nee dàkà yii diwaal bí tiban deeyaaa? Àá tiwaŋkuti tin bi tōotì-dèe nanti di bí tiban ní?.

eng: All were baptized into Moses in the cloud and in the sea.
local: Ditagbandee nì dimɔɔlee poon ní bi fùl̄-bi bí nīn nóó Mooyiisi.

eng: No, but this kind of sacrifice is sacrificed to demons and not to God. I dont want you to be sharing in demons.
local: Aayee, kpataaa! Di sá tin bi tòotēe, bi cáa tìì arasiniibi ní, an kaa sá Unimbɔti. An puee maa là ni nì arasiniibi ń kpāfì ubaanti.

eng: You cant drink the cup of the Lord and the cup of demons; you cant participate in the table of the Lord and the table of demons.
local: Naa ŋūǹ kí nyɔ̀ ti-Dindaan daam kí gíti nyɔ̀ arasiniibi mun yam. Naa tí ŋūǹ kí jī ti-Dindaan jikaati kí gíti jī arasiniibi mun yati.

eng: Or should we make the Lord jealous? We arent stronger than he is, are we?.
local: Ti là kí táká ti-Dindaan tibɔti kí yòon̄ u-ŋuul naaa? Ti pɔ̄ɔǹ-uaaa?.

eng: Everything is permitted, but everything isnt beneficial. Everything is permitted, but everything doesnt build others up.
local: Biba lì yii: «M cáá nsan kí ŋá tikɔkɔ,» ama tikɔkɔ mun kaa ŋān̄ ki tīī-ni. «Ti cáá nsan kí ŋá tikɔkɔ,» ama an kaa sá tikɔkɔ di tútúǹ-ti nnimbɔtisan poon.

eng: No one should look out for their own advantage, but they should look out for each other.
local: Ubɔ ń taa nín̄ nyàab̀ úǹ u-ba ŋanti, ama ní nyàab̀ ni-jutiibi ŋanti.

eng: Eat everything that is sold in the marketplace, without asking questions about it because of your conscience.
local: Ni ŋūǹ kí dā tinan tin bi nyáfí kinyɔŋinee kí ŋmɔ́ nì dipɔɔsɔŋkil kí taa bàlifì yii tì sá ba yati.

eng: The earth and all that is in it belong to the Lord .
local: Kun puee Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon lī yii: «Kitiŋ nì tin kɔkɔ pée bí ki-puee, ti-Dindaan di yì.».

eng: If an unbeliever invites you to eat with them and you want to go, eat whatever is served, without asking questions because of your conscience.
local: Tí lì yii ubɔ kaa nóó Yeesu ní ki yíiń-ni ní jī tijin. Ni yaa là ki cūtèe, tin u yaa cáań ki yóoǹ-nee, jīmaan. Ní taa lì yii ni là ni-pɔbiliŋ ń nīn sɔ̀m̀ee puee, ni làá bàlifì-u yii u-nanti gbanti sá ba yati.

eng: But if someone says to you, This meat was sacrificed in a temple, then dont eat it for the sake of the one who told you and for the sake of conscience.
local: Ama ubɔ yaa tùkū-ni yii an sá tiwaatootinanee, ní taa ŋmɔ́maan. Un tùkū-nee puee, ní taa ŋmɔ́maan án páb́ u-pɔbil.

eng: Now when I say conscience I dont mean yours but the other persons. Why should my freedom be judged by someone elses conscience?.
local: - An kaa sá nimbi ní án taa páb́, un tùkū-nee ní án taa páb́. «Ama ba pu ní an sá yii, án taa páb́ ubɔ puee, man batɔl cáá kigbeennyɔkɔ ní?.

eng: All ate the same spiritual food.
local: ní bi-kɔkɔ jíń tijikaal tin nyēeń Unimbɔti ceeyee.

eng: If I participate with gratitude, why should I be blamed for food I thank God for?.
local: M yaa làá jī tijin ní ki yaa jáam̀ Unimbɔti ti-pu ní ki nín jíńee, ba pu ní bi gíti kpākantí-mi tijin tin pu m jáam̀ ki nín jíńee?».

eng: So, whether you eat or drink or whatever you do, you should do it all for Gods glory.
local: An puee, an yaa sá tijin ní ni bí ki jīǹaaa, an yaa tí sá ndaam ní ni yaa tí bí ki nyùɔɔɔ, tin kɔkɔ ní ni bíi ŋáańee, ní yàntī án nīn tìì Unimbɔti tinyool.

eng: Dont offend either Jews or Greeks, or Gods church.
local: Ni-bimbim ń taa yàntī bin sá Juuda yabee nì bin kaa sée, nì bin nóó Yeesuee ń lítí.

eng: This is the same thing that I do. I please everyone in everything I do. I dont look out for my own advantage, but I look out for many people so that they can be saved.
local: Nín̄ ŋáaḿmaan kii man nín ŋáań puee: m pée pɔ̄ɔntí m-ba kí nīn fáatíǹ báà ŋma tikɔkɔ poon ní. Maa nyàab̀ man m-ba ŋanti, ama báà ŋma ŋanti ní m nyàab̀, án nín làá ŋá pu bí ká difiilee.

eng: and all drank the same spiritual drink. They drank from a spiritual rock that followed them, and the rock was Christ.
local: Bi-kɔkɔ di tí nyùn̄ nnyim min nyēeń Unimbɔti ceeyee. Di sá yii bi nìn nyùn̄ ditaŋkpakpaandi din nyánní Unimbɔti cee ki cēentí-bee nyim ní, ditaŋkpalee nìn sá Kriisitoo ní.

eng: However, God was unhappy with most of them, and they were struck down in the wilderness.
local: Ama nì míǹ kɔkɔee, bi-kansikin tikpil nìn ŋáań min kaa fáatiǹ Unimbɔtee, an pu ní bi nìn kpíì kuteeun.

eng: These things were examples for us, so we wont crave evil things like they did.
local: Tin nìn ŋá-bi nee sá kudaaŋŋu ki tīī timbi tí taa nín̄ cáá ilandɔkɔkpiti kii bíǹ nín nìn cáá ilandɔkɔkpiti puee.

eng: Dont worship false gods like some of them did, as it is written, The people sat down to eat and drink and they got up to play .
local: Ni mun ń taa ti nóó awaa kii bi-kansikin biba nín nìn nóó puee, a lì kii an nín ŋmàn̄ Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon puee yii: «Kunipaau kāl̀ ki jíń ki nyùn̄ ní ki yíkì ki ŋōō ki gɔ́ǹ diwaal.».

eng: Lets not practice sexual immorality, like some of them did, and twenty-three thousand died in one day.
local: Ti mun ń taa ŋá tilaakpaal kii bi-kansikin biba nín nìn ŋá puee, an di nìn yāntì binib kutukub mmuŋku nì bita lítì ki kpíì ŋwimbaanti.

eng: Lets not test Christ, like some of them did, and were killed by the snakes.
local: Tí taa ti cɔ̄ŋkìmaan ti-Dindaan kii bi-kansikin biba nín nìn cɔ́ŋkì-u pu kin ikoobu dāa júntì ki kūū-bee.

eng: Follow my example, just like I follow Christs.
local: Ní nīn dīmaan man tabɔbiliŋ kii ma mun nín dī Kriisitoo tabɔbiliŋ puee.

eng: Because of this a woman should have authority over her head, because of the angels.
local: An pu ní an sá yii ituun puee, unimpu máaǹ kí nīn yíĺ tiba kí bìin̄ u-yil pu, míǹ di dàkà yii waa yì u-ba.

eng: However, woman isnt independent from man, and man isnt independent from woman in the Lord.
local: Ti-Dindaan saŋŋu poonee, uninja kaa yì u-ba unimpu cee, míǹ ní unimpu mun kaa yì u-ba uninja cee. Ubɔ kaa ŋūǹ kí nīn bí ubɔ ń nín kpá.

eng: As woman came from man so also man comes from woman. But everything comes from God.
local: Di sá uninja pu ní bi lēē tiba ki náań unimpu, ní unimpu mun di màal̀ uninja, ama bíǹ kɔkɔee nyánní Unimbɔti cee ní.

eng: Judge for yourselves: Is it appropriate for a woman to pray to God with her head uncovered?.
local: Nimbi ni-ba, ní dàkafì kí kpèè yii an ŋān̄ unimpu ń nīn mèé Unimbɔti kaa yíĺ tiba u-yil puɔɔɔ?.

eng: Doesnt nature itself teach you that if a man has long hair, it is a disgrace to him.
local: Duulinya u-ba dàkà yii an kaa ŋān̄ uninja ń nīn tú tiyikpiti sùuu.

eng: but if a woman has long hair, it is her glory? This is because her long hair is given to her for a covering.
local: Ama unimpuee, tiyikpiti yaa fɔ̀kɔ̀ee di sá u-nyooti, kun puee tiyikpiti nín fɔ̀kɔ̀ puee di fòol̄ ŋŋaabu ŋun u bàà làá yíĺee paaŋŋu.

eng: But if someone wants to argue about this, we dont have such a custom, nor do Gods churches.
local: Ubɔ yaa cáá tiba kí kpákáǹ an puɔɔɔ, timbi nì Unimbɔti nikitiŋ nín ŋúb́ miŋyee dee.

eng: Now I dont praise you as I give the following instruction because when you meet together, it does more harm than good.
local: Kɔŋkɔnnee m nín gbìl̄ tiŋyee puee m làá wòoǹ-ni ní, di sá yii ni nín kōontí ikoon in an bàà sá án tūtuǹ-ni kin an púntí-nee.

eng: First of all, when you meet together as a church, I hear that there are divisions among you, and I partly believe it.
local: Mpeepeekaamee, m gbìl̄ yii ni yaa kpáfì kí jāam̀ Unimbɔtee, ni kpákà ní ki gàntī tɔb. - Man m-ba, m fōō yii an máaǹ ki sá míǹ.

eng: Its necessary that there are groups among you, to make it clear who is genuine.
local: An lafun máaǹ digàntil ń nīn bí ni-kansikin, bí ŋmā kí béé bin yú deedeeyee.

eng: I praise you because you remember all my instructions, and you hold on to the traditions exactly as I handed them on to you.
local: M pàkà-ni kii ni nín téetí m-pu ŋyunti kamaa pu, ki ŋúb́ tibɔti tin m dàkā-nee kii m nín pée dàkā-ni-tì puee.

eng: So when you get together in one place, it isnt to eat the Lords meal.
local: Ni nín kpáfí ki jáaḿ Unimbɔtee, an kaa sá ti-Dindaan jinti ní ni jīǹ.

eng: Each of you goes ahead and eats a private meal. One person goes hungry while another is drunk.
local: Di sá yii nimbee, báà ŋma ŋáań malaa ní kí yòon̄ u-jinti ki bíi jīǹ u-baba. Ni nín ŋá mimmee ní ŋkùm làá kpɔ̀ biba ní bin kíǹee ń gbí ndaam.

eng: Dont you have houses to eat and drink in? Or do you look down on Gods churches and humiliate those who have nothing? What can I say to you? Will I praise you? No, I dont praise you in this.
local: Naa cáá idumpu kí jī kí nyɔ̀ niinaaa? Unimbɔti nikitil ní ni kpèé fám naaa, bin kaa cáá tibee ní ni kɔ̄ntí icoo ní? Ni cí yii ḿ lì-ni tiba ní naa! Ḿ pàkà-naaa? Aayee, maa làá pàkà-ni báà fiii!.

eng: I received a tradition from the Lord, which I also handed on to you: on the night on which he was betrayed, the Lord Jesus took bread.
local: Ní kpèè tibɔti tin ti-Dindaan dàkā-mi m dū ki nāatì-nee. Di sá yii kunyeeu kun bi dū ti-Dindaan Yeesu ki ŋúbiǹ u-nannanliibee, u nìn yōoǹ kpɔnɔ.

eng: After giving thanks, he broke it and said, This is my body, which is for you; do this to remember me.
local: ki jáam̀ Unimbɔti an pu ní ki cúu gífì, ní ki bí yii: «Doo di sá man wunti tin m dūu tīī ni-puee, nín̄ ŋáaḿmaan míǹ ki téetí m-pu.».

eng: He did the same thing with the cup, after they had eaten, saying, This cup is the new covenant in my blood. Every time you drink it, do this to remember me.
local: Bi jíń tijikaal ki dóòyee ní u tí yōoǹ ndaam ní ki bí yii: «Ndaam nee di sá m-fatikuu un làá nyēe, an di sá mpèéyee daaŋku. Ní nīn ŋáań míǹ ki nyù ki téetí m-pu.».

eng: Every time you eat this bread and drink this cup, you broadcast the death of the Lord until he comes.
local: Di sá yii, iyunti in kɔkɔ ní ni yaa bíi ŋmóò kpɔnɔ nee ki nyù ndaam neeyee, ni bí ki sòoǹ ti-Dindaan kúm pu dee kí dāá sāā ŋyunti ŋun u làá fàtīí gítińee.

eng: This is why those who eat the bread or drink the cup of the Lord inappropriately will be guilty of the Lords body and blood.
local: An puee, ubɔ yaa kaa bàà máaǹ kí ŋmɔ́ kpɔnɔ kí nyɔ̀ ndaam gbanti ní ki yaa ŋmɔ́ ki nyùn̄ee, u yīntiǹ ti-Dindaan wunti nì u-fatikuu.

eng: Each individual should test himself or herself, and eat from the bread and drink from the cup in that way.
local: Míǹ puee, báà ŋma ń cɔ̄ŋkì u-ba kí wàatī ní kí nín ŋmɔ́ kpɔnɔ kí nyɔ̀ ndaam gbanti.

eng: Those who eat and drink without correctly understanding the body are eating and drinking their own judgment.
local: Kun puee un di yaa ŋmɔ́ kpɔnɔ ki nyùn̄ ndaam kaa nyí ti-Dindaan wunti bɔti taapuee, u nín ŋmɔ́ ki nyùn̄ mimmee, ditafadaaldi ní u dáaĺ ki tɔ́ u-ba pu.

eng: Now I want you to know that the head of every man is Christ, and the head of the woman is the man, and the head of Christ is God.
local: Ama nì míǹ kɔkɔee, tibɔti tin m là ní bééyee sèé: Kriisitoo sá uninja kamaa yidaaŋwu, ní unimpu yidaaŋwu sá u-cal, ní Kriisitoo yidaaŋwu sá Unimbɔti.

eng: Because of this, many of you are weak and sick, and quite a few have died.
local: An pu ní ni-kansikin biba sá bibunliibi nì bideembi, ní tikpil mun kpíì.

eng: But if we had judged ourselves, we wouldnt be judged.
local: Ti yaa cɔ́ŋkì ti-bee, Unimbɔti kaa dátí ti-tafal.

eng: However, we are disciplined by the Lord when we are judged so that we wont be judged and condemned along with the whole world.
local: Ama ti-Dindaan yaa dátí ti-tafalee, an sá kí sāŋkì-ti, án nín làá ŋá pu tí taa láa nīn bí ditafadaakpaandi din poon duulinya nib làá dāá kɔ́ee nee.

eng: For these reasons, my brothers and sisters, when you get together to eat, wait for each other.
local: An puee nnabiyaamu, ni yaa kpáfì kí jī ti-Dindaan jikaatee, cīitímaan tɔb.

eng: If some of you are hungry, they should eat at home so that getting together doesnt lead to judgment. I will give directions about the other things when I come.
local: Un ní ŋkùm yaa cááyee, udaan ń jīń u-dumpu, án nín làá ŋá pu ní taa nín̄ kpáfí ki dátí ditafadaaldi ki tòó ni-ba puee. An yaa sá tibɔti tin kíǹee, m yaa láa dɔ́mińee ní m dāa dàkà-ni-tì.

eng: Every man who prays or prophesies with his head covered shames his head.
local: Uninja di yaa bí ki mèé àá Unimbɔti tīī-u dinyɔɔbundi diba ní u bíi nàatī ní ki cíkí tiba u-yil puee, u kpèé Kriisitoo fám dee.

eng: Every woman who prays or prophesies with her head uncovered disgraces her head. It is the same thing as having her head shaved.
local: Ama unimpu di yaa bí ki mèé Unimbɔti àá Unimbɔti tīī-u dinyɔɔbundi diba ú nàatī ní tiba kaa bīī u-yil puee, u kpèé u-cal un sá u-yidaaŋwuee fám dee. An bí kii u tú diyibaakpandee ní.

eng: If a woman doesnt cover her head, then she should have her hair cut off. If it is disgraceful for a woman to have short hair or to be shaved, then she should keep her head covered.
local: Ama unimpu yaa kaa là kí dū tiba kí bìin̄ u-yil puee, ú cā u-yikpiti àá ú kòol̀. Diyikpical àá dikooldi yaa tí cáá-u icooyaaa, ú nīn bìintí tiba u-yil pu.

eng: A man shouldnt have his head covered, because he is the image and glory of God; but the woman is mans glory.
local: Uninja kaa máaǹ kí bìin̄ tiba u-yil pu, di sá yii úǹ sá Unimbɔti naŋku, ní ki sá u-nyooti. Ní unimpu sá uninja nyooti.

eng: Man didnt have his origin from woman, but woman from man.
local: Di sá yii an kaa sá unimpu pu ní Unimbɔti lēē tiba ki náań uninja, ama an sá uninja pu ní u lēē tiba ki dūu náań unimpu.

eng: and man wasnt created for the sake of the woman, but the woman for the sake of the man.
local: An kaa tí sá unimpu pu ní u náań uninja, ama an sá uninja pu ní u náań unimpu.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I dont want you to be ignorant about spiritual gifts.
local: Nnabiyaamu, m là ní béé Unimbɔti Fam nín pūǹ dipɔl diŋyee bɔti.

eng: performance of miracles to another, prophecy to another, the ability to tell spirits apart to another, different kinds of tongues to another, and the interpretation of the tongues to another.
local: Unimbɔti Fam pūǹ ubɔ mpɔn ú nīn ŋáań maamaacib, ní kí pɔ̄ ubɔ mun ú nīn nàatī Unimbɔti nyɔɔbundi, kí pɔ̄ ubɔ mun ú nīn nyí kí gàntī Unimbɔti Fam tundi nì arasiniibi tuuŋŋi, ní kí pɔ̄ ubɔ ní úǹ sòoǹ isooncaan kupaau, ní kí pɔ̄ ubɔ ní úǹ ń gbìl̄ isooncaan taapu kí nàatī.

eng: All these things are produced by the one and same Spirit who gives what he wants to each person.
local: Unimbɔti Fam mbaantiimee di tūǹ an tuuŋŋi kɔkɔ. Mì nín lēē miŋyee ní mì yàkatī mi-pɔl ki tìì báà ŋma.

eng: Christ is just like the human body—a body is a unit and has many parts; and all the parts of the body are one body, even though there are many.
local: Kii tiwun nín sá tibaantiil ki cáá ajan kupaau puee, nì min kɔkɔ ajan gbanti bí a-ba a-ba, ŋi-kɔkɔ kpáfì ki ŋá tiwumbaantiilee, míǹ ní ti nì Kriisitoo sá.

eng: We were all baptized by one Spirit into one body, whether Jew or Greek, or slave or free, and we all were given one Spirit to drink.
local: Míǹ ní bin sá Juuda yabee nì bin kaa sée, nì bin sá iyumbuee nì bin kaa sée, Unimbɔti Fam mbaantiim ní bi fùl̄ ti-kɔkɔ ti kpántì tiwumbaantiil. Míǹ ní Unimbɔti tīī ti-kɔkɔ u-Fam gbanti mbaantiim.

eng: Certainly the body isnt one part but many.
local: Tiwun kaa sá mbaantiim, an yākatì ajan kupaau ní.

eng: If the foot says, Im not part of the body because Im not a hand, does that mean its not part of the body?.
local: Ditaal yaa bí yii: «Man kaa sá ŋŋal, an puee maa kútì tiwun pu», mimmee dàa lafun kútì tiwun puɔɔɔ?.

eng: If the ear says, Im not part of the body because Im not an eye, does that mean its not part of the body?.
local: Àá ditafal yaa bí yii: «Man kaa sá dinimbil, míǹ puee maa kútì tiwun pu», mimmee dàa lafun kútì tiwun puɔɔɔ?.

eng: If the whole body were an eye, what would happen to the hearing? And if the whole body were an ear, what would happen to the sense of smell?.
local: Tiwun kɔkɔ yaa sá dinimbilee, tì bàà ŋáań mana kí nīn gbìl̀? Àá tiwun kɔkɔ yaa bàà sá ditafalee, mana ní tì bàà ŋáań kí nīn gbìl̀ nsɔnsɔfi?.

eng: But as it is, God has placed each one of the parts in the body just like he wanted.
local: Ama min Unimbɔti lèe ní u kāaǹ tiwun jaŋŋi tiwun pu.

eng: If all were one and the same body part, what would happen to the body?.
local: Ajan kɔkɔ yaa dūu ŋá dijambaantiilee, tiwun kaa bàà bí deeyaa!.

eng: You know that when you were Gentiles you were often misled by false gods that cant even speak.
local: Ni téetí yii ni nín kaa nìn dáá nóó Yeesuee, an nìn dátí-ni ní ki cáǹ awaa ŋin bi máń ŋaa gbìl̀ kaa sòoǹee cee.

eng: But as it is, there are many parts but one body.
local: Tiŋmanee unil wunti jaŋŋi bí kupaau, ama ní ki sá tiwumbaantiil.

eng: So the eye cant say to the hand, I dont need you, or in turn, the head cant say to the feet, I dont need you.
local: An puee, dinimbil kaa ŋūǹ kí lì ŋŋal yii: «Maa là-si,» míǹ ní diyil mun kaa tí ŋūǹ kí lì ataa yii: «Maa là-ni.».

eng: Instead, the parts of the body that people think are the weakest are the most necessary.
local: Unil wunti jaŋŋi ŋin bi kpèé yii ŋaa pɔ̄ɔ̄yee, ŋíǹ di nín kèetī.

eng: The parts of the body that we think are less honorable are the ones we honor the most. The private parts of our body that arent presentable are the ones that are given the most dignity.
local: Ní ŋin ti kpèé ki ŋèen̄ee, ŋíǹ ní ti tìì tinyool, ní ŋin bɔti taa lèe, ŋíǹ ní ti kpèé tiŋan.

eng: The parts of our body that are presentable dont need this. But God has put the body together, giving greater honor to the part with less honor.
local: Ama ajan ŋin sá amɔntee kaa nyàab̀ bí tīī-ŋi tinyool, Unimbɔti ŋá tiwun míǹ kí tīī dijandi din lóò tinyoolee tinyool ní.

eng: so that there wont be division in the body and so the parts might have mutual concern for each other.
local: án nín làá ŋá pu digàntil ń taa nín̄ bí tiwun jaŋŋi kansikin ŋi-kɔkɔ ń nīn sūǹ tɔb puee.

eng: If one part suffers, all the parts suffer with it; if one part gets the glory, all the parts celebrate with it.
local: Falaa yaa cúú dijandi dibee, an cúú ki kpáaǹ ajan kɔkɔ dee. Bi yaa tí bíi pàkà dijandi dibee, ŋin kɔkɔ kíǹee fàal̀ ní.

eng: You are the body of Christ and parts of each other.
local: Ni-kɔkɔ sá Kriisitoo wunti ní, ní ni-kansikin báà ŋma sá tiwun jandi diba.

eng: In the church, God has appointed first apostles, second prophets, third teachers, then miracles, then gifts of healing, the ability to help others, leadership skills, different kinds of tongues.
local: Míǹ ní Unimbɔti péé péé ki kāaǹ akpambal Kriisitoo nikitil kansikin, nliliitiimee, Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi, ntataatiimee, bidakaliibi, boonee ní u dū bin kíǹee ki páaǹ. U pú biba bí nīn ŋáań maamaacib, ki pú biba bí nīn cāantí bibunliibi, ní ki tīī biba ŋŋmam bí nīn tútúǹ binib, ní ki pú biba bí nīn kpòotī binib, ní ki pú biba bí nīn sòoǹ isooncaan kupaau.

eng: All arent apostles, are they? All arent prophets, are they? All arent teachers, are they? All dont perform miracles, do they?.
local: Bi-kɔkɔ di sá akpambalaaa? Bi-kɔkɔ di sá Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibaaa? Bi-kɔkɔ di sá bidakaliibaaa? Bi-kɔkɔ di ŋáań maamaacibaaa?.

eng: So I want to make it clear to you that no one says, Jesus is cursed! when speaking by Gods Spirit, and no one can say, Jesus is Lord, except by the Holy Spirit.
local: An pu ní m là ní béé yii unil un Unimbɔti Fam kpòotēe kaa ŋūǹ kí lì yii mbusu ń jēetí Yeesu pu, míǹ ní ubɔ kaa tí ŋūǹ kí lì yii: «Yeesu sá ti-Dindaan» Unimbɔti Fam yaa kaa kpōotì udaan.

eng: All dont have gifts of healing, do they? All dont speak in different tongues, do they? All dont interpret, do they?.
local: Bi-kɔkɔ ní Unimbɔti pú bí nīn cāantí bibunliibaaa? Bi-kɔkɔ di sòoǹ isooncaanaaa? Bi-kɔkɔ di gbìl̀ isooncaan taapu ki nàatāaa?.

eng: Use your ambition to try to get the greater gifts. And Im going to show you an even better way.
local: An puee, ní nyàab̀ Unimbɔti ń pɔ̄-ni dipɔl din jítiǹ ŋin kíǹeeyee. Ama m làá gíti dàkà-ni nsan ŋun jítiǹ i-kɔkɔee.

eng: There are different spiritual gifts but the same Spirit.
local: Unimbɔti Fam nín pūǹ dipɔl diŋyee bí u-ba u-ba, ama Unimbɔti Fam mbaantiimee di tìì míǹ.

eng: and there are different ministries and the same Lord.
local: Ní báà ŋma tundi nyēē di-ba di-ba, ama ní ti-kɔkɔ tūǹ ki tìì ti-Dindaan ubaanti.

eng: and there are different activities but the same God who produces all of them in everyone.
local: Ní báà ŋma cáá min ní u tūǹ u-tundee, ama Unimbɔtibaanti di yàntī ti-kɔkɔ ŋūǹ ki ŋáań míǹ.

eng: A demonstration of the Spirit is given to each person for the common good.
local: Unimbɔti Fam tìì binib dipɔl ní bi tūǹ atuun an tútúǹ báà ŋma.

eng: A word of wisdom is given by the Spirit to one person, a word of knowledge to another according to the same Spirit.
local: Unimbɔti Fam pūǹ ubɔ iciin ú sòoǹ tibɔti, míǹ mbaantiim ní mì tí pūǹ ubɔ mun dinyil ú sòoǹ tibɔti.

eng: faith to still another by the same Spirit, gifts of healing to another in the one Spirit.
local: Unimbɔti Famee mbaantiim di pūǹ ubɔ fookii, ní ki pūǹ ubɔ mun ú nīn ŋūǹ ki cāantí iween.

eng: If I speak in tongues of human beings and of angels but I dont have love, Im a clanging gong or a clashing cymbal.
local: Báà m yaa sòoǹ isooncaan, binib nì ituun sooŋyi, ní kaa cáá dineendee, tin m sòoǹee sá fám ní kii dawula àá diwaŋkilindi nín sūǹ pu fám an kaa cáá taapu puee ní.

eng: but when the perfect comes, what is partial will be brought to an end.
local: Ama tikɔkɔ yaa láa dɔ́ntì ki ŋá mmɔmmee, tin nìn wɔ̀b̄ee làá dāá wāŋkì.

eng: When I was a child, I used to speak like a child, reason like a child, think like a child. But now that I have become a man, Ive put an end to childish things.
local: M nín nìn dáá sá kibikee, m nìn sòoǹ kii kibikee, ní ki dàkafī kii kibikee, ní ki kítí m-bɔti kii kibikee. Ama, m nín wíiǹ ki kpántì ukpaanee, m fá m nín nìn sá kibiki ki ŋáań min miŋyee.

eng: Now we see a reflection in a mirror; then we will see face-to-face. Now I know partially, but then I will know completely in the same way that I have been completely known.
local: Kɔŋkɔnnee, ti nín dáá wāĺ puee bí pùuu kii bi nín kpèé digitikpil poon ki wāĺ puee ní, ama ŋyunti dòoń, ti làá dāá wāl̀ bɔ́iii. Díǹ pu nee, maa nyí ki dóò, ama ŋyunti dòoń, m làá dāá béé nyíki nyiki kii Unimbɔti nín nyí-mi pu nan ní.

eng: Now faith, hope, and love remain—these three things—and the greatest of these is love.
local: Kɔŋkɔnnee tiwan tita di bí: fookii, dimákal nì dineendi, ama tiwan titee kansikinee, dineendi di līī.

eng: If I have the gift of prophecy and I know all the mysteries and everything else, and if I have such complete faith that I can move mountains but I dont have love, Im nothing.
local: M ŋūǹ kí fōō dipɔl kí nīn nàatī Unimbɔti nyɔɔbundi, ki ŋūǹ kí nīn cáá dinyil kɔkɔ nì di-nyɔkɔm kí béé tin kɔkɔ bí dibalinee, kí nīn cáá fookii tiŋan án nīn kpìkitī ajoon, míǹ kɔkɔee m yaa kaa cáá dineendee, m sá fám ní.

eng: If I give away everything that I have and hand over my own body to feel good about what Ive done but I dont have love, I receive no benefit whatsoever.
local: Báà m yaa dū tiwan tin m cááyee kɔkɔ ki yākatì bifalaadambi, ki yaa dū m-ba gba ki tīī bi wùn̄ ŋŋmi, ama ní kaa cáá dineendee, tin kɔkɔ m ŋée sá fám ní, maa cáá ipaati.

eng: Love is patient, love is kind, it isnt jealous, it doesnt brag, it isnt arrogant.
local: Un cáá dineendee cáá kimɔtii ní ki cáá tiniti. Waa cáá nnimbitaataam, kaa dàkà u-ba, kaa cáá dilambil.

eng: it isnt rude, it doesnt seek its own advantage, it isnt irritable, it doesnt keep a record of complaints.
local: Un cáá dineendee kaa ŋáań min kaa kítèe, kaa cáá ikummunti, kaa cáá diŋuul, kaa díkì unil pu.

eng: it isnt happy with injustice, but it is happy with the truth.
local: Tin kaa bí deedeeyee kaa tìì un cáá dineendee dipɔɔpindi, see tin bí deedeeyee.

eng: Love puts up with all things, trusts in all things, hopes for all things, endures all things.
local: Úǹ pūǹ binib yìntitam kɔkɔ ní, ki cáá fookii nì dimákal, nì kimɔtii ŋyunti kamaa.

eng: Love never fails. As for prophecies, they will be brought to an end. As for tongues, they will stop. As for knowledge, it will be brought to an end.
local: Unimbɔti nyɔɔbundi nàatitam làá dāá gbèen̄, isooncaan sòontam làá dāá gbèen̄, dinyil làá dāá kúntí, ama dineendi kpá kí kúntí jaanjaan.

eng: We know in part and we prophesy in part.
local: Di sá ti-nyil din ti nyée cáá di-gbeennyɔkɔm ní, míǹ ní Unimbɔti nyɔɔbundi nàatitam mun cáá mi-gbèenlaŋki, kun puee taa nyí ki dóò, kaa tí ŋūǹ kí sòoǹ kí dóó tikɔkɔ mun.

eng: Pursue love, and use your ambition to try to get spiritual gifts but especially so that you might prophesy.
local: An puee ní péé péé kí nyàab̀ dineendi, kí nyàab̀ dipɔl din Unimbɔti Fam pūǹee, cilinjee: nyàab̀maan Unimbɔti nyɔɔbundi nàatitam pɔl.

eng: It will be as if you are speaking into the air! There are probably many language families in the world, and none of them are without meaning.
local: M nyí yii isoon nín bí i-ba i-ba puee wīikí duulinya poon, ama isoon iba kpá kaa cáá taapu.

eng: So if I dont know the meaning of the language, then I will be like a foreigner to those who speak it, and they will be like foreigners to me.
local: Ama, ubɔ yaa bíi sòoǹ nì mi isooncaanee, udaan sá man cee unicaan ní ma mun sá úǹ cee unicaan.

eng: The same holds true for you: since you are ambitious for spiritual gifts, use your ambition to try to work toward being the best at building up the church.
local: Kii ni nín bí ki nyàab̀ Unimbɔti Fam pɔl puee, ní nyàab̀ din làá yàntī Kriisitoo nikitil ń cù nyɔkɔpu nnimbɔtisan poonee tikpil.

eng: Therefore, those who speak in a tongue should pray to be able to interpret.
local: An puee un sòoǹ isooncaanee ń mèè Unimbɔti ń pɔ̄-u ŋŋmam ú nīn nàatī tin u sòoǹee taapu.

eng: If I pray in a tongue, my spirit prays but my mind isnt productive.
local: Di sá yii m yaa bíi mèé nì isooncaanee, an sá m-fam di mèé, ní m-landɔkɔŋu kaa bíi ŋáań diba.

eng: What should I do? Ill pray in the Spirit, but Ill pray with my mind too; Ill sing a psalm in the Spirit, but Ill sing the psalm with my mind too.
local: Mimmee, an máaǹ ḿ mèè mana? M làá mèè nì m-fam ní kí mèè nì m-landɔkɔŋu mun binib ń gbìl̄ taapu, kí gāā ilan nì m-fam ní kí tí dū m-landɔkɔŋu kí gāā binib ń gbìl̄ taapu.

eng: After all, if you praise God in the Spirit, how will the people who arent trained in that language say Amen! to your thanksgiving, when they dont know what you are saying?.
local: M yaa bíi mèé ki pàkà Unimbɔti nì ŋfamin ŋmaniŋma, mana ní ubɔ un kaa gbìl̀ taapuee ŋūǹ kí kíí yii «Ameeni», ŋyunti ŋun ní waa gbìl̀ tin a sòoǹee taapuee?.

eng: You may offer a beautiful prayer of thanksgiving, but the other person is not being built up.
local: Tiŋman a-wimmeem gbanti bàà ŋān̄, ama an kaa tútúǹ a-juti.

eng: I thank God that I speak in tongues more than all of you.
local: M jáaḿ Unimbɔti, di sá yii m sòoǹ isooncaan ki jítiǹ ni-kɔkɔ.

eng: But in the church Id rather speak five words in my right mind than speak thousands of words in a tongue so that I can teach others.
local: Ama Kriisitoo nikitil kansikinee, an yaa sá anyɔɔbun aŋmoo ní ḿ sòoǹ binib ń gbìl̄ taapu án tūtuǹ-bee, m néeń míǹ ki jítiǹ ḿ sòoǹ isooncaan poon anyɔɔbun kutukub saalaa.

eng: This is because those who speak in a tongue dont speak to people but to God; no one understands it—they speak mysteries by the Spirit.
local: Un sòoǹ isooncaanee kaa bíi sòoǹ nì binib, u sòoǹ nì Unimbɔti ní, kun puee binib kaa gbìl̀ tin u sòoǹee taapu. Unimbɔti Fam pɔŋŋu di yàntī u sòoǹ tin bí dibalinee.

eng: Brothers and sisters, dont be like children in the way you think. Well, be babies when it comes to evil, but be adults in your thinking.
local: Nnabiyaamu, ní taa nín̄ dàkafī kii mbiyaamu nín dàkafī puee, nín̄ cáámaan binicikitikaabi landɔkɔŋu. Ama ikpiti jandi púee, ní nīn bí kii mbiyaawaamuee kí taa nín̄ ŋáań ikpiti.

eng: In the Law it is written: I will speak to this people with foreign languages and foreigners lips, but they will not even listen to me this way , says the Lord.
local: An ŋmàn̄ Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon yii: ti-Dindaan lī yii: «M làá dāá dī binicambi biba pu ní bí sòoǹ nì m-samaau nee inicansoon, ama nì míǹ kɔkɔee, baa làá pīlifiǹ-mi.».

eng: So then, tongues are a sign for those who dont believe, not for those who believe. But prophecy is a sign for believers, not for those who dont believe.
local: An puee Unimbɔti Fam nín tìì isooncaan iŋyee sá Unimbɔti daaŋku kubɔ ní ki tīī bin kaa nóó Yeesuee, an kaa sá ki tīī bin nóó Yeesuee. Ama kí fōō tibɔti Unimbɔti cee kí dūú nàatēe di sá Unimbɔti daaŋku ki tīī bin nóó Yeesuee, ní kaa sá ki tīī bin kaa nyí Yeesuee.

eng: So suppose that the whole church is meeting and everyone is speaking in tongues. If people come in who are outsiders or unbelievers, wont they say that you are out of your minds?.
local: An yaa sá yii Kriisitoo nikitil kɔkɔ yaa kpáfì kí jāam̀ Unimbɔti ní báà ŋma bí ki sòoǹ isooncaanee, un kaa nyí Yeesuee àá un pée kóoń kí pīlifèe yaa gbìl̄ ni nín sòoǹ miŋyee, waa yíì-ni biwaatiibaaa?.

eng: But if everyone is prophesying when an unbeliever or outsider comes in, they are tested by all and called to account by all.
local: Ama ni-kɔkɔ yaa bí ki fòol̄ Unimbɔti cee tibɔti ki nàatēe, án làá gbēē un kaa nyí Unimbɔtee àá un pée kóoń kí pīlifèe pɔbil ní ú béé yii u sá ukpiti, kun puee u gbìl̄ tin ni sòoǹee taapu.

eng: The secrets of their hearts are brought to light. When that happens, they will fall on their faces and worship God, proclaiming out loud that truly God is among you!.
local: ní ki wál̀ u-landɔkɔi in bál̀ u-pɔbilinee. Niinee ní ú tātìi dòon̄ dicincibil Unimbɔti nimbiin nì ijaam ní kí bí yii: «Tiŋman, Unimbɔti lafun bí ni-kansikin.».

eng: What is the outcome of this, brothers and sisters? When you meet together, each one has a psalm, a teaching, a revelation, a tongue, or an interpretation. All these things must be done to build up the church.
local: Mimmee nnabiyaamu, an sá tí ŋá mana ní? Ni yaa kpáfì kí jāam̀ Unimbɔtee, ubɔ ń gāā ilan, ubɔ ń dàkà Unimbɔti bɔti. Unimbɔti yaa dàkā ubɔ tiba, ú sòoǹ, ní ubɔ ń sòoǹ isooncaan, ní ubɔ ń nàatī isooncaanee taapu. Tin báà ŋma làá ŋée ń tūtuǹ Kriisitoo nikitil ń cù nyɔkɔpu nnimbɔtisan poon.

eng: If some speak in a tongue, then let two or at most three speak, one at a time, and someone must interpret.
local: Ni yaa làá sòoǹ isooncaanee, án nīn sá binib bili kii bita, bí nīn sòoǹ ubɔ ubɔ, boonee ubɔ ń nàatī tin bi sòon̄ee taapu.

eng: However, if there is no interpreter, then they should keep quiet in the meeting. They should speak privately to themselves and to God.
local: Ubɔ yaa kaa bí kí nàatī tin bi sòoǹee taapuee, un bíi sòoǹ isooncaanee ń ŋmīĺ kí nīn kíǹ ki sòoǹ úǹ nì Unimbɔti kansikin ŋmaniŋma.

eng: In the case of prophets, let two or three speak and have the rest evaluate what is said.
local: Bin fòol̄ tibɔti Unimbɔti cee ki nàatēe máaǹ kí nīn bí bili kii bita ní bin kíǹee ń nīn pílifí ki dāantí tin bi sòoǹee.

eng: Those who prophesy speak to people, building them up, and giving them encouragement and comfort.
local: Ama un fòol̄ Unimbɔti cee tibɔti ki nàatēe sòoǹ nì binib ní, ní bi-nimbɔtinóotam mɔ̀tīi cá nyɔkɔpu ki tìì-bi kunyannyaau ki sɔ̀ŋkī bi-pɔbiliŋ.

eng: And if some revelation comes to someone else who is sitting down, the first one should be quiet.
local: Ama Unimbɔti yaa nyántì tibɔti tiba ki dàkā ni-kansikin ubɔee, un bíi sòoǹee ń ŋmīĺ.

eng: You can all prophesy one at a time so that everyone can learn and be encouraged.
local: Ni ŋūǹ ni-kɔkɔ ń nīn nàatī Unimbɔti nyɔɔbundi ubɔ ubɔ án nín làá ŋá pu ní béé kí ká kunyannyaauee.

eng: The spirits of prophets are under the control of the prophets.
local: Ubɔnaatil máaǹ kí nīn cáá Ŋfam min tìì-u tibɔti u cáa nàatēe pu mpɔn.

eng: God isnt a God of disorder but of peace. Like in all the churches of Gods people.
local: Kun puee Unimbɔti kaa sá fikil fikil Nimbɔtiu, ama ŋgbansɔŋfi Nimbɔtiu ní u sá. Kii Unimbɔti nikitiŋ kɔkɔ poon an nín pée sá puee.

eng: the women should be quiet during the meeting. They are not allowed to talk. Instead, they need to get under control, just as the Law says.
local: Kriisitoo nikitil kansikinee, binimpoobi máaǹ kí nīn kā ki ŋmí ní. Baa cáá nsan kí sòoǹ tiba. Bi máaǹ kí sìntīǹ bi-ba taapu kii Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon nín lī puee.

eng: If they want to learn something, they should ask their husbands at home. It is disgraceful for a woman to talk during the meeting.
local: Bi yaa kaa gbìl̄ tiba taapuee, bí tin bàlifì bi-calib mmɔŋki. An kaa ŋān̄ binimpoobi ń sòoǹ tiba Unimbɔti nikitiŋ kansikin.

eng: Did the word of God originate with you? Has it come only to you?.
local: Nimbi cee ní Unimbɔti bɔti nyáńaaa? Àá nimbi baba cee ní Unimbɔti bɔti dɔ́miń ní?.

eng: If anyone thinks that they are prophets or spiritual people, then let them recognize that what Im writing to you is the Lords command.
local: Un yaa nyí yii u sá Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu àá Unimbɔti Fam tīī-u dipɔl dibee máaǹ kí béé yii tibɔti tin m ŋmàn̄ nee sá ti-Dindaan marau ní.

eng: If someone doesnt recognize this, they arent recognized.
local: Un kaa béè yii an sá ti-Dindaan marauee, ní yàntī udaan bɔti.

eng: So then, brothers and sisters, use your ambition to try to get the gift of prophecy, but dont prevent speaking in tongues.
local: An puee nnabiyaamu, nyàab̀maan Unimbɔti ń tīī-ni dipɔl din làá yàntī ní nīn fòol̄ u-cee tibɔti ki nàatēe, ama kí taa pìin̄ bin sòoǹ isooncaanee nsan.

eng: People who speak in a tongue build up themselves; those who prophesy build up the church.
local: Un sòoǹ isooncaanee, úǹ baba ní an tútúǹ, ama un fòol̄ Unimbɔti cee tibɔti ki nàatēe tútúǹ Kriisitoo nikitil kɔkɔ dí cù nyɔkɔpu ní.

eng: Everything should be done with dignity and in proper order.
local: Ní nīn ŋáań tikɔkɔ án nīn kítí ki dī nì nsan.

eng: I wish that all of you spoke in tongues, but Id rather you could prophesy. Those who prophesy are more important than those who speak in tongues, unless they are able to interpret them so that the church might be built up.
local: M là báà ŋma ń nīn sòoǹ isooncaan, ama tin m baasii nyàab̀ ní nīn cááyee di sá ní nīn fòol̄ Unimbɔti cee tibɔti ki nàatī. Di sá un fòol̄ Unimbɔti cee tibɔti ki nàatēe jítiǹ un sòoǹ isooncaanee, asee ubɔ yaa bí ki ŋūǹ kí nàatī isooncaan taapu án tūtuǹ báà ŋma ń cù nyɔkɔpu baba.

eng: After all, brothers and sisters, if I come to you speaking in tongues, how will I help you unless I speak to you with a revelation, some knowledge, a prophecy, or a teaching?.
local: Nnabiyaamu, tí dū tibɔti nee kí ŋmàntī: tí lì yii m dɔ́miń ni-cee ní ki bí ki sòoǹ nì ni isooncaan in naa gbìl̀ee. Tin m sòoǹee yaa kaa kútì-ni tiba, àá kaa cántì, àá naa béè tiba poon, àá naa gbìl̄ Unimbɔti nín tīī dinyɔɔbundi diŋyee, àá kaa fōō didàkal diba poonee, mana ní án làá tūtuǹ-ni?.

eng: Likewise, things that arent alive like a harp or a lyre can make a sound, but if there arent different notes in the sounds they make, how will the tune from the harp or the lyre be recognized?.
local: Tí tí dū kí ŋmàntī nì tigbiŋgbiiliwan diwul àá kubeeu. Tiwanee liitil siim yaa kaa nyēē u-ba u-bee, mana ní bi ŋūǹ kí gàntī kí béé yii diwul di sūǹ nee, àá kubeeu di sūǹ nee?.

eng: And if a trumpet call is unrecognizable, then who will prepare for battle?.
local: Ukantámbunli yaa kaa búń dijanjakantándi ki cántì, mana ní bin kíǹee làá béé kí bɔ́ntí kujaau?.

eng: Its the same way with you: If you dont use language that is easy to understand when you speak in a tongue, then how will anyone understand what is said?.
local: Míǹ mbaantiim, ni yaa bíi sòoǹ isoon in baa gbìl̀ee, bi làá ŋá mana ní kí gbìl̄ taapu? Mimmee ni sòoǹ ki féeń dee.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I want to call your attention to the good news that I preached to you, which you also received and in which you stand.
local: Nnabiyaamu, m téetí-ni Tibɔmɔntiil tin m nìn sòon̄ nì ni, ni fōō ki kíí ki sí ti-puee bɔti.

eng: I am what I am by Gods grace, and Gods grace hasnt been for nothing. In fact, I have worked harder than all the others—that is, it wasnt me but the grace of God that is with me.
local: Unimbɔti ŋanti pu ní m sá kun m sá díǹ nee. Tiŋan tin u ŋá-mee cáá mpɔn ki yāntì m túń ditundi ki jítiǹ akpambal ŋin kíǹee. An kaa sá man m-ba di túń an tuuŋŋi, an sá Unimbɔti ŋanti tin bí m-nee di túń ditundi gbanti.

eng: So then, whether you heard the message from me or them, this is what we preach and this is what you have believed.
local: An puee an yaa sá mamaaa, an yaa tí sá bin kíǹeeyaaa, tin pu ti sòon̄ee dee, tin ni mun fōō ki kííyee dee.

eng: So if the message that is preached says that Christ has been raised from the dead, then how can some of you say, Theres no resurrection of the dead?.
local: Ti yaa sòon̄ ki tùkū-ni yii Unimbɔti fīkitì Kriisitoo ditaŋkpiilinee, mana ní ni-kansikin biba lì yii bitaŋkpiibi kaa bí kí fìkitī?.

eng: If theres no resurrection of the dead, then Christ hasnt been raised either.
local: Bitaŋkpiibi yaa kaa fìkitī ditaŋkpiilinee, mimmee Kriisitoo mun kaa fīkitì deeyaa!.

eng: If Christ hasnt been raised, then our preaching is useless and your faith is useless.
local: Kriisitoo yaa kaa bàà fīkitèe, Tibɔmɔntiil tin ti sòoǹee bàà sá fám ní, ní ni mun nín fōō ki kíí miŋyee ŋá fám.

eng: We are found to be false witnesses about God, because we testified against God that he raised Christ, when he didnt raise him if its the case that the dead arent raised.
local: Áli gba ti bàà sá binyimɔndambi, kun puee ti mɔ́ntì ki túǹ Unimbɔti pu yii u fīkitì Kriisitoo ditaŋkpiilin, kin u mun kaa fīkitì-u, an yaa lafun sá tiŋman yii bitaŋkpiibi kaa fìkitēe.

eng: If the dead arent raised, then Christ hasnt been raised either.
local: Bitaŋkpiibi yaa kaa fìkitēe, mimmee Kriisitoo mun kaa fīkitì dee.

eng: If Christ hasnt been raised, then your faith is worthless; you are still in your sins.
local: Kriisitoo yaa kaa fīkitì ditaŋkpiilinee, mimmee ni-fookiiu sá fám dee, ni dáá gbíntì ki bí ni-kpitii poon dee.

eng: and whats more, those who have died in Christ are gone forever.
local: An yaa sá mimmee, bin nóó Kriisitoo ki kpíìyee wáŋkì.

eng: If we have a hope in Christ only in this life, then we deserve to be pitied more than anyone else.
local: An yaa sá dimaŋfal din poon ti bí nee ŋmaniŋma pu ní ti yaa máká Kriisitoo puee, mimmee timbi falaau jítiǹ binib kɔkɔ yɔu dee.

eng: You are being saved through it if you hold on to the message I preached to you, unless somehow you believed it for nothing.
local: Tibɔmɔntiil gbanti pu ní Unimbɔti fīī-ni. An puee ní nīn gbíntì ki ŋúb́-tì kii m nín nìn sòon̄ nì ni puee. Yaa sá fám, ni fōō ki kíí fám dee.

eng: But in fact Christ has been raised from the dead. Hes the first crop of the harvest of those who have died.
local: Ama Kriisitoo fīkitì ditaŋkpiilin tiŋman. Bitaŋkpiibi bin làá dāá fìkitēe, úǹ di sá upeepeekaa ki fīkitì.

eng: Since death came through a human being, the resurrection of the dead came through one too.
local: Di sá yii unibaanti pu ní ŋkúm dī ki jéetì binib pu. Míǹ mbaantiim ní bitaŋkpiibi fìkititam tí dīń unibaanti pu kí fìkitī báà ŋma.

eng: In the same way that everyone dies in Adam, so also everyone will be given life in Christ.
local: Kii inifunaan kɔkɔ nín cáá mpèé nì Adam ki kúù puee, míǹ mbaantiim ní bin kɔkɔ ŋá ubaanti nì Kriisitooyee làá dāá ká dimaŋfal.

eng: Each event will happen in the right order: Christ, the first crop of the harvest, then those who belong to Christ at his coming.
local: Ama an sá báà ŋma nì u-yuntiŋu ní. Kriisitoo di sá upeepeekaa ki fīkitì ditaŋkpiilin, ní bin nóó Kriisitooyee mun ń dāá fìkitī ŋyunti ŋun u yaa láa fātìi gítińee.

eng: and then the end, when Christ hands over the kingdom to God the Father, when he brings every form of rule, every authority and power to an end.
local: Boonee ní duulinya kúntitam ń nín bàń. Ŋyunti ŋun u yaa láa kúntì ipɔn kɔkɔ nì i-nyɔkɔmee ní ú dū Dibeel kí tīī u-Baa Unimbɔti.

eng: It is necessary for him to rule until he puts all enemies under his feet .
local: Kriisitoo làá jī dibeel míǹ ní kí tin sāā ŋyunti ŋun u làá já kí pɔ̄ɔkiǹ u-nannanliibi kí dū-bi kí ŋá u-taaŋi taapuee.

eng: Death is the last enemy to be brought to an end.
local: U-nannanliu un u làá kúntí katanjooliiyee di sá ŋkúm.

eng: since he has brought everything under control under his feet. When it says that everything has been brought under his control, this clearly means everything except for the one who placed everything under his control.
local: Kun puee an lī Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon yii: «U dū tikɔkɔ ki ŋá u-taaŋi taapu.» Unimbɔti nín lī yii u dū tikɔkɔ ki ŋá u-taaŋi taapuee, kaa sá ki kpáaǹ úǹ Unimbɔti u-ba ní u kpáàa dūu ŋá u-taapu.

eng: But when all things have been brought under his control, then the Son himself will also be under the control of the one who gave him control over everything so that God may be all in all.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun ní tikɔkɔ yaa láa sīntiǹ ti-ba ki tīī Kriisitooyee, niinee ní úǹ Unimbɔti Jipɔɔn mun u-ba ń dāá sìntīǹ u-ba kí tīī Unimbɔti un sīntiǹ tikɔkɔ taapu ki tīī-uee, ní Unimbɔti ń nīn yì tikɔkɔ nì tikɔkɔ.

eng: Otherwise, what are those who are getting baptized for the dead doing? If the dead arent raised, then why are they being baptized for them?.
local: Kpèèmaan bin yàntī bi fùul̀-bi Unimbɔti nyim bin kpíìyee yil puee: bin kúùyee yaa kaa bàà fìkitēe, ba pu ní bi yàntī bi fùul̀-bi Unimbɔti nyim bitaŋkpiibi yil pu?.

eng: I passed on to you as most important what I also received: Christ died for our sins in line with the scriptures.
local: Tin bi dàkā-mee, tíǹ ní m cáań ki dàkā-ni. Tibɔti gbanti poonee, tin páb́ tikpilee di sá yii Kriisitoo kpíì timbi kpitii pu ní, kii Unimbɔti gbɔŋku nín lī puee.

eng: And what about us? Why are we in danger all day every day?.
local: Bi yaa kúù kaa fìkitī ní, ba pu ní ŋyunti kamaayee timbi bí dinimbinaamandin ní?.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I swear by the pride I have in you in Christ Jesus our Lord, Im facing death every day.
local: Nnabiyaamu, baabadalee ŋkúm tà ki kíbití-mi ní. Ni nín nóó ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo puee nín tìì-mi disíndi diŋyee di yì-tì ní m lì míǹ.

eng: From a human point of view, what good does it do me if I fought wild animals in Ephesus? If the dead arent raised, lets eat and drink because tomorrow well die .
local: An yaa sá yii unil yaa kpíì waa ti fìkitēe, mimmee m nín jáń nì kumooun peelii in bí Eefeesee, ba paatii ní m ká? An yaa sá yii bitaŋkpiibi kaa fìkitēe, mimmee càmaan tí ŋá kii dilaŋkpandi nín lī puee yii: «Tí jī kí nyɔ̀maan, kun puee fool ti làá fu kpú.».

eng: Dont be deceived, bad company corrupts good character.
local: Ní taa yàntī ubɔ ń kīil̀-ni: «Ni yaa nóó binib bin bimbim kaa ŋān̄ee, see ni mun ń dāá bīil̀.».

eng: Sober up by acting like you should and dont sin. Some of you are ignorant about God—I say this because you should be ashamed of yourselves!.
local: Ní gítiḿmaan ni-ban kí nīn cáá ilandɔkɔmɔnti kí taa ti ŋá ikpiti. Ni-kansikin biba kaa nyí Unimbɔti. M nín lī pu nee, an sá ni-cooi ní.

eng: But someone will say, How are the dead raised? What kind of body will they have when they come back.
local: Ama ubɔ làá lì yii: «Mana ní bin kpíìyee làá fìkitī? Tiwun tilati ní bi làá dāá nīn cáá dee?».

eng: Look, fool! When you put a seed into the ground, it doesnt come back to life unless it dies.
local: Ugbaan, a yaa búĺ ŋwamboolee, ŋwamboolee yaa kaa kpíìyee, ŋuaa nyēē.

eng: What you put in the ground doesnt have the shape that it will have, but its a bare grain of wheat or some other seed.
local: Kí kūtèe, kun a búĺee sá idi àá iwambool iba ní an nyā ki sá tifaal, kaa ti sá ŋwambool ŋun a búĺee.

eng: God gives it the sort of shape that he chooses, and he gives each of the seeds its own shape.
local: Unimbɔti di tìì ŋwamboolee u nín là ŋú nyā kí nīn bí miŋyee. Úǹ di tìì iwambool kɔkɔ nín máaǹ kí nyā kí nīn bí miŋyee.

eng: All flesh isnt alike. Humans have one kind of flesh, animals have another kind of flesh, birds have another kind of flesh, and fish have another kind.
local: Tiwan tin kɔkɔ cáá kufoouee wunti kaa sá tibaantiil, binib wunti bí ti-ba, ipeel yati bí ti-ba, nnɔmbiyaamu yati bí ti-ba, ní ijɔm mun yati bí ti-ba.

eng: he was buried, and he rose on the third day in line with the scriptures.
local: Bi síb́-u ní iwiin ita dalee, Unimbɔti fīkitì-u, kii an nín ŋmàn̄ pu Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poonee.

eng: There are heavenly bodies and earthly bodies. The heavenly bodies have one kind of glory, and the earthly bodies have another kind of glory.
local: Tiwun tin sá yilpu yatee bí, ní tin mun sá taapu dooyee yati bí. Tin sá yilpuee nyaaŋku nyáń ku-ba nì tin sá taapu dooyee.

eng: The sun has one kind of glory, the moon has another kind of glory, and the stars have another kind of glory .
local: Ŋwìiń nyaaŋku bí ku-ba ní uŋmal mun yɔkɔ bí ku-ba, áli gba iŋmalbijaa kansikinee i-nyaaŋku bí ku-ba ku-ba ní.

eng: Its the same with the resurrection of the dead: a rotting body is put into the ground, but what is raised wont ever decay.
local: Míǹ mbaantiim ní bitaŋkpiibi yaa láa fīkitèe, an dāa bí. Tiwun tin kúùyee ní bi dù kí síb́, ní tí dāá fìkitī nì tiwun tin kaa kúùyee.

eng: Its degraded when its put into the ground, but its raised in glory. Its weak when its put into the ground, but its raised in power.
local: Kí wàatī bí dū-tì kí síb́ee, tàa ŋān̄, kaa pɔ̄ɔ̄ mun. Ama ní tí dāá fìkitī nì kunyaaŋŋu nì mpɔn.

eng: Its a physical body when its put into the ground, but its raised as a spiritual body. If theres a physical body, theres also a spiritual body.
local: Taapu doo wunti ní bi dùú síb́ ní tì yaa fīkitèe, Circir Fam ń dāá tīī-ti dimaŋfal. Míǹ puee, taapu doo wunti bí ní tiwun tin Circir Fam tīī dimaŋfalee mun bí.

eng: So it is also written, The first human, Adam, became a living person, and the last Adam became a spirit that gives life.
local: Kii Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon nín lī puee yii: «Adam unipeepeekaayee, Unimbɔti náań-u ní ki tīī-u dimaŋfal», ama Adam joolkaayee, úǹ sá Ŋfam min tìì dimaŋfalee ní.

eng: But the physical body comes first, not the spiritual one—the spiritual body comes afterward.
local: An kaa sá un nyánní Unimbɔti ceeyee di péetí kí dāan̄, an sá un nyánní taapu dooyee di péetí, ní un nyēeń Unimbɔti ceeyee ń páanní boon.

eng: The first human was from the earth made from dust; the second human is from heaven.
local: Ntam ní Unimbɔti dūu náań Adam peepeekaa ní u sá taapu doo, ama Adam liliitee nyánní Unimbɔti cee ní.

eng: The nature of the person made of dust is shared by people who are made of dust, and the nature of the heavenly person is shared by heavenly people.
local: Binib bin sá taapu doo yabee náań nì un Unimbɔti dū ntam ki náańee ní, ní bin sá yilpu yabee náań nì un nyánní yilpuee.

eng: We will look like the heavenly person in the same way as we have looked like the person made from dust.
local: Ti nì un sá taapu dooyee nín náań pu nee, míǹ mbaantiim ní ti nì un sá yilpuee làá dāá nīn tí náań.

eng: He appeared to Cephas, then to the Twelve.
local: U lēē u-ba ki dàkā Peetroo nì u-kpambaliŋ saalaa nì alee.

eng: This is what Im saying, brothers and sisters: Flesh and blood cant inherit Gods kingdom. Something that rots cant inherit something that doesnt decay.
local: Nnabiyaamu, tin m là kí tùkù-nee sèé: tiwun nì fatiku kaa ŋūǹ kí kɔ́ Unimbɔti Beelin, míǹ ní tin kúùyee kaa ŋūǹ kí fōō tin kaa kúùyee.

eng: Listen, Im telling you a secret: All of us wont die, but we will all be changed.
local: M làá tùkù-ni tibɔti tin nìn bí dibalinee: di sá yii ti-kɔkɔ kaa làá dāá kpú, ama ti-kɔkɔ wunti di làá dāá kpántí.

eng: in an instant, in the blink of an eye, at the final trumpet. The trumpet will blast, and the dead will be raised with bodies that wont decay, and we will be changed.
local: Dikantánjoolkaal yaa láa búńee, niin niin dicilpu kii dinimbil nín pée kábití puee, bitaŋkpiibi kpáàa fīkitì nì tiwun tin kaa ti kúùyee, ní timbi bin kíǹ kaa kpíìyee wunti ń cúú kpántí.

eng: Its necessary for this rotting body to be clothed with what cant decay, and for the body that is dying to be clothed in what cant die.
local: Di sá yii tiwun tin bíiĺee dāa kpántí tin kaa bíiĺee, míǹ ní tiwun tin kúùyee dāa kpántí tin kaa kúùyee.

eng: And when the rotting body has been clothed in what cant decay, and the dying body has been clothed in what cant die, then this statement in scripture will happen: Death has been swallowed up by a victory .
local: Ŋyunti ŋun ní tiwun tin bíiĺee yaa láa kpántì tin kaa bíiĺee, ní tiwun tin kúùyee yaa láa kpántì tin kaa ti kúùyee, ŋyunti gbantee an ŋá kii an nín ŋmàn̄ Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon puee dee, yii: «Bi kúntì ŋkúm, dijajil ŋá dimɔndi.».

eng: Where is your victory, Death? Where is your sting, Death? .
local: «Too, ŋkúm, a-jajil bí la? Too, ŋkúm, a-looti bí la?».

eng: Deaths sting is sin, and the power of sin is the Law.
local: Ŋkúm looti di sá ikpiti, ní ikpiti pɔŋŋu sá mara.

eng: Thanks be to God, who gives us this victory through our Lord Jesus Christ!.
local: Tí jāam̀maan Unimbɔti u nín dìŋfī ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo pu ki tìì-ti dijajil diŋyee pu.

eng: As a result of all this, my loved brothers and sisters, you must stand firm, unshakable, excelling in the work of the Lord as always, because you know that your labor isnt going to be for nothing in the Lord.
local: An puee, m-nabineeŋkaamu, ní nīn ŋúb́ an píkí, kí báań inyaan kí nīn tūm̀maan ti-Dindaan tundi kí taa gbèen̄, kí nīn nyí yii ni-tundi kaa làá gbíntí fám.

eng: and then he appeared to more than five hundred brothers and sisters at once—most of them are still alive to this day, though some have died.
local: Boonee u-boonnooliibi alaataa aŋmoo mun ká-u dicilpu. Bi-kansikin tikpil dáá bí kaa kpíì, ama biba mun kpíì.

eng: Then he appeared to James, then to all the apostles.
local: Boonee ní u lēē u-ba ki dàkā Yakubu, nì akpambal kɔkɔ.

eng: and last of all he appeared to me, as if I were born at the wrong time.
local: Bíǹ kɔkɔ boon kpataaayee, ní u lēē u-ba ki dàkā ma mun, man un bí kii m-ŋmalii kaa bàn̄ ní bi màl̄-mee.

eng: Im the least important of the apostles. I dont deserve to be called an apostle, because I harassed Gods church.
local: Man di sá kiwaai akpambal kansikin. Maa máaǹ bí yíiń-mi Yeesu kpambal báà fiii, kun puee m sāaǹ Unimbɔti nikitil.

eng: Concerning the collection of money for Gods people: You should do what I have directed the churches in Galatia to do.
local: Tibɔti tin m cáá kí tùkù-ni tootoo un ni tòó kí tīī Kriisitoo yab bin bí Jeerusaleemee puee sɔ̀. Ni mun ń nīn ŋáań kii m nín dàkā Kriisitoo nikitiŋ ŋin bí Galaati tiŋkinee puee.

eng: If Timothy comes to you, be sure that he has no reason to be afraid while hes with you, because he does the work of the Lord just like I do.
local: Tiimootee yaa bāań ni-ceeyee, ní fōō-u mmɔntiimin u-pɔbil ń nīn sɔ̀m̀, kun puee u mun tūǹ ti-Dindaan tundi kii mamee ní.

eng: So dont let anyone disrespect him, but send him on in peace so he can join me. Im waiting for him along with the brothers and sisters.
local: Ubɔ ń taa kpèè kí sìŋkīǹ-u, ama ní tūtuǹ-u ú fàtī kí gítiń m-cee nì dipɔɔsɔŋkil, di sá man nì nnabiyaamu mun bí dooyee kā ki cí-u.

eng: Concerning Apollos our brother: I strongly encouraged him to visit you with the brothers and sisters, but he didnt want to go now. Hell come when he has an opportunity.
local: M bàà tīī ti-nabiki Apooloosi kunyannyaau u nì nnabiyaamu mubɔ bí bàà dāan̄ ni-cee, ama waa là kí dāan̄ kɔŋkɔnnee. Ŋyunti ŋun sá ú dāan̄ee yaa láa bāańee, u dāa dòoń.

eng: Stay awake, stand firm in your faith, be brave, be strong.
local: Ní nīn nyí nì ni-ba kí nīn ŋúb́ ni-fookiiu Kriisitoo ni án píkí, kí nīn cáá dipɔbil, kí nīn pɔ̄ɔ̄.

eng: Everything should be done in love.
local: Tin kɔkɔ ni yaa làá ŋée, ní nīn ŋáań nì dineendi.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I encourage you to do something else. You know that the people in Stephanas household were the first crop of the harvest to come from the mission to Achaia. They have dedicated themselves to the service of Gods people.
local: Nnabiyaamu, m làá gíti kūtì-ni tiba: Kéè ni nyí Steefanasi nì u-dicaŋkuɔɔ, ni téetí yii bíǹ di sá bin péé péé ki fōō Yeesu ki kíí Greeki tiŋkin ki tútúǹ Unimbɔti yabee.

eng: So accept the authority of people like them and of anyone who cooperates and works hard.
local: An puee, ní dū ni-ba kí tīī an nibee jutiibi nì bin kɔkɔ bí bi-cee ki tūǹ ditundi nì dipɔbil dibee.

eng: Im so happy that Stephanas, Fortunatus, and Achaicus have arrived; theyve made up for my missing you.
local: Steefanasi nì Fɔrtunatuusi nì Akayikuusi dāantam mɔ́ɔkiǹ-mi, di sá ni nín kpá m-ceeyee, bíǹ di téeǹ ni-yil pu ki ŋáań tin ni yaa bàà bí ni bàà làá ŋá kí tīī-mee.

eng: Indeed theyve provided my spirit and yours with a much-needed rest. Therefore, give them proper recognition.
local: Bi tīī-mi kunyannyaau kii bi nín tīī ni mun puee. Binimɔntiibi bin bí mimmee máaǹ bí pàkà-bi.

eng: The churches in the province of Asia greet you. Aquila and Prisca greet you warmly in the Lord, together with the church that meets in their house.
local: Kriisitoo nikitiŋ ŋin bí Asii timmunee jáaḿ-ni. Akiilasi nì Priisiil nì bin kɔkɔ kōontí bi-dumpu ki jáaḿ Unimbɔtee jáaḿ-ni tiŋan ti-Dindaan pu.

eng: On the first day of the week, each of you should set aside whatever you can afford from what you earn so that the collection wont be delayed until I come.
local: Kɔɔsil kamaayee, báà ŋma ń lēē min u kée dijandi din u làá tīīyee kí yóoń, án taa nín̄ sá yii man ḿ bàń ní ní nín kíĺ animbil ditɔl.

eng: All the brothers and sisters greet you. You in turn should greet each other with a holy kiss.
local: Nnabiyaamu mun kɔkɔ bí dooyee jáaḿ-ni. Ní jāam̀ tɔb nì kunabitiiu jaamii.

eng: Here is my greeting in my own handwriting—Paul.
local: Man Pɔɔl m-ba di dū m-ŋal ki ŋmàn̄ anyɔɔbun nee kí jāam̀-ni.

eng: A curse on anyone who doesnt love the Lord. Come, Lord!.
local: Un kaa là ti-Dindaanee, udaan ń ŋá fám. Maranata – Ti-Dindaan dòoń!.

eng: The grace of the Lord Jesus be with you.
local: Ti-Dindaan Yeesu ŋanti ń nīn bí ni-pu.

eng: My love is with all of you in Christ Jesus.
local: M néeń ni-kɔkɔ Yeesu Kriisitoo pu.

eng: Then when I get there, Ill send whomever you approve to Jerusalem with letters of recommendation to bring your gift.
local: Ní lēē binib biba, m yaa láa bāańee ḿ tīī-bi kugbɔŋu, kí tɔ̄-bi bí cáá ni-tootoou kí tīī Jeerusaleem yab.

eng: If it seems right for me to go too, theyll travel with me.
local: An yaa tí sá yii ḿ cù m-baaa, man nì bi ń nóó kí cù.

eng: Ill come to you after I go through Macedonia, and because Im going through Macedonia.
local: M yaa làá dāan̄ ni-ceeyee, m làá dīń Maseeduwaan tiŋkin ní kí nín bàń-ni, kun puee m là kí dī Maseeduwaan ní.

eng: I may stay with you or even spend the winter there in Corinth so that you can send me on my way to wherever Im off to next.
local: An yaa yíì m làá kàl̄ ni-cee fiii. Ni yaa kaa kpēē gba, ni-cee ní m làá kàl̄ ní tiwool yuntiŋu ń jītí, án nín làá ŋá pu ní tūtuǹ-mi ḿ nìkī m-saŋŋu laakin sá ḿ cùee.

eng: I dont want to make a quick visit to you, since I hope to spend some time with you if the Lord lets it happen.
local: Maa ti là kí dāá kàl̄ ni-cee fiii, ti-Dindaan yaa kííyee, m làá kàl̄ ni-cee kí yúntí ní.

eng: Ill stay here in Ephesus until the Festival of Pentecost.
local: Ama m làá nīn bí Eefeesi tiŋkin ní kí tin sāā Pantikooti jiŋaaliu dal.

eng: In spite of the fact that there are many opponents, a big and productive opportunity has opened up for my mission here.
local: Di sá yii nsan ŋubɔ pīitì ki dōoǹ ḿ dī kí tɔ̄ dituŋkpaandi, nì min kɔkɔ m-nannanliibi wīikí tikpilee.

eng: When I came to you, brothers and sisters, I didnt come preaching Gods secrets to you like I was an expert in speech or wisdom.
local: Nnabiyaamu, m nín nìn dɔ́miń ni-cee ki sòon̄ nì ni Unimbɔti bɔti tin bí dibalinee, maa nìn dū anyɔɔbuŋkpaanaaa, kaa nìn tí dū binib ciiŋyi ní ki sòon̄ nì ni.

eng: God has revealed these things to us through the Spirit. The Spirit searches everything, including the depths of God.
local: Di sá yii timbi ní Unimbɔti dī u-Fam pu ki dàkā-ti abɔɔn gbanti. Di sá yii Unimbɔti Fam gbanti di ŋūǹ kí béé tin Unimbɔti cááyee, ki kpáaǹ tin bí dibalin poon poon kábuee.

eng: Who knows a persons depths except their own spirit that lives in them? In the same way, no one has known the depths of God except Gods Spirit.
local: Ulanil ni-kansikin di ŋūǹ kí béé u-juti landɔkɔŋu? See úǹ u-ba fam min bí u-nee di ŋūǹ kí béé kun u sée. Míǹ mbaantiim ní ubɔ kaa ŋūǹ kí béé Unimbɔti landɔkɔŋu, see Unimbɔti Fam di ŋūǹ kí béé u-landɔkɔŋu.

eng: We havent received the worlds spirit but Gods Spirit so that we can know the things given to us by God.
local: Timbee, an kaa sá duulinya Fam ní ti fōō, an sá Unimbɔti di tīī-ti u-Fam, án nín làá ŋá pu tí béé tiŋan tin kɔkɔ Unimbɔti ŋá-tee.

eng: These are the things we are talking about—not with words taught by human wisdom but with words taught by the Spirit—we are interpreting spiritual things to spiritual people.
local: An puee, anyɔɔbun ŋin ti cáa sòoǹee kaa sá binib nín dàkà iciin iŋyee nyɔɔbuŋŋi, ama an sá anyɔɔbun ŋin Unimbɔti Fam tīī-tee ní ti cáa sòoǹ ki bɔ́bití ibaamɔn in nyēeń Unimbɔti Fam nee ki tùkù bin cáá Unimbɔti Famee.

eng: But people who are unspiritual dont accept the things from Gods Spirit. They are foolishness to them and cant be understood, because they can only be comprehended in a spiritual way.
local: Unil un dàkafī nniyaamin ŋmaniŋmee kaa ŋūǹ kí fōō ibaamɔn in Unimbɔti Fam sòoǹee. Úǹ ceeyee, an sá tiwaatibɔti ní. Waa ŋūǹ kí fōō-tì, kun puee Unimbɔti Fam baba di yàntī unil ń ŋmā kí béé kí gàntī.

eng: Spiritual people comprehend everything, but they themselves arent understood by anyone.
local: Unil un cáá Unimbɔti Famee di ŋūǹ kí nīn nyí tikɔkɔ ki gàntī, ama ubɔ kaa gbìl̀ úǹ bɔti taapu kí gàntī.

eng: Who has known the mind of the Lord, who will advise him? But we have the mind of Christ.
local: Kii Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon nín lī puee yii: «Ŋma di nyí ti-Dindaan landɔkɔŋu? Ŋma di ŋūǹ kí sāŋkì-u?» Ama timbee, ti cáá Kriisitoo landɔkɔŋu ti-ni.

eng: I had made up my mind not to think about anything while I was with you except Jesus Christ, and to preach him as crucified.
local: M lēē yii maa làá sòoǹ ni-kansikin báà tiba nì tiba, an yaa kaa sá Yeesu Kriisitoo un bi kpáá ndɔpuntikaa puee bɔti.

eng: I stood in front of you with weakness, fear, and a lot of shaking.
local: An pu ní m kāl̀ ni-kansikin kii uwɔfee nì tifaandi yɔ́kɔ́ yɔkɔ.

eng: My message and my preaching werent presented with convincing wise words but with a demonstration of the Spirit and of power.
local: Unimbɔti bɔti tin m nìn cáań-nee nì min min m nìn dàkā-ni tibɔti gbantee kaa nìn nyánní binib ciiŋyi dàkatamin, ama an nìn sá Unimbɔti Fam pɔŋŋu tundi ní.

eng: I did this so that your faith might not depend on the wisdom of people but on the power of God.
local: Mimmee ni-fookiiu kaa cɔ́ binib ciiŋyi pu, ama Unimbɔti pɔŋŋu pu ní an cɔ́.

eng: What we say is wisdom to people who are mature. It isnt a wisdom that comes from the present day or from todays leaders who are being reduced to nothing.
local: An puee bin béè nnimbɔtisan mmɔntiiminee, iciin ní ti dàkā-bi. An kaa nìn sá duulinya yab ciiŋyi, kaa tí sá ipɔŋkpiti in jíń abee duulinyan ki làá dāá kúntée ciiŋyi.

eng: We talk about Gods wisdom, which has been hidden as a secret. God determined this wisdom in advance, before time began, for our glory.
local: Timbee, Unimbɔti ciiŋyi in nìn bí dibalinee pu ní ti sòoǹ. Unimbɔti nìn pée lēē-i ki yóoǹ-ti án nīn sá ti-nyooti ki wāatì ní ki nín náań duulinya.

eng: It is a wisdom that none of the present-day rulers have understood, because if they did understand it, they would never have crucified the Lord of glory!.
local: Duulinya bɔtiibi báà ubɔ kaa ŋúń ki béè Unimbɔti ciiŋyi gbanti. Bi yaa bàà nìn béè-i, baa bàà nìn kpáà ti-Dindaan tinyooldaan ndɔpuntikaa pu.

eng: But this is precisely what is written: God has prepared things for those who love him that no eye has seen, or ear has heard, or that havent crossed the mind of any human being .
local: An bí kii Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon nín ŋmàn̄ puee yii: «Tin unil nimbil kaa kée, nì tin u-tafal kaa gbìl̄ee, nì tin unil kaa dākafì ki kée, tíǹ ní Unimbɔti bɔ́ntì ki yóoǹ bin néeń-uee.».

eng: Brothers and sisters, I couldnt talk to you like spiritual people but like unspiritual people, like babies in Christ.
local: Nnabiyaamu, m nín dɔ́miń ni-ceeyee, maa ŋúń ki sòon̄ nì ni kii m nín sòoǹ bin cáá Unimbɔti Famee puee, ama m sòon̄ nì ni kii binib bin dī duulinyee ní, kii bin sá mbiyaapɔmu Kriisitoo saŋŋu poonee.

eng: I laid a foundation like a wise master builder according to Gods grace that was given to me, but someone else is building on top of it. Each person needs to pay attention to the way they build on it.
local: Diyimɔɔl din Unimbɔti pú-mi ḿ nīn sá udimakpaan un cáá iciinee puee, m bíl̀ kudiiyee nyeel ní ubɔ ń dāá mā kí tɔ́kɔń, ama báà ŋma ń béé u nín māā ki tɔ̄kɔntí puee.

eng: No one can lay any other foundation besides the one that is already laid, which is Jesus Christ.
local: Bi cóoǹ kudii ki bíl̀, kúǹ di sá Yeesu Kriisitoo. Báà ubɔ kaa ti ŋūǹ kí cóoń kupɔŋu.

eng: So, whether someone builds on top of the foundation with gold, silver, precious stones, wood, grass, or hay.
local: Biba làá dū sindaariya ní kí mā kí tɔ́kɔń, ní biba ń dū animbil kúti kí mā, ní biba ń dū ataŋkpanyaan kí mā. Ama biba mun làá dū idɔ, biba ń dū ikpaŋkpaan, biba ń dū timool kí mā kí tɔ́kɔń.

eng: each ones work will be clearly shown. The day will make it clear, because it will be revealed with fire—the fire will test the quality of each ones work.
local: Un yaa dū kun ní ki máń ki tɔ́kɔǹ kudiiyee puee, daliŋkin Unimbɔti làá dāá sòoǹ binib bɔtee, yaadal ní báà ŋma tuuŋŋi dāa nyēē mpaampiin pu. Di sá bi làá dāá dū báà ŋma tuuŋŋi ní kí ŋá ŋŋmin. Ŋŋmi ní bi làá dāá dūú cɔ̄ŋkì báà ŋma tundi kí béé di nín sá diŋyee.

eng: If anyones work survives, theyll get a reward.
local: Sii un di yaa dū kun ní ki máń ki tɔ́kɔǹ kudiiyee pu ní ŋŋmi kaa wùn̄ee làá dāá fōō a-paati.

eng: But if anyones work goes up in flames, theyll lose it. However, they themselves will be saved as if they had gone through a fire.
local: Ama un cee ní ŋŋmi yaa wùn̄ee, asaluu gbáń udaan dee. U làá dāá ká difiil, ama an dāa sá kii unil diiku nín fòol̄ pu ú sāǹ kí nyā kaa báb́ tiba puee ní.

eng: Dont you know that you are Gods temple and Gods Spirit lives in you?.
local: Naa nyí yii ni sá Unimbɔti diiku ní u-Fam kóò ni-naaa?.

eng: If someone destroys Gods temple, God will destroy that person, because Gods temple is holy, which is what you are.
local: An puee, unil yaa kɔ́ǹ Unimbɔti diiku tijɔkɔndee, see Unimbɔti ń dāá dáaĺ udaan tafal, kun puee Unimbɔti diiku bí circir ní, ní nimbi di sá kudii gbanti.

eng: Dont fool yourself. If some of you think they are worldly-wise, then they should become foolish so that they can become wise.
local: Ubɔ ń taa kīil̀ u-ba. Un yaa bí ni-kansikin ki dàkafī yii u cáá duulinya ciiŋyee, ú kpántí ugbaan kí ŋmā kí ká iciin in nyēeń Unimbɔti ceeyee.

eng: This worlds wisdom is foolishness to God. As its written, He catches the wise in their cleverness. .
local: Kun puee duulinya ciiŋyi sá Unimbɔti cee digbaandi ní. Míǹ ní an ŋmàn̄ Unimbɔti gbɔŋkun yii: «Unimbɔti gīintí biciŋfitiibi ciiŋyi ní ki fàtīi cáa cútí-bi.».

eng: I gave you milk to drink instead of solid food, because you werent up to it yet.
local: An puee m tīī-ni nnaabiim, an kaa sá tijin tin pɔ̄ɔ̄ naa ŋūǹee. Áli gba ki dāa sāā nìn díǹee, naa ŋūǹ tin pɔ̄ɔ̄yee.

eng: And also, The Lord knows that the thoughts of the wise are silly .
local: An gíti tí lī yii: «Ti-Dindaan nyí biciŋfitiibi landɔkɔi, ki nyí i nín sá fámee.».

eng: So then, no one should brag about human beings. Everything belongs to you.
local: An puee, an kaa sá ubɔ pu ní ni làá nīn gà ni-biŋŋi, kun puee nimbi di yì tikɔkɔ.

eng: Paul, Apollos, Cephas, the world, life, death, things in the present, things in the future—everything belongs to you.
local: an yaa sá Pɔɔlaaa, Apooloosaaa, Peetrooyaaa, an yaa tí sá duulinyaaa, dimaŋfalaaa, ŋkúmaaa, an yaa tí sá tiwan tin bí kɔŋkɔnneeyaaa, tin bí ki dòońeeyaaa, nimbi di yì tikɔkɔ.

eng: but you belong to Christ, and Christ belongs to God.
local: ní Kriisitoo yì nimbi, ní Unimbɔti yì Kriisitoo.

eng: Now you are still not up to it because you are still unspiritual. When jealousy and fighting exist between you, arent you unspiritual and living by human standards?.
local: kun puee ni dáá bí kii duulinya nib nín bí puee ní. Di sá ŋyunti ŋun ní ipoompoon nì kunikpɔŋkpɔkɔu dáá bí ni-kansikinee, an dàkà yii ni sá duulinya yab dee ki ŋáań nniyaamin.

eng: When someone says, I belong to Paul, and someone else says, I belong to Apollos, arent you acting like people without the Spirit?.
local: Ni-kansikin biba lì yii bíǹ sá Pɔɔl yab ní, ní biba mun lì yii bíǹ sá Apooloosi yab. Ni yaa bíi ŋáań mimmee, naa bíi ŋáań nniyaamin deeee?.

eng: After all, what is Apollos? What is Paul? They are servants who helped you to believe. Each one had a role given to them by the Lord.
local: Ŋma di sá Apooloosi? Ní ŋma di sá Pɔɔl ní? Ti sá Unimbɔti tɔntɔnliibi bin pu ni dī ki fōō Unimbɔti ki kííyee ní. Unimbɔti di yākatì min sá tí ŋée ki tīī-ti.

eng: I planted, Apollos watered, but God made it grow.
local: Man búĺ ní Apooloosi yāaǹ nnyim, ní Unimbɔti yāntì an nyáń ki wíiǹ.

eng: Because of this, neither the one who plants nor the one who waters is anything, but the only one who is anything is God who makes it grow.
local: An puee, an kaa sá un búĺee di sá tiba, kaa tí sá un yāaǹ nnyimee di tí sá tiba, ama Unimbɔti un yāntì an nyáń ki wíiǹee baba di sá tiba.

eng: The one who plants and the one who waters work together, but each one will receive their own reward for their own labor.
local: Un búĺee nì un yāaǹ nnyimee ŋmà ní. Unimbɔti dāa pà-bi bi-tundi din bi túńee nín máaǹ puee ní.

eng: We are Gods coworkers, and you are Gods field, Gods building.
local: Kun puee ti nì Unimbɔti di pēē ki tūǹ ditundi. Nimbi di sá u-saaku, ní ki sá u-diiku.

eng: So a person should think about us this way—as servants of Christ and managers of Gods secrets.
local: Míǹ puee ní nīn nyí yii ti sá Kriisitoo tɔntɔnliibi bin bin ní Unimbɔti dū u-bɔbalkaati ki ŋá ti-ŋaan tí sòoǹee ní.

eng: We are fools for Christ, but you are wise through Christ! We are weak, but you are strong! You are honored, but we are dishonored!.
local: Kriisitoo puee, timbi kpántì biwaatiibi ní, ní nimbi nín fōō-u ki kíí puee, ni kpántì biciŋfitiibi. Míǹ ní timbi sá ŋwɔsɔfimu ní nimbi sá bipɔɔbi. Binib kpèé timbi fám ní ki tìì nimbi jilma.

eng: Up to this very moment we are hungry, thirsty, wearing rags, abused, and homeless.
local: Áli ki dāa sāā ŋyunti ŋun poon ti bí nee ti gbíntì ki bí ki jīǹ ŋkùm nì nnyinnyoo falaau. Taa cáá tiwampeeŋkaal, ní bi gbáà-ti ní ti tú ki yìiǹ ajan kɔkɔ pu.

eng: We work hard with our own hands. When we are insulted, we respond with a blessing; when we are harassed, we put up with it.
local: Míǹ boonee ti tūǹ atuun ki gì kí ŋmā kí ká tiba kí jī. Bi yaa sìí-tee, timbi mèé Unimbɔti ń bìin̄ bi-pu. Míǹ ní bi yaa sàantí-tee ní timbi jīǹ bi-pu kimɔtii.

eng: when our reputation is attacked, we are encouraging. We have become the scum of the earth, the waste that runs off everything, up to the present time.
local: Bi kúŋfí ti-boon ní timbi sòoǹ-bi tin mɔ̄ee, áli nìn díǹee, bi kpèé-ti kii duulinya tampummootee ní, a lì kii tiwaŋkpitil tin kaa bí tibanee.

eng: Im not writing these things to make you ashamed but to warn you, since you are my loved children.
local: Maa ŋmàn̄ tibɔti nee kí kɔ́ǹ-ni icoo, ama kí kpāafì-ni kii ni nín sá m-biyaamu m néeń-ni puee ní.

eng: You may have ten thousand mentors in Christ, but you dont have many fathers. I gave birth to you in Christ Jesus through the gospel.
local: Báà binib kutukub saalaa di yaa làá kpòotī-ni nnimbɔtisan poon, ni-baa làá nīn pée sá ubambaanti ní. Ní nīn nyí yii man di sá ni-baa, kun puee man di cáań Tibɔmɔntiil ki sòon̄ nì ni, ní ni fōō ki kíí.

eng: so I encourage you to follow my example.
local: An puee, m bàlifī-ni yii ní fōō man bimbim kí nīn ŋáań man nín ŋáań puee.

eng: This is why Ive sent Timothy to you; hes my loved and trusted child in the Lord; hell remind you about my way of life in Christ Jesus. Hell teach the same way as I teach everywhere in every church.
local: An pu ní m dū m-jipɔɔn Tiimootee un sá m-bineeŋkaaki ki yú deedee ti-Dindaan saŋŋunee ki túnní ni-cee. U làá téetí-ni man nín dī Kriisitoo saŋŋu miŋyee, kii m nín dàkà Kriisitoo nikitiŋ kɔkɔ kansikin puee.

eng: Some have become arrogant as if Im not coming to see you.
local: Ni-kansikin biba nín dākafì yii maa ti làá gítiń ni-ceeyee puee, bi bí ki yòontí bi-ba yilpu.

eng: But, if the Lord is willing, Ill come to you soon. Then I wont focus on what these arrogant people say, but Ill find out what power they possess.
local: Ama ti-Dindaan yaa là, an kaa làá ti yúntí, m làá dāan̄ ni-cee. M yaa láa bāańee, an kaa dāa sá bilambidambi gbanti nyɔɔbuŋŋi ní m làá pīlifì, ama tin bi ŋūǹ kí ŋée ní m làá kpèè.

eng: In this kind of situation, what is expected of a manager is that they prove to be faithful.
local: Bi yaa dū ditundi ki ŋá a-ŋaanee, tin bi kíǹ ki kpèé a-ceeyee di sá á tɔ̄ ditundee bí béé a-baamɔŋyi.

eng: Gods kingdom isnt about words but about power.
local: Di sá yii Unimbɔti Beel kaa sá nì anyɔɔbun ŋmaniŋma, an sá Circir Fam nín tìì unil mpɔn ú ŋá kuŋkuŋyee.

eng: Which do you want? Should I come to you with a big stick to punish you, or with love and a gentle spirit?.
local: Ni là ḿ dāan̄ ni-cee nì mbambaanaaa, ḿ dāan̄ nì dineendi nì nsɔŋfi ní?.
