eng: I couldnt care less if Im judged by you or by any human court; I dont even judge myself.
local: Mamee, nimbi di yaa bíi sòoǹ m-bɔtaaa, an yaa tí sá bibɔsoonliibaaa, tíǹ kaa ká-mi. Man m-ba kaa tí bíi sòoǹ m-ba bɔti.

eng: Im not aware of anything against me, but that doesnt make me innocent, because the Lord is the one who judges me.
local: Ibaamɔn, m-pɔbil poonee maa gbìl̄ yii m ŋá tiba, ama míǹ di kaa dàkà yii maa ŋá tiba. Ti-Dindaan di sá un sòoǹ man bɔtee.

eng: So dont judge anything before the right time—wait until the Lord comes. He will bring things that are hidden in the dark to light, and he will make peoples motivations public. Then there will be recognition for each person from God.
local: An puee ní taa sòoǹ ubɔ bɔti kí nīn cí ŋyunti ń bàń. Ní taa sòoǹ ubɔ bɔti kí nīn cí ŋyunti ŋun ti-Dindaan làá bàńee. U yaa láa bāańee, u làá yàntī ŋwalifi ń ŋmíntí tin bál̀ dibɔmbɔndinee kí nyāntí binib landɔkɔi mpaampiin pu. Niin ní báà ŋma dāa fòol̄ kupampakau kun u máaǹee Unimbɔti cee.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I have applied these things to myself and Apollos for your benefit. Ive done this so that you can learn what it means not to go beyond what has been written and so none of you will become arrogant by supporting one of us against the other.
local: Nnabiyaamu, m dū man nì Apooloosi bɔti tin m sòon̄ee ki dàkā kí cántí-ni tibɔti ní. Ní bīfì man nì Apooloosi cee min ni máaǹ kí nīn bée kí taa lákatí tin bi ŋmàn̄ Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poonee pu. Ní taa ŋá dilambil kí lēē ti-kansikin ubɔ, kí bí yii úǹ di sá tiba kí kpèè un kíǹee fám.

eng: Who says that you are better than anyone else? What do you have that you didnt receive? And if you received it, then why are you bragging as if you didnt receive it?.
local: Ŋma di yōoǹ-si yilpu ní a jítiǹ a-juti ní? Tin a cááyee Unimbɔti di kaa tīī-saaa? Kin Unimbɔti di yaa tīī-si ní, ba pu ní a cáá dilambil kii ubɔ di kaa tīī-see?.

eng: Youve been filled already! Youve become rich already! You rule like kings without us! I wish you did rule so that we could be kings with you!.
local: Ni pūtì kpáàa ká tin kɔkɔ ni lèe! Ki pūtì kpáàa kpántì bigaajab ki kpántì bibɔtiibi timbi boon! Ni yaa bàà lafun kpántì bibɔtiibi ti mun ń bàà dāá kūtì ni-pu, an bàà ŋān̄!.

eng: I suppose that God has shown that we apostles are at the end of the line. We are like prisoners sentenced to death, because we have become a spectacle in the world, both to angels and to humans.
local: An bí-mi kii Unimbɔti gíiǹ timbi akpambal boon binib kansikinee ní. Ti bí kii binib bin bi làá kpɔ̀ kunipaau nimbiinee, kun puee ti kpántì tinimbikɔntiwan ní duulinya nì ituun nì binib kpèé.

eng: Everyone has heard that there is sexual immorality among you. This is a type of immorality that isnt even heard of among the Gentiles—a man is having sex with his fathers wife!.
local: M gbìl̄ bi sòoǹ tijɔkɔndi tin bí ni-kansikinee pu. An jɔkɔntee, bin kaa fōō Yeesu ki kííyee kaa kíikí kí ŋá míǹ gba. M gbìl̄ yii ni-kansikin ubɔ cáá u-baa nimpuaa!.

eng: But I wasnt talking about the sexually immoral people in the outside world by any means—or the greedy, or the swindlers, or people who worship false gods—otherwise, you would have to leave the world entirely!.
local: Maa nìn lì bin bí duulinyan ki tūǹ ajɔkɔntuunee nì nnimbitaataam dambi nì binaayub nì biwaatootiliibi. An yaa bàà sá bíǹ pu ní m lèe, m bàà lì yii ní nyā duulinya poon ní.

eng: But now Im writing to you not to associate with anyone who calls themselves brother or sister who is sexually immoral, greedy, someone who worships false gods, an abusive person, a drunk, or a swindler. Dont even eat with anyone like this.
local: M nín baasii là kí lì min kugbɔŋu gbanti poonee di sá: unil un lì yii u sá Kriisitoo yɔu, ama ní ki tūǹ ajɔkɔntuun àá ki cáá nnimbitaataam àá ki sá uwaatootil àá ki sìí binib àá ki sá udanyɔgbil àá unaayuuyee ní m lì. Ni nì an niliuee ń taa kpāfì kí jī tijin.

eng: What do I care about judging outsiders? Isnt it your job to judge insiders?.
local: Man di kaa làá sòoǹ bin kaa nóó Yeesuee bɔti. Bin sá Kriisitoo yabee bɔti ní an sá ní sòoǹ.

eng: God will judge outsiders. Expel the evil one from among you! .
local: Unimbɔti di làá sòoǹ bin kaa sá Kriisitoo yabee bɔti. Nyāntímaan unikpiti ni-kansikin.

eng: And youre proud of yourselves instead of being so upset that the one who did this thing is expelled from your community.
local: Ni ŋáań míǹ ní ki gíti cáá dilambil kaa bí kunimbaaun kí nyāntí un ŋá an tundee ni-kansikin.

eng: Though Im absent physically, Im present in the spirit and Ive already judged the man who did this as if I were present.
local: Maa bí ni-cee ni wāĺ-mi, ama m-landɔkɔŋu bí ni-cee. M pūtì kpáàa kūū un ŋá an tuŋkpitilee bɔti a lì kii m bí ni-kansikinee.

eng: When you meet together in the name of our Lord Jesus, Ill be present in spirit with the power of our Lord Jesus.
local: Ní kpāfìmaan nì ti-Dindaan Yeesu yindi pu, m-landɔkɔŋu làá dāá nīn bí ni-kansikin. Nì ti-Dindaan Yeesu pɔŋŋu puee.

eng: At that time we need to hand this man over to Satan to destroy his human weakness so that his spirit might be saved on the day of the Lord.
local: ní dāá dū an niliuee kí tīī Sitaan. U làá sàaǹ u-wunti ní u-naŋki ń dāá ŋmátí ti-Dindaan gítintam.

eng: Your bragging isnt good! Dont you know that a tiny grain of yeast makes a whole batch of dough rise?.
local: Kun pu ní ni dàkafī ki cáá dilambilee kaa ŋān̄. Naa nyí yii ŋkpɔnɔnyɔkɔ fiiiyee ní bi dù kí ŋá bukpɔnɔsaa ní án jɔ̄m̀aaa?.

eng: Clean out the old yeast so you can be a new batch of dough, given that youre supposed to be unleavened bread. Christ our Passover lamb has been sacrificed.
local: Nyāntímaan nnyɔɔkpiki gbanti ŋun sá ikpitee kí nīn sá circirdambi kii bukpɔnɔsaa bun kaa cáá nnyɔkɔee. Ni kpántì míǹ ki dóò, kun puee Kriisitoo un sá-ti Dilákatil jiŋaaliu pibilee dū u-ba ki ŋá saraa ipii yil pu ki tīī timbi.

eng: so lets celebrate the feast with the unleavened bread of honesty and truth, not with old yeast or with the yeast of evil and wickedness.
local: An puee, tí jīmaan ti-jiŋaaliu gbanti nì kpɔnɔb bin poon baa ŋá ŋkpɔnɔnyɔkɔ ŋun sá kii ikpiti nì tijɔkɔndee, ama án nīn sá kii kpɔnɔb bin kaa cáá nnyɔkɔee, ki sá circir nì ibaamɔnee.

eng: I wrote to you in my earlier letter not to associate with sexually immoral people.
local: M nìn ŋmàn̄-ni kugbɔŋu ki tùkū-ni yii ní taa yàntī ni nì bin tūǹ ajɔkɔntuunee ń nīn kpáfí.

eng: When someone in your assembly has a legal case against another member, do they dare to take it to court to be judged by people who arent just, instead of by Gods people?.
local: Ba pu ní ni-kansikin ubɔ nì u-juti yaa cáá tɔb tibɔtee, ni cáa cáǹ bin kaa nóó Yeesuee cee, kaa cáa cáǹ bin nóó Yeesuee cee ní?.

eng: thieves, the greedy, drunks, abusive people, and swindlers wont inherit Gods kingdom.
local: nì binaayub nì nnimbitaataam dambi nì bidanyɔgbiliibi nì bin sìí binibee nì biyipɔɔdambee kaa làá dāá kɔ́ Unimbɔti Beelin.

eng: That is what some of you used to be! But you were washed clean, you were made holy to God, and you were made right with God in the name of the Lord Jesus Christ and in the Spirit of our God.
local: Míǹ poon ní ni-kansikin biba nìn bí. Ama Unimbɔti fùl̄-ni ki lēē ni-kpitii, ki gāntì-ni ki yóoǹ ní nīn sá u-yab. Ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo nì Unimbɔti Fam puee, ni kpántì deedeedambi.

eng: I have the freedom to do anything, but not everything is helpful. I have the freedom to do anything, but I wont be controlled by anything.
local: Ni-kansikin biba lì yii: «M cáá nsan kí ŋá tikɔkɔ», ama tikɔkɔ di kaa ŋān̄ ki tīī-mi. Báà m yaa cáá nsan kí ŋá tikɔkɔɔɔ, an kaa sá báà kuŋyee ní ḿ dū m-ba kí tīī kuyuŋu.

eng: Food is for the stomach and the stomach is for food, and yet God will do away with both. The body isnt for sexual immorality but for the Lord, and the Lord is for the body.
local: «Tijin bí ki tīī dipool ní, ní dipool mun bí ki tīī tijin,» ama Unimbɔti làá dāá kúntí tíǹ liitil. Tiwun tíǹ kaa bí ki tīī dijɔkɔntundi, tì bí ki tīī ti-Dindaan ní, ní ti-Dindaan mun bí ki tīī tiwun.

eng: God has raised the Lord and will raise us through his power.
local: Ní Unimbɔti un fīkitì ti-Dindaanee ń dāá dū u-pɔŋŋuee kí fìkitī ti mun.

eng: Dont you know that your bodies are parts of Christ? So then, should I take parts of Christ and make them a part of someone who is sleeping around? No way!.
local: Naa nyí yii ni-wunti sá Kriisitoo wunjaŋŋi níii? M kíikí kí yòon̄ Kriisitoo wunjaŋŋi kí dūú kpāfì nì kilaakpaai yɔŋuɔɔɔ? Aayee, báà fiii!.

eng: Dont you know that anyone who is joined to someone who is sleeping around is one body with that person? The scripture says, The two will become one flesh .
local: Naa nyí yii un nì kilaakpaai yaa kpáfèe, udaan wunti nì kilaakpaai yati ŋá tibaantiilaaa? Kun puee Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon lī yii: «Bi-liitilee ŋá ubaanti dee.».

eng: The one who is joined to the Lord is one spirit with him.
local: Ama un nì ti-Dindaan yaa kpáfèe, udaan nì ti-Dindaan ŋá ubaanti ŋfamin dee.

eng: Avoid sexual immorality! Every sin that a person can do is committed outside the body, except those who engage in sexual immorality commit sin against their own bodies.
local: Ní fámaan ajɔkɔntuun, di sá yii ikpiti in kíǹ binib ŋáańee kaa gbèetī tiwun, ama un ŋáań ajɔkɔntuunee, úǹ u-ba wunti ní u ŋáań ikpiti.

eng: Or dont you know that your body is a temple of the Holy Spirit who is in you? Dont you know that you have the Holy Spirit from God, and you dont belong to yourselves?.
local: Àá naa nyí yii ni-wunti sá Unimbɔti Fam min u tīī-nee diiku ní naa yì ni-ba?.

eng: Or dont you know that Gods people will judge the world? If the world is to be judged by you, are you incompetent to judge trivial cases?.
local: Naa nyí yii bin fōō Yeesu ki kííyee di làá dāá sòoǹ duulinya yab bɔtaaa? Nimbi bin làá dāá sòoǹ duulinya yab bɔtee di kaa ŋūǹ kí sòoǹ tibɔti tin bí sɔkɔmiiyeeee?.

eng: You have been bought and paid for, so honor God with your body.
local: Unimbɔti dá-ni nì kudapɔɔu ní, an puee ní kpáaǹ ni-wunti kí tīī-u tinyool.

eng: Dont you know that we will judge angels? Why not ordinary things?.
local: Naa nyí yii timbi di làá dāá sòoǹ ituun bɔtaaa? Kin ataa aŋa pu ní taa ŋūǹ kí sòoǹ binib bɔti.

eng: So then if you have ordinary lawsuits, do you appoint people as judges who arent respected by the church?.
local: Di sá yii nimbi kansikinee, ni yaa cáá tɔb tibee, bin Kriisitoo nikitil kaa dūu yíiń binibee, bíǹ ní nimbi cāabìń bí nīn sá bibɔsoonliibi.

eng: Im saying this because you should be ashamed of yourselves! Isnt there one person among you who is wise enough to pass judgment between believers?.
local: Mimmee ni-kansikin ubɔ kaa bí ki cáá iciin kí nīn ŋáŋkí ni-kansikin bɔtaaa? Ni-cooi ní m bíi lì nee.

eng: But instead, does a brother or sister have a lawsuit against another brother or sister, and do they do this in front of unbelievers?.
local: Nimbee, ni nì ni-nabiki yaa cáá tɔb tibɔtee, see bin kaa nyí Unimbɔtee cee ní ni tin sòoǹ-tì.

eng: The fact that you have lawsuits against each other means that youve already lost your case. Why not be wronged instead? Why not be cheated?.
local: Kɔŋkɔnnee ni yīntì kpataaa ni nì ni-nabiyaamu nín cáá tɔb tibɔtee pu. Ba pu ní bi yaa ŋá-ni tin kaa kítèe, naa jīǹ kimɔtii ní? Ba pu ní naa yàntī un yùn̄-nee ń nīn cáa cá ní?.

eng: But instead you are doing wrong and cheating—and youre doing it to your own brothers and sisters.
local: Ama nimbi gba di ŋáań tin kaa kítèe ní ki yù. Ni-nabiyaamu mun nóó Kriisitooyee ní ni ŋáań mimmee!.

eng: Dont you know that people who are unjust wont inherit Gods kingdom? Dont be deceived. Those who are sexually immoral, those who worship false gods, adulterers, both participants in same-sex intercourse.
local: Naa nyí yii bin ŋáań tin kaa kítèe kaa làá dāá kɔ́ Unimbɔti Beelinaaa? Ubɔ ń taa mɔ̄ntì u-ba. Bin ŋáań tijɔkɔndee nì biwaatootiliibi nì bilaakpaadambi nì bin dɔ̀ nì bi-ninjajutiibee.

eng: Now, about what you wrote: Its good for a man not to have sex with a woman.
local: Tí kpèè kugbɔŋu kun ni ŋmàn̄ee bɔti. An ŋān̄ ki tīī unil ń taa bàà cáá unimpu.

eng: Im passing on the Lords command to those who are married: A wife shouldnt leave her husband.
local: Nimbi binimpucaaliibi nì bicamɔnliibi ní m tìì mara nee, yii: Unimpu ń taa yīkì u-cal cee.

eng: but if she does leave him, then she should stay single or be reconciled to her husband. And a man shouldnt divorce his wife.
local: U yaa tí cāntì u-calaaa, ú nīn kā míǹ kí taa mɔ̄ǹ ubɔ, àá u nì u-cal ń ŋāŋkì bi-kansikin ú fàtīí gítiń-u. Míǹ mbaantiim, uninja mun ń taa jà u-nimpuu.

eng: Im telling everyone else : If a believer has a wife who doesnt believe, and she agrees to live with him, then he shouldnt divorce her.
local: Nimbi bin kíǹee, tin m làá tùkù-nee sèé, M lī yii a yaa cáá unimpu ní ki dāa fōō Yeesu ki kíí ní a-nimpuu kaa fōō Yeesu ki kíí, ama ní ki kíí kí nīn gbíntìi sá a-nimpuuee, á taa yàntī-u.

eng: If a woman has a husband who doesnt believe and he agrees to live with her, then she shouldnt divorce him.
local: Míǹ ní unimpu mun yaa tí nóó Yeesu ní u-cal kaa nóó Yeesu, ama ní ki kíí ú nīn gbíntìi sá u-nimpuuee, ú taa yīkì-u.

eng: The husband who doesnt believe belongs to God because of his wife, and the wife who doesnt believe belongs to God because of her husband. Otherwise, your children would be contaminated by the world, but now they are spiritually set apart.
local: Uninja un kaa nóó Yeesuee nimpuu nín nóó Yeesuee puee, u kíǹ ki pìl̄ Unimbɔti dee. Ní unimpu un kaa nóó Yeesuee mun kíǹ ki pìl̄ Unimbɔti u-cal un nóó Yeesuee pu. An yaa kaa sá mimmee, bi-biyaamu kaa bàà pìl̄ nì Unimbɔti. An nín sá mimmee puee, bi-biyaamu bí circir.

eng: But if a spouse who doesnt believe chooses to leave, then let them leave. The brother or sister isnt tied down in these circumstances. God has called you to peace.
local: Ama a yaa nóó Yeesu ní a-cal àá a-nimpuu kaa nóó Yeesu ní ki là kí càntī-see, pɔ̄-u nsan ú nīn cá, mara ubɔ kaa bóob́-si á cúú-u mpɔn kí kàan̄. Di sá yii Unimbɔti yíiń-ti tí dāan̄ kí nīn bí ŋgbansɔŋfin ní.

eng: How do you know as a wife if you will save your husband? Or how do you know as a husband if you will save your wife?.
local: Unimpu, a nyí yii sii nì a-cal yaa làá nīn gbíntì ki bí, a làá ŋmā kí fīī-uaaa? Àá uninja, a nyí yii sii nì a-nimpuu yaa làá nīn gbíntì ki bí a làá ŋmā kí fīī-u?.

eng: Nevertheless, each person should live the kind of life that the Lord assigned when he called each one. This is what I teach in all the churches.
local: Tin bée di sá yii báà ŋma ń nīn ŋúb́ min ti-Dindaan tīī-u ki wāatì ní u nín fōō Yeesu ki kííyee. An marau ní m síiǹ Kriisitoo nikitiŋ kɔkɔ kansikin.

eng: If someone was circumcised when called, he shouldnt try to reverse it. If someone wasnt circumcised when he was called, he shouldnt be circumcised.
local: Sii un yaa gíí dikpancandi ní ki nín fōō Yeesu ki kííyee ń taa nín̄ yáliŋkí yii a làá gíti ŋá mana. Sii un kaa gíí dikpancandi ní ki fōō Yeesu ki kííyee ń taa gíí.

eng: Circumcision is nothing; not being circumcised is nothing. What matters is keeping Gods commandments.
local: A yaa gííyaaa, a yaa kaa gííyaaa, tíǹ bɔti kaa páb́, tin pée páb́ee di sá á nīn ŋúb́ Unimbɔti marab.

eng: Each man should have his own wife, and each woman should have her own husband because of sexual immorality.
local: Ama, án taa láa yàntī ní kɔ́ ajɔkɔntuun poonee, an máaǹ uninja kamaa ń nīn cáá u-nimpuu, ní unimpu kamaa ń nīn cáá u-cal.

eng: Each person should stay in the situation they were in when they were called.
local: Báà ŋma ń nīn gbíntì ki bí min ní u nìn pée bí ní ki nín fōō Yeesu ki kííyee.

eng: If you were a slave when you were called, dont let it bother you. But if you are actually able to be free, take advantage of the opportunity.
local: A yaa nìn sá uyumbu ní ki nín fōō Yeesu ki kíí, á taa nín̄ yáliŋkí, ama a yaa tí ŋūǹ kí fōō a-baaa, á pɔ́ɔǹ kí fōō a-ba.

eng: Anyone who was a slave when they were called by the Lord has the status of being the Lords free person. In the same way, anyone who was a free person when they were called is Christs slave.
local: Uyumbu un fōō ti-Dindaan ki kííyee, ti-Dindaan nyántì-u ikpiti yuŋkun, míǹ ní un yì u-ba ki fōō Yeesu ki kííyee sá Kriisitoo yumbuu.

eng: You were bought and paid for. Dont become slaves of people.
local: Unimbɔti dá-ni nì kudapɔɔu ní, an puee ní taa gíti dū ni-ba kí ŋá binib yumbui.

eng: So then, brothers and sisters, each of you should stay with God in the situation you were in when you were called.
local: Nnabiyaamu, ni nín nìn pée bí min ki wāatì ki nín fōō Yeesu ki kííyee, ní nīn gbíntìi bí míǹ kí dū ni-ba kí tīī Unimbɔti.

eng: I dont have a command from the Lord about people who have never been married, but Ill give you my opinion as someone you can trust because of the Lords mercy.
local: An yaa kíǹ bin làá cáá binimpoobi nì bin làá mɔ̄ǹee bɔtee, ti-Dindaan kaa tīī-mi mara ḿ yóoń-bi, ama man nín dàkafī puee ní m làá lì kii m nín sá unil un ti-Dindaan pú diyimɔɔl bi cáá m-pu dimákalee pu.

eng: So I think this advice is good because of the present crisis: Stay as you are.
local: Man ká yii, kii dinimbinaamandi din bí kɔŋkɔnnee puee, an ŋān̄ unil ń taa cáá unimpu.

eng: If you are married, dont get a divorce. If you are divorced, dont try to find a spouse.
local: A yaa gíti dáá cáá unimpuee, taa ti nyàab̀ nsan yii a làá jà-u. A yaa kaa láá tí cááyaaa, á taa ti cáá.

eng: But if you do marry, you havent sinned; and if someone who hasnt been married gets married, they havent sinned. But married people will have a hard time, and Im trying to spare you that.
local: Ama nì míǹ kɔkɔee, uninja yaa cáá unimpu, waa yīntì, míǹ ní unimpu mun yaa mɔ́ǹ waa yīntì. Ama digìŋ́kpaandi di làá bà binimpucaaliibi nì bicamɔnliibi. An pu ní m bàà là kí nyāntí-bi digìńdin.

eng: This is what Im saying, brothers and sisters: The time has drawn short. From now on, those who have wives should be like people who dont have them.
local: Nnabiyaamu, tin m làá lèe di sá yii ŋyunti kaa ti náaǹ. An puee bin cáá binimpoobee ń nīn bí kii bin kaa cááyee.

eng: The husband should meet his wifes sexual needs, and the wife should do the same for her husband.
local: Uninja ń tɔ̄ u-tundi nín sá puee kí tīī u-nimpuu, ní unimpu mun ń ŋá míǹ mbaantiim kí tīī u-cal.

eng: Those who are sad should be like people who arent crying. Those who are happy should be like people who arent happy. Those who buy something should be like people who dont have possessions.
local: bin sūǹee ń nīn bí kii kunimbaau kaa cáá-bee, bin lāāyee ń nīn bí kii an kaa mɔ̄ǹ-bee, binyampoobi bin dāāyee ń nīn bí kii baa cáá tibee.

eng: Those who use the world should be like people who arent preoccupied with it, because this world in its present form is passing away.
local: Bin bí ki jīǹ duulinya puee ń nīn bí kii baa bíi páatí tibee. Kun puee, ni nín wāĺ duulinya bí min nee, u làá dāá kúntí.

eng: I want you to be free from concerns. A man who isnt married is concerned about the Lords concerns—how he can please the Lord.
local: M bàà là yii ní nīn tɔ́ ni-ba kí taa nín̄ nìiǹ, di sá yii ti-Dindaan tundi ŋmaniŋma pu ní un kaa cáá unimpuee nimbiliŋ máǹ ki nyàab̀ kí fáatí ti-Dindaan.

eng: But a married man is concerned about the worlds concerns—how he can please his wife.
local: Ama un cáá unimpuee nimbiliŋ máǹ duulinya tuuŋŋi pu ní u nyàab̀ kí fáatíǹ u-nimpuu.

eng: His attention is divided. A woman who isnt married or who is a virgin is concerned about the Lords concerns so that she can be dedicated to God in both body and spirit. But a married woman is concerned about the worlds concerns—how she can please her husband.
local: Niinee u-landɔkɔi ŋá ili dee. Míǹ ní unimpu àá usapɔɔn un kaa mɔ́ǹee nimbiliŋ máǹ ti-Dindaan tundi pu, án nín làá ŋá pu u-wunti nì u-pɔbil ń nīn bí circiree. Ama unimpu àá usapɔɔn un mɔ́ǹee nimbiliŋ máǹ duulinya tuuŋŋi pu ní u nyàab̀ kí nīn fáatì u-cal.

eng: Im saying this for your own advantage. Its not to restrict you but rather to promote effective and consistent service to the Lord without distraction.
local: Ni-ŋanti pu ní m sòoǹ tin bí nee, an kaa sá kí bàkan̄-ni. M là ní nīn ŋáań min ŋān̄ ki kítèe ní, kí dū ni-ba kɔkɔ kí ŋá ti-Dindaan ŋaan kí taa kíń báà laaba.

eng: If someone thinks he is acting inappropriately toward an unmarried woman whom he knows, and if he has strong feelings and it seems like the right thing to do, he should do what he wants—hes not sinning—they should get married.
local: Unaacimpɔɔn un di yaa bàà lēē yii waa làá cáá u-bɔɔn ní ki dāa gíti ká yii waa ŋūǹ kí cúú u-ba, án làá nīn pée páb́-u míǹ kí nīn cée, ú cáá u-bɔɔn, waa ŋá ikpiti.

eng: But if a man stands firm in his decision, and doesnt feel the pressure, but has his own will under control, he does right if he decides in his own heart not to marry the woman.
local: Ama un di lēē u-pɔbil poon kaa ti bí kí gítí u-boon ki yì u-ba ki nyí min u lēēyee, u nín baasii lēē u-pɔbil poon kí ŋá miŋyee deeyee, udaan lēē min ŋān̄ee.

eng: Therefore, the one who marries the unmarried woman does right, and the one who doesnt get married will do even better.
local: Un kaa ŋúń ki cúú u-ba ki cáá u-bɔɔnee cee ŋān̄. Ama un ŋúń ki cúú u-ba kaa cáá u-bɔɔnee cee ŋān̄ ki lákatì.

eng: A woman is obligated to stay in her marriage as long as her husband is alive. But if her husband dies, she is free to marry whomever she wants, only it should be a believer in the Lord.
local: Unimpu un mɔ́ǹee máaǹ kí nīn pée náká u-cal ní bí nīn bí iyunti kɔkɔ. Ama u-cal yaa kpíìyee, u tɔ́ u-ba kí mɔ̄ǹ un u yaa lèe. Ama un fōō Yeesu ki kííyee ní an sá ú fàtīí mɔ̄ǹ.

eng: The wife doesnt have authority over her own body, but the husband does. Likewise, the husband doesnt have authority over his own body, but the wife does.
local: Di sá yii an kaa sá unimpu di yì u-wunti u-ba, an sá u-cal di yì. Míǹ mbaantiim an kaa sá uninja di yì u-wunti u-ba, an sá u-nimpuu di yì.

eng: But in my opinion, she will be happier if she stays the way she is. And I think that I have Gods Spirit too.
local: Man ceeyee, u yaa gbíntì míǹ kaa mɔ́ǹ, u-pɔbil làá nīn sɔ̀m̀ ki jítiǹ u yaa mɔ́ǹ. Man nín dàkafī puee dee, ní m fōō ki kíí yii ma mun cáá Unimbɔti Fam.

eng: Dont refuse to meet each others needs unless you both agree for a short period of time to devote yourselves to prayer. Then come back together again so that Satan might not tempt you because of your lack of self-control.
local: An puee, ubɔ ń taa ŋmìntī u-juti báà tiba, asee ni yaa sòon̄ ki gbìlīǹ tɔb kí dū ŋyunti ŋubɔ kí taa gbēē tɔb kí ŋmā kí mèè. Ni nín mēē míǹ ki dóòyee, ní ní fàtīí kpāfì. An yaa kaa sá míǹ, ni nín kaa ŋūǹ kí nīn gbíntì ki ŋúb́ ni-ba ki ŋmèéyee puee, Sitaan ŋūǹ kí dī míǹ pu kí cɔ̄ŋkì-ni.

eng: Im saying this to give you permission; its not a command.
local: Tin m sòoǹ-ni nee, kaa sá mara ní m síiǹ-ni, m sáŋkí-ni ní.

eng: I wish all people were like me, but each has a particular gift from God: one has this gift, and another has that one.
local: M bàà là yii bininjab kɔkɔ ń bàà nín̄ bí kii man nín bí pu nee ní, ama báà ŋma nì Unimbɔti nín tīiń-u puee ní. U tīī ubɔ tiba ní ki tīī un kíǹee mun tiba.

eng: Im telling those who are single and widows that its good for them to stay single like me.
local: Ama, m tùkù bin kaa cáá binimpoobi àá kaa mɔ́ǹee nì bikpiipoobi yii an bàà ŋān̄ bí gbíntí míǹ kii man nín bí pu nee.

eng: But if they cant control themselves, they should get married, because its better to marry than to burn with passion.
local: Ama bi yaa kaa ŋūǹ kí cúú bi-ba kí kàl̄ míǹ, bí mɔ̄ǹ àá bí cáá binimpoobi, kun puee an sɔ́ á cáá unimpu àá á mɔ̄ǹ ki jítiǹ á nīn kā ki wù fɔ̀rrr fám.

eng: Now concerning meat that has been sacrificed to a false god: We know that we all have knowledge. Knowledge makes people arrogant, but love builds people up.
local: Tiwaŋkuti tin bi tōotì awaayee nanti bɔtee, ti nyí yii báà ŋma nyí, ama dinyil cāabìń dilambil ní, ní dineendi yàntī binib cá nyɔkɔpu nnimbɔtisan poon.

eng: Suppose someone sees you eating in an idols temple. Wont the person with a weak conscience be encouraged to eat the meat sacrificed to false gods?.
local: Un kaa pɔ̄ɔ̄ nnimbɔtisan poonee yaa ká sii un nyí Unimbɔti bɔti tiŋanee yaa kā ditaŋgbandi diba taapu ki jīǹee, an kaa téekí-u ú ŋmɔ́ tiwaatootinanaaa?.

eng: The weak brother or sister for whom Christ died is destroyed by your knowledge.
local: Ní sii nyítam puee, kinabiki kin kaa láá béè ki dóòyee, kinabiki kin pu Kriisitoo kpíìyee ń wāŋkàaa?.

eng: You sin against Christ if you sin against your brothers and sisters and hurt their weak consciences this way.
local: Ni nín bí ki ŋáań ni-nabiyaamu ikpiti min ki kɔ̄ntí bin kaa láá béè ki dóòyee landɔkɔi iweenee, an sá Kriisitoo ní ni ŋáań ikpiti gbanti.

eng: This is why, if food causes the downfall of my brother or sister, I wont eat meat ever again, or else I may cause my brother or sister to fall.
local: An puee, tijin jītam di yaa làá yàntī m-nabiki ń lítée, m kpá kí ŋmɔ́ tinan, án nín làá ŋá pu m-nabiki ń taa lítée.

eng: If anyone thinks they know something, they dont yet know as much as they should know.
local: Un dàkafī yii u nyí tibee kaa láá béè min sá ú bééyee.

eng: But if someone loves God, then they are known by God.
local: Ama un yaa néeń Unimbɔtee, Unimbɔti béè udaan dee.

eng: So concerning the actual food involved in these sacrifices to false gods, we know that a false god isnt anything in this world, and that there is no God except for the one God.
local: Ni nín bālifì yii ni ŋūǹ kí ŋmɔ́ tiwaatootinanaaayee, ti nyí yii diwaal kaa sá tiba duulinya poon, míǹ ní báà unimbɔti ubɔ gíti kpá see Unimbɔtibaantee.

eng: Granted, there are so-called gods, in heaven and on the earth, as there are many gods and many lords.
local: Binib dàkafī yii inimbɔti wīikí, iba bí yilpu ní iba bí taapu. Tiŋman, bíǹ ceeyee, inimbɔti wīikí ní bidindambi mun wīikí.

eng: However, for us believers, There is one God the Father. All things come from him, and we belong to him. And there is one Lord Jesus Christ. All things exist through him, and we live through him.
local: Timbee, ti cáá Unimbɔtibambaanti ní, úǹ di sá Ubaati un náań tikɔkɔee. Úǹ pu ní ti bí. Ní ti-Dindaan sá ubaanti, úǹ di sá Yeesu Kriisitoo. Úǹ pu ní tiwan kɔkɔ bí, u-pu ní ti mun bí.

eng: But not everybody knows this. Some are eating this food as though it really is food sacrificed to a real idol, because they were used to idol worship until now. Their conscience is weak because it has been damaged.
local: Ama bi-kɔkɔ kaa nyí míǹ. Di sá yii awaatuun mántiǹ biba, ní bi gbíntì ki ŋmóò tiwaatootinan, a lì kii diwaal di lafun baasii yì tinan gbantee, ní bi-pɔbiliŋ nín kaa pɔ̄ɔ̄ puee ní bí ŋá ikpiti.

eng: Food wont bring us close to God. Were not missing out if we dont eat, and we dont have any advantage if we do eat.
local: Di sá yii tijin di kaa làá yàntī tí sútí kí pìl̄ Unimbɔti. Ti yaa jíńaaa, an kaa làá bàtī-ti tiba, ti yaa kaa jíńaaa, an kaa làá gbéeǹ-ti tiba.

eng: But watch out or else this freedom of yours might be a problem for those who are weak.
local: Ama ní nīn nyíǹ ni-ba nì ni nín tɔ́ ni-ba ki ŋáań miŋyee, án taa yàntī bin kaa pɔ̄ɔ̄ nnimbɔtisan poonee ń lítí.

eng: Am I not free? Am I not an apostle? Havent I seen Jesus our Lord? Arent you my work in the Lord?.
local: Maa tɔ́ m-baaa? Maa sá Yeesu kpambalaaa? Maa ká Yeesu ti-Dindaanaaa? M nín túń ti-Dindaan tundi ki nyántì kuŋyee di kaa sá nimbaaa?.

eng: or did he say this entirely for our sake? It was written for our sake because the one who plows and the one who threshes should each do so with the hope of sharing the produce.
local: An kaa sá timbi pu ní an sòon̄ mimmaaa? Tiŋman, an sá timbi pu ní bi sòon̄ míǹ. An máaǹ un kù idee nì un gbáàyee ń nīn cáá dimákal yii bi làá ká bi-paatii poon.

eng: If we sowed spiritual things in you, is it so much to ask to harvest some material things from you?.
local: Unimbɔti bɔti ní timbi búĺ ni-pɔbiliŋin. Mimmee ni-wanti tin ni cáá duulinyanee, ti yaa fōō tiba poonee, ti fōō ki lákatàaa?.

eng: If others have these rights over you, dont we deserve them all the more? However, we havent made use of this right, but we put up with everything so we dont put any obstacle in the way of the gospel of Christ.
local: Biba yaa cáá nsan ki jīǹ ni-puee, timbi kaa máaǹ kí jī ni-pu kí jītíǹ bidambaaa? Ama min bàà máaǹ ní ŋá kí tīī-tee, ti ŋmēē kaa bālifì-ni an pu, án nín làá ŋá pu tiba ń taa lìkin̄-ni Kriisitoo Bɔmɔntiiti saŋŋunee.

eng: Dont you know that those who serve in the temple get to eat food from the temple, and those who serve at the altar share part of what is sacrificed on the altar?.
local: Ni nyí yii bin tūǹ ditundi kunimbɔtidiinee, niin jinti ní bi jīǹ, ní bin ŋáań saraabee mun cáá bi-jandi saraabee poon.

eng: In the same way, the Lord commanded that those who preach the gospel should get their living from the gospel.
local: Míǹ ní ti-Dindaan pú kinyɔkɔ yii bin sòoǹ Tibɔmɔntiilee ń nīn jīǹ bin cee bi sòoǹee ŋaan.

eng: But I havent taken advantage of this. And Im not writing this so that it will be done for me. Its better for me to die than to lose my right to brag about this!.
local: Ama maa bālifì tin an sá ní ŋá kí tīī-mee tiba nì tiba pu. Maa tí bíi ŋmà yii ní ŋá kí tīī-mi. M yaa gbíntì míǹ ki kpíì, an sɔ́ ki jítiǹ ubɔ ń bàtī m-nyooti.

eng: If I preach the gospel, I have no reason to brag, since Im obligated to do it. Im in trouble if I dont preach the gospel.
local: Tibɔmɔntiil sòontamee kaa sá-mi dilambitundi, ama an páb́-mi yii ḿ sòoǹ ní, kun puee an sá-mi mbusu ní m yaa kaa sòoǹ Tibɔmɔntiil gbantee.

eng: If I do this voluntarily, I get rewarded for it. But if Im forced to do it, then Ive been charged with a responsibility.
local: M yaa tūǹ ditundi gbanti nì dipɔbil dibee, m cáá tipaal. M yaa kaa tí túń-dì nì dipɔbil dibaaa, ditundi din Unimbɔti tīī-mee dee.

eng: What reward do I get? That when I preach, I offer the good news free of charge. Thats why I dont use the rights to which Im entitled through the gospel.
local: Mimmee, m-paati di sá ba? M-paati di sá ḿ sòoǹ Tibɔmɔntiil kí tīī binib faalaa kí taa fōō tiba, ama ki mun bàà cáá nsan kí fōō tiba.

eng: Although Im free from all people, I make myself a slave to all people, to recruit more of them.
local: M sá unil un yì u-bee ní, maa sá uyumbu. Ama kí ŋmā kí dáaliń kunipaau kí cáań Kriisitoo ceeyee, see m nín dū m-ba ki gíiǹ báà ŋma yumbuuee.

eng: If Im not an apostle to others, at least I am to you! You are the seal that shows Im an apostle.
local: Báà bin kíǹee yaa bí yii maa sá bíǹ cee Yeesu kpambal, nimbi máaǹ kí nīn nyí-mi yii m sá dikpambal. Ni nì ti-Dindaan nín ŋá ubaanti puee di dàkà yii m sá dikpambal.

eng: I act like a Jew to the Jews, so I can recruit Jews. I act like Im under the Law to those under the Law, so I can recruit those who are under the Law .
local: M yaa bí Juuda yab ceeyee, m gīintí m-ba kii Juuda yab nín bí puee ní, kí ŋmā kí cáań-bi Kriisitoo cee. M yaa bí bin dī Mooyiisi marabee ceeyee, m gīintí m-ba kii bi mun nín bí puee ní, kí ŋmā kí cáań-bi Kriisitoo cee. Ama ní kaa bí m-ba marabee taapu.

eng: I act like Im outside the Law to those who are outside the Law, so I can recruit those outside the Law .
local: M yaa tí bí bin kaa nóó Mooyiisi marabee ceeyee, m tí gīintí m-ba kii unil un kaa nóó marabee ní, kí ŋmā kí cáań-bi Kriisitoo cee. Míǹ kaa dàkà yii maa tɔ́ Unimbɔti marab. M tɔ́-bi, Kriisitoo marab taapu ní m bí.

eng: I act weak to the weak, so I can recruit the weak. I have become all things to all people, so I could save some by all possible means.
local: M yaa bí bin kaa pɔ̄ɔ̄ nnimbɔtisanee ceeyee, m gīintí m-ba kii bi mun nín bí puee ní, kí ŋmā kí cáań-bi Kriisitoo cee. Míǹ ní m gīintí m-ba báà ŋma nín bí miŋyee kí ŋmā kí fīī biba tiŋman pu.

eng: All the things I do are for the sake of the gospel, so I can be a partner with it.
local: Tibɔmɔntiil pu ní m pée bí ki ŋáań míǹ kɔkɔee kí ŋmā kí dāá ká tiwammɔntiil an poon.

eng: Dont you know that all the runners in the stadium run, but only one gets the prize? So run to win.
local: Naa nyí yii bin kɔkɔ cá ki sáń kpaŋkpakantikuee, ubaanti di lìntī ní kí fōō tipaalaaa? Ni mun, nín̄ sáḿmaan min máaǹ ní sāǹ kí lìntī kí fōō tipaalee.

eng: Everyone who competes practices self-discipline in everything. The runners do this to get a crown of leaves that shrivel up and die, but we do it to receive a crown that never dies.
local: Bin bin cá ditaatandi kpaŋkpakantikuuee kòó tiwan tikpil, mbeefuulimu mun kúùyee pu ní bíǹ ŋáań kpaŋkpakantikubee. Ama timbee, ti sáń kí fōō kifuulii kin làá nīn bí jaanjaanee ní.

eng: So now this is how I run—not without a clear goal in sight. I fight like a boxer in the ring, not like someone who is shadowboxing.
local: An pu ní m sáń m-nimbiliŋ bá kun m sáń ki nyàab̀ee pu. M yaa làá mɔ̀ee, m bɔ́ntí ní kí nín bɔ̄, maa bōotí fám fám.

eng: Rather, Im landing punches on my own body and subduing it like a slave. I do this to be sure that I myself wont be disqualified after preaching to others.
local: M sàantí m-wunti ní ki ŋúb́-tì. M pɔ̄ɔntí ní, án nín làá ŋá pu ḿ taa sòoǹ nì bin kíǹee Unimbɔti bɔti kí wāŋkì m-bee.

eng: This is my defense against those who criticize me.
local: M nín sòoǹ tin ki tùkù bin kpākantí-mee dee.

eng: Dont we have the right to eat and drink?.
local: Man kaa cáá nsan kí jī kí nyɔ̀ m-tundi puɔɔɔ?.

eng: Dont we have the right to travel with a wife who believes like the rest of the apostles, the Lords brothers, and Cephas?.
local: Maa cáá nsan ki mun ń yòon̄ unimpu un nóó Yeesuee kí ŋá m-nimpuu ú nīn táá-mi kii akpambal ŋin kíǹee nì ti-Dindaan nabiyaamu nì Peetroo nín ŋá pueeee?.

eng: Or is it only I and Barnabas who dont have the right to not work for our living?.
local: Àá an sá yii man nì Baarnaabaasi baba ní ń tɔ̄ ditundi ní kí nín ká kí jī ní?.

eng: Who joins the army and pays their own way? Who plants a vineyard and doesnt eat its fruit? Who shepherds a flock and doesnt drink its milk?.
local: Ŋma di kóò tisoojati baa pà-u? Àá unil kaa ŋmóò busubu bun u cóoǹee biliŋaaa? Àá unaakpaal kaa nyù inaa in u kpàáyee naabiimaaa?.

eng: Im not saying these things just based on common sense, am I? Doesnt the Law itself say these things?.
local: An kaa sá min binib nyí ki ŋáań díǹ nì foolee pu ní m sòoǹ, Mooyiisi marau mun lī míǹ ní.

eng: In Moses Law its written: You will not muzzle the ox when it is threshing . Is God worried about oxen.
local: An ŋmàn̄ Mooyiisi marab poon yii: «Á taa bóob́ unaa un gbáà idee nyɔkɔ.» Mimmee inaa bɔti páb́ Unimbɔtaaa?.

eng: We announce to you what existed from the beginning, what we have heard, what we have seen with our eyes, what we have seen and our hands handled, about the word of life.
local: Ti ŋmàn̄ kugbɔŋu nee kí tùkù-ni dimaŋfal bɔti. Dimaŋfal gbantee nìn pée bí doooo ŋkilkaamin ní. Ti gbìl̄ dì nín nìn sòon̄ puee, ki wál̀-dì nì ti-nimbiliŋ, ki máań-dì nì ti-ŋalii mun.

eng: If we claim, We have never sinned, we make him a liar and his word is not in us.
local: Ama ti yaa bí yii taa ŋá ikpitee, mimmee ti yíì Unimbɔti unyimɔndaan dee. An dàkà yii u-nyɔɔbundi kaa bí ti-pɔbiliŋin.

eng: The life was revealed, and we have seen, and we testify and announce to you the eternal life that was with the Father and was revealed to us.
local: Dimaŋfal gbanti nyáń mpaampiin pu, ní ti ká-dì. An pu ní ti sòoǹ ki tùkù-ni dimaŋfal din kaa cáá dikúntilee bɔti. Dì nìn bí ti-Baa Unimbɔti cee ní u nyántì-dì ki dàkā-ti.

eng: What we have seen and heard, we also announce it to you so that you can have fellowship with us. Our fellowship is with the Father and with his Son, Jesus Christ.
local: Míǹ puee tin tin ti kée nì tin ti gbìl̄ee ní ti cáa bíi tùkù ni mun, án nín làá ŋá pu ti nì ni kɔkɔ ń kpántí ubaantee. Kun puee timbi nì ti-Baa Unimbɔti nì u-Jipɔɔn Yeesu Kriisitoo ŋá ubaanti.

eng: We are writing these things so that our joy can be complete.
local: Ti ŋmà kugbɔŋu nee án nín làá ŋá pu ti-pɔbiliŋ ń mɔ̀tīí pīil̀ee.

eng: This is the message that we have heard from him and announce to you: God is light and there is no darkness in him at all.
local: Dinyɔɔbundi din ti gbìl̄ Yeesu cee ki cáa tùkù-nee di sá yii Unimbɔti sá ŋwalifi, ní dibɔmbɔndi kpá u-ni báà fiii.

eng: If we claim, We have fellowship with him, and live in the darkness, we are lying and do not act truthfully.
local: Míǹ puee, ti yaa bí yii ti nì u ŋá ubaanti ní ki gbíntìi còom̄ dibɔmbɔndinee, ti mɔ́ntí, taa dī ibaamɔn.

eng: But if we live in the light in the same way as he is in the light, we have fellowship with each other, and the blood of Jesus, his Son, cleanses us from every sin.
local: Ama ti yaa dī ŋwalifi saŋŋu kii Unimbɔti u-ba nín sá ŋwalifi puee, niinee ní ti nì ti-nabiyaamu ŋáań ubaanti, ní u-Jipɔɔn Yeesu fatikuu fíntí ti-pɔbiliŋ circir ki lèekī ti-kpitii kɔkɔ.

eng: If we claim, We dont have any sin, we deceive ourselves and the truth is not in us.
local: Ti yaa bí yii taa cáá ikpitee, mimmee ti bíi kíiĺ ti-ba ní ibaamɔn kaa bí ti-ni.

eng: But if we confess our sins, he is faithful and just to forgive us our sins and cleanse us from everything weve done wrong.
local: Ti yaa bīitì ti-kpitii pu, Unimbɔti sá deedeedaan ki cáá ibaamɔn ki làá dū ti-kpitii kí pɔ̄-ti, kí fíntí tin kɔkɔwoooo bí ti-pɔbiliŋin kaa ŋān̄ee, tí nīn bí circir.

eng: My little children, Im writing these things to you so that you dont sin. But if you do sin, we have an advocate with the Father, Jesus Christ the righteous one.
local: M-biyaamu, m ŋmà-ni tin bí nee án nín làá ŋá pu ní taa ŋá ikpitee. Ama ubɔ yaa jéetì ikpitinee, ti cáá un mɔ́tí ti-puee ti-Baa Unimbɔti cee. Úǹ di sá Yeesu Kriisitoo ibaamɔndaan.

eng: The person loving a brother and sister stays in the light, and there is nothing in the light that causes a person to stumble.
local: Ama sii un di yaa néeń a-nabikee còom̄ ŋwalifin dee, a kpá kí yàntī ubɔ ń lítí.

eng: But the person who hates a brother or sister is in the darkness and lives in the darkness, and doesnt know where to go because the darkness blinds the eyes.
local: Sii un di yaa ná a-nabikee, mimmee a bí dibɔmbɔndin ki còom̄, kaa nyí laakin pú a cée, kun puee dibɔmbɔndi yāntì a jɔ̀b̄.

eng: Little children, Im writing to you because your sins have been forgiven through Jesus name.
local: M-biyaamu, m ŋmà-ni tin bí nee, kun puee Yeesu yindi pu ní ni ká ni-kpitii pɔ̄tam.

eng: Parents, Im writing to you because you have known the one who has existed from the beginning. Young people, Im writing to you because you have conquered the evil one.
local: Bibaatiibi, m ŋmà-ni tin bí nee, kun puee ni nyí ti-Dindaan un nìn pée bí doooo ŋkilkaaminee. Binaacimpɔmbi, m ŋmà-ni tin bí nee, kun puee ni jáń ki pɔ́ɔkiǹ Sitaan.

eng: Little children, I write to you because you know the Father. Parents, I write to you because you have known the one who has existed from the beginning. Young people, I write to you because you are strong, the word of God remains in you, and you have conquered the evil one.
local: Mbiyaamu, m ŋmàn̄-ni tin bí nee, kun puee ni nyí ti-Baa Unimbɔti. Bibaatiibi, m ŋmàn̄-ni tin bí nee, kun puee ni nyí ti-Dindaan un nìn bí doooo ŋkilkaaminee. Binaacimpɔmbi, m ŋmàn̄-ni tin bí nee, kun puee Unimbɔti bɔti bí ni-ni, ni pɔ̄ɔ̄ ki ŋúń ki jáń ki pɔ́ɔkiǹ Sitaan.

eng: Dont love the world or the things in the world. If anyone loves the world, the love of the Father is not in them.
local: Ní taa nín̄ néeń duulinya nì tin u cááyee. Sii un di yaa néeń duulinyee, aa ŋūǹ kí nīn néeń Unimbɔti.

eng: Everything that is in the world—the craving for whatever the body feels, the craving for whatever the eyes see and the arrogant pride in ones possessions—is not of the Father but is of the world.
local: Kun puee tin kɔkɔ bí duulinyan, an dàkà yii mbimbikpitim kɔdeebi, nì nnimbitaataam, nì unil ń nīn néeń timɔn ki dàkà tigaajatee, tíǹ kɔkɔee kaa nyēeń ti-Baa Unimbɔti cee, an nyēeń duulinyan ní.

eng: And the world and its cravings are passing away, but the person who does the will of God remains forever.
local: Ní duulinya nì u-nimbitaataam kɔkɔ jītí, ama un di yaa ŋáań min ní Unimbɔti lèe, udaan làá dāá nīn bí ní jaanjaan.

eng: Little children, it is the last hour. Just as you have heard that the antichrist is coming, so now many antichrists have appeared. This is how we know it is the last hour.
local: M-biyaamu, ŋyuntijoolkaa sèé. Kii ni nín gbìl̄ yii Kriisitoo nannanliu dòońee, kɔŋkɔnnee Kriisitoo nannanliibi tikpil pūtì kpáàa bāań. Míǹ pu ní ti béè yii ŋyuntijoolkaa sèé nee.

eng: They went out from us, but they were not really part of us. If they had been part of us, they would have stayed with us. But by going out from us, they showed they all are not part of us.
local: Ti-kansikin ní binannanliibi gbanti nyáń. Ama baa nìn sá ti-yab, kun puee bi yaa bàà nìn sá ti-yabee, ti-kɔkɔ bàà bímaan mbaantiim ní. An nín ŋá pu nee di dàkà tiŋan yii ti nì bin nìn bée, bi-kɔkɔ kaa nìn sá ti-yab.

eng: He is Gods way of dealing with our sins, not only ours but the sins of the whole world.
local: U dū u-ba ki ŋá saraa ti-kpitii pu. Ama an kaa sá timbi kpitii ŋmaniŋma pu, an sá ki kpáaǹ binib kɔkɔwoooo kpitii pu ní.

eng: But you have an anointing from the holy one, and all of you know the truth.
local: Ama nimbee, ti-Dindaan circirdaan sītì ni-pu Circir Fam, an di yì-tì ní ni-kɔkɔ nyí ibaamɔn.

eng: I dont write to you because you dont know the truth but because you know it. You know that no lie comes from the truth.
local: Míǹ puee, m yaa ŋmàn̄-ni tin bí neeyee, kaa sá yii naa nyí ibaamɔn, ni nyí-i, ki béè yii ibaamɔn poonee, baa kāntí nnyimɔn báà ŋubɔ.

eng: Who is the liar? Isnt it the person who denies that Jesus is the Christ? This person is the antichrist: the one who denies the Father and the Son.
local: Ŋma di sá unyimɔndaan an yaa kaa sá un néè yii Yeesu kaa sá Kriisitooyee? Udaan di sá Kriisitoo nannanliu kaa là Ubaati, kaa là u-Jipɔɔn mun.

eng: Everyone who denies the Son does not have the Father, but the one who confesses the Son has the Father also.
local: Un di yaa kaa là Unimbɔti Jipɔɔn Yeesuee, kaa fōō u-Baa mun dee, ama un di yaa lì binib nimbiin yii Yeesu sá Ubaati Jipɔɔnee fōō Ubaati u-ba mun dee.

eng: As for you, what you heard from the beginning must remain in you. If what you heard from the beginning remains in you, you will also remain in relationship to the Son and in the Father.
local: Míǹ puee, tin tin ní ni nìn pée gbìl̄ doooo ŋkilkaaminee, ní kpāafìmaan-tì ni-pɔbiliŋin. Sii un yaa kpáafì tin ní a pée gbìl̄ doooo ŋkilkaaminee, mimmee sii ŋá ubaanti nì ti-Baa Unimbɔti nì u-Jipɔɔn dee.

eng: This is the promise that he himself gave us: eternal life.
local: Dipoondi din Yeesu pōoǹ-tee di sá dimaŋfal din kaa cáá dikúntilee.

eng: I write these things to you about those who are attempting to deceive you.
local: M ŋmàn̄ tin bí nee kí sòoǹ nì ni bin là kí ŋmàtiǹ-nee pu.

eng: As for you, the anointing that you received from him remains on you, and you dont need anyone to teach you the truth. But since his anointing teaches you about all things , remain in relationship to him just as he taught you.
local: Ama nimbee, Yeesu sītì ni-pu u-Fam. An puee, an kaa ti bàlifī ubɔ ń dàkà-ni. Kun puee u-Famee di dàkà-ni tikɔkɔ, ní tin ní u dàkèe sá ibaamɔn, nnyimɔn kpá poon. Míǹ puee, ní kpāafìmaan u-Fam nín dàkà-ni puee, ni nì Yeesu ń nīn sá ubaanti.

eng: And now, little children, remain in relationship to Jesus, so that when he appears we can have confidence and not be ashamed in front of him when he comes.
local: M-biyaamu, ní nīn bí Yeesu cee, án nín làá ŋá pu ŋyunti ŋun u yaa láa páaǹnée, tí dāá nīn cáá kunyannyaau kí yūĺ u-nimbiin kí taa nín̄ cáá icoo daliŋkin u làá bàńee.

eng: If you know that he is righteous, you also know that every person who practices righteousness is born from him.
local: Míǹ puee, ni yaa nyí yii u sá deedeedaanee, béémaan yii un sá kamaa yaa nóó deedeeyee, Unimbɔti di fātìi màl̄ udaan mpɔm.

eng: This is how we know that we know him: if we keep his commandments.
local: Ti yaa kpáafí Unimbɔti marabee, niinee ní ti béetí yii ti nyí-u.

eng: The one who claims, I know him, while not keeping his commandments, is a liar, and the truth is not in this person.
local: Un yaa bí yii: «M nyí Unimbɔti,» ama ní kaa ŋúb́ u-marabee sá unyimɔndaan, ibaamɔn kaa bí udaanni.

eng: But the love of God is truly perfected in whoever keeps his word. This is how we know we are in him.
local: Ama sii un di yaa kpáafí Unimbɔti bɔtee, mimmee sii di néeń Unimbɔti tiŋman. An pu ní a béetí yii a bí Unimbɔtin.

eng: The one who claims to remain in him ought to live in the same way as he lived.
local: Sii un di yaa lī yii a bí Unimbɔtinee, a máaǹ kí nīn còom̄ kii Yeesu u-ba nín nìn còom̄ puee.

eng: Dear friends, Im not writing a new commandment to you, but an old commandment that you had from the beginning. The old commandment is the message you heard.
local: M-nabineeŋkaamu, an kaa sá marapɔɔn ubɔ ní m gíti bíi ŋmà-ni, an sá marakpiki un ni nìn pée nyí doooo ŋkilkaaminee ní. An marakpikiuee di sá Unimbɔti bɔti tin ni nìn pée gbìl̄ee.

eng: On the other hand, I am writing a new commandment to you, which is true in him and in you, because the darkness is passing away and the true light already shines.
local: Nì míǹ kɔkɔee, u sá marapɔɔn ní m bíi ŋmà-ni. Ibaamɔn in bí mara un nyánní Yeesunee bí ni mun ni, kun puee dibɔmbɔndi bíi jītí ní ŋwalifimɔnti dɔ́miń ki ŋmíntì ti-pu.

eng: The one who claims to be in the light while hating a brother or sister is in the darkness even now.
local: Sii un di yaa bí yii a còom̄ ŋwalifin ní ki gbíntìi ná a-nabikee, a dáá gbíntìi bí dibɔmbɔndin.

eng: See what kind of love the Father has given to us in that we should be called Gods children, and that is what we are! Because the world didnt recognize him, it doesnt recognize us.
local: Ní kpèèmaan ti-Baa Unimbɔti nín cáá ti-pu dineeŋkpaandi diŋyee! Di sá yii u yíì-ti u-biyaamu. Míǹ ní tiŋman ti lafun sá u-biyaamu. Kun pu duulinya kaa nyí-tee di sá waa béè Unimbɔti.

eng: This is how Gods children and the devils children are apparent: everyone who doesnt practice righteousness is not from God, including the person who doesnt love a brother or sister.
local: Kun gàntī Unimbɔti biyaamu nì Sitaan yɔmuee di sá, sii un yaa kaa ŋáań tin bí deedeeyee nì sii un yaa kaa néeń a-nabikee kaa sá Unimbɔti yɔu.

eng: This is the message that you heard from the beginning: love each other.
local: Dinyɔɔbundi din ni nìn gbìl̄ doooo ŋkilkaaminee tùkū-ti yii tí nīn néeń tɔb.

eng: Dont behave like Cain, who belonged to the evil one and murdered his brother. And why did he kill him? He killed him because his own works were evil, but the works of his brother were righteous.
local: Tí taa nín̄ bí kii Kayeen nín nìn bí puee. Úǹ nìn sá Sitaan niliu ní, ní ki kūū u-naal. Ba pu ní u nìn kūū u-naal míǹ? Kun pu u nìn kūū-uee di sá Kayeen tuuŋŋi sá akpiti, ní u-naal tuuŋŋi sá deedee yaŋ.

eng: Dont be surprised, brothers and sisters, if the world hates you.
local: Nnabiyaamu, duulinya yaa ná-nee, an taa bàkatīǹ-ni.

eng: We know that we have transferred from death to life, because we love the brothers and sisters. The person who does not love remains in death.
local: Ti nyí yii ti nyáń ŋkúmin ki póotì dimaŋfalin, kun puee ti néeń ti-nabiyaamu. Un yaa kaa néeń u-nabikee dáá gbíntìi bí ŋkúmin.

eng: Everyone who hates a brother or sister is a murderer, and you know that murderers dont have eternal life residing in them.
local: Un yaa kaa néeń u-nabikee sá unikpɔl ní, ní ni nyí yii unikpɔl báà ubɔ kaa cáá dimaŋfal din kaa cáá dikúntilee u-ni.

eng: This is how we know love: Jesus laid down his life for us, and we ought to lay down our lives for our brothers and sisters.
local: Kun di yì-tì ti béè dineendi nín sá kuŋyee di sá Yeesu nín dū u-maŋfal ki tīī ti-puee. Míǹ mun mbaantiim ní ti mun máaǹ kí dū ti-maŋfal kí tīī ti-nabiyaamu pu.

eng: But if someone has material possessions and sees a brother or sister in need but refuses to help—how can the love of God dwell in a person like that?.
local: Ubɔ yaa cáá ŋŋmam duulinya nee poon, ní ki ká u-nabiki bí falaani, ní u ŋmí, kaa tīī-u tibee, mimmee ba di dàkà yii Unimbɔti neendi bí udaan ni?.

eng: Little children, lets not love with words or speech but with action and truth.
local: M-biyaamu, tí taa nín̄ lì nì ti-nyɔmmun fám yii ti néeń tɔb, ama tí yàntī ti-tuuŋŋi ń dàkà ibaamɔn yii ti lafun cáá dineendi.

eng: This is how we will know that we belong to the truth and reassure our hearts in Gods presence.
local: Míǹ ní ti dùú béé yii ti sá ibaamɔn nib, ní ki kāntí dipɔɔsɔŋkil Unimbɔti nimbiin.

eng: Dear friends, now we are Gods children, and it hasnt yet appeared what we will be. We know that when he appears we will be like him because well see him as he is.
local: M-nabineeŋkaamu, kí yòon̄ díǹ kí nīn cée, ti kpántì Unimbɔti biyaamu. Ti nín làá dāá nīn bí miŋyee kaa láá nyáń mpaampiin pu. Ama ti nyí yii ŋyunti ŋun ti-Dindaan yaa láa páaǹnée, ti làá dāá nīn bí kii u nín bí puee ní, kun puee ti làá dāá ká-u kí béé un ní u baasii sée.

eng: Even if our hearts condemn us, God is greater than our hearts and knows all things.
local: Báà ti-pɔbiliŋ yaa máfí yii ti sá binikpitibee, Unimbɔti jítiǹ ti-pɔbiliŋ ki nyí tikɔkɔ mun.

eng: Dear friends, if our hearts dont condemn us, we have confidence in relationship to God.
local: M-nabineeŋkaamu, sii un pɔbil yaa kaa tí máfí yii a ŋá tibee, mimmee a cáá dimákal Unimbɔti nimbiin dee.

eng: We receive whatever we ask from him because we keep his commandments and do what pleases him.
local: Báà tin sá kamaa ní a yaa mēē-uee u làá tīī-si kudɔŋu, kun puee a kpáafí u-marab ki ŋáań min fáatì-uee.

eng: This is his commandment, that we believe in the name of his Son, Jesus Christ, and love each other as he commanded us.
local: Mara un u tīī-tee di sá tí fōō u-Jipɔɔn Yeesu Kriisitoo kí kíí, kí nīn néeń tɔb, kii Yeesu u-ba nín bí yii tí nīn néeń tɔb puee.

eng: Those who keep his commandments dwell in God and God dwells in them. This is how we know that he dwells in us, because of the Spirit he has given us.
local: Un di yaa kpáafí u-marab gbantee, udaan ŋá ubaanti nì Unimbɔti, ní Unimbɔti bí udaanni. Kun pu ní ti nyí yii u bí ti-nee di sá u-Fam min u tīī-tee pu.

eng: And all who have this hope in him purify themselves even as he is pure.
local: Sii un di yaa cáá dimákal gbanti Yeesunee bɔ́ntí a-pɔbil circir, kii a-Dindaan u-ba nín bí pu circiree ní.

eng: Every person who practices sin commits an act of rebellion, and sin is rebellion.
local: Un yaa ŋá ikpitee yīntì marab dee, di sá yii marab yìntitam di sá ikpiti.

eng: You know that he appeared to take away sins, and there is no sin in him.
local: Ni nyí yii Kriisitoo dɔ́miń kí lēē binib kpitii ní, ní úǹ u-ba kaa cáá ikpiti.

eng: Every person who remains in relationship to him does not sin. Any person who sins has not seen him or known him.
local: Sii un nì Kriisitoo yaa ŋá ubaantee kaa ti ŋáań ikpiti, ama sii un di yaa ŋáań ikpitee kaa ká-uaaa, kaa nyí-u mun.

eng: Little children, make sure no one deceives you. The person who practices righteousness is righteous, in the same way that Jesus is righteous.
local: M-biyaamu, ní taa yàntī ubɔ ń ŋmàtiǹ-ni. Un yaa ŋáań min bí deedeeyee sá deedeedaan, kii ti-Dindaan u-ba nín bí deedee puee.

eng: The person who practices sin belongs to the devil, because the devil has been sinning since the beginning. Gods Son appeared for this purpose: to destroy the works of the devil.
local: Ama un yaa ŋáań ikpitee sá Sitaan yɔu, kun puee Sitaan pée sá ikpitidaan ní doooo ŋkilkaamin. An pu ní Unimbɔti Jipɔɔn dɔ́miń ki kpītì Sitaan tuuŋŋi.

eng: Those born from God dont practice sin because Gods DNA remains in them. They cant sin because they are born from God.
local: Sii un Unimbɔti yaa fātìi màl̄ mpɔmee kaa ti ŋáań ikpiti, kun puee a náań nì a-Baa un màl̄-see. Aa ti ŋūǹ kí nīn ŋáań ikpiti, kun puee Unimbɔti di fātìi màl̄-si mpɔm.

eng: Dear friends, dont believe every spirit. Test the spirits to see if they are from God because many false prophets have gone into the world.
local: M-nabineeŋkaamu, ní taa nín̄ máká bin kɔkɔ lì yii bi cáá Ŋfamee, ama ní cɔ̄ŋkì kí dáań kí kpèè yii an sá Unimbɔti Famaaa, an kaa sá. Di sá yii nnyimɔn bɔnaatiliibi gbóotì duulinyan tikpil.

eng: This is love: it is not that we loved God but that he loved us and sent his Son as the sacrifice that deals with our sins.
local: Kaa sá timbi di péé ki néeń Unimbɔti, an sá Unimbɔti di péé ki néeń timbi, ní ki dū u-Jipɔɔn ki túnní ú kpú tí ká ti-kpitii pɔ̄tam, míǹ di dàkà dineendi.

eng: Dear friends, if God loved us this way, we also ought to love each other.
local: M-nabineeŋkaamu, Unimbɔti yaa néeń-ti míǹ bulifee, ti mun máaǹ kí nīn néeń tɔb.

eng: No one has ever seen God. If we love each other, God remains in us and his love is made perfect in us.
local: Ubɔ kaa pée ká Unimbɔti dalba, ama ti yaa néeń tɔbee, Unimbɔti bí ti-ni, ní u-neendee bí ti-ni tiŋman.

eng: This is how we know we remain in him and he remains in us, because he has given us a measure of his Spirit.
local: Kun di yì-tì ti béè yii ti ŋá ubaanti nì Unimbɔti, ní Unimbɔti bí ti-nee, di sá u-Fam min u tīī-tee pu.

eng: We have seen and testify that the Father has sent the Son to be the savior of the world.
local: ní ti ká ki béè, ki kíǹ ki tùkù binib yii ti-Baa Unimbɔti dū u-Jipɔɔn ki túnní ú dāan̄ kí nīn sá duulinya Fiiliu.

eng: If any of us confess that Jesus is Gods Son, God remains in us and we remain in God.
local: Sii un di yaa lì nì ibaamɔn yii Yeesu sá Unimbɔti Jipɔɔnee, Unimbɔti bí a-ni ní si nì u ŋá ubaanti.

eng: We have known and have believed the love that God has for us. God is love, and those who remain in love remain in God and God remains in them.
local: Timbi béè dineendi din Unimbɔti cáá ti-puee ní ki fōō-dì ki kíí. Di sá yii Unimbɔti di sá dineendi, un yaa cáá dineendee, udaan nì Unimbɔti ŋá ubaanti, ní Unimbɔti bí udaanni.

eng: This is how love has been perfected in us, so that we can have confidence on the Judgment Day, because we are exactly the same as God is in this world.
local: Kun di dàkà yii a néeń-u tiŋmanee di sá, daliŋkin Yeesu làá dāá sòoǹ binib bɔtee, sii dāa cáá dimákal u-nimbiin ní, kun puee a-bimbim min a bí duulinya nee nee nì u-yam náań.

eng: There is no fear in love, but perfect love drives out fear, because fear expects punishment. The person who is afraid has not been made perfect in love.
local: Sii un cáá dineendee kaa fàŋkí, kun puee dineembaamɔndi jì tifaandi ní. Ditafadaaldi di cāabìń tifaandi, míǹ puee, sii un cáá tifaandi a-nee kaa cáá dineembaamɔndi dee.

eng: We love because God first loved us.
local: Kun pu timbi cáá dineendee di sá úǹ di péé ki néeń timbi.

eng: This is how you know if a spirit comes from God: every spirit that confesses that Jesus Christ has come as a human is from God.
local: Kun di làá yàntī ní béé un Unimbɔti Fam kpòotēe di sá un di yaa lì nì ibaamɔn yii Yeesu Kriisitoo kpántì unil ki dɔ́miń duulinyanee, mimmee Unimbɔti Fam di yāntì udaan sòon̄.

eng: Those who say, I love God and hate their brothers or sisters are liars. After all, those who dont love their brothers or sisters whom they have seen can hardly love God whom they have not seen!.
local: Ubɔ yaa bí yii: «M néeń Unimbɔti», ní ki yaa ná u-nabikee, udaan sá unyimɔndaan. A yaa kaa néeń a-nabiki kin a wāĺee, a làá ŋá mana ní kí nīn néeń Unimbɔti un saa wāĺee?.

eng: This commandment we have from him: Those who claim to love God ought to love their brother and sister also.
local: Mara un Yeesu tīī-tee sèé yii, sii un yaa néeń Unimbɔtee máaǹ kí nīn néeń a-nabiki mun.

eng: and every spirit that doesnt confess Jesus is not from God. This is the spirit of the antichrist, which you have heard is coming and is now already in the world.
local: Un yaa yíì ki bí yii Yeesu kaa kpántì unil ki dɔ́miń duulinyanee, an kaa sá Unimbɔti Fam di yāntì udaan sòon̄, an sá Kriisitoo nannanliu fam. Ni nìn pée gbìl̄ yii unannanli gbanti làá dāan̄, ama u pūtì kpáàa bāań ki bí duulinyan.

eng: You are from God, little children, and you have defeated these people because the one who is in you is greater than the one who is in the world.
local: M-biyaamu, nimbi sá Unimbɔti yab ní, an pu ní ni jáń ki pɔ́ɔkiǹ bin mɔ́ntí yii bi sá Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibee. Kun puee Unimbɔti Fam min bí ni-nee pɔ̄ɔ̄ ki jítiǹ Sitaan un bí duulinyanee.

eng: They are from the world. So they speak from the worlds point of view and the world listens to them.
local: Binyimɔndambi gbantee, bíǹ sá duulinya yab ní. Bi sòoǹ tin duulinya nyée ní, míǹ pu ní an mɔ̄ǹ duulinya yab pílifíǹ-bi.

eng: We are from God. The person who knows God listens to us. Whoever is not from God doesnt listen to us. This is how we recognize the Spirit of truth and the spirit of error.
local: Ama timbi sá Unimbɔti yab ní. Un yaa nyí Unimbɔtee di pílifíǹ-ti. Un kaa là kí pīlifiǹ-tee kaa sá Unimbɔti yɔu. An di yì-tì ní ti ŋūǹ kí gàntī Unimbɔti Fam min sá ibaamɔnee nì Sitaan yam min mɔ́ntée.

eng: Dear friends, lets love each other, because love is from God, and everyone who loves is born from God and knows God.
local: M-nabineeŋkaamu, tí nīn néeń tɔb, kun puee dineendi nyēeń Unimbɔti cee ní. Mimmee, sii un yaa cáá dineendee di sá un Unimbɔti fātìi màl̄ mpɔmee, ki nyí Unimbɔti mun.

eng: The person who doesnt love does not know God, because God is love.
local: Sii un yaa kaa cáá dineendee kaa nyí Unimbɔti, kun puee Unimbɔti di sá dineendi.

eng: This is how the love of God is revealed to us: God has sent his only Son into the world so that we can live through him.
local: Kun yāntì ti ká dineendi din u cáá ti-puee di sá u dū u-Jipɔmbaabil ki túnní duulinyan, tí ŋmā kí ká dimaŋfal u-pu.

eng: Everyone who believes that Jesus is the Christ has been born from God. Whoever loves someone who is a parent loves the child born to the parent.
local: Un sá kamaa yaa fōō ki kíí yii Yeesu sá Kriisitooyee, Unimbɔti di fātìi màl̄ udaan mpɔm. Un di yaa tí néeń Ubaatee néeń u-biyaamu mun ní.

eng: The one who believes in Gods Son has the testimony within; the one who doesnt believe God has made God a liar, because that one has not believed the testimony that God gave about his Son.
local: Un yaa fōō Unimbɔti Jipɔɔn ki kííyee cáá seera gbanti u-pɔbilin. Un yaa kaa fōō seera un Unimbɔti lēē u-Jipɔɔn puee ki kííyee, udaan kaa fōō Unimbɔti ki kíí ki yíì-u unyimɔndaan.

eng: And this is the testimony: God gave eternal life to us, and this life is in his Son.
local: Unimbɔti nín lī puee sèé yii, u tīī-ti dimaŋfal din kaa cáá dikúntilee. Dimaŋfal gbanti bí u-Jipɔɔn cee ní.

eng: The one who has the Son has life. The one who doesnt have Gods Son does not have life.
local: Un yaa fōō u-Jipɔɔnee cáá dimaŋfal, un yaa kaa fōō u-Jipɔɔnee kaa cáá dimaŋfal.

eng: I write these things to you who believe in the name of Gods Son so that you can know that you have eternal life.
local: Nimbi bin fōō Unimbɔti Jipɔɔn ki kííyee, m ŋmàn̄-ni tin bí nee ní béé yii ni cáá dimaŋfal din kaa cáá dikúntilee.

eng: This is the confidence that we have in our relationship with God: If we ask for anything in agreement with his will, he listens to us.
local: Ti cáá dipɔbil kí cù u-cee, di sá yii ti yaa mēē-u tiba ní an yaa sá min ní u lèe, u làá pīlifiǹ-ti.

eng: If we know that he listens to whatever we ask, we know that we have received what we asked from him.
local: Míǹ puee, ti yaa nyí yii u pílifíǹ-tee, tin kɔkɔwoooo ti yaa mēē-uee, tí béé yii ti fōō kudɔŋu.

eng: If anyone sees a brother or sister committing a sin that does not result in death, they should pray, and God will give life to them—that is, to those who commit sins that dont result in death. There is a sin that results in death—Im not saying that you should pray about that.
local: Ubɔ yaa ká u-nabiki bíi ŋáań ikpiti in kaa cáǹ-u ŋkúminee, udaan ń mèè, Unimbɔti làá tīī u-nabiki gbanti dimaŋfal. Bin pu m sòoǹee di sá bin ŋáań ikpiti in kaa cáǹ-bi ŋkúminee, kun puee ikpiti iba cáǹ unil ŋkúmin. Kaa sá un ŋá ikpiti gbantee pu ní m bí yii ní mèè.

eng: Every unrighteous action is sin, but there is a sin that does not result in death.
local: Tin kɔkɔ kaa bí deedeeyee sá ikpiti ní, ama ní ikpiti iba bí kaa cāabìń ŋkúm.

eng: We know that everyone born from God does not sin, but the ones born from God guard themselves, and the evil one cannot touch them.
local: Ti nyí yii un sá kamaa Unimbɔti fātìi màl̄ mpɔmee kaa ŋáań ikpiti, kun puee Unimbɔti Jipɔɔn mɔ́tí u-pu, Sitaan Ukpiti kaa ŋūǹ kí ŋá-u tiba.

eng: We know we are from God, and the whole world lies in the power of the evil one.
local: Ti nyí yii ti sá Unimbɔti yab, ní Ukpiti tɔ́kɔ́ duulinya kɔkɔ pu.

eng: This is how we know that we love the children of God: when we love God and keep Gods commandments.
local: Min ní ti béetí yii ti néeń Unimbɔti biyaamuee di sá ti yaa néeń Unimbɔti ki kpáafí u-marabee.

eng: We know that Gods Son has come and has given us understanding to know the one who is true. We are in the one who is true by being in his Son, Jesus Christ. This is the true God and eternal life.
local: Ti tí nyí mun yii Unimbɔti Jipɔɔn dɔ́miń ki tīī-ti iciin tí ŋmā kí béé ibaamɔn Nimbɔtiu, ní timbi nì Unimbɔti ibaamɔndaanee nì Yeesu Kriisitoo u-Jipɔɔn mun ŋá ubaanti. Úǹ di sá Unimbɔti ibaamɔn, ki sá dimaŋfal din kaa cáá dikúntilee.

eng: Little children, guard yourselves from idols!.
local: M-biyaamu, ní nīn nyí nì ni-ba kí taa nóó duulinya nimbɔtii.

eng: This is the love of God: we keep Gods commandments. Gods commandments are not difficult.
local: Tí nīn néeń Unimbɔtee taapu di sá yii tí nīn kpáafí u-marab. U-marab gbanti mun kaa pɔ̄ɔ̄.

eng: because everyone who is born from God defeats the world. And this is the victory that has defeated the world: our faith.
local: kun puee un sá kamaa ní Unimbɔti fātìi màl̄ mpɔmee ŋūǹ kí já kí pɔ̄ɔkiǹ duulinya. Kun pu ní a ŋūǹ kí já kí pɔ̄ɔkiǹ duulinyee di sá fookii un a cáá Yeesu puee.

eng: Who defeats the world? Isnt it the one who believes that Jesus is Gods Son?.
local: Ŋma di ŋūǹ kí já kí pɔ̄ɔkiǹ duulinya an yaa kaa sá sii un yaa fōō ki kíí yii Yeesu sá Unimbɔti Jipɔɔnee?.

eng: This is the one who came by water and blood: Jesus Christ. Not by water only but by water and blood. And the Spirit is the one who testifies, because the Spirit is the truth.
local: An sá úǹ Yeesu Kriisitoo di dɔ́miń duulinyan nì nnyim nì fatiku. An kaa sá nì nnyim ŋmaniŋma, ama an sá nì nnyim nì fatiku ní. Ní Unimbɔti Fam mun lèekī seera yii an sá míǹ, kun puee mì sá mbaamɔn ní.

eng: The three are testifying.
local: Biseeraleeliibi sá bita ní.

eng: the Spirit, the water, and the blood—and the three are united in agreement.
local: Ŋfam nì nnyim nì fatiku, ní bi-taatilee kpáfì ki lèekī seerabaanti.

eng: If we receive human testimony, Gods testimony is greater, because this is what God testified: he has testified about his Son.
local: Ti yaa fòol̄ binib seerau ki kíikée, an máaǹ tí fōō Unimbɔti seerau kí kíí, kun puee Unimbɔti seerau pɔ̄ɔ̄ ki jítiǹ unil yɔu.

eng: Peter, an apostle of Jesus Christ, To Gods chosen strangers in the world of the diaspora, who live in Pontus, Galatia, Cappadocia, Asia, and Bithynia.
local: Man Peetroo un sá Yeesu Kriisitoo kpambalee di jáaḿ-ni. M ŋmàn̄ kugbɔŋu nee kí tīī nimbi bin Unimbɔti lútì ni yàl̄ ki bíń kunicaŋu Pɔnti nì Galaati nì Kapadoosi nì Asii nì Bitinii timmunee ní.

eng: The prophets, who long ago foretold the grace that youve received, searched and explored, inquiring carefully about this salvation.
local: Bibɔnaatiliibi nìn nyàab̄ mmɔntiim kí béé difiil bɔti tiŋan. Bi nìn sòon̄ dipɔl din ni làá fōō nee pu ki síiǹ.

eng: They wondered what the Spirit of Christ within them was saying when he bore witness beforehand about the suffering that would happen to Christ and the glory that would follow. They wondered what sort of person or what sort of time they were speaking about.
local: Kriisitoo Fam di nìn bí bi-ni ní ki dàkā-bi falaa un u làá dāá jēe nì tinyool tin làá dāá páanní falaayee boonee. Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi nìn nyàab̄ kí béé ŋyunti nì min ní tíǹ gbanti kɔkɔ làá bàńee.

eng: It was revealed to them that in their search they were not serving themselves but you. These things, which even angels long to examine, have now been proclaimed to you by those who brought you the good news. They did this in the power of the Holy Spirit, who was sent from heaven.
local: Unimbɔti nìn lēē ki dàkā u-bɔnaatiliibi yii tibɔti tin bi bí ki sòoǹee, bíǹ kaa làá ká ti-poon tiba, ama nimbi di dāa yì. Kɔŋkɔnnee bin sòoǹ Kriisitoo Bɔmɔntiitee sòon̄ nì ni tibɔbaantiil gbanti ní, kun puee Unimbɔti Fam min nyánní yilpuee di dàkā-bi. Áli gba Unimbɔti tuuŋyi mun bàà là kí béé min ní an bɔtee làá jītée.

eng: Therefore, once you have your minds ready for action and you are thinking clearly, place your hope completely on the grace that will be brought to you when Jesus Christ is revealed.
local: Míǹ puee, ní bɔ́ntí ni-ba ni-nimbiliŋ ń nīn cɔ́ tiŋan tin pu ni máká yii Yeesu Kriisitoo yaa láa gítìń ni làá dāá fōōyee pu, kí nīn ŋúb́ ni-ba.

eng: Dont be conformed to your former desires, those that shaped you when you were ignorant. But, as obedient children.
local: Ní nīn tɔ́ Unimbɔti, kí taa nín̄ gíti nóó ni-bimbikpitim min naa nìn dáá nyí ki ŋáańee.

eng: you must be holy in every aspect of your lives, just as the one who called you is holy.
local: Ní nīn bí circir ni-bimbim kɔkɔ poon kii Unimbɔti un yíiń-nee nín bí circir puee.

eng: It is written, You will be holy, because I am holy. .
local: A lì kii Unimbɔti nín lī u-gbɔŋku poon puee yii: «Nín̄ bímaan circir kun puee man bí circir ní.».

eng: Since you call upon a Father who judges all people according to their actions without favoritism, you should conduct yourselves with reverence during the time of your dwelling in a strange land.
local: Ni-wimmeem poonee ni yíì Unimbɔti yii ni-Baa. Ama ní téetí yii úǹ di sòoǹ báà ŋma bɔti ki nóó nì min udaan ŋée, kaa gàntī, an puee iyunti in kíǹ ní dū kí kàl̄ duulinyanee, dàkàmaan-u ni-bimbim poon ni nín tɔ́-u puee.

eng: Live in this way, knowing that you were not liberated by perishable things like silver or gold from the empty lifestyle you inherited from your ancestors.
local: Nimbi ni-ba nyí Unimbɔti nín dī min ki nyántìń-ni ni-naanjab saŋyi in ni nìn nóó fám tiba kpá poonee. An kaa sá tiwan tin bíiĺ kii animbilee àá kii sindaariyee ní u dū ki nyántì-ni.

eng: Instead, you were liberated by the precious blood of Christ, like that of a flawless, spotless lamb.
local: Ama an sá nì Kriisitoo fatikuu un jítiǹ tikɔkɔ nì tikɔkɔee ní u dū ki nyántì-ni. Kriisitoo di sá kii dipibimɔntiil din pu baa kāntí tikpitil tiba nì tibee.

eng: God the Father chose you because of what he knew beforehand. He chose you through the Holy Spirits work of making you holy and because of the faithful obedience and sacrifice of Jesus Christ. May Gods grace and peace be multiplied to you.
local: Ti-Baa Unimbɔti lútì-ni ní nīn sá u-yab u-Fam pu. U nìn pée lī míǹ ki yóoǹ dooooyee ní. Kun puee u là ní nīn tɔ́ Yeesu Kriisitoo u-fatikuu ń fíntí-ni ní nyā. Unimbɔti ń ŋá-ni tiŋan kí tīī-ni ŋgbansɔŋfi tikpil.

eng: Christ was chosen before the creation of the world, but was only revealed at the end of time. This was done for you.
local: Unimbɔti lēē Kriisitoo ki wāatì ní ki nín náań duulinya. Ni-puee ní u nyántì-u ŋyuntijoolkaa nee poon.

eng: who through Christ are faithful to the God who raised him from the dead and gave him glory. So now, your faith and hope should rest in God.
local: U-pu ní ni fōō Unimbɔti un fīkitì-u ditaŋkpiilin ki tīī-u tinyoolee ki kíí, án nín làá ŋá pu ni-fookiiu nì ni-mákal ń nīn cɔ́ Unimbɔti puee.

eng: As you set yourselves apart by your obedience to the truth so that you might have genuine affection for your fellow believers, love each other deeply and earnestly.
local: Ni nín nóó mbaamɔn saŋŋuee ní ni kpántì circirdambi, ní ki néeń ni-nabiyaamu bin mun fōō ki kííyee mbaamɔmin. Míǹ puee nín̄ gbíntì ki néeḿmaan tɔb tikpil nì dipɔbil diba.

eng: Do this because you have been given new birth—not from the type of seed that decays but from seed that doesnt. This seed is Gods life-giving and enduring word.
local: Di sá yii Unimbɔti di tīī-ni dimaŋfapɔndi. Dinyɔɔbundi gbantee kaa sá kii ŋwambool ŋun kúùyee cee. Dinyɔɔbundee kaa kúù, di bí ní ki cá jaanjaan.

eng: Thus, All human life on the earth is like grass, and all human glory is like a flower in a field. The grass dries up and its flower falls off, .
local: Kii Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon nín lī puee yii: «Binib kɔkɔ bí kii kumoouee ní, ní bi-nyooti bí kii mpumpukumee, kumoou kōō ní mpumpukum ń gbátí.

eng: but the Lords word endures forever. This is the word that was proclaimed to you as good news.
local: Ama ti-Dindaan bɔti tíǹ pée bí ní kí nīn cá jaanjaan.» Tibɔti gbanti di sá Kriisitoo Bɔmɔntiiti tin bi cáań ki sòon̄ nì ni nee.

eng: May the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ be blessed! On account of his vast mercy, he has given us new birth. You have been born anew into a living hope through the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead.
local: Tí nyɔ̄ŋkì Unimbɔti ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo Baa, kun puee u súń ti-saai tikpil. U nín fīkitì Yeesu Kriisitoo ditaŋkpiilinee ní u pú-ti nsan tí kɔ́ dimaŋfapɔndin. Kɔŋkɔnnee ti cáá dimákal tiŋman pu yal.

eng: You have a pure and enduring inheritance that cannot perish—an inheritance that is presently kept safe in heaven for you.
local: An pu ní ti cí ú tīī-ti difakal din u yóoǹ kí dāá tīī bin sá u-yabee. Difakal gbantee kaa bíiĺ, kaa jòontí, kaa dóotí. Unimbɔti dū-dì ki kpáafì yilpu ki yóoǹ nimbi ní.

eng: Through his faithfulness, you are guarded by Gods power so that you can receive the salvation he is ready to reveal in the last time.
local: Ni nín fōō Unimbɔti bɔti ki kííyee ní u ŋúb́-ni taapu doo nì u-pɔŋŋu, án nín làá ŋá pu ŋwindalee, ú dāá tīī-ni difiil din u bɔ́ntì ki yóoǹee, báà ŋma ń dāá wāl̀.

eng: You now rejoice in this hope, even if its necessary for you to be distressed for a short time by various trials.
local: Míǹ puee ní yàntī án nīn mɔ̄ǹ-ni báà ŋyunti fiii ŋun poon ti bí nee gba ní icɔŋki nín bí puee nì i-nyɔkɔm yaa cāabìń-ni kunimbaau.

eng: This is necessary so that your faith may be found genuine. Your genuine faith will result in praise, glory, and honor for you when Jesus Christ is revealed.
local: An falaabee sá kí cɔ̄ŋkì kí kpèè yii ni-fookiiu pɔ̄ɔ̄yaaa, waa pɔ̄ɔ̄ ní. Kpèèmaan, sindaariya un bí ki làá wāŋkì ŋwindalee. Bi ŋáań-u ŋŋmin ní kí kpèè yii u sá umɔntaaa, waa sá. Míǹ mbaantiimin ní falaa sá kí cɔ̄ŋkì ni-fookiiu un jítiǹ sindaariya pu kpataaayee kí kpèè yii u pɔ̄ɔ̄yaaa, waa pɔ̄ɔ̄. Ni-fookiiuee cáá kudapɔɔu ki jítiǹ sindaariya pu kpataaa. Ni yaa jáń ki nyáń icɔŋki poonee ní Yeesu Kriisitoo ń dāá pàkà-ni kí tīī-ni tinyool nì jilma u-gítintam dal.

eng: Although youve never seen him, you love him. Even though you dont see him now, you trust him and so rejoice with a glorious joy that is too much for words.
local: Yeesu Kriisitoo gbantee, naa ká-u nì ni-nimbiliŋ, ama ni néeń-u. Ni fōō-u ki kíí kaa wāĺ-u, ki fàal̀ nì dipɔɔpiŋkpaandi din baa ŋūǹ kí māafèe.

eng: You are receiving the goal of your faith: your salvation.
local: Kun puee ni làá dāá fōō ni-fookiiu paati tin sá ni-nammu fīitamee.

eng: Therefore, get rid of all ill will and all deceit, pretense, envy, and slander.
local: Míǹ puee, ní fá tibɔkpitil kɔkɔ nì inyimɔn kɔkɔ nì mpoonnyoon nì ipoompoon nì tibɔfan disoondi.

eng: Once you werent a people, but now you are Gods people. Once you hadnt received mercy, but now you have received mercy.
local: Dooooyee, naa nìn sá Unimbɔti nibul, ama kɔŋkɔnnee ni kpántì u-nibul. Dooooyee, naa nìn nyí yii Unimbɔti sūǹ binib saai, ama kɔŋkɔnnee u súń ni-saai ni béè.

eng: Dear friends, since you are immigrants and strangers in the world, I urge that you avoid worldly desires that wage war against your lives.
local: M-nabineeŋkaamu, m mèé-ni kii ni nín sá binicambi duulinya nee poon puee, ní cúúmaan ni-ba nì bikɔdeekpitib, kun puee an di jáaǹ kinaŋ.

eng: Live honorably among the unbelievers. Today, they defame you, as if you were doing evil. But in the day when God visits to judge they will glorify him, because they have observed your honorable deeds.
local: Ní nīn cáá mbimbimɔntiim bin kaa nyí Unimbɔtee kansikin. Báà biba yaa bàà bíi kúŋfí ni-boon yii ni sá binikpitibaaa, bi yaa ká ni-tunnyaaŋŋee, Unimbɔti ń yàntī bí béé yii an tunnyaaŋŋi nyánní úǹ cee ní, ní bí pàkà-u ŋyunti ŋun u yaa láa dɔ́mińee.

eng: For the sake of the Lord submit to every human institution. Do this whether it means submitting to the emperor as supreme ruler.
local: Ti-Dindaan puee, nín̄ tɔ́maan biyidambi, kí nīn tɔ́ gɔmina un cáá mpɔn ki kpèé kitiŋ kɔkɔee.

eng: or to governors as those sent by the emperor. They are sent to punish those doing evil and to praise those doing good.
local: kí nīn tɔ́ bibɔtiibi bin bi kāaǹ bí nīn dátí digaŋgandambi tafal, kí nīn pàkà bin ŋáań tiŋanee.

eng: Submit to them because its Gods will that by doing good you will silence the ignorant talk of foolish people.
local: Unimbɔti nín là puee di sá ní nīn ŋáań tiŋan án pìin̄ bigbaambi bin kaa nyí tibee nyɔmmu, bí taa ká tiba kí sòoǹ ni-pu.

eng: Do this as Gods slaves, and yet also as free people, not using your freedom as a cover-up for evil.
local: Ní nīn bímaan kii binib bin Kriisitoo tīī dibatɔlee. Ní taa nín̄ sá binib bin dì dibatɔl pu ki kāntí inaakpal ki ŋáań ikpitee. Ama dūmaan ni-batɔlee kí nīn ŋáań kii Unimbɔti tɔntɔnliibee.

eng: Honor everyone. Love the family of believers. Have respectful fear of God. Honor the emperor.
local: Nín̄ tɔ́maan binib kɔkɔ, kí nīn néeń Kriisitoo yab, kí fàŋkìmaan Unimbɔti, kí nīn tɔ́ gɔmina.

eng: Household slaves, submit by accepting the authority of your masters with all respect. Do this not only to good and kind masters but also to those who are harsh.
local: Nimbi bin kā binib ceeyee, ní sìntīǹ ni-ba taapu kɔkɔ kí tīī ni-kpilib kí nīn tɔ́-bi mmɔntiimin. An kaa sá bidambi yaa sá binib ki mɔ̄ee baba ní ní nīn tɔ́-bi, báà bidambi bɔti yaa pɔ̄ɔ̄yaaa, ní nīn tɔ́-bi.

eng: Now, it is commendable if, because of ones understanding of God, someone should endure pain through suffering unjustly.
local: Ni nín ŋáań Unimbɔti lammee puee ní bidambi yaa bíi sàantí-ni fámee, an ŋān̄. Unimbɔti làá bìin̄ ni-boon.

eng: Instead, like a newborn baby, desire the pure milk of the word. Nourished by it, you will grow into salvation.
local: Ní nīn néeń Unimbɔti bɔti kii mbipɔmu nín néeń mbiim puee kí ŋmā kí cù nyɔkɔpu Unimbɔti saŋŋu poon kí tin kátí-ku.

eng: But what praise comes from enduring patiently when you have sinned and are beaten for it? But if you endure steadfastly when youve done good and suffer for it, this is commendable before God.
local: Bi yaa bíi gbáà-si digaŋgam pu ní a jīǹ kimɔtiiyee, ipaati ilai ní a làá ká poon? Ama a yaa ŋá tin ŋān̄ee ní bi yaa bíi sàantí-si ní a jīǹ kimɔtiiyee, míǹ di ŋān̄ Unimbɔti nimbiin.

eng: You were called to this kind of endurance, because Christ suffered on your behalf. He left you an example so that you might follow in his footsteps.
local: Kun pu Unimbɔti là ní nīn bí mimmee di sá Kriisitoo u-ba jíń falaa míǹ ni-pu, ní ki bí yii ni mun ń nóó u-tabɔbiliŋ kí jī falaa gbanti.

eng: He committed no sin, nor did he ever speak in ways meant to deceive.
local: Waa ŋá ikpiti báà iba, baa gbìl̄ u-nyɔkɔn nnyimɔn ŋubɔ.

eng: When he was insulted, he did not reply with insults. When he suffered, he did not threaten revenge. Instead, he entrusted himself to the one who judges justly.
local: Bi nín bí ki sìí-uee, waa sīī ubɔ ki téeǹ. Bi nín sāaǹ-uee waa tɔ́-bi mbusu, ama u dū ki ŋá Unimbɔti un sòoǹ tibɔti nì deedeeyee ŋaan ní.

eng: He carried in his own body on the cross the sins we committed. He did this so that we might live in righteousness, having nothing to do with sin. By his wounds you were healed.
local: Úǹ Kriisitoo di fōō ti-kpitii ki ŋá u-ba pu ní ki fōō ti-kpitii tafadaaldi ndɔpuntikaa pu, án nín làá ŋá pu tí nyā ikpiti poon kí nīn kíǹ ki bí deedee maŋfal poonee. Bi nín gbáń-u u-wunti léetì puee di cāantí ni-weeŋyi.

eng: Though you were like straying sheep, you have now returned to the shepherd and guardian of your lives.
local: Kun puee dooooyee ni nìn wáŋkì kii ipii in kaa cáá ukpaalee ní. Ama ní kɔŋkɔnnee ni fātìi gítìń un sá upikpaal ki sá un cí ni-nammuee cee.

eng: since you have tasted that the Lord is good.
local: An bí kii Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon nín lī puee yii: «Ni dáká ki ká ti-Dindaan nín mɔ̄ puee.».

eng: Now you are coming to him as to a living stone. Even though this stone was rejected by humans, from Gods perspective it is chosen, valuable.
local: Sútiń kí pìl̄maan ti-Dindaan Yeesu un tìì dimaŋfalee. U sá kii ditaŋkpal din bí dimaŋfalin ní binib dūu fée, ama Unimbɔti ká yii dì cáá kudapɔɔuee ní u lēē-dì.

eng: You yourselves are being built like living stones into a spiritual temple. You are being made into a holy priesthood to offer up spiritual sacrifices that are acceptable to God through Jesus Christ.
local: Nimbi bin mun ká u-cee dimaŋfalee, sútiḿmaan kí nīn sá kii ataŋkpeeyee Unimbɔti ń dūú mā u-kɔŋkooŋŋu. Ní yàntī Unimbɔti Fam ń dū-ni kí kpántí bisaraaŋaliibi bin bí circiree, kí dū ni-ba kí tīī Unimbɔti. Yeesu Kriisitoo puee ní nīn sá saraa un fáatì-uee.

eng: Thus it is written in scripture, Look! I am laying a cornerstone in Zion, chosen, valuable. The person who believes in him will never be shamed. .
local: Kpèèmaan Unimbɔti nín lī u-gbɔŋku poon puee yii: «M lēē ditaŋkpal din cáá kudapɔɔuee ki dū-dì ki tíĺ kii ditampinjalee m-diiku kun bí Siiyɔn jool puee. Un kamaa di yaa fōō-dì ki kííyee, icoo kpá kí jī udaan.».

eng: So God honors you who believe. For those who refuse to believe, though, the stone the builders tossed aside has become the capstone.
local: Dipoondi gbantee sá ki tīī nimbi bin fōō ki kííyee ní, nimbi bin nyí yii ditaŋkpalee sá-ni tiwaŋkpaandi ki jítiǹ tikɔkɔ nì tikɔkɔee. Ama bin kaa fōō ki kííyee pu ní Unimbɔti gbɔŋku sòon̄ ditaŋkpalee pu yii: «Ditaŋkpal din bidimaliibi nìn dūu fée di dāa kpántì ditaŋkpal din ŋúb́ kudii pɔŋŋuee.».

eng: This is a stone that makes people stumble and a rock that makes them fall. Because they refuse to believe in the word, they stumble. Indeed, this is the end to which they were appointed.
local: An gíti lī laaba yii: «Ditaŋkpal din binib gbéetée dee, díǹ di yàntī binib lítí.» Kun pu bi lítée di sá baa là kí pīlifì Unimbɔti bɔti. An di yì-tì ní Unimbɔti yóoǹ yii an bàń-bi míǹ.

eng: But you are a chosen race, a royal priesthood, a holy nation, a people who are Gods own possession. You have become this people so that you may speak of the wonderful acts of the one who called you out of darkness into his amazing light.
local: Ama nimbee, ni sá dinibul din Unimbɔti lútì dí nīn sá u-yalee ní. Nimbi di sá u-Beelin saraaŋaliibi ní ki sá Unimbɔti bindi pu timbundi. U yíiń-ni ní nyānní dibɔmbɔndin kí kɔ́ u-walifimɔntiŋun, kí ŋmā kí lēē u-tunnyaaŋŋi pu seera.

eng: Wives, likewise, submit to your own husbands. Do this so that even if some of them refuse to believe the word, they may be won without a word by their wives way of life.
local: Nimbi binimpoobi mun, ní sìntīmaan ni-ba taapu kí tīī ni-calib. Niinee ni-calib kansikin biba yaa kaa fōō Unimbɔti bɔti ki kííyee, bi yaa ká ni-bimbimɔntiimee, bí fōō kí kíí án taa nín̄ gíti sá nì kinyɔkɔ ní ní dū kí sòoǹ nì bi.

eng: For those who want to love life and see good days should keep their tongue from evil speaking and their lips from speaking lies. .
local: Kii Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon nín lī puee, yii: «Un yaa là kí kàl̄ kí jī mmɔn u-wiiŋyi poonee, ú cúú u-limbil kí taa nín̄ sòoǹ tibɔkpitil kí taa nín̄ mɔ́ntí.

eng: They should shun evil and do good; seek peace and chase after it. .
local: Ú fá tikpitil kí nīn ŋáań tin ŋān̄ee, kí nīn nyàab̀ ŋgbansɔŋfi nì dipɔ́ɔǹdi baabadal.

eng: The Lords eyes are on the righteous and his ears are open to their prayers. But the Lord cannot tolerate those who do evil. .
local: Kun puee ti-Dindaan kpèé bin bí deedeeyee tiŋan ní ki fòol̄ bi-wimmeem, ní ki fàtī u-kpaŋkpamboon ki tìì bikpitiŋaliibi.».

eng: Who will harm you if you are zealous for good?.
local: Ni yaa cáá difaŋgiil ki ŋáań tiŋanee, ŋma di làá ŋá-ni tikpitil?.

eng: But happy are you, even if you suffer because of righteousness! Dont be terrified or upset by them.
