local: Báà tiŋan tin ni ŋáańee pu ní ni yaa bíi jīǹ falaayee, ní taa yàntī án nīn wù-ni. Ní taa yàntī binib ń bīil̀ ni-pɔbiliŋ kí kɔ́ǹ-ni tifaandi.

eng: Instead, regard Christ the Lord as holy in your hearts. Whenever anyone asks you to speak of your hope, be ready to defend it.
local: Ama, ní nīn tɔ́maan ti-Dindaan Kriisitoo ni-pɔbiliŋin, kí bɔ́ntí kí nīn cí baabadal un kamaa di yaa bālifì-ni ni-mákal din ni cáá Kriisitoonee taapuee, ní ŋmā kí kíí-u.

eng: Yet do this with respectful humility, maintaining a good conscience. Act in this way so that those who malign your good lifestyle in Christ may be ashamed when they slander you.
local: Ama ní kíí an nibee soooo kí nīn tɔ́maan-bi, kí taa yàntī bí ká ni-pu ibɔkpiti iba kí sòoǹ. Ní nīn gbíntì ki dàkà ni-bimbimɔntiim min ni ká Kriisitoonee, án nín làá ŋá pu ubɔ yaa bàà làá bīil̀-nee, icoo ń cúú udaanee.

eng: It is better to suffer for doing good than for doing evil.
local: An ŋān̄ á jī falaa tiŋan tin a ŋáańee pu, míǹ di yaa sá min Unimbɔti lèe, ki jítiǹ á jī falaa a-kpitii pu.

eng: Christ himself suffered on account of sins, once for all, the righteous one on behalf of the unrighteous. He did this in order to bring you into the presence of God. Christ was put to death as a human, but made alive by the Spirit.
local: Di sá yii míǹ ní Kriisitoo u-ba mun kpíì binib kpitii pu. U kpíì dibaba ja ní binib kpitii pu, úǹ un bí deedeeyee kpíì bin kaa bí deedeeyee pu. U ŋá míǹ kí ŋmā kí cáań-ti Unimbɔti cee ní. Bi kūū-u ama ní Unimbɔti Fam fātìi gíiǹní-u dimaŋfalin.

eng: And it was by the Spirit that he went to preach to the spirits in prison.
local: U kpíìyee ní ki cūtì ditaŋkpiilin ki sòon̄ Unimbɔti bɔti ki tīī bitaŋkpiibi bin bí sarkan niinee.

eng: After all, they will have observed the reverent and holy manner of your lives.
local: Di sá yii bi làá ká yii ni cáá mbimbimɔntiim ní ki tɔ́-bi.

eng: In the past, these spirits were disobedient—when God patiently waited during the time of Noah. Noah built an ark in which a few lives were rescued through water.
local: an dàkà yii binib bin kɔkɔ nìn bí Noowa yuntiŋuee. Bi-kansikinee, binib binii jantanja di nìn kɔ́ meelan ki dēeǹ nnyim pu ki ŋmátì. Ama bin kíǹee kpíì, kun puee bi kpēē Unimbɔti mɔtiiki fám ŋyunti ŋun Noowa nìn bíi kpáà meelee.

eng: Baptism is like that. It saves you now—not because it removes dirt from your body but because it is the mark of a good conscience toward God. Your salvation comes through the resurrection of Jesus Christ.
local: Nnyim min nìn fīī binib biniiyee náań nì min ni fùul̀ ki kāntí difiil nan ní. Unimbɔti nyim difaldee kaa sá kí lēē tiwun pu jɔkɔnti, an sá kí mèè Unimbɔti ú fíntí ti-pɔbiliŋ ń nyā circir ní. Yeesu Kriisitoo fìkititam pu ní Unimbɔti nyim difaldi cáá mpɔn kí fīī-ti.

eng: who is at Gods right side. Now that he has gone into heaven, he rules over all angels, authorities, and powers.
local: U búntì yilpu ki kāl̀ Unimbɔti ŋaŋgiiliŋu pú, kí jī dibeel ituun nì bin kɔkɔ cáá ipɔn ki bí yilpuee pu.

eng: Dont try to make yourselves beautiful on the outside, with stylish hair or by wearing gold jewelry or fine clothes.
local: Unimpu yaa là kí nīn ŋān̄ee, an kaa sá ú nīn dàkafī tiwun nyaaŋku ŋmaniŋma pu, an dàkà yii diyikpiboobil nì sindaariya falfatib nì an léeŋkaati, nì tiwammɔntiil dilòondi.

eng: Instead, make yourselves beautiful on the inside, in your hearts, with the enduring quality of a gentle, peaceful spirit. This type of beauty is very precious in Gods eyes.
local: Ama u máaǹ kí dàkafì u-bimbim min u cááyee pu, an dàkà yii u-pɔbil poon ń nīn ŋān̄, ú nīn mɔ̄, ki bí soooo. An nyaaŋkuee di kaa jòontí. Míǹ di ŋān̄ ki jítiǹ tikɔkɔ Unimbɔti nimbiin.

eng: For it was in this way that holy women who trusted in God used to make themselves beautiful, accepting the authority of their own husbands.
local: Dooooyee nimpoobi bin nìn bí circir ki máká Unimbɔti puee nín nìn bɔ́ntí bi-ba puee dee, ní ki sìntīǹ bi-ba taapu ki tìì bi-calib.

eng: For example, Sarah accepted Abrahams authority when she called him master . You have become her children when you do good and dont respond to threats with fear.
local: Ti-naanja Abraam nimpuu Saara nín nìn bí puee dee. U nìn sīntiǹ u-ba ní ki tīī u-cal Abraam, ní ki yíì-u u-dindaan. Ni yaa ŋáań tiŋan, kaa yàntī tiba ń fàaǹ-nee, mimmee, ni kpántì Saara naantiibi dee.

eng: Husbands, likewise, submit by living with your wife in ways that honor her, knowing that she is the weaker partner. Honor her all the more, as she is also a coheir of the gracious care of life. Do this so that your prayers wont be hindered.
local: Míǹ mbaantiim ní nimbi bininjabee, ni mun ń nīn ŋúb́ ni-nimpoobi tiŋan, kí nīn nyí yii bi-pɔŋŋu kaa bàn̄ ni-yɔŋu, kí nīn tɔ́-bi, kun puee dimaŋfal din kaa kúntée mbaanti ní Unimbɔti làá dāá tīī uninja nì unimpu. Ni yaa bí mimmee, tiba kaa lìkintí ni-wimmeem.

eng: Finally, all of you be of one mind, sympathetic, lovers of your fellow believers, compassionate, and modest in your opinion of yourselves.
local: Nimbi bin kíǹee, ni-kɔkɔ ń nīn cáámaan nlandɔkɔbaanti kí nīn sūǹ tɔb saai kí nīn néeń tɔb kii nnabiyaamuee, kí nīn ŋáaḿmaan tɔb tiŋan kí nīn sìntīǹ ni-ba taapu ki tìì tɔb.

eng: Dont pay back evil for evil or insult for insult. Instead, give blessing in return. You were called to do this so that you might inherit a blessing.
local: A-juti yaa ŋá-si min kaa ŋān̄ee á taa téeǹ-u. U yaa tí sīī-saaa, á taa sìì-u kí téeǹ, ama mèè Unimbɔti ń bìin̄ udaan pu. Di sá Unimbɔti pōoǹ yii u làá bìin̄ ni-pu ní, an pu ní u yíiń-ni.

eng: Therefore, since Christ suffered as a human, you should also arm yourselves with his way of thinking. This is because whoever suffers is finished with sin.
local: Ni nyí yii Kriisitoo kpíì, míǹ puee ni mun ń nīn bímaan kii ni kpíìyee. Kun puee un kpíìyee, úǹ kaa ti ŋáań ikpiti.

eng: And serve each other according to the gift each person has received, as good managers of Gods diverse gifts.
local: Kii Unimbɔti nín pú-ni ŋŋmam mì bí tɔbin puee, nín̄ cáa tútúm̀maan tɔb. Ni yaa bíi ŋáań mimmee, binib làá ká Unimbɔti ŋanti kɔkɔ tin u là kí tīī-bee.

eng: Whoever speaks should do so as those who speak Gods word. Whoever serves should do so from the strength that God furnishes. Do this so that in everything God may be honored through Jesus Christ. To him be honor and power forever and always. Amen.
local: Un ní Unimbɔti pú ú nīn sòoǹ u-bɔtee, ń sòoǹ-tì. Un ní u yaa tí pú ŋŋmam ú nīn tútúǹ u-jutiibee máaǹ kí dū ŋŋmam min Unimbɔti tīī-uee ní kí dūú tūtuǹ binib, án nín làá ŋá pu tin kɔkɔ ni ŋáań nì Yeesu Kriisitoo tūtuntam puee, án nīn tìì Unimbɔti tinyoolee. Úǹ di pée yì tinyool nì mpɔn kí nīn cá jaanjaan! Ameeni.

eng: Dear friends, dont be surprised about the fiery trials that have come among you to test you. These are not strange happenings.
local: Nnabineeŋkaamu, án taa ŋá-ni maamaaci icɔŋkikpaan in poon ni bí nee kii an sá-ni tibɔcanee.

eng: Instead, rejoice as you share Christs suffering. You share his suffering now so that you may also have overwhelming joy when his glory is revealed.
local: An yaa sá yii ní jī falaa un Kriisitoo jíńee, ní nīn fàal̀maan, án nín làá ŋá pu daliŋkin u làá dāá dàkà u-nyootee, ní dāá ká dipɔɔpiŋkpaandi yaadalee.

eng: If you are mocked because of Christs name, you are blessed, for the Spirit of glory—indeed, the Spirit of God—rests on you.
local: Ni nín nóó Kriisitooyee pu ní bi yaa sìí-nee, án nīn mɔ̄ǹ-ni, kun puee Unimbɔti Fam min cáá tinyoolee bí ni-pu.

eng: Now none of you should suffer as a murderer or thief or evildoer or rebel.
local: Ni-kansikin ubɔ falaau ń taa nín̄ sá yii u kūū unil àá u yùn̄ tiba, àá u sá digaŋgandaan, àá u cáá u-ba ki tɔ́ biba bɔtin.

eng: But dont be ashamed if you suffer as one who belongs to Christ. Rather, honor God as you bear Christs name. Give honor to God.
local: Ama an yaa sá yii a nín nóó Kriisitooyee pu ní a yaa bíi jīǹ falaayee, án taa nín̄ cáá-si icoo, á nīn jáaḿ Unimbɔti yii binib béè yii a sá Kriisitoo yɔu.

eng: because its time for judgment to begin with Gods own household. But if judgment starts with us, what will happen to those who refuse to believe Gods good news?.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun Unimbɔti làá sòoǹ báà ŋma bɔtee bāań ní nee. U làá péé péé kí sòoǹ bin sá u-yabee bɔti ní. Mimmee, u yaa ká timbi bin sá u-yabee pu tiba ki sòon̄ee, mana ní bin yíì kaa fōō Unimbɔti Bɔmɔntiiti ki kííyee cee dāa nín bí dee?.

eng: If the righteous are barely rescued, what will happen to the godless and sinful?.
local: Kii Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon nín lī puee yii: «An yaa pɔ̄ɔ̄ ki tīī bin nóó Unimbɔtee ń tin ŋmátée, mimmee bin kaa nóó Unimbɔtee nì bikpitiŋaliibee, bíǹ cee dāa nín bí mana dee?».

eng: So then, those who suffer because they follow Gods will should commit their lives to a trustworthy creator by doing what is right.
local: Míǹ puee, bin bí ki ŋáań min Unimbɔti là ní ki bíi jīǹ falaayee ń pɔ́ɔǹ bi-ba kí mɔ̀tīí nīn ŋáań tiŋan, kí dū bi-mákal kɔkɔ kí ŋá bi-náanliu un ŋáań tin u pōoǹee ni.

eng: As a result, they dont live the rest of their human lives in ways determined by human desires but in ways determined by Gods will.
local: Kí yòon̄ kɔŋkɔnnee kí nīn cée, ní dū ni-yuntii in kíǹ ní dūú kàl̄ duulinyanee kí dūú ŋá min Unimbɔti lèe, kí taa ti ŋá min fáatì ni-wuntee.

eng: You have wasted enough time doing what unbelievers desire—living in their unrestrained immorality and lust, their drunkenness and excessive feasting and wild parties, and their forbidden worship of idols.
local: Dooooyee, ni nìn dū ŋyunti tikpil ki ŋá kii bin kaa nyí Unimbɔtee nín ŋáań puee. Ni nìn ŋáań tin kɔkɔ cāabìń ntimbiilee, ní ki ŋáań tijɔkɔndi, ki bí jiŋaalib poon baabadal, ki nyù idafi ki gbéè, ki jīǹ ki lákatí ni-ba pu, ki tòotī awaa, kun mun kaa ŋān̄ kpataaa Unimbɔti nimbiinee dee. Kɔŋkɔnnee an ŋmāntì.

eng: They think its strange that you dont join in these activities with the same flood of unrestrained wickedness. So they slander you.
local: An ŋá bin kaa nyí Unimbɔtee maamaaci ni nì bi nín kaa ti kpáfí ki ŋáań tin cāabìń ntimbiilee, ní bi sìí-ni.

eng: They will have to reckon with the one who is ready to judge the living and the dead.
local: Ama bi làá dāá yūĺ kí sòoǹ bi-tuŋkpitiŋ gbanti pu Unimbɔti un bɔ́ntì ki cí kí sòoǹ binifoobi nì bitaŋkpiibi bɔtee nimbiin.

eng: Indeed, this is the reason the good news was also preached to the dead. This happened so that, although they were judged as humans according to human standards, they could live by the Spirit according to divine standards.
local: An pu ní Tibɔmɔntiil bàn̄ bitaŋkpiibi, án nín làá ŋá pu bi yaa sòon̄ bi-bɔti kii binifoobee, bí nīn bí dimaŋfalin ŋfamin Unimbɔti nimbiinee.

eng: The end of everything has come. Therefore, be self-controlled and clearheaded so you can pray.
local: Duulinya kúntitam tóobiń, ní ŋúb́ ni-ba mmɔntiim kí fìntīmaan kí ŋmā kí nīn mèé.

eng: Above all, show sincere love to each other, because love brings about the forgiveness of many sins.
local: Ama kí wàatēe, ní nīn néeḿmaan tɔb tikpil, kun puee un cáá dineendi tikpilee bìintí ikpiti tikpil pu ní.

eng: Open your homes to each other without complaining.
local: Ní nīn fòol̄ ni-jutiibi ni-dumpui kí taa nín̄ gbùkum̄.

eng: Therefore, I have a request for the elders among you. I urge the elders.
local: Kɔŋkɔnnee m bíi sòoǹ nì Kriisitoo nikitil kpilib bin bí ni-kansikinee ní nee. Ma mun sá Kriisitoo nikitil kpiliu ní m-ba. M ká Kriisitoo falaabi nì m-nimbiliŋ, ní ki làá dāá fōō m-jandi tinyool tin làá dāá nyēe poon.

eng: After you have suffered for a little while, the God of all grace, the one who called you into his eternal glory in Christ Jesus, will himself restore, empower, strengthen, and establish you.
local: Ni làá jī falaa ŋyunti ŋubɔ fiii ní. Ama Unimbɔti un néeń-ni tikpilee yíiń-ni ni mun ń dāan̄ kí ká u-nyooti tin bí kaa kúntée ni nì Kriisitoo nín ŋá ubaantee pu. U làá yàntī ní kpántí deedee nib kí nīn bá ki pɔ̄ɔ̄, ní ubɔ kaa dāa gíti bí ki ŋūǹ kí téé-ni kí lúkú.

eng: To him be power forever and always. Amen.
local: Úǹ di yì mpɔn kí nīn pée cáa cá jaanjaan! Ameeni.

eng: I have written and sent these few lines to you by Silvanus. I consider him to be a faithful brother. In these lines I have urged and affirmed that this is the genuine grace of God. Stand firm in it.
local: Siilasi ní m kàan̄ anyɔɔbuŋkaaŋkaa nee ki tùkū, ki yāntì u ŋmàn̄-ni kugbɔŋu nee, kun puee m nyí yii u sá kinabiki kin yú deedee nnimbɔtisan poonee. M là kí tīī-ni kunyannyaau kí mɔ̀tīí tùkù-ni nì ibaamɔn yii Unimbɔti ŋanti tin ni fōōyee, tì lafun sá míǹ ní, ní nīn ŋúb́-tì án nīn pɔ̄ɔ̄.

eng: The fellow-elect church in Babylon greets you, and so does my son Mark.
local: Babiloon nikitil din Unimbɔti lútì kii u nín lútì ni mun puee, jáaḿ-ni. Maarki un kpántì kii m-jipɔɔnee mun jáaḿ-ni.

eng: Greet each other with the kiss of love. Peace to you all who are in Christ.
local: Ní jāam̀maan tɔb nnabiyaamin. Unimbɔti ń tīī nimbi bin kɔkɔ sá Kriisitoo yabee ŋgbansɔŋfi.

eng: Like shepherds, tend the flock of God among you. Watch over it. Dont shepherd because you must, but do it voluntarily for God. Dont shepherd greedily, but do it eagerly.
local: M bàlifī yii ní kpàà Unimbɔti nikitil din u dūu ŋúbiǹ-nee. Ní dū nlammɔntiim min fáatì Unimbɔtee kí kpàà-dì, án taa nín̄ sá nì kugaau, kí taa nín̄ tí sá yii ni tūǹ ki nyàab̀ animbil. Ama nín̄ tūm̀maan nì ni-pɔbiliŋ kɔkɔ.

eng: Dont shepherd by ruling over those entrusted to your care, but become examples to the flock.
local: Ní taa nín̄ wɔ́b́ binib bin bi dū ki ŋúbiǹ-ni ní kpààyee, ama nín̄ sámaan binib bin bimbim sá mmɔntiim bí nīn kpèé ki tááyee.

eng: And when the chief shepherd appears, you will receive an unfading crown of glory.
local: Ni yaa ŋúb́-bi míǹ, áli ki tin sāā daliŋkin ní Kriisitoo upikpaakpil làá bàńee, u làá dāá tīī-ni tinyool beefuuliiki kin làá nīn bí jaanjaanee.

eng: In the same way, I urge you who are younger: accept the authority of the elders. And everyone, clothe yourselves with humility toward each other. God stands against the proud, but he gives favor to the humble.
local: Nimbi binaacimpɔmbi mun, ní sìntīǹ ni-ba taapu kí tīī bikpilib. Ni-kɔkɔ ń sìntīǹ ni-ba kí tīīmaan tɔb kí nīn tūǹ atuun ki tìì tɔb, kun puee Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon lī yii: «Unimbɔti kaa gèetíǹ bilambidambi, ama ní ki ŋáań tiŋan ki tìì bin sìntīǹ bi-ba taapuee.».

eng: Therefore, humble yourselves under Gods power so that he may raise you up in the last day.
local: Míǹ puee, sìntīm̀maan ni-ba Unimbɔti Mpɔŋkɔdaan taapu, án nín làá ŋá pu ŋyunti ŋun Unimbɔti yóoǹee yaa láa bāańee, ú dāá yòon̄-ni yilpuee.

eng: Throw all your anxiety onto him, because he cares about you.
local: Túutímaan ni-gìŋ́ŋi kɔkɔ kí páań u-pu, di sá yii úǹ di pée kpèé-ni.

eng: Be clearheaded. Keep alert. Your accuser, the devil, is on the prowl like a roaring lion, seeking someone to devour.
local: Ní nīn wálifì, kí nīn nyíǹ ni-ba, kun puee ni-nannanliu Sitaan bí ki yìiǹ kii gbikinti nimbiliŋ nín máǹ pu u nyàab̀ kun u yaa pée kée ú cúú kí ŋmɔ́ee ní.

eng: Resist him, standing firm in the faith. Do so in the knowledge that your fellow believers are enduring the same suffering throughout the world.
local: Míǹ puee, ní ŋúb́ ni-ba ni-fookiiu poon, kí yíí Sitaan. Kun puee ni nyí yii ni-nabiyaamu mun yàl̄ duulinya jaŋŋi kɔkɔnee bí ki jīǹ falaa gbanti ubaanti míǹ ní.

eng: From Paul, Silvanus, and Timothy. To the Thessalonians church that is in God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ. Grace and peace to all of you.
local: Man Pɔɔl nì Siilasi nì Tiimootee di ŋmàn̄ kugbɔŋu nee. Ti ŋmàn̄-ku kí tīī nimbi Kriisitoo nikitil din bí Teesalooniiki ki sá ti-Baa Unimbɔti nì ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo yabee. Unimbɔti ń ŋá-ni tiŋan kí tīī-ni ŋgbansɔŋfi.

eng: and you are waiting for his Son from heaven. His Son is Jesus, who is the one he raised from the dead and who is the one who will rescue us from the coming wrath.
local: Ni kā ki cí yii u-Jipɔɔn Yeesu ń nyānní yilpu kí dāan̄. Úǹ ní Unimbɔti fīkitì ditaŋkpiilin. Úǹ di fīī-ti ki nyántì Unimbɔti ŋuul din bí ki dòońee poon.

eng: We always thank God for all of you when we mention you constantly in our prayers.
local: Ti pàkà Unimbɔti ní baabadal ni-kɔkɔ pu, ní ti-wimmeem poonee ti téetí ki mèé ni-pu múlmul.

eng: This is because we remember your work that comes from faith, your effort that comes from love, and your perseverance that comes from hope in our Lord Jesus Christ in the presence of our God and Father.
local: Ti-wimmeem poon ti-Baa Unimbɔti nimbiinee, ti téetí atuun ŋin ni tūǹ ni-fookiiu nì ni-neendi puee nì ni-mákal din ni cáá ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo puee nín pɔ̄ɔ̄ miŋyee.

eng: Brothers and sisters, you are loved by God, and we know that he has chosen you.
local: Nnabiyaamu, ti nyí yii Unimbɔti néeń-ni ní ki lútì-ni ní nīn sá u-yab.

eng: We know this because our good news didnt come to you just in speech but also with power and the Holy Spirit and with deep conviction. You know as well as we do what kind of people we were when we were with you, which was for your sake.
local: Kun puee Tibɔmɔntiil tin ti sòon̄ nì nee kaa sá nì kinyɔkɔn ŋmaniŋma, ama ti nìn sòon̄ nì Unimbɔti pɔŋŋu nì u-Fam tūtuntam ki tɔ́kɔ́ ibaamɔn pu páaa ní. Ni nyí ti-pu ti nín nìn dɔ́miń ki bí ni-kansikin nì mbimbim min kí tūtuǹ-nee.

eng: You became imitators of us and of the Lord when you accepted the message that came from the Holy Spirit with joy in spite of great suffering.
local: Ni nín fōō Unimbɔti bɔti ki kíí nì falaabi tikpil poon nì dipɔɔpindi din Unimbɔti Fam tììyee puee, ni kpántì bin fōō timbi nì ti-Dindaan didandee dee.

eng: As a result you became an example to all the believers in Macedonia and Achaia.
local: Min ni ŋá nee dàkà Maseeduwaan nì Akaayi yab bin fōō ki kííyee mun ń fōō míǹ dee.

eng: The message about the Lord rang out from you, not only in Macedonia and Achaia but in every place. The news about your faithfulness to God has spread so that we dont even need to mention it.
local: Kun puee nimbi cee ní ti-Dindaan bɔti gbītì, ki bàn̄ Maseeduwaan nì Akaayi yab cee. Kaa sá bíǹ baba, ajan ŋin kɔkɔ kíǹee yab mun gbìl̄ ni nín fōō Unimbɔti ki kíí miŋyee. An kaa ti bàlifī yii timbi ń māafì-bi gba.

eng: People tell us about what sort of welcome we had from you and how you turned to God from idols. As a result, you are serving the living and true God.
local: Báà ŋma máafí ti nín dɔ́miń ni-cee ni fōō-ti pu, ki fá ni-waaŋi ki kíǹ ki nóó ibaamɔn Nimbɔtiu un bí dimaŋfalinee.

eng: As you yourselves know, brothers and sisters, our visit with you wasnt a waste of time.
local: Nnabiyaamu, kii nimbi ni-ba nín pée nyí puee, kaa sá yii ti dɔ́miń ni-dumpu fám.

eng: You and God are witnesses of how holy, just, and blameless we were toward you believers.
local: Nimbi ni-ba ká. Unimbɔti mun ká yii ti-bimbim min ti dūu kāl̀ nimbi bin fōō Unimbɔti bɔti ki kííyee kansikinee bí circir ki bí deedee. Taa yīntì báà tiba nì tiba poon.

eng: Likewise, you know how we treated each of you like a father treats his own children.
local: Min ní ni nyí yii ubaati ŋáań u-biyaamuee, míǹ ní ti ŋá ni-kansikin ubɔ ubɔ.

eng: We appealed to you, encouraged you, and pleaded with you to live lives worthy of the God who is calling you into his own kingdom and glory.
local: Ti sáŋkì-ni ki tīī-ni kunyannyaau ki páb́-ni yii ní nīn còom̄ min fáatiǹ Unimbɔtee, úǹ un yíiń-ni ní dāá kɔ́ u-Beel nì u-nyootinee.

eng: We also thank God constantly for this: when you accepted Gods word that you heard from us, you welcomed it for what it truly is. Instead of accepting it as a human message, you accepted it as Gods message, and it continues to work in you who are believers.
local: Kun pu ti gíti pàkà Unimbɔti kaa gbèentée di sá ti nín cáań Unimbɔti bɔti ki sòon̄ nì nee, ni pílifì-tì ki fōō-tì kaa dūu yíiń binib bɔti, ama ki fōō-tì yii tì sá Unimbɔti bɔti tiŋman. Tibɔti gbantee di tūǹ nimbi bin fōō ki kííyee pɔbiliŋ poon atuun.

eng: Brothers and sisters, you became imitators of the churches of God in Judea, which are in Christ Jesus. This was because you also suffered the same things from your own people as they did from the Jews.
local: An puee, nnabiyaamu, ni dī Unimbɔti nikitil din bí Juudee ki sá Yeesu Kriisitoo yabee tabɔbiliŋ dee. Di sá yii falaabi bin ni jíń ni-jutiibi ceeyee, falaabee bibaantiibi ní bi mun jíń Juuda yab ŋaan.

eng: They killed both the Lord Jesus and the prophets and drove us out. They dont please God, and they are hostile to the entire human race.
local: Bíǹ di kūū ti-Dindaan Yeesu nì Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi ki sāaǹ ti mun. Unimbɔti kaa gèetíǹ-bi ní bi sá binib kɔkɔ pulincɔntiibi.

eng: when they try to stop us from speaking to the Gentiles so they can be saved. Their sins are constantly pushing the limit. Gods wrath has caught up with them in the end.
local: Bi pìintí-ti nsan tí taa sòoǹ nì bin kaa sá Juuda yabee Unimbɔti bɔti tin ŋūǹ kí cáań-bi difiilee. Bi mɔ̀tīi gbēentí bi-kpitii pu ŋyunti kamaa. Ama Unimbɔti fōō bi-pu diŋuul ki dóò.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we were separated from you for a while physically but not in our hearts. We made every effort in our desire to see you again face-to-face.
local: An yaa sá timbee, nnabiyaamu, doooo ŋyunti ŋun ti yāaǹ tɔbin nì tiwun yàantam, kaa sá nì ti-pɔbiliŋee, ti nyàab̄ nì dinimbinaamandi kí bàà fàtīí ká ni-nimbiin ti-pɔbiliŋ ń bàà sɔ̀ŋkì.

eng: We wanted to come to you—I, Paul, tried over and over again—and Satan stopped us.
local: Ti mɔ̀n̄ yii tí gítiń ni-cee, cilinjee man Pɔɔl, m mākaǹ ŋfunyɔm kupaau kí bàà gítiń ni-cee. Ama Sitaan pīiǹ-ti nsan.

eng: What is our hope, joy, or crown that we can brag about in front of our Lord Jesus when he comes? Isnt it all of you?.
local: Nimbi di sá bin pu timbi cɔ́tée, ki sá ti-pɔɔpindi nì ti-jajil daaŋku kun pu ti-Dindaan Yeesu yaa láa gítìńee, ti dāa gbàntī u-nimbiinee.

eng: On the contrary, we had the courage through God to speak Gods good news in spite of a lot of opposition, although we had already suffered and were publicly insulted in Philippi, as you know.
local: Ni nyí yii bi sāaǹ-ti ki sīī-ti Fiiliipi tiŋkin ní ti dāa nín bāań ni-cee Teesalooniiki. Ama ní Unimbɔti tīī-ti kunyannyaau tí sòoǹ nì ni u-Bɔmɔntiiti nì dikɔ̀ndi poon.

eng: You are our glory and joy!.
local: Tiŋman nimbi di sá timbi nyooti nì ti-pɔɔpindi.

eng: Our appeal isnt based on false information, the wrong motives, or deception.
local: Ti-bɔti tin ti sòoǹee kaa sá tinyimɔmbɔti, kaa sá ilandɔkɔkpiti bɔti, kaa tí sá tibɔŋmatiŋkaal.

eng: Rather, we have been examined and approved by God to be trusted with the good news, and thats exactly how we speak. We arent trying to please people, but we are trying to please God, who continues to examine our hearts.
local: Ama Unimbɔti cɔ́ŋkì-ti ní ki dū u-Bɔmɔntiiti ki ŋúbiǹ-ti tí sòoǹ. An puee ní ti sòoǹ min làá nīn fáatiǹ Unimbɔti un wāĺ ti-pɔbiliŋinee, an kaa sá kí fáatíǹ binib.

eng: As you know, we never used flattery, and God is our witness that we didnt have greedy motives.
local: Nimbi ni-ba nyí yii taa sòon̄ nì ni tibɔkiilkaal ki ká jaanjaan. Taa tí sòoǹ ki nyàab̀ ni-cee animbil yam, Unimbɔti nyí ti-pu míǹ.

eng: We didnt ask for special treatment from people—not from you or from others.
local: Taa nyàab̀ ubɔ ń tīī-ti tinyool. Taa nyàab̀ nimbi nyootaaa, kaa nyàab̀ bin kíǹee mun yati.

eng: although we could have thrown our weight around as Christs apostles. Instead, we were gentle with you like a nursing mother caring for her own children.
local: Ama ti bàà cáá nsan kii ti nín sá Kriisitoo kpambaliŋee puee kí tùkù-ni yii nimbi di máaǹ kí nīn kpèé-ti, ama taa ŋá míǹ. Ti kāl̀ ni-cee ki mɔ̄ kii ubinaati nín mɔ̄ pu ki ŋúb́ u-biyaamuee ní.

eng: We were glad to share not only Gods good news with you but also our very lives because we cared for you so much.
local: Ti nín néeń-ni puee, ti là yii án taa nín̄ sá Unimbɔti bɔti ŋmaniŋma ní tí tīī-ni, ama tí dū ti-maŋfal kí kūtì kí tīī-ni, kun puee ni kɔ́-ti tiŋan tiŋan.

eng: You remember, brothers and sisters, our efforts and hard work. We preached Gods good news to you, while we worked night and day so we wouldnt be a burden on any of you.
local: Nnabiyaamu, nimbi ni-ba ká ti nín túń atuun pu ŋwiimpu nì kunyeeu ki gīī kí ŋmā kí ŋúb́ ti-ba kí nīn sòoǹ Unimbɔti bɔti, án taa nín̄ sá yii ni-kansikin ubɔ ní ń nīn tú ti-gìńdee.

eng: So when we couldnt stand it any longer, we thought it was a good idea to stay on in Athens by ourselves.
local: Ti nín kaa nìn gíti ŋūǹ kí nīn kā míǹ kaa gbìl̄ ni-puee ní timbi lēē yii min ŋān̄ee di sá tí gbíntí kí kàl̄ Ateeni ti-baba.

eng: Night and day, we pray more than ever to see all of you in person and to complete whatever you still need for your faith.
local: Ti mèé ki páb̀-u ŋwiimpu nì kunyeeu yii ú pée yàntī tí gíti ká-ni án nín làá ŋá pu ní kun naa cááyee tí tīī-ni ní dūú gbéeǹ ni-fookiiu puee.

eng: Now may our God and Father himself and our Lord Jesus guide us on our way back to you.
local: Ti-Baa Unimbɔti u-ba nì ti-Dindaan Yeesu ń ŋāŋkì ti-saŋŋu tí ŋmā kí dāá ká-ni.

eng: May the Lord cause you to increase and enrich your love for each other and for everyone in the same way as we also love you.
local: Ti-Dindaan ń yàntī dineendi din ni cáá tɔb pu nì binib kɔkɔ puee ń wíiǹ ní mɔ̀tīí nīn néeń tɔb míǹ tikpil kí nīn pée cáa cá, án nīn bí kii ti mun nín néeń-ni puee.

eng: May the love cause your hearts to be strengthened, to be blameless in holiness before our God and Father when our Lord Jesus comes with all his people. Amen.
local: Ú yàntī ni-pɔbiliŋ ń nīn tɔ́kɔ́ ibaamɔn pu ki pɔ̄ɔ̄, kí nīn bí circir bí taa ká ni-pu ikpiti iba ti-Baa Unimbɔti nimbiin ŋyunti ŋun ti-Dindaan Yeesu yaa láa gítìń nì bin kɔkɔ sá u-yabee. Ameeni.

eng: and we sent you Timothy, who is our brother and Gods coworker in the good news about Christ. We sent him to strengthen and encourage you in your faithfulness.
local: Ní ti dū ti-nabiki Tiimootee ti nì un tūǹ Unimbɔti tundi ki sòoǹ Kriisitoo Bɔmɔntiitee ki túnní ni-cee ú tīī-ni kunyannyaau kí kūtì-ni mpɔn ni-fookiiu poon.

eng: We didnt want any of you to be shaken by these problems. You know very well that we were meant to go through this.
local: Ni-kansikin ubɔ ń taa yàntī falaa un poon ni bée ń ŋmā-u. Kun puee ni nyí yii an sá falaa di pée dɔ̄ ki cí-ti tí jī.

eng: In fact, when we were with you, we kept on predicting that we were going to face problems exactly like what happened, as you know.
local: Ti nín nìn dáá bí ni-ceeyee, ti nìn pée tùkū-ni ki yóoǹ yii falaa dɔ̄ ki cí-ti, an di bāań nee kii ni nín nìn gbìl̄ puee.

eng: Thats why I sent Timothy to find out about your faithfulness when I couldnt stand it anymore. I was worried that the tempter might have tempted you so that our work would have been a waste of time.
local: M nín kaa ti ŋūǹ kí nīn gbíntìi kā ki cée puee ní m túnní Tiimootee ú dāá kpèè ni-fookiiu nín bí puee, kun puee m fàŋkí yii Sitaan ń taa láa cɔ̄ŋkì kí líí-ni ti-tundi din ti túńee ń ŋá fám.

eng: Now Timothy has returned to us from you and has given us good news about your faithfulness and love! He says that you always have good memories about us and that you want to see us as much as we want to see you.
local: Ama kɔŋkɔnnee Tiimootee nín nyánní ni-cee ki fātìi bāańee, u cáań-ti faawiimɔnti ni-fookiiu nì ni-neendi pu. U máafì-ti yii ni téetí ti-pu múlmul mmɔntiimin ki nyàab̀ kí bàà ká-ti kii ti mun nín nyàab̀ kí ká ni mun puee.

eng: Because of this, brothers and sisters, we were encouraged in all our distress and trouble through your faithfulness.
local: Míǹ puee nnabiyaamu, ni-puee ti kāntí kunyannyaau ti-falaabi nì ti-nimbinaamandi kɔkɔ poon. Ni-fookiiu di tīī-ti dipɔɔsɔŋkil.

eng: For now we are alive if you are standing your ground in the Lord.
local: Díǹ pu nee timbi ŋmátì ní, kun puee ni gbíntìi bí ti-Dindaanni ki ŋúb́ an píkí.

eng: How can we thank God enough for you, given all the joy we have because of you before our God?.
local: Dipɔɔpiŋkpaandi din ti cáá ni-pu ki fàal̀ Unimbɔti nimbiinee, taa ti nyí gba ti nín làá pàkà-u kí gbèen̄ puee.

eng: So then, brothers and sisters, we ask and encourage you in the Lord Jesus to keep living the way you already are and even do better in how you live and please God—just as you learned from us.
local: Kí kúntí tibɔtee, nnabiyaamu, ti dàkā-ni min ní ni máaǹ kí nīn còom̄ Unimbɔti ń nīn fáatì ni-bɔtee. Ibaamɔn, ni bíi còom̄ min fáatì Unimbɔtee, ama ti dū ti-Dindaan Yeesu ki gbāam̀-ni yii ní nīn gbíntìi ŋáań min jítiǹ mimmee ki cá.

eng: In fact, you are doing loving deeds for all the brothers and sisters throughout Macedonia. Now we encourage you, brothers and sisters, to do so even more.
local: An neendee gba ní ni cáá nnabiyaamu mun kɔkɔ bí Maseeduwaan tiŋki kɔkɔnee pu. Ama ti bàlifī-ni yii ní gíti ŋá min jítiǹ min ni nìn ŋáań dooooyee.

eng: Aim to live quietly, mind your own business, and earn your own living, just as I told you.
local: Ní pɔ́ɔǹ ni-ba kí nīn bímaan ŋgbansɔŋfi poon kí nīn tūǹ atuun kí kpíiǹ ni-ba, kii ti nín nìn pée tùkū-ni dooooyee puee.

eng: That way youll behave appropriately toward outsiders, and you wont be in need.
local: Ni yaa ŋá mimmee, an di làá yàntī bin kaa nyí Unimbɔtee ń nīn tɔ́-ni, ní naa báká ubɔ pu.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we want you to know about people who have died so that you wont mourn like others who dont have any hope.
local: Nnabiyaamu, ti là yii ní nīn nyí bin kpíìyee bɔti nín làá dāá jītí puee mbaamɔmin. Taa là yii ní nīn bí kunimbaaun kii bin kaa cáá ti-Dindaan pu dimákalee nín bí puee.

eng: Since we believe that Jesus died and rose, so we also believe that God will bring with him those who have died in Jesus.
local: Kun puee ti nyí yii Yeesu kpíì ki fātìi fīkitì, míǹ ní ti nyí yii Unimbɔti làá fàtīí tí gíiǹní bin nóó Yeesu ki kpíìyee dimaŋfalin, kii u nín fīkitì Yeesu puee.

eng: What we are saying is a message from the Lord: we who are alive and still around at the Lords coming definitely wont go ahead of those who have died.
local: Ní pīlifì, ti-Dindaan bɔti tin u nìn sòoǹee tiba sɔ̀. Ti-Dindaan yaa láa bāańee, timbi bin dáá bí dimaŋfalinee kaa làá dāá péeǹ bin kpíìyee kí lìntī.

eng: This is because the Lord himself will come down from heaven with the signal of a shout by the head angel and a blast on Gods trumpet. First, those who are dead in Christ will rise.
local: Di sá yii ti-Dindaan u-ba làá dāá pɔ̄ kinyɔkɔ ní ituun kpiliu ń téeń ní Unimbɔti kantándi ń búń. Niinee ní ti-Dindaan ń nyānní yilpu kí sìntīń, ní bin fōō-u ki kíí ki kpíìyee ń péé péé kí fìkitī.

eng: Then, we who are living and still around will be taken up together with them in the clouds to meet with the Lord in the air. That way we will always be with the Lord.
local: Ní timbi bin dáá kíǹ kaa kpíìyee mun ń yīkì kí kūtì bi-pu ní án yòon̄-ti kutaa pu tí tūŋkì ti-Dindaan atagbanni, ní ti nì ti-Dindaan ń nīn kíǹ ki bí baabadal.

eng: So encourage each other with these words.
local: Ní nīn cáá tibɔti nee ki sɔ̀ŋkīmaan tɔb pɔbiliŋ.

eng: You know the instructions we gave you through the Lord Jesus.
local: Ni nyí ti nín nìn dàkā-ni tibɔti tin ti-Dindaan Yeesu yindi puee.

eng: Gods will is that your lives are dedicated to him. This means that you stay away from sexual immorality.
local: Unimbɔti nín là kuŋyee di sá ní nīn bí circir kí nīn ŋúb́ ni-ba kí taa ŋá tilaakpaal.

eng: and learn how to control your own body in a pure and respectable way.
local: Ni-kansikin báà ŋma nimpucáatam ń nīn sá circir ki cáá tinyool.

eng: Dont be controlled by your sexual urges like the Gentiles who dont know God.
local: kí taa dū ni-ba kí tīī ajɔkɔntuun kii bin kaa nyí Unimbɔtee nín ŋáań puee.

eng: No one should mistreat or take advantage of their brother or sister in this issue. The Lord punishes people for all these things, as we told you before and sternly warned you.
local: Tibɔti nee poonee, ni-kansikin ubɔ ń taa ŋá u-nabiki min kaa kítèeyaaa, kí taa mùul̀-u. Ti nìn tùkū-ni an bɔti dooooyee ki gíti kpáafì-ni-tì tiŋan yii ti-Dindaan làá dāá dáaĺ bin ŋáań an kpitiiyee tafal.

eng: God didnt call us to be immoral but to be dedicated to him.
local: Di sá yii Unimbɔti kaa yíiń-ti tí dāá nīn bí tijɔkɔndi poon, ama u yíiń-ti tí dāá nīn sá circirdambi ní.

eng: Therefore, whoever rejects these instructions isnt rejecting a human authority. They are rejecting God, who gives his Holy Spirit to you.
local: An puee un di yaa dū ti-dàkatam nee ki fée, an kaa sá unil bɔti ní u fá, ama Unimbɔti un tīī-ni u-Circir Famee bɔti ní u fá.

eng: You dont need us to write about loving your brothers and sisters because God has already taught you to love each other.
local: An yaa kíǹ ni nì ni-nabiyaamu kansikin neendee, an kaa ti bàlifī yii tí ŋmà-ni an pu gba, kun puee Unimbɔti dàkā-ni ni nín máaǹ kí nīn néeń tɔb puee.

eng: We dont need to write to you about the timing and dates, brothers and sisters.
local: Nnabiyaamu, an kaa ti bàlifī yii tí ŋmà kí tùkù-ni iyunti in ní an bɔtee làá bàńee.

eng: Jesus died for us so that, whether we are awake or asleep, we will live together with him.
local: Úǹ Kriisitoo di kpíì ti-pu, án nín làá ŋá pu báà ti yaa láa bí dimaŋfalinaaa, ti yaa láa kpíìyaaa, u yaa láa gítìńee ti nì u ń kpāfì kí nīn bí mbaantiiminee.

eng: So continue encouraging each other and building each other up, just like you are doing already.
local: Míǹ puee ní nīn tììmaan tɔb kunyannyaau ki kútí tɔb mpɔn kii ni nín pée ŋáań puee.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we ask you to respect those who are working with you, leading you, and instructing you.
local: Nnabiyaamu, ti mèé-ni yii ní nīn tɔ́ bin bí ni-kansikin ki tūǹ ditundi, ki cáá ti-Dindaan bɔti ki dàkà-ni, ki kpòotī-nee.

eng: Think of them highly with love because of their work. Live in peace with each other.
local: Nín̄ cáámaan bi-pu dineeŋkpaandi atuun ŋin bi bí ni-kansikin ki tūǹee pu. Ŋgbansɔŋfi ń nīn bí ni-kansikin.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we urge you to warn those who are disorderly. Comfort the discouraged. Help the weak. Be patient with everyone.
local: Ti mèé-ni, nnabiyaamu, yii ní kpāafì biwutijoodambee, kí tīī bifaantidambee kunyannyaau, kí tūtuǹ bin wunti yɔ̄tèe, kí nīn cáá kimɔtii báà ŋma pu.

eng: Make sure no one repays a wrong with a wrong, but always pursue the good for each other and everyone else.
local: Ní nīn nyíǹ ni-ba ni-jutiibi yaa ŋá-ni ikpitee, ní taa dū ikpiti kí téeǹ-bi, ama nín̄ nyàab̀maan tiŋan diŋál baabadal ki ŋáań ni-jutiibi nì binib kɔkɔ.

eng: Rejoice always.
local: Ní nīn fàal̀maan baabadal.

eng: Pray continually.
local: kí nīn mèé Unimbɔti kí taa gbèen̄.

eng: Give thanks in every situation because this is Gods will for you in Christ Jesus.
local: Báà tin kamaa di yaa pée bāańee, ní nīn jáaḿ Unimbɔti. Min Unimbɔti là bin sá Yeesu Kriisitoo yabee ń nīn bée dee.

eng: Dont suppress the Spirit.
local: Ní taa kpɔ̀ Unimbɔti Fam tuuŋŋi ni-ni.

eng: You know very well that the day of the Lord is going to come like a thief in the night.
local: Di sá yii nimbi ni-ba ni nyí ti-Dindaan dāantam bɔti tiŋan yii u làá bàń kii unaayuu nín dòoń kunyeeu puee ní.

eng: Dont brush off Spirit-inspired messages.
local: kí taa kpèè Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi bɔti tin bi sòon̄ee fám.

eng: but examine everything carefully and hang on to what is good.
local: Ama, ní kpèè tiwan kɔkɔ kí lēē tin ŋān̄ee.

eng: Avoid every kind of evil.
local: kí nīn nyíǹ ni-ba nì tikpitil kɔkɔ nì ti-bulifi.

eng: Now, may the God of peace himself cause you to be completely dedicated to him; and may your spirit, soul, and body be kept intact and blameless at our Lord Jesus Christs coming.
local: Unimbɔti un tìì ŋgbansɔŋfee u-ba ń yàntī ní dū ni-ba kɔkɔ kí tīī-u. Ú ŋúb́ ni-Fam nì ni-nammu nì ni-wunti kɔkɔ, án nín làá ŋá pu ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo bàńtamee, ní nīn bí circir bí taa láa ká ni-pu ikpiti ibee.

eng: The one who is calling you is faithful and will do this.
local: Un yíiń-nee làá ŋá míǹ kí tīī-ni kun puee u sá ibaamɔndaan ní.

eng: Brothers and sisters, pray for us.
local: Nnabiyaamu, ní nīn mèé ki tìì ti mun.

eng: Greet all the brothers and sisters with a holy kiss.
local: Ní jāam̀ ti-yab nì kunabitiiu jaamii kí tīī-mi.

eng: By the Lords authority, I order all of you to have this letter read aloud to all the brothers and sisters.
local: Nì ti-Dindaan yindi puee, m tùkù-ni yii ní dūmaan kugbɔŋu nee kí kàaǹ nnabiyaamu kɔkɔ nimbiin.

eng: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with all of you.
local: Ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo ŋanti ń nīn bí ni-pu.

eng: When they are saying, There is peace and security, at that time sudden destruction will attack them, like labor pains start with a pregnant woman, and they definitely wont escape.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun binib yaa láa bí ki lì yii: «Ŋgbansɔŋfi, ti ŋmátì dee», dicilpu kpalaa niin ní nnaabu ń jēetí bi-pu kí nàan̄-bi. Báà ubɔ kaa dāa ŋmátí, an dāa bí kii imatiŋween nín bàń upɔdaan puee ní.

eng: But you arent in darkness, brothers and sisters, so the day wont catch you by surprise like a thief.
local: Ama nimbee, nnabiyaamu, naa ti bí dibɔmbɔndin ŋwiin gbanti ń dāá túuǹ-ni kii unaayuu nín tūuntí binib puee.

eng: All of you are children of light and children of the day. We dont belong to night or darkness.
local: Di sá yii nimbi kɔkɔ sá ŋwalifi biyaamu ní ki bí ŋwiimpu. Taa sá kunyeeu yabaaa, kaa sá dibɔmbɔndi yab.

eng: So then, lets not sleep like the others, but lets stay awake and stay sober.
local: Míǹ puee, tí taa nín̄ gèeǹ kii bin kíǹee nín gèeǹ puee, ama timbi ń fìntī kí nīn ŋúb́ ti-ba.

eng: People who sleep sleep at night, and people who get drunk get drunk at night.
local: Kunyeeu ní bin gèeǹee gèeǹ, ní bin nyù ki gbéèyee nyù kunyeeu.

eng: Since we belong to the day, lets stay sober, wearing faithfulness and love as a piece of armor that protects our body and the hope of salvation as a helmet.
local: Ama timbi bin sá ŋwalifin yabee. Ti máaǹ kí nīn ŋúb́ ti-ba tiŋan. Ti máaǹ kí dū ti-fookiiu nì ti-neendi kí dūú pèen̄ kii dijanjabɔkutilee, ní kí dū ti-fiil din pu ti mákée kí cíkiń kii kifuuliiyee.

eng: God didnt intend for us to suffer his wrath but rather to possess salvation through our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Di sá yii Unimbɔti kaa lēē-ti ki yóoǹ tí nīn sá bin pu u làá dāá fōō diŋuulee, ama tí nīn sá bin làá ká difiil ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo puee.

eng: From Paul, who is an apostle of Jesus Christ by the command of God our savior and of Christ Jesus our hope.
local: Man Pɔɔl, un sá Yeesu Kriisitoo kpambal din Unimbɔti ti-Fiiliu nì Yeesu Kriisitoo un pu ti mákée túnnée di ŋmàn̄ kugbɔŋu nee.

eng: They are people who are sexually unfaithful, and people who have intercourse with the same sex. They are kidnappers, liars, individuals who give false testimonies in court, and those who do anything else that is opposed to sound teaching.
local: nì bin ŋáań tilaakpaalee, nì bin dɔ̀ nì bi-ninjajutiibee, nì bin nyáfí binibee, nì binyimɔndambi, nì bin pùtī fám fámee, nì bin kɔkɔ ŋáań tin jáà nì ibaamɔn bɔti tin bi dàkà-tee pu ní bi yóoǹ marab.

eng: Sound teaching agrees with the glorious gospel of the blessed God that has been trusted to me.
local: Tinyool Nimbɔtiu un bí mmɔnnee Bɔmɔntiiti tin u dūu ŋá m-ŋaanee poon ní tin m sòon̄ ki tīī-ni nee kā.

eng: I thank Christ Jesus our Lord, who has given me strength because he considered me faithful. So he appointed me to ministry.
local: M jáaḿ ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo un di tīī-mi mpɔn ki cáá m-pu dimákal yii ḿ tɔ̄ u-tundee.

eng: even though I used to speak against him, attack his people, and I was proud. But I was shown mercy because I acted in ignorance and without faith.
local: Nì min kɔkɔ m nìn sòoǹ u-pu min kaa kítèe ki sàantí u-yab ki wɔ́b̀-bee, Unimbɔti súń m-saai, kun puee maa nìn fōō Yeesu ki kíí, kaa nìn nyí yii m bíi yìntī.

eng: Our Lords favor poured all over me along with the faithfulness and love that are in Christ Jesus.
local: Ti-Dindaan ŋá-mi tiŋan tikpil ki yāntì m fōō ki kíí, ní ki tīī-mi dineendi din nyēeń Yeesu Kriisitoo ceeyee.

eng: This saying is reliable and deserves full acceptance: Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners—and Im the biggest sinner of all.
local: Tibɔti nee sá mbaamɔn ní ki máaǹ báà ŋma ń fōō-tì kí kíí mmɔntiimin. Di sá yii Yeesu Kriisitoo dɔ́miń duulinya nee ni kí fīī binikpitib, binikpitibee kansikinee man di sá unikpitikpaan.

eng: But this is why I was shown mercy, so that Christ Jesus could show his endless patience to me first of all. So Im an example for those who are going to believe in him for eternal life.
local: Ama ní Unimbɔti súń m-saai ki yāntì Yeesu Kriisitoo dī man unikpitikpaan pu ki dàkā u-mɔtiiki nín wīikí min bulifee, án nīn sá kudaaŋŋu kí tīī bin làá dāá fōō-u kí kíí foolboon kí ká dimaŋfal din kaa kúntée.

eng: Now to the king of the ages, to the immortal, invisible, and only God, may honor and glory be given to him forever and always! Amen.
local: An puee, bí tīī Unimbɔti un bí talku, kaa kúù, baa wālifí-uee jilma nì tinyool. Úǹ baba di sá Unimbɔti, kí nīn cá abin abin! Ameeni.

eng: Timothy, my child, Im giving you these instructions based on the prophecies that were once made about you. So if you follow them, you can wage a good war.
local: Sii m-biki Tiimootee, m dū tibɔti nee ki ŋá a-ŋaan. Kii tibɔti tin bi nìn fōō Unimbɔti cee a-pu dooooyee ki dūu nāatèe, á tɔ́kɔń tibɔtee pu kí já kujamɔntiiu.

eng: because you have faith and a good conscience. Some people have ruined their faith because they refused to listen to their conscience.
local: Á nīn gbíntìi ŋúb́ tin a nìn fōō ki kííyee kí nīn cáá nlandɔkɔmɔnti. Biba fá nlandɔkɔmɔnti ní bi-fookiiu ŋá fám.

eng: To Timothy, my true child in the faith. Grace, mercy, and peace from God the Father and from Christ Jesus our Lord.
local: M ŋmàn̄-ku kí tīī sii Tiimootee un sá m-ba bindi pu jipɔɔn a nín fōō Yeesu Kriisitoo ki kííyee puee ní. Ti-Baa Unimbɔti nì ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo ń ŋá-si tiŋan, kí sɔ̄ a-saai, kí tīī-si ŋgbansɔŋfi.

eng: such as Hymenaeus and Alexander. Ive handed them over to Satan so that they can be taught not to speak against God.
local: Ti kāntí binib gbanti kansikinee Imeenee nì Aleekisandir, bin m dū ki líiǹ Sitaan, án nín làá ŋá pu án cíiǹ-bi bí béé kí taa nín̄ gíti sòoǹ ki bíiĺ Unimbɔtee.

eng: When I left for Macedonia, I asked you to stay behind in Ephesus so that you could instruct certain individuals not to spread wrong teaching.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun m làá nìn yīkì kí nīn cá Maseeduwaanee, m nìn tùkū-si yii á nīn kā Eefeesi kí pìin̄ bin bí niin ki dàkà tinyimɔmbɔtee nsan bí taa ti dàkà. Á nīn ŋáań míǹ.

eng: They shouldnt pay attention to myths and endless genealogies. Their teaching only causes useless guessing games instead of faithfulness to Gods way of doing things.
local: ki dàkà-bi bí taa nín̄ kā ki máafí mmaam ki kàaǹ atiŋkpikiyin ŋin kaa dóotée. An cāabìń kunikpɔŋkpɔkɔu, kaa tútúǹ unil ń béé Unimbɔti nín yóoǹ puee: an dàkà yii tí fōō-u kí kíí.

eng: The goal of instruction is love from a pure heart, a good conscience, and a sincere faith.
local: Kun pu ní a kpáafí-bi mimmee di sá a là bí nīn cáá dineendi din nyēeń dipɔɔnyaandinee ní, bi-landɔkɔjaatiŋu ń nīn sá mmɔnti bi-fookiiu ń nīn bá ki pɔ̄ɔ̄.

eng: Because they missed this goal, some people have been distracted by talk that doesnt mean anything.
local: Biba dū an saŋŋuee ki fá, ní ki cūtìi kɔ́ tibɔfan tin kaa cáá taapuee ni.

eng: They want to be teachers of Law without understanding either what they are saying or what they are talking about with such confidence.
local: Bi bàà là kí nīn dàkà binib Unimbɔti marab, ama nì min kɔkɔ bi dàkà nì mpɔnee, bíǹ biba kaa gbìl̀aaa, kaa nyí tibɔti tin bi sòoǹee taapu.

eng: Now we know that the Law is good if used appropriately.
local: Ti nyí yii marab sá tiwammɔntiil ní, a yaa dū marab gbanti ki ŋá min sá á dūú ŋée.

eng: We understand this: the Law isnt established for a righteous person but for people who live without laws and without obeying any authority. They are the ungodly and the sinners. They are people who are not spiritual, and nothing is sacred to them. They kill their fathers and mothers, and murder others.
local: Ti nyí yii an kaa sá binimɔntiibi pu ní bi yóoǹ marab, an sá bin gáatèe, nì bin yiliŋ pɔ̄ɔ̄yee, nì bin kaa tɔ́ Unimbɔtee, nì bikpitiŋaliibi, nì binib bin kaa là Unimbɔtee, nì bin bíiĺ Unimbɔti wantee, nì bin kù bi-baabi nì bi-nabee, nì binikpɔliibi.

eng: First of all, then, I ask that requests, prayers, petitions, and thanksgiving be made for all people.
local: Tibɔpeepeekaal tin m kpáafí bí ŋée di sá bí nīn mèé ki gbàam̄ ki jáaḿ Unimbɔti binib kɔkɔ pu.

eng: They should make themselves attractive by doing good, which is appropriate for women who claim to honor God.
local: Ama bí nīn tūǹ atummɔnti ŋin máaǹ binimpoobi bin bí yii bi nóó Unimbɔtee tuuŋŋee.

eng: A wife should learn quietly with complete submission.
local: Bi yaa bíi dàkà Unimbɔti bɔtee, binimpoobi ń sìntī bi-ba taapu kí nīn ŋmí.

eng: I dont allow a wife to teach or to control her husband. Instead, she should be a quiet listener.
local: Maa pūǹ nsan yii binimpoobi ń nīn dàkà bininjab, kí taa nín̄ wɔ́b̀ bininjab, bi máaǹ kí nīn kā soooo ní.

eng: Adam was formed first, and then Eve.
local: Di sá yii Adam ní Unimbɔti péé péé ki náań, boonee ní ki nín náań Awa mun.

eng: Adam wasnt deceived, but rather his wife became the one who stepped over the line because she was completely deceived.
local: An kaa tí sá Adam ní Sitaan ŋmātiǹ, u-nimpuu ní Sitaan ŋmātiǹ, ní u yīntì Unimbɔti marau.

eng: But a wife will be brought safely through childbirth, if they both continue in faith, love, and holiness, together with self-control.
local: Ama ní u-maaliŋu puee, u yaa gbíntì ki pɔ̄ɔntí u-fookiiu poon nì dineendi kí nīn bí circir kí taa nín̄ dàkà u-bee, u làá dāá ká difiil.

eng: Pray for kings and everyone who is in authority so that we can live a quiet and peaceful life in complete godliness and dignity.
local: Bí nīn mèé bibɔtiibi nì biyidambi kɔkɔ pu, án nín làá ŋá pu tiba ń taa páb́-ti, tí nīn bí nsɔŋfin kí nīn nóó Unimbɔti mmɔntiimin kí lafun nín̄ sá binimɔntiibee.

eng: This is right and it pleases God our savior.
local: Á mèè kí tīī binibee ŋān̄ Unimbɔti ti-Fiiliu cee.

eng: who wants all people to be saved and to come to a knowledge of the truth.
local: kun puee u là yii binib kɔkɔ ń ká difiil kí béé ibaamɔn.

eng: There is one God and one mediator between God and humanity, the human Christ Jesus.
local: Di sá yii Unimbɔti sá ubaanti, ní ugbaamil un yú timbi binib nì Unimbɔti kansikinee mun sá ubaanti, úǹ di sá Yeesu Kriisitoo un kpántì unilee.

eng: who gave himself as a payment to set all people free. This was a testimony that was given at the right time.
local: U dū u-ba ki tīī ki pàn̄ ti-kɔkɔ pu ki fōō-ti ki líì. U ŋá míǹ ki dàkā yii Unimbɔti là binib kɔkɔ ń ká difiil ŋyunti ŋun u síiǹee ní.

eng: I was appointed to be a preacher and apostle of this testimony—Im telling the truth and Im not lying! Im a teacher of the Gentiles in faith and truth.
local: Ní Unimbɔti dū-mi ki ŋá un sòoǹ u-bɔtee nì u-kpambal, ḿ nīn dàkà bin kaa sá Juuda yabee u-bɔti tin sá ibaamɔnee bí fōō kí kíí. Tin m lì nee sá ibaamɔn ní, maa mɔ́ntí.

eng: Therefore, I want men to pray everywhere by lifting up hands that are holy, without anger or argument.
local: An puee, m là yii, báà laakiŋyee, bininjab ń yòon̄ bi-ŋalii yilpu kí nīn mèé, bi-pɔbiliŋ poon ń nīn bí circir, bí taa nín̄ cáá diŋuul nì kunikpɔŋkpɔkɔu.

eng: In the same way, I want women to enhance their appearance with clothing that is modest and sensible, not with elaborate hairstyles, gold, pearls, or expensive clothes.
local: M là yii binimpoobi mun ń nīn pèentí tiwan min kítèe, kí taa nín̄ nyāantí bi-ba ki gíti lákatí, kí taa nín̄ dàkà bi-ba, bi-yikpibóobitam ń taa nín̄ nyēē mbamin. Bí taa nín̄ cáá sindaariya nì abeedimbil wanti ki nyāantí bi-ba, kí taa nín̄ pèentí tiwan tin pɔ̄ɔ̄ kudaauee.

eng: This saying is reliable: if anyone has a goal to be a supervisor in the church, they want a good thing.
local: Tibɔti tin m làá sòoǹee sá ibaamɔn ní: di sá yii unil un là kí nīn kpòotī Kriisitoo nikitilee lēē ditummɔntiil.

eng: They should also be tested and then serve if they are without fault.
local: Kí wàatī bí lēē-bee, bí cɔ̄ŋkì kí kpèè bi-bimbim nín bí puee. Bi yaa kaa ká bi-pu tibee, ní bí ŋmā kí nīn sá bin tūǹ Kriisitoo nikitil kansikin ditundee.

eng: In the same way, women who are servants in the church should be dignified and not gossip. They should be sober and faithful in everything they do.
local: Míǹ mbaantiim, binimpoobi mun máaǹ kí nīn cáá mbimbimɔntiim, kí taa nín̄ sòoǹ ki bíiĺ binib, kí nīn ŋúb́ bi-ba, kí nīn bí deedee tikɔkɔ nì tikɔkɔ poon.

eng: Servants must be faithful to their spouse and manage their children and their own households well.
local: Bin tūǹ Kriisitoo nikitil kansikin ditundee máaǹ kí nīn cáá unimpu ubɔ ubɔ, ní kí ŋmā kí kpòotī bi-biyaamu nì bi-dicaŋŋi mmɔntiimin.

eng: Those who have served well gain a good standing and considerable confidence in the faith that is in Christ Jesus.
local: Bi yaa tūǹ bi-tuuŋŋi mmɔntiiminee, binib tɔ́-bi ní, ní bi sòoǹ Yeesu Kriisitoo bɔti, kaa gùgufī kpataaa.

eng: I hope to come to you quickly. But Im writing these things to you so that.
local: M dá láá ŋmàn̄-si kugbɔŋu nee ki máká yii an kaa làá ti yúntí, m làá dāan̄ a-cee.

eng: if Im delayed, youll know how you should behave in Gods household. It is the church of the living God and the backbone and support of the truth.
local: M yaa kaa tí ŋúń ki dɔ́miń malaayaaa, kugbɔŋu nee ŋūǹ kí tūtuǹ-si á béé a nín máaǹ kí nīn bí min Unimbɔti dicaŋku poonee, an dàkà yii Unimbɔti un kaa kúùyee nikitil din sá kii jaantalkub ki tíkí ibaamɔnee.

eng: Without question, the mystery of godliness is great: he was revealed as a human, declared righteous by the Spirit, seen by angels, preached throughout the nations, believed in around the world, and taken up in glory.
local: Tiŋman, dinimbɔtinool bɔti tin nìn bí dibalin Unimbɔti nyántì ki dàkā-tee sá tiwaŋkpaandi ní! Yeesu kpántì unil ní ki dɔ́miń ti-cee, Circir Fam dàkā yii u sá deedeedaan, Unimbɔti tuuŋyi ká-u. Bi sòon̄ u-pu atimbun kɔkɔn, kitiŋ pu nib fōō-u ki kíí ní Unimbɔti yōoǹ-u ki jɔ́m̀ yilpu tinyool laŋki.

eng: So the churchs supervisor must be without fault. They should be faithful to their spouse, sober, modest, and honest. They should show hospitality and be skilled at teaching.
local: An máaǹ bí taa ká un kpòotī Kriisitoo nikitilee pu tikpitil tiba. Ú nīn cáá unimpubaanti, kí ŋúb́ u-ba, kí nīn cáá iciin, kí nīn sá unimɔnti, kí nīn fòol̄ binicambi, kí nīn ŋūǹ ki dàkà binib Unimbɔti bɔti.

eng: They shouldnt be addicted to alcohol or be a bully. Instead, they should be gentle, peaceable, and not greedy.
local: Ú taa nín̄ sá udanyɔl nì kílkil niliu, ama ú nīn sá unil un mɔ̄ee, kí taa nín̄ cáá kunikpɔŋkpɔkɔu, kí taa nín̄ néeń animbil.

eng: They should manage their own household well—they should see that their children are obedient with complete respect.
local: U máaǹ kí ŋmā kí kpòotī u-ba dicaŋku tiŋan, kí yàntī u-biyaamu ń nīn tɔ́-u nì tibulcin.

eng: because if they dont know how to manage their own household, how can they take care of Gods church?.
local: Di sá yii unil yaa kaa ŋūǹ kí kpòotī u-ba dicaŋku tiŋanee, mana ní u làá ŋmā kí kpòotī Unimbɔti nikitil?.

eng: They shouldnt be new believers so that they wont become proud and fall under the devils spell.
local: Udaan ń taa nín̄ sá un fōō Yeesu ki kíí mpɔmee. An yaa sá fám, u dù kí ŋá dilambil, ní kí kɔ́ Unimbɔti bɔtin kii Sitaan nín kɔ́ puee.

eng: They should also have a good reputation with those outside the church so that they wont be embarrassed and fall into the devils trap.
local: Bin kaa nóó Unimbɔtee mun máaǹ kí nīn nyí udaan pu yii u sá unimɔnti, án nín làá ŋá pu bí taa nín̄ kpèé-u fám, ú dāá jēetí Sitaan talintinee.

eng: In the same way, servants in the church should be dignified, not two-faced, heavy drinkers, or greedy for money.
local: Míǹ mbaantiimin, Kriisitoo nikitil kpilib máaǹ kí nīn cáá mbimbimɔntiim, kí taa nín̄ bí mili mili, kí taa nín̄ gbéè ndaam, kí taa dī isaŋkpiti pu kí nyàab̀ animbil.

eng: They should hold on to the faith that has been revealed with a clear conscience.
local: Bi máaǹ kí nīn ŋúb́ Unimbɔti saŋŋu ŋun nìn bí dibalin u nyántì ki dàkā-tee nì dipɔɔbaantiil.

eng: The Spirit clearly says that in latter times some people will turn away from the faith. They will pay attention to spirits that deceive and to the teaching of demons.
local: Unimbɔti Fam lī ki cántì yii, iyuntijoolkaa poonee, binib biba làá dāá fá nnimbɔtisan ní ki kíǹ ki tɔ́ arasiniibi bin kíiĺ binibee, ní ki nóó bi-dàkatam.

eng: We work and struggle for this: Our hope is set on the living God, who is the savior of all people, especially those who believe.
local: Kun pu ti tūǹ ditundi ki gì ki bí kujaau poonee di sá ti dū ti-mákal ki ŋá Unimbɔti un bí kaa kúùyee ni, Unimbɔti un fìil̄ binib kɔkɔee: kí cántée, bin fōō Yeesu ki kííyee ní an sá.

eng: Command these things. Teach them.
local: Á dàkà binib tibɔti nee kɔkɔ kí yàntī bí ŋá míǹ.

eng: Dont let anyone look down on you because you are young. Instead, set an example for the believers through your speech, behavior, love, faith, and by being sexually pure.
local: Á taa yàntī bí kpèè kí sìŋkīǹ-si yii a nín sá kinaacimpɔmbikee pu. Ama á nīn sá un bin fōō Yeesu ki kííyee làá nīn kpèé ki ŋáań min a ŋáańee, kí fōō a-sòontam nì a-bimbim nì a-neendi nì a-fookiiu nì a-circir bítam.

eng: Until I arrive, pay attention to public reading, preaching, and teaching.
local: Kí wàatī ḿ nín dāan̄ee, á dū a-ba kí tīī Unimbɔti gbɔŋku dikàandi binib cee, kí nīn sòoǹ ki tìì-bi kunyannyaau, ki dàkà-bi Unimbɔti bɔti.

eng: Dont neglect the spiritual gift in you that was given through prophecy when the elders laid hands on you.
local: Mpɔn ŋun Unimbɔti nìn pú-si ŋyunti ŋun bi nìn fōō a-pu Unimbɔti cee tibɔti ki nāatì, ní Kriisitoo nikitil kpilib dū bi-ŋalii ki páaǹ a-puee, ŋu bí a-ni. Á dū-ŋu kí tɔ̄ ditundi.

eng: Practice these things, and live by them so that your progress will be visible to all.
local: Á taa gbīiliǹ ditundi gbanti. Á dū a-ba kɔkɔ kí kúntí di-poon, án nín làá ŋá pu báà ŋma ń ká a nín cá nyɔkɔpu miŋyee.

eng: Focus on working on your own development and on what you teach. If you do this, you will save yourself and those who hear you.
local: Á nīn nyí nì a-ba bítam nì tin a dàkèe, kí cúú an píkí. A yaa lafun ŋá míǹ, a làá fīī a-ba, kí kpáàa fīī bin pílifíǹ-see mun.

eng: They will be controlled by the pretense of lying, and their own consciences will be seared.
local: Mpoonnyoondambi bin mɔ́ntí binib, bi-pɔbiliŋ tīlifèe di làá dāá yàntī bí gáatí.

eng: They will prohibit marriage and eating foods that God created—and he intended them to be accepted with thanksgiving by those who are faithful and have come to know the truth.
local: Bi dāa yíití yii an kaa ŋān̄ unil ń cáá unimpu, ní ki kòó tijin tiba, tijikaal tin Unimbɔti nyántì yii bin fōō Yeesu ki kíí ki nyí ibaamɔn kɔkɔee ń mèè kí jāam̀ Unimbɔti ní kí jēe.

eng: Everything that has been created by God is good, and nothing that is received with thanksgiving should be rejected.
local: Tin kɔkɔ Unimbɔti náańee ŋān̄ ní, tiba kaa sá tí dūú fá. Tí fōō tin kɔkɔ bée kí jāam̀ Unimbɔti.

eng: These things are made holy by Gods word and prayer.
local: Unimbɔti bɔti nì ŋwimmeem yàntī tijin ń kpántí timɔntiil ní u-nimbiin.

eng: If you point these things out to the believers, you will be a good servant of Christ Jesus who has been trained by the words of faith and the good teaching that youve carefully followed.
local: A yaa dū tibɔti nee ki dàkā ti-nabiyaamuee, a kpántí Yeesu Kriisitoo tɔntɔmmɔntiu, ní kí nīn cáá nnimbɔtisan bɔti nì didàkamɔntiil din a nóó tiŋanee ki kpīintí a-ba.

eng: But stay away from the godless myths that are passed down from the older women. Train yourself for a holy life!.
local: Á fá mmaam min nyáń mi-ba nì nnimbɔtisan bɔti ki sá tibɔfanee, kí pɔ́ɔǹ a-ba kí mɔ̀tīí nóó Unimbɔti mbaamɔmin.

eng: While physical training has some value, training in holy living is useful for everything. It has promise for this life now and the life to come.
local: A yaa sáń ki kpàtī a-giiŋyee, an tútúǹ-si fiii, ama á nóó Unimbɔti mmɔntiiminee di tútúǹ tikɔkɔ poon, kun puee an pòontí-si dimaŋfal kɔŋkɔnnee ki kpáaǹ foolboon.

eng: This saying is reliable and deserves complete acceptance.
local: Tibɔti nee di sá ibaamɔn ki máaǹ báà ŋma ń fōō-tì kí kíí.

eng: Dont correct an older man, but encourage him like hes your father; treat younger men like your brothers.
local: Á taa wòoǹ utikpil mpɔn. Á sòoǹ nì u soooo kii a-baayee, kí nīn sòoǹ nì binaacimpɔmbi kii a-naalibee.

eng: She should have a reputation for doing good: raising children, providing hospitality to strangers, washing the feet of the saints, helping those in distress, and dedicating herself to every kind of good thing.
local: Ú nīn sá unimpu un bi pàkà yii u tūǹ atummɔnti ki ŋúń ki ŋúb́ u-biyaamu tiŋan, ki fòol̄ binicambi mmɔntiimin, ki fíntí Unimbɔti nib taaŋi, ki tútúǹ bifalaadambi, ki pée tūǹ atummɔnti ŋmaniŋmee.

eng: But dont accept younger widows for the list. When their physical desires distract them from Christ, they will want to get married.
local: An yaa sá bikpiipusapɔmbee, bí taa ŋmà bi-yiŋŋi kugbɔŋu poon, kun puee nninjaneel ŋūǹ kí cúú-bi bí dū Kriisitoo kí fá, kí cùú mɔ̄ǹ.

eng: Then they will be judged for setting aside their earlier commitment.
local: Bi nín ŋá mimmee ní kí kɔ́ Unimbɔti bɔtin, kun puee bi nín nìn pōoǹ yii bi làá ŋá miŋyee, baa ti ŋúń ki ŋá.

eng: Also, they learn to be lazy by going from house to house. They are not only lazy, but they also become gossips and busybodies, talking about things they shouldnt.
local: Kí kūtèe, bi nín kaa cáá tiba kí ŋée puee ní bi yìiǹ ki póotí acin. Tin gíti púntèe di sá bi kíǹ ki cáá kubɔcaau ní ki sòoǹ icoo bɔti, nì tin tin kaa bàà máaǹ bí sòoǹee.

eng: So I want younger widows to marry, have children, and manage their homes so that they wont give the enemy any reason to slander us. .
local: An pu ní m là yii bikpiipoobi bin dáá pɔ̀ɔǹee ń mɔ̄ǹ kí màal̀ mbiyaamu kí nīn kpèé bi-ciŋŋi, án nín làá ŋá pu bí taa pɔ̄ unannanli nsan bí sòoǹ kí bīil̀-tee.

eng: Some have already turned away to follow Satan.
local: Kun puee bikpiipoobi biba pūtì kpáàa dū nsammɔnti ki fá, ki kíǹ ki nóó Sitaan.

eng: If any woman who is a believer has widows in her family, she should take care of them and not burden the church, so that it can help other widows who are truly needy.
local: Unimpu un fōō ki kííyee yaa cáá bikpiipoobi u-dumpuee, u máaǹ kí tūtuǹ-bi, kí taa dū ditúndi gbanti kí túń Kriisitoo nikitil pu. Míǹ di làá yàntī Kriisitoo nikitil ń ŋmā kí nīn tútúǹ bikpiipoobi bin kaa pée cáá báà ubɔ nì ubɔee.

eng: Elders who lead well should be paid double, especially those who work with public speaking and teaching.
local: Bin kpòotī Kriisitoo nikitil mmɔntiiminee máaǹ tipaal mili pu. Cilinjee bin pu m sòoǹee di sá bin pɔ̄ɔntí ki sòoǹ Unimbɔti bɔti ki dàkà muee.

eng: The scripture says, Dont put a muzzle on an ox while it treads grain , and Workers deserve their pay .
local: Di sá Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon lī yii: «Á taa bóob́ unaa un tà ki gbátí adibilee nyɔkɔ», ní ki gíti lī yii: «Utɔntɔnli máaǹ u-paati.».

eng: Dont accept an accusation made against an elder unless it is confirmed by two or three witnesses.
local: Ubɔ yaa dɔ́miń ki tùkū-si yii bin kpòotī Kriisitoo nikitilee ubɔ ŋá tibee, á taa fōō kí kíí, see binib bili kii bita di yaa ká ní ki tùkū-si.

eng: treat older women like your mother, and treat younger women like your sisters with appropriate respect.
local: Á nīn sòoǹ nì binimpukpaambi kii a-nabee, nì binimpusapɔmbi kii a-niisalibee nì tibulcin poon.

eng: Discipline those who are sinning in front of everyone so that all the others will be afraid.
local: Á nīn wòoǹ bin ŋáań ikpitee binib kɔkɔ nimbiin, án nín làá ŋá pu bin kíǹee ń fàŋkèe.

eng: I charge you before God and Christ Jesus and the elect angels to follow these practices without bias, and without playing favorites.
local: M kpáafí-si Unimbɔti nì Yeesu Kriisitoo nì Unimbɔti tuuŋyi in u lútèe nimbiin, yii á ŋúb́ tibɔti tin m sòoǹ nì si nee kí taa nín̄ cáá dinigàntil, kí taa lēē biba ki ŋáań bíǹ tiŋan ki yàntī bin kíǹee.

eng: Dont rush to commission anyone to leadership, and dont participate in the sins of others. Keep yourself morally pure.
local: Á taa ŋá kujinjikilaapaau kí dū a-ŋalii kí páań ubɔ pu kí mèè u-pu kí tīī-u ditundi diba Kriisitoo nikitil kansikin, kí taa kɔ́ biba kpitiin. Nín̄ nyí nì a-ba kí nīn bí circir.

eng: Dont drink water anymore, but use a little wine because of your stomach problems and your frequent illnesses.
local: Á taa ti nín̄ nyù nnyim ŋmaniŋma. Nín̄ nyù ndamam fiii ipɔŋween nì a-wunti nín nyàŋfī múlmulee pu.

eng: The sins of some people are obvious, and the sins are judged before the people must face judgment, but the sins of other people show up later.
local: Bi wālifí biba kpitii kí wàatī gba ní kí nín sòoǹ bidambi bɔti, ama bibee, ibɔsoon poon ní bi dāa nín bìitī bíǹ kpitii pu.

eng: In the same way, the good that people do is also obvious and cant be hidden.
local: Míǹ ní bi tí kāntí atummɔnti mun. Ŋin ní baa kāntí malaayee gba mun kpá kí gbíntí dibalin.

eng: Take care of widows who are truly needy.
local: Á nīn ŋúb́ bikpiipoobi bin kaa cáá báà ubɔ nì ubɔee kí nīn tɔ́-bi.

eng: But if a particular widow has children or grandchildren, they should first learn to respect their own family and repay their parents, because this pleases God.
local: Ama ukpiipu ubɔ yaa cáá mbiyaamu, an yaa tí sá u-naantiibaaa, bíǹ di máaǹ kí béé nnimbɔtisan tundi bi-ba dicaŋku poon kí wàatī ní kí ŋúb́ bi-nab nì bi-naambi kí téeǹ, min fáatì Unimbɔtee dee.

eng: A widow who is truly needy and all alone puts her hope in God and keeps on going with requests and prayers, night and day.
local: Unimpu un yaa lafun sá ukpiipu, kaa cáá ubɔ ń kpèè-uee dù u-mákal kí ŋá Unimbɔtin ní, ki mèé ki gbàam̄ Unimbɔti ŋwiimpu nì kunyeeu.

eng: But a widow who tries to live a life of luxury is dead even while she is alive.
local: An yaa sá ukpiipu un ŋáań min kaa kítèe, u bí dimaŋfalin ama ní ki kpíì.

eng: Teach these things so that the families will be without fault.
local: Tin tin a máaǹ kí dūú dàkà-bee dee, án nín làá ŋá pu ubɔ ń taa ká ikpiti iba bi-puee.

eng: But if someone doesnt provide for their own family, and especially for a member of their household, they have denied the faith. They are worse than those who have no faith.
local: Un kaa ŋúb́ u-dicaŋku tiŋanee, m lì ki bántí u-diiku poon nib kíŋŋŋ ní, mimmee udaan fá fookii, ní bin kaa fōō Yeesu ki kííyee sɔ́ǹ-u.

eng: Put a widow on the list who is older than years old and who was faithful to her husband.
local: Bí taa ŋmà ukpiipu ubɔ yindi kí kūtì bikpiipoobi gbɔŋku poon, asee ukpiipu un yaa cáá abin jítì abin imuŋku ita puee baba. Kí wàatēe, ú nīn sá un nìn mɔ́ǹ uninjabaantee.

eng: Those who are under the bondage of slavery should consider their own masters as worthy of full respect so that Gods name and our teaching wont get a bad reputation.
local: Iyumbu máaǹ kí nīn tɔ́ i-dindambi nì i-pɔbiliŋ kɔkɔ. Mimmee binib kaa sòoǹ kí bīil̀ Unimbɔti yindi nì tibɔti tin ti dàkèe.

eng: The love of money is the root of all kinds of evil. Some have wandered away from the faith and have impaled themselves with a lot of pain because they made money their goal.
local: Di sá yii nnimbineel di cāabìń mbusub kɔkɔ. Biba nín néeń animbil puee, bi dū nnimbɔtisan ki fá ki dū bi-ba ki kɔ́ǹ dinimbinaamandin tikpil.

eng: But as for you, man of God, run away from all these things. Instead, pursue righteousness, holy living, faithfulness, love, endurance, and gentleness.
local: Ama sii Unimbɔti niliu, nyā tíǹ kɔkɔn, kí nyàab̀ kí nīn bí deedee, kí ŋúb́ dinimbɔtinool, kí nīn cáá fookii, kí nīn cáá dineendi nì kimɔtii, kí nīn mɔ̄.

eng: Compete in the good fight of faith. Grab hold of eternal life—you were called to it, and you made a good confession of it in the presence of many witnesses.
local: Á já kujamɔntiiu kun sá fookii jaakuee, kí cúú dimaŋfal din kaa kúntée. Di sá yii tíǹ pu ní Unimbɔti yíiń-si á béé-ŋu ŋyunti ŋun a nìn sòon̄ a nín fōō ki kíí kunipaau nimbiin miŋyee.

eng: I command you in the presence of God, who gives life to all things, and Christ Jesus, who made the good confession when testifying before Pontius Pilate.
local: Unimbɔti un tìì tiwan kɔkɔ dimaŋfalee nimbiin, nì Yeesu Kriisitoo un nìn sòon̄ ibaamɔn Pɔnsi Piilati cee mmɔntiimee mun nimbiin ní m kpáafí-si.

eng: Obey this order without fault or failure until the appearance of our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Á kpèè tibɔti tin m ŋúbiǹ-si nee tiŋan kí taa yìntī. Bí taa ká a-pu tikpitil tiba kí nīn cí ŋyunti ŋun ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo làá dāá páannée.

eng: The timing of this appearance is revealed by God alone, who is the blessed and only master, the King of kings and Lord of lords.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun Unimbɔti yóoǹee yaa láa bāańee, ní ú yàntī Yeesu ń páanní. Úǹ di sá nnaam nyeel ní ki sá tikɔkɔ Dindaan, bibɔtiibi kɔkɔ Bɔtiu nì bidindambi kɔkɔ Dindaan.

eng: He alone has immortality and lives in light that no one can come near. No human being has ever seen or is able to see him. Honor and eternal power belong to him. Amen.
local: Úǹ baba di kaa kúù, ki kóò ŋwalifi ŋun ubɔ kaa ŋūǹ kí sútí kí pìl̄ee ni. Báà unil kaa láá ká-u, ní kaa tí ŋūǹ kí ká-u. Úǹ di máaǹ tinyool nì mpɔn kí nīn cá jaanjaan! Ameeni.

eng: Tell people who are rich at this time not to become egotistical and not to place their hope on their finances, which are uncertain. Instead, they need to hope in God, who richly provides everything for our enjoyment.
local: Á kpāafì bin cáá tigaajati duulinya nee nee yii bí taa nín̄ cáá dilambil, kí taa nín̄ máká bi-gaajati tin kaa làá nīn bí ki cá jaanjaanee pu. Ama bí dū bi-mákal kí ŋá Unimbɔti un di pūǹ-ti tikɔkɔ nì tikɔkɔ tikpil tikpil tí nīn bí mmɔnnee ni.

eng: Tell them to do good, to be rich in the good things they do, to be generous, and to share with others.
local: Bí nīn ŋáań tiŋan. Bi-gaajawanti nín wīikí puee, bi-tummɔntiiŋi mun ń nīn wīikí míǹ, bí nīn cáá tiniti ki yàkatī tin bi cááyee nì bin kíǹee.

eng: When they do these things, they will save a treasure for themselves that is a good foundation for the future. That way they can take hold of what is truly life.
local: Bi yaa ŋá mimmee, bi kōontí tigaajamɔntiil tin kaa bíiĺee ki sīintí án nīn dɔ̄ ki cí-bi foolboon dee, ní bí ŋmā kí ká ibaamɔn maŋfal.

eng: And those who have masters who are believers shouldnt look down on them because they are brothers. Instead, they should serve them more faithfully, because the people who benefit from your good service are believers who are loved. Teach and encourage these things.
local: Iyumbu in dindambi nóó Kriisitooyee, í taa kpèè-bi fám, yii bi nín sá i-nabiyaamuee pu. Míǹ gba ní i nín máaǹ kí nīn tūǹ ditundi mmɔntiimin, kun puee bin i tūǹ ditundi ki tììyee sá i-nabineeŋkaamu mun nóó Kriisitooyee ní. Min a máaǹ kí nīn dàkà binib ki kpáafí-bee sèé.

eng: Timothy, protect what has been given to you in trust. Avoid godless and pointless discussions and the contradictory claims of so-called knowledge.
local: Sii Tiimootee, kpāafì tin Unimbɔti tīī-see tiŋan, kí gɔ́ntí tibɔfan tin kaa nàaǹ nì nnimbɔtisanee, kí taa cèeǹ a-nyɔkɔ bin bí yii bi cáá iciin kin kaa cááyee nikpɔŋkpɔkɔku poon.

eng: When some people adopted this false knowledge, they missed the goal of faith. May grace be with you all.
local: Di sá biba nìn bí yii bíǹ di nyí dinyil gbanti ní ki dāa dū nnimbɔtisan ki fá. Kriisitoo ŋanti ń nīn bí ni-pu.

eng: If anyone teaches anything different and doesnt agree with sound teaching about our Lord Jesus Christ and teaching that is consistent with godliness.
local: Ubɔ yaa bíi dàkà tinyimɔmbɔti tiba, ki yíì, kaa nóó ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo baamɔmbɔti nì bi nín dàkā-ti unil nín máaǹ kí nīn bí min Unimbɔti nimbiinee.

eng: that person is conceited. They dont understand anything but have a sick obsession with debates and arguments. This creates jealousy, conflict, verbal abuse, and evil suspicions.
local: udaan lámintí, kaa nyí tiba. Udaan būǹ tibɔti weeŋyi ní ki kpákà anyɔɔbun pu. An di cāabìń nnimbidiki, kujaau, asiibil, nì ilandɔkɔkpiti bi-jutiibi pu.

eng: There is constant bickering between people whose minds are ruined and who have been robbed of the truth. They think that godliness is a way to make money!.
local: Baa ti dàkafī nnimɔntiyaamin, baa ti nyí ibaamɔn, ki kpākantí tɔb an kaa kúntí. Bi nyí kii nnimbɔtisan sá laakin bi làá dī kí ká tigaajatee ní.

eng: Actually, godliness is a great source of profit when it is combined with being happy with what you already have.
local: Tiŋman, nnimbɔtisan lafun sá tigaajakpaandi ní, ŋyunti ŋun ní an mɔ̄ǹ-si tin a cááyee puee.

eng: We didnt bring anything into the world and so we cant take anything out of it.
local: Ti dɔ́miń duulinya nee ni nì iŋaŋkulim ní, míǹ ní ti làá dāá fàtīí tí gítí nì iŋaŋkulim.

eng: well be happy with food and clothing.
local: Míǹ puee, ti yaa cáá kun ti làá jēe nì kun ti làá pèen̄ee, án ŋmàntīǹ-tee.

eng: But people who are trying to get rich fall into temptation. They are trapped by many stupid and harmful passions that plunge people into ruin and destruction.
local: Ama bin nimbiliŋ máǹ tigaajati puee làá kɔ́ icɔŋkin kí lítí, ní nnimbitaataam tiwan tikpil pu min kaa cáá taapu ki cāabìń diwaŋkilee sàtī-bi kii kutaliŋŋuee. An di téekí binib ki cáǹ asaluun ki nàantí-bi.

eng: From Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by Gods will, and Timothy our brother. To Gods church that is in Corinth, along with all of Gods people throughout Achaia.
local: Man Pɔɔl un Unimbɔti là m sá Yeesu Kriisitoo kpambalee, nì ti-nabiki Tiimootee di ŋmàn̄ kugbɔŋu nee kí tīī nimbi Unimbɔti yab bin kóò Kɔranti tiŋkinee nì bin kɔkɔ sá Unimbɔti nikitil ki kóò Akaayi timmu mun kíǹee nee.

eng: God rescued us from a terrible death, and he will rescue us. We have set our hope on him that he will rescue us again.
local: Úǹ di nyántì-ti dinimbinaamandi kúm gbanti poon, ní ki làá dāá nyāntí-ti. Ní ti cáá dimákal u-pu yii úǹ di làá dāá gíti nyāntí-ti.

eng: since you are helping with your prayer for us. Then many people can thank God on our behalf for the gift that was given to us through the prayers of many people.
local: kun puee ti nyí yii ni bíi tútúǹ-ti ŋwimmeem poon. Di sá yii kunipaau yaa mēē ti-pu ní Unimbɔti fōō bi-wimmeemee, kunipaau làá dāá tí pàkà-u u nín ŋá tiŋan tin ki tīī-tee pu.

eng: We have conducted ourselves with godly sincerity and pure motives in the world, and especially toward you. This is why we are confident, and our conscience confirms this. We didnt act with human wisdom but we relied on the grace of God.
local: Kun pu an mɔ̄ǹ-tee di sá yii ti-pɔbiliŋ poonee, ti nyí yii ti dū dipɔbil diba nì mbimbimɔntiim ní ki kāl̀ duulinyan, cilinjee ni-kansikin. Tíǹ kɔkɔ nyánní Unimbɔti cee ní. An sá Unimbɔti ŋanti di kpōotì-ti, kaa sá unil ciiŋyi.

eng: We dont write anything to you except what you can read and also understand. I hope that you will understand totally.
local: Di sá yii tigbɔn tin kɔkɔ ti ŋmàn̄ ki tīī-nee poonee, taa ti kútì tiba kii tin ni kàan̄ ki gbìl̄ee. Ní m máká yii ni làá gbìl̄ tibɔti nee taapu tiŋan.

eng: since you have already understood us partly. Understand that in the day of our Lord Jesus, we will make you proud as you will also make us proud.
local: - Di sá yii naa láá gbìl̄ kɔkɔ ki dóò -: Ama ti-Dindaan Yeesu gítintam dalee, timbi puee, án làá dāá mɔ̄ɔkiǹ-ni, ní ni mun puee, án làá dāá mɔ̄ɔkiǹ ti mun.

eng: Because I was sure of this, I wanted to visit you first so that you could have a second opportunity to see me.
local: Tíǹ pu ní m máká ki bàà là kí dīń ni-cee án nín làá ŋá pu ní ká tiŋan mili puee.

eng: I wanted to visit you on my way to Macedonia, and then come to you again on my way back from Macedonia, at which point I was hoping you would help me on my way to Judea.
local: M bàà dākafì yii ḿ dīń ni-cee ní kí nín càatī Maseeduwaan tiŋkin, ní boonee kí fàtīí tí dīń ni-cee ní bàà tūtuǹ-mi ḿ nìkī m-saŋkindi kí nīn cá Juudee tiŋkin.

eng: So I wasnt unreliable when I planned to do this, was I? Or do I make decisions with a substandard human process so that I say Yes, yes and No, no at the same time?.
local: M nín bɔ́ntì kí còom̀ m-saŋŋu puee, an sá man di gāā m-bindi ki là kí cù-ŋu naaa? Àá m sá unil un nyɔkɔ kaa bīī tibɔti kí lì yii iiin, ní kí dāá fàtīí lì yii aayeeyee ní?.

eng: But as God is faithful, our message to you isnt both yes and no.
local: Ibaamɔn, Unimbɔti sá m-seeradaaŋwu. M-bɔti tin m sòon̄ nee kaa sá «Iiin» nì «Aayee» bɔti.

eng: Gods Son, Jesus Christ, is the one who was preached among you by us—through me, Silvanus, and Timothy—he wasnt yes and no. In him it is always yes.
local: Di sá yii Unimbɔti Jipɔɔn Yeesu Kriisitoo un bɔti man nì Siilasi nì Tiimootee sòon̄ ni-cee niinee kaa sá «Iiin» nì «Aayee» bɔti. Ama, u-nee, an pée sá «Iiin» ŋmaniŋma ní.

eng: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and from our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Ti-Baa Unimbɔti nì ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo ń ŋá-ni tiŋan kí tīī-ni ŋgbansɔŋfi.

eng: All of Gods promises have their yes in him. That is why we say Amen through him to the glory of God.
local: Kun puee Unimbɔti nín pōoǹ tiŋyee, Yeesu pu ní an sá «Iiin» tiŋman. An puee Yeesu Kriisitoo pu ní ti lì yii «Ameeni» ki nyɔ́ŋkí Unimbɔti.

eng: God is the one who establishes us with you in Christ and who anointed us.
local: Unimbɔti di báaǹ timbi nì nimbi inyaan Kriisitoo ni. Unimbɔti u-ba di lútì-ti.

eng: God also sealed us and gave the Spirit as a down payment in our hearts.
local: Úǹ di dū u-daaŋku ki ŋá ti-pu, ní ki dū u-Fam ki ŋá ti-pɔbiliŋin kí dàkà-ti yii ti sá u-yab u kpáafì tiwammɔntiil ki yóoǹ kí dāá tīī-ti fool boon.

eng: I call on God as my witness—I didnt come again to Corinth because I wanted to spare you.
local: Unimbɔti pu ní m sòoǹ ibaamɔn. Kun pu maa dīń ni-cee Kɔranti tiŋkinee di sá maa là kí yòon̄ ni-pɔbiliŋ. M yaa mɔ́ntì ú kpɔ̀-mi.

eng: It isnt that we are trying to control your faith, but we are working with you for your happiness, because you stand firm in your faith.
local: Di sá yii taa là kí dū mpɔn kí dàkà-ni ni nín máaǹ kí fōō kí kíí puee. Ama ti là kí jòò-ni ní nīn cáá dipɔɔpindi ní, kun puee ni-fookiiu pɔ̄ɔ̄.

eng: May the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ be blessed! He is the compassionate Father and God of all comfort.
local: Tí nyɔ̄ŋkìmaan Unimbɔti ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo Baa un di sūǹ ti-saai ki ŋmēentí-ti tikɔkɔ poonee.

eng: Hes the one who comforts us in all our trouble so that we can comfort other people who are in every kind of trouble. We offer the same comfort that we ourselves received from God.
local: Falaabi bin kɔkɔ jēetí ti-puee, úǹ di ŋmēentí-ti, án nín làá ŋá pu ti mun ń dū diŋmeendi din ti fòol̄ u-ceeyee kí ŋméeǹ bin mun bí báà falaa un kamaa poonee.

eng: That is because we receive so much comfort through Christ in the same way that we share so many of Christs sufferings.
local: Kii Kriisitoo pu ti nín jīǹ falaa tikpil tikpil puee, míǹ mbaantiim ní u-puee ti tí fòol̄ diŋmeendi tikpil tikpil mun.

eng: So if we have trouble, it is to bring you comfort and salvation. If we are comforted, it is to bring you comfort from the experience of endurance while you go through the same sufferings that we also suffer.
local: Timbi yaa bí ki jīǹ falaayee, an sá nimbi ń ká diŋmeendi nì difiil pu. Ní timbi yaa ká diŋmeendee, an sá yii ni mun ń ká diŋmeendi kí ŋmā kí ŋmèè nì kimɔtii falaabi bin timbi bíi jīǹee jutiibi yaa láa bāań-nee.

eng: Our hope for you is certain, because we know that as you are partners in suffering, so also you are partners in comfort.
local: Ti máká ni-pu mbaamɔmin, kun puee ti nyí yii ni mun bí ki jīǹ falaa kii ti mun nín bí ki jīǹ pu nan ní, an puee ní ni ŋūǹ kí fōō diŋmeendi din bí ki tīī-tee mun.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we dont want you to be unaware of the troubles that we went through in Asia. We were weighed down with a load of suffering that was so far beyond our strength that we were afraid we might not survive.
local: Nnabiyaamu, ti là ní béé falaa un ti jíń Asii tiŋkinee. Falaa gbanti nìn cúú-ti ki lákatì ti-pɔŋŋu pu. Taa nìn gíti máká yii ti làá nīn bí dimaŋfalin.

eng: It certainly seemed to us as if we had gotten the death penalty. This was so that we would have confidence in God, who raises the dead, instead of ourselves.
local: Timbi ti-ba nìn pée ká yii tiba kaa ti bí, see ŋkúm ní ti làá kpú. Tin bí nee nìn bāań ti-pu án nín làá ŋá pu tí taa dū ti-mákal kí páań timbi ti-ba pu, ama tí nīn máká Unimbɔti un fìkitī bitaŋkpiibee pu.

eng: I, Paul, make a personal request to you with the gentleness and kindness of Christ. Im shy when Im with you, but Im bossy when Im away from you!.
local: Man Pɔɔl m-ba, m dū Kriisitoo basintil nì u-niti ki sáŋkí-ni, man un ni lì yii m yaa bí ni-cee m-nyɔkɔ pɔ̀fì, ama ní m yaa bíń ndan, m sòoǹ nì anyɔɔbun ŋin pɔ̄ɔ̄yee.

eng: I know what some people are saying: His letters are severe and powerful, but in person he is weak and his speech is worth nothing.
local: Di sá yii binib lì yii: «Pɔɔl gbɔnti tin u ŋmèe poonee, u sòoǹ ní an pɔ̄ɔ̄ kpaŋkpam, ama ní u yaa bí ti-kansikin ki sòoǹee ní u sá uwɔfi ní u-nyɔkɔ pɔ̀fì.

eng: These people need to think about this—that when we are with you, our actions will show that we are the same as the words we wrote when we were away from you.
local: Bin bin dàkafī an landɔkɔŋuee ń béé yii ti nín yaa bí ndan ti sòoǹ ti-gbɔŋku poon an pɔ̄ɔ̄ miŋyee, míǹ ní ti yaa pée tí bí ni-nimbiinee, ti làá tí sòoǹ.

eng: We wont dare to place ourselves in the same league or to compare ourselves with some of those who are promoting themselves. When they measure themselves by themselves, and compare themselves with themselves, they have no understanding.
local: Taa là kí dū ti-ba kí ŋmàntīǹ kii biba nín pàkà bi-ba min ki cáǹ bi-ba ki ŋmàntīǹ min bi lèe, an sá digbaandi ní.

eng: We wont take pride in anything more than what is appropriate. Lets look at the boundaries of our work area that God has assigned to us. Its an area that includes you.
local: Timbi kaa cáǹ ti-ba laakin taa bàn̄ee. Unimbɔti nín yóoǹ tí tɔ̄ kí gbèen̄ nimbi nyɔkɔmee di sá ti túń ki bàn̄ ni-cee nee.

eng: We arent going out of bounds, as if our work area doesnt extend as far as you. We were the first ones to travel as far as Corinth with the gospel of Christ.
local: Timbi kaa lákatí laakin an sá tí sòoǹ kí gbèen̄ee pu. Míǹ ní taa tí ŋūǹ kí gbèen̄ nsanni kí taa bàń-ni. Míǹ pu ní ti péé ki dɔ́miń ni-cee ki sòon̄ nì ni Kriisitoo Bɔmɔntiiti.

eng: We dont take pride in what other people do outside of our boundaries. We hope that our work will be extended even more by you as your faith grows, until it expands fully .
local: An puee taa lákatí ti-gbeennyɔkɔm pu, taa tí pàkà ti-ba biba tuuŋŋi pu. Ama ti-mákal di sá yii ni-fookiiu yaa bíi wīintée, ti nín túń ditundi ni-cee ki gbēeǹ laakiŋyee, an nìkī kí cù nyɔkɔpu tikpil, ama kí taa nín lákatí ti-kpaanyɔkɔ.

eng: We hope that our work grows even to the point of the gospel being preached in places beyond Corinth, without bragging about what has already been done in another persons work area.
local: Boonee ní ti dāa ŋūǹ kí cáá Tibɔmɔntiilee kí cùnnì ntimu mun bí ni-nyɔkɔpu ubɔ kaa láá cūtèe, kí taa nín̄ fòol̄ atuun ŋin biba túń laan sá bí tɔ̄ee nyooti ki gà ti-biŋŋi.

eng: But, the one who brags should brag in the Lord. .
local: An ŋmàn̄ Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon yii: «Un pàkèe ń pàkà ti-Dindaan.».

eng: It isnt the person who promotes himself or herself who is approved but the person whom the Lord commends.
local: An kaa sá a yaa bí ki pàkà a-bee di dàkà yii a lafun máaǹ kupampakau, see sii un Unimbɔti pàkèe.

eng: I beg you that when Im with you in person, I wont have to boss you around. Im afraid that I may have to use that kind of behavior with those people who think we live by human standards.
local: Tin m mèé-nee di sá, m yaa láa bāań ni-ceeyee, ní taa láa yàntī ḿ tāā kí bīikì ni-kansikin biba pu, kun puee m nyí yii m yaa láa bāań, see ḿ dāá tāā kí bīikì bin lì yii ti-bimbim nì ti-bɔti tin ti dàkèe sá nniyaaminee pu.

eng: Although we live in the world, we dont fight our battles with human methods.
local: Tiŋman ti lafun sá binib, ama dikɔ̀ndi din poon ti bí nee, taa jáà nniyaamin.

eng: Our weapons that we fight with arent human, but instead they are powered by God for the destruction of fortresses. They destroy arguments.
