local: Di sá tijanjawan tin ti cáa jáàyee kaa sá binib nín mɔ́ɔ̀ tiŋyee. Ama an sá mpɔn ŋun nyánní Unimbɔti ceeyee ní, ní ki bíiĺ ipɔn in pu binib mákée. Ní ti cáa kpìtī bi-nyimɔmbɔti.

eng: and every defense that is raised up to oppose the knowledge of God. They capture every thought to make it obedient to Christ.
local: nì alambil ŋin kɔkɔ bi cáá kaa là kí béé Unimbɔtee. An ní ti cáa cútí binib landɔkɔi ki gīintiń-bi bí nīn tɔ́ Kriisitoo.

eng: Once your obedience is complete, we are ready to punish any disobedience.
local: Ti bɔ́ntì ki cí ŋyunti ŋun nimbi yaa láa dū ni-ba kɔkɔ ki tīī Unimbɔtee ní tí dāá dáaĺ bin bin kaa tɔ́ Unimbɔtee tafal.

eng: Look at what is right in front of you! If anyone is sure about belonging to Christ, that person should think again. We belong to Christ just like that person.
local: Ní nīn kpèémaan tiwan ti-pú. Un yaa nyí u-ba pu yii u sá Kriisitoo yɔuee, ú béé tiŋan yii ti mun sá míǹ ní.

eng: Even if I went on to brag about our authority, I wouldnt be ashamed of it. The Lord gave us that authority to build you up and not to destroy you.
local: Ti-Dindaan tīī-ti mpɔn tí dūú tūtuǹ-ni nnimbɔtisan poon, an kaa sá kí ŋá-ni tikpitil. An pu ní m sòoǹ kí gúntí m-ba, ní an kaa cáá-mi icoo!.

eng: I dont want it to seem like Im trying to intimidate you with my letters.
local: Kaa sá m nyàab̀ kí nīn ŋmà tigbɔn ki fàantí-ni.

eng: I hope that you will put up with me while I act like a fool. Well, in fact, you are putting up with me!.
local: M yaa gbíntì ki sòoǹ kii uwaatee, ní jīmaan m-pu kimɔtii, ní lafun jīmaan m-pu kimɔtii!.

eng: Since Christs truth is in me, I wont stop telling the entire area of Greece that Im proud of what I did.
local: M sòoǹ nì ni Kriisitoo baamɔŋyi in bí m-nee ní yii: Akaayi tiŋki kɔkɔ poon ubɔ kpá kí lēē an nyandee m-pu.

eng: Why? Is it because I dont love you? God knows that I do!.
local: Maa néeń-ni naaa, ba pu ní m lī míǹ? Unimbɔti nyí m nín néeń-nee.

eng: But Im going to continue to do what Im doing. I want to contradict the claims of the people who want to be treated like they are the same as us because of what they brag about.
local: Min m ŋáań nee, m làá nīn pée gbíntìi ŋáań míǹ ní, kí kpìtī biŋmatinliibi bin mun là kí nīn pàkà bi-ba ki lì yii bi sá akpambal kii timbee dàkatam.

eng: Such people are false apostles and dishonest workers who disguise themselves as apostles of Christ.
local: Binib gbantee kaa sá akpambal. Bi mɔ́ntí yii bi tūǹ Unimbɔti tundi ní ki gīintí bi-ba Kriisitoo kpambaliŋ yam.

eng: And no wonder! Even Satan disguises himself as an angel of light.
local: Tinee kaa sá maamaaci bɔti, kun puee Sitaan u-ba ŋūǹ kí kpántí ŋwalifi tuuŋŋu.

eng: It is no great surprise then that his servants also disguise themselves as servants of righteousness. Their end will be what their actions deserve.
local: An puee an kaa sá atulim bɔti yii u-tɔntɔnliibi mun ŋūǹ kí gíiǹ bi-ba kii bitɔntɔnliibi bin bí deedeeyee. Unimbɔti làá dáaĺ bi-tafal bi-tuuŋŋi nín sá miŋyee ní.

eng: I repeat, no one should take me for a fool. But if you do, then allow me to be a fool so that I can brag like a fool for a bit.
local: M gíti bùsī ki tùkù-ni yii ní taa dàkafì yii m sá uwaati. Ni yaa tí dàkafī mimmaaa, ní jī m-pu kimɔtii an pu, kí yàntī ma mun ḿ pàkà m-ba fiii.

eng: Im not saying what Im saying because the Lord tells me to. Im saying it like Im a fool. Im putting my confidence in this business of bragging.
local: M nín bíi lì pu nee, an kaa sá ti-Dindaan di bí yii ḿ nīn sòoǹ ki pàkà m-ba míǹ. M bíi sòoǹ kii unil un yil lábitèe ní. Kun puee m cáá tin pu ní m làá sòoǹ kí pàkà m-bee.

eng: Since so many people are bragging based on human standards, that is how Im going to brag too.
local: Binib bí ki pàkà bi-ba duulinyan doo wanti pu, an pu ní ma mun bíi pàkà m-ba.

eng: Because you, who are so wise, are happy to put up with fools.
local: Nimbi di bí yii ni cáá iciin, ama ní ki jīǹ kimɔtii biwaatiibi pu an mɔ̄ǹ-ni.

eng: Im deeply concerned about you with the same concern that God has. As your father, I promised you in marriage to one husband. I promised to present you as an innocent virgin to Christ himself.
local: M néeń-ni kaa mɔ̄ ni-pu, ní dineendi gbanti nyánní unimbɔti cee. Di sá yii m bɔ́ntì-ni kí dū-ni kí tīī uninjabaanti ní: úǹ di sá Kriisitoo. Tiŋman, úǹ ní m làá dū-ni kí tīī kii usapɔɔn un kaa nyí uninjee.

eng: You put up with it if someone enslaves you, if someone exploits you, if someone takes advantage of you, if someone places themselves over you, or if someone hits you in the face.
local: Bi cáá-ni ki sàantí kii iyumbuee, ki jīǹ ni-pu, ki kíiĺ-ni, ki kpèé-ni fám, ki fátí-ni itampa, ní ni jīǹ bi-pu kimɔtii.

eng: Im ashamed to say that we have been weak in comparison! But in whatever they challenge me, I challenge them .
local: An sá-ti icoo ní yii timbi kaa cáá mpɔn kí ŋmā kí ŋá-ni míǹ. Ubɔ yaa ŋūǹ kí pàkà u-ba tiba puee, ma mun ŋūǹ kí pàkà m-ba. M bíi sòoǹ ŋwaatiyaamin dee.

eng: Are they Hebrews? So am I. Are they Israelites? So am I. Are they descendants of Abraham? So am I.
local: Di sá an nibee yaa dàkà bi-ba yii bi sá Eebru yabaaa, ma mun sá ueebruja ní, bi yaa bí yii bi sá Isirayeel yabaaa, ma mun sá Uisirayeelja ní. Bi yaa bí yii bi sá Abraam naantiibaaa, ma mun sá Abraam naantiiu ní.

eng: Are they ministers of Christ? Im speaking like a crazy person. What Ive done goes well beyond what theyve done. Ive worked much harder. Ive been imprisoned much more often. Ive been beaten more times than I can count. Ive faced death many times.
local: Bi yaa bí yii bi sá Kriisitoo tɔntɔnliibaaa, man di nín sá ki jítiǹ-bi, m bíi sòoǹ ŋwaatiyaamin dee. M túń ditundi ki jítiǹ-bi. Bi pìintí-mi kudii múlmul ki jítiǹ-bi. Bi gbáń-mi ki jítiǹ-bi, m jēetí nnaabu ŋun sá ŋkúmee ni múlmul ki jítiǹ-bi.

eng: I received the forty lashes minus one from the Jews five times.
local: Juuda yab gbáń-mi anaalab diba kpá imuŋku ili ŋfunyɔm miŋmoo pu.

eng: I was beaten with rods three times. I was stoned once. I was shipwrecked three times. I spent a day and a night on the open sea.
local: Room yab gbáń-mi ndɔ ŋfunyɔm mita pu, bi yáŋkì-mi ataŋkpee kí bàà kpɔ̀-mi diba pu, ŋfunyɔm mita pu ní iŋaliŋfi múĺ nnyimin nnyim bàà làá yòon̄-mi, ki gbíntì dalba nnyimin ŋwiimpu nì kunyeeu.

eng: Ive been on many journeys. I faced dangers from rivers, robbers, my people, and Gentiles. I faced dangers in the city, in the desert, on the sea, and from false brothers and sisters.
local: M-saŋkiŋŋi ŋin m cōom̀ tikpilee poonee, m kántì dinimbinaamandi inyiŋgbeenni, ki kántì dinimbinaamandi digaŋgam dambi cee, ki kántì dinimbinaamandi m-jutiibi Juuda yab nì bin kaa sá Juuda yabee cee, ki kántì dinimbinaamandi ntimu poon nì kuteeun. M kántì dinimbinaamandi tiŋku pu, nì bin mɔ́ntí yii bi sá Kriisitoo yabee cee.

eng: I faced these dangers with hard work and heavy labor, many sleepless nights, hunger and thirst, often without food, and in the cold without enough clothes.
local: M túń atuun ki gīī, ki ká dinimbinaamandi ki jíń falaa, kaa gèeǹ. Ŋkùm nì nnyinnyoo cúú-mi, m gbíntí ŋkùm múlmul, tiwool cútí-mi tiwampeeŋkaal kpá ḿ pèen̄.

eng: Besides all the other things I could mention, theres my daily stress because Im concerned about all the churches.
local: Falaabi tikpil kíǹ maa lī. Baabadalee Kriisitoo nikitiŋ kɔkɔ bɔti sá-mi diwaŋgul ní ki nyìm̀ m-pu.

eng: Who is weak without me being weak? Who is led astray without me being furious about it?.
local: Un kaa pɔ̄ɔ̄yee puee, see ma mun ń nīn bí kii úŋ̀wuee kí taa nīn pɔ̄ɔ̄. Ubɔ yaa lítì ikpitinee, man ní an wù pɔ̀lpɔl.

eng: But Im afraid that your minds might be seduced in the same way as the snake deceived Eve with his devious tricks. You might be unable to focus completely on a genuine and innocent commitment to Christ.
local: Ama kii ukoobu nín nìn kíil̀ Awa puee, míǹ ní m fàŋkí ubɔ ń taa láa dāá cúú ni-landɔkɔi kí lábití kí yàntī ní fá Kriisitoo un ni nóó ibaamɔn nì dipɔbil dibee.

eng: If its necessary to brag, Ill brag about my weaknesses.
local: An yaa sá ḿ pàkà m-bee, m-wɔfii pu ní m bàà pàkà m-ba.

eng: The God and Father of the Lord Jesus, the one who is blessed forever, knows that Im not lying.
local: Unimbɔti ti-Dindaan Yeesu Baa yindi ń ká inyɔŋki kí nīn pée cá, di sá u nyí yii tin m sòoǹ nee maa mɔ́ntí.

eng: At Damascus the governor under King Aretas was guarding the city of Damascus in order to capture me.
local: M nín nìn bí Damaasi tiŋkinee, gɔmina un nìn bí ubɔti Areetaasi tundinee nìn yāntì u-soojab yúl̀ kitiŋ gundi punyɔmmu caŋin kí bàà cúú-mi.

eng: but I got away from him by being lowered in a basket through a window in the city wall.
local: ní m-bɔɔbi dū-mi ki ŋá kukabikun ki dūu nāatì nì ŋŋmii digundi tɔkɔlau pu, ní m ŋmátì.

eng: If a person comes and preaches some other Jesus than the one we preached, or if you receive a different Spirit than the one you had received, or a different gospel than the one you embraced, you put up with it so easily!.
local: Kun puee ni jīǹ kimɔtii ki fòol̄ bin dòoń ki sòoǹ nì ni Yeesu ubɔ pu, kaa sá un timbi sòon̄ nì ni nee ní. Míǹ mbaantiim ní ni fòol̄ ŋfam miba min kaa sá Unimbɔti yam min ni fōōyee, ní ki tí fòol̄ tibɔcan tiba mun ki kíikí.

eng: I dont consider myself as second-rate in any way compared to the super-apostles.
local: M pàkā yii bíǹ bin ni yíì akpambamɔntee kaa jítiǹ-mi.

eng: But even if Im uneducated in public speaking, Im not uneducated in knowledge. We have shown this to you in every way and in everything we have done.
local: Nì min kɔkɔ ní maa ŋūǹ kí sòoǹee, m nyí Unimbɔti bɔti. Ti dàkā-ni míǹ ŋfunyɔm kupaau nimbi ni-ba ká míǹ.

eng: Did I commit a sin by humbling myself to give you an advantage because I preached the gospel of God to you free of charge?.
local: Mimmee, m nín cáań Unimbɔti bɔti ki sòon̄ ni-cee faalaa ki sīntì m-ba taapu nimbi ń yīkì yilpuee, m yīntàaa?.

eng: I robbed other churches by taking a salary from them in order to serve you!.
local: M nín dɔ́miń ni-cee ki sòon̄ Unimbɔti bɔtee, Kriisitoo nikitiŋ aba di nìn yì m-paati. M nìn fúkuĺ bíǹ ní ki cáa tútúǹ nimbi.

eng: While I was with you, I didnt burden any of you even though I needed things. The believers who came from Macedonia gave me everything I needed. I kept myself from being a financial drain on you in any way, and I will continue to keep myself from being a burden.
local: M nín nìn bí ni-ceeyee, maa nìn báká ubɔ pu. Animbil nín nìn lóò-mee, nnabiyaamu mun nìn nyánní Maseeduwaanee di nìn cáań-mi tin kɔkɔ nìn lóò-mee. M kpēē m-ba kaa nìn báká ubɔ pu báà tiba poon, ní ki làá nīn pée gbíntìi kpèé m-ba míǹ kí nīn cá.

eng: It is necessary to brag, not that it does any good. Ill move on to visions and revelations from the Lord.
local: Míǹ puee ḿ nīn lámintáa! An kaa tútúǹ-ni. M làá tùkù-ni tin ti-Dindaan lēē ki dàkā-mi ki yāntì m béèyee.

eng: Therefore, Im all right with weaknesses, insults, disasters, harassments, and stressful situations for the sake of Christ, because when Im weak, then Im strong.
local: An pu ní m-wɔfii bɔti mɔ̄ǹ-mi. Bi nín sìí-mi puee, nì falaa un m jīǹee, nì bi nín páb̀-mi puee, nì dinimbinaamandi din m kāntée, Kriisitoo pu ní tíǹ kɔkɔ bàń m-pu míǹ, kun puee iwɔfi yaa kɔ́-mee, míǹ ní m kāntí mpɔn.

eng: Ive become a fool! You made me do it. Actually, I should have been commended by you. Im not inferior to the super-apostles in any way, even though Im a nonentity.
local: M yaa bíi sòoǹ kii uwaatee, nimbi di yāntì m sòoǹ míǹ, di sá yii nimbi di bàà máaǹ kí sòoǹ kí mɔ̄tì m-pu. Báà m yaa kaa bí tibanaaa, ni-kpambamɔntiŋee kaa jítiǹ-mi tiba nì tiba poon.

eng: The signs of an apostle were performed among you with continuous endurance through signs, wonders, and miracles.
local: Maamaacib nì tidaandi tin Unimbɔti pɔŋŋu yāntì m ŋá nì kimɔtii tikɔkɔ poon ni-ceeyee dàkà yii m sá Yeesu kpambal.

eng: How were you treated worse than the other churches, except that I myself wasnt a financial burden on you? Forgive me for this wrong!.
local: Ba ní m ŋá ki tīī Kriisitoo nikitiŋ ŋin kíǹee ní kaa ŋá ki tīī nimbi. Tin baba maa yāntì ní ŋá kí tīī-mee di sá ni nín kaa kpíiǹ-mee, an puee ní dū m-yìntitam kí pɔ̄-mi!.

eng: Look, Im ready to visit you a third time, and I wont be a burden on you. I dont want your things; I want you. It isnt the childrens responsibility to save up for their parents but parents for children.
local: M tí bɔ́ntì kí dāan̄ ni-cee ntataatiim. Nimbi di kaa làá nīn yì m-jītam, di sá yii nimbi ní m là, kaa sá ni-nimbiliŋ. Kun puee an kaa sá mbiyaamu di kpīintí bibaatiibi, ama bibaatiibi di kpīintí mbiyaamu.

eng: I will very gladly spend and be spent for your sake. If I love you more, will you love me less?.
local: Mamee, an mɔ̄ǹ-mi ḿ dū tin kɔkɔ m cááyee kí kúntí ni-pu kí kpáaǹ m-ba kí tūtuǹ-ni. Man yaa néeń nimbi míǹ tikpilee, nimbi kaa làá nīn néeń-maaa?.

eng: We all know that I didnt place a burden on you, but in spite of that you think Im a con artist who fooled you with a trick.
local: Nimbi ni-ba, ni làá ká yii maa sá digìńdi ki tīī-ni, ama biba lì yii an sá nciin ní m ŋá kí ŋmàtiǹ-ni kí jī.

eng: I havent taken advantage of you through any of the people I sent to you, have I?.
local: M dī bin m túnní ni-ceeyee ubɔ pu ki jíń-ni tibaaa?.

eng: I strongly encouraged Titus to go to you and sent the brother with him. Titus didnt take advantage of you, did he? Didnt we live by the same Spirit? Didnt we walk in the same footsteps?.
local: Man nín dū Tiiti nì kinabiki kiba ki tú bí cù ni-ceeyee, bi jíń ni-wanti tibaaa? Man nì Tiiti landɔkɔŋu sá mbaanti ní, ní ti-ŋáaǹtam mun mbaantiim.

eng: Have you been thinking up to now that we are defending ourselves to you? Actually, we are speaking in the sight of God and in Christ. Dear friends, everything is meant to build you up.
local: An yaa yíì ni pée kíĺ ki dàkafī dooooyee yii ti là ní nīn yíì-ti binimɔntiibee pu ní m sòoǹ míǹ? Aayee, kaa sá míǹ. Ti sòoǹ min Kriisitoo lèe ní Unimbɔti nimbiin. Nnabineeŋkaamu, tin kɔkɔ ti sòoǹ nì nee, an sá kí tūtuǹ-ni ní cù nyɔkɔpu nnimbɔtisan poon ní.

eng: I know a man in Christ who was caught up into the third heaven fourteen years ago. I dont know whether it was in the body or out of the body. God knows.
local: M nyí kijapaai kiba, u nóó Kriisitoo. An nìn yōoǹ-u ki cáa jɔ́m̀ kutagbɔŋu yilpu yilpu fɔ́ŋŋŋ Unimbɔti cee dibindi diba kpá saalku sèé.

eng: Im afraid that maybe when I come you will be different from the way I want you to be, and that Ill be different from the way you want me to be. Im afraid that there might be fighting, obsession, losing your temper, competitive opposition, backstabbing, gossip, conceit, and disorderly conduct.
local: M fàŋkí yii ḿ taa láa bàń ni-cee kí ká-ni min poon maa bàà lèe, ni mun ń ká man min poon naa bàà lèe. Maa là kí bàń kí ká-ni kujaau nì kináŋ nì diŋuul nì timɔn nì asiibil poon. Maa tí là kí bàń kí ká ni sòoǹ fám fám nì dilambil ki bí fìkil fikil poon.

eng: Im afraid that when I come again, my God may embarrass me in front of you. I might have to go into mourning over all the people who have sinned before and havent changed their hearts and lives from what they used to practice: moral corruption, sexual immorality, and doing whatever feels good.
local: M fàŋkí yii m-dāantam ni-ceeyee, m-Nimbɔtiu ń taa sìntīǹ-mi taapu ni-cee ḿ sɔ̄ bin nìn ŋá ikpiti ki gbíntì ki ŋúb́ bi-bimbikpitim, tijɔkɔndi nì tilaakpaalee pu.

eng: I know that this man was caught up into paradise and that he heard unspeakable words that were things no one is allowed to repeat. I dont know whether it was in the body or apart from the body. God knows.
local: Tiŋman m nyí yii kijapaayee nìn jɔ́m̀ áli Unimbɔti Beelin.

eng: I know that this man was caught up into paradise and that he heard unspeakable words that were things no one is allowed to repeat. I dont know whether it was in the body or apart from the body. God knows.
local: Niin ní u gbìl̄ tibɔti tin unil kaa ŋūǹ kí fàtīí gíiǹ-tì kí lèe, kaa máaǹ kí dū-tì kí tùkù ubɔ muee.

eng: Ill brag about this man, but I wont brag about myself, except to brag about my weaknesses.
local: An japaakee pu ní m máaǹ kí nīn lámintí, kaa sá man m-ba pu. M yaa làá tí lāmintàaa, see m-wɔfii pu.

eng: If I did want to brag, I wouldnt make a fool of myself because Id tell the truth. Im holding back from bragging so that no one will give me any more credit than what anyone sees or hears about me.
local: M yaa bàà là kí pàkà m-bee, maa bàà sámaan uwaati, m bàà sòoǹ ibaamɔn ní. Ama maa là kí pàkà m-ba, kun puee maa là binib ń dàkafì m-pu ŋkpaamin bi nín wāĺ m ŋáań tiŋyee nì tin bi gbìl̄ m sòoǹee pu.

eng: I was given a thorn in my body because of the outstanding revelations Ive received so that I wouldnt be conceited. Its a messenger from Satan sent to torment me so that I wouldnt be conceited.
local: Kí wàatī ḿ taa dū tin Unimbɔti yāntì m béè an kaa bí dimaafilee kí ŋá dilambilee, ní u dū iween iba ní ki ŋá m-wunti pu. Iweenee sá kii Sitaan tuuŋŋuee ní ki gbáà-mi, án nín làá ŋá pu ḿ taa nín̄ cáá dilambilee.

eng: I pleaded with the Lord three times for it to leave me alone.
local: Ŋfunyɔm mita ní m mēē ti-Dindaan iween gbanti pu, yii ú lēē-i m-pu.

eng: He said to me, My grace is enough for you, because power is made perfect in weakness. So Ill gladly spend my time bragging about my weaknesses so that Christs power can rest on me.
local: Ní u kíí-mi yii: «M-ŋanti tin m ŋáań-see ŋmāntì-si, kun puee sii wɔfii pu ní man dì ki dàkà m-pɔŋŋu.» Míǹ puee an mɔ̄ǹ-mi ḿ nīn pàkà m-ba m-wɔfii pu, án nín làá ŋá pu Kriisitoo pɔŋŋu ń nīn tūǹ m-pu ditundee.

eng: This is the third time that Im coming to visit you. Every matter is settled on the evidence of two or three witnesses.
local: Ntataatiim ní m dòoń ni-cee nee. An ŋmàn̄ Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon yii: «Binib bili kii bita seerau di cáá mbaamɔn.».

eng: This is why Im writing these things while Im away. Im writing so that I wont need to act harshly when Im with you by using the authority that the Lord gave me. He gave it to me so that I could build you up, not tear you down.
local: Kun pu m bí ndan ki ŋmà-nee di sá maa là ḿ dāá bàń ni-cee kí dū mpɔn ŋun ti-Dindaan tīī-mee kí wɔ́b́-ni. Mpɔn gbantee sá ḿ dūú tūtuǹ-ni ni-nimbɔtinóotam ń cù nyɔkɔpu ní, an kaa sá kí dūú nàan̄-ni.

eng: Finally, brothers and sisters, good-bye. Put things in order, respond to my encouragement, be in harmony with each other, and live in peace—and the God of love and peace will be with you.
local: Nnabiyaamu, tin m làá dū kí kàan̄ nee sèé: ní nīn cáámaan dipɔɔpindi, ni-nimbɔtinóotam ń nīn sá tiŋman pu yal kí nīn tìì tɔb kunyannyaau, kí nīn pēē kinyɔkɔ nì ŋgbansɔŋfi poon. Unimbɔti un sá dineendi nì ŋgbansɔŋfi daanee làá nīn bí ni-cee.

eng: Say hello to each other with a holy kiss. All of Gods people say hello to you.
local: Ní jāam̀maan tɔb kí kpàl̄ tɔb nnabiyaamin. Unimbɔti nikitil din bí dooyee jáaḿ-ni.

eng: The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ, the love of God, and the fellowship of the Holy Spirit be with you all.
local: Ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo ŋanti nì Unimbɔti neendi nì u-Fam nín cāabìń nnyɔɔpee ŋuŋyee ń nīn bí ni-kɔkɔ cee.

eng: When I was with you on my second visit, I already warned those who continued to sin. Now Im repeating that warning to all the rest of you while Im at a safe distance: if I come again, I wont spare anyone.
local: M cáá tibɔti kí kpāafì bin bin nìn ŋá ikpitee nì bin kɔkɔ kíǹee. M nín nìn dɔ́miń ni-cee nliliitiimee, m nìn lī-tì ní ki gíti bíi bùsī ki lì díǹ m-kpátam ni-cee. M nín làá dāan̄ min nee, m làá ŋá an nín máaǹ puee ní, maa làá kpèè ubɔ nimbiin.

eng: Since you are demanding proof that Christ speaks through me, Christ isnt weak in dealing with you but shows his power among you.
local: Kii ni nín là kí ká kudaaŋŋu yii Kriisitoo di dì m-pu ki sòoǹee, ni làá lafun ká. Kriisitoo dàkà u-pɔŋŋu ni-kansikin, ama waa sá kiwɔfii.

eng: Certainly he was crucified because of weakness, but he lives by the power of God. Certainly we also are weak in him, but we will live together with him, because of Gods power that is directed toward you.
local: Tiŋman, u dū u-ba ki ŋá kii uwɔfee ní bi kpáá-u ndɔpuntikaa pu, ama Unimbɔti pɔŋŋu yāntì u bí dimaŋfalin. Ti nì u nín ŋá ubaantee puee, ti mun bí iwɔfin ní, ama u-puee ti cáá dimaŋfal. Nì Unimbɔti pɔŋŋu puee ti bí dimaŋfalin ki tīī-ni.

eng: Examine yourselves to see if you are in the faith. Test yourselves. Dont you understand that Jesus Christ is in you? Unless, of course, you fail the test.
local: Ní cɔ̄ŋkìmaan ni-ba kí kpèè ni yaa lafun fōō Yeesu Kriisitoo ki kíí tiŋman. Ní kpèè ni-ban tiŋan, naa ká yii u bí ni-naaa? U bí ni-ni, asee ni nín cɔ́ŋkì ki kpēēyee, ni yaa ká yii naa fōō-u ki kíí baba.

eng: But I hope that you will realize that we dont fail the test.
local: Míǹ ní ni làá béé yii ti mun cɔ́ŋkì ti-ba ní ki béè yii ti sá Kriisitoo tɔntɔnliibi tiŋman.

eng: We pray to God that you dont do anything wrong, not because we want to appear to pass the test but so that you might do the right thing, even if we appear to fail.
local: Ti mèé Unimbɔti ni-pu ní taa ŋá ikpiti iba. An kaa sá kí dàkà ti-ba yii ti túń ditummɔntiil, ama ti là ní nīn ŋáań tiŋan báà bi yaa bíi sòoǹ ki bíiĺ timbi.

eng: We cant do anything against the truth but only to help the truth.
local: Kun puee taa cáá mpɔn kí dūú já nì ibaamɔn, ti cáá mpɔn kí dūú tūtuǹ ibaamɔn ní.

eng: We are happy when we are weak but you are strong. We pray for this: that you will be made complete.
local: An mɔ̄ǹ-ti ŋyunti kamaa timbi yaa bí iwɔfin, nimbi ń nīn pɔ̄ɔ̄yee pu. Ti mèé Unimbɔti ni-pu yii ní nīn sá binimbɔtinooliibi tiŋman pu yab.

eng: So I decided that, for my own sake, I wouldnt visit you again while I was upset.
local: An pu ní m ká yii an ŋān̄ ḿ taa dāan̄ ni-cee kí fàtīí tí kɔ́ǹ-ni kunimbaau.

eng: If you forgive anyone for anything, I do too. And whatever Ive forgiven , I did it for you in the presence of Christ.
local: Ni yaa dū unil un kpitii ki pú-uee, ma mun làá dūú pɔ̄-u. Tiba yaa bí ki sá ḿ dūú pɔ̄ udaanee, nimbi puee, m dūu pú-u Kriisitoo nimbiin.

eng: This is so that we wont be taken advantage of by Satan, because we are well aware of his schemes.
local: án nín làá ŋá pu tí taa yàntī Sitaan ń jī ti-pu dinyandee, kun puee ti nyí u-landɔkɔŋu.

eng: When I came to Troas to preach Christs gospel, the Lord gave me an opportunity to preach.
local: M nín bàn̄ Troowaasi tiŋkin kí sòoǹ Kriisitoo bɔmɔntiitee, m nìn ká yii ti-Dindaan pīitì-mi nsan ḿ sòoǹ-tì.

eng: But I was worried because I couldnt find my brother Titus there. So I said good-bye to them and went on to Macedonia.
local: Ama m nín kaa ká m-nabiki Tiiti niinee, m-pɔbil kaa nìn sɔ̀m̀ ḿ kàl̄ niin. An pu ní m yíkì bi-cee ki búntì Maseeduwaan.

eng: But thank God, who is always leading us around through Christ as if we were in a parade. He releases the fragrance of the knowledge of him everywhere through us.
local: Tí jāam̀ Unimbɔti Kriisitoo pu, u nín cáá ti mun ki ŋáań u-jajil faalii poon puee. Ti nì Kriisitoo nín ŋá ubaantee puee, u dì ti-pu ki gbìtī ajan kɔkɔ Kriisitoo bɔti kii talaaluee.

eng: We smell like the aroma of Christs offering to God, both to those who are being saved and to those who are on the road to destruction.
local: Ti sá kii talaalu un sɔ́fèe ní Kriisitoo cáa tìì Unimbɔti. Míǹ ní ti tí sá ki tīī binib bin ká difiilee nì bin mun wáŋkèe.

eng: We smell like a contagious dead person to those who are dying, but we smell like the fountain of life to those who are being saved. Who is qualified for this kind of ministry?.
local: Bin wáŋkèe ceeyee, an sá ŋkúm nunuŋu ŋun cáǹ ŋkúminee, ní bin ká difiilee ceeyee, an sá dimaŋfal sɔnsɔfiŋu ŋun tìì dimaŋfalee. Ŋma di bàn̄ kí tɔ̄ an tundi gbanti?.

eng: We arent like so many people who hustle the word of God to make a profit. We are speaking through Christ in the presence of God, as those who are sincere and as those who are sent from God.
local: Timbi kaa sá kii binib tikpil bin dū Unimbɔti bɔti ki kpántì dibàntilee, ti sá ibaamɔn nib ní, Unimbɔti di lēē-ti ki tú. Ti nín ŋá ubaanti nì Kriisitooyee pu ní ti sòoǹ Unimbɔti nimbiin.

eng: If I make you sad, who will be there to make me glad when you are sad because of me?.
local: Kun puee nimbi di tìì-mi kunyannyaau. Kin m yaa kɔ́ǹ-ni kunimbaauee, bilab di làá gíti tīī-mi dipɔɔpindi?.

eng: Thats why I wrote this very thing to you, so that when I came I wouldnt be made sad by the ones who ought to make me happy. I have confidence in you, that my happiness means your happiness.
local: Kun yì-tì m ŋmàn̄-ni kugbɔŋuee di sá maa là kí bàń kí ká bin bàà làá tīī-mi dipɔɔpindee ń kɔ́ǹ-mi kunimbaaun, di sá m nyí yii man pɔɔpindi sá nimbi yal, ní ni mun pɔɔpindi sá man yal.

eng: I wrote to you in tears, with a very troubled and anxious heart. I didnt write to make you sad but so you would know the overwhelming love that I have for you.
local: An nìn sá-mi kunimbaau nì dipɔɔbiildi nì nsiim ní m ŋmàn̄-ni kugbɔŋu. An kaa nìn sá kí kɔ́ǹ-ni kunimbaau pu ní m ŋmàn̄-ni, ama an nìn sá ní ŋmā kí béé dineendi din bulifi m cáá ni-puee ní.

eng: But if someone has made anyone sad, that person hasnt hurt me but all of you to some degree .
local: Unil un di yaa cáań kunimbaau neeyee, an kaa sá man baba ní u kɔ́ǹ kunimbaau, u kɔ́ǹ ki kpáaǹ ni-kɔkɔ ní. Ama kí taa sòoǹ kí càatēe, ni-kansikin biba ní u kɔ́ǹ kunimbaau.

eng: The punishment handed out by the majority is enough for this person.
local: Ni-kansikin kupaau nín kpáfì kinyɔkɔ ki dáaĺ udaan tafal miŋyee ŋmāntì.

eng: This is why you should try your best to forgive and to comfort this person now instead, so that this person isnt overwhelmed by too much sorrow.
local: Kɔŋkɔnnee ni máaǹ kí dūú pɔ̄-u kí tīī-u kunyannyaau ú taa gíti kɔ́ kunimbaakpaŋkpaaŋŋun.

eng: So I encourage you to show your love for this person.
local: An puee m gbāam̀-ni, ní dàkàmaan ni-neendi udaan pu.

eng: This is another reason why I wrote you. I wanted to test you and see if you are obedient in everything.
local: M nìn ŋmàn̄-ni tibɔtee kí cɔ̄ŋkì-ni kí kpèè yii ni tɔ́-mi tiŋman puɔɔɔ!.

eng: Are we starting to commend ourselves again? We dont need letters of introduction to you or from you like other people, do we?.
local: Mimmee ti fātìi tí bíi dàkà ti-ba yii ti ŋá tiba deeyaaa? Àá an bàlifī yii tí cáań tigbɔn tin bi ŋmàn̄ ki pàkā-tee kí dàkà-ni naaa? Àá an sá yii tí bàlifì-ni ní ŋmà kugbɔŋu kí pàkà-ti ní, a lì kii biba nín ŋáań puee?.

eng: In fact, what was glorious isnt glorious now, because of the glory that is brighter.
local: Taa ŋūǹ kí dū tinyool tin nìn jítèe kí ŋmàntīǹ tin bí nee báà fiii. Kun puee, tíǹee sá tikpaandi ní.

eng: If the glory that fades away was glorious, how much more glorious is the one that lasts!.
local: Tin bí ki làá jītée di yaa cáá tinyool mimmee, kin tin làá nīn pée bí kí nīn cée fáà!.

eng: So, since we have such a hope, we act with great confidence.
local: Ti nín cɔ́tí tin pu tikpilee dee. An pu ní ti cáá dipɔbil ki sòoǹ ki cántí tikɔkɔ.

eng: We arent like Moses, who used to put a veil over his face so that the Israelites couldnt watch the end of what was fading away.
local: Timbi kaa làá ŋá kii Mooyiisi nín nìn dū ŋŋaabu ki bīiǹ u-nimbiin Isirayeel yab ń taa kpèè-u kí nīn cí ŋyunti ŋun ní ŋwalifee làá dāá lēēyee.

eng: But their minds were closed. Right up to the present day the same veil remains when the old covenant is read. The veil is not removed because it is taken away by Christ.
local: Ama bi-landɔkɔi kaa cútí tibɔti malaa, a lì kii ŋŋaabuee dáá gbíntì ki lìkī bi-nimbiinee. Di sá yii bi yaa kàaǹ Dipooŋkpikil gbɔŋku poonee, baa gbìl̀ taapu, áli ki dāa sāā díǹee, bi-landɔkɔi kaa cútí tibɔti. See unil un fōō Kriisitoo ki kííyee baba nimbiin ní ŋŋaabu bìitī.

eng: Even today, whenever Moses is read, a veil lies over their hearts.
local: Áli ki dāa sāā díǹ, bi yaa bíi kàaǹ Mooyiisi gbɔntee, an bí kii ŋŋaabuee lìkī bi-landɔkɔi ní baa gbìl̀ taapuee.

eng: But whenever someone turns back to the Lord, the veil is removed.
local: Unil un yaa fōō ti-Dindaan ki kííyee nimbiin ní ŋŋaabuee lèekī.

eng: The Lord is the Spirit, and where the Lords Spirit is, there is freedom.
local: Di sá yii ti-Dindaanee, Ŋfam dee. Laakin ti-Dindaan Fam yaa bée, niin ní dibatɔl bí.

eng: All of us are looking with unveiled faces at the glory of the Lord as if we were looking in a mirror. We are being transformed into that same image from one degree of glory to the next degree of glory. This comes from the Lord, who is the Spirit.
local: Timbi bin kɔkɔ nimbiin ŋŋaabuee lēēyee di sá bin pu ní bi wāĺ ti-Dindaan nyootee. Ti-Dindaan un di sá Ŋfamee di kpántí-ti míǹ, ní ti náań nì u, ní ki cáá tinyool tin wīintí ki cée.

eng: You are our letter, written on our hearts, known and read by everyone.
local: Nimbi Kɔranti yab di sá kugbɔŋu kun báà ŋma wāĺ ki kàaǹee, ní kugbɔŋuee ŋmàn̄ ti-pɔbiliŋin.

eng: You show that you are Christs letter, delivered by us. You werent written with ink but with the Spirit of the living God. You werent written on tablets of stone but on tablets of human hearts.
local: Báà ŋma wāĺ yii ni sá kugbɔŋu kun Kriisitoo dī ti-pu ki ŋmàn̄ee ní. Waa ŋmàn̄ nì ŋgbaŋŋmamɔɔn. Ama Unimbɔti un bí dimaŋfalinee Fam di ŋmàn̄. Waa ŋmàn̄ ataŋkpee pu, u ŋmàn̄ binib pɔbiliŋin ní.

eng: This is the confidence that we have through Christ in the presence of God.
local: Kriisitoo puee dimákal din ti cáá Unimbɔti puee dee.

eng: It isnt that we ourselves are qualified to claim that anything came from us. No, our qualification is from God.
local: Di sá taa bí yii ti ŋūǹ kí ŋá tiba ti-ba pɔŋŋu pu. Tin ti yaa ŋúń ki ŋée, an sá Unimbɔti pɔŋŋu di cāntì ti ŋúń ki ŋá.

eng: He has qualified us as ministers of a new covenant, not based on what is written but on the Spirit, because what is written kills, but the Spirit gives life.
local: Kun puee úǹ di tìì-ti ŋŋmam ní ti sòoǹ dipoompɔndi din u ŋée Bɔmɔntiiti. Dipoondi gbanti kaa sá mara un nìn sá nì tiŋmaŋmabitee, ama dì sá nì Unimbɔti Fam tundi ní. Di sá mara kù ní, ama Unimbɔti Fam míǹ tìì binib dimaŋfal ní.

eng: The ministry that brought death was carved in letters on stone tablets. It came with such glory that the Israelites couldnt look for long at Moses face because his face was shining with glory, even though it was a fading glory.
local: Unimbɔti nín nìn ŋmàn̄ u-marab ataŋkpee puee, bi nìn ká u-nyooti. Ní Mooyiisi mun nimbiin nìn kíǹ ki jìl̀, ní Isirayeel yab kaa nìn gíti ŋūǹ kí kpèè-u. An nyootee sá tin jītée ní. Marab bin bi yaa yīntì an cāabìń ŋkúmee yaa nìn dɔ́miń nì kunyaaŋŋu míǹ.

eng: Wont the ministry of the Spirit be much more glorious?.
local: kin ataa aŋa pu ní Unimbɔti Fam min tìì dimaŋfalee nyooti kaa máaǹ ki jítiǹ míǹ.

eng: If the ministry that brought condemnation has glory, how much more glorious is the ministry that brings righteousness?.
local: An yaa sá yii marab bin làá cáań ŋkúmee pu ní Unimbɔti nyooti dɔ́miń ki jìl̀ mimmee, mana ní Unimbɔti Fam tundi din làá yàntī binib ń kpántí deedeedambi Unimbɔti nimbiinee nyooti làá nīn jìl̀ dee?.

eng: This is why we dont get discouraged, given that we received this ministry in the same way that we received Gods mercy.
local: An sá tiŋan ní Unimbɔti ŋá-ti ní ki dū ditundi nee ki ŋúbiǹ-ti. An pu ní ti-wunti kaa yɔ̀tī di-pu.

eng: We always carry Jesus death around in our bodies so that Jesus life can also be seen in our bodies.
local: Baabadalee bi dī-ti kí kpɔ̀-ti kii bi nín nìn dī Yeesu puee, án nín làá ŋá pu bí ká dimaŋfal din Yeesu cááyee ti mun nee.

eng: We who are alive are always being handed over to death for Jesus sake so that Jesus life can also be seen in our bodies that are dying.
local: Yeesu puee, ŋyunti kamaayee ŋkúm pée kpàkaǹ-ti ní. Báà ti yaa jíń falaa ní ki dāa kpíì, binib làá béé yii Yeesu bí ti-ni.

eng: So death is at work in us, but life is at work in you.
local: Timbi bí ŋkúmin, ama ní ki cāabìń nimbi dimaŋfal.

eng: We have the same faithful spirit as what is written in scripture: I had faith, and so I spoke. We also have faith, and so we also speak.
local: A lì kii Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon nín lī puee yii: «M fōō ki kíí, an pu ní m sòon̄.» Nlandɔkɔ gbanti mbaanti ní ti mun cáá, ti mun fōō Unimbɔti ki kíí, an pu ní ti sòoǹ.

eng: We do this because we know that the one who raised the Lord Jesus will also raise us with Jesus, and he will bring us into his presence along with you.
local: Ti nyí un fīkitì ti-Dindaan Yeesu ditaŋkpiilinee làá dāá fìkitī ti mun kii u nín fīkitì Yeesu puee, ní kí dāá cáá ti nì ni-kɔkɔ kí kàan̄ u-cee.

eng: All these things are for your benefit. As grace increases to benefit more and more people, it will cause gratitude to increase, which results in Gods glory.
local: Nimbi pu ní ti jīǹ falaa gbanti, án nín làá ŋá pu Unimbɔti nín tīī diyimɔɔl diŋyee ń bà báà ŋma, bí kpāfì kunipaau kí jāam̀ Unimbɔti tinyooldaan u-ŋanti puee.

eng: So we arent depressed. But even if our bodies are breaking down on the outside, the person that we are on the inside is being renewed every day.
local: An pu ní ti-wunti kaa yɔ̀tī. Báà an yaa sá yii ti-wunti tin poon ti bí nee yaa bíi nàantí ki sìntāaa, dimaŋfapɔndi din bí ti-nee làá nīn pée pɔ̀ntī ní ki cá.

eng: Our temporary minor problems are producing an eternal stockpile of glory for us that is beyond all comparison.
local: Kɔŋkɔnnee ti nín bíi jīǹ falaa un fiii nee di bíi bɔ́ntí tinyookpaandi tin kaa cáá dikúntil taa ŋūǹ kí dūú ŋmàntīǹ tibee ki yōontí-ti.

eng: We dont focus on the things that can be seen but on the things that cant be seen. The things that can be seen dont last, but the things that cant be seen are eternal.
local: An puee timbi nimbikpeeŋu bá tin baa wāĺee pu ní, kaa sá tin bi wāĺ nee pu. Di sá tin bi wāĺee kaa yúntí, ama tin baa wāĺee, tíǹ làá nīn pée bí ní kí nīn cá jaanjaan.

eng: Instead, we reject secrecy and shameful actions. We dont use deception, and we dont tamper with Gods word. Instead, we commend ourselves to everyones conscience in the sight of God by the public announcement of the truth.
local: Ti fá dibɔmbɔndi nì icoo tuuŋŋi. Taa ŋmàtíǹ binib, kaa kpántí Unimbɔti bɔti, ama Unimbɔti nimbiin ní ti sòoǹ ibaamɔn ki cántí ki tùkù binib ki nyàab̀ báà ŋma ń béé yii tin ti sòoǹee sá ibaamɔn.

eng: And even if our gospel is veiled, it is veiled to those who are on the road to destruction.
local: An puee bin wáŋkèe ceeyee, Unimbɔti bɔti tin ti sòoǹ nee sá dibɔmbɔndi ní ki tīī-bi.

eng: The god of this age has blinded the minds of those who dont have faith so they couldnt see the light of the gospel that reveals Christs glory. Christ is the image of God.
local: Kun pu baa fōō-tì ki kííyee di sá Sitaan un sá duulinya nee bɔtiuee jɔ̀b̄-bi ki yāntì baa wāĺ Tibɔmɔntiilee nín ŋmíntí ki dàkà-bi Kriisitoo un sá Unimbɔti naŋkuee nyooti.

eng: We dont preach about ourselves. Instead, we preach about Jesus Christ as Lord, and we describe ourselves as your slaves for Jesus sake.
local: Di sá yii taa bíi sòoǹ nì ni timbi ti-ba bɔti, Yeesu Kriisitoo bɔti ní ti sòoǹ nì ni yii úǹ di sá ti-Dindaan. Úǹ pu ní timbi sá ni-tɔntɔnliibi.

eng: God said that light should shine out of the darkness. He is the same one who shone in our hearts to give us the light of the knowledge of Gods glory in the face of Jesus Christ.
local: Di sá Unimbɔti un bí yii: «Ŋwalifi ń ŋmíntí dibɔmbɔndinee,» úǹ u-ba di ŋmíntì ti-pɔbiliŋ poon ki yāntì ti ká Kriisitoo nimbiin Unimbɔti nyooti nín jìl̀ miŋyee.

eng: But we have this treasure in clay pots so that the awesome power belongs to God and doesnt come from us.
local: Ama timbi bin u dū Tibɔmɔntiil tin sá tigaajawanee ki ŋúbiǹee, ti sá kii aboonee ní. Taa pɔ̄ɔ̄, ní tigaajawan kā ti-ni, án nín làá ŋá pu binib ń béé yii mpɔŋkpaan ŋun bí ti-ni ki tūǹ ditundee nyánní Unimbɔti ceeyee, an kaa nyánní timbi cee.

eng: We are experiencing all kinds of trouble, but we arent crushed. We are confused, but we arent depressed.
local: Falaabi kɔkɔ nì bi-nyɔkɔm nyìitī-ti, ama ti mun kaa wìil̄. Tiwan tikpil bàkantí-ti, ama ti mun kaa páatí ti-mákal Unimbɔti pu.

eng: We are harassed, but we arent abandoned. We are knocked down, but we arent knocked out.
local: Bi jì-ti, ama Unimbɔti kaa yàntī bí cúú-ti. Bi líikí-ti kitiŋ, ama ti mun kaa kúù.

eng: We know that if the tent that we live in on earth is torn down, we have a building from God. Its a house that isnt handmade, which is eternal and located in heaven.
local: Ti nyí yii tiwun tin poon ti kóò duulinya nee poonee yaa kpíìyaaa, Unimbɔti kpáafì-ti tiwumpɔn tiba yilpu. Tiwun gbanti tíǹ sá kii kudii kun úǹ u-ba máńee ní tí nīn kóò poon jaanjaan.

eng: We all must appear before Christ in court so that each person can be paid back for the things that were done while in the body, whether they were good or bad.
local: Kun puee an sá yii ti-kɔkɔ ń dāá kpāfì Kriisitoo nimbiin ní ú sòoǹ ti-bɔti ti nín kāl̀ duulinya poon ki dū ti-yuntiŋu ki ŋá diŋyee. An yaa sá ikpitaaa, an yaa tí sá tiŋanaaa, ní ú dāá pà báà ŋma u nín máaǹ tipaal tiŋyee.

eng: So we try to persuade people, since we know what it means to fear the Lord. We are well known by God, and I hope that in your heart we are well known by you as well.
local: Ti nín fàŋkí Unimbɔti puee ní ti bí ki nyàab̀ binib ń fōō-u kí kíí. Unimbɔti wāĺ-ti míǹ pílpil, m nyí yii ni mun nyí ti-pu míǹ ni-pɔbiliŋ poon.

eng: We arent trying to commend ourselves to you again. Instead, we are giving you an opportunity to be proud of us so that you could answer those who take pride in superficial appearance, and not in what is in the heart.
local: Kaa sá ti là kí fàtīí tí pàkà ti-ba ni-cee, ti là kí pɔ̄-ni nsan ní nīn mɔ̀ɔǹ ti-pu ní. Míǹ ní ni ŋūǹ kí ká tiba kí kíí bin di bí ki sòoǹ bi-nyɔmmu poon tibɔti fám ki kāntí tinyool, ama ní bi-pɔbiliŋ poon kūńee.

eng: If we are crazy, its for Gods sake. If we are rational, its for your sake.
local: Bi yaa yíì-ti biwaatiibaaa, an sá Unimbɔti pu ní bi yíì-ti míǹ. Bi yaa tí yíì-ti binimɔntiibaaa, an sá nimbi paatii ní.

eng: The love of Christ controls us, because we have concluded this: one died for the sake of all; therefore, all died.
local: Kriisitoo neendi di jíń mpaan timbi pu, timbi bin nyí yii unibaanti di kpíì ti-kɔkɔ puee, mimmee ti-kɔkɔ mun kpíì deeyee.

eng: He died for the sake of all so that those who are alive should live not for themselves but for the one who died for them and was raised.
local: Úǹ di kpíì ti-kɔkɔ pu, án nín làá ŋá pu bin bí dimaŋfalinee ń taa nín̄ bí ki tīī bi-ba, ama bí nīn bí ki tīī un kpíì ki fīkitì bi-puee.

eng: So then, from this point on we wont recognize people by human standards. Even though we used to know Christ by human standards, that isnt how we know him now.
local: An puee taa ti kpèé ubɔ nniyaamin. Ki kpáaǹ dooooyee ti yaa nìn kpèé Kriisitoo nniyaaminaaa, kɔŋkɔnnee taa ti kpèé-u míǹ.

eng: So then, if anyone is in Christ, that person is part of the new creation. The old things have gone away, and look, new things have arrived!.
local: Un nì Kriisitoo ŋá ubaantee kpántì unipɔɔn dee, tin sá tikpikilee jítì, tipɔn di kíǹ ki bí.

eng: All of these new things are from God, who reconciled us to himself through Christ and who gave us the ministry of reconciliation.
local: Unimbɔti di ŋá míǹ kɔkɔ. U dī Kriisitoo pu ki yāntì ti nì u mɔ́ɔkì ní ki dū ditundi nee ki ŋá ti-ŋaan yii tí cáań binib u nì bi ń mɔ̄ɔkì.

eng: In other words, God was reconciling the world to himself through Christ, by not counting peoples sins against them. He has trusted us with this message of reconciliation.
local: Di sá yii Kriisitoo pu ní Unimbɔti dī ki cáań binib ní u nì bi mɔ́ɔkì, kaa fātìi tɔ́ bi-kpitii in bi ŋée pu. U dū dimɔɔkil gbanti tundi ki ŋúbiǹ-ti tí sòoǹ nì binib.

eng: We groan while we live in this residence. We really want to dress ourselves with our building from heaven.
local: Ama kɔŋkɔnnee ti bí ki yáliŋkí ki bàà nyàab̀ kí fōō yilpu wunti tin sá kii kudii kun Unimbɔti u-ba máńee.

eng: So we are ambassadors who represent Christ. God is negotiating with you through us. We beg you as Christs representatives, Be reconciled to God.
local: An puee Kriisitoo nyɔkɔpu ní ti làá sòoǹee, a lì kii Unimbɔti u-ba di dī timbi pu ki yíì-nee. Ti dū Kriisitoo ki gbāam̀-ni, ní kíí kí yàntī ni nì Unimbɔti ń mɔ̄ɔkì.

eng: God caused the one who didnt know sin to be sin for our sake so that through him we could become the righteousness of God.
local: Timbi kɔkɔ puee, Unimbɔti yāntì Kriisitoo un kaa ŋá ikpiti ki kée fōō timbi kpitii, án nín làá ŋá pu u-puee, tí kpántí deedeedambi Unimbɔti nimbiinee.

eng: since we assume that when we take off this tent, we wont find out that we are naked.
local: Kun puee taa làá tin nín̄ bí yilpu kaa cáá tiwun.

eng: Yes, while we are in this tent we groan, because we are weighed down. We want to be dressed not undressed, so that what is dying can be swallowed up by life.
local: Tiŋman timbi bin dáá kóò tiwun nee poonee, ti bí digìńdi nì falaabi poon ní. Ama taa là kí nyā tiwun tin sá taapu doo yatee poon. Ti là kí dū yilpu wunti ní kí dūú bìin̄ tin béeyee pu, án nín làá ŋá pu ní tiwun tin kúùyee ń nīn fúuń tin kaa kúùyee poon kí ká dimaŋfalee.

eng: Now the one who prepared us for this very thing is God, and God gave us the Spirit as a down payment for our home.
local: Unimbɔti u-ba di bí yii ti-wunti ń dāá kpántí míǹ. U-Fam min u tīī-tee di dàkà yii u kpáafì tiwammɔntiil ki yóoǹ-ti ti làá dāá fōō.

eng: So we are always confident, because we know that while we are living in the body, we are away from our home with the Lord.
local: An puee ní ti cáá kunyannyaau baabadal. Ti nyí yii ti nín dáá kóò tiwun tin poonee, ti kóò ki dátiǹ ti-Dindaan.

eng: We live by faith and not by sight.
local: Ti fōō Unimbɔti nín lī puee ní ki kíí, taa láá ká nì ti-nimbiliŋ.

eng: We are confident, and we would prefer to leave the body and to be at home with the Lord.
local: Ti cáá kunyannyaau an júkufí-ti ti bàà là kí yīkì ti-wunti tin bí nee poon kí cù kí nīn bí ti-Dindaan cee.

eng: So our goal is to be acceptable to him, whether we are at home or away from home.
local: Ama tin ti nyàab̀ee di sá báà ti yaa bí tiwun nee poonaaa, ti yaa nyáń ti-naaa, tí fáatí-u.

eng: Since we work together with him, we are also begging you not to receive the grace of God in vain.
local: Di sá yii timbi nì Unimbɔti di bí ki tūǹ ditundi, an pu ní ti mèé-ni yii ní taa yàntī diyimɔɔl din ni fōō u-ceeyee ń ŋá fám.

eng: as going through pain but always happy, as poor but making many rich, and as having nothing but owning everything.
local: Bi kɔ̄ntí-ti kunimbaau, ní timbi bí dipɔɔpindin. Bi kpèé-ti yii ti sá bisoodambi, ama timbi di tìì binib tikpil Unimbɔti gaajati. Bi kpèé-ti yii taa pée cáá tiba nì tiba, ama ní ti mun di cáá tikɔkɔ nì tikɔkɔ.

eng: Corinthians, we have spoken openly to you, and our hearts are wide open.
local: Nimbi Kɔranti yab, ti sòon̄ nì ni ti-poon bɔti kɔkɔ ki pīitì ti-pɔbiliŋ ki tīī-ni.

eng: There are no limits to the affection that we feel for you. You are the ones who placed boundaries on your affection for us.
local: Timbi pīitì ti-pɔbiliŋ ki tīī-ni, nimbi di kaa pīitì ni-yaŋ.

eng: But as a fair trade—Im talking to you like you are children—open your hearts wide too.
local: M bíi sòoǹ nì ni kii ubaati nín sòoǹ nì u-biyaamu puee ní: kii ti nín néeń-ni miŋyee puee, pìitīmaan ni-pɔbiliŋ kí tīī-ti.

eng: Dont be tied up as equal partners with people who dont believe. What does righteousness share with that which is outside the Law? What relationship does light have with darkness?.
local: Ni nì binib bin kaa nyí Unimbɔtee ń taa kpāfì kí ŋá ubaanti. Nnyɔɔpee ŋulɔŋu di bí tin bí deedeeyee nì tin kaa bí deedeeyee kansikin? Mana ní dibɔmbɔndi nì ŋwalifi ŋūǹ kí kpāfì?.

eng: What harmony does Christ have with Satan? What does a believer have in common with someone who doesnt believe?.
local: Mana ní Kriisitoo nì Sitaan ŋūǹ kí pèen̄ kinyɔkɔ? Àá ba nyɔɔpeeŋu di bí unimbɔtinool nì un kaa nyí Unimbɔtee kansikin ní?.

eng: What agreement can there be between Gods temple and idols? Because we are the temple of the living God. Just as God said, I live with them, and I will move among them. I will be their God, and they will be my people. .
local: Mana ní Unimbɔti nì awaa ŋun kí pèen̄ kudii? Timbi sá Unimbɔti un bí dimaŋfalinee diiku ní, a lì kii Unimbɔti u-ba nín lī puee, yii: «M làá nīn kóò m-nibul cee, ní kí nīn còom̄ bi-kansikin. Man di làá nīn sá bi-Nimbɔtiu, ní bíǹ ń nīn sá man nibul.».

eng: Therefore, come out from among them and be separated, says the Lord. Dont touch what is unclean. Then I will welcome you. .
local: An pu ní Unimbɔti lī u-nibul yii: «Ní nyā binib nee kansikin kí gàntīmaan ni-ba, kí taa gbēē tin sá tijɔkɔndee, ní man ń nín fōō-ni.

eng: I will be a father to you, and you will be my sons and daughters, says the Lord Almighty .
local: kí nīn kíǹ ki sá ni-Baa, ní ni mun ń nīn kíǹ ki sá man jipɔmbi nì m-bisalib. Ti-Dindaan Mpɔŋkɔdaan di lī míǹ.».

eng: He says, I listened to you at the right time, and I helped you on the day of salvation. Look, now is the right time! Look, now is the day of salvation!.
local: Unimbɔti lī u-gbɔŋku poon yii: «M-niti yuntiŋuee, m nìn pílifiǹ-si, m nìn tútúǹ-si difiil wiiŋŋu a ká difiil.» An puee Unimbɔti niti yuntiŋu sèé, difiil wiiŋŋu sèé.

eng: We dont give anyone any reason to be offended about anything so that our ministry wont be criticized.
local: Taa là kí bàkan̄-ni báà tiba nì tiba poon, án nín làá ŋá pu ubɔ ń taa ká ibɔkpiti iba ti-tundi nee pu kí lèe.

eng: Instead, we commend ourselves as ministers of God in every way. We did this with our great endurance through problems, disasters, and stressful situations.
local: Ama ti pée dàkà ti-bimbim kɔkɔ poon yii ti sá Unimbɔti tɔntɔnliibi ní, ní ki jīǹ kimɔtii tikpil, falaabi nì kunimbaau nì dinimbinaamandi poon.

eng: We went through beatings, imprisonments, and riots. We experienced hard work, sleepless nights, and hunger.
local: Bi gbáà-ti ki cáǹ-ti ki pìintí kudii, ki mèé tijáti ki tòó ti-pu, ki yàntī ti tūǹ atuun ŋin pɔ̄ɔ̄yee, kaa kāntí ŋgeem, kaa jīǹ tijin.

eng: We displayed purity, knowledge, patience, and generosity. We served with the Holy Spirit, genuine love.
local: Ti-nyaŋkanti nì ti-nyil nì kimɔtii nì ti-niti nì Circir Fam pɔŋŋu nì dineembaamɔndi ní ti cáa dàkà yii ti sá Unimbɔti tɔntɔnliibi.

eng: telling the truth, and Gods power. We carried the weapons of righteousness in our right hand and our left hand.
local: Ti sòoǹ Unimbɔti bɔti tin sá ibaamɔnee nì u-pɔŋŋu ní ki dàkà yii ti sá Unimbɔti tɔntɔnliibi. Ti nín yú deedee nì Unimbɔtee di sá tijanjawan tin ti cáa jáàyee, nì tin ti cáa cí ti-bee.

eng: We were treated with honor and dishonor and with verbal abuse and good evaluation. We were seen as both fake and real.
local: Ŋyunti ŋubɔee, bi tìì-ti tinyool, ní ŋyunti ŋubɔee, bi kɔ̄ntí-ti icoo. Ŋyunti ŋubɔee, bi sòoǹ ti-pu tin ŋān̄ee, ní ŋyunti ŋubɔee, bi sòoǹ ti-pu tikpitil mun. Bi kpèé-ti kii binyimɔndambee, ama ní ti mun sòoǹ ibaamɔn.

eng: as unknown and well known, as dying—and look, we are alive! We were seen as punished but not killed.
local: Bi kpèé-ti kii báà ubɔ kaa nyí-tee, ama ní báà ŋma nyí-ti. Bi kpèé-ti kii bin làá kpúee, ama ní ti mun kaa kúù. Bi dátí ti-tafal, ama ní kaa kù-ti.

eng: My dear friends, since we have these promises, lets cleanse ourselves from anything that contaminates our body or spirit so that we make our holiness complete in the fear of God.
local: Nnabineeŋkaamu, an nín sá yii timbi ní Unimbɔti pōoǹ ipoon gbantee, tí fámaan atuŋkpiti ŋin kɔkɔ bíiĺ tiwun nì kinaŋee, kí fàŋkì Unimbɔti kí nyāntí ti-pɔbiliŋ poon tikpiil kɔkɔ kí nīn bí circir.

eng: Godly sadness produces a changed heart and life that leads to salvation and leaves no regrets, but sorrow under the influence of the world produces death.
local: Kun puee kunimbaau kun Unimbɔti kɔ̄ntí binibee cāabìń dibakpántil ní, ní unil ń ká difiil, ní kunimbaauee kaa ti páb̀-u. Ama kunimbaau kun duulinya nee gìŋ́ŋi tììyee cáǹ ŋkúmin ní.

eng: Look at what this very experience of godly sadness has produced in you: such enthusiasm, what a desire to clear yourselves of blame, such indignation, what fear, what purpose, such concern, what justice! In everything you have shown yourselves to be innocent in the matter.
local: Ni ká Unimbɔti nín kɔ́ǹ-ni kunimbaau kuŋyee nín cáań-ni tiŋan tiŋyeeyaa! Ni kíǹ ki ŋúb́ Unimbɔti bɔti mmɔntiimin, ki kíǹ ki bíi gbàam̄ ni-bɔkpil pu. Tikpitil tin ŋée kaa mɔ́ɔkiǹ-ni, ni kíǹ ki fàŋkí Unimbɔti, ní an júkufí-ni ní ká-ti. Ni kíǹ ki cáá difaŋgiil, ki kíǹ ki wāĺ yii an máaǹ bí dáaĺ ikpitidaan tafal. Tin ni ŋá nee puee, ni dàkā yii naa bí tibɔti gbantin.

eng: So although I wrote to you, it wasnt for the sake of the one who did wrong, or for the sake of the one who was wronged, but to show you your own enthusiasm for us in the sight of God.
local: An puee m yaa nìn ŋmàn̄-ni kugbɔŋuee, an kaa sá un ŋá ikpitee pu, kaa tí sá un bi ŋá ikpitee mun pu. Ama m là ní ká difaŋgiil din ni cáá ti-puee Unimbɔti nimbiin ní.

eng: Because of this we have been encouraged. And in addition to our own encouragement, we were even more pleased at how happy Titus was. His mind has been put at rest by all of you.
local: An di tīī-ti kunyannyaau. Tiiti nín bāańee, an kaa sá kunyannyaau ŋmaniŋma ní ti ká, ama ti ká dipɔɔpiŋkpaandi mun, kun puee an mɔ́ɔkiǹ-u tikpil kunyannyaau kun ni-kɔkɔ tīī-u ni-cee niinee.

eng: If Ive bragged about you to him in any way, I havent been embarrassed. Instead, our bragging to Titus has also been proven to be true, just like everything we said to you was true.
local: Kí wàatī ú dāan̄ ni-ceeyee, ti nìn tīī-ni disíndi u-cee, ní naa kɔ́ǹ-ti icoo kii ti nín pée sòoǹ nì ni ibaamɔn baabadal puee. Míǹ ní disíndi din ti tīī-ni Tiiti ceeyee cūtì ki kāl̀ deedeedee.

eng: His devotion to you is growing even more as he remembers how all of you were obedient when you welcomed him with fear and trembling.
local: U yaa téetì ni-kɔkɔ nín nìn tɔ́-u min min, ki pílifíǹ-u, ki fōō-u min min nì tifaandi yɔ́kɔ́ yɔkɔee, ní dineendi din u cáá ni-puee mɔ̀tīi wīintí.

eng: Im happy, because I can completely depend on you.
local: An mɔ́ɔkiǹ-mi tikpil, kun puee kɔŋkɔnnee m kíǹ ki cáá ni-pu dimákal tikɔkɔ poon.

eng: Make room in your hearts for us. We didnt do anything wrong to anyone. We didnt ruin anyone. We didnt take advantage of anyone.
local: Ní dū ni-pɔbiliŋ kí tīīmaan-ti, di sá yii taa yīntiǹ ubɔ ni-kansikin, kaa bíil̀ ubɔ ni-kansikin, kaa jíń ni-kansikin ubɔ.

eng: Im not saying this to make you feel guilty. Ive already said that you are in our hearts so that we die and live together with you.
local: Kaa sá m là kí wòoǹ-nee pu ní m lì míǹ, di sá m pée lī dooooyee yii ni kɔ́ ti-pɔbiliŋin tiŋan. Báà dimaŋfalinaaa, ŋkúminaaa, ti làá nīn gbíntì ki bí míǹ ní.

eng: I have every confidence in you. Im terribly proud of you. Im filled with encouragement. Im overwhelmed with happiness while in the middle of our problems.
local: M cáá dimákakpaandi ni-pu, ni-bɔti mɔ̄ǹ-mi tikpil. Ti-falaabi poonee, dipɔɔpiŋkpaandi din m cáá ni-puee tīī-mi kunyannyaau.

eng: Even after we arrived in Macedonia, we couldnt rest physically. We were surrounded by problems. There was external conflict, and there were internal fears.
local: Di sá yii ti nín bāań Maseeduwaanee, taa gbìl̄ mmɔn, ti kántì agìń kɔkɔ nì ŋi-bul ní timbi ti-ba bí kujaaun ní tifaandi kɔ́-ti.

eng: However, God comforts people who are discouraged, and he comforted us by Titus arrival.
local: Ama Unimbɔti un tìì bin wunti yɔ̄tèe kunyannyaauee tīiń-ti kunyannyaau ŋyunti ŋun ní Tiiti bāań ti-ceeyee.

eng: We werent comforted only by his arrival but also by the comfort he had received from you. He told us about your desire to see me, how you were sorry, and about your concern for me, so that I was even happier.
local: Kaa sá u nín dɔ́miń ti-ceeyee ŋmaniŋma pu ní ti ká kunyannyaau, ama u nín máafì-ti nimbi ni-ba nín tīī u mun kunyannyaau kuŋyee pu. U máafì-ti ni nín mɔ̀ ki nyàab̀ kí bàà gíti ká-mee, nì ni nín bí ki yáliŋkí m-pu miŋyee, an di gíti mɔ́ɔkiǹ-mi ki lákatì.

eng: Even though my letter hurt you, I dont regret it. Well—I did regret it just a bit because I see that that letter made you sad, though only for a short time.
local: An puee kugbɔŋu kun m nìn ŋmàn̄-nee yaa nìn kɔ́ǹ-ni kunimbaauaaa, an kaa ti páb́-mi kɔŋkɔnnee. Tin nìn páb́-mee di sá m nín ká yii kugbɔŋu gbanti kɔ́ǹ-ni kunimbaau ŋyunti ŋubɔ poon fiiiyee.

eng: Now Im glad—not because you were sad but because you were made sad enough to change your hearts and lives. You felt godly sadness so that no one was harmed by us in any way.
local: Ama kɔŋkɔnnee an mɔ́ɔkiǹ-mi. An kaa sá an nín kɔ́ǹ-ni kunimbaauee, ama an nín kpántì ni-bimbimee pu. Mimmee timbi kaa ŋá-ni ikpiti iba dee, an sá Unimbɔti di dī ni-nimbaaku pu ki gíiǹní-ni u-cee.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we want to let you know about the grace of God that was given to the churches of Macedonia.
local: Nnabiyaamu, ti là ní béé Unimbɔti nín ŋá tiŋan tin ki tīī Kriisitoo nikitiŋ ŋin bí Maseeduwaan ŋi túń ditundi diŋyee ní.

eng: Im giving you my opinion about this. Its to your advantage to do this, since you not only started to do it last year but you wanted to do it too.
local: Man nín ká tin tibɔti nee poonee ní m là kí tùkù-ni: m ká yii an ŋān̄ ní nìkī ni-tundi din ni nìn kíĺ jiilee, kun puee nimbi di nìn péé péé ki kánní an landɔkɔŋu ní ki nìn péé ki tɔ́ mun.

eng: Now finish the job as well so that you finish it with as much enthusiasm as you started, given what you can afford.
local: An puee kɔŋkɔnnee, ní púntímaan ni-tundi gbanti, án nín làá ŋá pu bí dāá ká min ni lēē ni-pɔbiliŋ poon yii ni làá ŋée, ní púntí kí ŋá ni-pɔŋŋu nyɔkɔm.

eng: A gift is appreciated because of what a person can afford, not because of what that person cant afford, if its apparent that its done willingly.
local: Ni yaa làá tīī tiba nì dipɔbil dibee, an kaa sá ni nín làá tīī kuŋyee wíiŋŋu di sá tiba Unimbɔti cee, ní tīī min nyɔkɔm ni ŋūǹee di sá.

eng: It isnt that we want others to have financial ease and you financial difficulties, but its a matter of equality.
local: An kaa sá yii ní dū tin ni cááyee kɔkɔ kí jòò bin kíǹee kí ŋmìntī ni-ba, ama an máaǹ un bi tútúǹee nì un tútúǹ-uee kɔkɔ ń nīn ŋmà ní.

eng: At the present moment, your surplus can fill their deficit so that in the future their surplus can fill your deficit. In this way there is equality.
local: Kɔŋkɔnnee ni cáá tikpil kí ŋmā kí tūtuǹ bin kaa cááyee, ní daliŋkin ní ni mun yaa lóòyee, ní bi mun ń jòoń nimbi, míǹ di làá yàntī ni-kɔkɔ ń ŋmàntī.

eng: As it is written, The one who gathered more didnt have too much, and the one who gathered less didnt have too little. .
local: A lì kii Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon nín lī puee yii: «Un fāā tikpilee kaa cáá ki lákatì, ní tiba kaa lóò un fāā fiiiyee.».

eng: But thank God, who put the same commitment that I have for you in Titus heart.
local: M jáaḿ Unimbɔti un di tīī Tiiti difaŋgiil ni-pu kii ti mun nín cáá ni-pu miŋyee.

eng: Not only has he accepted our challenge but hes on his way to see you voluntarily, and hes excited.
local: Ti nín bālifì-u yii ú cù ni-ceeyee, u kíí-ti yii u làá cù, áli gba úǹ u-ba nìn pée bɔ́ntì yii u dòoń ni-cee.

eng: We are sending the brother who is famous in all the churches because of his work for the gospel along with him.
local: Ti dū u nì kinabiki kin túń dituŋkpaandi Unimbɔti bɔti sòontam poon, Kriisitoo nikitiŋ kɔkɔ pàkà-uee, ki túnní ni-cee.

eng: In addition to this, he is chosen by the churches to be our traveling companion in this work of grace, which we are taking care of for the sake of the glory of the Lord himself, and to show our desire to help.
local: Kriisitoo nikitiŋ mun gíti lēē-u yii ú céeǹ-ti tí cáá tootoo un ti ŋá nì tinitee kí cùnnì Jeerusaleem. An di làá dàkà ti nín néeń kí tūtuǹ ti-jutiibi puee ní kí tīī ti-Dindaan tinyool.

eng: While they were being tested by many problems, their extra amount of happiness and their extreme poverty resulted in a surplus of rich generosity.
local: Bi nìn bí agìń tikpil poon, ama ní bi-pɔɔpindi nìn gíti lákatì. An di yāntì ní min kɔkɔ bi sá bisoodambee, bi dàkā bi-niti an nyāŋkì.

eng: We are trying to avoid being blamed by anyone for the way we take care of this large amount of money.
local: Taa là biba ń nīn bí ki sòoǹ ki bíiĺ-ti kunimbigbaau kun ti yōoǹ kí cáá yàkatī binib nee pu.

eng: We care about doing the right thing, not only in the Lords eyes but also in the eyes of other people.
local: Ti là yii tin ti ŋáańee ń nīn ŋān̄ ti-Dindaan ceeyaaa, kí nīn ŋān̄ binib mun cee.

eng: We are sending our brother with them. We have tested his commitment in many ways and many times. Now hes even more committed, because he has so much confidence in you.
local: Ti túnní-bi ni-cee bi nì ti-nabiki kiba kin ti cɔ́ŋkì ŋfunyɔm tikpil ki ká yii u cáá difaŋgiil tikɔkɔ poonee. Ama kɔŋkɔnnee, u gíti mɔ̄tìi ká difaŋgiil dimákakpaandi din u cáá ni-puee pu.

eng: If there is any question about Titus, he is my partner and coworker among you. If there is any question about our brothers, they are the churches apostles and an honor to Christ.
local: Tiiti úǹ sá man bɔɔ ní ki sá m-tɔntɔnjuti ni-cee. Bin kíǹee, Kriisitoo nikitiŋ di túnní-bi ní bi-bimbim tìì Kriisitoo tinyool.

eng: So show them the proof of your love and the reason we are so proud of you, in such a way that the churches can see it.
local: Dàkàmaan-bi ni-neendi, Kriisitoo nikitiŋ ń béé ibaamɔn yii ti cáá nsan kí nīn fàal̀ ni-pu.

eng: I assure you that they gave what they could afford and even more than they could afford, and they did it voluntarily.
local: M lēē bi-mɔ́ɔŋŋu yii bi tīī nì dipɔbil diba min bi ŋúńee áli ki jítiǹ min bi ŋūǹee pu kpataaa.

eng: They urgently begged us for the privilege of sharing in this service for the saints.
local: Bi gbāam̀-ti tikpil, yii tí pée pɔ̄ bi mun diyimɔɔl bí tɔ̄ tootoo kí tūtuǹ Kriisitoo yab bin bí Juudeeyee.

eng: They even exceeded our expectations, because they gave themselves to the Lord first and to us, consistent with Gods will.
local: Bi tīī ki jítì min pu timbi bàà dàkafēe, di sá yii bi dá láá péé ki dū bi-ba ki tīī ti-Dindaan ki wāatì ní ki ŋá Unimbɔti nín là puee, ki dū bi-ba ki tīī ti mun.

eng: As a result, we challenged Titus to finish this work of grace with you the way he had started it.
local: An pu ní ti gbāam̀ Tiiti yii ú gítiń ni-cee kí tīī-ni kunyannyaau ní púntí ni-tootoou un u nìn tīī-ni kunyannyaau ni kíĺee kí tɔ̄ nì tiniti.

eng: Be the best in this work of grace in the same way that you are the best in everything, such as faith, speech, knowledge, total commitment, and the love we inspired in you.
local: Ni cáá tikɔkɔ nì tikɔkɔ: ni cáá fookii, ki nyí disoondi, ki cáá dinyil, ki cáá difaŋgiil tikɔkɔ pu, nì dineendi ti mun pu. An puee ti là yii ní dū tin kɔkɔ ni cááyee kí dàkà tootoo nee poon ni-niti.

eng: Im not giving an order, but by mentioning the commitment of others, Im trying to prove the authenticity of your love also.
local: Kaa sá mara ní m bíi tìì-ni, ama m bíi máafí-ni biba faŋgiil ní, ni mun ń ŋmā kí dàkà ni-neendi nín sá ibaamɔn puee.

eng: You know the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ. Although he was rich, he became poor for your sakes, so that you could become rich through his poverty.
local: Kun puee ni nyí tiŋan tin ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo ŋá-tee. Úǹ un sá ugaajee, u gíiǹ u-ba usoodaan, timbi ń kpántí bigaajab úǹ soodaŋku pu.

eng: Its unnecessary for me to write to you about this service for Gods people.
local: An kaa bàlifī ḿ gíti ŋmà-ni kugbɔŋu kí tùkù-ni ti nín máaǹ kí tūtuǹ Kriisitoo yab bin bí Juudeeyee puee.

eng: The one who supplies seed for planting and bread for eating will supply and multiply your seed and will increase your crop, which is righteousness.
local: Unimbɔti un tìì ukpaal iwambool, ki tìì tijikaalee, làá tīī-ni iwambool kɔkɔ nì i-nyɔkɔm kí yàntī í lòoǹ, án nín làá ŋá pu ni-niti ń lòoǹ abimɔntee.

eng: You will be made rich in every way so that you can be generous in every way. Such generosity produces thanksgiving to God through us.
local: U làá yàntī ní nīn sá bigaajab kí ŋmā kí nīn ŋáań tiniti, án nín làá ŋá pu binib tikpil ń nīn jáaḿ Unimbɔti tin ni ŋáań ki tìì-bi ti cāabìń-bee pu.

eng: Your ministry of this service to Gods people isnt only fully meeting their needs but it is also multiplying in many expressions of thanksgiving to God.
local: An kaa sá Kriisitoo yab bin lóò tibee ŋmaniŋma ní ni-tundi din ni tūǹee tútúǹ, ama an yàntī binib tikpil jáaḿ Unimbɔti múlmul.

eng: They will give honor to God for your obedience to your confession of Christs gospel. They will do this because this service provides evidence of your obedience, and because of your generosity in sharing with them and with everyone.
local: Ni-tundi din ni tūǹee di dàkà binib bin ní ni sée, ní ki yàntī binib nyɔ́ŋkí Unimbɔti ni nín tɔ́ Kriisitoo Bɔmɔntiiti puee, ní ki nyɔ́ŋkí Unimbɔti ni-niti tin ni ŋáań ki tìì bíǹ nì bin kɔkɔ kaa cááyee.

eng: They will also pray for you, and they will care deeply for you because of the outstanding grace that God has given to you.
local: Bi làá mèè ni-pu ní ki dàkà-ni dineeŋkpaandi, kun puee Unimbɔti ŋá-ni tiŋaŋkpaandi.

eng: Thank God for his gift that words cant describe!.
local: Tí jāam̀maan Unimbɔti u-pɔ̄tam nín kaa cáá jutee pu!.

eng: I know about your willingness to help. I brag about you to the Macedonians, saying, Greece has been ready since last year, and your enthusiasm has motivated most of them.
local: M nyí ni-landɔkɔmɔntii pu, m tīī-ni disíndi Maseeduwaan yab cee ki tùkū-bi yii doooo jiil ní nimbi nnabiyaamu Akaayi yab bɔ́ntì, ní ni-faŋgiil tīī binib tikpil kunyannyaau.

eng: But Im sending the brothers so that our bragging about you in this case wont be empty words, and so that you can be prepared, just as I keep telling them you will be.
local: An pu ní m dū nnabiyaamu nee ki túnní ni-cee, án nín làá ŋá pu disíndi din m tīī-nee, bí lafun ká yii ni máaǹ-dèe, kí lafun bɔ́ntí tikɔkɔ kii m nín tùkū-bi yii ní bɔ́ntí puee.

eng: If some Macedonians should come with me and find out that you arent ready, we would be embarrassed as far as this project goes.
local: An yaa sá fám, man nì Maseeduwaan yab biba yaa táań ki bāań ní naa láá bɔ́ntì ki dóòyee, ti-mákal din ti cáá ni-puee làá kpántí-ti icoo ní, icoo gbanti làá jī kí kpáaǹ ni mun.

eng: This is why I thought it was necessary to encourage the brothers to go to you ahead of time and arrange in advance the generous gift you have already promised. I want it to be a real gift from you. I dont want you to feel like you are being forced to give anything.
local: An puee ní m mēē nnabiyaamu nee bí péeǹ timbi kí dāan̄ ni-cee. Ní bɔ́ntí kí ŋá ni-niti tin ni pōoǹ kí ŋée, man dāa bàńee, an bɔ́ntì dee. Míǹ di làá dàkà yii ni tīī nì dipɔbil diba, naa tìì ki yáliŋkí.

eng: What I mean is this: the one who sows a small number of seeds will also reap a small crop, and the one who sows a generous amount of seeds will also reap a generous crop.
local: Téetímaan yii un yaa búĺ fiiiyee dāa tí cēē fiii ní, ní un búĺ tikpilee mun dāa tí cēē tikpil.

eng: Everyone should give whatever they have decided in their heart. They shouldnt give with hesitation or because of pressure. God loves a cheerful giver.
local: An puee báà ŋma ń tīī min u-pɔbil fáatì ú tīīyee, án taa nín̄ sá nì kunimbaauaaa, dipabil bɔti, kun puee Unimbɔti néeń un tìì nì dipɔɔpindee ní.

eng: God has the power to provide you with more than enough of every kind of grace. That way, you will have everything you need always and in everything to provide more than enough for every kind of good work.
local: Unimbɔti cáá mpɔn kí tīī-ni tiŋan kɔkɔ nì ti-nyɔkɔm tikpil tikpil, án nín làá ŋá pu ní nīn cáá baabadal tin kɔkɔ ni yaa lèe án lákatí ní nīn cáa tūǹ atummɔnti kɔkɔee.

eng: As it is written, He scattered everywhere; he gave to the needy; his righteousness remains forever. .
local: A lì kii Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon nín lī puee yii: «Un ŋáań biwɔfib tinitee, Unimbɔti làá bìin̄ u-boon ní u-niti ń nīn pée bí ki cá.».

eng: From the elder. To the chosen gentlewoman and her children, whom I truly love.
local: An sá man Ukpil di jáaḿ sii ti-Na un Unimbɔti lútèe nì a-biyaamu. Ibaamɔn, m néeń ni-kɔkɔ. An kaa sá man baba di néeń-ni, an sá ki kpáaǹ bin kɔkɔ béè ibaamɔnee.

eng: Whoever comes to you who does not affirm this teaching should neither be received nor welcomed into your home.
local: Míǹ puee, un yaa dɔ́miń kaa cáań-ni tibɔti gbantee, ní taa fōō-u ni-dumpu kí taa jāam̀-u gba.

eng: because welcoming people like that is the same thing as sharing in their evil actions.
local: kun puee, un yaa jáam̀-uee mun ŋmàl̄ atuŋkpiti ŋin udaan tūǹee ni dee.

eng: I have a lot to tell you. I dont want to use paper and ink, but I hope to visit you and talk with you face-to-face, so that our joy can be complete.
local: Tibɔti tikpil bàà bí ḿ sòoǹ nì ni, ama maa là kí dūú ŋmà kugbɔŋu poon, kun puee m là kí dāan̄ ni-cee niin kí sòoǹ nì ni ní, án mɔ̀tīí mɔ̄ɔkiǹ man nì ni.

eng: Your chosen sisters children greet you.
local: A-Naal un Unimbɔti lútèe biyaamu mun jáaḿ-si.

eng: because of the truth that remains with us and will be with us forever.
local: kun puee ibaamɔn bí ti-ni ki làá nīn pée bí kí nīn cá jaanjaan.

eng: Grace, mercy, and peace from God the Father and from Jesus Christ, the Son of the Father, will be ours who live in truth and love.
local: Ti-Baa Unimbɔti nì Yeesu Kriisitoo, Ubaati Jipɔɔn ń ŋá-ti tiŋan, kí sɔ̄ ti-saai, kí tīī-ti ŋgbansɔŋfi, tí nīn cáá ibaamɔn kí nīn néeń tɔb.

eng: I was overjoyed to find some of your children living in the truth, just as we had been commanded by the Father.
local: An mɔ́ɔkiǹ-mi tikpil m nín ká a-biyaamu mubɔ nóó ibaamɔnee, kii ti-Baa Unimbɔti nín tīī-ti mara uŋyee.

eng: Now, dear friends, I am requesting that we love each other. Its not as though Im writing a new command to you, but its one we have had from the beginning.
local: Kɔŋkɔnnee, ti-Na, m gbàam̄-si, tí nīn néeḿmaan tɔb. Dinyɔɔbundi gbanti kaa sá marapɔɔn, an sá mara un ti nìn pée fōō doooo ŋkilkaaminee ní.

eng: This is love: that we live according to his commands. This is the command that you heard from the beginning: live in love.
local: Dineendi taapu di sá tí nóó Unimbɔti marab. Kii ni nín gbìl̄ doooo ŋkilkaamin puee, mara gbanti di sá tí nīn cáá dineendi.

eng: Many deceivers have gone into the world who do not confess that Jesus Christ came as a human being. This kind of person is the deceiver and the antichrist.
local: Kun puee biŋmatinliibi gbóotì duulinyan ki yíì, kaa fōō ki kíí yii Yeesu Kriisitoo kpántì unil ki dɔ́miń duulinyan. Un néè mimmee di sá uŋmatinli ki sá Kriisitoo nannanliu.

eng: Watch yourselves so that you dont lose what weve worked for but instead receive a full reward.
local: Míǹ puee, ní nīn nyí nì ni-ba, ní taa fá ti-tundi nín cáań ipaati iŋyee, án nín làá ŋá pu ní dāá ŋmā kí fōō ni-paati kɔkɔee.

eng: Anyone who goes too far and does not continue in the teaching about Christ does not have God. Whoever continues in this teaching has both the Father and the Son.
local: Un di yaa lákatì, kaa là kí nīn ŋúb́ Kriisitoo bɔtee, kaa nyí Unimbɔti, ama un yaa ŋúb́ tibɔti gbantee, úǹ di nyí ti-Baa Unimbɔti nì u-Jipɔɔn.

eng: From Simon Peter, a slave and apostle of Jesus Christ. To those who received a faith equal to ours through the justice of our God and savior Jesus Christ.
local: Man Siimɔn Peetroo un sá Yeesu Kriisitoo kpambal ki sá u-tɔntɔnliu muee di ŋmàn̄ kugbɔŋu nee. M ŋmàn̄-ku kí tīī nimbi bin fōō Unimbɔti bɔti ki kíí mbaantiim kii timbee. Yeesu Kriisitoo un sá Unimbɔti ki sá ti-Fiiliuee di ŋá míǹ ki tīī-ti, kun puee u sá deedeedaan.

eng: Therefore, brothers and sisters, be eager to confirm your call and election. Do this and you will never ever be lost.
local: Kɔŋkɔnnee nnabiyaamu, ni nyí yii Unimbɔti lútì-ni ki yíiń-ni ní nīn sá u-yab ní. Ni yaa pɔ̄ɔntí ki gbíntìi nóó nsan gbantee, ni kpá kí jēetí tikpitilin.

eng: In this way you will receive a rich welcome into the everlasting kingdom of our Lord and savior Jesus Christ.
local: Ní Unimbɔti ń dāá pɔ̄-ni nsan ní kɔ́ ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo un sá ti-Fiiliuee Beel din kaa cáá dikúntilee ni sɔkɔmii.

eng: So Ill keep reminding you about these things, although you already know them and stand secure in the truth you have.
local: M nín là yii ní ká tin m sòon̄ee pu ní m gbíntì ki téetí-ni múlmul, nì min kɔkɔ ni nìn pée nyí-tì ki yú ibaamɔn bɔti tin ni fōō ki kííyee poon ki ŋúb́ an píkée.

eng: I think its right that I keep stirring up your memory, as long as Im alive.
local: Ama m là yii m nín dáá bí nee, ḿ nīn pée cɔ́ an bɔti pu ki téetí-ni múlmul.

eng: After all, our Lord Jesus Christ has shown me that I am about to depart from this life.
local: Kun puee ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo tùkū-mi yii an kaa làá ti yúntí m làá kpú.

eng: Im eager for you always to remember these things after my death.
local: An puee m làá pɔ́ɔǹ nì m-pɔŋŋu kɔkɔ kí dàkà-ni nsàmbiyaamu mun ní ni làá dī kí nīn téetí an bɔti pu baabadal m-kúm boonee.

eng: We didnt repeat crafty myths when we told you about the powerful coming of our Lord Jesus Christ. Quite the contrary, we witnessed his majesty with our own eyes.
local: Di sá yii ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo dāantam nì u-pɔŋŋu bɔti tin ti sòon̄ ki tùkū-nee kaa sá mmaam min binib nyí bi-landɔkɔyin ki sòon̄ee. Timbi ti-ba di baasii ká-u nì ti-nimbiliŋ u nín sá ukpaan miŋyee.

eng: He received honor and glory from God the Father when a voice came to him from the magnificent glory, saying, This is my dearly loved Son, with whom I am well-pleased.
local: Timbi nimbilin ní u-Baa Unimbɔti tīī-u jilma nì tinyool. Unimbɔti un sá ukpaan tinyooldaanee bí yii: «Un bí nee di sá m-Jipɔɔn un m néeńee, m-pɔbil sɔ̀m̀ u-pu.».

eng: We ourselves heard this voice from heaven while we were with him on the holy mountain.
local: Timbi nì u di nìn bí circir jool pu ní Unimbɔti sòon̄ní míǹ yilpu.

eng: In addition, we have a most reliable prophetic word, and you would do well to pay attention to it, just as you would to a lamp shining in a dark place, until the day dawns and the morning star rises in your hearts.
local: An puee, ti baasii mɔ̄tìi fōō ki kíí Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi nín sòon̄ tiŋyee ní. Ní gíiǹ ni-landɔkɔi tibɔti gbanti pu, kun puee tì bí kii fitila nín yú dibɔmbɔndin ki wāĺ pu áli ŋwìiń ń dāá nyānní puee ní, ní Kriisitoo un sá kii utaafaaŋmalbijee ń dāá ŋmíntí ni-pɔbiliŋ.

eng: May you have more and more grace and peace through the knowledge of God and Jesus our Lord.
local: Kii ni nín nyí Unimbɔti nì ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo puee, ú ŋá-ni tiŋan kí tīī-ni ŋgbansɔŋfi tikpil.

eng: Most important, you must know that no prophecy of scripture represents the prophets own understanding of things.
local: Kí wàatēe, tin m là ní bééyee sèé: Ubɔ kaa ŋūǹ kí dū úǹ u-ba ciiŋŋu kí béé tibɔti tin Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi ŋmàn̄ ki ŋá kugbɔŋunee.

eng: because no prophecy ever came by human will. Instead, men and women led by the Holy Spirit spoke from God.
local: Kun puee tin kɔkɔ Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi lēe, tiba nì tiba kaa nyánní bi-làtamin, ama an sá Unimbɔti Fam di kpōotì-bi, ní bi fōō Unimbɔti paaŋŋu ki sòon̄ u-bɔti.

eng: By his divine power the Lord has given us everything we need for life and godliness through the knowledge of the one who called us by his own honor and glory.
local: Yeesu Kriisitoo pɔŋŋu tīī-ti ŋŋmam min ti ŋūǹ kí nīn bí ki nákáǹ Unimbɔtee, ki yāntì ti béè un yíiń-ti tí dāan̄ kí fōō u-nyooti nì u-ŋantee.

eng: Through his honor and glory he has given us his precious and wonderful promises, that you may share the divine nature and escape from the worlds immorality that sinful craving produces.
local: Míǹ mbaantiim ní u tí pōoǹ-ti apooŋkpaan ŋin jítiǹ tikɔkɔee, án nín làá ŋá pu ni yaa fōō ipoon gbantee, ní taa nín̄ cáá nnimbitaataam tikpitil pu kí wāŋkì, ama ní fōō mbimbim min náań nì Unimbɔti yamee.

eng: This is why you must make every effort to add moral excellence to your faith; and to moral excellence, knowledge.
local: An puee, kii ni nín fōō ki kííyee puee, ní pɔ́ɔm̀maan ni-ba kí nyàab̀ mbimbimɔntiim kí kūtì fookii pu. Ni yaa ká mbimbimɔntiimee, ní nyàab̀ dinyil kí kūtì.

eng: and to knowledge, self-control; and to self-control, endurance; and to endurance, godliness.
local: kí pɔ́ɔm̀maan kí ŋúb́ ni-ba kí kūtì dinyil pu. Ni yaa ŋúń ki ŋúb́ ni-bee, ní nyàab̀ kimɔtii. Kimɔtii boonee ní nyàab̀ kí nīn tɔ́ Unimbɔti mmɔntiim.

eng: and to godliness, affection for others; and to affection for others, love.
local: Ni yaa tɔ́ Unimbɔti mmɔntiiminee, ní nīn néeń ni-nabiyaamu mun nóó Kriisitooyee kɔkɔ, boonee ní kí nīn néeń báà ŋma.

eng: If all these are yours and they are growing in you, theyll keep you from becoming inactive and unfruitful in the knowledge of our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Mbimbimɔntiim min ni máaǹ kí nīn cááyee dee. Ni yaa cáá-mì ní mì wīikí ni-ni tikpilee, án di làá yàntī ní nīn béetí ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo mmɔntiim kí nīn cá, ní kí nīn bí kii isufi in lòoǹ asubimɔntee.

eng: Whoever lacks these things is shortsighted and blind, forgetting that they were cleansed from their past sins.
local: Ama un kaa cáá an bimbimɔntiimee kaa ŋūǹ kí wāl̀ kí bà ndan Unimbɔti saŋŋu poon, ní ki pūtì kpáàa súntì yii Unimbɔti nìn fíntì u-pɔbilin kpitii kɔkɔ.

eng: But false prophets also arose among the people. In the same way, false teachers will come among you. They will introduce destructive opinions and deny the master who bought them, bringing quick destruction on themselves.
local: Iyunti in jítèe poonee, bin nìn mɔ́ntí yii bi sá Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibee nìn kɔ́ Unimbɔti nikitil kansikin. Míǹ mbaantiim ní bin dàkà tinyimɔmbɔtee mun làá tí kɔ́ ni mun kansikin. Bíǹ làá cáań tinyimɔmbɔti tin làá nàan̄ binibee ní kí dàkà, ní kí dū bi-kpiliu un fīī-bee kí fá kí yàntī Unimbɔti tafadaaldi ń jēetí bi-pu bí kpú kí céetí dicilpu.

eng: This is especially true for those who follow after the corrupt cravings of the sinful nature and defy the Lords authority. These reckless, brash people arent afraid to insult the glorious ones.
local: Bin tafal Unimbɔti làá dāá dáaĺ tikpilee di sá bin dū bi-ba ki tīī bikɔdeekpitib ki kpèé Unimbɔti pɔŋŋu fámee. An dàkà yii bin bɔti m bàà sòon̄ nì ni yii bi dàkà tinyimɔmbɔtee. Baa tɔ́ báà ubɔ nì ubɔ ní bi-pɔbiliŋ nyìm̀. Baa ti tɔ́ Tinyooldambi bin bí yilpuee gba, ama bi sìí-bi ní.

eng: yet angels, who are stronger and more powerful, dont use insults when pronouncing the Lords judgment on them.
local: Ituun in cáá mpɔn ki jítiǹ bin dàkà tinyimɔmbɔti gbantee gba kaa bíi sòoǹ tibɔkpitil bi-pu ti-Dindaan nimbiin.

eng: These false teachers are like irrational animals, mere creatures of instinct, born to be captured and destroyed. They slander what they dont understand and, like animals, they will be destroyed.
local: Binib gbanti ŋáań kii kumooun peelii in pée sá bí nīn cútí-i ki kùee ní. Bi sìí tin baa nyée. Bi làá dāá pée nàan̄ kí céetí-bi ní.

eng: In this way, they will receive payment for their wrongdoing. They even enjoy unruly parties in broad daylight. They are blots and blemishes, taking delight in their seductive pleasures while feasting with you.
local: Bi nín yāntì binib jīǹ falaa miŋyee, an falaau ní Unimbɔti làá dāá gíiǹ kí téeǹ-bi. Bíǹ pɔɔsɔŋkil di sá bí nīn ŋáań tin kɔkɔ sá ntimbiilee ŋwiimpu. Naa ká yii binib gbanti yaa dɔ́miń ni nì bi ń jī tijinee, bi kɔ̄ntí-ni tijɔkɔndi nì icoooo? Ní an mɔ̄ǹ-bi bi nín kíiĺ binib puee.

eng: They are always looking for someone with whom to commit adultery. They are always on the lookout for opportunities to sin. They ensnare people whose faith is weak. They have hearts trained in greed. They are under Gods curse.
local: Ní bi-nimbiliŋ máǹ bí ŋá tijɔkɔndi. Baa gì nì ikpiti diŋál, ní ki pīil̀ ki sàtī binib bin pɔ̀fì nnimbɔtisan poonee. Bi-landɔkɔŋu di pée sá yii bí ká animbil. Bi sá mbusudambi ní.

eng: Leaving the straight path, they have gone off course, following the way of Balaam son of Bosor, who loved the payment of doing wrong.
local: Bi fá nsammɔnti ní ki gáatì ki nóó Boosɔɔr jipɔɔn Balaam nín nìn là animbil ki dī nsaŋkpiti ŋuŋyee.

eng: But Balaam was rebuked for his wrongdoing. A donkey, which has no voice, spoke with a human voice and put a stop to the prophets madness.
local: Ama Unimbɔti nìn wòon̄-u u nín kaa tɔ́-uee pu, di sá u nìn yāntì kimaŋ sòon̄ kii unilee ní ki pīiǹ digbaantundi din Balaam bàà nìn là kí tɔ̄ee saŋŋu.

eng: These false teachers are springs without water, mists driven by the wind. The underworld has been reserved for them.
local: Binib gbanti bí kii ibun in kaa cáá nnyimee, ní ki bí kii atagban ŋin kaa cáá ntaanyim kubuŋu cáá-ŋi ki yìiǹee. Mpaan ŋun Unimbɔti bɔ́ntì ki yóoǹ-bee nyóò ní, ki sá dibɔmbɔndi.

eng: With empty, self-important speech, they use sinful cravings and unrestrained immorality to ensnare people who have only just escaped life with those who have wandered from the truth.
local: An nibee dàkà bi-ba ní ki sòoǹ anyɔɔbuŋkpaan ki ŋmàtíǹ binib ní ki sàtī bin pɔ́ɔǹ bi-ba ki nyáń ikpiti saŋŋu poonee ki yàntī bi fàtī ki gítí ikpiti saŋŋu poon.

eng: These false teachers promise freedom, but they themselves are slaves of immorality; whatever overpowers you, enslaves you.
local: Bi pòontí binib yii bi yaa ŋáań min bíǹ sòoǹee ní bi kāntí dibatɔl, ama ní bíǹ bi-ba sá ikpiti yumbui. Bi súntì yii unil sá kun kpòotī-uee yumbuu ní.

eng: Many will follow them in their unrestrained immorality, and because of these false teachers the way of truth will be slandered.
local: Binib tikpil làá dāá nóó bi-bimbikpitim gbanti saŋŋu. Bi-yaŋyam puee, binib làá dāá nīn kpākantí mbaamɔn saŋŋu bɔti.

eng: If people escape the moral filth of this world through the knowledge of our Lord and savior Jesus Christ, then get tangled up in it again and are overcome by it, they are worse off than they were before.
local: Bin di béè ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo ti-Fiiliu, ki nyáń duulinya bimbikpitim poon, ní ki dāa fātìi gíti yāntì bi-bimbikpitimee cúú-bee, tin poon bi làá kɔ́ee dāa jítiǹ tin poon bi nìn bí dooooyee kpataaa.

eng: It would be better for them never to have known the way of righteousness than, having come to know it, to turn back from the holy commandment entrusted to them.
local: Bidambi yaa kaa bàà béèmaan mbaamɔn saŋŋu, an bàà sɔ́ nì bí béé-ŋu, ní boonee kí dū-ŋu kí fá kaa ti tɔ́ maramɔntiibi bin Unimbɔti nìn tīī-bi bi ŋúb́ee.

eng: They demonstrate the truth of the proverb: A dog returns to its own vomit, and a washed sow wallows in the mud.
local: Tin bāań-bi nee dàkà yii dilaŋkpandi nee sá mbaamɔn dee, yii: «Kibɔkɔ wūliŋkì ki fātìi gíiǹ ki ŋmɔ́», ní «Ugbeeti un bi fùl̄ee fātìi lítì tikpatin ki dɔ̄ ki bíntí.».

eng: In their greed they will take advantage of you with lies. The judgment pronounced against them long ago hasnt fallen idle, nor is their destruction sleeping.
local: Bin dàkà tinyimɔmbɔtee là kí kīil̀-ni kí fōō ni-nimbiliŋ ní. Ama dooooyee ní un làá sòoǹ bi-bɔtee bɔ́ntì ki cí kí tɔ̄ u-tundi, un làá céetí-bee kaa dɔ̄ ki gèeǹ.

eng: God didnt spare the angels when they sinned but cast them into the lowest level of the underworld and committed them to chains of darkness, keeping them there until the judgment.
local: Di sá yii Unimbɔti kaa nìn tāaǹ ituun in nìn ŋá ikpitee ki cúú-i ki tɔ́ ditafadaaldi paaŋŋun. Bi bóob́-i nì ikusaka i kā dibɔmbɔndin ki cí daliŋkin Unimbɔti làá sòoǹ i-bɔtee.

eng: And he didnt spare the ancient world when he brought a flood on the world of ungodly people, even though he protected Noah, a preacher of righteousness, along with seven others.
local: Unimbɔti kaa nìn tāaǹ duulinyakpiki, ama u nìn cāntì nnyiŋgbeen dɔ́miń ki kúntì binikpitib kɔkɔ, ní ki fīī binib biluli ki kútì Noowa un nìn sòoǹ Unimbɔti bɔti tin bí deedeeyee ki tùkù binibee.

eng: God condemned the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah to total destruction, reducing them to ashes as a warning to ungodly people.
local: Unimbɔti nìn dáaĺ ntimu mun bi yíì Soodoom nì Goomoorayee yab tafal ki yāntì ŋŋmi dɔ́miń ki wùn̄-bi búkú buku. U ŋá míǹ kí dàkà u nín làá dāá dáaĺ binikpitib tafal puee ní.

eng: And he rescued righteous Lot, who was made miserable by the unrestrained immorality of unruly people. .
local: Ŋyunti gbantee, Unimbɔti nìn fīī Looti baba ní, kun puee u nìn bí deedee, ní binikpitibee bimbim nì bi-digaŋgamiu nìn kɔ̄ntí Looti inimɔɔn.

eng: While that righteous man lived among them he felt deep distress every day on account of the immoral actions he saw and heard.
