local: Di sá yii Looti nìn kóò bi-kansikin, ki gbìl̀ ní ki kāntí baabadal ikpiti in bi ŋáańee. U nín nìn sá unimɔntee puee, an nìn kɔ̄ntí-u kunimbaau, ki páb́-u tikpil.

eng: These things show that the Lord knows how to rescue the godly from their trials, and how to keep the unrighteous for punishment on the Judgment Day.
local: Míǹ puee ti-Dindaan nyí u nín dì pu ki nyāntí bin yú nì u deedeeyee dinimbinaamandi poonee, ní ki nyí u nín làá yóoń binikpitib laakin kí dāá dáaĺ bi-tafal ibɔsoon dalee.

eng: My dear friends, this is now my second letter to you. I have written both letters to stir up your sincere understanding with a reminder.
local: M-nabineeŋkaamu, kugbɔnliliitiiu ní m ŋmà-ni nee. Bi-liitil gbanti poonee m là kí téetí-ni tibɔti tin bi nìn dàkā-nee ní ŋmā kí dàkafì ti-pu mmɔntiim.

eng: But the day of the Lord will come like a thief. On that day the heavens will pass away with a dreadful noise, the elements will be consumed by fire, and the earth and all the works done on it will be exposed.
local: Ama nì míǹ kɔkɔee, ti-Dindaan dāantam làá dāá túuǹ-ti ní kii unaayuu nín tūuntí binib puee. Kutagbɔŋu dāa gà kuwookpaaŋŋu kubɔ ní kí wāŋkì fílamm, ní tiwan tin bí yilpu kutagbɔŋu puee ń wà búkú buku, ní taapu doo wanti kɔkɔ ń wāŋkì.

eng: Since everything will be destroyed in this way, what sort of people ought you to be? You must live holy and godly lives.
local: Kii tiwan kɔkɔ nín làá dāá kúntí miŋyee puee, ni máaǹ kí béé ni nín làá bɔ́ntí ni-ba pu kí nīn cáá mbimbimɔntiim kí mɔ̀tīí nīn tɔ́ Unimbɔti mmɔntiimee.

eng: waiting for and hastening the coming day of God. Because of that day, the heavens will be destroyed by fire and the elements will melt away in the flames.
local: Kí nīn cí ŋwiin ŋun Unimbɔti làá dāá dāan̄ee, ŋwiin gbanti ní ŋŋmi làá dāá wà kutagbɔŋu lìblib ní tin kɔkɔ bí yilpuee ń dùntī. An puee pɔ́ɔm̀maan ni-ba án nín làá ŋá pu ŋwiinee ń bàń malaayee.

eng: But according to his promise we are waiting for a new heaven and a new earth, where righteousness is at home.
local: Kun puee Unimbɔti pōoǹ yii u làá fàtīí náań-ti kutagbɔmpɔŋu nì kitimpɔŋ. Duulinya gbantinee, bin baba ŋáań Unimbɔti lammee di dāa kíǹ ki bí. Duulinya un ti kā ki cée dee.

eng: Therefore, dear friends, while you are waiting for these things to happen, make every effort to be found by him in peace—pure and faultless.
local: Míǹ puee m-nabineeŋkaamu, ŋyunti ŋun ni dáá kā ki cí duulinya gbanti ń bàńee, ní pɔ́ɔm̀maan nì ni-pɔŋŋu nyɔkɔm kí nīn bímaan circir, Unimbɔti ń taa ká ni-pu tiba. Nimbi nì u kansikinee, ŋgbansɔŋfi ń nīn bí.

eng: Consider the patience of our Lord to be salvation, just as our dear friend and brother Paul wrote to you according to the wisdom given to him.
local: Ní nīn nyí yii ní ŋmā kí ká difiilee pu ní ti-Dindaan bíi jīǹ kimɔtii kaa láá bāań nìn díǹ. Míǹ ní Unimbɔti tīī ti-nabiki Pɔɔl iciin u mun ŋmàn̄-ni an bɔti gbanti.

eng: speaking of these things in all his letters. Some of his remarks are hard to understand, and people who are ignorant and whose faith is weak twist them to their own destruction, just as they do the other scriptures.
local: U-gbɔnti tin kɔkɔ u ŋmàn̄ee, míǹ ní u sòon̄ an bɔti pu. U nín ŋmàn̄ tiŋyee, ajan aba pɔ̄ɔ̄ bí gbìl̄ taapu malaa, ní binib bin nyil kaa náaǹee nì bin kaa béè Unimbɔti bɔti ki kɔ́ poonee kpántí tibɔti gbanti taapu mbamin, kii bi nín pée tí kpántí Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon ajan aba puee. An nibee bí ki nyàab̀ mbusu ní ki tòó bi-ba pu.

eng: Therefore, dear friends, since you have been warned in advance, be on guard so that you arent led off course into the error of sinful people, and lose your own safe position.
local: Ama nimbee, m-nabineeŋkaamu, kɔŋkɔnnee ti kpáafì-ni, ní nīn nyím̀maan ni-ba, án nín làá ŋá pu ní taa gáatí kí nóó binikpitib bin bí diyìntil poonee kí fá difiil paaŋŋu ŋun poon ni yúee.

eng: Instead, grow in the grace and knowledge of our Lord and savior Jesus Christ. To him belongs glory now and forever. Amen.
local: Ama ní nīn gbíntì ki nìkì ki béetí ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo un sá ti-Fiiliuee ki cá, nì u-ŋanti tin u ŋáań-tee poon. Tí tīīmaan-u tinyool kɔŋkɔnnee kí nīn cáa cá jaanjaan! Ameeni.

eng: I want you to recall what the holy prophets foretold as well as what the Lord and savior commanded through your apostles.
local: M là ní béé tibɔti tin Unimbɔti circir bɔnaatiliibi nìn lī dooooyee, nì tin ti-Dindaan un sá ti-Fiiliuee dūu ŋúbiǹ u-kpambaliŋ dàkā-nee.

eng: Most important, know this: in the last days scoffers will come, jeering, living by their own cravings.
local: Ama ní nīn nyí iwinjoolkaa poonee, binib biba làá nyā kí nīn ŋáań min fáatiǹ-bee, ki làá dāá nīn bèel̄-ni.

eng: and saying, Where is the promise of his coming? After all, nothing has changed—not since the beginning of creation, nor even since the ancestors died.
local: ní ki dāa lì-ni yii: «Kéè, Yeesu Kriisitoo pōoǹ-ni yii u gítińaaa? Kin, doooo nee, u bí la ní kaa bàń? Doooo bitiŋkpikib áli nì díǹ, waa gítìń. Doooo duulinya nín náańee, tin pée bée di gbíntìi bí nee.».

eng: But they fail to notice that, by Gods word, heaven and earth were formed long ago out of water and by means of water.
local: Binib bin sòoǹ mimmee, bi nyí, ama ní ki yíì kaa fōō min Unimbɔti lēe. Unimbɔti nìn sòon̄ u-nyɔkɔn ní kutagbɔŋu nì kitiŋ nyáń. Nì nnyim ní u dūu náań kitiŋ, ní ki gāntì kitiŋ nì nnyim.

eng: And it was through these that the world of that time was flooded and destroyed.
local: Nì nnyim mbaantiimee ní u yāntì nnyiŋgbeen dɔ́miń ki kúntì dooooyee duulinyau.

eng: But by the same word, heaven and earth are now held in reserve for fire, kept for the Judgment Day and destruction of ungodly people.
local: Unimbɔti di tí lī yii yilpu nì taapu un bí nee dɔ̄ ki cí ŋŋmi ń dāá wà daliŋkin u làá dāá sòoǹ binikpitib bɔti kí kúntí-bee.

eng: Dont let it escape your notice, dear friends, that with the Lord a single day is like a thousand years and a thousand years are like a single day.
local: Ama, m-nabineeŋkaamu, tibɔti tiba di bí tin pu naa máaǹ kí súntée. Tíǹ di sá yii: Ti-Dindaan ceeyee, ŋwiin ŋubɔ sá kii abin kutukuee ní, ní abin kutuku sá u-cee kii ŋwiin ŋubɔee.

eng: The Lord isnt slow to keep his promise, as some think of slowness, but he is patient toward you, not wanting anyone to perish but all to change their hearts and lives.
local: An kaa sá yii ti-Dindaan sɔ̀m̀ kaa là kí ŋá kii u nín nìn pōoǹ puee, kii biba nín dàkafī puee. Ama u bíi jīǹ ni-pu kimɔtii ki là yii báà ŋma ń kpántí u-bimbim, báà ubɔ ń taa wāŋkì ní.

eng: From Paul, Silvanus, and Timothy: To the church of the Thessalonians, which is in God our Father, and in the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Man Pɔɔl nì Siilasi nì Tiimootee di ŋmàn̄ kugbɔŋu nee. Ti ŋmàn̄-ku kí tīī nimbi Kriisitoo nikitil din bí Teesalooniiki ki sá ti-Baa Unimbɔti nì ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo yabee ní.

eng: This will happen when he comes on that day to receive honor from his holy people and to be admired by everyone who has believed—and our testimony to you was believed.
local: Daliŋkin u yaa láa gítìń bin sá u-yabee nì bin kɔkɔ fōō-u ki kííyee cee bí tīī-u tinyoolee kí pàkà-uee, ni mun dāa bí niin, kun puee ni fōō ti-bɔti tin ti sòon̄ nì nee ki kíí.

eng: We are constantly praying for you for this: that our God will make you worthy of his calling and accomplish every good desire and faithful work by his power.
local: An puee ti mèé Unimbɔti ni-pu baabadal ki bàlifī yii ú yàntī ní nīn sá binib bin máaǹ dimaŋfal din pu u yíiń-nee. Ti gíti mèé-u yii u-pɔŋŋu ń yàntī ní ŋmā kí ŋá tiŋan tin ni dàkafī kí ŋée, kí yàntī ni-fookiiu tuuŋŋi ń còom̀ mmɔntiim.

eng: Then the name of our Lord Jesus will be honored by you, and you will be honored by him, consistent with the grace of our God and the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Mimmee, ni-puee ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo yindi làá ká tinyool, ní u mun puee ni mun ń ká tinyool nì ti-Nimbɔtiu nì ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo ŋanti pu.

eng: Grace and peace to all of you from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Unimbɔti ti-Baa nì ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo ń ŋá-ni tiŋan kí tīī-ni ŋgbansɔŋfi.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we must always thank God for you. This is only right because your faithfulness is growing by leaps and bounds, and the love that all of you have for each other is increasing.
local: Nnabiyaamu, ti ŋmāntì kí nīn jáaḿ Unimbɔti ni-pu múlmul. An lafun máaǹ míǹ, kun puee ni-fookiiu mɔ̀tīi wīintí ki cá, ní dineendi din ni cáá tɔb puee mun mɔ̀tīi wīintí.

eng: Thats why we ourselves are bragging about you in Gods churches. We tell about your endurance and faithfulness in all the harassments and trouble that you have put up with.
local: Nimbi pu ní timbi mɔ̀ɔǹ Unimbɔti nikitiŋ cee, ni nín gbíntìi pɔ̄ɔntí ki ŋúb́ ni-fookiiu falaabi kɔkɔ nì agìń ŋin poon ni bée.

eng: This shows that Gods judgment is right, and that you will be considered worthy of Gods kingdom for which you are suffering.
local: Míǹ di dàkà Unimbɔti bɔsooŋyi nín bí deedee miŋyee, kun puee an di làá yàntī ní ŋmā kí kɔ́ Unimbɔti Beel din pu ni bí ki jīǹ falaayee.

eng: After all, its right for God to pay back the ones making trouble for you with trouble.
local: Kun puee Unimbɔti sá deedeedaan ní, falaa ní u làá dāá gíiǹ kí téeǹ bin yàntī ni jīǹ falaayee.

eng: and to pay back you who are having trouble with relief along with us. This payback will come when the Lord Jesus is revealed from heaven with his powerful angels.
local: U làá dāá kàan̄ nimbi bin jíń falaayee nì timbi nsɔŋfin ŋyunti ŋun ti-Dindaan Yeesu yaa láa nyánní yilpu nì u-tuuŋyi ipɔndambee.

eng: He will give justice with blazing fire to those who dont recognize God and dont obey the good news of our Lord Jesus.
local: U làá dāan̄ nì ŋŋmi kí dáaĺ bin kaa nyí Unimbɔti, kaa fōō ti-Dindaan Yeesu Bɔmɔntiitee ki kííyee tafal.

eng: They will pay the penalty of eternal destruction away from the Lords presence and away from his mighty glory.
local: Bi-tafadaaldi di dāa sá bí nàan̄-bi kí nīn cá jaanjaan, bí taa nín̄ dàbiǹ ti-Dindaan nì u-nyooti nì u-pɔŋŋu.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we have a request for you concerning our Lord Jesus Christs coming and when we are gathered together to be with him.
local: Nnabiyaamu, ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo làá gítiń ní ti làá dāá kóoǹ kí kpāfì u-cee. Tibɔti gbanti puee, tin ti cáá kí kpāafì-nee sɔ̀.

eng: It will happen with every sort of wicked deception of those who are heading toward destruction because they have refused to love the truth that would allow them to be saved.
local: U dāa cáá tin pée sá ikpitee ki kíiĺ bin wáŋkèe, kun puee bi yíì kaa néeń ibaamɔn in bàà ŋūǹ kí fīī-bee.

eng: This is why God will send them an influence that will mislead them so that they will believe the lie.
local: An pu ní Unimbɔti dū mpɔn ŋun làá yàntī bí gáatée ki tīiń-bi án nín làá ŋá pu bí nīn nóó tinyimɔmbɔtee.

eng: The result will be that everyone will be judged who is not convinced by the truth but is happy with injustice.
local: Míǹ puee Unimbɔti làá dāá dáaĺ bin yíì kaa fōō ibaamɔn ki kíí ki néeń ikpitee tafal.

eng: But we always must thank God for you, brothers and sisters who are loved by God. This is because he chose you from the beginning to be the first crop of the harvest. This brought salvation, through your dedication to God by the Spirit and through your belief in the truth.
local: Nnabiyaamu, ti làá nīn pée jáaḿ Unimbɔti ni-pu ní baabadal, nimbi bin ti-Dindaan néeńee. Di sá yii Unimbɔti péé ki lútì-ni ki fīī-ni nì u-Fam min yāntì ni kpántì u-yabee nì ni nín fōō ibaamɔn ki kííyee pu.

eng: God called all of you through our good news so you could possess the honor of our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Míǹ pu ní Unimbɔti dī Tibɔmɔntiil tin ti sòoǹ nì ni nee pu ki yíiń-ni ní nīn cáá ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo nyooti.

eng: So then, brothers and sisters, stand firm and hold on to the traditions we taught you, whether we taught you in person or through our letter.
local: Míǹ puee nnabiyaamu, tibɔti tin ti cáań ki dàkà-ni nee, ní nīn ŋúb́-tì an píkí. An yaa sá tin ti sòon̄ kinyɔkɔneeyaaa, an yaa tí sá tin ti ŋmàn̄-neeyaaa, ní kpāafìmaan-tì tiŋan.

eng: Our Lord Jesus Christ himself and God our Father loved us and through grace gave us eternal comfort and a good hope.
local: Ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo u-ba nì ti-Baa Unimbɔti un néeń-ti ki ŋá-ti tiŋan ki tīī-ti dipɔɔsɔŋkil din kaa cáá dikúntilee nì dimákamɔntiilee.

eng: May he encourage your hearts and give you strength in every good thing you do or say.
local: ń gbéeǹ ni-pɔbiliŋ kunyannyaau kí tīī-ni mpɔn ní ŋmā kí nīn ŋáań tin ŋān̄ee ni-tuuŋŋi nì ni-bɔsoontam poon.

eng: We dont want you to be easily confused in your mind or upset if you hear that the day of the Lord is already here, whether you hear it through some spirit, a message, or a letter supposedly from us.
local: Ní taa yàntī ni-pɔbiliŋ ń bīil̀ binib nín lì yii ti-Dindaan gbanti pūtì kpáàa bāańee pu. Báà ubɔ di yaa bí yii u fōō Unimbɔti cee tibɔti ní ki nāatì-naaa, ubɔ di yaa tí bí yii tibɔti àá kugbɔŋu ní u fōō ti-cee kí dūú nàatī-nee, ní taa fōō kí kíí.

eng: Dont let anyone deceive you in any way. That day wont come unless the rebellion comes first and the person who is lawless is revealed, who is headed for destruction.
local: Ní taa pée yàntī ubɔ ń dī tiba pu kí ŋmàtiǹ-ni. See binib ń dāá fá Unimbɔti ní utɔŋfiti un ti-Dindaan làá dāá nàan̄ee ń nyā kí jī mpaan, kí wàatī ní ti-Dindaan wiiŋŋu ń nín bàń.

eng: He is the opponent of every so-called god or object of worship and promotes himself over them. So he sits in Gods temple, displaying himself to show that he is God.
local: Utɔŋfitee kaa dāa kíikí binib ń nóó Unimbɔtaaa, kaa dāa kíikí bí jāam̀ tiba nì tiba. U làá dāá yíí kí mɔ̄tì míǹ ní kí tin kpáàa kɔ́ kí kàl̄ kunimbɔtidiin ní kí dū u-ba kí gíiǹ Unimbɔti.

eng: You remember that I used to tell you these things while I was with you, dont you?.
local: Naa téetí yii m nín nìn dáá bí ni-ceeyee, m nìn tùkū-ni an bɔtaaa?.

eng: Now you know what holds him back so that he can be revealed when his time comes.
local: Ama tiba di dáá ŋúb́-u ní an bɔtee kaa láá bíi ŋáań kɔŋkɔnnee. Ni mun nyí kudɔŋu kun dáá ŋúb́-uee. An puee, see utɔŋfitee yuntiŋu yaa láa bàn̄ee ní ú nín nyā kí jī mpaan.

eng: The hidden plan to live without any law is at work now, but it will be secret only until the one who is holding it back is out of the way.
local: U-pɔŋŋu bí dibalin ki bíi tūǹ ditundi dooooyee. Ama un ŋúb́-uee yaa láa búntèe.

eng: Then the person who is lawless will be revealed. The Lord Jesus will destroy him with the breath from his mouth. When the Lord comes, his appearance will put an end to him.
local: niinee ní utɔŋfitee ń nyā, ní ti-Dindaan ń dāá púfí-u ní ú kpú, ní u-dāantam nín dāa jìl̀ puee ń nàan̄-u.

eng: When the person who is lawless comes, it will happen through Satans effort, with all kinds of fake power, signs, and wonders.
local: Utɔŋfitee làá dāá dāan̄ nì Sitaan pɔŋŋu ní ki ŋáań maamaacib kɔkɔ nì tidaandi nì tinyimɔŋkpaŋkpantiil.

eng: Finally, brothers and sisters, pray for us so that the Lords message will spread quickly and be honored, just like it happened with you.
local: Nnabiyaamu, m-bɔjoolkaati sèé: ní nīn mèé ti-pu, án nín làá ŋá pu Unimbɔti bɔti ń gbìtī kí bà ntimu poon malaa malaa, kí nīn cáá tinyool kii an nín ŋá ni-cee miŋyee.

eng: Even when we were with you we were giving you this command: If anyone doesnt want to work, they shouldnt eat.
local: Di sá ti nín nìn bí ni-ceeyee, ti nìn tùkū-ni tibɔti tiba yii: «Un kaa là kí tɔ̄ ditundee kaa máaǹ kí jī mun.».

eng: We hear that some of you are living an undisciplined life. They arent working, but they are meddling in other peoples business.
local: Kun puee ti gbìl̄ yii ni-kansikin biba sá biwutijoodambi ki kā kaa ŋáań diba ní ki cáǹ bi-ba ki kɔ̄ntí bi-jutiibi bɔtin.

eng: By the Lord Jesus Christ, we command and encourage such people to work quietly and put their own food on the table.
local: Nì ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo yindi puee, ti tùkù ki kpáafí an nibee yii bí tɔ̄ ditundi nì dipɔɔsɔŋkil poon kí kpíiǹ bi-ba.

eng: Brothers and sisters, dont get discouraged in doing what is right.
local: Ama nimbee, nnabiyaamu, ní taa yàntī tiŋan diŋál.

eng: Take note of anyone who doesnt obey what we have said in this letter. Dont associate with them so they will be ashamed of themselves.
local: Un di yaa kaa tɔ́ tibɔti tin ti ŋmàn̄-ni kugbɔŋu nee poonee, ní dáań-u, ni nì u ń taa ti nín̄ nōotí, icoo ń kɔ́-u.

eng: Dont treat them like enemies, but warn them like you would do for a brother or sister.
local: Ní taa nín dū-u kí kpántí ni-nannanliu ní, ama ní sāŋkì-u kii unil nín sáŋkí u-nabiki tibɔti puee.

eng: May the Lord of peace himself give you peace always in every way. The Lord be with all of you.
local: Ti-Dindaan un tìì ŋgbansɔŋfee u-ba ń tīī-ni ŋgbansɔŋfi ŋyunti kamaa nì tikɔkɔ poon. Ti-Dindaan ń nīn bí ni-kɔkɔ cee.

eng: I, Paul, am writing this greeting with my own hand. This verifies that the letter is from me, as in every letter of mine. This is how I write.
local: Ijaam nee, man Pɔɔl m-ba ŋal di ŋmàn̄. M-gbɔnti kɔkɔ poonee, m nín nyìitī m-ŋal puee dee. Kpèèmaan m-ŋmaŋmabiti dee.

eng: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with all of you.
local: Ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo ŋanti ń nīn bí ni-kɔkɔ pu.

eng: Pray too that we will be rescued from inappropriate and evil people since everyone that we meet wont respond with faith.
local: Ní mèè ti-pu, án nín làá ŋá pu Unimbɔti ń nyāntí-ti binikpitib nì bitɔŋfitiibi ŋaanee, kun puee an kaa sá binib kɔkɔ di fòol̄ Unimbɔti bɔti ki kíikí.

eng: But the Lord is faithful and will give you strength and protect you from the evil one.
local: Ama ti-Dindaan sá ibaamɔndaan ní. U làá yàntī ní nīn ŋúb́ an pɔ̄ɔ̄, kí mɔ̄tì ni-pu Sitaan ukpiti ń taa ŋmā-ni.

eng: We are confident about you in the Lord—that you are doing and will keep doing what we tell you to do.
local: Ti-Dindaan puee, ti cáá ni-pu dimákal ki béè mbaamɔn yii tin ti dàkā-nee, ni bíi ŋáań míǹ, ki làá nīn gbíntì ki ŋáań míǹ ki cá.

eng: May the Lord lead your hearts to express Gods love and Christs endurance.
local: Ti-Dindaan ń yàntī ní béé ni-pɔbiliŋ poon Unimbɔti neendi nì Kriisitoo nín tìì kimɔtii kiŋyee.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we command you in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ to stay away from every brother or sister who lives an undisciplined life that is not in line with the traditions that you received from us.
local: Nnabiyaamu, nì ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo yindi puee, ní gàntīmaan nnabiyaamu mun di sá biwutijoodambi kaa là kí ŋá min ní ti dàkà-nee.

eng: You yourselves know how you need to imitate us because we were not undisciplined when we were with you.
local: Nimbi ni-ba nyí ni nín máaǹ kí fōō min timbi ŋáańee, kun puee ti nín nìn bí ni-ceeyee, taa nìn sá biwutijoodambi.

eng: We didnt eat anyones food without paying for it. Instead, we worked night and day with effort and hard work so that we would not impose on you.
local: Taa dɔ́miń ki kā ubɔ pu u kpīintí-ti fám. Ama kí taa nín̄ kā ubɔ pu udaan ń nīn kpīintí-tee puee, ti nìn tūǹ ditundi ní ŋwiimpu nì kunyeeu ki gì.

eng: We did this to give you an example to imitate, not because we didnt have a right to insist on financial support.
local: Ti nín nìn bí ki ŋáań mimmee, an kaa sá yii taa nìn cáá nsan kí fōō ni-jikaati, ama ti ŋá míǹ ki dàkā-ni ní fōō min timbi ŋáańee ní.

eng: From Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by Gods will, to promote the promise of life that is in Christ Jesus.
local: Man Pɔɔl di ŋmàn̄ kugbɔŋu nee, man un Unimbɔti là ḿ nīn sá Yeesu Kriisitoo kpambal ki sòoǹ dimaŋfal din u pōoǹ Yeesu Kriisitoo tìì bin fōō-u ki kííyee bɔtee.

eng: Now his grace is revealed through the appearance of our savior, Christ Jesus. He destroyed death and brought life and immortality into clear focus through the good news.
local: Kɔŋkɔnnee u nyántì u-ŋanti gbanti mpaampiin pu ŋyunti ŋun ti-Fiiliu Yeesu Kriisitoo dɔ́mińee. Úǹ di dɔ́miń ki kúntì ŋkúm pɔŋŋu, ní ki cáań dimaŋfal din kaa kúntée Tibɔmɔntiil tin u cáańee pu.

eng: I was appointed a messenger, apostle, and teacher of this good news.
local: An Bɔmɔntiiti gbanti ní Unimbɔti dūu ŋúbiǹ-mi ḿ nīn sá u-kpambal ki báakí tibɔtee ki dàkà-tì binib.

eng: This is also why Im suffering the way I do, but Im not ashamed. I know the one in whom Ive placed my trust. Im convinced that God is powerful enough to protect what he has placed in my trust until that day.
local: An pu ní m bíi jīǹ falaa un m jīǹ nee. Ama an kaa cáá-mi icoo, kun puee m nyí un m fōō ki kííyee nín sá uŋyee. Míǹ ní m nyí yii u cáá mpɔn kí kpāafì tin u dūu ŋúbiǹ-mee kí dāá sāā u-gítintam dal.

eng: Hold on to the pattern of sound teaching that you heard from me with the faith and love that are in Christ Jesus.
local: Á nīn ŋúb́ ibaamɔn bɔti tin m sòon̄ ki tùkū-see, kí nīn sòoǹ-tì kii a nín gbìl̄-tì m-cee puee. Nín̄ cáá fookii nì dineendi ti nín ŋá ubaanti nì Yeesu Kriisitooyee pu.

eng: Protect this good thing that has been placed in your trust through the Holy Spirit who lives in us.
local: Á kpāafì Tibɔmɔntiil tin Unimbɔti dūu ŋúbiǹ-see nì u-Fam min kóò ti-nee tūtuntam pu.

eng: You know that everyone in Asia has turned away from me, including Phygelus and Hermogenes.
local: Kii a nín nyí puee, bin kɔkɔ sá Asiiyee dū-mi ki fá ki kpáaǹ Fiijeel nì Eemɔgeeni.

eng: May the Lord show mercy to Onesiphorus household, because he supported me many times and he wasnt ashamed of my imprisonment.
local: Ti-Dindaan ń ŋá Ɔneesiifɔɔr dicaŋku tiŋan. Di sá yii u nìn ŋmēentí-mi múlmul, kaa cáá man usarkadaan pu icoo.

eng: After I arrived in Rome, he quickly looked for me and found me.
local: Áli gba u nín dɔ́miń Roomee, u nyàab̄-mi nì dinimbinaamandi, ní ki dāa ká-mi.

eng: May the Lord allow him to find his mercy on that day .
local: Ibɔsoon dalee, ti-Dindaan ń dāá ŋá Ɔneesiifɔɔr tiŋan. Tin kɔkɔ u ŋá ki tīī-mi Eefeesi tiŋkinee, a nyí ki jítiǹ báà ŋma.

eng: To Timothy, my dear child. Grace, mercy, and peace from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Lord.
local: M ŋmàn̄ kugbɔŋu nee ki tīī sii Tiimootee m-bineeŋkaaki ní. Ti-Baa Unimbɔti nì ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo ń ŋá-si tiŋan, kí sɔ̄ a-saai, kí tīī-si ŋgbansɔŋfi.

eng: Im grateful to God, whom I serve with a good conscience as my ancestors did. I constantly remember you in my prayers day and night.
local: M jáaḿ Unimbɔti un m nóó nì dipɔɔmɔntiil, kii m-naanjab mun nín nìn nóó-u puee. M jáaḿ-u tikpil ŋyunti ŋun m téetí a-pu ki mèé múlmul ŋwiimpu nì kunyeeuee.

eng: When I remember your tears, I long to see you so that I can be filled with happiness.
local: Di sá yii m téetí a-nyinnyim pu, ki mɔ̀ ki nyàab̀ kí ká-si, m-pɔbil ń pīil̀.

eng: Im reminded of your authentic faith, which first lived in your grandmother Lois and your mother Eunice. Im sure that this faith is also inside you.
local: M dáá gbíntìi téetí fookiibaamɔn un a cáá Yeesu puee pu, fookii un a-naan Loowiisi nì a-na Eeniisi nìn pée cááyee. M nyí nì ibaamɔn yii a mun cáá fookii gbanti.

eng: Because of this, Im reminding you to revive Gods gift that is in you through the laying on of my hands.
local: An pu ní m téetí-si yii á mɔ̀tīí nyàatī mpɔn ŋun Unimbɔti tīī-si ŋyunti ŋun m nìn dū m-ŋalii ki páaǹ a-pu a fōōyee.

eng: God didnt give us a spirit that is timid but one that is powerful, loving, and self-controlled.
local: Kun puee Unimbɔti nín tīī-ti Ŋfam miŋyee kaa sá Ŋfam min tìì tifaandee, ama an sá Ŋfam min tìì mpɔn, nì dineendi kí yàntī unil ń ŋmā kí ŋúb́ u-bee ní.

eng: So dont be ashamed of the testimony about the Lord or of me, his prisoner. Instead, share the suffering for the good news, depending on Gods power.
local: Míǹ puee á taa nín̄ cáá icoo kí sòoǹ ti-Dindaan pu, kí taa nín̄ cáá icoo ma mun pu, nì min kɔkɔ m sá usarkadaan ti-Dindaan puee. Ama Tibɔmɔntiil puee, á jī falaa un man bíi jīǹee nì Unimbɔti pɔŋŋu.

eng: God is the one who saved and called us with a holy calling. This wasnt based on what we have done, but it was based on his own purpose and grace that he gave us in Christ Jesus before time began.
local: Úǹ di fīī-ti ní ki dū-ti ki kpántì u-yab. An kaa sá atuun aba ní ti túń ní u fīī-ti, an sá úǹ u-ba di lēē kí ŋá-ti tiŋan gbanti doooo jaanjaan Yeesu Kriisitoo pu.

eng: So, my child, draw your strength from the grace that is in Christ Jesus.
local: Míǹ puee sii man biki, pɔ́ɔǹ a-ba nì tiŋan tin ti ká ti nì Yeesu Kriisitoo nín ŋá ubaantee pu.

eng: This is why I endure everything for the sake of those who are chosen by God so that they too may experience salvation in Christ Jesus with eternal glory.
local: An pu ní m ŋmèé tikɔkɔ án nín làá ŋá pu bin Unimbɔti lútèe ń ŋmā kí ká difiil din bí Yeesu Kriisitoonee, nì tinyool tin làá nīn bí jaanjaanee.

eng: This saying is reliable: If we have died together, we will also live together.
local: Tibɔti tin m làá sòoǹee sá ibaamɔn ní: «Ti nì u yaa kpáfì ki kpíìyee, see ti nì u ń dāá nīn tí bí dimaŋfalin.

eng: If we endure, we will also rule together. If we deny him, he will also deny us.
local: Ti yaa pɔ́ɔǹ ki tin kátì-kuee, ti nì u di dāa tí jīǹ dibeel. Ti yaa néè yii taa nyí-u, u mun làá dāá néé yii waa nyí-ti.

eng: If we are disloyal, he stays faithful because he cant be anything else than what he is.
local: Ti yaa kaa yúliǹ-u deedee, úǹ làá nīn yú deedee, kun puee waa ŋūǹ kí néé kí kpántí u-deedeebítam.».

eng: Remind them of these things and warn them in the sight of God not to engage in battles over words that arent helpful and only destroy those who hear them.
local: Á téetí bi-kɔkɔ kí tùkù-bi yii nì Unimbɔti puee, bí taa nín̄ kpākantí tɔb anyɔɔbun aba pu. An nikpɔŋkpɔkɔkuee kaa cāabìń tiŋan, ama an bíiĺ bin pílifée ní.

eng: Make an effort to present yourself to God as a tried-and-true worker, who doesnt need to be ashamed but is one who interprets the message of truth correctly.
local: Pɔ́ɔǹ a-ba Unimbɔti ń ká-si kii utɔntɔnli un tuuŋŋi kaa cāabìń icooyee, kí sòoǹ ibaamɔn Bɔmɔntiitee nín sá puee deedee.

eng: Avoid their godless discussions, because they will lead many people into ungodly behavior.
local: Taa cèeǹ a-nyɔkɔ tibɔfan nikpɔŋkpɔkɔku kun nyáń tiba nì Yeesu Kriisitoo bɔti tin a fōōyee, di sá bin ŋúb́ an bɔtee mɔ̀tīi gáatí Unimbɔti nimbiin ní.

eng: and their ideas will spread like an infection. This includes Hymenaeus and Philetus.
local: Binib gbanti dàkatam bí kii mbuunee ní ki ŋmóò ki lɔ̀tī tiwun. Imeenee nì Fiileeti mun bí binib gbanti kansikin.

eng: who have deviated from the truth by claiming that the resurrection has already happened. This has undermined some peoples faith.
local: Bi nyáń ibaamɔn saŋŋu poon ki yāntì bi-jutiibi bin fōō Yeesu ki kííyee fá bi-fookiiu bi nín lì yii dooooyee ní bitaŋkpiibi fīkitèe pu.

eng: Gods solid foundation is still standing with this sign: The Lord knows the people who belong to him, and Everyone who confesses the Lords name must avoid wickedness. .
local: Ama nì míǹ kɔkɔee, Unimbɔti bɔnyeel din u dūu tíĺ taapuee pée pɔ̄ɔ̄ ní ki yú di-laŋki. An bɔnyeelee di sá yii: «Ti-Dindaan nyí bin sá u-yabee», ní «Un kamaa nyí yii u sá ti-Dindaan yɔuee máaǹ kí fá ikpiti.».

eng: Take the things you heard me say in front of many other witnesses and pass them on to faithful people who are also capable of teaching others.
local: A nín gbìl̄ m sòon̄ tin diniwul nimbiinee, dū-tì kí ŋúbiǹ binib bin yú deedee ki ŋūǹ kí dàkèe, bi mun ń ŋmā kí dūú dàkà bin kíǹee.

eng: In a mansion, there arent just gold and silver bowls but also some bowls that are made of wood and clay. Some are meant for special uses, some for garbage.
local: Diciŋkpaandinee, an kaa sá sindaariya nì animbil kúti booŋŋi baba di bí, ama aboon ŋin bi máń nì kuyɔkɔuee nì itúun mun bí. Sindaariya nì animbil kúti booŋŋi yú ki cí iwiŋkpaan dal ní, ní itúun nì aboon ŋin bi máń nì kuyɔkɔuee sá báà ŋma ń nīn cáa ŋáań min u lèe.

eng: So if anyone washes filth off themselves, they will be set apart as a special bowl. They will be useful to the owner of the mansion for every sort of good work.
local: Un yaa fá ikpiti in bɔti m sòoǹee kpántì umɔnti dee ki sá kii sindaariya booŋŋi ŋin yú ki cí iwiŋkpaanee. Di sá yii u bí ki tīī u-kpiliu an kɔkɔ pu ú dū-u kí tɔ̄ atummɔnti.

eng: Run away from adolescent cravings. Instead, pursue righteousness, faith, love, and peace together with those who confess the Lord with a clean heart.
local: Á fá tinaacimpɔn landɔkɔkpitii kí nyàab̀ deedeebítam nì fookii nì dineendi nì ŋgbansɔŋfi, sii nì bin kɔkɔ jáaḿ ti-Dindaan nì dipɔɔmɔntiilee.

eng: Avoid foolish and thoughtless discussions, since you know that they produce conflicts.
local: Ama á fá fám fám nikpɔŋkpɔkɔku kun sá tibɔfanee, kun puee sii a-ba nyí yii an nikpɔŋkpɔkɔkuee pée kúntí nì kujaau ŋma ní.

eng: Gods slave shouldnt be argumentative but should be kind toward all people, able to teach, patient.
local: An mun kaa máaǹ yii ti-Dindaan tɔntɔnliu nì binib ń nīn jáà, ama u máaǹ kí nīn néeń báà ŋma, kí nīn ŋūǹ ki dàkà binib nì kimɔtii.

eng: and should correct opponents with gentleness. Perhaps God will change their mind and give them a knowledge of the truth.
local: U máaǹ kí nīn sá unil un mɔ̄ee kí ŋmā kí dàkà bin kpākantí-uee. An yaa yíì Unimbɔti táá yàntī bí kpántí bi-bimbim kí ŋmā kí béé ibaamɔn.

eng: They may come to their senses and escape from the devils trap that holds them captive to do his will.
local: kí gítiń nsammɔnti poon kí ŋmátí Sitaan taliŋku kun sàl̄-bi ki yāntì bi ŋáań min u lèe.

eng: Accept your share of suffering like a good soldier of Christ Jesus.
local: Si mun kūtì kí jī falaa kii Yeesu Kriisitoo janjamɔntiuee.

eng: Nobody who serves in the military gets tied up with civilian matters, so that they can please the one who recruited them.
local: Ujanjal un là kí tɔ̄ ditundi kí fáatíǹ u-kpiliuee kaa ti yì u-ba kí ŋá kii binifantantiibi nín tɔ́ bi-ba ki ŋáań miŋyee.

eng: Also in the same way, athletes dont win unless they follow the rules.
local: Kí wàatī un cūtì ditaatandi kpaŋkpakantikuuee ń ŋmā kí sāǹ kí jītí bin kíǹee pu kí nīn sá upeepeekaa kí fōō tipaalee, see ú nīn sáń ki táá nì kpaŋkpakantiku marau.

eng: A hardworking farmer should get the first share of the crop.
local: Ukpaal un di kùn̄ u-saaku nì digìńdee di dāa tí sá upeepeekaa kí lēeń tijin u-saakun.

eng: Think about what Im saying; the Lord will give you understanding about everything.
local: Á dàkafì tibɔti tin m sòoǹee pu, kun puee ti-Dindaan làá tūtuǹ-si á gbìl̄ ti-taapu kɔkɔ.

eng: Remember Jesus Christ, who was raised from the dead and descended from David. This is my good news.
local: Á téetí Yeesu Kriisitoo un fīkitì ditaŋkpiilinee pu. U sá Daafiidi naantiiu kii Tibɔmɔntiil tin m sòoǹee nín tùkū-ti puee ní.

eng: This is the reason Im suffering to the point that Im in prison like a common criminal. But Gods word cannot be imprisoned.
local: Tibɔmɔntiil gbanti sòontam pu ní m bí ki jīǹ falaa bi bóob́-mi ikusaka kii m sá unikpitee, ama baa bóob́ Unimbɔti bɔti tíǹ.

eng: Understand that the last days will be dangerous times.
local: Á nīn nyí yii iyuntijoolkaa poonee, án làá dāá nīn pɔ̄ɔ̄.

eng: But you have paid attention to my teaching, conduct, purpose, faithfulness, patience, love, and endurance.
local: Ama sii ká m-dàkatam nì m-bimbim nì m-landɔkɔi nì m-fookiiu nì m-mɔtiiki nì m-neendi nì m-pɔ́ɔǹtam.

eng: You have seen me experience physical abuse and ordeals in places such as Antioch, Iconium, and Lystra. I put up with all sorts of abuse, and the Lord rescued me from it all!.
local: nì bi nín páb́-mi puee, nì m-falaau. A nyí tin nìn jítì m-pu Antiyɔɔsi nì Ikooniyum nì Liistiree. Falaa ulɔu ní maa jíń niin? Ama ti-Dindaan nyántì-mi tikɔkɔ poon.

eng: In fact, anyone who wants to live a holy life in Christ Jesus will be harassed.
local: Di sá yii bin di yaa fōō Yeesu Kriisitoo ki kíí ki là kí nīn yú deedee Unimbɔti nimbiinee, see bidambi ń ká digìńdi.

eng: But evil people and swindlers will grow even worse, as they deceive others while being deceived themselves.
local: Ama bitɔŋfitiibi nì bikiililiibee làá nīn mɔ̀tīi múl̀ ki kóò tin kaa ŋān̄ee poon ní baabadal. Bi wáŋkì, ní ki bíi kíiĺ bi-jutiibi mun ń wāŋkì.

eng: But you must continue with the things you have learned and found convincing. You know who taught you.
local: Míǹ puee, sii ń ŋúb́ tibɔti tin a bífì ki fōō ki kíí tiŋanee, kun puee sii nyí bin di dàkā-si an bɔtee.

eng: Since childhood you have known the holy scriptures that help you to be wise in a way that leads to salvation through faith that is in Christ Jesus.
local: Doooo a-biyatin ní a pée nyí Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon bɔti. An di ŋūǹ kí tīī-si iciin in cáǹ unil difiilinee nì fookii un a cáá Yeesu Kriisitoo puee.

eng: Every scripture is inspired by God and is useful for teaching, for showing mistakes, for correcting, and for training character.
local: Unimbɔti di yāntì bi ŋmàn̄ tibɔti gbanti kɔkɔ, ki sá bí dūú dàkà binib ń béé ibaamɔn, kí dàkà unil yìntitam, kí kpòotī-u, kí dàkà unil ú nīn cáá deedee bimbim.

eng: so that the person who belongs to God can be equipped to do everything that is good.
local: án nín làá ŋá pu kí bɔ́ntí Unimbɔti niliu mmɔntiimin ú kítí kí tɔ̄ atummɔnti kɔkɔee.

eng: People will be selfish and love money. They will be the kind of people who brag and who are proud. They will slander others, and they will be disobedient to their parents. They will be ungrateful, unholy.
local: Di sá yii binib dāa kíǹ ki cáá ikummunti, ní ki néeń animbil, ní ki gà bi-biŋŋi, ki cáá alambil, ki sòoǹ ki bíiĺ Unimbɔti, kaa tɔ́ bi-baabi nì bi-nab, kaa dāa nyí tiŋan, kaa tɔ́ tin bí circiree.

eng: unloving, contrary, and critical. They will be without self-control and brutal, and they wont love what is good.
local: Baa dāa cáá dineendi, kaa dāa sūǹ binib saai, ki dāa kúŋfí binib boon, kaa dāa ŋūǹ kí cúú bi-ba, ki dāa sá bitɔŋfitiibi, ki dāa ná tin ŋān̄ee.

eng: They will be people who are disloyal, reckless, and conceited. They will love pleasure instead of loving God.
local: Bi dāa sá bin nyēē binib boon ki nyáfí-bee. Bi dāa sá binib bin an yòontée, ki dāa néeń timɔn, ki dāa néeń tin fáatiǹ-bee ki jítiǹ Unimbɔti.

eng: They will look like they are religious but deny Gods power. Avoid people like this.
local: Bi dāa ŋúb̀ nnimbɔtisan bɔti ní kí yíí kaa là mbaamɔn pɔŋŋu ŋun bí tibɔti gbanti poonee. Á fá an nibee.

eng: Some will slither into households and control immature women who are burdened with sins and driven by all kinds of desires.
local: Bi-kansikin biba kóò binimpoobi bin landɔkɔi pátì bi ŋáań ikpiti ki cáá kɔdeebi kupaauee dumpui ní ki yàntī bi fòol̄ tin bi sòoǹee ki kíikí.

eng: These women are always learning, but they can never arrive at an understanding of the truth.
local: An nimpoobee pílifí tibɔti ní baabadal, ama doooo kii nee, baa ŋūǹ kí béé ibaamɔn.

eng: These people oppose the truth in the same way that Jannes and Jambres opposed Moses. Their minds are corrupt and their faith is counterfeit.
local: Kii Jaaneesi nì Jambiree nín kaa nìn là Mooyiisi bɔti puee, míǹ mbaantiim ní an nibee kaa là ibaamɔn. An sá binib bin landɔkɔi gábitèe, baa nóó Unimbɔti.

eng: But they wont get very far. Their foolishness will become obvious to everyone like those others.
local: Ama baa làá nīn bí kí cù ndan, kun puee báà ŋma làá dāá béé bi-pu yii bi sá bigbaambi, a lì kii Jaaneesi nì Jambiree cee nín nìn jítì puee.

eng: Im giving you this commission in the presence of God and of Christ Jesus, who is coming to judge the living and the dead, and by his appearance and his kingdom.
local: M bíi kpáafí-si tibɔti nee Unimbɔti nimbiin nì Yeesu Kriisitoo un làá dāá sòoǹ binifoobi nì bitaŋkpiibi bɔtee nimbiin, úǹ un làá dāá dāan̄ kii ubɔtee.

eng: Demas has fallen in love with the present world and has deserted me and has gone to Thessalonica. Crescens has gone to Galatia, and Titus has gone to Dalmatia.
local: di sá yii Deemasi dū-mi ki fá ki búntì Teesalooniiki, kun puee u néeń duulinya un poon ti bí nee tikpil. Kriiseensi búntì Galaati, ní Tiiti mun búntì Dalmatii.

eng: Only Luke is with me. Get Mark, and bring him with you. He has been a big help to me in the ministry.
local: Luuki baba di pée kíǹ m-cee. A yaa dòońee, á kpáaǹní-mi Maarki, kun puee u ŋūǹ kí dāá tūtuǹ-mi m-tundi poon.

eng: I sent Tychicus to Ephesus.
local: M dū Tiisiiki ki tú Eefeesi.

eng: When you come, bring along the coat I left with Carpus in Troas. Also bring the scrolls and especially the parchments.
local: A yaa dòońee, á taa súntí kí kpáaǹní-mi m-bɔkutifɔkɔl din m nìn yāntìń Troowaasi tiŋkin Kaarpusi ceeyee, kí kpáaǹní-mi m-gbɔnti, cilinjee tigbɔn tin sá igbanee.

eng: Alexander, the craftsman who works with metal, has really hurt me. The Lord will pay him back for what he has done.
local: Ucaa Aleekisandir ŋá-mi inikpiti tikpil. Ti-Dindaan làá dāá pà-u atuun ŋin u túńee paati.

eng: But watch out for him, because he opposes our teaching.
local: A mun ń nīn nyíǹ-u, kun puee u nìn yíì kpataaa kaa là tibɔti tin ti sòoǹee.

eng: No one took my side at my first court hearing. Everyone deserted me. I hope that God doesnt hold it against them!.
local: Bi nín péé péé ki cúú-mi ki cáá ibɔsoonee, báà ubɔ kaa yúl̀ m-boon. Bi-kɔkɔ dū-mi ki fá ní. Unimbɔti ń taa dūú ŋá-bi tibɔti.

eng: But the Lord stood by me and gave me strength, so that the entire message would be preached through me and so all the nations could hear it. I was also rescued from the lions mouth!.
local: Ama ti-Dindaan yúl̀ m-boon ki tīī-mi mpɔn m ŋúń ki sòon̄ Unimbɔti bɔti mmɔntiimin ki yāntì bin kaa sá Juuda yabee kɔkɔ gbìl̄ tibɔti gbanti, ní m nyáń gbikinti nyɔkɔn.

eng: The Lord will rescue me from every evil action and will save me for his heavenly kingdom. To him be the glory forever and always. Amen.
local: Ti-Dindaan làá nīn pée nyāntí-mi tibɔkpitil kamaa poon míǹ ní kí dāá cáá-mi kí kɔ́ǹ yilpu Beelin nì alaafiya. Úǹ di sá tinyooldaan kí nīn pée cá jaanjaan! Ameeni.

eng: Say hello to Prisca and Aquila and the household of Onesiphorus.
local: Á jāam̀ Priisiil nì Akiilasi, ní kí jāam̀ Ɔneesiifɔɔr dicaŋkun yab.

eng: Preach the word. Be ready to do it whether it is convenient or inconvenient. Correct, confront, and encourage with patience and instruction.
local: Á sòoǹ Unimbɔti bɔti. Báà ŋyunti yaa pɔ̄ɔ̄yaaa, ŋu yaa pɔ̀fàaa, á nīn pée cɔ́ tibɔti gbanti pu, kí dàkà binib bi-yìntitam kí wòoǹ-bi bi-kpitii pu, kí tīī-bi kunyannyaau. Á nīn cáá kimɔtii tikpil a-dàkatam poon.

eng: Erastus stayed in Corinth, and I left Trophimus in Miletus because of his illness.
local: Eeraasiti gbíntì Kɔranti ní Troofiim būǹ. M yāntì u gbíntì Miilee.

eng: Try hard to come to me before winter. Eubulus, Pudens, Linus, Claudia, and all the brothers and sisters say hello.
local: Á pɔ́ɔǹ a-ba kí bàń-mi kí wàatī tiwool yuntiŋu ń nín bàń. Eebulusi nì Pudansi nì Liinusi nì Kloodiya nì nnabiyaamu kɔkɔ jáaḿ-si.

eng: The Lord be with your spirit. Grace be with you all.
local: Ti-Dindaan ń nīn bí a-cee. Unimbɔti ŋanti ń nīn bí ni-pu.

eng: There will come a time when people will not tolerate sound teaching. They will collect teachers who say what they want to hear because they are self-centered.
local: Kun puee iyunti iba dòoń binib kaa dāa gíti là kí pīlifì ibaamɔn dàkatam. Ama bi dāa nōotí bíǹ bi-ba nín fáatì miŋyee ní ki dāa kpáfí bidakaliibi kupaau bi-cee, ní bíǹ dàkà-bi tin bi là kí nīn pílifée.

eng: They will turn their back on the truth and turn to myths.
local: Baa dāa gíti là kí pīlifì ibaamɔn, see bitiŋkpikib nín nìn ŋáań tiŋyee ní bi dāa néeń ki pílifí.

eng: But you must keep control of yourself in all circumstances. Endure suffering, do the work of a preacher of the good news, and carry out your service fully.
local: Ama sii ń ŋúb́ a-ba an kɔkɔ poon, ní kí ŋmèè falaa kí tɔ̄ a-tundi din sá Tibɔmɔntiil sòontamee. Tɔ̄ ditundi din sá Unimbɔti tɔntɔnliu tundee.

eng: Im already being poured out like a sacrifice to God, and the time of my death is near.
local: Ama an yaa kíǹ mamee, ŋyunti bāań ḿ dū m-ba kí ŋá saraa, man kúm yuntiŋu bāań.

eng: I have fought the good fight, finished the race, and kept the faith.
local: M jáń kujamɔntiiu, m sáǹ ki bàn̄ ditaatandi kúntilaŋki ki gbíntìi ŋúb́ m-fookiiu.

eng: At last the champions wreath that is awarded for righteousness is waiting for me. The Lord, who is the righteous judge, is going to give it to me on that day. Hes giving it not only to me but also to all those who have set their heart on waiting for his appearance.
local: Kɔŋkɔnnee dijajil paati di dɔ̄ ki cí-mi. Tíǹ di sá difiil beefuuliiki kin Yeesu un sá ibaamɔn bɔsoonliuee làá dāá tīī-mi ŋwindalee. An kaa dāa sá man baba ní u làá tīī, ama bin bin kɔkɔ néeń ki cí u-gítintamee.

eng: Do your best to come to me quickly.
local: Á pɔ́ɔǹ kí dāan̄ m-cee malaa.

eng: From the elder. To my dear friend Gaius, whom I truly love.
local: An sá man Ukpil di ŋmàn̄ kugbɔŋu nee kí jāam̀ m-nabineeŋkaaki Gaayusi, un m néeń nì ibaamɔnee.

eng: Because of this, if I come, I will bring up what he has done—making unjustified and wicked accusations against us. And as if that were not enough, he not only refuses to welcome the brothers and sisters but stops those who want to do so and even throws them out of the church!.
local: U gífí inyimɔn nì tibɔkpitil ki tūntí ti-pu. Míǹ kɔkɔ kaa yá ki kɔ́-u, kun puee u yíití kaa fòol̄ nnabiyaamu mun jītí u-ceeyee mun, ní bin yaa bàà là kí fōō-bee, u pìintí-bi nsan ki là kí nyāntí-bi Kriisitoo nikitil kansikin. Míǹ puee, m yaa láa bāańee, m làá dāá kàaǹ tin kɔkɔwoooo u ŋáańee binib nimbiin.

eng: Dear friend, dont imitate what is bad but what is good. Whoever practices what is good belongs to God. Whoever practices what is bad has not seen God.
local: M-nabineeŋkaaki, á taa nóó binib bin ŋáań tikpitilee tabɔbiliŋ, ama nín̄ nóó bin ŋáań tiŋanee tabɔbiliŋ. Un ŋáań tiŋanee di sá Unimbɔti niliu, un ŋáań tikpitilee kaa nyí Unimbɔti.

eng: Everyone speaks highly of Demetrius, even the truth itself. We also speak highly of him, and you know that what we say is true.
local: Ama Deemeetriyusi ní báà ŋma pàkà, ní ibaamɔn bɔti tin tin u bí ki sòoǹee mun pàkà-u, ní ti mun pàkà-u. Sii a-ba nyí yii tin ti yaa lēe sá ibaamɔn ní.

eng: I have a lot to say to you, but I dont want to use pen and ink.
local: M bàà cáá tibɔti tikpil kí sòoǹ nì si, ama maa là kí dū ŋgbaŋŋmamɔɔn kí ŋmà.

eng: I hope to see you soon, and we will speak face-to-face.
local: Kun puee m là kí dāan̄ a-cee iwiin ili nee poon, niin ní ti dāa ŋūǹ kí kàl̄ kí sòoǹ tikɔkɔ.

eng: Peace be with you. Your friends here greet you. Greet our friends there by name.
local: Unimbɔti ń tīī-si ŋgbansɔŋfi. A-bɔɔbi jáaḿ-si. Á jāam̀ ti-bɔɔbi kɔkɔ kí tīī-mi.

eng: Dear friend, Im praying that all is well with you and that you enjoy good health in the same way that you prosper spiritually.
local: M-nabineeŋkaaki, m mèé yii tikɔkɔ ń còom̀-si tiŋan á nīn cáá ŋgbampɔn kii a-naŋki nín cáá ŋgbampɔn puee.

eng: I was overjoyed when the brothers and sisters arrived and spoke highly of your faithfulness to the truth, shown by how you live according to the truth.
local: An mɔ́ɔkiǹ-mi tikpil nnabiyaamu nín bāań ki tùkū-mi a nín ŋúb́ ibaamɔn an píkí puee, kii a nín pée còom̄ ibaamɔn poon puee.

eng: I have no greater joy than this: to hear that my children are living according to the truth.
local: Tiba kaa ti bí ki mɔ̄ǹ-mi ki bàn̄ ḿ gbìl̄ yii m-biyaamu bí ki còom̄ ibaamɔn saŋŋu poon, án mɔ́ɔkíǹ-mi tikpil ní.

eng: Dear friend, you act faithfully in whatever you do for our brothers and sisters, even though they are strangers.
local: Kinabineeŋkaai, a nín tūǹ atummɔnti ŋin ki tìì nnabiyaamu ki kpáaǹ bin sá-si binicambee gbee, a lafun dàkā yii a fōō ki kíí tiŋman pu.

eng: They spoke highly of your love in front of the church. You all would do well to provide for their journey in a way that honors God.
local: Bi bāańee, bi máafì a-neendi gbanti pu Kriisitoo nikitil kɔkɔ nimbiin. Mimmee, m mèé-si, á tūtuǹ-bi bi-saŋŋu gbanti poon min làá fáatíǹ Unimbɔtee.

eng: because they left on their journey for the sake of Jesus Christ without accepting any support from the Gentiles.
local: Di sá yii Yeesu tundi pu ní bi còom̄ nsan, kaa fōō tiba bin kaa nyí Unimbɔtee cee.

eng: Therefore, we ought to help people like this so that we can be coworkers with the truth.
local: An puee, bi yaa bāań ti-ceeyee, ti máaǹ kí tūtuǹ binib gbanti. Mimmee, ti mun kútì bi-pu ki cáá ibaamɔn tundi ki cáǹ nyɔkɔpu dee.

eng: I wrote something to the church, but Diotrephes, who likes to put himself first, doesnt welcome us.
local: M nìn ŋmàn̄ kugbɔŋu ki tīī Kriisitoo nikitil ki sòon̄ nì bi kibɔgiŋ, ama Diyootreefi là kí nīn sá bi-kpiliu ní kaa là kí pīlifì tin man sòoǹee.

eng: Theophilus, the first scroll I wrote concerned everything Jesus did and taught from the beginning.
local: Teewoofiil, M nìn ŋmàn̄ m-gbɔmpeepeekaaku poon tin kɔkɔ Yeesu nìn kíĺ ki ŋáań, ki dàkèe.

eng: While he was going away and as they were staring toward heaven, suddenly two men in white robes stood next to them.
local: Bi dáá gbíntìi yú ki kpèé yilpu u nín cá puee, kpalaayee, bininjab bili dee bi-nimbiin ki pēē tiwampin.

eng: They said, Galileans, why are you standing here, looking toward heaven? This Jesus, who was taken up from you into heaven, will come in the same way that you saw him go into heaven.
local: ní ki bí-bi yii: «Galiilee yab, ba pu ní ni yú ki kpèé kutaa pu míǹ? Yeesu un ni ká u yíkì ni-kansikin ki jɔ́ḿ yilpu nee, làá dāá fàtīí tí gítiń míǹ mbaantiim kii ni nín ká u jɔ́m̀ pu nee ní.».

eng: Then they returned to Jerusalem from the Mount of Olives, which is near Jerusalem—a sabbath days journey away.
local: Niinee ní Yeesu kpambaliŋ yíkìń Ɔliifi sufii jool pu ki fātìi nín gítiń Jeerusaleem. Dijoolee kaa nìn dátiǹ Jeerusaleem: an dandatiŋu nìn sá ditaŋkpal diba ní.

eng: When they entered the city, they went to the upstairs room where they were staying. Peter, John, James, and Andrew; Philip and Thomas; Bartholomew and Matthew; James, Alphaeus son; Simon the zealot; and Judas, James son.
local: Bi bāań niinee, ki jɔ́m̀ diditɔntɔkɔl din ni bi nìn pée kpààa kpáfée. An nìn sá Peetroo nì Yoowaneesi nì Yakubu nì Andree nì Fiiliipi nì Toomasi nì Baateeleemii nì Maatiiu nì Yakubu Alfee jipɔɔn nì Siimɔn un nìn là kí jáǹ Room yabee, nì Yakubu jipɔɔn Juudi.

eng: all were united in their devotion to prayer, along with some women, including Mary the mother of Jesus, and his brothers.
local: Bíǹ kɔkɔ nìn kpáfì kinyɔɔbaantii ki mɔ̀tīi mèé ŋwimmeem, bi nì binimpoobi biba, nì Yeesu na Maarii, nì Yeesu naalib mun.

eng: During this time, the family of believers was a company of about one hundred twenty persons. Peter stood among them and said.
local: Iwiin gbanti poonee, Peetroo síl̀ nnabiyaamu mun nìn kpáfì ki bàn̄ kii binib imuŋku iluuyee kansikin, ki bí-bi yii.

eng: Brothers and sisters, the scripture that the Holy Spirit announced beforehand through David had to be fulfilled. This was the scripture concerning Judas, who became a guide for those who arrested Jesus.
local: «Nnabiyaamu, an máaǹ yii Unimbɔti gbɔŋku nín nìn sòon̄ min Juudasi pu dooooyee, an ŋá míǹ. Kun puee Unimbɔti Fam dī Daafiidi pu ki sòon̄ Juudasi un nìn līntì nsan bi cúú Yeesuee pu.

eng: This happened even though he was one of us and received a share of this ministry. .
local: U nìn ŋmàl̄ ti-kansikin ki mun nìn sá ti-tundi nee poon yɔu ubɔ.

eng: In fact, he bought a field with the payment he received for his injustice. Falling headfirst, he burst open in the middle and all his intestines spilled out.
local: Unil gbanti dū ntoonam nimbiliŋ ki dá kusaau. Niin ní u lítì dicincibil, u-pool pōō, u-nai kɔkɔ yāaǹ.

eng: This became known to everyone living in Jerusalem, so they called that field in their own language Hakeldama, or Field of Blood.
local: Jeerusaleem yab kɔkɔ nín gbìl̄ an bɔti gbantee, ní bi pú kusaau gbanti diyindi bi-sooŋyin yii: Akeeldama, an taapu sá yii: fatiku saaku.

eng: right up to the day when he was taken up into heaven. Before he was taken up, working in the power of the Holy Spirit, Jesus instructed the apostles he had chosen.
local: doooo ŋkilkaam áli ki tin sāā ŋwiin ŋun u jɔ́m̀ yilpuee. U-kúm nì u-fìkititam boonee, kí wàatī kí nín jɔ̄m̀ee, u kpáafì akpambal ŋin u nìn lútèe nì Unimbɔti Fam pɔŋŋu.

eng: It is written in the Psalms scroll, Let his home become deserted and let there be no one living in it; and Give his position of leadership to another. .
local: Ilan gbɔŋkun ní ubɔti Daafiidi nìn sòon̄ ki síiǹ yii: <U-kɔŋkooŋŋu ń kpántí kudigbuŋu, ubɔ ń taa ti kɔ́ kudii gbantin,> ní ki gíti ŋmàn̄ yii: <Ubɔ ń fōō u-tundi.>.

eng: Therefore, we must select one of those who have accompanied us during the whole time the Lord Jesus lived among us.
local: «Binib biba nìn céeǹ-ti ŋyunti ŋun Yeesu nìn bí kitiŋ pu doo ti nì u yìin̄ee.

eng: beginning from the baptism of John until the day when Jesus was taken from us. This person must become along with us a witness to his resurrection.
local: kí yòon̄ daliŋkin Yoowaneesi fùl̄-u Unimbɔti nyim kí tin cáá sāā ŋwiin ŋun u yíkì ki nín jɔ́ḿ yilpuee. An máaǹ yii binib gbanti kansikin ubɔ nín kūtì ti-pu kí kpántí Yeesu fìkititam seeraleeliu.».

eng: So they nominated two: Joseph called Barsabbas, who was also known as Justus, and Matthias.
local: Peetroo lī míǹ ki dóòyee, ní bi cáań binib bili, Jooseefi un bi yíì Baarsabaasi ki gítì páaǹ-u diyindi yii Justuusee, nì Maatiyasi.

eng: They prayed, Lord, you know everyones deepest thoughts and desires. Show us clearly which one you have chosen from among these two.
local: Ní bi mēē yii: «Ti-Dindaan, sii di nyí báà ŋma pɔbil. Dàkà-ti un ní a lēē bi-kansikinee.

eng: to take the place of this ministry and apostleship, from which Judas turned away to go to his own place.
local: udaan ń kpántí a-kpambal kí nīn bí mpaan ŋun Juudasi dūu fá ki búntì u-laŋkee.».

eng: When they cast lots, the lot fell on Matthias. He was added to the eleven apostles.
local: Bi tɔ́ kasee, Maatiyasi jíń, ní bi dū-u ki ŋàl̄ akpambal saalaa nì dibee pu.

eng: After his suffering, he showed them that he was alive with many convincing proofs. He appeared to them over a period of forty days, speaking to them about Gods kingdom.
local: U nín kpíì ki fīkitèe, u nyántì u-ba ki dàkā u-boonnooliibi ŋfunyɔm tikpil áli ki tin sāā iwiin imuŋku ili kí dàkà yii u lafun bí dimaŋfalin. Ní bi-nimbil ká-u, ní u sòon̄ nì bi Unimbɔti Beel bɔti.

eng: While they were eating together, he ordered them not to leave Jerusalem but to wait for what the Father had promised. He said, This is what you heard from me.
local: U nì bi nín nìn dáá bée, u nìn kpáafì-bi yii bí taa yīkì Jeerusaleem tiŋkin. U nìn bí-bi yii: «Ní kàl̄ kí cīití ti-Baa Unimbɔti nín nìn pōoǹ dipoondi diŋyee, dipoondi din bɔti m nìn sòon̄ nì nee.

eng: John baptized with water, but in only a few days you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit.
local: Kun puee nì nnyim ní Yoowaneesi nìn fùul̀ binib. Ama nimbee, nì iwiŋkaaŋkaa poonee, Unimbɔti làá fàl̀-ni nì Circir Fam.».

eng: As a result, those who had gathered together asked Jesus, Lord, are you going to restore the kingdom to Israel now.
local: Ŋyuntee, akpambal nì Yeesu nìn kpáfì ki kēe, bi bālifì Yeesu yii: «Ti-Dindaan, an sá ŋyunti nee ní a làá fàtīí yàntī Isirayeel yab ń fōō dibeelaaa?».

eng: Jesus replied, It isnt for you to know the times or seasons that the Father has set by his own authority.
local: Ní u kíí-bi yii: «M-Baa nín dū úǹ u-ba pɔŋŋu ki síiǹ iyunti nì iwiin iŋyee kaa sá ni-yati.

eng: Rather, you will receive power when the Holy Spirit has come upon you, and you will be my witnesses in Jerusalem, in all Judea and Samaria, and to the end of the earth.
local: Ama Circir Fam làá kpákatiń ni-pu kí tīī-ni mpɔn ŋubɔ. Míǹ boonee, ni làá lēē m-pu seera Jeerusaleem tiŋkin nì Juudee nì Samaarii timmun, kí tin sāā kutagbɔŋu nì kitiŋ kúntinyɔkɔ.».

eng: After Jesus said these things, as they were watching, he was lifted up and a cloud took him out of their sight.
local: U lī míǹ ki dóòyee, u-kpambaliŋ bíi kpèé-uee, ní an yōoǹ-u ki cáa nín jɔ́ḿ yilpu, ní ditagbandi diba dɔ́miń ki līkiǹ u nì bi kansikin, ní baa ti wāĺ-u.

eng: There was a man in Caesarea named Cornelius, a centurion in the Italian Company.
local: Uninja ubɔ nìn bí Seesaaree, bi yíì-u Kɔɔrneeyi. U nìn sá usoojakpil disoojakitil din bi yíì Italii yalee kansikin.

eng: He became hungry and wanted to eat. While others were preparing the meal, he had a visionary experience.
local: Ŋkùm nìn cúú-u, u bàà là kí jī. Ŋyunti ŋun bi bíi ŋáań-u tijinee, ní an ŋá-u kii ŋgeemee.

eng: He saw heaven opened up and something like a large linen sheet being lowered to the earth by its four corners.
local: Ní kutagbɔŋu pīitì, ní u ká tiwan tiba bí kii satakpaanee bi bóob́ ajannaatil pu ki sīntìń kitiŋ u-nimbiin.

eng: Inside the sheet were all kinds of four-legged animals, reptiles, and wild birds.
local: Tiwan tin còom̄ ataa anaayee nì tin fùee nì nnɔmbiyaamu kɔkɔ nìn kā satee poon.

eng: A voice told him, Get up, Peter! Kill and eat.
local: ní nneen ŋubɔ bí-u yii: «Peetroo, yīkìm̄ kí kpɔ̀ kí ŋmɔ́!».

eng: Peter exclaimed, Absolutely not, Lord! I have never eaten anything impure or unclean.
local: Ní Peetroo bí yii: «Aayee, m-Dindaan! Maa láá pée jíń tijin tin kòóyee àá tin kaa bí circiree.».

eng: The voice spoke a second time, Never consider unclean what God has made pure.
local: Ní an gíti sòon̄ nì u nliliitiim yii: «Tin tin Unimbɔti ŋáŋkèe, sii ń taa lī yii taa ŋān̄!».

eng: This happened three times, then the object was suddenly pulled back into heaven.
local: An jítì míǹ diba dibee mita pu, ní tiwanee fātìi nín jɔ́ḿ yilpu.

eng: Peter was bewildered about the meaning of the vision. Just then, the messengers sent by Cornelius discovered the whereabouts of Simons house and arrived at the gate.
local: Peetroo bākatì ki kíǹ ki bíi dàkafī tin tin u bàà wál̀ee taapuee, niinee, bininjab bin Kɔɔrneeyi túnnée bālifì ki béè Siimɔn dumpu ki dɔ́miń ki yú kipunyɔkɔpu.

eng: Calling out, they inquired whether the Simon known as Peter was a guest there.
local: ki yíiń ki bālifì yii: «Doo ní Siimɔn un bi yíì Peetrooyee kóòoo?».

eng: While Peter was brooding over the vision, the Spirit interrupted him, Look! Three people are looking for you.
local: Peetroo gbíntìi bíi dàkafī tin u bàà wál̀ee taapuee, ní Unimbɔti Fam bí-u yii: «Bininjab bita biba di nyàab̀-see.

eng: He and his whole household were pious, Gentile God-worshippers. He gave generously to those in need among the Jewish people and prayed to God constantly.
local: Uninja gbanti nìn nóó Unimbɔti ki sá unimɔnti, u nì u-dicaŋku kɔkɔ nìn fàŋkí Unimbɔti. U nìn pūǹ Juuda yab wɔfib tiwan ní ki mèé Unimbɔti baabadal.

eng: Go downstairs. Dont ask questions; just go with them because I have sent them.
local: Yīkìí kpákatí, taa gùgufì, si nì bi kɔkɔ ń nīn cá, kun puee man di túnní-bi.».

eng: So Peter went downstairs and told them, Im the one you are looking for. Why have you come.
local: Niinee ní Peetroo kpákatìń, ní ki bí bininjabee yii: «Man un ni nyàab̀ee sèé. Ba di ŋá ní ni dɔ́miń doo?».

eng: They replied, Weve come on behalf of Cornelius, a centurion and righteous man, a God-worshipper who is well-respected by all Jewish people. A holy angel directed him to summon you to his house and to hear what you have to say.
local: Ní bi kíí-u yii: «Kɔɔrneeyi un sá usoojakpil ki bí deedee ki fàŋkí Unimbɔti, Juuda yab kɔkɔ sòoǹ u-pu yii u sá unimɔntee, Unimbɔti tuuŋŋu ŋubɔ tùkū-u yii ú yíiń-si, á dāan̄ u-dumpu ú pīlifì tin tin a cáá kí tùkù-uee.».

eng: Peter invited them into the house as his guests. The next day he got up and went with them, together with some of the believers from Joppa.
local: Ní Peetroo fōō-bi ki bíl̀. Kutaa wúntèe, u nì bi yíkìi búntì. Nnabiyaamu mun nìn bí Joopeeyee biba mun nìn céeǹ-u.

eng: They arrived in Caesarea the following day. Anticipating their arrival, Cornelius had gathered his relatives and close friends.
local: Ku wúntèe ní u dūu bàn̄ Seesaaree. Kɔɔrneeyi nìn yíiń u-yab nì u-bɔɔtimɔntiibi ki kíǹ ki kā ki cí-bi.

eng: As Peter entered the house, Cornelius met him and fell at his feet in order to honor him.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun Peetroo kóònée, ní Kɔɔrneeyi túŋkì-u ki gbāaǹ ki póofì-u kunɔpoou.

eng: But Peter lifted him up, saying, Get up! Like you, Im just a human.
local: Ama Peetroo yáatì-u, ní ki bí yii: «Yīkìm̄! Ma mun sá unil ní.».

eng: As they continued to talk, Peter went inside and found a large gathering of people.
local: Bi bíi sòoǹee ní ki nín kóò, ní Peetroo bàn̄ ki ká kunipaau kpáfì ki kā.

eng: He said to them, You all realize that it is forbidden for a Jew to associate or visit with outsiders. However, God has shown me that I should never call a person impure or unclean.
local: Ní u bí-bi yii: «Kéè, ni nyí yii an sá timbi Juuda yab dikoobil ti nì binicambi ń kpāfì àá tí kɔ́ bi-dumpuyaa! Ama Unimbɔti dàkā-mi yii ḿ taa kpèè yii ubɔ sá ukpiti àá waa ŋān̄.

eng: For this reason, when you sent for me, I came without objection. I want to know, then, why you sent for me.
local: Míǹ pu ní ni tú ki yíiń-mi, m dɔ́miń kaa kpákáǹ. Too, ba pu ní ni yíiń-mi?».

eng: One day at nearly three oclock in the afternoon, he clearly saw an angel from God in a vision. The angel came to him and said, Cornelius.
local: Dalbee, an cá kii tikúti titee, u ká Unimbɔti tuuŋŋu kpìrrr, ŋu kóoń u-cee ní ki bí-u yii: «Kɔɔrneeyi!».

eng: Cornelius answered, Four days ago at this same time, three oclock in the afternoon, I was praying at home. Suddenly a man in radiant clothing stood before me.
local: Ní Kɔɔrneeyi kíí-u yii: «Iwiin inaa sèé, tikúti tita, ŋyunti ŋun bí nee deedee, m bíi mèé m-dumpu. M kpēēyee, uninja ubɔ dee ki pēē dibɔkutil diba di jìl̀, ki yú m-nimbiin.

eng: He said, Cornelius, God has heard your prayers, and your compassionate acts are like a memorial offering to him.
local: ní ki bí yii: <Kɔɔrneeyi, Unimbɔti fōō a-wimmeem ki téetì a nín ŋáań biwɔfib tiŋan puee.

eng: Therefore, send someone to Joppa and summon Simon, who is known as Peter. He is a guest in the home of Simon the tanner, located near the seacoast.
local: Tɔ̄ Joopee bí yíiń Siimɔn un bi yíì Peetrooyee, u kóò Siimɔn ugbanjɔb un dumpu nákáǹ tiŋkuee.>.

eng: I sent for you right away, and you were kind enough to come. Now, here we are, gathered in the presence of God to listen to everything the Lord has directed you to say.
local: Dicilpu ní m tú binib a-cee, ní a ŋá tiniti ki dɔ́miń. Kɔŋkɔnnee, ti-kɔkɔ bí Unimbɔti nimbiin kí pīlifì tin ti-Dindaan tīī-si á tùkù-tee.».

eng: Peter said, I really am learning that God doesnt show partiality to one group of people over another.
local: Niinee, Peetroo kíĺ ki bíi sòoǹ nì bi yii: «Ibaamɔn, m béè yii Unimbɔti kaa gàntī ubɔ.

eng: Rather, in every nation, whoever worships him and does what is right is acceptable to him.
local: Ntimu kɔkɔn, un yaa fàŋkí-u ki ŋáań min kítèe ŋān̄ u-nimbiin.

eng: This is the message of peace he sent to the Israelites by proclaiming the good news through Jesus Christ: He is Lord of all!.
local: «Ni nyí yii Unimbɔti túnní Tibɔmɔntiil Isirayeel yab cee, kí tùkù-bi yii nì Yeesu Kriisitoo puee bi làá ká nsɔŋfi: Yeesuee di sá ti-kɔkɔ Dindaan.

eng: You know what happened throughout Judea, beginning in Galilee after the baptism John preached.
local: Yoowaneesi nín sòon̄ Unimbɔti nyim difaldi bɔti ki dóò boonee, ni nyí tin jítì Juudee tiŋki kɔkɔn ki kíliń Galiileeyee.

eng: You know about Jesus of Nazareth, whom God anointed with the Holy Spirit and endowed with power. Jesus traveled around doing good and healing everyone oppressed by the devil because God was with him.
local: Ni nyí yii Unimbɔti nìn tīiń Yeesu Naasaareeti yɔu u-Fam ki yāntì u cáá mpɔŋkpaan. U nìn cá ajan kɔkɔ ki ŋáań tiŋan ki cāantí bin kɔkɔ Sitaan pɔŋŋu nìn ŋúb́ee, kun puee Unimbɔti nìn bí u-cee.

eng: We are witnesses of everything he did, both in Judea and in Jerusalem. They killed him by hanging him on a tree.
local: Timbi kántì tin kɔkɔ u ŋáaǹ Juuda yab tiŋkin ki kpáaǹ Jeerusaleemee mun. Úǹ ní bi kpáá ndɔ pu, ki kūū-u.

eng: Startled, he stared at the angel and replied, What is it, Lord? The angel said, Your prayers and your compassionate acts are like a memorial offering to God.
local: Tifaandi nìn kɔ́-u, ní u kíǹ ki bíi kpèé Unimbɔti tuuŋŋu, ní ki bí yii: «Ba ŋá ní, m-Dindaan?» Ní ntuun bí-u yii: «A-wimmeem nì a nín pūǹ tiŋyee bàn̄ Unimbɔti, ní u téetì a-pu.

eng: but God raised him up on the third day and allowed him to be seen.
local: Ama Unimbɔti fīkitì-u winta dal, ki yāntì bi ká-u.

eng: not by everyone but by us. We are witnesses whom God chose beforehand, who ate and drank with him after God raised him from the dead.
local: An kaa sá samaa kɔkɔ di ká-u, asee Unimbɔti nín nìn pée lēē timbi bin bin ń ká-uee. Di sá u nín nìn fīkitì ditaŋkpiilinee, boonee, ti nì u di jíń ki nyùn̄.

eng: He commanded us to preach to the people and to testify that he is the one whom God appointed as judge of the living and the dead.
local: Ní u bí-ti yii tí nīn sòoǹ u-pu samaa cee, kí dàkà-bi yii úǹ Yeesu baba ní Unimbɔti lēē u-bɔsoonliu, ú sòoǹ bin wāĺee nì bin kpíìyee bɔti.

eng: All the prophets testify about him that everyone who believes in him receives forgiveness of sins through his name.
local: Úǹ pu ní Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi kɔkɔ lēekì seera yii un sá kamaa yaa fōō-u ki kííyee kāntí u-kpitii pɔ̄tam u-yindi pu.».

eng: While Peter was still speaking, the Holy Spirit fell on everyone who heard the word.
local: Peetroo bíi sòoǹ mimmee, ní Circir Fam kpákatìń bin kɔkɔ nìn bíi pílifí u-nyɔɔbundee pu.

eng: The circumcised believers who had come with Peter were astonished that the gift of the Holy Spirit had been poured out even on the Gentiles.
local: Kriisitoo yab bin sá Juuda yab ki céeǹní Peetrooyee nìn bākatì. Di sá yii Unimbɔti nìn pú bin kaa sá Juuda yabee gba Circir Fam.

eng: They heard them speaking in other languages and praising God. Peter asked.
local: Bi nìn gbìl̄ binicambi bíi sòoǹ isooncaan ki nyɔ́ŋkí Unimbɔti. Niinee ní Peetroo bí yii.

eng: These people have received the Holy Spirit just as we have. Surely no one can stop them from being baptized with water, can they.
local: «Binib nee fōō Circir Fam kii timbee dee. Mimmee, ti ŋūǹ kí yíí yii taa làá fàl̀-bi Unimbɔti nyimaaa?».

eng: He directed that they be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ. Then they invited Peter to stay for several days.
local: Ní u pú nsan yii bí fàl̀-bi Unimbɔti nyim Yeesu Kriisitoo yindi pu, ní bi gbāam̀-u yii ú kàl̄ bi-cee iwiŋkaaŋkaa.

eng: Send messengers to Joppa at once and summon a certain Simon, the one known as Peter.
local: Tɔ̄ bininjab kɔŋkɔnnee Joopee bí yíiń un bi yíì Siimɔn ki páaǹ-u diyindi yii Peetrooyee.

eng: He is a guest of Simon the tanner, whose house is near the seacoast.
local: U bí ugbanjɔb un bi yíì Siimɔŋyee cee, un diiku bí tiŋku caŋinee.».

eng: When the angel who was speaking to him had gone, Cornelius summoned two of his household servants along with a pious soldier from his personal staff.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun ntuun ŋun nìn dɔ́miń ki sòon̄ nì Kɔɔrneeyee búntèe, ní u yíiń u-tɔntɔnliibi bili nì sooja ubɔ un nìn bí u-cee ki fàŋkí Unimbɔtee.

eng: He explained everything to them, then sent them to Joppa.
local: U nìn máafì-bi tibɔtee ní ki dū-bi ki tú Joopee.

eng: At noon on the following day, as their journey brought them close to the city, Peter went up on the roof to pray.
local: Kutaa wúntèe, bi bí nsanni ki tóob́ kitiŋ gbantee, Peetroo jɔ́m̀ kudii yilpu kuwiŋkuluu yuntiŋu kí mèè.

eng: The apostles and the brothers and sisters throughout Judea heard that even the Gentiles had welcomed Gods word.
local: Akpambal nì nnabiyaamu mun nìn bí Juudeeyee gbìl̄ yii bin kaa sá Juuda yabee mun fōō Unimbɔti nyɔɔbundi.

eng: This happened three times, then everything was pulled back into heaven.
local: Áli míǹ diba dibee mita, ní an wanti kɔkɔ fātìi nín jɔ́ḿ yilpu.

eng: At that moment three men who had been sent to me from Caesarea arrived at the house where we were staying.
local: Dicilpu niinee, bininjab bita bin bi nìn túnní Seesaaree m-ceeyee bāań kudii kun ni m nìn kóòyee nyɔkɔpu.

eng: The Spirit told me to go with them even though they were Gentiles. These six brothers also went with me, and we entered that mans house.
local: Ní Circir Fam bí yii man nì bi ń nīn cá, ḿ taa gùgufì. Man nì nnabiyaamu muluu nee di nìn nóó ki cūtì Kɔɔrneeyi dumpu.

eng: He reported to us how he had seen an angel standing in his house and saying, Send to Joppa and summon Simon, who is known as Peter.
local: Ní u mun máafì-ti u nín nìn kā u-dumpu Unimbɔti tuuŋŋu ŋubɔ bāań ki tùkū-u yii: <Tɔ̄ Joopee, bí yíiń-si Siimɔn un bi páaǹ diyindi yii Peetrooyee.

eng: He will tell you how you and your entire household can be saved.
local: U làá tùkù-si tibɔti tin pu sii nì a-samaau kɔkɔ làá dī kí ká difiilee.>.

eng: When I began to speak, the Holy Spirit fell on them, just as the Spirit fell on us in the beginning.
local: M kíĺ ki bíi sòoǹ nì bee, ní Circir Fam kpákatìń bi-pu kii mì nín nìn kpákatìń ti mun pu mpeepeekaam puee.

eng: I remembered the Lords words: John baptized with water, but you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit.
local: Niinee, m téetì ti-Dindaan nyɔɔbundi nee pu yii: <Yoowaneesi nìn fùul̀ binib Unimbɔti nyim, ama Unimbɔti làá dāá fàl̀ nimbi nì Circir Fam ní.>.

eng: If God gave them the same gift he gave us who believed in the Lord Jesus Christ, then who am I? Could I stand in Gods way.
local: Unimbɔti pú bi mun u-Fam mbaantiim kii u nín nìn pú ti mun puee ní, kun puee ti-kɔkɔ fōō ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo ki kíí. Kin ma mun ŋma ní ḿ kpákáǹ Unimbɔti?».

eng: Once the apostles and other believers heard this, they calmed down. They praised God and concluded, So then God has enabled Gentiles to change their hearts and lives so that they might have new life.
local: Bi gbìl̄ mimmee, kaa ti bíi kpākantí-u, ki kíǹ ki bíi nyɔ́ŋkí Unimbɔti ki bí yii: «Mimmee, Unimbɔti yāntì bin kaa sá Juuda yabee mun fá bi-kpitii kí ŋmā kí ká dimaŋfal dee.».

eng: Now those who were scattered as a result of the trouble that occurred because of Stephen traveled as far as Phoenicia, Cyprus, and Antioch. They proclaimed the word only to Jews.
local: Bin bin bi nìn páb́ bi yàl̄ Eetiyeen kúm boonee bàn̄ áli Feenisii nì Siipiri nì Antiyɔɔsi, ki sòoǹ Unimbɔti bɔti Juuda yab ŋmaniŋma cee.

eng: When Peter went up to Jerusalem, the circumcised believers criticized him.
local: Peetroo nìn fātìi bāań Jeerusaleemee, bin nìn fōō ki kíí ki sá Juuda yabee nìn bíi wòoǹ-u.

eng: Among them were some people from Cyprus and Cyrene. They entered Antioch and began to proclaim the good news about the Lord Jesus also to Gentiles.
local: Ama bi-kansikin biba bin nyánní Siipiri nì Siireenee bāań Antiyɔɔsi ki mun bíi tùkù bin kaa sá Juuda yabee Tibɔmɔntiil tin sòoǹ ti-Dindaan Yeesu puee.

eng: The Lords power was with them, and a large number came to believe and turned to the Lord.
local: Ti-Dindaan pɔŋŋu nìn bí bi-cee, ní kunipaau nìn fōō ki kíí ki nóó ti-Dindaan.

eng: When the church in Jerusalem heard about this, they sent Barnabas to Antioch.
local: Kriisitoo nikitil din bí Jeerusaleemee nín gbìl̄ an bɔtee ní bi dū Baarnaabaasi ki tú Antiyɔɔsi.

eng: When he arrived and saw evidence of Gods grace, he was overjoyed and encouraged everyone to remain fully committed to the Lord.
local: U bàn̄ee ní ki ká Unimbɔti nín bīiǹ bi-pu miŋyee ní an mɔ́ɔkiǹ-u, ní u bíi tìì-bi kunyannyaau, yii bi-kɔkɔ ń nīn yú nì dipɔbil diba kí nīn nákáǹ ti-Dindaan.

eng: Barnabas responded in this way because he was a good man, whom the Holy Spirit had endowed with exceptional faith. A considerable number of people were added to the Lord.
local: Di sá yii u nìn sá unimɔnti, ki gbí Circir Fam ki cáá fookii. An puee binib tikpil nìn dɔ́miń ki nóó ti-Dindaan.

eng: Barnabas went to Tarsus in search of Saul.
local: Míǹ boonee ní Baarnaabaasi búntì Taarsi kí nyàab̀ Sɔɔl.

eng: When he found him, he brought him to Antioch. They were there for a whole year, meeting with the church and teaching large numbers of people. It was in Antioch where the disciples were first labeled Christians.
local: U bàn̄ ki ká-uee, ní ki cáań-u Antiyɔɔsi. Dibimmɔndi kɔkɔee, bi nìn dūu kāl̀ Kriisitoo nikitil kansikin ki dàkà binib tikpil Unimbɔti bɔti. Antiyɔɔsi tiŋkin ní bi péé péé ki yíiń Yeesu boonnooliibi yii Kriisitoo yab.

eng: About that time, some prophets came down from Jerusalem to Antioch.
local: Ŋyunti gbantee, Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi biba nyánní Jeerusaleem ki sīntì Antiyɔɔsi.

eng: One of them, Agabus, stood up and, inspired by the Spirit, predicted that a severe famine would overtake the entire Roman world.
local: Bi-kansikin ubɔ ní bi nìn yíì Agabusi. Unimbɔti Fam yāntì kijapaayee yíkìi lī yii, ŋkùm miba làá nyā kitiŋ kɔkɔ pu kí nīn pɔ̄ɔ̄. - Ní mì lafun bāań Kloodi beel yuntiŋu.

eng: The disciples decided they would send support to the brothers and sisters in Judea, with everyone contributing to this ministry according to each persons abundance.
local: Ní Yeesu boonnooliibi bíi màkà báà ŋma ń tīī u nín ŋūǹ puee bí dūú tūtuǹ bi-nabiyaamu mun kóò Juudeeyee.

eng: They accused him, You went into the home of the uncircumcised and ate with them.
local: yii u kɔ́ bin kaa gíí dikpancandee dumpu, u nì bi jíń tijin.

eng: They sent Barnabas and Saul to take this gift to the elders.
local: Bi tīīyee, ní ki yāntì Baarnaabaasi nì Sɔɔl yōoǹ ki cáa cūnnì Jeerusaleem Kriisitoo nikitil kpilib cee.

eng: Step-by-step, Peter explained what had happened.
local: Ní Peetroo bíi máafí-bi tin jítèe, an nín kíliń pu ki tin kúntì puee, yii.

eng: I was in the city of Joppa praying when I had a visionary experience. In my vision, I saw something like a large linen sheet being lowered from heaven by its four corners. It came all the way down to me.
local: «M nìn bí Joopee tiŋki poon ki bíi mèé ní Unimbɔti ŋúbitì m-nimbiliŋ m wál̀ tiwan tiba, tì bí kii satakpaanee ki bóob́ ajannaatil pu ki nyánní yilpu ki sīntìń m-cee.

eng: As I stared at it, wondering what it was, I saw four-legged animals—including wild beasts—as well as reptiles and wild birds.
local: Ní m bíi kpèé sata poon dímmmee, ní ki ká tiwaŋkuti tin còom̄ ataa anaayee nì ipeel, nì tin fùee nì nnɔmbiyaamu.

eng: I heard a voice say, Get up, Peter! Kill and eat.
local: ní m gbìl̄ nneen ŋubɔ bí-mi yii: <Peetroo, yīkìí kpɔ̀ kí ŋmɔ́!>.

eng: I responded, Absolutely not, Lord! Nothing impure or unclean has ever entered my mouth.
local: Ní m bí yii: <Aayee, m-Dindaan! Doooo nee tiwan tin ti kòó àá tin kaa bí circiree kaa láá pée kɔ́ m-nyɔkɔn.>.

eng: The voice from heaven spoke a second time, Never consider unclean what God has made pure.
local: Nliliitiimin ní nneenee gíti lī yii: <Tin Unimbɔti ŋáŋkèe, sii taa lì yii taa ŋān̄!>.

eng: About that time King Herod began to harass some who belonged to the church.
local: Ŋyunti gbantee, ubɔti Eeroodi kíĺ ki bíi sàantí Kriisitoo yab biba.

eng: They passed the first and second guards and came to the iron gate leading to the city. It opened for them by itself. After leaving the prison, they proceeded the length of one street, when abruptly the angel was gone.
local: Bi jítì soojapeekaabi pu ki tin jítì bililiitiibi mun pu, ní ki dūu bàn̄ tikúti punyɔkɔki kin túĺ kitiŋinee. Kipunyɔkɔee cúu pīitì ki-ba bi-nimbiin, ní bi nyáń ki tākaǹ didandi diba pu. Kpalaayee, ntuunee yíkì Peetroo cee.

eng: At that, Peter came to his senses and remarked, Now Im certain that the Lord sent his angel and rescued me from Herod and from everything the Jewish people expected.
local: Niinee ní Peetroo béè tin bāań-uee ní ki bí yii: «Kɔŋkɔnnee, tiŋman, m nyí yii ti-Dindaan di túnní u-tuuŋŋu ŋú dāá nyāntí-mi Eeroodi ŋaan nì tin kɔkɔwoooo Juuda yab bàà cí kí ŋá-mee.».

eng: Realizing this, he made his way to Marys house. Many believers had gathered there and were praying.
local: U yúl̀ ki dākafèe, ní ki nín dòoń Maarii, Yoowaneesi un bi páaǹ diyindi yii Maarkee na dumpu. Niin ní binib tikpil nìn kpáfì ki bíi mèé.

eng: When Peter knocked at the outer gate, a female servant named Rhoda went to answer.
local: U bàn̄ ki fáá kunaakoou punyɔkɔ, ní utɔntɔnli ubɔ, u sá unimpu bi yíì Roodee sútìń kí pīlifì.

eng: She was so overcome with joy when she recognized Peters voice that she didnt open the gate. Instead, she ran back in and announced that Peter was standing at the gate.
local: U béè Peetroo neenee, ní an mɔ́ɔkiǹ-u. Ú bàà pìitī-uee, ní u sáǹ ki cūtìi lī yii Peetroo yú kipunyɔkɔpu.

eng: Youve lost your mind! they responded. She stuck by her story with such determination that they began to say, It must be his guardian angel.
local: Ní bi bí-u yii: «A wáatì níii?» Ní u bí yii: «An baasii sá ibaamɔn ní!» Niinee ní bi bí yii: «An làá nīn sá Unimbɔti tuuŋŋu ŋun cí-uee ní!».

eng: Meanwhile, Peter remained outside, knocking at the gate. They finally opened the gate and saw him there, and they were astounded.
local: Ŋyunti gbantee, Peetroo pée gbíntìi fátí. Bi cúu pīitì ki ká-uee, ní ki bākatì sèeee.

eng: He gestured with his hand to quiet them down, then recounted how the Lord led him out of prison. He said, Tell this to James and the brothers and sisters. Then he left for another place.
local: Ní Peetroo dū u-ŋal ki téefì-bi yii bí ŋmīĺee, ní u máafì-bi Unimbɔti nín nyántì-u kusarkadiin puee. Ní u bí-bi yii: «Cùú tùkùmaan Yakubu nì nnabiyaamu mun kíǹee.» Ní u nyáń ki búntì laaba mun.

eng: The next morning the soldiers were flustered about what had happened to Peter.
local: Kutaa wúntèe, soojab pɔbiliŋ bíil̀ tikpil, ní bi bíi bàlifī tɔb yii: «Ba di nín ŋá Peetroo dee?».

eng: Herod called for a thorough search. When Peter didnt turn up, Herod interrogated the guards and had them executed. Afterward, Herod left Judea in order to spend some time in Caesarea.
local: Eeroodi tú bí nyàab̀-u, ama ní baa ká-u. Ní u bí yii bí bàlifì soojab tibɔti, ní ki pú nsan bí kpɔ̀-bi. Míǹ boonee ní Eeroodi yíkì Juudee ki cūtìi kāl̀ Seesaaree ŋyunti ŋubɔ poon.

eng: He had James, Johns brother, killed with a sword.
local: ní ki yāntì bi dū kitàataajiki ki kūū Yakubu un sá Yoowaneesi maanee.

eng: Herod had been furious with the people of Tyre and Sidon for some time. They made a pact to approach him together, since their region depended on the kings realm for its food supply. They persuaded Blastus, the kings personal attendant, to join their cause, then appealed for an end to hostilities.
local: Eeroodi nìn fōō Tiir nì Siidɔn yab pu diŋuul, ní bíǹ kpáfì dicilpu kí cùú ká-u. U-tiŋkin ní bi nìn tūntí tijin ki cāabìń bíǹ cee. Míǹ puee, bi píí ubɔti Eeroodi cindin kpiliu Blaastuusi kubɔɔtiiu ki wāatì, ní ki cūtìi gbāam̀ ubɔti Eeroodi.

eng: On the scheduled day Herod dressed himself in royal attire, seated himself on the throne, and gave a speech to the people.
local: Ŋwiin ŋun bi nìn síiǹee bāańee, Eeroodi pēeǹ u-beebɔkutil ki kā u-beejal pu ki bíi sòoǹ nì bi tibɔti.

eng: Those assembled kept shouting, over and over, This is a gods voice, not the voice of a mere human.
local: Ní samaa bíi kūuntí yii: «An sá unimbɔti ubɔ di sòoǹ, an kaa sá unil!».

eng: Immediately an angel from the Lord struck Herod down, because he didnt give the honor to God. He was eaten by worms and died.
local: Dicilpu niin ní Unimbɔti tuuŋŋu ŋubɔ cáań iween ki tɔ́ u-pu, kun puee waa tīī Unimbɔti tinyool. Ní akoosibil aba kɔ́ u-ni ki ŋmɔ́ ki kūū-u.

eng: Gods word continued to grow and increase.
local: Ŋyunti gbantee, Unimbɔti nyɔɔbundi mɔ̀tīi gbìtī, ní bin fòol̄ ki kíikée wíiŋŋu gbēentí.

eng: Barnabas and Saul returned to Antioch from Jerusalem after completing their mission, bringing with them John, who was also known as Mark.
local: Baarnaabaasi nì Sɔɔl dóò bi-tuuŋŋi Jeerusaleemee, ní ki fātìi gítìń, ki kpáaǹní Yoowaneesi un bi páaǹ diyindi yii Maarkee.

eng: When he saw that this pleased the Jews, he arrested Peter as well. This happened during the Festival of Unleavened Bread.
local: U ká yii an ŋān̄ Juuda yab ceeyee, ní u yāntì bi cúú Peetroo mun. An nìn sá iwiin in poon bi ŋmóò kpɔnɔb bin baa ŋá ŋkpɔnɔnyɔkɔee ní.

eng: He put Peter in prison, handing him over to four squads of soldiers, sixteen in all, who guarded him. He planned to charge him publicly after the Passover.
local: Bi cúú-u ki tɔ́ sarkanee, ní ubɔti lēē asoojakiti anaa, dikitil kamaa cáá soojab binaa, bí nīn cīití-u ki kpántí. U là yii, bi yaa jíń Dilákatil jiŋaaliu ki dóòyee, ú cáań-u samaa nimbiin kí sòoǹ u-bɔti.

eng: While Peter was held in prison, the church offered earnest prayer to God for him.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun Peetroo nìn bí kusarkadiinee, Kriisitoo nikitil nìn bí ki mèé Unimbɔti u-pu, kaa gbèentí.

eng: The night before Herod was going to bring Peters case forward, Peter was asleep between two soldiers and bound with two chains, with soldiers guarding the prison entrance.
local: Kutaa ń wúntí Eeroodi ń cáá-u ibɔsoon samaa nimbiinee, kunyeeuee, Peetroo nìn sáká soojab bili kansikin ki dɔ̄ ki gèeǹ. Bi nìn bóob́-u ikusaka ili, ní soojab bili yú kipunyɔkɔpu ki cí kusarkadii.

eng: Suddenly an angel from the Lord appeared and a light shone in the prison cell. After nudging Peter on his side to awaken him, the angel raised him up and said, Quick! Get up! The chains fell from his wrists.
local: Kpalaayee, Unimbɔti tuuŋŋu ŋubɔ dɔ́miń, ní kusarkadiin wál̀ nyáka nyaka. Ní ntuun fáá Peetroo takakal ki fīntì-u, ní ki bí yii: «Yīkì malaa!» Ní ikusakee cúu fɔ́fì u-ŋaliiyin ki lítì.

eng: The angel continued, Get dressed. Put on your sandals. Peter did as he was told. The angel said, Put on your coat and follow me.
local: Ní ntuun bí yii: «Gbìn̄ a-dambalau kí táká a-naatakaŋ.» Ní u ŋá míǹ. Ní ŋu gíti bí yii: «Dū a-satau kí pèen̄, kí nīn nóó-mi.».

eng: Following the angel, Peter left the prison. However, he didnt realize the angel had actually done all this. He thought he was seeing a vision.
local: Ní Peetroo nóó-u ki nyánní kusarkadiin, kaa nyí min ntuun bíi ŋáańee sá tibɔŋman. Úǹ nìn dākafì yii an sá kudamintiiu ní.

eng: The church at Antioch included prophets and teachers: Barnabas, Simeon , Lucius from Cyrene, Manaen , and Saul.
local: Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi nì bidakaliibi biba nìn bí Kriisitoo nikitil din bí Antiyɔɔsee kansikin. Bíǹ di sá: Baarnaabaasi, Simeeyɔn un bi nìn páaǹ diyindi yii Niijeeree, nì Lusiyusi un sá Siireen tiŋkinee, nì Manayeen un nì Eeroodi, Galiilee gɔminau nìn jíń tibiyati mbaantiimee nì Sɔɔl.

eng: said, You are a deceiver and trickster! You devil! You attack anything that is right! Will you never stop twisting the straight ways of the Lord into crooked paths?.
local: ní ki bí-u yii: «Sitaan biki, unyimɔndaan un bí nee! A kíiĺ binib ki jáà nì ibaamɔn kɔkɔ. Aa làá gbèen̄ ki gbíntìi bíiĺ Unimbɔti sàmbiyaamu mun tèen̄eeee?.

eng: Listen! The Lords power is set against you. You will be blind for a while, unable even to see the daylight. At once, Bar-Jesus eyes were darkened, and he began to grope about for someone to lead him around by the hand.
local: Too, kɔŋkɔnnee, Unimbɔti làá yàntī á kpántí ujɔfu. Aa làá ti wāl̀ ŋwìiń iyunti iba poon.» Dicilpu niinee, u kpántì ujɔfu, ní u kíǹ ki bíi táfí ki nyàab̀ un làá dàtī-uee.

eng: When the governor saw what had taken place, he came to believe, for he was astonished by the teaching about the Lord.
local: Gɔminee nín ká tin jítèe ní u fōō ki kíí. Kun puee ti-Dindaan bɔti tin bi dàkèe kɔ́-u tiŋan.

eng: Paul and his companions sailed from Paphos to Perga in Pamphylia. John deserted them there and returned to Jerusalem.
local: Ní Pɔɔl nì bin nìn cēentí-uee kɔ́ meela ki yíkì Pafɔsi ki bàn̄ Peeji, Paŋfilii watil. Ní Yoowaneesi-Maarki gbēeǹ niin ki nín gítiń Jeerusaleem.

eng: They went on from Perga and arrived at Antioch in Pisidia. On the Sabbath, they entered and found seats in the synagogue there.
local: Bi yíkì Peeji, ki nín nìkì bi-saŋŋu ki tin bàn̄ Antiyɔɔsi tiŋki, Piisidii watil. Ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa dalee, bi kɔ́ Juuda yab kpafidiikun ki kāl̀.

eng: After the reading of the Law and the Prophets, the synagogue leaders invited them, Brothers, if one of you has a sermon for the people, please speak.
local: Bi nín kàan̄ mara nì Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi gbɔnti poon ki dóòyee, ní kukpafidii kpilib túnní bi-cee yii: «Nnabiyaamu, ni yaa cáá isaŋki iba kí tīī samaa, ní sòoǹ.».

eng: Standing up, Paul gestured with his hand and said, Fellow Israelites and Gentile God-worshippers, please listen to me.
local: Ní Pɔɔl yíkì ki yōoǹ u-ŋal yilpu ki jáam̀-bi, ní ki bí-bi yii: «Nimbi Isirayeel yab, nì nimbi bin fàŋkí Unimbɔtee, pīlifìmaan!.
