eng: The God of this people Israel chose our ancestors. God made them a great people while they lived as strangers in the land of Egypt. With his great power, he led them out of that country.
local: Isirayeel yab Nimbɔtiu nìn lēē ti-naanjab ki yāntì bi būtì ŋyunti ŋun bi nìn bí kunicaŋu Eejipiti tiŋkinee. Unimbɔti nìn dū u-pɔŋŋu ki nyántì-bi.

eng: For about forty years, God put up with them in the wilderness.
local: ki jíń bi-pu kimɔtii bi-bimbim pu ki ŋúb́-bi abin imuŋku ili nyɔkɔm kuteeun.

eng: God conquered seven nations in the land of Canaan and gave the Israelites their land as an inheritance.
local: U nìn jáń ki kúntì atimbun aluli Kanaan tiŋkin, ní ki dū Isirayeel yab ki kāaǹ bi kpántì bitindambi.

eng: As they were worshipping the Lord and fasting, the Holy Spirit said, Appoint Barnabas and Saul to the work I have called them to undertake.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun bi bóob́ kinyɔkɔ ki bíi nyɔ́ŋkí ti-Dindaanee, ní Unimbɔti Fam bí yii: «Gàntīmaan Baarnaabaasi nì Sɔɔl kí yóoń-mi ditundi din pu m yíiń-bee pu.».

eng: This happened over a period of about four hundred fifty years. After this, he gave them judges until the time of the prophet Samuel.
local: Bi kāl̀ niin kii abin tilaafati tili nì imuŋku ili nì saalaa. «Boonee ní u tīī-bi bikpilib bin làá nīn kpòotī-bee ki tin sāā Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu Samiyeel yuntiŋu.

eng: The Israelites requested a king, so God gave them Saul, Kishs son, from the tribe of Benjamin, and he served as their king for forty years.
local: Ŋyunti gbanti ní bi bí yii bi là ubɔti. Niinee, Unimbɔti tīī-bi Sayuul, Kiisi jipɔɔn un nyánní Beenjameen naakookunee, u jíń dibeel abin imuŋku ili.

eng: After God removed him, he raised up David to be their king. God testified concerning him, I have found David, Jesses son, a man who shares my desires. Whatever my will is, he will do.
local: Boonee, Unimbɔti dū-u ki fá, ní ki dū Daafiidi ki kāaǹ. Úǹ pu ní u sòon̄ yii: <M ká Daafiidi Jeesee jipɔɔn. Úǹ di fáatì m-pɔbil. U làá dāá ŋá min kɔkɔ m lèe.>».

eng: From this mans descendants, God brought to Israel a savior, Jesus, just as he promised.
local: Ní Pɔɔl nìkī ki lī yii: «U-maaliŋu poon ní Unimbɔti yāntì bi màl̄ Ufiil ubɔ ki tīī Isirayeel yab, kii u nín nìn pōoǹ puee: úǹ di sá Yeesu.

eng: Before Jesus appearance, John proclaimed to all the Israelites a baptism to show they were changing their hearts and lives.
local: Kí wàatī ú bàńee, Yoowaneesi nìn sòon̄ nì Isirayeel yab kɔkɔ bí fá bi-kpitii kí yàntī bí fàl̀-bi Unimbɔti nyim.

eng: As John was completing his mission, he said, Who do you think I am? Im not the one you think I am, but he is coming after me. Im not worthy to loosen his sandals.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun Yoowaneesi nìn làá dóó u-tundee, ní u bí-bi yii: <Ŋma ní ni dàkafī yii m sá ní? Maa sá un ní ni dàkafī yii m sée! Ama ubɔ dòoń man boon, maa kpántì kí bútí u-naataaŋmiii gba.>.

eng: Brothers, children of Abrahams family, and you Gentile God-worshippers, the message about this salvation has been sent to us.
local: «Nnabiyaamu, nimbi Abraam maaliŋun biyaamu, nì nimbi binicambi bin fàŋkí Unimbɔtee, timbi ní Unimbɔti dū dinyɔɔbundi nee ki tīiń, tí ká difiil.

eng: The people in Jerusalem and their leaders didnt recognize Jesus. By condemning him they fulfilled the words of the prophets that are read every Sabbath.
local: Binib bin kóò Jeerusaleemee nì bi-bɔtiibi kaa nìn béè un ní Yeesu sée, kaa béè Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi bɔti tin bi kàaǹ ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa sá kamaa dalee taapu mun. Bi nín yāntì bi kūū Yeesu puee, bi ŋá min Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi nìn sòon̄ ki yóoǹee dee.

eng: Even though they didnt find a single legal basis for the death penalty, they asked Pilate to have him executed.
local: Nì min sá kamaa ní baa ká tin u ŋá ki máaǹ ŋkúmee, bi mēē Piilati yii ú kpɔ̀-u.

eng: When they finished doing everything that had been written about him, they took him down from the cross and laid him in a tomb.
local: Bi ŋá tin kɔkɔwoooo bi nìn ŋmàn̄ ki síiǹ u-puee, ní bi kpákatìń-u ndɔpuntikaa pu, ki dū-u ki ŋá dikaakulin.

eng: After they fasted and prayed, they laid their hands on these two and sent them off.
local: Bi bóob́ kinyɔkɔ ki mēē ki dóòyee, ní bi dū bi-ŋalii ki páaǹ Baarnaabaasi nì Sɔɔl pu, ní ki yāntì bi búntì.

eng: But God raised him from the dead!.
local: Ama Unimbɔti fīkitì-u ditaŋkpiilin.

eng: He appeared over many days to those who had traveled with him from Galilee to Jerusalem. They are now his witnesses to the people.
local: Boonee, bin nìn nyánní Galiilee ki céeǹní-u Jeerusaleemee nìn ká-u iwiin tikpil poon. Kɔŋkɔnnee, binibee di kíǹ ki sá seeradambi ki lèekī seera u-pu ki tìì samaa.

eng: We proclaim to you the good news. What God promised to our ancestors.
local: «Míǹ pu ní timbi bíi tùkù-ni tibɔmɔntiil yii, dipoondi din Unimbɔti nìn pōoǹ ti-naanjabee.

eng: he has fulfilled for us, their children, by raising up Jesus. As it was written in the second psalm, You are my son; today I have become your father. .
local: u nín fīkitì Yeesuee, u ŋá min u nìn pōoǹee ki tīī timbi bi-naantiibi dee. A lì kii an nín ŋmàn̄ Ilan gbɔŋku poon ayil puee, yii: <A sá m-Jipɔɔn, m dàkā díǹ yii man di màl̄-si.>.

eng: God raised Jesus from the dead, never again to be subjected to deaths decay. Therefore, God said, I will give to you the holy and firm promises I made to David. .
local: Unimbɔti nìn sòon̄ ki síiǹ yii u làá fìkitī-u ditaŋkpiilin ní u-wunti ń taa sūū. U nín nìn sòon̄ puee di sá yii: M làá dāá tīī-ni tin m nìn pōoǹ Daafiidee, tin bí circir ki sá tiŋmanee.>.

eng: In another place it is said, You will not let your holy one experience deaths decay. .
local: Míǹ puee ní Daafiidi lī u-gbɔŋku poon laaba Yeesu pu yii: <Aa làá yàntī a-circir tɔntɔnliu wunti ń sūū.>.

eng: David served Gods purpose in his own generation, then he died and was buried with his ancestors. He experienced deaths decay.
local: Daafiidi yuntiŋuee u nìn sá Unimbɔti tɔntɔnliu ní ki ŋáań min ní Unimbɔti lēē ki síiǹee. U dāa kpíì, ní bi síb́-u ki kɔ́ĺ u-naanjab, ní u-wunti sūū.

eng: but the one whom God has raised up didnt experience deaths decay.
local: Ama Yeesu un Unimbɔti fīkitèe, úǹ wunti kaa sūū.

eng: Therefore, brothers and sisters, know this: Through Jesus we proclaim forgiveness of sins to you. From all those sins from which you couldnt be put in right relationship with God through Moses Law.
local: Nnabiyaamu, béémaan yii tin tin ní ti sòoǹ ki tùkù-ni nee di sá, úǹ pu ní ni kpántí deedeedambi Unimbɔti nimbiin. Ni nín nìn nóó Mooyiisi marabee, naa nìn ŋūǹ kí kpántí deedeedambi Unimbɔti nimbiin.

eng: through Jesus everyone who believes is put in right relationship with God.
local: Ama un yaa fōō Yeesu ki kííyee, Unimbɔti dù u-kpitii kɔkɔ ní kí pɔ̄-u.

eng: After the Holy Spirit sent them on their way, they went down to Seleucia. From there they sailed to Cyprus.
local: Baarnaabaasi nì Sɔɔl bin Unimbɔti Fam nìn túee nín sìntī Seelusii ki bàn̄ee, ki kɔ́ meela ki nín cá Siipiri.

eng: Take care that the prophets words dont apply to you.
local: Ní kpèèmaan tiŋan, tibɔti tin Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi nìn sòon̄ee ń taa bàń-ni, yii.

eng: Look, you scoffers , marvel and die . Im going to do work in your day — a work you wont believe even if someone told you .
local: Unimbɔti bí yii: <Kpeefandambi kpèèmaan, bàkatīmaan kí wāŋkìmaan! Di sá yii ni-yuntii nee poon m làá tɔ̄ ditundi diba din bi yaa láa bíi máafí-ni gba, naa làá fōō kí kííyee.>».

eng: As Paul and Barnabas were leaving the synagogue, the people urged them to speak about these things again on the next Sabbath.
local: Pɔɔl nì Baarnaabaasi nyáń kukpafidiinee, ní bi mēē-bi yii ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa ŋun dòońee, bí dāá gíti sòoǹ tibɔti gbanti pu.

eng: When the people in the synagogue were dismissed, many Jews and devout converts to Judaism accompanied Paul and Barnabas, who urged them to remain faithful to the message of Gods grace.
local: Binib kɔkɔ yāaǹ ki nín kùǹee, Juuda yab nì binicambi bin fōō Juuda yab marabee tikpil nìn nóó Pɔɔl nì Baarnaabaasi, ní bíǹ bíi sòoǹ ki tìì-bi kunyannyaau, yii bí nīn yú deedee ki ŋúb́ diyimɔɔl din Unimbɔti tīī-bee.

eng: On the next Sabbath, almost everyone in the city gathered to hear the Lords word.
local: Ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa dal fātìi bāańee, kitiŋ yab kɔkɔ ŋman nìn pée kóoǹní ní, kí dāá pīlifì Unimbɔti bɔti.

eng: When the Jews saw the crowds, they were overcome with jealousy. They argued against what Paul was saying by slandering him.
local: Juuda yab ká kunipaauee, ní ki kíǹ ki bíi ŋáań Pɔɔl nì Baarnaabaasi ipoompoon, ki bí yii Pɔɔl nín sòon̄ tiŋyee kaa sá míǹ, ní ki sìí-u.

eng: Speaking courageously, Paul and Barnabas said, We had to speak Gods word to you first. Since you reject it and show that you are unworthy to receive eternal life, we will turn to the Gentiles.
local: Ní Pɔɔl nì Baarnaabaasi tùkū-bi ibaamɔn, kaa fāŋkì, yii: «An sá nimbi cee ní ti máaǹ kí péé kí sòoǹ Unimbɔti bɔti. Ama, kii ni nín yíì tibɔti gbanti ki dàkafī yii naa máaǹ dimaŋfal din kaa cáá dikúntilee, an puee ti làá nīn kíǹ ki sòoǹ bin kaa sá Juuda yabee cee ní.

eng: This is what the Lord commanded us: I have made you a light for the Gentiles, so that you could bring salvation to the end of the earth .
local: Kun puee ti-Dindaan nín bí tí ŋá miŋyee dee. Ti-Dindaan bí yii: <M lēē-si á nīn sá ŋwalifi kí cùnnì difiil áli kitiŋgbeennyɔkɔ.>».

eng: When the Gentiles heard this, they rejoiced and honored the Lords word. Everyone who was appointed for eternal life believed.
local: Bin kaa sá Juuda yabee gbìl̄ mimmee, ní ki bíi fàal̀ ki pàkà ti-Dindaan nyɔɔbundi. Ní bin kɔkɔ nìn sá bin làá ká dimaŋfal din kaa cáá dikúntilee fōō ki kíí.

eng: and the Lords word was broadcast throughout the entire region.
local: Ní ti-Dindaan nyɔɔbundi gbītì kitiŋ gbanti kɔkɔn.

eng: In Salamis they proclaimed Gods word in the Jewish synagogues. John was with them as their assistant.
local: Bi bàn̄ Salamiinee, ki bíi sòoǹ Unimbɔti bɔti Juuda yab kpafidiiŋŋin. Yoowaneesi-Maarki nìn bí bi-cee kí nīn tútúǹ-bi.

eng: However, the Jews provoked the prominent women among the Gentile God-worshippers, as well as the citys leaders. They instigated others to harass Paul and Barnabas, and threw them out of their district.
local: Ama Juuda yab téé binimpukpaambi bin tɔ́ Unimbɔti marabee nì kitiŋ poon yidambi, ní bi yíkì ki páb́ Pɔɔl nì Baarnaabaasi áli ki jìn̄-bi bi-tiŋki pu.

eng: Paul and Barnabas shook the dust from their feet and went to Iconium.
local: Ní Pɔɔl nì Baarnaabaasi gbáfì bi-taaŋi kpɔŋkpɔm, ní ki búntì Ikooniyum.

eng: Because of the abundant presence of the Holy Spirit in their lives, the disciples were overflowing with happiness.
local: Ama bin nìn fōō Yeesu ki kíí Antiyɔɔsi tiŋkinee nìn gbí dipɔɔpiŋkpaandi nì Circir Fam.

eng: They traveled throughout the island until they arrived at Paphos. There they found a certain man named Bar-Jesus, a Jew who was a false prophet and practiced sorcery.
local: Ní bi cōom̀ Siipiri timmu kɔkɔn, áli ki tin bàn̄ Pafɔsi. Niin ní bi tin ká ujuudaja ubɔ, u sá ukpaŋkpanti bi yíì-u Baar-Yeesu, u mɔ́ntí yii u sá Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu.

eng: He kept company with the governor of that province, an intelligent man named Sergius Paulus. The governor sent for Barnabas and Saul since he wanted to hear Gods word.
local: Gɔmina Seegusi Poolusi uciŋfiti cee ní u nìn bí. Gɔminee là kí pīlifì Unimbɔti nyɔɔbundee ní ki yíiń Baarnaabaasi nì Sɔɔl.

eng: But Elymas the sorcerer opposed them, trying to steer the governor away from the faith.
local: Ama Eelmaasi ukpaŋkpantee - u-yindi dee greeki sooŋyi poon - nìn mɔ́tí, ki là kí pìkiliǹ gɔmina ń taa fōō Unimbɔti bɔti kí kíí.

eng: Empowered by the Holy Spirit, Saul, also known as Paul, glared at Bar-Jesus and.
local: Niinee, Unimbɔti Fam gbí Sɔɔl un bi gíti yíì Pɔɔlee, ní u kpēē-u soooo.

eng: The same thing happened in Iconium. Paul and Barnabas entered the Jewish synagogue and spoke as they had before. As a result, a huge number of Jews and Greeks believed.
local: Ikooniyum tiŋkinee, Pɔɔl nì Baarnaabaasi tí kɔ́ Juuda yab kpafidiikun mbaantiim, ki sòon̄ tiŋan, áli binib tikpil, Juuda yab nì Greeki yab, fōō ki kíí.

eng: Raising his voice, Paul said, Stand up straight on your feet! He jumped up and began to walk.
local: ní u téeń mpɔn yii: «Yīkìí yūĺ mɔ́lmɔl a-taaŋi pu!» Ní u nyīī ki yíkì kùrub ki bíi còom̄.

eng: Seeing what Paul had done, the crowd shouted in the Lycaonian language, The gods have taken human form and come down to visit us.
local: Samaa nìn ká min Pɔɔl ŋée, ní bi bíi tátí nì Liikayoonii sooŋyin yii: «Awaa aba kpántì binib ki kpákatìń ti-cee!».

eng: They referred to Barnabas as Zeus and to Paul as Hermes, since Paul was the main speaker.
local: Ní bi pú Baarnaabaasi diyindi yii Seeyusi , ní ki pú Pɔɔl mun yii Eermeesi , kun puee úǹ di nìn sòoǹ nì samaa.

eng: The priest of Zeus, whose temple was located just outside the city, brought bulls and wreaths to the city gates. Along with the crowds, he wanted to offer sacrifices to them.
local: Ní utandaan un nìn tòotī diwaal din bi yíì Seeyusi, di-diiku yú kitiŋ kpaanceetiŋuee, dūń nsupumpukum ki léeǹ inajaa ki cáań kitiŋ punyɔkɔpu, u nì samaa là kí dūú tòotī-bi.

eng: When the Lords messengers Barnabas and Paul found out about this, they tore their clothes in protest and rushed out into the crowd. They shouted.
local: Yeesu kpambaliŋ Pɔɔl nì Baarnaabaasi gbìl̄ mimmee, an wùn̄-bi, ní bi cátì tiwan tin bi pēēyee ki sáǹ ki nín cá samaayee kansikin ki tátí yii.

eng: People, what are you doing? We are humans too, just like you! We are proclaiming the good news to you: turn to the living God and away from such worthless things. He made the heaven, the earth, the sea, and everything in them. .
local: «Ti-yab, ba ŋá ní ni ŋáań míǹ? Ti mun pée sá binib kii nimbee ní, tiwumbaantiil. Ti bíi tùkù-ni Tibɔmɔntiil yii ní fá ni-waaŋi gbanti ŋin sá fámee kí nóó Unimbɔti un bée. Úǹ di náań yilpu nì taapu nì tiŋku, nì tin kɔkɔwoooo pée bée.

eng: In the past, he permitted every nation to go its own way.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun jítèe, u nìn yāntì ditimbundi kamaa nóó di-saŋŋu.

eng: Nevertheless, he hasnt left himself without a witness. He has blessed you by giving you rain from above as well as seasonal harvests, and satisfying you with food and happiness.
local: Nì míǹ kɔkɔee, u dàkā-ŋi u-laŋŋaŋfitiiku. Di sá yii u yàntī utaa nì, tiwan lòoǹ, tijikaal gbóotí ki tìì-ni dipɔɔpindi.».

eng: Even with these words, they barely kept the crowds from sacrificing to them.
local: Mpɔn nì mpɔn ní Yeesu kpambaliŋ dū tibɔti gbanti ki dūu pīiǹ samaa nsan bí taa ŋmā kí tòotī-bi.

eng: Jews from Antioch and Iconium arrived and won the crowds over. They stoned Paul and dragged him out of the city, supposing he was dead.
local: Boonee, Juuda yab biba nyánní Antiyɔɔsi nì Ikooniyum ki ŋúń ki ŋmātiǹ samaa. Bi nìn yáŋkì Pɔɔl ataŋkpee ki fùn̄-u ki cáa bíl̀ kitiŋ boon ŋkpaanceeti, ki dàkafī yii u kpíì.

eng: However, the Jews who rejected the faith stirred up the Gentiles, poisoning their minds against the brothers.
local: Ama Juuda yab bin kaa fōō ki kííyee téé bin kaa sá Juuda yabee bí cùú ŋá Pɔɔl nì Baarnaabaasi tin kaa ŋān̄ee.

eng: When the disciples surrounded him, he got up and entered the city again. The following day he left with Barnabas for Derbe.
local: Ama Kriisitoo yab dāa dɔ́miń ki māntìi tú-u, ní Pɔɔl yíkì ki nín cá kitiŋin. Kutaa wúntèe, ní u nì Baarnaabaasi búntì Deerbi.

eng: Paul and Barnabas proclaimed the good news to the people in Derbe and made many disciples. Then they returned to Lystra, Iconium, and Antioch, where.
local: Bi sòon̄ Tibɔmɔntiil kitiŋ gbantin ki yāntì binib tikpil kpántì Kriisitoo yab. Boonee ní bi nín gítiń Liista, Ikooniyum nì Antiyɔɔsi.

eng: they strengthened the disciples and urged them to remain firm in the faith. They told them, If we are to enter Gods kingdom, we must pass through many troubles.
local: Bi nìn tìì Kriisitoo yab kunyannyaau, ki sáŋkí-bi yii bí nīn pɔ̄ɔntí bi-ba fookii poon, ní ki bí-bi yii: «An sá nì falaakpaan ní tí nín ŋmā kí kɔ́ Unimbɔti Beelin.».

eng: They appointed elders for each church. With prayer and fasting, they committed these elders to the Lord, in whom they had placed their trust.
local: Bi nìn lēekì bikpilib Kriisitoo nikitil sá kamaa poon. Bi bóob́ kinyɔkɔ ki mēē ki dóòyee, ní ki dū-bi ki ŋá ti-Dindaan un bi fōō ki kííyee ŋaan.

eng: After Paul and Barnabas traveled through Pisidia, they came to Pamphylia.
local: Bi dīń Piisidii puee, ki bāań Paŋfilii.

eng: They proclaimed the word in Perga, then went down to Attalia.
local: Bi sòon̄ Unimbɔti bɔti Peeji ki dóòyee, ní ki nín sìntī Atalii.

eng: From there they sailed to Antioch, where they had been entrusted by Gods grace to the work they had now completed.
local: Niin ní bi kɔ́ meela ki búntì Antiyɔɔsi, laakin Kriisitoo yab nìn mēē Unimbɔti ń tūtuǹ-bi atuun ŋin bi túńee puee.

eng: On their arrival, they gathered the church together and reported everything that God had accomplished through their activity, and how God had opened a door of faith for the Gentiles.
local: Bi bàn̄ee ní ki kpáfì Kriisitoo nikitil ki máafì tin kɔkɔwoooo Unimbɔti ŋá ki tīī-bee, nì min ní u pīitì nsan bin kaa sá Juuda yabee kɔkɔ ń fōō kí kííyee.

eng: They stayed with the disciples a long time.
local: Bi nìn kāl̀ ki yúntì fiii Kriisitoo yab cee.

eng: Nevertheless, Paul and Barnabas stayed there for quite some time, confidently speaking about the Lord. And the Lord confirmed the word about his grace by the signs and wonders he enabled them to perform.
local: Nì míǹ kɔkɔee, Pɔɔl nì Baarnaabaasi kāl̀ ki yúntì Ikooniyum ki sòon̄ ti-Dindaan bɔti, kaa fàŋkí, ní ti-Dindaan yāntì bi ŋáań tidaandi nì maamaacib kí dàkà yii bi nín sòoǹ u-ŋanti puee, an sá ibaamɔn.

eng: The people of the city were divided—some siding with the Jews, others with the Lords messengers.
local: Ní kitiŋin yab cúu yākatì mili: biba bí Juuda yab jandi pú, ní bin kíǹee mun bí Yeesu kpambaliŋ jandi pú.

eng: Then some Gentiles and Jews, including their leaders, hatched a plot to mistreat and stone Paul and Barnabas.
local: Ní Juuda yab nì bin kaa sá Juuda yabee, ki kpáaǹ bi-bɔtiibi, kpáfì ki bíi bɔ́ntí ki là kí sàaǹ-bi, kí yāŋkì-bi ataŋkpee kí kpɔ̀.

eng: When they learned of it, these two messengers fled to the Lycaonian cities of Lystra and Derbe and the surrounding area.
local: Pɔɔl nì Baarnaabaasi béè mimmee, ní ki sáǹ ki búntì ki tin bàn̄ Liista nì Deebi, ntimu mun bí Liikayoonii tiŋkinee, nì mu-kpaanceetii.

eng: where they continued to proclaim the good news.
local: Bi nìn bí niin ní ki sòoǹ Yeesu bɔmɔntiiti.

eng: In Lystra there was a certain man who lacked strength in his legs. He had been crippled since birth and had never walked. Sitting there, he.
local: Ufaliŋfaan ubɔ nìn bí Liista. Bi pée màl̄-uee, u-taaŋi fáká ní waa cōom̀ ki ká.

eng: heard Paul speaking. Paul stared at him and saw that he believed he could be healed.
local: Dalba uninjee bíi pílifí Pɔɔl nyɔɔbundee, ní Pɔɔl dū u-nimbikpeeŋu ki báaǹ u-pu, ki béè yii u cáá fookii Unimbɔti pu ki ŋūǹ kí ká dicáaǹdee.

eng: Some people came down from Judea teaching the family of believers, Unless you are circumcised according to the custom weve received from Moses, you cant be saved.
local: Bininjab biba nìn nyánní Juudee ki dɔ́miń Antiyɔɔsi, ki kíĺ ki bíi dàkà nnabiyaamu yii: «Ni yaa kaa gíí dikpancandi kii Mooyiisi marau nín lī puee, naa ŋūǹ kí ká difiil.».

eng: Why then are you now challenging God by placing a burden on the shoulders of these disciples that neither we nor our ancestors could bear?.
local: Ba pu ní ni cɔ́ŋkí Unimbɔti ki cáá diwaŋgul din ti-naanjab nì timbi ti-ba kaa ŋúń ki túǹee, ki cáa tūntí Kriisitoo yab?.

eng: On the contrary, we believe that we and they are saved in the same way, by the grace of the Lord Jesus.
local: An sá nì ti-Dindaan Yeesu ŋanti pu ní ti máká yii ti ká difiil kii bi muee.».

eng: The entire assembly fell quiet as they listened to Barnabas and Paul describe all the signs and wonders God did among the Gentiles through their activity.
local: Niinee ní samaa kɔkɔ ŋmíl̀, ní ki kíǹ ki bíi pílifí Baarnaabaasi nì Pɔɔl nín bíi máafí Unimbɔti nín nìn dī bi-pu ki ŋáaǹ tidaandi nì maamaacib bin kaa sá Juuda yabee kansikinee.

eng: When Barnabas and Paul also fell silent, James responded, Fellow believers, listen to me.
local: Bi sòon̄ ki dóòyee, ní Yakubu fōō dinyɔɔbundi ki bí yii: «M-nabiyaamu, pīlifìmaan-mi!.

eng: Simon reported how, in his kindness, God came to the Gentiles in the first place, to raise up from them a people of God.
local: Siimɔn bàà máafì-ni min ní Unimbɔti nìn kpēē ki lútì doooo ŋkilkaamin bin kaa sá Juuda yabee kansikin bin ń nīn sá u-yabee.

eng: The prophets words agree with this; as it is written.
local: An nóó nì Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi nín nìn sòon̄ puee, kun puee an nìn ŋmàn̄ Unimbɔti gbɔŋkun yii.

eng: After this I will return, and I will rebuild Davids fallen tent; I will rebuild what has been torn down. I will restore it .
local: <Míǹ boonee, m làá dāá gítiń kí fàtīí mā Daafiidi diiku kun nìn lítèe. M làá dāá fàtīí mā u-guŋŋi mɔ́lmɔl.

eng: so that the rest of humanity will seek the Lord, even all the Gentiles who belong to me. The Lord says this, the one who does these things .
local: kí ŋmā kí yàntī binib bin kɔkɔ kíǹee ń nyàab̀ man bi-Dindaan, ki kpáaǹ atimbun ŋin kɔkɔ m yíiń ŋí nīn sá m-yabee.

eng: known from earliest times.
local: Man ni-Dindaan un nìn yāntì bi béè dooooyee tin tin m làá dāá ŋée di lī míǹ.>.

eng: Therefore, I conclude that we shouldnt create problems for Gentiles who turn to God.
local: «Míǹ puee ní man Yakubu ká yii, an kaa máaǹ tí nīn páb́ bin kaa sá Juuda yab ki kpántì bi-ba ki nóó Unimbɔtee.

eng: Paul and Barnabas took sides against these Judeans and argued strongly against their position. The church at Antioch appointed Paul, Barnabas, and several others from Antioch to go up to Jerusalem to set this question before the apostles and the elders.
local: Niinee, Pɔɔl nì Baarnaabaasi kaa kíí, ní kunikpɔŋkpɔkɔkpaaŋŋu kɔ́ bi-kansikin. Ní bi bí yii Pɔɔl nì Baarnaabaasi nì Kriisitoo yab biba mun ń jɔ̄m̀ Jeerusaleem Yeesu kpambaliŋ nì bikpilib cee, bí kpèè an bɔti.

eng: Instead, we should write a letter, telling them to avoid the pollution associated with idols, sexual immorality, eating meat from strangled animals, and consuming blood.
local: Ama tí ŋmà kí tùkùmaan-bi bí yàntī kí taa ti nín̄ jīǹ tiwan tin bi dū ki tōotì awaayee, kí taa ŋá tilaakpaal, kí taa ŋmɔ́ fatiku, kí taa jī tiwan tin baa lāntì fatiku nyáńee.

eng: After all, Moses has been proclaimed in every city for a long time, and is read aloud every Sabbath in every synagogue.
local: Di sá yii, doooo jaanjaan ki dāa dūu sāā díǹ, binib gbíntìi bí ki sòoǹ Mooyiisi marab pu ntimu sá kamaa poon, ki kàaǹ u-gbɔnti akpafidiinni ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa sá kamaa dal.».

eng: The apostles and the elders, along with the entire church, agreed to send some delegates chosen from among themselves to Antioch, together with Paul and Barnabas. They selected Judas Barsabbas and Silas, who were leaders among the brothers and sisters.
local: Niinee ní Yeesu kpambaliŋ, nì bikpilib, nì Kriisitoo yab kɔkɔ bí yii bi làá lēē bi-kansikin biba kí tɔ̄ Antiyɔɔsi, bi nì Pɔɔl nì Baarnaabaasi. Bin bi lēēyee di sá Juudi un bi nìn yíì Baarsabaasee, nì Siilasi. Bininjab bili gbanti ní nnabiyaamu kɔkɔ nìn tɔ́.

eng: They were to carry this letter: The apostles and the elders, to the Gentile brothers and sisters in Antioch, Syria, and Cilicia. Greetings!.
local: Ní bi tīī-bi kugbɔŋu kun poon bi ŋmàn̄ yii: «Timbi ni-nabiyaamu Yeesu kpambaliŋ nì bikpilib jáamiń nimbi nnabiyaamu mun kaa sá Juuda yab ki bí Antiyɔɔsi nì Siirii nì Siilisiiyee.

eng: Weve heard that some of our number have disturbed you with unsettling words we didnt authorize.
local: Ti gbìl̄ yii ti-yab biba dɔ́miń ni-cee ki sòon̄ nì ni tibɔti, ki yāntì ni bākatì nì ni-ba, ki bíil̀ ni-pɔbiliŋ. Ti mun kaa túnní-bi ni-cee.

eng: We reached a united decision to select some delegates and send them to you along with our dear friends Barnabas and Paul.
local: Míǹ pu ní ti mun sòon̄ ki kpáfì ki lēē binib biba ki dū-bi ki túnní ni-cee, bi nì Baarnaabaasi nì Pɔɔl, ti-nabineeŋkaamu.

eng: These people have devoted their lives to the name of our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: binib bin dū bi-ba ki tīī ŋkúm ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo yindi puee.

eng: Therefore, we are sending Judas and Silas. They will confirm what we have written.
local: Míǹ puee ní ti dū binib bili nee, Juudi nì Siilasi ki túnní ni-cee, bí sòoǹ ni-nimbiin tibɔbaantiil gbanti pu.

eng: The Holy Spirit has led us to the decision that no burden should be placed on you other than these essentials.
local: Min ŋān̄ Circir Fam cee ki kpáaǹ ti mun ceeyee di sá tí taa ti dū awaŋgul aba kí túń-ni, an yaa kaa tin sá tin máaǹ ní ŋée.

eng: refuse food offered to idols, blood, the meat from strangled animals, and sexual immorality. You will do well to avoid such things. Farewell.
local: Tíǹ di sá yii ni máaǹ kí yíí kí taa ti ŋmɔ́ tiwaatootinan, kí taa ŋmɔ́ fatiku, kí taa ŋmɔ́ tiwan tin kpíì baa lāntì fatiku nyáńee, kí taa ŋá tilaakpaal. Min ŋān̄ ki tīī-nee di sá ní kòò tiwan gbanti. Ní nīn bí tiŋan!».

eng: The church sent this delegation on their way. They traveled through Phoenicia and Samaria, telling stories about the conversion of the Gentiles to everyone. Their reports thrilled the brothers and sisters.
local: Ní Kriisitoo nikitil nyántì-bi nsanni, ní bi dīń Feenisii nì Samaarii ki máafí bin kaa sá Juuda yabee nín kpántì bi-bimbim ki fōō Yeesu ki kíí puee. Niinee ní an mɔ́ɔkiǹ Kriisitoo nikitil kɔkɔ.

eng: When Barnabas, Paul, and the delegates were sent on their way, they went down to Antioch. They gathered the believers and delivered the letter.
local: Niinee ní binib bin bi túee búntì Antiyɔɔsi. Bi bàn̄ee ki kpáfì Kriisitoo yab ki dū kugbɔŋu ki tīī-bi.

eng: The people read it, delighted with its encouraging message.
local: Bi kàan̄ kugbɔŋuee ki dóòyee, ní an mɔ́ɔkiǹ báà ŋma, kun puee kugbɔŋuee cáań-bi dipɔɔsɔŋkil.

eng: Judas and Silas were prophets, and they said many things that encouraged and strengthened the brothers and sisters.
local: Juudi nì Siilasi mun nìn sá Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi, ní ki sòon̄ tibɔti tikpil ki sɔ̄ŋkì nnabiyaamu pɔbiliŋ, ní an kútì-bi mpɔn.

eng: Judas and Silas stayed there awhile, then were sent back with a blessing of peace from the brothers and sisters to those who first sent them.
local: An dāa ŋmāntì ŋyunti ŋubɔee, ní nnabiyaamu mun cee bi cūtèe kāaǹ-bi nì dipɔɔpindi, ki yāntì bi nín gítiń bin túnní-bee cee. .

eng: Paul and Barnabas stayed in Antioch, where, together with many others, they taught and proclaimed the good news of the Lords word.
local: Pɔɔl nì Baarnaabaasi gbíntì Antiyɔɔsi bi nì binib tikpil bí ki sòoǹ ki dàkà ti-Dindaan bɔti.

eng: Some time later, Paul said to Barnabas, Lets go back and visit all the brothers and sisters in every city where we preached the Lords word. Lets see how they are doing.
local: Iyunti iba jítì boonee, ní Pɔɔl bí Baarnaabaasi yii: «Yàntī tí gítí ntimu mun poon ti nìn sòon̄ ti-Dindaan bɔtee kí màntì ti-nabiyaamu pu kí kpèè yii bi bí mana.».

eng: Barnabas wanted to take John Mark with them.
local: Ní Baarnaabaasi bàà là bí kpáaǹ Yoowaneesi un bi yíì Maarkee.

eng: Paul insisted that they shouldnt take him along, since he had deserted them in Pamphylia and hadnt continued with them in their work.
local: ní Pɔɔl kaa nìn kíí yii, baa ŋūǹ kí kpáaǹ unil un nìn dū-bi ki fá doooo Paŋfilii, bi nì u kaa ti nóó ki tūǹ ditundee.

eng: Their argument became so intense that they went their separate ways. Barnabas took Mark and sailed to Cyprus.
local: Ní bi nìn kpáká áli an pɔ́ɔkì, ní ki dāa kpáàa dūu yāaǹ tɔbin. Ní Baarnaabaasi yōoǹ Maarki, bi kɔ́ meela ki nín cá Siipiri.

eng: When they arrived in Jerusalem, the church, the apostles, and the elders all welcomed them. They gave a full report of what God had accomplished through their activity.
local: Bi bàn̄ Jeerusaleemee, ní Yeesu kpambaliŋ nì bikpilib nì Kriisitoo nikitil kɔkɔ fōō-bi. Bi nìn máafì Unimbɔti nín dū-bi ki túń atuun puee.

eng: Paul chose Silas and left, entrusted by the brothers and sisters to the Lords grace.
local: Ní Pɔɔl mun lēē Siilasi. Kí wàatī Pɔɔl ń nīn nín cée, nnabiyaamu mēē ti-Dindaan ń bìin̄ u-boon.

eng: He traveled through Syria and Cilicia, strengthening the churches.
local: Ní u dī Siirii nì Siilisii timmu, ki mɔ̀tīi kútí Kriisitoo nikitiŋ mpɔn.

eng: Some believers from among the Pharisees stood up and claimed, The Gentiles must be circumcised. They must be required to keep the Law from Moses.
local: Niinee ní Fariisab jandi din nìn fōō ki kííyee yíkì, kaa kíí, yii, bi máaǹ kí gīfì dikpancandi bin kaa sá Juuda yabee kí yàntī bí nīn ŋúb́ Mooyiisi marab kí wàatī ní bí nīn nín sá Kriisitoo yab.

eng: The apostles and the elders gathered to consider this matter.
local: Ní Yeesu kpambaliŋ nì bikpilib kpáfì kí kpèè an bɔti.

eng: After much debate, Peter stood and addressed them, Fellow believers, you know that, early on, God chose me from among you as the one through whom the Gentiles would hear the word of the gospel and come to believe.
local: Bi nìn kpáká ki yúntì, ní Peetroo dāa nín yíkì ki bí yii: «M-nabiyaamu, ni nyí yii, doooo ŋkilkaam ní Unimbɔti lēē-mi ni-kansikin ḿ sòoǹ tibɔmɔntiil kí tīī bin kaa sá Juuda yabee, bí gbìl̄ kí fōō kí kíí.

eng: God, who knows peoples deepest thoughts and desires, confirmed this by giving them the Holy Spirit, just as he did to us.
local: Ní Unimbɔti un nyí binib pɔbiliŋinee tīī-bi Circir Fam kii timbee, kí dàkà yii u fōō-bi.

eng: He made no distinction between us and them, but purified their deepest thoughts and desires through faith.
local: Waa gāntì timbi nì bíǹ. Bi nín fōō-u ki kííyee ní u ŋá bi-pɔbiliŋ circir.

eng: Paul reached Derbe, and then Lystra, where there was a disciple named Timothy. He was the son of a believing Jewish woman and a Greek father.
local: Pɔɔl bàn̄ Deebi nì Liistee, Kriisitoo yɔu ubɔ nìn bí niin, bi yíì-u Tiimootee. U-na nìn sá Ujuudanimpu ní ki fōō ki kíí, ní u-baa sá Ugreekija.

eng: Immediately after he saw the vision, we prepared to leave for the province of Macedonia, concluding that God had called us to proclaim the good news to them.
local: Pɔɔl ká mimmee, ní ti béè yii Unimbɔti di yíì-ti tí cùú sòoǹ u-bɔmɔntiiti Maseeduwaan. Dicilpu niinee ní ti bɔ́ntì ki nín cá.

eng: We sailed from Troas straight for Samothrace and came to Neapolis the following day.
local: Ti kɔ́ meela ki yíkì Troowaasi, ní ki túĺ dicancaal ki búntì Samootraasi. Kutaa wúntèe, ti nín cá Neeyapooliisi.

eng: From there we went to Philippi, a city of Macedonias first district and a Roman colony. We stayed in that city several days.
local: ní ki dāa yíkì niin ki búntì Fiiliipi, kitiŋkpaaŋŋi kin bí Maseeduwaan tiŋkin, ki bí Room yab ŋaanee. Ti nìn kāl̀ kitiŋ gbantin iwiŋkaaŋkaa iba.

eng: On the Sabbath we went outside the city gate to the riverbank, where we thought there might be a place for prayer. We sat down and began to talk with the women who had gathered.
local: Ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa dalee, ti nyáń kitiŋ kpaanceetiŋu ŋkpin ŋubɔ caŋin, ki nyàab̀ laakin bi kpáfí ki mèé Unimbɔtee. Ti kāl̀ee, ní ki sòon̄ nì binimpoobi bin nìn kpáfì niinee.

eng: One of those women was Lydia, a Gentile God-worshipper from the city of Thyatira, a dealer in purple cloth. As she listened, the Lord enabled her to embrace Pauls message.
local: Unimpu ubɔ nìn bí bin nìn bíi pílifée kansikin, bi yíì-u Liidiya. U nìn nyánní Tiyatiir tiŋkin ki sá unyampu ki nyáfí bisatamambi. U nìn tɔ́ Unimbɔti marab, ní ti-Dindaan pīitì u-pɔbil ki yāntì u fōō Pɔɔl bɔti.

eng: Once she and her household were baptized, she urged, Now that you have decided that I am a believer in the Lord, come and stay in my house. And she persuaded us.
local: Unimpuee yāntì ti fùl̄ u nì u-samaau Unimbɔti nyim. Ní u mēē-ti yii: «Ni yaa lafun nyí yii tiŋman m fōō ti-Dindaan ki kíí, ní dāan̄ kí nīn kóò m-dumpu.» Ní u páb́-ti áli ti lafun cūtì u-dumpu.

eng: One day, when we were on the way to the place for prayer, we met a slave woman. She had a spirit that enabled her to predict the future. She made a lot of money for her owners through fortune-telling.
local: Dalba ti nìn cá ŋwimmeem paaŋŋu puee, ti tú uyumbusapɔɔn ubɔ. U nìn cáá arasinii ubɔ u-ni ki wāĺ. An di nìn yì-tì u kāntí animbil tikpil ki cāabìń u-dindambi.

eng: She began following Paul and us, shouting, These people are servants of the Most High God! They are proclaiming a way of salvation to you.
local: Ní usapɔɔnee kíǹ ki nóó ti nì Pɔɔl, ki tátí yii: «Bininjab nee sá yilpu Nimbɔtiu tɔntɔnliibi ní, bi bíi sòoǹ nì ni min ní ni làá dī kí ká difiilee ní!».

eng: She did this for many days. This annoyed Paul so much that he finally turned and said to the spirit, In the name of Jesus Christ, I command you to leave her! It left her at that very moment.
local: U dū iwiin tikpil ki nóó-ti ki sòoǹ an bɔti. Pɔɔl dāa gīīyee, ní ki fātìi bí arasiniiyee yii: «Nì Yeesu Kriisitoo yindi pu ní m bí yii á nyā usapɔɔn nee ni.» Ní arasiniiyee kpáàa nyáń niin dicilpu.

eng: Her owners realized that their hope for making money was gone. They grabbed Paul and Silas and dragged them before the officials in the city center.
local: Uyumbuee dindambi ká yii Pɔɔl bíil̀ nsan ŋun pu bi dì ki kāntí animbilee, ní bi cúú u nì Siilasi ki dáaĺ-bi ki cáa cūnnì bikpilib nimbiin laakin kunipaau nìn kpáfée.

eng: The brothers and sisters in Lystra and Iconium spoke well of him.
local: Nnabiyaamu mun nìn bí Liista nì Ikooniyumee nìn sòoǹ kijapaayee pu yii u sá unimɔnti.

eng: When her owners approached the legal authorities, they said, These people are causing an uproar in our city. They are Jews.
local: ní ki dāa yōoǹ-bi ki cūnnì biyidambi cee ki bí yii: «Binib nee di bíiĺ kitiŋ. Bi sá Juuda yab ní.

eng: who promote customs that we Romans cant accept or practice.
local: Marab bin bi dàkèe, timbi Room yab kaa ŋūǹ kí fōōyaaa, kaa ŋūǹ kí dī-bi mun.».

eng: The crowd joined in the attacks against Paul and Silas, so the authorities ordered that they be stripped of their clothes and beaten with a rod.
local: Niinee ní kunipaauee kɔkɔ dɔ́miń ki kútì bi-pu, ní bi kpáfì Pɔɔl nì Siilasi pu, ní biyidambi dáaĺ Pɔɔl nì Siilasi bɔkutiŋ ki pēetì, ní ki bí yii bí gbá-bi anaalab.

eng: When Paul and Silas had been severely beaten, the authorities threw them into prison and ordered the jailer to secure them with great care.
local: Bi yāntì bi tāfì-bi míǹ ki dóòyee, ní bi cáá-bi ki tɔ́ sarkan, ní ki bí yii sooja un cí bisarkadambee ń nīn kpèé-bi tiŋan.

eng: When he received these instructions, he threw them into the innermost cell and secured their feet in stocks.
local: Bi kpáafì soojee míǹ ki dóòyee, ní u fōō-bi ki cáa tɔ́ kusarkadii poon ki bá-bi abaa.

eng: Around midnight Paul and Silas were praying and singing hymns to God, and the other prisoners were listening to them.
local: Kunyataasiiu nyɔkɔmee, Pɔɔl nì Siilasi bíi mèé ki gà ilan ki nyɔ́ŋkí Unimbɔti, ní bisarkadambi bin kíǹee bíi pílifí.

eng: All at once there was such a violent earthquake that it shook the prisons foundations. The doors flew open and everyones chains came loose.
local: Kpalaa kpalaa niinee, kitiŋ cīŋkì ki kpáaǹ kusarkadii. Dicilpu niinee, mpunyɔmu kɔkɔ cúu pīitì, ní tin bi bóob́ bisarkadambee kɔkɔ cúu bútì.

eng: When the jailer awoke and saw the open doors of the prison, he thought the prisoners had escaped, so he drew his sword and was about to kill himself.
local: Sooja un cí bisarkadambee nín fīntì, mpunyɔmu wāāyee, u dàkafī yii bisarkadambi kɔkɔ sáǹ ki kátì dee, ní u dáaĺ ki fɔ́fì u-tàataajiki kí bàà kpɔ̀ u-ba.

eng: But Paul shouted loudly, Dont harm yourself! Were all here.
local: Niinee ní Pɔɔl téeń mpɔn yii: «Taa kpɔ̀ a-ba! Ti-kɔkɔ sèé doo.».

eng: The jailer called for some lights, rushed in, and fell trembling before Paul and Silas.
local: Ní soojee bí yii bí pɔ̄ń-u ŋŋmi. Niinee ní u tóotì malaa ki cūtìi gbāaǹ Pɔɔl nì Siilasi nimbiin, tiwun jìkì.

eng: Paul wanted to take Timothy with him, so he circumcised him. This was because of the Jews who lived in those areas, for they all knew Timothys father was Greek.
local: Pɔɔl nìn là Tiimootee ń nīn bí u-ceeyee, ní u kpáaǹ-u. Juuda yab bin nìn kóò niinee puee, u nìn gíí-u dikpancandi, kun puee bi kɔkɔ nìn nyí yii Tiimootee baa sá Ugreekija.

eng: He led them outside and asked, Honorable masters, what must I do to be rescued.
local: Ní u cáá-bi ki nyántì mmɔŋki, ní ki bālifì-bi yii: «M-dindambi, m làá ŋá mana ní kí nín ká difiil dee?».

eng: They replied, Believe in the Lord Jesus, and you will be saved—you and your entire household.
local: Ní bi kíí-u yii: «Fōō ti-Dindaan Yeesu kí kíí, a làá ká difiil, sii nì a-dicaŋku kɔkɔ.».

eng: They spoke the Lords word to him and everyone else in his house.
local: Niinee ní bi bíi sòoǹ nì u nì bin kɔkɔ nìn bí u-dumpuee ti-Dindaan nyɔɔbundi.

eng: Right then, in the middle of the night, the jailer welcomed them and washed their wounds. He and everyone in his household were immediately baptized.
local: Ní soojee yōoǹ-bi kunyeeu kukúluu gbanti poon ki fíntì bi-buuŋyi. Niin dicilpu ní bi fùl̄ u nì u-dicaŋku kɔkɔ Unimbɔti nyim.

eng: He brought them into his home and gave them a meal. He was overjoyed because he and everyone in his household had come to believe in God.
local: Boonee ní u yōoǹ-bi ki cáa jɔ́m̀ u-dumpu yilpu, ní ki ŋá-bi tijin. Ní u nì u-dicaŋku kíǹ ki bí dipɔɔpindin bi nín fōō Unimbɔti ki kííyee pu.

eng: The next morning the legal authorities sent the police to the jailer with the order Release those people.
local: Kutaa wúntèe ní biyidambee túnní pilisib ń dāá tùkù soojee ú líí binibee.

eng: So the jailer reported this to Paul, informing him, The authorities sent word that you both are to be released. You can leave now. Go in peace.
local: Ní soojee cūtìi tùkū Pɔɔl yii: «Biyidambi túnní yii ḿ líí-ni. Míǹ puee, nyā kí nīn cámaan nì ŋgbansɔŋfi.».

eng: Paul told the police, Even though we are Roman citizens, they beat us publicly without first finding us guilty of a crime, and they threw us into prison. And now they want to send us away secretly? No way! They themselves will have to come and escort us out.
local: Ní Pɔɔl bí pilisibee yii: «Bi cúú timbi bin sá Room tindambiyaamuee, kaa sòon̄ ti-bɔti ki ká ti-bɔkpil ní ki gbáń-ti samaa kɔkɔ nimbiin ki cáań-ti ki pīiǹ kudii. Ní kɔŋkɔnnee, bi là kí báĺ soooo kí nyāntí-taaa? Ti yíì kpataaa, see biyidambi bi-ba ń dāan̄ kí nyāntí-ti!».

eng: The police reported this to the legal authorities, who were alarmed to learn that Paul and Silas were Roman citizens.
local: Pilisib cáá dinyɔɔbundee ki fātìi bàn̄ biyidambee cee. Bi nín gbìl̄ yii njapaamu gbanti sá Room tindambiyaamuee, tifaandi kɔ́-bi.

eng: They came and consoled Paul and Silas, escorting them out of prison and begging them to leave the city.
local: Ní bi dɔ́miń ki sɔ̄ŋkì bi-pɔbiliŋ ki nín nyántì-bi, ní ki gbāam̀-bi yii bí yīkì kitiŋ gbanti poon.

eng: As Paul and his companions traveled through the cities, they instructed Gentile believers to keep the regulations put in place by the apostles and elders in Jerusalem.
local: Ntimu mun kɔkɔwoooo poon bi jītée, bi sòoǹ ki tùkù Kriisitoo yab yii Yeesu kpambaliŋ nì Jeerusaleem kpilib nín nìn kpáfì ki ká tiŋyee, bí nīn ŋúb́-tì tiŋan.

eng: Paul and Silas left the prison and made their way to Lydias house where they encouraged the brothers and sisters. Then they left Philippi.
local: Bi nyáń kusarkadiinee, ní ki cūtìi kɔ́ Liidiya cee ki ká nnabiyaamu ki kútì-bi tibɔti, ní ki nín yíkì niin.

eng: So the churches were strengthened in the faith and every day their numbers flourished.
local: An nìn yāntì Kriisitoo nikitiŋ nìn mɔ̀tīi fòol̄ ki kíikí ní bi-wíiŋŋu gbēentí kutaa sá kamaa.

eng: Paul and his companions traveled throughout the regions of Phrygia and Galatia because the Holy Spirit kept them from speaking the word in the province of Asia.
local: Circir Fam nìn yíì yii Pɔɔl ń taa sòoǹ Unimbɔti bɔti Asii tiŋkinee, ní bi dūu dī Friijii nì Galaati timmun watil.

eng: When they approached the province of Mysia, they tried to enter the province of Bithynia, but the Spirit of Jesus wouldnt let them.
local: Bi tóob́ Miisii tiŋkee ki bàà mākafì kí cù Bitinii, ama ní Yeesu Fam tí yíì, yii bí taa dī niin.

eng: Passing by Mysia, they went down to Troas instead.
local: Niinee ní bi dūu dī Miisii ki nín sìntī Troowaasi.

eng: A vision of a man from Macedonia came to Paul during the night. He stood urging Paul, Come over to Macedonia and help us.
local: Kunyeeuee ní Unimbɔti ŋúbitì Pɔɔl nimbiliŋ, u ká umaseeduwaanja ubɔ yú ki gbàam̄-u yii: «Dīń Maseeduwaan kí dāá jòoń-ti!».

eng: Paul and Silas journeyed through Amphipolis and Apollonia, then came to Thessalonica, where there was a Jewish synagogue.
local: Pɔɔl nì Siilasi dī Aŋfiipooliisi pú ki cūtìi jítì Apooloonii, ní ki nín bàn̄ Teesalooniiki. Juuda yab nìn tí cáá niin kukpafidii.

eng: As soon as it was dark, the brothers and sisters sent Paul and Silas on to Beroea. When they arrived, they went to the Jewish synagogue.
local: Kunyeeu mɔ́ kpalaayee, nnabiyaamu yāntì Pɔɔl nì Siilasi búntì Beeree. Bi bàn̄ niinee, ní ki nín cá Juuda yab kpafidiikun.

eng: The Beroean Jews were more honorable than those in Thessalonica. This was evident in the great eagerness with which they accepted the word and examined the scriptures each day to see whether Paul and Silas teaching was true.
local: Binib gbanti nìn cáá mbimbimɔntiim ki jítiǹ Teesalooniiki yab, kun puee bíǹ nìn fōō Unimbɔti bɔti mmɔntiimin. Bi nìn kpátí Unimbɔti gbɔŋku ŋwiin kamaa ki kpèé yii tin Pɔɔl sòoǹ nì bee yaa sá ibaamɔn.

eng: Many came to believe, including a number of reputable Greek women and many Greek men.
local: An pu ní bi-kansikin kunipaau, ki kpáaǹ Greeki pijab nì bininjab tikpil, nìn fōō ki kíí.

eng: The Jews from Thessalonica learned that Paul also proclaimed Gods word in Beroea, so they went there too and were upsetting and disturbing the crowds.
local: Ama Juuda yab bin bí Teesalooniikee nín béè yii Pɔɔl tí bíi sòoǹ Unimbɔti bɔti Beereeyee, ní bi dɔ́miń niin ki cáań fìkil fikil kitiŋin, ki yāntì samaa pɔbiliŋ bíil̀.

eng: The brothers and sisters sent Paul away to the seacoast at once, but Silas and Timothy remained at Beroea.
local: Niinee ní nnabiyaamu yāntì Pɔɔl búntì tiŋku caŋin. Siilasi nì Tiimootee, bíǹ nìn gbíntìi bí Beeree niin.

eng: Those who escorted Paul led him as far as Athens, then returned with instructions for Silas and Timothy to come to him as quickly as possible.
local: Bin nìn céeǹ Pɔɔlee nìn cáá-u ki cūnnì áli Ateeni ki fàtīi gítińee, ní Pɔɔl bí yii bi yaa bàn̄ee bí tùkù Siilasi nì Tiimootee yii bí kíntiń-u malaa.

eng: While Paul waited for them in Athens, he was deeply distressed to find that the city was flooded with idols.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun Pɔɔl nìn kā Ateeni ki cí-bee, an nìn páb́-u tikpil, kun puee kitiŋ gbanti sá awaa ŋmaniŋma ní.

eng: He began to interact with the Jews and Gentile God-worshippers in the synagogue. He also addressed whoever happened to be in the marketplace each day.
local: Ní u kíĺ ki bíi sòoǹ nì Juuda yab nì bin nìn tɔ́ Unimbɔti marabee kukpafidiin, ní ki sòoǹ ŋwiin kamaa kinyɔŋin, laakin binib kpáfée.

eng: Certain Epicurean and Stoic philosophers engaged him in discussion too. Some said, What an amateur! Whats he trying to say? Others remarked, He seems to be a proclaimer of foreign gods.
local: Anikiti ali aba nì Pɔɔl nìn bíi sòoǹ. Binib gbantee nìn nyí kugbɔŋu tiŋan. Dinikitil diba sá Eepiikuur yab, ní din kíǹee sá Stooyiiki yab. Ní biba bí yii: «Unifan nee kalin sòoǹ ba yati ní?» Ní bin kíǹee bí yii: «U máaǹ ki làá nīn sá un sòoǹ inimbɔticaan bɔtee ní.» Kun pu bi lì mimmee di sá yii Pɔɔl nìn bíi sòoǹ Yeesu nì difìkitil bɔti pu.

eng: They took him into custody and brought him to the council on Mars Hill. What is this new teaching? Can we learn what you are talking about?.
local: Ní bi yōoǹ Pɔɔl ki cūnnì kitiŋ yidambi nimbiin, ní ki bí-u yii: «A-dàkatam nee sá tibɔcan, mimmee, a ŋūǹ kí tùkù-ti tibɔcan tin a dàkà nee taapuɔɔɔ?.

eng: As was Pauls custom, he entered the synagogue and for three Sabbaths interacted with them on the basis of the scriptures.
local: Pɔɔl nìn kɔ́ ki bíi dàkà kii u nín pée ŋáań puee, ki ŋá nnyɔmu muta. Kinyɔŋ sá kamaa poonee, u nìn cá kukpafidiin ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa dal ki sòoǹ nì bi.

eng: Youve told us some strange things and we want to know what they mean. .
local: Kun puee tin ti pée gbìl̀ a sòoǹee, taa láá gbìl̄-tì ki ká dalba dalba. Míǹ puee, ti bàà là kí béé tibɔti gbanti nín sá puee.».

eng: They said this because all Athenians as well as the foreigners who live in Athens used to spend their time doing nothing but talking about or listening to the newest thing.
local: Di sá yii Ateeni yab nì binicambi bin bí bi-kansikinee yati di nìn pée sá bí nīn kā ki pílifí tibɔcan ŋma.

eng: Paul stood up in the middle of the council on Mars Hill and said, People of Athens, I see that you are very religious in every way.
local: Ní Pɔɔl yáatìi yúl̀ biyidambi nimbiin ki bí yii: «Ateeni yab, m ká yii, binib kansikinee, nimbi di sá bin nóó inimbɔti tiŋanee.

eng: As I was walking through town and carefully observing your objects of worship, I even found an altar with this inscription: To an unknown God. What you worship as unknown, I now proclaim to you.
local: Di sá yii m nín jītí ni-tiŋki nee poonee, m ká tiwan tin ní ni jáaḿee. Áli gba, m kpáàa ká nsaraafan ŋun pu bi ŋmàn̄ yii: <Unimbɔti un baa nyée.> Un ni jáaḿ kaa nyée, úǹ pu ní m dɔ́miń kí sòoǹ nì ni.

eng: God, who made the world and everything in it, is Lord of heaven and earth. He doesnt live in temples made with human hands.
local: Úǹ di sá Unimbɔti un náań duulinya nì tin kɔkɔwoooo pée bée, ki sá yilpu nì taapu Dindaan. Waa kóò adiin ŋin binib cáá bi-ŋalii ki māāyee ni.

eng: Nor is God served by human hands, as though he needed something, since he is the one who gives life, breath, and everything else.
local: kaa nyàab̀ binib tūtuntam báà an yaa pée sá tintee. An kaa sá úǹ di tìì báà ŋma dimaŋfal nì ŋfam nì tikɔkɔɔɔ?.

eng: From one person God created every human nation to live on the whole earth, having determined their appointed times and the boundaries of their lands.
local: Úǹ di dī unibaanti pu ki nyántì anibul ki gbéeǹ kitiŋ kɔkɔ pu, ki síiǹ atimbun kɔkɔ nì ŋi-kɔŋkooŋyi nyɔkɔm.

eng: God made the nations so they would seek him, perhaps even reach out to him and find him. In fact, God isnt far away from any of us.
local: U ŋá míǹ ki là yii bin yaa táfí ki nyàab̀ úǹ Unimbɔtee yaa táá ŋmā kí ká-u. Ama Unimbɔti gbanti kaa dátiǹ-ti.

eng: In God we live, move, and exist. As some of your own poets said, We are his offspring.
local: di sá yii: <Úǹ pu ní ti fòotī ki pɔ̄ɔ̄ ní ki bí.> Kii ni-laŋgaaliibi biba nín gāā puee, yii: <Ti mun pée sá u-biyaamu ní.>.

eng: Therefore, as Gods offspring, we have no need to imagine that the divine being is like a gold, silver, or stone image made by human skill and thought.
local: Kii ti nín sá u-biyaamu puee, taa máaǹ kí dàkafì yii Unimbɔti sá kii awaa ŋin binib dākafì ki dū bi-ciiŋyi ki dū sindaariyab nì animbil ki mɔ́ɔ́yee, àá kii tiwaŋkpítikaalee.

eng: Through his interpretation of the scriptures, he demonstrated that the Christ had to suffer and rise from the dead. He declared, This Jesus whom I proclaim to you is the Christ.
local: ki kàaǹ ki dàkà-bi Unimbɔti gbɔŋku, laakin an nìn ŋmàn̄ yii Meesiya làá dāá kpú kí dāá fìkitī bitaŋkpiibi kansikinee. Ní u bí-bi yii: «Yeesu un bɔti m sòoǹ-ni nee, úǹ di sá Meesiya.».

eng: God overlooks ignorance of these things in times past, but now directs everyone everywhere to change their hearts and lives.
local: Ama Unimbɔti kaa dū ŋyunti ŋun binib kaa nìn nyí tibee ki dūu ŋá tibɔti. Míǹ puee ní kɔŋkɔnnee u bíi tùkù ajan kɔkɔ nib yii bi-kɔkɔ máaǹ kí fá bi-kpitii.

eng: This is because God has set a day when he intends to judge the world justly by a man he has appointed. God has given proof of this to everyone by raising him from the dead.
local: Di sá yii u yóoǹ ŋwiin ŋubɔ ŋun ní u làá yàntī Yeesu ń sòoǹ duulinya kɔkɔ bɔti nì ibaamɔnee, kun puee uninja gbanti ní u fīkitì bitaŋkpiibi kansikin ki dàkā-ti kudaaŋŋu páaa yii úǹ ní u lēē.».

eng: When they heard about the resurrection from the dead, some began to ridicule Paul. However, others said, Well hear from you about this again.
local: Bi nín gbìl̄ u sòon̄ bitaŋkpiibi fìkititam puee, ní biba bíi bèel̄-u, ní bin kíǹee bí yii: «Dalba á dāá gíti sòoǹ tibɔti gbanti pu.».

eng: At that, Paul left the council.
local: Niinee ní Pɔɔl yíkì bi-kansikin ki nyáń.

eng: Some people joined him and came to believe, including Dionysius, a member of the council on Mars Hill, a woman named Damaris, and several others.
local: Nì míǹ kɔkɔee, binib biba nìn fōō ki kíí, ki dū bi-ba ki nákáǹ Pɔɔl. Uyidaan un bi yíì Deeniiyee nì unimpu ubɔ bi yíì-u Damariisi nìn bí bi-kansikin. Biba mun nìn kútì.

eng: Some were convinced and joined Paul and Silas, including a larger number of Greek God-worshippers and quite a few prominent women.
local: Juuda yab kansikin biba béè mimmee, ki dū bi-ba ki nákáǹ Pɔɔl nì Siilasi. Míǹ mun ní ki kpáaǹ Greeki yab bin nìn tɔ́ Unimbɔti marabee tikpil, nì binimpugaakaabee tikpil mun nìn dū bi-ba ki nákáǹ-bi.

eng: But the Jews became jealous and brought along some thugs who were hanging out in the marketplace. They formed a mob and started a riot in the city. They attacked Jasons house, intending to bring Paul and Silas before the people.
local: Ama Juuda yab ŋá ipoompoon ki cūtì kitiŋin ki nyàabìń anileen ŋin còom̄ adan puee ki kpáfì kunipaau, ki cáań fìkil fikil kitiŋ poon. Dinikitilee yíkìi cūtì Jasɔn dumpu kí nyàabìń Pɔɔl nì Siilasi kí cáań samaa nimbiin bí bàlifì-bi tibɔti.

eng: When they didnt find them, they dragged Jason and some believers before the city officials. They were shouting, These people who have been disturbing the peace throughout the empire have also come here.
local: Bi bàn̄ Jasɔn dumpu, kaa ká-bee, ní ki cúuń Jasɔn nì nnabiyaamu mubɔ ki cáań bikpilib nimbiin, ní ki tátí yii: «Bitiŋwiiliibi bin yìiǹ duulinya kɔkɔnee bāań ti-mun cee doo.

eng: What is more, Jason has welcomed them into his home. Every one of them does what is contrary to Caesars decrees by naming someone else as king: Jesus.
local: Jasɔn fōō-bi bi kóò u-dumpu. Binib gbanti kaa fàŋkí gɔminakpaan Seesaar marab, kun puee bi cáá yii ubɔti ubɔ gíti bí, bi yíì-u Yeesu.».

eng: This provoked the crowd and the city officials even more.
local: An bɔtee bíil̀ samaa kɔkɔ pɔbiliŋ ki kpáaǹ bikpilib.

eng: After Jason and the others posted bail, they released them.
local: ní bikpilibee gíí Jasɔn nì bin kíǹee dipɔŋkpaandi bí pà kí wàatī bí nín líí-bi.

eng: After this, Paul left Athens and went to Corinth.
local: Míǹ boonee, Pɔɔl dāa yíkì Ateeni ki búntì Kɔranti.

eng: Im with you and no one who attacks you will harm you, for I have many people in this city.
local: di sá yii m bí a-cee. Ubɔ kaa làá sútiń kí pìlīń-si ní kaa nín ŋá-si tikpitil. Kun puee, m cáá binib tikpil kitiŋ nee poon.».

eng: So he stayed there for eighteen months, teaching Gods word among them.
local: Pɔɔl nìn kāl̀ bi-kansikin dibindi diba nì dijandi, ki dàkà-bi Unimbɔti bɔti.

eng: Now when Gallio was the governor of the province of Achaia, the Jews united in their opposition against Paul and brought him before the court.
local: Bi kāaǹ Galiyɔn gɔmina Akaayi tiŋkinee, ní Juuda yab kpáfì ki yíkìi cúú Pɔɔl, ki cáa cūnnì Galiyɔn nimbiin.

eng: This man is persuading others to worship God unlawfully, they declared.
local: ní ki bí yii: «Unil nee yàntī bi nōotí Unimbɔti mbamin, min ní marab kaa lēe.».

eng: Just as Paul was about to speak, Gallio said to the Jews, If there had been some sort of injury or criminal behavior, I would have reason to accept your complaint.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun Pɔɔl bàà làá wàan̄ u-nyɔkɔ kí sòoǹee, ní gɔmina Galiyɔn bí Juuda yab yii: «Juuda yab, u yaa bàà ŋá tibɔkpitil tiba àá digaŋgam ubɔ, m bàà ŋūǹ kí pīlifiǹ-ni.

eng: However, since these are squabbles about a message, names, and your own Law, deal with them yourselves. I have no desire to sit in judgment over such things.
local: Ama an yaa sá yii ni yaa bíi kpákà nimbi ni-ba marab pu tibɔti tiba nì ayin aba puee, m yíì, maa làá sòoǹ an bɔti. Nimbi ni-ba ń kpèè-tì!».

eng: He expelled them from the court.
local: Ní u jìn̄-bi ki nyántì.

eng: but everyone seized Sosthenes, the synagogue leader, and gave him a beating in the presence of the governor. None of this mattered to Gallio.
local: Ní bi-kɔkɔ cúú Sɔsteen kukpafidii kpiliu, ki bíi gbáà gɔmina nimbiin. Ní gɔmina kaa kpáàa fātìi tɔ́.

eng: After Paul stayed in Corinth for some time, he said good-bye to the brothers and sisters. At the Corinthian seaport of Cenchreae he had his head shaved, since he had made a solemn promise. Then, accompanied by Priscilla and Aquila, he sailed away to Syria.
local: Pɔɔl gíti kāl̀ Kɔranti ki yúntì fiii. Boonee, u dāa yíkì nnabiyaamu cee ki kɔ́ meela ki búntì Siirii, u nì Priisiil nì Akiilasi. Kí wàatī ú nín yīkì Saŋkireeyee, Pɔɔl nìn kōol̀ diyibaakpandi kí dàkà yii u pōoǹ kí ŋá tiba kí tīī Unimbɔti.

eng: After they arrived in Ephesus, he left Priscilla and Aquila and entered the synagogue and interacted with the Jews.
local: Bi bàn̄ Eefeesee, ní bin nì Pɔɔl nìn nóóyee gbíntì niin. Pɔɔl nìn kɔ́ kukpafidiin ki tí bíi sòoǹ nì Juuda yab.

eng: There he found a Jew named Aquila, a native of Pontus. He had recently come from Italy with his wife Priscilla because Claudius had ordered all Jews to leave Rome. Paul visited with them.
local: Niin ní u ká uninja ubɔ, bi yíì-u Akiilasi, u sá Ujuudaja ní bi màl̄-u Pɔnti. U nìn nyánní Italii ki bāań kpalaa u nì u-nimpuu Priisiil, kun puee gɔminakpaan Kloodi nìn bí yii Juuda yab kɔkɔ ń nyā Room tiŋkin. Niinee bíǹ nì Pɔɔl dūu píí tɔb kubɔɔtiiu.

eng: They asked him to stay longer, but he declined.
local: Binib gbanti nìn bíi mèé-u yii ú gíti kàl̄ iwiŋkaaŋkaa bi-cee. Ní waa kíí.

eng: As he said farewell to them, though, he added, God willing, I will return. Then he sailed off from Ephesus.
local: U làá yīkì bi-kansikinee, ní ki bí-bi yii: «Unimbɔti yaa là, m dāa gítiń ni-cee dalba.».

eng: He arrived in Caesarea, went up to Jerusalem and greeted the church, and then went down to Antioch.
local: U yíkì Eefeesee, ki kɔ́ meela ki kpákatì Seesaaree, ní ki jɔ́m̀ Jeerusaleem ki jáam̀ Kriisitoo yab, ní ki fātìi sīntìń Antiyɔɔsi.

eng: After some time there he left and traveled from place to place in the region of Galatia and the district of Phrygia, strengthening all the disciples.
local: U kāl̀ ŋyunti ŋubɔee, ní ki yíkìi cūtì Galaati nì Friijii timmu kɔkɔn, ki tìì Kriisitoo yab kɔkɔ kunyannyaau.

eng: Meanwhile, a certain Jew named Apollos arrived in Ephesus. He was a native of Alexandria and was well-educated and effective in his use of the scriptures.
local: Ujuudaja ubɔ nìn bí, bi yíì-u Apooloosi. Bi nìn màl̄-u Aleekisandirii, ní u dɔ́miń Eefeesi. Uninja gbantee nìn sá un nyí ki sòoǹ tiŋanee, ki nyí Unimbɔti gbɔŋku mun tikpil.

eng: He had been instructed in the way of the Lord and spoke as one stirred up by the Spirit. He taught accurately the things about Jesus, even though he was aware only of the baptism John proclaimed and practiced.
local: Bi nìn dàkā-u ti-Dindaan bɔti, ní u mun cáa sòoǹ deedee ki dàkà Yeesu bɔti nì kunyannyaau. Nì míǹ kɔkɔee, Yoowaneesi nyiŋfàltam baba ní u nìn pée nyí.

eng: He began speaking with confidence in the synagogue. When Priscilla and Aquila heard him, they received him into their circle of friends and explained to him Gods way more accurately.
local: Ní u mɔ̄tìi bíi sòoǹ ki cántí-bi kukpafidiin. Priisiil nì Akiilasi gbìl̄ mimmee ní ki yōoǹ-u ki cáa cūnnì bi-cee, ki mɔ̄tìi dàkā-u Unimbɔti saŋŋu páaa.

eng: When he wanted to travel to Achaia, the brothers and sisters encouraged him and wrote to the disciples so they would open their homes to him. Once he arrived, he was of great help to those who had come to believe through grace.
local: Ní Apooloosi lēē yii u làá cù Akaayi. Niinee, nnabiyaamu tīī-u kunyannyaau ki ŋmàn̄ kugbɔŋu ki tīī Kriisitoo yab bin bí lapuee, yii bí fōō-u tiŋan. U nín bàn̄ niinee, nì Unimbɔti pɔŋŋu puee, u tútúǹ bin fōō Yeesu ki kííyee tikpil.

eng: He would vigorously defeat Jewish arguments in public debate, using the scriptures to prove that Jesus was the Christ.
local: Di sá yii, u nì Juuda yab nìn kpákà samaa nimbiin, ní u nìn sòoǹ ki líikí-bi, ki dàkà-bi Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon yii Yeesu di sá Meesiya.

eng: Because they practiced the same trade, he stayed and worked with them. They all worked with leather.
local: Kii bi-kɔkɔ nín nìn tūǹ ditumbaantiil puee, ní u kɔ́ bi-cee, ní bi kpáfì ki tūǹ ditundi dicilpu. Bi-kɔkɔ nìn sá bitantiluliibi ní.

eng: Every Sabbath he interacted with people in the synagogue, trying to convince both Jews and Greeks.
local: Ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa dal sá kamaayee, Pɔɔl nìn pɔ̄ɔntí ní ki sòoǹ kukpafidiin kí ŋmā Juuda yab nì Greeki yab.

eng: Once Silas and Timothy arrived from Macedonia, Paul devoted himself fully to the word, testifying to the Jews that Jesus was the Christ.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun Siilasi nì Tiimootee dāa nyánní Maseeduwaan ki bàn̄ee, ní Pɔɔl dū u-yuntiŋu kɔkɔ ki kíǹ ki cáa sòoǹ Unimbɔti bɔti, ki tùkù Juuda yab yii Yeesu di sá Meesiya.

eng: When they opposed and slandered him, he shook the dust from his clothes in protest and said to them, You are responsible for your own fates! Im innocent! From now on Ill go to the Gentiles.
local: Ama Juuda yab kaa nìn là min u sòoǹee, ní ki bíi sìí-u. Ní u gbáfì u-bɔkutiŋ, ki bí-bi yii: «Ni-busuu ń gítí ni-pu! Man nyáń ni-bɔtin. Kɔŋkɔnnee m làá nīn cá bin kaa sá Juuda yabee cee.».

eng: He left the synagogue and went next door to the home of Titius Justus, a Gentile God-worshipper.
local: U nìn yíkì niin, ki cūtìi kɔ́ uninja un nìn tɔ́ Unimbɔti marabee dumpu, bi yíì-u Titiyusi Justuusi. U-dumpu nìn nákáǹ kukpafidii.

eng: Crispus, the synagogue leader, and his entire household came to believe in the Lord. Many Corinthians believed and were baptized after listening to Paul.
local: Ní Kriisipusi kukpafidii kpiliu nì u-dicaŋku kɔkɔ fōō ti-Dindaan ki kíí. Kɔranti yab bin nìn gbìl̄ Pɔɔl nyɔɔbundee tikpil nìn fòol̄ ki kíikí ki yàntī bi fùul̀-bi Unimbɔti nyim.

eng: One night the Lord said to Paul in a vision, Dont be afraid. Continue speaking. Dont be silent.
local: Kunyeeu kubɔee ti-Dindaan nyántì u-ba ki dàkā Pɔɔl ní ki bí-u yii: «Á taa fàŋkì, ama nín̄ sòoǹ kí taa gbèen̄.

eng: While Apollos was in Corinth, Paul took a route through the interior and came to Ephesus, where he found some disciples.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun Apooloosi nìn bí Kɔrantee, ní Pɔɔl dīń kitiŋ kin bí ajoon puee ki bāań Eefeesi. Niin ní u ká Kriisitoo yab biba.

eng: This went on for two years, so that everyone living in the province of Asia—both Jews and Greeks—heard the Lords word.
local: U nìn dàkā-bi míǹ áli abin ali. An di yāntì Juuda yab nì Greeki yab bin nìn kóò Asii tiŋkinee kɔkɔ ŋúń ki gbìl̄ ti-Dindaan bɔti.

eng: God was doing unusual miracles through Paul.
local: Unimbɔti nìn dì Pɔɔl pu ki ŋáań maamaacikpaambi.

eng: Even the small towels and aprons that had touched his skin were taken to the sick, and their diseases were cured and the evil spirits left them.
local: Di sá yii Pɔɔl ŋaabikaaa, u-wampeeŋkaati tiba ní bi yaa nìn cáań ki gbēē bibunliibee, iween nìn càntī-bi, ní arasiniibi mun nìn nyēē bi-pu.

eng: There were some Jews who traveled around throwing out evil spirits. They tried to use the power of the name of the Lord Jesus against some people with evil spirits. They said, In the name of the Jesus whom Paul preaches, I command you.
local: Binyɔɔdambi biba nìn bí ki sá Juuda yab, ki yìiǹ ki cāantí bin cáá arasiniibee. Bi mun nìn mākaǹ kí yíiń ti-Dindaan Yeesu yindi kí jà arasiniibi. Bi nìn lī yii: «Nì Yeesu un pu Pɔɔl sòoǹee yindi pu ní m bí yii ní nyā.».

eng: The seven sons of Sceva, a Jewish chief priest, were doing this.
local: Sikeefa, Juuda yab saraaŋakpiliu ubɔ jipɔmbi biluli di nìn ŋáań mimmee.

eng: The evil spirit replied, I know Jesus and Im familiar with Paul, but who are you.
local: Ní arasiniiyee fātìi kíí-bi yii: «Man nyí Yeesu ki nyí Pɔɔl mun. Kin ni mun fáà, ni sá bilab?».

eng: The person who had an evil spirit jumped on them and overpowered them all with such force that they ran out of that house naked and wounded.
local: Niinee, uninja un pu arasinii kēe ŋàan̄ ki cìb̄-bi, ní ki pɔ́ɔkiǹ bi-kɔkɔ, ki yāntì bi sáǹ mpɔn ki nyáń dicindi gbantin nì dicancaŋgbeel nì tiweendi.

eng: This became known to the Jews and Greeks living in Ephesus. Everyone was seized with fear and they held the name of the Lord Jesus in the highest regard.
local: Juuda yab nì Greeki yab bin nìn kóò Eefeesee kɔkɔ nín gbìl̄ mimmee, ní tifaandi kɔ́-bi ní bi mɔ̀tīi tìì ti-Dindaan Yeesu yindi tinyool.

eng: Many of those who had come to believe came, confessing their past practices.
local: Bin nìn fōō ki kííyee tikpil nìn dòoń samaa nimbiin ki bìitī tin bi nìn ŋáaǹee pu.

eng: This included a number of people who practiced sorcery. They collected their sorcery texts and burned them publicly. The value of those materials was calculated at more than someone might make if they worked for one hundred sixty-five years.
local: Bi-kansikin tikpil bin nìn ŋáań tikpaŋkpantiilee nìn cáań bi-gbɔnti tin bi nìn cáa ŋáań tikpaŋkpantiilee ki wùn̄ ŋŋmi samaa kɔkɔ nimbiin. Bi dāa kàan̄ ki kpēēyee, an sá animbil jitɔmbi kutukub imuŋku ili nì saalaa ní bi nìn dūu dá tigbɔn gbanti.

eng: He asked them, Did you receive the Holy Spirit when you came to believe? They replied, Weve not even heard that there is a Holy Spirit.
local: ki bālifì-bi yii: «Ni nín fōō ki kííyee, ni fōō Circir Famaaa?» Ní bi kíí-u yii: «Timbi kaa kpáàa gbìl̄ bi sòon̄ Unimbɔti Fam pu gba ní kaa nín fōō-mì.».

eng: In this way the Lords word grew abundantly and strengthened powerfully.
local: Míǹ ní ti-Dindaan bɔti gbītì an kɔkɔ ki báaǹ inyaan mpɔn.

eng: Once these things had come to an end, Paul, guided by the Spirit, decided to return to Jerusalem, taking a route that would carry him through the provinces of Macedonia and Achaia. He said, After I have been there, I must visit Rome as well.
local: Tíǹ jítèe, ní Pɔɔl bíi màkà yii u làá dī Maseeduwaan nì Akaayi pú kí cù Jeerusaleem. U lī mimmee, ní ki bí yii, u yaa bàn̄ niinee, see ú cù Room mun.

eng: He sent two of his assistants, Timothy and Erastus, to Macedonia, while he remained awhile in the province of Asia.
local: Ní u dū binib bili bin nìn bí u-cee ki tútúǹ-uee, Tiimootee nì Eeraasiti, ki dūu tú Maseeduwaan. Úǹ u-ba nìn gbíntìi kāl̀ Asii tiŋkin fiii.

eng: At that time a great disturbance erupted about the Way.
local: Ŋyunti gbantee fìkil fikil ubɔ nìn kɔ́ní Eefeesi tiŋkin ti-Dindaan saŋŋu pu.

eng: There was a silversmith named Demetrius. He made silver models of Artemis temple, and his business generated a lot of profit for the craftspeople.
local: Di sá yii uninja ubɔ nìn bí, bi yíì-u Deemeetriyusi. Kijapaayee nìn cáá tikúti ki mɔ́ɔ̀ ŋwaadibiyaamu ki cáa nántíǹ diwaal Ariteemiisi diiku, ki nìn tìì u-tɔntɔnliibi bi mun ń nīn ŋáań ki kāntí ipaati tiŋan.

eng: He called a meeting with these craftspeople and others working in related trades and said, Friends, you know that we make an easy living from this business.
local: Ní u kpáfì u-tɔntɔnliibi nì bin kɔkɔ pée tūǹ an tundee, ní ki bí-bi yii: «Ti-yab, kéè, ni-kɔkɔ nyí yii ti-tundi nee di yì ti-jimaa!.

eng: And you can see and hear that this Paul has convinced and misled a lot of people, not only in Ephesus but also throughout most of the province of Asia. He says that gods made by human hands arent really gods.
local: Kéè, ni ká ki gbìl̄ Asii timmu kɔkɔn, an kaa sá Eefeesi ŋmaniŋma, Pɔɔl gbanti nín ŋúń ki dáaliń binib tikpil ki gíiǹní u-jandi pueeyaa! Pɔɔl ubaantee cáá yii inimbɔti in binib dū bi-ŋalii ki ŋée kaa sá inimbɔti.

eng: This poses a danger not only by discrediting our trade but also by completely dishonoring the great goddess Artemis. The whole province of Asia—indeed, the entire civilized world—worships her, but her splendor will soon be extinguished.
local: An sá mbusu ní, kun puee án làá yàntī baa làá nīn gíti tɔ́ ti-tundi nee. Áli gba, baa dāa gíti dù diwaakpaandi Ariteemiisi diiku kí yíiń tiba, ní diwaakpaandi gbanti din Eefeesi tiŋki nì duulinya timmu kɔkɔ nóóyee làá dāá ŋá fám!».

eng: Once they heard this, they were beside themselves with anger and began to shout, Great is Artemis of the Ephesians.
local: Bi gbìl̄ an bɔtee, ní ki fōō diŋuul, ní ki kíǹ ki bíi tátí yii: «Eefeesi yab waal Ariteemiisi di sá diwaakpaandi!».

eng: The city was thrown into turmoil. They rushed as one into the theater. They seized Gaius and Aristarchus, Pauls traveling companions from the province of Macedonia.
local: Niinee ní dipɔɔbiildi kɔ́ kitiŋ kɔkɔn. Ní bi cúú Maseeduwaan yab Gaayusi nì Ariistaarki bin nì Pɔɔl nìn nōotée, ki yōoǹ-bi ki cáa cūnnì laakin samaa nìn kpáfée.

eng: Then he said, What baptism did you receive, then? They answered, Johns baptism.
local: Ní Pɔɔl bālifì-bi yii: «Mimmee, dinyiŋfaldi dilal ní bi fùl̄-ni ní?» Ní bi kíí-u yii: «Yoowaneesi yal ní ti fùl̄.».

eng: Paul wanted to appear before the assembly, but the disciples wouldnt allow him.
local: Pɔɔl bàà nìn là kí cù niin samaa cee, ní Kriisitoo yab yíì.

eng: Even some officials of the province of Asia, who were Pauls friends, sent word to him, urging him not to risk going into the theater.
local: Áli u-bɔɔbi biba bin nìn sá Eefeesi kpilibee nìn túnní u-cee, bí gbàam̀-u ú taa dāan̄ samaayee kansikin.

eng: Meanwhile, the assembly was in a state of confusion. Some shouted one thing, others shouted something else, and most of the crowd didnt know why they had gathered.
local: Dikpafil gbanti poonee, biba nìn tátí ki sòoǹ tiba, ní biba mun ŋúb́ tiba. An ŋá fìkil fikil, ní binib tikpil kaa kpáàa nyí kun pu ní bi baasii kpáfèe.

eng: The Jews sent Alexander to the front, and some of the crowd directed their words toward him. He gestured that he wanted to offer a defense before the assembly.
local: Kunipaauee kansikinee biba nìn cūtìi ká Aleekisandir, ní Juuda yab téé-u ki nyántìń ú sòoǹ. Ní Aleekisandir yōoǹ ŋŋal ki téefì-bi bí ŋmīĺ ú bàà sòoǹ nì samaa.

eng: but when they realized he was a Jew, they all shouted in unison, Great is Artemis of the Ephesians! This continued for about two hours.
local: Ama bi nín dāa dūu béè yii u sá Ujuudajee, ní bi-kɔkɔ kpáfì ki tátí dicilpu áli tikúmɔn tili nyɔkɔm, ní ki cáá yii: «Eefeesi yab waal Ariteemiisi di sá diwaakpaandi!».

eng: The city manager brought order to the crowd and said, People of Ephesus, doesnt everyone know that the city of Ephesus is guardian of the temple of the great Artemis and of her image, which fell from heaven?.
local: Niinee ní ukpil ubɔ yāntì samaa ŋmíl̀, ní u bí yii: «Eefeesi yab, ŋma di kaa nyí yii Eefeesi tiŋki yab di kpèé diwaakpaandi Ariteemiisi din nyánní yilpu ki lítèe nì di-diiku?.

eng: Therefore, since these facts are undeniable, you must calm down. Dont be reckless.
local: Ubɔ kaa ŋūǹ kí kpákáǹ an bɔti. Míǹ puee, kàl̄maan soooo, taa jìkìmaan tibɔti nee pu.

eng: The men you brought here have neither robbed the temple nor slandered our goddess.
local: Binib bin ni cáań nee, baa ŋá digaŋgam ubɔ kuwaadiin, kaa ti sīī ti-waal mun.

eng: Therefore, if Demetrius and the craftspeople with him have a charge against anyone, the courts are in session and governors are available. They can press charges against each other there.
local: Míǹ puee, Deemeetriyusi nì bitɔntɔnliibi bin bí u-ceeyee, ubɔ yaa ŋá-bi tibee, iwiin in bi sòoǹ tibɔtee bí, bibɔsoonliibi mun bí! Bí yíiń tɔb ibeeyiin kí sòoǹ.

eng: Additional disputes can be resolved in a legal assembly.
local: An yaa tí sá bikpilib bɔtaaa, ní bí kpāfì kí kpèè, kii bi nín pée ŋáań puee.

eng: Paul explained, John baptized with a baptism by which people showed they were changing their hearts and lives. It was a baptism that told people about the one who was coming after him. This is the one in whom they were to believe. This one is Jesus.
local: Niinee ní Pɔɔl kíí-bi yii: «Yoowaneesi nyiŋfàltam sá ki tīī bin fá bi-kpitiiyee ní. U nìn tùkù samaa yii bí dāá fōō un làá dāá dāan̄ úǹ boonee kí kíí: an dàkà yii Yeesu.».

eng: As for us, we are in danger of being charged with rioting today, since we cant justify this unruly gathering.
local: Ama tin bāań díǹ neeyee làá yàntī bí lì yii ti là kí bīil̀ gɔmina. Di sá yii taa ŋūǹ kí lì kí dàkà kun pu ní ti kpáfì dooyee taapu.».

eng: After he said this, he dismissed the assembly.
local: U lī mimmee, ní ki bí yii báà ŋma ń nīn kùǹ.

eng: After they listened to Paul, they were baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
local: Bi gbìl̄ an bɔtee, ní ki yāntì bi fùl̄-bi nnyim ti-Dindaan Yeesu yindi pu.

eng: When Paul placed his hands on them, the Holy Spirit came on them, and they began speaking in other languages and prophesying.
local: Ní Pɔɔl dū u-ŋalii ki páaǹ bi-pu. Niinee, Circir Fam kpákatìń bi-pu, ní bi kíǹ ki bíi sòoǹ isooncaan ki nàatī Unimbɔti bɔti.

eng: Altogether, there were about twelve people.
local: Bininjab gbanti nìn bàn̄ binib saalaa nì bili nyɔkɔm.

eng: Paul went to the synagogue and spoke confidently for the next three months. He interacted with those present and offered convincing arguments concerning the nature of Gods kingdom.
local: Niinee ní Pɔɔl ŋá iŋmal ita ki kíǹ ki bí kukpafidiin ki mɔ̀tīi sòoǹ ki cántí-bi Unimbɔti Beel bɔti, án nín làá ŋá pu ní ú ŋmā-bee.

eng: Some people had closed their minds, though. They refused to believe and publicly slandered the Way. As a result, Paul left them, took the disciples with him, and continued his daily interactions in Tyrannus lecture hall.
local: Ama biba nìn pɔ́ɔkì bi-pɔbiliŋ, kaa là kí fōō kí kíí, ní ki bí ki sòoǹ ki bíiĺ ti-Dindaan bɔti samaa nimbiin. Niinee ní Pɔɔl yíkì bi-cee, ki kpáaǹ Kriisitoo yab ki kākatì ní ki kíǹ ki dàkà-bi Unimbɔti bɔti ŋwiin kamaa Tranusi sukuliun.

eng: When Pentecost Day arrived, they were all together in one place.
local: Pantikooti wiiŋŋu dalee, bi-kɔkɔ nìn kpáfì ki bí dibɔɔbaantiil.

eng: Phrygia and Pamphylia, Egypt and the regions of Libya bordering Cyrene; and visitors from Rome.
local: Friijii nì Paŋfilii nì Eejipiti nì Liibii jandi din pìlīǹ Siireenee yab, nì bin kɔkɔ nyánní Roomee.

eng: Cretans and Arabs—we hear them declaring the mighty works of God in our own languages.
local: Biba sá Juuda yab, ní biba mun sá binicambi bin fōō Juuda yab marabee, Kreeti yab nì Bilaalibub. Kin an ŋá mana ní ti gbìl̀ bi sòoǹ Unimbɔti tunnyaaŋŋi bɔti timbi kɔkɔ sooŋyin?».

eng: They were all surprised and bewildered. Some asked each other, What does this mean.
local: Ní bi bākatì, kaa ti nyí tin poon ní bi bée, ní ki kíǹ ki lì tɔb cee yii: «Ba bɔti nín sèé dee?».

eng: Others jeered at them, saying, Theyre full of new wine.
local: Ama biba nìn bí ki bèel̄-bi, yii: «Bi nyùn̄ ndaam ní ki gbí!».

eng: Peter stood with the other eleven apostles. He raised his voice and declared, Judeans and everyone living in Jerusalem! Know this! Listen carefully to my words!.
local: Niinee ní Peetroo yáatìi yúl̀, u nì akpambal saalaa nì dibee, ní ki yōoǹ dinyɔɔbundi ki téeń mpɔn ki bí-bi yii: «Nimbi Juuda yab, nì nimbi bin kɔkɔ kóò Jeerusaleemee, ŋátímaan ni-tafaliŋ kí pīlifì tibɔti tin m làá sòoǹ nee.

eng: These people arent drunk, as you suspect; after all, its only nine oclock in the morning!.
local: Binib nee kaa gbí ndaam, kii nimbi nín dàkafī puee, kun puee an dáá sá ntaafaawìiń ní.

eng: Rather, this is what was spoken through the prophet Joel.
local: Ama tin ni wāĺ nee, Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu Jooweel nín nìn lī puee dee yii.

eng: In the last days, God says, I will pour out my Spirit on all people. Your sons and daughters will prophesy. Your young will see visions. Your elders will dream dreams. .
local: <Iyuntijoolkaayee, m làá dāá yàntī m-Fam ń kpákatiń unil sá kamaa pu, ni-jipɔmbi nì ni-bisalib dāa nàatī m-nyɔɔbuŋŋi, ni-naacimpɔmbi dāa wālifí tin dòońee, ní ni-jaŋkpilib mun dāa dámintí tidamintiil.

eng: Even upon my servants, men and women, I will pour out my Spirit in those days, and they will prophesy. .
local: Ibaamɔn, iwiin gbantee, m dāa yàntī m-Fam ń kpákatiń m-tɔntɔnliibi bininjab nì binimpoobi pu, ní bí nàatī m-nyɔɔbuŋŋi.

eng: I will cause wonders to occur in the heavens above and signs on the earth below, blood and fire and a cloud of smoke. .
local: M dāa ŋáań maamaacib yilpu, ní kí dàkà tidaandi taapu. Ni làá dāá ká fatiku nì ŋŋmi nì ŋŋminyiim.

eng: Suddenly a sound from heaven like the howling of a fierce wind filled the entire house where they were sitting.
local: Dicilpu kpalaayee, kuwoou kubɔ nyánní yilpu kii kubuŋkpaaŋŋu kubɔee, ní ki dɔ́miń ki gbí kudii kun poon bi nìn kēe.

eng: The sun will be changed into darkness, and the moon will be changed into blood, before the great and spectacular day of the Lord comes. .
local: Ŋwìiń dāa kpántí dibɔmbɔndi ní uŋmal ń kpántí kii fatikuee kí wàatī ní ti-Dindaan wiŋkpaaŋŋuee nyooti ń nín bàń.

eng: And everyone who calls on the name of the Lord will be saved. .
local: Niinee, un sá kamaa yaa láa fōō ki kíí ki mēē ti-Dindaanee dāa kāntí difiil.>.

eng: Fellow Israelites, listen to these words! Jesus the Nazarene was a man whose credentials God proved to you through miracles, wonders, and signs, which God performed through him among you. You yourselves know this.
local: «Isirayeel yab, pīlifìmaan tibɔti nee. Unimbɔti yāntì Yeesu Naasaareeti yɔu ŋáaǹ maamaaci tuuŋŋi nì tidaandi ni-kansikin, ki dàkā-ni yii úǹ di lafun túnní-u ni-cee, kii nimbi ni-ba nín nyí puee, kun puee tikɔkɔ jítì ni-nimbiin ní.

eng: In accordance with Gods established plan and foreknowledge, he was betrayed. You, with the help of wicked men, had Jesus killed by nailing him to a cross.
local: Kii Unimbɔti nín nyí ki là, ki lēē ki yóoǹ puee, ní ni dū-u ki tīī bin kaa nyí Unimbɔtee kpáá-u ndɔpuntikaa pu ki kūū-u.

eng: God raised him up! God freed him from deaths dreadful grip, since it was impossible for death to hang on to him.
local: Ní Unimbɔti fīkitì-u, ki bútì-u ŋkúm kusakai. Kun puee ŋkúm kaa ŋūǹ kí nīn ŋúb́-u.

eng: David says about him, I foresaw that the Lord was always with me; because he is at my right hand I wont be shaken. .
local: A lì kii Daafiidi nín nìn sòon̄ min u-puee yii: <M wāĺ ti-Dindaan ŋyunti sá kamaa m-nimbiin, tiba kaa làá ŋá-mi kun puee u tíkí-mi m-ŋaŋgiil pú.

eng: Therefore, my heart was glad and my tongue rejoiced. Moreover, my body will live in hope, .
local: Míǹ puee m bí dipɔɔpindin ní ki sòoǹ an mɔ̄ǹ-mi. Nì min kɔkɔ m-wunti bí iwɔfinee, m máká yii m làá dāá fàtī kí ká dimaŋfal.

eng: because you wont abandon me to the grave, nor permit your holy one to experience decay. .
local: kun puee aa làá dāá fá-mi ditaŋkpiilin, kaa làá yàntī a-tɔntɔmmɔntiu un a lēēyee wunti ń sūū.

eng: You have shown me the paths of life; your presence will fill me with happiness. .
local: A yāntì m béè dimaŋfal sàmbiyaamu, a-nimbiin ní a làá dāá gbéeǹ-mi dipɔɔpindi.>.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I can speak confidently about the patriarch David. He died and was buried, and his tomb is with us to this very day.
local: «Nnabiyaamu, càntī ḿ mɔ̀tīí tùkùmaan-ni ti-naanja Daafiidi bɔti. U kpíì ní bi síb́-u. U-kaakul gbíntìi dáá bí ti-kansikin áli nìn díǹ.

eng: They saw what seemed to be individual flames of fire alighting on each one of them.
local: Niinee bi ká alimbil aba kii aŋmilimbilee bāań-bi, ki cúu yākatì tɔbin ní ki tɔ́kɔǹ báà ŋma pu.

eng: Because he was a prophet, he knew that God promised him with a solemn pledge to seat one of his descendants on his throne.
local: Daafiidi nìn sá Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu ní. U nìn nyí Unimbɔti nín pūtì ki pōoǹ-u yii u làá dū úǹ Daafiidi naantiiu ubɔ kí kàan̄ u-beejal puee.

eng: Having seen this beforehand, David spoke about the resurrection of Christ, that he wasnt abandoned to the grave, nor did his body experience decay .
local: An nìn sá Meesiya fìkititam pu ní u nìn sòon̄ ki yóoǹ yii: <Waa làá dāá gbíntí ditaŋkpiilin, u-wunti mun kaa làá dāá sūū.>.

eng: This Jesus God raised up. We are all witnesses to that fact.
local: «Yeesu gbanti ní Unimbɔti fīkitì, timbi kɔkɔ ká ki sá seeradambi.

eng: He was exalted to Gods right side and received from the Father the promised Holy Spirit. He poured out this Spirit, and you are seeing and hearing the results of his having done so.
local: Ti-Baa Unimbɔti kāaǹ-u yilpu u-ŋaŋgiil pú, ní ki tīī-u u-Fam, kii u nín nìn pée pōoǹ puee. Tin tin ni wāĺ ki gbìl̄ kɔŋkɔnneeyee, Circir Fam min Yeesu tīiń-tee tuuŋŋi dee.

eng: David didnt ascend into heaven. Yet he says, The Lord said to my Lord, Sit at my right side, .
local: Di sá Daafiidi u-ba kaa nìn jɔ́m̀ yilpu, ama ki mun lī ki síiǹ yii: <Ti-Dindaan tùkū m-Dindaan yii: Kàl̄ m-ŋaŋgiil pú.

eng: until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet. .
local: kí nīn cí ŋyunti ŋun m làá dāá yàntī á nīn kíǹ ki tɔ́kɔ́ a-nannanliibi puee.>.

eng: Therefore, let all Israel know beyond question that God has made this Jesus, whom you crucified, both Lord and Christ.
local: «Isirayeel maaliŋun yab kɔkɔ, béémaan nì ibaamɔn poon yii, Yeesu un ni kpáá ndɔpuntikaa puee, Unimbɔti dū-u ki ŋá ti-Dindaan ki lēē yii ú nīn sá Meesiya.».

eng: When the crowd heard this, they were deeply troubled. They said to Peter and the other apostles, Brothers, what should we do.
local: Bi nín gbìl̄ tibɔti gbantee, ní bi-pɔbiliŋ máĺ tiŋan, ní bi fātìi bālifì Peetroo nì akpambal ŋin kíǹee yii: «Ti-nabiyaamu, mimmee, ti làá ŋá mana?».

eng: Peter replied, Change your hearts and lives. Each of you must be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of your sins. Then you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit.
local: Ní Peetroo kíí-bi yii: «Kpántímaan ni-bimbim, báà ŋma ń yàntī bí fàl̀-u Unimbɔti nyim Yeesu Kriisitoo yindi pu, ní Unimbɔti ń dū ni-kpitii kí pɔ̄-ni. Ní ní fōō Circir Fam min Unimbɔti nìn pōoǹee.

eng: This promise is for you, your children, and for all who are far away—as many as the Lord our God invites.
local: Kun puee u-poondi gbanti sá ki tīī nimbi, nì ni-biyaamu, ki kpáaǹ bin bí ndanee, nì dinikitikpaandi din kɔkɔwoooo ti-Dindaan ti-Nimbɔtiu làá dāá yíińee.».

eng: They were all filled with the Holy Spirit and began to speak in other languages as the Spirit enabled them to speak.
local: Ní Circir Fam sīntìń ki kɔ́ bi-kɔkɔn mmɔntiim, ní bi kíǹ ki sòoǹ isooncaan in Ŋfam tīī-bi bí sòoǹee.

eng: With many other words he testified to them and encouraged them, saying, Be saved from this perverse generation.
local: Peetroo gíti yōoǹ anyɔɔbun tikpil ki sòoǹ ki sáŋkí-bi yii: «Nyāmaan waanee nib bin nóó isaŋkpitee kansikin, kí kámaan difiil!».

eng: Those who accepted Peters message were baptized. God brought about three thousand people into the community on that day.
local: Bin fōō Peetroo bɔti ki kííyee yāntì bi fùl̄-bi Unimbɔti nyim. Yaadal wiiŋŋuee, binib bin kútì Yeesu boonnooliibi puee wíiŋŋu nìn bàn̄ kutukub bita nyɔkɔm.

eng: The believers devoted themselves to the apostles teaching, to the community, to their shared meals, and to their prayers.
local: Bi nìn pɔ̄ɔntí bi-ba ki bífí tin akpambal dàkà-bee, ki kpáfí kinyɔɔbaantii nì kunabitiiun, ki jīǹ ti-Dindaan jikaati, ki kpáfí dicilpu ki mèé Unimbɔti.

eng: A sense of awe came over everyone. God performed many wonders and signs through the apostles.
local: Tifaandi nìn kɔ́ báà ŋma maamaacib nì tidaandi tin akpambal nìn ŋáańee pu.

eng: All the believers were united and shared everything.
local: Bin nìn fōō ki kííyee nìn kpáfí dibɔbibaantiil, ní báà ŋma wanti nìn sá ki tīī bi-kɔkɔ.

eng: They would sell pieces of property and possessions and distribute the proceeds to everyone who needed them.
local: Bi nìn nyáfí bi-sati nì bi-wammɔntiiti, ní ki yàkatī ki tìì báà ŋma nín máaǹ puee.

eng: Every day, they met together in the temple and ate in their homes. They shared food with gladness and simplicity.
local: Kutaa kamaayee, bi nìn dòoń múlmul kunimbɔtidiin dicilpu ki nyɔ́ŋkí Unimbɔti, ki kpáfí akiti akiti adiinni ki jīǹ ti-Dindaan jikaati, ki jīǹ tijikaal nì dipɔɔbaantiil nì dipɔɔpindi poon.

eng: They praised God and demonstrated Gods goodness to everyone. The Lord added daily to the community those who were being saved.
