local: ki nyɔ́ŋkí Unimbɔti. Samaa kɔkɔ nìn néeń bi-bɔti. Ní kutaa sá kamaayee, ti-Dindaan gbēentí bin kāntí difiilee wíiŋŋu.

eng: There were pious Jews from every nation under heaven living in Jerusalem.
local: Iwiin gbantee, Juuda yab bin nóó Unimbɔti deedeeyee nìn dɔ́miń Jeerusaleem, ki nyánní atimbun ŋin kɔkɔ bí duulinyanee.

eng: When they heard this sound, a crowd gathered. They were mystified because everyone heard them speaking in their native languages.
local: Kuwoou bíi gèe, ní samaa sáǹní ki bāańee, ní ki gíti bākatì, kun puee bi-kɔkɔ nìn gbìl̀ bi-sooŋyin binibee nín sòoǹ puee.

eng: They were surprised and amazed, saying, Look, arent all the people who are speaking Galileans, every one of them?.
local: Ní bi bākatì sèeee, an ŋá-bi bitii, ní bi kíǹ ki bíi sòoǹ nì tɔb yii: «Bin kɔkɔ sòoǹ nee kaa sá Galiilee yabaaa?.

eng: How then can each of us hear them speaking in our native language?.
local: Kin, an ŋá mana ní ti-kɔkɔ nee gbìl̀ nì ti-timmu sooŋyi poon tin bi sòoǹ nee?.

eng: Parthians, Medes, and Elamites; as well as residents of Mesopotamia, Judea, and Cappadocia, Pontus and Asia.
local: Ti-kansikin biba sá Paati, biba sá Meedi nì Eelam, biba mun sá Meesoopootamii nì Juudee nì Kapadoosi nì Pɔnti nì Asii nì.

eng: When the riot was over, Paul sent for the disciples, encouraged them, said good-bye, and left for the province of Macedonia.
local: Fìkil fikilee jítèe, ní Pɔɔl yíiń Kriisitoo yab, ki tīī-bi kunyannyaau, ní ki kāaǹ-bi, ní ki yíkì bi-kansikin ki búntì Maseeduwaan.

eng: Paul went down, fell on him and embraced him, then said, Dont be alarmed. Hes alive.
local: Niinee ní Pɔɔl kpákatìń ki bōoǹ ki yōoǹ-u ní ki bí yii: «Ni-pɔbiliŋ ń taa máĺ, kun puee u fātìi jɔ́mìń!».

eng: Then Paul went back upstairs and ate. He talked for a long time—right up until daybreak—then he left.
local: Ní u fātìi nín jɔ́ḿ ki cūtìi yākatì kpɔnɔb bi ŋmɔ́, ní u fātìi tí kíĺ ki bíi sòoǹ nì bi áli kutaa nì ku-wuntil, ní boonee u dāa nín yíkì.

eng: They took the young man away alive, and they were greatly comforted.
local: Ní bi fātìi gíiǹ unaacimpɔɔnee u-dumpu nì alaafiya, ní an nìn mɔ́ɔkiǹ báà ŋma tikpil.

eng: We went on to the ship and sailed for Assos, where we intended to take Paul on board. Paul had arranged this, since he intended to make his way there by land.
local: Ní timbi kɔ́ meela ki līntì kí cùú kíntí Pɔɔl Asoosi tiŋkin, kii u nín nìn pée sòon̄ puee, kun puee úǹ làá dāá còomìń ataa pu ní.

eng: When he met us at Assos, we took him aboard and went on to Mitylene.
local: U bāań ki ká-ti Asɔsee, ní ti yōoǹ-u ki nín cáa cá Miitileen.

eng: The next day we sailed from there and arrived opposite Chios. On the day after, we sailed to Samos, and on the following day we came to Miletus.
local: Niinee ní ti nín nìkì. Áli kutaa nì ku-wuntilee ní ti dūu bàn̄ Kiyoo. Ku tí wúntèe ní ti dūu bàn̄ Samɔsi, ní ku gíti tí wúntèe ní ti yúl̀ Troogiliyɔn ní ki nín bàn̄ Miilee.

eng: Paul had decided to sail past Ephesus so that he wouldnt need to spend too much time in the province of Asia. He was hurrying to reach Jerusalem, if possible, by Pentecost Day.
local: Pɔɔl nìn lēē yii u là kí gɔ́ntí Eefeesi kí taa bīil̀ ŋyunti kitiŋ gbantin. Míǹ pu ní u nìn túĺ dicancaal, án ŋá-u malaa u yaan ŋūǹ, ú bàà nín̄ bí Jeerusaleem Pantikooti wiiŋŋu dal.

eng: From Miletus he sent a message to Ephesus calling for the churchs elders to meet him.
local: Pɔɔl bí Miileeyee, ní ki tú bí cù Eefeesi kí yíiń niin Kriisitoo nikitil kpilib.

eng: When they arrived, he said to them, You know how I lived among you the whole time I was with you, beginning with the first day I arrived in the province of Asia.
local: Bi bāań u-ceeyee, ní u bí-bi yii: «Kéè, nimbi ni-ba nyí doooo ŋwimpeekaa ŋun m kóoń Asii tiŋkinee m nín bí nì ni baabadal miŋyeeyaa!.

eng: I served the Lord with great humility and with tears in the midst of trials that came upon me because of the Jews schemes.
local: Míǹ ní m nìn sīntiǹ m-ba ki túń ti-Dindaan tundi nì nnyinnyim, ki bí falaabi kansikin kun puee Juuda yab nìn nyàab̀ isan kí kpɔ̀-mi.

eng: He traveled through that region with a message of encouragement. When he came to Greece.
local: U dī Maseeduwaan tiŋgbammun, ki sòon̄ nì Kriisitoo yab ki sáŋkí-bi tikpil ki wāatì ní ki nín bàn̄ Greeki tiŋkin.

eng: You know I held back nothing that would be helpful so that I could proclaim to you and teach you both publicly and privately in your homes.
local: Míǹ ní ni nyí yii maa nìn bál̀ tin ŋūǹ kí tūtuǹ-nee báà tiba, ki sòoǹ ki dàkà-ni Unimbɔti bɔti laakin binib kpáfée ki kpáaǹ ni-dumpui.

eng: You know I have testified to both Jews and Greeks that they must change their hearts and lives as they turn to God and have faith in our Lord Jesus.
local: M nìn sáŋkí Juuda yab ki kpáaǹ Greeki yab tiŋan, yii bí fá ikpiti, kí fōō ti-Dindaan Yeesu kí kíí kí nóó Unimbɔti.

eng: Now, compelled by the Spirit, Im going to Jerusalem. I dont know what will happen to me there.
local: «Kɔŋkɔnnee, m làá nīn cá Jeerusaleem dee kii Unimbɔti Fam nín bí ḿ ŋá puee, kaa nyí tin làá ŋá-mi niinee.

eng: What I do know is that the Holy Spirit testifies to me from city to city that prisons and troubles await me.
local: Ama ntimu mun kɔkɔ poon m jītée, Circir Fam tùkù-mi yii sarka nì dinimbinaamandi dɔ̄ ki cí-mi.

eng: But nothing, not even my life, is more important than my completing my mission. This is nothing other than the ministry I received from the Lord Jesus: to testify about the good news of Gods grace.
local: Ama, man maŋfal bɔti kaa páb́-mi, a lì kii an kaa sá-mi tiwammɔntiil tibee ní. Tin di páb́-mee di sá ḿ tɔ̄ ditundi din m fōō ti-Dindaan ceeyee: díǹ di sá ḿ sòoǹ Tibɔmɔntiil kí tùkù binib dineendi din Unimbɔti cáá ti-puee.

eng: I know that none of you will see me again—you among whom I traveled and proclaimed the kingdom.
local: «Míǹ puee, nimbi bin cee m jítì ki sòon̄ Unimbɔti Beel pu bɔtee, kɔŋkɔnnee m nyí yii naa làá ti ká-mi.

eng: Therefore, today I testify to you that Im not responsible for anyones fate.
local: An di yì-tì ní m tùkù-ni an bɔti díǹ kí síiń, boonee, ubɔ yaa láa gíti wáŋkèe, an kaa dāa gíti sá m-cee.

eng: I havent avoided proclaiming the entire plan of God to you.
local: Di sá yii m tùkū-ni kaa bál̀-ni Unimbɔti nín là kí dāá ŋá min miŋyee kɔkɔ.

eng: Watch yourselves and the whole flock, in which the Holy Spirit has placed you as supervisors, to shepherd Gods church, which he obtained with the death of his own Son.
local: Míǹ puee, ní kpèèmaan ni-ba tiŋan, kí kpèèmaan dinikitil din pu Circir Fam lēē-ni yii ní nīn sá bikpilibee, kí kpòotī Unimbɔti nikitil din u dū u-ba fatikuu ki dée tiŋan.

eng: I know that, after my departure, savage wolves will come in among you and wont spare the flock.
local: M nyí yii, m-kpátam boonee, binib làá dāá kóoń ni-kansikin. Baa làá dāá tàan̄ dinikitil gbanti, a lì kii agbiŋgbil nín cáá ntoom, kaa tàantí dipikitil puee.

eng: he stayed for three months. Because the Jews hatched a plot against Paul as he was about to sail for Syria, he decided instead to return through Macedonia.
local: U nìn ŋá niin iŋmal ita ní. Ŋyunti ŋun u bɔ́ntì ki làá kɔ́ meela kí nīn cá Siiriiyee, ní u béè yii Juuda yab sòon̄ ki bɔ́ntì nsàmbiyaamu mun pu bi làá dī kí kpɔ̀-uee. Niinee ní u fātìi dūu dīń Maseeduwaan pú.

eng: Some of your own people will distort the word in order to lure followers after them.
local: Míǹ ní ni-kansikin bininjab biba mun làá dāá yòon̄ tinyimɔmbɔti kí sòoǹ kí ŋmàtiǹ Kriisitoo yab biba ń nīn nóó-bi.

eng: Stay alert! Remember that for three years I constantly and tearfully warned each one of you. I never stopped warning you!.
local: Míǹ puee, ní nīn nyímaan nì ni-ba kí nīn téetí m nín nìn sáŋkí-ni pu nì nnyinnyim ŋwiimpu nì kunyeeu áli abin atee.

eng: Now I entrust you to God and the message of his grace, which is able to build you up and give you an inheritance among all whom God has made holy.
local: «Kɔŋkɔnnee, m dū-ni ki ŋá Unimbɔti ŋaan, ní nīn ŋúb́ u-bɔmɔntiitee. Tíǹ di cáá mpɔn kí yàntī ní béé Unimbɔti bɔti tikpil kí dāá fōō ipaati in u síiǹ kí tīī bin kɔkɔ sá u-yabee.

eng: I havent craved anyones silver, gold, or clothing.
local: Ni nyí yii man kaa nìn là ubɔ nimbiliŋ, kaa nìn là ubɔ mun bɔkutil.

eng: You yourselves know that I have provided for my own needs and for those of my companions with my own hands.
local: Nimbi ni-ba nìn nyí yii nì m-ŋalii in bí nee ní m nìn cáa tūǹ ditundi ní man nì bin nìn nóóyee jīǹ.

eng: In everything I have shown you that, by working hard, we must help the weak. In this way we remember the Lord Jesus words: It is more blessed to give than to receive.
local: M dàkā-ni ni-kɔkɔ ń béé yii ni máaǹ kí nīn tūǹ ditundi tikpil kí ŋmā kí tūtuǹ biwɔfib, kí nīn téetí ti-Dindaan Yeesu u-ba nín nìn lī dinyɔɔbundi diŋyee, yii: <Á pɔ̄ tiwanee mɔ̄ ki jítiǹ bí pɔ̄-si.>».

eng: After he said these things, he knelt down with all of them to pray.
local: Pɔɔl lī míǹ ki dóòyee, ní ki gbāaǹ, ní u nì bi kɔkɔ bíi mèé.

eng: They cried uncontrollably as everyone embraced and kissed Paul.
local: Niinee ní bi-kɔkɔ bíi sūǹ, ní ki dū bi-ŋalii ki kpàl̄ Pɔɔl.

eng: They were especially grieved by his statement that they would never see him again. Then they accompanied him to the ship.
local: Tin nìn kɔ́ǹ-bi kunimbaauee di sá u nín lī yii dalba dalba baa làá ti ká-uee. Niinee ní bi céeǹ-u áli meela taapu.

eng: He was accompanied by Sopater, Pyrrhus son from Beroea, Aristarchus and Secundus from Thessalonica, Gaius from Derbe, Timothy, and Tychicus and Trophimus from the province of Asia.
local: Bin nìn nóó-uee di sá: Sɔpateer, Piirusi jipɔɔn un nìn sá Beereeyee, nì Ariistaarki nì Seekundusi, bíǹ sá Teesalooniiki yab, Gaayusi un sá Deebee, nì Tiimootee nì Tiisiiki nì Troofiim, bíǹ nyánní Asii.

eng: They went on ahead and waited for us in Troas.
local: Bíǹ kɔkɔ nìn līntì ki cūtìi cíitì-ti Troowaasi.

eng: We sailed from Philippi after the Festival of Unleavened Bread and met them five days later in Troas, where we stayed for a week.
local: Iwiin in poon Juuda yab ŋmóò kpɔnɔb bin baa ŋá ŋkpɔnɔnyɔkɔee boonee, timbi kɔ́nní meela ki yíkìń Fiiliipi tiŋkin, ki ŋá iwiin iŋmoo ní ki nín bàn̄ bi-cee Troowaasi, ní ki ŋá iwiin iluli niin.

eng: On the first day of the week, as we gathered together for a meal, Paul was holding a discussion with them. Since he was leaving the next day, he continued talking until midnight.
local: Kinyɔŋ wimpeepeekaaŋu dalee, ti nìn kpáfì kí jī ti-Dindaan jikaati, ní Pɔɔl kíĺ ki bíi sòoǹ nì bin kpáfì niinee. U nìn sòon̄ ki yúntì tikpil, áli kunyataasiiu tikúti saalaa nì tili, kun puee an nìn sá kutaa ń wúntí ú yīkì ní.

eng: There were many lamps in the upstairs room where we had gathered.
local: Fitilab nìn wīikí diditɔntɔkɔl din poon ti nìn kpáfèe ni tikpil.

eng: A young man named Eutychus was sitting in the window. He was sinking into a deep sleep as Paul talked on and on. When he was sound asleep, he fell from the third floor and died.
local: Unaacimpɔɔn ubɔ nìn bí niin, bi yíì-u Eetiiki. U nìn kā diditɔntɔkɔl tɔkɔlau nyɔɔgbandi pu. Ŋyunti ŋun Pɔɔl gbíntìi bíi sòoǹ nì bi ki cée, ní ŋgeem cúú-u. U bíi gèeǹ tikpil tikpilee, ní ki dāa kpáàa nyánní diditɔntɔkɔl yilpu kuditataatiiu poon ki lítì kitiŋ. Bi pée yōoǹ-uee, u kpíì ní.

eng: After we tore ourselves away from them, we set sail on a straight course to Cos, reaching Rhodes the next day, and then Patara.
local: Timbi nì bi dāa cúu yākatì tɔbinee, ní timbi kɔ́ meela ki túĺ dicancaal ki búntì Kɔɔsi, ní ku wúntèe ti dūu bàn̄ Roodi. Niinee ní ti cāatì Patara.

eng: After staying there for several days, a prophet named Agabus came down from Judea.
local: Ti nìn kāl̀ u-cee ki yúntì fiii, ní Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu ubɔ, bi yíì-u Agabusi, nyánní Juudee ki dɔ́miń ti-cee.

eng: He came to us, took Pauls belt, tied his own feet and hands, and said, This is what the Holy Spirit says: In Jerusalem the Jews will bind the man who owns this belt, and they will hand him over to the Gentiles.
local: Kijapaayee yōoǹ Pɔɔl dambalau ní ki dūu bóob́ u-taaŋi nì u-ŋalii, ní ki bí yii: «Pīlifìmaan Circir Fam nín lī puee! Uninja un di yaa yì dambala neeyee, Juuda yab làá bóob́ udaan míǹ ní Jeerusaleem, ní kí dū-u kí tīī bin kaa sá Juuda yabee.».

eng: When we heard this, we and the local believers urged Paul not to go up to Jerusalem.
local: Ti gbìl̄ mimmee, ní timbi nì laakin ti kóòyee yab bíi gbàam̄ Pɔɔl yii ú taa cù Jeerusaleem.

eng: Paul replied, Why are you doing this? Why are you weeping and breaking my heart? Im ready not only to be arrested but even to die in Jerusalem for the sake of the name of the Lord Jesus.
local: Ní Pɔɔl bí-ti yii: «Ba ŋá ní ni sūǹ ki bíiĺ m-pɔbil míǹ? Báà bi yaa làá bóob́-mi kí kpɔ̀-mi gba Jeerusaleem niin ti-Dindaan Yeesu puɔɔɔ, maa mɔ́tí!».

eng: Since we couldnt talk him out of it, the only thing we could say was, The Lords will be done.
local: Ti lī-u tikɔkɔ kaa ŋúń-uee, ní ti cāntì-u, ní ki bí-u yii: «Too, ti-Dindaan yaa là miŋyee, án ŋá míǹ!».

eng: After this, we got ready and made our way up to Jerusalem.
local: Iwiin gbanti boonee, ní ti bɔ́ntì ki nín jɔ́ḿ Jeerusaleem.

eng: Some of the disciples from Caesarea accompanied us and led us to Mnasons home, where we were guests. He was from Cyprus and had been a disciple a long time.
local: Kriisitoo yab bin bí Seesaareeyee biba céeǹní-ti ki cáa cūnnì kijapaai kin bi yíì Minaasɔnee dumpu. U nìn sá Siipiri, ki nìn fōō ki kíí dooooyee. Úǹ cee ní bi nìn kɔ́ǹ-ti.

eng: When we arrived in Jerusalem, the brothers and sisters welcomed us warmly.
local: Ti bàn̄ Jeerusaleemee, ní nnabiyaamu fōō-ti nì dipɔɔpindi.

eng: On the next day Paul and the rest of us went to see James. All of the elders were present.
local: Kutaa wúntèe ní ti nì Pɔɔl búntì Yakubu cee. Niin ní bikpilib kɔkɔ nìn kpáfì.

eng: After greeting them, he gave them a detailed report of what God had done among the Gentiles through his ministry.
local: U jáam̀-bi ki dóòyee, ní ki bíi sòoǹ ki bɔ́bití-bi Unimbɔti nín dī u-pu ki túń ditundi din bin kaa sá Juuda yabee kansikinee.

eng: We found a ship crossing over to Phoenicia, boarded, and put out to sea.
local: Ní ki ká meela un cá Feenisiiyee, ní ti kɔ́ úǹ ki búntì.

eng: Those who heard this praised God. Then they said to him, Brother, you see how many thousands of Jews have become believers, and all of them keep the Law passionately.
local: Bi gbìl̄ mimmee, ní ki nyɔ́ŋkì Unimbɔti. Ní bi bí-u yii: «Ti-nabiki, a ká Juuda yab kansikin binib kutukub tikpil nín kpántì Kriisitoo yab, bi-kɔkɔ ŋúb́ Mooyiisi marab tiŋan pueeyaa!.

eng: They have been informed that you teach all the Jews who live among the Gentiles to reject Moses, telling them not to circumcise their children nor to live according to our customs.
local: Di sá bi tùkū-bi yii a dàkà Juuda yab bin kóò binicambi kansikinee yii bí fá Mooyiisi marab kí taa ti nín̄ gífí bi-biyaamu akpancan, kí fá ti-naanjab koobiliŋ mun.

eng: What about this? Without a doubt, they will hear that you have arrived.
local: Báà an ŋá pu bi-kɔkɔ làá béé yii a dɔ́miń. Mimmee, ti làá ŋá mana kɔŋkɔnnee?.

eng: You must therefore do what we tell you. Four men among us have made a solemn promise.
local: Míǹ puee, ti làá tùkù-si a nín làá ŋá puee. Bininjab binaa biba bí ti-kansikin doo ki pōoǹ ipoon.

eng: Take them with you, go through the purification ritual with them, and pay the cost of having their heads shaved. Everyone will know there is nothing to those reports about you but that you too live a life in keeping with the Law.
local: Yòon̄-bi kí cù si nì bi kɔkɔ ń ŋá saraabi kí kòotī-bi, kí pà animbil bí kòol̀-bi. Niinee ní báà ŋma làá béé yii tin ní bi sòoǹ a-puee kaa sá ibaamɔn, ní kí ká yii sisi aba bí kii bin dī marabee nín bí puee.

eng: As for the Gentile believers, we wrote a letter about what we decided, that they avoid food offered to idols, blood, the meat from strangled animals, and sexual immorality.
local: An yaa kíǹ bin kaa sá Juuda yab ki fōō Yeesu ki kííyee, ti nìn ŋmàn̄ kugbɔŋu ki tùkū-bi yii timbi kpēē yii an ŋān̄ bí taa ti ŋmɔ́ tiwaatootinan, kí taa ŋmɔ́ fatiku nì tiwan tin baa lāntì fatiku nyáńee, kí taa ŋá tilaakpaal mun.».

eng: The following day Paul took the men with him and went through the purification ritual with them. He entered the temple and publicly announced the completion of the days of purification, when the offering would be presented for each one of them.
local: Kutaa wúntèe ní Pɔɔl yōoǹ bininjabee, ní u nì bi cūtì ki ŋá saraabi, ní ki kɔ́ kunimbɔtidiin. Niin ní Pɔɔl yóoǹ ŋwiin ŋun bi làá kòotēe nì báà ŋma nín làá dāá cáań saraabi bin biŋyee.

eng: When the seven days of purification were almost over, the Jews from the province of Asia saw Paul in the temple. Grabbing him, they threw the whole crowd into confusion by shouting.
local: Ŋwinluluútee cá dikúntilee, ní Juuda yab biba bin nyánní Asiiyee ká Pɔɔl kunimbɔtidiin. Niinee ní bi fíiǹ báà ŋma landɔkɔŋu ŋá mbaanti, ní bi cúú Pɔɔl.

eng: Fellow Israelites! Help! This is the man who teaches everyone everywhere against our people, the Law, and this place. Not only that, he has even brought Greeks into the temple and defiled this holy place. .
local: Bi cúú-uee, ní ki kíǹ ki bíi tátí yii: «Isirayeel yab, jòoḿmaan-ti! Kpèèmaan, uninjee dee un cá an kɔkɔ ki sòoǹ báà ŋma cee ki bíiĺ Isirayeel yab yindi nì Mooyiisi marab nì kunimbɔtidiiyee. Áli gba, u yāntì bin kaa sá Juuda yabee kɔ́ kunimbɔtidiin, ki bíil̀ circir kɔŋkooŋŋu gbanti!».

eng: They said this because they had seen Trophimus the Ephesian in the city with him earlier, and they assumed Paul had brought him into the temple.
local: Kun yì-tèe di sá, bi nìn ká u nì Troofiim un sá Eefeesee kitiŋin, ní bi dākafì yii Pɔɔl nìn cáá-u ki kɔ́ǹ kunimbɔtidiin.

eng: We spotted Cyprus, but passed by it on our left. We sailed on to the province of Syria and landed in Tyre, where the ship was to unload its cargo.
local: Ti tin gbīitì Siipiri tiŋki kin nnyim māntì ki túee ní ki dū-kì ki fá ŋŋaŋgan pú, ní ki dūu dī Siirii watil pú, ní ki tin bàn̄ Tiir, laakin meela làá tàkatī u-wantee.

eng: The entire city was stirred up. The people came rushing, seized Paul, and dragged him out of the temple. Immediately the gates were closed.
local: Niin ní kitimmɔŋee fōō ki píĺ dàii, ní binib nyánní ajan kɔkɔ pu ki dɔ́miń ki cúú Pɔɔl ki dáaĺ-u ki nyántì kunimbɔtidiin, ní ki dū mpunyɔmu ki pīiǹ dicilpu niin.

eng: While they were trying to kill him, a report reached the commander of a company of soldiers that all Jerusalem was in a state of confusion.
local: Bi nín nìn pée nyàab̀ kí kpɔ̀-uee puee, an pūtì kpáàa pée bàn̄ Room yab soojakpikpaaŋwu, yii fìkil fikil ubɔ kóoń Jeerusaleem tiŋki kɔkɔn.

eng: Without a moments hesitation, he took some soldiers and officers and ran down to the mob. When the mob saw the commander and his soldiers, they stopped beating Paul.
local: Dicilpu niinee, kijapaayee yōoǹ soojab ki kpáaǹ bi-kpilib biba ki sáń ki nín cá samaayee cee. Bi dāa ká usoojakpilee nì u-yab páaǹnée, ní bi gbēeǹ, kaa ti bíi gbáà Pɔɔl.

eng: When the commander arrived, he arrested Paul and ordered him to be bound with two chains. Only then did he begin to ask who Paul was and what he had done.
local: Niinee ní Room yab soojakpikpaaŋwu dāatìi cúú Pɔɔl, ní ki bí yii bí dū ikusaka ili kí bóob́-u, ní u bālifì-bi un Pɔɔl sée nì tin u ŋée.

eng: Some in the crowd shouted one thing, others shouted something else. Because of the commotion, he couldnt learn the truth, so he ordered that Paul be taken to the military headquarters.
local: Kunipaau kansikinee, biba tátí ki lì tiba, ní bin kíǹee mun cáá tiba, an di nìn yì-tì waa nìn ŋúń ki béè mbaamɔn, ní u bí yii bí cáá-u kí cùnnì soojab dumpu.

eng: When Paul reached the steps, he had to be carried by the soldiers in order to protect him from the violence of the crowd.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun bi bāań akɔl puee, samaayee kɔkɔ nín páb́ Pɔɔl mpɔn puee ní soojab yōoǹ-u ki túǹ, kí mɔ̄tì u-pu.

eng: The mob that followed kept screaming, Away with him.
local: Kun puee binib nìn wīikí tikpil ki pá u-boon ki tátí yii bí kpɔ̀-u.

eng: As Paul was about to be taken into the military headquarters, he asked the commander, May I speak with you? He answered, Do you know Greek?.
local: Bi tóob́ kí kɔ́ soojab dumpuee, ní Pɔɔl bālifì usoojakpikpaan yii: «A kíikí ḿ sòoǹ nì si tibaaa?» Ní ukpilee fātìi bālifì-u yii: «A gbìl̀ Greeki yab sooŋyaaa?.

eng: Arent you the Egyptian who started a revolt and led four thousand terrorists into the desert some time ago.
local: Sii di kaa sá Ueejipitija un là kí bīil̀ kitiŋ yaadal nee, ki yíkìi yōoǹ digaŋgandambi kutukub binaa ki cūnnì kuteeuneeee?».

eng: Paul replied, Im a Jew from Tarsus in Cilicia, a citizen of an important city. Please, let me speak to the people.
local: Ní Pɔɔl kíí-u yii: «Man sá Ujuudaja ní, ní bi màl̄-mi Taarsi, Siilisii tiŋkpaaŋkin. M baasii sá kitiŋkpaaŋŋi tindambiki ní. M gbāam̀-si, pɔ̄-mi nsan ḿ sòoǹ nì samaa!».

eng: We found the disciples there and stayed with them for a week. Compelled by the Spirit, they kept telling Paul not to go to Jerusalem.
local: Niin ní ti ká Kriisitoo yab biba, ní ti kāl̀ iwiin iluli bi-cee. Circir Fam yāntì bi béè ki tùkū Pɔɔl yii ú taa jɔ̄m̀ Jeerusaleem.

eng: With the commanders permission, Paul stood on the steps and gestured to the people. When they were quiet, he addressed them in Aramaic.
local: Niinee ní usoojakpikpaanee pú-u mpaan. Ní Pɔɔl yúl̀ akɔl pu ki yōoǹ u-ŋal ki téefì samaa. Niinee ní báà ŋma ŋmíl̀ fɔ́ŋfɔŋ. Ní u yōoǹ dinyɔɔbundi ki bíi sòoǹ nì samaa nì eebru sooŋyin, yii.

eng: When our time had come to an end, we departed. All of them, including women and children, accompanied us out of town where we knelt on the beach and prayed.
local: Ti nín kāl̀ iwiin gbanti dóò ti làá yīkìńee, bi nì bi-nimpoobi nì bi-biyaamu kɔkɔ céeǹní-ti áli ki nyántì kitiŋ kpaanceetiŋu. Niinee ní ti gbāaǹ dimɔɔgbandi ki mēē.

eng: We said good-bye to each other, then we boarded the ship and they returned to their homes.
local: Ti mēē ki dóòyee, ní ki kāaǹ tɔb, ní timbi búntì kí cùú kɔ́ meela, ní bi mun nín fàtī bi-dumpui.

eng: Continuing our voyage, we sailed from Tyre and arrived in Ptolemais. We greeted the brothers and sisters there and spent a day with them.
local: Ní ti yíkì Tiir ki cūtì Tooleemayiisi, ki cūtìi jáam̀ nnabiyaamu, ki kāl̀ bi-cee ŋwiin ŋubɔ. Niin ní ti kúntì dimɔɔl pu saŋkindi.

eng: The next day we left and came to Caesarea. We went to the house of Philip the evangelist, one of the Seven, and stayed with him.
local: Kutaa wúntèe ní ti yíkì niin ki bàn̄ Seesaaree, ní ki kɔ́ Fiiliipi dumpu. U sá un sòoǹ Yeesu bɔmɔntiiti ki sá binib biluli bin bi nìn lēē bí nīn yàkatī tijinee kansikin ubɔee ní.

eng: He had four unmarried daughters who were involved in the work of prophecy.
local: U nìn cáá bisapɔmbi binaa bin kaa nyí uninjee, bi nìn sá Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi.

eng: Brothers and fathers, listen now to my defense.
local: «M-baabi nì m-nabiyaamu, pīlifìmaan kɔŋkɔnnee tibɔti tin m cáá kí tùkù-nee!».

eng: I asked, What should I do, Lord? Get up, the Lord replied, and go into Damascus. There you will be told everything you have been appointed to do.
local: Niinee ní m bí yii: <Mimmee, m làá ŋá mana, m-Dindaan?> Ní ti-Dindaan bí-mi yii: <Yīkìí nīn cá Damaasi. Niin ní bi làá tin tùkù-si tin tin Unimbɔti bí yii á ŋée.>.

eng: I couldnt see because of the brightness of that light, so my companions led me by the hand into Damascus.
local: Ŋwalifee nín nìn jīliŋkì m-nimbiliŋ puee, maa nìn gíti wāĺ, ní man nì bin nìn nóóyee cúú m-ŋal ki dātì-mi ki cūnnì Damaasi.

eng: There was a certain man named Ananias. According to the standards of the Law, he was a pious man who enjoyed the respect of all the Jews living there.
local: «Uninja ubɔ nìn bí niin, bi yíì-u Ananiyasi. U nìn nóó Unimbɔti deedee ki tɔ́ ti-marab, ní Juuda yab bin nìn kóò Damaasee kɔkɔ nìn pàkà-u.

eng: He came and stood beside me. Brother Saul, receive your sight! he said. Instantly, I regained my sight and I could see him.
local: Kijapaayee dɔ́miń ki yúl̀ m-cee, ní ki bí yii: <M-nabiki Sɔɔl, ŋúbití a-nimbiliŋ!> Kpalaa niinee, ní m cúu ŋúbitì, ní ki wál̀-u.

eng: He said, The God of our ancestors has selected you to know his will, to see the righteous one, and to hear his voice.
local: Ní u bí-mi yii: <Ti-naanjab Nimbɔtiuee di lēē-si dooooyee ki yóoǹ yii á béé u nín là puee kí ká Deedeedaan un u lēē ki túnnée kí gbìl̄ u-neen.

eng: You will be his witness to everyone concerning what you have seen and heard.
local: Sii di làá nīn sá u-seeraleeliu, kí sòoǹ binib kɔkɔ nimbiin tin tin a kée nì tin a gbìl̄ee.

eng: What are you waiting for? Get up, be baptized, and wash away your sins as you call on his name.
local: Míǹ puee ba di gíti ŋúb́-si kɔŋkɔnnee? Yīkìm̄ kí kúuǹ kí yíiń ti-Dindaan bí fàl̀-si Unimbɔti nyim á ká a-kpitii pɔ̄tam.>».

eng: When I returned to Jerusalem and was praying in the temple, I had a visionary experience.
local: Pɔɔl nìkī ki lī yii: «M dāa fātìń Jeerusaleemee, ní ki kɔ́ kunimbɔtidiin ki bíi mèé Unimbɔti, ní an fītì-mi ní m wál̀ ti-Dindaan.

eng: I saw the Lord speaking to me. Hurry! he said. Leave Jerusalem at once because they wont accept your testimony about me.
local: Ní u tùkū-mi yii: <Nyā Jeerusaleem malaa, kun puee baa làá fōō tibɔti tin a bíi sòoǹ m-puee kí kíí.>.

eng: I responded, Lord, these people know I used to go from one synagogue to the next, beating those who believe in you and throwing them into prison.
local: Ní m fātìi kíí-u yii: <Ti-Dindaan, kéè, bíǹ bi-ba nyí-mi, m nín nìn yìiǹ Jeerusaleem akpafidiinni ki cútí bin nóó-see ki gbáà-bi ki cáa tòó sarkeeyaa!.

eng: When they heard him address them in Aramaic, they became even more quiet.
local: Bi nín gbìl̄ u bíi sòoǹ nì bi Eebru sooŋyee, ní bi mɔ̄tìi ŋmíl̀ díkí diki. Ní Pɔɔl bí-bi yii.

eng: When Stephen your witness was being killed, I stood there giving my approval, even watching the clothes that belonged to those who were killing him.
local: Kéè, bi nyí, daliŋkin bi bíi kù a-seeraleeliu Eetiyeenee, man di nìn bí niin ki bí yii u máaǹ ŋkúm, ní ki fōō bin bíi kù-uee bɔkutiŋ ki ŋúb́eeyaa!>.

eng: Then the Lord said to me, Go! I will send you far away to the Gentiles.
local: Ama ní u bí-mi yii: <Nín̄ cá. Bin bí ndan kaa sá Juuda yabee cee ní m tūǹ-si!>».

eng: The crowd listened to Paul until he said this. Then they shouted, Away with this man! Hes not fit to live.
local: Samaayee pílifiǹ Pɔɔl ki tin sāā laakin u bí yii ti-Dindaan tú-u bin kaa sá Juuda yabee cee. Niinee ní bi yíkì ki bíi tátí yii: «Bí kpɔ̀maan uninja nee! Bí taa tàan̄-u! Waa máaǹ kí nīn bí!».

eng: As they were screaming, throwing off their garments, and flinging dust into the air.
local: Niinee ní bi bíi pùlifī ki pèetī bi-bɔkutiŋ ki fīintí, ní ki jáfí ntam ki tòó kutaa.

eng: the commander directed that Paul be taken into the military headquarters. He ordered that Paul be questioned under the whip so that he could find out why they were shouting at him like this.
local: Ní usoojakpikpaanee bí yii bí cáá Pɔɔl kí kɔ́ǹ soojab dumpu kí gbá-u anaalab kí bàlifì-u kun di yì-tì ní samaa kɔkɔ tátí u-pu mimmee.

eng: As they were stretching him out and tying him down with straps, Paul said to the centurion standing there, Can you legally whip a Roman citizen who hasnt been found guilty in court.
local: Bi bóob́-u ki dóò ki làá gbá-uee, ní Pɔɔl bí usoojakpil un nìn bí niinee yii: «An máaǹ ní gbá Room tindambiki un bɔti gba baa láá sòon̄eeee?».

eng: When the centurion heard this, he went to the commander and reported it. He asked, What are you about to do? This man is a Roman citizen.
local: Ukpilee gbìl̄ an nyɔɔbundee, ní ki cūtìi tùkū usoojakpikpaanee yii: «Á nīn nyí nì a-ba! Kijapaayee sá Room tindambiki ní.».

eng: The commander went to Paul and demanded, Tell me! Are you a Roman citizen? He said, Yes.
local: Niinee ní usoojakpikpaanee dɔ́miń ki bālifì Pɔɔl yii: «A sá Room tindambiki níii?» Ní Pɔɔl kíí-u yii: «Iiin.».

eng: The commander replied, It cost me a lot of money to buy my citizenship. Paul said, Im a citizen by birth.
local: Niinee ní usoojakpikpaanee bí yii: «Man pàn̄ animbil tikpil ní ki nín kpántì Room tindambiki.» Ní Pɔɔl fātìi bí-u yii: «Bi pée màl̄-mee, m sá Room tindambiki ní.».

eng: At once those who were about to examine him stepped away. The commander was alarmed when he realized he had bound a Roman citizen.
local: Kpalaayee, bin bàà làá gbá-u kí yàntī ú sòoǹee kākatì ki wāatì-u. Usoojakpikpaanee nín dāa béè yii Pɔɔl sá Room tindambikee, ní tifaandi kɔ́ u mun u-ba, kun puee u nìn dū ikusaka ní ki bóob́-u.

eng: Paul continued, Im a Jew, born in Tarsus in Cilicia but raised in this city. Under Gamaliels instruction, I was trained in the strict interpretation of our ancestral Law. I am passionately loyal to God, just like you who are gathered here today.
local: «Man sá ujuudaja ní, bi màl̄-mi Taarsi Siilisii tiŋkin, ama kitiŋ nee poon ní m kāl̀ ki wíiǹ. Gamaliyeel cee ní m nìn bí ki bífì ti-naanjab marab kɔkɔ, ní ki nìn cáá dipɔ́ɔǹdi Unimbɔti bɔtin tiŋan, kii díǹ wiiŋŋu ni mun nín cáá dipɔ́ɔǹdi pu nan ní.

eng: The commander still wanted to know the truth about why Paul was being accused by the Jews. Therefore, the next day he ordered the chief priests and the entire Jerusalem Council to assemble. Then he took Paul out of prison and had him stand before them.
local: Usoojakpikpaanee là kí béé kun di yì-tì ní Juuda yab ŋúb́ Pɔɔlee. An pu ní kutaa wúntèe u bútì-u ikusaka ní ki bí yii bisaraaŋakpilib nì bibɔsoonliibi kitil kɔkɔ ń kpāfì. Bi kpáfèe, ní u cáań Pɔɔl ki yóoǹ bi-nimbiin.

eng: I harassed those who followed this Way to their death, arresting and delivering both men and women into prison.
local: M nìn páb́ bin nìn nóó ti-Dindaan saŋŋuee, áli ki kūū biba, ki cútì bininjab nì binimpoobi ki tɔ́ sarkan.

eng: The high priest and the whole Jerusalem Council can testify about me. I received letters from them, addressed to our associates in Damascus, then went there to bring those who were arrested to Jerusalem so they could be punished.
local: Usaraaŋakpil nì bibɔsooŋkpilib kɔkɔ nìn kántì m nín nìn ŋáaǹ puee. Bíǹ di ŋūǹ kí tùkù-ni mbaamɔn. Bíǹ gba di nìn tīī-mi tigbɔn ḿ cáá tīī Juuda yab kpilib bin kóò Damaasee kí ŋmā kí cúú Kriisitoo yab bin bí niinee, kí bóobiń-bi ikusaka kí cáań Jeerusaleem bí dáaĺ bi-tafal.».

eng: During that journey, about noon, as I approached Damascus, suddenly a bright light from heaven encircled me.
local: «Ki cá kuwiŋkuluuee, m nìn bí nsanni ki tóob́ Damaasi. Dicilpu niinee ní ŋwalifikpaŋkpaan ŋubɔ nyánní yilpu ki dɔ́miń ki māntìi tú-mi ki wāĺ nyáka nyaka.

eng: I fell to the ground and heard a voice asking me, Saul, Saul, why are you harassing me.
local: ní m lítì kitiŋ. Niinee ní m gbìl̄ nneen ŋubɔ bíi lì-mi yii: <Sɔɔl, Sɔɔl, ba ŋá ní a páb́-mi míǹ?>.

eng: I answered, Who are you, Lord? I am Jesus the Nazarene, whom you are harassing, he replied.
local: Ní m fātìi bālifì-u yii: <An sá sii ŋma, m-Dindaan?> Ní u kíí-mi yii: <An sá man Yeesu Naasaareeti yɔu un a páb́ee.>.

eng: My traveling companions saw the light, but they didnt hear the voice of the one who spoke to me.
local: Bin nìn bí m-ceeyee nìn ká ŋwalifi, ama ní kaa nìn nín gbìl̄ nneen ŋun sòon̄ nì mee pu.

eng: Paul stared at the council and said, Brothers, I have lived my life with an altogether clear conscience right up to this very day.
local: Ní Pɔɔl dū u-nimbikpeeŋu ki báaǹ bikpilibee pu, ní ki bí-bi yii: «M-nabiyaamu, m nyí yii m ŋáaǹ tin kɔkɔwoooo m máaǹ kí ŋá kí tīī Unimbɔtee nì dipɔbil diba, ki dāa sāā díǹ wiiŋŋu nee.».

eng: The dispute became so heated that the commander feared they might tear Paul to pieces. He ordered soldiers to go down and remove him by force from their midst. Then they took him back to the military headquarters.
local: Kunikpɔŋkpɔkɔu mɔ̄tìi pɔ́ɔkèe, ní usoojakpikpaan fāŋkì yii bí taa ŋée kí cúú Pɔɔl kí tɔ̀ntī. Ní u yāntì soojab kpákatìi cúú Pɔɔl ki nyántì bi-kansikin, ki cáa cūnnì soojab dumpu.

eng: The following night the Lord stood near Paul and said, Be encouraged! Just as you have testified about me in Jerusalem, so too you must testify in Rome.
local: An nyeekuee, ti-Dindaan dɔ́miń Pɔɔl cee, ní ki bí-u yii: «Pɔ́ɔǹ a-ba! Kii a nín sòon̄ m-pu Jeerusaleem doo puee, án máaǹ á cù Room mun kí tin sòoǹ m-pu niin mbaantiim míǹ.».

eng: The next morning some Jewish leaders formulated a plot and solemnly promised that they wouldnt eat or drink until they had killed Paul.
local: Kutaa wúntèe ní Juuda yab kpáfì kinyɔkɔ ki pūtì Unimbɔti yii, bi yaa kaa kūū Pɔɔl, baa làá jāaa, kaa làá nyɔ̀ mun.

eng: More than forty people were involved in the conspiracy.
local: Bin nìn kpáfì kinyɔkɔ ki pūtì mimmee nìn jítì binib imuŋku ili pu.

eng: They went to the chief priests and elders and said, We have solemnly promised to eat nothing until we have killed Paul.
local: Ní bi cūtìi ká bisaraaŋakpilib nì bikpilib, ní ki bí-bi yii: «Timbi baasii pūtì ki tíntì yii, ti yaa kaa kūū Pɔɔl, taa làá dáká tiba.

eng: You and the council must explain to the commander that you need Paul brought down to you. Pretend that you want to examine his case more closely. Were prepared to kill him before he arrives.
local: Míǹ puee, nimbi nì bibɔsoonliibi kitil kɔkɔ, ní mèè usoojakpikpaan ú cáań-ni Pɔɔl, yii nimbi là kí mɔ̀tīí bàlifì-u tin páaa ní u baasii ŋée. Mimmee, timbi ń bɔ́ntí, bi yaa cáá-u ki tóobińee, timbi ń kíntí kí kpɔ̀-u!».

eng: Pauls sister had a son who heard about the ambush and he came to the military headquarters and reported it to Paul.
local: Niinee, Pɔɔl niisal biki gbìl̄ bi nín kpáfì ki sòon̄ tiŋyee, ní u búntì soojab dumpu ki kɔ́ ki tùkū Pɔɔl tin jítèe.

eng: Paul called for one of the centurions and said, Take this young man to the commander because he has something to report to him.
local: Ní Pɔɔl yíiń bisoojakpilibee ubɔ ní ki bí-u yii: «Cáá kinaacimpɔmbiki nee kí cùnnì usoojakpikpaan cee, kun puee kì cáá tiba ki là kí tùkù-u.».

eng: He took him to the commander and said, The prisoner Paul asked me to bring this young man to you. He has something to tell you.
local: Ukpilee yōoǹ-kì ki cáá-kì ki bàn̄ usoojakpikpaanee cee, ní ki bí yii: «Usarkadaan Pɔɔl di yíiń-mi ki gbāam̀ yii ḿ cáań-si kinaacimpɔmbiki nee, kì cáá tiba ki làá tùkù-si.».

eng: The commander took him by the hand and withdrew to a place where they could speak privately. He asked, What do you have to report to me.
local: Niinee ní usoojakpikpaanee cúú kinaacimpɔmbikee ŋal ki kpáàa kākatì ŋkpaanceeti, ní ki bí-kì yii: «Tilati ní a cáań ní?».

eng: The high priest Ananias ordered those standing beside Paul to strike him in the mouth.
local: Niinee ní usaraaŋakpil, un bi yíì Ananiyasee, bí bin yú ki pìl̄-uee yii bí cùú fáá u-nyɔkɔ.

eng: He replied, The Jewish leaders have conspired to ask that you bring Paul down to the council tomorrow. They will pretend that they want to investigate his case more closely.
local: Ní kì kíí-u yii: «Juuda yab kpáfì kí mèè-si yii á cáań-bi Pɔɔl bibɔsoonliibi kitil nimbiin, bi là kí ŋá kii bi làá gíti mɔ̀tīí bàlifì-u tibɔti tiba yamee.

eng: Dont fall for it! More than forty of them are waiting to ambush him. They have solemnly promised not to eat or drink until they have killed him. They are ready now, awaiting your consent.
local: Á taa pīlifiǹ-bi! Di sá yii bi-kansikin binib imuŋku ili nì ipaa pūtì yii, bi yaa kaa kūū Pɔɔl, bíǹ kaa làá jāaa, kaa làá nyɔ̀ mun. Bi bɔ́ntì kí báĺ kí cīití-u. Míǹ puee, bi kíǹ ki cí tin sii cááyee ní nee.».

eng: The commander dismissed the young man, ordering him, Dont tell anyone that you brought this to my attention.
local: Niinee, usoojakpikpaanee bí kinaacimpɔmbikee yii: «Nín̄ kùǹ. Á taa sòoǹ ubɔ cee yii a tùkū-mi tibɔti gbanti!».

eng: The commander called two centurions and said, Prepare two hundred soldiers, seventy horsemen, and two hundred spearmen to leave for Caesarea at nine oclock tonight.
local: Boonee ní usoojakpikpaanee yíiń bisoojakpilib bili, ní ki bí yii bí kpāfì soojab kulaafaau nì soojab bin cáá itaamuee imuŋku ita nì saalaa, nì soojab bin tòó ŋkpamuee mun kulaafaau, bí bɔ́ntí kí nīn cá Seesaaree tikúti tiwa kunyeeu nee.

eng: Have horses ready for Paul to ride, so they may take him safely to Governor Felix.
local: Bí nyàab̀ itaamu kí kàan̄ Pɔɔl, kí cáá-u kí bànnì gɔmina Feeliikisi cee nì alaafiya.

eng: He wrote the following letter.
local: Ní usoojakpikpaanee ŋmàn̄ kugbɔŋu ki tīī gɔmina yii.

eng: Claudius Lysias, to the most honorable Governor Felix: Greetings.
local: «Man Kloodi Liisiyasi di jáaḿ m-kpiliu gɔmina Feeliikisi .

eng: This man was seized by the Jews and was almost killed by them. I was nearby with a unit of soldiers, and I rescued him when I discovered that he was a Roman citizen.
local: Di sá yii Juuda yab di cúú uninja nee ki là kí kpɔ̀-u. M nín gbìl̄ yii u sá Room tindambikee, ní man nì m-soojab cūtìi fōoń-u.

eng: I wanted to find out why they were accusing him, so I brought him to their council.
local: M nìn là kí béé kun pu ní Juuda yab ŋúb́-u mimmee, ní m cáá-u ki cūnnì bi-bɔsoonliibi kitil cee.

eng: I discovered that they were accusing him about questions related to their Law. I found no charge deserving of death or imprisonment.
local: Niinee ní m ká yii bi bí yii u yīntì bi-marab, ama an kaa sá tibɔti tin pu u máaǹ ŋkúmeeyaaa, kaa tí máaǹ sarka mun.

eng: Then Paul said to him, God is about to strike you, you whitewashed wall! You sit and judge me according to the Law, yet disobey the Law by ordering that I be struck.
local: Ní Pɔɔl bí yii: «Unimbɔti làá dāá fáá si mun, upɔnyɔŋfiti! Sii kā ki cáá marab ki sòoǹ man bɔti, ki yìntī marab gbanti a-ba ní ki cáá yii bí gbá mama!».

eng: When I was informed of a conspiracy against his life, I sent him to you at once and ordered his accusers to bring their case against him before you.
local: M nín dāa béè yii Juuda yab kpáfì ki nyàab̀ isan kí kpɔ̀-uee puee, ní m dū-u ki tīiń-si malaa, ní ki bí u-nannanliibi yii bí dāan̄ kí yíiń-u a-cee ibeeyiin.».

eng: Following their orders, the soldiers took Paul during the night and brought him to Antipatris.
local: Soojabee lafun ŋá u nín lī puee, ki yōoǹ Pɔɔl ki cáá-u ki bānnì áli Antiipatriisi kunyeeu gbanti.

eng: The following day they let the horsemen continue on with Paul while they returned to the military headquarters in Jerusalem.
local: Ku wúntèe ní soojab bin nìn còom̄ ataa puee nín gítiń, ní itaamu dambee kpáaǹ Pɔɔl ki cáa nín cá.

eng: The horsemen entered Caesarea, delivered the letter to the governor, and brought Paul before him.
local: Bi bàn̄ Seesaareeyee, ní ki dū kugbɔŋu ki tīī gɔmina, ní ki dū Pɔɔl ki dàkā-u.

eng: After he read the letter, he asked Paul about his home province. When he learned that he was from Cilicia.
local: Gɔmina kàan̄ kugbɔŋuee ki dóòyee, ní ki bālifì Pɔɔl yii u sá kitiŋ kilaka ní. Ní Pɔɔl bí-u yii u sá Siilisii. U béè mimmee.

eng: the governor said, I will hear your case when your accusers arrive. Then he ordered that Paul be kept in custody in Herods palace.
local: ní ki bí yii: «Si nì bin kpákèe yaa láa bāańee, á dāá sòoǹ tin a cááyee ḿ pīlifì.» U lī mimmee ní ki bí yii bí cáá-u kí cùnnì ubɔti Eeroodi dumpu.

eng: Those standing near him asked, You dare to insult Gods high priest.
local: Ní bin nìn yú ki kɔ́ĺ Pɔɔlee bí-u yii: «Mimmee, a bíi sìí Unimbɔti saraaŋakpiliu deeyaa!».

eng: Paul replied, Brothers, I wasnt aware that he was the high priest. It is written, You will not speak evil about a ruler of your people .
local: Ní Pɔɔl fātìi kíí-bi yii: «M-nabiyaamu, maa nyí yii an sá usaraaŋakpil. An yaa sá fam, maa bàà sòoǹ míǹ, kun puee Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon tùkù-ti yii: <A taa sòoǹ ni-bɔtiu bɔfam.>».

eng: Knowing that some of them were Sadducees and the others Pharisees, Paul exclaimed in the council, Brothers, Im a Pharisee and a descendant of Pharisees. I am on trial because of my hope in the resurrection of the dead.
local: Pɔɔl nìn nyí yii bibɔsoonliibee jandi diba sá Sadusab ní dijandi diba mun sá Fariisab. Míǹ pu ní u nìn téeń samaayee nimbiin yii: «M-nabiyaamu, ma mun sá Fariisa ní, Ufariisaja di màl̄-mi. Dimákal din m cáá bitaŋkpiibi fìkititam puee di yì-tì ní bi cáań-mi doo kí sòoǹ m-bɔti!».

eng: These words aroused a dispute between the Pharisees and Sadducees, and the assembly was divided.
local: U lī míǹ ki dóòyee, ní kunikpɔŋkpɔkɔu kɔ́ Sadusab nì Fariisab kansikin, ní samaa gbanti cúu yākatì tɔbin.

eng: This is because Sadducees say that theres no resurrection, angel, or spirit, but Pharisees affirm them all.
local: Di sá yii Sadusab, bíǹ bí yii bitaŋkpiibi gíti kpá kí dāá fìkitī, Unimbɔti tuuŋyi kpá, kinaŋ mun kpá, ama Fariisab, bíǹ bí yii tíǹ kɔkɔ bí.

eng: Council members were shouting loudly. Some Pharisees who were legal experts stood up and insisted forcefully, We find nothing wrong with this man! What if a spirit or angel has spoken to him.
local: Niinee ní kujakpaaŋŋu jéetì bi-kansikin, ní bimaradakaliibi bin nìn sá Fariisab jandi púee biba yíkì, kaa kíí, ki bíi mɔ́tí yii: «Timbi kaa ká kijapaai nee ŋá tiba. An yaa yíì, Unimbɔti tuuŋŋu di làá nīn máaǹ ki sòon̄ nì u.».

eng: Five days later the high priest Ananias came down with some elders and a lawyer named Tertullus. They pressed charges against Paul before the governor.
local: Iwiin iŋmoo boonee ní usaraaŋakpil Ananiyasi bāań Seesaaree gɔmina cee, u nì bikpilib biba nì umɔtil ubɔ un bi yíì Teertuluusee, ki yíiń Pɔɔl ibeeyiin.

eng: The governor nodded at Paul, giving him permission to speak. He responded, I know that you have been judge over this nation for many years, so I gladly offer my own defense.
local: Boonee ní gɔmina pú Pɔɔl nsan yii u mun ń sòoǹ. Ní Pɔɔl yōoǹ dinyɔɔbundi ki bí yii: «M nyí yii doooo abin kupaau sèé a nín sá kitiŋ nee poon ubɔsoonlee. An puee nì dimákal poon ní m làá sòoǹ tin m cááyee a-nimbiin.

eng: You can verify that I went up to worship in Jerusalem no more than twelve days ago.
local: Sii a-ba yaa bālifì gba, a làá béé yii an kaa láá bàn̄ iwiin saalaa nì ili m nín jɔ́m̀ Jeerusaleem kí nyɔ̄ŋkì Unimbɔtee.

eng: They didnt find me arguing with anyone in the temple or stirring up a crowd, whether in the synagogue or anywhere else in the city.
local: Dalba dalba baa láá ká-mi kunimbɔtidiin man nì ubɔ bíi kpákà. Baa láá tí ká-mi m bí akpafidiinnaaa, kitiŋ poon laaba ki sòoǹ ki yòontí binib pɔbiliŋ.

eng: Nor can they prove to you the allegations they are now bringing against me.
local: Binib nee kaa ŋūǹ kí dàkà-si yii kun pu bi ŋúb́-mee baasii sèé.

eng: I do admit this to you, that I am a follower of the Way, which they call a faction. Accordingly, I worship the God of our ancestors and believe everything set out in the Law and written in the Prophets.
local: Nì ibaamɔn poon ní m sòoǹ nì si nee, m nóó Yeesu saŋŋu ŋun bíǹ yíì yii ŋuaa ŋān̄ee, ní ki jáaḿ ti-naanjab Nimbɔtiu. Ama man nyí yii Mooyiisi nì Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi nín ŋmàn̄ tin tigbɔn poonee, an sá ibaamɔn.

eng: The hope I have in God I also share with my accusers, that there will be a resurrection of both the righteous and the unrighteous.
local: M cáá dimákal Unimbɔti pu, kii bi mun bi-ba nín cáá dimákal u-pu puee, yii binimɔntiibi nì binikpitib kɔkɔ bí kí dāá fìkitī ditaŋkpiilin.

eng: On account of this, I have committed myself to maintaining a clear conscience before God and with all people.
local: Míǹ pu ní man bí ki pɔ̄ɔntí m-ba kí nīn sá unimɔnti Unimbɔti nimbiin nì binib mun cee.

eng: After an absence of several years, I came to Jerusalem to bring gifts for the poor of my nation and to offer sacrifices.
local: «Maa nìn gíti bí Jeerusaleem abiŋkaaŋkaa aba poon. Boonee ní m fātìi gítìń, ki cáań animbil kí tūtuǹ m-tiŋki nib, kí kpáàa ŋá saraabi.

eng: When they found me in the temple, I was ritually pure. There was no crowd and no disturbance.
local: M ŋá akoobil ki dóò, boonee ní ki bíi ŋáań saraabi gbanti kunimbɔtidiin, ní bi bāań ki ká-mi. Biba kaa nìn kpáfì m-cee, fìkil fikil mun nìn kpá.

eng: But there were some Jews from the province of Asia. They should be here making their accusations, if indeed they have something against me.
local: Juuda yab bin kóò Asii ki nìn ká-mee di yaa bàà bímaan bi-ba a-nimbiin doo kí bàà sòoǹ tin ní m ŋá-bee, an bàà ŋān̄.

eng: After the governor summoned Paul, Tertullus began to make his case against him. He declared, Under your leadership, we have experienced substantial peace, and your administration has brought reforms to our nation.
local: Bi yíiń Pɔɔl, ní Teertuluusi kíĺ ki bíi kàaǹ tibɔti yii: «Ukpimɔnti Feeliikisi , sii di yì ŋgbansɔŋfi ŋun poon ti bí, an kaa kúntí nee. A-ciiŋŋu pu ní a yāntì kitiŋ kpántì ní ki ŋáŋkì.

eng: In their absence, have these people who are here declare what crime they found when I stood before the Jerusalem Council.
local: Àá bin bí nee ń tùkù-si, bi nín nìn cáá-mi bibɔsoonliibi kitil nimbiinee, tikpitil tin bi ká m ŋá.

eng: Perhaps it concerns this one statement that I blurted out when I was with them: I am on trial before you today because of the resurrection of the dead.
local: an yaa kaa sá m nín nìn yú bi-kansikin ki téeń mpɔn dinyɔɔbumbaantiil yii: <M nín fōō ki kíí yii bitaŋkpiibi làá dāá fìkitēe puee ní m yú ni-nimbiin díǹ, ni bíi sòoǹ m-bɔtaa!>».

eng: Felix, who had an accurate understanding of the Way, adjourned the meeting. He said, When Lysias the commander arrives from Jerusalem, I will decide this case.
local: Pɔɔl sòon̄ míǹ ki dóòyee, ní Feeliikisi , un di nìn nyí kun di sá Yeesu saŋŋu tiŋanee, dāa bí-bi yii bí nīn kùǹ. Ní ki bí yii: «Asee usoojakpikpaan Liisiyasi yaa láa dɔ́mińee, m dāa béetí ni-bɔti gbanti.».

eng: He arranged for a centurion to guard Paul. He was to give Paul some freedom, and his friends were not to be hindered in their efforts to provide for him.
local: U lī mimmee, ní ki bí yii usoojakpil ubɔ ń cáá Pɔɔl kí pìin̄, ama kí yàntī ú tɔ̄ u-ba ŋwaam pu, kí nīn pūǹ u-bɔɔbi nsan bí nīn dòoń u-cee ki cāabìń tin tin u yaa lèe.

eng: After several days, Felix came with his wife Drusilla, who was Jewish, and summoned Paul. He listened to him talk about faith in Christ Jesus.
local: Iwiin iba boonee ní Feeliikisi nì u-nimpuu Drusii un sá ujuudanimpuee dɔ́miń ní ki tú bi cūtìi yíiń Pɔɔl. Ní Pɔɔl sòon̄ u pílifì min di sá yii bí fōō Yeesu Kriisitoo kí kííyee.

eng: When he spoke about upright behavior, self-control, and the coming judgment, Felix became fearful and said, Go away for now! When I have time, Ill send for you.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun Pɔɔl kíĺ ki bíi sòoǹ unil nín máaǹ kí nīn bí deedee kí fá u-wunti kɔdeeu puee nì ibɔsoon in bí ki dòońee puee, ní Feeliikisi fāŋkì, ní ki bí yii: «Nín̄ cá, daliŋkin m yaa ká mpaanee, m làá tí fàtīí yíiń-si.».

eng: At the same time, he was hoping that Paul would offer him some money, so he often sent for him and talked with him.
local: U nìn dàkafī yii Pɔɔl làá tīī-u animbil ní, míǹ pu ní u nìn yíì-u múlmul bi sòoǹ.

eng: When two years had passed, Felix was succeeded by Porcius Festus. Since Felix wanted to grant a favor to the Jews, he left Paul in prison.
local: Abin ali boonee ní Pɔɔsiyusi Feestuusi fōō Feeliikisi yil ki kíǹ ki sá gɔmina. Feeliikisi là an mɔ̄ɔkiǹ Juuda yabee, ní u yāntì Pɔɔl gbíntìi bí sarkan.

eng: Always and everywhere, most honorable Felix, we acknowledge this with deep gratitude.
local: Ti kāntí a-ŋanti gbanti an kɔkɔ nì an kɔkɔ. Míǹ puee, ti jáaḿ-si tiŋan.

eng: I dont want to take too much of your time, so I ask that you listen with your usual courtesy to our brief statement of the facts.
local: Maa là kí bīil̀ a-yuntiŋu tikpilee, an di yì-tì ní m mèé-si a-laŋŋaŋfitiiku puee, á pīlifiǹ-ti fiii.

eng: We have found this man to be a troublemaker who stirs up riots among all the Jews throughout the empire. Hes a ringleader of the Nazarene faction.
local: Di sá yii timbi ká yii kijapaai nee sá unil un sá asaluu daanee. Úǹ di yì-tì ní fìkil fikil kɔ́ Juuda yab bin bí duulinya nee nee kɔkɔ kansikin. Úǹ di sá Naasaareeti yab kitil kpiliu ubɔ.

eng: and even tried to defile the temple. Thats when we arrested him.
local: U là gba yii ú bīil̀ kunimbɔtidii, an pu ní timbi cúú-u. Ti bàà mākafì yii tí dī ti-marab pu kí dáaĺ u-tafal.

eng: By examining him yourself, you will be able to verify the allegations we are bringing against him.
local: ní ki bí yii u nì bin kpákèe ń nīn dòoń sii cee. Míǹ puee, sii a-ba yaa bālifì-u, a béetí kun pu ní ti ŋúb́-uee.».

eng: The Jews reinforced the action against Paul, affirming the truth of these accusations.
local: Ní Juuda yab bí yii Teertuluusi nín lī puee, míǹ ní an sá.

eng: Three days after arriving in the province, Festus went up to Jerusalem from Caesarea.
local: Feestuusi nìn bàn̄ Juudee watil. Iwiin ita boonee ní u yíkì Seesaaree ki nín jɔ́ḿ Jeerusaleem.

eng: Paul replied, Im standing before Caesars court. I ought to be tried here. I have done nothing wrong to the Jews, as you well know.
local: Ní Pɔɔl kíí-u yii: «Gɔminakpaan Seesaar bɔsoondiikun ní man sí ki bí nee, niin ní an máaǹ bí sòoǹ m-bɔti! Kun puee maa ŋá Juuda yab tikpitil tiba, sii a-ba gba nyí míǹ tiŋan.

eng: If Im guilty and have done something that deserves death, then I wont try to avoid death. But if there is nothing to their accusations against me, no one has the authority to hand me over to them. I appeal to Caesar.
local: M yaa sá unikpiti ní ki ŋá tin tin máaǹ ŋkúmee, mimmee maa mɔ́tí ŋkúm pu. Ama, an yaa tí sá yii tin bi sòoǹ m-puee yaa sá nnyimɔn, ubɔ kaa ŋūǹ kí dū-mi kí tīī-bi. See ḿ bà gɔminakpaan Seesaar nimbiin!».

eng: After Festus conferred with his advisors, he responded, You have appealed to Caesar. To Caesar you will go.
local: Niinee ní Feestuusi nì u-kpilib kāl̀ ki sòon̄ ki kpōotì tɔb, ní ki kíí-u yii: «Kéè, a bí yii a là kí cù gɔminakpaan nimbiinaa! Ti kíí á lafun cù u-nimbiin.».

eng: After several days had passed, King Agrippa and Bernice arrived in Caesarea to welcome Festus.
local: Iwiin ili boonee, ní ubɔti Agriipa nì Beereeniisi bāań Seesaaree kí jāam̀ Feestuusi .

eng: Since they were staying there for many days, Festus discussed the case against Paul with the king. He said, There is a man whom Felix left in prison.
local: Bi kāl̀ iwiŋkaaŋkaa iba poonee, ní Feestuusi dū Pɔɔl bɔti ki kàan̄ ki tùkū ubɔti yii: « Feeliikisi yāntì uninja ubɔ sarkan doo.

eng: When I was in Jerusalem, the Jewish chief priests and elders brought charges against him and requested a guilty verdict in his case.
local: M nín nìn cūtì Jeerusaleemee ní bisaraaŋakpilib nì Juuda yab kpilib tùkū-mi yii u nìn ŋá ikpiti ní, ní ki mēē-mi yii ḿ dáaĺ u-tafal.

eng: I told them it is contrary to Roman practice to hand someone over before they have faced their accusers and had opportunity to offer a defense against the charges.
local: Ní m kíí-bi yii Room yab bíǹ kaa dù unil kí tīī bí dáaĺ u-tafal, asee bí pɔ̄-u kinyɔkɔ u mun ń sòoǹ u-nannanliibi nimbiin kun pu ní bi bí ki jáàyee.

eng: When they came here, I didnt put them off. The very next day I took my seat in the court and ordered that the man be brought before me.
local: Bi dɔ́miń doo nee, kutaa wúntèe maa nìn gíti tāaǹ-bi, ní ki kɔ́ ki kāl̀ m-bɔsoondiikun ní ki bí bí cáań uninja gbanti.

eng: When the accusers took the floor, they didnt charge him with any of the crimes I had expected.
local: Ní u-nannanliibi mun kàan̄. Ama maa ká tikpitil tin ní m bàà dàkafī yii u máaǹ ki ŋée.

eng: Instead, they quibbled with him about their own religion and about some dead man named Jesus, who Paul claimed was alive.
local: An nìn sá bíǹ bi-ba marab nì un bi yíì Yeesu u kpíì kin Pɔɔl bí yii u bée ŋmaniŋma pu ní bi bíi kpákà.

eng: The chief priests and Jewish leaders presented their case against Paul. Appealing to him.
local: Ní bisaraaŋakpilib nì Juuda yab nikpaambi dɔ́miń ki kàan̄ Pɔɔl bɔti ki tùkū-u, ní ki gbāam̀ Feestuusi.

eng: Since I had no idea how to investigate these matters, I asked if he would be willing to go to Jerusalem to stand trial there on these issues.
local: Maa nìn nyí m nín làá ŋá pu kí béé tibɔti gbanti yilee, an pu ní m bālifì Pɔɔl yii u yaa là kí cù Jeerusaleem ḿ sòoǹ u-bɔti gbanti niin.

eng: However, Paul appealed that he be held in custody pending a decision from His Majesty the emperor, so I ordered that he be held until I could send him to Caesar.
local: Ní Pɔɔl yíì ki lī yii bí yàntī ú nīn bí sarkan kí nīn cí ŋyunti ŋun ní gɔminakpaan Seesaar làá dāá sòoǹ tibɔti gbantee. Ní m bí bí dū-u kí pìin̄ kí nīn cí ŋyunti ŋun m làá dāá dū-u kí tīī gɔminakpaanee.».

eng: Agrippa said to Festus, I want to hear the man myself. Tomorrow, Festus replied, you will hear him.
local: Ní Agriipa bí yii: «Man m-ba bàà là uninja gbanti ń sòoǹ ḿ pīlifì.» Ní Feestuusi kíí-u yii: «Tin u yaa cááyee, fool a fu gbìl̀.».

eng: The next day Agrippa and Bernice came with great fanfare. They entered the auditorium with the military commanders and the citys most prominent men. Festus then ordered that Paul be brought in.
local: Kutaa wúntèe ní binib tikpil fōō Agriipa nì Beereeniisi, ní bi nì bisoojakpilib nì kitiŋ poon nikpaambi táá ki kɔ́ kubɔsoondiin. Ní Feestuusi yāntì bi cáań Pɔɔl.

eng: Festus said, King Agrippa and everyone present with us: You see this man! The entire Jewish community, both here and in Jerusalem, has appealed to me concerning him. Theyve been calling for his immediate death.
local: ní ki bí yii: «Ubɔti Agriipa nì nimbi bin kɔkɔwoooo kpáfì doo nee, Juuda yab kɔkɔ dɔ́miń m-cee Jeerusaleem nì doo ki yíiń uninja un ni wāĺ nee ibeeyiin, ní ki tátí yii bi máaǹ kí kpɔ̀-u.

eng: Ive found that he has done nothing deserving death. When he appealed to His Majesty, I decided to send him to Rome.
local: Ama man nyí yii waa ŋá tin máaǹ ŋkúmee, ní úǹ u-ba dāa bí yii gɔminakpaan ń sòoǹ u-bɔti. M kpēē yii an lafun máaǹ mimmee, ní m là kí dū-u kí tīī-u.

eng: I have nothing definite to write to our lord emperor. Therefore, Ive brought him before all of you, and especially before you, King Agrippa, so that after this investigation, I might have something to write.
local: Maa láá nyí u-bɔti pu tiba kí ŋmà kí tīī ti-yidaaŋwu Seesaar kɔŋkɔnnee. Míǹ pu ní m cáań-u ni-cee, nì sii a-ba ubɔti Agriipa nimbiin, ní bi yaa bíi bàlifī-u anyɔɔbunee, ḿ ŋmā kí ká tiba poon kí ŋmà.

eng: After all, it would be foolish to send a prisoner without specifying the charges against him.
local: Di sá yii an kaa kítì ḿ dū usarkadaan kí tīī míǹ, kaa lī tin pu ní bi ŋúb́-uee.».

eng: they asked as a favor from Festus that he summon Paul to Jerusalem. They were planning to ambush and kill him along the way.
local: yii ú jī kimɔtii kí yàntī Pɔɔl ń dāan̄ Jeerusaleem. Di sá yii bi bɔ́ntì ki cí kí kpɔ̀-u nsanni.

eng: But Festus responded by keeping Paul in Caesarea, since he was to return there very soon himself.
local: Ní Feestuusi kíí-bi yii: «Pɔɔl kā sarkan Seesaaree, man m-ba bíi bɔ́ntí kí fàtīí gítí niin ní nee.».

eng: Some of your leaders can come down with me, he said. If hes done anything wrong, they can bring charges against him.
local: Ní u gíti bí-bi yii: «Mimmee, ni-kpilib ń nóó-mi tí cù Seesaaree bí tin sòoǹ kí kpèè uninja gbanti yaa ŋá tikpitil tiba.».

eng: He stayed with them for no more than eight or ten days, then went down to Caesarea. The following day he took his seat in the court and ordered that Paul be brought in.
local: Feestuusi nìn ŋá iwiin inii kii saalaa pu bi-kansikin, ní ki fātìi gítìń Seesaaree. Kutaa wúntèe ní u kɔ́ ki kāl̀ kubɔsoondiin ní ki bí bí yíiń Pɔɔl.

eng: When he arrived, many Jews who had come down from Jerusalem surrounded him. They brought serious charges against him, but they couldnt prove them.
local: Pɔɔl bāańee Juuda yab bin nyánní Jeerusaleemee yú ki kɔ́ĺ-u ki sòoǹ u-pu yii u ŋáaǹ tibɔkpaandi tikpil. Ama baa nìn ŋūǹ kí cáań tiba bí béé yii tin pu bi sòoǹee sá ibaamɔn.

eng: In his own defense, Paul said, Ive done nothing wrong against the Jewish Law, against the temple, or against Caesar.
local: Ní Pɔɔl mun yōoǹní tin u cááyee ki lī yii: «Maa yīntì nì Juuda yab marab ubɔ, kaa tí yīntì nì kunimbɔtidii, kaa yīntì nì gɔminakpaan Seesaar mun.».

eng: Festus, wanting to put the Jews in his debt, asked Paul, Are you willing to go up to Jerusalem to stand trial before me concerning these things.
local: Feestuusi nìn là án mɔ̄ɔkiǹ Juuda yabee, ní u bālifì Pɔɔl yii: «A là kí jɔ̄m̀ Jeerusaleem bí sòoǹ a-bɔti nee m-nimbiinaaa?».

eng: Agrippa said to Paul, You may speak for yourself. So Paul gestured with his hand and began his defense.
local: Niinee ní Agriipa bí Pɔɔl yii: «A ŋūǹ kí nīn sòoǹ tin a cááyee.» Ní Pɔɔl yōoǹ u-ŋal ki jáam̀ samaa, ní ki yōoǹní tin u cááyee ki lī yii.

eng: And thats exactly what I did in Jerusalem. I locked up many of Gods holy people in prison under the authority of the chief priests. When they were condemned to death, I voted against them.
local: Min min m nìn ŋáań Jeerusaleemee dee. Bisaraaŋakpilib nìn tīī-mi mpɔn m cútí bin fōō Yeesu ki kííyee tikpil ki tòó sarkan, ní ŋyunti ŋun bi yaa là kí kpɔ̀-bee, man di nìn lì yii bi lafun máaǹ ŋkúm.

eng: In one synagogue after another—indeed, in all the synagogues—I would often torture them, compelling them to slander God. My rage bordered on the hysterical as I pursued them, even to foreign cities.
local: Ŋyunti sá kamaayee, m nìn dátí bi-tafal akpafidiinni, ki bàà là bí yíí Yeesu. M nìn fōō diŋuul bi-pu an tin lákatì, áli ki nìn cá ntimu mubɔn ki tin páb̀-bi.».

eng: On one such journey, I was going to Damascus with the full authority of the chief priests.
local: «Míǹ pu ní bisaraaŋakpilib nìn tú-mi ki tīī-mi mpɔn, ní m búntì Damaasi.

eng: While on the road at midday, King Agrippa, I saw a light from heaven shining around me and my traveling companions. That light was brighter than the sun.
local: M bí nsanni kuwiŋkuluu ki cée, ní ki ká ŋwalifi ŋubɔ nyánní yilpu, ki wāĺ nyáka nyaka ki jítiǹ ŋwìiń, ki māntìi tú man nì bin nìn nóó ki cá nsanee.

eng: We all fell to the ground, and I heard a voice that said to me in Aramaic, Saul, Saul, why are you harassing me? Its hard for you to kick against a spear.
local: Niinee ní ti-kɔkɔ lítì kitiŋ, ní m gbìl̄ nneen ŋubɔ sòon̄ nì mi eebru sooŋyin yii: <Sɔɔl, Sɔɔl, ba ŋá ní a páb́-mi míǹ? An làá nīn pɔ̄ɔ̄ ki tīī-si á tɔ̄ ipiin pu ŋgbèen.>.

eng: Then I said, Who are you, Lord? The Lord replied, I am Jesus, whom you are harassing.
local: Niinee ní m bālifì yii: <An sá sii ŋma, m-Dindaan?> Ní ti-Dindaan bí-mi yii: <An sá man Yeesu un a páb̀ee!.

eng: Get up! Stand on your feet! I have appeared to you for this purpose: to appoint you as my servant and witness of what you have seen and what I will show you.
local: Ama yīkìí yūĺ a-taaŋi pu. M dɔ́miń kí dū-si kí ŋá m-tɔntɔnliu ní. Sii di làá tùkù bin kíǹee tin a ká m-cee díǹee, nì tin tin m làá dāá tí yàntī á kée.

eng: I will rescue you from your own people and from the Gentiles. I am sending you.
local: M làá dāá tɔ̄-si Juuda yab nì bin kaa sá Juuda yabee cee, ki làá dāá mɔ̄tì a-pu bí taa ŋá-si tiba.

eng: to open their eyes. Then they can turn from darkness to light and from the power of Satan to God, and receive forgiveness of sins and a place among those who are made holy by faith in me.
local: A làá cù kí ŋúbití bi-nimbiliŋ, kí nyāntiń-bi dibɔmbɔndin kí cáań-bi ŋwalifin, kí fōō-bi Sitaan pɔŋŋun kí cáań Unimbɔti cee. Bi yaa fōō-mi ki kííyee ní bí ká bi-kpitii pɔ̄tam nì tipaal tin Unimbɔti làá dāá tīī bin u gāntì bi sá u-yabee.>».

eng: So, King Agrippa, I wasnt disobedient to that heavenly vision.
local: «Míǹ pu ní, ubɔti Agriipa , maa kpēē tin nyánní yilpu m kée fám.

eng: King Agrippa, I consider myself especially fortunate that I stand before you today as I offer my defense concerning all the accusations the Jews have brought against me.
local: «Ubɔti Agriipa, an mɔ̄ǹ-mi díǹ wiiŋŋu tikpil ḿ yūĺ a-nimbiin kí sòoǹ kí nyāntí m-ba tibɔti tin kɔkɔ Juuda yab cáa tūntí m-puee ni.

eng: Instead, I proclaimed first to those in Damascus and Jerusalem, then to the whole region of Judea and to the Gentiles. My message was that they should change their hearts and lives and turn to God, and that they should demonstrate this change in their behavior.
local: M nìn kíĺ ki tùkū Damaasi yab ki wāatì, ní boonee ní m tùkū Jeerusaleem yab, ní ki póotì Juudee tiŋki nib kɔkɔ, nì atimbun ŋin kɔkɔ kíǹee yab, yii bí fá bi-kpitii kí gítiń Unimbɔti cee, kí nīn cáá mbimbim min dàkà yii bi lafun kpántì bi-bee.

eng: Because of this, some Jews seized me in the temple and tried to murder me.
local: Kun di yì-tì ní m nìn bí kunimbɔtidiin Juuda yab dɔ́miń ki cúú-mi ki bàà là kí kpɔ̀-mee dee.

eng: God has helped me up to this very day. Therefore, I stand here and bear witness to the lowly and the great. Im saying nothing more than what the Prophets and Moses declared would happen.
local: Ama Unimbɔti mɔ́tì m-pu áli ki dāa sāā díǹ. M gbíntìi bí ki lèekī m-seerau biwɔfib nì biyidambi nimbiin. Maa ti cáá tiba ki kútì tin tin Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi nì Mooyiisi nìn lī ki yóoǹee pu.

eng: that the Christ would suffer and that, as the first to rise from the dead, he would proclaim light both to my people and to the Gentiles.
local: yii Meesiya làá dāá jī falaa áli kí dāá kpú. Úǹ di dāa sá upeepeekaa un làá fìkitī bitaŋkpiibi kansikin, kí cáań Juuda yab nì bin kaa sá Juuda yabee ŋwalifee.».

eng: At this point in Pauls defense, Festus declared with a loud voice, Youve lost your mind, Paul! Too much learning is driving you mad.
local: Pɔɔl bíi sòoǹ tin u cááyee, ní Feestuusi téeń mpɔn yii: «A wáatì ní, Pɔɔl! A nín bífì tigbɔn tikpil puee di bíil̀ a-yil.».

eng: But Paul replied, Im not mad, most honorable Festus! Im speaking what is sound and true.
local: Ní Pɔɔl kíí-u yii: «Ukpil Feestuusi, maa wáatì. Tibɔti tin m sòoǹee sá ibaamɔn ní ki sá tibɔmɔntiil.

eng: King Agrippa knows about these things, and I have been speaking openly to him. Im certain that none of these things have escaped his attention. This didnt happen secretly or in some out-of-the-way place.
local: Ubɔti béè-tì, míǹ puee ní m ŋūǹ kí sòoǹ u-nimbiin kaa fàŋkí. M nyí yii u nyí tikɔkɔ, kun puee an kaa nìn sá dibalin ní tibɔtee nìn jítì.

eng: King Agrippa, do you believe the prophets? I know you do.
local: Too, ubɔti Agriipa, a fōō ki kíí yii Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi nín nìn sòon̄ tiŋyee sá ibaamɔnaaa? Man nyí yii a fōō ki kíí yii an sá ibaamɔn.».

eng: Agrippa said to Paul, Are you trying to convince me that, in such a short time, youve made me a Christian.
local: Ní Agriipa bí Pɔɔl yii: «An kíǹ fiii, á bàà dū-mi kí kpántí Kriisitoo yɔu!».

eng: Paul responded, Whether it is a short or a long time, I pray to God that not only you but also all who are listening to me today will become like me, except for these chains.
local: Ní Pɔɔl kíí-u yii: «An yaa kíǹ fiiiyaaa, an yaa làá tí yúntáaa, m bàà là Unimbɔti ń ŋá míǹ. Án taa nín̄ sá gba sii baba, ama án nīn sá ki kpáaǹ bin kɔkɔ bíi pílifíǹ-mi díǹ nee, ama bí taa nín bóob́ nimbi nì ikusaka nee ní!».

eng: This is because you understand well all the Jewish customs and controversies. Therefore, I ask you to listen to me patiently.
local: Di sá yii a nyí Juuda yab bimbim nì bi-nikpɔŋkpɔkɔku kɔkɔ pu. Míǹ puee m gbāam̀-si á jī kimɔtii kí pīlifiǹ-mi.».

eng: The king stood up, as did the governor, Bernice, and those sitting with them.
local: Niinee ní ubɔti nì gɔmina nì Beereeniisi nì bin kɔkɔ nìn kā bi-ceeyee.

eng: As they left, they were saying to each other, This man is doing nothing that deserves death or imprisonment.
local: yíkì ki nyáń, ní ki bíi lì yii: «Kijapaai nee kaa yīntì tiba tin máaǹ bí kpɔ̀-ueeyaaa, kaa tí máaǹ bí cáá-u kí ŋá sarka mun.».

eng: Agrippa said to Festus, This man could have been released if he hadnt appealed to Caesar.
local: Ní Agriipa bí Feestuusi yii: «Kijapaai nee yaa kaa nìn bí yii bí cáá-u gɔminakpaan cee, ti bàà ŋūǹ kí gíiǹ-u kí líí.».

eng: Every Jew knows the way of life I have followed since my youth because, from the beginning, I was among my people and in Jerusalem.
local: «Juuda yab kɔkɔ nyí doooo m-naacimpɔntin m nín nìn bí miŋyee. Di sá yii doooo ŋkilkaamee, m nìn bí Jeerusaleem ní, m-yab kansikin.

eng: They have known me for a long time. If they wanted to, they could testify that I followed the way of life set out by the most exacting group of our religion. I am a Pharisee.
local: Bíǹ bi-ba nyí-mi dooooyee. Bi yaa là gba, bi ŋūǹ kí lì yii m nìn sá Fariisa yab bin ŋúb́ marab tiŋanee kansikin ubɔ.

eng: Today I am standing trial because of the hope in the promise God gave our ancestors.
local: Kun di yāntì bi bíi sòoǹ m-bɔti kɔŋkɔnnee di sá m nín máká Unimbɔti nín nìn pōoǹ ti-naanjab puee.

eng: This is the promise our twelve tribes hope to receive as they earnestly worship night and day. The Jews are accusing me, King Agrippa, because of this hope!.
local: Ti-samaau tinaakɔti saalaa nì tilee máká yii bi làá dāá ká an poondi. An pu ní bi pɔ̄ɔntí ki nóó Unimbɔti ŋwiimpu nì kunyeeu. Too, ubɔti, m nín máká tin pu Juuda yab ŋúb́-mee dee.

eng: Why is it inconceivable to you that God raises the dead?.
local: Míǹ puee, ba pu ní nimbi nyí yii Unimbɔti kaa làá dāá fìkitī bitaŋkpiibi ní?.

eng: I really thought that I ought to oppose the name of Jesus the Nazarene in every way possible.
local: Man m-ba nìn dākafì yii an máaǹ ḿ dū iciin kɔkɔ kí já nì Yeesu Naasaareeti yɔu yindi.

eng: When it was determined that we were to sail to Italy, Paul and some other prisoners were placed in the custody of a centurion named Julius of the Imperial Company.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun bi bí yii tí dāá kɔ́ meela kí nīn cá Italiiyee, ní bi dū Pɔɔl nì bisarkadambi biba ki tīī kijapaai kin bi nìn yíì Juliyusee, u sá usoojakpil dikitil din bi nìn yíì «gɔminakpaan kitilee» poon.

eng: Men, I see that our voyage will suffer damage and great loss, not only for the cargo and ship but also for our lives.
local: «Ti-yab, man wāĺ yii ti-saŋŋu nee làá cáań-ti dinimbinaamandi, kun puee meela nì tiwan tin u túee làá ká falaa tikpil, an yaa yíì gba, timbi ti-ba làá kpú.».

eng: But the centurion was persuaded more by the ships pilot and captain than by Pauls advice.
local: Ama usoojakpilee kaa nìn fōō Pɔɔl nín sòon̄ puee ki kíí, ní ki nìn máká un nìn sá ukpil meelanee nì meeladaan pu.

eng: Since the harbor was unsuitable for spending the winter, the majority supported a plan to put out to sea from there. They thought they might reach Phoenix in Crete and spend the winter in its harbor, which faced southwest and northwest.
local: Meela sililaŋki gbanti nín kaa ŋān̄ kuseeunee puee, ní binib tikpil nìn là kí yīkì niin, bi yaan ŋūǹ kí bà meela sililaŋki Feeniisi, Kreeti tiŋki kin nìn túliń ŋwìiń jeetilaŋki púee, kí kàl̄ niin kuseeu ń jītí.

eng: When a gentle south wind began to blow, they thought they could carry out their plan. They pulled up anchor and sailed closely along the coast of Crete.
local: Kubuŋu kubɔ nyánní ŋŋaŋgan pú ki kíĺ ki bíi jītí fiii fiii. Niinee ní bi dākafì yii bi làá ŋmā kí bà laakin bi bàà màkēe, ní ki dáaĺ dikújal din ŋúb́ meela nnyiminee ki cúu kútì, ní ki búntì ki kpākaǹ ki pìlīǹ Kreeti tiŋkugbandi.

eng: Before long, a hurricane-strength wind known as a northeaster swept down from Crete.
local: An kaa yúntì, kpalaayee ní kubuŋkpaaŋŋu kun bi yíì Urakilɔneeyee nyánní Kreeti jooŋŋin ki sīntìń.

eng: The ship was caught in the storm and couldnt be turned into the wind. So we gave in to it, and it carried us along.
local: ki dɔ́miń ki yōoǹ meela. Taa nìn gíti ŋūǹ kí ŋá tiba kí pèefì meela, asee ti nín yāntì ku yōoǹ-ti ki kpáàa búntèe.

eng: After sailing under the shelter of an island called Cauda, we were able to control the lifeboat only with difficulty.
local: Ní ti cūtìi jítì kitimbilii kin bí tiŋku kansikinee bi yíì-kì yii Kooda. Nì falaakpaŋkpaan ní ti dāa nín ŋúń ki cúú buŋalimbu bun nìn nɔ̀b̄ meelee kitiŋ gbanti caŋin.

eng: They brought the lifeboat aboard, then began to wrap the ship with cables to hold it together. Fearing they might run aground on the sandbars of the Gulf of Syrtis, they lowered the anchor and let the ship be carried along.
local: ki dáaliń-bu ki kɔ́ǹ meelan, ní ki dū iŋmii ki bóob́ ki māntìi tú meela taapu. Bi nín nìn fàŋkí yii tí taa cùú lítí Siirtii bul kpaanceetii puee, ní bi sīntì iŋaabu taapu, ní kubuŋkpaaŋŋuee cáá-ti ki cáa nín cá.

eng: We were so battered by the violent storm that the next day the men began throwing cargo overboard.
local: Ku nín gbíntìi mɔ̄tìi páb́-tee pu, ní kutaa wúntèe bi dū tinyɔmpuwan tiba ki tɔ́ tiŋkun.

eng: On the third day, they picked up the ships gear and hurled it into the sea.
local: Winta dalee, ní bíǹ bi-ba dū tin bi cáa kpòotī meelee ki tɔ́ nnyimin.

eng: We boarded a ship from Adramyttium that was about to sail for ports along the coast of the province of Asia. So we put out to sea. Aristarchus, a Macedonian from Thessalonica, came with us.
local: Ní ti kɔ́ meela un nyánní Adramiiti ki làá nīn cá Asii timmu mun kɔkɔ bí tiŋku caŋinee, ní ti yíkì. Ariistaarki un sá Maseeduwaan Teesalooniiki tiŋkinee nìn bí ti-cee.

eng: When neither the sun nor the moon appeared for many days and the raging storm continued to pound us, all hope of our being saved from this peril faded.
local: Iwiin tikpil poonee, taa ti ká ŋwìiń nì iŋmalbijaa, ní kubuŋkpaaŋŋuee gbíntìi jītí iwiin kɔkɔ ki pɔ̄ɔ̄. Niinee taa ti dàkafī gba yii ti làá ŋmátí.

eng: For a long time no one had eaten. Paul stood up among them and said, Men, you should have complied with my instructions not to sail from Crete. Then we would have avoided this damage and loss.
local: Áli an yúntì bin nìn kā meelanee kaa jíń tiba. Niinee ní Pɔɔl yúl̀ bi-kansikin ki bí yii: «Ti-yab, ni yaa nìn pílifiǹ-mee, taa bàà yíkìń Kreeti. Taa bàà kāntí falaa nì asaluu un bí nee.

eng: Now I urge you to be encouraged. Not one of your lives will be lost, though we will lose the ship.
local: Ama kɔŋkɔnnee m mèé-ni, ní pɔ́ɔǹ ni-ba, kun puee ni-kansikin báà ubɔ kaa làá wāŋkì, asee meela baba di làá bīil̀.

eng: Last night an angel from the God to whom I belong and whom I worship stood beside me.
local: Kun puee kunyeeu nee, Unimbɔti un yì-mi m nóó-uee túnní u-tuuŋŋu ŋubɔ m-cee.

eng: The angel said, Dont be afraid, Paul! You must stand before Caesar! Indeed, God has also graciously given you everyone sailing with you.
local: ní ŋu bí-mi yii: <Pɔɔl, taa fàŋkì! Asee á bà gɔminakpaan nimbiin bí sòoǹ a-bɔti. Míǹ puee, Unimbɔti làá ŋá-si tiŋan kí yàntī bin kɔkɔwoooo kā meelanee ń ŋmátí!>.

eng: Be encouraged, men! I have faith in God that it will be exactly as he told me.
local: An puee ti-yab, pɔ́ɔm̀maan ni-ba! Kun puee m cáá dimákal Unimbɔti pu yii u nín lī puee, míǹ ní án làá ŋá.

eng: However, we must run aground on some island.
local: Ama án làá cáá-ti kí kpàkan̄ kitimbilii kiba kin nnyim māntì ki túee kpaanceetiŋu laaba ní.».

eng: On the fourteenth night, we were being carried across the Adriatic Sea. Around midnight the sailors began to suspect that land was near.
local: An nìn bàn̄ ŋwiin ŋubɔ kpá saalku nyeeku dalee, kubuŋu gbanti gbíntìi cáá-ti ki yìiǹ Meediiteeranee tiŋkuu pu. Kunyataasiiuee ní bimeelaŋaaliliibi bíi lì yii ti cīkiǹ kitiŋ kiba.

eng: They dropped a weighted line to take soundings and found the water to be about one hundred twenty feet deep. After proceeding a little farther, we took soundings again and found the water to be about ninety feet deep.
local: Ní bi dū tiŋmantikaal ki tɔ́ tiŋkun, ní ki ká yii nnyim nyóoŋŋu nìn sá meetab bita kpá imuŋku ili. Ti gíti sútì fiiiyee, ki tí dūu tɔ́ mpɔm, ní ki tí ká yii nnyim nyóoŋŋu sá meetab mmuŋku nì binii.

eng: Afraid that we might run aground somewhere on the rocks, they hurled out four anchors from the stern and began to pray for daylight.
local: Niinee ní bi kíǹ ki fàŋkí yii ti-meelau nee ń taa ŋée kí cùú yā ataŋkpee. Míǹ puee ní bi dū akújal anaa ki tɔ́ boon án nīn ŋúb́ meela, ní ki kíǹ ki kā ki pòontí yii kutaa ń wúntí.

eng: The next day we landed in Sidon. Julius treated Paul kindly and permitted him to go to some friends so they could take care of him.
local: Kutaa wúntèe ní ti bàn̄ Siidɔn. Kii Juliyusi nín nìn ŋúb́ Pɔɔl tiŋan puee, u pú-u kinyɔkɔ ú cù u-bɔɔbi cee kí nyàabìń tin u yaa lèe.

eng: The sailors tried to abandon the ship by lowering the lifeboat into the sea, pretending they were going to lower anchors from the bow.
local: Bimeelaŋaaliliibi nìn nyàab̀ kí nyā meelanee, ní bi gíiǹ buŋalimbu bun nìn nɔ̀b̄ meelee ki tɔ́ nnyimin, ní ki mɔ́ntí yii bíǹ bàà là kí cáá akújal aba kí kúĺ nyɔkɔpu mun ní.

eng: Paul said to the centurion and his soldiers, Unless they stay in the ship, you cant be saved from peril.
local: Ní Pɔɔl bí usoojakpilee nì u-soojab yii: «Binib nee yaa kaa kāl̀ meelan soooo, naa làá ŋmátí!».

eng: The soldiers then cut the ropes to the lifeboat and let it drift away.
local: Niinee ní soojab gíí iŋmii in nɔ̀b̄ buŋalimbuee ki tīl̀, ní bu gbíntì nnyimin.

eng: Just before daybreak, Paul urged everyone to eat. He said, This is the fourteenth day youve lived in suspense, and youve not had even a bite to eat.
local: An kíǹ fiii kutaa ń wúntée, ní Pɔɔl mēē bi-kɔkɔ bí dāá jī tijin, ní ki bí-bi yii: «Ŋwiin ŋubɔ kpá saalku sèé nee ni nín kā kaa jīǹ tibee.

eng: I urge you to take some food. Your health depends on it. None of you will lose a single hair from his head.
local: Míǹ puee, m gbàam̄-ni, ní jīmaan tijin, kun puee tijin bɔti páb́ ki tīī ni-maŋfal. Di sá yii ni-kansikin báà ubɔ yikpilku kaa làá wāŋkì.».

eng: After he said these things, he took bread, gave thanks to God in front of them all, then broke it and began to eat.
local: U lī míǹ ki dóòyee, ní ki yōoǹ kpɔnɔ ki jáam̀ Unimbɔti tijin gbanti pu bi-kɔkɔ nimbiin, ní ki gíí kpɔnɔ ki kíĺ ki bíi ŋmóò.

eng: Everyone was encouraged and took some food. .
local: Niinee ní báà ŋma ká kunyannyaau, ki kíĺ ki bíi ŋmóò.

eng: In all, there were two hundred seventy-six of us on the ship.
local: Timbi bin kɔkɔ nìn kā meelanee nìn sá binib kulaafaau nì binaa kpá imuŋku inaa.

eng: When they had eaten as much as they wanted, they lightened the ship by throwing the grain into the sea.
local: Bi jíń ki bál̀ee, ki là yii meela ń taa nín̄ gíti nyìm̀ kí nīn bí fúku fukuee, ní bi dū ŋkpɔnɔyim ki dūu tɔ́ nnyimin.

eng: In the morning light they saw a bay with a sandy beach. They didnt know what land it was, but they thought they might possibly be able to run the ship aground.
local: Kutaa wúntèe, bimeelaŋaaliliibi kaa béè kitiŋ gbanti, ama ní ki ká dibunlɔkɔl diba, digbándi sá dibul ŋma, ní bi bí yii, bi yaan ŋūǹ kí bàà ŋàal̀ meela kí cùnnì niin.

eng: From there we sailed off. We passed Cyprus, using the island to shelter us from the headwinds.
local: Ti dāa yíkì niinee, ní ki dūu dī ki kpàkaǹ Siipiri tiŋki kin dimɔɔl māntì ki túee, kun puee kubuŋu nìn pɔ̄ɔ̄ ní ki túŋkiń-ti.

eng: They cut the anchors loose and left them in the sea. At the same time, they untied the ropes that ran back to the rudders. They raised the foresail to catch the wind and made for the beach.
local: Niinee ní bi cúú iŋmii ki cúu bútì, ní akújalee gbíntì nnyimin, ní bi cúú ŋŋmii ki cúu kútì tiwan tin kpòotī meelee, ní ki kpátì ŋŋaabu ŋun poon kubuŋu kóò ki téekí meelee. Kubuŋu bíi téekí meelee, ní bi dūu dī tiŋku gbandi watil pú.

eng: But they struck a sandbar and the ship ran aground. The bow was stuck and wouldnt move, and the stern was broken into pieces by the force of the waves.
local: Niinee ní meela cūtìi sàl̄ dibukpaandi diba kansikin ki gbíntì niin, ní u-nyɔkɔpu múĺ dibulin, kaa ti lèekī, ní tinyiŋgban dɔ́miń ki fáá ki wīī boon.

eng: The soldiers decided to kill the prisoners to keep them from swimming to shore and escaping.
local: Niinee ní soojab bàà là kí kpɔ̀ bisarkadambi, yii bí taa ŋée kí wāŋkì nnyim kí nyā kí sāǹ kí kátí.

eng: However, the centurion wanted to save Paul, so he stopped them from carrying out their plan. He ordered those who could swim to jump overboard first and head for land.
local: Ama usoojakpilee là kí fīī Pɔɔlee, ní ki yíì yii bí taa ŋá míǹ, ní ki bí yii bin ŋūǹ nnyimee ń péé kí yùkū kí tìin̄ kí wāŋkì kí nīn cá ditiŋkugbandi.

eng: He ordered the rest to grab hold of planks or debris from the ship. In this way, everyone reached land safely.
local: ní bin kíǹee ń nīn cáá idɔ àá ameelakati kí nīn wáŋkí ki pá bi-boon. Míǹ ní ti ŋá, ní ti-kɔkɔ nyáń dibuŋgbándi nì alaafiya.

eng: We sailed across the open sea off the coast of Cilicia and Pamphylia, and landed in Myra in Lycia.
local: Ní ti dūtì tiŋku un bí ki nákáǹ Siilisii nì Paŋfiliiyee, ní ki dūu bàn̄ Miira, Liisii tiŋkin.

eng: There the centurion found an Alexandrian ship headed for Italy and put us on board.
local: Niin ní usoojakpilee nìn ká meela ubɔ nyánní Aleekisandirii ki cá Italii ní u yāntì ti kɔ́ úǹ.

eng: After many days of slow and difficult sailing, we arrived off the coast of Cnidus. The wind wouldnt allow us to go farther, so we sailed under the shelter of Crete off Salmone.
local: Meelee nìn dū iwiin tikpil ki còom̄ soooo soooo nì falaa, ki wāatì ní ki nín bàn̄ ki ŋmāntì nì Kiniidi tiŋki. Kubuŋu nín nìn páb́-ti, taa ŋūǹ kí dī niin kí nīn cá ndanee puee, ní ti dūu dīń Salmoonee watil pú ki dūu bāań Kreeti caŋin.

eng: We sailed along the coast only with difficulty until we came to a place called Good Harbors, near the city of Lasea.
local: Ti bíi kpàkaǹ ki cá nì falaayee, ní ki dūu bàn̄ kitiŋ kin bi yíì Laaseeyee caŋin laakin bi yíì Ditiŋkugbammɔntiilee.

eng: Much time had been lost, and the voyage was now dangerous since the Day of Reconciliation had already passed. Paul warned them.
local: Ti nìn cōom̀ ki yúntì tikpil áli disaŋkindee dāa dūu kpántì mbusu, kun puee Juuda yab nyɔɔboobil wiiŋŋu jítì ní kitiŋ gbanti seeku mun nìn tóobiń. Míǹ puee ní Pɔɔl nìn bí ki kpáafí-bi yii.

eng: After reaching land safely, we learned that the island was called Malta.
local: Ti ŋmátì ki dóòyee, ní ki nín béè yii bi yíì kitiŋ gbanti Maalti.

eng: They honored us in many ways. When we were getting ready to sail again, they supplied us with what we needed.
local: Binibee nìn ŋá-ti tiŋan tikpil, ní daliŋkin ti làá yīkèe, bi nìn tīī-ti tin kɔkɔ ti lèe.

eng: After three months we put out to sea in a ship that had spent the winter at the island. It was an Alexandrian ship with carvings of the twin gods Castor and Pollux as its figurehead.
local: Iŋmal ita boonee ní ti kɔ́ meela un nìn yúl̀ kitiŋ gbantin kuseeu jítèe. U nìn nyánní Aleekisandirii, bi kpítì awaa ali ki nántiǹ atab ki nákáǹ u-pu.

eng: We landed in Syracuse where we stayed three days.
local: Ti bàn̄ Sirakuusi tiŋkinee, ní ki kāl̀ niin iwiin ita.

eng: From there we sailed to Rhegium. After one day a south wind came up, and we arrived on the second day in Puteoli.
local: Niinee, ti yíkì ki kpàkaǹ ditiŋkugbandee, ní ki tin dūu bàn̄ Reejiyoo. Kutaa wúntèe, ní kubuŋu nyánní ti-boon pú ki kíĺ ki bíi jītí, ní ti dū iwiin ili ki dūu bàn̄ Pusɔl.

eng: There we found brothers and sisters who urged us to stay with them for a week. In this way we came to Rome.
local: Kitiŋ gbantin ní ti nìn ká nnabiyaamu mubɔ, ní bi bí yii tí kàl̄ bi-cee kinyɔŋ kiba. Ti nín cōom̀ pu ki dāa bàn̄ Roomee dee.

eng: When the brothers and sisters there heard about us, they came as far as the Forum of Appius and the Three Taverns to meet us. When Paul saw them, he gave thanks to God and was encouraged.
local: Nnabiyaamu mun bí Roomee nín gbìl̄ ti-puee, ní bi yíkìi dɔ́miń áli Apiyusi nyɔŋkin nì kitimbilii kin bi yíì Inicaŋkɔŋkoon itee, ki túŋkì-ti. Pɔɔl nín pée ká-bee, ní u bíi nyɔ́ŋkí Unimbɔti ní ki ká kunyannyaau.

eng: When we entered Rome, Paul was permitted to live by himself, with a soldier guarding him.
local: Ti nín bàn̄ Roomee, ní bi pú Pɔɔl nsan ú nīn kóò u-baba sooja ń nīn cí-u.

eng: Three days later, Paul called the Jewish leaders together. When they gathered, he said, Brothers, although I have done nothing against our people or the customs of our ancestors, Im a prisoner from Jerusalem. They handed me over to the Romans.
local: Iwiin ita boonee, ní Pɔɔl yíiń Juuda yab kpilib bin nìn kóò Roomee bí kpāfì u-cee. Bi dɔ́miń ki kpáfèe, ní u bí-bi yii: «M-nabiyaamu, maa kpáàa ŋá ti-samaau tiba, kaa yīntiǹ ti-naanjab koobiliŋ mun. Ama bi cúú-mi Jeerusaleem, ki dū-mi ki tīī Room yab.

eng: who intended to release me after they examined me, because they couldnt find any reason for putting me to death.
local: Ní bíǹ bālifì-mi anyɔɔbun, ní ki bàà là kí gíiǹ-mi kí líí, kun puee baa ká kun di yì-tì ní bi máaǹ kí kpɔ̀-mee.

eng: When the Jews objected, I was forced to appeal to Caesar. Dont think I appealed to Caesar because I had any reason to bring charges against my nation.
