local: Ama Juuda yab kaa nìn kíí, yii bí taa líí-mi. An di yì-tì ní m nìn bí yii gɔminakpaan ń sòoǹ m-bɔti. Nì míǹ kɔkɔee, maa làá bīil̀ m-yab.

eng: The islanders showed us extraordinary kindness. Because it was rainy and cold, they built a fire and welcomed all of us.
local: Binib bin nìn kóò niinee baasii ŋúb́-ti mmɔntiimin. Utaa nìn kíĺ ki kíǹ ki nì, ní tiwool bí. Míǹ puee bi nìn kpāā ŋŋmi ní ki yíiń-ti ní ti-kɔkɔ dɔ́miń ki kāl̀ ki māntìi tú ŋŋmee ki wùl̀.

eng: This is why I asked to see you and speak with you: its because of the hope of Israel that I am bound with this chain.
local: Kun pu ní m là kí ká-ni kí sòoǹ nì nee dee. Di sá yii dipoondi din pu Isirayeel yab kā ki mákée pu ní ikusaka kā m-ŋaan nee.».

eng: They responded, We havent received any letters about you from Judea, nor have any of our brothers come and reported or said anything bad about you.
local: Ní bi kíí Pɔɔl yii: «Juudee yab kaa ŋmàn̄ní-ti kugbɔŋu kubɔ ki sòon̄ a-bɔti pu tiba. Ti-nabiki kiba mun kaa tí nyánní niin ki dɔ́miń ki máafì-ti a-bɔti tiba. Ubɔ mun kaa tí tùkū-ti yii a ŋá tikpitil tiba.

eng: But we think its important to hear what you think, for we know that people everywhere are speaking against this faction.
local: Míǹ puee, ti bàà là kí béé tin tin a cááyee, kun puee ti nyí yii báà laakiŋyee, binib tikpil kaa là nnimbɔtisan ŋun a nóó nee.».

eng: On the day scheduled for this purpose, many people came to the place where he was staying. From morning until evening, he explained and testified concerning Gods kingdom and tried to convince them about Jesus through appealing to the Law from Moses and the Prophets.
local: Ní bi fātìi yóoǹ-u ŋwiin, ní binib tikpil dāa dɔ́miń ŋwiin gbanti. Pɔɔl nìn kíĺ kutaafaau ki cáa sāā kujoou ki cáá Mooyiisi marab nì Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi gbɔnti ki sòoǹ nì bi Unimbɔti Beel bɔti taapu, ki nyàab̀ bí ŋmā kí fōō Yeesu kí kíí.

eng: Some were persuaded by what he said, but others refused to believe.
local: U nín nìn sòon̄ puee, biba nìn gbìl̄ ki fōō ki kíí, ama biba mun nìn yíì, kaa fōō ki kíí.

eng: They disagreed with each other and were starting to leave when Paul made one more statement: The Holy Spirit spoke correctly when he said to your ancestors through Isaiah the prophet.
local: Kunikpɔŋkpɔkɔu nìn kɔ́ bi-kansikin. Bi làá nīn cée, ní Pɔɔl lī-bi dinyɔɔbundi nee yii: «Tiŋman, an sá ibaamɔn ní Unimbɔti Fam dī u-bɔnaatiliu Yeesaya pu dooooyee ki sòon̄ nì ni-naanjab, yii.

eng: Go to this people and say: You will hear, to be sure, but never understand; and you will certainly see but never recognize what you are seeing. .
local: <Cù binib nee cee kí tùkù-bi yii: Ni làá dāá gbìl̄ nì ni-tafaliŋ ní kaa nyí taapu, ki làá dāá nīn kpèé nì ni-nimbiliŋ ní kaa wāĺ.

eng: This peoples senses have become calloused, and theyve become hard of hearing, and theyve shut their eyes so that they wont see with their eyes or hear with their ears or understand with their minds, and change their hearts and lives that I may heal them. .
local: Di sá yii binib gbanti pɔbiliŋ gbìfì, bi kpáá bi-tafaliŋ ki ŋúb́ bi-nimbiliŋ. Baa là kí nīn wāĺ, kaa là kí nīn gbìl̀, kaa là bi-landɔkɔi mun ń béé taapu, bí bàà gítiń m-cee ḿ cáaǹ-bi!>».

eng: Therefore, be certain of this: Gods salvation has been sent to the Gentiles. They will listen.
local: Ní Pɔɔl gíti kútì-bi yii: «Ní nīn nyímaan yii Unimbɔti tú u-fiil nyɔɔbundi nee bin kaa sá Juuda yabee cee! Bíǹ làá fōō-dì.» .

eng: Paul gathered a bunch of dry sticks and put them on the fire. As he did, a poisonous snake, driven out by the heat, latched on to his hand.
local: Ní Pɔɔl cūtìi báań tikpaŋkpaal dibɔ́ndi diba, ukoowaati kā niin, ki cáań ki tɔ́ ŋŋmin. Niinee ní ŋŋmifaam píĺ ukoowaatee, ní u nyáń ki jú u-ŋal ki báaǹ.

eng: Paul lived in his own rented quarters for two full years and welcomed everyone who came to see him.
local: Pɔɔl nìn kāl̀ abin ali amɔn kudii kun u fōō ki pà ki kóòyee poon, ki nìn fòol̄ bin kɔkɔ dòoń u-ceeyee.

eng: Unhindered and with complete confidence, he continued to preach Gods kingdom and to teach about the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: U nìn sòoǹ Unimbɔti Beel pu, ki dàkà ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo bɔti nì tininjati, ubɔ kaa páb́-u.

eng: When the islanders saw the snake hanging from his hand, they said to each other, This man must be a murderer! He was rescued from the sea, but the goddess Justice hasnt let him live.
local: Binibee nín ká yii ukoobu lúń Pɔɔl ŋal puee, ní bi bíi lì tɔb cee yii: «Uninja nee máaǹ ki làá nīn sá unikpɔl ní. Di sá yii, min kɔkɔwoooo u ŋmátì ki nyáń tiŋkunee, ti-waal deedeedaan kaa pú-u mpaan ú tɔ̄ u-ba.».

eng: Paul shook the snake into the fire and suffered no harm.
local: Niinee ní Pɔɔl gbéeǹ ukoobuee ki fíiǹ ki tɔ́ ŋŋmin, kaa gbìl̄ tiba.

eng: They expected him to swell up with fever or suddenly drop dead. After waiting a long time and seeing nothing unusual happen to him, they changed their minds and began to claim that he was a god.
local: Ní bi kíǹ ki kā ki kpèé yii an làá díkáaa, u làá lítí kí kpú niin niin. Ama bi kāl̀ ki cíitìi kpēē ki gīī, tiba kaa ŋá-uee, ní bi kpántì bi-landɔkɔi, ki kíǹ ki bíi cáá yii an sá diwaal diba ní.

eng: Publius, the islands most prominent person, owned a large estate in that area. He welcomed us warmly into his home as his guests for three days.
local: Niin caŋin ní kitiŋ gbanti bɔtiu un bi yíì Pubiliyusee saaku nìn bí. Kijapaayee nìn fōō-ti mmɔntiimin, ti dōoǹ u-dumpu iwiin ita.

eng: Publius father was bedridden, sick with a fever and dysentery. Paul went to see him and prayed. He placed his hand on him and healed him.
local: Pubiliyusi baa nìn dɔ̄ u-wunti píĺ kpaŋkpam ki cɔ̀tī. Ní Pɔɔl cūtìi māntì u-pu, ní ki mēē ki tīī-u, ní ki dū u-ŋalii ki páaǹ u-pu, ní u pɔ́ɔkì.

eng: Once this happened, the rest of the sick on the island came to him and were healed.
local: Niinee ní bibunliibi bin bí niinee kɔkɔ dɔ́miń ki ká dicáaǹdi.

eng: Peter and John were going up to the temple at three oclock in the afternoon, the established prayer time.
local: Dalba ní Peetroo nì Yoowaneesi nìn nóó ki jɔ́ḿ kunimbɔtidiin tikúti tita wimmeem yuntiŋu.

eng: They recognized him as the same one who used to sit at the temples Beautiful Gate asking for money. They were filled with amazement and surprise at what had happened to him.
local: bi béè-u yii úǹ di nìn kàal̀ kunimbɔtidii punyɔkɔnyaaŋkee nyɔkɔpu ki mèé animbilee. Ní an ŋá-bi maamaaci, bi bākatì kijapaayee nín ká dicáaǹdi puee.

eng: While the healed man clung to Peter and John, all the people rushed toward them at Solomons Porch, completely amazed.
local: Uninjee gbíntìi ŋúb́ Peetroo nì Yoowaneesi ki nóó-bee, binib kɔkɔ bākatì ki sáǹní ki dɔ́miń bi-cee kadantiku un bi yíì Saloomɔn kadantikuuee taapu.

eng: Seeing this, Peter addressed the people: You Israelites, why are you amazed at this? Why are you staring at us as if we made him walk by our own power or piety?.
local: Peetroo ká mimmee, ní ki bí samaayee yii: «Nimbi Isirayeel yab, ba ŋá ní tin bí nee ŋá-ni maamaaci? Ba ŋá ní ni dū ni-nimbikpeeŋu ki báaǹ ti-pu míǹ, a lì kii timbi ti-ba pɔŋŋu àá ti-deedeeu Unimbɔti nimbiin di yāntì uninja nee ká dicáaǹdee?.

eng: The God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob—the God of our ancestors—has glorified his servant Jesus. This is the one you handed over and denied in Pilates presence, even though he had already decided to release him.
local: Di sá Abraam nì Isaaki nì Jaakɔb Nimbɔtiu, ti-naanjab Nimbɔtiuee di ŋá míǹ kí tīī u-tɔntɔnliu Yeesu tinyool. Úǹ ní ni cúú ki tīī Piilati yii naa là-u, bí kpɔ̀-u, ŋyunti ŋun Piilati u-ba bàà nìn là kí gíiǹ-u kí lííyee.

eng: You rejected the holy and righteous one, and asked that a murderer be released to you instead.
local: Ni yíì yii naa là Yeesu un sá circir ki bí deedeeyee, ama ní ki gbāam̀ yii ú gíiǹ unikpɔl kí tīī-ni.

eng: You killed the author of life, the very one whom God raised from the dead. We are witnesses of this.
local: ní ki kūū dimaŋfal Dindaan. Ama Unimbɔti fīkitì-u ditaŋkpiilin, timbi kɔkɔwoooo nìn ká ki sá biseeradambi.

eng: His name itself has made this man strong. That is, because of faith in Jesus name, God has strengthened this man whom you see and know. The faith that comes through Jesus gave him complete health right before your eyes.
local: Uninja un ni wāĺ ki nyí nee, Yeesu un ti fōō ki kííyee yindi pu ní kijapaai nee ká dicáaǹdi. Ti nín fōō-u ki kííyee di yì-tì ní uninja nee pɔ́ɔkì tiŋan ni-kɔkɔ nimbiin nee.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I know you acted in ignorance. So did your rulers.
local: «Kɔŋkɔnnee, m-nabiyaamu, m nyí yii naa nìn nyí ní ki ŋáaǹ tin bí nee, ki kpáaǹ ni-yidambi mun.

eng: But this is how God fulfilled what he foretold through all the prophets: that his Christ would suffer.
local: Ama Unimbɔti nín nìn dī u-bɔnaatiliibi kɔkɔ nyɔmmu pu ki sòon̄ puee, yii Meesiya làá dāá jī falaayee, an lafun ŋá míǹ.

eng: Change your hearts and lives! Turn back to God so that your sins may be wiped away.
local: Míǹ puee, fámaan ni-kpitii kí gítímaan Unimbɔti cee kí wàatī ní ú kpìtī ni-kpitii kɔkɔ.

eng: Meanwhile, a man crippled since birth was being carried in. Every day, people would place him at the temple gate known as the Beautiful Gate so he could ask for money from those entering the temple.
local: Kunimbɔtidiiyee nìn cáá kipunyɔkɔ kin bi nìn yíì yii Kipunyɔkɔnyaaŋŋee. Uninja ubɔ, u nìn sá ufaliŋfaan doooo u-biyatin, ní bi nìn cāabìń-u ki kàantí kutaa sá kamaa Kipunyɔkɔnyaaŋŋee nyɔkɔpu, ú nīn mèé bin jītí ki kóò kunimbɔtidiinee animbil.

eng: Then the Lord will provide a season of relief from the distress of this age and he will send Jesus, whom he handpicked to be your Christ.
local: Niinee, ti-Dindaan làá tīiń-ni diŋuŋfil yuntiŋu, kí tɔ̄ń Yeesu un u lēē yii ú nīn sá Meesiya ki tīī-nee.

eng: Jesus must remain in heaven until the restoration of all things, about which God spoke long ago through his holy prophets.
local: Kun puee u dáá kā Unimbɔti cee kí nīn cí ŋyunti ŋun Unimbɔti làá dāá kpántí tikɔkɔ mpɔm, kii u nín nìn yāntì u-circir bɔnaatiliibi sòon̄ dooooyee puee.

eng: Moses said, The Lord your God will raise up from your own people a prophet like me. Listen to whatever he tells you. .
local: Mooyiisi nìn lī yii: <Ti-Dindaan ni-Nimbɔtiu làá dāá lēē ni-nabiyaamu kansikin ubɔ, ú nīn sá Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu kii mamee. Ní dāá pīlifì tin kɔkɔwoooo u làá sòoǹ nì nee.

eng: Whoever doesnt listen to that prophet will be totally cut off from the people. .
local: Un sá kamaa yaa kaa dāa pílifiǹ Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu gbantee, bi làá dāá nyāntí-u Unimbɔti samaau kansikin kí kpɔ̀-u.>».

eng: All the prophets who spoke—from Samuel forward—announced these days.
local: Ní Peetroo nìkī ki bí yii: «Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi kɔkɔ, bin nìn sòon̄ doooo Samiyeel yuntiŋu ki kpáaǹ bin dāa páaǹní u-boonee, báà ŋma nìn nāatì tibɔti gbanti wiiŋyi pu.

eng: You are the heirs of the prophets and the covenant that God made with your ancestors when he told Abraham, Through your descendants, all the families on earth will be blessed. .
local: Nimbi di sá bin jíń tin Unimbɔti nìn pōoǹ ki tīī u-bɔnaatiliibee, ki fōō u nín nìn pūtì ki pōoǹ tin kí ŋá kí tīī ti-naanjabee mun. Dipoondi gbanti poon ní u lī Abraam yii: <A-dicaŋku pu ní m làá dāá dī kí bìin̄ duulinya timbuliŋ kɔkɔ pu.>.

eng: After God raised his servant, he sent him to you first—to bless you by enabling each of you to turn from your evil ways.
local: Nimbi pu ní Unimbɔti fīkitì u-tɔntɔnliu Yeesu. U dū-u ki túnní ni-cee mpeepeekaam, ú dāan̄ kí ŋá-ni tiŋan kí nyāntí báà ŋma u-tuŋkpitiŋ poon.».

eng: When he saw Peter and John about to enter, he began to ask them for a gift.
local: Uninjee ká Peetroo nì Yoowaneesi cá kí kɔ́ee, ní u bíi mèé-bi animbil.

eng: Peter and John stared at him. Peter said, Look at us.
local: Ní bi dū bi-nimbikpeeŋu ki báaǹ u-pu, ní Peetroo bí yii: «Kpèè doo!».

eng: So the man gazed at them, expecting to receive something from them.
local: Niinee ní u bíi kpèé-bi címmm, ki bàà cí kí fōō tiba bi-cee.

eng: Peter said, I dont have any money, but I will give you what I do have. In the name of Jesus Christ the Nazarene, rise up and walk.
local: Ní Peetroo bí-u yii: «Maa cáá animbil kaa cáá sindaariya, ama tin m cááyee, m làá tīī-si kudɔŋu. Nì Yeesu Kriisitoo Naasaareeti yɔu yindi puee, yīkìí còom̀!».

eng: Then he grasped the mans right hand and raised him up. At once his feet and ankles became strong.
local: Ní Peetroo cúú u-ŋaŋgiil ki dúkutì-u yilpu. Dicilpu niinee, u-taaŋi ŋin fàl̄ee tāntì ki ŋáŋkì.

eng: Jumping up, he began to walk around. He entered the temple with them, walking, leaping, and praising God.
local: ní u yùkū ki yúl̀, ní ki kíǹ ki bíi còom̄. U nì bi kɔkɔ táá ki kɔ́ kunimbɔtidiin, ní u còom̄ ki yùkufī ki tìintí ki nyɔ́ŋkí Unimbɔti.

eng: All the people saw him walking and praising God.
local: Kunipaau kɔkɔ nín ká u còomìń ki nyɔ́ŋkí Unimbɔtee.

eng: While Peter and John were speaking to the people, the priests, the captain of the temple guard, and the Sadducees confronted them.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun Peetroo nì Yoowaneesi nìn dáá bíi sòoǹ nì kunipaauee, ní bisaraaŋaliibi nì Sadusab nì bin cí kunimbɔtidiiyee kpiliu bāań.

eng: If so, then you and all the people of Israel need to know that this man stands healthy before you because of the name of Jesus Christ the Nazarene—whom you crucified but whom God raised from the dead.
local: Míǹ puee, ni-kɔkɔ ń béé tiŋan, ki kpáaǹ Isirayeel yab kɔkɔ, yii an sá Yeesu Kriisitoo Naasaareeti yɔu yindi pu ní u pɔ́ɔkì. An sá u-pɔŋŋu pu ní uninja nee yú ni-nimbiin nì u-gbampɔŋŋu nee. Nimbi di kpáá Yeesu ndɔpuntikaa pu, ama Unimbɔti fīkitì-u ditaŋkpiilin.

eng: This Jesus is the stone you builders rejected; he has become the cornerstone!.
local: Úǹ pu ní an ŋmàn̄ Unimbɔti gbɔŋkun yii: <Ditaŋkpal din nimbi bidimaliibi dūu fée di kpántì ditaŋkpal din ŋúb́ kudii pɔŋŋuee.>.

eng: Salvation can be found in no one else. Throughout the whole world, no other name has been given among humans through which we must be saved.
local: Baa ŋūǹ kí dī ubɔ pu kí ká difiil see Yeesu baba pu. Di sá yii taapu doo Unimbɔti kaa ti tīī ufiil ubɔ un pu timbi binib máaǹ kí dī kí ká difiilee.».

eng: The council was caught by surprise by the confidence with which Peter and John spoke. After all, they understood that these apostles were uneducated and inexperienced. They also recognized that they had been followers of Jesus.
local: Biyidambee nín ká Peetroo nì Yoowaneesi sòoǹ nì tininjatee, an ŋá-bi maamaaci, kun puee bi ká yii bi sá binifantantiibi, kaa nyí kugbɔŋu. Ama bi béè yii bi nì Yeesu di nìn tāatí.

eng: However, since the healed man was standing with Peter and John before their own eyes, they had no rebuttal.
local: Bi nín ká uninja un pɔ́ɔkèe yú bi-ceeyee, baa ti ŋūǹ kí lì tiba.

eng: After ordering them to wait outside, the council members began to confer with each other.
local: Ní bi bí Peetroo nì Yoowaneesi ń nyā kubɔsoondiin kí wàatī-bi, ní ki kíǹ ki bàlifī tɔb yii.

eng: What should we do with these men? Everyone living in Jerusalem is aware of the sign performed through them. Its obvious to everyone and we cant deny it.
local: «Ti làá nín ŋá binib nee mana dee? Kun puee Jeerusaleem yab kɔkɔ ká yii bi ŋá maamaacikpaan ubɔ, timbi ti-ba kaa ti ŋūǹ kí néé.

eng: To keep it from spreading further among the people, we need to warn them not to speak to anyone in this name.
local: Ama, tibɔti gbanti ń taa gbìtī kí cù ndan samaa kɔkɔ ceeyee, tí dū mpɔmpiin kí pìim̄maan-bi nsan bí taa ti pée sòon̄ Yeesu yindi gbanti pu báà ubɔ cee.».

eng: When they called Peter and John back, they demanded that they stop all speaking and teaching in the name of Jesus.
local: Niinee ní bi yíiń-bi ki pīiǹ-bi nsan bí taa ti sòoǹ kí dàkà binib Yeesu gbanti yindi pu báà fiii.

eng: Peter and John responded, Its up to you to determine whether its right before God to obey you rather than God.
local: Ní Peetroo nì Yoowaneesi fātìi kíí-bi yii: «An ŋān̄ Unimbɔti nimbiin yii tí pīlifiǹ nimbi kí yàntī Unimbɔtaaa? Kíímaan-ti!.

eng: They were incensed that the apostles were teaching the people and announcing that the resurrection of the dead was happening because of Jesus.
local: An kaa nìn mɔ́ɔkiǹ-bi, kun puee bi bíi sòoǹ nì samaa Yeesu bɔti ki dàkà yii bitaŋkpiibi làá dāá fìkitī.

eng: As for us, we cant stop speaking about what we have seen and heard.
local: Ama timbee, taa ŋūǹ kí nīn ŋmí kí taa sòoǹ tin tin ti kée nì tin ti gbìl̄ee.».

eng: They threatened them further, then released them. Because of public support for Peter and John, they couldnt find a way to punish them. Everyone was praising God for what had happened.
local: Niinee ní bi fātìi tí kpīiǹ-bi ki gíiǹ-bi ki líì, kaa nyí bi nín làá ŋá pu kí dáaĺ bi-tafalee, kun puee samaa kɔkɔ nìn pàkà Unimbɔti tin bi nìn ŋée pu.

eng: because the man who had experienced this sign of healing was over years old.
local: Uninja un nìn ká maamaaci cáaǹtam gbantee nìn jítì abin imuŋku ili pu.

eng: After their release, Peter and John returned to the brothers and sisters and reported everything the chief priests and elders had said.
local: Bi gíiǹ Peetroo nì Yoowaneesi ki líìyee, ní bi búntì bi-yab cee ní ki bàn̄ ki máafì-bi tin kɔkɔ bisaraaŋakpilib nì bikpilib līń-bee.

eng: They listened, then lifted their voices in unison to God, Master, you are the one who created the heaven, the earth, the sea, and everything in them.
local: Bi gbìl̄ mimmee, bi-kɔkɔ kpáfì dicilpu kinyɔɔbaantii ki mēē Unimbɔti yii: «Ti-Dindaan, sii di náań yilpu nì taapu, kitiŋ nì tiŋku nì tin kɔkɔwoooo pée bée.

eng: You are the one who spoke by the Holy Spirit through our ancestor David, your servant: Why did the Gentiles rage , and the peoples plot in vain? .
local: Sii di yāntì a-Circir Fam dī ti-naanja a-tɔntɔnliu Daafiidi pu ki sòon̄ yii: <Ba ŋá ní atimbun yòontí ŋi-ba yilpu ki sòoǹ tibɔfan míǹ? Ba ŋá ní samaa cáá ilandɔkɔkpiti in kaa làá tɔ̄ ditundee míǹ?.

eng: The kings of the earth took their stand and the rulers gathered together as one against the Lord and against his Christ. .
local: Duulinya bɔtiibi nì biyidambi kpáfì ki bóob́ dikɔ̀ndi kí jáǹ ti-Dindaan Unimbɔti nì ubɔti un u lēēyee.>.

eng: Indeed, both Herod and Pontius Pilate, with Gentiles and Israelites, did gather in this city against your holy servant Jesus, whom you anointed.
local: An lafun ŋá míǹ, yii kitiŋ nee poon Eeroodi nì Pɔnsi Piilati nì binicambi nì Isirayeel yab kpáfì kí jáǹ a-circir tɔntɔnliu Yeesu, un a lēē u sá Meesiyayee.

eng: They did what your power and plan had already determined would happen.
local: Kujaau kun bi jáń nì Yeesuee yāntì bi ŋáaǹ tin kɔkɔwoooo a nìn lī ki yóoǹ a-pɔŋŋu nì a-ciiŋyi pu dooooyee.

eng: Now, Lord, take note of their threats and enable your servants to speak your word with complete confidence.
local: Kɔŋkɔnnee, ti-Dindaan, kpèè bi nín lī yii bi làá ŋá-ti ntoomee puee, kí tūtuǹ timbi a-tɔntɔnliibi tí nīn sòoǹ a-bɔti nì tininjati.

eng: They seized Peter and John and put them in prison until the next day.
local: Ní bi cúú Peetroo nì Yoowaneesi ki cáa tɔ́ sarkan áli kutaa nì ku-wuntil, kun puee an nìn sá kujoou ní.

eng: Stretch out your hand to bring healing and enable signs and wonders to be performed through the name of Jesus, your holy servant.
local: Á tàntī a-ŋal kí dàkà a-pɔŋŋu, bibunliibi ń ká dicáaǹdi, maamaacib nì tidaandi ń ŋá a-circir tɔntɔnliu Yeesu yindi pu.».

eng: After they prayed, the place where they were gathered was shaken. They were all filled with the Holy Spirit and began speaking Gods word with confidence.
local: Bi mēē ki dóòyee, dibɔbilin niinee kitiŋ cīŋkì, ní Circir Fam gbí bi-kɔkɔ mmɔntiim, ní bi kíĺ ki bíi sòoǹ Unimbɔti bɔti, kaa fàŋkí.

eng: The community of believers was one in heart and mind. None of them would say, This is mine! about any of their possessions, but held everything in common.
local: Bin kɔkɔwoooo nìn fōō Yeesu ki kííyee nìn pēē kinyɔkɔ ní bi-pɔbiliŋ nìn sá diba. Ubɔ kaa nìn lì yii tin u cááyee sá u-baba yati, ama báà ŋma wanti nìn sá ki tīī bi-kɔkɔ ní.

eng: The apostles continued to bear powerful witness to the resurrection of the Lord Jesus, and an abundance of grace was at work among them all.
local: Nì mpɔŋkpaan ní Yeesu kpambaliŋ nìn sòoǹ ki lèekī ti-Dindaan Yeesu fìkititam seerau, ní Unimbɔti biindi nìn bí bi-pu tikpil.

eng: There were no needy persons among them. Those who owned properties or houses would sell them, bring the proceeds from the sales.
local: Bi-kansikinee, ubɔ kaa nìn bí ki sá usoodaan, kun puee bin nìn cáá akpaan àá adiinee nìn nyáfí ní ki cāabìń tin bi nyáfèe nimbiliŋ ki bīil̀ Yeesu kpambaliŋ nimbiin.

eng: and place them in the care and under the authority of the apostles. Then it was distributed to anyone who was in need.
local: ní bi yàkatī bi-kansikin báà ŋma nín máaǹ miŋyee.

eng: Joseph, whom the apostles nicknamed Barnabas , was a Levite from Cyprus.
local: Míǹ ní Jooseefi ubɔ nìn bí ki sá Leefii naakookun ní bi màl̄-u Siipiri tiŋkin. Yeesu kpambaliŋ nìn páaǹ-u diyindi yii Baarnaabaasi, an taapu sá yii, un ŋmēentí binib ki tìì kunyannyaauee.

eng: He owned a field, sold it, brought the money, and placed it in the care and under the authority of the apostles.
local: U nìn cáá bukpaa bubɔ ki dūu nyáfì, ní ki cáań animbil ki bíl̀ Yeesu kpambaliŋ nimbiin.

eng: Many who heard the word became believers, and their number grew to about five thousand.
local: Nì míǹ kɔkɔee, bin nìn gbìl̄ bi-nyɔɔbuŋŋi gbantee tikpil nìn fōō ki kíí. Bi-wíiŋŋu nìn bàn̄ kii kutukub biŋmoo nyɔkɔm.

eng: The next day the leaders, elders, and legal experts gathered in Jerusalem.
local: Kutaa wúntèe, Juuda yab yidambi nì bitindaŋkpilib nì bimaradakaliibi kpáfì Jeerusaleem.

eng: along with Annas the high priest, Caiaphas, John, Alexander, and others from the high priests family.
local: Bin nìn bée di sá usaraaŋakpil Aani, nì Kaayiifi nì Yoowaneesi nì Aleekisandir nì bin kɔkɔwoooo nìn sá usaraaŋakpil dicaŋkunee.

eng: They had Peter and John brought before them and asked, By what power or in what name did you do this.
local: Ní bi nyántì Peetroo nì Yoowaneesi ki yóoǹ bi-kansikin ní ki bālifì-bi yii: «Ŋma pɔŋŋu pu nì ŋma yindi pu ní ni ŋá míǹ ní?».

eng: Then Peter, inspired by the Holy Spirit, answered, Leaders of the people and elders.
local: Niinee Unimbɔti Fam kpákatìń Peetroo pu, ní u kíí-bi yii: «Biyidambi nì bikpilib, m ŋá-ni gaafalaa.

eng: are we being examined today because something good was done for a sick person, a good deed that healed him?.
local: Ni bàlifī kí béé díǹ tiŋan tin ní ti ŋá uninja neeyee taapu, nì an nín ŋá pu ní u ká dicáaǹdeeee?.

eng: However, a man named Ananias, along with his wife Sapphira, sold a piece of property.
local: Ama, uninja ubɔ nìn bí, bi yíì-u Ananiyasi. U nì u-nimpuu Safiira nìn kpáfì ki nyáfì bi-kpaatanti.

eng: At that very moment, she dropped dead at his feet. When the young men entered and found her dead, they carried her out and buried her with her husband.
local: Kpalaa niinee, ní u mun lítì Peetroo taaŋi taapu ki kpíì. Binaacimpɔmbi kóońee, ki ká u kpíì, ní bi yōoǹ u mun ki cáa síb́ ki kɔ́ĺ u-cal.

eng: Trepidation and dread seized the whole church and all who heard what had happened.
local: Tifaandi nìn kɔ́ Kriisitoo nikitil kɔkɔ nì bin kɔkɔ nìn pée gbìl̄ an bɔtee.

eng: The apostles performed many signs and wonders among the people. They would come together regularly at Solomons Porch.
local: Yeesu kpambaliŋ nìn ŋáań tidaandi nì maamaacib tikpil samaa kansikin. Bin fōō ki kííyee nìn kpáfí dicilpu dibɔɔbaantiil Saloomɔn kadantikuu taapu.

eng: No one from outside the church dared to join them, even though the people spoke highly of them.
local: Tifaandi nìn kɔ́ bin kaa fōō ki kííyee, ní bi-kansikin báà ubɔ kaa ti là kí sútíí pìl̄-bi, ama nì míǹ kɔkɔee bi nìn pàkà-bi.

eng: Indeed, more and more believers in the Lord, large numbers of both men and women, were added to the church.
local: Bininjab nì binimpoobi bin nìn fòol̄ ti-Dindaan ki kíikée nìn wīikí tikpil, ki gbēentí ŋyunti kamaa, ní ki dūu ŋá dinikitikpaandi.

eng: As a result, they would even bring the sick out into the main streets and lay them on cots and mats so that at least Peters shadow could fall on some of them as he passed by.
local: An yāntì áli gba binib cāabìń bibunliibi ki bīil̀ nlambamu nì tikampati pu adan pu, án nín làá ŋá pu Peetroo yaa jītée, u-jiliŋku kpaŋkpam ń lítí biba puee.

eng: Even large numbers of persons from towns around Jerusalem would gather, bringing the sick and those harassed by unclean spirits. Everyone was healed.
local: Binib tikpil mun nìn nyēeń ntimu mun kaa dátiǹ Jeerusaleemee, ki cāabìń bibunliibi nì bin pu arasiniibi nìn kā bi wáatèe, ní bi-kɔkɔ kāntí dicáaǹdi.

eng: The high priest, together with his allies, the Sadducees, was overcome with jealousy.
local: Niinee usaraaŋakpil nì u-yab kɔkɔ, an dàkà yii Sadusab jandi, kíǹ ki ná Yeesu kpambaliŋ.

eng: They seized the apostles and made a public show of putting them in prison.
local: Bi nìn yíkìi cúú Yeesu kpambaliŋ ki pīiǹ-bi kudii.

eng: An angel from the Lord opened the prison doors during the night and led them out. The angel told them.
local: Ama ní kunyeeuee ti-Dindaan tuuŋŋu ŋubɔ dɔ́miń ki pīitì kusarkadii punyɔmmu ki nyántì-bi, ní ki bí-bi yii.

eng: With his wifes knowledge, he withheld some of the proceeds from the sale. He brought the rest and placed it in the care and under the authority of the apostles.
local: ní ki bātì animbil, ki kíǹ ŋin kíǹee ki cáa bíl̀ Yeesu kpambaliŋ nimbiin. U-nimpuu mun nìn nyí min u-cal ŋée.

eng: Go, take your place in the temple, and tell the people everything about this new life.
local: «Cù kí yūĺmaan kunimbɔtidii poon kí dū dimaŋfal bɔti kɔkɔ kí sòoǹ, samaa ń gbìl̄.».

eng: Early in the morning, they went into the temple as they had been told and began to teach. When the high priest and his colleagues gathered, they convened the Jerusalem Council, that is, the full assembly of Israels elders. They sent word to the prison to have the apostles brought before them.
local: Bi gbìl̄ mimmee, ní ki péé kutaafaau ki kɔ́ kunimbɔtidiin ní ki kíĺ ki bíi dàkà Unimbɔti bɔti. Niinee, usaraaŋakpil nì u-yab bāań ki yíiń bibɔsoonliibi kitil. Bi kpáfì ki tú bí yíiń Yeesu kpambaliŋee kusarkadiin.

eng: However, the guards didnt find them in the prison. They returned and reported.
local: Pilisib bàn̄ee, kaa ká-bi kusarkadiin, ní ki fātìi gítìń ki tùkū-bi yii.

eng: We found the prison locked and well-secured, with guards standing at the doors, but when we opened the doors we found no one inside.
local: «Ti bàn̄ee, kusarkadii pīī kpálkpal, ní soojab yú mpunyɔmu nyɔkɔpui, ní ti cúu pīitèe, báà ubɔ nì ubɔ kpá poon!».

eng: When they received this news, the captain of the temple guard and the chief priests were baffled and wondered what might be happening.
local: Bi gbìl̄ mimmee, ní bin kpèé kunimbɔtidiiyee kpiliu nì bisaraaŋakpilib bākatì nì Yeesu kpambaliŋ nín wáŋkì puee, kun puee tiba yaa làá gíti bàń-bi tibɔti nee poon sɔ̀, baa nyí.

eng: Just then, someone arrived and announced, Look! The people you put in prison are standing in the temple and teaching the people.
local: Ní ubɔ bāań ki bí-bi yii: «Binib bin ni cúú ki tɔ́ sarkanee di yú kunimbɔtidiin ki dàkà samaayee.».

eng: Then the captain left with his guards and brought the apostles back. They didnt use force because they were afraid the people would stone them.
local: Ní bin kpèé kunimbɔtidiiyee kpiliu nì u-pilisib cūtì ki cáań-bi soooo, kaa ŋá-bi mpɔn, kun puee bi fàŋkí samaa ń taa tɔ̄-bi ataŋkpee.

eng: The apostles were brought before the council where the high priest confronted them.
local: Bi cáań-bee, ní ki cáá-bi ki cūnnì bibɔsoonliibi kitil nimbiin. Ní usaraaŋakpil bālifì-bi tibɔti yii.

eng: In no uncertain terms, we demanded that you not teach in this name. And look at you! You have filled Jerusalem with your teaching. And you are determined to hold us responsible for this mans death.
local: «Taa bí-ni yii ní taa ti sòoǹ diyindi din pu ni sòoǹ nee pu kpataaa? Kpèèmaan, díǹ pu nee ni yāntì ni-bɔti gbanti gbītì Jeerusaleem kɔkɔ! Mimmee, ni là timbi ń jī kijapaai nee kpuulool deeyaa!».

eng: Peter and the apostles replied, We must obey God rather than humans!.
local: Ní Peetroo nì Yeesu kpambaliŋ ŋin kíǹee, kíí-u yii: «An máaǹ tí gbìl̄ Unimbɔti nyɔkɔ kí yàntī binib.

eng: Peter asked, Ananias, how is it that Satan has influenced you to lie to the Holy Spirit by withholding some of the proceeds from the sale of your land?.
local: Ní Peetroo bí yii: «Ananiyasi, ba ŋá ní a yāntì Sitaan jíń a-pɔbil poon mpaan míǹ? A mɔ́ntì Unimbɔti Fam ki bātì tikpaatan nimbiliŋ pu.

eng: The God of our ancestors raised Jesus from the dead—whom you killed by hanging him on a tree.
local: Nimbi di kpáá Yeesu ndɔpuntikaa pu ki kūū-u, ní ti-naanjab Nimbɔtiu fīkitì-u.

eng: God has exalted Jesus to his right side as leader and savior so that he could enable Israel to change its heart and life and to find forgiveness for sins.
local: Unimbɔti dū-u ki kāaǹ u-ŋaŋgiil pú, ú nīn sá Ubɔti nì Ufiil ki tīī Isirayeel yab bí ŋmā kí fá bi-kpitii, Unimbɔti ń dūú pɔ̄-bi.

eng: We are witnesses of such things, as is the Holy Spirit, whom God has given to those who obey him.
local: Timbi nìn ká ki sá biseeradambi an bɔti pu, ki kpáaǹ Circir Fam min Unimbɔti tìì bin tɔ́-uee.».

eng: When the council members heard this, they became furious and wanted to kill the apostles.
local: Bi gbìl̄ an bɔtee, ní ki fōō diŋuul ki là kí kpɔ̀ akpambal.

eng: One council member, a Pharisee and teacher of the Law named Gamaliel, well-respected by all the people, stood up and ordered that the men be taken outside for a few moments.
local: Ama Fariisab kansikin ubɔ nìn bí niin, bi yíì-u yii Gamaliyeel , u sá umaradakal un samaa kɔkɔwoooo nìn tɔ́ee. Kijapaayee nìn yáatìi yúl̀ bibɔsoonliibi kitil kansikin ki pú kinyɔkɔ yii bí dá láá nyāntí akpambalee.

eng: He said, Fellow Israelites, consider carefully what you intend to do to these people.
local: Ní u bí-bi yii: «Isirayeel yab, ní kpèèmaan tiŋan nì tin ní ni là kí ŋá nì njapaamu nee!.

eng: Some time ago, Theudas appeared, claiming to be somebody, and some four hundred men joined him. After he was killed, all of his followers scattered, and nothing came of that.
local: An kaa yúntì ní un bi nìn yíì Teedasee yíkì ki yíì u-ba yii u sá unikpaan, ní binib bàn̄ kii tilaafati tili ki nóó-u. Ní bi kūū-u ní bin kɔkɔwoooo nìn nóó-uee yàl̄, báà ubɔ kaa ti kíǹ.

eng: Afterward, at the time of the census, Judas the Galilean appeared and got some people to follow him in a revolt. He was killed too, and all his followers scattered far and wide.
local: Úǹ boonee, ní Juudasi ugaaliileeja mun yíkì ŋyunti ŋun bi kàan̄ binibee, ní ki dáaliń kunipaau ki gíiǹ u-jandi pú. Ní bi kūū u mun ní bin kɔkɔwoooo nìn nóó-uee yàl̄.

eng: Heres my recommendation in this case: Distance yourselves from these men. Let them go! If their plan or activity is of human origin, it will end in ruin.
local: Míǹ puee, tin m cáá ki làá tùkù-nee di sá yii, ní càntīmaan binib nee kí taa ŋá-bi tiba, kun puee atuun ŋin bi ŋáań nee yaa nyánní binib cee, ŋi bí kí dāá kúntí.

eng: If it originates with God, you wont be able to stop them. Instead, you would actually find yourselves fighting God! The council was convinced by his reasoning.
local: Ama an yaa nyánní Unimbɔti cee, naa ŋūǹ kí pìin̄-bi nsan. Ní kpèèmaan tiŋan kí taa jáǹ Unimbɔti kujaau!».

eng: Wasnt that property yours to keep? After you sold it, wasnt the money yours to do with whatever you wanted? What made you think of such a thing? You havent lied to other people but to God.
local: Sii di yì a-kpaatanti, a ŋūǹ kí dū-tì kí yóoń. A nín tí nyáfì-tèe, sii di yì an nimbiliŋ. Kin an ŋá mana ní a yāntì nlandɔkɔkpiti fōō mpaan a-pɔbil poon míǹ? An kaa sá binib ní a mɔ́ntì, an sá Unimbɔti.».

eng: After calling the apostles back, they had them beaten. They ordered them not to speak in the name of Jesus, then let them go.
local: Niinee, bi-kɔkɔ kpáfì ki gbìlīǹ-u ní ki yíiń Yeesu kpambaliŋ, ní ki gbáń-bi anaalab, ní ki bí-bi yii bí taa ti sòoǹ Yeesu yindi pu, ní ki gíiǹ-bi ki líì.

eng: The apostles left the council rejoicing because they had been regarded as worthy to suffer disgrace for the sake of the name.
local: Nì dipɔɔpindi ní Yeesu kpambaliŋ nìn nyáń bibɔsoonliibi kitil nimbiin, kun puee Unimbɔti ŋá-bi tinyool ki yāntì bi jíń falaa Yeesu yindi pu.

eng: Every day they continued to teach and proclaim the good news that Jesus is the Christ, both in the temple and in houses.
local: Ní baabadal bi bí kunimbɔtidiin, nì binib dumpui ki sòoǹ ki dàkà tibɔmɔntiil tin sòoǹ Yeesu, Meesiya puee, kaa gbèentí.

eng: When Ananias heard these words, he dropped dead. Everyone who heard this conversation was terrified.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun Ananiyaasi gbìl̄ mimmee, ní u lítì ki kpíì. Tifaandi nìn kɔ́ bin kɔkɔwoooo gbìl̄ an bɔtee.

eng: Some young men stood up, wrapped up his body, carried him out, and buried him.
local: Ní binaacimpɔmbi yíkì ki dū sata ki bīiǹ-u, ní ki yōoǹ-u ki cáa síb́.

eng: About three hours later, his wife entered, but she didnt know what had happened to her husband.
local: An yúntì ki bàn̄ kii tikúti titee, ní u-nimpuu kóoń, kaa kpáàa béè tin jítèe.

eng: Peter asked her, Tell me, did you and your husband receive this price for the field? She responded, Yes, thats the amount.
local: Ní Peetroo bālifì-u dinyɔɔbundi yii: «Tùkù-mi, ni nín nyáfì tikpaatan puee nimbiliŋ sɔ̀ɔɔ?» Ní u kíí yii: «Iiin, ti nín nyáfì puee dee.».

eng: He replied, How could you scheme with each other to challenge the Lords Spirit? Look! The feet of those who buried your husband are at the door. They will carry you out too.
local: Niinee Peetroo bí-u yii: «Ba ŋá ní sii nì a-cal kpáfì ki cɔ́ŋkì ti-Dindaan Fam míǹ? Kpèè, bin síb́ a-calee di dòoń kupunyɔkɔpuee! Bi làá dāá yòon̄ si mun.».

eng: About that time, while the number of disciples continued to increase, a complaint arose. Greek-speaking disciples accused the Aramaic-speaking disciples because their widows were being overlooked in the daily food service.
local: Iwiin gbanti poonee Yeesu boonnooliibi wíiŋŋu nìn gbéeǹ, ní Juuda yab bin gbìl̀ Greeki sooŋyee bíi gbùkum̄ nì Juuda yab bin pée bí bi-dumpu dooooyee, yii baa tìì bíǹ kpiipoobi tiwan tin bi yàkatī kutaa sá kamaayee kii bi nín tìì bin béeyee puee.

eng: However, they couldnt resist the wisdom the Spirit gave him as he spoke.
local: Ama baa nìn ŋūǹ kí sòoǹ kí líí-u, iciin in Circir Fam nìn yàntī u sòoǹee pu.

eng: Then they secretly enticed some people to claim, We heard him insult Moses and God.
local: Míǹ puee bi tīī njapaamu mubɔ tiba dibalin bí lì yii: «Ti gbìl̄ u sòoǹ tibɔti ki bíiĺ Mooyiisi nì Unimbɔti yindi.».

eng: They stirred up the people, the elders, and the legal experts. They caught Stephen, dragged him away, and brought him before the Jerusalem Council.
local: Ní an cúú kunipaauee poon ki kūtì ki kpáaǹ bitindaŋkpilib nì bimaradakaliibi, ní bi cūtìi cúuń-u mpɔn ki cáań bibɔsoonliibi kitil nimbiin.

eng: Before the council, they presented false witnesses who testified, This man never stops speaking against this holy place and the Law.
local: Ní bi yāntì binib biba mɔ́ntì nnyimɔn u-pu yii: «Uninja nee pée sòoǹ kaa gbèentí ki bíiĺ kunimbɔtidii nee nì Mooyiisi marab.

eng: In fact, we heard him say that this man Jesus of Nazareth will destroy this place and alter the customary practices Moses gave us.
local: kun puee timbi gbìl̄ u bí yii Yeesu Naasaareeti yɔu nee làá kpìtī kunimbɔtidii nee kí kpántí akoobil ŋin Mooyiisi nìn tīī ti-naanjabee.».

eng: Everyone seated in the council stared at Stephen, and they saw that his face was radiant, just like an angels.
local: Bibɔsoonliibi bin nìn kēe kɔkɔ dū bi-nimbikpeeŋu ki báaǹ Eetiyeen pu, ní ki ká yii u-nimbiin kpántì kii Unimbɔti tuuŋŋu nimbiinee.

eng: The Twelve called a meeting of all the disciples and said, It isnt right for us to set aside proclamation of Gods word in order to serve tables.
local: Ní Yeesu kpambaliŋ saalaa nì alee yíiń biboonnooliibi kɔkɔ, ní ki bí-bi yii: «An kaa máaǹ tí yàntī Unimbɔti bɔti disoondi kí nīn kpèé tijin laŋki.

eng: Brothers and sisters, carefully choose seven well-respected men from among you. They must be well-respected and endowed by the Spirit with exceptional wisdom. We will put them in charge of this concern.
local: Míǹ puee, nnabiyaamu, lēēmaan ni-kansikin bininjab biluli bin pu báà ŋma nyí yii bi sá binimɔntiibee nì bin ni Circir Fam gbí tiŋanee, binib bin cáá iciinee, tí dū ditundi gbanti kí ŋúbiǹ-bi.

eng: As for us, we will devote ourselves to prayer and the service of proclaiming the word.
local: Mimmee, timbi kāntí mpaan kí dū ti-yuntiŋu kɔkɔ kí mèè ŋwimmeem kí sòoǹ Unimbɔti bɔti tin sá ti-tundee.».

eng: This proposal pleased the entire community. They selected Stephen, a man endowed by the Holy Spirit with exceptional faith, Philip, Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and Nicolaus from Antioch, a convert to Judaism.
local: Tibɔti gbanti mɔ́ɔkiǹ samaa kɔkɔ, ní bi lēē Eetiyeen, un nìn fōō ki kíí tikpil Circir Fam bí u-ni tiŋan puee, nì Fiiliipi nì Prookɔɔr nì Niikanɔɔr nì Tiimɔn nì Paameenasi nì Niikoolaa un sá Antiyɔɔsi ki fōō Juuda yab marabee.

eng: The community presented these seven to the apostles, who prayed and laid their hands on them.
local: Bi cáań-bi ki dàkā Yeesu kpambaliŋ, ní akpambalee mēē Unimbɔti ki tīī-bi, ní ki dū bi-ŋalii ki páaǹ bi-pu.

eng: Gods word continued to grow. The number of disciples in Jerusalem increased significantly. Even a large group of priests embraced the faith.
local: Unimbɔti nyɔɔbundi mɔ̀tīi gbìtī ajan kɔkɔ, ní Yeesu boonnooliibi mɔ̀tīi wīintí tikpil Jeerusaleem tiŋkin, ki kpáaǹ bisaraaŋaliibi tikpil nìn fòol̄ Yeesu bɔti ki kíikí.

eng: Stephen, who stood out among the believers for the way Gods grace was at work in his life and for his exceptional endowment with divine power, was doing great wonders and signs among the people.
local: Unimbɔti nìn pú Eetiyeen diyimɔɔl nì mpɔn tikpil, ní u nìn ŋáań maamaacikpaambi nì tidaandi samaa kansikin.

eng: Opposition arose from some who belonged to the so-called Synagogue of Former Slaves. Members from Cyrene, Alexandria, Cilicia, and Asia entered into debate with Stephen.
local: Dikitil din bi nìn yíì iyumbu in bi líìyee kitilee nib nìn yíkì ki bíi kpākantí Eetiyeen. Bi nìn sá Juuda yab bin nyánní Siireen nì Aleekisandirii nì Siilisii nì Asiiyee ní.

eng: The high priest asked, Are these accusations true.
local: Ní usaraaŋakpil bālifì-u yii: «An sá míǹ tiŋmanaaa?».

eng: and rescued him from all his troubles. The grace and wisdom he gave Joseph were recognized by Pharaoh, king of Egypt, who appointed him ruler over Egypt and over his whole palace.
local: ki nyántì-u u-falaau kɔkɔ poon, ní ki tīī-u iciin ki yāntì u-bɔti fáatì Faaroo Eejipiti bɔtiu, ní Faaroo dū-u ki kāaǹ ú nīn sá gɔmina Eejipiti tiŋki kɔkɔ pu, ki kpáaǹ úǹ ubɔti cindi.

eng: A famine came upon all Egypt and Canaan, and great hardship came with it. Our ancestors had nothing to eat.
local: Niinee ní ŋkùm miba nyáń Eejipiti nì Kanaan timmu kɔkɔn. An nìn sá falaakpaan ní. Ti-naanjab kaa nìn kāntí tiba kí jī.

eng: When Jacob heard there was grain in Egypt, he sent our ancestors there for the first time.
local: Jaakɔb nín nìn gbìl̄ yii tijikaal bí Eejipitee, ní u tú ti-naanjab mpeepeekaam niin.

eng: During their second visit, Joseph told his brothers who he was, and Pharaoh learned about Josephs family.
local: Nliliitiimee ní Jooseefi yāntì u-nabiyaamu béè-u, ní Faaroo béè kudicaŋu kun poon ní Jooseefi sée.

eng: Joseph sent for his father Jacob and all his relatives—seventy-five in all—and invited them to live with him.
local: Niinee ní Jooseefi tú bí cáań u-baa Jaakɔb nì u-samaau kɔkɔ: bi nìn sá binib biŋmoo kpá imuŋku inaa.

eng: So Jacob went down to Egypt, where he and our ancestors died.
local: Jaakɔb cūtì Eejipitee, ní ki tin kpíì niin. Míǹ ní ti-naanjab mun kpíì niin.

eng: Their bodies were brought back to Shechem and placed in the tomb that Abraham had purchased for a certain sum of money from Hamors children, who lived in Shechem.
local: Ní bi yōoǹ bi-wunti ki cáa cūnnì Siikeem, ki tin síb́ dikaakul din Abraam nìn dá nì animbil Amɔɔr biyaamu cee Siikeemee.

eng: When it was time for God to keep the promise he made to Abraham, the number of our people in Egypt had greatly expanded.
local: «Ŋyunti ŋun an tóob́ Unimbɔti ń ŋá kii u nín nìn pōoǹ pu ki tīī Abraamee, ní Isirayeel yab būtì ki wíiǹ Eejipiti tiŋkin.

eng: But then another king rose to power over Egypt who didnt know anything about Joseph. .
local: Ní ubɔtipɔɔn kāl̀, kaa nyí Jooseefi.

eng: He exploited our people and abused our ancestors. He even forced them to abandon their newly born babies so they would die.
local: U dū iciin ki ŋúń ti-naanjab, ní ki cáá-bi ki sàantí, áli ki yāntì bi nyāntí bi-biyaamu ki féeń mu kúù.

eng: Stephen responded, Brothers and fathers, listen to me. Our glorious God appeared to our ancestor Abraham while he was still in Mesopotamia, before he settled in Haran.
local: Ní Eetiyeen kíí-u yii: «Nimbi m-baabi nì m-nabiyaamu, m ŋá-ni gaafalaa. Pīlifìmaan! Unimbɔti tinyooldaan dɔ́miń ti-naanja Abraam cee ŋyunti ŋun u dáá bí Meesoopootamiiyee, ki wāatì ní u nín kɔ́ Kaaran.

eng: Thats when Moses was born. He was highly favored by God, and for three months his parents cared for him in their home.
local: An yuntiŋu ní bi nìn màl̄ Mooyiisi un nìn ŋān̄ Unimbɔti ceeyee, ní bi kpíiǹ-u iŋmal ita u-baa diiku poon.

eng: After he was abandoned, Pharaohs daughter adopted and cared for him as though he were her own son.
local: Bi dāa nyántì-u ki ŋman dūu fée, ní Faaroo bisal píí-u ki ŋúb́-u kii úǹ u-ba bikee.

eng: Moses learned everything Egyptian wisdom had to offer, and he was a man of powerful words and deeds.
local: An di yì-tì ní Mooyiisi bífì Eejipiti yab ciiŋyi kɔkɔ, ki nìn pɔ̄ɔ̄ dibɔsoondin ki kpáaǹ atuunni.

eng: When Moses was years old, he decided to visit his family, the Israelites.
local: «U bàn̄ abin imuŋku ilee, ní an dɔ́miń u-pɔbil poon yii ú cù kí màntì u-nabiyaamu Isirayeel yab pu.

eng: He saw one of them being wronged so he came to his rescue and evened the score by killing the Egyptian.
local: Niin ní u ká Ueejipitija ubɔ bí ki sàantí u-nabiki kiba, ní u mɔ́tì ki kútì u-nabiki kin bi bíi sàantée pu, ní ki kūū Ueejipitijee ki tɔ̀n̄ ipɔntoon.

eng: He expected his own kin to understand that God was using him to rescue them, but they didnt.
local: U bàà nyí yii u-nabiyaamu làá béé yii úǹ pu ní Unimbɔti làá dī kí fīī-bi, ama baa ŋúń ki béè.

eng: The next day he came upon some Israelites who were caught up in an argument. He tried to make peace between them by saying, You are brothers! Why are you harming each other.
local: Kutaa wúntèe, u ká bi-ŋmaniŋma kansikin bili bíi jáà, ní u bíi sɔ̀ŋkī bi-pɔbiliŋ.

eng: The one who started the fight against his neighbor pushed Moses aside and said, Who appointed you as our leader and judge? .
local: ní ki sáŋkì-bi yii: <M-bɔɔbi, ni sá nnabiyaamu ní. Ba ŋá ní ni sàantí tɔb míǹ?>.

eng: Are you planning to kill me like you killed that Egyptian yesterday? .
local: Ní un bíi sàantí u-jutee téé Mooyiisi ní ki fātìi bālifì-u yii: <Ŋma di kāaǹ-si ti-kpiliu nì ti-bɔsoonliu? A là kí kpɔ̀-mi kii a nín fu kūū Ueejipitija puee níii?>.

eng: When Moses heard this, he fled to Midian, where he lived as an immigrant and had two sons.
local: An bɔti puee, Mooyiisi sáǹ ki cūtìi kíǹ ki kóò Madiyan tiŋkin. Niin ní u màl̄ mbininjabiyaamu muli.

eng: God told him, Leave your homeland and kin, and go to the land that I will show you.
local: ní ki bí-u yii: <Nyā a-tiŋkin nì a-dicaŋkun kí cù kitiŋ kin m làá dàkà-see ni.>.

eng: Forty years later, an angel appeared to Moses in the flame of a burning bush in the wilderness near Mount Sinai.
local: «Abin imuŋku ili boonee, ní Unimbɔti tuuŋŋu dɔ́miń u-cee kisugbooi kin wù bìlbilee poon kuteeun, dijool din bi yíì Siinaayee caŋin.

eng: Enthralled by the sight, Moses approached to get a closer look and he heard the Lords voice.
local: Mooyiisi ká mimmee, ní an ŋá-u maamaaci. U sútì ki pìl̄ kí mɔ̀tīí kpèèyee, ní u gbìl̄ Unimbɔti neen lī yii.

eng: I am the God of your ancestors, the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. Trembling with fear, Moses didnt dare to investigate any further.
local: <Man di sá a-naanjab Nimbɔtiu, Abraam nì Isaaki nì Jaakɔb Nimbɔtiu!> Ní Mooyiisi wunti kíǹ ki bíi jìkì, ní waa ti là kí kpèè niin.

eng: The Lord continued, Remove the sandals from your feet, for the place where you are standing is holy ground. .
local: Ní ti-Dindaan bí-u yii: <Tákatí a-naatakaŋ, kun puee kitiŋ kin pu a yú nee sá circir tiŋki ní.

eng: I have clearly seen the oppression my people have experienced in Egypt, and I have heard their groaning. I have come down to rescue them. Come! I am sending you to Egypt .
local: M ká ki gíti ká falaa un poon ní m-samaau bí Eejipitee, ki gbìl̄ bi-yaliŋkii mun pu, ní m kpákatìń kí fīī-bi. Kɔŋkɔnnee, nín̄ cá, m làá tɔ̄-si Eejipiti.>.

eng: This is the same Moses whom they rejected when they asked, Who appointed you as our leader and judge? This is the Moses whom God sent as leader and deliverer. God did this with the help of the angel who appeared before him in the bush.
local: «Mooyiisi un Isirayeel yab nìn yíì ki bí yii: <Ŋma kāaǹ-si á nīn sá ti-kpiliu nì ti-bɔsoonliuee?> úǹ ní Unimbɔti túnní bi-cee ú nīn sá bi-bɔtiu kí nīn sá bi-fiiliu, ní ntuun ŋun u nìn ká kisugbooyinee nìn bí ki tútúǹ-u.

eng: This man led them out after he performed wonders and signs in Egypt at the Red Sea and for forty years in the wilderness.
local: Úǹ di ŋáaǹ maamaacib nì tidaandi Eejipiti tiŋkin ki nyántì-bi niin, ki gíti ŋáaǹ maamaacib nì tidaandi Tikati mɔɔl poon, ki kpáaǹ kuteeun, laakin bi ŋá abin imuŋku ilee.

eng: This is the Moses who told the Israelites, God will raise up for you a prophet like me from your own people. .
local: Mooyiisi gbanti ubaanti tùkū Isirayeel yab yii: <Unimbɔti làá dāá lēē ni-nabiyaamu kansikin ubɔ, ú nīn sá Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu kii mamee.>.

eng: This is the one who was in the assembly in the wilderness with our ancestors and with the angel who spoke to him on Mount Sinai. He is the one who received life-giving words to give to us.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun samaa nìn kpáfì kuteeunee, Mooyiisi di nìn sí ntuun ŋun nìn sòoǹ nì u Siinaayi jool pu nì ti-naanjab kansikinee. Mooyiisi di nìn fōō dimaŋfal nyɔɔbuŋŋi Unimbɔti cee ki cáań ki tīī-ti.

eng: Hes also the one whom our ancestors refused to obey. Instead, they pushed him aside and, in their thoughts and desires, returned to Egypt.
local: «Ama ti-naanjab nìn yíì, kaa là kí pīlifiǹ-u. Bi-landɔkɔi gítì Eejipiti watil.

eng: So Abraham left the land of the Chaldeans and settled in Haran. After Abrahams father died, God had him resettle in this land where you now live.
local: Ní u nyáń Kaldee tiŋkin ní ki cūtì ki kóò Kaaran tiŋkin. U-baa kúm boonee ní Unimbɔti cáań-u kitiŋ kin poon ni bí nee.

eng: They told Aaron, Make us gods that will lead us. As for this Moses who led us out of Egypt, we dont know whats happened to him! .
local: Ní bi bí Aarɔn yii: <Mā-ti awaa ŋin làá nīn līī ti-nyɔkɔpuee, kun puee Mooyiisi un nyántìń-ti Eejipiti tiŋkinee, taa nyí kun di bāań-uee.>.

eng: Thats when they made an idol in the shape of a calf, offered a sacrifice to it, and began to celebrate what they had made with their own hands.
local: Ŋyunti gbantee ní bi mɔ́ɔ́ dinabil, ní ki ŋá saraa ki tīī-dì ní ki bí ki jīǹ jiŋaal kun bíǹ bi-ba mɔ́ɔ́ nì bi-ŋaliiyee pu.

eng: So God turned away from them and handed them over to worship the stars in the sky, just as it is written in the scroll of the Prophets: Did you bring sacrifices and offerings to me for forty years in the wilderness, house of Israel? .
local: Niinee, Unimbɔti fātì u-boon ki tīī-bi, ki dū-bi ki líiǹ iŋmalbijaa, bí nīn tòotī-i, kii Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi nín nìn ŋmàn̄ bi-gbɔnti poon puee yii: <Unimbɔti bí yii, Isirayeel yab, man ní ni nìn ŋá saraabi ki tīī abin imuŋku ili ŋin ni nìn ŋá kuteeuneeee?.

eng: No! Instead, you took the tent of Moloch with you, and the star of your god Rephan, the images that you made in order to worship them. Therefore, I will send you far away, farther than Babylon. .
local: Aayee, kun puee ni nìn tūntí diwaal Mooleeki waadiiku kun ni gāā nì satee, nì diwaal din bi yíì Rafanee ŋmalbijau. An waaŋi ŋin ni nìn máń ki jáaḿee puee, m làá cáá-ni Babiloon tiŋkin kuyuŋu.>.

eng: The tent of testimony was with our ancestors in the wilderness. Moses built it just as he had been instructed by the one who spoke to him and according to the pattern he had seen.
local: Kuteeunee, ti-naanjab nìn cáá tanti un poon ataŋkpee ali ŋin pu marab nìn ŋmàn̄ ki kēe bée. Di sá yii Unimbɔti nìn tùkū Mooyiisi yii ú gāā tanti kii un u dàkā-uee nín bí puee ní Mooyiisi lafun ŋá míǹ.

eng: In time, when they had received the tent, our ancestors carried it with them when, under Joshuas leadership, they took possession of the land from the nations whom God expelled. This tent remained in the land until the time of David.
local: Boonee, ti-naanjab fōō tantee bi-baabi cee ki kpáàa nóó Joosuwa ki kɔ́ ntimu mun nibuliŋ Unimbɔti nìn jìn̄ bi-nyɔkɔpuee poon. Niin ní tanti gbanti yúl̀ ki dāa sāā Daafiidi yuntiŋu.

eng: God approved of David, who asked that he might provide a dwelling place for the God of Jacob.
local: Daafiidi gbanti bɔti nìn fáatì Unimbɔti pɔbil, ní u mēē Unimbɔti yii ú càntī ú mā ŋkɔŋkoon kí tīī úǹ, bi-naanja Jaakɔb Nimbɔtiu.

eng: But it was Solomon who actually built a house for God.
local: Asee Saloomɔn di dāa máń kunimbɔtidii gbanti.

eng: However, the Most High doesnt live in houses built by human hands. As the prophet says.
local: «Ama yilpu Nimbɔtiu kaa kóò adiin ŋin binib cáá bi-ŋalii ki māāyee. A lì kii Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi nín nìn lī puee, yii.

eng: Heaven is my throne, and the earth is my footstool. What kind of house will you build for me, says the Lord, or where is my resting place? .
local: <Ti-Dindaan bí yii: Yilpu sá m-beejal, ní kitiŋ sá m-taaŋi siinlaŋki, kudii kulɔkɔ ní ni làá gíti mā-mi, àá mpaan ŋulɔŋu ní m làá dāá kàl̄ kí ŋùŋfì?.

eng: God didnt give him an inheritance here, not even a square foot of land. However, God did promise to give the land as his possession to him and to his descendants, even though Abraham had no child.
local: Waa nìn tīī-u kitiŋ nee poon dijandi báà diba, áli gba waa nìn cáá tikpaatan wāā ki bàn̄ u-tabɔbil. Unimbɔti pōoǹ-u yii u làá dū kitiŋ gbanti kí tīī-u, án kpántí úǹ nì u-naantiibi kɔkɔ wanti. Míǹ gbee, Abraam kaa nìn dáá cáá kibiki.

eng: Didnt I make all these things with my own hand? .
local: An kaa sá mama m-ba di náań tin bí nee kɔkɔɔɔ?>».

eng: You stubborn people! In your thoughts and hearing, you are like those who have had no part in Gods covenant! You continuously set yourself against the Holy Spirit, just like your ancestors did.
local: Eetiyeen nìkī ki lī yii: «Nimbi binib nee yiliŋ pɔ̄ɔ̄, ni-pɔbiliŋ dátiǹ Unimbɔti, naa là kí pīlifiǹ-u mun. Baabadal ni jáà nì Circir Fam, kii ni-naanjab nín nìn ŋáań puee.

eng: Was there a single prophet your ancestors didnt harass? They even killed those who predicted the coming of the righteous one, and youve betrayed and murdered him!.
local: Bíǹ di nìn sāaǹ Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi kɔkɔ, ki kūū bin nìn sòon̄ ki yóoǹ yii Deedeedaan làá dāá dāan̄ee. Kɔŋkɔnnee ni nyáfì Yeesu Deedeedaanee ki kūū-u.

eng: You received the Law given by angels, but you havent kept it.
local: Nimbi di fōō Unimbɔti tuuŋŋu cee marab ki mun di kaa là kí ŋúb́-bi.».

eng: Once the council members heard these words, they were enraged and began to grind their teeth at Stephen.
local: Bibɔsooŋkpaambee gbìl̄ mimmee, an wùn̄-bi, ní bi fōō Eetiyeen pu diŋuul tikpil ní bi-nyiŋŋi gà tɔb kàkaka.

eng: But Stephen, enabled by the Holy Spirit, stared into heaven and saw Gods majesty and Jesus standing at Gods right side.
local: Ama Circir Fam gbí Eetiyeen, ní u dū u-nimbikpeeŋu ki báaǹ yilpu, ki ká Unimbɔti nyooti ní Yeesu yú Unimbɔti ŋaŋgiil pú.

eng: He exclaimed, Look! I can see heaven on display and the Human One standing at Gods right side.
local: Ní u bí yii: «Kpèèmaan, m ká yilpu cúu pīitì, ní Yeesu Kinibiki yú Unimbɔti ŋaŋgiil pú!».

eng: At this, they shrieked and covered their ears. Together, they charged at him.
local: Ní bi yíkìi tátí mpɔn ki kpáà bi-tafaliŋ, ní bi-kɔkɔ yíkìń u-pu kílkil.

eng: threw him out of the city, and began to stone him. The witnesses placed their coats in the care of a young man named Saul.
local: ki cúú-u ki dáaĺ-u ki nyántì kitiŋ kpaanceetiŋu, ní ki yáŋkì-u ataŋkpee ki kūū. Bin nìn lēē u-pu seerakpitee nìn cáań bi-bɔkutiŋ ki bíl̀ unaacimpɔɔn un bi nìn yíì Sɔɔlee cee.

eng: As they battered him with stones, Stephen prayed, Lord Jesus, accept my life.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun bi bíi yāŋkí Eetiyeen ataŋkpeeyee, ní u bíi mèé yii: «Ti-Dindaan Yeesu, fōō m-naŋki!».

eng: God put it this way: His descendants will be strangers in a land that belongs to others, who will enslave them and abuse them for four hundred years. .
local: Unimbɔti bí-u yii: <A-naantiibi làá nīn sá binicambi kitiŋ kiban, kí kpántí iyumbu, bí sàaǹ-bi áli abin tilaafati tili.>.

eng: Falling to his knees, he shouted, Lord, dont hold this sin against them! Then he died.
local: Niinee ní Eetiyeen gbāaǹ ki téeń mpɔn yii: «Ti-Dindaan, taa dū bi-kpitii nee kí dáaĺ bi-tafal!» U lī míǹ ki dóòyee, ní ki sīntì.

eng: And I will condemn the nation they serve as slaves, God said, and afterward they will leave that land and serve me in this place.
local: Unimbɔti gíti lī-u yii: <Ama kitiŋ kin yab làá dāá sàaǹ-bi mimmee, mama m-ba di làá dāá dáaĺ bi-tafal. Boonee, a-naantiibee làá dāá nyā kitiŋ gbantin kí dāan̄ doo kí nīn nyɔ́ŋkí-mi.>.

eng: God gave him the covenant confirmed through circumcision. Accordingly, eight days after Isaacs birth, Abraham circumcised him. Isaac did the same with Jacob, and Jacob with the twelve patriarchs.
local: Niinee Unimbɔti tīī-u digiil marau din sá u-poondi daaŋkuee. Míǹ puee Abraam màl̄ Isaakee, ní ki gíí-u dikpancandi iwiin inii dal, ní Isaaki mun gíí Jaakɔb, ní Jaakɔb gíí ti -naanjab bin sá tinaakɔti saalaa nì tilee.

eng: Because the patriarchs were jealous of Joseph, they sold him into slavery in Egypt. God was with him, however.
local: «Ti-naanjabee nìn ná Jooseefi, ní ki dū-u ki nyáfì bí cáá-u kí cùnnì Eejipiti. Ama Unimbɔti nìn bí u-cee.

eng: Saul was in full agreement with Stephens murder. At that time, the church in Jerusalem began to be subjected to vicious harassment. Everyone except the apostles was scattered throughout the regions of Judea and Samaria.
local: Sɔɔl úǹ nìn ká yii Eetiyeen lafun máaǹ ŋkúm gbanti. Ŋwiin gbantee, dinimbinaamaŋkpaandi nìn jéetì Kriisitoo nikitil din bí Jeerusaleemee pu. Bi-kɔkɔ yàl̄ ki búntì Juudee nì Samaarii tiŋgbammun, see Yeesu kpambaliŋ baba di nìn kíǹ.

eng: Everyone, from the least to the greatest, gave him their undivided attention and referred to him as the power of God called Great.
local: Binikpaambi nì mbiyaamu kɔkɔ nìn pílifíǹ-u tiŋan, ní ki lì yii: «Unil nee di sá Unimbɔti pɔŋŋu ŋun bi yíì mpɔŋkpaanee.».

eng: He had their attention because he had baffled them with sorcery for a long time.
local: Bi nìn pílifíǹ-u tiŋan kun puee u nìn cáá u-kpaŋkpantiiku ki ŋáań maamaacib an yúntì.

eng: After they came to believe Philip, who preached the good news about Gods kingdom and the name of Jesus Christ, both men and women were baptized.
local: Fiiliipi nìn sòon̄ tibɔmɔntiil tin sá Unimbɔti Beel nì Yeesu Kriisitoo bɔtee, ní bi fōō u-bɔti ki kíí, ní bininjab nì binimpoobi yāntì bi fùl̄-bi Unimbɔti nyim.

eng: Even Simon himself came to believe and was baptized. Afterward, he became one of Philips supporters. As he saw firsthand the signs and great miracles that were happening, he was astonished.
local: Siimɔn u-ba fōō ki kíí. Bi fùl̄-u ki dóòyee, waa ti là kí sútí kí wàatī Fiiliipi. U nín ká Fiiliipi ŋáań tidaandi nì maamaacikpaŋkpaambee, ní u bākatì tikpil.

eng: When word reached the apostles in Jerusalem that Samaria had accepted Gods word, they commissioned Peter and John to go to Samaria.
local: Yeesu kpambaliŋ bí Jeerusaleem ki gbìl̄ yii Samaarii yab fōō Unimbɔti bɔti ki kííyee, ní bi lēē Peetroo nì Yoowaneesi ki túnní bi-cee.

eng: Peter and John went down to Samaria where they prayed that the new believers would receive the Holy Spirit. .
local: Bi bāań Samaarii yab ceeyee, ní ki mēē ki tīī-bi bí ŋmā kí fōō Circir Fam.

eng: This was because the Holy Spirit had not yet fallen on any of them; they had only been baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
local: kun puee Circir Fam kaa nìn dáá kpákatìń Samaarii yab báà ubɔ pu. Bi nìn pée fùl̄-bi nnyim ti-Dindaan Yeesu yindi ŋmaniŋma pu ní.

eng: So Peter and John laid their hands on them, and they received the Holy Spirit.
local: Ní Peetroo nì Yoowaneesi dū bi-ŋalii ki páaǹ Samaarii yab pu, ní bi fōō Circir Fam.

eng: When Simon perceived that the Spirit was given through the laying on of the apostles hands, he offered them money.
local: Siimɔn nín ká yii akpambal dū bi-ŋalii ki páaǹ bi-pu bi fōō Circir Famee, ní u tīī Peetroo nì Yoowaneesi animbil.

eng: He said, Give me this authority too so that anyone on whom I lay my hands will receive the Holy Spirit.
local: ní ki bí-bi yii: «Tīīmaan ma mun an pɔŋŋu, un pu ní m yaa dū m-ŋalii ki páaǹee, ú fōō Circir Fam.».

eng: Some pious men buried Stephen and deeply grieved over him.
local: Bininjab bin nìn nóó Unimbɔti deedeeyee di nìn síb́ Eetiyeen ní ki súń u-pu tikpil.

eng: Peter responded, May your money be condemned to hell along with you because you believed you could buy Gods gift with money!.
local: Ní Peetroo fātìi kíí-u yii: «A nín dākafì yii bi dù animbil kí dā Unimbɔti pɔl puee, si nì a-nimbiliŋee kɔkɔ ń ŋá fám!.

eng: You can have no part or share in Gods word because your heart isnt right with God.
local: Aa pée cáá tiba nì tiba tin bíi jītí nee poon. Kun puee a-pɔbil kaa bí deedee Unimbɔti nimbiin.

eng: Therefore, change your heart and life! Turn from your wickedness! Plead with the Lord in the hope that your wicked intent can be forgiven.
local: Fá a-landɔkɔkpitii kí mèè ti-Dindaan, an yaa yíì min min a dākafì a-pɔbilinee u làá dūú pɔ̄-si.

eng: for I see that your bitterness has poisoned you and evil has you in chains.
local: kun puee m wāĺ a-poon tū kii tiloolee ní ikpiti kusakai bá-si.».

eng: Simon replied, All of you, please, plead to the Lord for me so that nothing of what you have said will happen to me.
local: Ní Siimɔn kíí-bi yii: «Gbàam̀maan ti-Dindaan ni-ba kí tīī-mi, án nín làá ŋá pu tin kɔkɔwoooo ni sòon̄ nee tiba ń taa bàń-mee!».

eng: After the apostles had testified and proclaimed the Lords word, they returned to Jerusalem, preaching the good news to many Samaritan villages along the way.
local: Peetroo nì Yoowaneesi lēekì seerab ki sòon̄ Unimbɔti bɔti ki dóò Samaariiyee, ní ki fātìi nín gítí Jeerusaleem ki sòoǹ Tibɔmɔntiil Samaarii timbilimmu tikpil poon.

eng: An angel from the Lord spoke to Philip, At noon, take the road that leads from Jerusalem to Gaza.
local: Ní ti-Dindaan tuuŋŋu ŋubɔ tùkū Fiiliipi yii: «Yīkì kí túĺ ŋgan pú kí dī nsan ŋun sìntī Jeerusaleem ki cá Gaasaayee, nsan ŋun baa ti dèe.».

eng: So he did. Meanwhile, an Ethiopian man was on his way home from Jerusalem, where he had come to worship. He was a eunuch and an official responsible for the entire treasury of Candace.
local: Ní u yíkìi búntì. Niinee, Ueetiiyoopiija ubɔ u sá ufaal ki bí Eetiiyoopii nimpubɔtiu Kandaasi cee ki sá ukàlinjakpaan. Úǹ di nìn kpèé unimpubɔtee pijawanti kɔkɔ.

eng: He was reading the prophet Isaiah while sitting in his carriage.
local: U nìn dɔ́miń Jeerusaleem ki jáam̀ Unimbɔti ki dóòyee ki fàtīi kùǹ. U nìn kā u-tɔrɔkuu pu itaamu dátí-u ní ki kàaǹ Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu Yeesaya gbɔŋku poon.

eng: The Spirit told Philip, Approach this carriage and stay with it.
local: Ní Unimbɔti Fam tùkū Fiiliipi yii: «Còom̀ kí kíntí tɔrɔku nee!».

eng: Saul began to wreak havoc against the church. Entering one house after another, he would drag off both men and women and throw them into prison.
local: Sɔɔl úǹ là kí kúntí Kriisitoo nikitilee, ní ki kóò adiinni ki cútí bininjab nì binimpoobi kɔkɔ ki cáǹ ki tòó sarkan.

eng: Running up to the carriage, Philip heard the man reading the prophet Isaiah. He asked, Do you really understand what you are reading.
local: Ní Fiiliipi sáǹ ki bàn̄ee, ki gbìl̄ kijapaayee bíi kàaǹ Yeesaya Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu gbɔŋku poon. Ní u bālifì-u yii: «A gbìl̀ a nín kàaǹ puee taapuɔɔɔ?».

eng: The man replied, Without someone to guide me, how could I? Then he invited Philip to climb up and sit with him.
local: Ní u kíí-u yii: «M yaa kaa ká ubɔ ń tùkù-mi taapu, m làá ŋá mana kí gbìl̄?» Ní u mēē Fiiliipi ń jɔ̄m̀ kí kàl̄ u-cee tɔrɔkun.

eng: This was the passage of scripture he was reading: Like a sheep he was led to the slaughter and like a lamb before its shearer is silent so he didnt open his mouth. .
local: Unimbɔti gbɔŋku jandi din u nìn bíi kàaǹee nín tùkù-ti puee sèé: «Bi cáá-u ki cūnnì kii bi nín cáǹ upii kí kpɔ̀ puee, kii bi nín cēē dipibil kuti di ŋmí kaa sūǹ puee, míǹ ní waa nìn wāaǹ ki lī tiba.

eng: In his humiliation justice was taken away from him. Who can tell the story of his descendants because his life was taken from the earth? .
local: U sīntì u-ba taapu, baa pɔ̀n̄-u. Ŋma di làá dāá ŋmā kí tùkù u-maaliŋu yab tin jítèe, kun puee bi lēē u-maŋfal duulinyan.».

eng: The eunuch asked Philip, Tell me, about whom does the prophet say this? Is he talking about himself or someone else.
local: Ní ukàlinjee bí Fiiliipi yii: «M gbàam̄-si, ŋma pu ní Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu sòon̄ míǹ ní? Úǹ u-ba pu ní u sòon̄ mimmaaa, ubɔ pu ní?».

eng: Starting with that passage, Philip proclaimed the good news about Jesus to him.
local: Niinee ní Fiiliipi yōoǹ dinyɔɔbundi ki kíĺ ki bíi sòoǹ ki tɔ́kɔ́ǹ dijandi din u kàan̄ee pu ki bíi tùkù-u Yeesu Bɔmɔntiiti.

eng: As they went down the road, they came to some water. The eunuch said, Look! Water! What would keep me from being baptized.
local: Bi còom̄ ki cée, ki bàn̄ laaba ki ká nnyim, ní ukàlinjee bí yii: «Kpèè, nnyim sèé nee. Ba di gíti lìkintí-mi bí taa fàl̀-mi Unimbɔti nyim?» .

eng: He ordered that the carriage halt. Both Philip and the eunuch went down to the water, where Philip baptized him.
local: Ní u bí yii bí yóoń tɔrɔku, ní u nì Fiiliipi liitil nín sìntī ŋkpinni, ní Fiiliipi tin fùl̄-u Unimbɔti nyim.

eng: When they came up out of the water, the Lords Spirit suddenly took Philip away. The eunuch never saw him again but went on his way rejoicing.
local: Bi nyánní nnyiminee, Unimbɔti Fam yōoǹ Fiiliipi. Ní ukàlinjee kaa ti ká-u, ama ní ki nìkī u-saŋŋu nì dipɔɔpindin.

eng: Those who had been scattered moved on, preaching the good news along the way.
local: Bin nìn yàl̄ee nìn cá ajan sá kamaa ki sòoǹ Meesiya bɔmɔntiiti.

eng: Philip found himself in Azotus. He traveled through that area, preaching the good news in all the cities until he reached Caesarea.
local: Fiiliipi, úǹ tin nyáń Asooti. Niinee u sòon̄ tibɔmɔntiil ki kpáaǹ ntimbilimu mun poon u jītée ki tin bàn̄ Seesaaree.

eng: Philip went down to a city in Samaria and began to preach Christ to them.
local: Fiiliipi nìn sīntì Samaarii tiŋkin ki bíi sòoǹ Kriisitoo bɔti.

eng: The crowds were united by what they heard Philip say and the signs they saw him perform, and they gave him their undivided attention.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun bi gbìl̄ Fiiliipi nyɔɔbundi, ki wāĺ maamaacib bin u nìn ŋáańee, samaa kɔkɔ nìn pílifí tin u sòoǹee tiŋan.

eng: With loud shrieks, unclean spirits came out of many people, and many who were paralyzed or crippled were healed.
local: Kun puee arasiniibi nìn gbáà ibiil ki nyēē biwaatiibi tikpil pu, ní bifaliŋfaambi nì bideembi tikpil kántì dicáaǹdi.

eng: There was great rejoicing in that city.
local: Ní dipɔɔpiŋkpaandi nìn jéetì kitiŋ gbanti poon.

eng: Before Philips arrival, a certain man named Simon had practiced sorcery in that city and baffled the people of Samaria. He claimed to be a great person.
local: Uninja ubɔ nìn bí kitiŋ gbantin, bi yíì-u Siimɔn. Kijapaayee nìn sá ukpaŋkpanti ki yāntì an ŋáań Samaarii yab maamaaci u-kpaŋkpantiiku pu, ki yíì u-ba yii unikpaan.

eng: Meanwhile, Saul was still spewing out murderous threats against the Lords disciples. He went to the high priest.
local: Ŋyunti gbantee, Sɔɔl kaa cāntì ti-Dindaan boonnooliibi nnikpaakoon. U nìn cūtì usaraaŋakpil cee.

eng: In Damascus there was a certain disciple named Ananias. The Lord spoke to him in a vision, Ananias! He answered, Yes, Lord.
local: Ama Yeesu boonnooliu ubɔ nìn bí Damaasi, bi yíì-u yii Ananiyasi. Ti-Dindaan nyántì u-ba ki dàkā-u, ní ki yíiń-u yii: «Ananiyasi!» Ní u kíí yii: «M sèé, m-Dindaan.».

eng: The Lord instructed him, Go to Judas house on Straight Street and ask for a man from Tarsus named Saul. He is praying.
local: Ní ti-Dindaan bí-u yii: «Yīkìí dī didandi din bi yíì didancancaalee kí nyàab̀ Juuda dumpu un bi yíì Sɔɔl u sá Taarsee. Sɔɔl gbanti bíi mèé ŋwimmeem.

eng: In a vision he has seen a man named Ananias enter and put his hands on him to restore his sight.
local: ní m yāntì u ká sii Ananiyasi kóoń ki dū a-ŋalii ki páaǹ u-pu ú ŋmā kí wāl̀.».

eng: Ananias countered, Lord, I have heard many reports about this man. People say he has done horrible things to your holy people in Jerusalem.
local: Ní Ananiyasi kíí-u yii: «M-Dindaan, m gbìl̄ binib tikpil cee tikpitil tin uninja nee ŋá a-yab bin bí Jeerusaleemee pu.

eng: Hes here with authority from the chief priests to arrest everyone who calls on your name.
local: U dɔ́miń doo ki cáá mpɔn ŋun bisaraaŋakpilib tīiń-u ú nīn cáa bōob̀ bin kɔkɔwoooo nóó-see.».

eng: The Lord replied, Go! This man is the agent I have chosen to carry my name before Gentiles, kings, and Israelites.
local: Ní ti-Dindaan bí-u yii: «Nín̄ cá, kun puee uninja gbanti di sá un m lēē ú lēē seera m-pu bin kaa sá Juuda yabee nì bi-bɔtiibi, nì Isirayeel yab kɔkɔ nimbiinee.

eng: I will show him how much he must suffer for the sake of my name.
local: Ní man m-ba ń dāá dàkà-u falaa un u làá jī m-yindi puee.».

eng: Ananias went to the house. He placed his hands on Saul and said, Brother Saul, the Lord sent me—Jesus, who appeared to you on the way as you were coming here. He sent me so that you could see again and be filled with the Holy Spirit.
local: Niinee, Ananiyasi búntì ki bàn̄ ki kɔ́ kudicaŋuee ni, ki dū u-ŋalii ki páaǹ Sɔɔl pu, ní ki bí yii: «M-nabiki Sɔɔl, ti-Dindaan di túnní-mi. Úǹ di sá Yeesu un a nìn ká nsanni a nín nìn dòoń puee. U là á wāl̀, kí gbí Circir Fam.».

eng: Instantly, flakes fell from Sauls eyes and he could see again. He got up and was baptized.
local: Dicilpu niin ní tiba nyánní u-nimbiliŋin ki lítì kii ijɔmpɔkɔee, ní u wál̀. Ní u yíkì bi fùl̄-u Unimbɔti nyim.

eng: After eating, he regained his strength. He stayed with the disciples in Damascus for several days.
local: Ní u jíń tijin ki fātìi ká mpɔn. Ní Sɔɔl kāl̀ Damaasi iwiŋkaaŋkaa Yeesu boonnooliibi cee.

eng: seeking letters to the synagogues in Damascus. If he found persons who belonged to the Way, whether men or women, these letters would authorize him to take them as prisoners to Jerusalem.
local: ki mēē-u yii ú tīī-u tigbɔn ú cáá cùnnì Damaasi kpafidiiŋŋi kpilib cee, án nín làá ŋá pu u yaa ká biba niin bin nóó nsan gbantee, uninjeeee, unimpueeee, ú cúú-u kí bóob́ kí cáań Jeerusaleemee.

eng: Right away, he began to preach about Jesus in the synagogues. He is Gods Son, he declared.
local: ní ki kpáàa kíĺ ki sòoǹ Yeesu pu akpafidiinni, yii u sá Unimbɔti Jipɔɔn.

eng: Everyone who heard him was baffled. They questioned each other, Isnt he the one who was wreaking havoc among those in Jerusalem who called on this name? Hadnt he come here to take those same people as prisoners to the chief priests.
local: An nìn ŋá bin kɔkɔwoooo nìn bíi pílifíǹ-uee bitii, ní bi lì yii: «Kaa sá un nìn bí Jeerusaleem ki páb̀ bin nóó Yeesuee deeee? Waa tí dɔ́miń doo kí cūtì-bi kí bóob́-bi kí cáá-bi bisaraaŋakpilib nimbiin deeee?».

eng: But Saul grew stronger and stronger. He confused the Jews who lived in Damascus by proving that Jesus is the Christ.
local: Ŋyunti gbantee, Sɔɔl nìn mɔ̀tīi kāntí mpɔn, ki sòoǹ ki pɔ́ɔkíǹ Juuda yab bin bí Damaasee, ki dàkà-bi yii Yeesu lafun sá Meesiya.

eng: After this had gone on for some time, the Jews hatched a plot to kill Saul.
local: Iyunti tikpil jítèe, ní Juuda yab kpáfì kinyɔkɔ ki là kí kpɔ̀-u.

eng: However, he found out about their scheme. They were keeping watch at the city gates around the clock so they could assassinate him.
local: Ama Sɔɔl béè tin bi là kí ŋée. Bi nìn dòoń ki nākantí ki cīití-u Damaasi tiŋki gundi punyɔmmu nyɔkɔpui ŋwiimpu nì kunyeeu kí ŋmā kí kpɔ̀-u.

eng: But his disciples took him by night and lowered him in a basket through an opening in the city wall.
local: Kunyeeuee, bin dī Sɔɔlee dū ŋŋmii ki kúĺ kukabiku ki dū Sɔɔl ki tākaǹ, ki dū-u ki nāatì digundi, ní u tin tīiǹ kitiŋ ki nyáń ki búntì.

eng: When Saul arrived in Jerusalem, he tried to join the disciples, but they were all afraid of him. They didnt believe he was really a disciple.
local: Niinee, Sɔɔl cūtì Jeerusaleem ki bàà là kí dū u-ba kí kɔ́ǹ Yeesu boonnooliibin. Ama bi-kɔkɔ nìn fàŋkí-u, kaa nyí yii u kpántì Yeesu boonnooliu.

eng: Then Barnabas brought Saul to the apostles and told them the story about how Saul saw the Lord on the way and that the Lord had spoken to Saul. He also told them about the confidence with which Saul had preached in the name of Jesus in Damascus.
local: Ní Baarnaabaasi cáá-u ki cūnnì Yeesu kpambaliŋ cee ki máafì-bi Sɔɔl nín nìn ká ti-Dindaan nsanni, u sòon̄ nì u puee, nì min Sɔɔl nìn sòon̄ Damaasi ki cántì ki pɔ̀n̄ Yeesuee.

eng: After this, Saul moved freely among the disciples in Jerusalem and was speaking with confidence in the name of the Lord.
local: U nì bi nìn kíǹ ki yìiǹ Jeerusaleem, ní u sòoǹ ti-Dindaan pu, kaa fàŋkí.

eng: He got into debates with the Greek-speaking Jews as well, but they tried to kill him.
local: Ní u nì Juuda yab bin gbìl̀ Greeki sooŋyee sòoǹ ki kpākantí tɔb, ní bíǹ nìn kíǹ ki nyàab̀ kí kpɔ̀-u.

eng: During the journey, as he approached Damascus, suddenly a light from heaven encircled him.
local: U yíkì u-saŋkindi pu ki tóob́ Damaasee, kpalaayee, ŋwalifi ŋubɔ nyánní yilpu ki māntìi tú-u ki jìliŋkī.

eng: When the family of believers learned about this, they escorted him down to Caesarea and sent him off to Tarsus.
local: Nnabiyaamu mun kíǹee gbìl̄ mimmee, ní ki yōoǹ-u ki cūnnì Seesaaree, ní ki yāntì u búntì Taarsi.

eng: Then the church throughout Judea, Galilee, and Samaria enjoyed a time of peace. God strengthened the church, and its life was marked by reverence for the Lord. Encouraged by the Holy Spirit, the church continued to grow in numbers.
local: Kriisitoo nikitil din nìn bí Juudee nì Galiilee nì Samaariiyee nìn kíǹ ki bí nsɔŋfin ki báaǹ inyaan ki fàŋkí ti-Dindaan, kaa là kí yìntīǹ-u. Ní bi-wíiŋŋu nìn gbēentí mun, kun puee Circir Fam nìn bí bi-cee.

eng: As Peter toured the whole region, he went to visit Gods holy people in Lydda.
local: Peetroo nìn yìiǹ niin timmu kɔkɔn ki màntī Kriisitoo yab pu, ní ki kpáàa sīntì bin nìn kóò Liida tiŋkinee cee.

eng: There he found a man named Aeneas who was paralyzed and had been confined to his bed for eight years.
local: ní ki tin ká uninja ubɔ, bi yíì-u Eenee. U nìn sá ufaliŋfaan ki dɔ̄ u-lambaŋki pu abin anii.

eng: Peter said to him, Aeneas, Jesus Christ heals you! Get up and make your bed. At once he got up.
local: Ní Peetroo bí-u yii: «Eenee, Yeesu Kriisitoo cáaǹ-si! Yīkìí kpáb́ a-lambaŋki.» Ní u yáatìi yúl̀ dicilpu niin.

eng: Everyone who lived in Lydda and Sharon saw him and turned to the Lord.
local: Bin kɔkɔwoooo nìn kóò Liida nì Saarɔnee nìn ká-u, ní ki kpántì bi-ba ki kíǹ ki nóó ti-Dindaan.

eng: In Joppa there was a disciple named Tabitha . Her life overflowed with good works and compassionate acts on behalf of those in need.
local: Unimpu ubɔ nìn bí Joopee ki nóó Yeesu, bi yíì-u Tabiita, - greeki sooŋyi poonee bi yíì-u Dɔɔrkaasi. - U nìn ŋáań binib tiŋan tikpil, ní ki pūǹ biwɔfib animbil mun.

eng: About that time, though, she became so ill that she died. After they washed her body, they laid her in an upstairs room.
local: Ŋyunti gbantee, u búŋkì ní ki kpíì. Bi fùl̄-u ki dóòyee, ki cáá-u ki jɔ́m̀ diditɔntɔkɔl pu ki bíl̀.

eng: Since Lydda was near Joppa, when the disciples heard that Peter was there, they sent two people to Peter. They urged, Please come right away.
local: Joopee nì Liida kaa nìn dátiǹ tɔb. Yeesu boonnooliibi gbìl̄ yii Peetroo bí Liida, ní bi tú bininjab bili u-cee bí cùú gbàamìń-u yii ú dāan̄ bi-cee malaa malaa.

eng: Peter went with them. Upon his arrival, he was taken to the upstairs room. All the widows stood beside him, crying as they showed the tunics and other clothing Dorcas made when she was alive.
local: Ní Peetroo yíkì dicilpu, u nì bininjabee kɔkɔ búntì. Bi bàn̄ee, ní bi cáá-u ki jɔ́m̀ diditɔntɔkɔl pu. Bikpiipusoodambi kɔkɔ nìn dɔ́miń ki māntì ki gɔ́ń Peetroo ki sūǹ ki dàkà Peetroo Dɔɔrkaasi nín nìn dáá bí ki lù abɔkuti nì satab biŋyee.

eng: He fell to the ground and heard a voice asking him, Saul, Saul, why are you harassing me.
local: ní u lítì kitiŋ. Niinee ní u gbìl̄ nneen ŋubɔ bí-u yii: «Sɔɔl, Sɔɔl, ba ŋá ní a páb́-mi míǹ?».

eng: Peter sent everyone out of the room, then knelt and prayed. He turned to the body and said, Tabitha, get up! She opened her eyes, saw Peter, and sat up.
local: Ní Peetroo nyántì binib kɔkɔ ní ki gbāaǹ ki bíi mèé, ní ki fātì utaŋkpiil pu ki bí yii: «Tabiita, yīkìm̄!» Ní u ŋúbitì u-nimbiliŋ ki kpēē Peetroo, ní ki yáatìi kāl̀.

eng: He gave her his hand and raised her up. Then he called Gods holy people, including the widows, and presented her alive to them.
local: Ní Peetroo tāntì u-ŋal ki cúú-u ki yáatì-u ki yóoǹ, ní ki yíiń Kriisitoo yab nì bikpiipusoodambee, ki dū un fīkitèe ki dūu dàkā-bi.

eng: The news spread throughout Joppa, and many put their faith in the Lord.
local: Joopee yab kɔkɔ gbìl̄ an bɔtee, ní binib tikpil fōō ti-Dindaan ki kíí.

eng: Peter stayed for some time in Joppa with a certain tanner named Simon.
local: Peetroo nìn kāl̀ Joopee ki yúntì ugbanjɔb un bi nìn yíì Siimɔŋyee cee.

eng: Saul asked, Who are you, Lord? I am Jesus, whom you are harassing, came the reply.
local: Ní u bālifì yii: «An sá sii ŋma ní, m-Dindaan?» Ní ti-Dindaan kíí-u yii: «An sá man Yeesu un a páb̀ee.

eng: Now get up and enter the city. You will be told what you must do.
local: Yīkìí kɔ́ kitiŋin. Niin ní bi làá tùkù-si min sá á ŋée.».

eng: Those traveling with him stood there speechless; they heard the voice but saw no one.
local: Tifaandi kɔ́ bininjab u nì bin nìn nóóyee. Bi yúl̀, kaa ti ŋūǹ kí wàan̄ kí lì tiba, kun puee bi gbìl̄ nneen pu ki mun kaa wāĺ báà ubɔ.

eng: After they picked Saul up from the ground, he opened his eyes but he couldnt see. So they led him by the hand into Damascus.
local: Ní Sɔɔl yáatìi yúl̀ ki ŋúbitì u-nimbiliŋee kaa ti wāĺ. Ní bi cúú u-ŋal ki dātì-u ki cáa cūnnì Damaasi.

eng: For three days he was blind and neither ate nor drank anything.
local: Ní u kāl̀ iwiin ita, kaa wál̀, kaa jíń, kaa nyùn̄ mun.

eng: From Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by Gods will, and Timothy our brother.
local: Man Pɔɔl un Unimbɔti là ḿ nīn sá Yeesu Kriisitoo kpambalee nì ti-nabiki Tiimootee di ŋmàn̄ kugbɔŋu nee.

eng: Were praying this so that you can live lives that are worthy of the Lord and pleasing to him in every way: by producing fruit in every good work and growing in the knowledge of God.
local: Ú yàntī ni-bimbim ń nīn nóó nì bin sá ti-Dindaan yabee bimbim kí ŋmā kí fáatí-u ŋyunti sá kamaa. Míǹ ní ni làá nīn lòoǹ abimɔnti ŋin sá ní nīn tūǹ atummɔnti kɔkɔee, kí nīn mɔ̀tīi béetí Unimbɔti.

eng: by being strengthened through his glorious might so that you endure everything and have patience.
local: ú nīn tìì-ni mpɔn ŋyunti sá kamaa, kii u nín sá ipɔn nì tinyooldaan puee. Mpɔn gbanti làá yàntī ní nīn jīǹ kimɔtii ki pɔ̄ɔntí ŋyunti sá kamaa.

eng: and by giving thanks with joy to the Father. He made it so you could take part in the inheritance, in light granted to Gods holy people.
local: Ní nīn jáaḿ ti-Baa Unimbɔti dipɔɔpindi poon u nín yāntì ní dāá ŋmā kí ká tin u síiǹ kí tīī u-yab u-dumpu, ŋwalifi laŋkee.

eng: He rescued us from the control of darkness and transferred us into the kingdom of the Son he loves.
local: Di sá yii u nyántì-ti dibɔmbɔndi pɔŋŋun ki cáań-ti u-Jipɔnneeŋkaau beel poon.

eng: He set us free through the Son and forgave our sins.
local: U-Jipɔɔnee di yāntì Unimbɔti fīī-ti ki líì, ki dū ti-kpitii ki pú-ti.

eng: The Son is the image of the invisible God, the one who is first over all creation.
local: Úǹ di sá Unimbɔti un baa wāĺee naŋku, ní ki līī tin kɔkɔwoooo Unimbɔti náańee nyɔkɔpu.

eng: Because all things were created by him: both in the heavens and on the earth, the things that are visible and the things that are invisible. Whether they are thrones or powers, or rulers or authorities, all things were created through him and for him.
local: Di sá yii tiwan tin kɔkɔ bí yilpuee nì tin bí taapuee, tin bi wāĺee nì tin baa wāĺee, an yaa sá ituun in jīǹ abeeyee nì in sá bikpilibeeyeeee, an yaa tí sá in cáá mpɔneeyeeee, Unimbɔti nìn dī u-Jipɔɔnee pu ní ki náań tíǹ kɔkɔ. U-Jipɔɔnee di náań tikɔkɔ ki yì an kɔkɔ mun.

eng: He existed before all things, and all things are held together in him.
local: Úǹ di nìn bí ní boonee tiwan kɔkɔ dɔ́miń. Úǹ di yàntī tiwan kɔkɔ bí ti-laŋki.

eng: He is the head of the body, the church, who is the beginning, the one who is firstborn from among the dead so that he might occupy the first place in everything.
local: Úǹ di sá u-nikitil yil ní u-nikitil sá u-wunti. Úǹ di tìì dimaŋfal. Bin fīkitì ditaŋkpiilinee kansikinee, úǹ di sá ukpil, án nín làá ŋá pu ú kpántí upeepeekaa tikɔkɔ poonee.

eng: Because all the fullness of God was pleased to live in him.
local: Di sá yii Unimbɔti lēē yii u-Jipɔɔnni ní u làá nīn bí mmɔmin.

eng: To the holy and faithful brothers and sisters in Christ in Colossae. Grace and peace to you from God our Father.
local: Ti ŋmàn̄-ku kí tīī nimbi bin sá Unimbɔti yab bin bí Kooloosi, ki kpántì ti-nabimɔntiimu Kriisitoo puee ní. Ti-Baa Unimbɔti ń ŋá-ni tiŋan kí tīī-ni ŋgbansɔŋfi.

eng: and he reconciled all things to himself through him— whether things on earth or in the heavens. He brought peace through the blood of his cross.
local: U bóob́ kí dī u-pu u nì duulinya kɔkɔ ń mɔ̄ɔkì. U-Jipɔɔnee nín kpíì ndɔpuntikaa pu puee di cáań ŋgbansɔŋfi tin kɔkɔ bí yilpuee nì tin kɔkɔ bí taapuee pu.

eng: Once you were alienated from God and you were enemies with him in your minds, which was shown by your evil actions.
local: Ni nín nìn cáá tilandɔkɔkpiil ki tūǹ atuŋkpiti puee, ni nì Unimbɔti kansikin nìn bíil̀, ní ni nìn sá u-nannanliibi.

eng: But now he has reconciled you by his physical body through death, to present you before God as a people who are holy, faultless, and without blame.
local: Ama kɔŋkɔnnee, Yeesu nín nìn kpántì unil ki kpíì puee ní Unimbɔti yāntì ni nì u mɔ́ɔkì. Mimmee, u làá cáań-ni u-nimbiin, ní nīn sá u-yab kí nīn bí circir, báà ubɔ ń taa ká ni-pu tikpitil kí lì.

eng: But you need to remain well established and rooted in faith and not shift away from the hope given in the good news that you heard. This message has been preached throughout all creation under heaven. And I, Paul, became a servant of this good news.
local: Ama, ni máaǹ tibɔti tin ni nìn fōō ki kííyee, ní nīn ŋúb́-tì mmɔntiimin, kí nīn sí deedee kí báań inyaan kí taa lēē dimákal Tibɔmɔntiil tin ni gbìl̄ee pu. An bɔtee di sá tin bi sòon̄ ki tīī báà ŋma ajan kɔkɔee. Man mun Pɔɔl kpántì un kíǹ ki sòoǹ an bɔtee ní.

eng: Now Im happy to be suffering for you. Im completing what is missing from Christs sufferings with my own body. Im doing this for the sake of his body, which is the church.
local: Kɔŋkɔnnee an mɔ́ɔkiǹ-mi m nín bíi jīǹ falaa ni-puee, an dàkà yii ḿ púntí falaa un Kriisitoo tīī-mee m-wuntin kí tīī u-nikitil din sá u-wuntee.

eng: I became a servant of the church by Gods commission, which was given to me for you, in order to complete Gods word.
local: Kii Unimbɔti nín túǹ-mi ditúndi ḿ tɔ̄ ditundi kí tīī nimbi Kriisitoo nikitil puee, man kpántì utɔntɔnli ki tīī-ni: m-tundi di sá ḿ sòoǹ u-bɔtee kɔkɔ mmɔntiim.
