local: Nì min kamaa m-weeŋyee bàà nìn ŋūǹ kí yàntī ní gīiǹ-mee, naa kpēē-mi fámaaa, kaa núutiǹ-mi. Áli gba, ni nìn gíti fōō-mi kii Unimbɔti tuuŋŋuaaa, Yeesu Kriisitoo u-bee ní.

eng: Where then is the great attitude that you had? I swear that, if possible, you would have dug out your eyes and given them to me.
local: Ba ŋá ní an kaa ti mɔ̄ǹ-ni kii dooooyee? Di sá yii m ŋūǹ kí sòoǹ ni-pu yii, ni yaan ŋūǹ, ni bàà lèekī ni-nimbiliŋ kí tīī-mi.

eng: So then, have I become your enemy by telling you the truth?.
local: Ibaamɔn in m sòoǹ nì nee puee ní naa ti là m-bɔtaaa?.

eng: They are so concerned about you, though not with good intentions. Rather, they want to shut you out so that you would run after them.
local: Binib nee nín cáá bi-ba ki nākantíǹ-ni dinakándi diŋyee, dàa sá dimɔntiil, bi là kí yàkatī man nì ni kansikin ní, kí dáaliń-ni kí nákáǹ bi-ba.

eng: However, its always good to have people concerned about you with good intentions, and not just when Im there with you.
local: An máaǹ tin ŋān̄ee pu ní unil ń dū u-ba kí nákáǹ-si kí nīn cá jaanjaan, kaa sá m yaa bí ni-kansikinee baba.

eng: My little children, Im going through labor pains again until Christ is formed in you.
local: M-bineeŋkaamu, an tí wù-mi tikpil kii imatiŋweenee kí nīn cí ŋyunti ŋun ni làá dāá kpántí kii Kriisitoo nín bí puee.

eng: However, they are placed under trustees and guardians until the date set by the parents.
local: U dáá bí binib bin ŋúb́-u ki kpèé u-wantee taapu ní ki cí ŋyunti ŋun u-baa yóoǹee ń dāá bàń.

eng: But I wish I could be with you now and change how I sound, because Im at a loss about you.
local: M yaan bímaan ni-kansikin ŋyunti nee kí ŋmā kí béé m nín làá sòoǹ-ni puee! Kɔŋkɔnnee, m bíi yáliŋkí ni-pu.

eng: Tell me—those of you who want to be under the Law—dont you listen to the Law?.
local: Nimbi bin là kí nīn bí Mooyiisi marab taapuee, tùkùmaan-mi, naa gbìl̀ marab gbɔŋku poon bɔtaaa?.

eng: Its written that Abraham had two sons, one by the slave woman and one by the free woman.
local: Di sá yii an ŋmàn̄ yii Abraam nìn màl̄ mbininjabiyaamu muli, kiba sá unimpuyumbu biki ní kin kíǹee sá unimpu un yì u-bee biki.

eng: The son by the slave woman was conceived the normal way, but the son by the free woman was conceived through a promise.
local: U màl̄ uyumbu biki nniyaamin, ama ní unimpu un yì u-bee biki màaltam sá Unimbɔti pòontam.

eng: These things are an allegory: the women are two covenants. One is from Mount Sinai, which gives birth to slave children; this is Hagar.
local: Tibɔti nee ŋūǹ kí nīn sá-ti tibɔŋmantikaal: binimpoobi bili gbanti ŋūǹ kí dàkà min ní Unimbɔti nìn bóob́ u nì binib kansikinee. Bi nín nìn bóob́ tin Siinaayi jool puee di sá Agaar ní ki màal̀ ŋyumbubiyaamu.

eng: Hagar is Mount Sinai in Arabia, and she corresponds to the present-day Jerusalem, because the city is in slavery with her children.
local: Agaar gbanti sá Siinaayi jool din bí Bilaalibub tiŋkinee, ní ki sá Jeerusaleem nì u-biyaamu mun bí kuyuŋun díǹ pu nan ní.

eng: But the Jerusalem that is above is free, and she is our mother.
local: Ama Jeerusaleem un bí yilpuee sá un yì u-bee, úǹ di sá timbi na.

eng: Its written: Rejoice, barren woman, you who have not given birth . Break out with a shout, you who have not suffered labor pains; because the woman who has been deserted will have many more children than the woman who has a husband .
local: Kun puee an ŋmàn̄ yii: «Unimpuŋmaŋŋmanti un kaa ŋúń ki màl̄ ki kée, án nīn mɔ̄ǹ-si! Sii un kaa nìn nyí imatiŋween ki kée, á nīn kūuntí nì dipɔɔpindi! Kun puee unimpu un bi cāntèe màl̄ mbiyaamu ki jítiǹ unimpu un cáá calee.».

eng: Brothers and sisters, you are children of the promise like Isaac.
local: Nnabiyaamu, míǹ ní nimbi sá Unimbɔti pòontam biyaamu, kii Isaaki nín nìn sá puee.

eng: But just as it was then, so it is now also: the one who was conceived the normal way harassed the one who was conceived by the Spirit.
local: Dooooyee, kibiki kin bi màl̄ nniyaaminee nìn páb̀ kibiki kin Unimbɔti Fam yāntì bi màl̄ee. Ní áli nìn díǹ an gbíntìi sá míǹ ní.

eng: In the same way, when we were minors, we were also enslaved by this worlds system.
local: Míǹ mbaantiim, ŋyunti ŋun taa láá béè Kriisitoo saŋŋuee, ti nìn sá duulinya marab yumbui ní.

eng: But what does the scripture say? Throw out the slave woman and her son, because the slave womans son wont share the inheritance with the free womans son .
local: An ŋmàn̄ Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon yii ba ní? An ŋmàn̄ yii: «Jà unimpuyumbu nì u-jipɔɔn. U-jipɔɔn nì unimpu un yì u-bee jipɔɔn kaa máaǹ kí yàkatī bi-baa gaajawanti.».

eng: Therefore, brothers and sisters, we arent the slave womans children, but we are the free womans children.
local: Míǹ puee, m-nabiyaamu, timbi sá unimpu un yì u-bee biyaamu ní, taa sá unimpuyumbu biyaamu.

eng: But when the fulfillment of the time came, God sent his Son, born through a woman, and born under the Law.
local: An sāā ŋyunti bāańee, ní Unimbɔti túnní u-Jipɔɔn ní ki yāntì unimpu màl̄-u, ní u mun bí Juuda yab marab taapu.

eng: This was so he could redeem those under the Law so that we could be adopted.
local: án nín làá ŋá pu ú fīī timbi bin bí mara taapuee kí dū-ti kí kpántí Unimbɔti biyaamuee.

eng: Because you are sons and daughters, God sent the Spirit of his Son into our hearts, crying, Abba, Father.
local: Kun dàkà yii ni lafun sá u-biyaamuee di sá Unimbɔti túnní u-Jipɔɔn Fam ti-pɔbiliŋin. Ŋfamee di yì-tì ti yíì-u yii: «Ti-Baa.».

eng: Therefore, you are no longer a slave but a son or daughter, and if you are his child, then you are also an heir through God.
local: Míǹ puee, aa ti sá uyumbu, a kíǹ ki sá u-biki ní. A nín sá u-bikee puee, ní u tìì-si u-gaajawanti.

eng: At the time, when you didnt know God, you were enslaved by things that arent gods by nature.
local: Dooooyee, naa nìn nyí Unimbɔti ní ki nìn sá awaa yumbui, awaa ŋin kaa sá Unimbɔti, kaa máaǹ bí nóó-ŋee.

eng: But now, after knowing God , how can you turn back again to the weak and worthless world system? Do you want to be slaves to it again?.
local: Kɔŋkɔnnee, ni béè Unimbɔti, an dàkà yii Unimbɔti di béè-ni. Ba ŋá ní ni gíti là kí gítí duulinya marab bin kaa cáá mpɔn, kaa bí tibanee taapu kí nīn sá bi-yumbui kii dooooyee ní?.

eng: Christ has set us free for freedom. Therefore, stand firm and dont submit to the bondage of slavery again.
local: Kriisitoo nyántì-ti kuyuŋun tí nīn tɔ́ ti-ba ní. Míǹ puee, ní nīn bímaan dibatɔlee ni, kí taa gíti kɔ́ kuyuŋun.

eng: Im convinced about you in the Lord that you wont think any other way. But the one who is confusing you will pay the penalty, whoever that may be.
local: Ama man máká ni-pu yii ti-Dindaan puee, naa làá kpántí ni-landɔkɔi. Un di yaa cáań fìkil fikil ni-kansikinee, báà udaan yaa yāntì ki sá un, ditafadaaldi dɔ̄ ki cí-u.

eng: Brothers and sisters, if Im still preaching circumcision, why am I still being harassed? In that case, the offense of the cross would be canceled.
local: Nnabiyaamu, m yaan bíi tùkù binib yii an máaǹ bí gíí dikpancandee, ba pu ní bi bàà gíti páb̀-mi? M yaan lī míǹ, Kriisitoo dɔpuntikaaŋu bɔti kaa bàà sá tibɔti tin páb̀ binibee.

eng: I wish that the ones who are upsetting you would castrate themselves!.
local: M bàà là bin páb́-ni ní gííyee ń cù bí fàatī-bi.

eng: You were called to freedom, brothers and sisters; only dont let this freedom be an opportunity to indulge your selfish impulses, but serve each other through love.
local: Míǹ puee, nnabiyaamu, ni ká yii Unimbɔti yíiń nimbi ní nīn bí dibatɔlin. Ní taa lákatí dibatɔlee pu kí nīn ŋáań min ni-bimbikpitim lèe, ama ní nīn néeń tɔb kí nīn tútúǹ tɔb.

eng: All the Law has been fulfilled in a single statement: Love your neighbor as yourself .
local: Di sá yii marab kɔkɔ taapu di sá unee, yii: «Á nīn néeń a-juti kii sii a-bee.».

eng: But if you bite and devour each other, be careful that you dont get eaten up by each other!.
local: Ama ní kpèè tiŋan: ni yaa bí ki júntí tɔb ki cátikí tɔbee, ni làá kúntí tɔb dee.

eng: I say be guided by the Spirit and you wont carry out your selfish desires.
local: Tin m tùkù-nee di sá yii ní yàntī Unimbɔti Fam ń kpòotī-ni, kí taa nín̄ nóó min ni-bimbikpitim bàlifēe.

eng: A persons selfish desires are set against the Spirit, and the Spirit is set against ones selfish desires. They are opposed to each other, so you shouldnt do whatever you want to do.
local: Di sá yii, ti-bimbikpitim nín là min miŋyee, Unimbɔti Fam kaa là míǹ, ní min min Unimbɔti Fam lèe, ti-bimbikpitim mun kaa là míǹ. Tiwan tilee jáaǹ tɔb ní. An di yì-tì ní taa ŋūǹ kí ŋá min ti bàà là kí ŋée.

eng: But if you are being led by the Spirit, you arent under the Law.
local: Ama Unimbɔti Fam yaa kpòotī-nee, naa ti bí Mooyiisi marab taapu.

eng: The actions that are produced by selfish motives are obvious, since they include sexual immorality, moral corruption, doing whatever feels good.
local: An kaa pɔ̄ɔ̄ tí béé mbimbikpitim nín tūǹ atuun ŋin ti-wuntinee: an di sá tilaakpaal, tijɔkɔndi, bimaramɔntiibi fátam.

eng: Look, I, Paul, am telling you that if you have yourselves circumcised, having Christ wont help you.
local: Ní pīlifìmaan-mi! Man Pɔɔl bíi tùkù-ni yii, ni yaa yāntì bi gíí-ni dikpancandee, naa ti bíi páatí tiba Kriisitoo pu.

eng: idolatry, drug use and casting spells, hate, fighting, obsession, losing your temper, competitive opposition, conflict, selfishness, group rivalry.
local: diwaatootil, dinyɔɔjil, kináŋ, kujaau, kudɔ̀ŋu, diŋuul, ikummunti, diyakatil, digàntil.

eng: jealousy, drunkenness, partying, and other things like that. I warn you as I have already warned you, that those who do these kinds of things wont inherit Gods kingdom.
local: nnimbidiki, ndanyoon, mpoo, nì tin kɔkɔ náań nì tibɔti neeyee. Kii m nín nìn tùkū-ni puee, m tí bíi kpáafí-ni yii bin ŋáań an bɔti gbantee kaa dāa kóò Unimbɔti Beelin.

eng: But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faithfulness.
local: Ama, dibil din Circir Fam yàntī unil ń lòoǹee di sá dineendi, dipɔɔpindi, ŋgbansɔŋfi, kimɔtii, á nīn sūǹ binib saai, kí nīn ŋáań tiŋan, kí nīn bí deedee.

eng: gentleness, and self-control. There is no law against things like this.
local: kí nīn mɔ̄, kí ŋúb́ a-ba. Mara ubɔ kaa bí ki jáà nì an bɔti gbanti.

eng: Those who belong to Christ Jesus have crucified the self with its passions and its desires.
local: Di sá yii Yeesu Kriisitoo yab dū bi-bimbikpitim nì tin mì néeń ki lèe ki kpáá ndɔpuntikaa pu.

eng: If we live by the Spirit, lets follow the Spirit.
local: Ŋfam nín tīī-ti dimaŋfalee, tí yàntī Ŋfamee ń nīn kpòotī ti-bimbim mun.

eng: Lets not become arrogant, make each other angry, or be jealous of each other.
local: Tí taa nín̄ yòontí ti-ba yilpu, kí taa nín̄ tákà tɔb tibɔti, kí taa nín̄ cáá ipoompoon tɔb pu.

eng: Again I swear to every man who has himself circumcised that he is required to do the whole Law.
local: M làá gíti bùsì kí tùkù uninja sá kamaa yii, un yaa yāntì bi gíí-u dikpancandee, an bàlifī ú nīn ŋúb́ Mooyiisi marab kɔkɔ ní.

eng: You people who are trying to be made righteous by the Law have been estranged from Christ. You have fallen away from grace!.
local: Nimbi bin kɔkɔ nyàab̀ kí nīn bí deedee Unimbɔti nimbiin nì marab ŋúbitam puee, ni nì Kriisitoo yākatì tɔbin, ni nyáń Unimbɔti bìintamin.

eng: We eagerly wait for the hope of righteousness through the Spirit by faith.
local: An yaa sá timbee, ti máká ní ki kā ki cí kí kpántí deedeedambi Unimbɔti nimbiin, kun puee ti fōō Kriisitoo ki kíí ní Unimbɔti Fam bí ti-ni.

eng: Being circumcised or not being circumcised doesnt matter in Christ Jesus, but faith working through love does matter.
local: A yaa kpántì Yeesu Kriisitoo yɔuee, a yaa gíí dikpancandaaa, a yaa kaa gííyaaa, an kaa páb́, kun di páb́ee di sá a-fookiiu ń nīn tūǹ ditundi nì dineendi.

eng: You were running well—who stopped you from obeying the truth?.
local: Ni nìn còom̄ tiŋan. Ŋma di pīiǹ-ni nsan ní gbèen̄ kí taa ti gbìl̄ kí tīiǹ ibaamɔn ní?.

eng: This line of reasoning doesnt come from the one who calls you.
local: An kaa sá un di yíì-ni ní nīn nóó-uee cee ní tíǹ nyánní.

eng: A little yeast works through the whole lump of dough.
local: Ŋkpɔnɔnyɔkɔ fiii di yàntī kpɔnɔ saabu kɔkɔ jɔ́ḿ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, if a person is caught doing something wrong, you who are spiritual should restore someone like this with a spirit of gentleness. Watch out for yourselves so you wont be tempted too.
local: Nnabiyaamu, ni yaa cúú ubɔ bíi ŋáań ikpitee, nimbi bin Unimbɔti Fam kpòotēe ń ŋá soooo kí gíiǹ-u nsammɔnti poon, ní kí nīn kpèé ni-ba tiŋan ki mun ń taa lítí ni-ba.

eng: So then, lets work for the good of all whenever we have an opportunity, and especially for those in the household of faith.
local: Míǹ puee, ŋyunti ŋun poon ti cáá mpaan nee, tí nīn ŋáań báà ŋma tiŋan, ama cilinjee tí nīn ŋáaḿmaan timbi nì bin pēē nnimbɔtisanee tiŋan.

eng: Look at the large letters Im making with my own handwriting!.
local: Kpèèmaan, m nín dū m-ŋal ki ŋmàn̄ tiŋmaŋmabiti an gbìfì puee!.

eng: Whoever wants to look good by human standards will try to get you to be circumcised, but only so they wont be harassed for the cross of Christ.
local: Bin téekí-ni ní gíí dikpancandee là binib ń nīn nyí bi-pu dee. Kun pu bi ŋáań mimmee di sá baa là kí ká digìńdi Kriisitoo dɔpuntikaaŋu pu.

eng: Those who are circumcised dont observe the Law themselves, but they want you to be circumcised, so they can boast about your physical body.
local: Di sá yii bin gífì dikpancandee bi-ba kaa ŋúb̀ Mooyiisi marab. Kun pu bi là ní gííyee di sá bi là kí gāā bi-biŋŋi yii, ni-wunti daaŋku nee, bíǹ di páb́-ni ní ni ŋá.

eng: But as for me, God forbid that I should boast about anything except for the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ. The world has been crucified to me through him, and I have been crucified to the world.
local: Maa là kí gāā m-bindi tiba pu kpataaa an yaa kaa sá ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo dɔpuntikaaŋu baba pu. Man ceeyee duulinya kpíì ndɔpuntikaa pu, ní duulinya ceeyee ma mun kpíì ndɔpuntikaa pu.

eng: Being circumcised or not being circumcised doesnt mean anything. What matters is a new creation.
local: A yaa gíí dikpancandaaa, a yaa kaa gííyaaa, an kaa páb́, tin páb́ee di sá á kpántí unipɔɔn.

eng: May peace and mercy be on whoever follows this rule and on Gods Israel.
local: Bin kɔkɔ làá nóó min m lī nee, Unimbɔti ń tīī-bi ŋgbansɔŋfi kí sɔ̄ bi-saai, ki kpáaǹ bin sá Isirayeel yab kiŋkiŋ Unimbɔti nibulee.

eng: From now on, no one should bother me because I bear the marks of Jesus on my body.
local: Kí pée yòon̄ díǹ kí nīn cá, ubɔ ń taa ti ŋá-mi digìńdi, kun puee m-wunti buŋwati di sá Yeesu daanti.

eng: Brothers and sisters, may the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Amen.
local: Nnabiyaamu, ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo ŋanti ń nīn bí ni-pu! Ameeni.

eng: Carry each others burdens and so you will fulfill the law of Christ.
local: Ní nīn tútúǹ tɔb ni-gìŋ́ŋi poon, mimmee, ni bíi ŋáań min Kriisitoo marau lēe dee.

eng: If anyone thinks they are important when they arent, theyre fooling themselves.
local: Ubɔ yaa dākafì yii u bí tiban ní kaa bée, udaan kíiĺ u-ba ní.

eng: Each person should test their own work and be happy with doing a good job and not compare themselves with others.
local: Báà ŋma ń cɔ̄ŋkì u-ba kí kpèè u-tuuŋŋi, ní u yaa làá nín gāā u-bindaaa, ú gāā an nīn sá úǹ u-ba ŋmaniŋma tuuŋŋi pu. Ú taa dū kí ŋmàntīǹ u-jutiibi yaŋ pu.

eng: Each person will have to carry their own load.
local: Di sá yii báà ŋma máaǹ kí kpèè u-ba bɔti ní.

eng: Those who are taught the word should share all good things with their teacher.
local: Un bi yaa dàkà Unimbɔti bɔtee, udaan máaǹ kí lēē u-gaajawanti tin kɔkɔ u cááyee poon kí tīī un dàkà-uee.

eng: Make no mistake, God is not mocked. A person will harvest what they plant.
local: Taa nín̄ kíiĺmaan ni-ba: baa gbíilíǹ Unimbɔti. Di sá yii kun unil yaa búĺee, kúǹ ní u dāa cēē.

eng: Those who plant only for their own benefit will harvest devastation from their selfishness, but those who plant for the benefit of the Spirit will harvest eternal life from the Spirit.
local: Un ŋáań min u-bimbikpitim lèe búĺ u-bimbikpitim dee, ní kí dāá cā ŋkúm. Ama un ŋáań min Circir Fam lèe búĺ Circir Fam dee, ní kí dāá cā dimaŋfal din kaa kúntée.

eng: Lets not get tired of doing good, because in time well have a harvest if we dont give up.
local: Tí taa gīī kí gbèen̄ tiŋan diŋál. Ti-wunti yaa kaa yɔ̄tì, ŋyunti yaa láa bāań, ti làá dāá fōō tipaal.

eng: In the past, God spoke through the prophets to our ancestors in many times and many ways.
local: Dooooyee, Unimbɔti nìn dī u-bɔnaatiliibi pu nì nsàmbiyaamu tikpil pu ki sòon̄ nì ti-naanjab ŋfunyɔm tikpil.

eng: And he says, You, Lord, laid the earths foundations in the beginning, and the heavens are made by your hands. .
local: Ní Unimbɔti gíti bí yii: «Ŋkilkaaminee, sii ti-Dindaan di nìn náań kitiŋ, a-ŋalii ní a dūu náań kutagbɔŋu.

eng: They will pass away, but you remain. They will all wear out like old clothes. .
local: Tíǹ kɔkɔ làá dāá púĺ ní kí sátí kii dibɔkutilee ní kí kpú, ama sii làá nīn pée bí ní.

eng: You will fold them up like a coat. They will be changed like a person changes clothes, but you stay the same, and the years of your life wont come to an end. .
local: A làá dāá kpáb́ yilpu nì taapu kii kukampeeuee, ní kí cáań tipɔn kí kpántí tikpikil kii bi nín kpántí dibɔkutil pu kí dū dipɔndi kí pèen̄ puee ní. Ama sii làá nīn pée gbíntì ki bí min a bée ní, aa làá púĺ jaanjaan.».

eng: When has he ever said to any of the angels, Sit at my right side until I put your enemies under your feet like a footstool? .
local: Unimbɔti kaa láá lī ntuun ŋubɔ dalba dalba yii: «Kàl̄ m-ŋaŋgiiliŋu pú yéè kí nīn cí ŋyunti ŋun m làá dū a-nannanliibi kí bíĺ a-taapu á tāā-bee.».

eng: Arent all the angels ministering spirits who are sent to serve those who are going to inherit salvation?.
local: Unimbɔti tuuŋyi di kaa sá bin kɔkɔ sá ŋfam ki bí ki tūǹ Unimbɔti tundeeee? U tūnní-bi ní bi dòoń ki tútúǹ bin làá ká difiilee.

eng: In these final days, though, he spoke to us through a Son. God made his Son the heir of everything and created the world through him.
local: Ama iyunti in sá ijoolkaa nee poonee, u dī u-Jipɔɔn pu ní ki sòon̄ nì ti. Unimbɔti dī u-Jipɔɔn gbanti pu ní ki náań duulinya nì tin kɔkɔ bí duulinyanee. Úǹ ní Unimbɔti lēē ní ki dū tikɔkɔ ki ŋá u-ŋaan.

eng: The Son is the light of Gods glory and the imprint of Gods being. He maintains everything with his powerful message. After he carried out the cleansing of people from their sins, he sat down at the right side of the highest majesty.
local: U-pu ní ti kāntí Unimbɔti nyooti nín jìl̀ miŋyee. Úǹ di sá Unimbɔti naŋku, ní ki cáá u-nyɔɔbundi din sá mpɔnee ki ŋúb́ duulinya kɔkɔ. U fíntì ti-kpitii. Boonee ní u nín jɔ́ḿ yilpu ki cūtìi kāl̀ Unimbɔti Mpɔŋkpaandaan ŋaŋgiil pú.

eng: And the Son became so much greater than the other messengers, such as angels, that he received a more important title than theirs.
local: Kii Unimbɔti Jipɔɔn nín jítiǹ ituun puee, míǹ ní u-yindi mun tí jítiǹ ituun yaŋ.

eng: After all, when did God ever say to any of the angels: You are my Son. Today I have become your Father? Or, even, I will be his Father, and he will be my Son? .
local: Di sá yii Unimbɔti kaa lī ntuun ŋubɔ dalba yii: «A sá m-Jipɔɔn, kí yòon̄ díǹ kí nīn cée, man di sá a-Baa.» Waa láá sòon̄ ntuun ŋubɔ pu yii: «Man di làá nīn sá ŋu-Baa ní ŋú nīn sá man Jipɔɔn.».

eng: But then, when he brought his firstborn into the world, he said, All of Gods angels must worship him. .
local: Ŋyunti ŋun u dū u-Jipɔŋkpiliu gbanti ki túnní duulinya poonee, ní u bí yii: «Unimbɔti tuuŋyi kɔkɔ ń gbàan̄ kí nyɔ̄ŋkì-u.».

eng: He talks about the angels: Hes the one who uses the spirits for his messengers and who uses flames of fire as ministers. .
local: Kpèè Unimbɔti nín sòon̄ ituun pu yii i sá kuŋyee sèé, yii: «Ituun yáb̀ kii kubuŋuee, ní ki tūǹ úǹ tundi, ní ki tùm̀ kpaŋkpam kii ŋŋmee ki tūǹ u-tundi.».

eng: But he says to his Son, God, your throne is forever and your kingdoms scepter is a rod of justice. .
local: Kpèè u nín sòon̄ u-Jipɔɔn pu miŋyee sèé, yii: «Unimbɔti, a-beejal pée bí ní kí nīn cá jaanjaan. A làá jī a-beel nì deedee ní.

eng: You loved righteousness and hated lawless behavior. That is why God, your God, has anointed you more than your companions with the oil of joy. .
local: A néeń tin bí deedeeyee ní ki ná tin kaa ŋān̄ee. An pu ní Unimbɔti, a-Nimbɔtiu, lútì-si, ki bātì dipɔɔpindi kpam ki sītì a-yil pu. U kāaǹ-si tinyool kakaaŋu, a jítiǹ a-jutiibi kɔkɔ.».

eng: The Law is a shadow of the good things that are coming, not the real things themselves. It never can perfect the ones who are trying to draw near to God through the same sacrifices that are offered continually every year.
local: Mooyiisi marab kaa nìn baasii dàkà tiŋan tin Yeesu làá cáań-tee mbaamɔmin, an nìn pée sá kunaŋu ní. Di sá yii bi nìn pée ŋáań saraabi gbanti míǹ ní dibintil sá kamaa an kaa kúntí, ama ní an kaa ŋūǹ kí yàntī bí kpántí bimɔntiibi páaa kí sútí kí pìl̄ Unimbɔti.

eng: We have been made holy by Gods will through the offering of Jesus Christs body once for all.
local: Yeesu Kriisitoo dɔ́miń ki ŋá min fáatiǹ Unimbɔtee. U dū u-ba ki ŋá saraa diba pu ki kúntì saraabi, ní u-saraauee fíntì ti-kpitii.

eng: Every priest stands every day serving and offering the same sacrifices over and over, sacrifices that can never take away sins.
local: Baabadalee, usaraaŋal yú u-tundi laŋki ní, ní ki ŋáań bisaraabaantiibi múlmul, ama ní an saraabee kaa ŋūǹ kí lēē unil kpitii.

eng: But when this priest offered one sacrifice for sins for all time, he sat down at the right side of God.
local: Ní Kriisitoo úǹ dū u-ba ki ŋá saraa un bí kí nīn cá jaanjaanee diba pu timbi kpitii pu. Boonee ní ki cūtìi kāl̀ Unimbɔti ŋaŋgiil pú.

eng: Since then, hes waiting until his enemies are made into a footstool for his feet.
local: U kā niin ki cí ŋyunti ŋun Unimbɔti làá dāá dū u-nannanliibi kí ŋá u-taaŋi taapu ú tāāyee.

eng: because he perfected the people who are being made holy with one offering for all time.
local: Di sá yii saraabambaanti ní u ŋá ní an fíntì binib kpitii ní saraa gbantee cáá mpɔn kí nīn cá, kí gàntī-bi kí yóoń bí nīn ŋān̄ Unimbɔti nimbiin.

eng: The Holy Spirit affirms this when saying.
local: Unimbɔti Fam lī yii míǹ ní an lafun sá. Mpeekaamee, mì bí yii.

eng: This is the covenant that I will make with them. After these days, says the Lord, I will place my laws in their hearts and write them on their minds. .
local: «Ti-Dindaan bí yii dipoondi din m làá dāá líí-bi mpèé ń nīn bí man nì bi kansikin ŋyunti gbantee sèé: M làá dāá dū m-marab kí ŋá bi-pɔbiliŋin ní bí ŋmā kí nīn nyí min m lèe, ní kí tí ŋmà marab gbanti bi-landɔkɔyin bí ŋmā kí nīn ŋúb́-bi kí taa súntí.».

eng: And I wont remember their sins and their lawless behavior anymore. .
local: Ní nliliitiimee mì gíti bí yii: «Maa làá dāá gíti téetí bi-kpitii nì bi-yìntitam pu dalba dalba.».

eng: When there is forgiveness for these things, there is no longer an offering for sin.
local: Di sá bi yaa dū unil kpitii ki pú-uee, an kaa ti cáá saraa ubɔ ú gíti ŋá ikpitee pu.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we have confidence that we can enter the holy of holies by means of Jesus blood.
local: An puee nnabiyaamu, Yeesu nín kpíì u-fatikuu nyáńee ní nsan dōoǹ tí jītí kí kɔ́ circir páaa laŋki laakin Unimbɔti bée.

eng: Otherwise, wouldnt they have stopped being offered? If the people carrying out their religious duties had been completely cleansed once, no one would have been aware of sin anymore.
local: Bin bin nìn ŋáań tiwaŋkuti saraabi ki tìì Unimbɔtee yaa nìn ŋá saraa míǹ diba pu bi-kpitii lēēyee, kɔnampu bi bàà nìn gbēeǹ disaraaŋal dooooyee, kun puee baa bàà gíti gbìl̀ bi-ban yii bi cáá ikpiti.

eng: through a new and living way that he opened up for us through the curtain, which is his body.
local: An sá nsampɔɔn ŋun tìì dimaŋfalee ní Yeesu pīitì-ti ki dī ŋŋaabu ŋun nìn pītì circir laŋki nì circir páaa laŋkee. Ŋŋaabuee di sá u-wunti.

eng: and we have a great high priest over Gods house.
local: Ti nín cáá usaraaŋakpil un Unimbɔti kāaǹ u-dicaŋku puee.

eng: Therefore, lets draw near with a genuine heart with the certainty that our faith gives us, since our hearts are sprinkled clean from an evil conscience and our bodies are washed with pure water.
local: tí sútí kí pìl̄ Unimbɔti nì dipɔbil diba nì dimákabaamɔndi, kí fíntí ti-pɔbiliŋ poon ilandɔkɔkpiti án nyā dándan, kí dū nnyim min ŋān̄ circiree kí fàl̀ ti-wunti ń nyā.

eng: Lets hold on to the confession of our hope without wavering, because the one who made the promises is reliable.
local: Tí nīn gbíntì ki ŋúb́ ti-mákal din ti lì yii ti cááyee gbīŋgbim, kun puee u nín pōoǹ yii u làá ŋá miŋyee, u làá lafun ŋá míǹ ní.

eng: And let us consider each other carefully for the purpose of sparking love and good deeds.
local: Tí nīn ŋúb́maan tɔb tiŋan kí nīn nyàatī tɔb, ti-neendi ń mɔ̀tīí nīn wīintí, kí nīn tūǹ atummɔnti.

eng: Dont stop meeting together with other believers, which some people have gotten into the habit of doing. Instead, encourage each other, especially as you see the day drawing near.
local: kí taa fàtī ti-boon kí tīī Kriisitoo nikitiŋ kooŋyi kii an nín kpántì biba bimbim baa ti dòoń puee. Ama nín̄ tììmaan tɔb kunyannyaau, kun puee nimbi ni-ba nyí yii ti-Dindaan wiiŋŋu tóobiń.

eng: If we make the decision to sin after we receive the knowledge of the truth, there isnt a sacrifice for sins left any longer.
local: Ti yaa béè ibaamɔn ní ki fōō-i, ní boonee ti gbíntì ki nìkì ki ŋáań ikpiti ti nín là puee, tí béé yii saraa ubɔ gíti kpá bí ŋá án lēē ti-kpitii.

eng: Theres only a scary expectation of judgment and of a burning fire thats going to devour Gods opponents.
local: Ama tin kíǹ tí nīn cée di sá ditafadaakpaandi din sá ŋŋmi ki gà lìblib ki làá wà Unimbɔti nannanliibee.

eng: When someone rejected the Law from Moses, they were put to death without mercy on the basis of the testimony of two or three witnesses.
local: Un yaa nìn yīntiǹ Mooyiisi marab, ní binib bili kii bita yaa lēē seera yii bi ká u nín yīntì puee, bi nìn kù-u ní baa sūǹ u-saai.

eng: How much worse punishment do you think is deserved by the person who walks all over Gods Son, who acts as if the blood of the covenant that made us holy is just ordinary blood, and who insults the Spirit of grace?.
local: Ní kpèè ditafadaaldi dilal ní un kpēē Unimbɔti Jipɔɔn fám kaa dū fatiku un Unimbɔti dū ki líì-ti dipoondee ki yíiń tibee máaǹ? Ama fatikuee ní bi dūu fíntì u-kpitii, ní boonee u kíǹ ki kɔ̄ntí Unimbɔti Fam min min pú-u an yimɔɔlee diŋuul.

eng: Instead, these sacrifices are a reminder of sin every year.
local: Ibaamɔnee, an saraabee sá kí nīn téetí binib dibindi kamaa bi-kpitii pu ní.

eng: We know the one who said, Judgment is mine; I will pay people back. And he also said, The Lord will judge his people. .
local: Ti nyí Unimbɔti un di lī yii: «An sá man Unimbɔti di làá dāá tɔ̀ ipɔntoon, man di làá dāá pà tipaal,» ní ki gíti bí yii: «Man di làá dāá sòoǹ m-samaau bɔti.».

eng: Its scary to fall into the hands of the living God!.
local: Sii un yaa kɔ́ Unimbɔti un bí nee bɔtinee, an dóò sii pu dee!.

eng: But remember the earlier days, after you saw the light. You stood your ground while you were suffering from an enormous amount of pressure.
local: Téetímaan ŋkpikim ni nín nìn fōō Kriisitoo ki kíí ki ká ŋwalifi ŋuŋyee, nì falaabi bin ní ni nìn jíń tikpil kaa fātìi tɔ́ee.

eng: Sometimes you were exposed to insults and abuse in public. Other times you became partners with those who were treated that way.
local: Iyunti ibee, bi sìí-ni, iyunti ibee, bi cáǹ-ni diniwul nimbiin ki sàantí, iyunti ibee, ni bí ki sūǹ bin bi sàantée saai.

eng: You even showed sympathy toward people in prison and accepted the confiscation of your possessions with joy, since you knew that you had better and lasting possessions.
local: Míǹ ní ni nì bisarkadambi yākatì bi-falaau. Bi yaa nìn cúú ni-wanti ki fōōyee, an kaa nìn páb́-ni, ni-pɔbiliŋ nìn sɔ̀m̀, kun puee ni nyí yii ni cáá tigaajamɔntiil tin làá nīn bí jaanjaanee an dɔ̄ yilpu.

eng: So dont throw away your confidence—it brings a great reward.
local: An puee ní taa páatí ni-mákal, ipaatikpaan dɔ̄ ki cí-ni.

eng: You need to endure so that you can receive the promises after you do Gods will.
local: An máaǹ ní nīn cáá dipɔ́ɔǹdi ní kí ŋmā kí ŋá min Unimbɔti lèe kí wàatī ní kí nín ká tin u pōoǹee.

eng: In a little while longer, the one who is coming will come and wont delay; .
local: An bí kii an nín ŋmàn̄ Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon puee yii: «An kíǹ ŋyunti fiii nàanyɔkɔ ní un làá dāan̄ee ń bàń, an kaa làá ti yúntí.

eng: but my righteous one will live by faith, and my whole being wont be pleased with anyone who shrinks back. .
local: Un bí deedee m-nimbiinee fookiiu pu ní u làá ŋmátí, ama u yaa gítì u-boonee, ubɔti kaa dāa gíti fáatiǹ-mi.».

eng: But we arent the sort of people who timidly draw back and end up being destroyed. Were the sort of people who have faith so that our whole beings are preserved.
local: Timbee, taa sá bin gítí bi-boon ki wáŋkée, ama ti sá bin fōō ki kíí ki cáá dimaŋfalee ní.

eng: because its impossible for the blood of bulls and goats to take away sins.
local: Kun puee inajaa nì iŋɔjaa fatikuu kaa ŋūǹ kí lēē ikpiti jaanjaan.

eng: Therefore, when he comes into the world he says, You didnt want a sacrifice or an offering, but you prepared a body for me; .
local: An pu ní ŋyunti ŋun Kriisitoo làá nīn dòoń duulinyanee, u bí Unimbɔti yii: «Aa là kuwaŋkuluu saraabi àá tijimpin saraabi. Man ní a tīī tiwun ḿ dū-tì kí ŋá saraa kí tīī-si.

eng: you werent pleased with entirely burned offerings or a sin offering. .
local: Bisaraawɔkaabi nì ikpiti pɔ̄tam saraabi kaa fáatì-si.

eng: So then I said, Look, Ive come to do your will, God. This has been written about me in the scroll. .
local: An pu ní m bí yii: Unimbɔti, m sèé, m làá cùú ŋá min làá fáatíǹ-see, kii an nín pée ŋmàn̄ m-pu min ki yóoǹ a-gbɔŋkunee.».

eng: He says above, You didnt want and you werent pleased with a sacrifice or an offering or with entirely burned offerings or a purification offering, which are offered because the Law requires them.
local: Yeesu dá láá péé ki lī yii: «Aa fáatì tiwaŋkuti saraabi nì tijimpin saraabi nì bisaraawɔkaabi nì ikpiti pɔ̄tam saraabi.» U nìn lī míǹ, nì min kɔkɔ mara di pú nsan yii bí nīn ŋáań saraabi gbanti kɔkɔee.

eng: Then he said, Look, Ive come to do your will . He puts an end to the first to establish the second.
local: Boonee ní ki dāa lī yii: «An pu ní man bí yii: M sèé, Unimbɔti, m làá cù kí ŋá min fáatì-see.» Ní u lafun dɔ́miń ki kpītì nsaŋkpiki ŋun binib nìn dī ki nóó Unimbɔtee ní ki náań mpɔɔn.

eng: Faith is the reality of what we hope for, the proof of what we dont see.
local: Á nīn cáá fookiiyee taapu sá yii: á nīn cɔ́tí tiwan tin pu a mákée, ama aa wāĺ-tì ní ki nyí yii tì bí.

eng: He was looking forward to a city that has foundations, whose architect and builder is God.
local: Di sá Abraam nìn kā ki cí kitiŋkpaaŋŋi kin diiŋŋi Unimbɔti u-ba kítì ki máń, ŋi yú ki pɔ̄ɔ̄yee ní.

eng: By faith even Sarah received the ability to have a child, though she herself was barren and past the age for having children, because she believed that the one who promised was faithful.
local: Fookii un Saara nìn cáá Unimbɔti puee di yì-tì ní nì min kɔkɔ u nìn púĺ ki jítì mmaal puee, u ŋúń ki màl̄ ki kpántì ditimbundi na, kun puee u nìn fōō ki kíí yii Unimbɔti nín pōoǹ miŋyee, u làá ŋá míǹ.

eng: So descendants were born from one man . They were as many as the number of the stars in the sky and as countless as the grains of sand on the seashore.
local: Míǹ ní Abraam unibaanti un púĺ ki jítì mmaal puee màl̄, u-naantiibi būtì ki wīikí kii iŋmalbijaayee, baa ŋūǹ kí kàaǹ-bi, a lì kii bi nín kaa ŋūǹ kí kàaǹ dibul din dɔ̄ tiŋku gbandee puee.

eng: All these people died in faith without receiving the promises, but they saw the promises from a distance and welcomed them. They confessed that they were strangers and immigrants on earth.
local: Nì fookii poon ní an nibee kɔkɔ dūu kpíì kaa kpáàa fōō tin Unimbɔti nìn pōoǹee. Bi nìn ká tì nín páaǹní ndan ki dòońee, ní bi jáam̀-tì, ní ki bí yii bíǹ sá binicambi ní ki kóò duulinyan.

eng: People who say this kind of thing make it clear that they are looking for a homeland.
local: Bi nín sòoǹ miŋyee dàkà ki cántí-ti yii bi bíi nyàab̀ kitiŋ ní kí nīn kóò.

eng: If they had been thinking about the country that they had left, they would have had the opportunity to return to it.
local: An yaa nìn sá kitiŋ kin poon bi nìn yíkèe pu ní bi yaa nìn bíi yáliŋkée, bi bàà nìn ŋūǹ kí fàtīí nīn gítí niinaa!.

eng: But at this point in time, they are longing for a better country, that is, a heavenly one. Therefore, God isnt ashamed to be called their God—he has prepared a city for them.
local: Ama ibaamɔnee, bi nìn baasii nyàab̀ kitimmɔntii ní, an dàkà yii yilpu tiŋki. An pu ní bi nín yíì Unimbɔti yii bi-Nimbɔtiuee, an kaa cáá-u icoo, kun puee u bɔ́ntì-bi kitiŋ ki yóoǹ yilpu.

eng: By faith Abraham offered Isaac when he was tested. The one who received the promises was offering his only son.
local: Fookii un Abraam nìn cáá Unimbɔti puee di yì-tì ŋyunti ŋun Unimbɔti nìn cɔ́ŋkì-u ki bí yii ú dū u-jipɔmbaabil Isaaki kí ŋá-u saraayee, waa nìn ŋmɔ́ mmɔ́ɔn, nì min kɔkɔ Unimbɔti nìn líì-u dipoondee.

eng: He had been told concerning him, Your legitimate descendants will come from Isaac. .
local: Unimbɔti nìn pōoǹ-u yii: «Isaaki di làá bùtī a-maaliŋu ŋun làá dāá fōō a-yindee.».

eng: He figured that God could even raise him from the dead. So in a way he did receive him back from the dead.
local: Ama Abraam nìn fōō ki kíí yii u yaa dū u-biki ki ŋá saraa ki tīī Unimbɔtee, Unimbɔti làá fàtīí fìkitī-kì, míǹ ní an lafun ŋá kii u fīkitì-kèe. Unimbɔti gíiǹ u-biki ki tīī-u, kun puee Abraam ŋman nìn pée kūū-kì ní.

eng: The elders in the past were approved because they showed faith.
local: Fookii un Unimbɔti nib dooooyee yab nìn cááyee pu ní bi ká disíndi Unimbɔti cee.

eng: By faith Isaac also blessed Jacob and Esau concerning their future.
local: Fookii un Isaaki nìn cáá Unimbɔti puee di yì-tì ní u nìn mēē ki bīiǹ Jaakɔb nì Eesayuu pu tin làá dāá bàń bi-pu foolboonee.

eng: By faith Jacob blessed each of Josephs sons as he was dying and bowed in worship over the head of his staff. .
local: Fookii un Jaakɔb nìn cáá Unimbɔti puee di yì-tì u nín púĺ ki cīkiǹ ŋkúmee, u nìn mēē Unimbɔti ki bīiǹ Jooseefi biyaamu liitil pu ki nyīī u-kpandɔŋu pu ki nyɔ́ŋkí Unimbɔti.

eng: By faith Joseph recalled the exodus of the Israelites at the end of his life, and gave instructions about burying his bones.
local: Jooseefi nìn fōō Unimbɔti ki kíí. An di yì-tì ní ŋyunti ŋun u tóob́ ŋkúmee, u nìn sòon̄ Unimbɔti nín làá dāá nyāntí Isirayeel yab Eejipiti tiŋkinee. An puee ní u nìn tīī-bi mara ki dàkā-bi bi nín làá dāá ŋá u-kpabiŋ puee.

eng: By faith Moses was hidden by his parents for three months when he was born, because they saw that the child was beautiful and they werent afraid of the kings orders.
local: Fookii un Mooyiisi baa nì u-na nìn cáá Unimbɔti puee di yì-tì ní bi nín màl̄ Mooyiisee, bi dū-u ki bál̀ áli iŋmal ita. Bi nín ká kibiki nín ŋān̄ miŋyee, baa ti fāŋkì ubɔti marau un u yóoǹ yii bí kpɔ̀ mbiyaamuee.

eng: By faith Moses refused to be called the son of Pharaohs daughter when he was grown up.
local: Fookii un Mooyiisi nìn cáá Unimbɔti puee di yì-tì ní u nín wíiǹee, u nìn yíì yii waa là kí nīn sá ubɔti Faaroo bisal jipɔɔn.

eng: He chose to be mistreated with Gods people instead of having the temporary pleasures of sin.
local: ki là kí nīn bí Unimbɔti nikitilin bí kpāfì bi-kɔkɔ kí sàaǹ. Míǹ nìn mɔ̄ǹ-u ki jítiǹ ú nīn bí ikpitin ki jīǹ mmɔn ŋun kaa làá yúntée.

eng: He thought that the abuses he suffered for Christ were more valuable than the treasures of Egypt, since he was looking forward to the reward.
local: U nìn ká yii, ú jī falaa kii Meesiya nín làá dāá jī uŋyee ŋān̄ ki jítiǹ Eejipiti gaajawanti kɔkɔ, kun puee u nìn wāĺ tipaal tin bí nyɔkɔpuee ní.

eng: By faith he left Egypt without being afraid of the kings anger. He kept on going as if he could see what is invisible.
local: Fookii un Mooyiisi nìn cáá Unimbɔti puee di yì-tì ní u nìn yíkì Eejipiti tiŋkin, kaa fāŋkì diŋuul din ubɔti fōōyee pu, ní ki ŋúb́ Unimbɔti un baa wāĺee bɔti an píkí gbīŋgbim kii u wāĺ-uee.

eng: By faith he kept the Passover and the sprinkling of blood, in order that the destroyer could not touch their firstborn children.
local: Fookii un u nìn cáá Unimbɔti puee di yì-tì u nìn fōō Unimbɔti nyɔkɔ ki jíń Dilákatil jiŋaaliu, ki nìn yāntì bi dū ipiiyee fatikuu ki ŋmītì mpunyɔmu pu, án nín làá ŋá pu ntuun ŋun làá kpɔ̀ Eejipiti yab jipɔŋkpilibee ń taa gbēē Isirayeel yab báà ubɔee.

eng: By faith they crossed the Red Sea as if they were on dry land, but when the Egyptians tried it, they were drowned.
local: Fookii un Isirayeel yab nìn cáá Unimbɔti puee di yì-tì ní bi nìn dūtì Tikati mɔɔl kii kitiŋkuliŋŋee, ama Eejipiti yab mun nìn mākaǹ kí dùtī ní dimɔɔl dū-bi ki náĺ.

eng: By faith we understand that the universe has been created by a word from God so that the visible came into existence from the invisible.
local: Fookii un ti cáá Unimbɔti puee di yì-tì ní ti nyí yii an sá nì u-nyɔɔbundi ní u dū ki náań duulinya, di sá duulinya wanti tin kɔkɔ ti wāĺ nee nyánní dinyɔɔbundi din taa wāĺee poon ní.

eng: By faith Jerichos walls fell after the people marched around them for seven days.
local: Fookii un Isirayeel yab nìn cáá Unimbɔti puee di yì-tì bi nìn cūtì ki cōom̀ ki māntì Jeeriikoo tiŋki. Iwiin iluli dalee, agun nìn cúu kpīkitì ki lítì.

eng: By faith Rahab the prostitute wasnt killed with the disobedient because she welcomed the spies in peace.
local: Fookii un Raab kinimpulaakpaayee nìn cáá Unimbɔti puee di yì-tì ní baa nìn kūū-u ki kpáaǹ binikpitib, kun puee u nìn ŋá tiniti ki fōō binib bin bi nìn tú bí cù kí cɔ̄ŋkì kitiŋee.

eng: What more can I say? I would run out of time if I told you about Gideon, Barak, Samson, Jephthah, David, Samuel, and the prophets.
local: M làá gíti lì yii ba? Mpaan kaa bí-mi ḿ sòoǹ Geedeeyɔn nì Baraaki nì Samsɔn nì Jeefita nì Daafiidi nì Samiyeel nì Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi pu.

eng: Through faith they conquered kingdoms, brought about justice, realized promises, shut the mouths of lions.
local: Bi fookiiu un bi nìn cáá Unimbɔti puee di yì-tì ní bi nìn jáà ki pɔ́ɔkiǹ bibɔtiibi bin kíǹee nì bi-timmu. Biba mun fookiiu pu ní bi nìn yāntì binib ŋáań tin bí deedeeyee, ní biba mun fookiiu pu ní bi nìn fōō tin Unimbɔti pōoǹ-bee. Bi nìn cúú biba ki tɔ́ laakin bi kpàá gbikintibee ní baa nìn ŋúń ki tábitì bi-nyɔmmu ní kaa nín ŋúń ki ŋá-bi tiba bi-fookiiu pu.

eng: put out raging fires, escaped from the edge of the sword, found strength in weakness, were mighty in war, and routed foreign armies.
local: Ŋŋmi gà lìblib bi téé biba ki tɔ́ poon, an kaa ŋá-bi tiba bi-fookiiu pu. An bàà nìn sá bí kpɔ̀ biba nì ntàataajimu, ama fookii un bi nìn cáá Unimbɔti puee puee, bi nìn ŋmátì. Biba nìn būǹ ki pɔ́ɔkì. Biba nìn sá ŋwɔsɔfimu, bi-fookiiu puee, bi kpántì bibaatandambi. Biba fookiiu puee, bi nìn cúú bi-nannanliibi.

eng: Women received back their dead by resurrection. Others were tortured and refused to be released so they could gain a better resurrection.
local: Binimpoobi fookiiu un bi nìn cáá Unimbɔti puee di yì-tì bi fīkitì bi-yab bin kpíìyee ki tīī-bi. Biba fookiiu puee, bi nìn sāaǹ-bi kí kpɔ̀-bi, ama bi nìn yíì yii bí taa fīī-bi, kun puee bi nyí yii bi làá dāá fìkitī kí nīn kíǹ ki bí dimaŋfal din kaa cáá dikúntilee ni.

eng: But others experienced public shame by being taunted and whipped; they were even put in chains and in prison.
local: Bi nìn bēel̀ biba ki gbáń-bi anaalab, ki nìn gúĺ biba nì ikusaka ki cáa tɔ́ sarka.

eng: They were stoned to death, they were cut in two, and they died by being murdered with swords. They went around wearing the skins of sheep and goats, needy, oppressed, and mistreated.
local: Biba fookiiu un bi nìn cáá Unimbɔti puee, bi nìn yáŋkì-bi ataŋkpee ki kūū, ki nìn sēekìi nābitì biba agin ali, ki nìn dū ntàataajimu ki kūū biba. Biba kaa nìn cáá tiba kí pèen̄, see ipii nì iŋoobu gbaŋyi ní bi nìn pèentí. Biba mun nìn sá bisoodambi, ní bi nìn páb́ biba ki sàantí-bi ní bi sáń ki póotí ajan kɔkɔ.

eng: The world didnt deserve them. They wandered around in deserts, mountains, caves, and holes in the ground.
local: Binib gbanti circiriu puee, baa bàà nìn máaǹ kí kàl̄ duulinyan doo. Bi nìn yíkí ní ki cá kuteeun nì ajoon pu, ki kóò itaŋkpalɔkɔn nì afaginni.

eng: All these people didnt receive what was promised, though they were given approval for their faith.
local: Unimbɔti u-ba ká yii an nibee kɔkɔ nìn lafun fōō-u ki kíí, ama nì míǹ kɔkɔee, baa nìn fōō tin Unimbɔti nìn pōoǹee.

eng: By faith Abel offered a better sacrifice to God than Cain, which showed that he was righteous, since God gave approval to him for his gift. Though he died, hes still speaking through faith.
local: An sá fookii un Abeel nìn cáá Unimbɔti puee di yì-tì saraa un u nìn ŋá ki tīī-uee nìn ŋān̄ ki jítiǹ Kayeen yɔu. U-fookiiu gbanti pu ní u kpántì deedeedaan Unimbɔti cee, kun puee Unimbɔti nìn fōō u-saraau. U-fookiiu puee, nì min kɔkɔ u kpíìyee, u-bɔti bí bi sòoǹ díǹ.

eng: God provided something better for us so they wouldnt be made perfect without us.
local: Kun puee Unimbɔti bɔ́ntì tin sá timɔntiilee ki yóoǹ, kaa dá láá dūu tīī-bi ki cíitì ti-kɔkɔ yaa kpáfèe, ú dūú yàkatī-ti.

eng: By faith Enoch was taken up so that he didnt see death, and he wasnt found because God took him up. He was given approval for having pleased God before he was taken up.
local: Eenɔki fookiiu un u nìn cáá Unimbɔti puee di yì-tì ní waa kpíì. Unimbɔti nìn yōoǹ-u ki cáá-u ki jɔ́miǹ u-cee ubɔ kaa ti ká-u. Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon lī yii Eenɔki nìn pée fáatì Unimbɔti dooooyee ní ki wāatì u dāa nín yōoǹ-u.

eng: Its impossible to please God without faith because the one who draws near to God must believe that he exists and that he rewards people who try to find him.
local: Unil yaa kaa fōō Unimbɔti ki kííyee, udaan kpá kí fáatí-u. Di sá yii un là kí sútiń kí pìl̄ Unimbɔtee máaǹ kí nīn nyí yii Unimbɔti bí, ki tìì bin nyàab̀-u nì dipɔbil dibee tipaal.

eng: By faith Noah responded with godly fear when he was warned about events he hadnt seen yet. He built an ark to deliver his household. With his faith, he criticized the world and became an heir of the righteousness that comes from faith.
local: Fookii un Noowa nìn cáá Unimbɔti puee di yì-tì ní Unimbɔti nín nìn kpáafì-u yii nnyiŋgbeen dòońee, u nìn fōō ki kíí kaa láá ká-mì, ní ki nìn kíĺ ki bíi kpáà meela un poon u nì u-dicaŋku làá kɔ́ kí ŋmátée. U-fookiiu un u nìn cáá Unimbɔti puee di dàkā duulinya yab bɔkpil ní úǹ kpántì deedeedaan Unimbɔti nimbiin.

eng: By faith Abraham obeyed when he was called to go out to a place that he was going to receive as an inheritance. He went out without knowing where he was going.
local: Fookii un Abraam nìn cáá Unimbɔti puee di yì-tì ní ŋyunti ŋun Unimbɔti nìn yíiń-u ki bí ú yīkì u-tiŋkin kí nīn cá kitiŋ kin u làá pòon̄ kí tīī-uee nee, u lafun nìn yíkì míǹ kaa nyí laakin pú ní u nìn túĺ ki cée.

eng: By faith he lived in the land he had been promised as a stranger. He lived in tents along with Isaac and Jacob, who were coheirs of the same promise.
local: Nì fookii un u nìn cáá Unimbɔti puee ní u bàn̄ kitimpooŋkaayin ki gāā tanti ki kóò kii unicaanee. Míǹ ní Isaaki nì Jaakɔb bin mun nìn yíkì ki tɔ́kɔǹ dipoondi gbanti puee nìn gāā tantib ki kóò.

eng: So then, with endurance, lets also run the race that is laid out in front of us, since we have such a great cloud of witnesses surrounding us. Lets throw off any extra baggage, get rid of the sin that trips us up.
local: Kii kunipaau kun nìn cáá fookii Unimbɔti puee nín māntì ki tú-ti pu, ki kpèé-ti puee, ní yàntī tí fámaan atuŋkpiti ŋin cāabìń-ti digìńdee nì ikpiti in kaa pɔ̄ɔ̄-ti dicuulee, kí sāǹ nì dipɔ́ɔǹdi ditaatandi din Unimbɔti náká ki yóoǹ-ti tí sāǹee.

eng: Our human parents disciplined us for a little while, as it seemed best to them, but God does it for our benefit so that we can share his holiness.
local: Ti-baabee, bíǹ dàkà-ti ŋyunti ŋubɔ ní kí tin sāā min bi lèe ní kí gbèen̄, ama Unimbɔti úǹ nín dàkà-tee, an sá ti-ŋanti ní, kun puee u là tí nīn bí circir kii úŋ̀wuee ní.

eng: No discipline is fun while it lasts, but it seems painful at the time. Later, however, it yields the peaceful fruit of righteousness for those who have been trained by it.
local: Ibaamɔn didàkal kaa mɔ̄, ŋyunti ŋun a bí di-poonee. An wù ní, ama boonee, bin bi dàkēe kpántí binimɔntiibi ní kí páatí dipɔɔsɔŋkil nì deedeebítam.

eng: So strengthen your drooping hands and weak knees!.
local: An puee, nimbi bin ŋalii nì ni-taaŋi yɔ̄tèe, pɔ́ɔm̀maan ni-ba kí ká kunyannyaau.

eng: Make straight paths for your feet so that if any part is lame, it will be healed rather than injured more seriously.
local: kí nìkī ni-taatandi kí dī nsan ŋun tèen̄ee pu án nín làá ŋá pu ditaal din wùee ń taa gíí ama dí pɔ̄ɔkèe.

eng: Pursue the goal of peace along with everyone—and holiness as well, because no one will see the Lord without it.
local: Ní pɔ́ɔǹ ni-ba ŋgbansɔŋfi ń nīn bí ni nì báà ŋma kansikin, kí nīn bímaan circir. Kun puee un yaa kaa bí circiree kpá kí ká ti-Dindaan.

eng: Make sure that no one misses out on Gods grace. Make sure that no root of bitterness grows up that might cause trouble and pollute many people.
local: Ní nīn nyíǹ ni-ba ubɔ ń taa fá diyimɔɔl din Unimbɔti tīī-tee, kí nīn nyíǹ ni-ba ubɔ ń taa kpántí kii kisubiki kin cáá tiloolee kí dū u-lootee kí bīil̀ kunipaau.

eng: Make sure that no one becomes sexually immoral or ungodly like Esau. He sold his inheritance as the oldest son for one meal.
local: Ní nīn nyíǹ ni-ba ubɔ ń taa ŋá tilaakpaal, kí taa kpèè Unimbɔti fám, kii Eesayuu nín nìn ŋá pu, tijin puee u dū u-kpiti ki nyáfèe.

eng: You know that afterward, when he wanted to inherit the blessing, he was rejected because he couldnt find a way to change his heart and life, though he looked for it with tears.
local: Kéè, ni nyí yii boonee, u nín dāa nyàab̄ kí fōō difakal din ujipɔŋkpil fòol̄ee, waa ti káa! Nì min kɔkɔ u dū nnyinnyim ki nyàab̄ u nín làá dī pu kí kpántí min u nìn lēe, waa ká.

eng: You havent drawn near to something that can be touched: a burning fire, darkness, shadow, a whirlwind.
local: An kaa sá dijool kii Siinaayi joolee ní ni sútìń ki pìl̄: laakin ŋŋmi wù, dibɔmbɔndi dɔ́miń, kubuŋkpaaŋŋu jítì.

eng: a blast of a trumpet, and a sound of words that made the ones who heard it beg that there wouldnt be one more word.
local: ní dikantándi búń ki pɔ̄ɔ̄ kpaŋkpam, ní nneen ŋubɔ sòon̄ee. Isirayeel yab nín gbìl̄ nneenee puee ní bi gbāam̀ yii ŋú taa ti sòoǹ.

eng: and fix our eyes on Jesus, faiths pioneer and perfecter. He endured the cross, ignoring the shame, for the sake of the joy that was laid out in front of him, and sat down at the right side of Gods throne.
local: Ti-nimbikpeeŋu ń nīn pée cɔ́ Yeesu pu. Úǹ di nāantí fookii ti-ni kí tin cáá kátí-ku. U kíí ki kpíì ndɔpuntikaa kúm min sá icoo nì kpeefamee, kun puee u nìn nyí dipɔɔpindi din dɔ̄ ki cí-uee. Kɔŋkɔnnee u cūtìi kāl̀ Unimbɔti ŋaŋgiiliŋu pú dibeelin.

eng: They couldnt stand the command, If even a wild animal touches the mountain, it must be stoned. .
local: Di sá yii bi nìn fāŋkì Unimbɔti nín sòon̄ yii: «Un yaa gbēē dijool neeyee, báà an yaa sá kuwaŋkuluuaaa, bí yāŋkì-ku ataŋkpee kí kpɔ̀ee.».

eng: The sight was so frightening that Moses said, Im terrified and shaking.
local: An nìn cáá dipɔɔbiildi kaa bí ŋkpèé, áli Mooyiisi u-ba bí yii: «M fāŋkì yɔ́kɔ́ yɔkɔ.».

eng: But you have drawn near to Mount Zion, the city of the living God, heavenly Jerusalem, to countless angels in a festival gathering.
local: Ama nimbi dɔ́miń Siiyɔn jool taapu nì Unimbɔti un bí dimaŋfalinee dumpu Jeerusaleem un bí yilpuee, laakin ituun cutimaanyi kpátì ki bí mmɔnnee ní.

eng: to the assembly of Gods firstborn children who are registered in heaven, to God the judge of all, to the spirits of the righteous who have been made perfect.
local: Nimbi dɔ́miń laakin bin sá Unimbɔti biyaamu mun u péé péé ki màl̄ bi-yiŋŋi ŋmàn̄ yilpuee kpáfée ní. Ni dɔ́miń Unimbɔti u-ba cee ní, úǹ di sòoǹ binib kɔkɔ bɔti nì binib bin bí deedee Unimbɔti yāntì bi-nammu kpántì mmɔntiimu baa kāntí mu-pu tikpitil tibee.

eng: to Jesus the mediator of the new covenant, and to the sprinkled blood that speaks better than Abels blood.
local: Nimbi dɔ́miń Yeesu un cáań-ti dipoompɔndi ki dū u-ba ki tīī u-fatikuu fíntì-tee cee ní, u-fatikuuee sòoǹ tiŋan ki jítiǹ Abeel yɔu.

eng: See to it that you dont resist the one who is speaking. If the people didnt escape when they refused to listen to the one who warned them on earth, how will we escape if we reject the one who is warning from heaven?.
local: An puee ní nīn nyíǹ ni-ba kí taa yíí un bí ki sòoǹ nì ni nee nyɔkɔ. Bin nìn yíì kaa fōō un nìn bí bi-cee taapu doo ki sòoǹ nì bee nyɔkɔee, bi nìn dáaĺ bi-tafal ní, kin ataa aŋa ní ti yaa yíì kaa fōō un nyánní yilpu ki sòon̄ nì tee nyɔkɔee, taa kāntí ditafadaaldi!.

eng: His voice shook the earth then, but now he has made a promise: Still once more I will shake not only the earth but heaven also .
local: U nín nìn sòon̄ dijool puee, kitiŋ nìn cīŋkì ní, ama kɔŋkɔnnee u pōoǹ yii u làá dāá gíti cìŋkì kitiŋ. Niinee an kaa dāa sá kitiŋ ŋmaniŋma, u làá dāá cìŋkì kí kpáaǹ yilpu mun.

eng: The words still once more reveal the removal of what is shaken—the things that are part of this creation—so that what isnt shaken will remain.
local: Anyɔɔbun ŋin sá yii «u làá gíti cìŋkèe» dàkà yii tin kɔkɔ u náańee, ní tin kaa bá ki pɔ̄ɔ̄yee ń lítí kí wāŋkì kí kíń tin bá ki pɔ̄ɔ̄yee.

eng: Therefore, since we are receiving a kingdom that cant be shaken, lets continue to express our gratitude. With this gratitude, lets serve in a way that is pleasing to God with respect and awe.
local: Kii Unimbɔti nín kāaǹ-ti Dibeel din bá ki pɔ̄ɔ̄yee poon puee, tí jāam̀-u u-ŋanti gbanti pu kí nīn ŋáań min fáatì-uee kí nīn tɔ́-u nì tifaandi poon kí dàkà-u yii ti nyí tiŋan tin u ŋá ki tīī-tee.

eng: because our God really is a consuming fire.
local: Di sá yii ti-Nimbɔtiu sá ŋŋmi ŋun wù ki céetée ní.

eng: Think about the one who endured such opposition from sinners so that you wont be discouraged and you wont give up.
local: Ní dàkafì u nín kaa mɔ́tì u-ba pu ki gīī digìńdi din binikpitib ŋaanee, an di ŋūǹ kí yàntī ní taa pɔ̄ nsan diwuŋyɔtil ń kɔ́-ni.

eng: In your struggle against sin, you havent resisted yet to the point of shedding blood.
local: Doooo nee, naa láá jáń nì ikpiti áli fatiku nyáń.

eng: and you have forgotten the encouragement that addresses you as sons and daughters: My child, dont make light of the Lords discipline or give up when you are corrected by him, .
local: Ni súntì isaŋki in Unimbɔti nìn sáŋkì-ni kii ubaati nín sáŋkí u-jipɔmbi pueeee? U nìn bí-ni yii: «M-jipɔɔn, ti-Dindaan yaa bíi dàkà-see, á taa dūú kpèè fám. U yaa dáaĺ a-tafalee, á taa yɔ̀tī a-wunti.

eng: because the Lord disciplines whomever he loves, and he punishes every son or daughter whom he accepts. .
local: Kun puee un ti-Dindaan néeńee ní u dàkà, ní ki dátí bin kɔkɔ u fōō ki ŋá u-jipɔmbee tafal.».

eng: Bear hardship for the sake of discipline. God is treating you like sons and daughters! What child isnt disciplined by his or her father?.
local: An puee, Unimbɔti yaa bíi sáŋkí-nee, ní pɔ́ɔǹ ni-ba. Ni nín sá u-biyaamuee pu ní u ŋáań-ni míǹ, kun puee kibiki kpá ki-baa kaa sáŋkí-kì.

eng: But if you dont experience discipline, which happens to all children, then you are illegitimate and not real sons and daughters.
local: Ubaati yaa bíi dàkà u-biyaamu ní kaa bíi dàkà nimbee, mimmee waa yì nimbi dee, naa sá u-bimaalkaamu.

eng: Whats more, we had human parents who disciplined us, and we respected them for it. How much more should we submit to the Father of spirits and live?.
local: Tí kpèè ti-baabi bin màl̄-tee nín dàkà-ti ti tɔ́-bi miŋyee, kin ataa aŋa pu ní taa máaǹ kí nīn tɔ́ Ubaati un yì ŋfamee kí ká dimaŋfal!.

eng: Keep loving each other like family.
local: Ní nīn néeḿmaan tɔb kii nnabiyaamu nín néeń tɔb puee án taa gbèen̄.

eng: We have an altar, and those who serve as priests in the meeting tent dont have the right to eat from it.
local: Timbi saraafaŋŋu ŋun ti cááyee, Juuda yab saraaŋaliibi kaa ŋūǹ kí jī nsaraafan gbanti pu jinti.

eng: The blood of the animals is carried into the holy of holies by the high priest as an offering for sin, and their bodies are burned outside the camp.
local: Di sá Juuda yab saraaŋakpiliu nìn kù tiwaŋkuti ní kí lēē fatiku kí kɔ́ǹ circir páaa laŋki kí ŋá saraa binib kpitii pu, ní kí yòon̄ tiwaŋkutee kí nyāntí kitiŋ kpaanceetiŋu kí dūú wà ŋŋmi.

eng: And so Jesus also suffered outside the city gate to make the people holy with his own blood.
local: Míǹ mbaantiim ní bi ŋá Yeesu, bi nyántì-u kitiŋ kpaanceetiŋu ní ki kūū-u. An ŋá míǹ u-fatikuu ń fíntí binib kpitii.

eng: So now, lets go to him outside the camp, bearing his shame.
local: An puee ti mun ń nyā kitiŋ kpaanceetiŋu kí cù u-cee bí bèel̀-ti kii bi nín bēel̀-u puee.

eng: We dont have a permanent city here, but rather we are looking for the city that is still to come.
local: Kun puee duulinya kaa sá-ti kitiŋ kin làá nīn bí ki cá jaanjaanee, an pu ní ti bí ki nyàab̀ yilpu tiŋki kin làá nīn bí jaanjaanee.

eng: So lets continually offer up a sacrifice of praise through him, which is the fruit from our lips that confess his name.
local: Yeesu puee ti-nyɔŋkii ń nīn sá saraa ti tìì Unimbɔti múlmul, an dàkà yii tí nīn pàkà u-yindi kí taa gbèen̄.

eng: Dont forget to do good and to share what you have because God is pleased with these kinds of sacrifices.
local: Ní taa súntí tiŋan diŋál pu, kí nīn tútúǹ tɔb, míǹ di sá saraabi bin ŋān̄ Unimbɔti ceeyee.

eng: Rely on your leaders and defer to them, because they watch over your whole being as people who are going to be held responsible for you. They need to be able to do this with pleasure and not with complaints about you, because that wouldnt help you.
local: Ní nīn tɔ́ bin kpòotī-nee kí sìntī ni-ba taapu kí tīī-bi. Bíǹ di mɔ́tí ni-nammu pu, kun puee bi dāa cáá kí sòoǹ bi-nyɔkɔpu yai Unimbɔti nimbiin. An puee, ni yaa tɔ́-bee, bi mun tūǹ bi-tundi nín sá puee nì dipɔɔpindi ní, ama bi yaa tūǹ ni-pu ditundi bi-pɔbiliŋ kaa sɔ̀m̀ee, nimbi ní an gīintí boon.

eng: Pray for us. Were sure that we have a good conscience, and we want to do the right thing in every way.
local: Ní nīn gbíntì ki mèé ti-pu. Ti nyí ti-pɔbiliŋ poon yii ti ŋá tin máaǹ tí ŋée. Ti là ŋyunti kɔkɔ poonee tí nīn ŋáań min bí deedeeyee ní.

eng: Im particularly asking you to do this so that I can be returned to you quickly.
local: M là ní baasii mèè m-pu, Unimbɔti ń pɔ̄-mi nsan ḿ ŋmā kí gítiń ni-cee malaa.

eng: Dont neglect to open up your homes to guests, because by doing this some have been hosts to angels without knowing it.
local: Ní téetí yii ni máaǹ kí nīn fòol̄ binicambi, kun puee biba nìn fōol̀ binicambi míǹ ní ki dāa kpáàa fōō Unimbɔti tuuŋyi kaa béè.

eng: May the God of peace, who brought back the great shepherd of the sheep, our Lord Jesus, from the dead by the blood of the eternal covenant.
local: Unimbɔti di tìì-ti ŋgbansɔŋfi. U fīkitì ti-Dindaan Yeesu un di sá upikpaakpilee ditaŋkpiilin. U-saraau fatikuu di náań dipoompɔndi din kaa kúntée ki tīī-ti.

eng: equip you with every good thing to do his will, by developing in us what pleases him through Jesus Christ. To him be the glory forever and always. Amen.
local: Unimbɔti gbanti ń tīī-ni ŋŋmam ní nīn pée ŋáań tin ŋān̄ee nì tin u lèe. Yeesu Kriisitoo puee, ú dī ti-pu kí ŋá min fáatiǹ-uee. Úǹ Yeesu Kriisitoo ní bí nīn pée tìì tinyool kí nīn cáa cá jaanjaan! Ameeni.

eng: I urge you, brothers and sisters, to put up with this message of encouragement, since Ive only written a short letter to you!.
local: Nnabiyaamu, tibɔti tin m ŋmàn̄-ni nee kaa fɔ̀kɔ̀, m mèé-ni ní jī kimɔtii kí pīlifì m-saŋkii gbanti.

eng: You should know that our brother Timothy has been set free. If he comes soon, we will travel together to see you.
local: M là ní kpáàa béé yii bi líì ti-nabiki Tiimootee sarkan. U yaa bāań ti-cee malaayee, man nì u di làá táá kí dāá màntì ni-pu.

eng: Greet your leaders and all of Gods holy people. The group from Italy greets you.
local: Ní jāam̀ ni-kpootiliibi bin kpòotī-ni nnimbɔtisan poonee nì bin kɔkɔ nóó Unimbɔtee. Ti-nabiyaamu mun bí Italii tiŋkinee jáaḿ-ni.

eng: May grace be with all of you.
local: Unimbɔti ŋanti ń nīn bí ni-kɔkɔ pu!.

eng: Remember prisoners as if you were in prison with them, and people who are mistreated as if you were in their place.
local: Ní nīn téetímaan bisarkadambi pu kí jī bi-falaau kii ni nì bi kɔkɔ di kpáfì ki bí sarkanee, kí nīn téetí bin bi sàantée pu kii ni nì bi kɔkɔ ní bi kpáfì ki sàantée.

eng: Marriage must be honored in every respect, with no cheating on the relationship, because God will judge the sexually immoral person and the person who commits adultery.
local: Ní tīīmaan dinimpucaal àá dicamɔndi tinyool, ní unimpu nì u-cal ń yūlíǹ tɔb deedee, di sá yii Unimbɔti làá dāá dáaĺ bilaakpaadambi nì bijɔkɔndambi tafal.

eng: Your way of life should be free from the love of money, and you should be content with what you have. After all, he has said, I will never leave you or abandon you .
local: Ní taa yàntī nnimbineel ń jī ni-pɔbiliŋ poon mpaan, ama ní yàntī án nīn mɔ̄ǹ-ni fiii un ni cááyee pu, kun puee Unimbɔti lī yii: «M kpá kí yàntī-si fám, maa làá dū-si kí fá míǹ jaanjaan.».

eng: This is why we can confidently say, The Lord is my helper, and I wont be afraid. What can people do to me? .
local: An pu ní ti cáá dimákal kí lì kí tɔ́kɔń dinyɔɔbundi nee pu yii: «Unimbɔti di jòoń-mi, an puee maa fàŋkí tiba, Ba ní unil làá ŋmā kí ŋá-mi?».

eng: Remember your leaders who spoke Gods word to you. Imitate their faith as you consider the way their lives turned out.
local: Téetímaan ni-kpilib bin nìn dàkā-ni Unimbɔti bɔtee pu, kí dàkafì kí kpèè min ní bi nìn nóó Unimbɔti ki dāa kpíìyee, ki mun ń bīfì bi-fookiiu un bi cáá Unimbɔti puee.

eng: Jesus Christ is the same yesterday, today, and forever!.
local: Kun puee Yeesu Kriisitoo kaa kpántí. U nín nìn pée bí puee, míǹ ní u gbíntì ki bí díǹ, ki làá nīn pée bí míǹ kí nīn cá jaanjaan.

eng: Dont be misled by the many strange teachings out there. Its a good thing for the heart to be strengthened by grace rather than by food. Food doesnt help those who live in this context.
local: Binib nyántì tibɔpɔn tin nyáń ti-bee ki dàkà, ní taa yàntī bí dū an bɔtee kí dàkà-ni ní gáatí. An ŋān̄ yii diyimɔɔl din Unimbɔti pú-tee pu ní tí nīn kāntí mpɔn, án taa nín̄ sá marab bin binib ŋúb̀ ki kòó tijin tibee pu. Bin ŋúb̀ an marab gbantee kaa ká ipaati iba poon.

eng: This is why its necessary for us to pay more attention to what we have heard, or else we may drift away from it.
local: An puee, an máaǹ yii tí ŋúb́ ibaamɔn bɔti tin ti gbìl̄ee tiŋan án nín làá ŋá pu tí taa wāŋkèe.

eng: It was appropriate for God, for whom and through whom everything exists, to use experiences of suffering to make perfect the pioneer of salvation. This salvation belongs to many sons and daughters whom hes leading to glory.
local: Unimbɔti di náań tikɔkɔ nì tikɔkɔ ní u-pu ní tikɔkɔ bí. U là inibool tikpil ń kpántí u-biyaamu kí kɔ́ u-nyootin. Di sá yii Yeesu di fìil̄-bi, míǹ puee an kítì yii Unimbɔti ń dī u-falaabi bin u jíńee pu kí yàntī ú kpántí mmɔm.

eng: This is because the one who makes people holy and the people who are being made holy all come from one source. That is why Jesus isnt ashamed to call them brothers and sisters when he says.
local: Un kpántí binib circiree nì bin kpántì circiree kɔkɔ Baa sá ubaanti ní, an pu ní an kaa cáá Yeesu icoo ú yíiń-bi u-naalib.

eng: I will publicly announce your name to my brothers and sisters. I will praise you in the middle of the assembly. .
local: U lī yii: «Unimbɔti, m làá sòoǹ nì m-naalib a nín sá uŋyee, kí gāā ilan kí pàkà-si kunipaau kansikin.».

eng: He also says, I will rely on him. And also, Here I am with the children whom God has given to me. .
local: Ní ki gíti lī yii: «Sii pu ní m làá nīn pée máká.» Ní ki gíti kūbitì ki lī yii: «Kpèè, man nì mbiyaamu mun Unimbɔti tīī-mee sèé.».

eng: Therefore, since the children share in flesh and blood, he also shared the same things in the same way. He did this to destroy the one who holds the power over death—the devil—by dying.
local: Mbiyaamu gbantee nín sá binib tiwun nì fatiku puee ní Yeesu gíiǹ u-ba tiwun nì fatiku kii bíǹ nín bí puee. U ŋá míǹ kí ŋmā kí kpú, u-kúmee ń nàan̄ Sitaan un ŋúb́ ŋkúm pɔŋŋuee.

eng: He set free those who were held in slavery their entire lives by their fear of death.
local: kí nyāntí bi nín pée màl̄ bin bi bí kuyuŋu poon kun puee bi fàŋkí ŋkúmee.

eng: Of course, he isnt trying to help angels, but rather hes helping Abrahams descendants.
local: Di sá yii an kaa sá ituun ní u dɔ́miń ki tútúǹ, ama an sá Abraam biyaamu ní u dɔ́miń ki tútúǹ.

eng: Therefore, he had to be made like his brothers and sisters in every way. This was so that he could become a merciful and faithful high priest in things relating to God, in order to wipe away the sins of the people.
local: An pu ní an sá yii ú dū u-ba kí nántíǹ u-naalib tikɔkɔ poon kí ŋmā kí nīn sá bi-saraaŋakpiliu ki cáá disaasɔl ki tūǹ u-tundi deedee Unimbɔti nimbiin, án nín làá ŋá pu bí ká bi-kpitii pɔ̄tamee.

eng: Hes able to help those who are being tempted, since he himself experienced suffering when he was tempted.
local: U nín jíń falaa icɔŋki poon ki kée di yì-tì ní u ŋūǹ kí jòò bin bí icɔŋkinee.

eng: If the message that was spoken by angels was reliable, and every offense and act of disobedience received an appropriate consequence.
local: Kun puee Unimbɔti nín nìn yāntì ituun sòon̄ tibɔti tiŋyee, bi ká ti-baamɔŋyi. Di sá yii bin nìn yīntì kaa tɔ́ ituunee nín lī puee nìn ká ditafadaaldi din máaǹ-bee.

eng: how will we escape if we ignore such a great salvation? It was first announced through the Lord, and then it was confirmed by those who heard him.
local: Ti mun yaa yíì kaa fōō difiil dikpaandi din bí neeyee, mana ní ti làá ŋá kí ŋmátí kí nyā ditafadaaldin? Ti-Dindaan Yeesu di péé ki sòon̄ difiil gbanti bɔti ní bin gbìl̄-tèe tùkū-ti tì nín sá ibaamɔn miŋyee.

eng: God also vouched for their message with signs, amazing things, various miracles, and gifts from the Holy Spirit, which were handed out the way he wanted.
local: Ní Unimbɔti ŋáaǹ tidaandi nì maamaacib ki dū u-pɔŋŋu ki túń atuun kupaau kí dàkà yii tin bi sòon̄ee sá mbaamɔn, ní ki yākatì u-Fam pɔŋŋu min fáatì-uee ki tīī binib.

eng: God didnt put the world that is coming under the angels control.
local: Di sá duulinya un bí ki dòoń ti bí ki sòoǹ u-pu nee, an kaa sá ituun di làá dāá nīn kpòotī-u.

eng: Instead, someone declared somewhere, What is humanity that you think about them? Or what are human beings that you care about them? .
local: Ubɔ lī Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon laaba yii: «Unil sá ba ní a téetí u-pu ní? Nisaal bí tilatin ní a kpèé-u ní?.

eng: For a while you made them lower than angels. You crowned human beings with glory and honor. .
local: A sīntiǹ-u taapu ŋyunti ŋubɔ fiii ituun ń jītíǹ-u. Ama kɔŋkɔnnee a tīī-u tinyool nì kupampakau poon.

eng: You put everything under their control. When he puts everything under their control, he doesnt leave anything out of control. But right now, we dont see everything under their control yet.
local: ní ki tīī-u mpɔn tiwan kɔkɔwoooo pu.» Unimbɔti nín tīī-u mpɔn tikɔkɔ puee dàkā yii tiba kaa kíǹ kaa bí u-taapu. Nì míǹ kɔkɔee, taa láá wāĺ tiwan kɔkɔ nín bí u-taapu kɔŋkɔnnee puee.

eng: However, we do see the one who was made lower in order than the angels for a little while—its Jesus! Hes the one who is now crowned with glory and honor because of the suffering of his death. He suffered death so that he could taste death for everyone through Gods grace.
local: Ama tin ti dáań ki kée di sá yii Yeesu un ní Unimbɔti sīntiǹ taapu ituun jítiǹ-u ŋyunti ŋubɔ poon fiiiyee, kɔŋkɔnnee ti wāĺ-u tinyool nì kupampakau poon ní. Ŋkúm min u jíń falaa ki kpíìyee pu ní Unimbɔti yōoǹ-u yilpu míǹ. Unimbɔti ŋanti pu ní Yeesu kpíì an kúm binib kɔkɔ pu.

eng: Therefore, brothers and sisters who are partners in the heavenly calling, think about Jesus, the apostle and high priest of our confession.
local: An puee m-nabiyaamu, nimbi bin Unimbɔti yíiń ni sá u-yabee, ní nīn kpèé Yeesu. Úǹ ní Unimbɔti túnní ú nīn sá ti-saraaŋakpiliu, ní u-pu ní ti fōō ki kíí.

eng: So I was angry with them. I said, Their hearts always go off course, and they dont know my ways. .
local: An pu ní m gíí binib gbanti pu diŋuul, ki bí yii: <Binib nee mɔ̀tīi gáatí baabadal ní, baa dī m-sàmbiyaamu.>.

eng: Because of my anger I swore: They will never enter my rest! .
local: An di yì-tì ní m nìn gíí diŋuul ki pūtì yii baa làá ti kɔ́ mpaan ŋun m bɔ́ntì bí bàà kɔ́ kí kàl̄ kí ŋùŋfèe ni!».

eng: Watch out, brothers and sisters, so that none of you have an evil, unfaithful heart that abandons the living God.
local: An puee m-nabiyaamu, ní nīn nyíǹ ni-ba, ubɔ ń taa nín̄ cáá dipɔɔkpitil din kaa cáá fookiiyee kí cúú u-ba kí nákatíǹ Unimbɔti un bí dimaŋfalinee.

eng: Instead, encourage each other every day, as long as its called today, so that none of you become insensitive to God because of sins deception.
local: Ama ní nīn tììmaan tɔb kunyannyaau baabadal ŋyunti nín dáá bí pu nee, án nín làá ŋá pu ikpiti ń taa kīil̀ ubɔ ni-kansikin udaan ń pɔ̄ɔkì u-pɔbilee.

eng: We are partners with Christ, but only if we hold on to the confidence we had in the beginning until the end.
local: Ti nín kíĺ ki cáá dimákal din Kriisitoo puee, ti yaa gbíntì ki ŋúb́ míǹ ki tin kátì-kuee di dàkà yii ti sá Kriisitoo yab.

eng: When it says, Today, if you hear his voice, dont have stubborn hearts as they did in the rebellion. .
local: A lì kii Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon nín lī puee yii: «Ni yaa gbìl̄ Unimbɔti bɔti díǹ, ní taa ŋá mpɔɔgbifin a lì kii ni-naanjab nín nìn ŋá mpɔɔgbifin puee.».

eng: Who was it who rebelled when they heard his voice? Wasnt it all of those who were brought out of Egypt by Moses?.
local: Bilab di nìn gbìl̄ Unimbɔti bɔti ní ki pɔ́ɔkì bi-pɔbiliŋ ní? An sá bin kɔkɔ Mooyiisi nìn nyántìń Eejipiti tiŋkinee.

eng: And with whom was God angry for forty years? Wasnt it with the ones who sinned, whose bodies fell in the desert?.
local: Bilab pu ní Unimbɔti nìn gíí diŋuul áli abin imuŋku ili? An sá bin nìn ŋá ikpiti ki kpíì kuteeunee.

eng: And against whom did he swear that they would never enter his rest, if not against the ones who were disobedient?.
local: Bilab pu ní Unimbɔti nìn pūtì yii: «Bi kpá kí kɔ́ mpaan ŋun m bɔ́ntì bí bàà kɔ́ kí kàl̄ kí ŋùŋfèe ni ní?» An sá bin nìn ŋá mpɔɔgbifinee ní u nìn lī míǹ.

eng: We see that they couldnt enter because of their lack of faith.
local: Ti lafun ká yii baa nìn ŋúń ki kɔ́ diŋuŋfil laŋki, kun puee baa nìn fōō Unimbɔti ki kíí.

eng: Jesus was faithful to the one who appointed him just like Moses was faithful in Gods house.
local: Di sá yii u yúliǹ Unimbɔti un lēē-u ú tɔ̄ an tundee deedee, kii Mooyiisi nín nìn túń Unimbɔti dicaŋkun ditundi deedee puee.

eng: But he deserves greater glory than Moses in the same way that the builder of the house deserves more honor than the house itself.
local: Bi pàkà un máń kudiiyee ki jítiǹ kudii ku-ba. Míǹ mbaantiim ní Yeesu nyooti jítiǹ Mooyiisi yati.

eng: Every house is built by someone, but God is the builder of everything.
local: Kudii kamaa cáá un máń-kuee ní, ní un di māā tikɔkɔee di sá Unimbɔti.

eng: Moses was faithful in all Gods house as a servant in order to affirm the things that would be spoken later.
local: Mooyiisi úŋ̀wuee, u sá utɔntɔnli ní ki túń ditundi nì deedee Unimbɔti dicaŋkun. U-tundee nìn sá kí dàkà tin tin Unimbɔti làá dāá sòoǹ foolboonee ní.

eng: But Jesus was faithful over Gods house as a Son. We are his house if we hold on to the confidence and the pride that our hope gives us.
local: Ama Kriisitoo úǹ sá Unimbɔti Jipɔɔn ní ki yì Unimbɔti dicaŋku. Timbi di sá Unimbɔti dicaŋkuee, ti yaa gbíntì ki máká u-pu, ki cɔ́tí u-pu ki tin kátì-kuee.

eng: So, as the Holy Spirit says, Today, if you hear his voice, .
local: An puee, kii Circir Fam nín lī puee, yii: «Ni yaa gbìl̄ Unimbɔti bɔti díǹ.

eng: dont have stubborn hearts as they did in the rebellion, on the day when they tested me in the desert. .
local: ní taa ŋá mpɔɔgbifin, a lì kii ni-naanjab nín nìn ŋá mpɔɔgbifin ki cɔ́ŋkì Unimbɔti kuteeun puee.

eng: That is where your ancestors challenged and tested me, though they had seen my work for forty years. .
local: Kuteeun niin ní ni-naanjab nìn cɔ́ŋkì-mi kí kpèè m nín sá uŋyee, ní m dàkā-bi m nín sá uŋyee, áli abin imuŋku ili.

eng: Therefore, since the promise that we can enter into rest is still open, lets be careful so that none of you will appear to miss it.
local: Kii dipoondi din sá tí kɔ́ Unimbɔti ŋuŋfilinee nín dáá bí pu nee, tí fàŋkìmaan, ni-kansikin ubɔ ń taa ŋá u-ba kí dāá gbíntí boon.

eng: The one who entered Gods rest also rested from his works, just as God rested from his own.
local: Un yaa kɔ́ laakin Unimbɔti kā ki ŋùŋfēe mun ŋùŋfī ní. Kii Unimbɔti u-ba nín náań duulinya ki dóò ki kā ki ŋùŋfī puee, míǹ ní udaan mun yàntī u-tuuŋŋi kí ŋùŋfì.

eng: Therefore, lets make every effort to enter that rest so that no one will fall by following the same example of disobedience.
local: An puee, timbi ń pɔ́ɔǹ ti-ba kí kɔ́ diŋuŋfil gbanti poon, kí taa fōō ti-naanjab yaŋyam kí lántí-dì.

eng: because Gods word is living, active, and sharper than any two-edged sword. It penetrates to the point that it separates the soul from the spirit and the joints from the marrow. Its able to judge the hearts thoughts and intentions.
local: Di sá yii Unimbɔti bɔtee, tì cáá dimaŋfal ní, ní ki tūǹ ditundi ki jītí ki jítiǹ bɔlfi un jītí ajan liitil puee, ki gífí malaa, kí cúú kinaŋ nì ŋfam kí yàkatī tɔbin, ki kóò dikpabilin ki bà ncoom, ki béetí unil nín màkà kí ŋá puee nì u-pɔbil poon landɔkɔi.

eng: No creature is hidden from it, but rather everything is naked and exposed to the eyes of the one to whom we have to give an answer.
local: Tiwannáaŋkaal tiba kaa bí Unimbɔti kaa wāĺ-tì. Tin kɔkɔ pée bí duulinyanee dɔ̄ mpaampiin pu ní úǹ cee. U-nimbiin ní ti làá dāá sòoǹ ti-nyɔkɔpu yai.

eng: Also, lets hold on to the confession since we have a great high priest who passed through the heavens, who is Jesus, Gods Son.
local: Yeesu, Unimbɔti Jipɔɔn di sá usaraaŋakpil un sá ukpaan ki jɔ́m̀ doooo yilpu fɔ́ŋŋŋ kí nīn tútúǹ-tee. An puee tí mɔ̀tīí nīn ŋúb́ fookii un ti cáá u-puee gbīŋgbim.

eng: because we dont have a high priest who cant sympathize with our weaknesses but instead one who was tempted in every way that we are, except without sin.
local: Kun puee timbi saraaŋakpiliuee sá un nyí ti-wɔfii ki sūǹ ti-saayee. Di sá yii u gíiǹ u-ba kii timbee ní, nniyaamin, ní ki béè iwɔsɔfi, ní Sitaan cɔ́ŋkì-u ama waa lítì ikpitin.

eng: Finally, lets draw near to the throne of favor with confidence so that we can receive mercy and find grace when we need help.
local: Míǹ puee, kámaan kunyannyaau kí sútiń kí pìl̄maan Unimbɔti beejal nì dimákal. Niin ní u-ŋanti bí. Niin ní u làá dàkà-ti u-ŋanti kí dū ti-kpitii kí pɔ̄-ti ní kí tūtuǹ-ti ŋyunti ŋun an máaǹee.

eng: We also had the good news preached to us, just as the Israelites did. However, the message they heard didnt help them because they werent united in faith with the ones who listened to it.
local: Kun puee bi sòon̄ Tibɔmɔntiil ti gbìl̄ kii bi nín nìn pée tí sòon̄ nì ti-naanjab bi mun gbìl̄ puee ní. Di sá bi nìn gbìl̄ tibɔtee kɔkɔ, ama ní kaa fōotìi yīkiǹ, kun puee bi nín nìn gbìl̄-tèe, baa nìn fōō-tì ki kíí.

eng: We who have faith are entering the rest. As God said, And because of my anger I swore: They will never enter into my rest! And yet Gods works were completed at the foundation of the world.
local: Timbi bin fōō ki kííyee, ti làá kɔ́ diŋuŋfil laŋki, laakin ní Unimbɔti lī yii: «M nìn gíí diŋuul ki pūtì yii mpaan ŋun m bɔ́ntì bí bàà kɔ́ kí kàl̄ kí ŋùŋfèe nee, bi kpá kí kɔ́!» U nìn lī míǹ, nì min kɔkɔ u nìn bɔ́ntì u-tundi ki síiǹ ki wāatì ní ki nín náań duulinyee.

eng: Then somewhere he said this about the seventh day of creation: God rested on the seventh day from all his works. .
local: Di sá Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon sòon̄ ŋwinluluúti pu yii: «Unimbɔti ŋūŋfì u-tuuŋŋi kɔkɔ pu ŋwinluluúti dal.».

eng: But again, in the passage above, God said, They will never enter my rest! .
local: Ní an dāa nín sòon̄ doo yii: «Unimbɔti bí yii: <Bi kpá kí kɔ́ mpaan ŋun m bɔ́ntì binib ń kɔ́ kí ŋùŋfèe ni.>».

eng: Therefore, its left open for some to enter it, and the ones who had the good news preached to them before didnt enter because of disobedience.
local: Bin bi nìn péé péé ki sòon̄ Tibɔmɔntiilee ki tīīyee nìn ŋá mpɔɔgbifin, an puee baa nìn ŋúń ki kɔ́ diŋuŋfil gbanti poon, ama dipoondee dáá gbíntìi bí, biba làá ŋmā kí kɔ́.

eng: Just as it says in the passage above, God designates a certain day as today, when he says through David much later, Today, if you hear his voice, dont have stubborn hearts. .
local: Kun puee Unimbɔti gíti yóoǹ ŋwimpɔɔn ŋun binib làá kɔ́ u-ŋuŋfil laŋkee. Ŋúǹ di sá «díǹ». Di sá yii abin kupaau boonee, u nìn gíti yāntì ubɔti Daafiidi sòon̄ an pu, kii ti nín dáá kàan̄ní-tì pu nee yii: «Ni yaa gbìl̄ Unimbɔti bɔti díǹ, ní taa ŋá mpɔɔgbifin.».

eng: If Joshua gave the Israelites rest, God wouldnt have spoken about another day later on.
local: Di sá yii laakin Joosuwa nìn cáá Isirayeel yab ki cūnnèe di yaan nìn sá diŋuŋfil laŋki, laakin Unimbɔti bée, Unimbɔti kaa bàà nìn gíti sòoǹ diŋuŋfil diba mun pu.

eng: So you see that a sabbath rest is left open for Gods people.
local: An puee, ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa mmɔnti ŋubɔ gíti bí nyɔkɔpu ki cí bin sá Unimbɔti yabee.

eng: Every high priest is taken from the people and put in charge of things that relate to God for their sake, in order to offer gifts and sacrifices for sins.
local: Binib kansikin ní bi lèekī ubɔ ú nīn sá usaraaŋakpil kí nīn ŋáań bin kɔkɔ kíǹee yil pu saraabi Unimbɔti nimbiin, ní u ŋáań saraabi binib kpitii pu.

eng: He was appointed by God to be a high priest according to the order of Melchizedek.
local: Unimbɔti di lēē-u ú nīn sá usaraaŋakpil kii Meelkiiseedeeki nín nìn sá puee.

eng: We have a lot to say about this topic, and its difficult to explain, because you have been lazy and you havent been listening.
local: Ti bàà cáá tibɔti tikpil kí sòoǹ nì ni an pu, ama ní an pɔ̄ɔ̄ tí bɔ́bití taapu kí tùkù-ni, kun puee ni sá binib bin sɔ̀m̀ kí gbìl̄ tibɔti taapu malaayee ní.

eng: Although you should have been teachers by now, you need someone to teach you an introduction to the basics about Gods message. You have come to the place where you need milk instead of solid food.
local: Ni bàà máaǹ ki kpántì doooo nee bin dàkà bin kíǹee Unimbɔti bɔtee, ama nimbi di gíti bí ki nyàab̀ bí dàkà-ni Unimbɔti bɔti tin bi péetí kí dàkà bin fōō ki kíí mpɔmee. An nín bàà sá ní nīn jīǹ binicikitikaabi jintee, ní ni dū mbiim ki ŋá ni-jinti kii mbiyaamu mun ŋáaḿee.

eng: Everyone who lives on milk is not used to the word of righteousness, because they are babies.
local: Un ní mbiim sá-u tijinee, udaan dáá sá kii kibiki kin ŋáaḿee dee, waa ŋūǹ kí béé min ŋān̄ Unimbɔti nimbiinee.

eng: But solid food is for the mature, whose senses are trained by practice to distinguish between good and evil.
local: Ama tijin tin nyìm̀ee sá ki tīī binicikitikaabi bin cáá nlandɔkɔ ki dàkafī ki nyí ki gàntī tin sá tiŋanee nì tin sá tikpitilee ní.

eng: The high priest is able to deal gently with the ignorant and those who are misled since he himself is prone to weakness.
local: Usaraaŋakpil gbanti nyí kí jī bin kaa nyí tibee nì bin yīntèe pu kimɔtii, kun puee úǹ u-ba sá unil ní ki cáá u-wɔfii.

eng: Because of his weakness, he must offer sacrifices for his own sins as well as for the people.
local: Kii u mun nín cáá u-wɔfiiyee puee, an sá yii ú ŋá u-kpitii pu saraa ní kí nín ŋá bin kíǹee kpitii pu saraau.

eng: No one takes this honor for themselves but takes it only when they are called by God, just like Aaron.
local: Ubɔ kaa ŋūǹ kí gāā u-bindi yii u làá ŋá disaraaŋatundi. Unimbɔti di lèekī kii u nín nìn lēē Aarɔn puee.

eng: In the same way Christ also didnt promote himself to become high priest. Instead, it was the one who said to him, You are my Son. Today I have become your Father .
local: Míǹ mbaantiim an kaa sá Kriisitoo u-ba di gāā u-bindi ki là jilma ní ki kpántì usaraaŋakpil. Un tīī-uee di sá un bí-u yii: «Sii di sá man Jipɔɔn, báà ŋma ń béé díǹ yii man di sá a-Baa.».

eng: as he also says in another place, You are a priest forever, according to the order of Melchizedek. .
local: An gíti lī laaba mun yii: «Bi lēē-si á nīn sá usaraaŋakpil kii Meelkiiseedeeki nín nìn sá puee kí nīn cá jaanjaan.».

eng: During his days on earth, Christ offered prayers and requests with loud cries and tears as his sacrifices to the one who was able to save him from death. He was heard because of his godly devotion.
local: Yeesu nín nìn dáá bí duulinyanee, u nìn mēē Unimbɔti un ŋūǹ kí fīī-u ŋkúminee ki gbāam̀-u nì ikuŋkuunti nì nnyinnyim. Unimbɔti nìn pílifiǹ-u, kun puee u nìn sīntiǹ u-ba taapu.

eng: Although he was a Son, he learned obedience from what he suffered.
local: Nì min kɔkɔ Yeesu sá Unimbɔti Jipɔɔnee, u bífì kí nīn tɔ́ Unimbɔti falaabi bin kɔkɔ u jíńee poon.

eng: After he had been made perfect, he became the source of eternal salvation for everyone who obeys him.
local: An pu ní u sá uniŋman umɔnti un pu baa kāntí tikpitil tibee, ki kpántì un tìì difiil din kaa cáá dikúntilee ki tīī bin kɔkɔ tɔ́-uee.

eng: So lets press on to maturity, by moving on from the basics about Christs word. Lets not lay a foundation of turning away from dead works, of faith in God.
local: An puee tí jītímaan tibɔpeepeekaal tin unil yaa fōō Kriisitoo ki kíí mpɔm bi dàkà-uee pu, kí nyàab̀ kí kpántí binicikitikaabi. An kaa máaǹ tí gbíntíǹ tibɔpeepeekaal tin un fōō ki kíí mpɔmee nyée pu, tin sá unil ń fá atuun ŋin kaa cáá ipaatee nì unil ń fōō Unimbɔti kí kííyee.

eng: God isnt unjust so that he forgets your efforts and the love you have shown for his names sake when you served and continue to serve Gods holy people.
local: Di sá yii Unimbɔti sá deedeedaan ní. Waa làá súntí dineendi din ni dàkà binib u-puee nì atummɔnti ŋin ni túń nì ŋin ni gbíntì ki nìkì ki tūǹ ki tìì bin sá u-yabee pu.

eng: But we desperately want each of you to show the same effort to make your hope sure until the end.
local: Tin ti nyàab̀ee di sá báà ŋma ń dàkà difaŋgiil míǹ kí tin kátí-ku, án nín làá ŋá pu ní dāá fōō kun pu ni máká ki tūǹ an tuuŋŋee.

eng: This is so you wont be lazy but follow the example of the ones who inherit the promises through faith and patience.
local: An puee ní taa bàtī ni-faŋgiil pu, ama ní táá bin dū bi-fookiiu nì bi-mɔtiiki ki fōō tin Unimbɔti pōoǹee tabɔbiliŋ.

eng: When God gave Abraham his promise, he swore by himself since he couldnt swear by anyone greater.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun Unimbɔti làá nìn pòon̄ Abraam dipoondee, kí dàkà-u yii u làá lafun ŋá min u pōoǹ-uee, Unimbɔti nìn pūtì ní. Tiba kaa nìn bí ki jítiǹ-u ú yíiń kudɔŋu yindi kí pùtēe puee, ní Unimbɔti dūu pūtì u-ba.

eng: He said, I will certainly bless you and multiply your descendants. .
local: U bí Abraam yii: «M pūtì m-ba yii m làá bìin̄ a-boon kí bùtī a-maaliŋu.».

eng: So Abraham obtained the promise by showing patience.
local: Abraam nín gbìl̄ mimmee, u jíń kimɔtii ní ki cíitì áli ŋyunti dāa bāań ní u fōō tin Unimbɔti pūtì ki pōoǹ yii u làá tīī-uee.

eng: People pledge by something greater than themselves. A solemn pledge guarantees what they say and shuts down any argument.
local: Binib bili yaa kpákèe, un jítiǹ-bi bi fàŋkée ní bi pùtī. Bi nín pūtì mimmee ní bi-nikpɔŋkpɔkɔkuee ń kúntí.

eng: When God wanted to further demonstrate to the heirs of the promise that his purpose doesnt change, he guaranteed it with a solemn pledge.
local: Míǹ mbaantiim ní Unimbɔti nín là kí dàkà u-yab yii u-poondee kaa bí kí kpántí jaanjaanee pu ní u pūtì.
