eng: So these are two things that dont change, because its impossible for God to lie. He did this so that we, who have taken refuge in him, can be encouraged to grasp the hope that is lying in front of us.
local: U tīī-ti tiwan tili tí ŋmā kí nīn máká ti-pu, dipoondi nì dipùtil. Tiwan tili gbanti puee, Unimbɔti kpá kí kpántí u-nyɔkɔ. U kpá kí mɔ̄ntì-ti, an puee timbi bin sáǹ ki dɔ́miń ki kɔ́-uee, tí kámaan kunyannyaau kí mɔ̀tīí dū ti-mákal kí báań tin u pōoǹ-tee pu tiŋan.

eng: This hope, which is a safe and secure anchor for our whole being, enters the sanctuary behind the curtain.
local: Dimákal din ti cáá yii ti làá dāá ká tin Unimbɔti pōoǹee di yì-tì ti bá ki yú ki ŋúb́ an pɔ̄ɔ̄. Dimákalee di jītí yilpu punyɔkɔki kí kɔ́ circir páaa laŋki.

eng: of teaching about ritual ways to wash with water, laying on of hands, the resurrection from the dead, and eternal judgment—all over again.
local: nì bi nín dàkà Unimbɔti nyiŋfàltam bɔti puee, nì bi nín pāantí iŋal binib pu ki mèé ki tìì-bee, nì bitaŋkpiibi fìkititam, nì ditafadaaldi din kaa cáá dikúntilee nín dɔ̄ ki cí binikpitib puee.

eng: Thats where Jesus went in advance and entered for us, since he became a high priest according to the order of Melchizedek.
local: Niin ní Yeesu līntì ti-nyɔkɔpu ki dī ki kɔ́ ki kpántì usaraaŋakpil kii Meelkiiseedeekee, ki gbàam̄ ti-pu kí nīn cá jaanjaan.

eng: Were going to press on, if God allows it.
local: An puee tí yàntīmaan tíǹ, Unimbɔti yaa lèe, tí póotí tipɔn.

eng: Because its impossible to restore people to changed hearts and lives who turn away once they have seen the light, tasted the heavenly gift, become partners with the Holy Spirit.
local: Di sá bin fōō Tibɔmɔntiil ki kíí ki wál̀ ibaamɔn, ki dáká diyimɔɔl din bí yilpuee mɔŋŋu, ki fōō Unimbɔti Fam.

eng: and tasted Gods good word and the powers of the coming age.
local: ki gbìl̄ Tibɔmɔntiilee taapu ki béè Unimbɔti pɔŋyi in bí ki dòońee.

eng: They are crucifying Gods Son all over again and exposing him to public shame.
local: ní boonee bidambi lítèe, baa ti ŋūǹ kí fàtīí gíiǹní-bi nsammɔnti poon. Di sá yii bidambi fātìi bí ki kpáà Unimbɔti Jipɔɔn ndɔpuntikaa pu nliliitiim, ki bí ki bíiĺ-u kunipaau kansikin dee.

eng: The ground receives a blessing from God when it drinks up the rain that regularly comes and falls on it and yields a useful crop for those people for whom it is being farmed.
local: Kitiŋ yaa bí ní ki fòol̄ ntaanyim múlmul ní ki lòoǹ tijin tin bi kùn̄ ki-puee, Unimbɔti bìintí ki-pu ní.

eng: But if it produces thorns and thistles, its useless and close to being cursed. It ends up being burned.
local: Ama kitiŋ gbanti yaa nyēē ikuŋkum nì abɔjanaakunee, mimmee bi ká-kì yii kàa bí tiban dee. Mbusu kaa pɔ̄ɔ̄ an tiŋkee dikɔl, kun puee ŋŋmi di dāa dòoń kí wà-kì.

eng: But we are convinced of better things in your case, brothers and sisters, even though we are talking this way—things that go together with salvation.
local: Nnabineeŋkaamu, nì min kɔkɔ ti bí ki sòoǹ nì ni míǹ nee, ti cáá ni-pu dimákal yii naa bí míǹ, ni tɔ́kɔ́ difiil saŋŋu pu.

eng: This Melchizedek, who was king of Salem and priest of the Most High God, met Abraham as he returned from the defeat of the kings, and Melchizedek blessed him.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun ní Abraam nìn cūtì ki jáń ki pɔ́ɔkiǹ bibɔtiibi ki fàtīi kùnnée, Meelkiiseedeeki di nìn túŋkì-u nsanni ki mēē ki bīiǹ u-pu. Meelkiiseedeeki gbanti di sá Saleem yab bɔtiu, ní ki gíti sá Unimbɔti un bí kutaa fɔ́ŋŋŋee saraaŋaliu.

eng: because he was still in his ancestors body when Abraham paid the tenth to Melchizedek.
local: Ŋyuntee, baa nìn dáá màl̄ Leefii, ama u nìn kā u-naanja Abraam poon ŋyunti ŋun Meelkiiseedeeki nìn túŋkìń-u nsannee.

eng: So if perfection came through the levitical office of priest , why was there still a need to speak about raising up another priest according to the order of Melchizedek rather than one according to the order of Aaron?.
local: Isirayeel nibul marau poonee, Leefii naakookun yab kansikin ní bi nìn lèekī bisaraaŋaliibi. Bi sá bisaraaŋaliibi Aarɔn yamee ní. Ama bi nín sá bisaraaŋaliibi puee kaa sá páaa, yaa sá fám, ba pu ní an bàà gíti bàlifī yii usaraaŋal ubɔ mun ń nīn gíti bí ki náań nì Meelkiiseedeeki? An kaa bàà gíti páb́ deeyaa.

eng: When the order of the priest changes, there has to be a change in the Law as well.
local: Mimmee, bi yaa kpántì bisaraaŋaliibee, see bí kpántí marab mun.

eng: The person we are talking about belongs to another tribe, and no one ever served at the altar from that tribe.
local: Ama ti-Dindaan un pu ní tibɔti nee sòon̄ ki dàkèe sá kunaakoou kun yab kaa pée ŋá disaraaŋatundi nsaraafan pu ki ká jaanjaanee ní.

eng: Its clear that our Lord came from the tribe of Judah, but Moses never said anything about priests from that tribe.
local: Báà ŋma nyí u-pu yii u sá Juuda naakookun ní. Mooyiisi kaa nìn tīī kunaakoou gbanti disaraaŋatundi báà fiii.

eng: And its even clearer if another priest appears who is like Melchizedek.
local: Tin bí nee gíti dàkà ki cántí tibɔti taapu, di sá usaraaŋal ubɔ gíti nyáń ki náań nì Meelkiiseedeeki.

eng: He has become a priest by the power of a life that cant be destroyed, rather than a legal requirement about physical descent.
local: Úǹ saraaŋátam nyáń u-ba, kun puee an kaa sá marab bin binib ŋmàn̄ ki yóoǹee pu ní u dī ki kpántì usaraaŋal, ama u nín cáá mpɔn kí nīn bí jaanjaanee pu ní u kpántì usaraaŋal.

eng: This is confirmed: You are a priest forever, according to the order of Melchizedek. .
local: Di sá yii Unimbɔti gbɔŋku sòon̄ u-pu yii: «Sii di làá nīn pée sá usaraaŋal kí nīn cáa cá jaanjaan, Meelkiiseedeeki yam.».

eng: On the one hand, an earlier command is set aside because it was weak and useless.
local: Mpeepeekaamee, bimarakpikib kpītì, kun puee baa pɔ̄ɔ̄ kaa tútúǹ binib.

eng: . On the other hand, a better hope is introduced, through which we draw near to God.
local: Nliliitiimee, marabee kaa ŋúń ki kítì-ti tí kpántí biniŋmambi bimɔntiibi bin pu baa kāntí ikpiti ibee. Ama Kriisitoo sá-ti dimákabaamɔndi din pu ti ŋūǹ kí dī kí sútí kí pìl̄ Unimbɔtee.

eng: Abraham gave a tenth of everything to him. His name means first king of righteousness, and then king of Salem, that is, king of peace.
local: Úǹ ní Abraam nìn lēē u-wanti tin u cááyee saalaa kamaa puee kubɔ kubɔ ki tīī. Meelkiiseedeeki taapu sá yii: Ubɔtikpaan un bí deedeeyee, ní boonee u gíti sá Saleem yab bɔtikpaaŋwu, an taapu sá yii ŋgbansɔŋfi bɔtikpaaŋwu.

eng: And this was not done without a solemn pledge! The others have become priests without a solemn pledge.
local: Kí wàatēe, Unimbɔti baasii pūtì ní ki bí yii Kriisitoo ń nīn sá usaraaŋal. Bisaraaŋaliibi bin kíǹee, waa pūtì ki lēē ubɔ míǹ.

eng: but this priest was affirmed with a solemn pledge by the one who said, The Lord has made a solemn pledge and will not change his mind: You are a priest forever. .
local: Ama Yeesu pu ní u pūtì ki lī yii: «Man ni-Dindaan di pūtì yii sii di làá nīn sá usaraaŋal kí nīn pée cáa cá jaanjaan, m kpá kí kɔ́ntí m-nyɔkɔ.».

eng: As a result, Jesus has become the guarantee of a better covenant.
local: An puee Yeesu di sá dipoommɔntiilee daaŋku kun dàkà yii án làá lafun ŋá mimmee.

eng: The others who became priests are numerous because death prevented them from continuing to serve.
local: Tin gíti nyáń u-ba nì Yeesu saraaŋátam nì bin kíǹee yamee di sá binib tikpil nìn túń disaraaŋatundi, kun puee bi nìn púl̀ ní ki kúù ní biba fòol̄.

eng: In contrast, he holds the office of priest permanently because he continues to serve forever.
local: Ama Yeesu úǹ pée bí ní jaanjaan, an puee ubɔ kaa fòol̄ u-saraaŋatundi.

eng: This is why he can completely save those who are approaching God through him, because he always lives to speak with God for them.
local: An pu ní u ŋūǹ kí fīī bin dì u-pu ki nyàab̀ Unimbɔtee an kɔkɔ pu, kun puee u pée bí ní. Waa sá un kúùyee, ní ki mèé Unimbɔti bi-pu.

eng: Its appropriate for us to have this kind of high priest: holy, innocent, incorrupt, separate from sinners, and raised high above the heavens.
local: Usaraaŋakpil un ti máaǹee dee. U bí circir, kaa ŋá ikpiti iba, ikpiti iba kaa bí u-pu. Unimbɔti cúú-u ki lēē bikpitib kansikin ki jɔ́m̀ yilpu fɔ́ŋŋŋ.

eng: He doesnt need to offer sacrifices every day like the other high priests, first for their own sins and then for the sins of the people. He did this once for all when he offered himself.
local: Waa bí kii bisaraaŋakpilib bin kíǹee nín bí puee, ki ŋáań bi-yil pu kpitii saraau baabadal kí wàatī kí nín ŋá samaa yɔuee. Ŋyunti ŋun u dū u-ba ki ŋá saraayee ní u kúntì saraabi ŋátam kɔkɔ.

eng: The Law appoints people who are prone to weakness as high priests, but the content of the solemn pledge, which came after the Law, appointed a Son who has been made perfect forever.
local: Mooyiisi marab nín yóoǹ bin bí nīn sá bisaraaŋakpilibee nìn sá binib ní. Baa cáá mpɔn bi-ba, ama ní boonee ní Unimbɔti sòon̄ ki pūtì ki lēē u-Jipɔɔn ki kāaǹ ú nīn sá usaraaŋakpil un sá uniŋman umɔnti un pu ikpiti iba kpée, kí nīn pée cáa cá jaanjaan.

eng: He is without father or mother or any family. He has no beginning or end of life, but hes like Gods Son and remains a priest for all time.
local: Meelkiiseedeeki gbantee kaa cáá baa, kaa cáá na, kaa cáá nnyeen. An kaa cáá yii bi màl̄-u, kaa cáá yii u kúù. U bí kii Unimbɔti Jipɔɔnee ní, u làá nīn pée sá usaraaŋal ní kí nīn cá jaanjaan.

eng: See how great Melchizedek was! Abraham, the father of the people, gave him a tenth of everything he captured.
local: Ní kpèè u nín sá ukpaan miŋyee. Ti-naanja Abraam u-ba nìn tīī-u tiwan tin u nìn jáń ki fōō kujaaunee saalaa kamaa puee kubɔ kubɔ ní.

eng: The descendants of Levi who receive the office of priest have a commandment under the Law to collect a tenth of everything from the people who are their brothers and sisters, though they also are descended from Abraham.
local: Mara poonee, Leefii naakookun yab bin sá bisaraaŋaliibee baba di nìn cáá nsan kí fōō bi-nabiyaamu mun sá Abraam maaliŋunee cee bi-wanti saalaa kamaa poonee kubɔ kubɔ.

eng: But Melchizedek, who isnt related to them, received a tenth of everything from Abraham and blessed the one who had received the promises.
local: Ama Meelkiiseedeeki gbanti kaa sá Leefii naakookun yab ubɔ ní ki fōō Abraam wanti saalaa kamaa puee kubɔ kubɔ, boonee ní ki mēē ki bīiǹ Abraam un Unimbɔti nìn líì dipoondee pu.

eng: Without question, the less important person is blessed by the more important person.
local: Ti-kɔkɔ pée nyí yii un jítiǹ-see di bìintí a-boon.

eng: In addition, in one case a tenth is received by people who die, and in the other case, the tenth is received by someone who continues to live, according to the record.
local: Juuda yab saraaŋaliibi bin fòol̄ bi-wanti saalaa kamaa pu kubɔ kubɔee sá binib ní, ki kúù, ama ti ká yii Meelkiiseedeeki úǹ pée bí ní kí nīn cá jaanjaan.

eng: It could be said that Levi, who received a tenth, paid a tenth through Abraham.
local: Ti ŋūǹ kí lì yii Juuda yab saraaŋaliibi bin bi-jutiibi nìn tìì bi-wanti saalaa kamaa pu kubɔ kubɔee naanja Leefii u-ba dī Abraam pu ki tīī Meelkiiseedeeki u-wanti saalaa kamaa puee kubɔ kubɔ.

eng: Now the main point of what we are saying is this: We have this kind of high priest. He sat down at the right side of the throne of the majesty in the heavens.
local: Tibɔti tin kɔkɔ ti sòon̄ nee, dibɔyil di sá yii, an saraaŋakpiliu juti ní ti cáá u kā yilpu Unimbɔti Mpɔŋkpaandaan ŋaŋgiiliŋu pú, dibeelin, tinyool laŋki.

eng: This is the covenant that I will make with the house of Israel after those days, says the Lord. I will place my laws in their minds, and write them on their hearts. I will be their God, and they will be my people. .
local: Ti-Dindaan bí yii: M nín làá dāá líí Isirayeel yab dipoondi diŋyee sèé: Ŋyunti gbantee, m làá dāá ŋmà m-marab kí ŋá bi-landɔkɔyin bí ŋmā kí nīn nyí min m lèe, ní kí dūú ŋmà bi-pɔbiliŋin bí ŋmā kí nīn ŋúb́-bi kí taa ti súntí. Míǹ ní m dāa sá bi-Nimbɔtiu ní bi mun dāa sá man samaau.

eng: And each person wont ever teach a neighbor or their brother or sister, saying, Know the Lord, because they will all know me, from the least important of them to the most important; .
local: Ubɔ kaa dāa gíti dàkà u-kɔŋkɔnnaatɔ àá u-nabiki yii ú nīn nyí ti-Dindaan. Kí yòon̄ bi-kansikin mbiyaawaamu kí tin cáá sāā bikpaambi, báà ŋma làá dāá béé-mi.

eng: because I will be lenient toward their unjust actions, and I wont remember their sins anymore. .
local: Kun puee m làá dāá sɔ̄ bi-saai kí dū bi-kpitii kí pɔ̄-bi, kaa làá ti pée téetí bi-kpitii gbanti pu.».

eng: When it says new, it makes the first obsolete. And if something is old and outdated, its close to disappearing.
local: Unimbɔti nín sòon̄ dipoompɔndi bɔtee, mimmee din béeyee ŋá dikpikil dee. Tiwan yaa ŋá tikpikilee ti púĺ dee, ki làá kúntí mun.

eng: Hes serving as a priest in the holy place, which is the true meeting tent that God, not any human being, set up.
local: Úǹ sá usaraaŋakpil un tūǹ ditundi laakin ti-Dindaan Unimbɔti u-ba máń an sá circiree ní, an kaa sá tanti un binib gāāyee poon kii bisaraaŋaliibi bin kíǹee nín ŋáań puee.

eng: Every high priest is appointed to offer gifts and sacrifices. So its necessary for this high priest also to have something to offer.
local: Bi kāaǹ bisaraaŋakpilib bí nīn ŋáań saraabi ki jáaḿ Unimbɔti ní kí nīn ŋáań saraabi binib kpitii pu. An puee u máaǹ kí nīn cáá tin tin u làá dūú ŋá saraayee.

eng: If he was located on earth, he wouldnt be a priest because there are already others who offer gifts based on the Law.
local: U yaa bàà bí duulinyan doo, waa bàà ŋūǹ kí nīn sá usaraaŋal kpaŋkpam. Di sá yii bisaraaŋaliibi bí ki ŋáań saraabi kii Juuda yab marab nín lī yii bí nīn ŋáań miŋyee.

eng: They serve in a place that is a copy and shadow of the heavenly meeting tent. This is indicated when Moses was warned by God when he was about to set up the meeting tent: See that you follow the pattern that I showed you on the mountain in every detail .
local: Saraabi bin bisaraaŋaliibi ŋáańee sá tin bí yilpuee naŋku ní, a lì kii Unimbɔti nín nìn dū yilpu diiku bítam kɔkɔ ki dàkā Mooyiisi u gāā-u tanti taapu doo puee ní. Unimbɔti bí-u yii: «Á kpèè kí gāā-mi tanti ú nīn bí kii un m nìn dàkā-si dijool puee deedee.».

eng: But now, Jesus has received a superior priestly service just as he arranged a better covenant that is enacted with better promises.
local: Ama kɔŋkɔnnee ditundi din Kriisitoo bí yilpu ki tūǹee jítiǹ Juuda yab saraaŋaliibi nín tūǹ ŋiŋyee, di sá Kriisitoo kúm di yì-tì ní Unimbɔti pōoǹ-ti dipoondi din sá dimɔntiil ki jítiǹ dikpikilee.

eng: If the first covenant had been without fault, it wouldnt have made sense to expect a second.
local: Dipoompeepeekaal din Unimbɔti nìn pōoǹ ti-naanjabee yaa nìn sá dimɔntiilee, an kaa bàà gíti bàlifī yii bí kpántí diba.

eng: But God did find fault with them, since he says, Look, the days are coming, says the Lord, when I will make a covenant with the house of Israel, and I will make a new covenant with the house of Judah. .
local: An pu ní Unimbɔti nìn bíi wòoǹ-bi ki tùkù-bi yii: «Man ni-Dindaan di lī yii iwiin bí ki dòoń in poon m làá fàtīí pòon̄ Isirayeel yab nì Juuda yab dipoompɔndee.

eng: It will not be like the covenant that I made with their ancestors on the day I took them by the hand to lead them out of the land of Egypt, because they did not continue to keep my covenant, and I lost interest in them, says the Lord. .
local: Dipoondi gbanti kaa dāa sá kii din m nìn pōoǹ bi-naanjab daliŋkin m nìn nyántìń-bi Eejipiti tiŋkinee. Boonee baa dū dipoondee ki túń ditundi, ní ma mun kaa ti fātìi tɔ́ bi-bɔti.

eng: So then the first covenant had regulations for the priests service and the holy place on earth.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun Unimbɔti nìn pōoǹ dipoompeepeekaalee, u nìn yóoǹ marab bin binib làá dī kí jāam̀-uee nì tanti un poon bi làá nīn jáaḿ-uee.

eng: These are superficial regulations that are only about food, drink, and various ritual ways to wash with water. They are regulations that have been imposed until the time of the new order.
local: An sá marab bin bi yóoǹ bi sá tijin nì tinyɔkaal nì tiwun difíntilee bɔti ní. An marabee kɔkɔ sá tiwun pu marab ní kí tin sāā ŋyunti ŋun Unimbɔti làá dāá cáań tipɔnee.

eng: But Christ has appeared as the high priest of the good things that have happened. He passed through the greater and more perfect meeting tent, which isnt made by human hands .
local: Kriisitoo dɔ́miń ki sá-ti usaraaŋakpil un cáań-ti tiŋan tin poon ti bí nee. Úǹ di kɔ́ kusaraadimɔntiiu kun sá kukpaaŋŋu kaa sá binib di máńee, an dàkà yii kuaa sá duulinya doo diiku.

eng: He entered the holy of holies once for all by his own blood, not by the blood of goats or calves, securing our deliverance for all time.
local: U kɔ́ diba ja Circir páaa laŋki Unimbɔti nimbiin. An kaa sá nì iŋɔjaa nì anabil fatikuu ní u cáa kɔ́ǹ, ama úǹ u-ba fatikuu. Míǹ ní u ŋá ní ki cáań-ti difiil din kaa cáá dikúntilee.

eng: If the blood of goats and bulls and the sprinkled ashes of cows made spiritually contaminated people holy and clean.
local: Dikoobil di nìn sá puee di sá bi nìn dū iŋɔjaa nì inajaa fatikuu nì unasal un bi nìn wù ŋŋmi kɔkɔee fantam kí yàaǹ bin ŋá ikpitee pu ní. Bi yaa nìn ŋá mimmee, an lēē ikpiti bi-wunti pu bi kpántì circirdambi dee.

eng: how much more will the blood of Jesus wash our consciences clean from dead works in order to serve the living God? He offered himself to God through the eternal Spirit as a sacrifice without any flaw.
local: An yaa nìn ŋūǹ kí ŋá míǹ, kin ataa aŋa pu ní Kriisitoo un Circir Fam tútúǹ u dū u-ba ki ŋá saraamɔnti ki tīī Unimbɔtee fatikuu kaa ŋūǹ kí fíntí atuŋkpiti ti-pɔbiliŋ poon tí ŋmā kí nóó Unimbɔti un bí dimaŋfalinee.

eng: This is why hes the mediator of a new covenant : so that those who are called might receive the promise of the eternal inheritance on the basis of his death. His death occurred to set them free from the offenses committed under the first covenant.
local: Kriisitoo di cáań-ti dipoompɔndi, án nín làá ŋá pu ní bin Unimbɔti fīīyee ń ŋmā kí fōō tigaajawan tin Unimbɔti pōoǹ tàa cáá dikúntilee. Kun pu bi làá ŋmā kí fōō tigaajawan gbantee di sá Kriisitoo kpíì ki nyántì-bi bi nín nìn bí dipooŋkpikil poon ki ŋáań ikpiti iŋyee ni.

eng: When there is a will, you need to confirm the death of the one who made the will.
local: Unil yaa bí yii a làá jī u-fakalee, u yaa kaa láá kpíì, saa yòontí tiba.

eng: This is because a will takes effect only after a death, since its not in force while the one who made the will is alive.
local: Daliŋkin u sòon̄ ki tùkū-si yii sii di làá jī u-fakalee, tibɔtee kaa láá tūǹ ditundi, see u yaa láa kpíìyee ní á nín yīkì kí tɔ́kɔń difakalee pu kii u nín nìn pée tùkū-si puee.

eng: So not even the first covenant was put into effect without blood.
local: An pu ní tiwaŋkuti fatikuu nìn nyáń ki wāatì ní dipoompeepeekaal nín ŋúń ki túń ditundi.

eng: Moses took the blood of calves and goats, along with water, scarlet wool, and hyssop, and sprinkled both the Law scroll itself and all the people after he had proclaimed every command of the Law to all the people.
local: Di sá Mooyiisi nín kàan̄ marab bin kɔkɔ Unimbɔti ŋmàn̄ee ki tùkū samaa kɔkɔ ki dóòyee, ní u yōoǹ anabil, nì iŋɔjaa fatikuu ki dū nnyim ki ŋmālim̀ ní ki dū tipikuti tin bi mántèe nì isɔpi faapibiti ki cáa sèetī fatiku poon ki yàantí kugbɔŋu kun poon bi ŋmàn̄ marabee nì samaa kɔkɔ pu.

eng: They pitched the first tent called the holy place. It contained the lampstand, the table, and the loaves of bread presented to God.
local: Bi nìn gāā tanti ní, ní ki dū sata ki pītì poon adiin ali, ní ki yíì kudipeepeekaauee circir laŋki. Niin ní fitilab tɔ́kɔŋkaati nìn bí, ní teebil yú ní bi cāabìń kpɔnɔb bin bi ŋáań Unimbɔti saraayee ki pāantí.

eng: While he did it, he said, This is the blood of the covenant that God established for you. .
local: U nìn lī yii: «Fatiku nee di sá dipoondi din Unimbɔti líì-nee daaŋku.».

eng: And in the same way he sprinkled the meeting tent and also all the equipment that would be used in the priests service with blood.
local: U ŋá mimmee ní ki yāaǹ Unimbɔti tantiu un bi gāāyee nì tiwan tin bi cáa ŋáań saraabee kɔkɔ pu.

eng: Almost everything is cleansed by blood, according to the Laws regulations, and there is no forgiveness without blood being shed.
local: Kii mara nín lī puee, fatiku ní bi ŋman nìn pée cáa yàantí tiwan kɔkɔ pu ní tí kpántí circir. An puee fatiku yaa kaa nyáńee, unil kaa kāntí u-kpitii pɔ̄tam.

eng: So it was necessary for the copies of the heavenly things to be cleansed with these sacrifices, but the heavenly things had to be cleansed with better sacrifices than these.
local: Bi yaa nìn máaǹ kí ŋá tiwan tin sá yilpu wanti naŋku circir mimmee, an bàlifī yii bí ŋá saraabi bin jítiǹ míǹ kpataaayee yilpu wanti pu.

eng: Christ didnt enter the holy place made by human hands, but into heaven itself, so that he now appears in Gods presence for us.
local: Kun puee an kaa sá yii Kriisitoo kɔ́ kudii kun binib máń ki nántiǹ yilpu yɔkɔee poon ní ki ŋá saraa. Ama yilpu, Unimbɔti cee ní u kɔ́, ki bí Unimbɔti nimbiin kɔŋkɔnnee ki gbàam̄ ti-pu.

eng: He didnt enter to offer himself over and over again, like the high priest enters the earthly holy place every year with blood that isnt his.
local: Kí kūtèe, dibindi sá kamaayee, usaraaŋakpil nìn kóò circir páaa laŋki nì tiwaŋkuti fatikuu ní kí ŋá saraabi. Ama Kriisitoo úǹ kaa kɔ́ ŋfunyɔm kupaau kí dū u-ba kí ŋá saraa.

eng: If that were so, then Jesus would have to suffer many times since the foundation of the world. Instead, he has now appeared once at the end of the ages to get rid of sin by sacrificing himself.
local: An yaa sá mimmee, doooo duulinya nín kíĺ ki dāa sāā díǹee, u bàà làá jī falaabi ŋfunyɔm kupaau ní. Ama duulinya nín tóob́ dikúntil pu nee ní u dɔ́miń ki dū u-ba ki ŋá saraa diba pu kí kúntí ikpiti.

eng: People are destined to die once and then face judgment.
local: An pée sá yii báà ŋma ń kpú diba ja ní, ní ŋkúm boonee, Unimbɔti ń sòoǹ báà ŋma bɔti.

eng: In the same way, Christ was also offered once to take on himself the sins of many people. He will appear a second time, not to take away sin but to save those who are eagerly waiting for him.
local: Míǹ mbaantiim ní Kriisitoo mun dū u-ba ki ŋá saraa diba ja ki nyántì binib tikpil ikpiti poon. U làá dāan̄ nliliitiim, kaa sá kí dāá nyāntí binib ikpiti poon, ama u dāa dòoń kí fīī bin kíǹ ki kā ki cí-uee ní.

eng: There was a tent behind the second curtain called the holy of holies.
local: Sataliliiti boonee, kudii kubɔ mun dee, niin ní bi yíì circir páaa laŋki.

eng: It had the gold altar for incense and the chest containing the covenant, which was covered with gold on all sides. In the chest there was a gold jar containing manna, Aarons rod that budded, and the stone tablets of the covenant.
local: Niin ní sindaariya saraafaŋŋu nìn yú ní bi tùutī talaalub an pu, ní dipoondi dakaau yú bi dū sindaariya ki nyūntì u-kɔkɔ. Dakaayee poonee, kibaŋŋi kiba yú ki sá sindaariya ŋma. Ki-poon ní tijin tin bi yíì maanee nìn kā. Dakaa ubaantee poon ní Aarɔn kpandɔŋu ŋun nìn púkuḿ nsupumpukumee nì ataŋkpee ŋin pu Unimbɔti nìn ŋmàn̄ dipoondi marabee kā.

eng: Above the chest there were magnificent winged creatures casting their shadow over the seat of the chest, where sin is taken care of. Right now we cant talk about these things in detail.
local: Ní bi dū sindaariya ki mɔ́ɔ́ keerubimbi bin sá Unimbɔti nyootee bili ki tɔ́kɔǹ dakaayee pu kí dàkà yii Unimbɔti u-ba bí niin. Keerubimbee nìn kpátì bi-feeŋyi ki bīiǹ laakin bi yaa ŋá ikpiti saraau bi cáá fatiku ki yàantée pu. Taa ŋūǹ kí sòoǹ dakaa gbanti bɔti nì u-tuuŋŋi nín sá puee kɔŋkɔnnee.

eng: When these things have been prepared in this way, priests enter the first tent all the time as they perform their service.
local: Bi nìn ŋá míǹ kɔkɔ ki dóòyee, kudipeepeekaauee poon ní bisaraaŋaliibi kóò baabadal ki ŋáań bi-saraatuuŋŋi.

eng: But only the high priest enters the second tent once a year. He never does this without blood, which he offers for himself and for the sins the people committed in ignorance.
local: Ama poon diiku laakin bi yíì yii circir páaa laŋkee, usaraaŋakpil baba di kóò niin dibindi poonee diba ja kí cáá tiwaŋkuti tin u kūū úǹ u-ba kpitii puee fatikuu nì tin bi kūū samaa kɔkɔ nín ŋá ikpiti in kaa nyée fatikuu kí kɔ́ǹ kí ŋá Unimbɔti saraa.

eng: With this, the Holy Spirit is showing that the way into the holy place hadnt been revealed yet while the first tent was standing.
local: Circir Fam nìn dàkà yii, ŋyunti ŋun ní nsan pú diiku dáá bée, nsan nìn kpá unil ń jītí kí kɔ́ circir páaa laŋki.

eng: This is a symbol for the present time. It shows that the gifts and sacrifices that are being offered cant perfect the conscience of the one who is serving.
local: An saraabee cántí tibɔti taapu ní ki dàkà timbi díǹ: an dàkà yii saraabi bin binib nìn ŋáań ki jáaḿ Unimbɔti nì bin bi nìn ŋáań bi-kpitii puee kɔkɔ kaa nìn ŋūǹ kí lēē bin ŋáań saraabi gbantee pɔbiliŋin ikpiti bí kpántí deedeedambi Unimbɔti nimbiin.

eng: From James, a slave of God and of the Lord Jesus Christ. To the twelve tribes who are scattered outside the land of Israel. Greetings!.
local: Man Yakubu un sá Unimbɔti nì ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo tɔntɔnliuee di ŋmàn̄ kugbɔŋu nee. M jáaḿ nimbi tinaakɔti saalaa nì tili tin yàl̄ ki kɔ́ duulinya jaŋŋi kɔkɔnee.

eng: Those who are wealthy should find satisfaction in their low status, because they will die off like wildflowers.
local: Míǹ ní án nīn mɔ̄ǹ kinabiki kin di sá ugaajee, kun puee Unimbɔti sīntiǹ-u taapu: di sá yii u làá dāá gbátí kí lítí kii mpumpukumee ní.

eng: The sun rises with its scorching heat and dries up the grass so that its flowers fall and its beauty is lost. Just like that, in the midst of their daily lives, the wealthy will waste away.
local: Ŋwìiń yaa nyáń ki píĺ sínsiŋee, ní mpumpukum ń kpā ní kí gbátí kí lítí, ní mi-nyaaŋku ń wāŋkì. Míǹ mbaantiim ní ugaaja dāa bí u-tuuŋŋi poon ní kí kpáàa wāŋkì.

eng: Those who stand firm during testing are blessed. They are tried and true. They will receive the life God has promised to those who love him as their reward.
local: Án nīn mɔ̄ǹ sii un di yaa bí dinimbinaamandin ní ki pɔ̄ɔntí ki ŋmèéyee. Di sá yii a yaa ŋmēē míǹ ki tin kátì-kuee, Unimbɔti làá pà-si tipaal tin sá dimaŋfal din kaa kúntí u pōoǹ ki yóoǹ bin néeń-uee.

eng: No one who is tested should say, God is tempting me! This is because God is not tempted by any form of evil, nor does he tempt anyone.
local: Sii un yaa bí dinimbinaamandinee ń taa lì yii: «Unimbɔti di bíi cɔ́ŋkí-mi.» Kun puee tiba kaa ŋūǹ kí yàntī Unimbɔti ń ŋá tikpitil, ní úǹ u-ba mun kaa yàntī ubɔ ń ŋá tikpitil.

eng: Everyone is tempted by their own cravings; they are lured away and enticed by them.
local: Ama an sá unil nimbitaataam di kíiĺ-u ki téekí-u ki cáǹ ikpitin.

eng: Once those cravings conceive, they give birth to sin; and when sin grows up, it gives birth to death.
local: Nnimbitaataam yaa kɔ́ unilee, ní mí màal̀ ikpiti, ní ikpiti yaa wíiǹee ní í cáań ŋkúm.

eng: Dont be misled, my dear brothers and sisters.
local: M-nabineeŋkaamu, ní taa ŋmàtim̀maan ni-ba.

eng: Every good gift, every perfect gift, comes from above. These gifts come down from the Father, the creator of the heavenly lights, in whose character there is no change at all.
local: Tiwannyaandi nì tiwammɔntiil tin kɔkɔ ti fòol̄ faalaayee nyēeń yilpu ní. Ti-Baa Unimbɔti iwalfi in bí yilpuee náanliu cee ní an nyēeń. Úǹ kaa kpántáaa, kaa fàtī ki ŋmíntí dijandi diba, din kíǹee dɔ̄ dibɔmbɔndin.

eng: He chose to give us birth by his true word, and here is the result: we are like the first crop from the harvest of everything he created.
local: Úǹ di là ní ki dī dinyɔɔbundi din sá ibaamɔnee pu ki tīī-ti dimaŋfal, án nín làá ŋá pu timbi ń nīn līī tiwan tin kɔkɔ u náańee nyɔkɔpuee.

eng: Know this, my dear brothers and sisters: everyone should be quick to listen, slow to speak, and slow to grow angry.
local: M-nabineeŋkaamu, ní nīn nyímaan tin bí nee: báà ŋma ń kpòon̄ u-tafal malaa kí pīlifì u-juti, kí taa nín̄ jìkì disoondi pu, kí taa nín̄ fòol̄ diŋuul malaa malaa.

eng: My brothers and sisters, think of the various tests you encounter as occasions for joy.
local: M-nabiyaamu, dinimbinaamandi nín bí puee nì di-nyɔkɔm yaa bāań ni-puee, ní yàntī án nīn mɔ̄ǹ-ni tikpil.

eng: This is because an angry person doesnt produce Gods righteousness.
local: Di sá yii sii un yaa fōō diŋuulee, tin a ŋáańee kaa kítì Unimbɔti cee.

eng: Therefore, with humility, set aside all moral filth and the growth of wickedness, and welcome the word planted deep inside you—the very word that is able to save you.
local: Míǹ puee, ní fámaan tin kɔkɔ bíiĺ unilee nì atuŋkpiti kɔkɔ, kí sìntīǹ ni-ba kí fōō dinyɔɔbundi din Unimbɔti dū ki búĺ ni-pɔbiliŋinee, kun puee díǹ di ŋūǹ kí fīī-ni.

eng: You must be doers of the word and not only hearers who mislead themselves.
local: Nín̄ ŋáaḿmaan dinyɔɔbundee nín bí yii ní ŋá puee. Sii un yaa pílifí Unimbɔti bɔti, kaa ŋáań tì nín lī puee, kíiĺ a-ba ní.

eng: Those who hear but dont do the word are like those who look at their faces in a mirror.
local: Sii un pílifí dinyɔɔbundee fám, ní kaa ŋáań min dì lì yii á ŋée, náań nì un kpèé digitil ki wāĺ u-nimbiinee ní.

eng: They look at themselves, walk away, and immediately forget what they were like.
local: U nín kpēē ki dóò ki búntèe, ní ú kpáàa súntí u nín bàà kpēē digitil poon ki bí miŋyee kɔkɔ.

eng: But there are those who study the perfect law, the law of freedom, and continue to do it. They dont listen and then forget, but they put it into practice in their lives. They will be blessed in whatever they do.
local: Ama sii un di yaa kpèé bimaramɔntiibi bin tìì dibatɔlee tiŋan ní ki yaa cáá a-ba ki tìì-bi, kaa pílifí fám boonee kí súntí, ama ki ŋáań min marabee bí yii á ŋée, Unimbɔti làá bìin̄ a-boon a-tuuŋŋi poon.

eng: If those who claim devotion to God dont control what they say, they mislead themselves. Their devotion is worthless.
local: Sii un yaa nyí yii a sá unimbɔtinool, ní kaa ŋúb́ a-nyɔkɔee, kíiĺ a-ba ní, a-nimbɔtinoolee sá fám ní.

eng: True devotion, the kind that is pure and faultless before God the Father, is this: to care for orphans and widows in their difficulties and to keep the world from contaminating us.
local: Dinimbɔtinool din ŋān̄ ki bí circir ti-Baa Unimbɔti ceeyee di sá: á kpèè ataŋkpiibil nì bikpiipoobi bin bí falaa poonee, kí nīn nyí nì a-ba kí taa jēetí duulinya kpitii poon.

eng: After all, you know that the testing of your faith produces endurance.
local: Di sá ni nyí yii dinimbinaamandi gbanti yaa bāań ki cɔ́ŋkì ni-fookiiu ní ni yaa gbíntì ki yú kaa lítèe, kimɔtii ní an cāabìń-ni.

eng: Let this endurance complete its work so that you may be fully mature, complete, and lacking in nothing.
local: Ama ní nīn cáá kimɔtii míǹ kí tin kátí-ku, án nín làá ŋá pu ní kpántí biniŋmambi bimɔntiibi ni-bimbim poon, tiba nì tiba ń taa lóó-nee.

eng: But anyone who needs wisdom should ask God, whose very nature is to give to everyone without a second thought, without keeping score. Wisdom will certainly be given to those who ask.
local: Míǹ puee, ni-kansikin ubɔ yaa lóò iciinee, udaan ń mèè Unimbɔti un mɔ̄ ki pūǹ báà ŋma nì dipɔbil diba, kaa wòoǹee, u làá tīī-u.

eng: Whoever asks shouldnt hesitate. They should ask in faith, without doubting. Whoever doubts is like the surf of the sea, tossed and turned by the wind.
local: Tin bée di sá yii udaan ń nīn máká yii u làá fōō kun u bíi mèéyee kí taa gùgufì, kun puee un mèé ki gùgufēe náań nì kii dimɔɔl nyim min kubuŋu téekí ki cáǹ ajan kɔkɔ púee ní.

eng: People like that should never imagine that they will receive anything from the Lord.
local: An niliuee ń taa dàkafì yii u làá ŋmā kí fōō tiba ti-Dindaan cee.

eng: They are double-minded, unstable in all their ways.
local: kun puee u cáá ilandɔkɔ ili ili, kaa dìŋfī nsambaanti.

eng: Brothers and sisters who are poor should find satisfaction in their high status.
local: Án nīn mɔ̄ǹ kinabiki kin di sá usoodaanee, kun puee Unimbɔti yōoǹ-u yilpu.

eng: My brothers and sisters, when you show favoritism you deny the faithfulness of our Lord Jesus Christ, who has been resurrected in glory.
local: M-nabiyaamu, kii ni nín bí ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo tinyooldaan saŋŋun puee, an kaa máaǹ ní nīn cáá dinigàntil.

eng: Anyone who tries to keep all of the Law but fails at one point is guilty of failing to keep all of it.
local: Di sá yii, a yaa ŋúb́ marab kɔkɔ ní ki yaa yīntiǹ ubambaanti bi-kansikinee, a yīntì bi-kɔkɔ dee.

eng: The one who said, Dont commit adultery , also said, Dont commit murder . So if you dont commit adultery but do commit murder, you are a lawbreaker.
local: kun puee Unimbɔti un lī yii: «Á taa ŋá tilaakpaalee», úǹ ubaantee di tí lī yii: «Á taa kpɔ̀ unil.» Míǹ puee, a yaa kaa ŋá tilaakpaal, ama ní ki yaa kūū unilee, a yīntì mara dee.

eng: In every way, then, speak and act as people who will be judged by the law of freedom.
local: Míǹ puee, ni-bɔsooŋyi nì ni-ŋáaǹtam kɔkɔnee, ní nīn ŋáań ki nyí yii ni sá binib bin Unimbɔti làá dū mara un cāabìń dibatɔlee ní kí sòoǹ ni-bɔtee.

eng: There will be no mercy in judgment for anyone who hasnt shown mercy. Mercy overrules judgment.
local: Sii un kaa súń a-jutiibi saayee, ibɔsoon dalee, Unimbɔti kaa làá dāá sɔ̄ si mun a-saai. A nín súń binib saayee di làá yàntī Unimbɔti ń dāá sɔ̄ si mun saai.

eng: My brothers and sisters, what good is it if people say they have faith but do nothing to show it? Claiming to have faith cant save anyone, can it?.
local: M-nabiyaamu, an kaa ŋān̄ unil ń bí yii úǹ fōō Yeesu ki kíí, ama ní kaa bíi tūǹ atummɔnti, an fookiiuee ŋūǹ kí fīī-uaaa?.

eng: Imagine a brother or sister who is naked and never has enough food to eat.
local: Kii ti-nabiki kiba, uninja àá unimpu, di yaa bí, kaa cáá tiwampeeŋkaalaaa, kaa cáá tijikaal baabadal kí jī.

eng: What if one of you said, Go in peace! Stay warm! Have a nice meal!? What good is it if you dont actually give them what their body needs?.
local: ní ni-kansikin ubɔ yaa tùkū udaan yii: «Án ŋá kí kūtì, á nīn bí tiŋan! Taa yàntī tiwool ń cúú-si, á nīn jīǹ tijin ki díkì!», ama ní kaa tīī-u ú jī, kaa tīī-u ú pèen̄ kí ŋmátée, an ŋān̄ lapu?.

eng: In the same way, faith is dead when it doesnt result in faithful activity.
local: Míǹ mbaantiim ní un yaa fōō Yeesu ki kíí ŋmaniŋma, ní kaa ŋáań fookii tundi nín sá puee, mimmee udaan fookiiu kpíì dee.

eng: Someone might claim, You have faith and I have action. But how can I see your faith apart from your actions? Instead, Ill show you my faith by putting it into practice in faithful action.
local: Ubɔ ŋūǹ kí lì yii: «Sii cáá fookii, ama ní man tūǹ atummɔnti. Too, dàkà-mi a nín fōō ki kíí min min, kaa tūǹ atummɔntee, ní ma mun ń dàkà-si m-tummɔntiŋ a béé yii m fōō ki kíí.

eng: Its good that you believe that God is one. Ha! Even the demons believe this, and they tremble with fear.
local: A nín fōō ki kíí yii Unimbɔtibaanti di bée, an ŋān̄: ama míǹ ní arasiniibi mun fōō ki kíí, ní ki fàŋkí yɔ́kɔ́ yɔkɔ.

eng: Imagine two people coming into your meeting. One has a gold ring and fine clothes, while the other is poor, dressed in filthy rags.
local: Tí lì yii ni-kpafil poon ní ugaaja ubɔ yaa kóoń ki fúuń sindaariya bɔmbiki ki pēē dibɔkutinyaandi, ní usoodaan ubɔ mun kóoń ki pēē ikpalcati.

eng: Are you so slow? Do you need to be shown that faith without actions has no value at all?.
local: Ugbaan, aa là kí béé yii a yaa fōō ki kíí ní kaa tūǹ atummɔntee, a-fookiiu sá fámaaa?.

eng: What about Abraham, our father? Wasnt he shown to be righteous through his actions when he offered his son Isaac on the altar?.
local: An ŋá mana ní ti-naanja Abraam dāa kpántì deedeedaan Unimbɔti nimbiin ní? An sá ditundi din u túńee pu, an dàkà yii u nín nìn dū u-jipɔɔn Isaaki ki páaǹ nsaraafan pu ki làá ŋá saraa kí tīī Unimbɔtee.

eng: See, his faith was at work along with his actions. In fact, his faith was made complete by his faithful actions.
local: A káa! U fōō ki kíí ní ki túń ditundi mun, u-fookiiu nì u-tuuŋŋi nín kpáfì mbaantiim ki táá tɔb puee ní u-fookiiu kpántì umɔnti.

eng: So the scripture was fulfilled that says, Abraham believed God, and God regarded him as righteous . What is more, Abraham was called Gods friend.
local: An ŋá kii Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon nín lī puee dee, yii: «Abraam fōō Unimbɔti ki kíí, ní Unimbɔti ká u-fookiiu un u cáá u-puee ní ki yíiń-u deedeedaan,» ní ki kíǹ ki yíì-u u-bɔɔ mun.

eng: So you see that a person is shown to be righteous through faithful actions and not through faith alone.
local: Ni káa! Unil tuuŋŋi pu ní u kpántí deedeedaan Unimbɔti cee, an kaa sá fookii un u cááyee ŋmaniŋma pu.

eng: In the same way, wasnt Rahab the prostitute shown to be righteous when she received the messengers as her guests and then sent them on by another road?.
local: Míǹ mbaantiim ní an nìn tí jítì Raab kinimpulaakpaayee cee. U mun nín nìn túń atuun ŋin ki kpántì deedeedaan Unimbɔti ceeyee di sá u nín fōō binib bin Juuda yab nìn túee ki dàkā-bi nsan ŋubɔ bi dī ki ŋmátèe.

eng: As the lifeless body is dead, so faith without actions is dead.
local: Kii ŋfam yaa nyáń tiwun nín kpántí utaŋkpiil puee, míǹ ní unil yaa fōō ki kíí ní kaa tūǹ atummɔntee, u-fookiiu kúù.

eng: Then suppose that you were to take special notice of the one wearing fine clothes, saying, Heres an excellent place. Sit here. But to the poor person you say, Stand over there; or, Here, sit at my feet.
local: ní ni yaa tīī ugaaja un pēē tiwammɔntiilee jilma ki bí-u yii: «Sii dāá kàl̄ mpaammɔnti nee pu.» Ama ní ki bí usoodaanee yii: «Sii nín̄ yú, a yaa tí làaa, dāá kàl̄ kitiŋ m-taaŋi caŋin yéè.».

eng: Wouldnt you have shown favoritism among yourselves and become evil-minded judges?.
local: Mimmee, naa gāntì-bi deeee? Ilandɔkɔkpiti ní ni dākafì ní ki ŋá míǹ.

eng: My dear brothers and sisters, listen! Hasnt God chosen those who are poor by worldly standards to be rich in terms of faith? Hasnt God chosen the poor as heirs of the kingdom he has promised to those who love him?.
local: M-nabineeŋkaamu, ní pīlifìmaan: Unimbɔti lútì duulinya nee poon bisoodambi bí fōō Yeesu Kriisitoo kí kíí, ní kí ká tigaajamɔntiil kí kɔ́ Unimbɔti Beel din u pōoǹ ki yóoǹ bin néeń-uee ni.

eng: But you have dishonored the poor. Dont the wealthy make life difficult for you? Arent they the ones who drag you into court?.
local: Ama ní nimbi kpèé bisoodambi fám. An kaa sá bigaajab di sàantí-ni ki cútí-ni ki cáǹ ibɔsoonaaa?.

eng: Arent they the ones who insult the good name spoken over you at your baptism?.
local: Bíǹ di kaa bíiĺ Yeesu Kriisitoo yinnyaandi din u pú-neeee?.

eng: You do well when you really fulfill the royal law found in scripture, Love your neighbor as yourself. .
local: Tiŋman, ni yaa nóó Unimbɔti Beel marau kii an nín ŋmàn̄ puee, yii: «Á nīn néeń a-juti kii sii a-bee,» mimmee, ni ŋáań min ŋān̄ee dee.

eng: But when you show favoritism, you are committing a sin, and by that same law you are exposed as a lawbreaker.
local: Ama ni yaa kpèé ki gàntī binibee, an sá ikpiti ní ni ŋáań, maree cúú-ni, kun puee ni yīntiǹ-u.

eng: My brothers and sisters, not many of you should become teachers, because we know that we teachers will be judged more strictly.
local: M-nabiyaamu, ni-kɔkɔ ń taa yīkì bìliku yii ni là kí nīn dàkà Unimbɔti bɔti, kun puee ni nyí yii timbi bidakaliibee bɔsooŋyi dāa pɔ̄ɔ̄ ki jítiǹ bin kíǹee yai.

eng: Blessing and cursing come from the same mouth. My brothers and sisters, it just shouldnt be this way!.
local: Kinyɔkɔn ní ijaam nyēē, kíǹ kibaantii poon ní mbusu tí nyēē. M-nabiyaamu, an kaa máaǹ kí nīn bí míǹ.

eng: Both fresh water and salt water dont come from the same spring, do they?.
local: Nnyimbul ŋubɔ kaa bí ki nyēē inyiŋfi ili: min tūee nì min mɔ̄ee dicilpu.

eng: My brothers and sisters, can a fig tree produce olives? Can a grapevine produce figs? Of course not, and fresh water doesnt flow from a saltwater spring either.
local: M-nabiyaamu, kɔŋki subu kaa lòoǹ tikpasampiil, ní nunufi mun kaa lòoǹ kɔŋki. Míǹ mbaantiim, nnyimbul ŋun nyēē nnyim min tūee kaa nyēē min mɔ̄ee.

eng: Are any of you wise and understanding? Show that your actions are good with a humble lifestyle that comes from wisdom.
local: Ni-kansikin ubɔ bí ki cáá iciin ki nyáaa? Ú nīn sìntīǹ u-ba taapu kí nīn tūǹ atummɔnti ŋin làá dàkà yii u lafun cáá iciinee.

eng: However, if you have bitter jealousy and selfish ambition in your heart, then stop bragging and living in ways that deny the truth.
local: Ama a yaa ná a-jutiibi, a-poon tū, ki sá unil un mɔ̀ kí nīn jítiǹ báà ŋmee, á taa nín̄ gà a-bindi yii a tɔ́kɔ́ ibaamɔn pu, kí taa mɔ̄ntì a-ba.

eng: This is not the wisdom that comes down from above. Instead, it is from the earth, natural and demonic.
local: An ciiŋyee kaa nyēeń yilpu, i sá taapu doo ní, nniyaamin, i nyēeń Sitaan cee ní.

eng: Wherever there is jealousy and selfish ambition, there is disorder and everything that is evil.
local: Kun puee laakin ní binib ná tɔb ní ki mɔ̀ kí nīn jítiǹ tɔbee, niin ní ni kāntí tin kaa kítèe nì tibɔkpiil kɔkɔ nì ti-bulifi.

eng: What of the wisdom from above? First, it is pure, and then peaceful, gentle, obedient, filled with mercy and good actions, fair, and genuine.
local: Ama iciin in nyēeń yilpuee, mpeepeekaamee i bí circir, ní boonee i cāabìń ŋgbansɔŋfi binib kansikin, ní ki mɔ̄, ki bí soooo, ní ki cáá tiniti tikpil, ki tūǹ atummɔnti, ní kaa gàntī binib, kaa cáá mpoonnyoon.

eng: Those who make peace sow the seeds of justice by their peaceful acts.
local: Bin cāabìń ŋgbansɔŋfi bi-jutiibi kansikinee būul̀ nsɔŋfin dee, ní kí dāá kìtī abil ŋin sá yii bi bí deedee Unimbɔti nimbiinee.

eng: We all make mistakes often, but those who dont make mistakes with their words have reached full maturity. Like a bridled horse, they can control themselves entirely.
local: Ti-kɔkɔ yìntī ajan tikpil poon, ama sii un yaa kaa yìntī a-bɔsooŋyi poonee di sá uniŋman ki ŋūǹ kí cúú a-ba kɔkɔ.

eng: When we bridle horses and put bits in their mouths to lead them wherever we want, we can control their whole bodies.
local: Ti kpàl̀ itaamu nyɔmmu tikúti í nīn tɔ́-ti ní ki kpáàa kpòotī i-wunti kɔkɔ.

eng: Consider ships: They are so large that strong winds are needed to drive them. But pilots direct their ships wherever they want with a little rudder.
local: Kpèèmaan, meelab mun nín sá bikpaambi miŋyee, ama báà kubuŋu yaa yāntì ki téekí-bi min kamaa, kipimbiwaŋwaai ní un ŋàal̀ee cáa kpòotī-bi ki cáǹ laakin u lèe.

eng: In the same way, even though the tongue is a small part of the body, it boasts wildly. Think about this: A small flame can set a whole forest on fire.
local: Míǹ mbaantiim ní unil wunti puee, dilimbil sá kijaŋwaai ní, ní ki ŋáań tibɔkpaandi. Kéè, ni nyí yii kiŋmibilii fiii yéè di gbátí kí wà kupɔɔuaa!.

eng: The tongue is a small flame of fire, a world of evil at work in us. It contaminates our entire lives. Because of it, the circle of life is set on fire. The tongue itself is set on fire by the flames of hell.
local: Dilimbil sá kii ŋŋmee ní. Duulinya bɔkpitii kɔkɔ bí dilimbilin ní dì kā ti-wunti pu ki bíiĺ tiwun kɔkɔ, ní kí cáań Sitaan ŋmiŋu kí tɔ̄ ti-pu.

eng: People can tame and already have tamed every kind of animal, bird, reptile, and fish.
local: Unil ŋūǹ kí kpàà ipeel nì inoon nì tin fù ti-pooŋi puee, nì ijɔm kɔkɔ, í ŋá u nín là puee, ní binib lafun ŋúb̀-i míǹ.

eng: No one can tame the tongue, though. It is a restless evil, full of deadly poison.
local: Ama doooo nee, ubɔ kaa láá ŋúń ki ŋúb́ dilimbil: dì sá kuwaŋkpitiu kun baa ŋūǹ kí cúúyee, ki gbí tilool tin cáá ŋkúmee ŋmaniŋma.

eng: With it we both bless the Lord and Father and curse human beings made in Gods likeness.
local: Díǹ ní ti cáa jáaḿ ti-Baa Unimbɔti, díǹ ní ti tí cáa bíiĺ binib bin Unimbɔti náań ki nántiǹ u-bee.

eng: What is the source of conflict among you? What is the source of your disputes? Dont they come from your cravings that are at war in your own lives?.
local: Ba di cāabìń kunikpɔŋkpɔkɔu nì kujaau ni-kansikin ní? An sá ni-kpɔkɔsib bin bí ni-wuntin ki jáà baabadalee di cāabìń tíǹ kɔkɔ.

eng: Humble yourselves before the Lord, and he will lift you up.
local: Sìntīmaan ni-ba kí tīī Unimbɔti, ní ú yòon̄-ni yilpu.

eng: Brothers and sisters, dont say evil things about each other. Whoever insults or criticizes a brother or sister insults and criticizes the Law. If you find fault with the Law, you are not a doer of the Law but a judge over it.
local: Nnabiyaamu, ní taa nín̄ sòoǹ ki bíiĺmaan tɔb. Sii un di yaa sòon̄ ki bíil̀ a-nabikee, àá a sòon̄ ki kūū u-bɔtee, mara ní a sòon̄ ki bíil̀ ki kūū. A yaa sòon̄ ki bíil̀ maree, aa ti tɔ́-u dee, a sòon̄ ki kūū-u dee.

eng: There is only one lawgiver and judge, and he is able to save and to destroy. But you who judge your neighbor, who are you?.
local: Unimbɔti baba di tìì marab ní ki sá ubɔsoonli. Úǹ baba di ŋūǹ kí fīī binib, úǹ di tí ŋūǹ kí kpɔ̀-bi. Sii yíì a-ba ŋma ní ki sòoǹ a-juti bɔti ní?.

eng: Pay attention, you who say, Today or tomorrow we will go to such-and-such a town. We will stay there a year, buying and selling, and making a profit.
local: Ní pīlifìmaan kɔŋkɔnnee, nimbi bin lì yii: «Díǹ àá fool, ti làá yīkì kí cù kitiŋ kinee ni, kí tin ŋá niin dibindi diba, kí bàntì kí ká animbilee.».

eng: You dont really know about tomorrow. What is your life? You are a mist that appears for only a short while before it vanishes.
local: Ni nyí kun làá fu bàń ni-pu fooleeee? Ni pée bí kii dipɔ̀ńdi nín nyēē kí páań pu, án kūtì fiii, dí wāŋkèe ní.

eng: Heres what you ought to say: If the Lord wills, we will live and do this or that.
local: An nín bàà máaǹ ní lì puee sèé: «Unimbɔti yaa là ní ti yaa bí ki pɔ̄ɔ̄yee, ti làá ŋá tinee nì tinee.».

eng: But now you boast and brag, and all such boasting is evil.
local: Ama nimbee, ni sá bilambidambi ní ki dàkà ni-ba, an nì an lambiliŋee kaa ŋān̄.

eng: It is a sin when someone knows the right thing to do and doesnt do it.
local: Sii un di nyí tiŋan tin bàà sá á ŋá ní kaa ŋée, ŋá ikpiti.

eng: You long for something you dont have, so you commit murder. You are jealous for something you cant get, so you struggle and fight. You dont have because you dont ask.
local: Ni cáá nnimbitaataam tiba pu, ní kaa ŋūǹ kí ká kudɔŋu, míǹ puee ní ni yaa ká unilee, ní ní kpɔ̀. Ni-nimbiliŋ máǹ tiba pu ní naa ŋūǹ kí ká kudɔŋu, ní kujaau nì kunikpɔŋkpɔkɔu kóò. Kun di yì-tì ní naa kāntí kun ní ni nyàab̀ee di sá naa mèé Unimbɔti un tìì kudɔŋu gbantee.

eng: You ask and dont have because you ask with evil intentions, to waste it on your own cravings.
local: Míǹ ní ni yaa tí mēē Unimbɔti, naa kāntí, kun puee ni dàkafī ilandɔkɔkpiti ní. Kun ni mèé ki nyàab̀ee, ni là kí dūú ŋá min fáatì-nee ní.

eng: You unfaithful people! Dont you know that friendship with the world means hostility toward God? So whoever wants to be the worlds friend becomes Gods enemy.
local: Nimbi bin fá Unimbɔti kii kinimpulaakpaai nín féeń u-cal puee, naa nyí yii un néeń duulinyee sá Unimbɔti pulincɔnti níii? Sii un píí duulinya kubɔɔtiiuee kpántì Unimbɔti pulincɔnti dee.

eng: Or do you suppose that scripture is meaningless? Doesnt God long for our faithfulness in the life he has given to us?.
local: Ni nyí fám ní Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon sòon̄ yii: «Unimbɔti kaa mɔ̄ ŋfam min u dūu ŋá ti-nee pu.».

eng: But he gives us more grace. This is why it says, God stands against the proud, but favors the humble .
local: Ama u ŋáań-ti tiŋan tin wīikí ki lákatèe, kii an nín ŋmàn̄ u-gbɔŋku poon puee, yii: «Unimbɔti kaa gèetíǹ bilambidambi, ama ní ki ŋáań bin sìntīǹ bi-ba taapuee tiŋan.».

eng: Therefore, submit to God. Resist the devil, and he will run away from you.
local: Míǹ puee, sìntīmaan ni-ba taapu kí tīī Unimbɔti, kí já nì Sitaan, see ú sāǹ kí wàatī-ni.

eng: Come near to God, and he will come near to you. Wash your hands, you sinners. Purify your hearts, you double-minded.
local: Sútímaan kí pìl̄ Unimbɔti, ní u mun ń sútiń kí pìl̄-ni. Ikpitidambi, fíntímaan ni-ŋalii ikpiti ń nyā, milimilidambi, fíntímaan ni-pɔbiliŋ poon ń nyā circir.

eng: Cry out in sorrow, mourn, and weep! Let your laughter become mourning and your joy become sadness.
local: Ni-kpitii puee, nín̄ bímaan kunimbaaun kí nīn sūǹ ki yáliŋkí, ni-laati ń kpántí nsiim, ni-pɔɔpindi ń kpántí-ni kunimbaau.

eng: Pay attention, you wealthy people! Weep and moan over the miseries coming upon you.
local: Nimbi bigaajabee, ní pīlifìmaan tibɔti nee: Ní sɔ̄ kí yāliŋkìmaan nnaabu ŋun làá bàń ni-puee.

eng: Brothers and sisters, take the prophets who spoke in the name of the Lord as an example of patient resolve and steadfastness.
local: Nnabiyaamu, téetímaan Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi bin nìn sòon̄ u-puee falaabi nì bi-mɔtiiki pu, kí nīn bímaan míǹ.

eng: Look at how we honor those who have practiced endurance. You have heard of the endurance of Job. And you have seen what the Lord has accomplished, for the Lord is full of compassion and mercy.
local: Ti pàkà-bi kun puee bi yúl̀ deedee. Ni gbìl̄ bi sòon̄ Yoob mɔtiiki pu, ki nyí u nín jíń kimɔtii ki kátì, ti-Dindaan ŋá-u miŋyee, kun puee ti-Dindaan sá usaasɔl ní ki ŋān̄.

eng: Most important, my brothers and sisters, never make a solemn pledge—neither by heaven nor earth, nor by anything else. Instead, speak with a simple Yes or No, or else you may fall under judgment.
local: M-nabiyaamu, tin kaa pée máaǹ ní ŋée di sá ní taa pùtī. Ní taa pùtī yilpu kí taa pùtī taapu, kí taa pùtī báà tiba nì tiba poon. An yaa sá Iiiŋyee, kíímaan «Iiin,» an yaa tí sá Aayeeyaaa, kíímaan «Aayee,» án nín làá ŋá pu ní taa kɔ́ Unimbɔti bɔtinee.

eng: If any of you are suffering, they should pray. If any of you are happy, they should sing.
local: Ni-kansikin ubɔ yaa bí falaa poonee, ú nīn mèé Unimbɔti. Ubɔ yaa tí bí ni-kansikin dipɔɔpindi poonaaa, ú nīn gà ilan ki nyɔ́ŋkí Unimbɔti.

eng: If any of you are sick, they should call for the elders of the church, and the elders should pray over them, anointing them with oil in the name of the Lord.
local: Ubɔ yaa tí bí ni-kansikin ki būǹee, ú tɔ̄ bí yíiń Kriisitoo nikitil kpilib ń dāan̄ kí dū ŋkpam kí jōō u-yil pu kí yíiń ti-Dindaan yindi kí mèè kí tīī-u.

eng: Prayer that comes from faith will heal the sick, for the Lord will restore them to health. And if they have sinned, they will be forgiven.
local: Bi yaa máká Unimbɔti pu ki mēē-u, see ubunlee ń ŋmátí. Ti-Dindaan làá tīī-u ŋgbampɔn. Báà u yaa ŋá ikpiti iba, Unimbɔti làá dūú pɔ̄-u.

eng: For this reason, confess your sins to each other and pray for each other so that you may be healed. The prayer of the righteous person is powerful in what it can achieve.
local: An puee, nín̄ bìitī ni-kpitii pu ki tùkùmaan tɔb, kí nīn mèé ki tììmaan tɔb, kí ŋmā kí nīn kāntí dicáaǹdi. Di sá yii unil un yú deedee Unimbɔti nimbiinee yaa dū u-pɔbil kɔkɔ ki mēē-uee, an tūǹ atuŋkpaan ní.

eng: Elijah was a person just like us. When he earnestly prayed that it wouldnt rain, no rain fell for three and a half years.
local: Eelii mun nìn pée tí sá unifutifoo kii ti nín bí pu nan ní, ní ki nìn mēē Unimbɔti nì u-pɔbil kɔkɔ, yii utaa ń taa ti nì, ní utaa kaa lafun gíti jōō kitiŋ pu áli abin ata nì dijandi.

eng: He prayed again, God sent rain, and the earth produced its fruit.
local: Boonee ní u fātìi tí mēē, ní utaa nìn̄, ní kitiŋ lòon̄ tijikaal.

eng: My brothers and sisters, if any of you wander from the truth and someone turns back the wanderer.
local: M-nabiyaamu, ni-kansikin ubɔ ŋūǹ kí dū Unimbɔti saŋŋu ki fá ní ubɔ mun ŋūǹ kí fàtīí gíiǹní-u.

eng: Your riches have rotted. Moths have destroyed your clothes.
local: Ni-gaajawanti sūū, ní akɔtuu mɔ́ ni-wampeeŋkaati.

eng: recognize that whoever brings a sinner back from the wrong path will save them from death and will bring about the forgiveness of many sins.
local: Too, ni máaǹ kí béé yii un di yaa nyántìń unil nsaŋkpitin ki gíiǹní-u nsammɔnti poonee, udaan fīī u-nabiki gbanti naŋki ki nyántì ŋkúm ŋaan dee. Udaan nín ŋá mimmee puee, Unimbɔti làá dūú pɔ̄-u.

eng: Your gold and silver have rusted, and their rust will be evidence against you. It will eat your flesh like fire. Consider the treasure you have hoarded in the last days.
local: Ni-sindaariyab nì ni-nimbiliŋ dámintì, ní an nín dámintì puee di làá dāá lēē mmɔ́ɔn yii ni nín ŋá miŋyee kaa ŋān̄, ní kí dāá wà ni-wunti kii ŋŋmee. Di sá yii ni báá tigaajati ŋyuntijoolkaa nee poon ní.

eng: Listen! Hear the cries of the wages of your field hands. These are the wages you stole from those who harvested your fields. The cries of the harvesters have reached the ears of the Lord of heavenly forces.
local: Bipaadambi bin kùn̄ ni-sati naa pàn̄-bee, pīlifìmaan, bíǹ di tíminsée. Bin cáń ni-jikaatee biilii bàn̄ Unimbɔti Mpɔŋkɔdaan cee.

eng: You have lived a self-satisfying life on this earth, a life of luxury. You have stuffed your hearts in preparation for the day of slaughter.
local: Ni kāl̀ duulinya poon ki jíń mmɔn ki ŋá min fáatì-nee, ní ki jíń ki ŋá ŋkpam ki cíitì ŋwiin ŋun bi làá cáá-ni kí kpɔ̀ee.

eng: You have condemned and murdered the righteous one, who doesnt oppose you.
local: Ni túǹ bin kaa ŋá tibee pu abɔgaan ki kūū-bi, baa kpákáǹ-ni.

eng: Therefore, brothers and sisters, you must be patient as you wait for the coming of the Lord. Consider the farmer who waits patiently for the coming of rain in the fall and spring, looking forward to the precious fruit of the earth.
local: Míǹ puee, nnabiyaamu, nín̄ cáámaan kimɔtii kí nīn cí ti-Dindaan gítintam. Kpèèmaan ukpaal nín jīǹ kimɔtii pu ki cīití u-wambɔlkaati ń bī puee. U jīǹ kimɔtii ní ki cīití utaapeekaa ń nì, ní utaajoolkaa mun ń dāá nì, ní ú nín cā u-jimmɔntiiti.

eng: You also must wait patiently, strengthening your resolve, because the coming of the Lord is near.
local: Ni mun ń nīn cáámaan an mɔtiiki juti, kí nīn pɔ̄ɔntí kun puee ti-Dindaan tóob́ kí bàń.

eng: Dont complain about each other, brothers and sisters, so that you wont be judged. Look! The judge is standing at the door!.
local: Nnabiyaamu, taa nín̄ gbùkum̄maan tɔb pu, án nín làá ŋá pu Unimbɔti ń taa yíiń-ni ibɔsoonee. Úǹ ubɔsoonlee kaa dátiǹ-ti, u yú kipunyɔkɔpu ki làá kíĺ ibɔsoon.

eng: In the beginning was the Word and the Word was with God and the Word was God.
local: Kí wàatī Unimbɔti ń náań duulinyee, ubɔ nìn bí bi yíì-u Dinyɔɔbundi ní ki bí Unimbɔti cee, ní ki sá Unimbɔti.

eng: The light was in the world, and the world came into being through the light, but the world didnt recognize the light.
local: Unimbɔti dū dinyɔɔbundee ki náań taapu doo wanti kɔkɔ, ama, ŋyunti ŋun dì nìn bí taapu dooyee, baa béè-dì.

eng: The light came to his own people, and his own people didnt welcome him.
local: Un sá Dinyɔɔbundee dɔ́miń u-yab cee, ní u-yab kaa fōō-u.

eng: But those who did welcome him, those who believed in his name, he authorized to become Gods children.
local: Ama bin kɔkɔ fōō-u ki gbìl̄ ki tīiǹ-uee, u yāntì bidambi kpántì Unimbɔti biyaamu.

eng: born not from blood nor from human desire or passion, but born from God.
local: mbiyaamu mun ní unil kaa màl̄, kaa tí sá unil làtamee, ama an sá Unimbɔti di màl̄ múǹ.

eng: The Word became flesh and made his home among us. We have seen his glory, glory like that of a fathers only son, full of grace and truth.
local: Un sá Dinyɔɔbundee kpántì unil ki kāl̀ ti-kansikin ki cáá tiŋan nì ibaamɔn tikpil, ní ti ká u-nyooti tin u nìn fōō Ubaati ceeyee, kun puee u sá Unimbɔti Jipɔmbaabil.

eng: John testified about him, crying out, This is the one of whom I said, He who comes after me is greater than me because he existed before me.
local: U-pu ní Yoowaneesi nìn kúuǹ ki lī yii: «Un m nìn lī yii u dòoń man boon ama úǹ jítiǹ mamee, dee! Di sá yii úǹ pée bí ní bi nín màl̄ man.».

eng: From his fullness we have all received grace upon grace.
local: Úǹ cee ní tiŋan kɔwoooo nyánní ní ki bāań-ti, ní timbi kɔkɔ fòol̄-tì múlmul.

eng: as the Law was given through Moses, so grace and truth came into being through Jesus Christ.
local: Unimbɔti nìn yāntì Mooyiisi tīī-ti marab, ama ní Yeesu Kriisitoo cáań-ti tiŋan nì ibaamɔn.

eng: No one has ever seen God. God the only Son, who is at the Fathers side, has made God known.
local: Báà ubɔ kaa wál̀ Unimbɔti dalba, ama u-Jipɔmbaabil un mun sá Unimbɔti ki bí ki nákáǹ u-Baa Unimbɔtee di yāntì ti béè-u.

eng: This is Johns testimony when the Jewish leaders in Jerusalem sent priests and Levites to ask him, Who are you.
local: Juuda yab kpilib bin nìn bí Jeerusaleemee nìn túnní bisaraaŋaliibi nì Leefii yab bin tūǹ Unimbɔti diiku poon ditundee biba Yoowaneesi cee bí dāá bàlifì-u yii: «Sii di sá ŋma?» Ní u kíí-bi yii.

eng: The Word was with God in the beginning.
local: Dinyɔɔbundi gbanti di nìn pée bí Unimbɔti cee doooo ŋkilkaamin.

eng: John confessed , Im not the Christ.
local: «Man di kaa sá Meesiya.» Waa nìn bál̀-bi, ki sòon̄ ki cántì-bi míǹ.

eng: They asked him, Then who are you? Are you Elijah? John said, Im not. Are you the prophet? John answered, No.
local: Ní bi fātìi bālifì-u yii: «Kin sii di sá ŋma ní? Eeliiii?» Ní u bí yii: «Aayee, maa sá Eelii.» Ní bi tí bālifì-u yii: «Sii di sá Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu un máaǹ kí dāan̄eeee?» Ní u tí bí yii: «Aayee.».

eng: They asked, Who are you? We need to give an answer to those who sent us. What do you say about yourself.
local: Ní bi fātìi tí bālifì-u yii: «Kin, sii baasii sá ŋma ní? Di sá ti là kí tin tùkù bin túnní-tee un ní a baasii sée. Sii lì yii a sá ŋma ní?».

eng: John replied, I am a voice crying out in the wilderness, Make the Lords path straight, just as the prophet Isaiah said.
local: Ní Yoowaneesi kíí-bi yii: «Man di sá un báakí kuteeun yii: <Tɔ̄maan nsan ŋun tèen̄ee kí tīī ti-Dindaan,>» - kii Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu Yeesaya mun nín nìn lī puee.

eng: Those sent by the Pharisees.
local: Fariisab biba nìn bí bin bi nìn túnní Yoowaneesi ceeyee kansikin.

eng: asked, Why do you baptize if you arent the Christ, nor Elijah, nor the prophet.
local: ní bíǹ bālifì-u yii: «A yaa kaa sá Meesiya, kaa sá Eelii, kaa tí sá Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu un ti cée, kin ba pu ní a fùul̀ binib Unimbɔti nyim?».

eng: John answered, I baptize with water. Someone greater stands among you, whom you dont recognize.
local: Ní Yoowaneesi kíí-bi yii: «Man fùul̀ binib nnyim ní, ama ubɔ bí ni-kansikin, naa nyí-u.

eng: He comes after me, but Im not worthy to untie his sandal straps.
local: úǹ di làá dāá dāan̄ man boon. Man kaa kpántì kí gútí u-naatakaŋ ŋmiii gba.».

eng: This encounter took place across the Jordan in Bethany where John was baptizing.
local: Bi nìn bí Beetanii, kitiŋ kin bí Jɔɔdan buŋgbandi lapu pu, laakin Yoowaneesi fùul̀ binib nnyimee, ní ki sòoǹ an bɔti gbanti.

eng: The next day John saw Jesus coming toward him and said, Look! The Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world!.
local: Kutaa wúntèe ní Yoowaneesi ká Yeesu dòoń u-cee, ní u bí yii: «Kpèèmaan, Unimbɔti pibil din lèekī binib kɔkɔ kpitiiyee dee!.

eng: Everything came into being through the Word, and without the Word nothing came into being. What came into being.
local: Díǹ ní Unimbɔti dū ki náań tikɔkɔ. Tiba kaa bí fám Unimbɔti kaa dū Dinyɔɔbundee ki náań kudɔŋu.

eng: This is the one about whom I said, He who comes after me is really greater than me because he existed before me.
local: Úǹ pu ní m fu sòon̄ yii: <Uninja ubɔ làá dāá dāan̄ man boon, ama úǹ jítiǹ man, kun puee úǹ pée bí ní bi nín màl̄ man.>.

eng: Even I didnt recognize him, but I came baptizing with water so that he might be made known to Israel.
local: Maa nìn nyí un ní an sée, ama an sá yii Isirayeel yab ń béé-uee pu ní man dɔ́miń ki bíi fùul̀ binib Unimbɔti nyim.».

eng: John testified, I saw the Spirit coming down from heaven like a dove, and it rested on him.
local: Ní Yoowaneesi gíti lī yii: «M ká Unimbɔti Fam lafun nyánní yilpu kii dibeenanjilee ki kpákatìń Yeesu pu.

eng: Even I didnt recognize him, but the one who sent me to baptize with water said to me, The one on whom you see the Spirit coming down and resting is the one who baptizes with the Holy Spirit.
local: Maa nìn nyí un ní an sée, ama Unimbɔti un túnní-mi ḿ nīn fùul̀ binib u-nyimee bí-mi yii: <A yaa láa ká man Fam kpákatìń ki kóò un puee, úǹ di làá fàl̀ binib Circir Fam.>.

eng: I have seen and testified that this one is Gods Son.
local: M ká mimmee, ní ki sòoǹ ki tùkù-ni yii úǹ di sá Unimbɔti Jipɔɔn.».

eng: The next day John was standing again with two of his disciples.
local: Kutaa tí wúntèe, Yoowaneesi nì u-boonnooliibi bili tí yú.

eng: When he saw Jesus walking along he said, Look! The Lamb of God.
local: U ká Yeesu bíi jītée ní u kpēē-u dímmm ki bí yii: «Kpèèmaan, Unimbɔti pibil sèé!».

eng: The two disciples heard what he said, and they followed Jesus.
local: Yoowaneesi boonnooliibi bilee gbìl̄ mimmee, ní ki nóó Yeesu.

eng: When Jesus turned and saw them following, he asked, What are you looking for? They said, Rabbi , where are you staying.
local: Yeesu fātìi kpēē bi nóó-uee, ní ki bí-bi yii: «Ni nyàab̀ ba?» Ní bi bí yii: «Rabii, la ní a kóò ní?» - Rabii taapu di sá yii, Udakal.

eng: He replied, Come and see. So they went and saw where he was staying, and they remained with him that day. It was about four oclock in the afternoon.
local: Ní Yeesu bí yii: «Dāam̄maan kí cùú béé laakin m kóòyee.» Ní bi nóó-u ki cūtìi béè laakin u kóòyee, ní ki kpáàa gbíntì niin yaadal wiiŋŋu. An nìn sá tikúti tinaa kujoou ní bi nìn cūtì.

eng: through the Word was life, and the life was the light for all people.
local: Di-ni ní dimaŋfal nìn bí, dimaŋfal gbanti di nìn tìì binib ŋwalifi.

eng: One of the two disciples who heard what John said and followed Jesus was Andrew, the brother of Simon Peter.
local: Andree un sá Siimɔn Peetroo naalee nìn sá binib bilee kansikin ubɔ bin nìn gbìl̄ Yoowaneesi nyɔɔbundi gbanti ki nóó Yeesuee.

eng: He first found his own brother Simon and said to him, We have found the Messiah .
local: ní Andree péé ki cūtìi ká u-maan Siimɔn ki tùkū-u yii: «Timbi ká Meesiya!» - An dàkà yii Kriisitoo.

eng: He led him to Jesus. Jesus looked at him and said, You are Simon, son of John. You will be called Cephas .
local: Ní u cáá Siimɔn ki cūnnì Yeesu cee. Ní Yeesu kpēē-u ní ki bí yii: «Sii di sá Siimɔn Yoowaneesi jipɔɔn. Bi làá nīn kíǹ ki yíì-si Keefaasi ní», an dàkà yii Peetroo.

eng: The next day Jesus wanted to go into Galilee, and he found Philip. Jesus said to him, Follow me.
local: Kutaa gíti tí wúntèe, Yeesu lēē yii u làá cù Galiilee, ní ki ká Fiiliipi, ní ki bí-u yii: «Nóó-mi!».

eng: Philip was from Bethsaida, the hometown of Andrew and Peter.
local: Fiiliipi nìn sá Beetisayida tiŋkin ní. Niin ní Andree nì Peetroo mun nìn sá.

eng: Philip found Nathanael and said to him, We have found the one Moses wrote about in the Law and the Prophets: Jesus, Josephs son, from Nazareth.
local: Boonee ní Fiiliipi ká Natanayeel, ki tùkū-u yii: «Timbi ká un pu ní Mooyiisi nìn ŋmàn̄ u-marab gbɔŋkun ki kpáaǹ Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi mun sòon̄ u-puee. An sá Yeesu Jooseefi jipɔɔn un sá Naasaareetee.».

eng: Nathanael responded, Can anything from Nazareth be good? Philip said, Come and see.
local: Ní Natanayeel bālifì-u yii: «Mimmee, tiwammɔntiil ŋūǹ kí nyānní Naasaareeti mun deeee?» Ní Fiiliipi bí-u yii: «Dāan̄ kí cùú kpèè.».

eng: Jesus saw Nathanael coming toward him and said about him, Here is a genuine Israelite in whom there is no deceit.
local: Yeesu kpēēyee, Natanayeel dòoń u-cee, ní u bíi sòoǹ u-pu yii: «Un bí nee di sá uisirayeelja páaa, kaa sá milimilidaan.».

eng: Nathanael asked him, How do you know me? Jesus answered, Before Philip called you, I saw you under the fig tree.
local: Ní Natanayeel bālifì-u yii: «A ŋá mana ní ki nyí-mi?» Ní Yeesu kíí-u yii: «Min a bí fiigi subu taapuee ní man ká-si ki wāatì ní Fiiliipi dāa nín yíiń-see.».

eng: Nathanael replied, Rabbi, you are Gods Son. You are the king of Israel.
local: Niinee ní Natanayeel bí-u yii: «Ukpil, a sá Unimbɔti Jipɔɔn ki sá Isirayeel yab mun bɔtiu!».

eng: The light shines in the darkness, and the darkness doesnt extinguish the light.
local: Ní ŋwalifee dɔ́miń ki ŋmíntí binib bin bí dibɔmbɔndinee, ní binibee kaa fōō-ŋu.

eng: Jesus answered, Do you believe because I told you that I saw you under the fig tree? You will see greater things than these!.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-u yii: «M nín bí yii m ká-si fiigi subu taapuee pu ní a fōō ki kííii? A làá gíti ká tin jítiǹ tin bí neeyee.

eng: I assure you that you will see heaven open and Gods angels going up to heaven and down to earth on the Human One.
local: Ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-ni, ni làá dāá ká kutagbɔŋu cúu pīitì, ní Unimbɔti tuuŋyi sìntīń ki jɔ́ḿ man Kinibiki pu.».

eng: A man named John was sent from God.
local: Unimbɔti nìn túnní uninja ubɔ, bi yíì-u Yoowaneesi.

eng: He came as a witness to testify concerning the light, so that through him everyone would believe in the light.
local: U nìn dɔ́miń kí sòoǹ nì binib ŋwalifi bɔti, án nín làá ŋá pu báà ŋma ń dī u-pu kí fōō kí kííyee.

eng: He himself wasnt the light, but his mission was to testify concerning the light.
local: Yoowaneesi u-ba kaa nìn sá ŋwalifi, u nìn dɔ́miń kí lēē ŋwalifee pu seera ní.

eng: The true light that shines on all people was coming into the world.
local: Dinyɔɔbundi di sá ŋwalifi mmɔnti, ki dɔ́miń kitiŋ pu ki ŋmíntí báà ŋma.

eng: I assure you that whoever doesnt enter into the sheep pen through the gate but climbs over the wall is a thief and an outlaw.
local: Yeesu bí yii: «Ibaamɔn, ibaamɔn ní m sòoǹ nì ni, un yaa kaa dī kipunyɔkɔ ki kɔ́ kupikpuŋkpuŋun ní ki yaa dī laabee, udaan sá digaŋgandaan ki sá unaayuu.

eng: The thief enters only to steal, kill, and destroy. I came so that they could have life—indeed, so that they could live life to the fullest.
local: Kun pu unaayuu dòońee di sá ú yɔ̀, kí kpɔ̀, kí bīil̀. Mamee m dɔ́miń í ká dimaŋfal ní, tiba nì tiba ń taa lóó-i dimaŋfal gbantin.

eng: I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd lays down his life for the sheep.
local: «Man di sá upikpaamɔnti, upikpaamɔnti piii pu ní u kúntí u-maŋfal.

eng: When the hired hand sees the wolf coming, he leaves the sheep and runs away. Thats because he isnt the shepherd; the sheep arent really his. So the wolf attacks the sheep and scatters them.
local: Upaadaan un kpàá ki nyàab̀ animbil, kaa sá upikpaal, kaa yì ipiiyee, u yaa ká usaakpan dòońee, u dù ipii ní kí fá kí sāǹ kí kátí, ní usaakpan ń dāan̄ kí cūtì ipii kí yàaǹ-i.

eng: Hes only a hired hand and the sheep dont matter to him.
local: Kun pu upaadaan sáńee di sá u nyàab̀ animbil ní, ipii bɔti kaa páb́-u.

eng: I am the good shepherd. I know my own sheep and they know me.
local: Man di sá upikpaamɔnti. M nyí m-piii ní m-piii mun nyí-mi.

eng: just as the Father knows me and I know the Father. I give up my life for the sheep.
local: kii m-Baa nín nyí-mi, ma mun nyí m-Baa puee ní. M kúntí m-maŋfal m-piii pu ní.

eng: I have other sheep that dont belong to this sheep pen. I must lead them too. They will listen to my voice and there will be one flock, with one shepherd.
local: M cáá ipii iba in kaa láá kóoń m-pikpuŋkpuŋku nee poonee. See ḿ cáań-i. I làá dāá gbìl̄ m-neen, ní kupicaŋkpuŋu ń ŋá kubaantiiu, ní upikpaal mun ń nīn sá ubaanti.

eng: This is why the Father loves me: I give up my life so that I can take it up again.
local: «Kun pu m-Baa néeń-mee di sá m tìì m-maŋfal ní kí fàtīí fōō-dì.

eng: No one takes it from me, but I give it up because I want to. I have the right to give it up, and I have the right to take it up again. I received this commandment from my Father.
local: Ubɔ kaa ŋūǹ kí tìl̄ man maŋfal, see man m-ba di là ní kí dū-dì kí tīī. M ŋūǹ kí dū-dì kí tīī, ki ŋūǹ kí fàtīí fōō-dì mun. M-Baa nín bí ḿ ŋá miŋyee dee.».

eng: There was another division among the Jews because of Jesus words.
local: Tibɔti gbanti pu ní Juuda yab cúu yākatì.

eng: The one who enters through the gate is the shepherd of the sheep.
local: Un yaa dī kipunyɔkɔ ki kɔ́ kupikpuŋkpuŋunee di sá upikpaal.

eng: Many of them said, He has a demon and has lost his mind. Why listen to him.
local: Bi-kansikin tikpil nìn lì yii: «Arasinii ubɔ di kā u-pu ní u sòoǹ fám fám. Ba pu ní ni pílifíǹ-u?».

eng: Others said, These arent the words of someone who has a demon. Can a demon heal the eyes of people who are blind.
local: Ní biba mun lì yii: «Unil un pu arasinii kēe kaa sòoǹ yéè. Mimmee arasinii ŋūǹ kí ŋúbití bijɔfub nimbiliŋaaa?».

eng: The time came for the Festival of Dedication in Jerusalem. It was winter.
local: An nìn sá dipeepɔndi ní. Bi nìn bíi jīǹ bi nín nìn ŋáŋkì kunimbɔtidii puee jiŋaaliu.

eng: and Jesus was in the temple, walking in the covered porch named for Solomon.
local: Yeesu nìn bí kunimbɔtidiin, laakin bi nìn yíì Saloomɔn kasarkauee taapu ki còom̄.

eng: The Jewish opposition circled around him and asked, How long will you test our patience? If you are the Christ, tell us plainly.
local: Ní Juuda yab dɔ́miń ki māntì ki tú-u ní ki bí yii: «Áli kí tin sāā ŋyunti ŋulɔŋu ní a làá sòoǹ-ti ibaamɔn ní ti-landɔkɔi ń wāl̀ ní? Sii di yaa sá Meesiya, á cántí kí tùkù-ti!».

eng: Jesus answered, I have told you, but you dont believe. The works I do in my Fathers name testify about me.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-bi yii: «M pée tùkū-ni dooooyee, ní naa fōō-mi ki kíí. Atuun ŋin m cáá m-Baa pɔŋŋu ki tūǹ nee sòoǹ m-pu.

eng: but you dont believe because you dont belong to my sheep.
local: ní naa fōō-mi ki kíí, kun puee naa sá m-piii.

eng: My sheep listen to my voice. I know them and they follow me.
local: M-piii gbìl̀ m-neen pu, ní ma mun nyí-i, ní i nóó-mi.

eng: I give them eternal life. They will never die, and no one will snatch them from my hand.
local: M tīī-i dimaŋfal din kaa cáá dikúntilee, i kpá kí kpú jaanjaan, báà ubɔ mun kaa tí ŋūǹ kí fōō-i m-ŋaan.

eng: My Father, who has given them to me, is greater than all, and no one is able to snatch them from my Fathers hand.
local: M-Baa un tīī-mi-iee cáá mpɔn ki jítiǹ báà ŋma. Míǹ ní ubɔ kaa ŋūǹ kí fōō tiba m-Baa ŋaan.

eng: The guard at the gate opens the gate for him, and the sheep listen to his voice. He calls his own sheep by name and leads them out.
local: Un cí kipunyɔkɔee pìitī-u, ní u-piii nyí u-neen. U yíì u-piii sá kamaa nì i-yiŋŋi, ní ki lìntī i-nyɔkɔpu ki cáǹ-i kupikpaaun.

eng: I and the Father are one.
local: Man nì m-Baa Unimbɔti, ti sá ubaanti ní.».

eng: Again the Jewish opposition picked up stones in order to stone him.
local: Niinee ní Juuda yab tí fātìi píí ataŋkpee kí yāŋkì kí kpɔ̀-u.

eng: Jesus responded, I have shown you many good works from the Father. For which of those works do you stone me.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-bi yii: «M dū m-Baa pɔŋŋu ki túń atummɔnti tikpil ni-nimbilin. Mimmee, atuun gbanti kansikinee, dilal pu ní ni là kí yāŋkì-mi ataŋkpee kí kpɔ̀ na?».

eng: The Jewish opposition answered, We dont stone you for a good work but for insulting God. You are human, yet you make yourself out to be God.
local: Ní Juuda yab kíí-u yii: «An kaa sá atummɔnti pu ní ti là kí yāŋkì-si ataŋkpee kí kpɔ̀, an sá a nín sòon̄ ki bíil̀ Unimbɔtee pu. Kun puee sii sá unifantanti ní ki ŋèeǹ nì Unimbɔti.».

eng: Jesus replied, Isnt it written in your Law, I have said, you are gods?.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-bi yii: «An kaa ŋmàn̄ ni-marau poon yii: <M bí yii, ni sá inimbɔtaaa?>.

eng: Scripture calls those to whom Gods word came gods , and scripture cant be abolished.
local: Mara gbɔŋku yaa ŋūǹ kí yíiń bin bin bi sòon̄ Unimbɔti bɔti ki tùkūee yii bi sá inimbɔtee.

eng: So how can you say that the one whom the Father has made holy and sent into the world insults God because he said, I am Gods Son?.
local: kin un Unimbɔti baasii lēē ki túnní duulinyanee fáà! Kin an dī mana ní nimbi lì yii m ŋèeǹ nì Unimbɔti m nín bí yii man di sá Unimbɔti Jipɔɔnee.

eng: If I dont do the works of my Father, dont believe me.
local: M yaa kaa bíi tūǹ m-Baa tuuŋŋi, ní taa fōō-mi kí kíí.

eng: But if I do them, and you dont believe me, believe the works so that you can know and recognize that the Father is in me and I am in the Father.
local: Ní m yaa bíi tūǹ-ŋi, báà ni yaa kaa làá fōō-mi kí kíí, fōōmaan atuun ŋin m bíi tūǹee kí kíí kí baasii béé yii Ubaati bí m-ni ní ma mun bí Ubaatin.».

eng: Again, they wanted to arrest him, but he escaped from them.
local: Niinee ní bi tí bíi nyàab̀ kí cúú-u ní u ŋmátì bi-ŋaan.

eng: Whenever he has gathered all of his sheep, he goes before them and they follow him, because they know his voice.
local: U yaa nyántì i-kɔkɔ kupikpuŋkpuŋun ki dóòyee, ní ú lìntī i-nyɔkɔpu ní í páań u-boon, kun puee i nyí u-neen.

eng: Jesus went back across the Jordan to the place where John had baptized at first, and he stayed there.
local: Ní u fātìi gítì Jɔɔdan buŋgbandi lapu pu, laakin Yoowaneesi nìn dáá kíĺ ki fùul̀ binib nnyimee, ki kāl̀ niin.

eng: Many people came to him. John didnt do any miraculous signs, they said, but everything John said about this man was true.
local: Binib tikpil nìn dòoń u-cee niin, ní ki lì yii: «Yoowaneesi kaa nìn ŋá maamaaci báà ubɔ.

eng: Many believed in Jesus there.
local: ama tin kɔkɔwoooo u nìn sòon̄ kijapaai nee puee, an sá ibaamɔn.» Binib tikpil nìn fōō Yeesu ki kíí niin.

eng: They wont follow a stranger but will run away because they dont know the strangers voice.
local: Iaa nōotí uniyaŋyan. I sáń-u ní, kun puee iaa nyí u-neen.».

eng: Those who heard Jesus use this analogy didnt understand what he was saying.
local: Yeesu nìn dū tibɔti nee ki ŋmāntì, ama ní baa nìn gbìl̄ ti-taapu.

eng: So Jesus spoke again, I assure you that I am the gate of the sheep.
local: Míǹ puee ní Yeesu gíti lī yii: «Ibaamɔn, ibaamɔn ní m sòoǹ nì ni, man di sá kupikpuŋkpuŋu punyɔkɔki.

eng: All who came before me were thieves and outlaws, but the sheep didnt listen to them.
local: Bin kɔkɔ péeǹ-mi ki bāańee sá digaŋgandambi, ní ki sá binaayub, ipii mun kaa pílifíǹ-bi.

eng: I am the gate. Whoever enters through me will be saved. They will come in and go out and find pasture.
local: Man di sá kipunyɔkɔ. Un yaa dīń man pu ki kóońee kāntí difiil. Udaan ŋūǹ kí nīn kóò ki nyēē ki kāntí u-jinti.

eng: A certain man, Lazarus, was ill. He was from Bethany, the village of Mary and her sister Martha. .
local: Uninja ubɔ nìn būǹ ki bí Beetanii, kitiŋ kin Maarii nì u-maan Maarta nìn kóòyee, bi yíì-u Laasaar.

eng: But whoever walks in the night does stumble because the light isnt in them.
local: Ama sii un yaa còom̄ kunyeeuee di gbéetí, kun puee ŋwalifi kaa ti bí-si.».

eng: He continued, Our friend Lazarus is sleeping, but I am going in order to wake him up.
local: Yeesu lī míǹ ki dóòyee, ní ki gíti kútì yii: «Ti-bɔɔ Laasaar gèen̄. Ama man làá cùú fìntī-u.».

eng: The disciples said, Lord, if hes sleeping, he will get well.
local: Ní bi kíí-u yii: «Ti-Dindaan, u yaa gèen̄ee, u làá pɔ̄ɔkì deeyaa!».

eng: They thought Jesus meant that Lazarus was in a deep sleep, but Jesus had spoken about Lazarus death.
local: Yeesu nìn là kí lì yii Laasaar kpíì ní, ama ní u-boonnooliibi bíǹ dākafì yii u sòoǹ ŋgeem min báà ŋma gèeǹee pu ní.

eng: Jesus told them plainly, Lazarus has died.
local: Míǹ puee ní Yeesu tùkū ki cántì-bi, yii: «Laasaar kpíì.

eng: For your sakes, Im glad I wasnt there so that you can believe. Lets go to him.
local: Ni-puee an mɔ̄ǹ-mi m nín kaa bí niinee, min làá ŋá ní fōō-mi kí kííyee dee. Kɔŋkɔnnee tí nīn cá u-cee!».

eng: Then Thomas said to the other disciples, Let us go too so that we may die with Jesus.
local: Niinee ní Toomasi bí Yeesu boonnooliibi bin kíǹee yii: «Ti mun ń táá-u ti nì ti-Kpiliu ń tin kpú dicilpu!».

eng: When Jesus arrived, he found that Lazarus had already been in the tomb for four days.
local: Yeesu bàn̄ee ní ki ká yii Laasaar nìn ŋá dikaakulin iwiin inaa.

eng: Bethany was a little less than two miles from Jerusalem.
local: Beetanii kaa nìn dátiǹ Jeerusaleem, an nìn sá kii ataŋkpee ata nyɔkɔm ní.

eng: Many Jews had come to comfort Martha and Mary after their brothers death.
local: An di yì-tì ní Juuda yab tikpil nìn dɔ́miń kí sɔ̀ŋkì Maarii nì Maarta pɔbiliŋ bi-niija kúm pu.

eng: This was the Mary who anointed the Lord with fragrant oil and wiped his feet with her hair. Her brother Lazarus was ill.
local: - Maarii gbanti di sá un nìn kpáatì talaalu ki sītì ti-Dindaan taaŋi pu ki dū u-yikpiti ki pítèe. U-niija di sá Laasaar un nìn būǹee.

eng: When Martha heard that Jesus was coming, she went to meet him, while Mary remained in the house.
local: Maarta nín gbìl̄ yii Yeesu dòońee, ní u yíkìi túŋkì-u, Maarii úǹ nìn gbíntìi kā kudiin.

eng: Martha said to Jesus, Lord, if you had been here, my brother wouldnt have died.
local: Ní Maarta bí Yeesu yii: «Ukpil, a yaa bàà nìn bí doo, m-niija kaa bàà kúù.

eng: Even now I know that whatever you ask God, God will give you.
local: Ama m nyí yii, kɔŋkɔnnee gba, tin tin kamaa ní a yaa mēē Unimbɔtee u làá tīī-si kudɔŋu.».

eng: Jesus told her, Your brother will rise again.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-u yii: «A-niija làá fìkitī.».

eng: Martha replied, I know that he will rise in the resurrection on the last day.
local: Ní Maarta kíí-u yii: «M nyí yii, ŋwinjoolkaa dal, difìkitil yuntiŋuee, u mun làá dāá fìkitī.».

eng: Jesus said to her, I am the resurrection and the life. Whoever believes in me will live, even though they die.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-u yii: «Man di sá difìkitil nì dimaŋfal. Sii un fōō-mi ki kííyee, báà a yaa kpíì gba, a làá nīn cáá dimaŋfal.

eng: Everyone who lives and believes in me will never die. Do you believe this.
local: ní sii un yaa bí dimaŋfalin ní ki fōō-mi ki kííyee kaa ti kúù jaanjaan. A fōō ki kíí mimmaaa?».

eng: She replied, Yes, Lord, I believe that you are the Christ, Gods Son, the one who is coming into the world.
local: Ní u kíí-u yii: «Iiin, m-Dindaan: M nyí yii sii di sá Meesiya, Unimbɔti Jipɔɔn un máaǹ kí dāan̄ duulinyanee.».

eng: After she said this, she went and spoke privately to her sister Mary, The teacher is here and hes calling for you.
local: U sòon̄ míǹ ki dóòyee, ní ki cūtì ki yíiń u-naal Maarii ki tùkū-u soooo yii: «Ukpil bāań ki bàlifī a-pu.».

eng: When Mary heard this, she got up quickly and went to Jesus.
local: Maarii gbìl̄ mimmee, ní ki yíkì dicilpu ki búntì Yeesu cee.

eng: So the sisters sent word to Jesus, saying, Lord, the one whom you love is ill.
local: Ní Laasaar niisalibee tú ubɔ ú cùú tùkù Yeesu yii: «Ti-Dindaan, a-bɔɔ būǹ.».

eng: He hadnt entered the village but was still in the place where Martha had met him.
local: Di sá yii Yeesu nìn dáá gbíntìi bí laakin Maarta nìn túŋkì-uee, kaa láá kɔ́ kitiŋin.

eng: When the Jews who were comforting Mary in the house saw her get up quickly and leave, they followed her. They assumed she was going to mourn at the tomb.
local: Juuda yab bin nìn bí Maarii cee kudiin ki ŋmēentí-uee nín ká u yíkì dicilpu ki nín nyēēyee, ní bi dī-u. Bíǹ dàkafī yii u cá dikaakul pu kí sɔ̄ dee.

eng: When Mary arrived where Jesus was and saw him, she fell at his feet and said, Lord, if you had been here, my brother wouldnt have died.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun Maarii bàn̄ laakin Yeesu bée ki ká-uee, ní u tātìi gbāaǹ u-nimbiin, ki bí-u yii: «Ti-Dindaan, a yaa bàà nìn bí doo, m-niija kaa bàà kúù.».

eng: When Jesus saw her crying and the Jews who had come with her crying also, he was deeply disturbed and troubled.
local: Yeesu nín ká u bíi sūǹee, ki kpáaǹ Juuda yab bin nìn nóoń-uee, ní kunimbaau kɔ́-u ki gbēē u-pɔbil.

eng: He asked, Where have you laid him? They replied, Lord, come and see.
local: ní u bālifì-bi yii: «La ní ni síb́-u ní?» Ní bi kíí-u yii: «Ti-Dindaan, dāá cùú kpèè.».

eng: Jesus began to cry.
local: Ní Yeesu bíi sūǹ.

eng: The Jews said, See how much he loved him.
local: Ní Juuda yab bí yii: «Kpèèmaan, u nín bàà baasii néeń-u puee!».

eng: But some of them said, He healed the eyes of the man born blind. Couldnt he have kept Lazarus from dying.
local: Ama ní bi-kansikin biba mun bí yii: «Úǹ un nìn ŋúbitì ujɔfuee nimbiliŋee kaan ŋūǹ kí yàntī Laasaar ń taa bàà kpúɔɔɔ?».

eng: Jesus was deeply disturbed again when he came to the tomb. It was a cave, and a stone covered the entrance.
local: An fātìi tí gbēē Yeesu pɔbil, ní u búntì dikaakul laŋki. An nìn sá ntaŋkpalɔkɔn, ní bi dū ditaŋkpal ki ŋéeb́ di-nyɔkɔ pu.

eng: Jesus said, Remove the stone. Martha, the sister of the dead man, said, Lord, the smell will be awful! Hes been dead four days.
local: Ní Yeesu bí yii: «Nyìkitīmaan ditaŋkpalee!» Niinee ní Maarta, utaŋkpiilee niisal, bí yii: «Ti-Dindaan, an ŋá iwiin inaa dee, u máaǹ ki tin núukì!».
