eng: When he heard this, Jesus said, This illness isnt fatal. Its for the glory of God so that Gods Son can be glorified through it.
local: Yeesu gbìl̄ mimmee, ní ki bí yii: «Laasaar weeŋyi nee kaa sá-u ŋkúm, i sá kí tīī Unimbɔti tinyool, ní ki làá tīī u-Jipɔɔn mun tinyool.».

eng: Jesus replied, Didnt I tell you that if you believe, you will see Gods glory.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-u yii: «Maa tùkū-si yii, a yaa fōō-mi ki kíí, a làá ká Unimbɔti nyootaaa?».

eng: So they removed the stone. Jesus looked up and said, Father, thank you for hearing me.
local: Niinee ní bi nyīkitì ditaŋkpal dikaakul nyɔkɔ pu. Ní Yeesu wáaǹ kutaa pu, ní ki bí yii: «M-baa, m jáam̀-si kii a nín pílifíǹ-mee pu.

eng: I know you always hear me. I say this for the benefit of the crowd standing here so that they will believe that you sent me.
local: M nyí yii a pée pílifíǹ-mi baabadal, ama bin māntì ki tú-mi nee pu ní m bíi lì, bí fōō kí kíí yii sii di túnní-mi.».

eng: Having said this, Jesus shouted with a loud voice, Lazarus, come out.
local: U lī míǹ ki dóòyee, ní ki téeń mpɔn yii: «Laasaar, nyānní!».

eng: The dead man came out, his feet bound and his hands tied, and his face covered with a cloth. Jesus said to them, Untie him and let him go.
local: Ní utaŋkpiil nyánní, u-taaŋi nì u-ŋalii fìn̄ iŋaabu, ki kpáaǹ u-nimbiin. Ní Yeesu bí yii: «Cúú fìntīmaan-u kí yàntī ú nīn cá!».

eng: Therefore, many of the Jews who came with Mary and saw what Jesus did believed in him.
local: Juuda yab kansikin bin nìn dɔ́miń Maarii cee ki ká min min Yeesu ŋée tikpil fōō-u ki kíí.

eng: But some of them went to the Pharisees and told them what Jesus had done.
local: Ama bi-kansikin biba cūtì ki ká Fariisab ki máafì-bi min Yeesu ŋée.

eng: Then the chief priests and Pharisees called together the council and said, What are we going to do? This man is doing many miraculous signs!.
local: Ní bi nì bisaraaŋakpilib kpáfì bibɔsoonliibi kitil kɔkɔ ki sòon̄ yii: «Ti làá ŋá mana ní kijapaayee ŋáań maamaacib tikpil míǹ na!.

eng: If we let him go on like this, everyone will believe in him. Then the Romans will come and take away both our temple and our people.
local: Ti yaa yāntì u gbíntìi ŋáań míǹ, báà ŋma làá fōō-u kí kíí. An làá yàntī Room gɔminau ń dāan̄ kí wīī ti-nimbɔtidiiku nì ti-tiŋki.».

eng: One of them, Caiaphas, who was high priest that year, told them, You dont know anything!.
local: Bi-kansikinee ubɔ nìn bí, bi yíì-u Kaayiifi, úǹ di nìn sá usaraaŋakpil dibindi gbanti, ní úǹ bí-bi yii: «Naa gbìl̀ tibɔti taapu.

eng: Jesus loved Martha, her sister, and Lazarus.
local: Yeesu nìn néeń Maarta nì u-naal nì bi-niija Laasaar.

eng: You dont see that it is better for you that one man die for the people rather than the whole nation be destroyed.
local: Naa dàkafī yii an ŋān̄ yii unibaanti ń kpú inifunaan pu ní ditimbundi kɔkɔ ń ŋmátáaa?».

eng: He didnt say this on his own. As high priest that year, he prophesied that Jesus would soon die for the nation.
local: An kaa nìn sá úǹ u-ba di là ní ki sòon̄ an bɔti. U nín nìn sá usaraaŋakpil dibindi gbantee puee, ní Unimbɔti yāntì u sòon̄ yii Yeesu làá dāá kpú Juuda yab pu.

eng: and not only for the nation. Jesus would also die so that Gods children scattered everywhere would be gathered together as one.
local: An kaa sá Juuda yab baba pu, an sá kí kpāfì Unimbɔti biyaamu bin yàl̄ee kɔkɔ bí ŋá ubaanti.

eng: From that day on they plotted to kill him.
local: Ki yōoǹ an wiiŋŋu gbanti ní bi bóob́ yii see bí kpɔ̀ Yeesu.

eng: Therefore, Jesus was no longer active in public ministry among the Jewish leaders. Instead, he left Jerusalem and went to a place near the wilderness, to a city called Ephraim, where he stayed with his disciples.
local: An di yì-tì ní Yeesu kíǹ ki còom̄, kaa yàntī Juuda yab kāntí-u, ní ki búntì kitiŋ kiban, kì bí kuteeu caŋin, bi yíì-kì Eefiraayiim. Niin ní u nì u-boonnooliibi nìn cūtìi kāl̀.

eng: It was almost time for the Jewish Passover, and many people went from the countryside up to Jerusalem to purify themselves through ritual washing before the Passover.
local: Dilákatil jiŋaaliu nìn tóobiń, ní binib tikpil nìn yíkí ki cá Jeerusaleem kí cù kí ŋá saraabi, kí nyā bi-jɔkɔntin kí wàatī ní jiŋaalee ń nín bàń.

eng: They were looking for Jesus. As they spoke to each other in the temple, they said, What do you think? He wont come to the festival, will he.
local: Bi nìn nyàab̄ Yeesu niin. Bi bí kunimbɔtidiinee, ní ki bíi bàlifī tɔb yii: «Ni dàkafī yii ba ní? U làá dāan̄ jiŋaal neeyaaa, waa làá dāan̄?».

eng: The chief priests and Pharisees had given orders that anyone who knew where he was should report it, so they could arrest him.
local: Di sá yii bisaraaŋakpilib nì Fariisab nìn síiǹ mara yii un yaa nyí laakin Yeesu bée ń lì bí cùú cúú-u.

eng: When he heard that Lazarus was ill, he stayed where he was. After two days.
local: Nì míǹ kɔkɔee, u nín gbìl̄ yii Laasaar būǹee, u gíti kāl̀ laakin u nìn bée iwiin ili.

eng: he said to his disciples, Lets return to Judea again.
local: Ní boonee, u tùkū u-boonnooliibi yii: «Tí gítímaan Juudee!».

eng: The disciples replied, Rabbi, the Jewish opposition wants to stone you, but you want to go back.
local: Ní u-boonnooliibi kíí-u yii: «Ukpil, yaadal nee ní Juuda yab kpilib kaa bíi nyàab̀ ataŋkpee kí yāŋkì kí kpɔ̀-saaa? Ní a là kí gítí niinaaa?».

eng: Jesus answered, Arent there twelve hours in the day? Whoever walks in the day doesnt stumble because they see the light of the world.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-bi yii: «Ŋwìiń ń nyā kí dāa jēetée tikúti saalaa nì tili di kaa báaa? Sii un yaa còom̄ ŋwiimpuee, aa gbéetí, kun puee ŋwìiń wāĺ an kɔkɔ.

eng: Six days before Passover, Jesus came to Bethany, home of Lazarus, whom Jesus had raised from the dead.
local: An nìn kíǹ iwiin iluu bí jī Dilákatil jiŋaaliu, ní Yeesu nìn bàn̄ Beetanii, laakin Laasaar, kijapaai kin u nìn fīkitèe nìn kóòyee.

eng: The chief priests decided that they would kill Lazarus too.
local: Ní bisaraaŋakpilibee sòon̄ ki yóoǹ yii bí kpɔ̀ Laasaar mun.

eng: It was because of Lazarus that many of the Jews had deserted them and come to believe in Jesus.
local: kun puee úǹ pu ní Juuda yab tikpil kíǹ ki nóó Yeesu, kaa ti là bíǹ cee.

eng: The next day the great crowd that had come for the festival heard that Jesus was coming to Jerusalem.
local: Kutaa wúntèe, kunipaau kun nìn dɔ́miń Dilákatil jiŋaaliuee gbìl̄ yii Yeesu dòoń Jeerusaleem.

eng: They took palm branches and went out to meet him. They shouted, Hosanna! Blessings on the one who comes in the name of the Lord! Blessings on the king of Israel.
local: Bi nìn bátì tikpakpati ki túŋkì-u nsanni, ní ki kūuntí yii: «Oosaana, tí dàntìmaan-u! Ti-Dindaan ń bìin̄ un dòoń úǹ yindi puee! Unimbɔti ń bìin̄ Isirayeel bɔtiu boon!».

eng: Jesus found a young donkey and sat on it, just as it is written.
local: Yeesu nìn ká dimampɔmbil ki kā, kii Unimbɔti gbɔŋku nín nìn lī ki yóoǹ puee, yii.

eng: Dont be afraid, Daughter Zion. Look! Your king is coming , sitting on a donkeys colt .
local: «Siiyɔn tiŋki, taa fàŋkì! Kpèè, a-bɔtiu di dòoń, ki kā dimampɔmbil puee!».

eng: His disciples didnt understand these things at first. After he was glorified, they remembered that these things had been written about him and that they had done these things to him.
local: Ŋyunti gbantee, u-boonnooliibi kaa nìn dáá láá gbìl̄ taapu. Ama ŋyunti ŋun u fōō u-nyootee ní bi nín téetì yii Unimbɔti gbɔŋku nín nìn sòon̄ tin u-puee, míǹ ní bi ŋá ki tīī-u nee.

eng: The crowd who had been with him when he called Lazarus out of the tomb and raised him from the dead were testifying about him.
local: Bin kɔkɔwoooo nìn bí ŋyunti ŋun Yeesu yíiń Laasaar dikaakulin ki fīkitì-u ditaŋkpiilinee bíi máafí tin bi ká Yeesu ŋée.

eng: Thats why the crowd came to meet him, because they had heard about this miraculous sign that he had done.
local: Di sá yii bi nín nìn gbìl̄ maamaaci un u nìn ŋée puee ní kunipaauee túŋkì-u.

eng: Therefore, the Pharisees said to each other, See! Youve accomplished nothing! Look! The whole world is following him.
local: Ní Fariisab bíi lì tɔb cee niin yii: «Ni káa! Naa làá ti ká báà tiba. Binib kɔkɔ di bìtifī ki cá kí nóó-uee.».

eng: Lazarus and his sisters hosted a dinner for him. Martha served and Lazarus was among those who joined him at the table.
local: Niin ní bi ŋá Yeesu tican. Maarta di nìn tìì tijin. Laasaar mun nìn ŋmàl̄ bin nì Yeesu nìn kā ki jīǹee kansikin.

eng: Some Greeks were among those who had come up to worship at the festival.
local: Greeki yab biba nìn ŋmàl̄ bin nìn dɔ́miń Jeerusaleem kí nyɔ̄ŋkì Unimbɔti jiŋaalee poonee kansikin.

eng: They came to Philip, who was from Bethsaida in Galilee, and made a request: Sir, we want to see Jesus.
local: Ní bi dɔ́miń ki ká Fiiliipi un sá Beetisayida Galiilee tiŋkinee, ki bíi gbàam̄-u yii: «Ukpil, ti là kí ká Yeesu.».

eng: Philip told Andrew, and Andrew and Philip told Jesus.
local: Ní Fiiliipi cūtìi tùkū Andree, ní bi-liitil cūtì ki tùkū Yeesu.

eng: Jesus replied, The time has come for the Human One to be glorified.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-bi yii: «Ŋyunti ŋun man Kinibiki làá fōō m-nyootee bāań dee.

eng: I assure you that unless a grain of wheat falls into the earth and dies, it can only be a single seed. But if it dies, it bears much fruit.
local: Ibaamɔn, ibaamɔn ní m sòoǹ nì ni, ŋwambool yaa kaa lítì kitiŋ ki kpíì, ŋu pée gbíntìi sá mbaanti ní. Ama ŋu yaa kpíìyee, ní ŋú nyā kí lòoǹ tikpil.

eng: Those who love their lives will lose them, and those who hate their lives in this world will keep them forever.
local: Sii un yaa néeń a-maŋfalee làá fá-dì, ní sii un yaa ná a-maŋfal taapu doo neeyee, siiyee di kpáafí-dì ki kāntí dimaŋfal din kaa cáá dikúntilee.

eng: Whoever serves me must follow me. Wherever I am, there my servant will also be. My Father will honor whoever serves me.
local: Un yaa là kí tɔ̄ m-tundee máaǹ kí nīn nóó-mi, ní laakin m yaa bée, m-tɔntɔnliu mun ń nīn bí niin. Un yaa tūǹ m-tundee, Ubaati làá tīī-u tinyool.».

eng: Now I am deeply troubled. What should I say? Father, save me from this time? No, for this is the reason I have come to this time.
local: «Kɔŋkɔnnee m bākatì. Mimmee, ḿ lì yii, m-Baa, nyāntí-mi tin làá bàń-mi ŋyunti nee pooneeee? Aayee, kun puee ŋyunti nee falaau pu ní m dɔ́miń.

eng: Father, glorify your name! Then a voice came from heaven, I have glorified it, and I will glorify it again.
local: Míǹ puee, m-Baa, tīī a-yindi tinyool!» Niinee ní Unimbɔti sòon̄ní yilpu ki bí yii: «M tīī m-yindi tinyool, ki làá gíti tí tīī-dì.».

eng: The crowd standing there heard and said, Its thunder. Others said, An angel spoke to him.
local: Kunipaau kun nìn yú niinee gbìl̄ Unimbɔti neen puee ní ki bí yii: «An sá utaa di fáá didundi.» Ní biba mun bí yii: «An sá Unimbɔti tuuŋŋu ŋubɔ di sòon̄ nì u.».

eng: Then Mary took an extraordinary amount, almost three-quarters of a pound, of very expensive perfume made of pure nard. She anointed Jesus feet with it, then wiped his feet dry with her hair. The house was filled with the aroma of the perfume.
local: Ní Maarii yōoǹní talaalumɔnti un bi yíì náaree, u pɔ̄ɔ̄ kudaau tikpil, ní ki cáań ki kpáatìi sītì Yeesu taaŋi pu, ní ki dū u-yikpiti ki pítì, ní kudii kɔkɔ jíń talaalu sɔnsɔfiŋu.

eng: Jesus replied, This voice wasnt for my benefit but for yours.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-bi yii: «An kaa sá man pu ní nneenee sòon̄, ama an sá nimbi pu ní ŋu sòon̄.

eng: Now is the time for judgment of this world. Now this worlds ruler will be thrown out.
local: Ŋyunti nee ní Unimbɔti làá sòoǹ duulinya bɔti, ní kí jà duulinya bɔtiu Sitaan.

eng: When I am lifted up from the earth, I will draw everyone to me. .
local: Bi yaa láa yōoǹ-mi yilpuee, m làá dāá dáaliń nnibiyaamu kɔkɔ kí cáań m-cee.».

eng: He said this to show how he was going to die.
local: U nìn lī míǹ kí dàkà ŋkúm min u làá kpúee nín sá miŋyee ní.

eng: The crowd responded, We have heard from the Law that the Christ remains forever. How can you say that the Human One must be lifted up? Who is this Human One.
local: Ní binibee kíí-u yii: «Ti bífì marab gbɔŋku poon yii Meesiya làá nīn pée bí ní kí nīn cá jaanjaan. Kin ba pu ní sii lī yii bi làá yòon̄ Kinibiki yilpu? Ŋma di sá Kinibiki gbanti ní?».

eng: Jesus replied, The light is with you for only a little while. Walk while you have the light so that darkness doesnt overtake you. Those who walk in the darkness dont know where they are going.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-bi yii: «Ŋwalifi dáá bí ni-kansikin ŋyunti ŋubɔ poon fiii. Ní nīn còom̄ ŋwalifi gbanti poon, án nín làá ŋá pu dibɔmbɔndi ń taa jēetí ni-puee, kun puee un còom̄ dibɔmbɔndinee kaa nyí laakin u cée.

eng: As long as you have the light, believe in the light so that you might become people whose lives are determined by the light. After Jesus said these things, he went away and hid from them.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun ŋwalifi dáá bí ni-cee nee, ní fōō-ŋu kí kíí án nín làá ŋá pu ní kpántí ŋwalifin nibee.» Yeesu sòon̄ míǹ ki dóòyee, ní ki yíkì bi-cee ki cūtì ndan ki bál̀-bi.

eng: Jesus had done many miraculous signs before the people, but they didnt believe in him.
local: Nì min kɔkɔ u ŋáaǹ maamaacib tikpil bi-nimbiinee, baa fōō-u ki kíí.

eng: This was to fulfill the word of the prophet Isaiah: Lord, who has believed through our message? To whom is the arm of the Lord fully revealed?.
local: Kii Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu Yeesaya nín nìn ŋmàn̄ pu dooooyee, míǹ ní an ŋá nee. U nìn ŋmàn̄ yii: «Ti-Dindaan, ŋma fōō ti-bɔti ki kíí? Ŋma béè sii ti-Dindaan pɔŋŋu?».

eng: Isaiah explains why they couldnt believe.
local: Yeesaya nìn gíti lī kun pu ní baa nìn ŋúń ki fōō ki kííyee: u nìn bí yii.

eng: Judas Iscariot, one of his disciples , complained.
local: Niinee ní Yeesu boonnooliibi kansikin ubɔ, Juudasi Isikaariyɔti un dāa dū-u ki nyáfèe, bí yii.

eng: He made their eyes blind and closed their minds so that they might not see with their eyes, understand with their minds, and turn their lives around— and I would heal them.
local: «Unimbɔti yāntì bi jɔ̀b̄ ní ki pɔ́ɔkì bi-pɔbiliŋ, án nín làá ŋá pu bi-nimbiliŋ ń taa nín̄ wāĺee ní bi-pɔbiliŋ ń nīn gbìfì bí taa kpántí bi-bimbim ú cáaǹ-bee.».

eng: Isaiah said these things because he saw Jesus glory; he spoke about Jesus.
local: Yeesaya lī míǹ kun puee u nìn ká Yeesu nyooti, ní ki sòon̄ u-pu.

eng: Even so, many leaders believed in him, but they wouldnt acknowledge their faith because they feared that the Pharisees would expel them from the synagogue.
local: Nì míǹ kɔkɔee, Juuda yab kpilib tikpil nìn fōō-u ki kíí. Ama Fariisab ń taa jà-bi kukpafidiin puee, baa nìn nyántì bi-ba.

eng: They believed, but they loved human praise more than Gods glory.
local: Di sá yii bi là binib ń pàkà-bi ki jítiǹ Unimbɔti ń pàkà-bi.

eng: Jesus shouted, Whoever believes in me doesnt believe in me but in the one who sent me.
local: Yeesu téeń mpɔn ki lī yii: «Un yaa fōō-mi ki kííyee, an kaa sá man ní u fōō ki kíí, u fōō un túnní-mee ní ki kíí.

eng: Whoever sees me sees the one who sent me.
local: Un yaa ká-mee ká ki kpáaǹ un túnní-mee dee.

eng: I have come as a light into the world so that everyone who believes in me wont live in darkness.
local: M dɔ́miń duulinyan kii ŋwalifee, án nín làá ŋá pu un sá kamaa yaa fōō-mi ki kííyee ń taa nín̄ gíti bí dibɔmbɔndinee.

eng: If people hear my words and dont keep them, I dont judge them. I didnt come to judge the world but to save it.
local: Ama un di yaa gbìl̄ m-bɔti ní ki yaa kaa kpáafì-tèe, an kaa sá man di làá kpɔ̀ udaan bɔti, kun puee maa dɔ́miń kí kpɔ̀ duulinya nib bɔti, m dɔ́miń kí fīī-bi ní.

eng: Whoever rejects me and doesnt receive my words will be judged at the last day by the word I have spoken.
local: Un kaa là-mi, kaa fōō m-bɔtee cáá tin làá dāá sòoǹ u-bɔtee: an di sá m-bɔti tin m nìn dàkēe, tíǹ di làá dāá kpɔ̀ udaan bɔti ŋwinjoolkaa dal.

eng: I dont speak on my own, but the Father who sent me commanded me regarding what I should speak and say.
local: Di sá yii an kaa sá man m-ba pɔŋŋu pu ní m sòon̄, an sá m-Baa un túnní-mee u-ba di tīī-mi tin sá ḿ sòoǹee nì tin sá ḿ dàkèe.

eng: This perfume was worth a years wages! Why wasnt it sold and the money given to the poor? .
local: «Ba pu ní baa dū talaalu nee kí nyāfì kí fōō animbil jitɔmbi alaataa ata kí dūú yàkatī biwɔfib?».

eng: I know that his commandment is eternal life. Therefore, whatever I say is just as the Father has said to me.
local: Ní m nyí yii tin ní u sòoǹee cāabìń dimaŋfal din kaa cáá dikúntilee. Míǹ puee, tin m sòon̄ee, tin m-Baa tīī-mi ḿ sòoǹee dee.».

eng: He said this not because he cared about the poor but because he was a thief. He carried the money bag and would take what was in it.
local: Tin u sòon̄ee, an kaa sá u sūǹ biwɔfib pu, u nìn sá unaayuu ní. Úǹ di nìn ŋúb́ bi-nimbiliŋ ní ki lèekī ŋi-poon.

eng: Then Jesus said, Leave her alone. This perfume was to be used in preparation for my burial, and this is how she has used it.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-u yii: «Yàntī-u! U yāaǹ ki cíitì daliŋkin bi làá dāá dū-mi kí ŋá dikaakulinee dee.

eng: You will always have the poor among you, but you wont always have me.
local: Biwɔfib làá nīn pée bí ni-kansikin ní baabadal, ama man nì ni kaa làá nīn bí baabadal.».

eng: Many Jews learned that he was there. They came not only because of Jesus but also to see Lazarus, whom he had raised from the dead.
local: Juuda yab tikpil nín gbìl̄ yii Yeesu bāań Beetaniiyee, ní bi nín cá niin. An kaa nìn sá Yeesu ŋmaniŋma pu ní bi cá, ama kí tin kpáàa kpèè Laasaar un u fīkitèe mun.

eng: Before the Festival of Passover, Jesus knew that his time had come to leave this world and go to the Father. Having loved his own who were in the world, he loved them fully.
local: Kutaa ń wúntí Dilákatil jiŋaaliuee, Yeesu nìn nyí yii ŋyunti ŋun u làá yīkì kitiŋ nee pu kí gítí u-Baa ceeyee bāań. U nìn néeń u-yab bin bí duulinyan dooyee, ki gíti néeń-bi ki lákatì áli ki tin kátì-ku.

eng: Jesus responded, Those who have bathed need only to have their feet washed, because they are completely clean. You disciples are clean, but not every one of you.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-u yii: «Un yaa fùl̄ee kaa ti fùul̀, see ataa ŋmaniŋma ní u fíntí, kun puee waa ti cáá tijɔkɔndi. Míǹ ní ni mun bí circir, ama an kaa sá ni-kɔkɔ.».

eng: He knew who would betray him. Thats why he said, Not every one of you is clean.
local: Di sá yii Yeesu nìn nyí un làá dū-u kí tīī u-nannanliibee, míǹ pu ní u bí yii: «An kaa sá ni-kɔkɔ di bí circir.».

eng: After he washed the disciples feet, he put on his robes and returned to his place at the table. He said to them, Do you know what Ive done for you?.
local: Yeesu fíntì bi-taaŋi ki dóòyee, ní ki fātìi dū u-bɔkutilee ki pēeǹ, ní ki fātìi kāl̀, ní ki bālifì-bi yii: «Tin m ŋá-ni nee, ni gbìl̄ taapuɔɔɔ?.

eng: You call me Teacher and Lord, and you speak correctly, because I am.
local: Ni yíì-mi yii Ukpil nì ni-Dindaan, ní ni máaǹ kí yíiń-mi míǹ, kun puee míǹ ní m lafun sá.

eng: If I, your Lord and teacher, have washed your feet, you too must wash each others feet.
local: Míǹ puee, man udakal nì ni-Dindaan yaa fíntì ni-taaŋee, ni mun máaǹ kí nīn fíntí tɔb taaŋi.

eng: I have given you an example: Just as I have done, you also must do.
local: M ŋá míǹ ki dàkā-ni ni mun ń nīn ŋáań míǹ ki tìì tɔb ní.

eng: I assure you, servants arent greater than their master, nor are those who are sent greater than the one who sent them.
local: Ibaamɔn, ibaamɔn ní m sòoǹ nì ni, utɔntɔnli kaa jítiǹ u-kpiliu, míǹ ní un bi túee kaa jítiǹ un túnní-uee.

eng: Since you know these things, you will be happy if you do them.
local: Kɔŋkɔnnee ni béè, ni yaa ŋáań míǹ, ni làá gbìl̄ mmɔn.

eng: Im not speaking about all of you. I know those whom Ive chosen. But this is to fulfill the scripture, The one who eats my bread has turned against me.
local: «An kaa sá ni-kɔkɔ pu ní m sòoǹ, kun puee m nyí bin m lútèe. Tin bí nee ń ŋá kii Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon nín lī puee, yii: <Un jīǹ m-jintee fātìi bíi jáà nì mi.>.

eng: Im telling you this now, before it happens, so that when it does happen you will believe that I Am.
local: M tùkù-ni tin bí nee ki yōontí kɔŋkɔnnee kí wàatī ní an bàń, án nín làá ŋá pu tì yaa láa bāańee ní fōō kí kíí yii <man di sá un m sée>.

eng: Jesus and his disciples were sharing the evening meal. The devil had already provoked Judas, Simon Iscariots son, to betray Jesus.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun u nì u-boonnooliibi kā ki jīǹ tijoojinee, mimmee Sitaan pée lūkutì Juudasi Isikaariyɔti, Siimɔn jipɔɔn ú dū Yeesu kí nyāfiǹ u-nannanliibi dee.

eng: I assure you that whoever receives someone I send receives me, and whoever receives me receives the one who sent me.
local: Ibaamɔn, ibaamɔn ní m sòoǹ nì ni, un yaa fōō un man túnnée, fōō ma mun dee. Un yaa fōō mamee, fōō un túnní-mee mun dee.».

eng: After he said these things, Jesus was deeply disturbed and testified, I assure you, one of you will betray me.
local: Yeesu lī míǹ ki dóòyee, ní an gbēē u-pɔbil, ní u bí yii: «Ibaamɔn, ibaamɔn ní m sòoǹ nì ni, ni-kansikin ubɔ di làá dū-mi kí tīī m-nannanliibi.».

eng: His disciples looked at each other, confused about which of them he was talking about.
local: Ní u-boonnooliibi fātìi bíi kpèé tɔb, kaa kpáàa béè un pu ní u bíi sòoǹee.

eng: One of the disciples, the one whom Jesus loved, was at Jesus side.
local: Un Yeesu nìn néeń u-boonnooliibi kansikinee kā ki nákáǹ-u.

eng: Simon Peter nodded at him to get him to ask Jesus who he was talking about.
local: Ní Siimɔn Peetroo ŋá-u kudaaŋŋu yii ú bàlifì-u kí kpèè un pu ní u sòon̄ee.

eng: Leaning back toward Jesus, this disciple asked, Lord, who is it.
local: Ní u sútì ki mɔ̄tìi nákáǹ Yeesu ní ki bālifì-u yii: «Ukpil, an sá ŋma ní?».

eng: Jesus answered, Its the one to whom I will give this piece of bread once I have dipped into the bowl.Then he dipped the piece of bread and gave it to Judas, Simon Iscariots son.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-u yii: «M làá sēē kpɔnɔ tikunni. Un m yaa dūu tīīyee, úǹ ní an sá.» Niinee ní Yeesu dū kpɔnɔ ki sēē tikunni ki fíiǹ ki tīī Juudasi, Siimɔn Isikaariyɔti jipɔɔn.

eng: After Judas took the bread, Satan entered into him. Jesus told him, What you are about to do, do quickly.
local: Juudasi fōō kpɔnɔee, ní Sitaan kɔ́ u-ni. Ní Yeesu bí-u yii: «Tin a cáá ki làá ŋée, á ŋá-tì malaa!».

eng: No one sitting at the table understood why Jesus said this to him.
local: Bin nìn kā ki jīǹee, báà ubɔ kaa béè kun pu ní u lī-u mimmee.

eng: Some thought that, since Judas kept the money bag, Jesus told him, Go, buy what we need for the feast, or that he should give something to the poor.
local: Biba nìn dàkafī yii Yeesu là ú cùú dā tin tin bi làá cáá jī jiŋaalee, àá u là ú tīī biwɔfib tiba ní. Di sá yii Juudasi di nìn ŋúb́ bi-nimbiliŋ.

eng: Jesus knew the Father had given everything into his hands and that he had come from God and was returning to God.
local: Yeesu mun nìn pée nyí yii u nyánní Unimbɔti cee ní ki làá fàtīí gítí Unimbɔti cee, ní ki nyí yii u-Baa Unimbɔti dū tikɔkɔwoooo ki ŋá u-ŋaan.

eng: So when Judas took the bread, he left immediately. And it was night.
local: Juudasi fōō kpɔnɔee, ní ki nyáń malaa. Kunyeeu nìn mɔ́.

eng: When Judas was gone, Jesus said, Now the Human One has been glorified, and God has been glorified in him.
local: Juudasi nyáńee, ní Yeesu bí yii: «Kɔŋkɔnnee man Kinibiki fōō m-nyooti dee, Unimbɔti ń ká tinyool man ni.

eng: If God has been glorified in him, God will also glorify the Human One in himself and will glorify him immediately.
local: Unimbɔti yaa ká u-nyooti man nee, míǹ ní ma mun làá ká m-nyooti Unimbɔtin an kaa làá yúntí.

eng: Little children, Im with you for a little while longer. You will look for me—but, just as I told the Jewish leaders, I also tell you now—Where Im going, you cant come.
local: M-biyaamu, ŋyunti fiii di kíǹ ḿ gíti kàl̄ ni-cee. Míǹ boonee ni làá dāá nyàab̀-mi. Kii m nín nìn tùkū Juuda yab puee, naa làá ŋmā kí cù laakin m cée. Kɔŋkɔnnee, m tùkū ni mun dee.

eng: I give you a new commandment: Love each other. Just as I have loved you, so you also must love each other.
local: M tīī-ni marapɔɔn ubɔ: nín̄ néeḿmaan tɔb. Kii man nín néeń nimbi puee, ni mun ń nīn néeḿmaan tɔb míǹ.

eng: This is how everyone will know that you are my disciples, when you love each other.
local: Ni yaa néeń tɔbee, niin di làá dàkà báà ŋma yii ni lafun sá m-boonnooliibi.».

eng: Simon Peter said to Jesus, Lord, where are you going? Jesus answered, Where I am going, you cant follow me now, but you will follow later.
local: Ní Siimɔn Peetroo bālifì-u yii: «Ti-Dindaan, la ní a cá?» Ní Yeesu kíí-u yii: «Aa ŋūǹ kí nóó-mi laakin m cée kɔŋkɔnnee, ama boonee, a dāa nōotí-mi.».

eng: Peter asked, Lord, why cant I follow you now? Ill give up my life for you.
local: Ní Peetroo bí-u yii: «Ti-Dindaan, ba pu ní maa ŋūǹ kí nóó-si kɔŋkɔnnee? Báà an yaa sá ŋkúm, m làá dū m-maŋfal kí tīī a-pu.».

eng: Jesus replied, Will you give up your life for me? I assure you that you will deny me three times before the rooster crows.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-u yii: «A làá lafun dū a-maŋfal kí tīī man puɔɔɔ? Ibaamɔn, ibaamɔn ní m sòoǹ nì si, kí wàatī ukooja ń bāakèe, a làá néé yii saa nyí-mi diba dibee mita.».

eng: So he got up from the table and took off his robes. Picking up a linen towel, he tied it around his waist.
local: U nì u-boonnooliibi bíi jīǹee ní Yeesu yíkì tijin pu, ní ki pēetì u-bɔkutifɔkɔl ní ki dū ŋŋaabu ŋubɔ ki gbìn̄ u-caŋ.

eng: Then he poured water into a washbasin and began to wash the disciples feet, drying them with the towel he was wearing.
local: ní ki jōō nnyim ki ŋá kisampeeyin, ní ki kíĺ ki bíi fíntí u-boonnooliibi taaŋi, ní ki cáá ŋŋaabu ŋun u gbìn̄ u-caŋee ki cáa pítí bi-taaŋi.

eng: When Jesus came to Simon Peter, Peter said to him, Lord, are you going to wash my feet.
local: U fíntì ki bāań Siimɔn Peetrooyee ní Siimɔn bí yii: «Ti-Dindaan, sii a-ba di làá fíntí man taaŋaaa?».

eng: Jesus replied, You dont understand what Im doing now, but you will understand later.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-u yii: «Tin m bíi ŋáań nee, aa gbìl̀ taapu kɔŋkɔnnee, ama boonee a làá dāá gbìl̄.».

eng: No! Peter said. You will never wash my feet! Jesus replied, Unless I wash you, you wont have a place with me.
local: Ní Peetroo bí yii: «M yíì kpataaa, a kpá kí fíntí m-taaŋi.» Ní Yeesu kíí-u yii: «A yaa kaa yāntì m fíntì-si, mimmee, aa sá m-yɔu.».

eng: Simon Peter said, Lord, not only my feet but also my hands and my head.
local: Ní Siimɔn Peetroo kíí-u yii: «An yaa sá míǹ, ti-Dindaan, taa fíntí m-taaŋi ŋmaniŋma, fíntí kí kpáaǹ m-ŋalii nì m-yil!».

eng: Dont be troubled. Trust in God. Trust also in me.
local: Ní Yeesu nìkī ki lī yii: «Ni-pɔbiliŋ ń taa bīil̀. Ní fōō Unimbɔti kí kíí, kí fōōmaan ma mun kí kíí.

eng: Dont you believe that I am in the Father and the Father is in me? The words I have spoken to you I dont speak on my own. The Father who dwells in me does his works.
local: Aa nyí yii man bí m-Baa ni, ní m-Baa mun bí man naaa? Tibɔti tin m sòoǹ nì nee, maa sòoǹ-tì m-ba pu, an sá m-Baa un bí m-nee di tūǹ u-tundi.

eng: Trust me when I say that I am in the Father and the Father is in me, or at least believe on account of the works themselves.
local: Fōō kí kíímaan yii m bí m-Baa ni ní m-Baa mun bí man ni. Báà ni yaa kaa fōō m-bɔti ki kíí, kpèèmaan m-tuuŋŋi ŋin m ŋá nee pu kí fōō kí kíí.

eng: I assure you that whoever believes in me will do the works that I do. They will do even greater works than these because I am going to the Father.
local: Ibaamɔn, ibaamɔn ní m sòoǹ nì ni, un yaa fōō-mi ki kííyee làá tɔ̄ atuun ŋin m tūǹee, úǹ làá tɔ̄ ŋin jítiǹ man yaŋ nee gba, kun puee m cá m-Baa cee ní.

eng: I will do whatever you ask for in my name, so that the Father can be glorified in the Son.
local: Ní tin kɔkɔ ni yaa mēē m-yindi puee, m làá ŋá míǹ kí tīī-ni, ní m-Baa ń ká tinyool u-Jipɔɔn pu.

eng: When you ask me for anything in my name, I will do it.
local: Ni yaa mēē tiba m-yindi pu, m làá ŋá míǹ kí tīī-ni.

eng: If you love me, you will keep my commandments.
local: «Ni yaa néeń-mee, nín̄ ŋúb́maan m-marab.

eng: I will ask the Father, and he will send another Companion, who will be with you forever.
local: ní man ń mèè m-Baa Unimbɔti ú gíti tīiń-ni Ututunli ubɔ un làá nīn bí ni-cee kí nīn cá jaanjaanee.

eng: This Companion is the Spirit of Truth, whom the world cant receive because it neither sees him nor recognizes him. You know him, because he lives with you and will be with you.
local: Úǹ di sá Unimbɔti Fam ibaamɔndaan, un duulinya nib kaa ŋūǹ kí fōōyee, kun puee baa wāĺ-uaaa, kaa nyí-u mun. Ama an yaa sá nimbi, ni nyí-u kun puee u kóò ni-cee ní ki làá dāá nīn bí ni-ni.

eng: I wont leave you as orphans. I will come to you.
local: «Maa làá dū-ni kí fá kii ataŋkpiibilee, m làá fàtīí gítiń ni-cee.

eng: Soon the world will no longer see me, but you will see me. Because I live, you will live too.
local: An kíǹ fiii duulinya nib kaa làá ti ká-mi, ama nimbi làá ká-mi, kun puee m bí dimaŋfalin, míǹ ní ni mun làá nīn bí dimaŋfalin.

eng: My Fathers house has room to spare. If that werent the case, would I have told you that Im going to prepare a place for you?.
local: Ikɔŋkoon tikpil bí m-Baa dumpu, m làá cù kí bɔ́ntí-i kí yóoń-ni. I yaa kaa bàà bí tiŋman, m bàà tùkù kí cántí-ni.

eng: On that day you will know that I am in my Father, you are in me, and I am in you.
local: Ŋwiin gbanti yaa láa bāańee, ni dāa béetí yii man bí m-Baa ni, ní nimbi bí man ni, ní man bí ni mun ni.

eng: Whoever has my commandments and keeps them loves me. Whoever loves me will be loved by my Father, and I will love them and reveal myself to them.
local: Un yaa cáá man marab ki ŋúb́-bee, udaanee di néeń-mi. Míǹ ní m-baa làá nīn néeń un néeń-mee, ma mun ń nīn néeń-u, ní kí nyāntí m-ba kí dàkà udaan.».

eng: Judas asked, Lord, why are you about to reveal yourself to us and not to the world.
local: Ní Juudi - an kaa nín sá Juudasi Isikaariyɔti - bí-u yii: «Ti-Dindaan, mana ní a làá ŋá ní kí nyāntí a-ba kí dàkà timbi, ní kí taa dàkà duulinya nib?».

eng: Jesus answered, Whoever loves me will keep my word. My Father will love them, and we will come to them and make our home with them.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-u yii: «Un yaa néeń-mee làá nīn ŋúb́ m-bɔti. M-Baa làá nīn néeń udaan, ní man nì m-Baa ń dāan̄ kí nīn kóò udaan cee.

eng: Whoever doesnt love me doesnt keep my words. The word that you hear isnt mine. It is the word of the Father who sent me.
local: Un kaa néeń-mee kaa ŋúb́ m-bɔti. M-bɔti tin m sòoǹ nì nee nyánní m-Baa un túnní-mee cee ní, an kaa sá m-ba pu ní m sòoǹ.

eng: I have spoken these things to you while I am with you.
local: «M tùkū-ni tin bí nee ŋyunti ŋun m dáá bí ni-cee nee.

eng: The Companion, the Holy Spirit, whom the Father will send in my name, will teach you everything and will remind you of everything I told you.
local: Ama Ututunlee, Circir Fam min m-Baa làá dāá tɔ̄ń man yindi puee, làá dāá dàkà-ni tikɔkɔ, ní kí téetí-ni tin kɔkɔ m sòon̄ nì nee.

eng: Peace I leave with you. My peace I give you. I give to you not as the world gives. Dont be troubled or afraid.
local: «Ŋgbansɔŋfi ní m cáań-ni, man gbansɔŋfiŋu ní m dūu tīī-ni, maa tìì kii duulinya nín tìì puee. Ní taa bàkatī kí taa fàŋkì mun.

eng: You have heard me tell you, Im going away and returning to you. If you loved me, you would be happy that I am going to the Father, because the Father is greater than me.
local: Ni gbìl̄ m nín lī yii m làá nīn cá kí dāá fàtīí gítiń ni-ceeyee. Ni yaa bàà néeń-mee, an bàà mɔ́ɔkíǹ-ni kii m nín cá m-Baa ceeyee pu, kun puee m-Baa jítiǹ man.

eng: I have told you before it happens so that when it happens you will believe.
local: M tùkū-ni kɔŋkɔnnee ki yóoǹ kí wàatī ní án nín bàń, ní ŋyunti ŋun ní an yaa láa bāańee, ní ŋmā kí mɔ̀tīí fōō kí kíí.

eng: When I go to prepare a place for you, I will return and take you to be with me so that where I am you will be too.
local: M yaa cūtì ki bɔ́ntì ipaan gbantee, ní ḿ gítiń kí yòon̄-ni, án nín làá ŋá pu laakin m làá nīn bée, ni mun ń nīn bí m-cee niinee.

eng: I wont say much more to you because this worlds ruler is coming. He has nothing on me.
local: Maa làá ti sòoǹ nì ni tikpil, kun puee duulinya bɔtiu Sitaan dòoń. Waa nín ŋūǹ kí ŋá-mi tiba.

eng: Rather, he comes so that the world will know that I love the Father and do just as the Father has commanded me. Get up. Were leaving this place.
local: Ama duulinya nib máaǹ kí béé yii m néeń m-Baa, an pu ní min ní m-Baa yaa bí ḿ ŋée, míǹ ní m ŋáań. Yīkìmaan, tí nīn cá.».

eng: You know the way to the place Im going.
local: Ni nyí laakin m cée, ki nyí nsan mun.».

eng: Thomas asked, Lord, we dont know where you are going. How can we know the way.
local: Ní Toomasi bí-u yii: «Ti-Dindaan, taa nyí laakin a cée! Ti làá ŋá mana kí béé nsan?».

eng: Jesus answered, I am the way, the truth, and the life. No one comes to the Father except through me.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-u yii: «Man di sá nsan, ki sá ibaamɔn ki sá dimaŋfal. Ubɔ kaa ŋūǹ kí dī laaba kí dāan̄ m-Baa Unimbɔti cee, see ú dīń man pu.

eng: If you have really known me, you will also know the Father. From now on you know him and have seen him.
local: Ni yaa bàà nyí-mee, ni bàà nyí m-Baa mun ní. Ama kɔŋkɔnnee ni nyí-u, ki ká-u mun.».

eng: Philip said, Lord, show us the Father; that will be enough for us.
local: Niinee ní Fiiliipi bí-u yii: «Ti-Dindaan, dàkà-ti a-Baa Unimbɔti ti-pɔbiliŋ ń sɔ̀ŋkì.».

eng: Jesus replied, Dont you know me, Philip, even after I have been with you all this time? Whoever has seen me has seen the Father. How can you say, Show us the Father?.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-u yii: «Fiiliipi, m nín bí ni-cee an yúntì pu nee, ní saa nyí-maaa? Un yaa ká-mee, ká m-Baa mun dee. Kin ba pu ní sii gíti lì yii ḿ dàkà-ni m-Baa?.

eng: I am the true vine, and my Father is the vineyard keeper.
local: Ní Yeesu bí yii: «Man di sá fiinyi subu tiŋman, ní m-Baa sá un ŋáŋkí-buee.

eng: If you keep my commandments, you will remain in my love, just as I kept my Fathers commandments and remain in his love.
local: Ni yaa ŋúb́ m-marabee, mimmee, ni bí m-neendin dee, kii ma mun nín ŋúb́ m-Baa marab ki bí u-neendin puee.

eng: I have said these things to you so that my joy will be in you and your joy will be complete.
local: «M sòoǹ nì ni tin bí nee án nín làá ŋá pu m-pɔɔpindi ń nīn bí ni-ni, ní ni-pɔbiliŋ ń mɔ̀tīí gíti pīil̀ee.

eng: This is my commandment: love each other just as I have loved you.
local: Mara un m síiǹ-nee sèé: ní nīn néeḿmaan tɔb, kii man nín néeń-ni puee.

eng: No one has greater love than to give up ones life for ones friends.
local: Dineeŋkpaandi diba kaa ti bí kí bà unil ń dū u-maŋfal kí kúntí u-bɔɔbi pu.

eng: You are my friends if you do what I command you.
local: Ni yaa ŋáań min m bàlifī-nee, mimmee ni kpántì m-bɔɔbi dee.

eng: I dont call you servants any longer, because servants dont know what their master is doing. Instead, I call you friends, because everything I heard from my Father I have made known to you.
local: Maa ti yíì-ni m-tɔntɔnliibi, kun puee utɔntɔnli kaa nyí tin u-kpiliu ŋáańee. M yíì-ni m-bɔɔbi ní, kun puee m dàkā-ni tin kɔkɔwoooo m gbìl̄ m-Baa ceeyee.

eng: You didnt choose me, but I chose you and appointed you so that you could go and produce fruit and so that your fruit could last. As a result, whatever you ask the Father in my name, he will give you.
local: Man di lēē nimbi, kaa sá nimbi di lēē man. M tú-ni ní cùú lòoǹ abil, abil ŋin làá yúntée, mimmee, m-Baa làá tīī-ni tin kɔkɔ ni yaa mēē-u man yindi puee.

eng: I give you these commandments so that you can love each other.
local: Tin tin m síiǹ-nee, di sá ní nīn néeń tɔb.».

eng: If the world hates you, know that it hated me first.
local: «Duulinya nib yaa ná-nee, ní béé yii man ní bi péé ki ná.

eng: If you belonged to the world, the world would love you as its own. However, I have chosen you out of the world, and you dont belong to the world. This is why the world hates you.
local: Ni yaa bàà sá duulinya yabee, duulinya yab bàà néeń-ni, kun puee ni sá u-yab. Ama m lútì-ni ki nyántì duulinya nib kansikin. Naa ti sá duulinya yab, an pu ní bi ná-ni.

eng: He removes any of my branches that dont produce fruit, and he trims any branch that produces fruit so that it will produce even more fruit.
local: Apan ŋin túkú m-pu kaa lòoǹee, u gà-ŋi ki féeń ní. Apan ŋin lòoǹee, u ŋáŋkí-ŋi, ní ŋí mɔ̀tīí lòoǹ.

eng: Remember what I told you, Servants arent greater than their master. If the world harassed me, it will harass you too. If it kept my word, it will also keep yours.
local: Ní téetímaan tin tin m nìn tùkū-nee, yii: <Utɔntɔnli kaa jítiǹ u-kpiliu.> Bi yaa páb́ man, bi làá dāá páb́ ni mun, bi yaa bàà pílifì man bɔti, bi bàà dāa pílifí ni mun yati.

eng: The world will do all these things to you on account of my name, because it doesnt know the one who sent me.
local: Man pu ní bi làá ŋá-ni tin bí nee kɔkɔ, kun puee baa nyí un túnní-mee.

eng: If I hadnt come and spoken to the people of this world, they wouldnt be sinners. But now they have no excuse for their sin.
local: M yaa kaa bàà dɔ́miń ní kaa bàà sòon̄ nì bi, baa bàà cáá ikpiti. Ama kɔŋkɔnnee, baa ti cáá nnaakpal báà ŋubɔ bi-kpitii pu.

eng: Whoever hates me also hates the Father.
local: Un yaa ná-mee ná m-Baa mun dee.

eng: If I hadnt done works among them that no one else had done, they wouldnt be sinners. But now they have seen and hated both me and my Father.
local: M yaa kaa bàà ŋá maamaacib bin bin báà ubɔ kaa ŋée bi-kansikin, baa bàà cáá ikpiti. Ama bi kántì m-maamaacib, ní ki gbíntì ki ná man nì m-Baa.

eng: This fulfills the word written in their Law, They hated me without a reason.
local: Too! An ŋá kii bi-marab gbɔŋku poon nín lī ki síiǹ puee dee yii: <Bi ná-mi fám, an kaa cáá taapu.>.

eng: When the Companion comes, whom I will send from the Father—the Spirit of Truth who proceeds from the Father—he will testify about me.
local: «M yaa bàn̄ m-Baa ceeyee, m làá tɔ̄ń-ni Ututunli, úǹ di sá Unimbɔti Fam ibaamɔndaan un nyánní m-Baa ceeyee. U yaa láa bāańee, u làá sòoǹ m-pu.

eng: You will testify too, because you have been with me from the beginning.
local: ní ni mun ń dāá lēē m-pu seera, kun puee ni pée bí m-cee doooo ŋkilkaamin ní.

eng: You are already trimmed because of the word I have spoken to you.
local: Tibɔti tin m sòon̄ nì nee, tì ŋáŋkì nimbi.

eng: Remain in me, and I will remain in you. A branch cant produce fruit by itself, but must remain in the vine. Likewise, you cant produce fruit unless you remain in me.
local: Ní nīn bímaan man ni, ní ma mun ń nīn bí ni-ni. Kii dipandi nín kaa ŋūǹ kí lòoǹ abil di-baba asee dí nīn túkú busubu puee, míǹ mbaantiim ní ni yaa kaa bí man ni, naa ŋūǹ kí lòoǹ abil.

eng: I am the vine; you are the branches. If you remain in me and I in you, then you will produce much fruit. Without me, you cant do anything.
local: «Man di sá fiinyi subu, ní nimbi sá apan. Un yaa bí man ni ma mun bí udaan nee lòoǹ abil ní tikpil, kun puee ni yaa kaa bí man ni, naa ŋūǹ kí ŋá tiba.

eng: If you dont remain in me, you will be like a branch that is thrown out and dries up. Those branches are gathered up, thrown into a fire, and burned.
local: Un yaa kaa bí man nee, bi gà-u kí fá kii disupandee ní, ní ú kpā. Asupaŋkulimee, bi kōontí-ŋi kí tɔ̄ ŋŋmin, ní án wà.

eng: If you remain in me and my words remain in you, ask for whatever you want and it will be done for you.
local: Ni yaa bí man ni, ní ma mun bɔti bí ni-nee, bàlifìmaan tin tin ni yaa lèe, ni làá ká kudɔŋu.

eng: My Father is glorified when you produce much fruit and in this way prove that you are my disciples.
local: Tin tìì m-Baa tinyoolee di sá ní lòoǹ abil tikpil ní kí nīn sá man boonnooliibi.

eng: As the Father loved me, I too have loved you. Remain in my love.
local: Kii m-Baa nín néeń-mi puee, míǹ ní ma mun néeń-ni. Nín̄ bímaan m-neendin.

eng: I have said these things to you so that you wont fall away.
local: «M sòon̄ nì ni tin bí nee án nín làá ŋá pu ní taa láa fá ni-fookiiu m-puee.

eng: He will show the world it was wrong about righteousness because Im going to the Father and you wont see me anymore.
local: ki làá yàntī bí béé kun sá deedeeyee, kun puee m nín cá m-Baa cee ní naa làá ti wāl̀-mi.

eng: He will show the world it was wrong about judgment because this worlds ruler stands condemned.
local: ní ki làá yàntī bí béé yii ibɔsoon dɔ̄ ki cí-bi, kun puee Unimbɔti sòon̄ duulinya bɔtiu bɔti.

eng: I have much more to say to you, but you cant handle it now.
local: «Tibɔti tikpil bàà bí ḿ sòoǹ nì ni, ama tì jítiǹ-ni kɔŋkɔnnee.

eng: However, when the Spirit of Truth comes, he will guide you in all truth. He wont speak on his own, but will say whatever he hears and will proclaim to you what is to come.
local: Ama Unimbɔti Fam ibaamɔndaanee yaa láa bāańee, úǹ di làá dàkà-ni ibaamɔn kɔkɔ. Waa làá sòoǹ u-ba pɔŋŋu pu, u làá dāá tùkù-ni tin tin u gbìl̄ee, kí dàkà-ni tin bí ki dòońee.

eng: He will glorify me, because he will take what is mine and proclaim it to you.
local: U làá dāá tīī-mi tinyool, kun puee u làá dāá fōō tin tin m cááyee kí tùkù-ni.

eng: Everything that the Father has is mine. Thats why I said that the Spirit takes what is mine and will proclaim it to you.
local: Tin kɔkɔ m-Baa Unimbɔti cááyee sá ma mun wanti ní. An pu ní m tùkū-ni yii Unimbɔti Famee làá dāá fōō tin m cááyee kí tùkù-ni.».

eng: Soon you wont be able to see me; soon after that, you will see me.
local: «An kíǹ fiii, naa làá ti ká-mi, ní an yaa láa tí kútì fiii boonee, ní ni làá fàtīí tí ká-mi.».

eng: Some of Jesus disciples said to each other, What does he mean: Soon you wont see me, and soon after that you will see me and Because Im going to the Father?.
local: Niinee ní u-boonnooliibi biba bíi lì tɔb cee yii: «Taapu nín sá yii ba dee, yii: <An kíǹ fiii naa làá ti ká-mi, ní an yaa fātìi kútì fiii boonee, ní ni làá fàtīí tí ká-mee?> U gíti lī mun yii: <M cá m-Baa cee>. Mimmee, u là kí tùkù-ti yii ba dee?.

eng: What does he mean by soon? We dont understand what hes talking about.
local: <An kíǹ fiii> gbanti taapu baasii nín sá yii ba dee? Taa gbìl̀ tin u là kí sòoǹ nì ti nee taapu!».

eng: Jesus knew they wanted to ask him, so he said, Are you trying to find out from each other what I meant when I said, Soon you wont see me, and soon after that you will see me?.
local: Yeesu béè yii bi là kí bàlifì-uee, ní u bí-bi yii: «M nín bí yii: <An kíǹ fiii naa làá ti ká-mi, ní an yaa fātìi tí kútì fiii boonee, ní ni làá fàtīí tí ká-mee> pu ní ni bíi bàlifī tɔb naaa?.

eng: They will expel you from the synagogue. The time is coming when those who kill you will think that they are doing a service to God.
local: Bi làá dāá jà-ni kí nyāntí akpafidiinni, áli gba, ŋyunti ŋubɔ bí ki dòoń bi làá kpɔ̀-ni ki dàkafī yii bi ŋá Unimbɔti lammi.

eng: I assure you that you will cry and lament, and the world will be happy. You will be sorrowful, but your sorrow will turn into joy.
local: Ibaamɔn, ibaamɔn ní m sòoǹ nì ni, ni làá dāá nīn sūǹ ki tíminsí ní duulinya yab fàal̀. Ni làá dāá nīn bí dinimbinaamandin, ama ni-nimbinaamandi làá dāá kpántí-ni dipɔɔpindi.

eng: When a woman gives birth, she has pain because her time has come. But when the child is born, she no longer remembers her distress because of her joy that a child has been born into the world.
local: Imatiŋween yaa bāań unimpuee, u kíǹ ki bí dinimbinaamandin ní, kun puee u-falaau yuntiŋu bāań-u. Ama u yaa màl̄ ki dóòyee ní án mɔ̄ɔkiǹ-u, ní ú súntí u-falaau pu, kun puee u màl̄ unil.

eng: In the same way, you have sorrow now; but I will see you again, and you will be overjoyed. No one takes away your joy.
local: «Míǹ mbaantiim ní ni mun bí dinimbinaamandin kɔŋkɔnnee. Ama m làá dāá fàtīí gíti ká-ni ní ni-pɔbiliŋ ń pīil̀. Báà ubɔ kaa dāa ŋūǹ kí lēē ni-pɔɔpindi gbanti.

eng: In that day, you wont ask me anything. I assure you that the Father will give you whatever you ask in my name.
local: «An wiiŋŋu gbantee, naa dāa gíti bàlifī-mi tiba nì tiba pu. Ibaamɔn, ibaamɔn ní m sòoǹ nì ni, tin kɔkɔ ni yaa mēē m-Baa m-yindi puee, u làá tīī-ni kudɔŋu.

eng: Up to now, you have asked nothing in my name. Ask and you will receive so that your joy will be complete.
local: Áli nìn díǹee, naa láá mēē m-yindi pu tiba, mèèmaan tiba, ni làá fōō kudɔŋu, ni-pɔbiliŋ ń mɔ̀tīí pīil̀.».

eng: Ive been using figures of speech with you. The time is coming when I will no longer speak to you in such analogies. Instead, I will tell you plainly about the Father.
local: «Tibɔŋmantikaal ní m cáa bíi sòoǹ nì ni tin bí nee kɔkɔ. Ŋyunti ŋubɔ dòoń ŋun ni maa làá gíti dū tibɔŋmantikaal kí sòoǹ nì nee. M làá dāá sòoǹ m-Baa Unimbɔti pu kí cántí.

eng: In that day you will ask in my name. Im not saying that I will ask the Father on your behalf.
local: Ŋwiin gbanti yaa láa bāańee, ni làá dāá nīn mèé m-Baa Unimbɔti man yindi pu. Maa bí yii man di làá dāá nīn mèé m-Baa ni-pu.

eng: The Father himself loves you, because you have loved me and believed that I came from God.
local: kun puee m-Baa Unimbɔti u-ba néeń-ni. U néeń-ni kun puee ni néeń man ki fōō ki kíí yii m nyánní Unimbɔti cee.

eng: I left the Father and came into the world. I tell you again: I am leaving the world and returning to the Father.
local: M nyánní m-Baa cee ní ki dɔ́miń duulinyan. Kɔŋkɔnnee m làá fàtīí yīkì duulinyan kí nīn gítí m-Baa cee.».

eng: His disciples said, See! Now you speak plainly; you arent using figures of speech.
local: Niinee ní u-boonnooliibi bí yii: «An kaa sá nì tibɔŋmantikaal ní a cáa bíi sòoǹ nì ti nee, min bí nee a baasii bíi sòoǹ ki cántí-ti ní nee.

eng: They will do these things because they dont know the Father or me.
local: Kun pu bi làá ŋá mimmee di sá yii baa nyí m-Baayaaa, kaa nyí ma mun.

eng: Now we know that you know everything and you dont need anyone to ask you. Because of this we believe you have come from God.
local: Kɔŋkɔnnee ti nyí yii a nyí tikɔkɔ, an kaa ti páb́ yii ubɔ ń bàlifì-si tiba. An pu ní ti fōō ki kíí yii a nyánní Unimbɔti cee.».

eng: Jesus replied, Now you believe?.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-bi yii: «Ni fōō ki kíí kɔŋkɔnneeyaa!.

eng: Look! A time is coming—and is here!—when each of you will be scattered to your own homes and you will leave me alone. Im not really alone, for the Father is with me.
local: Too, ŋyunti ŋubɔ dòoń, ŋu bāań gba, ŋun ni ni-kɔkɔ làá sāǹ kí yàl̄ ni-ba ni-ba kí kíń man babee, ama maa bí m-baba nì m-baba, m-Baa bí m-cee.

eng: Ive said these things to you so that you will have peace in me. In the world you have distress. But be encouraged! I have conquered the world.
local: M tùkū-ni tin bí nee án nín làá ŋá pu man puee, ní nīn cáá ŋgbansɔŋfee. Falaa dɔ̄ ki cí-ni duulinyan doo ní jī, ama ni-wunti ń taa yɔ̀tī! Di sá yii m jáń ki pɔ́ɔkiǹ duulinya.».

eng: But I have said these things to you so that when their time comes, you will remember that I told you about them. I didnt say these things to you from the beginning, because I was with you.
local: Ama m tùkū-ni tin bí nee, ní ti-yuntiŋu yaa láa bāańee ní dāá téetí yii m nìn pée tùkū-ni-tì. «Maa tùkū-ni tin bí nee doooo ŋkilkaamin kun puee m dáá bí ni-ceeyee pu.

eng: But now I go away to the one who sent me. None of you ask me, Where are you going.
local: «Kɔŋkɔnnee, m làá nīn cá un túnní-mee cee, ní ni-kansikin ubɔ kaa bālifì-mi yii: <A cá la?>.

eng: Yet because I have said these things to you, you are filled with sorrow.
local: M nín sòon̄-ni puee ní kunimbaau kɔ́-ni.

eng: I assure you that it is better for you that I go away. If I dont go away, the Companion wont come to you. But if I go, I will send him to you.
local: Ama, ibaamɔn ní m sòoǹ nì ni, m nín cá m-Baa ceeyee, an sá ni-paatii, kun puee m yaa búntèe, m làá tɔ̄ń Ututunli ni-cee. M yaa kaa yíkì, waa làá dāan̄ ni-cee.

eng: When he comes, he will show the world it was wrong about sin, righteousness, and judgment.
local: U yaa láa bāańee, u làá yàntī duulinya nib ń béé yii bi bí ikpitin, kí yàntī bí béé kun di sá deedeeyee, kí yàntī bí béé Unimbɔti bɔsooŋyi in dɔ̄ ki cí-bee.

eng: He will show the world it was wrong about sin because they dont believe in me.
local: U làá yàntī bí béé yii bi bí ikpitin, kun puee baa fōō-mi ki kíí.

eng: When Jesus finished saying these things, he looked up to heaven and said, Father, the time has come. Glorify your Son, so that the Son can glorify you.
local: Yeesu sòon̄ míǹ ki dóòyee, ní ki wáaǹ ki kpēē kutaa ní ki bí yii: «M-Baa, kɔŋkɔnnee ŋyunti bāań! Tīī a-Jipɔɔn tinyool, án nín làá ŋá pu a-Jipɔɔn mun ń tīī-si tinyoolee.

eng: Everything that is mine is yours and everything that is yours is mine; I have been glorified in them.
local: Tin kɔkɔ m cááyee, sii di yì, ní tin kɔkɔ si mun cááyee, man di yì. Bíǹ pu ní bi tìì-mi tinyool.

eng: Im no longer in the world, but they are in the world, even as Im coming to you. Holy Father, watch over them in your name, the name you gave me, that they will be one just as we are one.
local: Man kaa ti bí duulinyan. Man dòoń sii cee ní, ní bíǹ làá nīn gbíntìi bí duulinyan. M-Baa circirdaan, dū mpɔn ŋun bí a-yindi din a tīī-mee nee kí ŋúb́-bi, án nín làá ŋá pu bí kpāfì ubaanti kii man nì si nín ŋá ubaanti puee.

eng: When I was with them, I watched over them in your name, the name you gave to me, and I kept them safe. None of them were lost, except the one who was destined for destruction, so that scripture would be fulfilled.
local: Man nì bi nín nìn bée, m dū a-pɔŋŋu ŋun a tīī-mee ki ŋúb́-bi, ní ki mɔ́tì bi-pu, ní báà ubɔ bi-kansikin kaa wáŋkì, asee un baba làá pée wāŋkì, kii a-gbɔŋku nín nìn pée sòon̄ ki síiǹ puee.

eng: Now Im coming to you and I say these things while Im in the world so that they can share completely in my joy.
local: Kɔŋkɔnnee, m dòoń a-cee. M nín dáá bí duulinyan ki sòoǹ tin nee, an sá kí ŋmā kí yàntī bí ká dipɔɔpindi, man pɔɔpimmɔntiil.

eng: I gave your word to them and the world hated them, because they dont belong to this world, just as I dont belong to this world.
local: M cáań-bi a-bɔti, ní duulinya yab ná-bi, kun puee baa sá duulinyan, kii ma mun nín kaa sá duulinyan puee.

eng: Im not asking that you take them out of this world but that you keep them safe from the evil one.
local: Maa mèé-si yii á yòon̄-bi duulinyan, á ŋúb́-bi, bí taa jēetí Sitaan ŋaan.

eng: They dont belong to this world, just as I dont belong to this world.
local: Baa sá duulinya yab, kii ma mun nín kaa sá duulinyan yɔu puee.

eng: Make them holy in the truth; your word is truth.
local: Á dū a-baamɔŋyi kí ŋá bi-ni, kí yàntī bí kpántí a-yab kɔkɔ: a-bɔti di sá ibaamɔn.

eng: As you sent me into the world, so I have sent them into the world.
local: Kii a nín túnní-mi duulinyan puee, míǹ ní ma mun tú-bi duulinyan.

eng: I made myself holy on their behalf so that they also would be made holy in the truth.
local: M kúntì m-ba kɔkɔ ki tīī-si bi-pu, án nín làá ŋá pu ibaamɔn ń yàntī bi mun ń kúntí bi-ba kí tīī-see.

eng: You gave him authority over everyone so that he could give eternal life to everyone you gave him.
local: Kun puee sii di tīī-u mpɔn binib kɔkɔ pu, yii ú tīī bin kɔkɔwoooo a dūu ŋá u-ŋaanee dimaŋfal din kaa cáá dikúntilee.

eng: Im not praying only for them but also for those who believe in me because of their word.
local: «An kaa sá bíǹ baba pu ní m mèé-si. M mèé-si ki kpáaǹ bin bin làá dāá pīlifì bi-bɔti kí fōō-mi kí kííyee pu ní.

eng: I pray they will be one, Father, just as you are in me and I am in you. I pray that they also will be in us, so that the world will believe that you sent me.
local: M-Baa, yàntī bi-kɔkɔ ń ŋá ubaanti kii sii nín bí man ni, ma mun bí sii ni puee. Bi-kɔkɔ ń ŋá ubaanti nì ti, án nín làá ŋá pu duulinya nib ń lafun béé yii sii di túnní-mee.

eng: Ive given them the glory that you gave me so that they can be one just as we are one.
local: M yāntì bi fōō a-nyooti tin a nìn tīī-mee án nín làá ŋá pu bi-kɔkɔ ń ŋá ubaantee, kii man nì si nín ŋá ubaanti puee.

eng: Im in them and you are in me so that they will be made perfectly one. Then the world will know that you sent me and that you have loved them just as you loved me.
local: Man ń nīn bí bi-ni kii sii nín bí man ni puee, án nín làá ŋá pu bí ŋá ubaanti mbaamɔmin, duulinya nib ń bée yii sii di túnní-mi, ki néeń-bi kii a nín néeń man puee.

eng: Father, I want those you gave me to be with me where I am. Then they can see my glory, which you gave me because you loved me before the creation of the world.
local: «M-Baa, m là yii laakin m bée, bin kɔkɔwoooo a tīī-mee ń nīn bí m-cee niin kí ká m-nyooti, tinyool tin a tīī-mee. Di sá yii a néeń-mi ki wāatì ní ki nín náań yilpu nì taapu.

eng: Righteous Father, even the world didnt know you, but Ive known you, and these believers know that you sent me.
local: M-Baa deedeedaan, duulinya nib kaa nyí-si, ama man nyí-si, ní bin bí nee béè yii sii di túnní-mi.

eng: Ive made your name known to them and will continue to make it known so that your love for me will be in them, and I myself will be in them.
local: M yāntì bi béè-si, ki làá yàntī bí mɔ̀tīí béé-si, án nín làá ŋá pu dineendi din a cáá m-puee ń nīn bí bi-ni, ní ma mun m-ba ń nīn bí bi-nee.».

eng: This is eternal life: to know you, the only true God, and Jesus Christ whom you sent.
local: Dimaŋfal gbanti di sá bí béé sii un baba sá Unimbɔtibaamɔnee, kí béé Yeesu Kriisitoo un a túnnée mun.

eng: I have glorified you on earth by finishing the work you gave me to do.
local: M dàkā duulinya nib a-nyooti, m dóò ditundi din a tīī-mi ḿ tɔ̄ee.

eng: Now, Father, glorify me in your presence with the glory I shared with you before the world was created.
local: M-baa, kɔŋkɔnnee, tīī-mi tinyool a-cee, tinyool tin m nìn cáá a-cee dooooyee, ki wāatì ní a nín náań duulinyee.

eng: I have revealed your name to the people you gave me from this world. They were yours and you gave them to me, and they have kept your word.
local: «M yāntì binib bin a nyántì duulinya nib kansikin ki tīī-mee béè-si. Sii di nìn yì-bi, ní ki dū-bi ki tīī-mi, ní bi fōō a-bɔti.

eng: Now they know that everything you have given me comes from you.
local: Kɔŋkɔnnee, bi béè yii tin kɔkɔ a tīī-mee nyánní a-cee.

eng: This is because I gave them the words that you gave me, and they received them. They truly understood that I came from you, and they believed that you sent me.
local: Tibɔti tin a tīiń-mee m cáań ki tùkū-bi, ní bi fōō ki béè yii ibaamɔn m nyánní sii cee, ní ki fōō ki kíí yii sii di túnní-mi.

eng: Im praying for them. Im not praying for the world but for those you gave me, because they are yours.
local: «Bíǹ pu ní m mèé-si nee. An kaa sá duulinya nib pu ní m mèé. M mèé-si binib bin a tīī-mi nee pu ní, kun puee sii di yì-bi.

eng: After he said these things, Jesus went out with his disciples and crossed over to the other side of the Kidron Valley. He and his disciples entered a garden there.
local: Yeesu sòon̄ an nyɔɔbuŋŋi ki dóòyee, ní u nì u-boonnooliibi búntì Seedrɔn buŋgbandi lapu pu. Bukpaa bubɔ nìn bí niin, ní u nì u-boonnooliibi cūtì ki kɔ́ bu-poon.

eng: Then Simon Peter, who had a sword, drew it and struck the high priests servant, cutting off his right ear.
local: Niinee ní Siimɔn Peetroo dáaĺ ki fɔ́fì u-tàataajiki, ki gāā ki wúkutì usaraaŋakpil tɔntɔnliu ubɔ ŋaŋgiitafal. Bi nìn yíì utɔntɔnli gbanti Maalkusi ní.

eng: Jesus told Peter, Put your sword away! Am I not to drink the cup the Father has given me.
local: Ní Yeesu bí Peetroo yii: «Gíiǹ a-tàataajiki kí fúuń kujitooun! M-Baa yaa tīī-mi falaa ḿ nyɔ̀ee, ḿ taa nyɔ̀ɔɔ?».

eng: Then the company of soldiers, the commander, and the guards from the Jewish leaders took Jesus into custody. They bound him.
local: Niinee ní disoojakitilee nì bi-kpiliu nì Juuda yab pilisib cúú Yeesu ki bóob́-u.

eng: and led him first to Annas. He was the father-in-law of Caiaphas, the high priest that year. .
local: ní ki dá láá cáá-u ki cūnnì Aani cee. Aani gbanti di nìn sá Kaayiifi un nìn sá usaraaŋakpil dibindi gbantee coo.

eng: Caiaphas was the one who had advised the Jewish leaders that it was better for one person to die for the people.
local: Kaayiifi gbantee di nìn sáŋkì Juuda yab yii: «An máaǹ unibaanti ń kpú binib kɔkɔ yil puee.».

eng: Simon Peter and another disciple followed Jesus. Because this other disciple was known to the high priest, he went with Jesus into the high priests courtyard.
local: Siimɔn Peetroo nì Yeesu boonnooliu ubɔ nìn nóó Yeesu. Uboonnoolee nóó Yeesu ki kɔ́ usaraaŋakpil cindin, kun puee usaraaŋakpil nìn nyí-u.

eng: However, Peter stood outside near the gate. Then the other disciple came out and spoke to the woman stationed at the gate, and she brought Peter in.
local: ní Peetroo nìn gbíntìi sí kipunyɔkɔ boon mmɔŋki. Ní uboonnool un usaraaŋakpil nìn nyée nyáń ki ká unimpu un nìn cí kipunyɔkɔpuee ní ki cáá Peetroo ki kɔ́ǹ.

eng: The servant woman stationed at the gate asked Peter, Arent you one of this mans disciples? Im not, he replied.
local: Ní unimpu un nìn cí kipunyɔkɔpuee bālifì Peetroo yii: «A mun kaa sá uninja nee boonnooliibi ubɔɔɔ?» Ní Peetroo bí-u yii: «Aayee, maa sá.».

eng: The servants and the guards had made a fire because it was cold. They were standing around it, warming themselves. Peter joined them there, standing by the fire and warming himself.
local: Tiwool nìn bí, an pu ní bitɔntɔnliibi nì pilisib kpāā ŋŋmi ki sí ki wùl̀, ní Peetroo mun yú bi-cee niin ki wùl̀.

eng: Meanwhile, the chief priest questioned Jesus about his disciples and his teaching.
local: Niinee ní usaraaŋakpil bíi bàlifī Yeesu u-boonnooliibi bɔti nì tibɔti tin u dàkèe pu.

eng: Judas, his betrayer, also knew the place because Jesus often gathered there with his disciples.
local: Juudasi un nìn nyáń u-boonee mun nyí niin, kun puee Yeesu nì u-boonnooliibi nìn pée cá niin múlmul.

eng: Jesus answered, Ive spoken openly to the world. Ive always taught in synagogues and in the temple, where all the Jews gather. Ive said nothing in private.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-u yii: «M sòon̄ mpaampiin pu báà ŋma nimbiin, ki dàkā m-bɔti akpafidiinni nì kunimbɔtidiin, laakin Juuda yab kɔkɔ kpáfée. Maa nìn sòon̄ tiba dibalin.

eng: Why ask me? Ask those who heard what I told them. They know what I said.
local: Ba pu ní a bàlifī-mi? Bàlifì bin pílifì tin m sòon̄ee. Bālifì-bi tin m lēe, bíǹ nyí tin m sòon̄ee.».

eng: After Jesus spoke, one of the guards standing there slapped Jesus in the face. Is that how you would answer the high priest? he asked.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun Yeesu lī mimmee, ní pilisi ubɔ un yú ki kɔ́ĺ-uee fáá-u ntampa, ní ki bí-u yii: «Míǹ ní bi kíikí usaraaŋakpilaaa?».

eng: Jesus replied, If I speak wrongly, testify about what was wrong. But if I speak correctly, why do you strike me.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-u yii: «M yaa sòon̄ min kaa kítèe, dàkà-mi min ní m yīntèe. M yaa tí sòon̄ min kítèe, ba pu ní a fáá-mi?».

eng: Then Annas sent him, bound, to Caiaphas the high priest.
local: Yeesu ŋalii gbíntìi bóob́ee, ní Aani dū-u ki tīī usaraaŋakpil Kaayiifi.

eng: Meanwhile, Simon Peter was still standing with the guards, warming himself. They asked, Arent you one of his disciples? Peter denied it, saying, Im not.
local: Ŋyunti gbantee, Peetroo yú ki wùl̀ ŋŋmi. Ní bi bālifì-u yii: «Si mun kaa sá uninja nee boonnooliuaaa?» Ní Peetroo néé yii: «Aayee, man kaa sá.».

eng: A servant of the high priest, a relative of the one whose ear Peter had cut off, said to him, Didnt I see you in the garden with him.
local: Ní usaraaŋakpil tɔntɔnliu ubɔ un nabiki tafal Peetroo bàà gāā ki wúkutèe bí-u yii: «Maa bàà ká si nì u bukpaanaaa?».

eng: Peter denied it again, and immediately a rooster crowed.
local: Ní Peetroo fātìi tí néé yii an kaa sá úǹ. Kpalaa niin ní ukooja báakì.

eng: The Jewish leaders led Jesus from Caiaphas to the Roman governors palace. It was early in the morning. So that they could eat the Passover, the Jewish leaders wouldnt enter the palace; entering the palace would have made them ritually impure.
local: Ní bi yōoǹní Yeesu Kaayiifi cee ki cáá-u ki nín cá gɔmina Piilati cee. An nìn dáá sá kutaafaau pɔ̀lpɔl ní. Juuda yab kaa nìn là kí kɔ́ gɔmina diikun kun puee bi là kí nīn bí circir kí ŋmā kí jī Dilákatil jiŋaaliu.

eng: So Pilate went out to them and asked, What charge do you bring against this man.
local: Míǹ pu ní gɔmina Piilati nyánní ki dɔ́miń bi-cee, ní ki bālifì-bi yii: «Ba ní uninja nee ŋá ní ni cáań-u ní?».

eng: Judas brought a company of soldiers and some guards from the chief priests and Pharisees. They came there carrying lanterns, torches, and weapons.
local: Bisaraaŋakpilib nì Fariisab nìn tīī Juudasi disoojakitil nì dipilisikitil, ní u yōoǹ-bi ki cáa nín cá bukpaayee poon. Bi nìn ŋúb́ iŋminaapool nì fitilab nì tijanjawan.

eng: They answered, If he had done nothing wrong, we wouldnt have handed him over to you.
local: Ní bi kíí-u yii: «U yaa kaa bàà sá unikpiti, taa bàà cāabìń-u kí tīī-si!».

eng: Pilate responded, Take him yourselves and judge him according to your Law. The Jewish leaders replied, The Law doesnt allow us to kill anyone. .
local: Ní Piilati bí-bi yii: «Yòom̄maan-u, ní sòom̀maan u-bɔti kí nóó nì ni-marau nín sá puee!» Ní bi kíí-u yii: «Timbi kaa cáá nsan kí kpɔ̀ unil.».

eng: This was so that Jesus word might be fulfilled when he indicated how he was going to die.
local: An ŋá míǹ kí dàkà Yeesu nín nìn sòon̄ u-kúm min u làá kpúee puee dee.

eng: Pilate went back into the palace. He summoned Jesus and asked, Are you the king of the Jews.
local: Ní Piilati nín kóò kudiin ki yíiń Yeesu, ní ki bālifì-u yii: «Mimmee, sii di sá Juuda yab bɔtiuaaa?».

eng: Jesus answered, Do you say this on your own or have others spoken to you about me.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-u yii: «Tin tin a lī nee, sii a-ba di dākafì míǹ naaa, ubɔ di tùkū-si míǹ man pu ní?».

eng: Pilate responded, Im not a Jew, am I? Your nation and its chief priests handed you over to me. What have you done.
local: Ní Piilati kíí-u yii: «M sá Ujuudajaaa? A-yab nì bisaraaŋakpilib di dū-si ki tīiń-mi. Mimmee, ba ní a ŋá ní?».

eng: Jesus replied, My kingdom doesnt originate from this world. If it did, my guards would fight so that I wouldnt have been arrested by the Jewish leaders. My kingdom isnt from here.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-u yii: «M-beel kaa sá taapu doo beel. M-beel yaa bàà sá duulinyan yal, m-tɔntɔnliibi bàà jáà kí mɔ̄tì m-pu ní. Baa bàà kíikí bí dū-mi kí tīī Juuda yab kpilib. Ama, m-beel kaa sá taapu doo yal.».

eng: So you are a king? Pilate said. Jesus answered, You say that I am a king. I was born and came into the world for this reason: to testify to the truth. Whoever accepts the truth listens to my voice.
local: Ní Piilati bí-u yii: «Mimmee, a sá ubɔti níii?» Ní Yeesu kíí-u yii: «Sii di lī. M dɔ́miń bi màl̄-mi duulinyan ḿ dàkà binib kun di sá ibaamɔnee ní. Un kamaa sá ibaamɔn yɔuee pílifí tin m sòoǹee ní.».

eng: What is truth? Pilate asked. After Pilate said this, he returned to the Jewish leaders and said, I find no grounds for any charge against him.
local: Ní Piilati bālifì-u yii: «Ba di sá ibaamɔn ní?» Piilati sòon̄ míǹ ki dóòyee, ní ki nyáń ki tí nín cá Juuda yab cee, ní ki bí-bi yii: «Maa ká kun pu ní kijapaai nee máaǹ ditafadaaldee.

eng: You have a custom that I release one prisoner for you at Passover. Do you want me to release for you the king of the Jews.
local: Kii ni-marau nín pée sá puee, Dilákatil jiŋaaliu kamaayee, m gīintí usarkadaan ubɔ ní kí líí-ni. Mimmee, ni là ḿ gíiǹ Juuda yab bɔtiu kí líí-naaa?».

eng: Jesus knew everything that was to happen to him, so he went out and asked, Who are you looking for.
local: Yeesu nyí tin dòoń-uee, ní ki sútì ki pìl̄-bi, ki bālifì-bi yii: «Ŋma ní ni nyàab̀ ní?».

eng: They shouted, Not this man! Give us Barabbas.
local: Niinee ní bi gíti tí bíi tátí yii: «Aayee, á taa gíiǹ úǹ kí líí, see Baaraabaasi ní á gíiǹ kí líí!» Baaraabaasi gbantee mun di nìn sá digaŋgandaan.

eng: They answered, Jesus the Nazarene. He said to them, I Am.
local: Ní bi kíí-u yii: «Ti nyàab̀ Yeesu Naasaareeti yɔuee ní.» Ní u kíí-bi yii: «Man sèé!» Juudasi, un nyáń u-boonee, sáká bi-kansikin.

eng: When he said, I Am, they shrank back and fell to the ground.
local: Yeesu nín kíí-bi yii: «Man sèéyee», ní bi fātìi túlifì bi-boon ki lítì kitiŋ.

eng: He asked them again, Who are you looking for? They said, Jesus the Nazarene.
local: Ní Yeesu fātìi tí bālifì-bi yii: «Ŋma ní ni nyàab̀ ní?» Ní bi bí yii: «Ti nyàab̀ Yeesu Naasaareeti yɔuee ní.».

eng: Jesus answered, I told you, I Am. If you are looking for me, then let these people go.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-bi yii: «Kéè, m pée tùkū-ni yii m sèéyaa! An yaa sá man ní ni nyàab̀ee, ní yàntī bin bí nee ń nīn cá!».

eng: This was so that the word he had spoken might be fulfilled: I didnt lose anyone of those whom you gave me.
local: Mimmee an ŋá kii u nín bàà sòon̄ puee dee, yii: «Maa fá bin a tīī-mee kansikin báà ubɔ.».

eng: Then Pilate had Jesus taken and whipped.
local: Niinee ní Piilati dū Yeesu ki tīī bí gbá-u anaalab.

eng: So Pilate said, You wont speak to me? Dont you know that I have authority to release you and also to crucify you.
local: Ní Piilati bí-u yii: «Man ní saa làá kííii? Aa nyí yii m cáá mpɔn kí gíiǹ-si kí líí, ki cáá mpɔn kí yàntī bí kpáá-si ndɔpuntikaa puɔɔɔ?».

eng: Jesus replied, You would have no authority over me if it had not been given to you from above. Thats why the one who handed me over to you has the greater sin.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-u yii: «Aa cáá mpɔn ŋubɔ ki kútì ŋun Unimbɔti tīiń-si yilpuee. Míǹ pu ní un dū-mi ki ŋúbíǹ-see kpitii wīikíǹ sii yai.».

eng: From that moment on, Pilate wanted to release Jesus. However, the Jewish leaders cried out, saying, If you release this man, you arent a friend of the emperor! Anyone who makes himself out to be a king opposes the emperor.
local: Ki yōoǹ ŋyunti gbanti ní Piilati bíi nyàab̀ kí gíiǹ Yeesu kí líí. Ama ní Juuda yab bíi kūuntí ki lì yii: «A yaa gíiǹ kijapaai nee ki líìyee, aa ti sá gɔmina Seesaar bɔɔ! Un kamaa yaa yíì u-ba ubɔtee di sá Seesaar pulincɔnti.».

eng: When Pilate heard these words, he led Jesus out and seated him on the judges bench at the place called Stone Pavement .
local: Piilati gbìl̄ mimmee, ní ki nyántìń Yeesu mmɔŋki ki dɔ́miń ki kāl̀ dibɔsoonjal pu laakin bi yíì ataŋkpee paaŋŋuee, .

eng: It was about noon on the Preparation Day for the Passover. Pilate said to the Jewish leaders, Heres your king.
local: An nìn sá kuwiŋkuluu nyɔkɔm ní kutaa ń wúntí Dilákatil jiŋaaliu. Ní u lī Juuda yab yii: «Kpèèmaan, ni-bɔtiu sèé!».

eng: The Jewish leaders cried out, Take him away! Take him away! Crucify him! Pilate responded, What? Do you want me to crucify your king? We have no king except the emperor, the chief priests answered.
local: Ní bi bíi tátí yii: «Bí kpɔ̀-u, bí kpɔ̀-u, bí kpáá-u ndɔpuntikaa pu!» Ní Piilati bí-bi yii: «Mimmee, ḿ yàntī bí kpáá ni-bɔtiu ndɔpuntikaa puɔɔɔ?» Ní bisaraaŋakpilib kíí-u yii: «Timbi kaa ti cáá ubɔti ubɔ ki kútì Seesaar pu.».

eng: Then Pilate handed Jesus over to be crucified. The soldiers took Jesus prisoner.
local: Niinee ní Piilati dū Yeesu ki tīī-bi bí cáá-u kí kpáá ndɔpuntikaa pu. Niinee ní bi cáá Yeesu ki búntì.

eng: Carrying his cross by himself, he went out to a place called Skull Place .
local: Ní Yeesu yōoǹ u-dɔpuntikaaŋu ki nyánní kitiŋin ki nín cá laakin bi yíì Kuyikpabiku laŋkee.

eng: Thats where they crucified him—and two others with him, one on each side and Jesus in the middle.
local: Niin ní soojab kpáá Yeesu ndɔpuntikaa pu, ní ki gíti kpáá binib bili ki ŋàl̄-u, ubɔ u-ŋaŋgiil pú, un kíǹee u-ŋaŋgan pú ní úǹ sáká kansikin.

eng: Pilate had a public notice written and posted on the cross. It read Jesus the Nazarene, the king of the Jews.
local: Piilati nìn ŋmàn̄ tiŋmaŋmabiti ki tīī bí cáá nákań Yeesu dɔpuntikaaŋu pu. U nìn ŋmàn̄ yii: «Yeesu Naasaareeti yɔu, un sá Juuda yab bɔtiuee.».

eng: The soldiers twisted together a crown of thorns and put it on his head, and dressed him in a purple robe.
local: Soojab nìn dū ikuŋkum ki lùn̄ kibeefuulii ki dūu cíkiǹ Yeesu, ní ki dū dibɔkutil diba dì máǹ cínciŋ ki dūu pēeǹ-u.

eng: Many of the Jews read this sign, for the place where Jesus was crucified was near the city and it was written in Aramaic, Latin, and Greek.
local: Juuda yab tikpil nìn kàan̄ tiŋmaŋmabiti gbanti, kun puee laakin bi nìn kpáá Yeesuee nìn pìlīǹ kitiŋin, ní bi nìn ŋmàn̄ eebru sooŋyin ki ŋmàn̄ Room sooŋyin ní ki ŋmàn̄ greeki sooŋyin.

eng: Therefore, the Jewish chief priests complained to Pilate, Dont write, The king of the Jews but This man said, I am the king of the Jews.
local: Ní Juuda yab saraaŋakpilib bí Piilati yii: «Taa ŋmà yii: <Un sá Juuda yab bɔtiuee>, ŋmà yii: <Uninja nee bí yii úǹ di sá Juuda yab bɔtiu.>».

eng: Pilate answered, What Ive written, Ive written.
local: Ní Piilati kíí-bi yii: «Tin tin m ŋmàn̄ee, tíǹ dee.».

eng: When the soldiers crucified Jesus, they took his clothes and his sandals, and divided them into four shares, one for each soldier. His shirt was seamless, woven as one piece from the top to the bottom.
local: Bijanjaliibi bin nìn kpáá Yeesu ndɔpuntikaa puee nìn pēetì-u ki yōoǹ u-wampeeŋkaati ki yākatì minaa pu, bi-kɔkɔ yōoǹ diba diba pu. An kíǹ dibɔkutifɔkɔl din bi nìn lùn̄ dicilpu, kaa ŋáĺ ki kpáfì kpáfèe.

eng: They said to each other, Lets not tear it. Lets cast lots to see who will get it. This was to fulfill the scripture, They divided my clothes among themselves, and they cast lots for my clothing. Thats what the soldiers did.
local: Ní soojabee bíi lì tɔb yii: «Tí taa cátí dibɔkutil gbanti, tí tɔ̄maan kasi kí kpèè ŋma di làá jī díǹ.» Ní bi lafun ŋá míǹ. Min bi ŋá nee nóó nì Unimbɔti bɔti nín nìn sòon̄ ki síiǹ puee, yii: «Bi dū m-wampeeŋkaati ki yākatì, ní ki tɔ́ kasi m-bɔkutifɔkɔl pu.» Soojab nín nìn ŋá puee dee.

eng: Jesus mother and his mothers sister, Mary the wife of Clopas, and Mary Magdalene stood near the cross.
local: Yeesu na nì Yeesu nawaa, nì Maarii Kloopasi nimpuu nì Maarii un sá Magidalaa tiŋkinee di nìn sí ki kɔ́ĺ Yeesu dɔpuntikaaŋu.

eng: When Jesus saw his mother and the disciple whom he loved standing nearby, he said to his mother, Woman, here is your son.
local: Yeesu ká u-na nì u-boonnooliu un u néeńee yú ki kɔ́ĺ tɔb, ní u bí u-na yii: «M-na, kpèè a-jipɔɔn sèé».

eng: Then he said to the disciple, Here is your mother. And from that time on, this disciple took her into his home.
local: ní ki bí u-boonnooliuee mun yii: «Kpèè, a-na sèé.» Ki yōoǹ ŋyunti gbantee ní u-boonnooliuee yōoǹ-u u kíǹ ki bí u-cee.

eng: After this, knowing that everything was already completed, in order to fulfill the scripture, Jesus said, I am thirsty.
local: Míǹ boonee, Yeesu ká yii kɔŋkɔnnee tikɔkɔ dóòyee, ní u bí yii: «Nnyinnyoo cáá-mi.» An ŋá kii Unimbɔti gbɔŋku nín nìn pée sòon̄ ki síiǹ puee dee.

eng: A jar full of sour wine was nearby, so the soldiers soaked a sponge in it, placed it on a hyssop branch, and held it up to his lips.
local: Dibool diba nìn yú niin, ndamam min gàm̀ee kā poon, ní bi dū kuca ki bɔ́ ndaamee poon ní ki dūu túuń isɔpi kpaŋkpaaŋŋu pu, ní ki cáa gbēē Yeesu nyɔkɔ.

eng: Over and over they went up to him and said, Greetings, king of the Jews! And they slapped him in the face.
local: Ní bi sútíi kɔ́l̀-u, ní ki lì yii: «Juuda yab bɔtiu, ti jáaḿ-si!» ní ki fátí-u.

eng: When he had received the sour wine, Jesus said, It is completed. Bowing his head, he gave up his life.
local: Yeesu nyùn̄ee ní ki bí yii: «Tikɔkɔ dóò», ní ki tīntì u-yil ní ki dū u-fam ki tīī ki kpíì.

eng: It was the Preparation Day and the Jewish leaders didnt want the bodies to remain on the cross on the Sabbath, especially since that Sabbath was an important day. So they asked Pilate to have the legs of those crucified broken and the bodies taken down.
local: Ŋwìiń bíi jēetí ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa ń kíĺee, Juuda yab kpilib kaa nìn là bin bi kpááyee wunti ń nīn lúń idɔpuntikaa pu ŋwiin gbanti. Ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa gbanti nìn sá ŋwiŋkpaan ní, míǹ pu ní bi nìn mēē Piilati yii bí kɔ̄fì bi-taaŋi bí kpú malaa, bí cúú-bi kí kpáatí.

eng: Therefore, the soldiers came and broke the legs of the two men who were crucified with Jesus.
local: Niinee soojab dɔ́miń ki kɔ́fì bin bin bi nìn kpáá idɔpuntikaa pu nì Yeesu dicilpuee upeepeekaa nì uliliiti taaŋi.

eng: When they came to Jesus, they saw that he was already dead so they didnt break his legs.
local: Bi bāań Yeesuee, ní ki ká yii u kpíì dooooyee, míǹ pu ní baa ti kɔ́fì úǹ taaŋi.

eng: However, one of the soldiers pierced his side with a spear, and immediately blood and water came out.
local: Ama ní sooja ubɔ bi-kansikin dū kikpaŋ ki sāā u-tikindi, ní fatiku nì nnyim nyáń.

eng: The one who saw this has testified, and his testimony is true. He knows that he speaks the truth, and he has testified so that you also can believe.
local: Un bíi lèekī seera tibɔti nee puee baasii ká-tì ní, ní u-seerauee sá ibaamɔn, ní u nyí yii tin u sòoǹee sá ibaamɔn, ni mun ń ŋmā kí fōō kí kíí.

eng: These things happened to fulfill the scripture, They wont break any of his bones .
local: Tin bí nee ŋá míǹ, án ŋmā kí nóó nì Unimbɔti gbɔŋku nín nìn lī ki síiǹ puee, yii: «Baa làá wīī u-kpabil báà diba.».

eng: And another scripture says, They will look at him whom they have pierced.
local: Laaba mun gíti lī yii: «Bi làá dāá ká un tikindi bi sāā ki cíl̀ee.».

eng: After this Joseph of Arimathea asked Pilate if he could take away the body of Jesus. Joseph was a disciple of Jesus, but a secret one because he feared the Jewish authorities. Pilate gave him permission, so he came and took the body away.
local: Míǹ boonee, Jooseefi un sá Arimatee tiŋkinee, cūtì ki mēē Piilati nsan kí yòon̄ Yeesu wunti. - Jooseefi gbanti nìn sá Yeesu boonnooliu ní kaa là binib ń béé, kun puee u nìn fàŋkí Juuda yab. - Ní Piilati pú-u nsan, ní u cūtìi yōoǹ Yeesu wunti.

eng: Nicodemus, the one who at first had come to Jesus at night, was there too. He brought a mixture of myrrh and aloe, nearly seventy-five pounds in all.
local: Niikoodeem un nìn cūtì dalba kunyeeu ki ká Yeesuee mun dɔ́miń, ki cáań tikaliŋkpun tin bi yíì miiree, tì ŋmàl̄ nì talaalu un bi yíì Alooweesi kiiloobi mmuŋku nì saalaa nyɔkɔm.

eng: Pilate came out of the palace again and said to the Jewish leaders, Look! Im bringing him out to you to let you know that I find no grounds for a charge against him.
local: Ní Piilati fātìi tí nyánní, ní ki bí Juuda yab yii: «Kpèèmaan, m làá nyāntiń-ni-u mmɔŋki, ní béé yii man kaa ká kun pu ní kijapaai nee máaǹ ditafadaaldee.».

eng: Following Jewish burial customs, they took Jesus body and wrapped it, with the spices, in linen cloths.
local: Bi yōoǹ Yeesu wunti mimmee, ní ki dū iŋaabu ki fìn̄-u ki dū talaalubee ki yāaǹ u-pu, kii Juuda yab koobiliŋ nín bàlifī bi yaa làá síb́ unil bí ŋá puee.

eng: There was a garden in the place where Jesus was crucified, and in the garden was a new tomb in which no one had ever been laid.
local: Laakin bi nìn kpáá Yeesuee, bukpaa bubɔ nìn bí niin. Bu-poon ní dikaakupɔndi din ni baa láá síb́ báà ubɔee nìn bí.

eng: Because it was the Jewish Preparation Day and the tomb was nearby, they laid Jesus in it.
local: Niin ní bi nìn cáá Yeesu ki síb́ kun puee án nìn sá kutaa ń wúntí Juuda yab wiŋŋuŋfikaaŋuaa, ní dikaakulee mun nìn pìl̄ niin.

eng: When Jesus came out, wearing the crown of thorns and the purple robe, Pilate said to them, Heres the man.
local: Ní Yeesu nyáń ki cíkí ikuŋkum fuuliiki ki pēē dibɔkutimandi cínciŋ, ní Piilati bí-bi yii: «Kpèèmaan unil!».

eng: When the chief priests and their deputies saw him, they shouted out, Crucify, crucify! Pilate told them, You take him and crucify him. I dont find any grounds for a charge against him.
local: Bisaraaŋakpilib nì bi-pilisibee nín pée ká-uee, ní bi bíi tátí yii: «Bí kpáá-u ndɔpuntikaa pu! Bí kpáá-u ndɔpuntikaa pu!» Ní Piilati bí-bi yii: «Yòom̄maan-u kí cùú kpáá-u nimbi ni-ba ndɔpuntikaa pu, kun puee man kaa ká kun ní u ŋá ki máaǹ ditafadaaldee!».

eng: The Jewish leaders replied, We have a Law, and according to this Law he ought to die because he made himself out to be Gods Son.
local: Ní Juuda yab kíí-u yii: «Timbi marau poonee, u máaǹ ŋkúm, kun puee u bí yii u sá Unimbɔti Jipɔɔn.».

eng: When Pilate heard this word, he was even more afraid.
local: Piilati gbìl̄ mimmee, ní tifaandi mɔ̄tìi gíti kɔ́-u.

eng: He went back into the residence and spoke to Jesus, Where are you from? Jesus didnt answer.
local: ní u nín kóò kudiin, ki bālifì Yeesu yii: «A baasii nyánní la ní?» Ní Yeesu kaa kíí-u báà tiba.

eng: On the third day there was a wedding in Cana of Galilee. Jesus mother was there, and.
local: Winta dalee, tinimpucan tiba nìn bí Kana, Galiilee timbiliikin. Yeesu na mun nìn bí niin.

eng: and said, Everyone serves the good wine first. They bring out the second-rate wine only when the guests are drinking freely. You kept the good wine until now.
local: ní ki bí-u yii: «Bi péetí kí cáań ndamɔntiim, ní binib yaa nyùn̄ an nyāatì-bee ní bí nín nyāntiń min kaa mɔ̄tìi mɔ̄ee. Ní sii dū ndamɔntiim ki kpáafì ki dāa cāabìń-mi naŋkɔnampuɔɔɔ?».

eng: This was the first miraculous sign that Jesus did in Cana of Galilee. He revealed his glory, and his disciples believed in him.
local: Kana timbiliiki kin bí Galiileeyee ní Yeesu nìn ŋá u-maamaacipeepeekaau ki dàkā u-nyooti, ní u-boonnooliibi fōō-u ki kíí.

eng: After this, Jesus and his mother, his brothers, and his disciples went down to Capernaum and stayed there for a few days.
local: Boonee ní u nì u-na nì u-naalib nì u-boonnooliibi nín sìntī Kapeernayum, ki kāl̀ niin iwiŋkaaŋkaa iba.

eng: It was nearly time for the Jewish Passover, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
local: Juuda yab jiŋaaliu un bi yíì Dilákatil jiŋaaliuee nìn tóobińee, ní Yeesu nín cá Jeerusaleem.

eng: He found in the temple those who were selling cattle, sheep, and doves, as well as those involved in exchanging currency sitting there.
local: U bàn̄ ki kɔ́ kunimbɔtidiinee ní ki ká bin nyáfí inaa, nì ipii nì abeenanjilee nì binimbikpantiliibi kā.

eng: He made a whip from ropes and chased them all out of the temple, including the cattle and the sheep. He scattered the coins and overturned the tables of those who exchanged currency.
local: Ní u nyàab̄ iŋmii ki dūu ŋá dinaalabil, ní ki jìn̄-bi ki nyántì nì bi-piii nì bi-naai kɔkɔ, ní ki dū binimbikpantiliibi nimbiliŋ ki yàl̄ kitiŋ, ní ki lábitì bi-teebilib ki cìb̄.

eng: He said to the dove sellers, Get these things out of here! Dont make my Fathers house a place of business.
local: ní ki bí bin nyáfí abeenanjilee yii: «Nyāntímaan ni-wanti nee doo. Ní taa nín̄ cáá m-Baa diiku ki kpántí kunyɔmpudii!».

eng: His disciples remembered that it is written, Passion for your house consumes me.
local: Niinee ní u-boonnooliibi téetì tibɔti tin bi ŋmàn̄ Unimbɔti gbɔŋkunee, yii: «Ti-Dindaan, m nín mɔ́tí a-diiku nee pu miŋyee di wù m-pɔbilin kii ŋŋmee.».

eng: Then the Jewish leaders asked him, By what authority are you doing these things? What miraculous sign will you show us.
local: Ní Juuda yab kpilib bí-u yii: «Dàkà-ti kudaaŋŋu ní tí béé yii a cáá nsan kí ŋá min a ŋá nee!».

eng: Jesus answered, Destroy this temple and in three days Ill raise it up.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-bi yii: «Wīīmaan kunimbɔtidii nee, iwiin ita dal m làá fàtīí mā-ku.».

eng: Jesus and his disciples were also invited to the celebration.
local: ní bi nìn yíiń Yeesu mun nì u-boonnooliibi bí dāan̄.

eng: The Jewish leaders replied, It took forty-six years to build this temple, and you will raise it up in three days.
local: Niinee ní Juuda yab kpilib bí yii: «Bi dū abin imuŋku ili nì aluu ní ki máń kunimbɔtidii nee, nì mana ní sii ŋūǹ kí dū iwiin ita kí fàtīí mā-ku?».

eng: But the temple Jesus was talking about was his body.
local: Ama Yeesu nìn sòoǹ u-wunti pu ní ki nántiǹ kunimbɔtidii.

eng: After he was raised from the dead, his disciples remembered what he had said, and they believed the scripture and the word that Jesus had spoken.
local: Boon u nín dāa fīkitì ditaŋkpiilinee ní u-boonnooliibi téetì yii u nìn lī míǹ, ní ki fōō tin bi nìn ŋmàn̄ Unimbɔti gbɔŋkunee nì Yeesu nín sòon̄ tin u-fìkititam puee ki kíí.

eng: While Jesus was in Jerusalem for the Passover Festival, many believed in his name because they saw the miraculous signs that he did.
local: Yeesu nín nìn bí Jeerusaleem Dilákatil jiŋaaliu yuntiŋuee ní binib tikpil kántì maamaacib bin u ŋáańee ní ki fōō-u ki kíí.

eng: But Jesus didnt trust himself to them because he knew all people.
local: Ama Yeesu kaa nìn máká bi-pu. Di sá yii u nyí bi-kɔkɔ nín sá biŋyee.

eng: He didnt need anyone to tell him about human nature, for he knew what human nature was.
