local: Waa nyàab̀ ubɔ ń tùkù-u kun bi sée, kun puee u nyí tin bí unil pɔbilinee.

eng: When the wine ran out, Jesus mother said to him, They dont have any wine.
local: Ndaam dāa dóòyee, ní Yeesu na bí-u yii: «Bi-daam dóòyaa!».

eng: Jesus replied, Woman, what does that have to do with me? My time hasnt come yet.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-u yii: «M-na, sii di làá tùkù-mi m nín làá ŋá pueeee? Ŋyunti ŋun m làá tɔ̄ m-tundee kaa láá ŋmāntì.».

eng: His mother told the servants, Do whatever he tells you.
local: Niinee ní Yeesu na bí bitɔntɔnliibi bin bí niinee yii: «U yaa bí ní ŋá puee, ŋámaan míǹ.».

eng: Nearby were six stone water jars used for the Jewish cleansing ritual, each able to hold about twenty or thirty gallons.
local: Ataŋkpee lɔnti tiluu nìn yú. Niin ní bi nìn jòotī nnyim ki fíntí, kii Juuda yab koobiliŋ nín sá puee. Kulɔŋŋu kamaa nìn fòol̄ nnyim liitirib dilaataal ní.

eng: Jesus said to the servants, Fill the jars with water, and they filled them to the brim.
local: Ní Yeesu bí bitɔntɔnliibee yii bí lúń nnyim kí gbéeǹ tilɔn gbanti. Ní bi lafun lúń ki gbéeǹ yátí yati.

eng: Then he told them, Now draw some from them and take it to the headwaiter, and they did.
local: Niinee ní Yeesu bí-bi yii: «Jōōmaan kí cáá tīī ukpil un kpèé tijinee!» Ní bi jōō ki cáa tīī-u.

eng: The headwaiter tasted the water that had become wine. He didnt know where it came from, though the servants who had drawn the water knew. The headwaiter called the groom.
local: Ukpilee dákée, nnyimee kpántì ndaam. Waa nìn nyí laakin ndaamee nyánnée, see bitɔntɔnliibi bin nìn lúń nnyimee di nyí. Niinee ní u yíiń unimpucaan cal.

eng: Early in the morning of the first day of the week, while it was still dark, Mary Magdalene came to the tomb and saw that the stone had been taken away from the tomb.
local: Ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa jítì kutaa wúntì, kutaafaau pɔ̀lpɔl an dáá bɔ̀ǹee, Maarii un sá Magidalaa tiŋkinee nín cá dikaakul laŋki, ní ki ká yii ditaŋkpal din bi nìn dūu pīiǹ dikaakul nyɔkɔ puee kaa ti pīī.

eng: Then the disciples returned to the place where they were staying.
local: Niin ní bi fātì ki nín gítí bi-dumpu.

eng: Mary stood outside near the tomb, crying. As she cried, she bent down to look into the tomb.
local: Maarii nìn yú ki kɔ́ĺ dikaakul ki sūǹ. U yú ki sūǹee, ní ki gbóoń ki kpēē dikaakul poon.

eng: She saw two angels dressed in white, seated where the body of Jesus had been, one at the head and one at the foot.
local: ní ki ká Unimbɔti tuuŋyi ili kā laakin bi nìn bíl̀ Yeesu wuntee ki pēē tiwampin. Ŋubɔ kā u-yil laŋki, ní ŋun kíǹee mun kā u-taaŋi laŋki.

eng: The angels asked her, Woman, why are you crying? She replied, They have taken away my Lord, and I dont know where theyve put him.
local: Ní ituunee bālifì-u yii: «Unimpuee, ba ŋá ní a sūǹ?» Ní u kíí-i yii: «Bi yōoǹ m-Dindaan. Maa nyí laakin bi dū-u ki ŋée!».

eng: As soon as she had said this, she turned around and saw Jesus standing there, but she didnt know it was Jesus.
local: U lī mimmee, ki fātèe ki ká Yeesu yú, ama waa béè yii an sá Yeesu.

eng: Jesus said to her, Woman, why are you crying? Who are you looking for? Thinking he was the gardener, she replied, Sir, if you have carried him away, tell me where you have put him and I will get him.
local: Ní Yeesu bālifì-u yii: «Unimpuee, ba ŋá ní a sūǹ ní? Ŋma ní a nyàab̀ ní?» Unimpuee nyí kii bukpaadaan di bíi bàlifī-u, míǹ pu ní u bí yii: «Ukpil, sii di yaa yōoǹ-u, dàkà-mi laakin a cáá-u ki ŋée, m làá cùú yòon̄-u».

eng: Jesus said to her, Mary. She turned and said to him in Aramaic, Rabbouni .
local: Ní Yeesu yíiń-u yii: «Maarii!» Ní u fātìi u-ba ki túĺ-u ní ki lī-u nì eebru sooŋyin yii: «Rabuunii!» - An taapu sá yii, Ukpil.

eng: Jesus said to her, Dont hold on to me, for I havent yet gone up to my Father. Go to my brothers and sisters and tell them, Im going up to my Father and your Father, to my God and your God.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-u yii: «Taa gbēē-mi, di sá yii maa láá jɔ́m̀ m-Baa cee. Ama nín̄ cá kí tùkù m-nabiyaamu yii m nín jɔ́ḿ m-Baa un sá ni-Baayee, m-Nimbɔtiu un sá ni-Nimbɔtiuee cee.».

eng: Mary Magdalene left and announced to the disciples, Ive seen the Lord. Then she told them what he said to her.
local: Niinee Maarii Magidalaa yɔu nín cá u-boonnooliibi cee ki tùkū-bi yii: «M ká ti-Dindaan!» Ní ki dū tin u tùkū-uee ki lī.

eng: It was still the first day of the week. That evening, while the disciples were behind closed doors because they were afraid of the Jewish authorities, Jesus came and stood among them. He said, Peace be with you.
local: Ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa jítì, ku wúntì kujoouee ní Yeesu boonnooliibi kpáfì ki kā kudii kubɔn ki pīiǹ mpunyɔmu nì saafii, kun puee bi fàŋkí Juuda yab kpilib. Ní Yeesu cílìń, ki dɔ́miń ki yúl̀ bi-kansikin, ní ki bí-bi yii: «Ni-pɔbiliŋ ń nīn sɔ̀m̀!».

eng: She ran to Simon Peter and the other disciple, the one whom Jesus loved, and said, They have taken the Lord from the tomb, and we dont know where theyve put him.
local: Ní u fātìi sáǹ, ki cūtì Siimɔn Peetroo nì Yeesu boonnooliu un u néeńee cee, ní ki bí-bi yii: «Bi cúú ti-Dindaan ki lēē dikaakulin. Taa nyí laakin bi cáá-u ki ŋée!».

eng: After he said this, he showed them his hands and his side. When the disciples saw the Lord, they were filled with joy.
local: U bíi sòoǹ mimmee, ní ki dàkā-bi u-ŋalii nì u-takakal. U-boonnooliibi nín ká ti-Dindaan mimmee, ní an mɔ́ɔkiǹ-bi tikpil.

eng: Jesus said to them again, Peace be with you. As the Father sent me, so I am sending you.
local: Ní Yeesu gíti lī-bi yii: «Ni-pɔbiliŋ ń nīn sɔ̀m̀! Kii m-Baa nín túnní-mi puee, ma mun tú nimbi.».

eng: Then he breathed on them and said, Receive the Holy Spirit.
local: U lī mimmee, ní ki púfì bi-pu, ní ki bí yii: «Fōōmaan Unimbɔti Fam.

eng: If you forgive anyones sins, they are forgiven; if you dont forgive them, they arent forgiven.
local: Ni yaa dū bin kpitii ki pú-bee, bi làá ká bi-kpitii pɔ̄tam. Ni yaa kaa dū bin kpitii ki pú-bee, baa làá ká bi-kpitii pɔ̄tam.».

eng: Thomas, the one called Didymus, one of the Twelve, wasnt with the disciples when Jesus came.
local: Ama ŋyunti ŋun Yeesu dɔ́mińee, Toomasi un bi gíti yíì Ata ki sá Yeesu kpambaliŋ saalaa nì alee kansikin dibee, nìn kpá.

eng: The other disciples told him, Weve seen the Lord! But he replied, Unless I see the nail marks in his hands, put my finger in the wounds left by the nails, and put my hand into his side, I wont believe.
local: Ní u-boonnooliibi bin nìn bée bí-u yii: «Ti ká ti-Dindaan!» Ní Toomasi kíí-bi yii: «M-nimbiliŋ yaa kaa ká u-ŋalii laakin bi kpááyee, m yaa kaa dū m-ŋambiki ki bɔ́ laakin bi kpááyee, ki yaa kaa dū m-ŋal ki bɔ́ u-takakal laakin bi nìn sāāyee, maa làá fōō kí kíí.».

eng: After eight days his disciples were again in a house and Thomas was with them. Even though the doors were locked, Jesus entered and stood among them. He said, Peace be with you.
local: Iwiin inii boonee, Yeesu boonnooliibi fātìi kpáfì kudiin. Toomasi mun nìn bí bi-kansikin. Bi dū mpunyɔmu ki pīiǹ ki kpàl̄, ama ní Yeesu cílìń bi-kansikin, ní ki bí-bi yii: «Ni-pɔbiliŋ ń nīn sɔ̀m̀!».

eng: Then he said to Thomas, Put your finger here. Look at my hands. Put your hand into my side. No more disbelief. Believe.
local: Ní u bí Toomasi yii: «Dāá kpèè m-ŋalii, kí cáań a-ŋambiki kí máań! Cáań a-ŋal kí máań m-takakal, kí fōō kí kíí, kí taa nín̄ gíti kpākantí!».

eng: Thomas responded to Jesus, My Lord and my God.
local: Niinee ní Toomasi ń bí-u yii: «M-Dindaan, m-Nimbɔtiu!».

eng: Jesus replied, Do you believe because you see me? Happy are those who dont see and yet believe.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-u yii: «A nín ká-mee ní a nín fōō ki kííyaa! Bin kaa ká-mi ki fōō ki kííyee bí mmɔnneeee!».

eng: Peter and the other disciple left to go to the tomb.
local: Ní Peetroo nì uboonnool un béeyee nín cá dikaakul laŋki.

eng: Then Jesus did many other miraculous signs in his disciples presence, signs that arent recorded in this scroll.
local: Yeesu nìn ŋáaǹ maamaacib tikpil u-boonnooliibi kansikin bin ní baa ŋmàn̄ ki ŋá kugbɔŋu nee poonee.

eng: But these things are written so that you will believe that Jesus is the Christ, Gods Son, and that believing, you will have life in his name.
local: Bi yaa ŋmàn̄ tin bí neeyee, an sá ní fōō kí kíí yii Yeesu sá Unimbɔti Jipɔɔn ki sá Meesiya. Ni yaa fōō-u ki kíí mimmee, ní ká dimaŋfal u-yindi pu.

eng: They were running together, but the other disciple ran faster than Peter and was the first to arrive at the tomb.
local: Bi-liitil kɔkɔ nìn sáń ki cá, ní uboonnool un béeyee sáǹ ki jítì Peetroo pu, ki péé ki bàn̄ dikaakul laŋki malaa.

eng: Bending down to take a look, he saw the linen cloths lying there, but he didnt go in.
local: U gbóońee, ki ká iŋaabu dɔ̄, ama waa kɔ́.

eng: Following him, Simon Peter entered the tomb and saw the linen cloths lying there.
local: Boonee ní Siimɔn Peetroo mun bāań ki nín kóò dikaakul poon, ní ki ká iŋaabu in nìn fìn̄ Yeesu wuntee dɔ̄ kitiŋ.

eng: He also saw the face cloth that had been on Jesus head. It wasnt with the other clothes but was folded up in its own place.
local: nì ŋŋaabu ŋun nìn fìn̄ u-yilee. Ŋŋaabu gbanti nì in nìn fìn̄ u-wuntee kaa nìn dɔ̄ dibɔɔbaantiil, ŋúǹ nìn póob́ ki dɔ̄ ŋkpaanceeti laaba.

eng: Then the other disciple, the one who arrived at the tomb first, also went inside. He saw and believed.
local: Niinee ní Yeesu boonnooliu un nìn péé péé ki bàn̄ee mun kɔ́, ki ká, ní ki fōō ki kíí yii u fīkitì.

eng: They didnt yet understand the scripture that Jesus must rise from the dead.
local: Ama Yeesu boonnooliibi kaa nìn dáá gbìl̄ Unimbɔti gbɔŋku nín lī yii Yeesu máaǹ kí fìkitī ditaŋkpiilinee taapu.

eng: Later, Jesus himself appeared again to his disciples at the Sea of Tiberias. This is how it happened.
local: Boonee ní Yeesu fātìi tí nyántì u-ba u-boonnooliibi ká-u Tiibeeriyaadi mɔɔgbandi. Bi nín ká-u, tin jítèe sèé.

eng: Jesus said to them, Bring some of the fish that youve just caught.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-bi yii: «Ní cáań ijɔm in ni cúúyee iba.».

eng: Simon Peter got up and pulled the net to shore. It was full of large fish, one hundred fifty-three of them. Yet the net hadnt torn, even with so many fish.
local: Ní Siimɔn Peetroo kɔ́ buŋalimbu poon ki dáaliń kulaaŋŋu ki bíl̀ kitiŋ, ijɔŋkpaan gbí poon, i nìn sá ijɔm dilaataal nì imuŋku ili nì saalaa nì ita. Nì min kɔkɔ ijɔm nìn wīikí kulaaŋŋuee poonee, kuaa nìn cátì.

eng: Jesus said to them, Come and have breakfast. None of the disciples could bring themselves to ask him, Who are you? They knew it was the Lord.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-bi yii: «Dāan̄ kí jīmaan tijin!» Ní u-boonnooliibi kansikin báà ubɔ kaa wāaǹ ki bālifì-u yii: «Sii di sá ŋma?» Bi nìn nyí yii an sá ti-Dindaan.

eng: Jesus came, took the bread, and gave it to them. He did the same with the fish.
local: Ní Yeesu sútìń, ki dū kpɔnɔ ki tīī-bi, ní ki tīī-bi ijɔm mun.

eng: This was now the third time Jesus appeared to his disciples after he was raised from the dead.
local: Yeesu nín nìn kpíì ki fīkitèe, min bí nee di sá ntataatiim min u nìn nyántì u-ba ki dàkā u-boonnooliibee.

eng: When they finished eating, Jesus asked Simon Peter, Simon son of John, do you love me more than these? Simon replied, Yes, Lord, you know I love you. Jesus said to him, Feed my lambs.
local: Bi jíń tijin ki dóòyee, ní Yeesu bālifì Siimɔn Peetroo yii: «Siimɔn, Yoowaneesi jipɔɔn, a néeń-mi ki jítiǹ bin bí neeee?» Ní u kíí-u yii: «Iiin, m-Dindaan, a nyí m nín néeń-see.» Ní Yeesu bí yii: «Á nīn kpàá m-pibiliŋ.».

eng: Jesus asked a second time, Simon son of John, do you love me? Simon replied, Yes, Lord, you know I love you. Jesus said to him, Take care of my sheep.
local: Ní u gíti bālifì-u nliliitiim yii: «Siimɔn, Yoowaneesi jipɔɔn, a néeń-maaa?» Ní u bí yii: «Iiin, m-Dindaan, a nyí m nín néeń-see.» Ní Yeesu bí-u yii: «Á nīn kpàá m-piii.».

eng: He asked a third time, Simon son of John, do you love me? Peter was sad that Jesus asked him a third time, Do you love me? He replied, Lord, you know everything; you know I love you. Jesus said to him, Feed my sheep.
local: Ní u gíti bālifì-u ntataatiim yii: «Siimɔn, Yoowaneesi jipɔɔn, a néeń-maaa?» An nìn kɔ́ Peetroo kunimbaau min ní Yeesu nìn bālifì-u ntataatiim yii, a néeń-maaayee, ní u kíí Yeesu yii: «M-Dindaan, a nyí tikɔkɔ, ki nyí m nín néeń-see!» Ní Yeesu bí-u yii: «Á nīn kpàá m-piii.

eng: I assure you that when you were younger you tied your own belt and walked around wherever you wanted. When you grow old, you will stretch out your hands and another will tie your belt and lead you where you dont want to go.
local: Ibaamɔn, ibaamɔn ní m sòoǹ nì si, a nín nìn sá unaacimpɔɔnee, a nìn gbìǹ a-dambalau a-ba, ní ki cá laakin a yaa lèe. Ama a yaa láa púĺee, a dāa tàntī a-ŋalii, ní ubɔ ń gbìn̄-si dambala, ní kí cáá-si kí cùnnì laakin aa bàà là kí cùee.».

eng: He said this to show the kind of death by which Peter would glorify God. After saying this, Jesus said to Peter, Follow me.
local: Yeesu nìn sòon̄ an bɔti kí dàkà Peetroo ŋkúm min u làá dāá kpú kí tīī Unimbɔti tinyoolee. U lī míǹ ki dóòyee, ní ki bí yii: «Nín̄ nóó-mi!».

eng: Simon Peter, Thomas , Nathanael from Cana in Galilee, Zebedees sons, and two other disciples were together.
local: Siimɔn Peetroo nì Toomasi un bi yíì Atee nì Natanayeel - un sá Kana Galiilee timbiliikinee - nì Seebeedee jipɔmbi nì Yeesu boonnooliibi bili biba mun di nìn kpáfì ki bí dicilpu.

eng: Peter turned around and saw the disciple whom Jesus loved following them. This was the one who had leaned against Jesus at the meal and asked him, Lord, who is going to betray you.
local: Peetroo fātèe, ki ká uboonnool un Yeesu néeńee di dòoń bi-boonee. Úǹ di sá un, bi nìn bíi jīǹ tijin u sútì ki nákáǹ Yeesu, ki bālifì-u yii: «Ti-Dindaan, ŋma di làá dū-si kí tīī a-nannanliibee?».

eng: When Peter saw this disciple, he said to Jesus, Lord, what about him.
local: Peetroo ká-uee, ní ki bālifì Yeesu yii: «Ti-Dindaan, tilati di làá bàń unee úǹ ní?».

eng: Jesus replied, If I want him to remain until I come, what difference does that make to you? You must follow me.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-u yii: «M yaa là ú nīn bí kí nīn cí m-gítintam, tíǹ kaa ká-si. Sii dee, nóó-mi!».

eng: Therefore, the word spread among the brothers and sisters that this disciple wouldnt die. However, Jesus didnt say he wouldnt die, but only, If I want him to remain until I come, what difference does that make to you.
local: An nyɔɔbundi pu ní nnabiyaamu kíǹ ki lì yii uboonnoolee úǹ kaa làá ti kpú. Ama Yeesu kaa nìn lī yii uboonnoolee kaa làá kpú. Tin tin u nìn lēe di sá yii: «M yaa là ú nīn bí kí nīn cí m-gítintam, tíǹ ká-si la?».

eng: This is the disciple who testifies concerning these things and who wrote them down. We know that his testimony is true.
local: Uboonnool gbanti di ŋmàn̄ tibɔti nee, ní ti nyí yii tin kɔkɔwoooo u sòon̄ Yeesu puee sá ibaamɔn.

eng: Jesus did many other things as well. If all of them were recorded, I imagine the world itself wouldnt have enough room for the scrolls that would be written.
local: Yeesu gíti túń atuun tikpil. Bi yaa bàà làá ŋmà u-tuuŋŋi ŋin u túńee diba diba, maa pàkā yii duulinya bàà ŋūǹ tigbɔn gbanti kɔkɔ.

eng: Simon Peter told them, Im going fishing. They said, Well go with you. They set out in a boat, but throughout the night they caught nothing.
local: Ní Siimɔn Peetroo bí-bi yii: «M cá dilaantɔl.» Ní bi bí-u yii: «Ti mun làá nóó-si kí cù.» Ní bi búntì ki cūtìi kɔ́ buŋalimbun. Ama, yaadal nyeekuee, baa nìn cúú báà tiba nì tiba.

eng: Early in the morning, Jesus stood on the shore, but the disciples didnt realize it was Jesus.
local: Kutaa bíi wúntée, Yeesu dɔ́miń ki yú dimɔɔgbandi, ama u-boonnooliibi kaa nìn béè yii an sá úǹ di yú.

eng: Jesus called to them, Children, have you caught anything to eat? They answered him, No.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-bi yii: «Binaacimpɔmbee, naa cúú ijɔmaaa?» Ní bi kíí-u yii: «Aayee, taa cúú.».

eng: He said, Cast your net on the right side of the boat and you will find some. So they did, and there were so many fish that they couldnt haul in the net.
local: Ní u bí-bi yii: «Dūmaan kulaaŋŋu kí tɔ̄ buŋalimbu ŋgiil pú, ni làá cúú ijɔm.» Ní bi dū kulaaŋŋu ki dūu tɔ́, ní an cúú ijɔm tikpil, áli gba baa ti kpáàa ŋūǹ kí dáaĺ kí nyāntiń.

eng: Then the disciple whom Jesus loved said to Peter, Its the Lord! When Simon Peter heard it was the Lord, he wrapped his coat around himself and jumped into the water.
local: Niinee ní uboonnool un Yeesu nìn néeńee bí Peetroo yii: «An sá ti-Dindaan!» Siimɔn Peetroo nín gbìl̄ yii an sá ti-Dindaanee, ní u dū u-bɔkutil ki pēeǹ, kun puee u nìn pēetì ní ki jéetì nnyimin ki wáŋkìi dūtì.

eng: The other disciples followed in the boat, dragging the net full of fish, for they werent far from shore, only about one hundred yards.
local: Ní u-boonnooliibi bin kíǹee mun dāa bāań nì buŋalimbu, ki dátiń kulaaŋŋu nì ijɔm, kun puee baa nìn gíti dátiǹ dimɔɔgbandi, an nìn kíǹ fiii kí bà meetab dilaataal.

eng: When they landed, they saw a fire there, with fish on it, and some bread.
local: Bi kpákatì kitiŋee ní ki ká ijɔm pá amɔsiŋkoon pu, nì kpɔnɔ.

eng: There was a Pharisee named Nicodemus, a Jewish leader.
local: Uninja ubɔ nìn bí, bi yíì-u Niikoodeem. U nìn bí Fariisab jandi pú ní ki sá Juuda yab kpiliu ubɔ.

eng: Jesus answered, You are a teacher of Israel and you dont know these things?.
local: Ní Yeesu fātìi kíí-u yii: «Sii un sá udakakpaan ki dàkà Isirayeel yabee di kaa nyí tin bí neeee?.

eng: I assure you that we speak about what we know and testify about what we have seen, but you dont receive our testimony.
local: Ibaamɔn, Ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-si nee, ti sòoǹ nì ni tin ti nyée nì tin ti kée, ní naa là kí fōō tin ti lèe.

eng: If I have told you about earthly things and you dont believe, how will you believe if I tell you about heavenly things?.
local: M sòon̄ nì ni taapu doo bɔti tin báà ŋma nyée, ní naa là kí fōō kí kíí, kin m yaa láa póotì ki bíi sòoǹ yilpu bɔti tin naa wāĺee, ni dāa ŋáań mana kí fōō tíǹ kí kíí?.

eng: No one has gone up to heaven except the one who came down from heaven, the Human One.
local: Báà ubɔ kaa láá pée jɔ́m̀ yilpu, asee man Kinibiki kin nyánní yilpu ki sīntìńee.

eng: Just as Moses lifted up the snake in the wilderness, so must the Human One be lifted up.
local: «Kii Mooyiisi nín nìn lúntì kukoonaŋu ndɔ pu kuteeun binib kpèé ki pɔ́ɔkée, míǹ mbaantiim ní bi làá dāá dū man Kinibiki kí lúntí yilpu.

eng: so that everyone who believes in him will have eternal life.
local: án nín làá ŋá pu un sá kamaa yaa láa fōō-mi ki kííyee ń dāá ká dimaŋfal din kaa cáá dikúntilee.

eng: God so loved the world that he gave his only Son, so that everyone who believes in him wont perish but will have eternal life.
local: Kun puee Unimbɔti néeń kitiŋ pu nib kɔkɔ tikpil, míǹ pu ní u dū u-Jipɔmbaabil ki tīī, án nín làá ŋá pu un sá kamaa yaa fōō-u ki kííyee ń taa kpú, ama ú ká dimaŋfal din kaa cáá dikúntilee.

eng: God didnt send his Son into the world to judge the world, but that the world might be saved through him.
local: Unimbɔti nín túnní u-Jipɔɔn duulinyan doo nee, an kaa sá u là kí kpɔ̀ binib bɔti, ama u là bi-kɔkɔ ń ká difiil u-Jipɔɔn pu ní.

eng: Whoever believes in him isnt judged; whoever doesnt believe in him is already judged, because they dont believe in the name of Gods only Son.
local: Unimbɔti kaa dāa gíti kù bin fōō u-Jipɔɔn gbanti ki kííyee bɔti, ama un kaa fōō-u ki kííyee bɔti kpíì, u nín kaa fōō Unimbɔti Jipɔmbaabil ki kííyee pu.

eng: This is the basis for judgment: The light came into the world, and people loved darkness more than the light, for their actions are evil.
local: Kun cāabìń dibɔkpilee sèé: di sá yii ŋwalifee dɔ́miń duulinyan doo, ní binib là dibɔmbɔndi, kaa là ŋwalifi, kun puee bi tūǹ atuŋkpiti.

eng: He came to Jesus at night and said to him, Rabbi, we know that you are a teacher who has come from God, for no one could do these miraculous signs that you do unless God is with him.
local: Ukpilee nìn dɔ́miń Yeesu cee kunyeeu kubɔ ní ki bí-u yii: «Ukpil, ti nyí yii Unimbɔti di túnní-si á dāan̄ kí dàkà-ti. Di sá yii, Unimbɔti yaa kaa bí unil cee, waa ŋūǹ kí ŋá maamaacib kii bin a ŋáań nee.».

eng: All who do wicked things hate the light and dont come to the light for fear that their actions will be exposed to the light.
local: Un sá kamaa yaa ŋáań ikpitee ná ŋwalifi ní kaa cá ŋwalifin, kun puee u fàŋkí yii ŋwalifi làá bìitī u-tuŋkpitiŋ pu.

eng: Whoever does the truth comes to the light so that it can be seen that their actions were done in God.
local: Ama un nóó ibaamɔnee cá Unimbɔti walifiŋun ní bí ká yii Unimbɔti pu ní u tūǹ atummɔnti.».

eng: After this Jesus and his disciples went into Judea, where he spent some time with them and was baptizing.
local: Míǹ boonee ní Yeesu nì u-boonnooliibi búntì Juudee watil pú ki kāl̀ niin ki fùul̀ binib Unimbɔti nyim.

eng: John was baptizing at Aenon near Salem because there was a lot of water there, and people were coming to him and being baptized. .
local: Yoowaneesi mun nìn bí Eenoon, Saaliim caŋin, ki fùul̀ binib Unimbɔti nyim, kun puee nnyim nìn dɔ̄ niin tikpil. Niin ní binib nìn cá bi fùul̀-bi.

eng: John hadnt yet been thrown into prison.
local: Ŋyunti gbantee, baa láá cúú Yoowaneesi ki pīiǹ kudii.

eng: A debate started between Johns disciples and a certain Jew about cleansing rituals.
local: Niinee ní Yoowaneesi boonnooliibi biba nì Ujuudaja ubɔ bíi kpákà bi-marab bin sá yii bí nīn fíntí bi-ba ki kpáaǹ tiwan kɔkɔee pu.

eng: They came to John and said, Rabbi, look! The man who was with you across the Jordan, the one about whom you testified, is baptizing and everyone is flocking to him.
local: Ní bi cūtì Yoowaneesi cee ki bí-u yii: «Ukpil, sii nì un nìn bí Jɔɔdan gbandi lapu puee, un pu a nìn sòon̄ yii u sá unikpaanee, báà ŋma di yíkí sii cee ki cá u-cee u fùul̀-bi Unimbɔti nyimee.».

eng: John replied, No one can receive anything unless it is given from heaven.
local: Ní Yoowaneesi kíí-bi yii: «Unil kaa ŋūǹ kí nīn cáá tiba Unimbɔti yaa kaa tīī-u kudɔŋu.

eng: You yourselves can testify that I said that Im not the Christ but that Im the one sent before him.
local: Nimbi ni-ba ni gbìl̄ m nín nìn lī yii: <Man di kaa sá Meesiya, bi túnní man ń lìntīń u-nyɔkɔpuee.>.

eng: The groom is the one who is getting married. The friend of the groom stands close by and, when he hears him, is overjoyed at the grooms voice. Therefore, my joy is now complete.
local: Un cáá unimpucaanee, unimpucaan cal dee, ama unimpucaan cal bɔɔ bí niin ki pílifí u-bɔɔ neen, ní an mɔ̄ǹ-u tikpil. An mɔŋŋuee di sá man yɔŋu nee.

eng: Jesus answered, I assure you, unless someone is born anew, its not possible to see Gods kingdom.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-u yii: «Ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-si, Unimbɔti yaa kaa fātìi màl̄ unil mpɔm, u kpá kí ká Unimbɔti Beel.».

eng: He must increase and I must decrease.
local: An máaǹ úǹ nyooti ń nīn wīintí ní man yati ń nīn bàtī!».

eng: The one who comes from above is above all things. The one who is from the earth belongs to the earth and speaks as one from the earth. The one who comes from heaven is above all things.
local: Di sá un nyánní yilpuee di tɔ́kɔ́ tikɔkɔ pu. Un bí taapu dooyee di sá taapu yɔu ní ki sòoǹ taapu doo bɔti. Un nyánní yilpu ki tɔ́kɔ́ tikɔkɔ puee.

eng: He testifies to what he has seen and heard, but no one accepts his testimony.
local: di sòoǹ tin u kée nì tin u gbìl̄ee, ní báà ubɔ kaa fōō u-bɔti ki kíí.

eng: Whoever accepts his testimony confirms that God is true.
local: Un di yaa fōō u nín lī puee ki kííyee, udaan dàkà yii Unimbɔti sá mbaamɔndaan dee.

eng: The one whom God sent speaks Gods words because God gives the Spirit generously.
local: kun puee un Unimbɔti túnnée sòoǹ Unimbɔti bɔti ní. Di sá Unimbɔti tìì-u u-Fam ní an kaa cáá bí dūú ŋmàntī.

eng: The Father loves the Son and gives everything into his hands.
local: Unimbɔti néeń u-Jipɔɔn ki dū tikɔkɔ ki ŋá u-ŋaan.

eng: Whoever believes in the Son has eternal life. Whoever doesnt believe in the Son wont see life, but the angry judgment of God remains on them.
local: Un yaa fōō u-Jipɔɔn gbanti ki kííyee kāntí dimaŋfal din kaa cáá dikúntilee. Un yíì kaa fōō-u ki kííyee kaa kāntí dimaŋfal gbanti. Unimbɔti ŋuul làá nīn pée dàn̄ udaan pu ní.

eng: Nicodemus asked, How is it possible for an adult to be born? Its impossible to enter the mothers womb for a second time and be born, isnt it.
local: Ní Niikoodeem bālifì-u yii: «Mimmee, mana ní bi gíti ŋūǹ kí màal̀ unil un púĺee mpɔm? Unil ŋūǹ kí fàtīí gíti kɔ́ u-na poon bí màal̀-uaaa?».

eng: Jesus answered, I assure you, unless someone is born of water and the Spirit, its not possible to enter Gods kingdom.
local: Ní Yeesu gíti bí-u yii: «Ibaamɔn, ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-si, ubɔ kaa ŋūǹ kí kɔ́ Unimbɔti Beelin bi yaa kaa fātìi màl̄ udaan mpɔm nì nnyim nì Unimbɔti Famin.

eng: Whatever is born of the flesh is flesh, and whatever is born of the Spirit is spirit.
local: Bin binib màal̀ee di sá nnibiyaamu, ní bin Unimbɔti Fam màal̀ee mun sá ŋfam.

eng: Dont be surprised that I said to you, You must be born anew.
local: M nín bí yii: <Ni-kɔkɔ máaǹ Unimbɔti ń fàtīí màal̀-ni mpɔmee>, án taa bàkatīǹ-si.

eng: Gods Spirit blows wherever it wishes. You hear its sound, but you dont know where it comes from or where it is going. Its the same with everyone who is born of the Spirit.
local: Kubuŋu jītí ki cá laakin pú ku yaa lèe, ní a gbìl̀ ku-wooku pu ní kaa nyí laakin pú ku nyáńeeyaaa, kaa nyí laakin pú ku cée mun, míǹ ní an ŋáań bin kɔkɔ Unimbɔti Fam màal̀ee.».

eng: Nicodemus said, How are these things possible.
local: Ní Niikoodeem bí-u yii: «Mana ní an ŋūǹ kí ŋá míǹ?».

eng: Jesus learned that the Pharisees had heard that he was making more disciples and baptizing more than John .
local: Yeesu béè yii Fariisab gbìl̄ yii u kíǹ ki fùul̀ binib tikpil Unimbɔti nyim ki jítiǹ Yoowaneesi ní bi kpántí u-boonnooliibee.

eng: Jesus responded, If you recognized Gods gift and who is saying to you, Give me some water to drink, you would be asking him and he would give you living water.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-u yii: «A yaa bàà nyí Unimbɔti pɔ̄tam nì man un mèé-si nnyimee, sii gba di bàà làá mèè man nnyim, ní man ń bàà tīī-si nnyim min tìì dimaŋfalee.».

eng: The woman said to him, Sir, you dont have a bucket and the well is deep. Where would you get this living water?.
local: Ní unimpuee bí-u yii: «Ukpil, aa cáá guuka, lɔkɔ mun di nyóòyee, kin a làá ŋá mana kí kánní nnyim min tìì dimaŋfalee gbanti?.

eng: You arent greater than our father Jacob, are you? He gave this well to us, and he drank from it himself, as did his sons and his livestock.
local: Ti-naanja Jaakɔb di nìn gbìn̄-ti lɔkɔ nee, ki nyùn̄ u-ba lɔkɔ gbanti nyim, ki kpáaǹ u-biyaamu nì u-waŋkuti. Mimmee, sii dàkafī yii a jítiǹ Jaakɔbaaa?».

eng: Jesus answered, Everyone who drinks this water will be thirsty again.
local: Ní Yeesu fātìi kíí-u yii: «Un sá kamaa yaa nyùn̄ nnyim neeyee, nnyinnyoo làá gíti cúú udaan.

eng: but whoever drinks from the water that I will give will never be thirsty again. The water that I give will become in those who drink it a spring of water that bubbles up into eternal life.
local: Ama un yaa nyùn̄ nnyim min man tììyee, nnyinnyoo kaa ti cútí udaan jaanjaan, kun puee nnyim min man tììyee kpántí nnyimbul u-pɔbilin, ní ki kíǹ ki pùub̀ ki tìì-u dimaŋfal din kaa cáá dikúntilee.».

eng: The woman said to him, Sir, give me this water, so that I will never be thirsty and will never need to come here to draw water.
local: Ní unimpuee bí-u yii: «Ukpil, yaa sée tīī-mi a-nyim gbanti ḿ nyɔ̀, nnyinnyoo ń taa ti cúú-mi, ḿ taa nín̄ gíti dòoń doo ki lúù nnyim.».

eng: Jesus said to her, Go, get your husband, and come back here.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-u yii: «Cùú yíiń a-cal kí fàtīí gítiń doo!».

eng: The woman replied, I dont have a husband. You are right to say, I dont have a husband, Jesus answered.
local: Ní u kíí-u yii: «Maa cáá cal.» Ní Yeesu bí-u yii: «A cáá nsan kí lì yii saa cáá cal.

eng: Youve had five husbands, and the man you are with now isnt your husband. Youve spoken the truth.
local: kun puee a mɔ́ǹ bininjab biŋmoo dee ki yíkì, ní un cee a bí kɔŋkɔnnee mun kaa tí sá a-cal. Míǹ puee, a sòon̄ a-baamɔŋyi ní.».

eng: The woman said, Sir, I see that you are a prophet.
local: Ní unimpuee bí yii: «Ukpil, kɔŋkɔnnee m ká yii a baasii sá Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu ní.

eng: although Jesus disciples were baptizing, not Jesus himself.
local: - ama an kaa nìn sá Yeesu u-ba di nìn fùul̀-bi nnyim, an sá u-boonnooliibi di nìn fùul̀-bi.

eng: Our ancestors worshipped on this mountain, but you and your people say that it is necessary to worship in Jerusalem.
local: Di sá ti-naanjab nìn jáaḿ Unimbɔti dijool nee pu ní, ní nimbi Juuda yab bí yii Jeerusaleem ní bi máaǹ kí cùú jāam̀ ti-Baa Unimbɔti.».

eng: Jesus said to her, Believe me, woman, the time is coming when you and your people will worship the Father neither on this mountain nor in Jerusalem.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-u yii: «Unimpuee, á pīlifiǹ-mi! Ŋyunti ŋubɔ dòoń an kaa dāa gíti bàlifī yii unil ń cù dijool nee puɔɔɔ, Jeerusaleem ní kí jāam̀ Unimbɔti.

eng: You and your people worship what you dont know; we worship what we know because salvation is from the Jews.
local: Nimbi Samaarii yab, naa nyí kun ni jáaḿee, ama timbee ti nyí un ti jáaḿee, kun puee Unimbɔti dū difiil ki dīń Juuda yab pu ní.

eng: But the time is coming—and is here!—when true worshippers will worship in spirit and truth. The Father looks for those who worship him this way.
local: Kɔŋkɔnnee, ŋyunti ŋubɔ dòoń, ŋu bāań gba, ŋun ni ní bin jáaḿ ti-Baa Unimbɔti mmɔntiiminee làá nīn jáaḿ-u nì Ŋfamin nì ibaamɔn. Kun puee bin jáaḿ-u mimmee ní ti-Baa Unimbɔti nyàab̀.

eng: God is spirit, and it is necessary to worship God in spirit and truth.
local: Unimbɔti sá Ŋfam ní: míǹ puee, bin jáaḿ-uee máaǹ kí jāam̀-u nì ŋfamin nì ibaamɔn.».

eng: The woman said, I know that the Messiah is coming, the one who is called the Christ. When he comes, he will teach everything to us.
local: Ní unimpuee bí-u yii: «M nyí yii Meesiya - an dàkà yii Kriisitoo - làá dāan̄. Úǹ yaa láa bāańee, ní u làá tùkù-ti tikɔkɔ.».

eng: Jesus said to her, I Am—the one who speaks with you.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-u yii: «An sá man un bíi sòoǹ nì si nee di sá Kriisitoo.».

eng: Just then, Jesus disciples arrived and were shocked that he was talking with a woman. But no one asked, What do you want? or Why are you talking with her.
local: Niinee ní Yeesu boonnooliibi bāań, ní an ŋá-bi bitii bi nín ká u nì unimpu bíi sòoǹee, ama baa bālifì yii: «A là ba u-cee?» àá «A sòoǹ nì u tilati?».

eng: The woman put down her water jar and went into the city. She said to the people.
local: Niinee ní unimpuee yāntì u-nyimbool ki fātìi nín gítí kitiŋin ki tùkū binib yii.

eng: Come and see a man who has told me everything Ive done! Could this man be the Christ.
local: «Dāá kpèèmaan uninja ubɔ bīitì m-bɔɔŋŋi pu! An kaa kalin sá Meesiya?».

eng: Therefore, he left Judea and went back to Galilee.
local: Ní u yíkì Juudee ki nín gítí Galiilee.

eng: They left the city and were on their way to see Jesus.
local: Ní bi yíkìń kitiŋin ki nín dòoń Yeesu cee.

eng: In the meantime the disciples spoke to Jesus, saying, Rabbi, eat.
local: Unimpuee búntèe, ní u-boonnooliibi bíi lì yii: «Ukpil, dāá jī tijin!».

eng: Jesus said to them, I have food to eat that you dont know about.
local: Ní u bí-bi yii: «Man cáá tijin tin nimbi kaa nyée.».

eng: The disciples asked each other, Has someone brought him food.
local: Niinee ní u-boonnooliibi kíǹ ki bíi bàlifī tɔb yii: «Ubɔ kalin cáań-u tijin níii?».

eng: Jesus said to them, I am fed by doing the will of the one who sent me and by completing his work.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-bi yii: «M-jinti di sá ḿ ŋá un túnní-mee nín là puee, kí tɔ̄ u-tundi.

eng: Dont you have a saying, Four more months and then its time for harvest? Look, I tell you: open your eyes and notice that the fields are already ripe for the harvest.
local: Kéè, ni cáá yii an kíǹ iŋmal inaa dee, kudiceeuaa! Ama man tùkù-ni yii ní wáaǹ kí kpèè, tijin kūń ki ŋmāntì dical.

eng: Those who harvest are receiving their pay and gathering fruit for eternal life so that those who sow and those who harvest can celebrate together.
local: Kii un cēēyee nín kōontí ki kpáfí ki fòol̄ u-paati puee, míǹ mbaantiim ni mun cáaḿmaan binib m-cee ḿ tīī-bi dimaŋfal din kaa cáá dikúntilee. Ní án mɔ̄ɔkiǹ un búĺee nì un cáńee kɔkɔ.

eng: This is a true saying, that one sows and another harvests.
local: Kun puee dilaŋkpandi din lì yii: <Ubɔ būul̀ ní ubɔ ń dāá cēe> sá tiŋman ní.

eng: I have sent you to harvest what you didnt work hard for; others worked hard, and you will share in their hard work.
local: Míǹ puee, m tú-ni ní cùú cā laakin biba búĺee ní, nimbi ń páatí bíǹ gìńdi pu.».

eng: Many Samaritans in that city believed in Jesus because of the womans word when she testified, He told me everything Ive ever done.
local: Unimpuee nín tùkū-bi yii Yeesu bīitì u-bɔɔŋŋi puee, ní Samaarii yab bin kóò kitiŋ gbantinee tikpil fōō Yeesu ki kíí.

eng: Jesus had to go through Samaria.
local: Kí wàatī kí nín bà Galiileeyee, see ú dī Samaarii watil pú.

eng: So when the Samaritans came to Jesus, they asked him to stay with them, and he stayed there two days.
local: Samaarii yab gbanti nín dāa bāań ki ká-uee, ní bi mēē-u yii ú kàl̄ bi-cee, ní u lafun kāl̀ iwiin ili.

eng: Many more believed because of his word.
local: Binib tikpil nìn gíti fōō-u ki kíí tin u nìn sòon̄ nì bee pu.

eng: and they said to the woman, We no longer believe because of what you said, for we have heard for ourselves and know that this one is truly the savior of the world.
local: Ní binibee lī unimpuee yii: «Ti nín fōō ki kíí nee, an kaa ti sá sii nín lī puee ŋmaniŋma pu. U sòon̄ ti mun ti-ba gbìl̄ ki béè yii, ibaamɔn, u sá duulinya kɔkɔ Fiiliu.».

eng: After two days Jesus left for Galilee. .
local: Iwiin ili gbanti boonee, Yeesu yíkì niin ki nín cá Galiilee.

eng: Jesus himself had testified that prophets have no honor in their own country.
local: Kun puee úǹ u-ba nìn lī yii: «Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu dumpu yab kaa tɔ́-u.».

eng: When he came to Galilee, the Galileans welcomed him because they had seen all the things he had done in Jerusalem during the festival, for they also had been at the festival.
local: U nín bàn̄ Galiileeyee, niin yab fōō-u tiŋan, kun puee bi nìn cūtì Jeerusaleem ki ká u nín nìn ŋá tin kɔkɔ jiŋaalinee.

eng: He returned to Cana in Galilee where he had turned the water into wine. In Capernaum there was a certain royal official whose son was sick.
local: Ní u fātìi gítì Kana, Galiilee timbiliiki, laakin u nìn dū nnyim ki kpántì ndaamee. Gɔmina kàlinjakpaaŋwu ubɔ nìn bí Kapeernayum u-jipɔɔn būǹ.

eng: When he heard that Jesus was coming from Judea to Galilee, he went out to meet him and asked Jesus if he would come and heal his son, for his son was about to die.
local: U nín gbìl̄ yii Yeesu nyánní Juudee ki dɔ́miń Galiileeyee, ní u dɔ́miń u-cee ki gbāam̀-u ú cù Kapeernayum kí cáaǹ u-jipɔɔn un būǹ ki làá kpúee.

eng: Jesus said to him, Unless you see miraculous signs and wonders, you wont believe.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-u yii: «Ni yaa kaa ká maamaacib nì tidaandi, naa fòol̄ kí kíí!».

eng: The royal official said to him, Lord, come before my son dies.
local: Ní ukàlinjakpaanee bí-u yii: «Ukpil, dāan̄ tí cù malaa, m-biki ń taa kpú!».

eng: He came to a Samaritan city called Sychar, which was near the land Jacob had given to his son Joseph.
local: Ní u dūu bàn̄ Samaarii tiŋki kin bi yíì Siikaar, kì pìlīǹ Jaakɔb saaku kun u nìn tīī u-jipɔɔn Jooseefee.

eng: Jesus replied, Go home. Your son lives. The man believed the word that Jesus spoke to him and set out for his home.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-u yii: «Nín̄ cá, a-jipɔɔn pɔ́ɔkì.» Uninjee fōō Yeesu nín lī puee ní ki nín cá.

eng: While he was on his way, his servants were already coming to meet him. They said, Your son lives.
local: U bí nsanni ki fàtīi kùǹee, ní u-tɔntɔnliibi biba túŋkìń-u ki tùkū-u yii: «A-jipɔɔn pɔ́ɔkì.».

eng: So he asked them at what time his son had started to get better. And they said, The fever left him yesterday at about one oclock in the afternoon.
local: Ní u bālifì-bi yii: «Ŋyunti ŋulɔŋu ní an yāntì-u?» Ní bi kíí-u yii: «Fool, wiŋkpooti kukúluu kubɔ, ní an cāntì-u, u-wunti kaa ti tùm̀.».

eng: Then the father realized that this was the hour when Jesus had said to him, Your son lives. And he and his entire household believed in Jesus.
local: Niinee ní u béè yii kukúluu gbanti deedee ní Yeesu tùkū-u yii: «A-jipɔɔn pɔ́ɔkì». Ní u nì u-dicaŋku kɔkɔ fōō Yeesu ki kíí.

eng: This was the second miraculous sign Jesus did while going from Judea to Galilee.
local: Yeesu nín yíkì Juudee ki fātì Galiileeyee, maamaacililiiti un u ŋée dee.

eng: Jacobs well was there. Jesus was tired from his journey, so he sat down at the well. It was about noon.
local: Niin ní Jaakɔb lɔkɔu bí. Yeesu cōom̀ ki gīīyee, ki kāl̀ lɔkɔee nyɔɔgbandi pu. An nìn ŋmāntì awinsaan.

eng: A Samaritan woman came to the well to draw water. Jesus said to her, Give me some water to drink.
local: U kēe ní Usamaariinimpu ubɔ dɔ́miń kí lúń nnyim, ní Yeesu bí-u yii: «Pɔ̄-mi nnyim ḿ nyɔ̀.».

eng: His disciples had gone into the city to buy him some food.
local: - Di sá yii u-boonnooliibi nìn búntì kitiŋin kí nyàabìń tijin.

eng: The Samaritan woman asked, Why do you, a Jewish man, ask for something to drink from me, a Samaritan woman.
local: Ní unimpuee kíí-u yii: «Sii sá Ujuudaja ní, ba pu ní a mèé man un sá Usamaariinimpuee nnyim?» - Di sá yii Juuda yab nì Samaarii yab kaa nìn kpí.

eng: After this there was a Jewish festival, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
local: Boonee ní Yeesu nín jɔ́ḿ Jeerusaleem kun puee Juuda yab nìn cáá jiŋaal ubɔ.

eng: The Jewish leaders said to the man who had been healed, Its the Sabbath; you arent allowed to carry your mat.
local: Míǹ pu ní Juuda yab kpilib nìn bí uninja un ká dicáaǹdee yii: «Díǹ di sá ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa dal. Aa cáá nsan kí bùkun̄ a-kampeeku.».

eng: He answered, The man who made me well said to me, Pick up your mat and walk.
local: Ní u kíí-bi yii: «Un cáaǹ-mee di bí-mi yii ḿ yòon̄ m-kampeeku kí nīn còom̄.».

eng: They inquired, Who is this man who said to you, Pick it up and walk.
local: Ní bi bālifì-u yii: «Ŋma di bí-si yii á yòon̄ a-kampeeku kí nīn còom̄aaa?».

eng: The man who had been cured didnt know who it was, because Jesus had slipped away from the crowd gathered there.
local: Uninjee kaa nìn nyí un di cáaǹ-uee, di sá yii Yeesu nìn nyáń kunipaau kansikin ki búntì.

eng: Later Jesus found him in the temple and said, See! You have been made well. Dont sin anymore in case something worse happens to you.
local: Boonee ní Yeesu dāa ká uninjee kunimbɔtidiin ní ki bí-u yii: «A nín pɔ́ɔkì pu nee, á taa ti ŋá ikpiti, án nín làá ŋá pu tiba ń taa láa gíti bàń-si kí jītíǹ tin bí neeyee!».

eng: The man went and proclaimed to the Jewish leaders that Jesus was the man who had made him well.
local: Niinee, uninjee cūtìi tùkū Juuda yab kpilib yii an sá Yeesu di cáaǹ-u.

eng: As a result, the Jewish leaders were harassing Jesus, since he had done these things on the Sabbath.
local: Míǹ pu ní Juuda yab nìn nyáń Yeesu pu u nín ŋá míǹ ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa dalee pu.

eng: Jesus replied, My Father is still working, and I am working too.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-bi yii: «M-Baa tūǹ ditundi baabadal, míǹ ní ma mun tūǹ.».

eng: For this reason the Jewish leaders wanted even more to kill him—not only because he was doing away with the Sabbath but also because he called God his own Father, thereby making himself equal with God.
local: Tibɔti tin u lēe puee, Juuda yab kpilib mɔ̄tìi bíi nyàab̀ kí kpɔ̀-u. An kaa sá u nín yīntì mara un sá bí taa tɔ̄ ditundi ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaayee ŋmaniŋma pu, an sá u nín lī yii Unimbɔti di sá u-Baayee, ki cáá u-ba ki ŋmàntīǹ Unimbɔtee pu.

eng: Jesus responded to the Jewish leaders, I assure you that the Son cant do anything by himself except what he sees the Father doing. Whatever the Father does, the Son does likewise.
local: Niinee ní Yeesu bí-bi yii: «Ibaamɔn, ibaamɔn ní m sòoǹ nì ni, tin Ubaati ŋáańee ní u-Jipɔɔn kāntí ki ŋáań. An yaa sá fám, waa ŋūǹ kí ŋá tiba u-ba pu. Tin kɔkɔ Ubaati ŋáańee, u-Jipɔɔn mun ŋáań míǹ mbaantiim ní.

eng: In Jerusalem near the Sheep Gate in the north city wall is a pool with the Aramaic name Bethsaida. It had five covered porches.
local: Dinyimbɔbil diba nìn bí Jeerusaleem, kadantikub biŋmoo māntìi tú-dì, bi yíì-dì eebru sooŋyin yii Beeteesda, di bí ki nákáǹ kipunyɔkɔ kin bi yíì ipii punyɔkɔkee.

eng: The Father loves the Son and shows him everything that he does. He will show him greater works than these so that you will marvel.
local: Kun puee Ubaati néeń u-Jipɔɔn, ní ki dàkà-u tin kɔkɔwoooo u ŋáańee. U làá dāá gíti dàkà-u tin jítiǹ tin bí neeyee gba, án làá ŋá-ni maamaaci.

eng: As the Father raises the dead and gives life, so too does the Son give life to whomever he wishes.
local: Kii Ubaati nín fìkitī bitaŋkpiibi ki tìì-bi dimaŋfal puee, míǹ mbaantiim ní u-Jipɔɔn mun tìì un un u yaa lèe dimaŋfal.

eng: The Father doesnt judge anyone, but he has given all judgment to the Son.
local: Ubaati kaa sòoǹ báà ubɔ bɔti. U dū ibɔsoon kɔkɔ ki ŋá u-Jipɔɔn ŋaan ní.

eng: so that everyone will honor the Son just as they honor the Father. Whoever doesnt honor the Son doesnt honor the Father who sent him.
local: án nín làá ŋá pu binib kɔkɔ ń nīn tìì-u jilma kii bi nín tìì Ubaati puee. Un kaa tīī Ubaati Jipɔɔn jilmee kaa tīī Ubaati un túnní-uee mun jilma dee.

eng: I assure you that whoever hears my word and believes in the one who sent me has eternal life and wont come under judgment but has passed from death into life.
local: «Ibaamɔn, ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-ni, un yaa pílifì m-bɔti ki fōō un túnní-mee ki kííyee kāntí dimaŋfal din kaa cáá dikúntilee. Udaan kaa dāa gíti bí ibɔsoon ni, u nyáń ŋkúmin ki póotì dimaŋfalin.

eng: I assure you that the time is coming—and is here!—when the dead will hear the voice of Gods Son, and those who hear it will live.
local: Ibaamɔn, ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-ni, ŋyunti ŋubɔ dòoń, ŋu bāań gba, ŋun ni bitaŋkpiibi làá gbìl̄ Unimbɔti Jipɔɔn neenee, ní bin yaa láa gbìl̄ u-neen gbantee dāa kāntí dimaŋfal.

eng: Just as the Father has life in himself, so he has granted the Son to have life in himself.
local: Ubaati nín cáá mpɔn kí tīī dimaŋfal puee, míǹ ní u tīī u-Jipɔɔn mpɔn u mun ń nīn tìì dimaŋfal.

eng: He gives the Son authority to judge, because he is the Human One.
local: ní ki tīī-u mpɔn ú nīn sá ubɔsoonli kun puee u sá Kinibiki.

eng: Dont be surprised by this, because the time is coming when all who are in their graves will hear his voice.
local: Tin bí nee kɔkɔ ń taa bàkatīǹ-ni, kun puee ŋyunti ŋubɔ dòoń ní bin kɔkɔ dɔ̄ akaakul poonee làá dāá gbìl̄ u-neen pu.

eng: Those who did good things will come out into the resurrection of life, and those who did wicked things into the resurrection of judgment.
local: kí nyānní. Bin yaa ŋá tiŋanee ń fìkitī kí ká dimaŋfal, ní bin yaa ŋá ikpitee ń fìkitī kí kɔ́ ibɔsoon ni.

eng: and a crowd of people who were sick, blind, lame, and paralyzed sat there.
local: Bijɔfub nì bideembi nì bifaliŋfaambi nì bibunliibi tikpil nìn dɔ̄ kadantikub taapu. Bi nìn cí ŋyunti ŋun Unimbɔti tuuŋŋu làá dāan̄ kí gbàtī nnyimee.

eng: I cant do anything by myself. Whatever I hear, I judge, and my judgment is just. I dont seek my own will but the will of the one who sent me.
local: Maa ŋūǹ kí ŋá tiba m-ba. M-bɔsooŋyi nóó nì un túnní-mee nín tùkù-mi puee ní. M sòoǹ tibɔti deedee kun puee maa nyàab̀ kí ŋá min man m-ba lèe, see un túnní-mee nín là puee.

eng: If I testify about myself, my testimony isnt true.
local: M yaa sòon̄ tiba m-ba pu, an kaa ŋūǹ kí dàkà ibaamɔn.

eng: There is someone else who testifies about me, and I know his testimony about me is true.
local: ama ubɔ di lèekī m-pu seera, ní m nyí yii tin u sòoǹ m-puee sá ibaamɔn.

eng: You sent a delegation to John, and he testified to the truth.
local: Ni nìn tú binib Yoowaneesi cee ní u sòon̄-ni ibaamɔn m-pu.

eng: Although I dont accept human testimony, I say these things so that you can be saved.
local: Kun pu m lì mimmee di sá m là ní ká difiil, an yaa sá fám, maa nyàab̀ ubɔ ń lēē m-pu seera.

eng: John was a burning and shining lamp, and, at least for a while, you were willing to celebrate in his light.
local: Yoowaneesi nìn sá fitila ní ki ŋmíntí nyáka nyaka, ní ni nìn kíí ki nóó u-walifiŋu gbanti ŋyunti ŋubɔ poon, ní an mɔ̄ǹ-ni.

eng: I have a witness greater than Johns testimony. The Father has given me works to do so that I might complete them. These works I do testify about me that the Father sent me.
local: Ama m cáá kun lèekī m-pu seera ki jítiǹ Yoowaneesee: di sá yii atuun ŋin m-Baa tīī-mi ḿ tɔ̄ m bíi tūnnèe sòoǹ ki dàkà m-pu yii m-Baa di lafun túnní-mi.

eng: And the Father who sent me testifies about me. You have never even heard his voice or seen his form.
local: Ní m-Baa un túnní-mee mun u-ba lèekī seera m-pu. Naa láá pée gbìl̄ u-neen pu dalba, kaa láá ká u-nimbiin dalba.

eng: and you dont have his word dwelling with you because you dont believe the one whom he has sent.
local: kaa kpáafí u-bɔti mun ni-ni, kun puee naa fōō un u túnnée ki kíí.

eng: Examine the scriptures, since you think that in them you have eternal life. They also testify about me.
local: Ni bífí Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon ki dàkafī yii niin ní ni ŋūǹ kí ká dimaŋfal din kaa cáá dikúntilee. An sá man pu ní kugbɔŋuee sòoǹ.

eng: yet you dont want to come to me so that you can have life.
local: ama naa là kí dāan̄ man cee kí ká dimaŋfal.

eng: I dont accept praise from people.
local: «Man kaa nyàab̀ binib pampakaku.

eng: but I know you, that you dont have Gods love in you.
local: di sá yii m nyí-ni, naa cáá Unimbɔti neendi ni-ni.

eng: I have come in my Fathers name, and you dont receive me. If others come in their own name, you receive them.
local: M-Baa di túnní-mi, ní naa là kí fōō-mi, ama bin dòoń bi-ba puee, ni fòol̄ bíǹ.

eng: How can you believe when you receive praise from each other but dont seek the praise that comes from the only God?.
local: Mana ní ni ŋūǹ kí fōō kí kíí, nimbi bin néeń ni-jutiibi ń nīn pàkà-nee, kaa nyàab̀ kupampakau kun Unimbɔtibaanti tììyee?.

eng: Dont think that I will accuse you before the Father. Your accuser is Moses, the one in whom your hope rests.
local: Ní taa dàkafì yii man làá dāá bàlifì-ni tibɔti m-Baa nimbiin. An sá Mooyiisi di làá dāá bàlifì-ni tibɔti, úǹ un pu ni dū ni-mákal ki ŋée.

eng: If you believed Moses, you would believe me, because Moses wrote about me.
local: Ni yaa bàà fōō Mooyiisi ki kííyee, ni bàà tí fòol̄ ma mun ní kí kíí, di sá yii man bɔti ní Mooyiisi nìn ŋmàn̄.

eng: If you dont believe the writings of Moses, how will you believe my words.
local: Ni nín kaa fōō tin Mooyiisi nìn ŋmàn̄ee ki kííyee, ni làá ŋá mana kí fōō man bɔti kí kíí?».

eng: A certain man was there who had been sick for thirty-eight years.
local: Uninja ubɔ nìn bí niin ki būǹ doooo abin ali kpá imuŋku ili.

eng: When Jesus saw him lying there, knowing that he had already been there a long time, he asked him, Do you want to get well.
local: Yeesu ká u dɔ̄ee, ní ki béè yii u būǹee an yúntì, ní u bālifì-u yii: «A là kí pɔ̄ɔkàaa?».

eng: The sick man answered him, Sir, I dont have anyone who can put me in the water when it is stirred up. When Im trying to get to it, someone else has gotten in ahead of me.
local: Ní ubunlee kíí-u yii: «Ukpil, bi yaa gbātì nnyimee, maa cáá ubɔ ń yòon̄-mi kí tɔ̄ niin. Ŋyunti ŋun m bíi pɔ̄ɔntí ki cée ní ubɔ ń péeǹ-mi kí cùú jēetí.».

eng: Jesus said to him, Get up! Pick up your mat and walk.
local: Niinee ní Yeesu bí-u yii: «Yīkìí yūĺ kí yòon̄ a-kampeeku kí còom̀!».

eng: Immediately the man was well, and he picked up his mat and walked. Now that day was the Sabbath.
local: Kpalaa niinee ní uninjee pɔ́ɔkì ki yōoǹ u-kampeeku ki kíǹ ki bíi còom̄. An nìn sá ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa dal ní.

eng: After this Jesus went across the Galilee Sea .
local: Míǹ boonee ní Yeesu dūtì Galiilee mɔɔgbandi lapu pu - bi gíti yíì dimɔɔlee yii Tiibeeriyaadi mɔɔl.

eng: Jesus said, Have the people sit down. There was plenty of grass there. They sat down, about five thousand of them.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-bi yii: «Yàntīmaan binib ń kàl̄!» Kumɔsɔŋu kubɔ nìn bí niin tikpil, ní bi kāl̀ ku-pu. Bi nìn bàn̄ kii kutukub biŋmoo nyɔkɔm.

eng: Then Jesus took the bread. When he had given thanks, he distributed it to those who were sitting there. He did the same with the fish, each getting as much as they wanted.
local: Ní Yeesu yōoǹ kpɔnɔbee ki jáam̀ Unimbɔti an pu ki wāatì ní ki dūu yākatì-bi, ní ki tí yākatì ijɔm mun, min báà ŋma lèe.

eng: When they had plenty to eat, he said to his disciples, Gather up the leftover pieces, so that nothing will be wasted.
local: Bi jíń ki díkée, ní u bí u-boonnooliibi yii: «Tòòmaan akati ŋin kíǹee, tiba ń taa bīil̀!».

eng: So they gathered them and filled twelve baskets with the pieces of the five barley loaves that had been left over by those who had eaten.
local: Bi kóoǹ kpɔnɔb biŋmooyee katiŋi ŋin bi nìn ŋmɔ́ ki kíǹee, ki gbéeǹ tikabiti saalaa nì tili.

eng: When the people saw that he had done a miraculous sign, they said, This is truly the prophet who is coming into the world.
local: Yeesu ŋá maamaaci gbanti samaa kée, ní bi bí yii: «Tilafuŋman, kijapaai nee di sá Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu un máaǹ kí dāan̄ duulinyanee.».

eng: Jesus understood that they were about to come and force him to be their king, so he took refuge again, alone on a mountain.
local: Yeesu béè yii bi làá cúú-u mpɔn kí kàan̄ dibeelee, ní u fātìi tí nín jɔ́ḿ digbantandi pu u-baba.

eng: When evening came, Jesus disciples went down to the lake.
local: Kujoou jóotèe ní Yeesu boonnooliibi nín sìntī dimɔɔlin.

eng: They got into a boat and were crossing the lake to Capernaum. It was already getting dark and Jesus hadnt come to them yet.
local: ki kɔ́ buŋalimbu ki fātìi nín cá Kapeernayum watil. Kunyeeu mun nìn mɔ́ Yeesu kaa láá bāań bi-cee.

eng: The water was getting rough because a strong wind was blowing.
local: Kubuŋkpaaŋŋu kubɔ kíĺ ki bíi jītí ki yòontí tinyiŋgban.

eng: When the wind had driven them out for about three or four miles, they saw Jesus walking on the water. He was approaching the boat and they were afraid.
local: Bi nín ŋàal̄ ki bàn̄ ataŋkpee aŋmoo kii aluu nyɔkɔmee, ní ki ká Yeesu nín tɔ́kɔń nnyim pu ki còomìń ki dòoń buŋalimbu ceeyee, ní bi fāŋkì.

eng: A large crowd followed him, because they had seen the miraculous signs he had done among the sick.
local: Kunipaau nín kántì u ŋáaǹ maamaacib bin ki cáaǹ bibunliibee puee ní bi nìn nóó-u.

eng: He said to them, I Am. Dont be afraid.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-bi yii: «Taa fàŋkìmaan, an sá man!».

eng: Then they wanted to take him into the boat, and just then the boat reached the land where they had been heading.
local: Niinee ní bi bí yii bí kpáaǹ-u, kpalaayee, buŋalimbu mun pūtì dūu sāā dimɔɔgbandi laakin bi cée.

eng: The next day the crowd that remained on the other side of the lake realized that only one boat had been there. They knew Jesus hadnt gone with his disciples, but that the disciples had gone alone.
local: Kutaa wúntèe, kunipaau gbíntìi kā dimɔɔgbandi lapu pu. Bi nìn ká yii buŋalimbaantiibu di yú niin, ní Yeesu kaa kɔ́ buŋalimbu gbanti, u-boonnooliibi ŋmaniŋma di kɔ́ ki búntì.

eng: Some boats came from Tiberias, near the place where they had eaten the bread over which the Lord had given thanks.
local: Ama iŋaliŋfi iba nìn nyánní Tiibeeriyaadi tiŋkin ki bāań laakin ti-Dindaan nìn mēē ki pàkā Unimbɔti ki wāatì ní bi nín ŋmɔ́ kpɔnɔbee caŋin.

eng: When the crowd saw that neither Jesus nor his disciples were there, they got into the boats and came to Capernaum looking for Jesus.
local: Kunipaauee nín kaa ká Yeesu kaa ká u-boonnooliibi muee ní bi kɔ́ iŋaliŋfee ki búntì Kapeernayum kí nyàab̀ Yeesu.

eng: When they found him on the other side of the lake, they asked him, Rabbi, when did you get here.
local: Bi bàn̄ niin ki ká-uee, ní ki bí yii: «Ukpil, ŋyunti ŋulɔŋu ní a bāań doo ní?».

eng: Jesus replied, I assure you that you are looking for me not because you saw miraculous signs but because you ate all the food you wanted.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-bi yii: «Ibaamɔn, ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-ni, ni nín ŋmɔ́ kpɔnɔb ki díkée pu ní ni nyàab̀-mi, kaa sá maamaacib bin dàkā-ni m nín sá uŋyee pu.

eng: Dont work for the food that doesnt last but for the food that endures for eternal life, which the Human One will give you. God the Father has confirmed him as his agent to give life.
local: Ní taa nín̄ tūǹ ditundi tijin tin kaa yúntée pu, nín̄ tūm̀maan tijin tin yúntí ki tìì dimaŋfal din kaa cáá dikúntilee pu. Tijin gbantee, man Kinibiki di làá tīī-ni tíǹ, di sá yii man ní m-Baa Unimbɔti tīī an pɔŋŋu daaŋku.».

eng: They asked, What must we do in order to accomplish what God requires.
local: Niinee ní bi bālifì-u yii: «Ti máaǹ kí ŋá mana ní án nīn sá min Unimbɔti lèe ní?».

eng: Jesus replied, This is what God requires, that you believe in him whom God sent.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-bi yii: «Unimbɔti nín là miŋyee di sá ní fōō man un u túnnée kí kíí.».

eng: Jesus went up a mountain and sat there with his disciples.
local: Ní u nì u-boonnooliibi jɔ́m̀ digbantandi diba pu ki kāl̀.

eng: They asked, What miraculous sign will you do, that we can see and believe you? What will you do?.
local: Ní bi bí-u yii: «Maamaaci ulɔu ní a làá dàkà-ti kí wàatī ní tí nín fōō-si kí kíí ní? Àá kudaaŋŋu kulɔkɔ ní a làá ŋá tí ká?.

eng: Our ancestors ate manna in the wilderness, just as it is written, He gave them bread from heaven to eat .
local: Ti-naanjab nìn jīǹ tijin tin bi yíì maanee kuteeun, kii an nín ŋmàn̄ Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon puee, yii: <U nìn tìì-bi tijin tin nyēeń yilpuee ní bi jīǹ.>».

eng: Jesus told them, I assure you, it wasnt Moses who gave the bread from heaven to you, but my Father gives you the true bread from heaven.
local: Niinee ní Yeesu kíí-bi yii: «Ibaamɔn, ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-ni, an kaa sá Mooyiisi di tīī-ni tijin tin nyēeń yilpuee, m-Baa di tìì tijimmɔntiil tin nyēeń yilpuee.

eng: The bread of God is the one who comes down from heaven and gives life to the world.
local: Tijin tin Unimbɔti tììyee di sá un nyánní yilpu ki tìì binib kɔkɔ dimaŋfalee.».

eng: They said, Sir, give us this bread all the time.
local: Ní bi bí-u yii: «Ukpil, nín̄ pée tìì-ti tijinee baabadal!».

eng: Jesus replied, I am the bread of life. Whoever comes to me will never go hungry, and whoever believes in me will never be thirsty.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-bi yii: «Man di sá tijin tin tìì dimaŋfalee. Un yaa dɔ́miń m-ceeyee, ŋkùm kaa ti cútí udaan jaanjaan, míǹ ní un yaa tí fōō-mi ki kííyee, nnyinnyoo kaa ti cútí udaan jaanjaan.

eng: But I told you that you have seen me and still dont believe.
local: Ama, m pée tùkū-ni: ni-nimbiliŋ ká tin m ŋée ní naa fōō-mi ki kíí.

eng: Everyone whom the Father gives to me will come to me, and I wont send away anyone who comes to me.
local: Bin bin m-Baa tīī-mee kɔkɔ làá dāan̄ m-cee. Un di yaa dɔ́miń m-ceeyee, maa làá fá udaan.

eng: I have come down from heaven not to do my will, but the will of him who sent me.
local: kun puee m nyánní yilpu ki dɔ́miń kí ŋá un túnní-mee nín là puee ní, an kaa sá man m-ba nín là puee.

eng: This is the will of the one who sent me, that I wont lose anything he has given me, but I will raise it up at the last day.
local: Un túnní-mee nín là puee di sá ḿ taa fá bin u tīī-mee kansikin báà ubɔ kí dāá fìkitī bi-kɔkɔ ŋwinjoolkaa dal.

eng: It was nearly time for Passover, the Jewish festival.
local: Juuda yab jiŋaaliu un bi yíì Dilákatil jiŋaaliuee nìn tóobiń.

eng: This is my Fathers will: that all who see the Son and believe in him will have eternal life, and I will raise them up at the last day.
local: M-Baa nín là min miŋyee sèé, yii un sá kamaa yaa ká man u-Jipɔɔn ki fōō-mi ki kííyee ń ká dimaŋfal din kaa cáá dikúntilee, ní man ń dāá fìkitī-u ŋwinjoolkaa dal.».

eng: The Jewish opposition grumbled about him because he said, I am the bread that came down from heaven.
local: Yeesu nín lī yii úǹ di sá tijin tin nyánní yilpuee pu ní Juuda yab bíi gbùkum̄ u-pu.

eng: They asked, Isnt this Jesus, Josephs son, whose mother and father we know? How can he now say, I have come down from heaven.
local: ki lì yii: «An kaa sá Yeesu, Jooseefi jipɔɔn un timbi nyí u-baa nì u-neeee? Kin an ŋá mana ní u gíti lì yii úǹ di nyánní yilpu ní?».

eng: Jesus responded, Dont grumble among yourselves.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-bi yii: «Ní taa nín̄ gbùkum̄ ni-ŋmaniŋma míǹ!.

eng: No one can come to me unless they are drawn to me by the Father who sent me, and I will raise them up at the last day.
local: Ubɔ kaa ŋūǹ kí dāan̄ m-cee an yaa kaa sá m-Baa un túnní-mee di cáań udaan, kí wàatī ní man ń nín fìkitī-u ŋwinjoolkaa dal.

eng: It is written in the Prophets, And they will all be taught by God. Everyone who has listened to the Father and learned from him comes to me.
local: Kii Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi nín ŋmàn̄ puee yii: <Unimbɔti làá dàkà bi-kɔkɔ bí béé-u.> Mimmee, bin kɔkɔ yaa pílifì m-Baa ki fōō u nín dàkà puee dòoń man cee ní.

eng: No one has seen the Father except the one who is from God. He has seen the Father.
local: Báà ubɔ kaa ká m-Baa Unimbɔti, asee man un nyánní u-ceeyee, man baba di ká-u.

eng: I assure you, whoever believes has eternal life.
local: «Ibaamɔn, ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-ni, un yaa fōō-mi ki kííyee cáá dimaŋfal din kaa cáá dikúntilee.

eng: I am the bread of life.
local: Man di sá tijin tin tìì dimaŋfalee.

eng: Your ancestors ate manna in the wilderness and they died.
local: Nì min kɔkɔ ni-naanjab nìn ŋmɔ́ maana kuteeunee, boonee bi dāa kpíì.

eng: Jesus looked up and saw the large crowd coming toward him. He asked Philip, Where will we buy food to feed these people.
local: Yeesu kpēēyee, kunipaau di dòoń u-ceeyee, ní u bí Fiiliipi yii: «La ní ti làá ká tijin kí dā kí tīī bin kɔkɔ bí nee ń jī dee?».

eng: This is the bread that comes down from heaven so that whoever eats from it will never die.
local: Ama tijin tin nyánní yilpuee, un yaa jíń tintee kaa ti kúù jaanjaan.

eng: I am the living bread that came down from heaven. Whoever eats this bread will live forever, and the bread that I will give for the life of the world is my flesh.
local: Man di sá tijin tin nyánní yilpu ki cáá dimaŋfalee. Un yaa jíń tijin gbantee làá nīn bí kí nīn cá jaanjaan. Tijin tin m làá tīī binib kɔkɔ ń nīn cáá dimaŋfalee di sá m-wunti.».

eng: Then the Jews debated among themselves, asking, How can this man give us his flesh to eat.
local: Niinee ní Juuda yab bíi kpákà bi-ŋmaniŋma tikpil yii: «Nì mana ní u ŋūǹ kí tīī-ti u-wunti tí ŋmɔ́ míǹ?».

eng: Jesus said to them, I assure you, unless you eat the flesh of the Human One and drink his blood, you have no life in you.
local: Ní Yeesu fātìi bí-bi yii: «Ibaamɔn, ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-ni, ni yaa kaa ŋmɔ́ man Kinibiki wunti, kaa nyùn̄ m-fatikuu muee, naa làá ká dimaŋfal.

eng: Whoever eats my flesh and drinks my blood has eternal life, and I will raise them up at the last day.
local: Ama sii un yaa ŋmɔ́ m-wunti ki nyùn̄ m-fatikuuee ká dimaŋfal din kaa cáá dikúntilee, ní ḿ dāá fìkitī-si ŋwinjoolkaa dal.

eng: My flesh is true food and my blood is true drink.
local: Kun puee m-wunti di sá tijimmɔntiil ní m-fatikuu mun sá tinyɔkaal tin sá timɔntiilee.

eng: Whoever eats my flesh and drinks my blood remains in me and I in them.
local: Un yaa ŋmɔ́ m-wunti ki nyùn̄ m-fatikuuee bí m-ni ní ma mun bí udaan ni.

eng: As the living Father sent me, and I live because of the Father, so whoever eats me lives because of me.
local: M-Baa un túnní-mee nín cáá dimaŋfal puee ní u-puee ma mun cáá dimaŋfal. Míǹ ní un yaa fōō-mi ki jíńee mun kāntí dimaŋfal man pu.

eng: This is the bread that came down from heaven. It isnt like the bread your ancestors ate, and then they died. Whoever eats this bread will live forever.
local: Tijin tin nyánní yilpuee nín bí puee dee. Tíǹ kaa bí kii tin ni-naanjab nìn jíń, boonee ki dāa kpíìyee. Sii un di yaa jíń tijin gbantee làá nīn kíǹ ki bí kí nīn cá jaanjaan.».

eng: Jesus said these things while he was teaching in the synagogue in Capernaum.
local: Tin tin Yeesu nìn sòon̄ Kapeernayum kpafidiikun ki dàkā binibee dee.

eng: Jesus said this to test him, for he already knew what he was going to do.
local: - Ama Yeesu nìn nyí min ní u làá ŋée, u nìn pée lī míǹ kí kpèè yii Fiiliipi làá kíí-u yii ba ní.

eng: Many of his disciples who heard this said, This message is harsh. Who can hear it.
local: Yeesu boonnooliibi tikpil nín gbìl̄ tibɔti gbantee, ní bi bíi lì yii: «Tibɔti nee pɔ̄ɔ̄ tikpil! Ŋma di ŋūǹ kí pīlifì-tì?».

eng: Jesus knew that the disciples were grumbling about this and he said to them, Does this offend you?.
local: Yeesu béè yii u-boonnooliibi bíi gbùkum̄ tibɔti gbanti puee ní u bālifì-bi yii: «Tibɔti gbanti páb́-ni níii?.

eng: What if you were to see the Human One going up where he was before?.
local: Mimmee, ni yaa láa ká man Kinibiki fātìi jɔ́ḿ laakin m nìn pée bíńee fáà?.

eng: The Spirit is the one who gives life and the flesh doesnt help at all. The words I have spoken to you are spirit and life.
local: Unil nì u-pɔ́ɔǹtam kɔkɔ sá fám ní, see Ŋfam di tìì dimaŋfal din kaa cáá dikúntilee. Míǹ ní m-bɔti tin m sòon̄ nì nee sá Unimbɔti Fam ní ki sá dimaŋfal.

eng: Yet some of you dont believe.Jesus knew from the beginning who wouldnt believe and the one who would betray him.
local: Ama ni-kansikin biba bí, kaa fōō ki kíí.» Kun di yì-tì Yeesu sòon̄ mimmee di sá u nìn pée nyí bin kaa fōō ki kííyee doooo ŋkilkaamin, nì un làá dāá dū-u kí tīī binannanliibee.

eng: He said,For this reason I said to you that none can come to me unless the Father enables them to do so.
local: Ní u lī-bi yii: «Míǹ pu ní m tùkū-ni yii ubɔ kaa ŋūǹ kí dāan̄ m-cee an yaa kaa tin sá m-Baa Unimbɔti di cáań-u.».

eng: At this, many of his disciples turned away and no longer accompanied him.
local: Ki yōoǹ an yuntiŋu ní Yeesu boonnooliibi tikpil yíì, kaa ti nóó-u.

eng: Jesus asked the Twelve, Do you also want to leave.
local: Niinee ní Yeesu bí u-kpambaliŋ saalaa nì alee yii: «Kin nimbi fáà, ni mun kaa làá nīn cáaa?».

eng: Simon Peter answered, Lord, where would we go? You have the words of eternal life.
local: Ní Peetroo kíí-u yii: «Ti-Dindaan, sii di cáá tibɔti tin tìì dimaŋfal din kaa cáá dikúntilee. Ŋma cee ní ti làá gíti cù?.

eng: We believe and know that you are Gods holy one.
local: Timbi fōō sii ní ki kíí, ní ki nyí yii sii di sá Circirdaan un nyánní Unimbɔti ceeyee.».

eng: Philip replied, More than a half years salary worth of food wouldnt be enough for each person to have even a little bit.
local: Ní Fiiliipi kíí-u yii: «Báà ti yaa dū animbil jitɔmbi alaataa ali ki dá kpɔnɔb, an kaa làá bà bi-kɔkɔ kikatii kikatii gba.».

eng: Jesus replied, Didnt I choose you twelve? Yet one of you is a devil.
local: Niinee ní Yeesu bí yii: «Man di kaa nìn lēē-ni binib saalaa nì bilaaa? Ama ni-kansikinee, ubɔ sá Sitaan ní!».

eng: He was speaking of Judas, Simon Iscariots son, for he, one of the Twelve, was going to betray him.
local: Juudasi un sá Siimɔn Isikaariyɔti jipɔɔnee pu ní u nìn sòon̄ míǹ. Di sá yii nì min kɔkɔ Juudasi mun nìn sá u-kpambaliŋ saalaa nì alee kansikin dibee, úǹ di làá dū-u kí ŋúbíǹ binannanliibi.

eng: One of his disciples, Andrew, Simon Peters brother, said.
local: Ní u-boonnooliibi kansikin ubɔ, Siimɔn Peetroo naal un bi yíì Andreeyee, bí-u yii.

eng: A youth here has five barley loaves and two fish. But what good is that for a crowd like this.
local: «Kinaacimpɔmbiki kiba bí doo ki cáań ɔɔji yim kpɔnɔb biŋmoo nì ijɔm ili. An kaa nín ŋūǹ kí ŋá kunipaau nee tiba ní.».

eng: After this Jesus traveled throughout Galilee. He didnt want to travel in Judea, because the Jewish authorities wanted to kill him.
local: Míǹ boonee, Yeesu nìn kíǹ ki bíi yìiǹ Galiilee timmun, kaa là kí yìiǹ Juudee watil, kun puee Juuda yab yidambi nìn nyàab̀ kí kpɔ̀-u.

eng: However, after his brothers left for the festival, he went too—not openly but in secret.
local: U-naalib búntì jiŋaal gbanti, boonee ní u mun dāa dī dibalin ki cūtì, kaa là bí ká-u.

eng: The Jewish leaders were looking for Jesus at the festival. They kept asking, Where is he.
local: Jiŋaal gbantinee, Juuda yab kpilib nìn nyàab̀-u ki bàlifī tɔb yii: «U nín bí la dee?».

eng: The crowds were murmuring about him. Hes a good man, some said, but others were saying, No, he tricks the people.
local: Ní binib tikpil nìn lɔ́kɔfí Yeesu bɔti pu samaa kansikin, biba bí yii: «U sá unimɔnti.» Ní biba mun bí yii: «Waa sá unimɔnti, u sá uŋmatinli ní.».

eng: No one spoke about him publicly, though, for fear of the Jewish authorities.
local: Míǹ puee, ubɔ kaa nìn sòoǹ mpɔn, di sá yii bi nìn fàŋkí Juuda yab kpilib.

eng: Halfway through the festival, Jesus went up to the temple and started to teach.
local: Bi jíń jiŋaal ki kɔ́ kansikinee, ní Yeesu nín jɔ́ḿ kunimbɔtidiin ki cūtìi bíi dàkà binib.

eng: Astonished, the Jewish leaders asked, Hes never been taught! How has he mastered the Law.
local: Juuda yab kpilib nìn bākatì sèeee, ní ki bíi lì yii: «An ŋá mana ní unil nee nyí Unimbɔti gbɔŋku míǹ, úǹ un kaa ŋá an sukuliuee?».

eng: Jesus responded, My teaching isnt mine but comes from the one who sent me.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-bi yii: «M-dàkatam nee nyēeń un túnní-mee cee ní, an kaa sá man m-ba dàkatam.

eng: Whoever wants to do Gods will can tell whether my teaching is from God or whether I speak on my own.
local: Un yaa là kí ŋá min Unimbɔti lèe, udaan làá béé m-dàkatam nee yaa nyēeń Unimbɔti ceeyaaa, an yaa tí sá man m-ba pɔŋŋu pu ní m sòoǹ.

eng: Those who speak on their own seek glory for themselves. Those who seek the glory of him who sent me are people of truth; theres no falsehood in them.
local: Un sòoǹ u-ba bindi puee nyàab̀ kí yòon̄ u-ba yilpu ní, ama un nyàab̀ kí yòon̄ un túnní-uee yindi yilpuee di cáá ibaamɔn, nnyimɔn kpá udaan úǹ ni.

eng: Didnt Moses give you the Law? Yet none of you keep the Law. Why do you want to kill me.
local: Mooyiisi di nìn tīī-ni marab àá an kaa sá míǹ? Kin, ba ŋá ní ni-kansikin báà ubɔ kaa tɔ́ marab gbanti?.

eng: When it was almost time for the Jewish Festival of Booths.
local: Juuda yab jiŋaaliu un bi yíì Tibìń jiŋaaliuee tóobińee.

eng: The crowd answered, You have a demon. Who wants to kill you.
local: Ba di yì-tì ní ni là kí kpɔ̀-mi?» Ní samaayee bí-u yii: «Arasinii kā a-pu níii? Ŋma di là kí kpɔ̀-si ní?».

eng: Jesus replied, I did one work, and you were all astonished.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-bi yii: «Ditumbaantiil din m ŋá ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa dalee pu ní ni kɔkɔ bākatì nee.

eng: Because Moses gave you the commandment about circumcision , you circumcise a man on the Sabbath.
local: Ama ni kíikí ki gífí ni-biyaamu akpancan ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa dal, kun puee Mooyiisi di nìn tīī-ni mara gbanti. - Ama an kaa nìn sá Mooyiisi di náanní, doooo ni-naanjab ní an nìn kíliń.

eng: If a man can be circumcised on the Sabbath without breaking Moses Law, why are you angry with me because I made an entire man well on the Sabbath?.
local: Ni nín kaa là kí yìntī Mooyiisi marabee puee, ní ni gífí ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa dal. Mimmee, ba pu ní ni fōō man pu diŋuul yii m cáaǹ uninja ubɔ weeŋyi kɔkɔ ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa dal?.

eng: Dont judge according to appearances. Judge with right judgment.
local: Ní taa pɔ̀ digàndi ama ní tɔ́kɔń ibaamɔn pu kí sòoǹ nì deedee.».

eng: Some people from Jerusalem said, Isnt he the one they want to kill?.
local: Niinee ní Jeerusaleem yab biba bíi lì yii: «An kaa sá un bi là kí kpɔ̀ee deeee?.

eng: Here he is, speaking in public, yet they arent saying anything to him. Could it be that our leaders actually think he is the Christ?.
local: Kin, an ŋá mana ní u yú ki tɔ́ u-ba mpaampiin pu doo ki sòoǹ, ubɔ kaa bíi lì-u tiba! Àá biyidambi kalin béè yii úǹ di sá Meesiya ní?.

eng: We know where he is from, but when the Christ comes, no one will know where he is from.
local: Ama uninja nee úŋ̀wuee, ti nyí laakin u nyánnée, an yaa sá Meesiyayee, ubɔ kaa dāa béetí laakin ní úǹ sée.».

eng: While Jesus was teaching in the temple, he exclaimed, You know me and where I am from. I havent come on my own. The one who sent me is true, and you dont know him.
local: Ŋyuntee, Yeesu nìn bí kunimbɔtidiin ki dàkà, ní ki téeń mpɔn yii: «Ni nyí-mi, ki nyí laakin mun m sée yaa! Maa dɔ́miń m-ba pu, un sá ibaamɔndaanee di túnní-mi, ní naa nyí-u.

eng: I know him because I am from him and he sent me.
local: Ama man nyí-u, kun puee u-cee ní m nyánní, úǹ di túnní-mi.».

eng: Jesus brothers said to him, Leave Galilee. Go to Judea so that your disciples can see the amazing works that you do.
local: ní Yeesu naalib bí-u yii: «Yīkì doo kí nīn cá Juudee, án nín làá ŋá pu a-boonnooliibi mun ń tin ká atuun ŋin a tūǹee.

eng: So they wanted to seize Jesus, but they couldnt because his time hadnt yet come.
local: Niinee ní bi bíi nyàab̀ kí cúú-u, ama báà ubɔ ŋal kaa máań u-pu, kun puee u-yuntiŋu kaa láá bāań.

eng: Many from that crowd believed in Jesus. They said, When the Christ comes, will he do more miraculous signs than this man does.
local: Samaa kansikinee, binib tikpil nìn fōō-u ki kíí, ní ki lì yii: «Mimmee, Meesiya yaa láa bāańee, u dāa ŋūǹ kí ŋá maamaacib kí jītíǹ kijapaai nee deeee?».

eng: The Pharisees heard the crowd whispering such things about Jesus, and the chief priests and Pharisees sent guards to arrest him.
local: Fariisab nín gbìl̄ samaa bíi sòoǹ Yeesu pu míǹ bi-ŋmaniŋmee, ní bi nì bisaraaŋakpilib tú soojab ń cù kí cúuń-u.

eng: Therefore, Jesus said, Im still with you for a little while before I go to the one who sent me.
local: Ní Yeesu tùkū-bi yii: «Ŋyunti fiii ní m làá gíti ŋá ni-kansikin, ní kí nīn gítí un túnní-mee cee.

eng: You will look for me, but you wont find me, and where I am you cant come.
local: Di sá yii ni làá dāá nyàab̀-mi, kaa làá dāá ká-mi, kun puee naa làá dāá ŋmā kí dāan̄ laakin m làá nīn bée.».

eng: The Jewish opposition asked each other, Where does he intend to go that we cant find him? Surely he doesnt intend to go where our people have been scattered and are living among the Greeks! He isnt going to teach the Greeks, is he?.
local: Niinee ní Juuda yab kpilib bíi bàlifī tɔb yii: «La ní u kalin nín cá ní ki nín tùkù-ti yii taa làá ŋmā kí ká-u ní? Àá, u làá kalin cù Juuda yab bin yàl̄ ki kɔ́ Greeki yab timmunee cee kí dàkà Greeki yab Unimbɔti bɔti ní?.

eng: What does he mean when he says, You will look for me, but you wont find me, and where I am you cant come.
local: U nín bí yii: Ni làá nyàab̀-mi, kaa làá ká-mi, ki gíti lī-ti yii: Naa làá ŋmā kí dāan̄ laakin m làá nīn bée, taapu nín sá yii ba dee?».

eng: On the last and most important day of the festival, Jesus stood up and shouted, All who are thirsty should come to me!.
local: Ŋwiin ŋun jiŋaal kúntée bɔti di nìn páb́. Ŋwiin gbanti ní Yeesu nìn yúl̀ samaa kansikin ki téeń yii: «Un ní nnyinnyoo yaa cááyee ń dāan̄ m-cee kí nyɔ̀!.

eng: All who believe in me should drink! As the scriptures said concerning me, Rivers of living water will flow out from within him. .
local: Un di yaa fōō-mi ki kííyee, nnyim min tìì dimaŋfalee làá kpántí mbun kí nīn pùub̀ udaan pɔbilin, kii Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon an nín ŋmàn̄ ki síiǹ puee.».

eng: Jesus said this concerning the Spirit. Those who believed in him would soon receive the Spirit, but they hadnt experienced the Spirit yet since Jesus hadnt yet been glorified.
local: Bin fōō Yeesu ki kííyee nín làá fōō Unimbɔti Fam miŋyee pu ní Yeesu nìn bíi sòoǹ nì bi. Ŋyunti gbantee, Unimbɔti Fam kaa láá dɔ́miń kun puee Yeesu kaa nìn dáá fōō u-nyooti.

eng: Those who want to be known publicly dont do things secretly. Since you can do these things, show yourself to the world.
local: Unil yaa là bí ká-uee, waa gíti tūǹ u-tuuŋŋi dibalin, míǹ puee, tɔ̄ a-tuuŋŋi gbanti báà ŋma ń ká.».

eng: When some in the crowd heard these words, they said, This man is truly the prophet.
local: Kunipaauee kansikin biba gbìl̄ an nyɔɔbuŋŋee, ní ki bí yii: «Unil nee lafun sá Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu un ti cée ní!».

eng: Others said, Hes the Christ. But others said, The Christ cant come from Galilee, can he?.
local: Ní biba mun bí yii: «Úǹ di sá Meesiya!» Ní biba mun gíti bí yii: «Meesiya ŋūǹ kí nyānní Galiileeee?.

eng: Didnt the scripture say that the Christ comes from Davids family and from Bethlehem, Davids village.
local: Ama Unimbɔti gbɔŋku tùkù-ti yii Meesiya làá dāá nīn sá Daafiidi maaliŋun, ní ki sá Beetileem, kitiŋ kin ni Daafiidi nìn sée.».

eng: So the crowd was divided over Jesus.
local: Yeesu puee kunipaauee nìn cúu yākatì mili.

eng: Some wanted to arrest him, but no one grabbed him.
local: Ní bi-kansikin biba bàà nìn là kí cúú-u, ama báà ubɔ ŋal kaa máań u-pu.

eng: The guards returned to the chief priests and Pharisees, who asked, Why didnt you bring him.
local: Soojab nín fātìi gítì bisaraaŋakpilib nì Fariisab ceeyee, ní bi bālifì soojabee yii: «Ba ŋá ní naa cáań-u ní?».

eng: The guards answered, No one has ever spoken the way he does.
local: Ní soojab kíí-bi yii: «Doooo nee, taa láá ká ubɔ sòoǹ kii kijapaai nee!».

eng: The Pharisees replied, Have you too been deceived?.
local: Ní Fariisab fātìi bí soojabee yii: «Ni mun yāntì u kíil̀-ni naaa?.

eng: Have any of the leaders believed in him? Has any Pharisee?.
local: Ni ká biyidambi kansikin ubɔ àá Fariisab kansikin ubɔ fōō-u ki kííii?.

eng: No, only this crowd, which doesnt know the Law. And they are under Gods curse.
local: An sá binifantantiibi bin kaa nyí Unimbɔti marab ki sá mbusudambee, bíǹ di fōō-u ki kíí.».

eng: His brothers said this because even they didnt believe in him.
local: Di sá yii Yeesu naalib mun gba kaa nìn là kí fōō-u kí kíí.

eng: Nicodemus, who was one of them and had come to Jesus earlier, said.
local: Niikoodeem un nìn cūtìi ká Yeesu ŋyunti ŋubɔee mun nìn ŋmàl̄ Fariisab kansikin. Úǹ di bí yii.

eng: Our Law doesnt judge someone without first hearing him and learning what he is doing, does it.
local: «Ti-marab poonee, an pú-ti nsan tí kpɔ̀ unil bɔti fám, kaa bālifì-u ki béè kun u ŋéeee?».

eng: They answered him, You are not from Galilee too, are you? Look it up and you will see that the prophet doesnt come from Galilee.
local: Ní bi kíí-u yii: «A mun sá Galiilee níii? Nyàab̀ Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon kí kpèè! Aa làá ká bi ŋmàn̄ laaba yii Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu ubɔ nyánní Galiilee.» .

eng: They each went to their own homes.
local: Niinee ní bi-kɔkɔ yāaǹ ki nín kùn̄ bi-dumpui.

eng: Jesus replied, For you, anytime is fine. But my time hasnt come yet.
local: Niinee ní Yeesu kíí-bi yii: «An yaa sá nimbee, iyunti kɔkɔ pée ŋān̄ ki tīī-ni ní, man yuntiŋu kaa láá bāań.

eng: The world cant hate you. It hates me, though, because I testify that its works are evil.
local: Duulinya yab kaa ŋūǹ kí nīn ná nimbi, ama bi ná man, kun puee man sòoǹ yii tin bi ŋáańee sá atuŋkpiti.

eng: You go up to the festival. Im not going to this one because my time hasnt yet come.
local: Nimbi nín̄ cámaan jiŋaal gbanti. Man kaa làá cù. Di sá yii m-yuntiŋu kaa láá ŋmāntì.».

eng: Having said this, he stayed in Galilee.
local: U lī-bi mimmee, ní ki gbíntìi kā Galiilee.

eng: And Jesus went to the Mount of Olives.
local: Ní Yeesu, úǹ nín jɔ́ḿ Ɔliifi sufii jool pu.

eng: Jesus stood up and said to her, Woman, where are they? Is there no one to condemn you.
local: Ní Yeesu yáatì ki bí yii: «Unimpuee, bin cáań-see bí la? Kéè, báà ubɔ kaa ŋá-si tibaa!».

eng: She said, No one, sir. Jesus said, Neither do I condemn you. Go, and from now on, dont sin anymore.
local: Ní unimpuee kíí-u yii: «Aayee, ukpil, báà ubɔ kaa ŋá-mi tiba.» Ní Yeesu bí-u yii: «Ma mun kaa làá ŋá-si tiba. Á nīn cá kí taa ti ŋá ikpiti.».

eng: Jesus spoke to the people again, saying, I am the light of the world. Whoever follows me wont walk in darkness but will have the light of life.
local: Yeesu fātìi yōoǹ dinyɔɔbundi ki bíi sòoǹ nì kunipaauee yii: «Man di sá ŋwalifi ki tīī duulinya. Un yaa nóó-mee làá ká ŋwalifi ŋun tìì dimaŋfalee, udaan kpá kí còom̀ dibɔmbɔndin.».

eng: Then the Pharisees said to him, Because you are testifying about yourself, your testimony isnt valid.
local: Ní Fariisab bí-u yii: «A nín bíi sòoǹ a-ba pu min nee, tin a sòoǹee kaa sá ibaamɔn.».

eng: Jesus replied, Even if I testify about myself, my testimony is true, since I know where I came from and where Im going. You dont know where I come from or where Im going.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-bi yii: «Nì min kamaa m bíi sòoǹ m-ba pu nee, an sá ibaamɔn ní, di sá m nyí laakin m nyánnée ki nyí laakin m cée mun. Ama nimbi kaa nyí laakin m nyánnéeyaaa, kaa nyí laakin m cée mun.

eng: You judge according to human standards, but I judge no one.
local: Nimbi sòoǹ tibɔti nniyaamin ní, man kaa sòoǹ báà ubɔ bɔti.

eng: Even if I do judge, my judgment is truthful, because Im not alone. My judgments come from me and from the Father who sent me.
local: Báà m yaa bíi sòoǹ ubɔ bɔtaaa, m sòoǹ-tì nì ibaamɔn ní, kun puee maa bí m-baba, m-Baa un túnní-mee bí m-cee.

eng: In your Law it is written that the witness of two people is true.
local: Ni-marab poonee, an ŋmàn̄ yii binib bili yaa sòon̄ tibɔti mbaantiiminee, an sá ibaamɔn ní.

eng: I am one witness concerning myself, and the Father who sent me is the other.
local: Míǹ di sá m sòoǹ m-ba pu, ní m-Baa un túnní-mee mun lèekī m-pu seera.».

eng: They asked him, Where is your Father? Jesus answered, You dont know me and you dont know my Father. If you knew me, you would also know my Father.
local: Ní bi bālifì Yeesu yii: «A-Baa bí la ní?» Ní u kíí-bi yii: «Naa nyí-maaa, kaa nyí m-Baa mun. Ni yaa bàà nyí-mi, ni bàà béetí m-Baa mun.».

eng: Early in the morning he returned to the temple. All the people gathered around him, and he sat down and taught them.
local: Ku wúntì kutaafaau pɔ̀lpɔlee, ní u fātìi nín gítiń kunimbɔtidiin, ní binib tikpil tí dɔ́miń u-cee. Niinee ní u kāl̀ ki bíi dàkà-bi Unimbɔti bɔti.

eng: He spoke these words while he was teaching in the temple area known as the treasury. No one arrested him, because his time hadnt yet come.
local: Yeesu sòon̄ tibɔti nee ŋyunti ŋun u bíi dàkà samaa kunimbɔtidiin, laakin bi ŋáań saraa nimbiliŋee, ní báà ubɔ kaa tāntì u-ŋal ki gbēē-u, kun puee u-yuntiŋu kaa láá bāań.

eng: Jesus continued, Im going away. You will look for me, and you will die in your sin. Where Im going, you cant come.
local: Ní Yeesu gíti bí-bi yii: «M làá nīn cá, boonee ni làá dāá nyàab̀-mi, kaa làá dāá ká-mi, ní kí dāá kpáàa kpú ni-kpitii poon. Naa làá ŋmā kí dāan̄ laakin m cée.».

eng: The Jewish leaders said, He isnt going to kill himself, is he? Is that why he said, Where Im going, you cant come.
local: Niinee ní Juuda yab bíi sòoǹ yii: «Mimmee, u làá kalin kpɔ̀ u-ba naaa? Ba pu ní u bí yii, naa ŋūǹ kí dāan̄ laakin m cée?».

eng: He said to them, You are from below; Im from above. You are from this world; Im not from this world.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-bi yii: «Nimbi sá taapu doo yab ní, man nyánní yilpu ní. Ni sá duulinya yab ní, man kaa sá duulinya yɔu.

eng: This is why I told you that you would die in your sins. If you dont believe that I Am, you will die in your sins.
local: An pu ní m tùkū-ni yii ni làá kpú ni-kpitii poon. Ni yaa kaa fōō ki kíí yii <man di sá un m sée>, ni làá kpú ni-kpitii poon ní.».

eng: Who are you? they asked. Jesus replied, Im exactly who I have claimed to be from the beginning.
local: Ní bi bālifì-u yii: «Sii di sá ŋma ní?» Ní u kíí-bi yii: «M nìn pée tùkū-ni doooo mpeekaam un m sée.

eng: I have many things to say in judgment concerning you. The one who sent me is true, and what I have heard from him I tell the world.
local: M cáá tibɔti tikpil kí sòoǹ ni-pu kí kpɔ̀ ni-bɔti. Un túnní-mee sá ibaamɔndaan, ní tin u tùkū-mee ní m cáa bíi sòoǹ ki tùkù nimbi taapu doo nib.».

eng: They didnt know he was speaking about his Father.
local: Ama Juuda yab kaa nìn nyí yii Unimbɔti Ubaati pu ní u bíi sòoǹ nì bi.

eng: So Jesus said to them, When the Human One is lifted up, then you will know that I Am. Then you will know that I do nothing on my own, but I say just what the Father has taught me.
local: Míǹ pu ní Yeesu nìn gíti tùkū-bi yii: «Ni yaa láa yōoǹ man Kinibiki yilpuee, ní ni dāa béetí m nín sá uŋyee. Maa ŋáań tiba m-ba pɔŋŋu pu, see tin m-Baa dàkà-mee ní m cáa sòoǹ.

eng: He who sent me is with me. He doesnt leave me by myself, because I always do what makes him happy.
local: Un túnní-mee bí m-cee, waa yàntī ḿ nīn bí m-baba, kun puee m pée ŋáań baabadal min fáatì-uee ní.».
