eng: The legal experts and Pharisees brought a woman caught in adultery. Placing her in the center of the group.
local: Ní bimaradakaliibi nì Fariisab cáań-u unimpu ubɔ, bi nìn cúú-u u bíi ŋáań tilaakpaal. Ní bi cáá-u ki yóoǹ kunipaau kansikin.

eng: While Jesus was saying these things, many people came to believe in him.
local: Yeesu nín nìn bíi sòoǹ tibɔti gbantee, binib tikpil nìn fōō-u ki kíí.

eng: Jesus said to the Jews who believed in him, You are truly my disciples if you remain faithful to my teaching.
local: Niinee ní Yeesu bí Juuda yab bin fōō-u ki kííyee yii: «Ni yaa ŋúb́ m-bɔti nee, ni làá nīn sá m-boonnooliibi tiŋman pu yab.

eng: Then you will know the truth, and the truth will set you free.
local: Ni làá béé ibaamɔn, ní ibaamɔnee ń yàntī ní tɔ̄ ni-ba.».

eng: They responded, We are Abrahams children; weve never been anyones slaves. How can you say that we will be set free.
local: Ní bi kíí Yeesu yii: «Ti sá Abraam naantiibi ní, doooo nee ubɔ kaa pée cúú timbi kuyuŋu. Ba di yāntì ní a bí yii ti làá tɔ̄ ti-ba?».

eng: Jesus answered, I assure you that everyone who sins is a slave to sin.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-bi yii: «Ibaamɔn, ibaamɔn ní m sòoǹ nì ni, un sá kamaa yaa ŋáań ikpitee sá ikpiti yumbuu ní.

eng: A slave isnt a permanent member of the household, but a son is.
local: Uyumbu kaa sá kudicaŋu poon niliu kí nīn cá, ama kudiidaan jipɔɔn úǹ làá nīn sá kudicaŋu poon niliu ní kí nīn pée cá jaanjaan.

eng: Therefore, if the Son makes you free, you really will be free.
local: Mimmee, man Ubaati Jipɔɔn yaa tīī-ni dibatɔlee, míǹ ní ni lafun tɔ́ ni-ba tiŋman pu.

eng: I know that you are Abrahams children, yet you want to kill me because you dont welcome my teaching.
local: M nyí yii ni sá Abraam naantiibi. Ama ni nyàab̀ kí kpɔ̀-mi. Di sá m-bɔti kaa ká mpaan ni-pɔbiliŋin.

eng: Im telling you what Ive seen when I am with the Father, but you are doing what youve heard from your father.
local: Man sòoǹ tin m-Baa dàkā-mee, ní ni mun ŋáań tin ni-baa tùkū-nee.».

eng: They replied, Our father is Abraham. Jesus responded, If you were Abrahams children, you would do Abrahams works.
local: Ní bi fātìi kíí-u yii: «Timbi baa di sá Abraam!» Ní Yeesu bí-bi yii: «Ni yaa bàà sá Abraam biyaamuee, ni bàà ŋáań min Abraam nìn ŋáańee ní.

eng: they said to Jesus, Teacher, this woman was caught in the act of committing adultery.
local: ní ki bí Yeesu yii: «Ukpil, unimpu nee bíi ŋáań tilaakpaal ní ti cúú-u.

eng: Instead, you want to kill me, though I am the one who has spoken the truth I heard from God. Abraham didnt do this.
local: Ama, nì min kɔkɔwoooo m sòoǹ ibaamɔn in m gbìl̄ m-Baa ceeyee, nimbi nyàab̀ kí kpɔ̀-mi. Abraam kaa nìn ŋá míǹ dalba.

eng: You are doing your fathers works. They said, Our ancestry isnt in question! The only Father we have is God.
local: Ni-baa nín ŋáań puee, míǹ ní ni ŋáań!» Ní bi kíí-u yii: «Timbi kaa sá nnaayubiyaamu, ti-baa sá ubaanti ní, úǹ di sá Unimbɔti.» Ní Yeesu kíí-bi yii.

eng: Jesus replied, If God were your Father, you would love me, for I came from God. Here I am. I havent come on my own. God sent me.
local: «Unimbɔti yaa bàà sá ni-Baayee, ni bàà néeń-mi ní, kun puee Unimbɔti cee ní m nyánní ki dɔ́miń, maa dɔ́miń m-ba pu, an sá úǹ di túnní-mi.

eng: Why dont you understand what Im saying? Its because you cant really hear my words.
local: Ba pu ní naa gbìl̀ m-bɔti tin m sòoǹee taapu? Kun puee naa ŋūǹ kí pīlifì-tì.

eng: Your father is the devil. You are his children, and you want to do what your father wants. He was a murderer from the beginning. He has never stood for the truth, because theres no truth in him. Whenever that liar speaks, he speaks according to his own nature, because hes a liar and the father of liars.
local: Nimbi baa di sá Sitaan, min min ni-baayee lèe, an ní ni là ki ŋáań. U pée sá unikpɔl doooo ŋkilkaamin ní. Waa sí ibaamɔnni kun puee waa cáá ibaamɔn u-ni. U yaa bíi mɔ́ntée, míǹ di sá u-bimbim, kun puee u sá unyimɔndaan ní, úǹ di sá inyimɔn na nì i-baa.

eng: Because I speak the truth, you dont believe me.
local: Man nín sòoǹ nì ni ibaamɔn puee ní naa là kí fōō-mi kí kíí.

eng: Who among you can show Im guilty of sin? Since I speak the truth, why dont you believe me?.
local: Ni-kansikin, ŋma di ŋūǹ kí dàkà diyìntil din m yīntèe? Mimmee, ni nín nyí yii tin m sòoǹee sá ibaamɔnee, ba ŋá ní naa làá fōō-mi kí kíí?.

eng: Gods children listen to Gods words. You dont listen to me because you arent Gods children.
local: Un sá Unimbɔti yɔuee gbìl̀ Unimbɔti bɔti ní. Ni yaa kaa gbìl̀ Unimbɔti bɔtee, naa sá u-yab dee.».

eng: The Jewish opposition answered, We were right to say that you are a Samaritan and have a demon, werent we.
local: Ní Juuda yab kpilib fātìi kíí-u yii: «Ti nín lī yii a sá usamaariija, arasinii mun kā a-puee, an kaa sá ibaamɔnaaa?».

eng: I dont have a demon, Jesus replied. But I honor my Father and you dishonor me.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-bi yii: «Arasinii ubɔ kaa kā m-pu, m-Baa ní m bíi tìì tinyool, ní nimbi kpèé ki sìŋkīǹ-mi.

eng: In the Law, Moses commanded us to stone women like this. What do you say.
local: Mooyiisi marab poonee, u bí-ti yii tí dū ataŋkpee kí yāŋkì an nimpoobee kí kpɔ̀. Mimmee, sii ká yii ba?».

eng: Im not trying to bring glory to myself. Theres one who is seeking to glorify me, and hes the judge.
local: Man kaa nyàab̀ bí pàkà-mi. Ubɔ bí ki làá pàkà-mi, udaan di sá ubɔsoonli.

eng: I assure you that whoever keeps my word will never die.
local: Ibaamɔn, ibaamɔn ní m sòoǹ nì ni, un ŋúb́ m-bɔtee kaa ti bí kí kpú jaanjaan.».

eng: The Jewish opposition said to Jesus, Now we know that you have a demon. Abraham and the prophets died, yet you say, Whoever keeps my word will never die.
local: Niinee ní Juuda yab kpilib bí-u yii: «Kɔŋkɔnnee ti lafun béè yii arasinii ubɔ kā a-pu! Di sá Abraam kpíì, Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi mun kpíì, ní sii bí yii un yaa ŋúb́ a-bɔtee kaa ti kúù jaanjaanaaa?.

eng: Are you greater than our father Abraham? He died and the prophets died, so who do you make yourself out to be.
local: Mimmee sii jítiǹ ti-baa Abraamaaa? Úǹ kpíì, ní Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi mun kpíì. Sii yíì a-ba ŋma ní?».

eng: Jesus answered, If I glorify myself, my glory is meaningless. My Father, who you say is your God, is the one who glorifies me.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-bi yii: «M yaa bíi tìì m-ba tinyoolee, mimmee m-nyootee kaa sá timɔntiil dee. Un tìì-mi tinyoolee di sá m-Baa un ni bí yii u sá ni-Nimbɔtiu.

eng: You dont know him, but I do. If I said I didnt know him, I would be like you, a liar. But I do know him, and I keep his word.
local: ama ní kaa nyí-uee. Man nyí-u. M yaa néè yii maa nyí-u, m làá kpántí unyimɔndaan kii nimbee ní. Man nyí-u, ní ki ŋúb́ u-bɔti.

eng: Your father Abraham was overjoyed that he would see my day. He saw it and was happy.
local: Ni-naanja Abraam nín béè yii m làá dāan̄ taapu dooyee, an mɔ́ɔkiǹ-u, ní u lafun ká ní u-pɔbil píil̀.».

eng: You arent even years old! the Jewish opposition replied. How can you say that you have seen Abraham.
local: Ní Juuda yab kpilib kíí-u yii: «Sii kaa láá bàn̄ abin imuŋku ili nì saalaa, a ká Abraam la?».

eng: I assure you, Jesus replied, before Abraham was, I Am.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-bi yii: «Ibaamɔn, ibaamɔn, man pée bí ní bi nín màl̄ Abraam!».

eng: So they picked up stones to throw at him, but Jesus hid himself and left the temple.
local: Niinee ní bi bíi píikí ataŋkpee kí yāŋkì-u, ní Yeesu nɔ̄ntì ki nyáń kunimbɔtidiin.

eng: They said this to test him, because they wanted a reason to bring an accusation against him. Jesus bent down and wrote on the ground with his finger.
local: Bi nìn píĺ Yeesu ki là ú sòoǹ kí kɔ́ bi-bɔtinee, ní bi cáań-u an bɔti. Ní Yeesu bōoǹ ki cáá u-ŋambiki ki ŋmà kitiŋ.

eng: They continued to question him, so he stood up and replied, Whoever hasnt sinned should throw the first stone.
local: Bi gbíntìi bíi bàlifī-uee, ní Yeesu yáatì, ní ki bí-bi yii: «Ni-kansikin un di yaa kaa pée ŋá ikpiti ki kée ń péé péé kí dū ditaŋkpal kí yā unimpu nee!».

eng: Bending down again, he wrote on the ground.
local: U lī mimmee ní ki fātìi tí bōoǹ ki bíi ŋmà kitiŋ.

eng: Those who heard him went away, one by one, beginning with the elders. Finally, only Jesus and the woman were left in the middle of the crowd.
local: Binibee nín gbìl̄ mimmee, ní bi kíǹ ki sútí ubɔ ubɔ ki wàatī unimpuee, ki kíliń bitikpilib ki dāa dūu kúntì ŋwaamu, ní an tin kíǹ Yeesu baba, ní unimpuee yú ki kɔ́ĺ-u.

eng: As Jesus walked along, he saw a man who was blind from birth.
local: Dalba Yeesu jītée, ní ki ká ujɔfu ubɔ. Bi nìn pée màl̄-u nì ijɔfi ní.

eng: So they asked him, How are you now able to see.
local: Ní bi bālifì-u yii: «Kin, an ŋá mana ní a wál̀?».

eng: He answered, The man they call Jesus made mud, smeared it on my eyes, and said, Go to the pool of Siloam and wash. So I went and washed, and then I could see.
local: Ní u kíí-bi yii: «Kijapaai kin bi yíì Yeesuee di sɔ̄ŋkì ntam ki ŋmītì m-nimbiliŋ ní ki bí yii ḿ nīn cá Siiloowee kí fíntí m-nimbiin. M nín cūtì ki fíntì m-nimbiinee, ní m wál̀.».

eng: They asked, Where is this man? He replied, I dont know.
local: Ní bi bālifì-u yii: «Kijapaayee bí la?» Ní u kíí-bi yii: «Maa nyí u-laŋki.».

eng: Then they led the man who had been born blind to the Pharisees.
local: Ní bi yōoǹ kijapaai kin nìn sá ujɔfuee ki cáa cūnnì Fariisab cee.

eng: Now Jesus made the mud and smeared it on the mans eyes on a Sabbath day.
local: An nìn sá ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa dal ní Yeesu nìn sɔ̄ŋkì ntam ki ŋúbitì ujɔfuee nimbiliŋ.

eng: So Pharisees also asked him how he was able to see. The man told them, He put mud on my eyes, I washed, and now I see.
local: Míǹ pu ní Fariisab mun bālifì kijapaayee yii: «An ŋá mana ní a wāĺ kɔŋkɔnnee ní?» Ní u kíí-bi yii: «U sɔ̄ŋkì ntam ki dūu ŋmītì m-nimbiliŋ, ní m cūtìi fíntì m-nimbiin ní ki kíǹ ki wāĺ kɔŋkɔnnee.».

eng: Some Pharisees said, This man isnt from God, because he breaks the Sabbath law. Others said, How can a sinner do miraculous signs like these? So they were divided.
local: Ní bi-kansikin biba bíi lì yii: «U nín kaa nyí ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa nee, kijapaayee kaa sá Unimbɔti niliu.» Ní biba mun lī yii: «Unikpiti mun ŋūǹ kí ŋá maamaacib yéèee?» An di nìn cáań diyakatil bi-kansikin.

eng: Some of the Pharisees questioned the man who had been born blind again: What do you have to say about him, since he healed your eyes? He replied, Hes a prophet.
local: Niinee ní bi fātìi bālifì un nìn jɔ̀b̄ee yii: «Un ŋúbitì a-nimbiliŋee, sii nyí yii u sá ŋma ní?» - Ní u kíí-bi yii: «U sá Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu ní.».

eng: The Jewish leaders didnt believe the man had been blind and received his sight until they called for his parents.
local: Juuda yab kpilibee kaa nìn fōō ki kíí yii kijapaayee nìn sá ujɔfu ní ki wál̀ kɔŋkɔnnee, an pu ní bi tú ki yíiń u-baa nì u-na.

eng: The Jewish leaders asked them, Is this your son? Are you saying he was born blind? How can he now see.
local: ki bālifì-bi yii: «Ni-jipɔɔn baasii sɔ̀ɔɔ? Ni lafun màl̄-u nì ijɔfaaa? Ní an ŋá mana ní u wāĺ kɔŋkɔnnee?».

eng: Jesus disciples asked, Rabbi, who sinned so that he was born blind, this man or his parents.
local: Ní u-boonnooliibi bālifì-u yii: «Ukpil, ba pu ní bi màl̄ kijapaai nee nì ijɔfi ní? Úǹ u-ba di ŋá ikpiti naaa, u-baa nì u-na di ŋá ikpiti ní?».

eng: His parents answered, We know he is our son. We know he was born blind.
local: Ní u-baa nì u-na kíí-bi yii: «Tiŋman, ti-jipɔɔn dee, ti lafun màl̄-uee u sá ujɔfu ní.

eng: But we dont know how he now sees, and we dont know who healed his eyes. Ask him. Hes old enough to speak for himself.
local: Ama taa nyí an nín ŋá pu ní u wāĺ kɔŋkɔnneeyee, kaa tí nyí un di yāntì u wál̀ee mun. Ní bàlifìmaan-u, kun puee u cíkitì ki ŋūǹ kí sòoǹ u-ba.».

eng: His parents said this because they feared the Jewish authorities. This is because the Jewish authorities had already decided that whoever confessed Jesus to be the Christ would be expelled from the synagogue.
local: Kun pu u-baa nì u-na nìn sòon̄ mimmee di sá bi nìn fāŋkì Juuda yab tindaŋkpilib, kun puee bi nìn sòon̄ ki yóoǹ yii un yaa pée lī yii Yeesu sá Meesiyayee, bí jà-u kukpafidiin ú taa ti nín̄ bí bi-kansikin.

eng: Thats why his parents said, Hes old enough. Ask him.
local: Kun yì-tì u-baa nì u-na nìn bí yii u cíkitì ki ŋūǹ kí kíí u-ba, bí bàlifì-uee dee.

eng: Therefore, they called a second time for the man who had been born blind and said to him, Give glory to God. We know this man is a sinner.
local: Niinee ní bi gíti tí yíiń kijapaai kin nìn jɔ̀b̄ee nliliitiim, ní ki bí-u yii: «Á pàkà Unimbɔti! Ama timbi nyí yii uninjee sá unikpiti ní.».

eng: The man answered, I dont know whether hes a sinner. Heres what I do know: I was blind and now I see.
local: Ní u kíí-bi yii: «U yaa sá unikpitaaa, u yaa kaa sáaa, maa nyí, kun m nyée di sá m nìn sá ujɔfu ní kɔŋkɔnnee m wāĺ.».

eng: They questioned him: What did he do to you? How did he heal your eyes.
local: Ní bi gíti bālifì-u yii: «U ŋá-si mana naaa? Mana ní u ŋúbitì a-nimbiliŋ ní?».

eng: He replied, I already told you, and you didnt listen. Why do you want to hear it again? Do you want to become his disciples too.
local: Ní u kíí-bi yii: «M pée tùkū-ni ní naa pílifiǹ-mi. Ba pu ní ni là ḿ gíti bùsì kí lì nliliitiim? Àá ni mun là kí kpántí u-boonnooliibi ní?».

eng: They insulted him: You are his disciple, but we are Moses disciples.
local: Niinee ní bi bíi sìí-u yii: «Sii di sá kijapaai gbanti boonnooliu, ama timbi sá Mooyiisi boonnooliibi ní.

eng: We know that God spoke to Moses, but we dont know where this man is from.
local: Kun puee, ti nyí yii Unimbɔti nìn sòon̄ nì Mooyiisi, ama un bí nee úŋ̀wuee, taa kpáàa nyí laakin gba ní u yaa nyánnée.».

eng: Jesus answered, Neither he nor his parents. This happened so that Gods mighty works might be displayed in him.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-bi yii: «An kaa sá úǹ di ŋá ikpitaaa, an kaa tí sá u-baa nì u-na di ŋá ikpiti, ama an sá Unimbɔti ń dī u-pu kí dàkà u-tundi, an pu ní bi màl̄-u míǹ.

eng: The man answered, This is incredible! You dont know where he is from, yet he healed my eyes!.
local: Ní kijapaayee kíí-bi yii: «Tin sá maamaacee sɔ̀. Nimbi kaa nyí laakin u nyánnée, u mun di ŋúbitì man nimbiliŋ.

eng: We know that God doesnt listen to sinners. God listens to anyone who is devout and does Gods will.
local: Ti nyí yii Unimbɔti kaa pílifíǹ binikpitib, ama un yaa tɔ́-u ki ŋáań min min ní u lèe, udaan ní u pílifíǹ.

eng: No one has ever heard of a healing of the eyes of someone born blind.
local: Doooo nee, taa láá gbìl̄ dalba dalba yii ubɔ ŋúbitì unil un bi màl̄ nì ijɔfee nimbiliŋ.

eng: If this man wasnt from God, he couldnt do this.
local: An niliuee yaa kaa nyánní Unimbɔti cee, waa ŋūǹ kí ŋá míǹ.».

eng: They responded, You were born completely in sin! How is it that you dare to teach us? Then they expelled him.
local: Ní bi kíí-u yii: «Sii pée sá unikpiti doooo a-na poon ní bi nín màl̄-si, ní sii là kí dàkà timbaa!» Niinee ní bi jìn̄-u ki nyántì.

eng: Jesus heard they had expelled the man born blind. Finding him, Jesus said, Do you believe in the Human One.
local: Yeesu gbìl̄ yii bi jìn̄ un nìn sá ujɔfuee. U dāa ká-uee ní ki bí-u yii: «A fōō Kinibiki ki kííii?».

eng: He answered, Who is he, sir? I want to believe in him.
local: Ní u kíí-u yii: «Ukpil, úǹ di sá ŋma ní ḿ fōō-u kí kíí ní?».

eng: Jesus said, You have seen him. In fact, he is the one speaking with you.
local: Ní Yeesu bí yii: «A wāĺ-uaa! Úǹ di bíi sòoǹ nì si nee.».

eng: The man said, Lord, I believe. And he worshipped Jesus.
local: Ní u bí yii: «M-Dindaan, m fōō-si ki kíí,» ní ki tātìi gbāaǹ Yeesu nimbiin ki jáam̀-u.

eng: Jesus said, I have come into the world to exercise judgment so that those who dont see can see and those who see will become blind.
local: Ní Yeesu bí yii: «M dɔ́miń duulinya nee ni ibɔsoon ní. Bin yaa jɔ̀b̄ee ń wāl̀ ní bin wāĺee ń jɔ̀b̄.».

eng: While its daytime, we must do the works of him who sent me. Night is coming when no one can work.
local: Ŋwìiń nín dáá bí pu nee, tí tɔ̄ un túnní-mee tundi, kun puee kunyeeu làá mɔ́ ubɔ kaa dāa gíti ŋūǹ kí tɔ̄ ditundi.

eng: Some Pharisees who were with him heard what he said and asked, Surely we arent blind, are we.
local: Fariisab biba nìn bí niin. Bi gbìl̄ an nyɔɔbuŋŋee, ní ki bālifì-u yii: «Mimmee, ti mun sá bijɔfub deeyaaa?».

eng: Jesus said to them, If you were blind, you wouldnt have any sin, but now that you say, We see, your sin remains.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-bi yii: «Ni yaa bàà sá bijɔfubee, naa bàà cáá ikpiti. Ama ni nín bí yii ni wāĺee puee, ni-kpitii làá nīn pée dī-ni ní.».

eng: While I am in the world, I am the light of the world.
local: M nín dáá bí duulinyan doo nee, man di sá ŋwalifi ki tīī duulinya.».

eng: After he said this, he spit on the ground, made mud with the saliva, and smeared the mud on the mans eyes.
local: Yeesu lī mimmee, ní ki tíil̀ timɔtan kitiŋ ní ki dū u-ŋambiki ki mɔ́ǹ ntam ki dūu ŋmītì ujɔfuee nimbiliŋ.

eng: Jesus said to him, Go, wash in the pool of Siloam . So the man went away and washed. When he returned, he could see.
local: ní ki bí-u yii: «Nín̄ cá Siiloowee nyimbɔbilin kí fíntí a-nimbiin.» - Siiloowee taapu di sá yii: «Bi túnní-u.» - Ujɔfuee nìn búntì kí fíntí u-nimbiin. U dāa fātìi gítìńee, u kíǹ ki wāĺ ní.

eng: The mans neighbors and those who used to see him when he was a beggar said, Isnt this the man who used to sit and beg.
local: Ní u-kɔŋkɔnnaatɔb nì bin nìn kāntí u kàal̀ ki mèé animbilee bíi lì yii: «An kaa sá kijapaai nee di nìn kàal̀ doo ki mèé ki jīǹeeee?».

eng: Some said, It is, and others said, No, its someone who looks like him. But the man said, Yes, its me.
local: Ní biba bí yii: «Tiŋman, an sá úǹ!» Ní bin kíǹee bí yii: «An kaa sá úǹ, bi náań ní.» Niinee ní un nìn sá ujɔfuee bí yii: «Tiŋman, an sá man!».

eng: Jude, a slave of Jesus Christ and brother of James. To those who are called, loved by God the Father and kept safe by Jesus Christ.
local: Man Juudi, Yakubu naal un sá Yeesu Kriisitoo tɔntɔnliuee di ŋmàn̄ kugbɔŋu nee. M ŋmàn̄-ku ki tīī nimbi bin ti-Baa Unimbɔti yíiń ki néeń-ni ki dū-ni ki kpáafì ki yóoǹ Yeesu Kriisitooyee ní.

eng: But these people slander whatever they dont understand. They are destroyed by what they know instinctively, as though they were irrational animals.
local: Ama ní bíǹ sìí tin baa nyée, ní tin bi nyí kii kumooun peelii nyítamee di kúntí-bi.

eng: They are damned, for they follow in the footsteps of Cain. For profit they give themselves over to Balaams error. They are destroyed in the uprising of Korah.
local: Mbusu dɔ̄ ki cí-bi, kun puee bi táá Kayeen saŋŋu, ní ki dū bi-ba ki tīī isaŋkpiti ki kāntí animbil kii Balaam nín nìn ŋá puee, ki kúù kii Kooree yab nín nìn yíì kaa tɔ́ Unimbɔti ki kpíì puee.

eng: These people are like jagged rocks just below the surface of the water waiting to snag you when they join your love feasts. They feast with you without reverence. They care only for themselves. They are waterless clouds carried along by the winds; fruitless autumn trees, twice dead, uprooted.
local: Ni nì bi yaa kpáfì ki jīǹ kunabitiiu jikaatee, bi-bimbim bíiĺ-ni ní. Bi jīǹ tikpil, an kaa cáá-bi icoo, ki sūǹ bi-ba pu ŋma. Bi bí kii atagban ŋin kubuŋu cáa yìiǹ, ntaanyim kpá poonee, ní ki bí kii isufi in kaa pée lòoǹ kpataaayee, ki kpíì an kɔkɔ pu, ki kpīkitì.

eng: wild waves of the sea foaming up their own shame; wandering stars for whom the darkness of the underworld is reserved forever.
local: Bi bí kii tiŋku nín wòoǹ pu tinyiŋgban yíkí ki pùkufī puee ní, bi nyāntiń bi-tuuŋŋi ŋin cáá icooyee. Bi tí bí kii iŋmalbijaa in nyēē ki pùntēe ní. Unimbɔti yóoǹ-bi mpaan ŋun sá dibɔmbɔŋkpaŋkpaandee, niin ní bi làá nīn bí baabadal.

eng: Enoch, who lived seven generations after Adam, prophesied about these people when he said, See, the Lord comes with his countless holy ones.
local: Bíǹ pu ní Eenɔki un sá Adam naantitɔntɔkɔl ululuútee nìn sòon̄ dooooyee ki yóoǹ yii: «Ní pīlifì, ti-Dindaan dòoń nì u-circir tuuŋyi kutukub kutukub.

eng: to execute judgment on everyone and to convict everyone about every ungodly deed they have committed in their ungodliness as well as all the harsh things that sinful ungodly people have said against him.
local: U làá sòoǹ báà ŋma bɔti, ní kí kpɔ̀ bin kɔkɔ kaa tɔ́-uee bɔti, bi-tuŋkpitiŋ ŋin bi ŋáańee kɔkɔ nì bi nín yíì Unimbɔti kaa fōō-uee, nì bi nín kaa tɔ́-u ki sòoǹ-u tibɔfan tiŋyee kɔkɔ pu.».

eng: These are faultfinding grumblers, living according to their own desires. They speak arrogant words and they show partiality to people when they want a favor in return.
local: Binib gbantee, baabadalee, an kaa pée mɔ̄ǹ bíǹ ŋma ní, ní bi yáliŋkí ní ki ŋáań tikpitil tin fáatì-bee, ní ki sòoǹ tilambibɔti, ki cáá nyɔɔmálmal ki ŋmàtíǹ binib ki jīǹ bi-pu.

eng: But you, dear friends, remember the words spoken beforehand by the apostles of our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Ama m-nabineeŋkaamu, nimbi ń téetí ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo kpambaliŋ nín nìn sòon̄ nì ni tibɔti tin ki síiǹ dooooyee pu.

eng: They said to you, In the end time scoffers will come living according to their own ungodly desires.
local: Bi nìn tùkū-ni yii, iwinjoolkaa poonee, binib làá dāá nīn bèel̄-ni, ní ki ŋáań tikpitil tin fáatì-bee.

eng: These people create divisions. Since they dont have the Spirit, they are worldly.
local: Bíǹ di cāabìń digàntil binib kansikin. Bi nín là miŋyee di kpòotī-bi, an kaa sá Unimbɔti Fam.

eng: May you have more and more mercy, peace, and love.
local: Unimbɔti ń nīn sūǹ ni-saai baabadal, kí nīn tìì-ni ŋgbansɔŋfi tiŋan, kí nīn néeń-ni tikpil.

eng: But you, dear friends: build each other up on the foundation of your most holy faith, pray in the Holy Spirit.
local: Ama nimbee, m-nabineeŋkaamu, nín̄ mɔ̀tīi cámaan nyɔkɔpu ni-fookiiu un bí circiree poon, ni-wimmeem ń nīn cáá Unimbɔti Fam pɔŋŋu.

eng: keep each other in the love of God, wait for the mercy of our Lord Jesus Christ, who will give you eternal life.
local: Ní nīn pée gbíntì ki bí Unimbɔti un néeń-nee ŋaan, kí nīn cí ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo usaasɔlee ń dāá tīī-ti dimaŋfal din kaa cáá dikúntilee.

eng: Have mercy on those who doubt.
local: Sɔ̄maan bin dàkafī ki gùkufēe saai.

eng: Save some by snatching them from the fire. Fearing God, have mercy on some, hating even the clothing contaminated by their sinful urges.
local: kí dáaliń-bi kí nyāntiń ŋŋmi poon kí fīī-bi. Ní sɔ̄ bin kíǹee mun saai, kí nīn nyíǹ ni-ba bí taa dū bi-kpitii kí tìntī-ni. An puee, ni-ŋalii ń taa gbēē bi-bɔkutiŋ ŋin bi pēē ki ŋáań bi-jɔkɔntuuŋŋee kpaŋkpam.

eng: To the one who is able to protect you from falling, and to present you blameless and rejoicing before his glorious presence.
local: Tí nyɔ̄ŋkì Unimbɔti un di ŋūǹ kí ŋúb́-ni ní taa lítée. Úǹ di ŋūǹ kí cáá-ni kí cùnnì u-cee tinyoolin nì dipɔɔpindi, bí taa ká ni-pu tiba.

eng: to the only God our savior, through Jesus Christ our Lord, belong glory, majesty, power, and authority, before all time, now and forever. Amen.
local: Tí nyɔ̄ŋkì Unimbɔtibaanti un dī ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo pu ki fīī-tee. Úǹ di yì tinyool nì tikpaandi nì mpɔn, nì ŋŋmam doooo ŋkilkaamin nì kɔŋkɔnnee kí nīn pée cáa cá jaanjaan! Ameeni.

eng: Dear friends, I wanted very much to write to you concerning the salvation we share. Instead, I must write to urge you to fight for the faith delivered once and for all to Gods holy people.
local: M-nabineeŋkaamu, m nìn baasii mɔ̀ kí ŋmàń-ni difiil din ti-kɔkɔ ká nee bɔti. M dāa lafun kpēēyee, an pée sá yii ḿ ŋmàń-ni kí tīiń-ni kunyannyaau ní ŋmā kí nīn jáà ibaamɔn in Unimbɔti dūu tīī u-yab iaa bí kí kpántée pu.

eng: Godless people have slipped in among you. They turn the grace of our God into unrestrained immorality and deny our only master and Lord, Jesus Christ. Judgment was passed against them a long time ago.
local: Di sá yii binib biba bin kaa tɔ́ Unimbɔtee ŋá soooo ki kóoń ni-kansikin ki kpántì tiŋan tin Unimbɔti ŋá-tee bɔti, ki ŋáań min kaa kítèe, ki yíì, kaa là Yeesu Kriisitoo un baba sá ti-kpiliu ki sá ti-Dindaanee. Ama dooooyee ní ditafadaaldi din dɔ̄ ki cí bidambee pée ŋmàn̄ Unimbɔti gbɔŋkun.

eng: I want to remind you of something you already know very well. The Lord, who once saved a people out of Egypt, later destroyed those who didnt maintain their faith.
local: M nyí yii ni pée nyí tibɔti nee ki yóoǹ dooooyee, ama m là kí gíti téetí-ni ti-pu. Kéè ni nyí ti-Dindaan nín nìn fīī Isirayeel yab ki nyántì Eejipitee, boonee u dāa fātìi kúntì bin kaa nìn là kí fōō kí kííyeeyaa!.

eng: I remind you too of the angels who didnt keep their position of authority but deserted their own home. The Lord has kept them in eternal chains in the underworld until the judgment of the great day.
local: Téetímaan tin nìn jítì ituun iba puee: iaa nìn là kí nīn bí mpaan ŋun pu Unimbɔti yóoǹ-iee, ama ní ki dūu fá ki póotì i-laŋki. Unimbɔti nìn bóob́-i nì ikusaka kí nīn cá jaanjaan, ní ki dū-i ki tɔ́ dibɔmbɔŋkpaandin í nīn kā niin ki cí ibɔsooŋkpaan dal.

eng: In the same way, Sodom and Gomorrah and neighboring towns practiced immoral sexual relations and pursued other sexual urges. By undergoing the punishment of eternal fire, they serve as a warning.
local: Ní téetímaan ntimu mun bi yíì Soodoom nì Goomoora nì ntimbilimu mun nìn bí ki pìlīǹ-muee bɔti. Bi mun nìn tí ŋáaǹ kii ituunee nín nìn ŋá puee ní, ki mun nìn dū bi-ba ki tīī dijɔkɔntundi nì atuŋkpiti ŋin unil kaa máaǹ kí ŋée. An puee ní Unimbɔti nìn yāntì ŋŋmi ŋun kaa kúùyee dɔ́miń ki wùn̄-bi, ki dáaĺ bi-tafal, án nīn sá kudaaŋŋu kí dūú dàkà báà ŋma.

eng: Yet, even knowing this, these dreamers in the same way pollute themselves, reject authority, and slander the angels.
local: Binib bin kóoń ni-kansikinee, míǹ ní bi mun tí ŋáań ki bí yii bi wāĺ, ní an càntī bi cáá bi-ba ki kɔ̄ntí tijɔkɔndin, ní ki kpèé Unimbɔti pɔŋŋu fám, ki sìí Tinyooldambi bin bí yilpuee.

eng: The archangel Michael, when he argued with the devil about Moses body, did not dare charge him with slander. Instead, he said, The Lord rebuke you.
local: Ama, ŋyunti ŋun ituun kpiliu un bi yíì Mikayeelee nì Sitaan nìn bíi jáà ki kpákà Mooyiisi wunti puee, ntuunee kaa nìn kpáàa dākafì kí sìì Sitaan gba, see ŋu nín pée tùkū-u yii: «Ti-Dindaan di làá dáaĺ a-tafal.».

eng: Many people have already applied themselves to the task of compiling an account of the events that have been fulfilled among us.
local: Ukpil Teewoofiil, Binib tikpil pɔ́ɔǹ ki ŋmàn̄ ditundi din Yeesu túń ti-kansikinee bɔti.

eng: All the people who gathered to worship were praying outside during this hour of incense offering.
local: Kunipaau kɔkɔ nìn bí mmɔŋki ki mèé ŋyunti ŋun úǹ bíi tùutī talaalu nsaraafan puee.

eng: An angel from the Lord appeared to him, standing to the right of the altar of incense.
local: U bí niinee, ní ti-Dindaan tuuŋŋu ŋubɔ dɔ́miń ki yúl̀ nsaraafan cee ŋŋaŋgiil pú.

eng: When Zechariah saw the angel, he was startled and overcome with fear.
local: U ká ntuunee, ní u-pɔbil bíil̀, ní tifaandi cúú-u.

eng: The angel said, Dont be afraid, Zechariah. Your prayers have been heard. Your wife Elizabeth will give birth to your son and you must name him John.
local: Ní ntuunee bí-u yii: «Sakari, taa fàŋkì! Di sá yii Unimbɔti fōō a-wimmeem. A-nimpuu Eeliisabeeti làá dāá màal̀-si kibininjabiki, á dāá pɔ̄-kì diyindi yii Yoowaneesi.

eng: He will be a joy and delight to you, and many people will rejoice at his birth.
local: Án làá dāá nīn sá-si dipɔɔpiŋkpaandi nì mmɔn ní, ní binib tikpil làá fàal̀ u-màaltam pu.

eng: for he will be great in the Lords eyes. He must not drink wine and liquor. He will be filled with the Holy Spirit even before his birth.
local: U làá dāá nīn sá unikpaan ti-Dindaan nimbiin. Waa dāa nyù ndaam nì tinyɔkaal tin kɔkɔ pée gbéèyee. U làá dāá gbí Unimbɔti Fam doooo u-na poon ní.

eng: He will bring many Israelites back to the Lord their God.
local: U làá dāá gíiǹ Isirayeel yab kupaau ti-Dindaan bi-Nimbɔtiu cee.

eng: He will go forth before the Lord, equipped with the spirit and power of Elijah. He will turn the hearts of fathers back to their children, and he will turn the disobedient to righteous patterns of thinking. He will make ready a people prepared for the Lord.
local: U làá dāá lìntīń ti-Dindaan nyɔkɔpu kí dāá nīn cáá Unimbɔti Fam nì mpɔn kii Eelii nín nìn cáá puee. U làá yàntī bibaatiibi nì mbiyaamu ń mɔ̄ɔkì, ní kí gíiǹní bin gáatèe deedeedambi ciiŋŋun, ní kí bɔ́ntí dinikitil mmɔntiim kí yóoń ti-Dindaan.».

eng: Zechariah said to the angel, How can I be sure of this? My wife and I are very old.
local: Niinee ní Sakari bí Unimbɔti tuuŋŋuee yii: «M làá ŋá mana kí béé yii tibɔti nee sá ibaamɔn? Kun puee man púĺaaa, m-nimpuu mun púĺ.».

eng: The angel replied, I am Gabriel. I stand in Gods presence. I was sent to speak to you and to bring this good news to you.
local: Ní ntuunee bí-u yii: «Man di sá Gabriyeel , man di bí Unimbɔti nimbiin ki tūǹ atuun. Unimbɔti di túnní-mi ḿ cáań dinyɔɔbummɔɔndi gbanti kí tùkù-si.

eng: They used what the original eyewitnesses and servants of the word handed down to us.
local: Bi pée ŋmàn̄-tì kii bin nìn ká tibɔtee kílilaŋki ki kíǹ ki sòoǹ ki nàatī Unimbɔti bɔtee nín máafì-tì puee ní.

eng: Know this: What I have spoken will come true at the proper time. But because you didnt believe, you will remain silent, unable to speak until the day when these things happen.
local: ní saa fōō m-bɔti tin m sòon̄ nì si nee ki kíí. Ama an yuntiŋu yaa bàn̄ án làá ŋá míǹ. Míǹ puee, a làá kpántí umɔɔmiin, aa ti làá ŋmā kí sòoǹ kí nīn cí ŋyunti ŋun tibɔti tin m sòon̄ nee ń dāá ŋá.».

eng: Meanwhile, the people were waiting for Zechariah, and they wondered why he was in the sanctuary for such a long time.
local: Ŋyunti gbantee, an ŋá bin bí mmɔŋki ki cí Sakari ń nyānnée bitii, kun di ŋúb́-u kudii poon ní waa nyēeńee.

eng: When he came out, he was unable to speak to them. They realized he had seen a vision in the temple, for he gestured to them and couldnt speak.
local: U nín dāa nyánnée, waa ti ŋūǹ kí sòoǹ. U kíǹ ki lábití u-ŋalii mmɔɔmiin yaamin ní. Niinee ní bi béè yii Unimbɔti ŋúbitì u-nimbiliŋ u wál̀ tiba kudii poon.

eng: When he completed the days of his priestly service, he returned home.
local: Iwiin in sá Sakari ń dūú tɔ̄ kunimbɔtidii poon tundee sāāyee, ní u nín kùǹ u-dumpu.

eng: Afterward, his wife Elizabeth became pregnant. She kept to herself for five months, saying.
local: Míǹ boonee ní u-nimpuu Eeliisabeeti cáá dipool, ní ki bál̀, kaa ti nyēē áli iŋmal iŋmoo.

eng: This is the Lords doing. He has shown his favor to me by removing my disgrace among other people.
local: ní ki bí yii: «Kɔŋkɔnnee, kpèèmaan Unimbɔti nín ŋá min ki tīī-mee! U ŋá míǹ kí lēē kun nìn kɔ̄ntí-mi icoo binib kansikinee dee.».

eng: When Elizabeth was six months pregnant, God sent the angel Gabriel to Nazareth, a city in Galilee.
local: Eeliisabeeti cáá dipool iŋmal iluuyee, ní Unimbɔti tú u-tuuŋŋu Gabriyeel Galiilee tiŋki kin bi yíì Naasaareetee.

eng: to a virgin who was engaged to a man named Joseph, a descendant of Davids house. The virgins name was Mary.
local: usapɔɔn un kaa nyí uninja bi yíì-u Maariiyee cee. Usapɔɔnee nìn cáá bɔɔn, bi yíì-u Jooseefi. U nìn sá ubɔti Daafiidi maaliŋun ní.

eng: When the angel came to her, he said, Rejoice, favored one! The Lord is with you.
local: Ní Unimbɔti tuuŋŋuee kɔ́ usapɔɔnee cee ní ki bí yii: «M jáaḿ-si, sii un Unimbɔti pú diyimɔɔlee, ti-Dindaan bí a-cee.».

eng: She was confused by these words and wondered what kind of greeting this might be.
local: U jáam̀ ki dóòyee, ní Maarii fāŋkì tikpil ní ki bālifì u-ba yii: «Ba jaamii di nín yá nan dee?».

eng: Now, after having investigated everything carefully from the beginning, I have also decided to write a carefully ordered account for you, most honorable Theophilus.
local: Míǹ puee, kii m nín nyàab̄ ki cúú mbaamɔm tin jítì doooo ŋkilkaaminee, an kítì yii ma mun ń dū-tì kí ŋmà kí tīī-si.

eng: The angel said, Dont be afraid, Mary. God is honoring you.
local: Niinee ní ntuunee bí-u yii: «Maarii, taa fàŋkì, diyimɔɔl ní a ká Unimbɔti cee.

eng: Look! You will conceive and give birth to a son, and you will name him Jesus.
local: A làá cáá dipool kí màal̀ kibininjabiki, á dāá pɔ̄-kì diyindi yii Yeesu.

eng: He will be great and he will be called the Son of the Most High. The Lord God will give him the throne of David his father.
local: U dāa sá unikpaan ní. Yilpu fɔ́ŋŋŋ Nimbɔtiu dāa yíì-u u-Jipɔɔn. Úǹ ní ti-Dindaan Unimbɔti làá dū u-naanja Daafiidi beel kí tīī.

eng: He will rule over Jacobs house forever, and there will be no end to his kingdom.
local: Úǹ di làá nīn sá Isirayeel yab bɔtiu kí nīn cá jaanjaan ní u-beel kaa dāa cáá dikúntil.».

eng: Then Mary said to the angel, How will this happen since I havent had sexual relations with a man.
local: Ní Maarii bí ntuunee yii: «Maa nyí uninja, kin án làá ŋá mana ní kí bàń míǹ?».

eng: The angel replied, The Holy Spirit will come over you and the power of the Most High will overshadow you. Therefore, the one who is to be born will be holy. He will be called Gods Son.
local: Ní ntuunee kíí-u yii: «Unimbɔti Fam di làá kpákatiń a-pu, ní Yilpu Fɔ́ŋŋŋ Nimbɔtiu pɔŋŋu ń bìin̄ a-pu kii mboonee. An pu ní circir biki kin a làá màal̀ee, bi dāa yíì-kì Unimbɔti Jipɔɔn.

eng: Look, even in her old age, your relative Elizabeth has conceived a son. This woman who was labeled unable to conceive is now six months pregnant.
local: Kpèè, a-kpilintɔ Eeliisabeeti unimpunaatee mun cáá kibininjabiki pool. Úǹ un bi nìn yíì uŋmaŋŋmantee bí u-ŋmaluluutiu poon ní nee.

eng: Nothing is impossible for God.
local: Di sá yii tiba kaa bí ki pɔ̄ɔ̄ Unimbɔti cee.».

eng: Then Mary said, I am the Lords servant. Let it be with me just as you have said. Then the angel left her.
local: Ní Maarii bí yii: «M sá ti-Dindaan yumbuu ní, a nín lī puee, án ŋá míǹ.» Ní ntuunee yíkì u-cee.

eng: Mary got up and hurried to a city in the Judean highlands.
local: Ŋyunti gbanti poon ní Maarii yíkì Naasaareeti ki nín cá Juudee jooŋŋi watil pú malaa.

eng: I want you to have confidence in the soundness of the instruction you have received.
local: án nín làá ŋá pu á ŋmā kí béé yii bi nín dàkā-si tibɔti tiŋyee sá ibaamɔnee.

eng: She entered Zechariahs home and greeted Elizabeth.
local: U kɔ́ Sakari dumpu ní ki jáam̀ Eeliisabeeti.

eng: When Elizabeth heard Marys greeting, the child leaped in her womb, and Elizabeth was filled with the Holy Spirit.
local: Eeliisabeeti nín gbìl̄ Maarii jaamiiyee, ní kibiki cīŋkì u-poon, ní u gbí Unimbɔti Fam.

eng: With a loud voice she blurted out, God has blessed you above all women, and he has blessed the child you carry.
local: ní kí téeń mpɔn yii: «Unimbɔti bīiǹ a-pu ki jítiǹ binimpoobi kɔkɔ, ní ki bīiǹ kibiki kin a làá màal̀ee mun pu.

eng: Why do I have this honor, that the mother of my Lord should come to me?.
local: Ma mun ŋma ní m-Dindaan na ń dāan̄ m-cee?.

eng: As soon as I heard your greeting, the baby in my womb jumped for joy.
local: Di sá m nín pée gbìl̄ a-jaamiiyee, ní kibiki cīŋkì m-poon nì ifaal.

eng: Happy is she who believed that the Lord would fulfill the promises he made to her.
local: Án nīn mɔ̄ǹ sii un fōō ki kíí yii ti-Dindaan nín lī miŋyee, an làá lafun ŋá mimmee.».

eng: Mary said, With all my heart I glorify the Lord!.
local: Ní Maarii bí yii: «M nyɔ́ŋkí ti-Dindaan nì m-pɔbil kɔkɔ u nín sá ukpaanee puee.

eng: In the depths of who I am I rejoice in God my savior.
local: an mɔ̄ǹ-mi tikpil Unimbɔti m-Fiiliu pu.

eng: He has looked with favor on the low status of his servant. Look! From now on, everyone will consider me highly favored.
local: kun puee u tīntìń ki kpēē man u-tɔntɔnliu nín sīntiǹ u-ba puee. Ní kí yòon̄ díǹ kí nīn cée, iyunti kɔkɔ nib làá yíiń-mi diyimɔɔl daan.

eng: because the mighty one has done great things for me. Holy is his name.
local: Kun puee Unimbɔti Mpɔŋkɔdaan un bí circiree túń atuŋkpaan ki tīī-mi. U-yindi bí circir.

eng: During the rule of King Herod of Judea there was a priest named Zechariah who belonged to the priestly division of Abijah. His wife Elizabeth was a descendant of Aaron.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun Eeroodi nìn sá ubɔti Juudee tiŋkinee, usaraaŋal ubɔ nìn bí, bi yíì-u Sakari. U nìn sá usaraaŋal Abiiya kitil poon ubɔ ní. Bi nìn yíì u-nimpuu Eeliisabeeti, u mun sá Aarɔn maaliŋu poon.

eng: He shows mercy to everyone, from one generation to the next, who honors him as God.
local: U-saasɔl bí kí nīn cá abin abin ki tīī bin fàŋkí-uee.

eng: He has shown strength with his arm. He has scattered those with arrogant thoughts and proud inclinations.
local: U-pɔŋŋu ní u dūu túń atuŋkpaan, ki yāaǹ bin dàkà bi-bee.

eng: He has pulled the powerful down from their thrones and lifted up the lowly.
local: U téé bibɔtiibi bi-jaliŋ pu ki lúkú, ki yōoǹ binandambi yilpu.

eng: He has filled the hungry with good things and sent the rich away empty-handed.
local: U yāntì bin ŋkùm nìn cááyee ká digbíl mmɔntiim ní ki jìn̄ bigaajab ki gíiǹ nì iŋaŋkulim.

eng: He has come to the aid of his servant Israel, remembering his mercy.
local: U jōoń u-tɔntɔnliibi Isirayeel yab, ki téetì u-poondi pu ki ŋá-bi tiŋan.

eng: just as he promised to our ancestors, to Abraham and to Abrahams descendants forever.
local: kii u nín pée pōoǹ ti-naanjab, Abraam nì u-maaliŋu kɔkɔ puee, kí nīn cá jaanjaan.».

eng: Mary stayed with Elizabeth about three months, and then returned to her home.
local: Maarii nìn kāl̀ Eeliisabeeti cee iŋmal ita nyɔkɔm ní ki fātìi nín kùǹ.

eng: When the time came for Elizabeth to have her child, she gave birth to a boy.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun Eeliisabeeti làá màal̀ee dūu bàn̄ ní u màl̄ kibininjabiki.

eng: Her neighbors and relatives celebrated with her because they had heard that the Lord had shown her great mercy.
local: U-kɔŋkɔnnaatɔb nì u-nabiyaamu nín gbìl̄ tiŋaŋkpaandi tin ti-Dindaan ŋá ki tīī-uee puee, ní bi nì u kɔkɔ bíi fàal̀.

eng: On the eighth day, it came time to circumcise the child. They wanted to name him Zechariah because that was his fathers name.
local: An tú iwiin iniiyee, ní bi dɔ́miń ki nyántì kibiki ní ki gíí-kì dikpancandi ní ki bàà là kí pɔ̄-kì ki-baa yindi Sakari.

eng: They were both righteous before God, blameless in their observance of all the Lords commandments and regulations.
local: Bi-liitil nìn yú deedee Unimbɔti nimbiin ní ki tɔ́ ti-Dindaan marab kɔkɔ mmɔntiim.

eng: But his mother replied, No, his name will be John.
local: Ní ki-na bí yii: «Aayee, ti làá nīn yíì-u Yoowaneesi ní.».

eng: They said to her, None of your relatives have that name.
local: Ní bi bí-u yii: «A-yɔu ŋma ní bi yíì míǹ?».

eng: Then they began gesturing to his father to see what he wanted to call him.
local: Niinee ní bi bālifì ubaati mmɔɔmiiŋyaamin diyindi din u là bí pɔ̄ kibikee.

eng: After asking for a tablet, he surprised everyone by writing, His name is John.
local: ní u yāntì bi tīī-u kupiliŋu, ní u ŋmàn̄ yii: «U-yindi di sá Yoowaneesi.» Ní báà ŋma bākatì sèeee.

eng: At that moment, Zechariah was able to speak again, and he began praising God.
local: Niinee, Sakari limbil cúu kpātì, ní u kíĺ ki bíi sòoǹ ki nyɔ́ŋkí Unimbɔti.

eng: All their neighbors were filled with awe, and everyone throughout the Judean highlands talked about what had happened.
local: An yāntì tifaandi kɔ́ bi-kɔŋkɔnnaatɔb, ní binib kíǹ ki máafí an bɔti Juudee tiŋki kin sá ajoonee kɔkɔn.

eng: All who heard about this considered it carefully. They said, What then will this child be? Indeed, the Lords power was with him.
local: Bin kɔkɔ nìn gbìl̄ an bɔtee nìn bíi dàkafī ti-pu, ní ki bàlifī bi-ba yii: «An bikee làá dāá nīn bí mana dee?» Tiŋman, ti-Dindaan pɔŋŋu nìn bí kibikee pu.

eng: Johns father Zechariah was filled with the Holy Spirit and prophesied.
local: Niinee ní Unimbɔti Fam gbí Sakari, kibikee baa, ní ki yāntì u bíi nàatī Unimbɔti bɔti yii.

eng: Bless the Lord God of Israel because he has come to help and has delivered his people.
local: «Ti-Dindaan Isirayeel Nimbɔtiu ń ká inyɔŋki, kun puee u gíiǹní u-landɔkɔŋu u-niboolii pu ki fīī-bi ki líì.

eng: He has raised up a mighty savior for us in his servant Davids house.
local: U nyántìń-ti Ufiil mpɔndaan ubɔ, udaan sá u-tɔntɔnliu ubɔti Daafiidi maaliŋu poon ní.

eng: They had no children because Elizabeth was unable to become pregnant and they both were very old.
local: ama baa nìn cáá kibiki, kun puee Eeliisabeeti nìn sá uŋmaŋŋmanti, ní bi-liitil nìn púĺ.

eng: just as he said through the mouths of his holy prophets long ago.
local: Kii u nín nìn yāntì u-circir bɔnaatiliibi bin nìn bí njaminee sòon̄ ki síiǹ puee.

eng: He has brought salvation from our enemies and from the power of all those who hate us.
local: yii: u làá nyāntí-ti ti-nannanliibi nì bin kɔkɔ ná-tee ŋaan.

eng: He has shown the mercy promised to our ancestors, and remembered his holy covenant.
local: U làá dàkà ti-naanjab u-saasɔl, kí téetí u-circir poondi din u nìn pōoǹ-bee pu.

eng: the solemn pledge he made to our ancestor Abraham. He has granted.
local: kii u nín nìn pūtì ki pōoǹ ti-naanja Abraam cee puee.

eng: that we would be rescued from the power of our enemies so that we could serve him without fear.
local: U nìn pōoǹ-u yii u làá nyāntí-ti ti-nannanliibi ŋaan, tí ŋmā kí nóó-u kí taa fàŋkì.

eng: in holiness and righteousness in Gods eyes, for as long as we live.
local: kí nīn bí circir nì deedee u-nimbiin áli ti-kúm dal.

eng: You, child, will be called a prophet of the Most High, for you will go before the Lord to prepare his way.
local: Ní sii, kibiki, bi làá dāá nīn yíì-si Yilpu Fɔ́ŋŋŋ Nimbɔtiu bɔnaatiliu ní. Di sá yii sii di làá dāá nīn līī ti-Dindaan nyɔkɔpu kí ŋāŋkì u-saŋŋu.

eng: You will tell his people how to be saved through the forgiveness of their sins.
local: ní u-nibul ń béé yii u làá dāá fīī-bi kí dū bi-kpitii kí pɔ̄-bi.

eng: Because of our Gods deep compassion, the dawn from heaven will break upon us.
local: Kun puee Unimbɔti nín cáá tiŋan u-poon mɔ̄ puee, u làá yàntī yilpu walifiŋu ń ŋmíntí ti-pu kii ŋwìiń nín nyēē puee.

eng: to give light to those who are sitting in darkness and in the shadow of death, to guide us on the path of peace.
local: Ŋu làá ŋmíntí bin bí dibɔmbɔndinee nì bin bin bí ŋkúm saŋŋu puee, kí kpòotī-ti kí gíiǹ ŋgbansɔŋfi saŋŋun.».

eng: One day Zechariah was serving as a priest before God because his priestly division was on duty.
local: Sakari kitil yuntiŋu bāańee, bi fōō saraa tundi ní Sakari kɔ́ kunimbɔtidiin ki bíi tūǹ u-tundi Unimbɔti nimbiin.

eng: The child grew up, becoming strong in character. He was in the wilderness until he began his public ministry to Israel.
local: Kibikee nìn wīintí ní ki-ciiŋyi mun wīintí. U nìn kóòń kuteeun ní áli ki tin sāā daliŋkin u kíĺ ki bíi sòoǹ nì Isirayeel yab Unimbɔti bɔtee.

eng: Following the customs of priestly service, he was chosen by lottery to go into the Lords sanctuary and burn incense.
local: Kii bisaraaŋaliibi nín ŋáań puee, bi nìn tɔ́ kasi ní ki lēē-u ú kɔ́ ti-Dindaan diiku poon kí tūū talaalu.

eng: After these things, the Lord commissioned seventy-two others and sent them on ahead in pairs to every city and place he was about to go.
local: Boonee ní ti-Dindaan gíti lēē binib imuŋku ita nì saalaa nì bili biba, ki tú-bi bili bili bí lìntī u-nyɔkɔpu kí cù ntimu mun kɔkɔn úǹ u-ba bàà là kí cùee.

eng: Whenever you enter a city and the people dont welcome you, go out into the streets and say.
local: Ama ni yaa kɔ́ kitiŋ kin poon ní baa fōō-nee, nyāmaan adan pu kí lì yii.

eng: As a complaint against you, we brush off the dust of your city that has collected on our feet. But know this: Gods kingdom has come to you.
local: <Ti gbáfì ni-tiŋki kpɔŋkpɔm min táká ti-taaŋee gba ki gíiǹ ki tīī-ni. Ama nì míǹ kɔkɔee ní béé yii Unimbɔti beel sútì ki pìl̄-ni.>.

eng: I assure you that Sodom will be better off on Judgment Day than that city.
local: Ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-ni, ibɔsoon dalee, Soodoom tiŋki tafadaaldi dāa sɔ́ǹ kitiŋ gbanti yal.».

eng: How terrible it will be for you, Chorazin. How terrible it will be for you, Bethsaida. If the miracles done among you had been done in Tyre and Sidon, they would have changed their hearts and lives long ago. They would have sat around in funeral clothes and ashes.
local: «Nimbi Kɔrasin tiŋki yab, mbusu dɔ̄ ki cí-ni, nimbi Beetisayida tiŋki nib. Mbusu dɔ̄ ki cí-ni, kun puee maamaaci tuuŋŋi ŋin m ŋáaǹ nimbi cee nee di yaa bàà nìn ŋá Tiir nì Siidɔn timmunee, kɔnampu nee ntimu gbanti nib bàà nìn pēeǹ ikpalcati ki dū ŋfantam ki pùkū bi-ba ki dàkā yii bi kpántì bi-bimbim.

eng: But Tyre and Sidon will be better off at the judgment than you.
local: Míǹ puee, ibɔsoon dalee, nimbi tafadaaldi dāa jítiǹ Tiir nì Siidɔn nib tafadaaldi.

eng: And you, Capernaum, will you be honored by being raised up to heaven? No, you will be cast down to the place of the dead.
local: Nimbi Kapeernayum tiŋki yab, ni nyí yii ni làá yīkì kí tin sāā kutagbɔŋu níii? Ni làá sìntī kí bà taapu taŋkpiibi ní.».

eng: Whoever listens to you listens to me. Whoever rejects you rejects me. Whoever rejects me rejects the one who sent me.
local: Ní u gíti lī u-boonnooliibi yii: «Un yaa pílifiǹ-nee, pílifiǹ man dee. Un yaa yíì-nee, yíì man dee, ní un yaa yíì mamee yíì un túnní-mee dee.».

eng: The seventy-two returned joyously, saying, Lord, even the demons submit themselves to us in your name.
local: Binib imuŋku ita nì saalaa nì bili bin Yeesu túee fātìi bāań nì dipɔɔpindi, ní ki bí yii: «Ukpil, áli gba arasiniibi sīntiǹ bi-ba ki tīī-ti a-yindi pu!».

eng: Jesus replied, I saw Satan fall from heaven like lightning.
local: Ní Yeesu bí yii: «M ká Sitaan nín nyánní kutagbɔŋu ki bí kii ntaaŋmɔŋmɔee ki lítì.

eng: Look, I have given you authority to crush snakes and scorpions underfoot. I have given you authority over all the power of the enemy. Nothing will harm you.
local: M tùkù-ni yii m tīī-ni mpɔn ní tāā ikoobu nì inoom pu kí còom̀ kí nàan̄ Sitaan pɔŋŋu kɔkɔ, tiba kpá kí ŋá-ni tikpitil.

eng: He said to them, The harvest is bigger than you can imagine, but there are few workers. Therefore, plead with the Lord of the harvest to send out workers for his harvest.
local: U nìn bí-bi yii: «Kudiceeu wīikí, ama bidicaliibi kaa náaǹ. Mèèmaan bukpaadaan ú tɔ̄ń bitɔntɔnliibi kí kūtì bí dāá cā u-jikaati.

eng: Nevertheless, dont rejoice because the spirits submit to you. Rejoice instead that your names are written in heaven.
local: Ní taa nín fàal̀ yii arasiniibi nín sīntiǹ bi-ba ki tīī-nee ŋmaniŋma pu, ama ní nīn fàal̀ yii ni-yiŋŋi nín ŋmàn̄ yilpuee pu.».

eng: At that very moment, Jesus overflowed with joy from the Holy Spirit and said, I praise you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because youve hidden these things from the wise and intelligent and shown them to babies. Indeed, Father, this brings you happiness.
local: Niinee ní Unimbɔti Fam yāntì Yeesu kíǹ ki bíi fàal̀, ní ki lī yii: «Óooo m-Baa, yilpu nì taapu Dindaan, m nyɔ́ŋkí-si a nín dū tibɔti nee ki bál̀ bin yíì bi-ba biciŋfitiibi nì binyiliibi bí taa béé-tì, ní ki dū-tì ki dàkā bin sá ŋwaamuee. M-Baa, tiŋman an sá míǹ, kun puee míǹ di ŋān̄ a-cee ki fáatì-si.

eng: My Father has handed all things over to me. No one knows who the Son is except the Father, or who the Father is except the Son and anyone to whom the Son wants to reveal him.
local: «M-Baa Unimbɔti dū tikɔkɔ ki tīī-mi. Báà ubɔ kaa nyí man un sá Unimbɔti Jipɔɔnee asee m-Baa, míǹ ní báà ubɔ kaa nyí un di sá Ubaatee, asee man u-Jipɔɔn nì bin man u-Jipɔɔn yaa là kí dū-u kí dàkèe.».

eng: Turning to the disciples, he said privately, Happy are the eyes that see what you see.
local: Ní u fātì u-boonnooliibi pu ní ki bí bíǹ ŋmaniŋma yii: «An sá diyimɔɔl ki tīī bin wāĺ tin ni wāĺ nee.

eng: I assure you that many prophets and kings wanted to see what you see and hear what you hear, but they didnt.
local: Ibaamɔn ní m sòoǹ nì ni, Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi nì bibɔtiibi tikpil bàà nìn pée nyàab̀ kí ká tin ni wāĺ nee ní kaa ká, ki bàà nìn nyàab̀ kí gbìl̄ tin nimbi gbìl̄ nee ní kaa gbìl̄.».

eng: A legal expert stood up to test Jesus. Teacher, he said, what must I do to gain eternal life.
local: Niinee ní umaradakal ubɔ là kí píĺ Yeesu tibɔti kí kpèè u-nyɔkɔn, ní ki yíkì ki bí-u yii: «Ukpil, mana ní an sá ḿ ŋá ní kí ká dimaŋfal din kaa cáá dikúntilee?».

eng: Jesus replied, What is written in the Law? How do you interpret it.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-u yii: «An ŋmàn̄ yii ba ní ti-marau poon? Tin tin a kàaǹee sòoǹ yii ba ní?».

eng: He responded, You must love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your being, with all your strength, and with all your mind, and love your neighbor as yourself .
local: Ní uninjee kíí-u yii: «An lī yii: <Á nīn néeń a-Dindaan a-Nimbɔtiu nì a-pɔbil kɔkɔ nì a-naŋki kɔkɔ nì a-pɔŋŋu kɔkɔ nì a-landɔkɔŋu kɔkɔ, ní kí nīn néeń a-juti kii sisi a-bee.>».

eng: Jesus said to him, You have answered correctly. Do this and you will live.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-u yii: «A kíí an ŋān̄, ŋá míǹ kí dāá ká dimaŋfal gbanti.».

eng: But the legal expert wanted to prove that he was right, so he said to Jesus, And who is my neighbor.
local: Umaradakalee là kí pɔ̀ u-bee ní ki bālifì Yeesu yii: «Ŋma di sá m-juti ní?».

eng: Go! Be warned, though, that Im sending you out as lambs among wolves.
local: Nín̄ cámaan. M tú-ni, ni làá nīn bí kii apibil ŋin bí isaakpan kansikinee ní.

eng: Jesus replied, A man went down from Jerusalem to Jericho. He encountered thieves, who stripped him naked, beat him up, and left him near death.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-u yii: «Uninja ubɔ di nyánní Jeerusaleem ki sìntī Jeeriikoo, ní digaŋgandambi biba kíntì-u nsanni ki tāfì-u tiŋan pu u fìkī. Bi fúkúl̀ u-wanti pílpil, ki búntì ki yāntì-u, u kíǹ ki dɔ̄ ki fìkī.

eng: Now it just so happened that a priest was also going down the same road. When he saw the injured man, he crossed over to the other side of the road and went on his way.
local: Ní usaraaŋal ubɔ mun yíkì ki dīń nsan gbanti. U bāań ki ká kijapaayee dɔ̄ee ní u páaǹ mpampa.

eng: Likewise, a Levite came by that spot, saw the injured man, and crossed over to the other side of the road and went on his way.
local: Ní Leefii yab ubɔ mun bāań ki ká kijapaayee dɔ̄, ní u mun páaǹ mpampa.

eng: A Samaritan, who was on a journey, came to where the man was. But when he saw him, he was moved with compassion.
local: Ama usamaariija ubɔ cá nsan ki bāań ki yīkiǹ uninjee, ní ki súń u-saai.

eng: The Samaritan went to him and bandaged his wounds, tending them with oil and wine. Then he placed the wounded man on his own donkey, took him to an inn, and took care of him.
local: ní ki sútì ki pìl̄-u, ní ki ŋá u-buuŋyi ŋkpam ní ki dū ndamam mun ki ŋá, ní ki póob́-u, ki yōoǹ-u ki kāaǹ u-maŋki pu, ní ki cáá-u ki cūnnì anicandiinni, ki kpēē-u tiŋan.

eng: The next day, he took two full days worth of wages and gave them to the innkeeper. He said, Take care of him, and when I return, I will pay you back for any additional costs.
local: Kutaa wúntèe, ní u nyántì animbil ki tīī ukpil un kpèé anicandiinee, ní ki bí-u yii: <Á kpèè-mi kijapaai nee tiŋan. A yaa gíti jíń animbil min nyɔkɔm u-puee, m yaa láa fātìńee, ḿ téeǹ-si.>».

eng: What do you think? Which one of these three was a neighbor to the man who encountered thieves.
local: Ní Yeesu bālifì umaradakalee yii: «Binib bitee kansikinee, ŋma ní a ká yii u sá un digaŋgandambi kíntì ki gbáńee juti ní?».

eng: Then the legal expert said, The one who demonstrated mercy toward him. Jesus told him, Go and do likewise.
local: Ní u kíí Yeesu yii: «Un ŋá-u tiŋanee di sá u-juti.» Ní Yeesu bí-u yii: «Too, a mun cùú ŋá míǹ mbaantiim.».

eng: While Jesus and his disciples were traveling, Jesus entered a village where a woman named Martha welcomed him as a guest.
local: Yeesu nì u-boonnooliibi nìn kíĺ nsan kí nīn cá ní ki dūu bàn̄ kitimbilii kiban. Unimpu ubɔ nìn fōō-u u-dumpu, bi yíì-u Maarta.

eng: She had a sister named Mary, who sat at the Lords feet and listened to his message.
local: U nìn cáá naal ubɔ, bi yíì-u Maarii. Maarii úǹ nìn kā ti-Dindaan taaŋi taapu ki pílifí u-bɔti.

eng: Carry no wallet, no bag, and no sandals. Dont even greet anyone along the way.
local: Ní taa yòon̄ animbil, nì kuloou nì anaataka, kí taa yūĺ nsanni kí jāam̀ ubɔ.

eng: By contrast, Martha was preoccupied with getting everything ready for their meal. So Martha came to him and said, Lord, dont you care that my sister has left me to prepare the table all by myself? Tell her to help me.
local: Maarta kaa nìn cáá mpaan, di sá yii atuun kupaau nìn ŋúb́-u, ní u dɔ́miń ki bí yii: «Ukpil, a-tafal kpá nì m-naal nín yāntì man baba pée bíi tūǹ atuun kɔkɔeeee? Tùkù-u ú dāá tūtuǹ-mi.».

eng: The Lord answered, Martha, Martha, you are worried and distracted by many things.
local: Ní ti-Dindaan kíí-u yii: «Maarta, Maarta, a nìiǹ ki jìkì tiwan tikpil pu.

eng: One thing is necessary. Mary has chosen the better part. It wont be taken away from her.
local: ama kuwambambaantiiu di páb́. Kúǹ ní Maarii lēē nee, ní ubɔ kaa ŋūǹ kí fōō-ku u-cee.».

eng: Whenever you enter a house, first say, May peace be on this house.
local: Ni yaa kɔ́ kudicaŋu kunnee, ní péé péé kí jāam̀-bi yii: <Ŋgbansɔŋfi ń nīn bí kudicaŋu nee pu.>.

eng: If anyone there shares Gods peace, then your peace will rest on that person. If not, your blessing will return to you.
local: Un yaa là ŋgbansɔŋfee yaa bí niinee, ní ú fōō ni-gbansɔŋfiŋuee. Un là ŋgbansɔŋfee yaa kpá niinee, ní kpáaǹ ni-gbansɔŋfiŋuee kí búntí.

eng: Remain in this house, eating and drinking whatever they set before you, for workers deserve their pay. Dont move from house to house.
local: Ni yaa kɔ́ laakin ní bi yaa fōō-nee, nín̄ kāmaan kudicaŋu gbantin. Tin kamaa ní bi yaa pée cáań-nee, ní jī kí nyɔ̀, kun puee utɔntɔnli máaǹ tipaal. Ní taa nín̄ kóò ki póotí adiin.

eng: Whenever you enter a city and its people welcome you, eat what they set before you.
local: Ni yaa kɔ́ kitiŋ kin poon ní bi yaa fōō-nee, jīmaan tin kɔkɔ bi yaa pée cáań-nee.

eng: Heal the sick who are there, and say to them, Gods kingdom has come upon you.
local: ní kí cáam̀maan kitiŋ gbanti poon bunliibi, kí tùkù ki-nib yii: <Unimbɔti Beel sútì ki pìlīń-ni.>.

eng: Jesus was praying in a certain place. When he finished, one of his disciples said, Lord, teach us to pray, just as John taught his disciples.
local: Dalbee, Yeesu bí laaba ki mèé Unimbɔti. U mēē ki dóòyee, ní u-boonnooliibi kansikin ubɔ bí-u yii: «Ukpil, dàkà-ti bi nín mèé Unimbɔti puee, a lì kii Yoowaneesi nín nìn dàkā u-boonnooliibi puee.».

eng: Everyone who asks, receives. Whoever seeks, finds. To everyone who knocks, the door is opened.
local: Kun puee, sii un yaa mēē Unimbɔti tibee, see á fōō kudɔŋu, ní sii un yaa nyàab̀ tibee, see á ká kudɔŋu. Sii un di yaa tí yú ki kpáafée, see bí pìitī-si.

eng: Which father among you would give a snake to your child if the child asked for a fish?.
local: «Ni-kansikin ŋma di yaa cáá kibiki, ní kì yaa mēē-u ujɔm, ní ú dū ukoobu kí tīī-kì?.

eng: If a child asked for an egg, what father would give the child a scorpion?.
local: Àá, kì yaa tí mēē-u dijindi, ní ú dū unoom kí tīī-kì?.

eng: If you who are evil know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will the heavenly Father give the Holy Spirit to those who ask him.
local: Too, nimbi bin sá binikpitibee yaa nyí ki tìì ni-biyaamu tiwammɔntiil, kin ataa aŋa pu ní Ubaati un bí yilpuee kaa nín tìì Circir Fam bin kɔkɔ yaa mèé-u Ŋfam gbantee?».

eng: Jesus was throwing out a demon that causes muteness. When the demon was gone, the man who couldnt speak began to talk. The crowds were amazed.
local: Kijapaai kiba nìn bí, arasinii kā u-pu ki yāntì u kpántì umɔɔmiin. Yeesu jìn̄ arasiniiyee, ní umɔɔmiinee kíǹ ki bíi sòoǹ, ní kunipaauee bākatì sèeee.

eng: But some of them said, He throws out demons with the authority of Beelzebul, the ruler of demons.
local: Ama biba bí yii: «An sá arasiniibi bɔtiu Beelseebuul di tīī-u mpɔn ní u cáa jì arasiniibi.».

eng: Others were testing him, seeking a sign from heaven.
local: Ní biba mun là kí píĺ-u tibɔti ní ki bí-u yii ú ŋá kudaaŋŋu kubɔ bí béé yii u-pɔŋŋu gbanti baasii nyánní Unimbɔti cee.

eng: Because Jesus knew what they were thinking, he said to them, Every kingdom involved in civil war becomes a wasteland, and a house torn apart by divisions will collapse.
local: Ama Yeesu nìn nyí bi-landɔkɔi ní ki bí yii: «Dibeel din nib di yaa cúu yākatì ki jáà nì tɔbee, see dí kúntí, ní di-diiŋŋi ń lítí kí yāŋkì tɔb kí wìil̀.

eng: If Satan is at war with himself, how will his kingdom endure? I ask this because you say that I throw out demons by the authority of Beelzebul.
local: Ní ni bí yii Beelseebuul di tīī-mi mpɔn ní m jì arasiniibaaa? Mimmee, Sitaan yaa fātìi bíi jáà nì úǹ u-bee, mana ní u-beel ŋūǹ kí nīn bí ki cá!.

eng: If I throw out demons by the authority of Beelzebul, then by whose authority do your followers throw them out? Therefore, they will be your judges.
local: Man yaa cáá Beelseebuul pɔŋŋu ní ki jì arasiniibee, mimmee ni làá lì yii arasiniibi bɔtiu Beelseebuul di tīī ni-jipɔmbi mun mpɔn ní bi jì arasiniibi deeyaa! Ni-yab bi-ba dàkà yii ni bí diyìntil poon dee.

eng: Jesus told them, When you pray, say: Father, uphold the holiness of your name. Bring in your kingdom.
local: Ní u bí-bi yii: «Ni yaa bíi mèéyee, ní lì yii: <Ti-Baa, yàntī bí béé yii a sá Unimbɔti circirdaan, kí yàntī a-Beel ń dāan̄.

eng: But if I throw out demons by the powerof God, then Gods kingdom has already overtaken you.
local: Ama, an yaa sá Unimbɔti pɔŋŋu ní m cáa jì arasiniibee, mimmee, an dàkà yii Unimbɔti Beel pɔŋŋu dɔ́miń ni-cee dee.

eng: When a strong man, fully armed, guards his own palace, his possessions are secure.
local: «Upɔɔl yaa kā nì u-janjawanti ki cí u-diikuee, tiba kaa gbèetī u-wanti.

eng: But as soon as a stronger one attacks and overpowers him, the stronger one takes away the armor he had trusted and divides the stolen goods.
local: Ama ubɔ mun yaa gíti bí ní ki pɔ̄ɔǹ-uee, u dòoń kí já nì u kí fōō u-janjawanti tin pu u mákée, ní kí kóoǹ u-wanti kí yàkatī u-yab.

eng: Whoever isnt with me is against me, and whoever doesnt gather with me, scatters.
local: «Un yaa kaa bí man jandi púee jáà nì man dee, ní un yaa kaa bíi tútúǹ-mi m kōontí ki kpáfée, bíi yàantí dee.».

eng: When an unclean spirit leaves a person, it wanders through dry places looking for a place to rest. But it doesnt find any. Then it says, Ill go back to the house I left.
local: «Arasinii yaa nyáń unilinee, u búntí kuteeu pu ní ki yìiǹ ki nyàab̀ ŋkɔŋkoon. U yaa nyàab̄ ki gīī, kaa kée, ní ú bí yii: <M làá fàtīí gítí laakin m nìn nyánnée.>.

eng: When it arrives, it finds the house cleaned up and decorated.
local: U yaa gítìń ki ká bi ŋātì kudii ki bɔ́ntì-kuee.

eng: Then it goes and brings with it seven other spirits more evil than itself. They go in and make their home there. That person is worse off at the end than at the beginning.
local: ní ú cùú nyàabìń arasiniibi biluli bin cáá ntoom ki jítiǹ úŋ̀wuee, ní bí dāan̄ kí kpāfì kí kàl̄ udaan pu, ní ú mɔ̀tīí bīil̀ kí jītíǹ mpeepeekaam.».

eng: While Jesus was saying these things, a certain woman in the crowd spoke up: Happy is the mother who gave birth to you and who nursed you.
local: Yeesu bíi sòoǹ mimmee, ní unimpu ubɔ téeń kunipaaun yii: «Unimpu un cáá a-pool ki màl̄-si a ŋáam̀ u-biilee bí mmɔnneeee!».

eng: But he said, Happy rather are those who hear Gods word and put it into practice.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-u yii: «Bin baasii bí mmɔnnee di sá bin pílifí Unimbɔti bɔti ki ŋáań tì nín lī puee!».

eng: When the crowds grew, Jesus said, This generation is an evil generation. It looks for a sign, but no sign will be given to it except Jonahs sign.
local: Kunipaau dɔ́miń ki wíiǹ Yeesu ceeyee, ní u bí yii: «Nimbi waanee nib sá bikpitib, ki bàlifī ḿ ŋá kudaaŋŋu kubɔ. Ama naa làá ká kudaaŋŋu kubɔ, asee kun náań nì Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu Yoona yɔkɔee.

eng: Give us the bread we need for today.
local: Tīī-ti kutaa sá kamaa kun ti làá jēe.

eng: Just as Jonah became a sign to the people of Nineveh, so the Human Onewill be a sign to this generation.
local: Kii Yoona nín nìn sá kudaaŋŋu ki tīī Niiniifi yab puee, míǹ ní man Kinibiki làá nīn sá kudaaŋŋu ki tīī nimbi waanee nib.

eng: The queen of the South will rise up at the judgment with the people of this generation and condemn them, because she came from a distant land to hear Solomons wisdom. And look, someone greater than Solomon is here.
local: Ibɔsoon dalee, unimpubɔti un nìn nyánní Seeba tiŋkinee làá dāá yīkì kí kpɔ̀ nimbi waanee nib bɔti, kun puee u nìn nyánní kitiŋkuntinyɔkɔ ki dɔ́miń ki pílifì Saloomɔn ciiŋyi. Ama ubɔ bí doo ki jítiǹ Saloomɔn.

eng: The people of Nineveh will rise up at the judgment with this generation and condemn it, because they changed their hearts and lives in response to Jonahs preaching—and one greater than Jonah is here.
local: Ibɔsoon dalee, kitiŋ kin bi yíì Niiniifee yab làá dāá yīkì nimbi waanee nib pu kí kpɔ̀ ni-bɔti, kun puee Yoona nín nìn sòon̄ nì bi Unimbɔti bɔtee, bi nìn kpántì bi-bimbim. Ama ubɔ bí doo ki jítiǹ Yoona ní ki sòon̄ ní nimbi kaa kpántì ni-bimbim.».

eng: People dont light a lamp and then put it in a closet or under a basket. Rather, they place the lamp on a lampstand so that those who enter the house can see the light.
local: «Ubɔ kaa tùutī fitila kí dūú báĺ, kaa tí dù dibool kí cíkiń u-pu. Bi dùú tɔ́kɔń kifaŋkɔlii pu ní bin kóòńee ń nīn wāĺ.

eng: Your eye is the lamp of your body. When your eye is healthy, your whole body is full of light. But when your eye is bad, your whole body is full of darkness.
local: A-nimbiliŋ sá a-wunti fitilau ní. A-nimbiliŋ yaa ŋān̄ee, a-wunti kɔkɔ wāĺ ní, ama ŋi yaa kaa ŋān̄ee, a-wunti kɔkɔ kɔ́ dibɔmbɔndin dee.

eng: Therefore, see to it that the light in you isnt darkness.
local: Míǹ puee, á kpèè tiŋan ŋwalifi ŋun bí a-nee ń taa kpántí dibɔmbɔndi.

eng: If your whole body is full of light—with no part darkened—then it will be as full of light as when a lamp shines brightly on you.
local: A-wunti kɔkɔ yaa wāĺ ní dijandi diba yaa kaa bí dibɔmbɔndinee, mimmee a-wunti kɔkɔ bí ŋwalifin dee, kii bi yaa tūū fitila ŋwalifi nín ŋmíntí-si pu a wāĺ puee.».

eng: While Jesus was speaking, a Pharisee invited him to share a meal with him, so Jesus went and took his place at the table.
local: Yeesu sòon̄ míǹ ki dóòyee, ní Fariisa ubɔ yíiń-u yii ú dāan̄ bí jī tijin, ní Yeesu kɔ́ u-cee ki kāl̀ kí jī.

eng: When the Pharisee saw that Jesus didnt ritually purify his hands by washing before the meal, he was astonished.
local: Fariisee ká yii Yeesu kaa fíntì u-ŋalii, kii bi-marau nín sá bí ŋá pu kí wàatī kí nín jī tijinee, ní an ŋá-u bitii.

eng: The Lord said to him, Now, you Pharisees clean the outside of the cup and platter, but your insides are stuffed with greed and wickedness.
local: Ní ti-Dindaan bí-u yii: «Nimbi Fariisab, ni fíntí tiyuti nì asambil boon kaa fíntí poon. Diwaŋfikil nì ntoom gbí ni-pɔbiliŋ poon.

eng: Forgive us our sins, for we also forgive everyone who has wronged us. And dont lead us into temptation.
local: Dū ti-kpitii kí pɔ̄-ti, kun puee ti mun pūǹ bin ŋáań-ti ikpitee bi-kpitii. Taa pɔ̄ mpaan Sitaan ń kīil̀-ti.>».

eng: Foolish people! Didnt the one who made the outside also make the inside?.
local: Bigbaambi, Unimbɔti un ŋá boonee, úǹ di kaa tí ŋá poonaaa?.

eng: Therefore, give to those in need from the core of who you are and you will be clean all over.
local: An bàà sá ní ŋá biwɔfib tiŋan, ní tikɔkɔ ń ŋāŋkì kí tīī-ni.

eng: How terrible for you Pharisees! You give a tenth of your mint, rue, and garden herbs of all kinds, while neglecting justice and love for God. These you ought to have done without neglecting the others.
local: «Mbusu bí ki tīī nimbi Fariisab, kun puee ni lèekī saalaa kamaa pu kubɔ kubɔ goosi faati nì ditunnuul nì tikuŋfaal tin kɔkɔ kíǹee pu ki tìì Unimbɔti, ama ní kaa là kí nīn bí deedee kí nīn néeń Unimbɔti. Kin, min ni bàà máaǹ kí péé kí ŋá kí wàatī kí nín ŋá tin kíǹee dee.

eng: How terrible for you Pharisees! You love the most prominent seats in the synagogues and respectful greetings in the marketplaces.
local: «Mbusu bí ki tīī nimbi Fariisab, kun puee ni néeń ki kàal̀ nyɔkɔpu jaliŋ akpafidiinni, ki néeń bí nīn jáaḿ-ni sampaambi pu ki tìì-ni jilma.

eng: How terrible for you! You are like unmarked graves, and people walk on them without recognizing it.
local: Mbusu bí ki tīī-ni, kun puee ni bí kii akaakul ŋin jīlifì ki nákáǹ kitiŋ, á dāá còom̀ kí jītí ŋi-pu, kaa kpāantíí béé yii tiwan tin kaa ŋān̄ee pu ní a jítèe ní.».

eng: One of the legal experts responded, Teacher, when you say these things, you are insulting us too.
local: Ní umaradakal ubɔ bí yii: «Ukpil, a nín sòoǹ pu nee, a sìí ki kpāantí timbi deeyaa!».

eng: Jesus said, How terrible for you legal experts too! You load people down with impossible burdens and you refuse to lift a single finger to help them.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-u yii: «Mbusu bí ki tīī nimbi bimaradakaliibi mun, kun puee ni cáá awaŋgul ŋin nyìm̀ee ki tūntí binib ní nimbi ni-ba kaa là kí dū ni-ŋambiki kí dáb́ kí tūtuǹ-bi.

eng: How terrible for you! You built memorials to the prophets, whom your ancestors killed.
local: Mbusu bí ki tīī-ni, kun puee ni māā Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi bin ni-naanjab nìn kūūyee akaakumɔnti.

eng: In this way, you testify that you approve of your ancestors deeds. They killed the prophets, and you build memorials!.
local: Ni ŋáań míǹ kí dàkà yii ni-naanjab nín nìn túń atuun ŋiŋyee ŋān̄ ni-cee, kun puee bíǹ kūū Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi, ní nimbi māā bi-kaakuliŋ.

eng: Therefore, Gods wisdom has said, I will send prophets and apostles to them and they will harass and kill some of them.
local: An pu ní Unimbɔti iciindaan nìn lī yii: <M làá tɔ̄ń m-bɔnaatiliibi nì m-kpambaliŋ bi-cee: bi làá kpɔ̀ biba ní kí sàaǹ bin kíǹee.>.

eng: He also said to them, Imagine that one of you has a friend and you go to that friend in the middle of the night. Imagine saying, Friend, loan me three loaves of bread.
local: Ní Yeesu gíti bí-bi yii: «Tí dūú ŋmàntī yii ni-kansikin ubɔ di yaa cáá bɔɔ ní ki yaa cūtì u-cee kunyataasiiu, ki bí-u yii. <M-bɔɔ, pìnnì-mi kpɔnɔb bita.

eng: As a result, this generation will be charged with the murder of all the prophets since the beginning of time.
local: An puee, nimbi waanee nib làá ká ditafadaaldi Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi bin kɔkɔ ni kūūyee pu, kí yòon̄ doooo Unimbɔti nín náań duulinya.

eng: This includes the murder of every prophet—from Abel to Zechariah—who was killed between the altar and the holy place. Yes, Im telling you, this generation will be charged with it.
local: kí yòon̄ní Abeel kí dāá sāā Sakari un bi nìn kūū nsaraafan nì kunimbɔtidii kansikinee. Tiŋman ní m tùkù-ni, nimbi waanee nib làá ká ditafadaaldi nnikpaakoon gbanti pu.

eng: How terrible for you legal experts! You snatched away the key of knowledge. You didnt enter yourselves, and you stood in the way of those who were entering.
local: «Mbusu bí ki tīī nimbi bimaradakaliibi, kun puee ni pīiǹ dinyil punyɔkɔ ki lēē saafii. Naa làá kɔ́ ni-ba ní ki jì bin bàà là kí kɔ́ee.».

eng: As he left there, the legal experts and Pharisees began to resent him deeply and to ask him pointed questions about many things.
local: Yeesu nyáń Fariisee dumpuee, ní bimaradakaliibi nì Fariisab fōō aŋuu ŋkpitim pu ní ki kíǹ ki bíi bàlifī-u tibɔti tikpil.

eng: They plotted against him, trying to trap him in his words.
local: Bi bíi pīil̀-u tibɔti ki là ú sòoǹ kí yìntī bí ká nsan kí cúú-u.

eng: because a friend of mine on a journey has arrived and I have nothing to set before him.
local: kun puee m-bɔɔ ubɔ nyánní nsan ki kɔ́-mi ní maa cáá báà tiba kí ŋá-u tijin.>.

eng: Imagine further that he answers from within the house, Dont bother me. The door is already locked, and my children and I are in bed. I cant get up to give you anything.
local: U-bɔɔyee yaa kíiń-u kudii poon yii: <Taa páb́-mi, man pīiǹ kudii, man nì m-biyaamu kɔ́ ki dōoǹ, maa ti ŋūǹ kí yīkì kí tīī-si kpɔnɔb.>.

eng: I assure you, even if he wouldnt get up and help because of his friendship, he will get up and give his friend whatever he needs because of his friends brashness.
local: M tùkù-ni ibaamɔn ní, báà u yaa kaa làá yīkì kí tīī-u kpɔnɔb u nín sá u-bɔɔyee puee, see ú yīkì kí tīī-u tin kɔkɔ u nyàab̀ee u nín pée gbíntìi páb́-uee pu.

eng: And I tell you: Ask and you will receive. Seek and you will find. Knock and the door will be opened to you.
local: Míǹ pu ní man tùkù-ni yii, mèèmaan Unimbɔti, u làá tīī-ni. Nyàab̀maan, u làá yàntī ní ká. Kpāafìmaan, u làá pìitī-ni.

eng: When a crowd of thousands upon thousands had gathered so that they were crushing each other, Jesus began to speak first to his disciples: Watch out for the yeast of the Pharisees—I mean, the mismatch between their hearts and lives.
local: Ŋyunti gbantee, binib nìn kpáfì Yeesu cee ki wīikí tikpil, an kaa cáá ŋkaan. Bi nín múkú tikpil puee, bi nìn kíǹ ki tà tɔb ní. Ní u kíĺ ki bíi sòoǹ nì u-boonnooliibi ki lī yii: «Tikɔkɔ poonee, ní nīn nyíǹ ni-ba nì Fariisab kpɔnɔnyɔkɔŋu pu, an dàkà yii bi-poonnyooŋŋu.

eng: Anyone who speaks a word against the Human Onewill be forgiven, but whoever insults the Holy Spirit wont be forgiven.
local: «Un di yaa sòon̄ ki bíil̀ man Kinibikee ŋūǹ kí ká u-kpitii pɔ̄tam, ama un di yaa sòon̄ ki bíil̀ Unimbɔti Famee kpá kí ká u-kpitii pɔ̄tam.

eng: When they bring you before the synagogues, rulers, and authorities, dont worry about how to defend yourself or what you should say.
local: «Bi yaa láa cúú-ni ki cūnnì akpafidiinni nì bitindaŋkpilib nì biyidambi nimbiin kí sòoǹ ni-bɔtee, ni nín máaǹ kí sòoǹ pu kí ŋmátée bɔti ń taa páb́-ni.

eng: The Holy Spirit will tell you at that very moment what you must say.
local: Di sá yii ŋyunti gbanti yaa bāańee, Unimbɔti Fam làá dàkà-ni tin sá ní sòoǹee.».

eng: Someone from the crowd said to him, Teacher, tell my brother to divide the inheritance with me.
local: Kunipaau kansikinee ní unil ubɔ bí-u yii: «Ukpil, tùkù m-maan ú yàkatī ti-baa fakal kí lēē man jandi kí tīī-mi.».

eng: Jesus said to him, Man, who appointed me as judge or referee between you and your brother.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-u yii: «Óò kijapaai, ŋma di kāaǹ-mi ni-bɔsoonliu nì ni-waŋyakatiliu?».

eng: Then Jesus said to them, Watch out! Guard yourself against all kinds of greed. After all, ones life isnt determined by ones possessions, even when someone is very wealthy.
local: Ní u bí báà ŋma yii: «Ní nīn nyíǹ ni-ba tiŋan kí taa nín̄ cáá nnimbitaataam, kun puee báà unil yaa yāntì ki sá ugaaja min, u-gaajawanti di kaa yì-tì ní u cáá dimaŋfal.».

eng: Then he told them a parable: A certain rich mans land produced a bountiful crop.
local: Ní u dū tibɔti nee ki ŋmāntì yii: «Ugaaja ubɔ di nìn bí ki cáá tikpaatan ki kùn̄, ní an lòon̄ tiŋan.

eng: He said to himself, What will I do? I have no place to store my harvest!.
local: Ní u lī u-ban yii: <M làá pée gíti ŋá mana deeyaaa? Maa ti cáá mpaan laaba kí cáá m-jikaati kí ŋá.>.

eng: Then he thought, Heres what Ill do. Ill tear down my barns and build bigger ones. Thats where Ill store all my grain and goods.
local: Niinee ní u bí yii: <M béè m nín làá ŋá puee: m làá kpìtī m-cakalaabi ní kí fàtīí gāā bikpaŋkpaambi, ní kí kóoǹ m-jikaati kɔkɔ nì m-wanti tin kíǹee kí gbéeǹ.

eng: Ill say to myself, You have stored up plenty of goods, enough for several years. Take it easy! Eat, drink, and enjoy yourself.
local: M yaa ŋá míǹ ki dóòyee, ní ḿ bí m-ba yii: M cáá tikɔkɔ nì tikɔkɔ tikpil kí jī kí nyɔ̀ kí nīn fàal̀ kí ŋùŋfì áli abin kupaau.>.

eng: Nothing is hidden that wont be revealed, and nothing is secret that wont be brought out into the open.
local: Tin kɔkɔ bí dibalinee, bi làá bìitī ti-pu, ní tin kɔkɔ baa nyée, bi làá béé-tì.

eng: But God said to him, Fool, tonight you will die. Now who will get the things you have prepared for yourself.
local: Ama ní Unimbɔti bí-u yii: <Ugbaan, kunyeeu nee gba, m làá fàtīí fōō a-naŋki. Tin kɔkɔ a bɔ́ntì ki yóoǹ a-bee, ubɔ di làá nīn kíǹ ki yì.>».

eng: This is the way it will be for those who hoard things for themselves and arent rich toward God.
local: Ní Yeesu gíti lī yii: «Míǹ ní án làá ŋá un kamaa nyàab̀ tigaajawan ki yōontí u-ba, ama ní kaa sá ugaaja Unimbɔti nimbiinee.».

eng: Then Jesus said to his disciples, Therefore, I say to you, dont worry about your life, what you will eat, or about your body, what you will wear.
local: Míǹ boonee ní Yeesu bí u-boonnooliibi yii: «An pu ní m tùkù-ni yii ní taa nín̄ nìiǹ tipɔjin nì tiwampeeŋkaal pu.

eng: There is more to life than food and more to the body than clothing.
local: kun puee dimaŋfal jítiǹ tijin, ní tiwun mun jítiǹ tiwampeeŋkaal.

eng: Consider the ravens: they neither plant nor harvest, they have no silo or barn, yet God feeds them. You are worth so much more than birds!.
local: Kpèèmaan inoon! Iaa būul̀, kaa cēē, kaa cáá kukaatuŋu, kaa tí cáá cakalaa, ama Unimbɔti kpīintí-i. Kin nimbi bin jítiǹ inoon kpataaayee fáà?.

eng: Who among you by worrying can add a single moment to your life?.
local: Báà a yaa bíi yáliŋkí nì a-ba, saa ŋūǹ kí gbéeǹ a-maŋfal fɔkɔŋŋu pu fiii.

eng: If you cant do such a small thing, why worry about the rest?.
local: Ni yaa kaa ŋūǹ kí ŋá tin bí fiii mimmee, kin ba pu ní ni yáliŋkí tin kíǹee pu?.

eng: Notice how the lilies grow. They dont wear themselves out with work, and they dont spin cloth. But I say to you that even Solomon in all his splendor wasnt dressed like one of these.
local: Kpèèmaan mpumpukum! Maa tūǹ ditundi ki gì, kaa lù satab, ama m tùkù-ni yii ubɔti Saloomɔn gba, nì u-gaajati kɔkɔee, waa ŋúń ki ká tiwannyaandi ki pēeǹ an bàn̄ mpumpukum nee kansikin miba nyaaŋku.

eng: If God dresses grass in the field so beautifully, even though its alive today and tomorrow its thrown into the furnace, how much more will God do for you, you people of weak faith!.
local: Unimbɔti yaa nyāantí míǹ timool tin bí tisatin díǹ fool ŋŋmi ń dāá wà-tèe, mimmee waa làá ŋá án jītíǹ míǹ kí tīī-naaa? Ba pu ní ni-fookiiu kaa náaǹ mimmee?.

eng: Dont chase after what you will eat and what you will drink. Stop worrying.
local: Ní taa nín̄ bìǹ ki nyàab̀ tin ni làá jēe nì tin ni làá nyɔ̀ee, kí taa nín̄ páb́ ni-ba an wanti pu.

eng: Therefore, whatever you have said in the darkness will be heard in the light, and whatever you have whispered in the rooms deep inside the house will be announced from the rooftops.
local: An puee, tin kɔkɔ ni yaa láa lī dibɔmbɔndinee, ŋwiimpu ní bi làá dāá gbìl̄-tì, ní tin ni yaa láa kɔ́ kudii poon ki lɔ́kɔfèe, mpaampiin pu ní bi làá dāá bāakì-tì.».

eng: All the nations of the world long for these things. Your Father knows that you need them.
local: Duulinya nib bin kaa nóó Unimbɔtee di nyàab̀ tíǹ kɔkɔ kaa gbèentí, ama nimbee, ni cáá Ubaati un nyí yii an bàlifī ní nīn cáá an wantee.

eng: Instead, desire his kingdom and these things will be given to you as well.
local: Péé kí nyàab̀maan Unimbɔti Beel, ní ú tīī-ni kí kpáaǹ tin kɔkɔ kíǹee.».

eng: Dont be afraid, little flock, because your Father delights in giving you the kingdom.
local: «Nimbi bin sá kinikitiwaayee, taa fàŋkìmaan, ni-Baa lēē yii u làá tīī-ni Dibeel.

eng: Sell your possessions and give to those in need. Make for yourselves wallets that dont wear out—a treasure in heaven that never runs out. No thief comes near there, and no moth destroys.
local: Nyāfìmaan ni-wanti kí dūú tīīmaan bisoodambi. Mimmee ni síiǹ animbitɔkɔl ŋin kaa cífée nì tigaajati tin kaa dóotée dee yilpu, laakin binaayub kaa ŋūǹ kí yɔ̀ɔɔ, dikɔtuul mun kaa ŋūǹ kí mɔ́ee.

eng: Where your treasure is, there your heart will be too.
local: Kun puee laakin a-gaajati bée, niin ní a-landɔkɔŋu mun bí.».

eng: Be dressed for service and keep your lamps lit.
local: «Bɔ́ntímaan ni-ba, kí nīn gbìn̄ ni-dambalab, ni-fitilab ń nīn fòol̄ ki cí m-gítintam.

eng: Be like people waiting for their master to come home from a wedding celebration, who can immediately open the door for him when he arrives and knocks on the door.
local: Ní nīn bí kii bitɔntɔnliibi bin kpiliu búntì animpucaŋgbiil, bi kā ki cí-u, u yaa láa bāań ki fáá kipunyɔkɔ bí pìitī-uee.

eng: Happy are those servants whom the master finds waiting up when he arrives. I assure you that, when he arrives, he will dress himself to serve, seat them at the table as honored guests, and wait on them.
local: An sá mmɔn ki tīī bitɔntɔnliibi bin kpiliu bāań ki ká bi kā ki wāĺ, kaa gèeǹee. Ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-ni, u làá dū ditɔntɔmbɔkutil kí pèen̄ ní kí yàntī bí kàl̄, ní ú cáań tijin kí tīī-bi.

eng: Happy are those whom he finds alert, even if he comes at midnight or just before dawn.
local: Báà an yaa sá kunyataasiiu àá ŋkoosiim ní u yaa bāań ki ká bi kā kaa gèeǹee, an sá mmɔn ki tīī bidambi.

eng: But know this, if the homeowner had known what time the thief was coming, he wouldnt have allowed his home to be broken into.
local: Ní gbìl̄maan tinee mun. Kudiidaan yaa nyí ŋyunti ŋun unaayuu làá dāan̄ kí yɔ̀-uee, u bàà kàal̀ kí cīití ní bí taa láa cíĺ u-diiku kí yɔ̀-u.

eng: I tell you, my friends, dont be terrified by those who can kill the body but after that can do nothing more.
local: «Nimbi m-bɔɔbi ní m tùkù tibɔti nee. Ní taa fàŋkìmaan bin kù binib, ní boonee kaa ti ŋūǹ kí ŋá tiba kí kūtèe.

eng: You also must be ready, because the Human Oneis coming at a time when you dont expect him.
local: Míǹ mbaantiim, ni mun ń bɔ́ntí kí nīn címaan, kun puee man Kinibiki làá bàń ŋyunti ŋun naa bàà dàkafēe ní.».

eng: Peter said, Lord, are you telling this parable for us or for everyone.
local: Ní Peetroo bí yii: «Ukpil, tibɔŋmantikaal gbantee, a lì timbi baba naaa, a lì báà ŋma ní?».

eng: The Lord replied, Who are the faithful and wise managers whom the master will put in charge of his household servants, to give them their food at the proper time?.
local: Ní ti-Dindaan kíí-u yii: «Tibɔŋmantikaal nee sá ki tīī utɔntɔnli kamaa un yú deedee ki cáá iciin, u-kpiliu dū u-cindi yab ki ŋúbiǹ-u ú nīn kpèé ki tìì-bi tijin ŋyunti ŋun sá ú tīī-bee ní.

eng: Happy are the servants whom the master finds fulfilling their responsibilities when he comes.
local: An sá mmɔn ki tīī utɔntɔnli gbanti u-kpiliu yaa bāań ki ká u bíi ŋáań min u tùkū-uee.

eng: I assure you that the master will put them in charge of all his possessions.
local: Ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-ni, u-kpiliu làá dū u-gaajati kɔkɔ ní kí ŋúbiǹ-u.

eng: But suppose that these servants should say to themselves, My master is taking his time about coming. And suppose they began to beat the servants, both men and women, and to eat, drink, and get drunk.
local: Ama ní utɔntɔnlee yaa bí yii: <M-kpiliu yúntìń tikpil,> ní ki kíĺ ki bíi gbáà bitɔntɔnliibi bin kíǹee bininjab nì binimpoobi, ní ki jīǹ ki nyù ki gbéè.

eng: The master of those servants would come on a day when they werent expecting him, at a time they couldnt predict. The master will cut them into pieces and assign them a place with the unfaithful.
local: niinee ní u-kpiliu ń dāá fàtīń ŋwiin ŋun pu waa dàkafī yii u làá gítińee nì kukúluu kun pu waa nyée. U yaa bāańee, see ú tɔ̀ntī-u ní kí dū-u kí tɔ̄ bin yíì kaa fōō Unimbɔtee laŋki.

eng: That servant who knew his masters will but didnt prepare for it or act on it will be beaten severely.
local: «Utɔntɔnli un yaa nyí min ní u u-kpiliu là, ama ní kaa bɔ́ntì míǹ ki tīī-uee, u-gbati làá nīn wīikí tikpil.

eng: The one who didnt know the masters will but who did things deserving punishment will be beaten only a little. Much will be demanded from everyone who has been given much, and from the one who has been entrusted with much, even more will be asked.
local: Ama, utɔntɔnli un yaa kaa nyí min u-kpiliu lèe ní ki yīntiǹ-uee, u-gbati kaa dāa náaǹ. Sii un Unimbɔti yaa tīī tikpilee, u dāa bàlifī-si tikpil mun ní, ní sii un Unimbɔti yaa ti kpāaǹ tikpilee, see ú dāá bàlifì-si tikpil mun.».

eng: I came to cast fire upon the earth. How I wish that it was already ablaze!.
local: Ní Yeesu bí yii: «M dɔ́miń kí cáań ŋŋmi kí tɔ̄ kitiŋ pu ní. M bàà là ŋú fōō dooooyee gba ní.

eng: Ill show you whom you should fear: fear the one who, after you have been killed, has the authority to throw you into hell. Indeed, I tell you, thats the one you should fear.
local: M làá dàkà-ni un ni máaǹ kí fàŋkèe. Fàŋkìmaan Unimbɔti un ŋūǹ kí lēē dimaŋfal ní ki gíti cáá mpɔn kí dū kinaŋ kí tɔ̄ ŋŋmin ŋkúm boonee. Tiŋman ní m tùkù-ni, un ní ni máaǹ kí fàŋkèe dee.

eng: I have a baptism I must experience. How I am distressed until its completed!.
local: M làá fàl̀ falaa kii nnyimee. An puee m bí digìŋ́kpaandin kí nīn cí ŋyunti ŋun án làá dāá ŋée.
