eng: Do you think that I have come to bring peace to the earth? No, I tell you, I have come instead to bring division.
local: Ni nyí yii m dɔ́miń kí cáań ŋgbansɔŋfi kitiŋ pu níii? Aayee, m cáań diyakatil ní.

eng: From now on, a household of five will be divided—three against two and two against three.
local: Di sá yii, kí yòon̄ kí nīn cée, binib yaa bí biŋmoo kudicaŋu poonee, bi làá yàkatī ní. Bita kaa làá nīn gbìliǹ bili, ní bili mun kaa làá nīn gbìliǹ bita.

eng: Father will square off against son and son against father; mother against daughter and daughter against mother; and mother-in-law against daughter-in-law and daughter-in-law against mother-in-law.
local: Ubaati kaa làá nīn gbìliǹ u-jipɔɔn ní u-jipɔɔn mun kaa làá nīn gbìliǹ ubaati. Unaati kaa làá nīn gbìliǹ u-bisal, ní u-bisal mun kaa làá nīn gbìliǹ-u. Unimpu kaa làá nīn gbìliǹ u-coopu, ní u-coopu mun kaa làá nīn gbìliǹ-u.».

eng: Jesus also said to the crowds, When you see a cloud forming in the west, you immediately say, Its going to rain. And indeed it does.
local: Ní Yeesu bí kunipaauee yii: «Ni yaa ká atagban yaa yíkìń ŋwìiń jeetilaŋkee, ní ní lì yii: <Utaa làá nì,> ní ú lafun nì.

eng: And when a south wind blows, you say, A heat wave is coming. And it does.
local: Ní dipeel buŋku yaa bāańee, ní ní bí yii: <Ti làá kɔ́ kutuntuŋun dee,> ní an lafun ŋáań míǹ.

eng: Hypocrites! You know how to interpret conditions on earth and in the sky. How is it that you dont know how to interpret the present time?.
local: Milimilidambi, ni nyí ki gàntī yilpu nì taapu daanti, ba pu ní naa ŋūǹ kí béé tin bíi jītí kɔŋkɔnneeyee?».

eng: And why dont you judge for yourselves what is right?.
local: «Ba pu ní naa dàkafī kí lēē min bí deedeeyee ni-ba?».

eng: As you are going to court with your accuser, make your best effort to reach a settlement along the way. Otherwise, your accuser may bring you before the judge, and the judge hand you over to the officer, and the officer throw you into prison.
local: Yeesu kútì ki lī yii: «Ubɔ yaa cáǹ-si ibɔsoon, ŋyunti ŋun ní ni dáá bí nsanni ki cée, pɔ́ɔǹ a-ba kí ŋāŋkì tibɔti kí kúntí-tì nsanni niin, án nín làá ŋá pu ú taa dáaĺ-si kí cùnnì ubɔsoonli cee, ní úǹ ń dū-si kí tīī pilisib ń cáá-si kí pìin̄ sarkee.

eng: I tell you, you wont get out of there until you have paid the very last cent.
local: M tùkù-si ibaamɔn ní, bi yaa cáá-si ki tɔ́ sarkanee, see á pà a-pɔndi kɔkɔ nyíki nyiki, báà gboo kaa kīntí, ní bí nín líí-si.».

eng: Arent five sparrows sold for two small coins?Yet not one of them is overlooked by God.
local: «Baa nyáfí seerib biŋmoo igboo ilaaa? Ama nì míǹ kɔkɔee, Unimbɔti kaa súntí seer báà ubɔ pu.

eng: Even the hairs on your head are all counted. Dont be afraid. You are worth more than many sparrows.
local: Unimbɔti kàan̄ ni-yikpiti kɔkɔ ki nyí ti-nyɔkɔm. An puee, taa fàŋkìmaan! Nimbi jítiǹ kuseerpaau.».

eng: I tell you, everyone who acknowledges me before humans, the Human Onewill acknowledge before Gods angels.
local: «M tùkù-ni yii un kamaa di yaa sòon̄ binib nimbiin yii u sá man yɔuee, man Kinibiki mun làá dāá lì Unimbɔti tuuŋyi cee yii u mun sá man yɔu.

eng: But the one who rejects me before others will be rejected before Gods angels.
local: Ama un di yaa néé binib nimbiin yii waa nyí-mee, man Kinibiki mun làá dāá néé Unimbɔti tuuŋyi nimbiin yii maa nyí-u.

eng: Some who were present on that occasion told Jesus about the Galileans whom Pilate had killed while they were offering sacrifices.
local: Ŋyunti gbantee ní binib biba dɔ́miń ki tùkū Yeesu yii Galiilee yab biba nìn bíi kù tiwaŋkuti ki ŋáań saraa ki tìì Unimbɔti, ní gɔmina Piilati yāntì bi dɔ́miń ki kūū-bi, ní bi-fatikuu nì tiwaŋkuti tin bi kūūyee fatikuu ŋmālim̀ tɔbin.

eng: Jesus was teaching in one of the synagogues on the Sabbath.
local: Ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa ŋubɔ dalee, Yeesu nìn bíi dàkà binib Unimbɔti bɔti kukpafidii kubɔn.

eng: A woman was there who had been disabled by a spirit for eighteen years. She was bent over and couldnt stand up straight.
local: Unimpu ubɔ nìn bí niin, arasinii ubɔ nìn ŋúb́-u doooo abin saalku nì ata ki yāntì an gúkúǹ-u u kpántì udeen, waa ti ŋūǹ kí yáatí báà fiii.

eng: When he saw her, Jesus called her to him and said, Woman, you are set free from your sickness.
local: Yeesu ká-uee, ní ki yíiń-u ní ki bí-u yii: «Unimpuee, a-weeŋyi yāntì-si,».

eng: He placed his hands on her and she straightened up at once and praised God.
local: ní ki dū u-ŋalii ki páaǹ u-pu. Kpalaa niin ní unimpuee yáatìi yúl̀ cáacaa, ní ki bíi pàkà Unimbɔti.

eng: The synagogue leader, incensed that Jesus had healed on the Sabbath, responded, There are six days during which work is permitted. Come and be healed on those days, not on the Sabbath day.
local: Niinee, an kaa mɔ́ɔkiǹ kukpafidii kpiliu Yeesu nín cáaǹ unimpuee ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa dalee, ní u tùkū bin kpáfì niinee yii: «Kinyɔŋ poonee iwiin iluu di sá báà ŋma ń dūú tɔ̄ u-tundi. Míǹ puee, dāam̄maan iwiin iluuyee poon bí cáaǹ-ni, án taa nín̄ sá ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa dal.».

eng: The Lord replied, Hypocrites! Dont each of you on the Sabbath untie your ox or donkey from its stall and lead it out to get a drink?.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-u yii: «Mpoonnyoondambi, kéè ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa dalee, ni gbìntī ni-naai nì ni-mammu kunaakooun ki cáǹ-i i nyùɔɔ!.

eng: Then isnt it necessary that this woman, a daughter of Abraham, bound by Satan for eighteen long years, be set free from her bondage on the Sabbath day.
local: Kin unimpu nee mun sá Abraam naantiiu ubɔ ní Sitaan bóob́-u doooo abin ali kpá mmuŋku. Úǹ di kaa nín máaǹ ḿ báatí-u dikpambaal ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa dalaaa?».

eng: When he said these things, all his opponents were put to shame, but all those in the crowd rejoiced at all the extraordinary things he was doing.
local: Yeesu nín kíí-bi mimmee, ní icoo kɔ́ u-nannanliibi gbanti, ama samaa un kíǹee nìn fàal̀ atunnyaan ŋin Yeesu nìn ŋáańee pu.

eng: Jesus asked, What is Gods kingdom like? To what can I compare it?.
local: Ní Yeesu gíti tí dū tibɔti tiba ki ŋmāntiǹ-bi yii: «M làá dū Unimbɔti Beel kí nántíǹ ba deeyaaa? Ba ní m làá dū-dì kí ŋmàntīǹ?.

eng: Its like a mustard seed that someone took and planted in a garden. It grew and developed into a tree and the birds in the sky nested in its branches.
local: Dì náań nì kisubiki kin bi yíì muutaadee bil ní ubɔ yōoǹ ki dūu búĺ u-kpaabun, ní dì nyáń ki kpántì busubu, ní nnɔmbiyaamu dɔ́miń ki páaǹ mu-diiŋŋi bu-paŋŋi pu.».

eng: He replied, Do you think the suffering of these Galileans proves that they were more sinful than all the other Galileans?.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-bi yii: «Mimmee, ni dàkafī yii Galiilee yabee nín kpíì nsansaaŋkúm gbantee, an dàkà yii bi sá binikpitib ki jítiǹ Galiilee yab bin kíǹee kɔkɔɔɔ?.

eng: Again he said, To what can I compare Gods kingdom?.
local: Ní u kútì ki lī yii. «Ba ní m gíti ŋūǹ kí dū Unimbɔti Beel kí ŋmàntīǹ?.

eng: Its like yeast, which a woman took and hid in a bushel of wheat flour until the yeast had worked its way through the whole.
local: An náań nì ŋkpɔnɔnyɔkɔ ŋun ní unimpu dū ki ŋmālim̀ ŋkpɔnɔyim tikpil poon ki búuń, ní busaa kɔkɔ jɔ́m̀ee ní.».

eng: Jesus traveled through cities and villages, teaching and making his way to Jerusalem.
local: Yeesu nìn còom̄ ki jītí ntiŋwaamu nì ŋkpaamu ki dàkà Unimbɔti bɔti ki cá Jeerusaleem.

eng: Someone said to him, Lord, will only a few be saved? Jesus said to them.
local: Ní ubɔ bālifì-u yii: «Ukpil, binib fiii di làá dāá pée ká difiilaaa?» Ní Yeesu kíí-u yii.

eng: Make every effort to enter through the narrow gate. Many, I tell you, will try to enter and wont be able to.
local: «Pɔ́ɔm̀maan ni-ba kí dīmaan kipunyɔkɔwaai kí kɔ́ Unimbɔti beelin! Mbaamɔn ní m tùkù-ni, binib tikpil làá dāá nyàab̀ kí kɔ́, kaa làá dāá ŋmā.

eng: Once the owner of the house gets up and shuts the door, then you will stand outside and knock on the door, saying, Lord, open the door for us. He will reply, I dont know you or where you are from.
local: «Unimbɔti kudiidaan yaa láa yíkì ki pīiǹ u-punyɔkɔkee, ni dāa kíǹ ki yú boon ki gbáà kipunyɔkɔ ní ki lì yii: <Ti-Dindaan, pìitī-ti!> Ní ú dāá kíí-ni yii: <Maa nyí laakin ní ni sée.>.

eng: Then you will begin to say, We ate and drank in your presence, and you taught in our streets.
local: Niinee ní ni dāa lì yii: <Ti nì si nìn jíń ki nyùn̄, a dɔ́miń ti-tiŋkin ki yúl̀ ti-daŋŋi pu ki dàkā Unimbɔti bɔti.>.

eng: He will respond, I dont know you or where you are from. Go away from me, all you evildoers.
local: Ní ú fàtīí kùbitī kí tùkù-ni yii: <Maa nyí laakin ní ni sée. Sútímaan niin kí wàatī-mi, nimbi bikpitiŋaliibee kɔkɔ.>.

eng: There will be weeping and grinding of teeth when you see Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and all the prophets in Gods kingdom, but you yourselves will be thrown out.
local: Niin ní ni làá dāá ká Abraam nì Isaaki nì Jaakɔb nì Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi kɔkɔ kā Unimbɔti Beelin, ní nimbee, bí dū-ni kí fá mmɔŋki, ní ní dāá sɔ̄ anyin gà tɔb kàkaka.

eng: People will come from east and west, north and south, and sit down to eat in Gods kingdom.
local: Binib làá dāá nyānní duulinya jaŋŋi kɔkɔn, kí dāan̄ kí kɔ́ Unimbɔti Beelin kí jī nnaam.

eng: No, I tell you, but unless you change your hearts and lives, you will die just as they did.
local: Aayee, baa sá, ama ni mun yaa kaa kpántì ni-ba, ni làá kpú kii bi mun nín nìn kpíì puee ní!.

eng: Look! Those who are last will be first and those who are first will be last.
local: Bin bin sá kɔŋkɔnnee biluŋkikaabee, biba di dāa sá bipeepeekaabi, ní bin sá kɔŋkɔnnee bipeepeekaabee, biba dāa sá biluŋkikaabi.».

eng: At that time, some Pharisees approached Jesus and said, Go! Get away from here, because Herod wants to kill you.
local: Ŋyunti gbantee ní Fariisab biba dɔ́miń Yeesu cee ní ki bí-u yii: «Yīkì doo kí nīn cá laaba, kun puee Eeroodi nyàab̀ kí kpɔ̀-si.».

eng: Jesus said to them, Go, tell that fox, Look, Im throwing out demons and healing people today and tomorrow, and on the third day I will complete my work.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-bi yii: «Cùú tùkùmaan Eeroodi un cáá iciin kii jiiŋkpaayee yii: <M làá jà arasiniibi kí cáaǹ bibunliibi díǹ nì fool, daapudalee, m dóò dee.>.

eng: However, its necessary for me to travel today, tomorrow, and the next day because its impossible for a prophet to be killed outside of Jerusalem.
local: An máaǹ ḿ còom̀ díǹ nì fool nì daapudal, kun puee an kaa pú nsan yii Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi ń kpú laaba an yaa kaa sá Jeerusaleem.

eng: Jerusalem, Jerusalem, you who kill the prophets and stone those who were sent to you! How often I have wanted to gather your people just as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you didnt want that.
local: «Jeerusaleem yab, Jeerusaleem yab, nimbi bin kù Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi ki cáá ataŋkpee ki yāŋkí ki kù bin Unimbɔti túnnée, ataa aŋa pu ní maa nyàab̄ kí kóoǹ-ni kí kpāfì m-cee kii ukɔl nín kōontí u-biyaamu kí ŋá u-taapu kí dū u-feeŋyi kí bìin̄ mu-pu miŋyee! Ama ní ni yíì.

eng: Look, your house is abandoned. I tell you, you wont see me until the time comes when you say, Blessings on the one who comes in the Lords name .
local: An puee Unimbɔti làá fá ni nì ni-diiku. Ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-ni, naa làá ti ká-mi kí nīn cí ŋyunti ŋun ni làá dāá sòoǹ m-pu yii: <Unimbɔti ń bìin̄ un u túnnée boonee.>».

eng: What about those eighteen people who were killed when the tower of Siloam fell on them? Do you think that they were more guilty of wrongdoing than everyone else who lives in Jerusalem?.
local: Àá, ni dàkafī yii binib saalku nì bita bin Siiloowee difɔkɔku lítì ki tàb̄ ki kūūyee sá digaŋgam nib ki jítiǹ Jeerusaleem yab bin kíǹee kɔkɔ?.

eng: No, I tell you, but unless you change your hearts and lives, you will die just as they did.
local: Aayee, ama ni mun yaa kaa kpántì ni-ba, ni làá kpú kii bi mun nín kpíì puee ní.».

eng: Jesus told this parable: A man owned a fig tree planted in his vineyard. He came looking for fruit on it and found none.
local: Ní Yeesu gíti dū tibɔti nee ki ŋmāntì ki lī yii: «Uninja ubɔ di bí ní ki cóoǹ busubu bun bi yíì fiigee u-kpaabun. Dalbee, u cūtì kí kìtī abil, ní kaa ká.

eng: He said to his gardener, Look, Ive come looking for fruit on this fig tree for the past three years, and Ive never found any. Cut it down! Why should it continue depleting the soils nutrients.
local: Ní u bí upaadaan un kpèé u-kpaabuee yii: <Kpèè, abin ata sèé m nín pée dòoń ki nyàab̀ busubu nee biliŋ, kaa kāntée. Gāā-bu kí fá. Ba pu ní bu yú ki jīǹ mpaan fám míǹ?>.

eng: The gardener responded, Lord, give it one more year, and I will dig around it and give it fertilizer.
local: Ní upaadaanee bí yii: <Ukpil, gíti yàntī-bu dibindi nee, ḿ yɔ̀kɔtī bu-taapu kí ŋá tinaabin.

eng: Maybe it will produce fruit next year; if not, then you can cut it down.
local: M yaa ŋá míǹ, an yaa yíì, jiil bu làá lòoǹ, bu yaa kaa nín lòon̄ee, á nín gāā-bu kí fá.>».

eng: One Sabbath, when Jesus went to share a meal in the home of one of the leaders of the Pharisees, they were watching him closely.
local: Ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa ŋubɔ dalee, ní Fariisab kpilib ubɔ yíiń Yeesu ú dāan̄ u-dumpu bí jī tijin. Yeesu bàn̄ee, ní bin bí niinee kā ki cɔ́ŋkí-u.

eng: Instead, when you receive an invitation, go and sit in the least important place. When your host approaches you, he will say, Friend, move up here to a better seat. Then you will be honored in the presence of all your fellow guests.
local: Míǹ puee, bi yaa yíiń-si njimee, cùú kàlīń boon, ní un yíiń-see yaa bāań ki ká-si a kāń boonee, ní ú bí-si yii: <M-bɔɔ, dāá kàl̄ nyɔkɔpu dijamɔntiil pu.> Niinee an sá diyindi ní ki tīī-si, sii nì bin kɔkɔ bí niin kí jēe nimbiin.

eng: All who lift themselves up will be brought low, and those who make themselves low will be lifted up.
local: Míǹ mbaantiim ní un kamaa yaa yōoǹ u-ba yilpuee, Unimbɔti làá sìntīǹ-u taapu, ní un yaa sīntì u-ba taapuee, Unimbɔti làá yòon̄-u yilpu.».

eng: Then Jesus said to the person who had invited him, When you host a lunch or dinner, dont invite your friends, your brothers and sisters, your relatives, or rich neighbors. If you do, they will invite you in return and that will be your reward.
local: Ní Yeesu fātìi bí un yíiń-uee yii: «A yaa làá yíiń binib bí dāan̄ a-dumpu kí jī tijinee, taa yíiń a-bɔɔbi nì a-nabiyaamu, nì bin nàaǹ-see nì a-gaajajutiibi, kun puee bi mun ŋūǹ kí dāá ŋá míǹ kí yíiń-si mun. Mimmee bi pàn̄-si tin a nìn ŋá ki tīī-bee dee.

eng: Instead, when you give a banquet, invite the poor, crippled, lame, and blind.
local: Ama a yaa ŋá tijin ki làá yíiń binib bí dāan̄ a-dumpu kí jēe, á yíiń bisoodambi, bideembi, bifaliŋfaambi nì bijɔfub.

eng: And you will be blessed because they cant repay you. Instead, you will be repaid when the just are resurrected.
local: A yaa ŋá mimmee, a làá nīn bí mmɔnni kun puee an nibee kaa ŋūǹ kí téeǹ-si, ní daliŋkin Unimbɔti yaa láa fīkitì deedeedambee, ní ú dāá pà-si.».

eng: When one of the dinner guests heard Jesus remarks, he said to Jesus, Happy are those who will feast in Gods kingdom.
local: Bin nì Yeesu nìn kā ki jīǹee kansikin ubɔ nín gbìl̄ an bɔtee, ní u bí Yeesu yii: «Un làá dāá jī Unimbɔti Beelin jikaatee bí mmɔnneeee!».

eng: Jesus replied, A certain man hosted a large dinner and invited many people.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-u yii: «Unimbɔti Beel nín náań nì kuŋyee sèé: Kijapaai kiba di nìn bɔ́ntì njiŋkpaammi miba ní ki yíiń binib tikpil.

eng: When it was time for the dinner to begin, he sent his servant to tell the invited guests, Come! The dinner is now ready.
local: Njimee yuntiŋu bāańee, ní u tú u-tɔntɔnliu yii ú cùú tùkù bin u yíińee yii: <Dāam̄maan, an kɔkɔ bɔ́ntì.>.

eng: One by one, they all began to make excuses. The first one told him, I bought a farm and must go and see it. Please excuse me.
local: Ní bi-kɔkɔ bíi tìì inaakpal fám kun pu ní baa làá ŋmā kí cùee. Upeepeekaayee bí yii: <M cíkitì-si kifuulii, maa làá ŋmā kí dāan̄, kun puee m dá kusaau kubɔ, m máaǹ kí cùú kpèè-ku.>.

eng: Another said, I bought five teams of oxen, and Im going to check on them. Please excuse me.
local: Ní ubɔ mun bí yii: <M cíkitì-si kifuulii, maa làá ŋmā kí dāan̄, di sá yii m dá inaa in bi cáa kù tikpaalee saalaa. M làá cáá-i kusaau kí màkan̄-i kí kpèè.>.

eng: A man suffering from an abnormal swelling of the body was there.
local: Ucùúdaan ubɔ nìn kā u-nimbiin.

eng: Another said, I just got married, so I cant come.
local: Ní ubɔ mun tí bí yii: <M dáá pée cáá unimpucaan dee, an puee maa làá ŋmā kí dāan̄.>.

eng: When he returned, the servant reported these excuses to his master. The master of the house became angry and said to his servant, Go quickly to the citys streets, the busy ones and the side streets, and bring the poor, crippled, blind, and lame.
local: Ní utɔntɔnlee gítìń ki tùkū u-kpiliu binibee nín kíí-u puee. Niinee ní ukpil fōō diŋuul ní ki bí-u yii: <Nín̄ cá malaa kitiŋin sampaambi pu nì adan pu kí cáań bisoodambi nì bideembi nì bijɔfub nì bifaliŋfaambi!>.

eng: The servant said, Master, your instructions have been followed and there is still room.
local: An kútì fiiiyee, ní utɔntɔnlee gítìń ki bí yii: <A nín bí yii ḿ ŋá puee, m ŋá míǹ, binib tikpil dɔ́miń, ama mpaan dáá gbíntìi bí.>.

eng: The master said to the servant, Go to the highways and back alleys and urge people to come in so that my house will be filled.
local: Ní ukpil bí-u yii: <Nín̄ cá isaasan pu nì ikpaabuku. A yaa pée ká binib biŋyee, á páb́-bi bí dāan̄ án nín làá ŋá pu m-diiku ń gbée.

eng: I tell you, not one of those who were invited will taste my dinner.
local: Ibaamɔn ní m sòoǹ nì ni, bin kɔkɔ m bàà péé péé ki yíińee, báà ubɔ kaa làá dáká m-jinti nee.>».

eng: Large crowds were traveling with Jesus. Turning to them, he said.
local: Dinikitikpaandi diba nì Yeesu nìn táá ki còom̄. Ní Yeesu fātì ki bí-bi yii.

eng: Whoever comes to me and doesnt hate father and mother, spouse and children, and brothers and sisters—yes, even ones own life—cannot be my disciple.
local: «Un yaa dɔ́miń man cee ki là kí kpántí m-boonnooliuee, see ú nīn néeń man ki jítiǹ u-baa nì u-na nì u-nimpuu nì u-biyaamu nì u-nabiyaamu, ní kí nīn néeń man ki jítiǹ úǹ u-ba.

eng: Whoever doesnt carry their own cross and follow me cannot be my disciple.
local: Sii un yaa kaa yōoǹ a-dɔpuntikaaŋu ki túǹ kí nīn nóó-mee kaa ŋūǹ kí nīn sá man boonnooliu.

eng: If one of you wanted to build a tower, wouldnt you first sit down and calculate the cost, to determine whether you have enough money to complete it?.
local: Ni-kansikin ŋma di yaa làá mā kudikpaaŋŋu, kaa kāl̀ ki cɔ́tì kunimbiŋmiiu kí béé animbil ŋin u cááyee yaa làá bà ú mā an diiku.

eng: Otherwise, when you have laid the foundation but couldnt finish the tower, all who see it will begin to belittle you.
local: An yaa sá fám, u cōontí kudii ní kí dūú gbèen̄, kaa ti ŋūǹ kí púntí, ní bin kɔkɔ jītí ki wāĺ-kuee kíǹ ki bèel̄-u yii.

eng: Jesus asked the lawyers and Pharisees, Does the Law allow healing on the Sabbath or not.
local: Ní Yeesu bālifì bimaradakaliibi nì Fariisab yii: «Ti-marau pú nsan tí cáaǹ ubunli ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa dalaaa, an kaa pú?».

eng: They will say, Heres the person who began construction and couldnt complete it.
local: <Kpèè, kijapaai nee bàà kíĺ ki bíi māā kudii dee ní ŋŋmam kaa ti bí-u ú púntí.>.

eng: Or what king would go to war against another king without first sitting down to consider whether his ten thousand soldiers could go up against the twenty thousand coming against him?.
local: Àá, ubɔti ulɔu di yaa làá cùú jáǹ u-bɔtijuti waa kàal̀ kí kpèè yii u-nib kutukub saalaa yaa ŋūǹ kí cùú jáǹ ubɔti un dòoń kí jáǹ-u nì binib kutukub mmuŋkuee.

eng: And if he didnt think he could win, he would send a representative to discuss terms of peace while his enemy was still a long way off.
local: U yaa kpēē yii waa làá ŋmēe, ŋyunti ŋun ubɔti un béeyee dáá bíń ndan ki dòońee ní ú tɔ̄ binib ń cù kí gbàam̀-u yii: <A là ḿ ŋá mana ní ŋgbansɔŋfi ń dāan̄ ti-kansikin ní?>».

eng: In the same way, none of you who are unwilling to give up all of your possessions can be my disciple.
local: Ní Yeesu gíti lī yii: «Míǹ mbaantiim ní ni-kansikin un yaa là kí nīn sá man boonnooliuee ń fá tin kɔkɔ u cááyee.».

eng: Salt is good. But if salt loses its flavor, how will it become salty again?.
local: «Ŋyaam sá tiwammɔntiil ní, ama ŋyaam yaa kpíìyee, mana ní bi làá ŋá ní mí fàtīí mɔ̄ɔkì?.

eng: It has no value, neither for the soil nor for the manure pile. People throw it away. Whoever has ears to hear should pay attention.
local: Màa ŋān̄ bí dūú bíĺ kitiŋ án tīī ntiŋkpamaaa, kaa tí ŋān̄ bí dūú bíĺ tiwambɔlkaal taapu án lòoǹ mun. Asee bí dūú fá ditampundi pu. Un yaa cáá ditafalee ń pīlifì.».

eng: But they said nothing. Jesus took hold of the sick man, cured him, and then let him go.
local: Ní baa kíí-u tiba. Ní u cúú ubunlee ŋal ní ki cáaǹ-u ní ki bí yii ú nīn kùǹ.

eng: He said to them, Suppose your child or ox fell into a ditch on the Sabbath day. Wouldnt you immediately pull it out.
local: Míǹ boonee ní Yeesu bí-bi yii: «Ni-kansikin ubɔ di yaa cáá jipɔɔn àá unaa, ní u yaa jéetì lɔkɔn ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa dal, waa lèekī-u malaaa?».

eng: But they had no response.
local: Ní baa ŋúń ki kíí-u an bɔti.

eng: When Jesus noticed how the guests sought out the best seats at the table, he told them a parable.
local: Yeesu dáań ki ká yii bin bi yíiń bí dāan̄ kí jī tijinee bíi nyàab̀ ikakaamɔnti, ní u dū tibɔŋmantikaal ki sòon̄ nì bi-kɔkɔ ki lī yii.

eng: When someone invites you to a wedding celebration, dont take your seat in the place of honor. Someone more highly regarded than you could have been invited by your host.
local: «Bi yaa yíiń-si tinimpucan jim ní a yaa bàn̄ee, taa kàl̄ nyɔkɔpu jal pu, an yaa yíì, bi yíiń unikpaan un jítiǹ-see mun.

eng: The host who invited both of you will come and say to you, Give your seat to this other person. Embarrassed, you will take your seat in the least important place.
local: A dāa kpèéyee, un yíiń nimbi liitilee bāań a-cee ní kí bí-si yii: <Yīkì kí pɔ̄ ukpil nee mpaan.> Ní á yīkì nì icoo gbòooo kí nīn gítí boon boon kí cùú kàlīń.

eng: All the tax collectors and sinners were gathering around Jesus to listen to him.
local: Bifɔtiliibi nì binikpitib bin kɔ kíǹee nìn sútìń ki pìl̄ Yeesu kí pīlifì tin u sòoǹee.

eng: In the same way, I tell you, joy breaks out in the presence of Gods angels over one sinner who changes both heart and life.
local: Ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-ni, míǹ mbaantiim Unimbɔti mun nì u-tuuŋyi bí dipɔɔpiŋkpaandin un fá u-kpitiiyee pu.».

eng: Jesus said, A certain man had two sons.
local: Ní Yeesu fātìi tí bí-bi yii: «Ukpil ubɔ di bí ní ki cáá jipɔmbi bili.

eng: The younger son said to his father, Father, give me my share of the inheritance. Then the father divided his estate between them.
local: Ní uwaalee bí u-baa yii: <M-baa, tīī-mi m-fakal kɔŋkɔnnee.> Ní u-baa lafun tīī bi-liitil, báà ŋma nì u-wanti.

eng: Soon afterward, the younger son gathered everything together and took a trip to a land far away. There, he wasted his wealth through extravagant living.
local: Iwiŋkaaŋkaa boonee, ní uwaalee dū u-jandi din bi tīī-uee ki nyáfì ní ki fōō animbil, ní ki yíkìi búntì ndandati kitiŋ kiban, ní ki bí niin ki jīǹ u-nimbiliŋ pùsi pusi.

eng: When he had used up his resources, a severe food shortage arose in that country and he began to be in need.
local: U jíń u-nimbiliŋ ki dóòyee, ní ŋkùm miba nyáń kitiŋ gbanti poon ki pɔ̄ɔ̄ tikpil. Niinee disool kíǹ ki cáá-u.

eng: He hired himself out to one of the citizens of that country, who sent him into his fields to feed pigs.
local: Ní u cūtì ki nyàab̄ kitiŋ gbanti niliu ubɔ cee ditundi ní úǹ dū-u ki tɔ́ kusaau digbeetikpaal.

eng: He longed to eat his fill from what the pigs ate, but no one gave him anything.
local: U bàà nìn là kí nīn jīǹ igbeeti jinti, ní báà ubɔ kaa tìì-u.

eng: When he came to his senses, he said, How many of my fathers hired hands have more than enough food, but Im starving to death!.
local: Niinee ní u kíĺ ki bíi dàkafī tin poon ní u kɔ́ee, ní ki bí yii: <M-baa tɔntɔnliibi tikpil bí mmɔŋki ki bíntí tijin pu, ní man dāa bí doo ki kúù ŋkùm nee.

eng: I will get up and go to my father, and say to him,Father, I have sinned against heaven and against you.
local: M làá yīkì kí fàtī m-baa cee. M yaa bàn̄ee, ḿ bí-u yii: M-baa, m yīntiǹ Unimbɔti ki yīntiǹ si mun, maa ti máaǹ á yíiń-mi a-biki. Dū-mi kí ŋá kii a-tɔntɔnliu ubɔee.>.

eng: The Pharisees and legal experts were grumbling, saying, This man welcomes sinners and eats with them.
local: An yāntì Fariisab nì bimaradakaliibi bíi gbùkum̄, yii: «Kijapaai nee fòol̄ binikpitib, u nì bi jīǹ tijin.».

eng: So he got up and went to his father. While he was still a long way off, his father saw him and was moved with compassion. His father ran to him, hugged him, and kissed him.
local: U dākafì míǹ ki dóòyee ní ki yíkì ki kíliń nsan ki nín dòoń u-baa cee. «U-baa ká u páaǹnée, ní an cúú-u falaa, ní u sáǹ ki túŋkì-u ní ki ŋá-u aatúuù.

eng: Then his son said, Father, I have sinned against heaven and against you. I no longer deserve to be called your son.
local: Ní u bí u-baa yii: <M yīntiǹ Unimbɔti ki yīntiǹ si mun, maa ti máaǹ á yíiń-mi a-biki.>.

eng: But the father said to his servants, Quickly, bring out the best robe and put it on him! Put a ring on his finger and sandals on his feet!.
local: Ama ní u-baa bí u-tɔntɔnliibi yii: <Cáaḿmaan dibɔkutil din ŋān̄ tikpilee malaa malaa kí pèem̄maan-u, kí cáań kibɔmbiki kí fúuń u-ŋambiki, kí cáań anaataka kí táká-u.

eng: Fetch the fattened calf and slaughter it. We must celebrate with feasting.
local: kí cúúmaan dinajapɔmbil din cáá ŋkpamee kí kpɔ̀ tí jī kí fàal̀.

eng: because this son of mine was dead and has come back to life! He was lost and is found! And they began to celebrate.
local: Kun puee m-biki nee ŋman nìn kpíì ní ki fātìi fīkitì, kì nìn wáŋkì ní m fātìi ká-kì.> Ní bi kíĺ ki bíi jīǹ jiŋaal.

eng: Now his older son was in the field. Coming in from the field, he approached the house and heard music and dancing.
local: «Ŋyuntee u-jipɔŋkpiliu dáá bíń kusaau. U kùnní ki tóobiń mmɔŋkee, ki gbìl̀ agbiil nì diŋool tùm̀ mmɔŋki.

eng: He called one of the servants and asked what was going on.
local: Ní u yíiń bitɔntɔnliibi ubɔ ki bālifì-u yii: <Ba ŋá mmɔŋki doo ní?>.

eng: The servant replied, Your brother has arrived, and your father has slaughtered the fattened calf because he received his son back safe and sound.
local: Ní utɔntɔnlee kíí-u yii: <A-naal di fātìi gítìń, ní a-baa kūū dinajapɔmbil din cáá ŋkpamee, kun puee u fātìi ká-u nì alaafiya.>.

eng: Then the older son was furious and didnt want to enter in, but his father came out and begged him.
local: Niinee, ujipɔŋkpilee fōō diŋuul ní ki yíì kaa là kí kɔ́ kudiin. Ní u-baa nyánní ki sɔ̄kɔl̀-u ú kóoń.

eng: He answered his father, Look, Ive served you all these years, and I never disobeyed your instruction. Yet youve never given me as much as a young goat so I could celebrate with my friends.
local: Ní u fātìi bí u-baa yii: <M-baa, kpèè abin kupaau sèé, m nín kā ki tūǹ ditundi ki tìì-see. Maa yíì a-tundi diba yii maa làá tɔ̄ kí tīī-si, ama a mun kaa tīī-mi dalba kiŋɔbiki yii man nì m-bɔɔbi ń jī jiŋaal.

eng: Jesus told them this parable.
local: Ní Yeesu dū tibɔti nee ki ŋmāntiǹ yii.

eng: But when this son of yours returned, after gobbling up your estate on prostitutes, you slaughtered the fattened calf for him.
local: Ama a-jipɔɔn nee fātìi bāańee, úǹ un yōoǹ a-gaajawanti u nì nnimpulaakpaamu jíńee, úǹ ní a cúú unajapɔɔn un cáá ŋkpamee ki kūū ki fōō-u!>.

eng: Then his father said, Son, you are always with me, and everything I have is yours.
local: Ní u-baa bí-u yii: <M-biki, sii pée bí m-cee doo ní baabadal, tin kɔkɔ m cááyee, sii di yì.

eng: But we had to celebrate and be glad because this brother of yours was dead and is alive. He was lost and is found.
local: Ama an máaǹ tí jī jiŋaal kí fàal̀, kun puee a-naal un ŋman nìn kpíìyee fātìi fīkitì, u nìn wáŋkì ní m fātìi ká-u.>».

eng: Suppose someone among you had one hundred sheep and lost one of them. Wouldnt he leave the other ninety-nine in the pasture and search for the lost one until he finds it?.
local: «Ni-kansikin ubɔ yaa cáá ipii dilaataal, ní ubɔ yaa wáŋkèe, waa dù ubɔ kpá dilaataalee kí yóoń kupikpaapɔɔun kí cùú nyàabìń un wáŋkèe áli kí tin kánní-uaaa?.

eng: And when he finds it, he is thrilled and places it on his shoulders.
local: U yaa kánní-uee, niinee an mɔ́ɔkíǹ-u ní ú dū-u kí bùkun̄ní.

eng: When he arrives home, he calls together his friends and neighbors, saying to them, Celebrate with me because Ive found my lost sheep.
local: U nín bāań mmɔŋkee, ní ú yíiń u-bɔɔbi nì u-kɔŋkɔnnaatɔb kí bí-bi yii: <Fàal̀ kí tūtum̀maan-mi, kun puee m fātìi ká m-piiu un nìn wáŋkèe.>.

eng: In the same way, I tell you, there will be more joy in heaven over one sinner who changes both heart and life than over ninety-nine righteous people who have no need to change their hearts and lives.
local: Ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-ni, míǹ mbaantiim ní unibaanti un fá u-kpitiiyee puee, dipɔɔpiŋkpaandi bí yilpu ki jítiǹ deedeedambi ubɔ kpá dilaataal bin an kaa ti bàlifī bí kpántí bi-bimbimee.».

eng: Or what woman, if she owns ten silver coins and loses one of them, wont light a lamp and sweep the house, searching her home carefully until she finds it?.
local: «Àá unimpu ubɔ di yaa cáá jitɔmbi saalaa bin sá-u tipijatee ní ubɔ yaa wáŋkì, waa tùutī fitila kudiin kí ŋàtī kí nyàab̀ tiŋan áli kí tin ká u-nimbiliŋ.

eng: When she finds it, she calls together her friends and neighbors, saying, Celebrate with me because Ive found my lost coin.
local: U yaa kée ní ú yíiń u-bɔɔbi nì u-kɔŋkɔnnaatɔb kí bí-bi yii: <Fàal̀ kí tūtum̀maan-mi, kun puee m-nimbiliŋ ŋin nìn wáŋkèe, m fātìi ká-ŋi.>.

eng: Jesus also said to the disciples, A certain rich man heard that his household manager was wasting his estate.
local: Yeesu gíti kútì ki lī u-boonnooliibi yii: «Ugaaja ubɔ di bí ní ki cáá utɔntɔnli un kpèé u-wanti kɔkɔee, ní bi tùkū-u yii utɔntɔnlee cáá u-wanti ki bíiĺ.

eng: Whoever is faithful with little is also faithful with much, and the one who is dishonest with little is also dishonest with much.
local: Un ŋúb̀ tiwan fiii nì deedeeyee tí ŋúb̀ tiwaŋkpaandi mun deedee ní. Un kaa ŋúb̀ tiwan fiii nì deedeeyee kaa tí ŋúb̀ tiwaŋkpaandi mun nì deedee.

eng: If you havent been faithful with worldly wealth, who will trust you with true riches?.
local: Ni yaa kaa ŋūǹ kí ŋúb́ duulinya gaajati tin kaa sá timɔntiilee, Unimbɔti kaa dāa dù u-gaajawammɔntiiti kí ŋúbíǹ-ni.

eng: If you havent been faithful with someone elses property, who will give you your own?.
local: Tin tin sá ubɔ wantee, ni yaa kaa ŋúb́-tì nì deedeeyee, Unimbɔti kaa tìì ni mun tin sá ni-yatee.

eng: No household servant can serve two masters. Either you will hate the one and love the other, or you will be loyal to the one and have contempt for the other. You cannot serve God and wealth.
local: «Utɔntɔnli ubɔ kaa ŋūǹ kí nīn bí bikpilib bili cee. Asee ú nīn ná ubɔ kí nīn néeń un kíǹee, kí dū u-ba kí tīī ubɔ, kí nīn kpèé un kíǹee fám. Naa ŋūǹ kí kpāfì Unimbɔti nì animbil kí nóó.».

eng: The Pharisees, who were money-lovers, heard all this and sneered at Jesus.
local: Fariisab nín gbìl̄ an bɔtee ní bi bíi bèel̄ Yeesu, kun puee bíǹ néeń animbil.

eng: He said to them, You are the ones who justify yourselves before other people, but God knows your hearts. What is highly valued by people is deeply offensive to God.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-bi yii: «Ni là binib ń nīn wāĺ-ni yii ni sá deedeedambi. Ama Unimbɔti nyí tin kā ni-pɔbiliŋ poonee. Tin binib kpèé yii an sá tiwaŋkpaandee, an sá ntimbiil wanti ní Unimbɔti nimbiin.

eng: Until John, there was only the Law and the Prophets. Since then, the good news of Gods kingdom is preached, and everyone is urged to enter it.
local: «Mooyiisi marab nì Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi nín sòoǹ tiŋyee nìn bí ki dāa sāā Yoowaneesi un fùul̀ binib Unimbɔti nyimee yuntiŋu. Ŋyunti ŋun Yoowaneesi bāańee ní binib gbìl̄ Unimbɔti Beel bɔti, ní báà ŋma kíǹ ki bíi mɔ̀ kí kɔ́ di-ni.

eng: Its easier for heaven and earth to pass away than for the smallest stroke of a pen in the Law to drop out.
local: An pɔ̀fì ki tīī kutagbɔŋu nì kitiŋ ń jītí ki jítiǹ marab ŋmaŋmabiku kubɔ ń kpìtī.

eng: Any man who divorces his wife and marries another commits adultery, and a man who marries a woman divorced from her husband commits adultery.
local: «Un kamaa yaa jìn̄ u-nimpuu ní ki fātìi cáá upɔɔnee, udaan ŋá tilaakpaal Unimbɔti nimbiin, ní un cáá unimpu un u-cal jìn̄ee mun ŋá tilaakpaal Unimbɔti nimbiin.».

eng: There was a certain rich man who clothed himself in purple and fine linen, and who feasted luxuriously every day.
local: Ní Yeesu bí yii: «Ugaaja ubɔ di nìn bí ki pèentí abɔkutinyaan ŋin pɔ̄ɔ̄ kudaau tikpilee ní ki pée bí ki jīǹ jiŋaal mmɔntiimin baabadal.

eng: He called the manager in and said to him, What is this I hear about you? Give me a report of your administration because you can no longer serve as my manager.
local: Ní u yíiń-u, ní ki bí-u yii: <An kaa mɔ́ɔkiǹ-mi m nín gbìl̄ tin a ŋáańee. Ŋá akɔntaa kí tīī-mi kí tùkù-mi a nín túń ditundi ki gíiǹ puee, kun puee aa làá nīn gíti kpèé m-wanti.>.

eng: At his gate lay a certain poor man named Lazarus who was covered with sores.
local: Usoodaan ubɔ nìn bí bi yíì-u Laasaar. U nìn dɔ̀ ugaajee punyɔkɔpu ibunsoon píń u-wunti pu.

eng: Lazarus longed to eat the crumbs that fell from the rich mans table. Instead, dogs would come and lick his sores.
local: U bàà nìn là kí nīn tòó ugaajee nín jīǹ tin gbátí ki lítí kitiŋee kí kpíiǹ u-ba, ní iboo mun gíti dòoń ki líntí u-buuŋyi.

eng: The poor man died and was carried by angels to Abrahams side. The rich man also died and was buried.
local: Usoodaanee dūu kpíì, ní Unimbɔti tuuŋyi dɔ́miń ki yōoǹ-u ki cáa nín jɔ́ḿ yilpu ki kāaǹ Abraam cee. Ní ugaajee mun dāa dūu kpíì, ní bi síb́-u.

eng: While being tormented in the place of the dead, he looked up and saw Abraham at a distance with Lazarus at his side.
local: U bàn̄ ditaŋkpiilinee ki bíi jīǹ falaakpaan. U bí falaa poonee ki wáaǹ ki kpēēyee ki ká Abraam ndan, Laasaar kā u-cee.

eng: He shouted, Father Abraham, have mercy on me. Send Lazarus to dip the tip of his finger in water and cool my tongue, because Im suffering in this flame.
local: Niinee ní u téeń mpɔn yii: <Ti-baa Abraam, sɔ̄ m-saai kí pée tɔ̄ń Laasaar ú dū u-ŋambiki kí sēē nnyim kí cáań kí mèel̀ m-limbil, kun puee m bíi jīǹ falaa ŋŋmi nee poon tikpil.>.

eng: But Abraham said, Child, remember that during your lifetime you received good things, whereas Lazarus received terrible things. Now Lazarus is being comforted and you are in great pain.
local: Ní Abraam kíí-u yii: <M-biki, téetí yii a nín nìn bí duulinyanee, a nìn jíń mmɔn, ní Laasaar úǹ jíń falaa. Kɔŋkɔnnee u mun bíi fòol̄ u-mɔŋŋu dee, ní si mun bí falaani.

eng: Moreover, a great crevasse has been fixed between us and you. Those who wish to cross over from here to you cannot. Neither can anyone cross from there to us.
local: Áli gba mbookpaan ŋubɔ di pītì timbi nì nimbi kansikin. Bin yaa bàà là kí nyānní timbi cee kí cù nimbi ceeyee ń taa ŋmāaa, bin mun yaa bàà là kí nyānní nimbi ceen kí dāan̄ timbi cee dooyee mun ń taa ŋmā.>.

eng: The rich man said, Then I beg you, Father, send Lazarus to my fathers house.
local: Ní ugaajee bí yii: <Mimmee, m gbàam̄-si, á tɔ̄ Laasaar m-baa dumpu.

eng: I have five brothers. He needs to warn them so that they dont come to this place of agony.
local: kun puee m cáá nnabiyaamu muŋmoo, ú cù kí kpāafì-bi bíǹ ń taa dāan̄ nnaabu ŋun poon man bí nee ni.>.

eng: Abraham replied, They have Moses and the Prophets. They must listen to them.
local: Ní Abraam kíí-u yii: <Bi cáá tin Mooyiisi nì Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi ŋmàn̄ee, bí nóó tíǹ.>.

eng: The household manager said to himself, What will I do now that my master is firing me as his manager? Im not strong enough to dig and too proud to beg.
local: Ní utɔntɔnlee bālifì u-ba yii: <M-kpiliu làá jà-mi dee, m làá ŋá mana dee? Ḿ cùú nīn kù tikpaalaaa? Maa cáá mpɔn kí kpɔ̀. Ḿ cùú nīn mèéee? An cáá-mi icoo!.

eng: The rich man said, No, Father Abraham! But if someone from the dead goes to them, they will change their hearts and lives.
local: Ní ugaajee bí yii: <Aayee, m-baa Abraam, ubɔ di yaa bàà fīkitì bitaŋkpiibi kansikin ki cūtèe, bi bàà kpántí bi-bimbim.>.

eng: Abraham said, If they dont listen to Moses and the Prophets, then neither will they be persuaded if someone rises from the dead.
local: Ní Abraam fātìi bí-u yii: <Bi yaa kaa làá nóó Mooyiisi nì Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi nín ŋmàn̄ tiŋyee, báà ubɔ yaa nyáń bitaŋkpiibi kansikin ki fīkitì ki sòon̄ nì bi, baa làá fōō udaan bɔti kí kíí.>».

eng: I know what Ill do so that, when I am removed from my management position, people will welcome me into their houses.
local: M béè m nín làá ŋá pu ní bi yaa jìn̄-mee, binib ń fōō-mi bi-dumpuyee.>.

eng: One by one, the manager sent for each person who owed his master money. He said to the first, How much do you owe my master.
local: Niinee ní u yíiń bin kɔkɔ cáá u-kpiliu apɔɔnee ubɔ ubɔ, ní ki bālifì upeepeekaa yii: <Mana ní a cáá m-kpiliu dipɔndi ní?>.

eng: He said, Nine hundred gallons of olive oil.The manager said to him, Take your contract, sit down quickly, and write four hundred fifty gallons.
local: Ní u bí-u yii <Ŋkpam aŋkɔrab dilaataal.> Ní u fātìi bí yii: <A-pɔndi gbɔŋku sèé, fōō kí kàl̄ kí ŋmà malaa yii a cáá dipɔndi aŋkɔrab ili nì saalaa.>.

eng: Then the manager said to another, How much do you owe? He said, One thousand bushels of wheat.He said, Take your contract and write eight hundred.
local: Ní u bí un kíǹee yii: <Sii fáà, a-pɔndi sá mana?> Ní úǹ bí yii: <Idi kutukub dilaataal,> ní u bi yii: <A-pɔndi gbɔŋku sèé, fōō kí ŋmà idi kutukub imuŋku inaa.>.

eng: The master commended the dishonest manager because he acted cleverly. People who belong to this world are more clever in dealing with their peers than are people who belong to the light.
local: Utɔntɔŋkpitee kpiliu nín gbìl̄ tin u ŋée, ní u pàkā-u u nín yáb́ ki ŋá miŋyee: di sá yii bin kaa sá Unimbɔti nibee wálifì bi-jutiibi bɔti pu ki jítiǹ bin sá u-yabee.».

eng: I tell you, use worldly wealth to make friends for yourselves so that when its gone, you will be welcomed into the eternal homes.
local: Ní Yeesu lī yii: «Man tùkù nimbi yii: Nín̄ cáámaan ni-gaajati ki ŋáań min fáatì Unimbɔtee, án nín làá ŋá pu, tì yaa láa kúntì ni-ceeyee, Unimbɔti ń fōō-ni kí kàan̄ ikɔŋkoon in bí jaanjaanee nee.

eng: Jesus said to his disciples, Things that cause people to trip and fall into sin must happen, but how terrible it is for the person through whom they happen.
local: Ní Yeesu tí bí u-boonnooliibi yii: «Kun làá nīn yàntī binib ń nīn ŋáań ikpitee làá nīn pée bí ní. Ama mbusu dɔ̄ ki cí un pu ní án làá dīńee.

eng: In the same way, when you have done everything required of you, you should say, We servants deserve no special praise. We have only done our duty.
local: Míǹ mbaantiim ní an sá ni mun cee. Ni yaa ŋá tin Unimbɔti bí ní ŋée kɔkɔ ki dóòyee, lìmaan yii: <Timbi sá bitɔntɔnliibi bin kaa bí tibanee ní, ti-tundi din ti máaǹ kí tɔ̄ee ní ti túń nee.>».

eng: On the way to Jerusalem, Jesus traveled along the border between Samaria and Galilee.
local: Yeesu nín nìn còom̄ ki cá Jeerusaleemee, u nìn jítì Samaarii nì Galiilee kansikin ní.

eng: As he entered a village, ten men with skin diseases approached him. Keeping their distance from him.
local: U làá kɔ́ kitimbilii kibanee ní binyaŋgbantiibi saalaa túŋkìń-u ki dɔ́miń ki yú kudandaŋu.

eng: they raised their voices and said, Jesus, Master, show us mercy.
local: ní ki káaǹ yii: «Ukpil Yeesu, sɔ̄ ti-saai!».

eng: When Jesus saw them, he said, Go, show yourselves to the priests. As they left, they were cleansed.
local: Yeesu ká-bee ní ki bí yii: «Cáámaan ni-ba kí dàkà bisaraaŋaliibi.» Ŋyunti ŋun bi cée, ní bi pɔ́ɔkì.

eng: One of them, when he saw that he had been healed, returned and praised God with a loud voice.
local: Bi-kansikin ubɔ nín dáań ki ká yii u pɔ́ɔkèe, ní úǹ gbēeǹ niin ki fātì u-taaŋi pu ní ki nín gítiń ki sòoǹ mpɔn ki pàkà Unimbɔti.

eng: He fell on his face at Jesus feet and thanked him. He was a Samaritan.
local: U dɔ́miń ki tātìi dōoǹ Yeesu nimbiin dicincibil ki jáam̀-u. Kijapaayee nìn sá usamaariija ní.

eng: Jesus replied, Werent ten cleansed? Where are the other nine?.
local: Ní Yeesu bí yii: «M cáaǹ binib saalaa ní, kin biwa bin kíǹee bí la?.

eng: No one returned to praise God except this foreigner.
local: Mimmee báà ubɔ kaa dākafì kí gítiń kí nyɔ̄ŋkì Unimbɔti see unicaan nee babaaa?».

eng: Then Jesus said to him, Get up and go. Your faith has healed you.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-u yii: «Yīkì kí nīn cá, a-fookiiu di fīī-si.».

eng: It would be better for them to be thrown into a lake with a large stone hung around their neck than to cause one of these little ones to trip and fall into sin.
local: Bi yaa bàà dū ŋŋmii ki kúĺ kutaŋkpanaau ki dūu léeǹ u-neen, ki cáá-u ki tɔ́ tiŋkun, an bàà sɔ́ nì ú yàntī ŋwaamu nee kiba ń lítí ikpitin.

eng: Pharisees asked Jesus when Gods kingdom was coming. He replied, Gods kingdom isnt coming with signs that are easily noticed.
local: Fariisab bālifì Yeesu yii: «Ŋyunti ŋulɔŋu ní Unimbɔti Beel làá bàń ní?» Ní Yeesu kíí-bi yii: «Unimbɔti Beel kaa dòoń yéè kpìrrr bí ká-dì.

eng: Nor will people say, Look, here it is! or There it is! Dont you see? Gods kingdom is already among you.
local: Baa dāa lì yii: Kpèèmaan di bí dooyaaa, di bíń lapu doo, ama ní béé yii Unimbɔti Beel kíǹ ki bí ni-kansikin doo.».

eng: Then Jesus said to the disciples, The time will come when you will long to see one of the days of the Human One,and you wont see it.
local: Ní u bí u-boonnooliibi yii: «Ŋyunti ŋubɔ dòoń ni dāa nyàab̀ man Kinibiki wiiŋyi nee ŋubɔ, kaa làá ká.

eng: People will say to you, Look there! or Look here! Dont leave or go chasing after them.
local: Bi dāa tùkù-ni yii: <U bí doo ní!> <Aayee, u bí lapu ní!> Taa sāǹ kí cùmaan laaba.

eng: The Human Onewill appear on his day in the same way that a flash of lightning lights up the sky from one end to the other.
local: Man Kinibiki dāantam wiiŋŋu dal dāa sá kii utaa nín ŋmóò ki ŋmíntí pu kí kíliń kutagbɔŋu kílilaŋki kí nīn cá ku-kuntilaŋkee ní.

eng: However, first he must suffer many things and be rejected by this generation.
local: Ama kí wàatī míǹ ń nín bàńee, see ḿ jī falaa tikpil, waanee nib ń dū-mi kí fá.

eng: As it was in the days of Noah, so it will be during the days of the Human One.
local: Tin nìn jítì Noowa yuntiŋuee, tíǹ tibaantiil di làá dāá ŋá man Kinibiki mun wiiŋŋu dal.

eng: People were eating, drinking, marrying, and being given in marriage until the day Noah entered the ark and the flood came and destroyed them all.
local: Binib nìn jīǹ, ki nyù, bininjab cāab̀ binimpoobi ní binimpoobi mɔ́ń ticati áli ki tin sāā ŋyunti ŋun Noowa dāa kɔ́ meelanee, ní nnyiŋgbeen dɔ́miń ki kúntì bi-kɔkɔ pílpil.

eng: Likewise in the days of Lot, people were eating, drinking, buying, selling, planting, and building.
local: «An làá dāá pée tí ŋá kii Looti yuntiŋuee ní: binib nìn jīǹ, ki nyù, ní bin dāāyee dāā, ní bin nyáfée nyáfí, ní bin kù tikpaalee bí ki kù, ní bidimaliibi bí ki māā.

eng: But on the day Lot left Sodom, fire and sulfur rained down from heaven and destroyed them all.
local: Ama daliŋkin Looti yíkì Soodoomee, ní farwata nì ŋŋmi nyánní yilpu kii utaayee ki nìn̄ kitiŋ pu ní ki wùn̄ ki kúntì bi-kɔkɔ nyíki nyiki.

eng: Watch yourselves! If your brother or sister sins, warn them to stop. If they change their hearts and lives, forgive them.
local: Ní nīn nyíǹ ni-ba tiŋan! «A-nabiki yaa ŋá ikpitee, á wòoǹ-u ú taa ti ŋá míǹ. U yaa bí yii u-bɔti kpíìyee, dūú pɔ̄-u.

eng: Thats the way it will be on the day the Human Oneis revealed.
local: «Daliŋkin man Kinibiki yaa láa páaǹnée, min làá dāá ŋée dee.

eng: On that day, those on the roof, whose possessions are in the house, shouldnt come down to grab them. Likewise, those in the field shouldnt turn back.
local: Yaadal wiiŋŋuee, un yaa láa bí dicindee ń taa lì yii u làá kpákatí kí kɔ́ kudii poon kí yòon̄ u-wanti tiba, míǹ ní un bí kusaauee mun ń taa fàtīń mmɔŋki.

eng: Remember Lots wife!.
local: Téetímaan Looti nimpuu pu.

eng: Whoever tries to preserve their life will lose it, but whoever loses their life will preserve it.
local: Un là kí fīī u-maŋfalee làá fá-dì. Ama un fá u-maŋfalee làá kpāafì-dì.

eng: I tell you, on that night two people will be in the same bed: one will be taken and the other left.
local: Ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-ni, an nyeekuee, binib bili dāa dɔ̄ kilambaŋ pu ní án yòon̄ ubɔ kí yàntī un kíǹee.

eng: Two women will be grinding grain together: one will be taken and the other left.
local: Binimpoobi bili dāa gbāā ki nà kunabaantiiu pu ní án yòon̄ ubɔ kí yàntī un kíǹee. .

eng: The disciples asked, Where, Lord? Jesus said, The vultures gather wherever theres a dead body.
local: Ní u-boonnooliibi bālifì-u yii: «Ukpil, la ní míǹ làá dāá ŋée?» Ní Yeesu kíí-bi yii: «Laakin diwaŋkpil yaa dɔ̄ee, niin ní kaajiikub kpáfí.».

eng: Even if someone sins against you seven times in one day and returns to you seven times and says, I am changing my ways, you must forgive that person.
local: U yaa yīntiǹ-si áli miluli ŋwimbaanti ní ki dɔ́miń miluli gbantee ki tùkù-si yii: <M-bɔti kpíìyee,> dūú pɔ̄-u.».

eng: The apostles said to the Lord, Increase our faith.
local: Ní Yeesu kpambaliŋ bí-u yii: «Ti-Dindaan, kūtì-ti fookii.».

eng: The Lord replied, If you had faith the size of a mustard seed, you could say to this mulberry tree, Be uprooted and planted in the sea, and it would obey you.
local: Ní ti-Dindaan kíí-bi yii: «Ni yaa bàà cáámaan fookii ní báà u yaa bí fiii kii dijɔnjɔmbilee, ni bàà ŋūǹ kí tùkù busubu nee yii: <Á kpìkitī laakin a yú nee kí cùú cóoń tiŋku poon,> ní bú ŋá ni nín lī puee.».

eng: Would any of you say to your servant, who had just come in from the field after plowing or tending sheep, Come! Sit down for dinner?.
local: «Ni-kansikin ubɔ yaa cáá utɔntɔnli, u gbāā ki kù-u tikpaal àá u kpàá-u tiwaŋkuti, ní utɔntɔnlee yaa nyánní kusaau ki bāańee, u kíikí kí tùkù-u yii: <Dāan̄ malaa kí dāá nīn jīǹ tijinaaa?>.

eng: Wouldnt you say instead, Fix my dinner. Put on the clothes of a table servant and wait on me while I eat and drink. After that, you can eat and drink?.
local: See ú tùkù-u yii: <Ŋá-mi tijin, kí kpántí a-bɔkutil kí yòon̄ tijin kí cáań-mi. Man yaa jíń ki nyùn̄ee, si mun ń nín jī a-yati.>.

eng: You wont thank the servant because the servant did what you asked, will you?.
local: Aa cáá ijaam kí jāam̀ a-tɔntɔnliu u nín túń ditundi din a tīī-uee pu.

eng: Jesus was telling them a parable about their need to pray continuously and not to be discouraged.
local: Ní Yeesu dū tibɔti nee ki ŋmāntì kí dàkà yii an máaǹ bí nīn pée mèé kí taa gbèen̄.

eng: Two people went up to the temple to pray. One was a Pharisee and the other a tax collector.
local: yii: «Binib bili di nìn cūtì kunimbɔtidiin kí mèè, ubɔ nìn sá Fariisa ní un kíǹee sá ufɔtil.

eng: The Pharisee stood and prayed about himself with these words, God, I thank you that Im not like everyone else—crooks, evildoers, adulterers—or even like this tax collector.
local: Fariisee nìn yú ki mèé u-ban, yii: <Óo Unimbɔti, m jáaḿ-si man nín kaa bí kii bin kíǹee nín bí pu ki sá binaayub, binikpitib, bilaakpaadambee, m jáaḿ-si man nín kaa bí kii ufɔtil un bí neeyee.

eng: I fast twice a week. I give a tenth of everything I receive.
local: M bōob̀ kinyɔkɔ mili kinyɔŋ poon, ní m-wanti tin kɔkɔ m kāntée, m lèekī saalaa sá kamaa puee kubɔ kubɔ ki tìì-si.>.

eng: But the tax collector stood at a distance. He wouldnt even lift his eyes to look toward heaven. Rather, he struck his chest and said, God, show mercy to me, a sinner.
local: Ní ufɔtilee, úǹ yúń kudandaŋu, kaa là kí yáatí u-yil gba, ki fátí u-bindi ki yáliŋkí u-kpitii pu ní ki lì yii: <Óò Unimbɔti, sɔ̄ m-saai, m sá unikpiti ní!>».

eng: I tell you, this person went down to his home justified rather than the Pharisee. All who lift themselves up will be brought low, and those who make themselves low will be lifted up.
local: Ní Yeesu gíti lī yii: «M tùkù-ni yii ufɔtilee fātì u-dumpu, Unimbɔti dū u-kpitii ki pú-u, kaa pú un béeyee. Kun puee un sá kamaa yaa yòontí u-ba yilpuee, Unimbɔti làá sìntīǹ-u taapu, ní un sìntīǹ u-ba taapuee, Unimbɔti làá yòon̄-u yilpu.».

eng: People were bringing babies to Jesus so that he would bless them. When the disciples saw this, they scolded them.
local: Binib nìn cāabìń mbiyaawaamu gba Yeesu cee ú dū u-ŋal kí páań mu-pu kí mèè. U-boonnooliibi nín ká mimmee ní bi bíi ki jì-bi.

eng: Then Jesus called them to him and said, Allow the children to come to me. Dont forbid them, because Gods kingdom belongs to people like these children.
local: Ní Yeesu yíiń mbiyaamuee u-cee ki bí yii: «Yàntīmaan mbiyaawaamu ń dāan̄ m-cee. Ní taa pìin̄-mu nsan, kun puee Unimbɔti Beel sá ki tīī bin náań nì muee ní.

eng: I assure you that whoever doesnt welcome Gods kingdom like a child will never enter it.
local: Ibaamɔn ní m sòoǹ nì ni, un kamaa yaa kaa fōō Unimbɔti Beel kii kibiyaawaayee kpá kí kɔ́ di-ni.».

eng: A certain ruler asked Jesus, Good Teacher, what must I do to obtain eternal life.
local: Niinee, Juuda yab kpiliu ubɔ bālifì Yeesu yii: «Ukpimɔnti, m làá ŋá mana ní kí ká dimaŋfal din kaa cáá dikúntilee ní?».

eng: Jesus replied, Why do you call me good? No one is good except the one God.
local: Ní Yeesu fātìi kíí-u yii: «Ba pu ní a yíiń-mi yii umɔnti? Ubɔ kaa bí ki sá umɔnti, see Unimbɔti.

eng: He said, In a certain city there was a judge who neither feared God nor respected people.
local: U nìn bí yii: «Ubɔsoonli ubɔ di nìn bí kitiŋ kiban, kaa fàŋkí Unimbɔtaa, kaa tɔ́ unil mun.

eng: You know the commandments: Dont commit adultery. Dont murder. Dont steal. Dont give false testimony. Honor your father and mother. .
local: Kéè, sii a-ba nyí Unimbɔti marabaa! An lī yii: <Á taa ŋá tilaakpaal, kí taa kpɔ̀ unil, kí taa yɔ̀, kí taa mɔ̄ntì kí páań ubɔ pu fám, kí nīn tɔ́ a-baa nì a-na.>».

eng: Then the ruler said, Ive kept all of these things since I was a boy.
local: Ní uninjee fātìi kíí-u yii: «Doooo m-naacimpɔntin ní m pée ŋúb́ an marab gbanti.».

eng: When Jesus heard this, he said, Theres one more thing. Sell everything you own and distribute the money to the poor. Then you will have treasure in heaven. And come, follow me.
local: Yeesu gbìl̄ u nín lī puee, ní ki bí-u yii: «Kuwambaantiiu di kíǹ-si á ŋá. Nín̄ cá kí nyāfì tin kɔkɔ a cááyee kí dūú yàkatī bifalaadambi kí tin ká tigaajati Unimbɔti cee. A yaa ŋá mimmee, á dāan̄ kí nīn nóó-mi.».

eng: When he heard these words, the man became sad because he was extremely rich.
local: Kijapaayee nín gbìl̄ mimmee, kunimbaau kɔ́-u, kun puee u nìn cáá tigaajawan tikpil.

eng: When Jesus saw this, he said, Its very hard for the wealthy to enter Gods kingdom!.
local: Yeesu nín ká yii kunimbaau kɔ́-uee, ní u bí yii: «An pɔ̄ɔ̄ tikpil ki tīī bigaajab bí kɔ́ Unimbɔti Beelineeee!.

eng: Its easier for a camel to squeeze through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter Gods kingdom.
local: An pɔ̀fì ki tīī ugutaamu ń jītí ujoo buŋŋun ki jítiǹ ugaaja ń kɔ́ Unimbɔti Beelin.».

eng: Those who heard this said, Then who can be saved.
local: Ní bin nìn bíi pílifíǹ-uee bí yii: «Kin, ŋma di làá nín ká difiil dee?».

eng: Jesus replied, What is impossible for humans is possible for God.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-bi yii: «Tin pɔ̄ɔ̄ binib ceeyee, kaa pɔ̄ɔ̄ Unimbɔti cee.».

eng: Peter said, Look, we left everything we own and followed you.
local: Ní Peetroo bí yii: «Kin timbi bin cāntì tin ti cááyee ki táá-see fáà?».

eng: Jesus said to them, I assure you that anyone who has left house, husband, wife, brothers, sisters, parents, or children because of Gods kingdom.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-u yii: «M sòoǹ nì ni ibaamɔn ní, un kamaa di yaa fá u-dumpu nì u-nimpuu nì u-nabiyaamu nì u-baa nì u-na nì u-biyaamu, Unimbɔti Beel puee.

eng: In that city there was a widow who kept coming to him, asking, Give me justice in this case against my adversary.
local: Kitiŋ gbanti poonee, ukpiipu ubɔ nìn bí ki dòoń ubɔsoonlee cee múlmul ki bí-u yii: <M-nannanliu ŋá-mi tibɔti, pɔ̀ m-bɔti kí tīī-mi.>.

eng: will receive many times more in this age and eternal life in the coming age.
local: làá fōō tipaal tikpil ŋyunti ŋun ti bí nee poon. U làá dāá gíti fōō dimaŋfal din kaa cáá dikúntilee ŋyunti ŋun dòońee.».

eng: Jesus took the Twelve aside and said, Look, were going up to Jerusalem, and everything written about the Human Oneby the prophets will be accomplished.
local: Ní Yeesu gāntì u-kpambaliŋ saalaa nì alee ní ki bí-bi yii: «Ní pīlifì tiŋan, Jeerusaleem un ti cá nee, tin kɔkɔ Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi nìn ŋmàn̄ ki yóoǹ yii an làá dāá bàń man Kinibiki puee, ŋyunti bāań ní nee án ŋá míǹ.

eng: He will be handed over to the Gentiles. He will be ridiculed, mistreated, and spit on.
local: Bi làá cúú-mi kí tīī bin kaa sá Juuda yabee bí bèel̀-mi, kí sìì-mi, kí tíiĺ timɔtan kí làbitì-mi.

eng: After torturing him, they will kill him. On the third day, he will rise up.
local: Bi làá gbá-mi anaalab ní kí kpɔ̀-mi. Ní iwiin ita dalee ḿ fìkitī.».

eng: But the Twelve understood none of these words. The meaning of this message was hidden from them and they didnt grasp what he was saying.
local: Ama u-kpambaliŋ kaa nìn gbìl̄ tíǹ gbanti kɔkɔ taapu, kaa nyí tin pu ní Yeesu sòoǹ mimmee, kun puee tibɔtee nìn dáá bí-bi dibalin.

eng: As Jesus came to Jericho, a certain blind man was sitting beside the road begging.
local: Yeesu nín tóobiń Jeeriikooyee, uninja ubɔ nìn kā disaŋkaŋkpil ki sá ujɔfu ki mèé tiwan.

eng: When the man heard the crowd passing by, he asked what was happening.
local: U gbìl̄ kunipaau jītée, ní u bālifì yii: «Án sá ba ní?».

eng: They told him, Jesus the Nazarene is passing by.
local: Ní bi tùkū-u yii: «Yeesu Naasaareeti yɔuee di jītí.».

eng: The blind man shouted, Jesus, Son of David, show me mercy.
local: Niinee ní u téeń mpɔn yii: «Yeesu, Daafiidi naantiiu, sɔ̄ m-saai!».

eng: Those leading the procession scolded him, telling him to be quiet, but he shouted even louder, Son of David, show me mercy.
local: Ní bin nìn līī nyɔkɔpuee bíi kpàtī u-pu yii ú ŋmīĺee. Ní u mɔ̄tìi bíi kāantí yii: «Daafiidi naantiiu, sɔ̄ m-saai!».

eng: For a while he refused but finally said to himself, I dont fear God or respect people.
local: Ní ubɔsoonlee tafal kpá, an tin yúntì, boonee ní u dāa bí u-ba yii: <Nì min kɔkɔ maa fàŋkí Unimbɔti, kaa tɔ́ unilee.

eng: Jesus stopped and called for the man to be brought to him. When he was present Jesus asked.
local: Ní Yeesu sāā ki síl̀, ní ki bí yii bí cáań-u u-cee. U bāańee, ní Yeesu bālifì-u yii.

eng: What do you want me to do for you? He said, Lord, I want to see.
local: «Ba ní a là ḿ ŋá kí tīī-si ní?» Ní u bí yii: «Ukpil, m là kí wāl̀ ní.».

eng: Jesus said to him, Receive your sight! Your faith has healed you.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-u yii: «Nín̄ kíǹ ki wāĺ! A-fookiiu di fīī-si.».

eng: At once he was able to see, and he began to follow Jesus, praising God. When all the people saw it, they praised God too.
local: Niin niin ní u-nimbiliŋ cúu ŋúbitì ní u wál̀ ní ki kíǹ ki táá Yeesu ki nyɔ́ŋkí Unimbɔti. Kunipaauee kɔkɔ nín ká mimmee, ní bi bíi pàkà Unimbɔti.

eng: but I will give this widow justice because she keeps bothering me. Otherwise, there will be no end to her coming here and embarrassing me.
local: ukpiipu nee nín dòoń ki páb̀-mi pu nee, m làá sòoǹ bi-bɔti nee kí tīī-u. An yaa sá fám, u làá nīn pée dòoń ki páb̀-mi yéè ní kí dāá tìl̄ m-fam.>».

eng: The Lord said, Listen to what the unjust judge says.
local: Ní ti-Dindaan gíti bí yii: «Pīlifìmaan ubɔsooŋkpitee nín lī puee.

eng: Wont God provide justice to his chosen people who cry out to him day and night? Will he be slow to help them?.
local: Míǹ ní Unimbɔti làá sòoǹ kí pɔ̀ bin u lútì bi tíminsí-u ŋwiimpu nì kunyeeuee, waa làá yàntī-bi, u làá tūtuǹ-bi malaa.

eng: I tell you, he will give them justice quickly. But when the Human Onecomes, will he find faithfulness on earth.
local: Ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-ni, see ú sòoǹ kí pɔ̀ bi-bɔti. Ama, man Kinibiki yaa láa fātìi gítìńee, m làá ká fookiidambi kitiŋ puɔɔɔ?».

eng: Jesus told this parable to certain people who had convinced themselves that they were righteous and who looked on everyone else with disgust.
local: Yeesu gíti dū tibɔti nee ki ŋmāntiǹ bin nìn lì yii bíǹ bí deedee Unimbɔti nimbiin nì bi-ba nyítam ki kpèé ki sìnkīǹ bin kíǹeeyee.

eng: Jesus entered Jericho and was passing through town.
local: Yeesu bàn̄ Jeeriikooyee, ki bíi còom̄ ki jītí kitiŋin.

eng: The Human Onecame to seek and save the lost.
local: Di sá yii man Kinibiki dɔ́miń kí nyàab̀ kí fīī bin nìn wáŋkèe ní.».

eng: As they listened to this, Jesus told them another parable because he was near Jerusalem and they thought Gods kingdom would appear right away.
local: Ní Yeesu gíti kútì tibɔŋmantikaal nee ki lī bin nìn bí ki pílifíǹ-uee, kun puee bi nìn dàkafī yii u nín tóob́ Jeerusaleem nee, Unimbɔti làá dū-u kí kàan̄ Dibeel kɔŋkɔnnee ní.

eng: He said, A certain man who was born into royalty went to a distant land to receive his kingdom and then return.
local: Ní u lī yii: «Uninja ubɔ di sá ubɔti maaliŋun, ní ki yíkì ki búntì kitiŋ kiba poon ndandati kí cù kí fōoń dibeel, kí gítiń u-tiŋki pu kí dāá kàl̄ tibɔtiil.

eng: He called together ten servants and gave each of them money worth four months wages.He said, Do business with this until I return.
local: Kí wàatī ú nín yīkèe, u yíiń u-tɔntɔnliibi kansikinee saalaa ní ki tīī bi-kɔkɔ kinimbifɔŋ kiba kiba bí nīn cáa bàntī ú dāan̄.

eng: His citizens hated him, so they sent a representative after him who said, We dont want this man to be our king.
local: Ama ní u-tiŋkin nib kaa là u-bɔti. U yíkì kpalaayee, ní bi tú binib u-boon bí cùú lì yii: «Timbi kaa là kijapaai nee ń nīn sá ti-bɔtiu.».

eng: After receiving his kingdom, he returned and called the servants to whom he had given the money to find out how much they had earned.
local: Ama u nín cūtì bi kāaǹní-u dibeel u fātìńee ní u yíiń u-tɔntɔnliibi saalaa bin u nìn tīī animbil bí nīn cáa bàntēe kí kpèè yii bi bāntì ki ká mana.

eng: The first servant came forward and said, Your money has earned a return of one thousand percent.
local: Ní upeepeekaa dɔ́miń ki bí yii: <Ukpil, a nín tīī-mi animbil ḿ dūú bàntēe, m bāntì ki ká míǹ saalaa mun ki kútì.>.

eng: The king replied, Excellent! You are a good servant. Because you have been faithful in a small matter, you will have authority over ten cities.
local: Ní u-kpiliu bí yii: <Mbòó, a sá utɔntɔmmɔnti, kun puee m tīī-si tiwan fiii ní a ŋúb́-tì nì deedee. An puee m kāaǹ-si á nīn sá ntimu saalaa bɔtiu.>.

eng: The second servant came and said, Master, your money has made a return of five hundred percent.
local: Ní uliliiti mun bāań ní ki bí yii: <Ukpil, a nín tīī-mi animbil ŋiŋyee, m dūu bāntì ki ká míǹ miŋmoo pu ki kútì.>.

eng: To this one, the king said, You will have authority over five cities.
local: Ní ukpilee bí yii: <Si mun ń nīn sá ntimu muŋmoo bɔtiu.>.

eng: A man there named Zacchaeus, a ruler among tax collectors, was rich.
local: Uninja ubɔ nìn bí niin, bi yíì-u Jaasee. U nìn sá bifɔtiliibi kpiliu ní ki sá ugaaja.

eng: Another servant came and said, Master, here is your money. I wrapped it up in a scarf for safekeeping.
local: Ní ubɔ mun bāań, ní ki bí yii: <Ukpil, kpèè a-nimbiliŋ sɔ̀. M dūu kúĺ kiŋaabikin ki yóoǹ-si.

eng: I was afraid of you because you are a stern man. You withdraw what you havent deposited and you harvest what you havent planted.
local: M fāŋkì-si, kun puee a sá unil un bɔti pɔ̄ɔ̄yee. Kun saa yóoǹee, kúǹ ní a yòontí, ní ki cēē kun saa búĺee.>.

eng: The king replied, I will judge you by the words of your own mouth, you worthless servant! You knew, did you, that Im a stern man, withdrawing what I didnt deposit, and harvesting what I didnt plant?.
local: Ní ubɔtee bí-u yii: <Utɔntɔŋkpiti ní a sá. Tin tin a sòon̄ee, tíǹ ní m làá gíiǹ kí dūú kpɔ̀ a-bɔti. A nyí m sá unil un bɔti pɔ̄ɔ̄yee, ní kun maa síiǹee ní m yòontí, ní ki cēē kun kun maa búĺee.

eng: Why then didnt you put my money in the bank? Then when I arrived, at least I could have gotten it back with interest.
local: Kin, ba pu ní aa cáá m-nimbiliŋ kí ŋá baŋkan, m yaa láa fātìi gítìńee, ḿ bàà fōō m-nimbiliŋ nì ipaati?>.

eng: He said to his attendants, Take his money and give it to the one who has ten times as much.
local: Ní u bí bin bí niinee yii: <Fōōmaan animbil u-cee kí dūú kūtì un cáá saalaayee.>.

eng: But Master, they said, he already has ten times as much.
local: Ní bíǹ bí yii: <Ukpil, u cáá saalaayaa!>.

eng: He replied, I say to you that everyone who has will be given more, but from those who have nothing, even what they have will be taken away.
local: Ní u kíí-bi yii: <M tùkù-ni yii, un kamaa yaa ŋúb́ tin u cááyee tiŋanee, bi dāa kútí-u ní. Ama un kamaa yaa kaa ŋúb́ tin u cááyee tiŋanee ní bí dāá fōō fiii un u cááyee.

eng: As for my enemies who dont want me as their king, bring them here and slaughter them before me.
local: An yaa kíǹ m-nannanliibi bin bí yii baa là ḿ nīn sá bi-bɔtiuee, cáaḿmaan-bi kí kpɔ̀-bi m-nimbiin doo.>».

eng: After Jesus said this, he continued on ahead, going up to Jerusalem.
local: Yeesu sòon̄ míǹ ki dóòyee, ní ki līntì bin táá-uee nyɔkɔpu ki búntì Jeerusaleem.

eng: As Jesus came to Bethphage and Bethany on the Mount of Olives, he gave two disciples a task.
local: U nín tóob́ Beetifagee nì Beetanii, kigbantaŋ kin bi yíì Ɔliifi sufii joolee caŋinee, ní u dū u-boonnooliibi bili ki tú u-nyɔkɔpu ki tùkū-bi yii.

eng: He was trying to see who Jesus was, but, being a short man, he couldnt because of the crowd.
local: Kijapaayee bàà pée nyàab̀ kí wāl̀ un ní Yeesu baasii sée, ama u nín sá kinigiŋgiŋee puee, waa nìn ŋūǹ kí wāl̀-u, kun puee binib nìn múkú Yeesu pu tikpil.

eng: He said, Go into the village over there. When you enter it, you will find tied up there a colt that no one has ever ridden. Untie it and bring it here.
local: «Nín̄ cámaan kitimbilii kin bí ti-nyɔkɔpu nee. Ni yaa bàn̄ ki kɔ́ ki-nee, ni làá ká dimampɔmbil diba gbìn̄, din pu báà ubɔ kaa láá kāl̀ee, ní gbìntīmaan-dì kí cáań-mi.

eng: If anyone asks, Why are you untying it? just say, Its master needs it.
local: Ubɔ yaa bālifì-ni yii: <Ba pu ní ni gbìntī-dèe?> ní kíí-u yii: <Ti-Dindaan di nyàab̀ kimaŋ.>».

eng: Those who had been sent found it exactly as he had said.
local: Bin Yeesu túee búntì ní ki bàn̄ ki ká kii u nín tùkūń-bi puee deedee.

eng: As they were untying the colt, its owners said to them, Why are you untying the colt.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun bi bíi gbìntī kimaŋee ní ki-dindambi bālifì-bi yii: «Ba pu ní ni gbìntī-kì?».

eng: They replied, Its master needs it.
local: Ní bi kíí-bi yii: «Ti-Dindaan di nyàab̀ kimaŋ.».

eng: They brought it to Jesus, threw their clothes on the colt, and lifted Jesus onto it.
local: Bi cáań dimampɔmbilee Yeesu cee ní ki pēetì bi-wampeeŋkaati ki páaǹ di-pu ní ki yāntì Yeesu kāl̀.

eng: As Jesus rode along, they spread their clothes on the road.
local: Yeesu còom̄ ki cée, ní binib kpátí bi-wampeeŋkaati ki bīil̀ nsan pu.

eng: As Jesus approached the road leading down from the Mount of Olives, the whole throng of his disciples began rejoicing. They praised God with a loud voice because of all the mighty things they had seen.
local: U bíi kpákatí Ɔliifi sufii jool pu ki tóobiń Jeerusaleemee, u-boonnooliibi kpáfì dinikitikpaandi an mɔ̄ǹ-bi tikpil bi fàal̀ ki pàkà Unimbɔti mpɔn maamaacib bin bi nìn ká Yeesu ŋáaǹee pu.

eng: They said, Blessings on the king who comes in the name of the Lord. Peace in heaven and glory in the highest heavens.
local: Ní bi lì yii: «Ti-Dindaan ń bìin̄ ubɔti un dòoń u-yindi puee boon. Ŋgbansɔŋfi bí yilpu ní tinyool sá ki tīī Unimbɔti!».

eng: Some of the Pharisees from the crowd said to Jesus, Teacher, scold your disciples! Tell them to stop.
local: Fariisab biba nìn bí kunipaaun, ní ki bí Yeesu yii: «Ukpil, tùkù a-boonnooliibi yii bí ŋmīĺee!».

eng: So he ran ahead and climbed up a sycamore tree so he could see Jesus, who was about to pass that way.
local: Ní u sáǹ ki līntì nyɔkɔpu ki cūtìi jɔ́m̀ busubu pu kí wāl̀ Yeesu, kun puee Yeesu làá nìn jītí niin ní.

eng: He answered, I tell you, if they were silent, the stones would shout.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-bi yii: «M tùkù-ni ibaamɔn ní, báà bi nee yaa ŋmíl̀aaa, ataŋkpee làá kūuntì.».

eng: As Jesus came to the city and observed it, he wept over it.
local: Yeesu nín tóobiń kí kɔ́ Jeerusaleem ki wál̀ kitiŋee ní u bíi sūǹ ki-nib pu.

eng: He said, If only you knew on this of all days the things that lead to peace. But now they are hidden from your eyes.
local: ní ki bí yii: «Díǹ wiiŋŋu nee ni yaa bàà ŋūǹ kí béé kun ŋūǹ kí cáań-ni ŋgbansɔŋfee, ama kɔŋkɔnnee an bí-ni dibalin.

eng: The time will come when your enemies will build fortifications around you, encircle you, and attack you from all sides.
local: An puee iwiin iba dòoń ni-nannanliibi làá dāan̄ kí bíĺ ataŋkpuku kí màntīí tū ni-tiŋki, ní kí lùtīí gɔ́ń-kì kí táká-ni.

eng: They will crush you completely, you and the people within you. They wont leave one stone on top of another within you, because you didnt recognize the time of your gracious visit from God.
local: Bi làá kúntí ni nì ni-tiŋki kɔkɔ, kí taa kíń báà kudigbuŋu, kun puee naa béè ŋyunti ŋun Unimbɔti dɔ́miń kí jòoń-nee.».

eng: When Jesus entered the temple, he threw out those who were selling things there.
local: Yeesu bāań Jeerusaleemee, ní ki kɔ́ kunimbɔtidiin, ki kíĺ ki bíi jì biwannyafiliibi.

eng: He said to them, Its written, My house will be a house of prayer, but you have made it a hideout for crooks. .
local: ní ki tùkū-bi yii: «An ŋmàn̄ Unimbɔti gbɔŋkun yii: <M-diiku làá nīn sá ŋwimmeem diiku ní, ama ní nimbi dū-ku ki kpántì kunaayudii!>».

eng: Jesus was teaching daily in the temple. The chief priests, the legal experts, and the foremost leaders among the people were seeking to kill him.
local: Yeesu nìn kíǹ ki bí baabadal kunimbɔtidiin ki dàkà Unimbɔti bɔti, ní bisaraaŋakpilib nì bimaradakaliibi nì bitindaŋkpilib bíi nyàab̀ bi nín làá dī pu kí kpɔ̀-uee.

eng: However, they couldnt find a way to do it because all the people were enthralled with what they heard.
local: Ama baa nìn nyí min ní bi làá ŋée, kun puee kunipaau kɔkɔ nìn bí ki pílifíǹ Yeesu kaa là kí lóó u-nyɔɔbundi báà diba.

eng: When Jesus came to that spot, he looked up and said, Zacchaeus, come down at once. I must stay in your home today.
local: Yeesu dūu bāań niinee, ní ki wáaǹ yilpu ní ki bí-u yii: «Jaasee, kpákatiń malaa, kun puee sii dumpu ní m làá kɔ́ díǹ.».

eng: So Zacchaeus came down at once, happy to welcome Jesus.
local: Ní Jaasee ŋá malaa malaa ki kpákatìń ní ki fōō Yeesu ki cūnnì u-dumpu nì dipɔɔpindi.

eng: Everyone who saw this grumbled, saying, He has gone to be the guest of a sinner.
local: Bin nìn bí niin ki ká mimmee nìn bíi gbùkum̄ yii: «Uninja nee cūtì ki kɔ́ unikpiti dumpu dee.».

eng: Zacchaeus stopped and said to the Lord, Look, Lord, I give half of my possessions to the poor. And if I have cheated anyone, I repay them four times as much.
local: Ní Jaasee síl̀ ti-Dindaan nimbiin ní ki bí yii: «Ti-Dindaan, kpèè, m làá lēē m-gaajati dijandi kí tīī bisoodambi, ní m yaa nìn mūul̀ ubɔ ki fōō animbil u-cee, m làá téeǹ-u min m mūul̀-uee minaa pu.».

eng: Jesus said to him, Today, salvation has come to this household because he too is a son of Abraham.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-u yii: «Difiil kɔ́nní kudii nee poon díǹ, kun puee uninja nee mun sá Abraam maaliŋun ní.

eng: In those days Caesar Augustus declared that everyone throughout the empire should be enrolled in the tax lists.
local: Ŋyuntee ní gɔminakpaan Oogusti yóoǹ mara yii bí kàaǹ ntimu kɔkɔ nib.

eng: The angel said, Dont be afraid! Look! I bring good news to you—wonderful, joyous news for all people.
local: Ní ntuunee bí-bi yii: «Taa fàŋkìmaan! Dinyɔɔbummɔɔndi din làá mɔ̄ɔkiǹ báà ŋma tikpilee ní m cáań-ni.

eng: Your savior is born today in Davids city. He is Christ the Lord.
local: Dinyɔɔbundi gbanti di sá yii díǹ nyeeku nee, bi màl̄-ni Ufiil ubɔ Daafiidi dumpu Beetileem. Úǹ di sá Kriisitoo, ti-Dindaan.

eng: This is a sign for you: you will find a newborn baby wrapped snugly and lying in a manger.
local: Min ni làá dūu béé an bikee di sá, ni yaa bàn̄ee, ni làá ká ki dɔ̄ laakin bi ŋáań tiwaŋkuti timoolee, bi dū iŋaabu ki fìn̄-kì.».

eng: Suddenly a great assembly of the heavenly forces was with the angel praising God. They said.
local: Kpalaayee, ní Unimbɔti tuuŋyi dikitikpaandi diba nyánní yilpu ki dɔ́miń ki kútì ŋun béeyee pu. I nìn kíĺ ki bíi nyɔ́ŋkí Unimbɔti yii.

eng: Glory to God in heaven, and on earth peace among those whom he favors.
local: «Inyɔŋki sá ki tīī Unimbɔti un bí yilpu fɔ́ŋŋŋee, ní u-gbansɔŋfiŋu sá ki tīī taapu doo nib bin fáatì-uee.».

eng: When the angels returned to heaven, the shepherds said to each other, Lets go right now to Bethlehem and see whats happened. Lets confirm what the Lord has revealed to us.
local: Ituunee yíkì ki nín gítí yilpuee, ní bipikpaaliibee sòon̄ yii: «Tí cùmaan Beetileem! See tí ká tin di bāań ní ti-Dindaan yāntì bi dɔ́miń ki tùkū-tee.».

eng: They went quickly and found Mary and Joseph, and the baby lying in the manger.
local: Niinee ní bi yíkì malaa malaa ki búntì. Bi bàn̄ee, ki ká Maarii nì Jooseefi nì kibiki, kíǹ dɔ̄ laakin bi ŋáań tiwaŋkuti timool tì ŋmóòyee poon.

eng: When they saw this, they reported what they had been told about this child.
local: Bi ká kibikee, ní ki máafì Unimbɔti tuuŋŋu nín dɔ́miń ki tùkū-bi kibiki gbanti bɔti puee.

eng: Everyone who heard it was amazed at what the shepherds told them.
local: Binib bin kɔkɔ nìn gbìl̄ tin bipikpaaliibee sòoǹee nìn bākatì ní sèeee.

eng: Mary committed these things to memory and considered them carefully.
local: Ama Maarii úǹ dū an bɔti kɔkɔ ki kpáafì u-pɔbilin ki dàkafī ti-pu.

eng: This first enrollment occurred when Quirinius governed Syria.
local: Dikaampeepeekaalee nìn sá ŋyunti ŋun Kriniyusi nìn sá gɔmina Siirii tiŋkinee ní.

eng: The shepherds returned home, glorifying and praising God for all they had heard and seen. Everything happened just as they had been told.
local: Ní bipikpaaliibi fātìi nín gítí bi-pikpaaku laŋki ki nyɔ́ŋkí Unimbɔti ki pàkà-u bi nín gbìl̄ tiŋyee nì tin bi kée pu. Di sá yii ntuunee nín pée tùkū-bi puee, míǹ deedee ní bi ká.

eng: When eight days had passed, Jesus parents circumcised him and gave him the name Jesus. This was the name given to him by the angel before he was conceived.
local: An tú winniiyee, bi gíí kibiki dikpancandi ní ki pú-kì diyindi yii Yeesu, kii Unimbɔti tuuŋŋu nín nìn pée tùkū u-na pu kí wàatī ú nín cáá dipoolee.

eng: When the time came for their ritual cleansing, in accordance with the Law from Moses, they brought Jesus up to Jerusalem to present him to the Lord. .
local: Ŋyunti ŋun sá bí ŋá Maarii akoobil kii Unimbɔti nín yāntì Mooyiisi ŋmàn̄ marab puee bàn̄ee, ní bi yōoǹ kibiki ki cáa cūnnì Jeerusaleem tiŋkin kí cáá-kì kí ŋá ti-Dindaan ŋaan.

eng: Its written in the Law of the Lord, Every firstborn male will be dedicated to the Lord.
local: An ŋmàn̄ ti-Dindaan marau poon yii: «Kibiseebiki yaa sá kininjabikee, an sá bí gàntī kíǹ ki-ba kí yóoń ti-Dindaan.

eng: They offered a sacrifice in keeping with whats stated in the Law of the Lord, A pair of turtledoves or two young pigeons .
local: ní kí cáá abeenanjipɔmbil ali àá nnanjilimu muli kí ŋá saraa, kii ti-Dindaan nín síiǹ mara puee.».

eng: A man named Simeon was in Jerusalem. He was righteous and devout. He eagerly anticipated the restoration of Israel, and the Holy Spirit rested on him.
local: Uninja ubɔ nìn bí Jeerusaleem, bi yíì-u Simeeyɔn. U nìn yú deedee ki tɔ́ Unimbɔti ní ki nìn kā ki cí un làá dāan̄ kí fīī Isirayeel yabee. Unimbɔti Fam nìn bí u-cee.

eng: The Holy Spirit revealed to him that he wouldnt die before he had seen the Lords Christ.
local: ní ki nìn tùkū-u yii u làá ká Meesiya ní kí nín kpú.

eng: Led by the Spirit, he went into the temple area. Meanwhile, Jesus parents brought the child to the temple so that they could do what was customary under the Law.
local: Unimbɔti Fam kpōotì Simeeyɔn ki kɔ́ǹ kunimbɔtidiin ŋyunti ŋun Yeesu na nì u-baa cáá-u niin kí ŋá mara nín bālifì miŋyee.

eng: Simeon took Jesus in his arms and praised God. He said.
local: Simeeyɔn nìn fōō-kì u-ŋaan ki jáam̀ Unimbɔti ki lī yii.

eng: Now, master, let your servant go in peace according to your word.
local: «M-Dindaan, a nín nìn pōoǹ puee, an ŋá míǹ! Kɔŋkɔnnee, yàntī a-tɔntɔnliu ń kpú nì ŋgbansɔŋfi.

eng: Everyone went to their own cities to be enrolled.
local: Báà ŋma nìn cá u-naanja tiŋkin bí kàaǹ-u.

eng: because my eyes have seen your salvation.
local: kun puee m-nimbil ká Ufiil.

eng: You prepared this salvation in the presence of all peoples.
local: un a bɔ́ntì ki yóoǹ inibool kɔkɔee.

eng: Its a light for revelation to the Gentiles and a glory for your people Israel.
local: Úǹ di sá ŋwalifi ŋun làá yàntī atimbul kɔkɔ ń béé-see. Úǹ di làá tīī a-nibul Isirayeel yab tinyool.».

eng: His father and mother were amazed by what was said about him.
local: Niinee, ní Yeesu baa nì u-na bākatì nì tibɔti tin Simeeyɔn bíi sòoǹ bi-biki puee.

eng: Simeon blessed them and said to Mary his mother, This boy is assigned to be the cause of the falling and rising of many in Israel and to be a sign that generates opposition.
local: Ní Simeeyɔn mēē Unimbɔti ń bìin̄ bi-pu. U mēē ki dóòyee, ní ki bí Maarii yii: «Kpèè, Unimbɔti lēē kibiki nee. Ki-puee Isirayeel yab tikpil làá lítí ní tikpil mun ń yīkì. Úǹ di làá dāá nīn sá Unimbɔti daaŋku kun binib làá dāá kpákaǹee.

eng: so that the inner thoughts of many will be revealed. And a sword will pierce your innermost being too.
local: ki làá dāá bìitī binib tikpil landɔkɔi in bál̀ bi-pɔbiliŋ poonee. Ama sii ubinaati, bi nín làá dāá ŋá-u puee làá dāá kɔ́ kí dóó-si, kii kitàataajiki ní bi dūu sāā a-pɔbilee.».

eng: There was also a prophet, Anna the daughter of Phanuel, who belonged to the tribe of Asher. She was very old. After she married, she lived with her husband for seven years.
local: Unimpu ubɔ mun nìn bí, bi yíì-u Ana. U nìn sá Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu. Fanuyeel un sá Aseer naantitɔntɔkɔlee bisal dee. U nín mɔ́ǹ ticatee, u ŋá abin aluli ní u-cal kpíì.

eng: She was now an -year-old widow. She never left the temple area but worshipped God with fasting and prayer night and day.
local: ní u gbíntì míǹ ki sá ukpiipu, kaa ti mɔ́ǹ, ní ki púĺ tikpil ki tin bàn̄ abin imuŋku inaa nì abin anaa. U nìn tūǹ Unimbɔti tundi ŋwiimpu nì kunyeeu ki bōob̀ kinyɔkɔ ki mèé Unimbɔti, kaa sútí kí wàatī kunimbɔtidii.

eng: She approached at that very moment and began to praise God and to speak about Jesus to everyone who was looking forward to the redemption of Jerusalem.
local: Ŋyunti gbanti ní unimpuee bàn̄ ki ŋmāntiǹ-bi kunimbɔtidiin ní ki bíi pàkā Unimbɔti, ní ki sòoǹ kibikee bɔti ki tùkù bin kɔkɔ kā ki cí Unimbɔti ń dāá fīī Jeerusaleem yabee.

eng: When Mary and Joseph had completed everything required by the Law of the Lord, they returned to their hometown, Nazareth in Galilee.
local: Yeesu baa nì u-na ŋá ti-Dindaan nín yóoǹ mara bí ŋá puee ki dóòyee, ní bi fātìi nín gítí bi-dumpu Naasaareeti, Galiilee tiŋkin.

eng: Since Joseph belonged to Davids house and family line, he went up from the city of Nazareth in Galilee to Davids city, called Bethlehem, in Judea.
local: Míǹ puee ní Jooseefi mun yíkì Naasaareeti Galiilee tiŋkin ki nín cá ubɔti Daafiidi dumpu Beetileem, Juudee tiŋkin, kun puee u nìn sá Daafiidi naantitɔntɔkɔl ní.

eng: The child grew up and became strong. He was filled with wisdom, and Gods favor was on him.
local: Kibikee nìn wīintí ki cíkití ki cáá iciin tikpil, ní Unimbɔti bīī ki-boon.

eng: Each year his parents went to Jerusalem for the Passover Festival.
local: Dibindi sá kamaayee, Yeesu baa nì u-na cá Jeerusaleem ní ki jīǹ Dilákatil jiŋaaliu.

eng: When he was years old, they went up to Jerusalem according to their custom.
local: Yeesu bàn̄ abin saalaa nì alee, u-baa nì u-na tí cá Jeerusaleem jiŋaal kii mara nín bālifì puee, ní ki kpáaǹ-u.

eng: After the festival was over, they were returning home, but the boy Jesus stayed behind in Jerusalem. His parents didnt know it.
local: Bi jíń jiŋaal ki dóòyee, ní Yeesu úǹ gbíntì niin, ní u-baa nì u-na nín kùnní, kaa nyí yii Yeesu gbíntìń.

eng: Supposing that he was among their band of travelers, they journeyed on for a full day while looking for him among their family and friends.
local: Bi nìn cōom̀ ŋwimmɔn ki dàkafī yii u bí bi-saŋkinjutiibi bin kíǹee cee. Boonee ní bi kíǹ ki bíi nyàab̀-u bi-nabiyaamu nì bi-bɔɔbi cee.

eng: When they didnt find Jesus, they returned to Jerusalem to look for him.
local: Baa ká-uee, ní ki nín gítí Jeerusaleem kí nyàab̀-u.

eng: After three days they found him in the temple. He was sitting among the teachers, listening to them and putting questions to them.
local: Winta dalee, ní bi dāa ká-u kunimbɔtidiin, u kā bimaradakaliibi kansikin ki pílifí bi nín sòoǹ puee, ní ki bàlifī-bi tibɔti.

eng: Everyone who heard him was amazed by his understanding and his answers.
local: Bin kɔkɔ nìn bí niin ki pílifée nìn bākatì u nín cáá iciin ki kíikí tibɔti miŋyee.

eng: When his parents saw him, they were shocked. His mother said, Child, why have you treated us like this? Listen! Your father and I have been worried. Weve been looking for you.
local: U-baa nì u-na bāań ki ká-u niinee, ní bi bākatì, ní u-na bí yii: «M-biki, ba ŋá ní a ŋá-ti mimmee! Man nì a-baa nyàab̄-si, ti-nimbiliŋ mántì.».

eng: Jesus replied, Why were you looking for me? Didnt you know that it was necessary for me to be in my Fathers house.
local: Ní u bí-bi yii: «Ba ŋá ní ni nyàab̄-mi? Naa nyí yii m máaǹ kí nīn bí m-Baa diikunaaa?».

eng: He went to be enrolled together with Mary, who was promised to him in marriage and who was pregnant.
local: U nì u-bɔɔn Maarii un nìn sá upɔdaanee di nìn cūtì dikàandi gbanti.

eng: But they didnt understand what he said to them.
local: Ama baa nìn gbìl̄ u nín sòon̄ puee taapu.

eng: Jesus went down to Nazareth with them and was obedient to them. His mother cherished every word in her heart.
local: Niinee ní u yíkìi nóó-bi ki nín gítí Naasaareeti, ní ki sīntiǹ u-ba ki tīī-bi. Ní u-na cúú tin kɔkɔ jítì kunimbɔtidiinee ki kpáafì u-pɔbilin.

eng: Jesus matured in wisdom and years, and in favor with God and with people.
local: Yeesu nìn wīintí ní u-ciiŋyi mun wīintí, ní u nìn mɔ̀tīi fáatíǹ Unimbɔti nì binib.

eng: While they were there, the time came for Mary to have her baby.
local: Bi bí Beetileem niinee, ní ŋwiin ŋun Maarii làá màal̀ee dūu bāań.

eng: She gave birth to her firstborn child, a son, wrapped him snugly, and laid him in a manger, because there was no place for them in the guestroom.
local: U nìn màl̄ u-biseemal kibininjabiki ní ki dū iŋaabu ki fìn̄-kì. U nìn dū-kì ki bíl̀ laakin bi ŋáań tiwaŋkuti timool tì ŋmóòyee, kun puee binib nìn gbí kunicandii poon, baa nìn ŋūǹ kí kɔ́ niin.

eng: Nearby shepherds were living in the fields, guarding their sheep at night.
local: Kitiŋ gbanti poonee, bipikpaaliibi biba nìn dɔ̄ tisatin ki kpàá bi-piii.

eng: The Lords angel stood before them, the Lords glory shone around them, and they were terrified.
local: Unimbɔti tuuŋŋu ŋubɔ nìn cílìń bi-pu, ní ti-Dindaan walifiŋu ŋmíntì ki māntì ki tú-bi ní bi fāŋkì tikpil.

eng: On one of the days when Jesus was teaching the people in the temple and proclaiming the good news, the chief priests, legal experts, and elders approached him.
local: Dalba, Yeesu bíi dàkà samaa ki sòoǹ tibɔmɔntiil kunimbɔtidiinee, ní bisaraaŋakpilib nì bimaradakaliibi nì bitindaŋkpilib cílìń bi-pu.

eng: When it was time, he sent a servant to collect from the tenants his share of the fruit of the vineyard. But the tenants sent him away, beaten and empty-handed.
local: Isufee biliŋ kìtitam bāańee, ní u túnní u-tɔntɔnliu ubɔ bipaadambee cee ú fōō u-jandi kí cáań-u. Ní bi cúú-u ki gbáń ní ki jìn̄-u ki gíiǹ nì iŋaŋkulim.

eng: The man sent another servant. But they beat him, treated him disgracefully, and sent him away empty-handed as well.
local: Ní bukpaadaanee gíti túnní u-tɔntɔnliu ubɔ mun bi-cee ní bi tí cúú u mun ki gbáń ki kɔ́ǹ-u icoo ní ki jìn̄-u ki gíiǹ nì iŋaŋkulim.

eng: He sent a third servant. They wounded this servant and threw him out.
local: Ní u gíti tú utataati, ní bi cúú-u ki ŋá-u tiweendi ní ki jìn̄-u ki nyántì.
