eng: The owner of the vineyard said, What should I do? Ill send my son, whom I love dearly. Perhaps they will respect him.
local: Ní bukpaadaan bí yii: <M làá gíti ŋá mana? See ḿ dū m-jipɔɔn un m néeńee kí tɔ̄. An yaa yíì, bi làá fàŋkì úǹ.>.

eng: But when they saw him, they said to each other, This is the heir. Lets kill him so the inheritance will be ours.
local: Ama bipaadambee nín wál̀ u-jipɔɔn dòońee ní bi bíi lì tɔb yii: <Kpèè, un làá dāá jī bukpaa fool boonee dee! Tí kpɔ̀maan-u, bukpaa ń kpántí ti-yɔbu.>.

eng: They threw him out of the vineyard and killed him. What will the owner of the vineyard do to them?.
local: Ní bi cìb̄ ki cúú-u ki cáá-u ki nyántì ŋkpaabuku ní ki kūū-u.» Ní Yeesu bālifì-bi yii: «Kɔŋkɔnnee, bukpaadaan làá ŋá-bi mana?.

eng: He will come and destroy those tenants and give the vineyard to others. When the people heard this, they said, May this never happen.
local: U dòoń ní kí kpɔ̀-bi ní kí dū u-kpaabu kí ŋúbiǹ biba.» Bin pílifèe nín gbìl̄ an bɔtee, ní bi lī yii: «Mbusu ń cù ndan.».

eng: Staring at them, Jesus said, Then what is the meaning of this text of scripture: The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone?.
local: Ní Yeesu dū u-nimbiliŋ ki báaǹ bi-pu, ní ki bālifì-bi yii: «Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon nín ŋmàn̄ puee làá lì yii ba dee, yii: <Ditaŋkpal din bidimaliibi nìn dūu fée, díǹ di dāa kpántì ditaŋkpal din ŋúb́ kudii pɔŋŋuee.>.

eng: Everyone who falls on that stone will be crushed. And the stone will crush the person it falls on.
local: Un kamaa di yaa lítì ditaŋkpal gbanti puee làá kɔ̄fì, ní un pu ní ditaŋkpalee yaa tàb̄ee, udaan làá nà ní dúsi dusi.».

eng: The legal experts and chief priests wanted to arrest him right then because they knew he had told this parable against them. But they feared the people.
local: Niinee, bimaradakaliibi nì bisaraaŋakpilib bíi nyàab̀ isan kí cúú Yeesu, kun puee bi béè yii bíǹ pu ní u dū tibɔti gbanti ki ŋmāntì, ama binib nín nìn wīikí u-cee puee, bi fāŋkì.

eng: They said, Tell us: What kind of authority do you have for doing these things? Who gave you this authority.
local: ní ki bí-u yii: «Nì nsan ŋulɔŋu pu ní a ŋáań tin bí nee ní? Ŋma di pú-si nsan á ŋá míǹ?».

eng: The legal experts and chief priests were watching Jesus closely and sent spies who pretended to be sincere. They wanted to trap him in his words so they could hand him over to the jurisdiction and authority of the governor.
local: Ní bi kíĺ ki bíi cɔ́ŋkí Yeesu ní ki nyántì binib biba ki túnní u-cee, bi dū bi-ba ki gíiǹ nnimɔntiyaamin kí píĺ-u tibɔti bí ŋmā kí cúú-u kí dūú ŋúbiǹ gɔmina ń dáaĺ u-tafal.

eng: They asked him, Teacher, we know that you are correct in what you say and teach. You dont show favoritism but teach Gods way as it really is.
local: Ní binibee bālifì Yeesu yii: «Ukpil, ti nyí yii tin a sòoǹee nì tin a dàkèe bí deedee, báà unil yaa yāntì ki bí miŋyee, aa fātìi tɔ́, ní ki dàkà nnimbɔtisan nì ibaamɔn.

eng: Does the Law allow people to pay taxes to Caesar or not.
local: Míǹ puee, á tùkù-ti, ti-marau pú-ti nsan yii tí pà lampoo kí tīī Room gɔminauaaa, an kaa pú?».

eng: Since Jesus recognized their deception, he said to them.
local: Yeesu béè bi nín bíi líbití-uee ní ki bí-bi yii.

eng: Show me a coin.Whose image and inscription does it have on it? Caesars, they replied.
local: «Cáaḿmaan-mi animbil.» Bi cáańee, ní u bālifì-bi yii: «Ŋma yindi nì u-yil di kā animbil pu na?» Ní bi kíí-u yii: «Gɔmina Seesaar.».

eng: He said to them, Give to Caesar what belongs to Caesar and to God what belongs to God.
local: Niinee ní u fātìi bí-bi yii: «Dūmaan tin gɔmina yèe kí tīī gɔmina, kí dū Unimbɔti mun yati kí tīī Unimbɔti.».

eng: They couldnt trap him in his words in front of the people. Astonished by his answer, they were speechless.
local: Baa ŋúń ki ká u sòon̄ dinyɔɔbuŋkpitil diba binib nimbiin bí bàà ŋmā kí cúú-u. U nín nìn kíí-bi puee, bi bākatì, ní kaa ti ká tiba kí gàtī.

eng: Some Sadducees, who deny that theres a resurrection, came to Jesus and asked.
local: Boonee ní Sadusab biba dɔ́miń. - Bíǹ di sá dikitil din lì yii difìkitil kaa bée. - Bi nìn bālifì Yeesu yii.

eng: Teacher, Moses wrote for us that if a mans brother dies leaving a widow but no children, the brother must marry the widow and raise up children for his brother .
local: «Ukpil, Mooyiisi ŋmàn̄ mara ki síiǹ-ti yii: <Unil yaa cáá kinabiki ní u kpíì ki yāntì u-nimpuu, kaa màl̄ee, u-nabikee ń fōō ukpiipuee kí màal̀ kí téeǹ u-nabiki kin kpíìyee yil pu.>.

eng: Now there were seven brothers. The first man married a woman and then died childless.
local: Kun pu ti bàlifēe di sá, nnabiyaamu mululi di nìn bí, upeepeekaa cáá unimpu ní ki kpíì ki yāntì u-nimpuu, kaa màl̄.

eng: He replied, I have a question for you. Tell me.
local: Ní u kíí-bi yii: «Ma mun làá bàlifì-ni tibɔti tiba.

eng: The second.
local: Ní uliliiti fōō-u ní ki mun kpíì, kaa màl̄.

eng: and then the third brother married her. Eventually all seven married her, and they all died without leaving any children.
local: ní utataati mun míǹ. Binib bilulee kɔkɔ cáá-u ki tin tú ki kpíì kpíì kaa màl̄ mbiyaamu.

eng: Finally, the woman died too.
local: Boonee, unimpuee u-ba mun dāa kpíì.

eng: In the resurrection, whose wife will she be? All seven were married to her.
local: Mimmee, difìkitil dalee, bi-kɔkɔ yaa láa fīkitèe, bi-kansikin ŋma di dāa yì unimpuee? Kun puee bíǹ binib bilulee kɔkɔ cáá-u ki ká.».

eng: Jesus said to them, People who belong to this age marry and are given in marriage.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-bi yii: «Duulinya nee poon ní bininjab cāab̀ binimpoobi, ní binimpoobi mun mɔ́ń bininjab.

eng: But those who are considered worthy to participate in that age, that is, in the age of the resurrection from the dead, wont marry nor will they be given in marriage.
local: Ama bininjab nì binimpoobi bin Unimbɔti ká yii bi máaǹ kí dāá fìkitī ditaŋkpiilin kí dāá kàl̄ duulinyapɔɔn un dòoń boonee, dinimpucaal nì dicamɔndi dāa kpá.

eng: They can no longer die, because they are like angels and are Gods children since they share in the resurrection.
local: Baa dāa gíti kúù, ní ki dāa bí kii Unimbɔti tuuŋyi nín bí puee, ní ki dāa sá Unimbɔti jipɔmbi, kun puee bi fātìi fīkitì ditaŋkpiilin.

eng: Even Moses demonstrated that the dead are raised—in the passage about the burning bush, when he speaks of the Lord as the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob .
local: Mooyiisi nìn ŋmàn̄ ki cántì yii bitaŋkpiibi làá dāá fìkitī, laakin u sòoǹ kisugbooi kin nìn fōō ŋŋmee bɔtee. U nìn yíiń ti-Dindaan yii: <Abraam Nimbɔtiu, Isaaki Nimbɔtiu, Jaakɔb Nimbɔtiu.>.

eng: He isnt the God of the dead but of the living. To him they are all alive.
local: Unimbɔti sá bin wāĺee Nimbɔtiu ní, waa sá bitaŋkpiibi Nimbɔtiu, kun puee úǹ ceeyee bíǹ kɔkɔ kaa kpíì.».

eng: Some of the legal experts responded, Teacher, you have answered well.
local: Niinee ní bimaradakaliibi biba bí-u yii: «Ukpil, a nín sòon̄ puee ŋān̄ tikpil.».

eng: Was Johns baptism of heavenly or of human origin.
local: Ní tùkù-mi Yoowaneesi nín fùul̀ binib Unimbɔti nyimee, ŋma di tīī-u an pɔŋŋu ní? Unimbɔtaaa, binib?».

eng: No one dared to ask him anything else.
local: Kun puee baa nìn gíti ŋūǹ kí bàlifì-u tibɔti tiba kí kpèè u-nyɔkɔn.

eng: Jesus said to them, Why do they say that the Christ is Davids son?.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-bi yii: «Nì mana ní ni ŋūǹ kí lì yii Meesiya sá Daafiidi naantiiu?.

eng: David himself says in the scroll of Psalms, The Lord said to my lord, Sit at my right side.
local: Daafiidi u-ba lī Inyɔŋki laŋyi gbɔŋku poon yii: <Ti-Dindaan tùkū m-Dindaan yii: Kàl̄ m-ŋaŋgiil pú yéè.

eng: until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet.
local: kí nīn cí ŋyunti ŋun m làá yàntī a-nannanliibi ń kpántí a-taaŋi tɔ́kɔŋkaatee.>.

eng: Since David calls him Lord, how can he be Davids son.
local: Daafiidi u-ba yíì-u u-Dindaan ní: kin mana ní u ŋūǹ kí nīn sá Daafiidi naantiiu mun?».

eng: In the presence of all the people, Jesus said to his disciples.
local: Yeesu bíi sòoǹ kunipaau bíi pílifíǹ-uee ní u bí u-boonnooliibi yii.

eng: Watch out for the legal experts. They like to walk around in long robes. They love being greeted with honor in the markets. They long for the places of honor in the synagogues and at banquets.
local: «Ní nīn nyíǹ ni-ba nì bimaradakaliibi. Bi néeń ki pèentí abɔkutikpaan ki yìiǹ, ki néeń bí nīn jáaḿ-bi sampaambi pu, ní ki néeń ki kàal̀ nyɔkɔpu jaliŋ akpafidiinni nì diinabin.

eng: They are the ones who cheat widows out of their homes, and to show off they say long prayers. They will be judged most harshly.
local: ní ki fìkì bikpiipoobi wanti, ki mèé ŋwimmeem yúntí, kí dàkà binib yii bi sá bimɔntiibi. An puee ditafadaaldi din gíti lákatèe dɔ̄ ki cí-bi.».

eng: They discussed among themselves, If we say, Its of heavenly origin, hell say, Why didnt you believe him.
local: Niinee ní bi kíĺ ki bíi sòoǹ bi-ŋmaniŋma yii: «Ti yaa kíí-u yii: <Unimbɔti di tīī-u an pɔŋŋu,> u làá lì yii: <Kin, ba ŋá ní naa fōō u-bɔti ki kíí?>.

eng: But if we say, Its of human origin, all the people will stone us to death because they are convinced that John was a prophet.
local: Ti yaa tí kíí-u yii: <Binib di tīī-u,> kunipaau nee kɔkɔ làá yāŋkì-ti ataŋkpee kí kpɔ̀, kun puee báà ŋma nyí ibaamɔn yii, Yoowaneesi sá Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu ní.».

eng: They answered that they didnt know where it came from.
local: Niinee ní bi kíí Yeesu yii: «Taa nyí un di tīī Yoowaneesi mpɔn ú fàl̀ binib Unimbɔti nyimee.».

eng: Then Jesus replied, Neither will I tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Ní u fātìi kíí-bi yii: «Ma mun kaa làá tùkù-ni nì nsan ŋun pu ní m ŋáań mimmee.».

eng: Jesus told the people this parable: A certain man planted a vineyard, rented it to tenant farmers, and went on a trip for a long time.
local: Boonee ní Yeesu dū tibɔŋmantikaal nee ki bíi sòoǹ nì samaa yii: «Uninja ubɔ di kùn̄ fiinyi kpaabu, ní ki dūu ŋúbiǹ bipaadambi, ní ki búntì nsan ki yúntìń.

eng: Looking up, Jesus saw rich people throwing their gifts into the collection box for the temple treasury.
local: Yeesu yáatì ki ká bigaajab nín tòó animbil saraa dakaaun pu kunimbɔtidiinee.

eng: Then Jesus said to them, Nations and kingdoms will fight against each other.
local: Ní u gíti lī yii: «Kitiŋ kiba yab dāa yíkí ní kí jáǹ kiba yab, ní dibeel diba yab ń yīkì kí jáǹ diba mun yab.

eng: There will be great earthquakes and wide-scale food shortages and epidemics. There will also be terrifying sights and great signs in the sky.
local: Ajan tikpil puee, kitiŋ làá dāá cìŋkì ŋkpitim pu, ní bìlbil kúm nì ŋkùm làá dāá nyā atinjan kupaau poon. Tiwan tikpil làá dāá ŋá kí kɔ́ǹ binib tifaandi, ní tidaandi tikpaandi dāa pāantí kutagbɔŋu pu.

eng: But before all this occurs, they will take you into custody and harass you because of your faith. They will hand you over to synagogues and prisons, and you will be brought before kings and governors because of my name.
local: «Kí wàatī tíǹ kɔkɔ ń bàńee, bi làá cūtì-ni kí sàaǹ-ni, kí cáá-ni ibɔsoon akpafidiinni, kí pìin̄-ni sarka. Bi làá cáá-ni bibɔtikpaambi nì biyidambi nimbiin man pu.

eng: This will provide you with an opportunity to testify.
local: Niin ní ni dāa kāntí mpaan kí sòoǹ nì bi tin ni nyí man puee.

eng: Make up your minds not to prepare your defense in advance.
local: Míǹ yaa bāań-nee, ní bɔ́ntí ni-ba kí taa nín̄ yáliŋkí tin ni làá tin sòoǹ kí fīī ni-bee pu.

eng: Ill give you words and wisdom that none of your opponents will be able to counter or contradict.
local: Kun puee mama m-ba di làá dāá tīī-ni iciin nì tibɔti tin ni làá sòoǹ kí líí ni-nannanliibi, bí taa ŋmā kí kpákáǹ-nee.

eng: You will be betrayed by your parents, brothers and sisters, relatives, and friends. They will execute some of you.
local: Ni-baabi nì ni-nab nì ni-nabiyaamu nì ni nì bin pēēyee, nì ni-bɔɔbi làá dāá dū-ni kí ŋúbíǹ ni-nannanliibi, ní bíǹ ń kpɔ̀ ni-kansikin biba.

eng: Everyone will hate you because of my name.
local: Báà ŋma làá dāá nīn ná-ni ni nín fōō man ki kííyee pu.

eng: Still, not a hair on your heads will be lost.
local: Ama, nì míǹ kɔkɔee, ni-yikpiliku báà kubɔ kaa làá lēē ni-yil pu kí wāŋkì.

eng: By holding fast, you will gain your lives.
local: Yūĺmaan mmɔntiimin, míǹ ní ni ŋūǹ kí dāá fīī ni-ba.».

eng: He also saw a poor widow throw in two small copper coins worth a penny.
local: ní u ká ukpiipusoodaan ubɔ cáań igboo ili ki tɔ́.

eng: When you see Jerusalem surrounded by armies, then you will know that its destruction is close at hand.
local: «Ni yaa láa ká soojab dɔ́miń ki māntì ki tú Jeerusaleem tiŋkee, ní béé ŋyunti gbantee yii butɔbu làá jàkà-kì dee.

eng: At that time, those in Judea must flee to the mountains, those in the city must escape, and those in the countryside must not enter the city.
local: An yuntiŋuee, bin yaa láa bíń Juudee tiŋkinee ń nīn sáń ki dàb̀ ajoon. Bin yaa láa bí Jeerusaleem tiŋkinee ń nīn sáń ki nyēē, ní bin yaa láa bíń tisatinee ń taa kpìnní mmɔŋki.

eng: These are the days of punishment, when everything written will find its fulfillment.
local: An dāa sá ipɔntoon wiiŋyi ní, kí ŋá kii Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon nín ŋmàn̄ ki síiǹ puee.

eng: How terrible it will be at that time for women who are pregnant or for women who are nursing their children. There will be great agony on the earth and angry judgment on this people.
local: Ama, falaa dɔ̄ ki cí bipɔdambi nì bimatib iwiin gbanti poon. An dāa sá kunimbaakpaaŋŋu ní kitiŋ kɔkɔn. Unimbɔti ŋuul làá dāá lítí dinibul nee pu tikpil.

eng: They will fall by the edge of the sword and be taken away as captives among all nations. Jerusalem will be plundered by Gentiles until the times of the Gentiles are concluded.
local: Bi làá kpɔ̀ binib tikpil nì ntàataajimu, kí cúú tikpil kí cáá cùnnì kuyuŋu atimbun kɔkɔn, ní bin kaa nyí Unimbɔtee ń dāá jī Jeerusaleem kí tāā pùb, kí dāá sāā bi-yuntiŋu.».

eng: There will be signs in the sun, moon, and stars. On the earth, there will be dismay among nations in their confusion over the roaring of the sea and surging waves.
local: «Ni làá dāá ká tidaandi ŋwìiń nì uŋmal nì iŋmalbijaa pu. Kitiŋ puee, atimbun dāa bí ki yáliŋkí nì ŋi-ba, ní tiŋku nyiŋgbanti wooku dāa kɔ̄ntí-bi tifaandi.

eng: The planets and other heavenly bodies will be shaken, causing people to faint from fear and foreboding of what is coming upon the world.
local: Binib yaa dākafì tin bí ki dòoń kitiŋ puee, an faantee làá dāá kpɔ̀-bi. Ní ipɔn in bí kutaa puee ń dāá cìŋkì.

eng: Then they will see the Human Onecoming on a cloud with power and great splendor.
local: Ŋyunti gbantee ní bi dāa kāntí man Kinibiki dòoń ditagbandi pu ki jìl̀ pàlpal nì mpɔn nì tinyool tikpil.

eng: Now when these things begin to happen, stand up straight and raise your heads, because your redemption is near.
local: Tin bí nee yaa láa bíi ŋáańee, nimbi ń mɔ̀tīí yūĺ kí ká kunyannyaau, ni-fiil tóobiń dee.».

eng: Jesus told them a parable: Look at the fig tree and all the trees.
local: Ní Yeesu dū tibɔŋmantikaal nee ki ŋmāntì-bi yii: «Kpèèmaan fiigi subu nì isufi in kɔkɔ kíǹee nín dàkà-ti tiŋyee.

eng: He said, I assure you that this poor widow has put in more than them all.
local: Ní u bí yii: «Ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-ni, Unimbɔti nimbiinee, ukpiipusoodaan nee tɔ́ ki jítiǹ bin kɔkɔ kíǹee.

eng: When they sprout leaves, you can see for yourselves and know that summer is near.
local: Ni yaa ká i bíi túkù tifaapɔnee, ni béetí yii kuseeu tóobiń dee.

eng: In the same way, when you see these things happening, you know that Gods kingdom is near.
local: Míǹ mbaantiim, ni yaa láa ká tin kɔkɔ m sòon̄ nì ni nee bíi jītée, béémaan yii Unimbɔti Beel tóobiń dee.

eng: I assure you that this generation wont pass away until everything has happened.
local: Ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-ni, nimbi waanee nib kaa làá kpú kí kúntí ní an bɔtee kɔkɔ ń bàń.

eng: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will certainly not pass away.
local: Kutagbɔŋu nì kitiŋ dāa bí kí kúntí, ama man bɔti kpá kí kúntí jaanjaan.».

eng: Take care that your hearts arent dulled by drinking parties, drunkenness, and the anxieties of day-to-day life. Dont let that day fall upon you unexpectedly.
local: «Ní nīn nyímaan nì ni-ba kí taa yàntī njinjim nì ndanyoon nì duulinya landɔkɔi ń jī mpaan ni-pɔbiliŋ poon. An yaa sá fám, Unimbɔti bɔsooŋyi wiiŋŋu gbanti làá dāá túuǹ-ni.

eng: like a trap. It will come upon everyone who lives on the face of the whole earth.
local: Ŋu dāa tòoń kii kulaaŋŋuee, kun puee an làá dāá bìin̄ kitiŋ kɔkɔ nì ki-nib pu ní.

eng: Stay alert at all times, praying that you are strong enough to escape everything that is about to happen and to stand before the Human One.
local: Míǹ puee ní taa nín̄ gèem̀maan. Nín̄ mèémaan múlmul, mbusu wiiŋyi gbanti yaa láa bāańee, ní ká mpɔn kí ŋmátí tikɔkɔ poon, kí ŋmā kí dāá yūĺ man Kinibiki nimbiin.».

eng: Every day Jesus was teaching in the temple, but he spent each night on the Mount of Olives.
local: Kinyɔŋ gbanti poonee, baabadalee, Yeesu nìn dàkà Unimbɔti bɔti kunimbɔtidiin, ní kunyeeuee ú cù kitimbilii kin bí dijool din bi yíì Ɔliifi sufii joolee pu kí dòon̄.

eng: All the people rose early in the morning to hear him in the temple area.
local: Ní samaa kɔkɔ nìn kíntiń-u kutaafaau pɔ̀lpɔl kunimbɔtidiin kí pīlifì tin u sòoǹee.

eng: All of them are giving out of their spare change. But she from her hopeless poverty has given everything she had to live on.
local: Di sá yii bin kíǹee dū bi-nimbiliŋ ŋin pá baa làá dūú ŋá nì tibee ní ki tɔ́, ama ukpiipu nee nì u-sool poonee, u nín bàà cáá ŋin kí dā tijin kí jēe ní u cáań kɔkɔ ki dūu tɔ́ nee.».

eng: Some people were talking about the temple, how it was decorated with beautiful stones and ornaments dedicated to God. Jesus said.
local: Binib biba nìn bíi sòoǹ kunimbɔtidii nyaaŋku pu, ataŋkpanyaan ŋin bi nìn dūu máń-kuee, nì tiwammɔntiil tin bi nìn dūu ŋá saraa ki tīī Unimbɔtee. Ní Yeesu bí-bi yii.

eng: As for the things you are admiring, the time is coming when not even one stone will be left upon another. All will be demolished.
local: «Kéè ni wāĺ kudii neeyaa, iwiin iba dòoń, naa dāa gíti kāntí ditaŋkpal diba tɔ́kɔ́ di-juti pu. An kɔkɔ làá dāá wīī ní.».

eng: They asked him, Teacher, when will these things happen? What sign will show that these things are about to happen.
local: Bi bālifì Yeesu yii: «Ŋyunti ŋulɔŋu ní tin a sòon̄ee làá bàń? Kudaaŋŋu kulɔkɔ di làá dàkà yii an tóobiń?».

eng: Jesus said, Watch out that you arent deceived. Many will come in my name, saying, Im the one! and Its time! Dont follow them.
local: Ní Yeesu bí yii: «Ní nīn nyíǹ ni-ba ubɔ ń taa ŋmàtiǹ-ni, kun puee binib tikpil làá dāá dū man yindi kí kpántí bi-yaŋ, kí bí yii bíǹ di sá man, kí tùkù-ni yii ŋyunti bāań. Ama ní taa nóó-bi.

eng: When you hear of wars and rebellions, dont be alarmed. These things must happen first, but the end wont happen immediately.
local: Ni yaa láa gbìl̄ bi sòoǹ tijáti nì fìkil fikil kitiŋ puee, ni-pɔbiliŋ ń taa bīil̀. An máaǹ tíǹ kɔkɔ ń bàń kí wàatī. Ní nīn nyí yii duulinya kúntitam kaa dá láá yá.».

eng: The Festival of Unleavened Bread, which is called Passover, was approaching.
local: Jiŋaal un bi ŋmóò kpɔnɔb bin baa ŋáań ŋkpɔnɔnyɔkɔ bi yíì-u Dilákatil jiŋaaliuee tóobiń.

eng: Jesus replied, When you go into the city, a man carrying a water jar will meet you. Follow him to the house he enters.
local: Ní u bí-bi yii: «Ní pīlifì, ni yaa làá kɔ́ kitiŋinee ni nì uninja ubɔ làá tin kpāfì u tú dinyimbool. Ní táá-u kí kɔ́ kudii kun ni u yaa kɔ́ee.

eng: Say to the owner of the house, The teacher says to you,Where is the guestroom where I can eat the Passover meal with my disciples.
local: kí bàlifì kudiidaan yii: <Ukpil bàlifī-si yii kudii kulɔkɔn ní u nì u-boonnooliibi làá jī jiŋaal jikaati ní?>.

eng: He will show you a large upstairs room, already furnished. Make preparations there.
local: Niinee, u làá dàkà-ni diditɔntɔkɔl puee, kudikpaaŋŋu kubɔ kun poon tikɔkɔ bɔ́ntì ki sée, niin ní ni làá ŋá tijikaal.».

eng: They went and found everything just as he had told them, and they prepared the Passover meal.
local: Ní bi búntì ki lafun bàn̄ ki ká min u lēe deedee, ní ki ŋá jiŋaalee jinti niin.

eng: When the time came, Jesus took his place at the table, and the apostles joined him.
local: Ŋyunti bāań Yeesu nì u-kpambaliŋ kāl̀ ki bíi jīǹ tijinee.

eng: He said to them, I have earnestly desired to eat this Passover with you before I suffer.
local: ní u bí-bi yii: «M lēē m-pɔbilin páaa yii man nì ni ń jī Dilákatil jiŋaaliu nee kí wàatī ní ḿ nín jī falaa.

eng: I tell you, I wont eat it until it is fulfilled in Gods kingdom.
local: M tùkù ki cántí-ni yii m gíti kpá kí jī jiŋaal nee kí nīn cí daliŋkin u-taapu làá nyā mpaampiin pu Unimbɔti Beelinee.».

eng: After taking a cup and giving thanks, he said, Take this and share it among yourselves.
local: Niinee ní u yōoǹ ndaam, ní ki jáam̀ Unimbɔti ní ki bí yii: «Fōōmaan kí nyɔ̀ kí kíń tɔb.

eng: I tell you that from now on I wont drink from the fruit of the vine until Gods kingdom has come.
local: M tùkù ki cántí-ni yii m gíti kpá kí nyɔ̀ ndaam nee kí nīn cí ŋyunti ŋun Unimbɔti Beel làá dāan̄ee.».

eng: After taking the bread and giving thanks, he broke it and gave it to them, saying, This is my body, which is given for you. Do this in remembrance of me.
local: Boonee ní u yōoǹ kpɔnɔ ní ki jáam̀ Unimbɔti ní ki gífì ki tīī-bi ní ki bí yii: «Doo di sá m-wunti tin m tīī ni-puee. Nín̄ ŋáaḿmaan míǹ ki téetí m-pu.».

eng: The chief priests and the legal experts were looking for a way to kill Jesus, because they were afraid of the people.
local: Ní bisaraaŋakpilib nì bimaradakaliibi nìn bíi nyàab̀ nsan kí cúú Yeesu, ama ní ki fàŋkí kunipaau.

eng: In the same way, he took the cup after the meal and said, This cup is the new covenant by my blood, which is poured out for you.
local: Bi ŋmɔ́ ki dóòyee, ní u gíti yōoǹ ndaam mbaantiim ki tīī-bi ní ki bí yii: «Ndaam nee di sá mpèépɔɔn ŋun làá nīn bí Unimbɔti nì binib kansikinee. M-fatikuu un làá nyā nimbi puee di tìì an poondi gbanti.

eng: But look! My betrayer is with me; his hand is on this table.
local: «Kpèè, un làá dū-mi kí tīī m-nannanliibee bí doo, man nì un jīǹ disambibaantiilinee.

eng: The Human Onegoes just as it has been determined. But how terrible it is for that person who betrays him.
local: Tiŋman, man Kinibiki làá kpú kii Unimbɔti nín nìn lī ki síiǹ puee, ama mbusu dɔ̄ ki cí un di làá dū-mi kí nyāfiǹ m-nannanliibee.».

eng: They began to argue among themselves about which of them it could possibly be who would do this.
local: Ní bi kíĺ ki bíi bàlifī tɔb yii: «Ŋma di bí ti-kansikin ki làá ŋá míǹ?».

eng: An argument broke out among the disciples over which one of them should be regarded as the greatest.
local: Niinee ní Yeesu kpambaliŋ kíĺ ki bíi kpákà ki là kí béé bi-kansikin un di làá nīn sá ukpaanee.

eng: But Jesus said to them, The kings of the Gentiles rule over their subjects, and those in authority over them are called friends of the people.
local: Ní Yeesu bí yii: «Atimbun bɔtiibi wɔ́b́ binib bin pu bi kā dibeelee ní, ní biyidambi là bí nīn yíì-bi bilaŋŋaŋfitiibi.

eng: But thats not the way it will be with you. Instead, the greatest among you must become like a person of lower status and the leader like a servant.
local: Ama án taa nín̄ sá míǹ nimbi cee. Un sá nimbi cee ukpaanee ń nīn sá kii kiwaayee, ní un là kí nīn sá uyidaanee ń kpántí ni-tɔntɔnliu.

eng: So which one is greater, the one who is seated at the table or the one who serves at the table? Isnt it the one who is seated at the table? But I am among you as one who serves.
local: Ŋma di sá ukpaan ní? Un kā ki jīǹeeyaaa, un yú ki tìì tijinee ní? Un kā ki jīǹee di sá ukpaan. Ama man sá ni-kansikin kii utɔntɔnlee ní, míǹ puee ni mun ń nīn bí kii mamee.

eng: You are the ones who have continued with me in my trials.
local: «Nimbi di bí m-cee m-falaabi poon baabadal, kaa fá-mi.

eng: And I confer royal power on you just as my Father granted royal power to me.
local: Míǹ puee m-Baa bɔ́ntì dibeel kí tīī-mi. Míǹ mbaantiim ní ma mun bɔ́ntì Dibeel kí tīī-ni.

eng: Then Satan entered Judas, called Iscariot, who was one of the Twelve.
local: Niinee ní Sitaan kɔ́ Juudasi un bi yíì Isikaariyɔtee ni. U nìn sá Yeesu kpambaliŋ saalaa nì alee kansikin diba ní.

eng: Thus you will eat and drink at my table in my kingdom, and you will sit on thrones overseeing the twelve tribes of Israel.
local: Ní ŋyunti ŋun m làá kàl̄ Dibeelee, ni làá kàl̄ m-cee m-Beelin kí jī kí nyɔ̀, ní kí kàl̄ abeejal pu kí nīn sòoǹ Isirayeel yab naakɔti saalaa nì tilee bɔti.».

eng: Simon, Simon, look! Satan has asserted the right to sift you all like wheat.
local: Ní ti-Dindaan lī yii: «Siimɔn, Siimɔn, á pīlifì, Sitaan bālifì kí bàkal̀-si kii bi nín bàkal̄ idi puee.

eng: However, I have prayed for you that your faith wont fail. When you have returned, strengthen your brothers and sisters.
local: Ama m mēē a-pu án nín làá ŋá pu a-fookiiu ń taa wāŋkèe. Boonee a yaa láa fātìi kpántì a-bee, á dāá nyàatī a-nabiyaamu.».

eng: Peter responded, Lord, Im ready to go with you, both to prison and to death.
local: Ní Peetroo bí-u yii: «Ukpil, man bɔ́ntì ki cí, an yaa sá sarkaaa, man nì si di làá kɔ́, an yaa tí sá ŋkúmaaa, man nì si di làá kpú.».

eng: Jesus replied, I tell you, Peter, the rooster wont crow today before you have denied three times that you know me.
local: Ní Yeesu fātìi kíí-u yii: «Peetroo, ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-si, díǹ gba kí wàatī ukooja ń bāakèe, a làá néé yii saa nyí-mi, diba dibee mita pu.».

eng: Jesus said to them, When I sent you out without a wallet, bag, or sandals, you didnt lack anything, did you? They said, Nothing.
local: Ní Yeesu gíti bí-bi yii: «M nín nìn tú-ni ki bí yii ní taa yòon̄ animbil nì ditɔkɔl, nì anaatakee, tiba nìn lóò-naaa?» Ní bi kíí yii: «Aayee.».

eng: Then he said to them, But now, whoever has a wallet must take it, and likewise a bag. And those who dont own a sword must sell their clothes and buy one.
local: Ní u bí-bi yii: «Kɔŋkɔnnee un yaa cáá animbilee ń yòon̄, un cáá ditɔkɔlee mun ń yòon̄, un kaa cáá kitàataajikee ń nyāfì u-bɔkutil kí cùú dā.

eng: I tell you that this scripture must be fulfilled in relation to me: And he was counted among criminals .Indeed, whats written about me is nearing completion.
local: Di sá yii m tùkù ki cántí-ni yii, see án bàń m-pu kii an nín ŋmàn̄ ki síiǹ Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon puee, yii: <Bi kàan̄-u ki tɔ́ bikpitibin.> Tiŋman tin kɔkɔwoooo Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi ŋmàn̄ ki síiǹ m-puee làá ŋá míǹ.».

eng: They said to him, Lord, look, here are two swords. He replied, Enough of that.
local: Niinee ní u-boonnooliibi bí yii: «Ukpil, kpèè, ntàataajimu muli sèé.» Ní Yeesu bí-bi yii: «An ŋmāntì.».

eng: Jesus left and made his way to the Mount of Olives, as was his custom, and the disciples followed him.
local: Bi lī míǹ ki dóòyee, ní u nyáń ki búntì Ɔliifi sufii jool pu kii u nín pée kpààa cá puee. Ní u-boonnooliibi nóó-u.

eng: He went out and discussed with the chief priests and the officers of the temple guard how he could hand Jesus over to them.
local: Ní Juudasi cūtì ki ká bisaraaŋakpilib nì soojab bin cí kunimbɔtidiiyee kpilib, ki sòon̄ nì bi nsan ŋun pu u làá dī kí cúú Yeesu kí ŋúbíǹ-bee.

eng: When he arrived, he said to them, Pray that you wont give in to temptation.
local: Bi bāań niinee ní u bí-bi yii: «Ní nīn mèémaan kí taa gèeǹ án nín làá ŋá pu ni yaa kɔ́ icɔŋkinee, ní taa lítée.».

eng: He withdrew from them about a stones throw, knelt down, and prayed.
local: Ní u sútì ki cūtì nyɔkɔpu ki ŋmāntì kii bi nín tòó ditaŋkpal puee ní ki gbāaǹ ki mēē yii.

eng: He said, Father, if its your will, take this cup of suffering away from me. However, not my will but your will must be done.
local: «M-Baa, a yaa là, kàkatī falaa un an sá ḿ nyɔ̀ nee kí wàatī-mi. Nì míǹ kɔkɔee, án taa nín̄ sá man lammi ní ń ŋá, án nīn sá sii yam.» .

eng: Then a heavenly angel appeared to him and strengthened him.
local: Niinee ní Unimbɔti tuuŋŋu ŋubɔ nyánní yilpu ki dɔ́miń ki tīī-u kunyannyaau.

eng: He was in anguish and prayed even more earnestly. His sweat became like drops of blood falling on the ground.
local: Dinimbinaamandi din nìn kɔ́-uee puee, u nìn mɔ̄tìi bíi mèé tiŋan tiŋan, áli u-tuntuŋku tin kpántì kii afatikudindikee ki lítí kitiŋ.

eng: When he got up from praying, he went to the disciples. He found them asleep, overcome by grief.
local: U mēē ki dóòyee ní ki yíkì ki nín gítiń u-boonnooliibi cee. U bāańee, bíǹ dɔ̄ ki gèeǹ, ki gīī púb, kun puee kunimbaau nìn kɔ́-bi tikpil.

eng: He said to them, Why are you sleeping? Get up and pray so that you wont give in to temptation.
local: Ní u fīntì-bi ki bālifì-bi yii: «Ba pu ní ni gèeǹ? Yīkìí mèèmaan kí taa ti gèeǹ, án nín làá ŋá pu ni yaa kɔ́ icɔŋkinee, ní taa lítée.».

eng: While Jesus was still speaking, a crowd appeared, and the one called Judas, one of the Twelve, was leading them. He approached Jesus to kiss him.
local: Yeesu kaa láá sòon̄ ki ŋmíl̀, ní dinikitil diba cílìń. Juudasi un sá u-kpambaliŋ kansikin dibee līiń bi-nyɔkɔpu, ní ki sútì ki pìlìń Yeesu kí kpàl̄-u.

eng: Jesus said to him, Judas, would you betray the Human Onewith a kiss.
local: Ní Yeesu bālifì-u yii: «Juudasi, mimmee nì dikpàldi poon ní a làá dū man Kinibiki kí ŋúbíǹ m-nannanliibaaa?».

eng: When those around him recognized what was about to happen, they said, Lord, should we fight with our swords.
local: Yeesu boonnooliibi béè yii bi là kí cúú-uee, ní bi bālifì-u yii: «Ti-Dindaan, tí dū ntàataajimu kí gàatì-baaa?».

eng: They were delighted and arranged payment for him.
local: Ní an mɔ́ɔkiǹ-bi ní bi síiǹ kunimbiŋmiiu kun bi làá tīī-uee.

eng: One of them struck the high priests servant, cutting off his right ear.
local: Niinee, bi-kansikin ubɔ dū kitàataajiki ki gāā ki wúkutì usaraaŋakpil tɔntɔnliu ŋaŋgiitafal.

eng: Jesus responded, Stop! No more of this! He touched the slaves ear and healed him.
local: Ama ní Yeesu bí yii: «Yàntīmaanee! An ŋmāntèe,» ní ki gbēē utɔntɔnlee tafal, ní u pɔ́ɔkì.

eng: Then Jesus said to the chief priests, the officers of the temple guard, and the elders who had come to get him, Have you come with swords and clubs to arrest me, as though I were a thief?.
local: Ní Yeesu bālifì bisaraaŋakpilib nì bin cí kunimbɔtidiiyee kpilib, nì bibɔsoonliibi bin dɔ́miń kí cúú-uee yii: «Ba pu ní ni ŋúbiń ntàataajimu nì atambɔn kí dāá cúú-mi kii m sá unikpitee?.

eng: Day after day I was with you in the temple, but you didnt arrest me. But this is your time, when darkness rules.
local: Man nì ni di pée bí kunimbɔtidiin baabadal, ba pu ní naa cúú-mi niin? Ama ŋyunti bāań ni nì dibɔmbɔndi bɔtiu ń tɔ̄ ni-tundi.».

eng: After they arrested Jesus, they led him away and brought him to the high priests house. Peter followed from a distance.
local: Ní bi cúú Yeesu ki cáá-u ki búntì usaraaŋakpil dumpu, ní Peetroo nóó-u aboon aboon.

eng: When they lit a fire in the middle of the courtyard and sat down together, Peter sat among them.
local: Bi nìn kpāā ŋŋmi dicindi kansikin ki bíi wùl̀, ní Peetroo dɔ́miń ki kāl̀ bi-cee.

eng: Then a servant woman saw him sitting in the firelight. She stared at him and said, This man was with him too.
local: Usapɔɔn ubɔ nìn tūǹ ditundi niin. U ká Peetroo nín kā ki kɔ́ĺ ŋŋmee, ní u dū u-nimbil ki báaǹ u-pu ki kpēē-u címmm, ní ki bí yii: «Uninja nee mun bí kijapaai kin bi cúú nee cee ní.».

eng: But Peter denied it, saying, Woman, I dont know him.
local: Ní Peetroo néé yii: «Unimpu, man kaa nyí-u.».

eng: A little while later, someone else saw him and said, You are one of them too. But Peter said, Man, Im not.
local: An kútì fiiiyee, ní ubɔ mun gíti ká-u, ní ki bí yii: «A mun sá bin nóó-uee kansikin ubɔ ní.» Ní Peetroo kíí-u yii: «Maa sá u-yɔu.».

eng: An hour or so later, someone else insisted, This man must have been with him, because he is a Galilean too.
local: An yúntì ki bàn̄ kii kukúluu kubɔ nyɔkɔmee, ní ubɔ mun gíti bí yii: «An pée sɔ̄ kí lā, uninja nee mun nìn bí kijapaai nee cee ní, kun puee u sá Galiilee ní.».

eng: He agreed and began looking for an opportunity to hand Jesus over to them—a time when the crowds would be absent.
local: Ní u kíí ní ki kíĺ ki bíi nyàab̀ ŋyunti ŋun di kítì ú dū Yeesu kí ŋúbíǹ-bi, samaa ń taa bééyee.

eng: Peter responded, Man, I dont know what you are talking about! At that very moment, while he was still speaking, a rooster crowed.
local: Ní Peetroo kíí-u yii: «M-bɔɔ, maa nyí tin tin a sòoǹee.» U bíi sòoǹee, niin niin ní ukooja báakì.

eng: The Lord turned and looked straight at Peter, and Peter remembered the Lords words: Before a rooster crows today, you will deny me three times.
local: Ní ti-Dindaan fātìi kpēē Peetroo dímmm. Niinee ní Peetroo téetì ti-Dindaan nín bàà tùkū-u puee yii: «Díǹ, a làá néé yii saa nyí-mi diba dibee mita pu ní ukooja ń bāakì.».

eng: And Peter went out and cried uncontrollably.
local: Ní Peetroo nyáń ki bíi sūǹ ki yáliŋkí.

eng: The men who were holding Jesus in custody taunted him while they beat him.
local: Binib bin nìn cí Yeesuee nìn bíi bèel̄-u ní ki gbáà-u.

eng: They blindfolded him and asked him repeatedly, Prophesy! Who hit you.
local: ní bi dū ŋŋaabu ki bóob́ u-nimbiin ní ki lì-u yii: «Béé un fáá-see kí lì.».

eng: Insulting him, they said many other horrible things against him.
local: Bi nìn gíti sīī-u asiibil tikpil ki kútì.

eng: As morning came, the elders of the people, both chief priests and legal experts, came together, and Jesus was brought before their council.
local: Kutaa wúntèe, Juuda yab tindaŋkpilib nì bisaraaŋakpilib nì bimaradakaliibi kpáfì ki làá sòoǹ u-bɔti, ní ki yāntì bi cáá-u ki cūnnì bi-cee.

eng: They said, If you are the Christ, tell us! He answered, If I tell you, you wont believe.
local: Ní bi bālifì-u yii: «Tùkù-ti sii di yaa sá Meesiya?» Ní u kíí-bi yii: «Báà m yaa pée tùkū-naaa, naa làá fōō kí kíí.

eng: And if I ask you a question, you wont answer.
local: m yaa tí bālifì-ni tibɔtaaa, naa làá kíí-mi.

eng: But from now on, the Human One will be seated on the right side of the power of God. .
local: Ama kí kíĺ kɔŋkɔnnee kí nīn cée, man Kinibiki làá nīn kā Unimbɔti Mpɔŋkɔdaan ŋaŋgiil pú.».

eng: The Day of Unleavened Bread arrived, when the Passover had to be sacrificed.
local: Jiŋaalee poonee, ŋwiin ŋun bi máaǹ kí kpɔ̀ apibilee bāań.

eng: They all said, Are you Gods Son, then? He replied, You say that I am.
local: Ní bi-kɔkɔ bí yii: «Mimmee a sá Unimbɔti Jipɔɔn deeyaa!» Ní u kíí-bi yii: «Kii nimbi ni-ba nín lī puee.».

eng: Then they said, Why do we need further testimony? Weve heard it from his own lips.
local: Niinee ní bi bí yii: «Ti làá gíti ŋá ba biseeradambi! U sòon̄ timbi ti-ba gbìl̄ dee.».

eng: Jesus sent Peter and John with this task: Go and prepare for us to eat the Passover meal.
local: ní Yeesu tú Peetroo nì Yoowaneesi yii: «Cùú ŋámaan Dilákatil jiŋaaliu jinti tí jī.».

eng: They said to him, Where do you want us to prepare it.
local: Ní bi bālifì-u yii: «La ní a là tí cù kí ŋá tijin gbanti?».

eng: The whole assembly got up and led Jesus to Pilate and.
local: Ní bikpilibee kɔkɔ yíkì ki yōoǹ Yeesu ki cáa cūnnì gɔmina Piilati cee.

eng: The chief priests and the legal experts were there, fiercely accusing Jesus.
local: Bisaraaŋakpilib nì bimaradakaliibi nìn bí niin ki sòoǹ mpɔn ki mɔ́ntí ki tūntí u-pu.

eng: Herod and his soldiers treated Jesus with contempt. Herod mocked him by dressing Jesus in elegant clothes and sent him back to Pilate.
local: Ní Eeroodi nì u-soojab kíĺ ki bíi bèel̄-u ki kpèé-u fám, ní ki dū dibɔkutinyaandi diba ki pēeǹ-u mbɔtiyaamin ní ki fātìi gíiǹ-u ki tīī Piilati.

eng: Pilate and Herod became friends with each other that day. Before this, they had been enemies.
local: Ŋwiin gbantee ní Piilati nì Eeroodi kansikin mɔ́ɔkì ní bi kpántì bibɔɔtiibi, dooooyee baa nìn kpí.

eng: Then Pilate called together the chief priests, the rulers, and the people.
local: Niinee ní Piilati kóoǹ bisaraaŋakpilib nì biyidambi nì samaa kɔkɔ ki kpáfì.

eng: He said to them, You brought this man before me as one who was misleading the people. I have questioned him in your presence and found nothing in this mans conduct that provides a legal basis for the charges you have brought against him.
local: ní ki bí-bi yii: «Ni cáań-mi unil nee yii u bíi gáatí binib, ní m bālifì-u ni-kɔkɔ nimbiin tibɔkpitil tin ni bí yii u ŋée, ama man kaa ká báà tiba u-pu.

eng: Neither did Herod, because Herod returned him to us. Hes done nothing that deserves death.
local: Eeroodi mun kaa ká u-bɔkpil, an pu ní u gíiǹní-u ki tīiń-ti. Kijapaai nee kaa ŋá tiba tin máaǹ ŋkúmee.

eng: Therefore, Ill have him whipped, then let him go.
local: Míǹ puee m làá yàntī bí gbá-u anaalab ní kí gíiǹ-u kí líí.» .

eng: But with one voice they shouted, Away with this man! Release Barabbas to us. .
local: Ní bi-kɔkɔ kíĺ ki bíi tátí dicilpu yii: «Bí kpɔ̀ kijapaai nee kí gíiǹ Baaraabaasi kí líí.».

eng: Barabbas had been thrown into prison because of a riot that had occurred in the city, and for murder.
local: - Bi nìn cúú Baaraabaasi gbanti ki tɔ́ sarkan kun puee u nìn yíkì kí já nì gɔmina ní ki kūū unil mun.

eng: began to accuse him. They said, We have found this man misleading our people, opposing the payment of taxes to Caesar, and claiming that he is the Christ, a king.
local: ní ki bíi mɔ́ntí ki tūntí u-pu yii: «Ti ká kijapaai nee bíi gáatí ti-nib yiliŋ yii bí taa pà lampoo kí tīī gɔminakpaan Seesaar, ní ki bí yii úǹ u-ba sá Meesiya, ubɔti.».

eng: Pilate addressed them again because he wanted to release Jesus.
local: Piilati nìn nyàab̀ kí líí Yeesuee, an pu ní u nìn gíti būsì ki sòon̄ nì samaa.

eng: They kept shouting out, Crucify him! Crucify him.
local: Ama ní bi bíi kūuntí yii: «Bí kpáá-u ndɔpuntikaa pu, bí pée kpáá-u ndɔpuntikaa pu.».

eng: For the third time, Pilate said to them, Why? What wrong has he done? Ive found no legal basis for the death penalty in his case. Therefore, I will have him whipped, then let him go.
local: Ní Piilati fātìi tí lī ntataatiim yii: «Tibɔkpitil tilati ní u baasii ŋá ní? Man kaa ká diyìntil diba u-pu din pu ní u máaǹ ŋkúmee. Míǹ puee m làá yàntī bí gbá-u anaalab kí gíiǹ-u kí líí.».

eng: But they were adamant, shouting their demand that Jesus be crucified. Their voices won out.
local: Ama ní bi yíì, ki mɔ̀tīi pée tátí mpɔn yii bí kpáá-u ndɔpuntikaa pu. Bi-tantatii nìn yāntì Piilati gīī ní ki fōō bi-nyɔkɔ.

eng: Pilate issued his decision to grant their request.
local: Ama ní bi yíì, ki mɔ̀tīi pée tátí mpɔn yii bí kpáá-u ndɔpuntikaa pu. Bi-tantatii nìn yāntì Piilati gīī ní ki fōō bi-nyɔkɔ.

eng: He released the one they asked for, who had been thrown into prison because of a riot and murder. But he handed Jesus over to their will.
local: Ní u gíiǹ uninja, un nìn yíkì ki jáà nì gɔmina ki kūū unil muee ki líì, ní ki dū Yeesu ki tīī-bi bí ŋá-u bi nín là puee.

eng: As they led Jesus away, they grabbed Simon, a man from Cyrene, who was coming in from the countryside. They put the cross on his back and made him carry it behind Jesus.
local: Bi yōoǹ Yeesu ki cáǹ kí kpááyee, ní ki dūu tú Siimɔn un sá Siireen tiŋkinee, u nìn nyánní kusaau. Ní bi cúú-u ní ki dū ndɔpuntikaa ki būkuǹ-u ú nīn bùkū ki pá Yeesu boon.

eng: A huge crowd of people followed Jesus, including women, who were mourning and wailing for him.
local: Dinikitikpaandi nìn dī Yeesu, ní binimpoobi mun nìn sūǹ ki yáliŋkí u-pu.

eng: Jesus turned to the women and said, Daughters of Jerusalem, dont cry for me. Rather, cry for yourselves and your children.
local: Ní Yeesu fātìi bí-bi yii: «Jeerusaleem nimpoobi, taa nín̄ sūm̀maan man pu, nín̄ sūm̀maan nimbi ni-ba nì ni-biyaamu pu.

eng: The time will come when they will say, Happy are those who are unable to become pregnant, the wombs that never gave birth, and the breasts that never nursed a child.
local: Kun puee ŋyunti bí ki dòoń ŋun ni bi dāa lì yii: <Biŋmaŋŋmantiibi bin kaa pée màl̄ ki kée, bin mbiyaamu kaa ŋáam̀ bi-biiŋee bí mmɔnneeee!>.

eng: Pilate asked him, Are you the king of the Jews? Jesus replied, Thats what you say.
local: Ní Piilati bālifì-u yii: «Sii di sá Juuda yab bɔtiuaaa?» Ní Yeesu kíí-u yii: «Sii di lī.».

eng: Then they will say to the mountains, Fall on us, and to the hills, Cover us.
local: Ní binib dāa lì ajoon yii: <Lítímaan ti-pu!> Ní kí lì ŋgbantamu yii: <Nyìkim̄maan ti-pu!>.

eng: If they do these things when the tree is green, what will happen when it is dry.
local: Bi yaa ŋá man un sá bususɔmbuee mimmee, kin nimbi bin sá isukulimee fáà?».

eng: They also led two other criminals to be executed with Jesus.
local: Soojab cáań bininjab bili, bi sá digaŋgam dambi ki kútì Yeesu pu kí kpɔ̀ bi mun.

eng: When they arrived at the place called The Skull, they crucified him, along with the criminals, one on his right and the other on his left.
local: Bi bàn̄ laakin bi yíì kuyikpabikuee, ní ki dū-u ki kpáá ndɔpuntikaa pu úǹ nì digaŋgam dambi bilee, ubɔ u-ŋaŋgiil pú, un kíǹee u-ŋaŋgan pú.

eng: Jesus said, Father, forgive them, for they dont know what theyre doing. They drew lots as a way of dividing up his clothing.
local: Ní Yeesu bí yii: «M-Baa, dūú pɔ̄-bi, kun puee baa nyí tin bi bíi ŋáań nee taapu.» Ní bi tɔ́ kasi bi-kansikin ki yākatì u-bɔkutiŋ.

eng: The people were standing around watching, but the leaders sneered at him, saying, He saved others. Let him save himself if he really is the Christ sent from God, the chosen one.
local: Ní kunipaau yú ki kpèé, ní Juuda yab yidambi bí ki bèel̄-u yii: «U fīī bin kíǹee, u yaa lafun sá Meesiya, un Unimbɔti lēēyee, ú fīī u-ba mun.».

eng: The soldiers also mocked him. They came up to him, offering him sour wine.
local: Ní soojab mun bí ki bèel̄-u, ní ki cáań ndaam min gàm̀ee ki tīī-u ú nyɔ̀.

eng: and saying, If you really are the king of the Jews, save yourself.
local: ní ki bí-u yii: «A yaa sá Juuda yab bɔtiu, á fīī a-ba.».

eng: Above his head was a notice of the formal charge against him. It read This is the king of the Jews.
local: Ní bi ŋmàn̄ u-yilpu yii: «Un bí nee di sá Juuda yab bɔtiu.».

eng: One of the criminals hanging next to Jesus insulted him: Arent you the Christ? Save yourself and us.
local: Ní digaŋgam dambi bin bi kpáá ki kútì u-puee ubɔ mun bí ki sìí-u yii: «Sii di kaa sá Meesiyaaa? Fīī a-ba kí fīī kí kpáaǹ ti-muaa!».

eng: Then Pilate said to the chief priests and the crowds, I find no legal basis for action against this man.
local: Niinee ní Piilati bí bisaraaŋakpilib nì binib kɔkɔ yii: «Maa ká kijapaai nee pu tikpitil báà tiba.».

eng: Responding, the other criminal spoke harshly to him, Dont you fear God, seeing that youve also been sentenced to die?.
local: Ní u-juti bíi wòoǹ-u yii: «Sii nì úǹ kɔkɔ bí ditafadaabaantiilin ní, aa fàŋkí Unimbɔtaaa?.

eng: We are rightly condemned, for we are receiving the appropriate sentence for what we did. But this man has done nothing wrong.
local: Timbi nín máaǹ puee di sá ditafadaaldi din poon ti bí nee, kun puee digaŋgam tuuŋŋi ŋin ti ŋáańee paati dee. Ama un bí nee, úǹ kaa ŋá digaŋgam ubɔ.».

eng: Then he said, Jesus, remember me when you come into your kingdom.
local: Ní u bí Yeesu yii: «A yaa láa gítìń kí jī dibeelee, á dāá téetí m-pu.».

eng: Jesus replied, I assure you that today you will be with me in paradise.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-u yii: «Ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-si, díǹ gba man nì si di làá nīn bí Unimbɔti Beelin.».

eng: It was now about noon, and darkness covered the whole earth until about three oclock.
local: Kuwiŋkuluu nyɔkɔmee, ní ŋwìiń wáŋkì ní dibɔmbɔndi jéetì kitiŋ kɔkɔ pu ki tin sāā tikúti tita.

eng: while the sun stopped shining. Then the curtain in the sanctuary tore down the middle.
local: Ní sata un bi nìn dūu pītì kunimbɔtidii poon ki gāntì laakin bi yíì circir páaa laŋkee nì laan kíǹee cúu cátì kansikin nì kansikin.

eng: Crying out in a loud voice, Jesus said, Father, into your hands I entrust my life . After he said this, he breathed for the last time.
local: Ní Yeesu fáá mbiil mpɔn yii: «M-Baa, m dū m-Fam ki ŋá a-ŋaan.» U lī mimmee, ní ki kpíì.

eng: When the centurion saw what happened, he praised God, saying, Its really true: this man was righteous.
local: Room soojakpiliu ká mimmee ní ki bíi pàkà Unimbɔti, ní ki bí yii: «Ibaamɔn, kijapaai nee sá unimɔnti ní.».

eng: All the crowds who had come together to see this event returned to their homes beating their chests after seeing what had happened.
local: Kunipaau kun nìn dɔ́miń kí kpèè bi nín kpáá Yeesu puee nín ká mimmee, ní bi fàtīi kùǹ ki fátí bi-biŋŋi nì kunimbaakpaaŋŋu.

eng: And everyone who knew him, including the women who had followed him from Galilee, stood at a distance observing these things.
local: Yeesu bɔɔbi kɔkɔ nì binimpoobi bin nìn céeǹní-u doooo Galiileeyee nìn yú kudandaŋu ní ki kpèé tin kɔkɔ bíi jītée.

eng: But they objected strenuously, saying, He agitates the people with his teaching throughout Judea—starting from Galilee all the way here.
local: Ní bi yíì, ki mɔ̀tīi sòoǹ mpɔn yii: «U yìiǹ ní ki dàkà ki gáatí binib ń taa nín̄ tɔ́ gɔmina. U kíliń Galiilee ní ki dɔ́miń ki jítì Juudee tiŋki kɔkɔ poon ní ki dāa bāań timbi cee doo.».

eng: Now there was a man named Joseph who was a member of the council. He was a good and righteous man.
local: Uninja ubɔ nìn bí, bi yíì-u Jooseefi. U nìn sá bibɔsoonliibee kansikin ubɔ ní ki sá unimɔnti ki yú deedee.

eng: He hadnt agreed with the plan and actions of the council. He was from the Jewish city of Arimathea and eagerly anticipated Gods kingdom.
local: Uninjee nìn sá Juuda yab tiŋki kin bi yíì Arimateeyee ní ki nìn kā ki cí Unimbɔti Beel. Waa nìn yú bibɔsoonliibi bin kíǹee boon, ní kaa nìn bí tin bi bɔ́ntì kí ŋée poon.

eng: This man went to Pilate and asked for Jesus body.
local: Úǹ di cūtì Piilati cee ki mēē-u nsan kí yòon̄ Yeesu wunti.

eng: Taking it down, he wrapped it in a linen cloth and laid it in a tomb carved out of the rock, in which no one had ever been buried.
local: U pú-u nsanee, ní u cūtì ki kpáatì Yeesu wunti ndɔpuntikaa pu, ní ki dū ŋŋaapiin ki fìn̄-u ki cáá-u ki bíl̀ dikaakul din bi nìn lùkū ditaŋkpalinee. Baa nìn dáá síb́ báà ubɔ dikaakul gbanti poon.

eng: It was the Preparation Day for the Sabbath, and the Sabbath was quickly approaching.
local: An nìn sá ŋwiin ŋun jooku ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa kífée, ní ŋwìiń tóob́ kí jēetí.

eng: The women who had come with Jesus from Galilee followed Joseph. They saw the tomb and how Jesus body was laid in it.
local: Binimpoobi bin nìn céeǹní Yeesu doooo Galiileeyee táá Jooseefi ki cūtì ki ká dikaakul nì bi nín bíl̀ u-wunti puee.

eng: then they went away and prepared fragrant spices and perfumed oils. They rested on the Sabbath, in keeping with the commandment.
local: Bi nìn fātìi nín kùǹ, ki dɔ́miń ki bɔ́ntì ikpafi nì talaalub kí dāá cáań kí yàaǹ utaŋkpiil pu, ní ki kāl̀ ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa, kii bi-marau nín sá puee.

eng: Hearing this, Pilate asked if the man was a Galilean.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun Piilati gbìl̄ mimmee, ní u bālifì yii: «Kijapaai nee sá ugaliileeja níii?».

eng: When he learned that Jesus was from Herods district, Pilate sent him to Herod, who was also in Jerusalem at that time.
local: Bi nín kíí-u yii Yeesu lafun nyánní Galiilee kitiŋ kin ni Eeroodi sá ubɔtee, ní u dū Yeesu ki tīī Eeroodi un nìn dɔ́miń Jeerusaleem ŋyunti gbantee.

eng: Herod was very glad to see Jesus, for he had heard about Jesus and had wanted to see him for quite some time. He was hoping to see Jesus perform some sign.
local: Eeroodi nín ká Yeesuee, an mɔ́ɔkiǹ-u tikpil, di sá yii u nìn gbìl̄ bi sòoǹ Yeesu pu an yúntì, u bàà pée nyàab̀ kí ká-u ú ŋá maamaaci ubɔ ú ká.

eng: Herod questioned Jesus at length, but Jesus didnt respond to him.
local: Ní u bālifì-u anyɔɔbun tikpil, ama Yeesu kaa kíí-u báà dinyɔɔbumbaantiil.

eng: Very early in the morning on the first day of the week, the women went to the tomb, bringing the fragrant spices they had prepared.
local: Kinyɔŋ poon wimpeekaaŋu dal ntaafaabɔmbɔminee, ní binimpoobee búntì dikaakul laŋki ki ŋúb́ ikpafi nì talaalub bin bi bɔ́ntèe.

eng: It was Mary Magdalene, Joanna, Mary the mother of James, and the other women with them who told these things to the apostles.
local: An nimpoobee di sá Maarii Magidalaa yɔu, nì Jaani, nì Maarii Yakubu na. Binimpoobi biba mun nìn céeǹ-bi, ki tùkū akpambal tibɔbaantiil.

eng: Their words struck the apostles as nonsense, and they didnt believe the women.
local: Ama binimpoobee nín dɔ́miń ki tùkū akpambalee, baa nìn fōō ki kíí, bi nìn dūu yíiń mmaam ní.

eng: But Peter ran to the tomb. When he bent over to look inside, he saw only the linen cloth. Then he returned home, wondering what had happened.
local: Ama ní Peetroo yíkìi sáǹ ki nín cá dikaakul laŋki. U bàn̄ ki gbóońee ŋŋaabu ŋun bi nìn dūu fìn̄-uee baba di dɔ̄. Ní u fātìi nín gítiń, ki bākatì nì an bɔti sèeee.

eng: On that same day, two disciples were traveling to a village called Emmaus, about seven miles from Jerusalem.
local: Ŋwimbaanti gbanti ní u-boonnooliibi bili yíkì ki nín cá kitimbilii kiba bi yíì-kì Eemaayusi. Kí yīkì Jeerusaleem kí cù kitiŋ gbantinee, an sá ataŋkpee saalaa nì diba ní.

eng: They were talking to each other about everything that had happened.
local: Ní bi còom̄ ki sòoǹ tin kɔkɔ jítì Yeesu puee.

eng: While they were discussing these things, Jesus himself arrived and joined them on their journey.
local: Bi nìn bíi sòoǹ míǹ ki kpákèe, ní Yeesu u-ba bāań bi-cee ní u nì bi táá ki nín cá.

eng: They were prevented from recognizing him.
local: Bi wál̀-u ní tiba yāntì baa béè-u.

eng: He said to them, What are you talking about as you walk along? They stopped, their faces downcast.
local: Ní Yeesu bālifì-bi yii: «Tilati ní ni còom̄ ki kpákà ní?» Ní bi sāā ki síl̀ nì kunimbaau.

eng: The one named Cleopas replied, Are you the only visitor to Jerusalem who is unaware of the things that have taken place there over the last few days.
local: Ní bi-kansikin ubɔ, bi yíì-u Kileewoopasi, bí-u yii: «M pàkā sii baba di pée kíǹ Jeerusaleem tiŋkin doo, kaa gbìl̄ tin jítì iwiin nee poonee.».

eng: He said to them, What things? They said to him, The things about Jesus of Nazareth. Because of his powerful deeds and words, he was recognized by God and all the people as a prophet.
local: Ní u bālifì-bi yii: «Ba bɔti di jítì ní?» Ní bi kíí-u yii: «Tin bāań Yeesu Naasaareeti yɔu, un nìn sá Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu ki ŋáań maamaaci tuuŋŋi, u-nyɔɔbuŋŋi mun cáá mpɔn Unimbɔti nì binib kɔkɔ nyée.

eng: They found the stone rolled away from the tomb.
local: Bi bàn̄ee, ki ká ditaŋkpal din bi nìn dūu pīiǹ dikaakul nyɔkɔee bíntì ki tin dɔ̄ ŋkpaanceeti.

eng: But our chief priests and our leaders handed him over to be sentenced to death, and they crucified him.
local: Ti-saraaŋakpilib nì ti-yidambi dū-u ki tīī bi sòon̄ ki kūū u-bɔti, ní bi dū-u ki kpáá ndɔpuntikaa pu.

eng: We had hoped he was the one who would redeem Israel. All these things happened three days ago.
local: Úǹ pu ní ti bàà máká yii u làá fīī Isirayeel yab. Nì míǹ kɔkɔee, an bɔti nín jítèe díǹ iwiin ita sèé nee.

eng: But theres more: Some women from our group have left us stunned. They went to the tomb early this morning.
local: Ama ti-kansikin binimpoobi biba cáań-ti maamaaci bɔti díǹ nee. Di sá yii bi péé kutaafaau ní ki cūtì u-kaakul laŋki.

eng: and didnt find his body. They came to us saying that they had even seen a vision of angels who told them he is alive.
local: Bi bàn̄ee, kaa ká u-wunti, ní ki fātìi gítìń ti-cee ki máafì-ti yii Unimbɔti tuuŋyi cílìń bi-pu ki tùkū-bi yii u fīkitì.

eng: Some of those who were with us went to the tomb and found things just as the women said. They didnt see him.
local: Ti-yab biba mun cūtì u-kaakul laŋki ki mun ká kii binimpoobee nín bàà lī puee, ama baa ká úǹ u-ba.».

eng: Then Jesus said to them, You foolish people! Your dull minds keep you from believing all that the prophets talked about.
local: Niinee ní Yeesu bí yii: «Óooo binib nee kaa cáá iciin, kaa yáb̀ kí fōō Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi nín sòon̄ tiŋyee kí kíí.

eng: Wasnt it necessary for the Christ to suffer these things and then enter into his glory.
local: Mimmee an kaa máaǹ Meesiya ń jī falaa míǹ kí nín kɔ́ u-nyootinaaa?».

eng: Then he interpreted for them the things written about himself in all the scriptures, starting with Moses and going through all the Prophets.
local: Ní u kíĺ ki bíi sòoǹ ki cántí-bi Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon tibɔti tin kɔkɔ Mooyiisi nì Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi nìn ŋmàn̄ ki síiǹ u-puee.

eng: When they came to Emmaus, he acted as if he was going on ahead.
local: Bi nín bāań kitimbilii kin ni bi cée, ní Yeesu ŋá kii u là kí càatī nyɔkɔpuee.

eng: But they urged him, saying, Stay with us. Its nearly evening, and the day is almost over. So he went in to stay with them.
local: Ní bi bí yii: «Aayee, gbíntí ti-cee, ŋwìiń làá jēetí kunyeeu ń mɔ́ dee.» Ní u kɔ́ bi-cee.

eng: but when they went in, they didnt find the body of the Lord Jesus.
local: ní bi kɔ́ dikaakul poon kaa ká ti-Dindaan Yeesu wunti.

eng: After he took his seat at the table with them, he took the bread, blessed and broke it, and gave it to them.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun bi kāl̀ ki làá nīn jīǹee, ní Yeesu yōoǹ kpɔnɔ ní ki jáam̀ Unimbɔti ní ki gífì ki tīī-bi.

eng: Their eyes were opened and they recognized him, but he disappeared from their sight.
local: Niinee ní bi-nimbiliŋ cúu ŋāatì ní bi béè-u, ní u wáŋkì bi-kansikin niin.

eng: They said to each other, Werent our hearts on fire when he spoke to us along the road and when he explained the scriptures for us.
local: Ní bi bíi lì tɔb cee yii: «U nín bíi sòoǹ nì ti ki lèekī Unimbɔti gbɔŋku taapu ki dàkà-ti nsannee, saa bàà ká an nín kɔ́ ti-pɔbiliŋin fíŋfiŋeeee?».

eng: They got up right then and returned to Jerusalem. They found the eleven and their companions gathered together.
local: Ní bi yíkì niin niin ki nín gítí Jeerusaleem. Bi bàn̄ee ki ká akpambal saalaa nì dibee nì bi-jutiibi bin kíǹee kpáfì.

eng: They were saying to each other, The Lord really has risen! He appeared to Simon.
local: ki bíi lì yii: «Tiŋman, ti-Dindaan lafun fīkitì! Di sá yii Siimɔn ká-u.».

eng: Then the two disciples described what had happened along the road and how Jesus was made known to them as he broke the bread.
local: Ní bi mun dū tin bi ká nsannee, nì Yeesu nín gíí kpɔnɔ bi béè-u puee ki máafì-bi.

eng: While they were saying these things, Jesus himself stood among them and said, Peace be with you.
local: Bi bíi sòoǹ mimmee, Yeesu bāań bi-kansikin ki jáam̀-bi yii: «Unimbɔti ń tīī-ni ŋgbansɔŋfi.».

eng: They were terrified and afraid. They thought they were seeing a ghost.
local: Ní bi fāŋkì tikpil bi-wunti kpíì, kun puee bi dàkafī yii an sá ufaŋfanti ní.

eng: He said to them, Why are you startled? Why are doubts arising in your hearts?.
local: Ní Yeesu bālifì-bi yii: «Ba pu ní ni-pɔbiliŋ máfí míǹ? Ba pu ní ni cáá ilandɔkɔ ili ili míǹ?.

eng: Look at my hands and my feet. Its really me! Touch me and see, for a ghost doesnt have flesh and bones like you see I have.
local: Man dee, kpèèmaan m-ŋalii poon nì m-tafati tibuŋwati. Gbēēmaan-mi kí kpèè! Ufaŋfanti kaa cáá tiwunaaa, kaa cáá akpab kii ni nín wāĺ man cáá pu nee.».

eng: They didnt know what to make of this. Suddenly, two men were standing beside them in gleaming bright clothing.
local: Bi bākatì ki kíǹ ki yú ki dàkafēe ní bininjab bili cílìń bi-pu ki pēē tiwampin káŋkaŋ.

eng: As he said this, he showed them his hands and feet.
local: U lī mimmee, ní ki dàkā-bi u-ŋalii nì u-taaŋi.

eng: Because they were wondering and questioning in the midst of their happiness, he said to them, Do you have anything to eat.
local: Baa nìn dáá baasii fōō ki kíí mbaamɔmin, kun puee an nìn sá-bi mmɔnnaaa, ki ŋá-bi bitii mun, an puee ní Yeesu bālifì-bi yii: «Ni cáá tiba doo kí jāaa?».

eng: They gave him a piece of baked fish.
local: Ní bi dū dijɔŋgindi din ŋɔ́fèe ki tīī-u.

eng: Taking it, he ate it in front of them.
local: ní u fōō ki ŋmɔ́ bi-nimbiin niin.

eng: Jesus said to them, These are my words that I spoke to you while I was still with you—that everything written about me in the Law from Moses, the Prophets, and the Psalms must be fulfilled.
local: Ní u bí-bi yii: «Naa téetí m nín nìn dáá bí ni-cee, ki tùkū-ni yii tin kɔkɔ bi ŋmàn̄ m-pu Mooyiisi marab nì Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi gbɔnti nì Inyɔŋki laŋyi gbɔŋku poonee máaǹ kí ŋá mimmaaa?».

eng: Then he opened their minds to understand the scriptures.
local: Niinee, ní u pīitì bi-landɔkɔi bí ŋmā kí gbìl̄ Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon bɔti taapu.

eng: He said to them, This is what is written: the Christ will suffer and rise from the dead on the third day.
local: ní ki bí-bi yii: «An nín ŋmàn̄ puee sèé, yii: Meesiya làá jī falaa kí kpú ní winta dalee ú fàtīí fìkitī.

eng: and a change of heart and life for the forgiveness of sins must be preached in his name to all nations, beginning from Jerusalem.
local: An máaǹ yii ní sòoǹ u-pu atimbun kɔkɔ nib cee, kí kíliń Jeerusaleem, yii binib ń kpántí bi-bimbim kí ká bi-kpitii pɔ̄tam.

eng: You are witnesses of these things.
local: Nimbi di sá seeradambi yii m kpíì, ki fīkitì.

eng: Look, Im sending to you what my Father promised, but you are to stay in the city until you have been furnished with heavenly power.
local: M làá yàntī Circir Fam min m-Baa nìn pōoǹee ń dāan̄ ni-pu. Ama ní nīn kā Jeerusaleem kitiŋ poon kí nīn cí ŋyunti ŋun Unimbɔti pɔŋŋuee làá kpákatiń ni-puee.».

eng: The women were frightened and bowed their faces toward the ground, but the men said to them, Why do you look for the living among the dead?.
local: Bi fāŋkì ki tīntì, ní binibee bí yii: «Ba pu ní ni nyàab̀ unil un wāĺee bitaŋkpiibi kansikin ní?.

eng: He led them out as far as Bethany, where he lifted his hands and blessed them.
local: Ní Yeesu yōoǹ-bi ki nyántì kitiŋ kpaanceetiŋu ki cá Beetanii watil pú. Niinee ní u yōoǹ u-ŋalii yilpu ki mēē ki bīiǹ bi-pu.

eng: As he blessed them, he left them and was taken up to heaven.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun u bíi mèé ki bìintí bi-puee, ní u yíkì bi-cee ki nín jɔ́ḿ yilpu.

eng: They worshipped him and returned to Jerusalem overwhelmed with joy.
local: Ní bíǹ gbāaǹ ki nyɔ́ŋkì-u, ní ki fātìi nín gítiń Jeerusaleem nì dipɔɔpiŋkpaandi.

eng: And they were continuously in the temple praising God.
local: Bi nìn bí baabadal kunimbɔtidiin ki pàkà Unimbɔti ki jáaḿ-u.

eng: He isnt here, but has been raised. Remember what he told you while he was still in Galilee.
local: Úǹ fīkitì, waa bí doo, téetímaan u nín nìn bí Galiilee ki tùkū-ni puee.

eng: that the Human One must be handed over to sinners, be crucified, and on the third day rise again.
local: yii: <An sá bí dū man Kinibiki kí ŋúbíǹ binikpitib ní bí kpáá-mi ndɔpuntikaa pu ḿ kpú, ní winta dalee ḿ fìkitī.>».

eng: Then they remembered his words.
local: Niinee ní bi fātìi téetì Yeesu bɔti gbanti pu.

eng: When they returned from the tomb, they reported all these things to the eleven and all the others.
local: Ní bi búntì ki cūtìi máafì an bɔti kɔkɔ akpambal saalaa nì dibee nì biboonnooliibi bin kíǹee kɔkɔ.

eng: In the fifteenth year of the rule of the emperor Tiberius—when Pontius Pilate was governor over Judea and Herod was ruler over Galilee, his brother Philip was ruler over Ituraea and Trachonitis, and Lysanias was ruler over Abilene.
local: Gɔminakpaan Tiibeer nín kāl̀ dibeel nee, an ŋá abin saalku dee. Dibindi gbanti poonee, Pɔnsi Piilati di nìn sá gɔmina Juudee tiŋkin, ní Eeroodi sá ubɔti Galiilee tiŋkin ní u-maan Fiiliipi mun sá ubɔti Iituree nì Trakoonii timmun, ní Liisaniyasi sá ubɔti Abileen tiŋkin.

eng: The crowds asked him, What then should we do.
local: Ní kunipaauee bíi bàlifī-u yii: «Mimmee, ti làá ŋá mana ní kí fáatí Unimbɔti?».

eng: He answered, Whoever has two shirts must share with the one who has none, and whoever has food must do the same.
local: Ní u kíí-bi yii: «Un yaa cáá abɔkuti alee ń lēē diba kí tīī un kaa cááyee. Un mun yaa tí cáá tijinee, ń lēē kí tīī un kaa cáá kí jēe.».

eng: Even tax collectors came to be baptized. They said to him, Teacher, what should we do.
local: Ní bifɔtiliibi mun dɔ́miń bí fàl̀-bi Unimbɔti nyim, ní ki bālifì Yoowaneesi yii: «Ukpil, ti máaǹ kí ŋá mana ní?».

eng: He replied, Collect no more than you are authorized to collect.
local: Ní u bí-bi yii: «Bi nín yóoǹ mara yii bí nīn fɔ́tí puee, ní taa fɔ́tí kí lákatí míǹ pu.».

eng: Soldiers asked, What about us? What should we do? He answered, Dont cheat or harass anyone, and be satisfied with your pay.
local: Ní soojab mun dɔ́miń ki bālifì-u yii: «Timbi máaǹ kí ŋá mana?» Ní u kíí-bi yii: «Ní taa nín̄ dàkà binib mpɔn, kí taa nín̄ gífí inyimɔn ki tūntí binib pu, ama ní yàntī tipaal tin ni fòol̄ uŋmal uŋmalee ń nīn mɔ̄ǹ-ni.».

eng: The people were filled with expectation, and everyone wondered whether John might be the Christ.
local: Kii báà ŋma nín nìn kā ki cí Meesiyayee puee ní bi kíǹ ki bàlifī bi-ba yii Yoowaneesi di kalin sá Meesiyaaa?.

eng: John replied to them all, I baptize you with water, but the one who is more powerful than me is coming. Im not worthy to loosen the strap of his sandals. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and fire.
local: Niinee ní Yoowaneesi bí bi-kɔkɔ yii: «Man fùul̀-ni nì nnyim ní, ama ubɔ dòoń, u-pɔŋŋu jítiǹ man yɔŋu. Man kaa kpántì kí nīn sá u-yumbuu kí kútí u-naataaŋmiii gba. Úǹ di làá dāá fàl̀-ni nì Unimbɔti Fam nì ŋŋmi.

eng: The shovel he uses to sift the wheat from the husks is in his hands. He will clean out his threshing area and bring the wheat into his barn. But he will burn the husks with a fire that cant be put out.
local: Úǹ di ŋúb́ u-yubakalkaaku ki làá bàkal̀ kí gàntī idi nì tifin, kí dū idee kí ŋá kulɔŋŋun, ní kí dū tifin kí tɔ̄ ŋŋmi ŋun kaa kúù jaanjaanee ni.».

eng: With many other words John appealed to them, proclaiming good news to the people.
local: Míǹ ní Yoowaneesi nìn sáŋkí binib, ki gíti kútí-bi tibɔti tikpil ní ki sòoǹ nì bi tibɔmɔntiil.

eng: But Herod the ruler had been criticized harshly by John because of Herodias, Herods brothers wife, and because of all the evil he had done.
local: Yoowaneesi wòon̄ ubɔti Eeroodi un nìn cáá u-maan nimpuu Eeroodiyadee nì u nín túń atuŋkpiti tikpil ŋin ki kútèe.

eng: during the high priesthood of Annas and Caiaphas—Gods word came to John son of Zechariah in the wilderness.
local: ní Ana nì Kaayiifi sá bisaraaŋakpilib. Ŋyunti gbanti ní Unimbɔti nìn sòon̄ nì Yoowaneesi, Sakari jipɔɔn kuteeun.

eng: He added this to the list of his evil deeds: he locked John up in prison.
local: Niinee ní Eeroodi gíti túń dituŋkpitil diba ki kútì, di sá yii u cúú Yoowaneesi ki pīiǹ kudii.

eng: When everyone was being baptized, Jesus also was baptized. While he was praying, heaven was opened.
local: Báà ŋma nìn cá bi fùul̀-bi Unimbɔti nyim. Yeesu mun nìn fùl̄ Unimbɔti nyim. U fùl̄ ki bíi mèéyee, kutagbɔŋu cúu pīitì.

eng: and the Holy Spirit came down on him in bodily form like a dove. And there was a voice from heaven: You are my Son, whom I dearly love; in you I find happiness.
local: ní Unimbɔti Fam kpákatìń u-pu ki náań nì dibeenanjil ní bi gbìl̄ Unimbɔti sòon̄ní kutaa pu yii: «Sii di sá man Jipɔɔn un m néeń tikpilee, sii ní m-pɔbil fáatì.».

eng: Jesus was about years old when he began his ministry. People supposed that he was the son of Joseph son of Heli.
local: Yeesu nìn ŋá kii abin mmuŋku nì saalaa ní ki kíĺ u-tundi. Binib nìn nyí-u yii u sá Jooseefi jipɔɔn, ní Jooseefi baa sá Eelii.

eng: son of Matthat son of Levi son of Melchi son of Jannai son of Joseph.
local: Eelii baa sá Maataati, ní Maataati baa sá Leefii, ní Leefii baa sá Meelki, ní Meelki baa sá Jaanayi, ní Jaanayi baa sá Jooseefi.

eng: son of Mattathias son of Amos son of Nahum son of Esli son of Naggai.
local: ní Jooseefi baa sá Maatatiyasi, ní Maatatiyasi baa sá Amoosi, ní Amoosi baa sá Nawum, ní Nawum baa sá Eeslii, ní Eeslii baa sá Nagayi.

eng: son of Maath son of Mattathias son of Semein son of Josech son of Joda.
local: ní Nagayi baa sá Maatiyasi, ní Maatiyasi baa sá Maatatiyasi, ní Maatatiyasi baa sá Seemeeyiin, ní Seemeeyiin baa sá Jooseeki, ní Jooseeki baa sá Jooda.

eng: son of Joanan son of Rhesa son of Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel son of Neri.
local: ní Jooda baa sá Joowana, ní Joowana baa sá Reesa, ní Reesa baa sá Sooroobabeel, ní Sooroobabeel baa sá Salatiyeel, ní Salatiyeel baa sá Neerii.

eng: son of Melchi son of Addi son of Cosam son of Elmadam son of Er.
local: ní Neerii baa sá Meelki, ní Meelki baa sá Adii, ní Adii baa sá Koosam, ní Koosam baa sá Eelmadam, ní Eelmadam baa sá Eer.

eng: son of Joshua son of Eliezer son of Jorim son of Matthat son of Levi.
local: ní Eer baa sá Yeesuwa, ní Yeesuwa baa sá Eeliyeeseer, ní Eeliyeeseer baa sá Jooriim, ní Jooriim baa sá Maataati, ní Maataati baa sá Leefii.

eng: John went throughout the region of the Jordan River, calling for people to be baptized to show that they were changing their hearts and lives and wanted God to forgive their sins.
local: Ní Yoowaneesi kíĺ ki bíi yìiǹ ntimu mun bí Jɔɔdan buŋŋu caŋinee ki sòoǹ yii: «Kpántímaan ni-bimbim bí fàl̀-ni Unimbɔti nyim, ní Unimbɔti ń dū ni-kpitii kí pɔ̄-ni.».

eng: son of Simeon son of Judah son of Joseph son of Jonam son of Eliakim.
local: ní Leefii baa sá Simeeyɔn, ní Simeeyɔn baa sá Juuda, ní Juuda baa sá Jooseefi, ní Jooseefi baa sá Joonam, ní Joonam baa sá Eeliyaakiim.

eng: son of Melea son of Menna son of Mattatha son of Nathan son of David.
local: ní Eeliyaakiim baa sá Meeleeya, ní Meeleeya baa sá Meena, ní Meena baa sá Maataata, ní Maataata baa sá Naatan, ní Naatan baa sá Daafiidi.

eng: son of Jesse son of Obed son of Boaz son of Sala son of Nahshon.
local: ní Daafiidi baa sá Jeesee, ní Jeesee baa sá Joobeedi, ní Joobeedi baa sá Boowaasi, ní Boowaasi baa sá Salmɔn, ní Salmɔn baa sá Naasɔn.

eng: son of Amminadab son of Admin son of Arni son of Hezron son of Perez son of Judah.
local: ní Naasɔn baa sá Amiinadaab, ní Amiinadaab baa sá Adameen, ní Adameen baa sá Arinii, ní Arinii baa sá Eesiroom, ní Eesiroom baa sá Fareesi, ní Fareesi baa sá Juuda.

eng: son of Jacob son of Isaac son of Abraham son of Terah son of Nahor.
local: ní Juuda baa sá Jaakɔb, ní Jaakɔb baa sá Isaaki, ní Isaaki baa sá Abraam, ní Abraam baa sá Teera.

eng: son of Serug son of Reu son of Peleg son of Eber son of Shelah.
local: ní Teera baa sá Naakɔɔr, ní Naakɔɔr baa sá Seeru, ní Seeru baa sá Ragoo, ní Ragoo baa sá Faleeki, ní Faleeki baa sá Eebeer.

eng: son of Cainan son of Arphaxad son of Shem son of Noah son of Lamech.
local: ní Eebeer baa sá Sala, ní Sala baa sá Kayinaam, ní Kayinaam baa sá Aripakisaadi, ní Aripakisaadi baa sá Seem, ní Seem baa sá Noowa.

eng: son of Methuselah son of Enoch son of Jared son of Mahalalel son of Cainan.
local: ní Noowa baa sá Lameeki, ní Lameeki baa sá Matuseela, ní Matuseela baa sá Eenɔki, ní Eenɔki baa sá Jareedi, ní Jareedi baa sá Maleeleel.

eng: son of Enos son of Seth son of Adam son of God.
local: ní Maleeleel baa sá Kayinan, ní Kayinan baa sá Eenɔsi, ní Eenɔsi baa sá Seeti, ní Seeti baa sá Adam, ní Adam úǹ sá un Unimbɔti péé ki náańee.

eng: This is just as it was written in the scroll of the words of Isaiah the prophet, A voice crying out in the wilderness: Prepare the way for the Lord; make his paths straight.
local: An nóó nì Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu Yeesaya nín ŋmàn̄ u-gbɔŋku poon puee yii: «Ubɔ báakí kuteeun yii: Bɔ́ntímaan ti-Dindaan saŋŋu kí tàntīmaan u-sàmbiyaamu kí cīití-u.

eng: Every valley will be filled, and every mountain and hill will be leveled. The crooked will be made straight and the rough places made smooth.
local: Atantaka kɔkɔ làá dāá gbīŋkì, ajoon nì ŋgbantamu kɔkɔ làá bìtī. Asaŋgɔŋgɔl làá tàntī. Isan in cáá abɔbil abɔbilee làá ŋāŋkì.

eng: All humanity will see Gods salvation. .
local: Ní báà ŋma ń dāá ká Unimbɔti nín fìil̄ binib puee.».

eng: Then John said to the crowds who came to be baptized by him, You children of snakes! Who warned you to escape from the angry judgment that is coming soon?.
local: Yoowaneesi nìn lì kunipaau kun nìn dòoń ú fàl̀-bi nnyimee yii: «Nimbi bin tū kii ikoowaatee, ŋma di tùkū-ni yii ni ŋūǹ kí sāǹ kí ŋmátí Unimbɔti ŋuul din dòońee ni?.

eng: Produce fruit that shows you have changed your hearts and lives. And dont even think about saying to yourselves, Abraham is our father. I tell you that God is able to raise up Abrahams children from these stones.
local: Lòom̀maan abil ŋin dàkà yii ni kpántì ni-bimbimee, kí taa nín̄ kā ki dàkafī yii Abraam sá ni-naanja! M tùkù-ni ibaamɔn ní, di sá Unimbɔti ŋūǹ kí dū ataŋkpee nee kí kpántí Abraam naantiibi.

eng: The ax is already at the root of the trees. Therefore, every tree that doesnt produce good fruit will be chopped down and tossed into the fire.
local: Áli gba, Unimbɔti bɔ́ntì kicɔkɔ ki bíl̀ isufi taapu: busubu bun kamaa yaa kaa lòoǹ abimɔntee, bi làá gāā-bu kí tɔ̄ ŋŋmin.».

eng: Jesus returned from the Jordan River full of the Holy Spirit, and was led by the Spirit into the wilderness.
local: Yeesu gbí Unimbɔti Fam ki nyánní Jɔɔdan, ní Ŋfamee cáá-u ki cūnnì kuteeun.

eng: for its written: He will command his angels concerning you, to protect you.
local: kun puee an ŋmàn̄ Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon yii, <Unimbɔti làá yàntī u-tuuŋyi ń nīn cí-si.>.

eng: and they will take you up in their hands so that you wont hit your foot on a stone. .
local: Ní ki gíti bí yii: <I làá nīn tíkí-si i-ŋaan a-taal ń taa gbéetí ditaŋkpal.>».

eng: Jesus answered, Its been said, Dont test the Lord your God. .
local: Ní Yeesu yíì ní ki bí-u yii: «Unimbɔti gbɔŋku lī yii: <Á taa cɔ̄ŋkì ti-Dindaan a-Nimbɔtiu.>».

eng: After finishing every temptation, the devil departed from him until the next opportunity.
local: Sitaan cɔ́ŋkì Yeesu isan kɔkɔ pu ki dóòyee, ní ki búntì kí nīn tí cí ŋyunti ŋubɔ.

eng: Jesus returned in the power of the Spirit to Galilee, and news about him spread throughout the whole countryside.
local: Ní Yeesu fātìi nín gítí Galiilee tiŋkin ki gbí Unimbɔti Fam pɔŋŋu tiŋan, ní u-yindi gbītì niin pú kɔkɔ.

eng: He taught in their synagogues and was praised by everyone.
local: U nìn dàkà binib Unimbɔti bɔti akpafidiinni ní báà ŋma pàkà-u.

eng: Jesus went to Nazareth, where he had been raised. On the Sabbath he went to the synagogue as he normally did and stood up to read.
local: Ní Yeesu búntì Naasaareeti, laakin u nìn kāl̀ ki wíiǹee. Ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa bāańee, ní u kɔ́ kukpafidiin, kii u nín pée kpààa ŋáań puee, ní ki yíkì kí kàaǹ Unimbɔti gbɔŋku.

eng: The synagogue assistant gave him the scroll from the prophet Isaiah. He unrolled the scroll and found the place where it was written.
local: ní bi dū kugbɔŋu kun sòoǹ Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu Yeesaya puee ki tīī-u, ní u fōō ki cúu kpátì ní ki ká laakin lī yii.

eng: The Spirit of the Lord is upon me, because the Lord has anointed me . He has sent me to preach good news to the poor, to proclaim release to the prisoners and recovery of sight to the blind, to liberate the oppressed.
local: «Ti-Dindaan Fam bí m-pu, u lēē-mi ḿ sòoǹ tibɔmɔntiil kí tīī biwɔfib. U túnní-mi ḿ bāakì kí tùkù bisarkadambi yii Unimbɔti làá líí-bi, kí tùkù bijɔfub mun yii bi làá wāl̀, kí tùkù bin bi sàantée yii bi làá ká dibatɔl.

eng: and to proclaim the year of the Lords favor.
local: kí tùkù binib yii ŋyunti nee ní ti-Dindaan làá sɔ̄ bi-saai.».

eng: There he was tempted for forty days by the devil. He ate nothing during those days and afterward Jesus was starving.
local: Ní Sitaan cɔ́ŋkì-u iwiin imuŋku ili. Waa nìn jíń tiba iwiin imuŋku ilee, ní ŋkùm cúú-u.

eng: He rolled up the scroll, gave it back to the synagogue assistant, and sat down. Every eye in the synagogue was fixed on him.
local: Ní Yeesu kpáb́ kugbɔŋuee ní ki gíiǹ ki tīī un kpèé kukpafidiiyee, ní ki kāl̀ kí nīn dàkà-bi. Bin kɔkɔ nìn bí niinee nìn kíǹ ki kā ki kpèé-u ní címmm.

eng: He began to explain to them, Today, this scripture has been fulfilled just as you heard it.
local: Ní u kíĺ ki bíi tùkù-bi yii: «Kí yòon̄ díǹ kí nīn cée, tibɔti tin m kàan̄ ni gbìl̄ nee bàan̄ dee.».

eng: Everyone was raving about Jesus, so impressed were they by the gracious words flowing from his lips. They said, This is Josephs son, isnt it.
local: Ní báà ŋma kíǹ ki sòoǹ min ŋān̄ee u-pu, ní an ŋá-bi bitii tibɔmɔntiil tin u sòoǹee pu. Ní bi bíi lì yii: «Jooseefi jipɔɔn kaa yáaa?».

eng: Then Jesus said to them, Undoubtedly, you will quote this saying to me: Doctor, heal yourself. Do here in your hometown what weve heard you did in Capernaum.
local: Ní u bí-bi yii: «M nyí yii ni làá gāā-mi dilaŋkpandi nee yii: <Unyɔɔdaan, cáaǹ a-ba> ní ki làá gíti lì-mi yii: <Ti gbìl̄ a nín ŋá min Kapeernayumee, ŋá a-tuuŋŋi gbanti abaanti a-tiŋki poon doo.>».

eng: He said, I assure you that no prophet is welcome in the prophets hometown.
local: Ní u gíti lī yii: «Ibaamɔn ní m sòoǹ nì ni, baa fòol̄ Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu u-dumpu mmɔntiimin.

eng: And I can assure you that there were many widows in Israel during Elijahs time, when it didnt rain for three and a half years and there was a great food shortage in the land.
local: Ibaamɔn ní m sòoǹ, Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu Eelii yuntiŋuee, utaa kaa nìn gíti nìn̄ abin ata nì dijandi ní ŋkùm nìn jéetì kitiŋ kɔkɔ pu. Bikpiipoobi nìn wīikí Isirayeel tiŋkin ŋyunti gbanti.

eng: Yet Elijah was sent to none of them but only to a widow in the city of Zarephath in the region of Sidon.
local: Ama Unimbɔti kaa nìn gíti tú Eelii Isirayeel kpiipuu báà ubɔ cee, asee unicaŋkpiipu un nìn kóò Sareepita tiŋki kin bí Siidɔn tiŋkinee baba cee ní u nìn tú-u.

eng: There were also many persons with skin diseases in Israel during the time of the prophet Elisha, but none of them were cleansed. Instead, Naaman the Syrian was cleansed.
local: Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu Eeliisee yuntiŋuee, binyaŋgbantiibi mun tikpil nìn tí bí Isirayeel tiŋkin. Ama bi-kansikin báà ubɔ kaa nìn ká dicáaǹdi, asee Naama Usiiriija baba.».

eng: When they heard this, everyone in the synagogue was filled with anger.
local: Niinee ní bin kɔkɔ nìn bí kukpafidiin ki gbìl̄ an bɔtee fōō aŋuu.

eng: They rose up and ran him out of town. They led him to the crest of the hill on which their town had been built so that they could throw him off the cliff.
local: Bi nìn yíkì ki cúú Yeesu ki dáaĺ ki nyántì kitiŋ kpaanceetiŋu, ní ki cáá-u ki nín jɔ́ḿ dijool din pu bi-diiŋŋi nìn tɔ́kɔ́ee yilpu, kí bàà téé-u kí tɔ̄ń taapu.

eng: The devil said to him, Since you are Gods Son, command this stone to become a loaf of bread.
local: Ní Sitaan bí-u yii: «A yaa lafun sá Unimbɔti Jipɔɔn, tīī ditaŋkpal nee mara dí kpántí kpɔnɔ.».

eng: But he passed through the crowd and went on his way.
local: Ama ní u dī bi-kansikin ki búntì.

eng: Jesus went down to the city of Capernaum in Galilee and taught the people each Sabbath.
local: Yeesu cūtì Kapeernayum, Galiilee tiŋkpaaŋki kiban, ní ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa dalee, u kɔ́ kukpafidiin ki bíi dàkà binib Unimbɔti bɔti.

eng: They were amazed by his teaching because he delivered his message with authority.
local: U-dàkatamee nìn ŋá binib bitii, kun puee mpɔn nìn bí u-bɔti tin u dàkèe ni.

eng: A man in the synagogue had the spirit of an unclean demon. He screamed.
local: Kijapaai kiba nìn bí kukpafidiiyee ni, arasinii kā u-pu, ní ki téeń yii.

eng: Hey! What have you to do with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are. You are the holy one from God.
local: «Yeesu Naasaareeti yɔu, a nyàab̀-ti nì tilati ní? A dɔ́miń kí kúntí-ti níii? M nyí un a sée, a sá circirdaan un Unimbɔti túnnée ní!».

eng: Silence! Jesus said, speaking harshly to the demon. Come out of him! The demon threw the man down before them, then came out of him without harming him.
local: Ní Yeesu kpātì arasiniiyee pu yii: «Á ŋmīĺ niin kí nyā uninja nee ni!» Ní arasiniiyee fíiǹ uninjee ki lúkú kitiŋ binib nimbiin, ní ki nyáń, kaa ŋá-u tiba.

eng: They were all shaken and said to each other, What kind of word is this, that he can command unclean spirits with authority and power, and they leave.
local: Ní báà ŋma bākatì sèeee, ní ki bàlifī tɔb yii: «Batii nyɔɔbundi ní u nín sòon̄ ní dì cáá mpɔn ki jì arasiniibi nan dee?».

eng: Reports about him spread everywhere in the surrounding region.
local: Ní Yeesu bɔti gbītì Kapeernayum tiŋgbammu kɔkɔn.

eng: After leaving the synagogue, Jesus went home with Simon. Simons mother-in-law was sick with a high fever, and the family asked Jesus to help her.
local: Yeesu nyáń kukpafidiinee, ní ki búntì Siimɔn dumpu. Siimɔn nimpuu na nìn būǹ ki dɔ̄ u-wunti píĺ kpaŋkpam, an páb́-u, ní bi mēē Yeesu ú cáaǹ-u.

eng: He bent over her and spoke harshly to the fever, and it left her. She got up at once and served them.
local: Ní Yeesu sútì ki pìl̄-u ní ki kpātì ŋweenee pu. Dicilpu niin ní u pɔ́ɔkì ki yíkì ki ŋá-bi tijin.

eng: Jesus replied, Its written, People wont live only by bread .
local: Ní Yeesu yíì ní ki kíí-u yii: «Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon lī yii: <An kaa sá tijin dijil ŋmaniŋma di yì unil maŋfal.>».

eng: When the sun was setting, everyone brought to Jesus relatives and acquaintances with all kinds of diseases. Placing his hands on each of them, he healed them.
local: Kujoou ŋwìiń bíi jēetée, bin bin cáá bibunliibee nìn cáań-bi Yeesu cee, báà ŋma nì u-weeŋŋu u-weeŋŋu, ní u cáá u-ŋalii ki pāantí bi-kɔkɔ pu ní bi pɔ́ɔkí.

eng: Demons also came out of many people. They screamed, You are Gods Son. But he spoke harshly to them and wouldnt allow them to speak because they recognized that he was the Christ.
local: Arasiniibi nìn nyēē binib tikpil pu ní ki gbáà ibiil yii: «A sá Unimbɔti Jipɔɔn ní!» Ama ní Yeesu kpàtī bi-pu, kaa pūǹ-bi kinyɔkɔ bí sòoǹ, kun puee bíǹ nìn nyí u nín sá Meesiyayee.

eng: When daybreak arrived, Jesus went to a deserted place. The crowds were looking for him. When they found him, they tried to keep him from leaving them.
local: Yeesu péé ntaafaabɔmbɔmin ki nyáń kitiŋin ki búntì ŋkpaanceeti, laakin binib kpée. Ní kunipaau bíi nyàab̀-u. Bi ká-uee, ní ki bàà là ú gbíntí bi-cee kí taa ti cù laaba.

eng: But he said to them, I must preach the good news of Gods kingdom in other cities too, for this is why I was sent.
local: Ní u bí-bi yii: «An máaǹ ḿ cù kí sòoǹ tibɔmɔntiil tin sá Unimbɔti Beel bɔtee ntimu mun kíǹee poon: kun pu Unimbɔti túnní-mee dee.».

eng: So he continued preaching in the Judean synagogues.
local: U nìn yìiǹ ki sòoǹ Unimbɔti bɔti Juudee kpafidiiŋŋin.

eng: Next the devil led him to a high place and showed him in a single instant all the kingdoms of the world.
local: Niinee ní Sitaan yōoǹ Yeesu ki jɔ́m̀ yilpu, ní ki yāntì u kpēē duulinya beeŋi kɔkɔ dicilpu.

eng: The devil said, I will give you this whole domain and the glory of all these kingdoms. Its been entrusted to me and I can give it to anyone I want.
local: ní ki bí-u yii: «M làá dū mpɔn gbanti kɔkɔ nì abee gbanti nyooti kí tīī-si, kun puee man ní bi dū an wanti kɔkɔ ki tīī, ní ma mun yaa là uŋyee ní ḿ tīī.

eng: Therefore, if you will worship me, it will all be yours.
local: Míǹ puee, a yaa gbāaǹ ki jáam̀ man, sii di làá nīn kíǹ ki yì an kɔkɔ.».

eng: Jesus answered, Its written, You will worship the Lord your God and serve only him. .
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-u yii: «An ŋmàn̄ Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon yii: <Ti-Dindaan a-Nimbɔtiu baba ní án sá á jāam̀, ní kí nīn bí úǹ baba tundin.>».

eng: The devil brought him into Jerusalem and stood him at the highest point of the temple. He said to him, Since you are Gods Son, throw yourself down from here.
local: Míǹ boonee ní Sitaan yōoǹ-u ki cáa cūnnì Jeerusaleem, ní ki cáá-u ki tɔ́kɔ́ǹ kunimbɔtidii yilpu kúnti kunti, ní ki bí-u yii: «A yaa sá Unimbɔti Jipɔɔn, á yùkū kí tìin̄ní tiba kaa làá ŋá-si.

eng: One day Jesus was standing beside Lake Gennesaret when the crowd pressed in around him to hear Gods word.
local: Ŋwiin ŋubɔee, Yeesu cūtì ki yú Geeneesareeti mɔɔgbandi, ní binib dɔ́miń ki múkúǹ u-pu kí pīlifì Unimbɔti bɔti.

eng: James and John, Zebedees sons, were Simons partners and they were amazed too. Jesus said to Simon, Dont be afraid. From now on, you will be fishing for people.
local: ní ki kɔ́ Seebeedee jipɔmbi Yakubu nì Yoowaneesi, bin sá u-jɔncuujutiibee mun. Ama ní Yeesu bí Siimɔn yii: «Taa fàŋkì! Di sá yii, kí yòon̄ díǹ kí nīn cée, a làá nīn kíǹ ki sá un cāabìń binib m-ceeyee ní.».

eng: As soon as they brought the boats to the shore, they left everything and followed Jesus.
local: Niinee ní bi cáań bi-ŋaliŋfii ki yóoǹ dimɔɔgbandi, ní ki yāntì tikɔkɔ ní ki kíǹ ki nóó Yeesu.

eng: Jesus was in one of the towns where there was also a man covered with a skin disease. When he saw Jesus, he fell on his face and begged, Lord, if you want, you can make me clean.
