local: Yeesu nìn bí kitiŋ kiban, ní uninja ubɔ dɔ́miń dinyaŋgbandi píń u-pu. U ká Yeesuee ní ki tātìi dōoǹ dicincibil u-nimbiin u-yigbeel tūū kitiŋ ní ki gbāam̀-u yii: «Ukpil, cáaǹ-mi kun puee m nyí yii a yaa là, a ŋūǹ kí cáaǹ-mi m-wunti ń ŋāŋkì.».

eng: Jesus reached out his hand, touched him, and said, I do want to. Be clean. Instantly, the skin disease left him.
local: Ní Yeesu tāntì u-ŋal ki gbēē-u, ní ki bí-u yii: «M là, Pɔ̄ɔkìm̄!» Niin niin ní dinyaŋgbandi kpáàa wáŋkì uninjee pu.

eng: Jesus ordered him not to tell anyone. Instead, Jesus said, go and show yourself to the priest and make an offering for your cleansing, as Moses instructed. This will be a testimony to them.
local: Ní Yeesu kpáafì-u yii ú taa pée tùkù báà ubɔ, ama ní ki bí-u yii: «Cáá a-ba kí dàkà usaraaŋakpil ú béé yii a-wunti ŋáŋkì ní kí cùú ŋá saraa kii Mooyiisi nín yóoǹ mara uŋyee ní usaraaŋalee ń tùkù báà ŋma yii a pɔ́ɔkì.».

eng: News of him spread even more and huge crowds gathered to listen and to be healed from their illnesses.
local: Niinee, Yeesu yindi mɔ̀tīi gbìtī ki cá, ní kunipaau dòoń ki pílifí u-bɔti kí kpáàa ká bi-weeŋyi cáaǹtam.

eng: But Jesus would withdraw to deserted places for prayer.
local: Ama Yeesu nìn cá u-baba laakin binib kpée ki mèé ŋwimmeem.

eng: One day when Jesus was teaching, Pharisees and legal experts were sitting nearby. They had come from every village in Galilee and Judea, and from Jerusalem. Now the power of the Lord was with Jesus to heal.
local: Ŋwiin ŋubɔee, Yeesu bíi dàkà binib Unimbɔti bɔti. Fariisab nì bimaradakaliibi nìn kā niin. Bi nìn nyánní Galiilee nì Juudee nì Jeerusaleem timbilimmu kɔkɔn ní. Ti-Dindaan pɔŋŋu nìn bí Yeesu pu ki yàntī u cāantí bibunliibi.

eng: Some men were bringing a man who was paralyzed, lying on a cot. They wanted to carry him in and place him before Jesus.
local: U bíi sòoǹee, ní binib biba ŋúbiń uninja ubɔ u sá ufaliŋfaan nlún pu, ki nyàab̀ kí kɔ́ǹ-u Yeesu cee kudiin.

eng: but they couldnt reach him because of the crowd. So they took him up on the roof and lowered him—cot and all—through the roof tiles into the crowded room in front of Jesus.
local: Nniwíin puee, baa béè bi nín làá ŋá pu kí bà Yeesuee. Míǹ puee bi jɔ́m̀ kudii yilpu ki cíl̀, ní ki dū ufaliŋfaanee nì u-lúŋŋu ki dūu dīń dibùndee ni ki kpákatì-u ki bíl̀ Yeesu nimbiin kunipaauee kansikin.

eng: Jesus saw two boats sitting by the lake. The fishermen had gone ashore and were washing their nets.
local: Ní u ká iŋaliŋfi ili yú dimɔɔgbandi, bilaantɔliibi kpákatì ki bíi fíntí bi-laanti.

eng: When Jesus saw their faith, he said, Friend, your sins are forgiven.
local: Yeesu nín ká binib gbanti fōō ki kíí yii u ŋūǹ kí cáaǹ ubunlee, ní u bí ufaliŋfaanee yii: «M-bɔɔ, m dū a-kpitii ki pú-si.».

eng: The legal experts and Pharisees began to mutter among themselves, Who is this who insults God? Only God can forgive sins.
local: Niinee ní bimaradakaliibi nì Fariisabee kíǹ ki bíi dàkafī bi-pɔbiliŋin yii: «Uninja nee sá ŋma ní ki ŋèeǹ nì Unimbɔti míǹ? Ŋma di ŋūǹ kí dū ikpiti kí pɔ̄? See Unimbɔti baba.».

eng: Jesus recognized what they were discussing and responded, Why do you fill your minds with these questions?.
local: Yeesu béè bi nín bíi dàkafī puee, ní ki bí-bi yii: «Ba pu ní ni bíi dàkafī míǹ ni-pɔbiliŋin?.

eng: Which is easier—to say, Your sins are forgiven, or to say, Get up and walk?.
local: Ba di kaa pɔ̄ɔ̄ dilil ní: <A ká a-kpitii pɔ̄tam> di kaa pɔ̄ɔ̄ dilil naaa, <Yīkì kí yūĺ kí nīn còom̄> di kaa pɔ̄ɔ̄ ní?.

eng: But so that you will know that the Human Onehas authority on the earth to forgive sins—Jesus now spoke to the man who was paralyzed,I say to you, get up, take your cot, and go home.
local: Ama ní béé yii man Kinibiki cáá mpɔn kitiŋ pu kí dū unil kpitii kí pɔ̄. Niinee ní u bí ufaliŋfaanee yii: <Sii yīkìm̄ kí yòon̄ a-lúŋŋu kí nīn kùǹ a-dumpu.>».

eng: Right away, the man stood before them, picked up his cot, and went home, praising God.
local: Kpalaayee ní u nyīī ki yíkì samaa nimbiin, ki yōoǹ u-lúŋŋu ŋun pu u bàà dɔ̄ee, ki pàkà Unimbɔti ki kùǹ u-dumpu.

eng: All the people were beside themselves with wonder. Filled with awe, they glorified God, saying, Weve seen unimaginable things today.
local: An yāntì báà ŋma fāŋkì ki bākatì sèeee ki pàkà Unimbɔti, ki lì yii: «Ti ká tibɔcan díǹ!».

eng: Afterward, Jesus went out and saw a tax collector named Levi sitting at a kiosk for collecting taxes. Jesus said to him, Follow me.
local: Míǹ boonee, Yeesu nyáń ki búntì, ní ki ká ufɔtil ubɔ, bi yíì-u Leefii u kā fɔtiku diikun. Ní Yeesu bí-u yii: «Dāan̄ kí nīn nóó-mi!».

eng: Levi got up, left everything behind, and followed him.
local: Ní u yíkì ki yāntì u-wanti kɔkɔ, ní ki lafun táá-u.

eng: Then Levi threw a great banquet for Jesus in his home. A large number of tax collectors and others sat down to eat with them.
local: Ní Leefii ŋá Yeesu tican u-dumpu, ní bifɔtiliibi nì binib biba mun tikpil dɔ́miń ki kútì bi-pu ki kpáfì ki bíi jīǹ tijin.

eng: Jesus boarded one of the boats, the one that belonged to Simon, then asked him to row out a little distance from the shore. Jesus sat down and taught the crowds from the boat.
local: Iŋaliŋfee kansikinee, Siimɔn yì bubɔ, ní Yeesu cūtìi kɔ́ bu-ni ní ki gbāam̀-u ki lī yii, ú sútí buŋalimbu kí wàatī digbándi kí kɔ́ǹ dimɔɔlin fiii ú sòoǹ nì binib bí ŋmā kí gbìl̄. Ní Yeesu kāl̀ buŋalimbun, ki bíi dàkà kunipaauee Unimbɔti bɔti.

eng: The Pharisees and their legal experts grumbled against his disciples. They said, Why do you eat and drink with tax collectors and sinners.
local: Fariisab nì bimaradakaliibi nín ká mimmee, an kaa mɔ́ɔkiǹ-bi, ní bi bālifì Yeesu boonnooliibi yii: «Ba pu ní ni nì bifɔtiliibi nì binikpitib nee kpáfì ki kā ki jīǹ ki nyù míǹ?».

eng: Jesus answered, Healthy people dont need a doctor, but sick people do.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-bi yii: «Bin pɔ̄ɔ̄yee kaa nyàab̀ unyɔɔdaan, bin būǹee di nyàab̀ unyɔɔdaan.

eng: I didnt come to call righteous people but sinners to change their hearts and lives.
local: Maa dɔ́miń kí yíiń deedeedambi, m dɔ́miń kí yíiń binikpitib bí kpántí bi-bimbim ní.».

eng: Some people said to Jesus, The disciples of John fast often and pray frequently. The disciples of the Pharisees do the same, but your disciples are always eating and drinking.
local: Ní bi lī Yeesu yii: «Yoowaneesi boonnooliibi bōob̀ kinyɔkɔ múlmul ki mèé ŋwimmeem, ní Fariisab boonnooliibi mun ŋáań míǹ, ama ní sii boonnooliibi jīǹ ki nyù.».

eng: Jesus replied, You cant make the wedding guests fast while the groom is with them, can you?.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-bi yii: «Ni ŋūǹ kí yàntī unimpucaan cal bɔɔbi ń bóob́ kinyɔkɔ ŋyunti ŋun u dáá bí bi-ceeyeeee?.

eng: The days will come when the groom will be taken from them, and then they will fast.
local: Ama ŋwiin ŋubɔ bí ki dòoń, bi làá dāá yòon̄ unimpucaan cal bi-kansikin. An yuntiŋu ní bi dāa bōob̀ nnyɔmu.».

eng: Then he told them a parable. No one tears a patch from a new garment to patch an old garment. Otherwise, the new garment would be ruined, and the new patch wouldnt match the old garment.
local: Ní Yeesu gíti dū tibɔti nee ki ŋmāntì-bi yii: «Baa gífí dibɔkutipɔndi ŋaakatil kí dūú lìb̄ dikpikil. Bi yaa ŋá mimmee bi gíí ki bíil̀ dibɔkutipɔndee fám dee, ní dijandi din bi gííyee kaa làá ŋmā kí lìb̄ kí kàl̄ dikpikil pu.

eng: Nobody pours new wine into old wineskins. If they did, the new wine would burst the wineskins, the wine would spill, and the wineskins would be ruined.
local: Baa tí dù ndapɔm kí fúuń tilɔkpikilin. Bi yaa ŋá mimmee, see tilɔtee ń pōō ní ndaam ń kpáatíí fá, ní tilɔti ń bīil̀.

eng: Instead, new wine must be put into new wineskins.
local: Ama bi dù ndapɔm kí sìtī tilɔpɔnni ní.

eng: No one who drinks a well-aged wine wants new wine, but says, The well-aged wine is better.
local: Bi yaa nyùn̄ ndakpikim ŋmaniŋmee, ní bi lì yii ndakpikim di ŋān̄, baa ti là ndapɔm.».

eng: When he finished speaking to the crowds, he said to Simon, Row out farther, into the deep water, and drop your nets for a catch.
local: U sòon̄ ki dóòyee, ní ki bí Siimɔn yii: «Sútí buŋalimbu kí cùnnì laakin nnyim nyóòyee, sii nì a-bɔɔbi ń tɔ̄ ni-laanti kí cúú ijɔm.».

eng: Simon replied, Master, weve worked hard all night and caught nothing. But because you say so, Ill drop the nets.
local: Ní Siimɔn bí yii: «Ukpil, ti dū kunyamɔŋu ní ki tɔ́ tilaandi, kaa cúú báà tiba, ama a nín lēe puee, m làá tɔ̄ kulaaŋŋu.».

eng: So they dropped the nets and their catch was so huge that their nets were splitting.
local: Ní bi dūu tɔ́, ní ki cúú ijɔm tikpil, áli bi-laanti bíi tìfī.

eng: They signaled for their partners in the other boat to come and help them. They filled both boats so full that they were about to sink.
local: Ní bi kábitì bi-bɔɔbi bin bí buŋalimbu bun béeyee nee bí dāan̄ kí tūtuǹ-bi bí dáaĺ ijɔm kí nyāntí. Ní bi dɔ́miń ki dáaĺ ki nyántìń ijɔm ki gbéeǹ iŋaliŋfi liitil, ní iŋaliŋfee bíi múl̀ nnyimin.

eng: When Simon Peter saw the catch, he fell at Jesus knees and said, Leave me, Lord, for Im a sinner.
local: Siimɔn Peetroo nín ká mimmee, ní u dɔ́miń ki gbāaǹ Yeesu nimbiin ní ki bí-u yii: «M-Dindaan, sútí kí wàatī-mi, kun puee man sá unikpiti ní.».

eng: Peter and those with him were overcome with amazement because of the number of fish they caught.
local: Di sá yii ijɔm in bi nìn cúúyee wíiŋŋu yāntì tifaandi nìn kɔ́ Siimɔn nì u-bɔɔbi.

eng: One Sabbath, as Jesus was going through the wheat fields, his disciples were picking the heads of wheat, rubbing them in their hands, and eating them.
local: Ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa ŋubɔ dalee, Yeesu nì u-boonnooliibi jītí bilee kpaaŋŋin, ní u-boonnooliibi bíi gífí bilee fiŋyi ki ŋmèem̄ abil ki ŋmóò.

eng: Looking around at them all, he said to the man, Stretch out your hand. So he did and his hand was made healthy.
local: Ní u fātìi kpēē bi-kɔkɔ ki māntì, ní ki bí un ŋal fákée yii: «Tàntī a-ŋal!» ní u tāntì-ŋu, ní u-ŋal fātìi gítìń ŋu-laŋki.

eng: They were furious and began talking with each other about what to do to Jesus.
local: Ní bi fōō mbum, ní ki bíi sòoǹ ki bɔ́ntí bi-kansikin kí béé bi nín làá ŋá Yeesu puee.

eng: During that time, Jesus went out to the mountain to pray, and he prayed to God all night long.
local: Ŋyuntee ní Yeesu jɔ́m̀ dijool diba pu kí mèè Unimbɔti, ní ki dū kunyamɔŋu ki mēē.

eng: At daybreak, he called together his disciples. He chose twelve of them whom he called apostles.
local: Kutaa wúntèe ní u yíiń u-boonnooliibi, ní ki lēē bi-kansikin saalaa nì bili bíǹ ń nīn sá u-kpambaliŋ.

eng: Simon, whom he named Peter; his brother Andrew; James; John; Philip; Bartholomew.
local: Akpambalee yiŋŋi sèé: Siimɔn un u nìn páaǹ diyindi yii Peetrooyee , nì Siimɔn naal Andree, nì Yakubu nì Yoowaneesi nì Fiiliipi nì Bateeleemii.

eng: Matthew; Thomas; James the son of Alphaeus; Simon, who was called a zealot.
local: nì Maatiiu nì Toomasi nì Yakubu un sá Alfee jipɔɔnee, nì Siimɔn un nìn là kí jáǹ Room yabee.

eng: Judas the son of James; and Judas Iscariot, who became a traitor.
local: nì Juuda, Yakubu jipɔɔn, nì Juudasi Isikaariyɔti un dāa dū Yeesu ki nyáfiǹ u-nannanliibee.

eng: Jesus came down from the mountain with them and stood on a large area of level ground. A great company of his disciples and a huge crowd of people from all around Judea and Jerusalem and the area around Tyre and Sidon joined him there.
local: Yeesu nì u-kpambaliŋ kpákatìń ki bāań ki yúl̀ ditantakalin, laakin u-boonnooliibi kupaau nì binifantantiibi tikpil mun nìn yúee. Binib gbantee, biba nìn nyánní Juudee timmu kɔkɔn, ní biba nyánní Jeerusaleem tiŋkin, ní biba mun nyánní Tiir nì Siidɔn, ntimu mun bí dimɔɔl caŋinee.

eng: They came to hear him and to be healed from their diseases, and those bothered by unclean spirits were healed.
local: Binibee nìn dɔ́miń kí pīlifì Yeesu nín sòoǹ Unimbɔti bɔti pueeyaaa, ki là ú cáaǹ-bi mun. Ní bin pu arasiniibi nìn kēe ká dicáaǹdi.

eng: The whole crowd wanted to touch him, because power was going out from him and he was healing everyone.
local: Báà ŋma nìn pée nyàab̀ kí gbēē Yeesu ní, kun puee mpɔn ŋubɔ nìn nyēeń u-ni ki cāantí bi-kɔkɔ.

eng: Some Pharisees said, Why are you breaking the Sabbath law.
local: Fariisab biba ká mimmee, ní ki bí-bi yii: «Ba pu ní ni ŋáań min kòóyee ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa dal?».

eng: Jesus raised his eyes to his disciples and said: Happy are you who are poor, because Gods kingdom is yours.
local: Ní Yeesu kpēē u-boonnooliibi ní ki bí yii: «Nimbi binandambi bí mmɔnneeee, kun puee ni sá Unimbɔti Beelin yab.

eng: Happy are you who hunger now, because you will be satisfied. Happy are you who weep now, because you will laugh.
local: Nimbi bin ŋkùm cáá kɔŋkɔnneeyee bí mmɔnneeee, kun puee ni làá dāá jī kí díkí. Nimbi bin bíi sūǹ kɔŋkɔnneeyee bí mmɔnneeee, kun puee ni làá dāá lā!.

eng: Happy are you when people hate you, reject you, insult you, and condemn your name as evil because of the Human One.
local: «Nimbi bin bi ná, ki jì, ki sìí, ki bíiĺ ni-yiŋŋi man Kinibiki puee bí mmɔnneeee!.

eng: Rejoice when that happens! Leap for joy because you have a great reward in heaven. Their ancestors did the same things to the prophets.
local: Yaadalee, ní nīn fàal̀ ki nyàkantī án nīn mɔ̄ǹ-ni, kun puee ni-paati wīikí yilpu. Míǹ mbaantiim ní bi-naanjab mun nìn sāaǹ Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi.

eng: But how terrible for you who are rich, because you have already received your comfort.
local: Ama nimbi bigaajab, mbusu dɔ̄ ki cí-ni, kun puee nimbi jíń ni-mɔŋŋu doo.

eng: How terrible for you who have plenty now, because you will be hungry. How terrible for you who laugh now, because you will mourn and weep.
local: Nimbi bin bí digbíl poon kɔŋkɔnneeyee, mbusu dɔ̄ ki cí-ni, kun puee ŋkùm làá dāá cúú-ni. Nimbi bin bí tilaal poon kɔŋkɔnneeyee, mbusu dɔ̄ ki cí-ni, kun puee ni làá dāá kɔ́ kunimbaaun kí sɔ̄.

eng: How terrible for you when all speak well of you. Their ancestors did the same things to the false prophets.
local: «Nimbi bin ní duulinya yab pàkà yii ni sá bimɔntiibee, mbusu dɔ̄ ki cí-ni, kun puee míǹ ní bi-naanjab nìn pàkà bin nìn mɔ́ntí yii bi sá Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibee.».

eng: But I say to you who are willing to hear: Love your enemies. Do good to those who hate you.
local: «Ama m tùkù nimbi bin pílifíǹ-mi nee yii, ní nīn néeḿmaan ni-nannanliibi kí nīn ŋáaḿmaan bin ná-nee tiŋan.

eng: Bless those who curse you. Pray for those who mistreat you.
local: Bin bin tòó-ni mbusuee, mèèmaan Unimbɔti ń ŋá-bi tiŋan, kí mèè kí tīī bin sàantí-nee.

eng: If someone slaps you on the cheek, offer the other one as well. If someone takes your coat, dont withhold your shirt either.
local: Ubɔ yaa fáá a-taŋkpanjil diba ntampee, á fàtī din kíǹee kí tīī-u ú fáá. Ubɔ yaa yōoǹ a-kpalibuee, á yàntī ú yòon̄ a-bɔkutiwaaki mun.

eng: Jesus replied, Havent you read what David and his companions did when they were hungry?.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-bi yii: «Naa láá pée kàan̄ ŋkùm nín nìn cúú ubɔti Daafiidi nì u-naacimbi u ŋá pueeee?.

eng: Give to everyone who asks and dont demand your things back from those who take them.
local: Ubɔ yaa mēē-si tibee, á tīī-u. Ubɔ yaa yōoǹ a-wanti, á taa bàlifì-u.

eng: Treat people in the same way that you want them to treat you.
local: Ni nín là bí ŋá min kí tīī-nee, ŋámaan míǹ kí tīī ni-jutiibi.

eng: If you love those who love you, why should you be commended? Even sinners love those who love them.
local: «Ni yaa néeń bin néeń-nee ŋmaniŋmee, ba paati ní ni gíti kā ki cí boon? Bin kaa nyí Unimbɔtee mun pée néeń bin néeń-bee ní.

eng: If you do good to those who do good to you, why should you be commended? Even sinners do that.
local: Ní ni yaa tí ŋáań tiŋan ki tìì bin ŋáań ni mun tiŋanee babee, ba paati ní ni gíti kā ki cí boon? Bin kaa nyí Unimbɔtee mun pée ŋáań tiŋan ki tìì bin ŋáań-bi tiŋanee ní.

eng: If you lend to those from whom you expect repayment, why should you be commended? Even sinners lend to sinners expecting to be paid back in full.
local: Ni yaa tí pìntí bin ni nyí yii bi làá téeǹ-nee ŋmaniŋmee, ba paati ní ni gíti kā ki cí boon? Binikpitib mun pée pìntí bi-jutiibi binikpitib ní ki nyí yii bi làá fàtīí téeǹ-bi bi-nimbiŋmiiku deedee.

eng: Instead, love your enemies, do good, and lend expecting nothing in return. If you do, you will have a great reward. You will be acting the way children of the Most High act, for he is kind to ungrateful and wicked people.
local: Min máaǹ ní ŋée di sá ní nīn néeḿmaan bin ná-nee, kí nīn ŋáań-bi tiŋan, kí nīn pìntímaan kí taa nín̄ dàkafī yii bi làá téeǹ-ni. Míǹ ní ni dāa fòol̄ tipaakpaandi ní ki dāa sá Unimbɔti un bí yilpuee biyaamu, kun puee úǹ ŋáań tiŋan ki tìì bin kaa nyí tiŋanee nì bin sá binikpitibee.

eng: Be compassionate just as your Father is compassionate.
local: Ní nīn cáá tiniti tikpil kii ni-Baa nín cáá tiniti tikpil puee.».

eng: Dont judge, and you wont be judged. Dont condemn, and you wont be condemned. Forgive, and you will be forgiven.
local: «Taa nín̄ sòoǹ a-juti bɔti, ní Unimbɔti mun ń taa láa sòoǹ a mun yati. Taa kpɔ̀ a-juti bɔti, ní Unimbɔti mun ń taa kpɔ̀ a-yati. Dū a-juti yìntitam kí pɔ̄-u, ní Unimbɔti ń dū a mun yìntitam kí pɔ̄-si.

eng: Give, and it will be given to you. A good portion—packed down, firmly shaken, and overflowing—will fall into your lap. The portion you give will determine the portion you receive in return.
local: Tīī a-juti, ní Unimbɔti ń dāá tīī-si mun. Bi làá dāá ŋmàntī ní kí cìŋkì kí tēefì, ní án gbí púm, ní bí dūú sìtī a-lookun. A nín ŋmāntì pu ki tīī a-jutee, míǹ ní Unimbɔti làá dāá ŋmàntī kí tīī si mun.».

eng: Jesus also told them a riddle. A blind person cant lead another blind person, right? Wont they both fall into a ditch?.
local: Ní Yeesu gíti dū tibɔti nee ki ŋmāntì yii: «Ujɔfu ŋūǹ kí dàtì u-jɔfujutaaa? Bi-liitil kaa lítí mboonaaa?.

eng: He broke the Law by going into Gods house and eating the bread of the presence, which only the priests can eat. He also gave some of the bread to his companions.
local: U nìn kɔ́ kunimbɔtidiin ní usaraaŋakpil tīī-bi kpɔnɔb bin bi nìn dūu ŋá Unimbɔti saraayee, ní u nì u-naacimbi ŋmɔ́, ama ti-marau poonee, báà ubɔ kaa máaǹ kí ŋmɔ́ an kpɔnɔb gbanti, see bisaraaŋaliibi baba di ŋmóò-bi.».

eng: Disciples arent greater than their teacher, but whoever is fully prepared will be like their teacher.
local: Utumbifil kaa bí ki jítiǹ u-kpiliu, ama, u yaa bífì tiŋanee, u dāa kpántí kii u-kpiliuee ní.

eng: Why do you see the splinter in your brothers or sisters eye but dont notice the log in your own eye?.
local: «Ba pu ní a wāĺ kimɔmbiki kin kā a-nabiki nimbilinee ní kaa wāĺ didɔjal din kā sii yalinee?.

eng: How can you say to your brother or sister, Brother, Sister, let me take the splinter out of your eye, when you dont see the log in your own eye? You deceive yourselves! First take the log out of your eye, and then you will see clearly to take the splinter out of your brothers or sisters eye.
local: Àá mana ní sii un kaa wāĺ didɔjal din kā a-nimbilinee ŋūǹ kí lì a-nabiki yii: <M-nabiki, yàntī ḿ lēē-si kimɔmbiki a-nimbilin!> Mpoonnyoondaan, lēē didɔjal din kā sii a-ba nimbilinee kí wàatī, kí nín wāl̀ mmɔntiimin kí lēē kimɔmbiki kin kā a-nabiki nimbilinee.».

eng: A good tree doesnt produce bad fruit, nor does a bad tree produce good fruit.
local: «Busumɔntiibu kaa lòoǹ asubikpiti, míǹ ní busukpitibu mun kaa lòoǹ asubimɔnti.

eng: Each tree is known by its own fruit. People dont gather figs from thorny plants, nor do they pick grapes from prickly bushes.
local: Busubu kamaa bil pu ní bi béetí-bu. Baa kìtī kɔŋkib abɔjanaakun pu, kaa tí tòó atuun kikuŋkumbɔŋ pu.

eng: A good person produces good from the good treasury of the inner self, while an evil person produces evil from the evil treasury of the inner self. The inner self overflows with words that are spoken.
local: Unimɔnti tūǹ atummɔnti ní, kun puee u-pɔbil poon ŋān̄, ní unikpiti tūǹ atuŋkpiti, kun puee u-pɔbil poon kaa ŋān̄. Di sá yii tin gbí unil pɔbilinee, tíǹ ní u-nyɔkɔ sòoǹ.».

eng: Why do you call me Lord, Lord and dont do what I say?.
local: «Ba pu ní ni yíì-mi <ti-Dindaan, ti-Dindaan> ní kaa ŋáań min m bí yii ní ŋée ní?.

eng: Ill show what its like when someone comes to me, hears my words, and puts them into practice.
local: M làá dàkà-ni bin dòoń m-cee ki pílifí m-bɔti ki kpáafí-tee nín náań nì kuŋyee.

eng: Its like a person building a house by digging deep and laying the foundation on bedrock. When the flood came, the rising water smashed against that house, but the water couldnt shake the house because it was well built.
local: Bi náań nì unil un di làá mā kudii, ki gbìn̄ kitiŋ an nyóò, ki dū kudii ki máń ki tɔ́kɔǹ ditaŋkpal puee ní. Utaakpaan dɔ́miń ki nìn̄, ní mbun gbí ki léeǹ kudiiyee pu, ama kuaa cīŋkì, kun puee ku tɔ́kɔ́ ditaŋkpal pu.

eng: But those who dont put into practice what they hear are like a person who built a house without a foundation. The floodwater smashed against it and it collapsed instantly. It was completely destroyed.
local: Ama bin di pílifí m-bɔti nee, kaa kpáafí-tee, náań nì unil un làá mā kudii, kaa gbìn̄ kitiŋ ní ki dū kudiiyee ki máń ki tɔ́kɔǹ kitiŋ pu fámee ní. Utaa nìn̄ ní nnyiŋgbeen dɔ́miń ki léeǹ kudiiyee pu: kpalaayee, ku lítì ní ki nàn̄ dúsi dusi.».

eng: Then he said to them, The Human Oneis Lord of the Sabbath.
local: Ní Yeesu gíti bí-bi yii: «Man Kinibiki di sá ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa dindaan.».

eng: On another Sabbath, Jesus entered a synagogue to teach. A man was there whose right hand was withered.
local: Ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa ŋubɔ mun dalee, Yeesu fātìi tí cūtì kukpafidiin, ní ki bíi dàkà binib Unimbɔti bɔti. Kijapaai kiba nìn bí niin, u-ŋaŋgiil fáká.

eng: The legal experts and the Pharisees were watching him closely to see if he would heal on the Sabbath. They were looking for a reason to bring charges against him.
local: Bimaradakaliibi nì Fariisab nìn bí ki cɔ́ŋkí Yeesu kí kpèè yii u làá cáaǹ ubɔ ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa dalaaa, waa làá cáaǹ, kun puee bi nìn bíi nyàab̀ nnaakpal kí dūú ŋá-u tibɔti.

eng: Jesus knew their thoughts, so he said to the man with the withered hand, Get up and stand in front of everyone. He got up and stood there.
local: Ama Yeesu nyí bi-landɔkɔi, ní ki bí un ŋal fákée yii: «Yīkì kí sīĺ binib kansikin,» ní u yáatìi yúl̀ bi-kansikin.

eng: Jesus said to the legal experts and Pharisees, Heres a question for you: Is it legal on the Sabbath to do good or to do evil, to save life or to destroy it.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-bi yii: «M bàlifī-ni, ti-marau lī yii ba ní? Tí ŋá tiŋan ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa dalaaa, tikpitil ní? Tí fīī unilaaa, tí kpɔ̀-u ní?».

eng: After Jesus finished presenting all his words among the people, he entered Capernaum.
local: Yeesu nín sòon̄ nì samaa míǹ ki dóòyee, ní u búntì Kapeernayum.

eng: When the centurions friends returned to his house, they found the servant restored to health.
local: Ní bin usoojakpilee túnnée fātìi nín gítí ki bàn̄ mmɔŋki, ki ká utɔntɔnlee pɔ́ɔkì.

eng: A little later Jesus went to a city called Nain. His disciples and a great crowd traveled with him.
local: Boonee ní Yeesu búntì Nayiin tiŋkin, ní u-boonnooliibi nì kunipaau mun nóó-u ki cūtì.

eng: As he approached the city gate, a dead man was being carried out. He was his mothers only son, and she was a widow. A large crowd from the city was with her.
local: Bi tóob́ kí bà kitiŋ gbanti punyɔkɔkee, kpalaayee binib biba cáǹ utaŋkpiil ubɔ kuceeun kí síb́. An nìn sá ukpiipu ubɔ jipɔmbaabil ní, ní kitiŋee nib kupaau nìn cēentí ukpiipu gbanti.

eng: When he saw her, the Lord had compassion for her and said, Dont cry.
local: Ti-Dindaan nín ká-uee, ní u-bɔti cúú-u falaa, ní u bí-u yii: «Taa ti sɔ̄!».

eng: He stepped forward and touched the stretcher on which the dead man was being carried. Those carrying him stood still. Jesus said, Young man, I say to you, get up.
local: ní ki sútìń ki dāatì u-ŋal ki gbēē nlundɔ, ní bin tú utaŋkpiilee síl̀. Ní Yeesu bí yii: «Unaacimpɔɔn, m pú-si kinyɔkɔ yii á yīkì!».

eng: The dead man sat up and began to speak, and Jesus gave him to his mother.
local: Ní utaŋkpiilee fīkitì ní ki yáatìi kāl̀ ní ki bíi sòoǹ. Ní Yeesu dū-u ki tīī u-na.

eng: Awestruck, everyone praised God. A great prophet has appeared among us, they said. God has come to help his people.
local: Niinee, tifaandi kɔ́ báà ŋma, ní bi bíi pàkà Unimbɔti ki lì yii: «Unimbɔti bɔnaatikpaaŋwu ubɔ nyáń kitiŋ pu, Unimbɔti dɔ́miń kí jòò u-nibul dee.».

eng: This news about Jesus spread throughout Judea and the surrounding region.
local: Ní Yeesu bɔti gbanti gbītì Juudee timmu kɔkɔn, ki kpáaǹ ikpaanceeti mun.

eng: Johns disciples informed him about all these things. John called two of his disciples.
local: Yoowaneesi boonnooliibi cūtì ki máafì-u Yeesu nín ŋáaǹ tiŋyee kɔkɔ, ní Yoowaneesi yíiń bi-kansikin bili.

eng: and sent them to the Lord. They were to ask him, Are you the one who is coming, or should we look for someone else.
local: ní ki dū-bi ki tú ti-Dindaan Yeesu cee, bí bàlifì-u yii: «Sii di sá un ti nyí yii u dòońeeyaaa, tí nīn kā ki cí ubɔ ní?».

eng: A centurion had a servant who was very important to him, but the servant was ill and about to die.
local: Room soojakpiliu ubɔ nìn bí niin ki cáá utɔntɔnli un u néeń tikpilee, u būǹ ki làá kpú.

eng: When they reached Jesus, they said, John the Baptist sent us to you. He asks, Are you the one who is coming, or should we look for someone else.
local: Bi bāań Yeesu ceeyee, ní ki bí-u yii: «Yoowaneesi un fùul̀ binib Unimbɔti nyimee di túnní-ti tí bàlifì-si yii sii di sá un ti nyí yii u dòońeeyaaa, tí nīn kā ki cí ubɔ ní?».

eng: Right then, Jesus healed many of their diseases, illnesses, and evil spirits, and he gave sight to a number of blind people.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun bi bāań Yeesu ceeyee, u bíi cāantí bibunliibi tikpil iween kupaau, ki jì arasiniibi binib pu, ki ŋúbití bijɔfub nimbiliŋ.

eng: Then he replied to Johns disciples, Go, report to John what you have seen and heard. Those who were blind are able to see. Those who were crippled now walk. People with skin diseases are cleansed. Those who were deaf now hear. Those who were dead are raised up. And good news is preached to the poor.
local: Ní u fātìi kíí bin Yoowaneesi túnnée yii: «Nín̄ cámaan kí tùkù Yoowaneesi tin ni kée nì tin ni gbìl̄ee: bijɔfub nimbiliŋ ŋúbití, bideembi còom̄, binyaŋgbantiibi pɔ́ɔkí, bitafakpaafub kíǹ ki gbìl̀, bitaŋkpiibi fìkitī, ní biwɔfib gbìl̀ tibɔmɔntiil.

eng: Happy is anyone who doesnt stumble along the way because of me.
local: Un fōō-mi ki kíí kaa gùgufēe bí mmɔnneeee!».

eng: After Johns messengers were gone, Jesus spoke to the crowds about John. What did you go out into the wilderness to see? A stalk blowing in the wind?.
local: Yoowaneesi boonnooliibee nín gítée, ní Yeesu bíi sòoǹ nì kunipaau Yoowaneesi pu yii: «Ni cūtì kuteeun kí kpèè Yoowaneesee, dinicil dilal ní u sá ní? U-bɔti kpántí kii kubuŋu nín fīintí dikaliŋgbaandi pueeee? Aayee.

eng: What did you go out to see? A man dressed up in refined clothes? Look, those who dress in fashionable clothes and live in luxury are in royal palaces.
local: Kin ba ní ni cūtìí kpèè ní? Uninja ubɔ un pēē abɔkutimɔnteeee? Aayee. Bin pēē agaajabɔkuti ki bí mmɔntiiminee bí bibɔtiibi dumpui ní.

eng: What did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and more than a prophet.
local: Kin ba pu ní ni baasii cūtì ki kpēē Yoowaneesi ní? Ni cūtìí kpèè-u yii u nín sá Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliuee níii? Tiŋman ní m sòoǹ nì ni, un ni ká niinee jítiǹ Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi.

eng: He is the one of whom its written: Look, Im sending my messenger before you, who will prepare your way before you .
local: Kun puee Yoowaneesi pu ní Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon sòon̄ yii: Unimbɔti bí yii: <M làá tɔ̄ń m-tuuŋŋu ŋú lìntīń a-nyɔkɔpu kí ŋānkì-si nsan.>».

eng: I tell you that no greater human being has ever been born than John. Yet whoever is least in Gods kingdom is greater than he.
local: Ní Yeesu gíti bí yii: «Ibaamɔn ní m sòoǹ, bin kɔkɔ unimpu màl̄ee kansikinee, ubɔ kaa bí ki sá ukpaan ki bàn̄ Yoowaneesi. Ama un sá kiwaai Unimbɔti Beelinee jítiǹ Yoowaneesi.».

eng: Everyone who heard this, including the tax collectors, acknowledged Gods justice because they had been baptized by John.
local: Dinikitil kɔkɔ, ki kpáaǹ bifɔtiliibi gba, pílifiǹ Yoowaneesi ní ki béè yii Unimbɔti sá deedeedaan ní ki yāntì Yoowaneesi fùl̄-bi Unimbɔti nyim.

eng: When the centurion heard about Jesus, he sent some Jewish elders to Jesus to ask him to come and heal his servant.
local: Usoojakpilee nín gbìl̄ bi sòoǹ Yeesu puee, ní u tú Juuda yab tindaŋkpilib biba bí cùú gbàam̀ Yeesu ú dāá cáaǹ u-tɔntɔnliu.

eng: But the Pharisees and legal experts rejected Gods will for themselves because they hadnt been baptized by John.
local: Ama ní Fariisab nì bimaradakaliibi yíì min Unimbɔti bàà là bí ŋée, kaa cāntì Yoowaneesi fùl̄-bi Unimbɔti nyim.

eng: To what will I compare the people of this generation? Jesus asked. What are they like?.
local: Ní Yeesu gíti bí yii: «Ba ní m làá dū kí ŋmàntīǹ nimbi waanee nib dee? Ni náań nì ba deeyaaa?.

eng: They are like children sitting in the marketplace calling out to each other, We played the flute for you and you didnt dance. We sang a funeral song and you didnt cry.
local: Ni náań nì mbiyaamu mun kā mmɔŋki ki tátí mu-jutiibi pu yii: <Ti búń-ni awul, naa ŋɔ́ń, ní ti gāā-ni ikpiisilan, naa súń.>.

eng: John the Baptist came neither eating bread nor drinking wine, and you say, He has a demon.
local: Di sá Yoowaneesi un fùul̀ binib Unimbɔti nyimee dɔ́miń, kaa jīǹaaa, kaa nyù ndaam, ní naa fōō-u ní ki bí yii arasinii di bí u-pu.

eng: Yet the Human Onecame eating and drinking, and you say, Look, a glutton and a drunk, a friend of tax collectors and sinners.
local: Ní man Kinibiki dɔ́miń ki jīǹ ki nyù ní ni bí yii: <Kpèèmaan, uninja nee, úǹ sá upoofu nì udanyɔl ní ki bɔ́ɔtí bifɔtiliibi nì binikpitib bin kɔkɔ kíǹee.>».

eng: But wisdom is proved to be right by all her descendants.
local: Ama bin fōō Unimbɔti ciiŋyi ki kííyee dàkà yii i sá deedee.

eng: One of the Pharisees invited Jesus to eat with him. After he entered the Pharisees home, he took his place at the table.
local: Dalbee ní Fariisa ubɔ yíiń Yeesu yii ú dāan̄ u-dumpu bí jī, ní Yeesu cūtì ní ki kāl̀ bí jī.

eng: Meanwhile, a woman from the city, a sinner, discovered that Jesus was dining in the Pharisees house. She brought perfumed oil in a vase made of alabaster.
local: Kinimpulaakpaai kiba nìn bí kitiŋ gbantin. U nín gbìl̄ yii Yeesu bí Fariisee dumpu ki jīǹee ní u yōoǹní talaalu un kā ditaŋkpal kpalbaunee.

eng: Standing behind him at his feet and crying, she began to wet his feet with her tears. She wiped them with her hair, kissed them, and poured the oil on them.
local: U bāańee ní ki yúl̀ Yeesu boon u-taaŋi cee ní ki bíi sūǹ ní nnyinnyim sìtī Yeesu taaŋi pu, ní u cáá u-yikpiti ki pítí nnyinnyim ki móotí Yeesu taaŋi ní ki dū talaaluee ki sītì ŋi-pu.

eng: When the Pharisee who had invited Jesus saw what was happening, he said to himself, If this man were a prophet, he would know what kind of woman is touching him. He would know that she is a sinner.
local: Ufariisaja un nìn yíiń Yeesu bí jēe nín ká mimmee ní u bíi dàkafī u-pɔbilin yii: «Kijapaai nee yaa bàà lafun sá Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu, u bàà béetí unimpu un gbèetī-u nee nín sá dinimpucil diŋyee, kun puee u sá kilaakpaai ní.».

eng: When they came to Jesus, they earnestly pleaded with Jesus. He deserves to have you do this for him, they said.
local: Bi bāań ki ká Yeesu, ní ki gbāam̀-u mmɔntiimin ki lī yii: «Usoojakpil gbanti pée máaǹ á tūtuǹ-u ní.

eng: Jesus replied, Simon, I have something to say to you. Teacher, speak, he said.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-u yii: «Siimɔn, m cáá tiba ki làá tùkù-si.» Ní Siimɔn kíí-u yii: «Ukpil, sòoǹ.».

eng: A certain lender had two debtors. One owed enough money to pay five hundred people for a days work.The other owed enough money for fifty.
local: Ní Yeesu bí yii: «Kijapaai kiba nìn pīnnì binib bili animbil. Ubɔ nìn cáá-u dipɔndi animbil jitɔmbi alaataa aŋmoo, ní un kíǹee imuŋku ili nì saalaa.

eng: When they couldnt pay, the lender forgave the debts of them both. Which of them will love him more.
local: Bi-liitilee kansikin, báà ubɔ kaa ŋūǹ kí pà-u, ní u ŋá bi-liitil tiŋan ki dū bi-pɔndee ki pú-bi. Bi-kansikinee, ŋma di làá nīn néeń un dū dipɔndi ki pú-bee tikpil?».

eng: Simon replied, I suppose the one who had the largest debt canceled. Jesus said, You have judged correctly.
local: Ní Siimɔn bí yii: «M pàkā yii un pɔndi wīikí bi ŋá-u tiŋanee di làá nīn néeń-u.» Ní Yeesu bí-u yii: «A lī an kāl̀.».

eng: Jesus turned to the woman and said to Simon, Do you see this woman? When I entered your home, you didnt give me water for my feet, but she wet my feet with tears and wiped them with her hair.
local: Ní Yeesu fātì unimpuee watil ní ki bí Siimɔn yii: «Kéè, a ká unimpu neeyaa? M nín kóoń a-dumpuee, aa pú-mi nnyim m fíntì m-taaŋi. Ama úǹ dū u-nyinnyim ní ki sītì m-taaŋi pu ní ki dū u-yikpiti ki pítì-ŋi.

eng: You didnt greet me with a kiss, but she hasnt stopped kissing my feet since I came in.
local: M nín páaǹnée, aa lī-mi yii: <Si nì nsan.> Ama, doooo m nín kɔ́nnée, unimpu nee pée bí ki móotí m-taaŋi ki dàkà u nín néeń-mi puee.

eng: You didnt anoint my head with oil, but she has poured perfumed oil on my feet.
local: Aa yāaǹ m-yil pu ŋkpam mun kpaŋkpam, ama úǹ sītì m-taaŋi pu talaalu.

eng: This is why I tell you that her many sins have been forgiven; so she has shown great love. The one who is forgiven little loves little.
local: An pu ní m lī-si yii unimpu nee dàkā dineendi tikpil m-pu, kun puee m dū u-kpitii kupaau ki pú-u. Un kpitii di yaa bí fiii ní bi dūu pú-uee, úǹ dàkà dineendi fiii ní.».

eng: Then Jesus said to her, Your sins are forgiven.
local: Ní Yeesu bí unimpuee yii: «M dū a-kpitii ki pú-si.».

eng: The other table guests began to say among themselves, Who is this person that even forgives sins.
local: Bin bin nìn kpáfìi kā ki jīǹee nìn bíi dàkafī bi-pɔbiliŋin yii: «Uninja nee sá ba yɔu ní ki tin pūǹ binib kpitii na?».

eng: He loves our people and he built our synagogue for us.
local: kun puee u néeń timbi Juuda yab. Úǹ di máń ti-kpafidiiku.».

eng: Jesus said to the woman, Your faith has saved you. Go in peace.
local: Ama ní Yeesu tùkū unimpuee yii: «A-fookiiu fīī-si, nín̄ cá nì ŋgbansɔŋfi.».

eng: Jesus went with them. He had almost reached the house when the centurion sent friends to say to Jesus, Lord, dont be bothered. I dont deserve to have you come under my roof.
local: Bi lī mimmee, ní Yeesu nóó-bi ki búntì. Bi tóob́ mmɔŋkee, ní usoojakpilee túnní u-bɔɔbi bí tùkù Yeesu yii: «Ukpil, taa sàaǹ a-ba, maa máaǹ á kóoń m-dumpu.

eng: In fact, I didnt even consider myself worthy to come to you. Just say the word and my servant will be healed.
local: An pu ní m dākafì yii man kaa kpántì kí dāan̄ a-cee. Á pée lì dinyɔɔbumbaantiil, m-tɔntɔnliu làá pɔ̄ɔkì.

eng: Im also a man appointed under authority, with soldiers under me. I say to one, Go, and he goes, and to another, Come, and he comes. I say to my servant, Do this, and the servant does it.
local: Di sá, man cáá bikpilib bin taapu m bée, ní ki cáá soojab bin mun bí man taapuee. M ŋūǹ kí tùkù ubɔ yii: <Cù doo,> ní ú cù, ki ŋūǹ kí tùkù ubɔ mun yii: <Dāan̄ doo,> ní ú dāan̄, ní kí bí m-tɔntɔnliu una yii: <Ŋá yéè,> ní ú ŋá.».

eng: When Jesus heard these words, he was impressed with the centurion. He turned to the crowd following him and said, I tell you, even in Israel I havent found faith like this.
local: Yeesu nín gbìl̄ mimmee, an ŋá-u bitii ní ki mɔ́ɔkiǹ-u, ní u fātì ki bí kunipaau kun nóó-uee yii: «Ibaamɔn ní m sòoǹ, maa láá pée ká ubɔ cáá fookii yéè Isirayeel yab kansikin gba.».

eng: Soon afterward, Jesus traveled through the cities and villages, preaching and proclaiming the good news of Gods kingdom. The Twelve were with him.
local: Boonee, Yeesu nìn kíǹ ki yìiǹ ntiŋkpaamun nì ntiŋwaamun ní ki sòoǹ tibɔmɔntiil tin sá Unimbɔti Beel bɔtee, ní u-boonnooliibi saalaa nì bilee táá-u.

eng: He said, You have been given the mysteries of Gods kingdom, but these mysteries come to everyone else in parables so that when they see, they cant see, and when they hear, they cant understand .
local: Ní u kíí-bi yii: «Unimbɔti yāntì nimbi béè u-Beel bɔti tin bí dibalinee taapu, ama bin kíǹee, án làá nīn sá bíǹ tibɔŋmantikaal ní, án nín làá ŋá pu bi-nimbiliŋ ń nīn kpítì bí taa nín̄ wāĺee, ní bí nīn pílifí kí taa nín̄ gbìl̀ee.».

eng: The parable means this: The seed is Gods word.
local: «M-bɔŋmantikaatee taapu sɔ̀. Ŋwamboolee di sá Unimbɔti nyɔɔbundi.

eng: The seed on the path are those who hear, but then the devil comes and steals the word from their hearts so that they wont believe and be saved.
local: Binib biba náań nì iwambool in lítì disaŋkaŋkpilee ní. Binibee gbìl̀ Unimbɔti bɔti ní Sitaan ń dāá cúú lēē bi-pɔbiliŋin bí taa fōō kí kíí kí ká difiil.

eng: The seed on the rock are those who receive the word joyfully when they hear it, but they have no root. They believe for a while but fall away when they are tempted.
local: Ní biba mun náań nì in lítì ataŋkpee poonee. Bíǹ gbìl̀ Unimbɔti bɔti ní ki fòol̄ ki kíikí nì dipɔɔpindi, ama ní kaa cáá inyaan kí báań kí sīĺ. Ŋyunti fiii ní bi dù bi-mákal kí páań Unimbɔti pu. Dinimbinaamandi yaa bāań-bee, ní bí lítí.

eng: As for the seed that fell among thorny plants, these are the ones who, as they go about their lives, are choked by the concerns, riches, and pleasures of life, and their fruit never matures.
local: Iwambool in lítì ikuŋkuminee náań nì bin gbìl̀ Unimbɔti bɔti ama ní ki yàntī ilandɔkɔ nì tigaajati nì duulinya mɔŋŋu jīǹ mpaan bi-ni, ní baa ti ŋūǹ kí lòoǹ abil án bēe.

eng: The seed that fell on good soil are those who hear the word and commit themselves to it with a good and upright heart. Through their resolve, they bear fruit.
local: Iwambool in lítì kitimmɔntiinee náań nì bin gbìl̀ Unimbɔti bɔti nì dipɔɔmɔntiil ki fòol̄-tì ki kíikí ki kpáafí-tì bi-pɔbiliŋ poon tiŋan, ki pɔ̄ɔntí mmɔntiimin kí lòoǹ abilee.».

eng: No one lights a lamp and then covers it with a bowl or puts it under a bed. Instead, they put it on top of a lampstand so that those who enter can see the light.
local: Ní Yeesu gíti bí-bi yii: «Baa tùutī fitila kí dū dibool kí cíkiń u-pu, kaa tí dù-u kí síiń kilambaŋ taapu, ama bi dù fitila kí tɔ́kɔń kifaŋkɔlii pu ní, ní án nīn wāĺ bin kɔkɔ kóòńee.

eng: Nothing is hidden that wont be exposed. Nor is anything concealed that wont be made known and brought to the light.
local: Tin kɔkɔ bí dibalin binib kaa wāĺee làá dāá bìitī, ní tin kɔkɔ bí dibalin binib kaa nyée làá dāá nyā mpaampiin pu bí béé.

eng: Therefore, listen carefully. Those who have will receive more, but as for those who dont have, even what they seem to have will be taken away from them.
local: «Ní kpèè tiŋan min ní ni pílifí tibɔtee. Di sá un yaa cáá tibee dāa fòol̄ kí kūtì, ní un yaa kaa cááyee, Unimbɔti dāa fòol̄ fiii un u bàà nyí yii u cááyee ní!».

eng: Jesus mother and brothers came to him but were unable to reach him because of the crowd.
local: Yeesu na nì u-naalib dɔ́miń kí ká-u, ama kunipaau puee baa ŋúń ki bàn̄ u-cee.

eng: along with some women who had been healed of evil spirits and sicknesses. Among them were Mary Magdalene.
local: Ní binimpoobi biba bin pu u nìn jìn̄ arasiniibee nì bin u nìn cáaǹ iweenee mun nóó-u. Binimpoobee biba yiŋŋi sèé: Maarii un nyánní Magidalaa tiŋkin u jìn̄ arasiniibi biluli u-puee.

eng: Someone told him, Your mother and brothers are standing outside, wanting to see you.
local: Ní bi tùkū Yeesu yii: «A-na nì a-naalib yú mmɔŋki ki là kí ká-si.».

eng: He replied, My mother and brothers are those who listen to Gods word and do it.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-bi yii: «Bin pílifí Unimbɔti bɔti ki kpáafí-tèe di sá m-na nì m-naalib.».

eng: One day Jesus and his disciples boarded a boat. He said to them, Lets cross over to the other side of the lake. So they set sail.
local: Ŋwiin ŋubɔee, Yeesu nì u-boonnooliibi kɔ́ buŋalimbun, ní u bí-bi yii: «Tí dùtīmaan lapu pu mɔɔgbandi», ní bi búntì.

eng: While they were sailing, he fell asleep. Gale-force winds swept down on the lake. The boat was filling up with water and they were in danger.
local: Bi bíi ŋàal̀ kí dùtēe, ní Yeesu dūu gèen̄. Kpalaayee, kubuŋkpaaŋŋu kubɔ dɔ́miń ki bíi jītí dimɔɔl pu, ní nnyim bíi gbéè buŋalimbu. Niinee animbil mántì.

eng: So they went and woke Jesus, shouting, Master, Master, were going to drown! But he got up and gave orders to the wind and the violent waves. The storm died down and it was calm.
local: ní u-boonnooliibi sútì ki pìl̄-u ní ki fīntì-u, ní ki bí-u yii: «Ukpil, ukpil, ti kpíì ní!» Yeesu fīntèe, ní ki kpātì kubuŋu nì nnyim pu. Niinee ní kubuŋu nì tinyiŋgban gbēeǹ ki ŋmíl̀ fɔ́ŋfɔŋ díkí diki.

eng: He said to his disciples, Where is your faith? Filled with awe and wonder, they said to each other, Who is this? He commands even the winds and the water, and they obey him.
local: ní u bālifì u-boonnooliibi yii: «Ni-fookiiu bí la?» Tifaandi kɔ́-bi, ní bi bākatì ki bàlifī tɔb yii: «Kijapaai nee nín sá ŋma ní ki sòoǹ nì kubuŋu nì nnyim gbìliǹ-u míǹ nan dee?».

eng: Jesus and his disciples sailed to the Gerasenes land, which is across the lake from Galilee.
local: Ní bi dūtì dimɔɔl ki bàn̄ Geeraseen tiŋki lapu mɔɔgbandi din túĺ Galiileeyee.

eng: As soon as Jesus got out of the boat, a certain man met him. The man was from the city and was possessed by demons. For a long time, he had lived among the tombs, naked and homeless.
local: Yeesu bíi kpákatí buŋalimbunee kpalaayee kitiŋee waatiu ubɔ dɔ́miń ki túŋkìń-u. Arasiniibi biba di nìn kā u-pu, ní u còom̄ dicancaŋgbeel kaa nìn gíti kóò mmɔŋki an yúntì, see kuceeun.

eng: When he saw Jesus, he shrieked and fell down before him. Then he shouted, What have you to do with me, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? I beg you, dont torture me.
local: U nín ká Yeesuee, ní u fáá mbiil ní ki lítì u-taaŋi taapu ki téeń mpɔn yii: «Yeesu Unimbɔti un bí yilpuee Jipɔɔn, a nyàab̀-mi nì tilati ní? M gbàam̄-si, taa sàaǹ-mi.».

eng: He said this because Jesus had already commanded the unclean spirit to come out of the man. Many times it had taken possession of him, so he would be bound with leg irons and chains and placed under guard. But he would break his restraints, and the demon would force him into the wilderness.
local: Kun pu uwaatee nìn lī mimmee di sá Yeesu nìn kíĺ ki bíi kpàtī arasiniiyee pu yii: «Á nyā uninja nee ni!» Arasinii gbantee nìn yíkí uninjee pu ní múlmul, ní báà bi yaa bàà dū ikusaka ki bóob́ u-taaŋi nì u-ŋalii kí kàan̄-u sooooyaaa, ní ú tìfì-i, ní arasiniiyee ń cáá-u kí cùnnì kuteeun.

eng: Joanna , Susanna, and many others who provided for them out of their resources.
local: nì Kusa un nìn kpèé ubɔti Eeroodi gaajatee nimpuu Jaani, nì Susana, nì binimpoobi tikpil bin nìn tìì tin bi cááyee ki tútúǹ Yeesu nì u-boonnooliibee.

eng: Jesus asked him, What is your name? Legion, he replied, because many demons had entered him.
local: Ní Yeesu bālifì-u yii: «Bi yíì-si yii ba?» Ní u bí yii: «Bi yíì-mi yii Kupaau.» Kun pu u lī mimmee di sá yii arasiniibee nìn kā u-pu ki wīikí tikpil.

eng: They pleaded with him not to order them to go back into the abyss.
local: Ní bi gbāam̀ Yeesu yii ú taa jà-bi kí kɔ́ǹ ditafadaaldi laŋki.

eng: A large herd of pigs was feeding on the hillside. The demons begged Jesus to let them go into the pigs. Jesus gave them permission.
local: Digbeetikitil diba nìn yú dijool pu niin ki jīǹ, ní bi gbāam̀ Yeesu ú pɔ̄-bi kinyɔkɔ bí cùú kɔ́ igbeetee ni, ní Yeesu pú-bi kinyɔkɔ.

eng: and the demons left the man and entered the pigs. The herd rushed down the cliff into the lake and drowned.
local: Niinee arasiniibee nyáń uninjee ni ní ki cūtìi kɔ́ igbeetee ni, ní digbeetikitilee sáǹ ki wúntì ki jéetì dimɔɔlin ki kpíì.

eng: When those who tended the pigs saw what happened, they ran away and told the story in the city and in the countryside.
local: Bin kpàá igbeetee nín ká mimmee, ní bi sáǹ ki cūtìi tùkū kitiŋin nì ikaan kɔkɔ.

eng: People came to see what had happened. They came to Jesus and found the man from whom the demons had gone. He was sitting at Jesus feet, fully dressed and completely sane. They were filled with awe.
local: Ní binib nyánní kí dāá kpèè tin di ŋée. Bi bāań Yeesu ceeyee, ní ki ká uninja un pu u jìn̄ arasiniibee pɔ́ɔkì ki kā u-cee ki pēē tiwan ki yì u-nimbiliŋ. Bi ká-uee, ní tifaandi kɔ́-bi.

eng: Those people who had actually seen what had happened told them how the demon-possessed man had been delivered.
local: Bin ká tin jítèe bíi máafí bin kíǹee an nín ŋá pu ní arasiniibi nyáń uninjee ni ní u pɔ́ɔkèe.

eng: Then everyone gathered from the region of the Gerasenes asked Jesus to leave their area because they were overcome with fear. So he got into the boat and returned across the lake.
local: Niinee ní Geeraseen tiŋkin yab kɔkɔ gbāam̀ Yeesu yii ú yīkì bi-tiŋkin, kun puee bi fāŋkì tikpil. Yeesu kɔ́ buŋalimbun kí búntée.

eng: The man from whom the demons had gone begged to come along with Jesus as one of his disciples. Jesus sent him away, saying.
local: ní uninja un pu u jìn̄ arasiniibee gbāam̀-u yii ú yàntī ú nīn bí u-cee.

eng: Return home and tell the story of what God has done for you. So he went throughout the city proclaiming what Jesus had done for him.
local: Ní Yeesu bí yii: «Nín̄ kùǹ a-dumpu kí tin māafì a-yab Unimbɔti nín ŋá min kɔkɔ ki tīī-see.» Ní kijapaayee lafun nín kùǹ, ki bàn̄ ki máafì kitiŋ kɔkɔ dituŋkpaandi din Yeesu túń ki tīī-uee.

eng: When a great crowd was gathering and people were coming to Jesus from one city after another, he spoke to them in a parable.
local: Ntimu kamaa poon nib nìn dòoń Yeesu cee, ní bi-kɔkɔ dūu kpáfì ki ŋá dinikitikpaandi, ní Yeesu dū tibɔŋmantikaal nee ki ŋmāntì-bi yii.

eng: When Jesus returned, the crowd welcomed him, for they had been waiting for him.
local: Yeesu nín fātìi dūtì dimɔɔgbandee, dinikitikpaandi nìn kā ki cí-u, kí fōō-u.

eng: A man named Jairus, who was a synagogue leader, came and fell at Jesus feet. He pleaded with Jesus to come to his house.
local: Niinee ní uninja ubɔ dɔ́miń u-cee, bi yíì-u yii Jayirusi, u sá kukpafidii kpiliu. U dɔ́miń ki gbāaǹ Yeesu nimbiin ní ki gbāam̀-u yii ú dāan̄ bí cù u-dumpu.

eng: because his only daughter, a twelve-year-old, was dying. As Jesus moved forward, he faced smothering crowds.
local: kun puee u-sapɔmbaabil bíi kúù. U nìn cáá abin saalaa nì ali nyɔkɔm. Ŋyunti ŋun Yeesu kíĺ nsan ki cá ukpilee dumpuee, binib nìn bīī u-pu bɔ̀mmm.

eng: A woman was there who had been bleeding for twelve years. She had spent her entire livelihood on doctors, but no one could heal her.
local: Unimpu ubɔ nìn bí ki fùul̀ ŋkpin an kaa gúǹ áli abin saalaa nì ali. U nìn bíil̀ u-nimbiliŋ kɔkɔ binyɔɔdambi cee, báà ubɔ mun kaa ŋúń ki cáaǹ-u.

eng: She came up behind him and touched the hem of his clothes, and at once her bleeding stopped.
local: Ní unimpuee dīń Yeesu boon pú ní ki gbēē u-bɔkutil kpaanceetii. Niin niin ní fatiku kpáàa gúń.

eng: Who touched me? Jesus asked. When everyone denied it, Peter said, Master, the crowds are surrounding you and pressing in on you.
local: Ní Yeesu bālifì yii: «Ŋma di gbēē-mee?» Ní bi-kɔkɔ bí-u yii baa gbēē-u. Ní Peetroo lī yii: «Ukpil, binib di múkú a-pu ní ki téekí-si.».

eng: But Jesus said, Someone touched me. I know that power has gone out from me.
local: Ní Yeesu bí yii: «Ubɔ gbēē-mi, kun puee m gbìl̄ mpɔn ŋubɔ nyáń m-ni.».

eng: When the woman saw that she couldnt escape notice, she came trembling and fell before Jesus. In front of everyone, she explained why she had touched him and how she had been immediately healed.
local: Unimpuee ká yii Yeesu béè u-puee, ní u dɔ́miń ki gbāaǹ Yeesu nimbiin, tiwun jìkì kpàkpakpa, ki lī kunipaaun niin kun weeŋyi u būǹee nì u nín gbēē Yeesu min ní ki ká dicáaǹdi dicilpu dicilpuee.

eng: Daughter, your faith has healed you, Jesus said. Go in peace.
local: Niinee ní Yeesu bí-u yii: «M-bisal, a-fookiiu di fīī-si. Nín̄ cá nì ŋgbansɔŋfi.».

eng: While Jesus was still speaking, someone came from the synagogue leaders house, saying to Jairus, Your daughter has died. Dont bother the teacher any longer.
local: Yeesu kaa láá ŋmíl̀ ní unil nyánní kukpafidii kpiliuee dumpu, ní ki bí kukpafidii kpiliu yii: «A-bisal kpíì, taa ti páb́ ukpil fám.».

eng: A farmer went out to scatter his seed. As he was scattering it, some fell on the path where it was crushed, and the birds in the sky came and ate it.
local: «Uninja ubɔ di nyáń kí yàaǹ u-wamboolii u-kpaabun. U bíi yàantée, abil aba lítì nsan pu ní binib tàn̄ tàn̄ ní inoon dāa dūu jíń.

eng: When Jesus heard this, he responded, Dont be afraid; just keep trusting, and she will be healed.
local: Yeesu gbìl̄ee, ní ki bí Jayirusi yii: «Taa fàŋkì, fōō kí kíí, u làá ŋmátí.».

eng: When he came to the house, he didnt allow anyone to enter with him except Peter, John, and James, and the childs father and mother.
local: Bi búntì ki bàn̄ mmɔŋkee, úǹ nì Peetroo nì Yoowaneesi nì Yakubu nì kibiki na nì ki-baa di kɔ́, waa yāntì bin kíǹee kɔ́ kudiin.

eng: They were all crying and mourning for her, but Jesus said, Dont cry. She isnt dead. Shes only sleeping.
local: Binib nìn bíi sūǹ ki yáliŋkí kibiki gbanti pu. Ní Yeesu bí-bi yii: «Taa ti sɔ̄maan! Kàa kpíì, kì gèeǹ ní.».

eng: They laughed at him because they knew she was dead.
local: Niinee ní bi bíi bèel̄ Yeesu, kun puee bíǹ nìn nyí yii kì kpíì.

eng: Taking her hand, Jesus called out, Child, get up.
local: Ní Yeesu cúú ki-ŋal ní ki téeń yii: «Kibikee, yīkì.».

eng: Her life returned and she got up at once. He directed them to give her something to eat.
local: Niinee ní ki-fam fātìi dɔ́miń ki-ni. Kì fīkitì kpalaayee ní ki yáatìi kāl̀. Ní Yeesu bí yii bí tīī-kì tijin kí jī.

eng: Her parents were beside themselves with joy, but he ordered them to tell no one what had happened.
local: An ŋá kibikee baa nì ki-na bitii ní Yeesu kpáafì-bi yii, tin ŋá nee, bí taa pée māafì-tì báà ubɔ cee.

eng: Other seed fell on rock. As it grew, it dried up because it had no moisture.
local: Aba mun lítì ataŋkpee ni ki nyáń ní ki pūtì kpáàa kūń, kun puee kitiŋ kaa sɔ̀m̀.

eng: Other seed fell among thorny plants. The thorns grew with the plants and choked them.
local: Ní aba mun lítì ikuŋkumin, ní an nì ikuŋkum nyáń dicilpu, ní ikuŋkumee wíiǹ ki múkúǹ ní ŋaa ŋúń ki lòon̄.

eng: Still other seed landed on good soil. When it grew, it produced one hundred times more grain than was scattered. As he said this, he called out, Everyone who has ears should pay attention.
local: Ama ní abil aba lítì laakin kitiŋ ŋān̄ee, ní ki nyáń ki wíiǹ ki cáá apoo, ki lòon̄, ní ŋfin kamaa lòon̄ dilaataal.» Ní Yeesu kútì ki lī yii: «Sii un yaa cáá ditafal kí gbìl̄ee ń gbìl̄.».

eng: His disciples asked him what this parable meant.
local: Ní Yeesu boonnooliibi bālifì-u yii: «Tibɔŋmantikaalee taapu dàkà yii ba?».

eng: Jesus called the Twelve together and he gave them power and authority over all demons and to heal sicknesses.
local: Yeesu yíiń u-kpambaliŋ saalaa nì alee ki kpáfì ki tīī-bi mpɔn bí nīn jì arasiniibi kɔkɔ, kí nīn cāantí bibunliibi.

eng: When the apostles returned, they described for Jesus what they had done. Taking them with him, Jesus withdrew privately to a city called Bethsaida.
local: Yeesu kpambaliŋ ŋin u nìn túee bāań ki kpáfì u-cee ki bíi máafí-u bi nín ŋáaǹ puee. Ní Yeesu cáá-bi ki kpōotì bi-ŋmaniŋma kitiŋ kin bi yíì Beetisayidee caŋin.

eng: When the crowds figured it out, they followed him. He welcomed them, spoke to them about Gods kingdom, and healed those who were sick.
local: Ama kunipaau nín dāa béè laakin bi túĺee ní bi nín jì bi-boon. Ní Yeesu fōō-bi ki sòon̄ nì bi Unimbɔti Beel bɔti, ní ki cáaǹ bin nìn nyàab̀ ú cáaǹ-bee.

eng: When the day was almost over, the Twelve came to him and said, Send the crowd away so that they can go to the nearby villages and countryside and find lodging and food, because we are in a deserted place.
local: Kujoou nìn bíi jóotée, ní Yeesu kpambaliŋ saalaa nì alee sútìń ki pìl̄-u ní ki bí-u yii: «Yàntī binib nee ń nīn cá ntimbilimun nì ikaan kí nyàab̀ tijin nì laakin bi làá dòon̄ee. Di sá yii laakin ti bí nee, binib kpá.».

eng: He replied, You give them something to eat. But they said, We have no more than five loaves of bread and two fish—unless we go and buy food for all these people.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-bi yii: «Nimbi ni-ba tīīmaan-bi bí jī.» Ní bi bí-u yii: «Timbi cáá kpɔnɔb biŋmoo nì njɔmbiyaamu muli ja ní, àá a là tí cùú dāń tijin kí cáań kí tīī samaa nee kɔkɔ ń jī ní.».

eng: Jesus said to his disciples, Seat them in groups of about fifty.
local: Kunipaauee poonee bininjab nìn bàn̄ kutukub biŋmoo nyɔkɔm. Ní Yeesu bí u-boonnooliibi yii: «Ní yàntī bí kàl̄ ikuul ikuul, ŋkuul kamaa ń nīn cáá binib ili nì saalaa nyɔkɔm.».

eng: They did so, and everyone was seated.
local: U-boonnooliibi nìn ŋá u nín lī puee ki yāntì bi kāl̀ ikuul ikuul.

eng: He took the five loaves and the two fish, looked up to heaven, blessed them, and broke them and gave them to the disciples to set before the crowd.
local: Ní Yeesu yōoǹ kpɔnɔb biŋmooyee nì njɔmbiyaamu mulee ní ki wáaǹ ki kpēē kutaa ki jáam̀ Unimbɔti tijin gbanti pu, ní ki gífì ní ki dūu tīī u-boonnooliibi bí yàkatī kunipaau kɔkɔ ń jī.

eng: Everyone ate until they were full, and the disciples filled twelve baskets with the leftovers.
local: Báà ŋma nìn jíń ki díkí, ní bi tōō akpɔnɔkati nì ajɔŋgin ŋin bi ŋmɔ́ ki kíǹee an gbí tikabiti saalaa nì tili.

eng: Once when Jesus was praying by himself, the disciples joined him, and he asked them, Who do the crowds say that I am.
local: Dalba Yeesu tí kākatì ki bí laaba u-baba ki mèé. U-boonnooliibi nìn bí u-cee ní u bālifì-bi yii: «Binib lì yii m sá ŋma ní?».

eng: They answered, John the Baptist, others Elijah, and still others that one of the ancient prophets has come back to life.
local: Ní bi kíí-u yii: «Biba lì yii a sá Yoowaneesi un fùul̀ binib Unimbɔti nyimee. Biba mun lì yii a sá Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu Eelii, biba mun lì yii an sá Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi bikpikib kansikin ubɔ di fīkitì.».

eng: He sent them out to proclaim Gods kingdom and to heal the sick.
local: U nìn tú-bi bí nīn sòoǹ Unimbɔti Beel bɔti kí nīn cāantí bibunliibi.

eng: He asked them, And what about you? Who do you say that I am? Peter answered, The Christ sent from God.
local: Ní Yeesu fātìi bālifì-bi yii: «Kin nimbi fáà, ŋma ní nimbi nyí yii m sá?» Ní Peetroo kíí-u yii: «A sá Meesiya un Unimbɔti túnnée ní.».

eng: Jesus gave them strict orders not to tell this to anyone.
local: Ní Yeesu kpáafì-bi tiŋan yii bí taa pée lì-tì báà ubɔ cee.

eng: He said, The Human Onemust suffer many things and be rejected—by the elders, chief priests, and the legal experts—and be killed and be raised on the third day.
local: ní ki gíti lī yii: «An máaǹ man Kinibiki ń jī falaa tikpil. Bitindaŋkpilib nì bisaraaŋakpilib nì bimaradakaliibi làá dāá yíí-mi, kí kpɔ̀-mi, ní ḿ dāá fìkitī winta dal.».

eng: Jesus said to everyone, All who want to come after me must say no to themselves, take up their cross daily, and follow me.
local: Ní u tùkū bi-kɔkɔ yii: «Un yaa là kí nóó-mee ń fá úǹ u-ba nín là puee kí yòon̄ u-dɔpuntikaaŋu kí nīn nóó-mi baabadal.

eng: All who want to save their lives will lose them. But all who lose their lives because of me will save them.
local: Un di yaa là kí fīī u-maŋfalee làá dāá fá-dì, ama un di yaa dū u-maŋfal ki fá man puee làá dāá fīī-dì.

eng: What advantage do people have if they gain the whole world for themselves yet perish or lose their lives?.
local: Unil, a yaa jíń duulinya nee kɔkɔ, ní sisi a-ba wáŋkèe, ba ní a páatì?.

eng: Whoever is ashamed of me and my words, the Human Onewill be ashamed of that person when he comes in his glory and in the glory of the Father and of the holy angels.
local: Un yaa cáá man nì m-bɔti pu icooyee, man Kinibiki mun làá dāá nīn cáá udaan pu icoo m yaa láa gítìń nì m-nyootin nì m-Baa nì u-circir tuuŋyi nyootinee.

eng: I assure you that some standing here wont die before they see Gods kingdom.
local: Ibaamɔn ní m sòoǹ nì ni, ni-kansikin biba làá ká Unimbɔti Beel kí wàatī ní kí nín kpú.».

eng: About eight days after Jesus said these things, he took Peter, John, and James, and went up on a mountain to pray.
local: Yeesu sòon̄ míǹ, iwiin inii boonee ní ki yōoǹ Peetroo nì Yoowaneesi nì Yakubu ki jɔ́m̀ dijool pu kí mèè ŋwimmeem.

eng: As he was praying, the appearance of his face changed and his clothes flashed white like lightning.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun u bíi mèéyee ní u-nimbiin cúu kpántì, ní u-bɔkutil píil̀ káŋkaŋ ní ki jìliŋkī.

eng: He told them, Take nothing for the journey—no walking stick, no bag, no bread, no money, not even an extra shirt.
local: Ní u bí-bi yii: «Ni yaa cée, ní taa yòon̄ báà tiba: ní taa ŋúb́ ŋkpandɔɔɔ, kuloouaaa, tijinaaa, animbilaaa, kí taa ŋúb́ abɔkuti ali ali.

eng: Two men, Moses and Elijah, were talking with him.
local: Kpalaayee, binib bili biba bíi sòoǹ nì u. An nìn sá Mooyiisi nì Eelii.

eng: They were clothed with heavenly splendor and spoke about Jesus departure, which he would achieve in Jerusalem.
local: Unimbɔti nyooti nìn māntìi tú-bi. Bi nìn bíi sòoǹ nì Yeesu u-tundi din sá yii ú kpú Jeerusaleemee pu ní.

eng: Peter and those with him were almost overcome by sleep, but they managed to stay awake and saw his glory as well as the two men with him.
local: Peetroo nì u-bɔɔbi nìn gèen̄ ki kɔ́ poon, ama bi dāa fīntèe, ní ki ká Yeesu walifiŋu nì binib bilee yú.

eng: As the two men were about to leave Jesus, Peter said to him, Master, its good that were here. We should construct three shrines: one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah—but he didnt know what he was saying.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun bi làá yīkì Yeesu ceeyee, ní Peetroo bí-u yii: «Ukpil, an ŋān̄ tí gbíntí doo, tí gāā tibìń tita, sii kubɔ, Mooyiisi kubɔ, Eelii mun kubɔ.» - Di sá waa nìn nyí tin u bíi sòoǹee.

eng: Peter was still speaking when a cloud overshadowed them. As they entered the cloud, they were overcome with awe.
local: U bíi sòoǹ mimmee, ní ditagbandi diba dɔ́miń ki bīiǹ bi-pu, ní tifaandi cúú Peetroo nì bin kíǹee.

eng: Then a voice from the cloud said, This is my Son, my chosen one. Listen to him.
local: Niinee ní bi gbìl̄ Unimbɔti sòon̄ní ditagbandin, yii: «Un bí nee di sá m-Jipɔɔn un m lēēyee! Ní pīlifiǹ-u.».

eng: Even as the voice spoke, Jesus was found alone. They were speechless and at the time told no one what they had seen.
local: Unimbɔti tin sòoǹ míǹ ki ŋmīlifée an kíǹ Yeesu baba, ní bi kpáafì an bɔti, kaa pée sòon̄ tin bi kée báà ubɔ cee.

eng: The next day, when Jesus, Peter, John, and James had come down from the mountain, a large crowd met Jesus.
local: Kutaa wúntì, bi bíi kpákatiń dijoolee, ní dinikitikpaandi diba túŋkìń Yeesu.

eng: A man from the crowd shouted, Teacher, I beg you to take a look at my son, my only child.
local: Binib gbanti poon ní uninja ubɔ bíi kūuntí ki lì yii: «Ukpil, m gbàam̄-si, pée fàtī kí kpèè m-jipɔɔn nee. An sá-mi dibibaabil ní.

eng: Look, a spirit seizes him and, without any warning, he screams. It shakes him and causes him to foam at the mouth. It tortures him and rarely leaves him alone.
local: Arasinii ubɔ di bí u-pu. An yaa bāań-uee, ní u tátí ní u-wunti jìkì, ní u-nyɔkɔ nyēē kupumpukuu. Arasiniiyee sàantí-u, kaa là kí yàntī-u malaa.

eng: Whatever house you enter, remain there until you leave that place.
local: Ni yaa kɔ́ kudicaŋu kun ni ní bi yaa fōō-nee, nín̄ bímaan niin kí tin sāā daliŋkin ni làá yīkèe.

eng: I begged your disciples to throw it out, but they couldnt.
local: M mēē a-boonnooliibi yii bí jà-mi-u, ní baa ŋúń.».

eng: Jesus answered, You faithless and crooked generation, how long will I be with you and put up with you? Bring your son here.
local: Ní Yeesu bí yii: «Óo binib nee, naa cáá fookii ki gáatì! Kí dāá sāā ŋyunti ŋulɔŋu ní ḿ bàà dūú kàl̄ ni-cee? M làá nīn jīǹ ni-pu kimɔtii kí tin sāā ŋyunti ŋulɔŋu ní? Uninjee, cáań a-bikee doo.».

eng: While he was coming, the demon threw him down and shook him violently. Jesus spoke harshly to the unclean spirit, healed the child, and gave him back to his father.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun kibikee dòoń Yeesu ceeyee, ní arasiniiyee fíiǹ-kì ki lúkú ní ki cīŋkì-kì ŋkpitim pu. Ní Yeesu kpātì arasiniiyee pu, ki cáaǹ kibikee, ki dū-kì ki tīī ki-baa.

eng: Everyone was overwhelmed by Gods greatness. While everyone was marveling at everything he was doing, Jesus said to his disciples.
local: Ní báà ŋma bākatì sèeee Unimbɔti pɔŋkpaaŋŋu pu. Báà ŋma nìn dáá bí ki pàkà Yeesu maamaacib bin kɔkɔ u ŋáańee pu, ní u bí u-boonnooliibi yii.

eng: Take these words to heart: the Human Oneis about to be delivered into human hands.
local: «Ní cúú tibɔti tin m làá tùkù-ni kɔŋkɔnneeyee tiŋan. Bi làá dū man Kinibiki kí dūú nyāfiǹ binib.».

eng: They didnt understand this statement. Its meaning was hidden from them so they couldnt grasp it. And they were afraid to ask him about it.
local: Ama baa nìn gbìl̄ tibɔtee taapu. An nìn dáá bí-bi dibalin, ní baa ŋúń kí béè taapu, ama ní ki fāŋkì, kaa bālifì-u mun.

eng: An argument arose among the disciples about which of them was the greatest.
local: Yeesu boonnooliibi nìn bíi kpákà tikpiti pu.

eng: Aware of their deepest thoughts, Jesus took a little child and had the child stand beside him.
local: Ní Yeesu béè bi-landɔkɔi, ní ki cáań kibiyaawaai ki yóoǹ u-cee.

eng: Jesus said to his disciples, Whoever welcomes this child in my name welcomes me. Whoever welcomes me, welcomes the one who sent me. Whoever is least among you all is the greatest.
local: ní ki bí-bi yii: «Un di yaa fōō kibiyaawaai kii kinee man puee, fōō man m-ba dee, ní un yaa fōō-mee, fōō un túnní-mee mun dee. Di sá yii un yaa sá kiyaawaai ni-kansikinee, úǹ di sá ukpaan.».

eng: John replied, Master, we saw someone throwing demons out in your name, and we tried to stop him because he isnt in our group of followers.
local: Ní Yoowaneesi bí Yeesu yii: «Ukpil, ti ká uninja ubɔ cáá a-yindi ki jì arasiniibi binib pu, ní timbi bàà nyàab̄ kí pìin̄-u nsan, kun puee waa sá ti-yɔu.».

eng: Wherever they dont welcome you, as you leave that city, shake the dust off your feet as a witness against them.
local: Laan kamaa ní ni yaa bàn̄ ní baa fōō-nee, ní yīkì kitiŋ gbanti pu kí gbāfì ni-taaŋi kpɔŋkpɔm, kí dàkà yii ni sòon̄ ki kpáafì-bi.».

eng: But Jesus replied, Dont stop him, because whoever isnt against you is for you.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-u yii: «Taa pìim̄maan-u nsan, kun puee un kaa kpākantí-nee sá ni-yɔu ní.».

eng: As the time approached when Jesus was to be taken up into heaven, he determined to go to Jerusalem.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun u làá yīkì duulinyan kí nīn jɔ́ḿ yilpuee tóobińee, ní u bóob́ yii see ú pée cù Jeerusaleem.

eng: He sent messengers on ahead of him. Along the way, they entered a Samaritan village to prepare for his arrival.
local: U nìn tú binib ń lìntī. Binib gbanti nìn cūtì ki kɔ́ Samaarii tiŋki kiban kí bɔ́ntí laakin u làá dāá kɔ́ee.

eng: but the Samaritan villagers refused to welcome him because he was determined to go to Jerusalem.
local: ní niin yab yíì, kaa fōō-u, u nín nìn cá Jeerusaleemee pu.

eng: When the disciples James and John saw this, they said, Lord, do you want us to call fire down from heaven to consume them.
local: U-boonnooliibi Yakubu nì Yoowaneesi nín gbìl̄ mimmee, ní bi bí yii: «Ti-Dindaan, tí mèè Unimbɔti ŋŋmi ń nyānní yilpu kí dāá wà kí céetí-baaa?».

eng: But Jesus turned and spoke sternly to them.
local: Ní Yeesu fātì ki kpīiǹ-bi.

eng: and they went on to another village.
local: Ní bi nìkī kitiŋgbaŋ kiban.

eng: As Jesus and his disciples traveled along the road, someone said to him, I will follow you wherever you go.
local: Bi bí nsanni ki cée, ní uninja ubɔ bí Yeesu yii: «M làá nīn pée nóó-si, báà laakin ní a yaa cée.».

eng: Jesus replied, Foxes have dens and the birds in the sky have nests, but the Human Onehas no place to lay his head.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-u yii: «Jiiŋkpaaibi cáá bi-kɔŋkooŋyi ki dɔ̀, ní nnɔmbiyaamu cáá anɔndiin ki dɔ̀, ama man Kinibiki kaa cáá laakin m làá bíĺ m-yil kí ŋùŋfèe.».

eng: Then Jesus said to someone else, Follow me. He replied, Lord, first let me go and bury my father.
local: Ní Yeesu bí kijapaai kiba yii: «Nóó-mi.» Ní kijapaayee kíí-u yii: «Ukpil, pɔ̄-mi nsan ḿ síb́ m-baa kí wàatī.».

eng: They departed and went through the villages proclaiming the good news and healing people everywhere.
local: U sòon̄ nì bi míǹ ki dóòyee, ní bi búntì ki yìiǹ ntimbilimu kɔkɔn ki sòoǹ Unimbɔti bɔti, ki cāantí bibunliibi an kɔkɔ.

eng: Jesus said to him, Let the dead bury their own dead. But you go and spread the news of Gods kingdom.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-u yii: «Yàntī bitaŋkpiibi ń nīn síb̀ bi-taŋkpiibi. Sii dee, cùú sòoǹ Unimbɔti Beel bɔti kí tùkù binib.».

eng: Someone else said to Jesus, I will follow you, Lord, but first let me say good-bye to those in my house.
local: Ní ubɔ mun gíti dɔ́miń ki bí Yeesu yii: «Ukpil, m làá nīn nóó-si, ama á dá láá pɔ̄-mi nsan ḿ cùú kàan̄ m-dumpu yab kí wàatī.».

eng: Jesus said to him, No one who puts a hand on the plow and looks back is fit for Gods kingdom.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-u yii: «Sii un yaa cúú dikool kí kpɔ̀ ní ki gíti bíi fàtīi kpèé a-boonee, kaa sá Unimbɔti Beelin yɔu.».

eng: Herod the ruler heard about everything that was happening. He was confused because some people were saying that John had been raised from the dead.
local: Ubɔti Eeroodi nín nìn gbìl̄ tin kɔkɔ bíi jītée, u bākatì, kun puee biba nìn bí yii: «Yoowaneesi di fātìi fīkitì.».

eng: others that Elijah had appeared, and still others that one of the ancient prophets had come back to life.
local: Ní biba mun bí yii: «Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu Eelii di nyánní yilpu ki fātìń.» Ní biba mun bí yii: «An sá Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi dooooyee yab ubɔ di fātìi fīkitì.».

eng: Herod said, I beheaded John, so now who am I hearing about? Herod wanted to see him.
local: Ama ní Eeroodi bí yii: «Man yāntì bi lēē Yoowaneesi yil, kin ŋma di gíti sá kijapaai kin pu bi sòoǹ míǹ nan dee?» Niinee ní u kíĺ ki nyàab̀ kí ká Yeesu.

eng: A record of the ancestors of Jesus Christ, son of David, son of Abraham.
local: Yeesu Kriisitoo naanjab yiŋŋi sɔ̀. U nìn sá Daafiidi naantiiu, ní Daafiidi nìn sá Abraam naantiiu.

eng: Hezekiah was the father of Manasseh. Manasseh was the father of Amos. Amos was the father of Josiah.
local: ní Eeseekiyaasi màl̄ Manasee, ní Manasee màl̄ Amɔn, ní Amɔn màl̄ Joosiyaasi.

eng: Josiah was the father of Jechoniah and his brothers. This was at the time of the exile to Babylon.
local: Joosiyaasi màl̄ Jeekooniyaasi nì u-nabiyaamu ŋyunti ŋun bi cúú Isirayeel yab ki cūnnì Babiloonee.

eng: After the exile to Babylon: Jechoniah was the father of Shealtiel. Shealtiel was the father of Zerubbabel.
local: Bi nín cáá Isirayeel yab ki cūnnì Babiloonee, boonee ní Jeekooniyaasi màl̄ Salatiyeel, ní Salatiyeel màl̄ Sooroobabeel.

eng: Zerubbabel was the father of Abiud. Abiud was the father of Eliakim. Eliakim was the father of Azor.
local: ní Sooroobabeel màl̄ Abiyuudi, ní Abiyuudi màl̄ Eeliyaakiim, ní Eeliyaakiim màl̄ Asɔɔr.

eng: Azor was the father of Zadok. Zadok was the father of Achim. Achim was the father of Eliud.
local: ní Asɔɔr màl̄ Sadooki, ní Sadooki màl̄ Akim, ní Akim màl̄ Eeliyuudi.

eng: Eliud was the father of Eleazar. Eleazar was the father of Matthan. Matthan was the father of Jacob.
local: ní Eeliyuudi màl̄ Eeleeyasaar, ní Eeleeyasaar màl̄ Maatan, ní Maatan màl̄ Jaakɔb.

eng: Jacob was the father of Joseph, the husband of Mary—of whom Jesus was born, who is called the Christ.
local: ní Jaakɔb màl̄ Jooseefi un cáá Maarii un màl̄ Yeesu un bi yíì Kriisitooyee.

eng: So there were fourteen generations from Abraham to David, fourteen generations from David to the exile to Babylon, and fourteen generations from the exile to Babylon to the Christ.
local: Kí yòon̄ Abraam yuntiŋu kí dāá sāā Daafiidi yuntiŋuee, anaantitɔntɔkɔ nìn sá diba kpá saalku ní, ní kí tí yòon̄ Daafiidi yuntiŋu kí dāá sāā ŋyunti ŋun bi cúú Isirayeel yab ki cūnnì Babiloonee muee, anaantitɔntɔkɔ nìn sá diba kpá saalku, ní kí yòon̄ ŋyunti gbanti kí dāá sāā Kriisitoo màaltam muee, anaantitɔntɔkɔ mun sá diba kpá saalku.

eng: This is how the birth of Jesus Christ took place. When Mary his mother was engaged to Joseph, before they were married, she became pregnant by the Holy Spirit.
local: Bi nín màl̄ Yeesu Kriisitoo puee sɔ̀. U-na Maarii nìn sá Jooseefi bɔɔn ní. Kí wàatī bí nín cáá tɔbee, Unimbɔti Fam pɔŋŋu yāntì Maarii cáá dipool.

eng: Joseph her husband was a righteous man. Because he didnt want to humiliate her, he decided to call off their engagement quietly.
local: U-bɔɔn Jooseefi nìn là kí nóó mara nín lī puee, ama ní kaa là kí bìitī Maarii mun pu, ní ki là kí yàntī-u dibalin.

eng: Abraham was the father of Isaac. Isaac was the father of Jacob. Jacob was the father of Judah and his brothers.
local: Abraam màl̄ Isaaki, ní Isaaki màl̄ Jaakɔb, ní Jaakɔb màl̄ Juuda nì u-nabiyaamu.

eng: As he was thinking about this, an angel from the Lord appeared to him in a dream and said, Joseph son of David, dont be afraid to take Mary as your wife, because the child she carries was conceived by the Holy Spirit.
local: U bíi dàkafī mimmee, ní ti-Dindaan tuuŋŋu dɔ́miń ki sòon̄ nì u kudamintiiun yii: «Jooseefi, Daafiidi naantiiu, taa fàŋkì kí cáá Maarii, kun puee Unimbɔti Fam di yāntì u cáá kibiki kin u làá màal̀ nee pool.

eng: She will give birth to a son, and you will call him Jesus, because he will save his people from their sins.
local: U làá màal̀ kibininjabiki ní á dāá pɔ̄-kì diyindi yii Yeesu, kun puee úǹ di làá dāá fīī u-niboolii bi-kpitiin.».

eng: Now all of this took place so that what the Lord had spoken through the prophet would be fulfilled.
local: Tin bí nee kɔkɔ bāań ki ŋá kii ti-Dindaan nín yāntì u-bɔnaatiliu sòon̄ miŋyee ní yii.

eng: Look! A virgin will become pregnant and give birth to a son, And they will call him, Emmanuel.
local: «Usapɔɔn un kaa nyí uninjee làá cáá dipool kí màal̀ kibininjabiki, bi làá pɔ̄-kì diyindi yii Eemanuyeel.» - Eemanuyeel taapu di sá yii «Unimbɔti bí ti-cee.».

eng: When Joseph woke up, he did just as an angel from God commanded and took Mary as his wife.
local: Jooseefi fīntì ŋgeem puee, ní ki cūtì ki cáá Maarii kii ti-Dindaan tuuŋŋu nín bí yii ú ŋá puee.

eng: But he didnt have sexual relations with her until she gave birth to a son. Joseph called him Jesus.
local: Ama waa gbēē-u áli ki dāa sāā ŋyunti ŋun u màl̄ kibininjabikee ní Jooseefi pú kibikee diyindi yii Yeesu.

eng: Judah was the father of Perez and Zerah, whose mother was Tamar. Perez was the father of Hezron. Hezron was the father of Aram.
local: Juuda màl̄ Fareesi nì Saara, . Fareesi màl̄ Eesiroom, ní Eesiroom màl̄ Aram.

eng: Aram was the father of Amminadab. Amminadab was the father of Nahshon. Nahshon was the father of Salmon.
local: ní Aram màl̄ Amiinadaab, ní Amiinadaab màl̄ Naasɔn, ní Naasɔn mun màl̄ Salmɔn.

eng: Salmon was the father of Boaz, whose mother was Rahab. Boaz was the father of Obed, whose mother was Ruth. Obed was the father of Jesse.
local: ní Salmɔn màl̄ Boowaasi, - Boowaasi na di nìn sá Raab, ní Boowaasi mun màl̄ Oobeedi. Oobeedi na di sá Ruuti, ní Oobeedi màl̄ Jeesee.

eng: Jesse was the father of David the king. David was the father of Solomon, whose mother had been the wife of Uriah.
local: ní Jeesee màl̄ ubɔti Daafiidi. Daafiidi màl̄ Saloomɔn. Saloomɔn na di nìn sá Uurii nimpuu.

eng: Solomon was the father of Rehoboam. Rehoboam was the father of Abijah. Abijah was the father of Asaph.
local: Saloomɔn màl̄ Rooboowaam, ní Rooboowaam màl̄ Abiiya, ní Abiiya màl̄ Asaa.

eng: Asaph was the father of Jehoshaphat. Jehoshaphat was the father of Joram. Joram was the father of Uzziah.
local: ní Asaa màl̄ Joosafaati, ní Joosafaati màl̄ Jooram, ní Jooram màl̄ Oosiyasi.

eng: Uzziah was the father of Jotham. Jotham was the father of Ahaz. Ahaz was the father of Hezekiah.
local: ní Oosiyasi màl̄ Joowatam, ní Joowatam màl̄ Akaasi, ní Akaasi màl̄ Eeseekiyaasi.

eng: He called his twelve disciples and gave them authority over unclean spirits to throw them out and to heal every disease and every sickness.
local: Yeesu yíiń u-boonnooliibi saalaa nì bilee, ní ki tīī-bi mpɔn bí jà arasiniibi kí cáaǹ iween kɔkɔ nì bin wɔ̀b̄ee kɔkɔ.

eng: Dont take a backpack for the road or two shirts or sandals or a walking stick.
local: Ní taa nyàab̀ tilɔti ni-saŋkindi pu, nì abɔkuti ali, nì anaataka nì ŋkpandɔ, kun puee bin kansikin ni bí ki tūǹ ditundee di máaǹ kí kpèè-ni.

eng: Whatever city or village you go into, find somebody in it who is worthy and stay there until you go on your way.
local: «Ni yaa bàn̄ ki kɔ́ kitiŋkpaaŋŋin àá kitiŋwaai poonee, nyàab̀maan kí béé un kítì kí fōō-nee. Ni yaa kɔ́ udaan ceeyee, ní kàl̄maan niin kí tin sāā ŋyunti ŋun ni làá dāá yīkì kitiŋ gbantinee.

eng: When you go into a house, say, Peace.
local: Ni yaa kɔ́ kudicaŋuee nee, ní péé kí jāam̀-bi, yii: <Unimbɔti ń tīī-ni ŋgbansɔŋfi!>.

eng: If the house is worthy, give it your blessing of peace. But if the house isnt worthy, take back your blessing.
local: Bin bí kudicaŋu gbantinee yaa kítèe, ní ni nín jáam̀ ki tīī-bi ŋgbansɔŋfi ŋuŋyee làá nīn bí bi-pu. Ama bi yaa kaa kítì kí fōō ŋgbansɔŋfi gbantee, ŋú fàtīí gítiń ni-cee.

eng: If anyone refuses to welcome you or listen to your words, shake the dust off your feet as you leave that house or city.
local: Ni yaa kɔ́ dicindi diban, àá kitiŋ kiban ní bi yaa yíì kaa fōō-ni, kaa là kí pīlifiǹ-nee, ní nyāmaan niin kí gbāfì ni-taaŋi kpɔŋkpɔm.

eng: I assure you that it will be more bearable for the land of Sodom and Gomorrah on Judgment Day than it will be for that city.
local: Ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-ni, ibɔsoon dalee, Unimbɔti nín nìn dáaĺ Soodoom nì Goomoora timmu tafal ki yāntì ŋŋmi nyánní yilpu ki wùn̄-mu puee dāa sɔ́ nì kitiŋ gbanti yab nín làá ká ditafadaaldi diŋyee.».

eng: Look, Im sending you as sheep among wolves. Therefore, be wise as snakes and innocent as doves.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-bi yii: «Ní pīlifì. M dū-ni ki tú kii ipiiyee isaakpan kansikin. An puee ní nīn nyíǹ ni-ba kii ikoobuee kí nīn bímaan soooo kii abeenanjilee.

eng: Watch out for people—because they will hand you over to councils and they will beat you in their synagogues.
local: Ama ní nīn nyíǹ ni-ba nì binib, kun puee bi làá nīn cútí-ni ki cáǹ ibɔsoon, kí nīn gbáà-ni anaalab bi-kpafidiiŋŋin.

eng: They will haul you in front of governors and even kings because of me so that you may give your testimony to them and to the Gentiles.
local: Bi làá nīn cáǹ-ni biyidambi nì bibɔtiibi cee man pu, án nín làá ŋá pu ní sòoǹ tin ni nyí man puee Juuda yab nì bin kaa sá Juuda yabee nimbiinee.

eng: Whenever they hand you over, dont worry about how to speak or what you will say, because what you can say will be given to you at that moment.
local: Bi yaa láa cáǹ-ni bibɔsoonliibi nimbiinee, ní taa nín̄ yáliŋkí min ní ni làá tin sòoǹee nì tin ni làá lèe pu. Ŋyunti gbantee ní Unimbɔti làá tīī-ni tibɔti tin sá ní lèe.

eng: Here are the names of the twelve apostles: first, Simon, who is called Peter; and Andrew his brother; James the son of Zebedee; and John his brother.
local: Yeesu kpambaliŋ saalaa nì alee yiŋŋi sɔ̀. Mbaayee, Siimɔn un bi gíti yíì Peetroo , nì u-naal Andree, nì Seebeedee jipɔmbi Yakubu nì u-naal Yoowaneesi.

eng: You arent doing the talking, but the Spirit of my Father is doing the talking through you.
local: An kaa sá nimbi ni-ba di làá sòoǹ, ni-Baa Unimbɔti Fam di làá dī ni-pu kí sòoǹ.

eng: Brothers and sisters will hand each other over to be executed. A father will turn his child in. Children will defy their parents and have them executed.
local: Unil dāa cútí u-nabiki kí tīī bí kpɔ̀, ní ubaati ń dāá cúú u-biki kí tīī bí kpɔ̀, ní mbiyaamu mun dāa yíkí kí jáǹ bibaatiibi nì binaatiibi kí yàntī bí kpɔ̀-bi.

eng: Everyone will hate you on account of my name. But whoever stands firm until the end will be saved.
local: Báà ŋma làá nīn ná-ni kun puee ni fōō-mi ki kíí. Ama un yaa pɔ́ɔǹ ki tin kátì-kuee dāa kāntí difiil.

eng: Whenever they harass you in one city, escape to the next, because I assure that you will not go through all the cities of Israel before the Human One comes.
local: Bi yaa láa bíi páb̀-ni kitiŋ kibanee, ní sāǹ kí póotí kiban. Ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-ni, naa làá yìiǹ kí dóó Isirayeel timmu kɔkɔ ní man Kinibiki ń bàń.

eng: Disciples arent greater than their teacher, and slaves arent greater than their master.
local: Utumbifil ubɔ kaa bí ki jítiǹ un dàkà-uee, míǹ ní utɔntɔnli ubɔ mun kaa bí ki jítiǹ u-kpiliu.

eng: Its enough for disciples to be like their teacher and slaves like their master. If they have called the head of the house Beelzebul, its certain that they will call the members of his household by even worse names.
local: An ŋmāntì utumbifil ń nīn kíǹ ki bí kii un dàkà-uee, ní utɔntɔnli mun ń nīn kíǹ ki bí kii u-kpiliuee. Bi yaa yíiń kudiidaan yii Beelseebuul, arasiniibi bɔtiuee, mana ní baa dāa ŋūǹ kí yíiń u-dicaŋku yab bíǹ míǹ?».

eng: Therefore, dont be afraid of those people because nothing is hidden that wont be revealed, and nothing secret that wont be brought out into the open.
local: An puee, ní taa fàŋkìmaan binib. Tin kɔkɔ bí dibalinee làá dāá nyā mpaampiin pu ní tin pu baa nyée, bi làá dāá béé-tì.

eng: What I say to you in the darkness, tell in the light; and what you hear whispered, announce from the rooftops.
local: Tin ní m tùkū-ni dibɔmbɔndinee, sòom̀maan-tì ŋwiimpu, ní tin ní m lɔ́kɔfì-ni dibalinee, dū-tì kí bāakìmaan.

eng: Dont be afraid of those who kill the body but cant kill the soul. Instead, be afraid of the one who can destroy both body and soul in hell.
local: Taa fàŋkìmaan bin ŋūǹ kí kpɔ̀-ni ama ní kaa ŋūǹ kí kpɔ̀ ni-naŋkee. Ama fàŋkìmaan Unimbɔti un ŋūǹ kí dū ni nì ni-naŋki kɔkɔ kí tɔ̄ ŋŋmin kí nàan̄ee.

eng: Arent two sparrows sold for a small coin? But not one of them will fall to the ground without your Father knowing about it already.
local: Baa nyáfí seerib bili gbòooo? Nì míǹ kɔkɔee, ni-Baa Unimbɔti yaa kaa pú nsan, seer ubɔ kaa lítiń kitiŋ.

eng: Philip; and Bartholomew; Thomas; and Matthew the tax collector; James the son of Alphaeus; and Thaddaeus.
local: nì Fiiliipi nì Baateeleemii, nì Toomasi nì Maatiiu un sá ufɔtilee, nì Yakubu Alfee jipɔɔn, nì Tadee.

eng: Even the hairs of your head are all counted.
local: Nimbee, Unimbɔti kàan̄ ni-yikpiti kɔkɔ ní.

eng: Dont be afraid. You are worth more than many sparrows.
local: An puee, ní taa fàŋkì: ni pɔ̄ɔ̄ kudaau ki jítiǹ kuseerpaau.

eng: Therefore, everyone who acknowledges me before people, I also will acknowledge before my Father who is in heaven.
local: Un kamaa yaa lī binib nimbiin yii u sá man yɔuee, ma mun dāa lì m-Baa un bí yilpuee nimbiin yii u sá m-yɔu ní.

eng: But everyone who denies me before people, I also will deny before my Father who is in heaven.
local: Ama un di yaa néè binib nimbiin yii waa nyí-mee, ma mun làá néé m-Baa un bí yilpuee nimbiin yii maa nyí-u.

eng: Dont think that Ive come to bring peace to the earth. I havent come to bring peace but a sword.
local: «Ní taa dàkafì yii m cáań ŋgbansɔŋfi kitiŋ pu, maa cáań ŋgbansɔŋfi, kitàataajiki ní m cáań.

eng: Ive come to turn a man against his father, a daughter against her mother, and a daughter-in-law against her mother-in-law .
local: M dɔ́miń kí yàkatī unil nì u-baa, usapɔɔn nì u-na, unimpu nì u-beel ní.

eng: Peoples enemies are members of their own households .
local: Unil nannanliibi di dāa sá u-dicaŋkun yab.

eng: Those who love father or mother more than me arent worthy of me. Those who love son or daughter more than me arent worthy of me.
local: «Un néeń u-baa àá u-na ki jítiǹ mamee kaa kítì kí nīn sá man yɔu, ní un néeń u-jipɔɔn àá u-bisal ki jítiǹ mamee kaa kítì kí nīn sá man yɔu.

eng: Those who dont pick up their crosses and follow me arent worthy of me.
local: Un yaa kaa yōoǹ u-dɔpuntikaaŋu ki túǹ kí nīn nóó-mee, kaa kítì kí nīn sá man boonnooliu.

eng: Those who find their lives will lose them, and those who lose their lives because of me will find them.
local: Un di nyàab̀ kí fīī u-maŋfalee làá fá-dì. Un di yaa dū u-maŋfal ki fá man puee di dāa kāntí u-maŋfal.».

eng: Simon the Cananaean; and Judas, who betrayed Jesus.
local: nì Siimɔn un nìn là kí jáǹ Room yabee, nì Juudasi Isikaariyɔti un dāa dū Yeesu ki nyáfiǹ u-nannanliibee.

eng: Those who receive you are also receiving me, and those who receive me are receiving the one who sent me.
local: «Un yaa fōō-nee, fōō man dee, ní un yaa fōō-mee fōō un túnní-mee dee.
