eng: Those who receive a prophet as a prophet will receive a prophets reward. Those who receive a righteous person as a righteous person will receive a righteous persons reward.
local: Un yaa fōō Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu yii u nín sá Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliuee puee, udaan làá dāá fōō tipaal tin Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliuee làá dāá fōōyee ní. Ní un yaa fōō un yú deedee Unimbɔti nimbiinee, yii u nín yú deedee Unimbɔti nimbiinee puee, udaan làá dāá fōō tipaal tin un yú deedee Unimbɔti nimbiinee làá dāá fōōyee ní.

eng: I assure you that everybody who gives even a cup of cold water to these little ones because they are my disciples will certainly be rewarded.
local: Un di yaa pú ŋwaamu nee kansikin kiba nnyinsɔm kpaŋkpam kì nyùn̄ yii kì nín sá man boonnooliuee puee, ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-ni, udaan làá ká u-paati.».

eng: Jesus sent these twelve out and commanded them, Dont go among the Gentiles or into a Samaritan city.
local: Yeesu nìn tú u-kpambaliŋ saalaa nì alee, ní ki kpáafì-bi yii: «Ní taa cù bin kaa sá Juuda yabee cee, kí taa kɔ́ Samaarii yab timmun.

eng: Go instead to the lost sheep, the people of Israel.
local: Túĺmaan Isirayeel yab bin bí kii ipii in wáŋkèe cee.

eng: As you go, make this announcement: The kingdom of heaven has come near.
local: Cùú sòom̀maan Unimbɔti bɔti yii: <Unimbɔti Beel sútì ki pìlīń-ni.>.

eng: Heal the sick, raise the dead, cleanse those with skin diseases, and throw out demons. You received without having to pay. Therefore, give without demanding payment.
local: Cáam̀maan bibunliibi kí fìkitī bitaŋkpiibi kí ŋāŋkì binyaŋgbantiibi wunti, kí jà arasiniibi. Kii ni nín fōō faalaa puee, ni mun tīīmaan faalaa.

eng: Workers deserve to be fed, so dont gather gold or silver or copper coins for your money belts to take on your trips.
local: Ní taa nyàab̀ sindaariya nì animbil nì tikúman nimbiliŋ kí fúuń ni-tɔkɔliŋin.

eng: When Jesus finished teaching his twelve disciples, he went on from there to teach and preach in their cities.
local: Yeesu nín tīī u-boonnooliibi saalaa nì bilee isansaŋki ki dóòyee, ní u yíkì ki nín nìkì ntimu mun kíǹee ni ki sòoǹ Unimbɔti bɔti ki dàkà binib.

eng: He is the one of whom it is written: Look, Im sending my messenger before you, who will prepare your way before you .
local: Kun puee Yoowaneesi sá un pu Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon sòon̄ yii: Unimbɔti bí yii: <M làá tɔ̄ń m-tɔntɔnliu ú lìntīń a-nyɔkɔpu kí bɔ́ntí-si nsan.> Ní Yeesu gíti bí-bi yii.

eng: I assure you that no one who has ever been born is greater than John the Baptist. Yet whoever is least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he.
local: Ibaamɔn ní m sòoǹ, bin kɔkɔ unimpu núuĺ ki màl̄ee kansikinee, ubɔ kaa bí ki jítiǹ Yoowaneesi. Ama un sá kiwaai Unimbɔti Beelinee jítiǹ Yoowaneesi.

eng: From the days of John the Baptist until now the kingdom of heaven is violently attacked as violent people seize it.
local: Kí yòon̄ ŋyunti ŋun Yoowaneesi un fùul̀ binib Unimbɔti nyimee sòon̄ Unimbɔti bɔti ki dāa sāā díǹee, Unimbɔti Beel dɔ́miń nì mpɔn ní, bin nimbiliŋ máǹ di-puee di kóò.

eng: All the Prophets and the Law prophesied until John came.
local: Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi kɔkɔ nì Mooyiisi marab sòon̄ Unimbɔti Beel pu bɔti ní ki dāa dūu sāā Yoowaneesi yuntiŋu.

eng: If you are willing to accept it, he is Elijah who is to come.
local: Ni yaa là kí gbìlīǹ-mee, Yoowaneesi di sá Eelii un bi nìn sòon̄ yii u làá gítińee.

eng: Let the person who has ears, hear.
local: Un yaa cáá ditafalee ń gbìl̄.».

eng: To what will I compare this generation? It is like a child sitting in the marketplaces calling out to others.
local: Ní Yeesu gíti bí yii: «Ba ní m làá dū waanee nib kí ŋmàntīǹ dee? Bi náań nì mbiyaamu mun kā sampaan puee ní, mubɔ kūuntí ki lì mu-jutiibi yii.

eng: We played the flute for you and you didnt dance. We sang a funeral song and you didnt mourn.
local: <Ti búń-ni awul, naa ŋɔ́ń, ki gāā-ni ikpiisilan, naa súń.>.

eng: For John came neither eating nor drinking, and they say, He has a demon.
local: Di sá yii Yoowaneesi un fùul̀ binib Unimbɔti nyimee dɔ́miń ní kaa là tijinaaa, kaa là ndaam, ní ni bí yii: <Arasinii di bí u-pu.>.

eng: Yet the Human One came eating and drinking, and they say, Look, a glutton and a drunk, a friend of tax collectors and sinners. But wisdom is proved to be right by her works.
local: Ní man Kinibiki dɔ́miń ki jīǹ ki nyù, ní ni bí yii: <Uninja nee sá upoofu, ki sá udanyɔl, ní ki bɔ́ɔtí bifɔtiliibi nì binikpitib bin kɔ kíǹee.> Ama bin fōō Unimbɔti ciiŋyi ki kííyee dàkà yii i sá deedee.».

eng: Now when John heard in prison about the things the Christ was doing, he sent word by his disciples to Jesus, asking.
local: Ní Yoowaneesi un fùul̀ binib Unimbɔti nyimee kāl̀ kusarkadiinee ki gbìl̄ Kriisitoo tuuŋŋi ŋin u tūǹee pu. Niinee ní u lēē u-boonnooliibi biba ki tú bí cùú bàlifì-u yii.

eng: Then he began to scold the cities where he had done his greatest miracles because they didnt change their hearts and lives.
local: Niinee ní Yeesu bíi wòoǹ ntimu mun poon u nìn ŋá maamaacib tikpilee nib, kun puee baa kpántì bi-bimbim.

eng: How terrible it will be for you, Chorazin! How terrible it will be for you, Bethsaida! For if the miracles done among you had been done in Tyre and Sidon, they would have changed their hearts and lives and put on funeral clothes and ashes a long time ago.
local: U bí yii: «Mbusu dɔ̄ ki cí nimbi Koorasin tiŋki nib, mbusu dɔ̄ ki cí ni mun Beetisayida tiŋki nib. Kun puee, maamaacib bin m ŋá ni-cee nee di yaa bàà nìn ŋámaan Tiir nì Siidɔn timmun, niin yab bàà nìn kpántìmaan bi-bimbim malaa, ki pēeǹ kpɔsatab ki dū ŋfantam ki pùkū bi-ba.

eng: But I say to you that Tyre and Sidon will be better off on Judgment Day than you.
local: An puee m tùkù-ni yii, ibɔsoon dalee, nimbi tafadaaldi dāa jítiǹ Tiir nì Siidɔn nib yal.

eng: And you, Capernaum, will you be honored by being raised up to heaven? No, you will be thrown down to the place of the dead. After all, if the miracles that were done among you had been done in Sodom, it would still be here today.
local: Kin, nimbi Kapeernayum tiŋki nib, ni nyí yii ni làá yīkì yilpu kí tin tūū kutagbɔŋu níii? Ni làá sìntī kí bà taapu taŋkpiibi ní, kun puee maamaacib bin m ŋá ni-cee nee di yaa bàà nìn ŋámaan Soodoom tiŋkinee, bíǹ bàà nìn kpántìmaan bi-bimbim ki bàà dáá gbíntìi bí díǹ.

eng: But I say to you that it will be better for the land of Sodom on the Judgment Day than it will be for you.
local: An puee ní m tùkù-ni yii, ibɔsoon dalee, Soodoom tiŋki tafadaaldi dāa sɔ́ǹ nimbi yal.».

eng: At that time Jesus said, I praise you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because youve hidden these things from the wise and intelligent and have shown them to babies.
local: Ŋyunti gbantee ní Yeesu bí yii: «Óooo, m-Baa, yilpu nì taapu Dindaan, m nyɔ́ŋkí-si a nín dū tibɔti nee ki bál̀ bin yíì bi-ba biciŋfitiibi nì binyiliibi, ki dū-tì ki dàkā bin sá ŋwaamuee.

eng: Indeed, Father, this brings you happiness.
local: M-Baa, tiŋman a ŋá míǹ kun puee míǹ di ŋān̄ a-cee ní ki fáatì-si.

eng: My Father has handed all things over to me. No one knows the Son except the Father. And nobody knows the Father except the Son and anyone to whom the Son wants to reveal him.
local: «M-Baa dū tikɔkɔ ki ŋúbiǹ-mi, ní báà ubɔ kaa nyí man u-Jipɔɔn, asee m-Baa baba. Míǹ ní ubɔ mun kaa tí nyí m-Baa asee man u-Jipɔɔn baba, nì bin man u-Jipɔɔnee yaa là kí dū-u kí dàkèe.

eng: Come to me, all you who are struggling hard and carrying heavy loads, and I will give you rest.
local: «Nimbi bin tú awaŋgul ki gīīyee, dāam̄maan man cee ḿ túutí-ni ní ŋùŋfì.

eng: Put on my yoke, and learn from me. Im gentle and humble. And you will find rest for yourselves.
local: kí yòom̄maan man kpaambooku kí fōōmaan m-bɔti. Kun puee m-poon mɔ̄, ní m-bɔti kaa pɔ̄ɔ̄, ni làá ká diŋuŋfil.

eng: Are you the one who is to come, or should we look for another.
local: «Sii di sá un ti nyí yii u dòońeeyaaa, tí nīn kā ki cí ubɔ ní?».

eng: My yoke is easy to bear, and my burden is light.
local: Man kpaambooku pɔ̀fì ní m-waŋgul mun bí fúku fuku.».

eng: Jesus responded, Go, report to John what you hear and see.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-bi yii: «Nín̄ cámaan kí māafì Yoowaneesi tin ni kée nì tin ni gbìl̄ee.

eng: Those who were blind are able to see . Those who were crippled are walking. People with skin diseases are cleansed. Those who were deaf now hear. Those who were dead are raised up. The poor have good news proclaimed to them .
local: Bijɔfub nimbiliŋ ŋúbitì, bideembi cōom̀, binyaŋgbantiibi wunti ŋáŋkì, bitafakpaafub kíǹ ki gbìl̀, bitaŋkpiibi fīkitì, m sòon̄ tibɔmɔntiil ki tīī binandambi.

eng: Happy are those who dont stumble and fall because of me.
local: Unil un fōō-mi ki kíí kaa gùgufēe bí mmɔnneeee!».

eng: When Johns disciples had gone, Jesus spoke to the crowds about John: What did you go out to the wilderness to see? A stalk blowing in the wind?.
local: Yoowaneesi boonnooliibi búntèe ní Yeesu bíi sòoǹ Yoowaneesi pu samaayee cee ki bí yii: «Ni nín cūtì kuteeun ki kpēē Yoowaneesee, dinicil dilal ní u sá? U sá kii dikaliŋgbaandi din kubuŋu fīintéeee?.

eng: What did you go out to see? A man dressed up in refined clothes? Look, those who wear refined clothes are in royal palaces.
local: Ba ní ni cūtì ki kpēē ní? Unil un pēē agaajabɔkuteeee? Bin pēē agaajabɔkutee, bi kāntí bíǹ abeecinni ní!.

eng: What did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and more than a prophet.
local: Kin, ba ní ni baasii cūtì niin ki kpēē ní? Ni cūtì ki kpēē Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu níii? Iiin tiŋman, m tùkù-ni yii, un ní ni ká niinee jítiǹ Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu pu gba.

eng: At that time Jesus went through the wheat fields on the Sabbath. His disciples were hungry so they were picking heads of wheat and eating them.
local: Boonee ní Yeesu nì u-boonnooliibi bíi jītí bilee kpaaŋŋin ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa dal. Ŋkùm cúú u-boonnooliibee, ní bi bíi gífí bilee fiŋyi ki ŋmèem̄ abil ki ŋmóò.

eng: A man with a withered hand was there. Wanting to bring charges against Jesus, they asked, Does the Law allow a person to heal on the Sabbath.
local: Uninja ubɔ nìn bí niin, u-ŋal fáká, ní bi bālifì Yeesu yii: «Ti-marau pú nsan bí cáaǹ ubunli ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa dalaaa?» Kun pu ní bi nìn bàlifī-u mimmee di sá bi nìn nyàab̀ nnaakpal kí kɔ́ǹ-u tibɔtin.

eng: Jesus replied, Who among you has a sheep that falls into a pit on the Sabbath and will not take hold of it and pull it out?.
local: Ní Yeesu bālifì-bi yii: «Ni-kansikin ubɔ di yaa cáá dipibaabil ní dì yaa jéetì mboon ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa dalee, waa cá kí nyāntí-dàaa?.

eng: How much more valuable is a person than a sheep! So the Law allows a person to do what is good on the Sabbath.
local: Kin, ŋma nín kàlaasì unil un jítiǹ upii kpataaayee. Míǹ puee an pú nsan tí ŋá tiŋan kí tīī unil ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa dal.».

eng: Then Jesus said to the man, Stretch out your hand. So he did and it was made healthy, just like the other one.
local: Ní u bí kijapaayee yii: «Tàntī a-ŋal!» Ní u tāntì-ŋu. Niinee ní u-ŋal pɔ́ɔkì, ki ŋáŋkì kii ŋun béeyee.

eng: The Pharisees went out and met in order to find a way to destroy Jesus.
local: Niinee, Fariisab búntì ki cūtì ki kpáfì kí kpèè bi nín làá ŋá pu kí kpɔ̀ Yeesuee.

eng: Jesus knew what they intended to do, so he went away from there. Large crowds followed him, and he healed them all.
local: Yeesu nín béè mimmee, ní u yíkì niin. Binib tikpil nóó-u ní u cāantí bibunliibi kɔkɔ.

eng: But he ordered them not to spread the word about him.
local: Ama ní u nìn kpáafí-bi tiŋan bí taa tùkù báà ubɔ un ní u sée.

eng: so that what was spoken through Isaiah the prophet might be fulfilled.
local: An ŋá míǹ kí dàkà Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu Yeesaya nín nìn lī tibɔti tiŋyee dee, yii.

eng: Look, my Servant whom I chose, the one I love, in whom I find great pleasure . Ill put my Spirit upon him, and hell announce judgment to the Gentiles.
local: «Unimbɔti bí yii: <Kpèè m-tɔntɔnliu un m lēēyee sèé. Úǹ ní m néeń ní m-pɔbil sɔ̀m̀ u-pu. M làá dū m-Fam kí ŋá u-pu. Úǹ di làá dāá sòoǹ deedee bɔti kí tùkù atimbun kɔkɔ.

eng: He wont argue or shout, and nobody will hear his voice in the streets.
local: Waa làá nyàab̀ báà ubɔ tibɔti, kaa làá dāá nīn tátí, baa làá dāá gbìl̄ u-nyɔkɔ pu sampaambi pu.

eng: When the Pharisees saw this, they said to him, Look, your disciples are breaking the Sabbath law.
local: Fariisab nín ká mimmee, ní bi bí Yeesu yii: «Kpèè a-boonnooliibi bíi ŋáań min ti-marau kòó ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa dalee!».

eng: He wont break a bent stalk, and he wont snuff out a smoldering wick, until he makes justice win.
local: U làá dāá tūtuǹ biwɔfib, kaa làá dāá púntí mmɔ́ɔn ŋun gíì ki gbàkēe, kaa làá dāá gbíntí fitila un fòol̄ bílbilee. U làá nīn ŋáań míǹ ní áli ibaamɔn ń dāá jī mpaan.

eng: And the Gentiles will put their hope in his name.
local: Ní atimbun kɔkɔ ń dāá nīn kíǹ ki máká u-pu.>».

eng: They brought to Jesus a demon-possessed man who was blind and unable to speak. Jesus healed him so that he could both speak and see.
local: Niinee ní bi cáań uninja ubɔ Yeesu cee, arasinii kā u-pu, u sá umɔɔmiin ki sá ujɔfu mun. Yeesu nìn cáaǹ-u, ní u kíǹ ki ŋūǹ ki sòoǹ ní ki wāĺ mun.

eng: All the crowds were amazed and said, This man couldnt be the Son of David, could he.
local: Ní kunipaau kun yú niinee bākatì sèeee ní ki lì yii: «Daafiidi naantiiuee kaa kalin sɔ̀ɔɔ?».

eng: When the Pharisees heard, they said, This man throws out demons only by the authority of Beelzebul, the ruler of the demons.
local: Ama Fariisab nín gbìl̄ bi lī mimmee, ní bíǹ bí yii: «An sá arasiniibi bɔtiu Beelseebuul di tīī uninja nee mpɔn ní u cáa jì arasiniibi.».

eng: Because Jesus knew what they were thinking, he replied, Every kingdom involved in civil war becomes a wasteland. Every city or house torn apart by divisions will collapse.
local: Ama Yeesu nyí bi-landɔkɔi, ní ki bí-bi yii: «Dibeel din nib di yaa cúu yākatì ki jáà nì tɔbee, see dí kúntí, dàa ŋūǹ kí nīn bí ki cá. Kitiŋ kin kamaa di yaa fātì ki bíi jáaǹ ki-bee, kaa ŋūǹ kí nīn bí ki cá. Míǹ ní kudicaŋu kun kamaa di yaa jáaǹ ku-bee, kaa ŋūǹ kí nīn bí ki cá.

eng: If Satan throws out Satan, he is at war with himself. How then can his kingdom endure?.
local: Míǹ mbaantiim, Sitaan yaa fātìi bíi jì Sitaanee, mimmee u yākatì ki jáaǹ u-ba dee, mana ní u-beel ŋūǹ kí nīn bí ki cá?.

eng: And if I throw out demons by the authority of Beelzebul, then by whose authority do your followers throw them out? Therefore, they will be your judges.
local: Ni bí yii arasiniibi bɔtiu Beelseebuul di tīī-mi mpɔn ní m jì arasiniibi, kin ŋma tīī ni-jipɔmbi bíǹ mpɔn ní bi cáa jì arasiniibi? Ni-jipɔmbi bi-ba dàkà yii ni bí diyìntil poon.

eng: But if I throw out demons by the power of Gods Spirit, then Gods kingdom has already overtaken you.
local: Ama an yaa sá nì Unimbɔti Fam pɔŋŋu ní m jì arasiniibee, mimmee Unimbɔti Beel pɔŋŋu bāań-ni dee.

eng: Can people go into a house that belongs to a strong man and steal his possessions, unless they first tie up the strong man? Then they can rob his house.
local: «A yaa kaa bóob́ upɔɔl, aa ŋūǹ kí kɔ́ u-dumpu kí kóoǹ u-wanti. Asee a yaa bóob́-u ki dóòyee, ní á nín kɔ́ kí kóoǹ u-wanti.

eng: But he said to them, Havent you read what David did when he and those with him were hungry?.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-bi yii: «Naa láá pée kàan̄ dalba ŋkùm nín nìn cúú Daafiidi nì u-naacimbi bi ŋá pueeee?.

eng: Whoever isnt with me is against me, and whoever doesnt gather with me scatters.
local: «Un yaa kaa bí man jandi púee jáà nì man dee, ní un kaa bíi tútúǹ-mi m kōontí ki kpáfée, udaan bíi kàfī ki yàantí dee.

eng: Therefore, I tell you that people will be forgiven for every sin and insult to God. But insulting the Holy Spirit wont be forgiven.
local: An pu ní m tùkù-ni yii ikpiti kamaa nì tibɔfan tin unil pée ŋūǹ kí sòoǹee, Unimbɔti ŋūǹ kí dūú pɔ̄ udaan. Ama un di yaa sòon̄ ki yīntiǹ Unimbɔti Famee kpá kí ká u-kpitii pɔ̄tam.

eng: And whoever speaks a word against the Human One will be forgiven. But whoever speaks against the Holy Spirit wont be forgiven, not in this age or in the age that is coming.
local: Un di yaa sòon̄ ki yīntiǹ man Kinibikee ŋūǹ kí ká u-kpitii pɔ̄tam. Ama, un di yaa sòon̄ ki yīntiǹ Unimbɔti Famee, u kpá kí ká u-kpitii pɔ̄tam ŋyunti nee poonaaa, ki kpáaǹ ŋyunti ŋun dāa bí ki dòońee ni.».

eng: Either consider the tree good and its fruit good, or consider the tree rotten and its fruit rotten. A tree is known by its fruit.
local: «Kí wàatī á ká disubimɔntiilee, see á nīn cáá busumɔntiibu. A yaa cáá busukpitibuee, see á ká asubikpiti mun. Busubu biliŋ pu ní bi béetí-bu.

eng: Children of snakes! How can you speak good things while you are evil? What fills the heart comes out of the mouth.
local: Nimbi bin tū kii ikoowaatee! Nimbi bin sá binikpitibee! Mana ní ni ŋūǹ kí sòoǹ tibɔmɔntiil? Tin gbí dipɔbilinee, tíǹ ní kinyɔkɔ sòoǹ. Unimɔnti lèekī tin ŋān̄ee u-bɔnyaanti tin sá u-gaajatee poon ní.

eng: Good people bring out good things from their good treasure. But evil people bring out evil things from their evil treasure.
local: ní unikpiti pɔbikpitil mun sá-u tigaajati ní u lèekī tikpitil di-poon.

eng: I tell you that people will have to answer on Judgment Day for every useless word they speak.
local: M tùkù ki cántí-ni yii, ibɔsoon dalee Unimbɔti làá dāá bàlifì tibɔfan tin bi sòoǹee kɔkɔ pu.

eng: By your words you will be either judged innocent or condemned as guilty.
local: Kun puee a-nyɔɔbuŋŋi pu ní Unimbɔti dāa dì kí pɔ̀ a-bɔti, a-nyɔɔbuŋŋi pu ní u dāa tí dì kí kpɔ̀ a-bɔti.».

eng: At that time some of the legal experts and the Pharisees requested of Jesus, Teacher, we would like to see a sign from you.
local: Niinee ní bimaradakaliibi nì Fariisab biba bí Yeesu yii: «Ukpil, ŋá maamaaci ubɔ kí dàkà-taa!».

eng: But he replied, An evil and unfaithful generation searches for a sign, but it wont receive any sign except Jonahs sign.
local: Ní u kíí-bi yii: «Nimbi waanee nib sá binikpitib, ni nyēē Unimbɔti boon, ní ki bàlifī yii ḿ ŋá maamaaci ubɔ kí dàkà-ni.» Naa làá ká maamaaci ubɔ asee un náań nì Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu Yoona yɔuee.

eng: He went into Gods house and broke the law by eating the bread of the presence, which only the priests were allowed to eat.
local: U nìn kɔ́ kunimbɔtidiin ní usaraaŋakpil tīī-u kpɔnɔb bin bi nìn dūu ŋá Unimbɔti saraayee u nì u-naacimbi ŋmɔ́, ama ti-marau poonee, an kaa nìn pú nsan yii u nì u-naacimbi ń ŋmɔ́ kpɔnɔb gbanti an yaa kaa sá usaraaŋal.

eng: Just as Jonah was in the whales belly for three days and three nights , so the Human One will be in the heart of the earth for three days and three nights.
local: Kii Yoona nín nìn ŋá ujɔŋkpaan poon iwiin ita ŋwiimpu nì kunyeeu ki dāa nyánnée, míǹ ní man Kinibiki làá dāá ŋá iwiin ita ŋwiimpu nì kunyeeu dikaakulin ní kí dāá fìkitī.

eng: The citizens of Nineveh will stand up at the judgment with this generation and condemn it as guilty, because they changed their hearts and lives in response to Jonahs preaching. And look, someone greater than Jonah is here.
local: Ibɔsoon dalee, kitiŋ kin bi yíì Niiniifee yab làá dāá kpɔ̀ nimbi waanee nib bɔti, kun puee, Yoona nín nìn cūtì ki sòon̄ nì bi Unimbɔti bɔtee, bi nìn kpántì bi-bimbim ní. Ama, ubɔ bí doo ki jítiǹ Yoona ní ki sòon̄ nimbi kaa kpántì ni-bimbim.

eng: The queen of the South will be raised up by God at the judgment with this generation and condemn it because she came from a distant land to hear Solomons wisdom. And look, someone greater than Solomon is here.
local: Ibɔsoon dalee, Seeba tiŋki nimpubɔtiu làá dāá yīkì kí kpɔ̀ waanee nib bɔti, kun puee úǹ nìn nyánní doooo kitiŋgbeennyɔkɔ ní ki dɔ́miń ki pílifì Saloomɔn nín sòoǹ nì iciin puee, ama ubɔ bí doo ki jítiǹ Saloomɔn.

eng: When an unclean spirit leaves a person, it wanders through dry places looking for a place to rest. But it doesnt find any.
local: «Arasinii yaa nyáń unilinee, u búntí ki yìiǹ kitimpiliŋ pu ní kí nyàab̀ ŋkɔŋkoon. U yaa nyàab̄ ki gīī, kaa kée.

eng: Then it says, Ill go back to the house I left. When it arrives, it finds the place vacant, cleaned up, and decorated.
local: ní ú bí yii: <M làá fàtīí gítí laakin m nìn nyánnée.> U yaa fātì niin ki ká ŋkɔŋkoonee dɔ̄ fám ki ŋātì dándanee.

eng: Then it goes and brings with it seven other spirits more evil than itself. They go in and make their home there. That person is worse off at the end than at the beginning. This is the way it will be also for this evil generation.
local: ní ú cùú cáań arasiniibi biluli bin gíti sá bikpitib ki jítiǹ úŋ̀wuee, ní bi-kɔkɔ ń dāan̄ kí kpāfì kí kàl̄ udaan pu, ní ú mɔ̀tīí bīil̀. Míǹ mbaantiim ní an dāa sá ki tīī waanee nib binikpitib.».

eng: While Jesus was speaking to the crowds, his mother and brothers stood outside trying to speak with him.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun Yeesu dáá bíi sòoǹ nì kunipaauee, ní u-na nì u-naalib bāań ki yú mmɔŋki ki bàà nyàab̀ kí sòoǹ nì u.

eng: Someone said to him, Look, your mother and brothers are outside wanting to speak with you.
local: Ní ubɔ bí-u yii: «Kpèè, a-na nì a-naalib yú mmɔŋki ki nyàab̀ kí sòoǹ nì si.».

eng: Jesus replied, Who is my mother? Who are my brothers.
local: Ní Yeesu fātìi kíí kijapaayee, yii: «Ŋma di sá m-na ní bilab di sá m-naalib, kpèè bin sá m-na nì m-naalibee sèé.».

eng: He stretched out his hand toward his disciples and said, Look, here are my mother and my brothers.
local: Ní u dàkā u-boonnooliibi ŋŋal ki bí yii: «Kpèè, m-na nì m-naalib sèé!.

eng: Or havent you read in the Law that on the Sabbath the priests in the temple treat the Sabbath as any other day and are still innocent?.
local: Àá, naa kàan̄ Mooyiisi marab gbɔŋkun yii ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa dalee, bisaraaŋaliibi yìntī ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa marau kunimbɔtidiin, an kaa sá-bi ikpiti?.

eng: Whoever does the will of my Father who is in heaven is my brother, sister, and mother.
local: Un kamaa di yaa ŋáań m-Baa un bí yilpuee nín là puee, udaan di sá m-naal, m-niisal àá m-na.».

eng: But I tell you that something greater than the temple is here.
local: Ama m tùkù ki cántí-ni yii, man un jítiǹ kunimbɔtidiiyee bí doo.

eng: If you had known what this means, I want mercy and not sacrifice , you wouldnt have condemned the innocent.
local: Ni yaa bàà nyímaan Unimbɔti nyɔɔbundi nee taapu yii: <Maa là saraabi, m là ní nīn sūǹ tɔb saai ní,> naa bàà kù ibaamɔndambi nee bɔti fám.

eng: The Human One is Lord of the Sabbath.
local: Kun puee man Kinibiki di sá ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa daan.».

eng: Jesus left that place and went into their synagogue.
local: Yeesu nyáń niinee ní ki búntì bi-kpafidiiku kubɔn.

eng: That day Jesus went out of the house and sat down beside the lake.
local: Ŋwiin gbantee, Yeesu nyáń kudiin ní ki cūtìi kāl̀ dimɔɔgbandi.

eng: Jesus disciples came and said to him, Why do you use parables when you speak to the crowds.
local: Yeesu boonnooliibi nìn sútìń ki pìl̄-u ní ki bālifì-u yii: «Ba pu ní a cáá tibɔŋmantikaal ki sòoǹ nì bi ní?».

eng: Jesus replied, Because they havent received the secrets of the kingdom of heaven, but you have.
local: Ní u kíí-bi yii: «Unimbɔti yāntì nimbi béè u-Beel bɔti tin bí dibalinee taapu. Ama an kaa yāntì bíǹ ń béé.

eng: For those who have will receive more and they will have more than enough. But as for those who dont have, even the little they have will be taken away from them.
local: Un yaa cááyee, bi làá gíti kūtì-u u-cee ń wíiǹ. Ama un kaa cááyee, bi dāa fòol̄ fiii un u cááyee ní ú gbíntí pílpil.

eng: This is why I speak to the crowds in parables: although they see, they dont really see; and although they hear, they dont really hear or understand.
local: Kun pu ní m cáá tibɔŋmantikaal ki sòoǹ nì bee dee: di sá bi kpèé ní kaa wāĺ, ki pílifí ní kaa gbìl̀.

eng: What Isaiah prophesied has become completely true for them: You will hear, to be sure, but never understand; and you will certainly see but never recognize what you are seeing .
local: Bíǹ puee an ŋá kii Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu Yeesaya nín nìn lī puee dee, yii Unimbɔti bí yii: <Ni làá dāá nīn pílifí, ama ní kaa làá gbìl̄. Ni làá dāá kpèè ama ní kaa làá wāl̀.

eng: For this peoples senses have become calloused, and theyve become hard of hearing, and theyve shut their eyes so that they wont see with their eyes or hear with their ears or understand with their minds, and change their hearts and lives that I may heal them .
local: Kun puee binib nee pɔbiliŋ tīlifì, ní bi kpáá bi-tafaliŋ ní ki ŋúb́ bi-nimbiliŋ, kí taa wāl̀aaa, kí taa gbìl̄aaa, kí taa béé taapu, kí taa kpántí bi-bimbim, ḿ bàà cáaǹ-bi.>.

eng: Happy are your eyes because they see. Happy are your ears because they hear.
local: Ama nimbee, ni bí mmɔnni kun puee ni-nimbiliŋ wāĺ man nì m-tuuŋŋi, ni-tafaliŋ mun gbìl̀ m-bɔti.

eng: I assure you that many prophets and righteous people wanted to see what you see and hear what you hear, but they didnt.
local: Ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-ni, Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi nì deedee nib tikpil bàà nìn nyàab̀ kí ká kun ni ká nee, kaa ŋúń ki ká, bi bàà nìn nyàab̀ kí gbìl̄ tin ni gbìl̄ nee, kaa ŋúń ki gbìl̄-tì.».

eng: Consider then the parable of the farmer.
local: «Mimmee, pīlifìmaan uwambooyaanli bɔŋmantikaati din m lēe taapu.

eng: Whenever people hear the word about the kingdom and dont understand it, the evil one comes and carries off what was planted in their hearts. This is the seed that was sown on the path.
local: Un pílifì bi nín sòon̄ Unimbɔti Beel bɔti ní kaa gbìl̀ee, náań nì ŋwambool ŋun lítì nsan puee ní. Sitaan ukpiti di dòoń ki cútí tin bi búĺ u-pɔbilinee ki lèekī.

eng: Such large crowds gathered around him that he climbed into a boat and sat down. The whole crowd was standing on the shore.
local: Kunipaau nìn kóoǹní ki kpáfì u-cee, ní u jɔ́m̀ ki kāl̀ buŋalimbun nnyim pu, ní kunipaauee yú dibuŋgbándi.

eng: As for the seed that was spread on rocky ground, this refers to people who hear the word and immediately receive it joyfully.
local: Ní un náań nì abil ŋin lítì kuteeunee, úǹ pílifí tibɔtee ní ki fòol̄-tì malaa nì dipɔɔpindi.

eng: Because they have no roots, they last for only a little while. When they experience distress or abuse because of the word, they immediately fall away.
local: ama waa yàntī tibɔtee ń kàl̄ u-ni kí yúntí kí báań inyaan. Unimbɔti bɔti puee, digìńdi kii disàandi diba yaa bāań-uee, ní ú dū nnimbɔtisan kí fá.

eng: As for the seed that was spread among thorny plants, this refers to those who hear the word, but the worries of this life and the false appeal of wealth choke the word, and it bears no fruit.
local: Un mun náań nì abil ŋin lítì ikuŋkuminee gbìl̄ Unimbɔti bɔti, ní duulinya poon landɔkɔi nì tigaajati tin ŋmàtíǹ binibee neendi ń dāá dūú múkúǹ an bɔti pu, ní tàa ŋūǹ kí lòoǹ.

eng: As for what was planted on good soil, this refers to those who hear and understand, and bear fruit and produce—in one case a yield of one hundred to one, in another case a yield of sixty to one, and in another case a yield of thirty to one.
local: Un pílifí Unimbɔti bɔti ki gbìl̀ taapuee làá tɔ̄ atummɔnti ŋin tìì Unimbɔti yindi tinyoolee kii iwambool in lítì kitimmɔntii pu ŋubɔ lòon̄ abil dilaataal, ŋubɔ mun imuŋku ita, ŋubɔ mun mmuŋku nì saalaayee ní.».

eng: Jesus told them another parable: The kingdom of heaven is like someone who planted good seed in his field.
local: Ní Yeesu gíti dū tibɔŋmantikaal nee ki sòon̄ nì bi yii: «Unimbɔti Beel náań nì uninja ubɔ un búĺ iwamboomɔnti u-kpaabunee ní.

eng: While people were sleeping, an enemy came and planted weeds among the wheat and went away.
local: Kunyeeu, báà ŋma kɔ́ ki dōoǹee, ní u-nannanliu dɔ́miń ki dū ukeenja ki búĺ idin, ní ki búntì.

eng: When the stalks sprouted and bore grain, then the weeds also appeared.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun idi nyáń ki lòon̄ee, ní ukeenja mun nyáń.

eng: The servants of the landowner came and said to him, Master, didnt you plant good seed in your field? Then how is it that it has weeds.
local: Ní bitɔntɔnliibi dɔ́miń ki bí bi-kpiliu yii: <Ukpil, an kaa sá idi ní a búĺ bukpaanaaa? Kin ukeenja mun nyáń la?>.

eng: An enemy has done this, he answered. The servants said to him, Do you want us to go and gather them.
local: Ní ukpilee kíí-bi yii: <An sá unannanli ubɔ di ŋá míǹ.> Ní bitɔntɔnliibi bālifì-u yii: <Mimmee, tí cù kí fùkù ukeenja gbanti kí fáaa?>.

eng: But the landowner said, No, because if you gather the weeds, youll pull up the wheat along with them.
local: Ní u kíí-bi yii: <Aayee, taa fùkùmaan-u, kun puee ni yaa làá fùkùee, see án kpáaǹ idi kí fùkù.

eng: He said many things to them in parables: A farmer went out to scatter seed.
local: Ní u cáá tibɔŋmantikaal ki sòoǹ nì bi tibɔti tikpil, ní ki bí yii: «Uninja ubɔ di nyáń kí yàaǹ iwambool.

eng: Let both grow side by side until the harvest. And at harvesttime Ill say to the harvesters,First gather the weeds and tie them together in bundles to be burned. But bring the wheat into my barn.
local: Míǹ puee, yàntīmaan an kɔkɔ ń nīn yú ki cí kudiceeu. Ŋyunti gbantee ní ḿ tùkù-bi yii: Fùkùmaan ukeenjee kí bíĺ afeencun afeencun kí tūū ŋŋmi, ama cáámaan m-dii kí ŋá m-cakalaaun.>».

eng: He told another parable to them: The kingdom of heaven is like a mustard seed that someone took and planted in his field.
local: Ní Yeesu gíti tí dū tibɔti tiba ki ŋmāntiǹ-bi yii: «Unimbɔti Beel náań nì kisubilii kin uninja ubɔ cáa búĺ u-kpaabunee.

eng: Its the smallest of all seeds. But when its grown, its the largest of all vegetable plants. It becomes a tree so that the birds in the sky come and nest in its branches.
local: Kí mun di sá kisubifiifiitii asubil kɔkɔ kansikin, ama kì yaa nyáń ki wíiǹ ki dóòyee, kì wīikíǹ tikuŋfaal tin kɔkɔ kíǹee, ní kí kpántí busubu, áli nnɔmbiyaamu dòoń ki pāantí mu-diiŋŋi bu-paŋŋi pu.».

eng: He told them another parable: The kingdom of heaven is like yeast, which a woman took and hid in a bushel of wheat flour until the yeast had worked its way through all the dough.
local: Ní Yeesu gíti tí dū tibɔŋmantikaal tiba mun ki tí ŋmāntiǹ-bi yii: «Unimbɔti Beel náań nì ŋkpɔnɔnyɔkɔ ní. Unimpu dù fiii ní kí ŋmàlim̀ ŋkpɔnɔyim tikpil poon kí búuń ní bukpɔnɔsaa kɔkɔ ń jɔ̄m̀.».

eng: Jesus said all these things to the crowds in parables, and he spoke to them only in parables.
local: Yeesu nìn dù tibɔŋmantikaal ní ki sòoǹ nì samaa. Waa nìn sòoǹ nì bi fám see ú dūú ŋmàntīǹ tiba.

eng: This was to fulfill what the prophet spoke: Ill speak in parables; Ill declare what has been hidden since the beginning of the world.
local: Mimmee, an ŋá kii Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu nín nìn sòon̄ ki síiǹ puee dee, yii: «Nì tibɔŋmantikaal ní m làá dāá dūú sòoǹ nì bi, m làá dāá tùkù-bi doooo m nín náań duulinyee tin bí dibalinee.».

eng: Jesus left the crowds and went into the house. His disciples came to him and said, Explain to us the parable of the weeds in the field.
local: Niinee, Yeesu yāntì kunipaauee ní ki nín kùǹ, ní u-boonnooliibi sútì ki pìl̄-u ki bí-u yii: «Tùkù-ti ukeenja un bi cáa búĺ bukpaanee bɔŋmantikaati taapu.».

eng: Jesus replied, The one who plants the good seed is the Human One.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-bi yii: «Un būul̀ idee di sá man Kinibiki.

eng: The field is the world. And the good seeds are the followers of the kingdom. But the weeds are the followers of the evil one.
local: Bukpaayee di sá duulinya, ní idi sá Unimbɔti Beelin biyaamu, ní ukeenjee sá Sitaan biyaamu.

eng: The enemy who planted them is the devil. The harvest is the end of the present age. The harvesters are the angels.
local: Unannanli un dɔ́miń ki búĺ ukeenjee di sá Sitaan. Kudiceeu di sá duulinya kúntitam, ní bidicaliibi sá Unimbɔti tuuŋyi.

eng: As he was scattering seed, some fell on the path, and birds came and ate it.
local: U bíi yàantée ní abil aba lítì nsan pu, ní nnɔmbiyaamu dɔ́miń ki tōō ki jíń.

eng: Just as people gather weeds and burn them in the fire, so it will be at the end of the present age.
local: Kii bi nín béeĺ ukeenja pu kí bíĺ afeencun kí tūū puee, míǹ ní an dāa sá duulinya kúntitam.

eng: The Human One will send his angels, and they will gather out of his kingdom all things that cause people to fall away and all people who sin.
local: Man Kinibiki làá dāá tɔ̄ń m-tuuŋyi ní í dāá lēē m-Beelin bin kɔkɔ sá nsugimu bi-jutiibi gbéetí ki lítée nì bin ŋáań ikpitee.

eng: He will throw them into a burning furnace. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: ní kí dū-bi kí tɔ̄ ŋŋmin kipaayin, laakin bi làá nīn kā ki sūǹ, bi-nyiŋŋi gà tɔb kàkakee.

eng: Then the righteous will shine like the sun in their Fathers kingdom. Those who have ears should hear.
local: Ama ní deedeedambi dāa jìl̀ kii ŋwìińee ní pàlpal bi-Baa Beelin. Un yaa cáá ditafalee ń gbìl̄.».

eng: The kingdom of heaven is like a treasure that somebody hid in a field, which someone else found and covered up. Full of joy, the finder sold everything and bought that field.
local: «Unimbɔti Beel náań nì kii tigaajati tin bál̀ bukpaa bubɔnee ní. Ubɔ kùn̄ ki bīitì ti-pu ní gíiǹ ki bīiǹ. An nín mɔ́ɔkiǹ-u puee, ní u cūtì ki nyáfì u-wanti tin kɔkɔ u cááyee pílpil ki cáa dá bukpaayee.

eng: Again, the kingdom of heaven is like a merchant in search of fine pearls.
local: Unimbɔti Beel tí náań nì kii unyampu nín nyàab̀ abeedimbimɔnti puee ní.

eng: When he found one very precious pearl, he went and sold all that he owned and bought it.
local: U nín ká ŋin cáá kudapɔɔuee ní ú cùú nyāfì tin kɔkɔ u cááyee pílpil ní kí cáań kí dā.».

eng: Again, the kingdom of heaven is like a net that people threw into the lake and gathered all kinds of fish.
local: «Unimbɔti Beel náań nì kii bi nín dù kulaaŋŋu kí tɔ̄ dimɔɔlin án cúú kujɔmbuluu kamaa puee ní.

eng: When it was full, they pulled it to the shore, where they sat down and put the good fish together into containers. But the bad fish they threw away.
local: Bi nín dū kulaaŋŋu ki tɔ́ an cúú ijɔmee, ní bí dáaĺ kí nyāntiń dimɔɔgbandi, ní kí kàl̄ kí lútí imɔnti kí ŋá tikabitin, ní kí dū in kaa ŋān̄ee kí tɔ̄ kí fá.

eng: Thats the way it will be at the end of the present age. The angels will go out and separate the evil people from the righteous people.
local: Míǹ ní duulinya kúntitamee, án làá dāá ŋá. Unimbɔti tuuŋyi dāa dòoń ní kí gàntī binikpitib nì bimɔntiibi.

eng: Other seed fell on rocky ground where the soil was shallow. They sprouted immediately because the soil wasnt deep.
local: Ní aba mun lítì kuteeun laakin kitiŋ kaa nyóòyee, ní ki pūtì kpáàa nyáń kun puee kitiŋ kaa nyóò.

eng: and will throw the evil ones into a burning furnace. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: ní kí dū bikpitibee kí tɔ̄ ŋŋmin kipaayin, niin ní bi làá nīn sūǹ bi-nyiŋŋi gà tɔb kàkaka.».

eng: Have you understood all these things?Jesus asked. They said to him, Yes.
local: Ní Yeesu bālifì u-boonnooliibi yii: «Ni gbìl̄ tibɔti tin kɔkɔ m sòon̄-ni nee taapuɔɔɔ?» Ní bi kíí yii: «Iiin ti gbìl̄».

eng: Then he said to them, Therefore, every legal expert who has been trained as a disciple for the kingdom of heaven is like the head of a household who brings old and new things out of their treasure chest.
local: Ní u bí-bi yii: «Míǹ ní umaradakal kamaa un di yaa fōō Unimbɔti Beel bɔtee náań nì kudiidaan un cáá tigaajati ki lèekī ti-poon tikpikil nì tipɔn ki cáa tútúǹ binibee ní.».

eng: When Jesus finished these parables, he departed.
local: Yeesu nín dū tibɔŋmantikaal ki sòon̄ nì bi ki dóòyee, ní u yíkì niin.

eng: When he came to his hometown, he taught the people in their synagogue. They were surprised and said, Where did he get this wisdom? Where did he get the power to work miracles?.
local: ní ki nín gítí u-tiŋkin laakin u kāl̀ ki wíiǹee. U bàn̄ee, ní ki kɔ́ niin kpafidiikun ki bíi dàkà u-dumpu yab Unimbɔti bɔti, ní bin kɔkɔ bí niinee bākatì sèeee, ní bi bíi lì yii: «U nín ká la iciin gbanti dee? An dī mana ní u ŋūǹ ki ŋáań maamaacib nee dee?.

eng: Isnt he the carpenters son? Isnt his mother named Mary? Arent James, Joseph, Simon, and Judas his brothers?.
local: Ti nyí-uaa! An sá kapinta jipɔɔnaa! U-na di sá Maariiyaa! U-naalib mun di sá Yakubu nì Jooseefi nì Siimɔn nì Juudaa!.

eng: And his sisters, arent they here with us? Where did this man get all this.
local: U-niisalib mun di kóò ti-kansikineeyaa! Kin, an ŋá mana ní u cáá mpɔn nee?».

eng: They were repulsed by him and fell into sin. But Jesus said to them, Prophets are honored everywhere except in their own hometowns and in their own households.
local: An di nìn yāntì ní baa fōō-u ki kíí. Ní Yeesu bí-bi yii: «Baa kpèé kí sìŋkīǹ Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu fám laaba, an yaa kaa tin sá u-tiŋkin nì u-cindin.».

eng: He was unable to do many miracles there because of their disbelief.
local: Bi nín kaa fōō-u ki kííyee pu ní waa nìn ŋá maamaacib tikpil niin.

eng: But when the sun came up, it scorched the plants, and they dried up because they had no roots.
local: Ŋwìiń dāa píĺee, ní an kpíì kun puee inyaan kaa kɔ́ kitiŋ.

eng: Other seed fell among thorny plants. The thorny plants grew and choked them.
local: Ní aba mun lítì ikuŋkumin, ní ikuŋkumee wíiǹ ki múkuǹ i-pu.

eng: Other seed fell on good soil and bore fruit, in one case a yield of one hundred to one, in another case a yield of sixty to one, and in another case a yield of thirty to one.
local: Ama ní abil aba lítì laakin kitiŋ ŋān̄ee ní ki lòon̄ abil. Ŋwambool ŋubɔ lòon̄ abil dilaataal, ŋubɔ imuŋku ita, ŋubɔ mun mmuŋku nì saalaa.».

eng: Everyone who has ears should pay attention.
local: Ní Yeesu bí yii: «Sii un yaa cáá ditafalee ń gbìl̄.».

eng: At that time Herod the ruler heard the news about Jesus.
local: Ŋyunti gbantee, Eeroodi un nìn sá ubɔti Galiileeyee gbìl̄ bi nín sòoǹ Yeesu pu miŋyee.

eng: Then he had John beheaded in prison.
local: ní ki tú ubɔ ń cù kusarkadiin kí tìlīń Yoowaneesi un fùul̀ binib Unimbɔti nyimee yil kí cáań.

eng: They brought his head on a plate and gave it to the young woman, and she brought it to her mother.
local: Ní bi tīl̀ Yoowaneesi yil ki ŋá kpaatan, ki cáań ki tīī usapɔɔnee, ní u mun cáa tīī u-na.

eng: But Johns disciples came and took his body and buried it. Then they went and told Jesus what had happened.
local: Yoowaneesi boonnooliibi nìn dɔ́miń ki yōoǹ-u ki cáa síb́, ní ki cūtì ki tùkū Yeesu tin jítèe.

eng: When Jesus heard about John, he withdrew in a boat to a deserted place by himself. When the crowds learned this, they followed him on foot from the cities.
local: Yeesu nín gbìl̄ mimmee, ní u yíkì niin u nì u-boonnooliibi kɔ́ buŋalimbu ki búntì laakin binib kpée, ama ní kunipaau nyánní ntimun ki còom̄ ataa ki nín jì u-boon.

eng: When Jesus arrived and saw a large crowd, he had compassion for them and healed those who were sick.
local: Yeesu nì u-boonnooliibi nyáń buŋalimbunee, ní ki ká kunipaau nín tíibińee. Binibee nìn cúú-u falaa ní u kíĺ ki bíi cāantí bibunliibi.

eng: That evening his disciples came and said to him, This is an isolated place and its getting late. Send the crowds away so they can go into the villages and buy food for themselves.
local: Kujoou bíi jóotée, ní u-boonnooliibi sútìń ki pìl̄-u, ní ki bí-u yii: «Binib kaa kóò laakin ti bí nee, kunyeeu mun bíi móotí. Míǹ puee, yàntī binib ń nīn cá ntimbilimun kí dā tijin kí jī.».

eng: But Jesus said to them, Theres no need to send them away. You give them something to eat.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-bi yii: «An kaa bàlifī bí cù, nimbi ni-ba ń tīīmaan-bi tijin bí jī.».

eng: They replied, We have nothing here except five loaves of bread and two fish.
local: Ní bi bí-u yii: «Kpɔnɔb biŋmoo nì ijɔm ili ja di bí.».

eng: He said, Bring them here to me.
local: Ní u bí yii: «Yòon̄ kí cáaḿmaan-mi!».

eng: He ordered the crowds to sit down on the grass. He took the five loaves of bread and the two fish, looked up to heaven, blessed them and broke the loaves apart and gave them to his disciples. Then the disciples gave them to the crowds.
local: Niinee, ní u bí yii samaa kɔkɔ ń kàl̄ kumɔsɔŋu pu, ní u fōō kpɔnɔb biŋmoo nì ijɔm ilee ní ki wáaǹ ki kpēē kutaa, ki jáam̀ Unimbɔti, ní ki gífì gífì kpɔnɔbee ki dūu tīī u-boonnooliibi, ní bíǹ dūu yākatì binib kɔkɔ.

eng: He said to his servants, This is John the Baptist. Hes been raised from the dead. This is why these miraculous powers are at work through him.
local: ní ki bí u-tɔntɔnliibi yii: «Unee di sá Yoowaneesi un fùul̀ binib Unimbɔti nyimee. U fātìi fīkitì ní, an pu ní u cáá mpɔn ki ŋáań maamaacib míǹ.».

eng: Everyone ate until they were full, and they filled twelve baskets with the leftovers.
local: Ní bi-kɔkɔ jíń ki díkí, an kíǹ, ní bi kóoǹ akpɔnɔkati nì ajɔŋgin ŋin kíǹee ki gbéeǹ tikabiti saalaa nì tili.

eng: About five thousand men plus women and children had eaten.
local: Binib bin nìn jíń tijin gbantee nìn bàn̄ kii bininjab kutukub biŋmoo, baa kàan̄ binimpoobi nì mbiyaamu.

eng: Right then, Jesus made the disciples get into the boat and go ahead to the other side of the lake while he dismissed the crowds.
local: Niinee ní Yeesu yāntì u-boonnooliibi kɔ́ buŋalimbu malaa ki līntì ki dūtì lapu buŋgbandi, ní Yeesu úǹ dáá bí niin ki tùkù binib yii bí nīn kùǹ.

eng: When he sent them away, he went up onto a mountain by himself to pray. Evening came and he was alone.
local: U yāntì bi nín kùǹee, ní u kākatì ŋkpaanceeti dijool pu kí mèè. Kunyeeu mɔ́ee u dáá gbíntìi bí niin u-baba.

eng: Meanwhile, the boat, fighting a strong headwind, was being battered by the waves and was already far away from land.
local: Buŋalimbuee mun nìn bàn̄ dimɔɔl kansikin kaa ti cá, kun puee kubuŋu nìn túŋkiń-bu ní tinyiŋgban nìn yāŋkí-bu.

eng: Very early in the morning he came to his disciples, walking on the lake.
local: Ki cá kutaawuntuŋuee, ní Yeesu tɔ́kɔǹ nnyim pu ki còom̄ ki cá u-boonnooliibi cee.

eng: When the disciples saw him walking on the lake, they were terrified and said, Its a ghost! They were so frightened they screamed.
local: Bi nín ká u páaǹní ki còomìń nnyim puee, ní bi-pɔbiliŋ máĺ, ní bi bí yii: «Ufaŋfanti dee!» ní tifaandi kɔ́-bi ní bi bíi gbáà ibiil.

eng: Just then Jesus spoke to them, Be encouraged! Its me. Dont be afraid.
local: Kpalaayee ní Yeesu bí-bi yii: «Cúúmaan ni-pɔbiliŋ, an sá man! Taa fàŋkìmaan!».

eng: Peter replied, Lord, if its you, order me to come to you on the water.
local: Niinee ní Peetroo bí-u yii: «M-Dindaan, an yaa lafun sá sii, á yàntī ḿ tɔ́kɔń nnyim pu kí còom̀ kí dāan̄ a-cee.».

eng: And Jesus said, Come. Then Peter got out of the boat and was walking on the water toward Jesus.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-u yii: «Nín̄ dòoń!» Ní Peetroo nyáń buŋalimbun ki tɔ́kɔǹ nnyim pu ki còom̀ ki nín cá Yeesu cee.

eng: Herod had arrested John, bound him, and put him in prison because of Herodias, the wife of Herods brother Philip.
local: Di sá yii Eeroodi nìn tīī mara bí cúú Yoowaneesi kí bóob́-u iŋmii ní kí cáá-u kí tɔ̄ sarkan. Kun pu ní u nìn ŋá mimmee di sá yii u nìn cáá u-maan Fiiliipi nimpuu, bi yíì-u Eeroodiyadi.

eng: But when Peter saw the strong wind, he became frightened. As he began to sink, he shouted, Lord, rescue me.
local: U nín bíi còom̄ ki cá ki ká kubuŋu nín jītí pu mpɔnee, ní u fāŋkì, ki kíĺ ki bíi múl̀ nnyimin, ní ki fáá mbiil yii: «M-Dindaan, fīī-mi.».

eng: Jesus immediately reached out and grabbed him, saying, You man of weak faith! Why did you begin to have doubts.
local: Kpalaayee ní Yeesu tāntì u-ŋal ki cúú-u ní ki bí-u yii: «A-fookiiu pátì, ba pu ní a fāŋkì?».

eng: When they got into the boat, the wind settled down.
local: Ní bi-liitil kɔ́ buŋalimbun, ní kubuŋu ŋmíl̀.

eng: Then those in the boat worshipped Jesus and said, You must be Gods Son.
local: Bin nìn kā buŋalimbunee gbāaǹ Yeesu nimbiin ki bí-u yii: «Ibaamɔn, a lafun sá Unimbɔti Jipɔɔn.».

eng: When they had crossed the lake, they landed at Gennesaret.
local: Bi dūtì dimɔɔl ki bàn̄ Geeneesareeti tiŋki jandi dibee ní ki kpákatì.

eng: When the people who lived in that place recognized him, they sent word throughout that whole region, and they brought to him everyone who was sick.
local: Niin yab kpáàa béè Yeesu, ní ki nākaǹ bi tùkū ajan kɔkɔ nib, ní bi yōoǹní bibunliibi kɔkɔ ki cáań u-cee.

eng: Then they begged him that they might just touch the edge of his clothes. Everyone who touched him was cured.
local: ki mèé-u yii ú yàntī bí gbēē u-bɔkutijúti kpaŋkpam, ní bin kɔkɔ lafun gbēēyee pɔ́ɔkì.

eng: Thats because John told Herod, Its against the law for you to marry her.
local: Yoowaneesi nìn tùkū Eeroodi yii: «An kaa máaǹ á cáá unimpu gbanti.».

eng: Although Herod wanted to kill him, he feared the crowd because they thought John was a prophet.
local: Niinee ní u kíǹ ki nyàab̀ kí kpɔ̀ Yoowaneesi, ama ní ki fàŋkí Juuda yab, kun puee báà ŋma nìn nyí Yoowaneesi pu yii u sá Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu.

eng: But at Herods birthday party Herodias daughter danced in front of the guests and thrilled Herod.
local: Daliŋkin bi bíi jīǹ Eeroodi maaliwiiŋŋu jiŋaaliuee, Eeroodiyadi bisal ŋɔ́ń bin bi yíiń jiŋaalee nimbiin, ní an mɔ́ɔkiǹ Eeroodi.

eng: Then he swore to give her anything she asked.
local: U nìn pūtì ki bí yii: «Kun kamaa ní a yaa lèe, á bàlifì-mi, m làá tīī-si kudɔŋu.».

eng: At her mothers urging, the girl said, Give me the head of John the Baptist here on a plate.
local: Ní usapɔɔnee na yāntì u lī yii: «Á tùkutī Yoowaneesi un fùul̀ binib Unimbɔti nyimee yil kí tàkan̄-mi kpaatan kɔŋkɔnnee.».

eng: Although the king was upset, because of his solemn pledge and his guests he commanded that they give it to her.
local: Niinee, an kɔ́ ubɔti kunimbaau. U nín gíti dáá pūtì bin u yíińee nimbiinee ní u kíí yii u làá tīī-u.

eng: Then Pharisees and legal experts came to Jesus from Jerusalem and said.
local: Fariisab nì bimaradakaliibi biba nyánní Jeerusaleem ki dɔ́miń Yeesu cee ní ki bālifì-u yii.

eng: Jesus called the crowd near and said to them, Listen and understand.
local: Míǹ boonee ní Yeesu yíiń kunipaauee u-cee ní ki bí-bi yii: «Ní pīlifì kí gbìl̄ tibɔti nee taapu tiŋan.

eng: Its not what goes into the mouth that contaminates a person in Gods sight. Its what comes out of the mouth that contaminates the person.
local: An kaa sá tin di kóò unil nyɔkɔnee di kɔ̄ntí-u tijɔkɔndi Unimbɔti nimbiin, ama an sá tin nyēē unil nyɔkɔnee di kɔ̄ntí-u tijɔkɔndi.».

eng: Then the disciples came and said to him, Do you know that the Pharisees were offended by what you just said.
local: Niinee, u-boonnooliibi sútìń ki bí-u yii: «Aa nyí yii tibɔti tin a bàà sòon̄ee wùn̄ Fariisabaaa?».

eng: Jesus replied, Every plant that my heavenly Father didnt plant will be pulled up.
local: Ní Yeesu bí yii: «Busubu bun kamaa ní m-Baa un bí yilpuee kaa cóoǹee, bi làá dāá fùkù-bu kí fá ní.

eng: Leave the Pharisees alone. They are blind people who are guides to blind people. But if a blind person leads another blind person, they will both fall into a ditch.
local: Yàntīmaan-bi. Bi sá bijɔfub ní ki dàtī bijɔfub: ujɔfu yaa dàtī ujɔfuee, see bi-liitil ń lítí mboon.».

eng: Then Peter spoke up, Explain this riddle to us.
local: Ní Peetroo bí-u yii: «Tùkù-ti dilaŋkpandi din a bàà gāāyee taapu.».

eng: Jesus said, Dont you understand yet?.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-u yii: «Ni mun kaa láá cáá iciin níii?.

eng: Dont you know that everything that goes into the mouth enters the stomach and goes out into the sewer?.
local: Naa nyí yii tin kɔkɔ kóò unil nyɔkɔnee cá u-poon, ní boonee ú cùú sɔ́ntáaa?.

eng: But what goes out of the mouth comes from the heart. And thats what contaminates a person in Gods sight.
local: Ama tin nyēē kinyɔkɔnee nyēē unil pɔbilin ní, an di kɔ̄ntí unil tijɔkɔndi Unimbɔti nimbiin.

eng: Out of the heart come evil thoughts, murders, adultery, sexual sins, thefts, false testimonies, and insults.
local: kun puee unil pɔbilin ní ilandɔkɔkpiti kɔkɔ nyēeń: nnikpaakoon, bininjab nì binimpoobi laakpaati, binaacimpɔmbi nì bisapɔmbi jɔkɔnti, bunaayubu, abɔgaan, asiibil.

eng: Why are your disciples breaking the elders rules handed down to us? They dont ritually purify their hands by washing before they eat.
local: «Ba pu ní a-boonnooliibi kaa nóó ti-yaajakoobiliŋ ní? Kun puee baa fíntí bi-ŋalii kí wàatī ní kí nín jī tijin, kii ti-koobiliŋ nín sá puee.».

eng: These contaminate a person in Gods sight. But eating without washing hands doesnt contaminate in Gods sight.
local: Tin kɔ̄ntí unil tijɔkɔndi Unimbɔti nimbiinee dee. An yaa sá á jī tijin kaa fíntì a-ŋalii kii akoobil nín sá puee, an kaa kɔ̄ntí-si tijɔkɔndi Unimbɔti nimbiin.».

eng: From there, Jesus went to the regions of Tyre and Sidon.
local: Boonee, Yeesu yíkì niin ki nín cá Tiir nì Siidɔn watil pú.

eng: A Canaanite woman from those territories came out and shouted, Show me mercy, Son of David. My daughter is suffering terribly from demon possession.
local: Ukanaannimpu ubɔ nìn nyánní niin pú ki dɔ́miń ní ki fáá mbiil yii: «Ukpil, Daafiidi naantiiu, sɔ̄ m-saai! Di sá arasinii ubɔ kā m-bisal pu ki páb́-u ŋkpitim pu.».

eng: But he didnt respond to her at all. His disciples came and urged him, Send her away; she keeps shouting out after us.
local: Ama ní Yeesu kaa pée gātì-u tiba, ní u-boonnooliibi dɔ́miń ki gbāam̀-u yii: «Jà unimpu nee, di sá u pá ti-boon ki tátí kaa gbèentí.».

eng: Jesus replied, Ive been sent only to the lost sheep, the people of Israel.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí yii: «Bi túnní-mi Isirayeel yab bin wáŋkì kii ipiiyee ŋmaniŋma cee ní.».

eng: But she knelt before him and said, Lord, help me.
local: Ama ní unimpuee dɔ́miń ki gbāaǹ u-nimbiin ní ki bí yii: «Ukpil, jòoń-mi!».

eng: He replied, It is not good to take the childrens bread and toss it to dogs.
local: Ní Yeesu lī yii: «An kaa máaǹ bí dū mbiyaamu jinti kí tīī mbɔbiyaamu.».

eng: She said, Yes, Lord. But even the dogs eat the crumbs that fall off their masters table.
local: Ní unimpuee bí yii: «Tiŋman Ukpil, ama nì míǹ kɔkɔee, mbɔbiyaamu dindambi yaa bíi jīǹee, mbɔbiyaamu mun tòó igbasigbasi in lítée ní ki jīǹ.».

eng: Jesus answered, Woman, you have great faith. It will be just as you wish. And right then her daughter was healed.
local: Niinee ní Yeesu bí yii: «Unimpu nee, a-fookiiu wīikéeee! A nín là puee, án ŋá míǹ kí tīī-si.» Niin niin ní u-bisalee pɔ́ɔkì.

eng: Jesus moved on from there along the shore of the Galilee Sea. He went up a mountain and sat down.
local: Yeesu yíkì niinee ní ki búntì Galiilee mɔɔgbandi, ní ki cūtìi jɔ́m̀ dijool diba pu ki kāl̀.

eng: Jesus replied, Why do you break the command of God by keeping the rules handed down to you?.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-bi yii: «Kin nimbi fáà, ni nín nóó ni-yaajakoobiliŋ ki yìntī Unimbɔti marabee!.

eng: Large crowds came to him, including those who were paralyzed, blind, injured, and unable to speak, and many others. They laid them at his feet, and he healed them.
local: Kunipaau nìn dɔ́miń u-cee ki yōoǹní bin tà bi-taaŋi puee nì bin wɔ̀b̄ee, nì bijɔfub, nì bimɔɔmiimbi, nì bibunliibi biba mun tikpil, ní bi cáań-bi ki bíl̀ u-nimbiin, ní u cáaǹ bi-kɔkɔ.

eng: So the crowd was amazed when they saw those who had been unable to speak talking, and the paralyzed cured, and the injured walking, and the blind seeing. And they praised the God of Israel.
local: An nìn ŋá kunipaauee maamaaci bi nín ká bimɔɔmiimbi sòoǹ, bin wɔ̀b̄ee pɔ́ɔkì, bitaakpalfub kíǹ ki còom̄ tiŋan, ní bijɔfub mun wál̀ee puee, ní bi bíi pàkà Isirayeel Nimbɔtiu.

eng: Now Jesus called his disciples and said, I feel sorry for the crowd because they have been with me for three days and have nothing to eat. I dont want to send them away hungry for fear they wont have enough strength to travel.
local: Yeesu yíiń u-boonnooliibi, ki bí-bi yii: «Nnibiim nee gbí-mi falaa: di sá iwiin ita sèé nee bi nín bí m-cee, kaa cáá tiba kí jēe. Maa là kí jà-bi bí nīn kùǹ nì ŋkùm, bi-wunti ń tin yɔ̀tī nsanni.».

eng: His disciples replied, Where are we going to get enough food in this wilderness to satisfy such a big crowd.
local: Ní u-boonnooliibi bí-u yii: «Kin, la ní ti làá nín kánní tijin kuteeu nee ni kí kpíiǹ nnibiim min bí nee dee?».

eng: Jesus said, How much bread do you have? They responded, Seven loaves and a few fish.
local: Ní Yeesu fātìi bālifì-bi yii: «Kpɔnɔb biŋa ní ni cáá ní?» Ní bi kíí-u yii: «Biluli, nì njɔŋkaaŋkaamu.».

eng: He told the crowd to sit on the ground.
local: Niinee ní u bí samaayee kɔkɔ ń kàl̄ taapu.

eng: He took the seven loaves of bread and the fish. After he gave thanks, he broke them into pieces and gave them to the disciples, and the disciples gave them to the crowds.
local: ní ki fōō kpɔnɔb bilulee nì ijɔmee, ki jáam̀ Unimbɔti an pu, ní ki gífì gífì ki tīī u-boonnooliibi yākatì kunipaauee.

eng: Everyone ate until they were full. The disciples collected seven baskets full of leftovers.
local: Ní báà ŋma jíń ki díkí, ní bi kóoǹ agin ŋin nìn kíǹee ki gbéeǹ tikabiti tiluli.

eng: Four thousand men ate, plus women and children.
local: Bin nìn jíń tijikaal gbantee nyáń bininjab kutukub binaa, baa kàan̄ binimpoobi nì mbiyaamu.

eng: After dismissing the crowds, Jesus got into the boat and came to the region of Magadan.
local: Bi jíń ki dóòyee, ní Yeesu yāntì kunipaauee nín kùǹ ní u kɔ́ buŋalimbu u nì u-boonnooliibi búntì Magadan watil pú.

eng: For God said, Honor your father and your mother, and The person who speaks against father or mother will certainly be put to death .
local: Unimbɔti bí yii: <Á nīn tɔ́ a-baa nì a-na,> ní ki gíti bí yii: <Un di yaa sòon̄ ki bíil̀ u-baa kii u-nee máaǹ bí kpɔ̀-u.>.

eng: But you say, If you tell your father or mother,Everything Im expected to contribute to you Im giving to God as a gift,then you dont have to honor your father.
local: Ama ní nimbi dàkà binib yii a ŋūǹ kí tùkù a-baa àá a-na yii: <M nín bàà làá tīī-si tiŋyee m dūu ŋá Unimbɔti saraa.>.

eng: So you do away with Gods Law for the sake of the rules that have been handed down to you.
local: A yaa gíti dáá lī mimmee, an kaa ti sá á tūtuǹ a-baa. Ni nín ŋáań pu ki kpìtī Unimbɔti bɔti ki ŋáań min ni-naanjab dàkèe dee.

eng: Hypocrites! Isaiah really knew what he was talking about when he prophesied about you.
local: Mpoonnyoondambi! Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu Yeesaya nín sòon̄ ni-pu miŋyee sá ibaamɔn. U nìn lī yii.

eng: This people honors me with their lips, but their hearts are far away from me.
local: <Unimbɔti bí yii: Binib nee nyɔ́ŋkí-mi nì bi-nyɔmmu fám ní, bi-pɔbiliŋ dátiǹ-mi.

eng: Their worship of me is empty since they teach instructions that are human rules .
local: Bi jáaḿ-mi fám ní, kun puee tin binib náańee ní bi dàkà, ní ki cáa yíì man marab.>».

eng: The Pharisees and Sadducees came to Jesus. In order to test him they asked him to show them a sign from heaven.
local: Fariisab nì Sadusab dɔ́miń Yeesu cee kí píĺ-u tibɔti ní ki bí-u yii ú ŋá maamaaci ubɔ kí dàkà-bi yii Unimbɔti bí u-cee.

eng: And the seven loaves that fed the four thousand and how many large baskets of leftovers you gathered?.
local: Àá naa téetí kpɔnɔb biluli bin m gífì ki tīī binib kutukub binaa ŋmɔ́ ki díkée? Tikabiti tiŋa ní ni nìn tōō bin kíǹee ní?.

eng: Dont you know that I wasnt talking about bread? But be on your guard for the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.
local: Ba pu ní m nín bí-ni yii ní nīn nyíǹ ni-ba nì Fariisab nì Sadusab kpɔnɔnyɔkɔŋu puee, naa béè yii an kaa sá kpɔnɔb pu ní m sòoǹ ní?.

eng: Then they understood that he wasnt telling them to be on their guard for yeast used in making bread. No, he was telling them to watch out for the teaching of the Pharisees and Sadducees.
local: Niinee ní u-boonnooliibi nín béè yii waa nìn bíi sòoǹ nì bi ŋkpɔnɔnyɔkɔ pu, ama Fariisab nì Sadusab dàkatam pu ní u nìn bíi sòoǹ.

eng: Now when Jesus came to the area of Caesarea Philippi, he asked his disciples, Who do people say the Human One is.
local: Ní Yeesu nì u-boonnooliibi búntì Seesaaree Fiiliipi tiŋki watil pú. Bi cée, ní u bālifì-bi yii: «Binib lì man Kinibiki pu yii m sá ŋma ní?».

eng: They replied, Some say John the Baptist, others Elijah, and still others Jeremiah or one of the other prophets.
local: Ní bi kíí-u yii: «Biba bí yii a sá Yoowaneesi un fùul̀ binib Unimbɔti nyimee, ní biba mun bí yii a sá Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu Eelii, ní biba mun bí yii sii di sá Yeereemii àá Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi kansikin ubɔ.».

eng: He said, And what about you? Who do you say that I am.
local: Ní Yeesu bālifì-bi yii: «Kin nimbi fáà, ŋma ní nimbi nyí yii m sá ní?».

eng: Simon Peter said, You are the Christ, the Son of the living God.
local: Ní Siimɔn Peetroo kíí-u yii: «Sii di sá Meesiya, ki sá Unimbɔti un bí dimaŋfalinee Jipɔɔn.».

eng: Then Jesus replied, Happy are you, Simon son of Jonah, because no human has shown this to you. Rather my Father who is in heaven has shown you.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-u yii: «Siimɔn, Yoowaneesi jipɔɔn, a bí mmɔnni! Di sá yii an kaa sá unil di lēē an baamɔŋyi ki dàkā-si, an sá m-Baa un bí yilpuee di dàkā-si.

eng: I tell you that you are Peter. And Ill build my church on this rock. The gates of the underworld wont be able to stand against it.
local: Man tùkù-si yii: sii di sá Peetroo, ditaŋkpakpaandi nee pu ní m làá mā m-nikitil kí tɔ́kɔń, ní ŋkúm nì mi-pɔŋŋu làá nyàfiǹ kí gúĺ, kaa làá ŋmā kí ŋá-dì tiba.

eng: Ill give you the keys of the kingdom of heaven. Anything you fasten on earth will be fastened in heaven. Anything you loosen on earth will be loosened in heaven.
local: M làá dū Unimbɔti Beel saafiibi kí tīī-si, ní a yaa sòon̄ ki síiǹ pu taapu dooyee, míǹ ní an sòon̄ ki síiǹ yilpu mun.».

eng: But he replied, At evening you say, It will be nice weather because the sky is bright red.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-bi yii: «Kujoouee, ni lì yii: <Kutagbɔŋu làá nīn ŋān̄, kun puee atagban ŋmàl̄ mmam.>.

eng: Then he ordered the disciples not to tell anybody that he was the Christ.
local: Yeesu lī míǹ ki dóòyee, ní ki bí u-boonnooliibi yii bí taa pée tùkù báà ubɔ yii úǹ di sá Meesiya.

eng: From that time Jesus began to show his disciples that he had to go to Jerusalem and suffer many things from the elders, chief priests, and legal experts, and that he had to be killed and raised on the third day.
local: Ŋyunti gbantee ní Yeesu kíĺ ki sòoǹ ki cántí u-boonnooliibi yii: «An sá yii ḿ cù Jeerusaleem, bitindaŋkpilib nì bisaraaŋakpilib nì bimaradakaliibi ń sàaǹ-mi tikpil, ní kí kpɔ̀-mi, winta dalee, ní ḿ fìkitī.».

eng: Then Peter took hold of Jesus and, scolding him, began to correct him: God forbid, Lord! This wont happen to you.
local: Ní Peetroo cáá-u ki kākatì ŋkpaanceeti ki wòon̄-u yii: «Ukpil, Unimbɔti ń cùnnì mbusu ndan! An kaa làá bàń-si míǹ.».

eng: But he turned to Peter and said, Get behind me, Satan. You are a stone that could make me stumble, for you are not thinking Gods thoughts but human thoughts.
local: Ní Yeesu fātìń Peetroo pu ní ki bí-u yii: «Sii un cáá Sitaan landɔkɔŋuee, sútí niin kí wàatī-mi. A sá-mi kisugiŋ ní nsan pu. Saa dàkafī Unimbɔti yam, a dàkafī nniyaamin ní.».

eng: Then Jesus said to his disciples, All who want to come after me must sayno to themselves, take up their cross, and follow me.
local: Niinee ní Yeesu tùkū u-boonnooliibi yii: «Un yaa là kí nóó-mee ń fá úǹ u-ba nín là puee, kí yòon̄ u-dɔpuntikaaŋu kí nīn nóó-mi.

eng: All who want to save their lives will lose them. But all who lose their lives because of me will find them.
local: Kun puee un di yaa là kí fīī u-maŋfalee làá dāá lántí-dì, ama un di yaa fá u-maŋfal man puee di làá dāá fīī-dì.

eng: Why would people gain the whole world but lose their lives? What will people give in exchange for their lives?.
local: Unil, a yaa jíń duulinya nee kɔkɔ ní ki yaa fá a-naŋkee, ba ní a páatì? Ba ní a ŋūǹ kí dūú pà kí fīī a-ba?.

eng: For the Human One is about to come with the majesty of his Father with his angels. And then he will repay each one for what that person has done.
local: Man Kinibiki làá dāan̄ nì m-Baa Unimbɔti nyooti, ní m-tuuŋyi dāa nóó-mi, ní ḿ dāá pà báà ŋma nì u-tundi nín sá puee.

eng: I assure you that some standing here wont die before they see the Human One coming in his kingdom.
local: Ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-ni, biba bí ni-kansikin doo kaa làá kpú ní kí ká man Kinibiki ń dāan̄ kii ubɔtee.».

eng: And in the morning you say, There will be bad weather today because the sky is cloudy. You know how to make sense of the skys appearance. But you are unable to recognize the signs that point to what the time is.
local: Ní kutaafaauee ni lì yii: <Utaa làá nì díǹ kun puee atagban bí gùŋgum.> Ni nyí kutaa bimbim míǹ, ní kaa ŋūǹ kí béé tidaandi tin Unimbɔti bíi ŋáań iyunti in bí nee poonee.

eng: An evil and unfaithful generation searches for a sign. But it wont receive any sign except Jonahs sign. Then he left them and went away.
local: Waanee nib binikpitib bin kaa yú deedee nì Unimbɔtee bàlifī yii ḿ ŋá kudaaŋŋu kubɔ kí dàkà-bi. Baa làá ká kudaaŋŋu kubɔ asee kun náań nì Yoona yɔkɔee!» U lī mimmee ní ki yāntì-bi ki búntì.

eng: When the disciples arrived on the other side of the lake, they had forgotten to bring bread.
local: U-boonnooliibi nín dūtì dimɔɔgbandee, bi súntì kaa kpáaǹní kpɔnɔb.

eng: Jesus said to them, Watch out and be on your guard for the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-bi yii: «Ní nīn nyí nì ni-ba nì Fariisab nì Sadusab kpɔnɔnyɔkɔŋu pu.».

eng: They discussed this among themselves and said, We didnt bring any bread.
local: Ní u-boonnooliibi bíi dàkafī bi-ban yii: «Ti nín kaa kalin kpáaǹní kpɔnɔbee pu ní u lī mimmaaa?».

eng: Jesus knew what they were discussing and said, You people of weak faith! Why are you discussing among yourselves the fact that you dont have any bread?.
local: Yeesu béè bi-landɔkɔi, ní ki lī-bi yii: «Ba pu ní ni bíi dàkafī ni-ban ni nín kaa ŋúbiń kpɔnɔbee pu? Ba pu ní ni-fookiiu kaa náaǹ míǹ?.

eng: Dont you understand yet? Dont you remember the five loaves that fed the five thousand and how many baskets of leftovers you gathered?.
local: Nìn díǹ naa láá gbìl̄ taapuɔɔɔ? Naa téetí kpɔnɔb biŋmoo bin m gífì ki tīī binib kutukub biŋmoo ŋmɔ́ ki díkí áli ni tōō bin kíǹee tikabiti saalaa nì tileeee?.

eng: Six days later Jesus took Peter, James, and John his brother, and brought them to the top of a very high mountain.
local: Iwiin iluu boonee, Yeesu yōoǹ Peetroo nì Yakubu nì Yoowaneesi un sá Yakubu naalee ki cáá-bi ki kpōotì ŋkpaanceeti dijoofɔkɔl diba pu.

eng: The disciples asked, Then why do the legal experts say that Elijah must first come.
local: Ní u-boonnooliibi bālifì-u yii: «Ba pu ní bimaradakaliibi bí yii Eelii di máaǹ kí péé kí dāan̄ kí wàatī ní?».

eng: Jesus responded, Elijah does come first and will restore all things.
local: Ní Yeesu fātìi kíí-bi yii: «Ibaamɔn, Eelii di máaǹ kí péé kí dāan̄ kí bɔ́ntí tikɔkɔ.

eng: In fact, I tell you that Elijah has already come, and they didnt know him. But they did to him whatever they wanted. In the same way the Human One is also going to suffer at their hands.
local: Ama m tùkù-ni yii Eelii dɔ́miń dooooyee ní binib kaa béè-u, ki ŋá-u bi nín là puee. Míǹ mbaantiim ní bi làá tí sàaǹ man Kinibiki mun.».

eng: Then the disciples realized he was telling them about John the Baptist.
local: Niinee ní u-boonnooliibi béè yii Yoowaneesi un fùul̀ binib Unimbɔti nyimee pu ní u lì.

eng: When they came to the crowd, a man met Jesus. He knelt before him.
local: Bi bàn̄ ki ŋmāntiǹ kunipaauee, ní uninja ubɔ dɔ́miń Yeesu cee ní ki gbāaǹ u-nimbiin.

eng: saying, Lord, show mercy to my son. He is epileptic and suffers terribly, for he often falls into the fire or the water.
local: ki bí-u yii: «Ukpil, sɔ̄ m-jipɔɔn saai! U būǹ abimbil ní ki sàań tikpil. An pée líikí-u ní múlmul, ŋyunti ŋubɔee ŋŋmin, ŋyunti ŋubɔ muee nnyimin.

eng: I brought him to your disciples, but they couldnt heal him.
local: Ní m bàà cáań-u a-boonnooliibi cee, ní baa ŋúń ki cáaǹ-u.».

eng: Jesus answered, You faithless and crooked generation, how long will I be with you? How long will I put up with you? Bring the boy here to me.
local: Ní Yeesu bí yii: «Óo binib nee, naa cáá fookii ki gáatì! Kí dāá sāā ŋyunti ŋulɔŋu ní ḿ bàà dūú kàl̄ ni-cee? M làá nīn jīǹ ni-pu kimɔtii kí tin sāā ŋyunti ŋulɔŋu ní? Cáaḿmaan-mi kibikee doo.».

eng: Then Jesus spoke harshly to the demon. And it came out of the child, who was healed from that time on.
local: Ní Yeesu kpātì arasinii un kā ki-puee pu, ní u nyáń, ní kibikee pɔ́ɔkì dicilpu niin.

eng: Then the disciples came to Jesus in private and said, Why couldnt we throw the demon out.
local: Niinee ní Yeesu boonnooliibi dɔ́miń u-cee bi-ŋmaniŋma ki bālifì-u yii: «Ba pu ní timbi kaa ŋúń ki jìn̄ arasinii gbanti?».

eng: He was transformed in front of them. His face shone like the sun, and his clothes became as white as light.
local: ní ki cúú u-ba ki kpántì bi-nimbiin, ní u-nimbiin kíǹ ki jìl̀ pàlpal kii ŋwìińee, ní u-wampeeŋkaati píil̀ bɔ́iii kii ŋwalifee.

eng: Because you have little faith, he said. I assure you that if you have faith the size of a mustard seed, you could say to this mountain, Go from here to there, and it will go. There will be nothing that you cant do.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-bi yii: «Di sá yii ni-fookiiu un ni cáá Unimbɔti puee di pátì. Ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-ni, ni-fookiiu yaa bàà náaǹ ki bàn̄ kii dijɔnjɔmbilee, ni bàà ŋūǹ kí tùkù dijool nee yii: <Yīkì laakin a bí nee kí nīn cá lapu doo!> Ní dí bàà lafun kpìkitī. Tiba kaa bàà ŋūǹ kí nīn pɔ̄ɔ̄ ki tīī-ni. .

eng: When the disciples came together in Galilee, Jesus said to them, The Human One is about to be delivered over into human hands.
local: Dalba Yeesu nì u-boonnooliibi kpáfì ki bí Galiileeyee, ní u tùkū-bi yii: «Bi làá cúú man Kinibiki kí tīī binib, bí kpɔ̀-mi.

eng: They will kill him. But he will be raised on the third day. And they were heartbroken.
local: Ama ní winta dalee ḿ fìkitī.» Niinee, an kɔ́-bi kunimbaau tikpil.

eng: When they came to Capernaum, the people who collected the half-shekel temple tax came to Peter and said, Doesnt your teacher pay the temple tax.
local: Yeesu nì u-boonnooliibi nín bàn̄ Kapeernayumee, bin nìn fòol̄ kunimbɔtidii lampoouee dɔ́miń Peetroo cee ní ki bālifì-u yii: «Ni-kpiliu kaa pà kunimbɔtidii lampoou níii?».

eng: Yes, he said. But when they came into the house, Jesus spoke to Peter first.What do you think, Simon? From whom do earthly kings collect taxes, from their children or from strangers.
local: Ní Peetroo kíí-bi yii: «U pà.» Ŋyunti ŋun Peetroo kóoń kudiin kpalaayee, ní Yeesu bí-u yii: «Siimɔn, a dàkafī yii ba? Bilab di pà fɔtiku nì lampoo ki tìì duulinya bɔtiibi ní? Bitindambaaa, binicambi ní?».

eng: From strangers, he said. Jesus said to him, Then the children dont have to pay.
local: Ní u kíí-u yii: «Binicambi.» Ní Yeesu bí-u yii: «Mimmee, bitindambi kaa pà dee.

eng: But just so we dont offend them, go to the lake, throw out a fishing line and hook, and take the first fish you catch. When you open its mouth, you will find a shekel coin. Take it and pay the tax for both of us.
local: Ama timbi kaa là kí bàkan̄ binib gbanti, míǹ puee, nín̄ cá dimɔɔlin kí tɔ̄ njɔmpiin. Ujɔmpeepeekaa un a làá cúúyee, á tábití u-nyɔkɔ, a làá ká animbil ŋin làá ŋmàntī binib bili lampoouee. Cáań animbilee kí pà man nì sii yil pu.».

eng: Moses and Elijah appeared to them, talking with Jesus.
local: Niinee ní u-kpambaliŋ kpēēyee Mooyiisi nì Eelii di sí bi nì Yeesu sòoǹee.

eng: Peter reacted to all of this by saying to Jesus, Lord, its good that were here. If you want, Ill make three shrines: one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.
local: Bi ká mimmee, ní Peetroo tùkū Yeesu yii: «M-Dindaan, an ŋān̄ tí nīn bí doo! A yaa là m làá gāā tibìń tita, sii kubɔ, Mooyiisi kubɔ, Eelii mun kubɔ.».

eng: While he was still speaking, look, a bright cloud overshadowed them. A voice from the cloud said, This is my Son whom I dearly love. I am very pleased with him. Listen to him.
local: U bíi sòoǹ mimmee, ní ditagbandi diba dɔ́miń ki bīiǹ bi-pu ki jìl̀ pàlpal, ní bi gbìl̄ Unimbɔti sòon̄ní ditagbandee ni yii: «Un bí nee di sá man Jipɔɔn un m néeń tiŋanee, u-pu ní m-pɔbil sɔ̀m̀. Ní pīlifìmaan-u.».

eng: Hearing this, the disciples fell on their faces, filled with awe.
local: U-boonnooliibi nín gbìl̄ an sòon̄ní mimmee ní bi fāŋkì tikpil ki lítì dicincibil.

eng: But Jesus came and touched them. Get up, he said. Dont be afraid.
local: ama ní Yeesu sútì ki pìl̄-bi, ní ki gbēē-bi ki bí yii: «Yīkìmaan, taa fàŋkìmaan!».

eng: When they looked up, they saw no one except Jesus.
local: Bi wáaǹ ki kpēēyee ní kaa ti ká bin kíǹee, see Yeesu baba.

eng: As they were coming down the mountain, Jesus commanded them, Dont tell anybody about the vision until the Human One is raised from the dead.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun bi fàtīi kpákatiń dijool puee, Yeesu kpáafì-bi yii: «Ní taa māafì báà ubɔ tin ni kée kí nīn cí ŋyunti ŋun man Kinibiki làá fìkitī ditaŋkpiilinee.».

eng: At that time the disciples came to Jesus and asked, Who is the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.
local: Ŋyunti gbantee ní Yeesu boonnooliibi dɔ́miń u-cee ki bālifì-u yii: «Ŋma di sá ukpaan Unimbɔti Beelin ní?».

eng: Be careful that you dont look down on one of these little ones. I say to you that their angels in heaven are always looking into the face of my Father who is in heaven.
local: «Ní nīn nyíǹ ni-ba kí taa kpèè mbiyaamu nee kansikin kiba fám. M tùkù-ni ibaamɔn ní yii ituun in kpèé-muee bí m-Baa un bí yilpuee nimbiin ŋyunti sá kamaa. .

eng: What do you think? If someone had one hundred sheep and one of them wandered off, wouldnt he leave the ninety-nine on the hillsides and go in search for the one that wandered off?.
local: «Ni dàkafī yii ba? Unil yaa cáá ipii dilaataal, ní ubɔ wáŋkì i-kansikinee, waa yàntī ubɔ kpá dilaataal in kíǹee ajoon pu kupikpaaun kí cùú nyàabìń ubɔ un wáŋkèeee?.

eng: If he finds it, I assure you that he is happier about having that one sheep than about the ninety-nine who didnt wander off.
local: M tùkù-ni yii, u yaa tin ká-uee, u-bɔti mɔ́ɔkíǹ-u kí jītíǹ ubɔ kpá dilaataal in kaa wáŋkèe bɔti.

eng: In the same way, my Father who is in heaven doesnt want to lose one of these little ones.
local: Míǹ mbaantiim ní ni-Baa un bí yilpuee kaa là mbiyaamu nee kansikin báà kiba ń wāŋkì.».

eng: If your brother or sister sins against you, go and correct them when you are alone together. If they listen to you, then youve won over your brother or sister.
local: «A-nabiki yaa yīntiǹ-see, cù u-cee kí dàkà-u min ní u ŋá-si an kaa kítèe sii nì u ŋmaniŋma. U yaa gbìl̄ ki tīiǹ-see, mimmee, a ŋúń ki gíiǹní a-nabiki nsammɔntin dee.

eng: But if they wont listen, take with you one or two others so that every word may be established by the mouth of two or three witnesses .
local: Ama u yaa yíì, kaa gbìlīǹ-see, á kpáaǹ unil ubɔ kii bili kí cù u-cee, án nín làá ŋá pu binib bili kii bita ń ká kí ŋmā kí sòoǹ min udaan ŋá an kaa kítèe, kii Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon nín lī puee.

eng: But if they still wont pay attention, report it to the church. If they wont pay attention even to the church, treat them as you would a Gentile and tax collector.
local: U yaa kaa tí gbìl̄ ki tīiǹ bi muee, á tùkù Unimbɔti nikitil. U yaa tí yíì, kaa gbìlīǹ Unimbɔti nikitilee, ú kpántí a-cee kii unil un kaa nóó Unimbɔtee.».

eng: I assure you that whatever you fasten on earth will be fastened in heaven. And whatever you loosen on earth will be loosened in heaven.
local: «Ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-ni, tin ní ni yaa bóob́ taapu dooyee, míǹ ní an bóob́ yilpu mun.

eng: Again I assure you that if two of you agree on earth about anything you ask, then my Father who is in heaven will do it for you.
local: «Ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-ni, binib bili ni-kansikin yaa kpáfì kinyɔkɔ taapu doo ki mēē báà kuŋyee, m-Baa un bí yilpuee làá tīī-bi kudɔŋu.

eng: Then he called a little child over to sit among the disciples.
local: Ní u yíiń kibiyaawaai kiba ki yóoǹ bi-nimbiin.

eng: For where two or three are gathered in my name, Im there with them.
local: Di sá yii laakin binib yaa kpáfì bili kii bita man yindi puee, m bí bi-kansikin.».

eng: Then Peter said to Jesus, Lord, how many times should I forgive my brother or sister who sins against me? Should I forgive as many as seven times.
local: Niinee ní Peetroo dɔ́miń Yeesu cee ki bālifì-u yii: «Ukpil, m-nabiki yaa yīntiǹ-mi, kí tin sāā miŋa ní an máaǹ ḿ dūú pɔ̄-u ní? Kí tin sāā milulaaa?».

eng: Jesus said, Not just seven times, but rather as many as seventy-seven times.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-u yii: «Aayee, an kaa sá miluli, an sá kí tin sāā imuŋku ita nì saalaa miluli pu ní á pɔ̄.».

eng: Therefore, the kingdom of heaven is like a king who wanted to settle accounts with his servants.
local: An puee, Unimbɔti Beel náań nì kii ubɔti ubɔ nín yíiń u-tɔntɔnliibi bí dāá pà-u bi-pɔɔŋŋi puee ní.

eng: When he began to settle accounts, they brought to him a servant who owed him ten thousand bags of gold.
local: U kíĺ ki bíi kàaǹee, ní bi cáań-u u-pɔ̀nliu ubɔ un cáá-u dipɔndi miiliyɔmbi kutukuee.

eng: Because the servant didnt have enough to pay it back, the master ordered that he should be sold, along with his wife and children and everything he had, and that the proceeds should be used as payment.
local: Kijapaayee nín kaa nìn cáá kun ní u làá dūú pèe, ní ukpilee bí yii bí dū u nì u-nimpuu nì u-biyaamu nì tin kɔkɔ u cááyee kí nyāfì án ŋmā kí bà kunimbiŋmiiu.

eng: But the servant fell down, kneeled before him, and said, Please, be patient with me, and Ill pay you back.
local: Ní utɔntɔnlee tātìi gbāaǹ u-kpiliu nimbiin, ki dɔ̄ kitiŋ ki fìlim̄ ki gbāam̀-u yii: «Á jī kimɔtii! M làá dāá pà-si kɔkɔ.».

eng: The master had compassion on that servant, released him, and forgave the loan.
local: U nín kaa ŋūǹ kí pà puee ní u-kpiliu súń u-saai ki dū u-pɔndi ki pú-u, ní ki bí yii ú nīn cá.

eng: When that servant went out, he found one of his fellow servants who owed him one hundred coins. He grabbed him around the throat and said, Pay me back what you owe me.
local: «Utɔntɔnlee nyáńee ní ki dūu tú u-tɔntɔnjuti un mun cáá úǹ dipɔndi kutukuee nsan, ní ki cìb̄ ki cúú-u ki ŋmáaḿ u-neen ki bí-u yii <Pà-mi m-nimbiliŋ!>.

eng: Then his fellow servant fell down and begged him, Be patient with me, and Ill pay you back.
local: Ní u-tɔntɔnjutee gbāaǹ u-nimbiin ní ki gbāam̀-u yii: <Á jī kimɔtii, m làá pà-si.>.

eng: and said, I assure you that if you dont turn your lives around and become like this little child, you will definitely not enter the kingdom of heaven.
local: ní ki bí-bi yii: «Ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-ni, ni yaa kaa kpántì ni-bimbim ki ŋá kii mbiyaamuee, ni kpá kí kɔ́ Unimbɔti Beelin.

eng: But he refused. Instead, he threw him into prison until he paid back his debt.
local: Ama waa kíí see u nín cáá-u ki tɔ́ sarka ú pà u-pɔndi kɔkɔee.

eng: When his fellow servants saw what happened, they were deeply offended. They came and told their master all that happened.
local: U-tɔntɔnjutiibi nín ká min jítèe, ní an kɔ́ bíǹ inimɔɔn, ní bi cūtìi kàan̄ tin jítèe kɔkɔ ki tùkū bi-kpiliu.

eng: His master called the first servant and said, You wicked servant! I forgave you all that debt because you appealed to me.
local: Niinee ní bi-kpiliu yíiń utɔntɔnlee ní ki bí-u yii: <A sá utɔŋfiti ní! A nín gbāam̀-mi puee ní m dū a-pɔndi ki pú-si.

eng: Shouldnt you also have mercy on your fellow servant, just as I had mercy on you.
local: An kaan máaǹ á sɔ̄ a-bɔɔ saai kii man nín súń a-saai pueeee?>.

eng: His master was furious and handed him over to the guard responsible for punishing prisoners, until he had paid the whole debt.
local: Ní u-kpiliuee gíí diŋuul, ní ki yāntì bi dū-u ki tɔ́ sarkan bí dáaĺ u-tafal áli ú dāá pà u-pɔndi kɔkɔ kí dóó.».

eng: My heavenly Father will also do the same to you if you dont forgive your brother or sister from your heart.
local: Ní Yeesu bí yii: «Míǹ ní m-Baa un bí yilpuee làá dāá ŋá un kamaa kaa dū u-nabiki kpitii ki pú-u nì u-pɔbil kɔkɔee.».

eng: Those who humble themselves like this little child will be the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.
local: Un sá ukpaan Unimbɔti Beelinee di sá un di sìntīǹ u-ba ki kpántí kii kibikee.

eng: Whoever welcomes one such child in my name welcomes me.
local: Un kamaa yaa fōō kibiyaawaai kiba kii kíǹee juti man puee, udaan fōō man dee.».

eng: As for whoever causes these little ones who believe in me to trip and fall into sin, it would be better for them to have a huge stone hung around their necks and be drowned in the bottom of the lake.
local: «Ama un di yaa yāntì mbiyaamu mun fōō-mi ki kíí nee kansikin kiba ŋá ikpitee, an sɔ́ bí dū kunakpaaŋŋu kí kúĺ udaan neen kí cáá-u kí tɔ̄ tiŋkun ú múĺ taapu taapu.

eng: How terrible it is for the world because of the things that cause people to trip and fall into sin! Such things have to happen, but how terrible it is for the person who causes those things to happen!.
local: Mbusu bí duulinya pu, kun puee tiwan tikpil yàntī binib ŋáań ikpiti. Tin yàntī binib ŋáań ikpitee làá nīn pée bí ní. Ama mbusu bí ki tīī un pu ní án làá dīńee.

eng: If your hand or your foot causes you to fall into sin, chop it off and throw it away. Its better to enter into life crippled or lame than to be thrown into the eternal fire with two hands or two feet.
local: «An yaa sá a-ŋal kii a-taal di yaa làá yàntī á ŋá ikpitee, gùutīí fá kí wàatī a-ba! Á kɔ́ dimaŋfal din kaa kúntée ni nì ditaagindi kii diŋaŋgindi sɔ́ nì a-ŋalii liitil nì a-taaŋi liitil ń nīn bí, bí cáá-si kí tɔ̄ ŋŋmi ŋun kaa kúù jaanjaanee ni.

eng: If your eye causes you to fall into sin, tear it out and throw it away. Its better to enter into life with one eye than to be cast into a burning hell with two eyes.
local: An yaa ti sá a-nimbil di yaa làá yàntī á ŋá ikpitee, á lúkutí-dì kí fá. Á kɔ́ dimaŋfal din kaa kúntée ni nì dinimbil dibee sɔ́ǹ a-nimbiliŋ liitil ń nīn kā, bí cáá-si kí tɔ̄ ŋŋmi ŋun kaa kúù jaanjaanee ni.».

eng: When Jesus finished saying these things, he left Galilee and came to the area of Judea on the east side of the Jordan.
local: Yeesu dàkā an bɔti ki dóòyee, ní ki yíkì Galiilee tiŋkin ki póotì Juudee watil, Jɔɔdan buŋgbandi lapu pu.

eng: His disciples said to him, If thats the way things are between a man and his wife, then its better not to marry.
local: Ní u-boonnooliibi bí yii: «An yaa sá míǹ ní uninja nì u-nimpuu kansikinee, mimmee, a yaa kaa pūtì cáá unimpu, an sɔ́ dee.».

eng: He replied, Not everybody can accept this teaching, but only those who have received the ability to accept it.
local: Ní u bí-bi yii: «An kaa sá báà ŋma di ŋūǹ tibɔti nee, see bin an pú bí ŋmēe.

eng: For there are eunuchs who have been eunuchs from birth. And there are eunuchs who have been made eunuchs by other people. And there are eunuchs who have made themselves eunuchs because of the kingdom of heaven. Those who can accept it should accept it.
local: Kun yàntī bininjab biba kaa cāab̀ binimpoobee bí tɔbin. Bibee, an sá doooo bi-na poon ní baa sá bininjab, ní bi fāatì biba, ní biba muee, an sá Unimbɔti Beel puee ní bi dū bi-ba ki ŋá kii baa sá bininjabee. Un yaa ŋūǹ kí gbìl̄ tibɔti nee taapuee ń gbìl̄.».

eng: Some people brought children to Jesus so that he would place his hands on them and pray. But the disciples scolded them.
local: Bi nìn cáań mbiyaamu Yeesu cee ú dū u-ŋalii kí páań mu-pu kí mèè kí tīī-mu, ní u-boonnooliibi bíi wòoǹ bin cáań-muee.

eng: Allow the children to come to me, Jesus said. Dont forbid them, because the kingdom of heaven belongs to people like these children.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-bi yii: «Càntīmaan mbiyaamu nee! Yàntīmaan mú dāan̄ m-cee kí taa pìin̄-mu nsan, kun puee Unimbɔti Beel sá ki tīī bin náań nì muee ní.».

eng: Then he blessed the children and went away from there.
local: Ní u dū u-ŋalii ki páaǹ mu-pu ki mēē ki tīī-mu ní ki búntì.

eng: A man approached him and said, Teacher, what good thing must I do to have eternal life.
local: Uninja ubɔ nìn dɔ́miń Yeesu cee ní ki bālifì-u yii: «Ukpil, ditummɔntiil dilal ní m làá ŋá ní kí ká dimaŋfal din kaa cáá dikúntilee ní?».

eng: Jesus said, Why do you ask me about what is good? Theres only one who is good. If you want to enter eternal life, keep the commandments.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-u yii: «Ba pu ní a bàlifī-mi timɔntiil pu? Unimbɔti baba di sá umɔnti. A yaa là kí kɔ́ dimaŋfal din kaa cáá dikúntilee nee, á nīn tɔ́ marab.» Ní u fātìi bālifì Yeesu yii: «Marab bilab?».

eng: The man said, Which ones? Then Jesus said, Dont commit murder. Dont commit adultery. Dont steal. Dont give false testimony .
local: Ní Yeesu bí-u yii: «Taa kpɔ̀ unil, taa ŋá tilaakpaal, taa yɔ̀, taa mɔ̄ntì ubɔ nyɔkɔ pu.

eng: Honor your father and mother, and love your neighbor as you love yourself .
local: kí nīn tɔ́ a-baa nì a-na, kí nīn néeń a-juti kii sii a-bee.».

eng: Large crowds followed him, and he healed them.
local: Kunipaau nìn nóó-u ní u cāantí bi-weeŋyi.

eng: The young man replied, Ive kept all these. What am I still missing.
local: Ní unaacimpɔɔnee bí-u yii: «M ŋá míǹ kɔkɔ, tilati di gíti kíǹ?».
