eng: Jesus said, If you want to be complete, go, sell what you own, and give the money to the poor. Then you will have treasure in heaven. And come follow me.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-u yii: «A yaa là kí ŋá min sá páaayee, cù kí dū tin kɔkɔ a cááyee kí nyāfì kí dū animbil kí yàkatī bifalaadambi, ní kí tin ká tigaajati Unimbɔti cee. A yaa ŋá míǹ ki dóòyee ní á dāá nīn nóó-mi.».

eng: But when the young man heard this, he went away saddened, because he had many possessions.
local: Unaacimpɔɔnee nín gbìl̄ an nyɔɔbundee, ní kunimbaau kɔ́-u ní u búntì soooo, kun puee u nìn cáá tigaajati tikpil.

eng: Then Jesus said to his disciples, I assure you that it will be very hard for a rich person to enter the kingdom of heaven.
local: Niinee ní Yeesu bí u-boonnooliibi yii: «Ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-ni, an dāa pɔ̄ɔ̄ ki tīī ugaaja ń kɔ́ Unimbɔti Beelin.

eng: In fact, its easier for a camel to squeeze through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter Gods kingdom.
local: M gíti tùkù-ni yii, an pɔ̀fì ki tīī ugutaamu ń jītí ujoo buŋŋun ki jítiǹ ugaaja ń kɔ́ Unimbɔti beelin.».

eng: When his disciples heard this, they were stunned. Then who can be saved? they asked.
local: U-boonnooliibi nín gbìl̄ mimmee, ní bi bākatì tikpil, ní ki bí yii: «Kin, ŋma di nín ŋūǹ kí ká difiil dee?».

eng: Jesus looked at them carefully and said, Its impossible for human beings. But all things are possible for God.
local: Ní Yeesu kpēē-bi dímmm ní ki bí yii: «An pɔ̄ɔǹ unil, ama Unimbɔti ceeyee tikɔkɔ sá sɔkɔmii ní.».

eng: Then Peter replied, Look, weve left everything and followed you. What will we have.
local: Niinee ní Peetroo bí-u yii: «Kin, timbi nín yíkì ki fá tikɔkɔ nì tikɔkɔ ki nóó-si nee, timbi paati dāa sá ba?».

eng: Jesus said to them, I assure you who have followed me that, when everything is made new, when the Human One sits on his magnificent throne, you also will sit on twelve thrones overseeing the twelve tribes of Israel.
local: Ní Yeesu bí yii: «Ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-ni duulinyapɔɔn un dòońee nee, man Kinibiki yaa láa kāl̀ m-beejal pu nì tinyoolee, nimbi bin nóó-mee mun dāa kàal̀ abeejal saalaa nì ali pu ní kí sòoǹ Isirayeel naakɔti saalaa nì tilee bɔti.

eng: And all who have left houses, brothers, sisters, father, mother, children, or farms because of my name will receive one hundred times more and will inherit eternal life.
local: Ní un kamaa yaa yíkì ki fá u-diiŋŋi nì u-nabiyaamu, nì u-niisalib, nì u-baa, nì u-na, nì u-biyaamu, nì u-kpaatanti man puee, Unimbɔti làá tin pà udaan míǹ dilaataal pu, ní ú gíti fōō dimaŋfal din kaa cáá dikúntilee.

eng: Some Pharisees came to him. In order to test him, they said, Does the Law allow a man to divorce his wife for just any reason.
local: Niinee Fariisab biba dɔ́miń u-cee kí píĺ-u tibɔti kí kpèè u-nyɔkɔn ní ki bālifì-u yii: «Ti-marau pú nsan yii á jà a-nimpuu báà tin ní u yaa pée ŋéeee?».

eng: But many who are first will be last. And many who are last will be first.
local: Ama bisanlintiliibi tikpil dāa kpántí biboompaanliibi, ní biboompaanliibi mun tikpil ń kpántí bisanlintiliibi.».

eng: Jesus answered, Havent you read that at the beginning the creator made them male and female?.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-bi yii: «Naa kàan̄ Unimbɔti gbɔŋku ki ká an nín lī puee níii? Yii ŋkilkaaminee, Unimbɔti unáanli náań uninja nì unimpu ní, ní ki bí yii.

eng: And God said, Because of this a man should leave his father and mother and be joined together with his wife, and the two will be one flesh .
local: <An sá uninja ń nyā u-baa nì u-na cee ní u nì u-nimpuu ń nīn bí ní bi-liitil ń kpántí unibaanti.>.

eng: So they are no longer two but one flesh. Therefore, humans must not pull apart what God has put together.
local: Mimmee, baa ti sá bili, bi ŋá unibaanti dee. Míǹ puee, unil ń taa yàkatī tin Unimbɔti dūu kpáfèe.».

eng: The Pharisees said to him, Then why did Moses command us to give a divorce certificate and divorce her? .
local: Niinee ní bi bālifì-u yii: «Kin mimmee ba pu ní Mooyiisi nìn pú nsan yii á ŋmà kugbɔŋu kun dàkà yii a jìn̄ a-nimpuuee kí ŋúbíǹ-u ní kí nín jà-u ní?>».

eng: Jesus replied, Moses allowed you to divorce your wives because your hearts are unyielding. But it wasnt that way from the beginning.
local: Ní u kíí-bi yii: «An sá ni-pɔɔgbifiŋŋu di yì-tì ní Mooyiisi pú-ni nsan yii ní jà ni-nimpoobi. Ŋkilkaaminee an kaa nìn sá míǹ.

eng: I say to you that whoever divorces his wife, except for sexual unfaithfulness, and marries another woman commits adultery.
local: M tùkù-ni yii, sii un cáá unimpu ní an kaa sá ticati tin kòóyee, ní a jìn̄-u ki fātìi cáá ubɔee, a ŋá tilaakpaal Unimbɔti nimbiin.».

eng: After Jesus was born in Bethlehem in the territory of Judea during the rule of King Herod, magi came from the east to Jerusalem.
local: Bi màl̄ Yeesu Beetileem ní, Juudee tiŋki kiban. Ŋyunti gbantee Eeroodi di nìn sá ubɔti, ní bin nyí iŋmalbijaa bɔtee biba nyánní ŋwìiń nyalaŋki pú ki bāań Jeerusaleem.

eng: When they saw the star, they were filled with joy.
local: Bi nín ká uŋmalbijee yúl̀ee ní an mɔ́ɔkiǹ-bi tikpil.

eng: They entered the house and saw the child with Mary his mother. Falling to their knees, they honored him. Then they opened their treasure chests and presented him with gifts of gold, frankincense, and myrrh.
local: ní bi kɔ́ kudiin ki ká kibiwaayee nì ki-na Maarii ní ki gbāaǹ ki jáam̀-kì, ní ki pīitì bi-gaajawanti ki lēē sindaariya nì talaalutuukaa nì talaalu un bi yíì miiree ki dūu pú-kì.

eng: Because they were warned in a dream not to return to Herod, they went back to their own country by another route.
local: Boonee ní Unimbɔti sòon̄ nì bi kudamintiiu poon yii bí taa fàtīí dī Eeroodi cee, ní bi páaǹ nsan ŋubɔ ki nín kùǹ bi-dumpu.

eng: When the magi had departed, an angel from the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream and said, Get up. Take the child and his mother and escape to Egypt. Stay there until I tell you, for Herod will soon search for the child in order to kill him.
local: Bin nyí iŋmalbijaa bɔtee nín kùǹee, ní Unimbɔti tuuŋŋu dɔ́miń ki tùkū Jooseefi kudamintiiun yii: «Yīkì kí yòon̄ kibiki nì ki-na kí sāǹ kí nīn cá Eejipiti tiŋkin kí kàl̄ niin kí nīn cí ŋyunti ŋun m làá tùkù-si á fàtīí gítińee, kun puee Eeroodi làá nyàab̀ kí kpɔ̀ kibiki nee.».

eng: Joseph got up and, during the night, took the child and his mother to Egypt.
local: Ní Jooseefi yíkì ki yōoǹ kibiki nì ki-na kunyeeu ki búntì Eejipiti.

eng: He stayed there until Herod died. This fulfilled what the Lord had spoken through the prophet: I have called my son out of Egypt .
local: ní ki kāl̀ niin áli Eeroodi dāa kpíì. Mimmee an ŋá min ti-Dindaan nìn yāntì u-bɔnaatiliu sòon̄ee dee yii: «M yíiń m-jipɔɔn ń nyānní Eejipiti.».

eng: When Herod knew the magi had fooled him, he grew very angry. He sent soldiers to kill all the children in Bethlehem and in all the surrounding territory who were two years old and younger, according to the time that he had learned from the magi.
local: Eeroodi nín dāa béè yii bin nyí iŋmalbijaa bɔtee ŋmātiǹ-u kaa ti gítìń u-ceeyee, ní u fōō diŋuul tikpil ní ki pú kinyɔkɔ yii bí cù Beetileem tiŋkin nì ntimu mun māntìi tú-kèe kí kpɔ̀ mbininjabiyaamu mun cáá abin alee nì mun kaa láá bàn̄ alee, ki táá nì ŋyunti ŋun bin nyí iŋmalbijaa bɔtee nìn tùkū-uee.

eng: This fulfilled the word spoken through Jeremiah the prophet.
local: An ŋá míǹ kí dàkà Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu Yeereemii nín nìn lī puee dee, yii.

eng: A voice was heard in Ramah, weeping and much grieving. Rachel weeping for her children, and she did not want to be comforted, because they were no more.
local: «Bi gbìl̄ Raama tiŋkin dituweel, nsiim, nì iyaliŋki tikpil, an sá Raseel di bíi sūǹ u-biyaamu pu, kaa là bí ŋméeǹ-u, kun puee mu kpíì.».

eng: After King Herod died, an angel from the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph in Egypt.
local: Ubɔti Eeroodi nín kpíìyee, ní ti-Dindaan tuuŋŋu dɔ́miń Jooseefi cee kudamintiiun Eejipiti.

eng: They asked, Where is the newborn king of the Jews? Weve seen his star in the east, and weve come to honor him.
local: ní ki bālifì yii: «Juuda yab bɔtiu un bi dáá màl̄ nee bí la? Ti ká u-ŋmalbijau nyáń ŋwìiń nyalaŋki pú ní ti dɔ́miń kí gbàan̄ kí jāam̀-u.».

eng: Get up, the angel said, and take the child and his mother and go to the land of Israel. Those who were trying to kill the child are dead.
local: ní ki bí-u yii: «Yīkìm̄ kí yòon̄ kibiki nì ki-na ní gítí Isirayeel tiŋkin, kun puee bin bàà nìn nyàab̀ kí kpɔ̀-kèe kpíì, baa ti bí.».

eng: Joseph got up, took the child and his mother, and went to the land of Israel.
local: Ní Jooseefi yíkì ki yōoǹ kibiki nì ki-na ki nín gítí Isirayeel tiŋkin.

eng: But when he heard that Archelaus ruled over Judea in place of his father Herod, Joseph was afraid to go there. Having been warned in a dream, he went to the area of Galilee.
local: Ama u fāŋkì kí cùú kàl̄ Juudee tiŋkin kun puee u gbìl̄ yii Arkeelayusi di fōō u-baa Eeroodi beel Juudee. Kudamintiiun ní Unimbɔti dàkā-u, ní u túĺ Galiilee watil pú.

eng: He settled in a city called Nazareth so that what was spoken through the prophets might be fulfilled: He will be called a Nazarene.
local: ní ki cūtìi kāl̀ Galiilee tiŋki kin bi yíì Naasaareetee. An ŋá kii Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi nín nìn sòon̄ ki síiǹ puee dee, yii: «Bi làá dāá nīn yíì-u unaasaareetija ní.».

eng: When King Herod heard this, he was troubled, and everyone in Jerusalem was troubled with him.
local: Ubɔti Eeroodi nín gbìl̄ mimmee, ní u-pɔbil bíil̀, ki kpáaǹ Jeerusaleem nib kɔkɔ.

eng: He gathered all the chief priests and the legal experts and asked them where the Christ was to be born.
local: U nìn yíiń Juuda yab saraaŋaliibi nì bimaradakaliibi ki kpáfì ní ki bālifì-bi yii: «La ní an lī yii bi làá màal̀ Meesiya ní?».

eng: They said, In Bethlehem of Judea, for this is what the prophet wrote.
local: Ní bi kíí-u yii: «Beetileem, kitiŋ kin bí Juudeeyee, kun puee Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu nìn ŋmàn̄ yii.

eng: You, Bethlehem, land of Judah, by no means are you least among the rulers of Judah, because from you will come one who governs, who will shepherd my people Israel.
local: <Sii Beetileem, kitiŋ kin bí Juudeeyee, tiŋmanee, aa sá kitiŋwaai Juudee tiŋkpaammu kansikin, kun puee sii poon ní ubɔti un làá kpàà Isirayeel yab man nibulee làá nyānní.>».

eng: Then Herod secretly called for the magi and found out from them the time when the star had first appeared.
local: Niinee ní Eeroodi yíiń bin nyí iŋmalbijaa bɔtee dibalin ki bālifì-bi ŋyunti ŋun páaa ní bi ká uŋmalbijee.

eng: He sent them to Bethlehem, saying, Go and search carefully for the child. When youve found him, report to me so that I too may go and honor him.
local: ní ki bí yii bí nīn cá Beetileem, kí nyàab̀ mmɔntiimin kí béé laakin kibikee bée. U nìn bí-bi yii: «Ni yaa ká-kèe, ní dāan̄ kí tùkù ma mun ń cù kí gbàan̄ kí jāam̀-kì.».

eng: When they heard the king, they went; and look, the star they had seen in the east went ahead of them until it stood over the place where the child was.
local: Ubɔti tùkū-bi mimmee, ní bi búntì, ní ki ká uŋmalbijee līntì bi-nyɔkɔpu ki bàn̄ laakin bi màl̄ kibikee, ní ki yúl̀ kutaa pu ki bíitì niin.

eng: The kingdom of heaven is like a landowner who went out early in the morning to hire workers for his vineyard.
local: «Unimbɔti Beel náań nì kii bukpaadaan ubɔ nín nyáń kutaafaau pɔ̀lpɔl kí nyàab̀ bipaadambi bí tɔ̄ ditundi u-kpaabun puee ní.

eng: Now when those hired first came, they thought they would receive more. But each of them also received a denarion.
local: Bin bin u péé péé ki cúúyee dàkafī yii bíǹ làá fōō tipaal án nīn wīikí ki jítiǹ bin lúŋkì boonee, ní bi dū ŋwiin ŋubɔ paati ki tīī báà ŋma.

eng: When they received it, they grumbled against the landowner.
local: Bi nín fōō animbilee, ní bi bíi gbùkum̄ ukpilee pu, yii.

eng: These who were hired last worked one hour, and they received the same pay as we did even though we had to work the whole day in the hot sun.
local: <Bin a lúŋkì ki fōō nee, kukúbambaantiiu ní bi dūu túń ditundi, ní a pàn̄-bi mbaantiim kii timbi bin kíĺ doooo kutaafaau ki kùn̄ ki gīī, ŋwìiń píĺ-tee.>.

eng: But he replied to one of them, Friend, I did you no wrong. Didnt I agree to pay you a denarion?.
local: Ní u fātìi kíí bi-kansikin ubɔ yii: <M-bɔɔ, maa mūul̀-si. Man nì si kaa sòon̄ a kíí yii a làá fōō ŋwiin ŋubɔ paati níii?.

eng: Take what belongs to you and go. I want to give to this one who was hired last the same as I give to you.
local: Fōō a-nimbiliŋ kí nīn cá, m là kí tīī ujoolkaa nee kii m nín tīī si mun puee ní.

eng: Dont I have the right to do what I want with what belongs to me? Or are you resentful because Im generous.
local: Maa cáá nsan kí dū m-nimbiliŋ kí ŋá m nín là pueeee? Àá, m nín ŋá un béeyee tiŋanee ní an wù-si ní?>».

eng: So those who are last will be first. And those who are first will be last.
local: Ní Yeesu gíti lī yii: «Biboompaanliibi làá dāá ŋá bisanlintiliibi, ní bisanlintiliibi ń dāá ŋá biboompaanliibi.».

eng: As Jesus was going up to Jerusalem, he took the Twelve aside by themselves on the road. He told them.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun Yeesu làá nīn cá Jeerusaleemee ní u yíiń u-boonnooliibi saalaa nì bilee ŋkpaanceeti, ki bí-bi yii.

eng: Look, we are going up to Jerusalem. The Human One will be handed over to the chief priests and legal experts. They will condemn him to death.
local: «Ni ká Jeerusaleem un ti jɔ́ḿ neeyaa, bi làá cúú man Kinibiki kí tīī bisaraaŋakpilib nì bimaradakaliibi, ní bíǹ ń dāá sòoǹ m-bɔti kí lì yii bí kpɔ̀-mi.

eng: They will hand him over to the Gentiles to be ridiculed, tortured, and crucified. But he will be raised on the third day.
local: Bi làá dū-mi kí ŋúbíǹ bin kaa sá Juuda yabee ní bíǹ ń bèel̀-mi kí gbá-mi anaalab, kí kpáá-mi ndɔpuntikaa pu kí kpɔ̀-mi, ní winta dalee ḿ fàtīí fìkitī.».

eng: After he agreed with the workers to pay them a denarion, he sent them into his vineyard.
local: U ká-bee, ní bi sòon̄ ki bɔ́ntì yii u làá pà-bi ŋwiin ŋubɔ tundi paati, ní u cáá-bi ki tɔ́ u-kpaabun.

eng: Then the mother of Zebedees sons came to Jesus along with her sons. Bowing before him, she asked a favor of him.
local: Niinee, Seebeedee jipɔmbi na yōoǹní u-jipɔmbi ki dɔ́miń Yeesu cee ki gbāaǹ u-nimbiin kí mèè-u tiba.

eng: What do you want? he asked. She responded, Say that these two sons of mine will sit, one on your right hand and one on your left, in your kingdom.
local: Ní Yeesu bālifì-u yii: «Ba ní a là?» Ní u bí-u yii: «Ŋyunti ŋun a yaa láa kpántì ubɔtee, á yàntī m-jipɔmbi bili nee ubɔ ń dāá nīn kā a-ŋaŋgiil pú, un kíǹee a-ŋaŋgan pú.».

eng: Jesus replied, You dont know what youre asking! Can you drink from the cup that Im about to drink from? They said to him, We can.
local: Ní Yeesu bí yii: «Naa nyí tin ni mèéyee. Ni ŋūǹ kí nyɔ̀ falaa un man làá nyɔ̀ naaa?» Ní bi kíí-u yii: «Iiin, ti ŋūǹ.».

eng: He said to them, You will drink from my cup, but to sit at my right or left hand isnt mine to give. It belongs to those for whom my Father prepared it.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-bi yii: «Tiŋman, an lafun sá ní nyɔ̀ falaa gbanti, ama an yaa kíǹ ŋŋaŋgiil nì ŋŋaŋgan kakaaŋu bɔtee, an kaa sá man di pūǹ an paaŋyi. Ipaan gbanti sá ki tīī bin pu m-Baa bɔ́ntì-i ki síiǹee ní.».

eng: Now when the other ten disciples heard about this, they became angry with the two brothers.
local: U-boonnooliibi saalaa bin kíǹee nín gbìl̄ mimmee, ní bi fōō nnabiyaamu mulee pu diŋuul.

eng: But Jesus called them over and said, You know that those who rule the Gentiles show off their authority over them and their high-ranking officials order them around.
local: Niinee ní Yeesu yíiń bi-kɔkɔ, ní ki bí-bi yii: «Ni nyí yii atimbun bɔtiibi wɔ́b́ bi-yab, ní binikpaambi tɔ́kɔ́ bin kíǹee pu ki dàkà-bi bi-pɔŋŋu.

eng: But thats not the way it will be with you. Whoever wants to be great among you will be your servant.
local: An kaa máaǹ kí nīn bí míǹ nimbi kansikin, ama un yaa là kí nīn sá ukpaan ni-kansikinee, udaan ń gíiǹ u-ba ni-tɔntɔnliu.

eng: Whoever wants to be first among you will be your slave.
local: ní ni-kansikin ubɔ yaa là kí nīn sá upeepeekaayee, udaan ń nīn sá ni-yumbuu.

eng: just as the Human One didnt come to be served but rather to serve and to give his life to liberate many people.
local: Míǹ ní man Kinibiki kaa dɔ́miń bí tɔ̄ ditundi kí tīī-mi, m dɔ́miń kí tɔ̄ atuun kí tīī binib ní kí dū m-maŋfal kí pà kí fōō binib tikpil kí líí.».

eng: As Jesus and his disciples were going out of Jericho a large crowd followed him.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun Yeesu bíi nyēē Jeeriikoo tiŋkee, dinikitikpaandi diba nìn nóó-u.

eng: Then he went out around nine in the morning and saw others standing around the marketplace doing nothing.
local: Tikúti tiwa gbáńee, u nyáń ki ká biba kā kinyɔŋin fám kaa bíi tūǹ diba.

eng: When two blind men sitting along the road heard that Jesus was passing by, they shouted, Show us mercy, Lord, Son of David.
local: Bijɔfub bili nìn kā nsan caŋin. Bi gbìl̄ Yeesu bíi jītée, ní bi bíi tátí yii: «Ukpil, Daafiidi naantiiu, sɔ̄ ti-saai!».

eng: Now the crowd scolded them and told them to be quiet. But they shouted even louder, Show us mercy, Lord, Son of David.
local: Ní diniwulee bíi wòoǹ-bi yii bí ŋmīĺee ní bi mɔ̄tìi bíi tátí mpɔn yii: «Ukpil, Daafiidi naantiiu, sɔ̄ ti-saai!».

eng: Jesus stopped in his tracks and called to them. What do you want me to do for you? he asked.
local: Niinee ní Yeesu sāā ki síl̀, ní ki yíiń-bi ki bālifì-bi yii: «Ba ní ni là ḿ ŋá kí tīī-ni ní?».

eng: Lord, we want to see, they replied.
local: Ní bi kíí-u yii: «Ukpil, ŋúbití ti-nimbiliŋ.».

eng: Jesus had compassion on them and touched their eyes. Immediately they were able to see, and they followed him.
local: Ní Yeesu súń bi-saai ní ki gbēē bi-nimbiliŋ, kpalaa niinee ní bi wál̀, ki kíǹ ki nóó-u.

eng: He said to them, You also go into the vineyard, and Ill pay you whatever is right.
local: ní u bí-bi yii: <Ni mun, nín̄ cámaan m-kpaabun, m làá dāá pà-ni an nín máaǹ puee.>.

eng: And they went. Again around noon and then at three in the afternoon, he did the same thing.
local: Ní bi búntì. Ní bukpaadaan tí nyáń tikúti saalaa nì tili, ní ki dāa nyáń wiŋkpooti tikúti tita ki ká biba mun.

eng: Around five in the afternoon he went and found others standing around, and he said to them, Why are you just standing around here doing nothing all day long.
local: Kujoou tikúti tiŋmoo watilee, ní u tí nyáń ki ká biba kā, ní u bālifì-bi yii: <Ba ŋá ní doooo kutaafaau ni kā doo fám, kaa bíi tūǹ diba?>.

eng: Because nobody has hired us, they replied. He responded, You also go into the vineyard.
local: Ní bi kíí-u yii: <Di sá yii ubɔ kaa cúú-ti pàá.> Ní u bí yii: <Too, ni mun nín̄ cámaan man kpaabun.>.

eng: When evening came, the owner of the vineyard said to his manager, Call the workers and give them their wages, beginning with the last ones hired and moving on finally to the first.
local: «Kujoou jóotèe, ní bukpaadaan bí un kpèé u-paadambee yii: <Yíiń bipaadambee kí pà-bi bi-paati, kí kíliń bin m lúŋkèe kí dāá kátí bin m pééyee.>.

eng: When those who were hired at five in the afternoon came, each one received a denarion.
local: Ní bin bi cúú kujoou tikúti tiŋmooyee dɔ́miń, ní bi-kɔkɔ fōō ŋwimmɔn paati.

eng: When they approached Jerusalem and came to Bethphage on the Mount of Olives, Jesus gave two disciples a task.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun bi tóob́ Jeerusaleem ki bàn̄ Beetifagee timbiliiki kin bí Ɔliifi sufii jool caŋinee, ní Yeesu lēē u-boonnooliibi bili ki tú.

eng: And when Jesus entered Jerusalem, the whole city was stirred up. Who is this? they asked.
local: Yeesu nín bàn̄ ki kɔ́ Jeerusaleemee, ní kitiŋ kɔkɔ fōō ki píĺ dàmmm, ní binib bàlifī yii: «Unil nee sá ŋma ní?».

eng: The crowds answered, Its the prophet Jesus from Nazareth in Galilee.
local: Ní kunipaauee kíikí yii: «An sá Yeesu Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu un sá Naasaareeti Galiilee tiŋkinee.».

eng: Then Jesus went into the temple and threw out all those who were selling and buying there. He pushed over the tables used for currency exchange and the chairs of those who sold doves.
local: Ní Yeesu kɔ́ kunimbɔtidiin ki jìn̄ biwannyafiliibi nì bidaliibi kɔkɔ ki nyántì, ní ki lábitì binimbikpantiliibi teebilib nì binanjinyafiliibi jaliŋ ki cìb̄.

eng: He said to them, Its written, My house will be called a house of prayer. But youve made it a hideout for crooks.
local: ní ki bí-bi yii: «An ŋmàn̄ Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon yii: <Bi làá dāá nīn yíì man diiku ŋwimmeem diiku ní,> ama ní nimbi dū-ku ki kpántì kunaayudii.».

eng: People who were blind and lame came to Jesus in the temple, and he healed them.
local: Niinee ní bijɔfub nì bin tà bi-taaŋi puee sútìń ki pìl̄-u kunimbɔtidii poon, ní u cáaǹ-bi.

eng: But when the chief priests and legal experts saw the amazing things he was doing and the children shouting in the temple, Hosanna to the Son of David! they were angry.
local: Bisaraaŋakpilib nì bimaradakaliibi nín ká maamaaci tuuŋŋi ŋin Yeesu ŋáaǹee, nì mbiyaamu nín bí kunimbɔtidii poon ki tátí yii: «Oosaana! Daafiidi naantiiu ń ká tinyoolee!» ní bi fōō diŋuul.

eng: They said to Jesus, Do you hear what these children are saying? Yes, he answered. Havent you ever read, From the mouths of babies and infants youve arranged praise for yourself? .
local: ní ki bí-u yii: «A gbìl̀ bi nín tátí ki lì pueeyaaa, saa gbìl̀ ní?» Ní u kíí-bi yii: «M gbìl̀! Naa láá pée kàan̄ Unimbɔti gbɔŋkun laakin an ŋmàn̄ yii: <A yāntì mbiyaamu ki kpáaǹ mun dáá bíi ŋáaḿee nyɔ́ŋkí-si>?».

eng: Then he left them and went out of the city to Bethany and spent the night there.
local: U lī mimmee, ní ki yāntì-bi, ki nyáń ki búntì Beetanii ki tin dōoǹ.

eng: Early in the morning as Jesus was returning to the city, he was hungry.
local: Ku wúntì kutaafaau, Yeesu fàtīi gítiń kitiŋinee ní ŋkùm cúú-u.

eng: He saw a fig tree along the road, but when he came to it, he found nothing except leaves. Then he said to it, Youll never again bear fruit! The fig tree dried up at once.
local: U kpēēyee ki ká busubu bun bi yíì fiigee yú nsan caŋin, ní u pāatì busubuee taapu ki bàà nyàab̀ asubil kí ŋmɔ́, ní ki ká yii an sá tifaal ŋmaniŋma ní. Ní u lī busubuee yii: «Sii pée gíti kpá kí lòoǹ dibil.» Kpalaa niin ní busubuee kūń.

eng: He said to them, Go into the village over there. As soon as you enter, you will find a donkey tied up and a colt with it. Untie them and bring them to me.
local: U nìn bí-bi yii: «Nín̄ cámaan kitimbilii kin bí ti-nyɔkɔpu nee. Ni yaa bàn̄ kpalaayee, ni làá ká kimansalii kiba nì ki-biki gbìn̄ niin. Ní gbìntīń-mu kí cáań-mi.

eng: When the disciples saw it, they were amazed. How did the fig tree dry up so fast? they asked.
local: U-boonnooliibi ká mimmee ní an ŋá-bi bitii, ní bi bālifì-u yii: «Mana ní busubuee pūtì kpáàa kūń míǹ dicilpuee?».

eng: Jesus responded, I assure you that if you have faith and dont doubt, you will not only do what was done to the fig tree. You will even say to this mountain, Be lifted up and thrown into the lake. And it will happen.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-bi yii: «Ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-ni, ni yaa fōō Unimbɔti ki kíí mbaamɔmin kaa gùgufēe, an kaa sá min m ŋá busubu nee ŋmaniŋma ní ni làá dāá ŋmā kí ŋá. Ni dāa ŋūǹ kí lì gba dijool nee yii: <Á yīkì laakin a yú nee kí cùú jēetí tiŋkun!> ní dí lafun ŋá míǹ.

eng: If you have faith, you will receive whatever you pray for.
local: Tin kamaa ní ni yaa mēē Unimbɔti ní ki yaa fōō ki kíí yii u làá ŋá kí tīī-nee, ni làá ká kudɔŋu.».

eng: When Jesus entered the temple, the chief priests and elders of the people came to him as he was teaching. They asked, What kind of authority do you have for doing these things? Who gave you this authority.
local: Yeesu tí kɔ́ kunimbɔtidiin ki bíi dàkà binib Unimbɔti bɔtee, ní bisaraaŋakpilib nì Juuda yab kpilib dɔ́miń u-cee ki bālifì-u yii: «Nì nsan ŋulɔŋu pu ní a tūǹ atuun ŋin a tūǹ nee ní? Ŋma di pú-si an saŋŋu?».

eng: Jesus replied, I have a question for you. If you tell me the answer, Ill tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Ní u bí-bi yii: «Dinyɔɔbumbaantiil ní m làá pée bàlifì-ni ní kíí-mi kí wàatī, ní ma mun ń tùkù-ni nì nsan ŋun pu ní m ŋáań mimmee.

eng: Where did John get his authority to baptize? Did he get it from heaven or from humans? They argued among themselves, If we say from heaven, hell say to us, Then why didnt you believe him.
local: Yoowaneesi nín fùul̀ binib Unimbɔti nyimee, ŋma di tīī-u an pɔŋŋu ní? Unimbɔtaaa, binib?» Niinee ní bi kíĺ ki bíi sòoǹ bi-ŋmaniŋma yii: «Ti yaa kíí yii: <An sá Unimbɔti di túnní-u,> u làá wòoǹ-ti yii: <Kin, ba ŋá ní naa fōō-u ki kíí?>.

eng: But we cant say from humans because were afraid of the crowd, since everyone thinks John was a prophet.
local: Ti yaa tí kíí-u yii: <An sá binib di túnní-uaaa,> ti fàŋkí kunipaau mun, kun puee báà ŋma kɔkɔ nyí yii Yoowaneesi sá Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu ní.».

eng: Then they replied, We dont know. Jesus also said to them, Neither will I tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Ní bi kíí Yeesu yii: «Taa nyí.» Ní u mun bi yii: «An yaa sée, ma mun kaa làá tùkù-ni nì nsan ŋun pu ní m ŋáań mimmee!».

eng: What do you think? A man had two sons. Now he came to the first and said, Son, go and work in the vineyard today.
local: Ní Yeesu gíti bí yii: «Ni dàkafī tibɔti nee pu yii ba? Uninja ubɔ di cáá binaacimpɔmbi bili ní ki bí upeepeekaa yii: <M-jipɔɔn, á cù kusaau díǹ kí tɔ̄ ditundi.>.

eng: No, I dont want to, he replied. But later he changed his mind and went.
local: Ní kibikee yíì ki lī yii: <Maa làá cù,> ama ní boonee ki dāa kpántì ki-landɔkɔŋu ní ki búntì kusaau.

eng: If anyone says anything to you, say that their master needs them. He sent them off right away.
local: Ubɔ yaa bālifì-ni tibee, kíímaan-u yii: <Ti-Dindaan di nyàab̀-mu,> niinee, udaan làá yàntī ní cáań-mu.».

eng: The father said the same thing to the other son, who replied, Yes, sir. But he didnt go.
local: Ní u cūtì un kíǹee cee ní ki lī u mun tibɔbaantiil. Ní úǹ kíí-u yii: <M-baa, m làá cù!> ama ní kaa gbóoń kusaau.

eng: Which one of these two did his fathers will? They said, The first one. Jesus said to them, I assure you that tax collectors and prostitutes are entering Gods kingdom ahead of you.
local: Binaacimpɔmbee liitil kansikinee, ulɔu di ŋá min bi-baa lèe?» Ní bi kíí yii: «Upeepeekaa di ŋá.» Niinee ní Yeesu bí-bi yii: «Ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-ni, bifɔtiliibi nì nnimpulaakpaamu làá lìntī ni-nyɔkɔpu kí kɔ́ Unimbɔti Beelin.

eng: For John came to you on the righteous road, and you didnt believe him. But tax collectors and prostitutes believed him. Yet even after you saw this, you didnt change your hearts and lives and you didnt believe him.
local: Di sá yii Yoowaneesi dɔ́miń ni-cee ki dàkà-ni deedee saŋŋu ní naa fōō-u ki kíí, ama ní bifɔtiliibi bin ni kpèé fámee nì nnimpulaakpaamu fōō-u ki kíí. Ni nín ká mimmee boonee naa kpántì ni-landɔkɔi kí fōō-u kí kíí.».

eng: Listen to another parable. There was a landowner who planted a vineyard. He put a fence around it, dug a winepress in it, and built a tower. Then he rented it to tenant farmers and took a trip.
local: Ní Yeesu bí yii: «Gíti pīlifìmaan tibɔŋmantikaal nee. Uninja ubɔ di kùn̄ fiinyi kpaabu. U cóoǹ kunaabakau ki māntì ki tú-bu, ki gbìn̄ mbòó ŋun ni u làá nīn ŋmāam̀ isufee biliŋee, ki gāā isupaa bukpaayee kansikin, ní ki dū isufee ki tīī bipaadambi biba, ní ki búntì nsan.

eng: When it was time for harvest, he sent his servants to the tenant farmers to collect his fruit.
local: Isufee biliŋ kìtitam bàn̄ee, ní ukpilee túnní u-tɔntɔnliibi biba u-paadambee cee bí fōō u-jandi kí cáań-u.

eng: But the tenant farmers grabbed his servants. They beat some of them, and some of them they killed. Some of them they stoned to death.
local: Ní bipaadambee cúú u-tɔntɔnliibee ubɔ ki gbáń ní ki kūū ubɔ mun ní ki yáŋkì utataati ataŋkpee ki kūū.

eng: Again he sent other servants, more than the first group. They treated them in the same way.
local: Ní bukpaadaanee gíti túnní bitɔntɔnliibi biba bíǹ wīikíǹ bin u péé péé ki túnnée, ní bipaadambee tí cúú bi mun ki ŋá kii bi nín ŋá bipeepeekaabee puee.

eng: Finally he sent his son to them. They will respect my son, he said.
local: Boonee ní u baasii dū u-jipɔɔn ki tú, ki dàkafī yii: <Úǹ sá m-jipɔɔnee, bi làá fàŋkì-u.>.

eng: But when the tenant farmers saw the son, they said to each other, This is the heir. Come on, lets kill him and well have his inheritance.
local: Ama bipaadambee nín ká u-jipɔɔnee páaǹnée, ní bi bíi lì tɔb cee yii: <Kpèè un làá dāá jī bukpaa fool boonee di dòońee! Tí kpɔ̀maan-u kí jī kusaau!>.

eng: They grabbed him, threw him out of the vineyard, and killed him.
local: Ní bi cúú-u ki nyántì ŋkpaabuku ki kūū-u.».

eng: Now this happened to fulfill what the prophet said.
local: An jítì míǹ kí dàkà Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi nín nìn lī ki síiǹ puee yii.

eng: When the owner of the vineyard comes, what will he do to those tenant farmers.
local: Ní Yeesu bālifì-bi yii: «Mimmee bukpaadaan yaa láa bāańee, u làá ŋá-bi mana?».

eng: They said, He will totally destroy those wicked farmers and rent the vineyard to other tenant farmers who will give him the fruit when its ready.
local: Ní bisaraaŋakpilib nì Fariisab kíí-u yii: «U yaa bāańee, see ú kpɔ̀ binikpitib gbanti nsansaaŋkúm ní kí dū u-kpaabu kí tīī bipaadambi bin ŋyunti yaa bāań, bi làá nīn tìiń-u an subiliŋee.».

eng: Jesus said to them, Havent you ever read in the scriptures, The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone. The Lord has done this, and its amazing in our eyes?.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-bi yii: «Naa láa pée kàan̄ Unimbɔti gbɔŋkun laakin lī yii: <Ditaŋkpal din bidimaliibi nìn dūu fée, díǹ di dāa kpántì ditaŋkpal din ŋúb́ kudii pɔŋŋuee. Ti-Dindaan nín ŋá tiŋyee kaa sá maamaaci ki tīī-taaa?>.

eng: Therefore, I tell you that Gods kingdom will be taken away from you and will be given to a people who produce its fruit.
local: An pu ní m tùkù-ni yii bi làá dāá fōō ni-cee Unimbɔti Beel kí dūú tīī ditimbundi din yab làá ŋá Unimbɔti lammee. .

eng: Whoever falls on this stone will be crushed. And the stone will crush the person it falls on.
local: Un di yaa lítì ditaŋkpalee puee làá yàaǹ, ama ní ditaŋkpalee yaa tàb̄ uŋyee, udaan làá nà ní dúsi dusi.».

eng: Now when the chief priests and the Pharisees heard the parable, they knew Jesus was talking about them.
local: Bisaraaŋakpilib nì Fariisab nín gbìl̄ Yeesu bɔŋmantikaati gbantee, ní bi béè yii bíǹ ní u lì.

eng: They were trying to arrest him, but they feared the crowds, who thought he was a prophet.
local: Ní bi bíi nyàab̀ nsan ŋun bi làá dī kí cúú-uee, ama ní ki fàŋkí kunipaau, kun puee báà ŋma nyí yii Yeesu sá Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu ní.

eng: Say to Daughter Zion, Look, your king is coming to you, humble and riding on a donkey, and on a colt the donkeys offspring. .
local: «Tùkùmaan Siiyɔn tiŋki nib, yii: Kpèèmaan, ni-bɔtiu dòoń ni-cee, ki sá unil un mɔ̄ee. U kā kimaŋ pu, u kāń dimampɔmbil, kimansalii biki pu.».

eng: The disciples went and did just as Jesus had ordered them.
local: Ní u-boonnooliibee búntì ki cūtìi ŋá kii Yeesu nín bí yii bí ŋá miŋyee.

eng: They brought the donkey and the colt and laid their clothes on them. Then he sat on them.
local: Bi cáań kimansaliiyee nì kimambiki, ní ki dū bi-bɔkutiŋ ki páaǹ mu-pu, ní Yeesu jɔ́m̀ ki kāl̀.

eng: Now a large crowd spread their clothes on the road. Others cut palm branches off the trees and spread them on the road.
local: Ní dinikitilee kansikin binib tikpil kpátí bi-bɔkutiŋ ki bīil̀ nsan pu, ní biba mun gà tikpakpati ki bīil̀ nsan pu.

eng: The crowds in front of him and behind him shouted, Hosanna to the Son of David! Blessings on the one who comes in the name of the Lord! Hosanna in the highest.
local: Ní kunipaau kun līī Yeesu nyɔkɔpuee nì kun pá u-boonee kūuntí yii: «Oosaana! Daafiidi naantiiu ń ká tinyool! Ti-Dindaan ń bìin̄ un dòoń u-yindi puee! Bí nyɔ̄ŋkì Unimbɔti un bí yilpuee!».

eng: Jesus responded by speaking again in parables.
local: Ní Yeesu tí dū tibɔŋmantikaal nee ki ŋmāntì ki lī yii.

eng: Then those servants went to the roads and gathered everyone they found, both evil and good. The wedding party was full of guests.
local: Ní bitɔntɔnliibee nyáń isantaan pu ki kpáfì bin kɔkɔ bi kée, bikpitib nì bimɔntiibi kɔkɔ, ní ki cáań-bi ki gbéeǹ kudii.

eng: Now when the king came in and saw the guests, he spotted a man who wasnt wearing wedding clothes.
local: «Ubɔtee kɔ́ kí kpèè bin u yíińee, ki dūu ká kijapaai kiba kíǹ kaa pēē jiŋaal bɔkutil din bin béeyee pēē ki dɔ́miń tinimpucan jiŋaaliuee.

eng: He said to him, Friend, how did you get in here without wedding clothes? But he was speechless.
local: Ní u bālifì-u yii: <M-bɔɔ, ba ŋá ní a kɔ́nní doo kaa pēē dijiŋaabɔkutil?> Ní kijapaayee kā ki ŋmí.

eng: Then the king said to his servants, Tie his hands and feet and throw him out into the farthest darkness. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: Niinee ní ubɔti bí u-tɔntɔnliibi yii: <Bóob́maan u-taaŋi nì u-ŋalii iŋmii kí dū-u kí tɔ̄ mmɔŋki dibɔmbɔndin. Niin ní u làá sɔ̄ u-nyiŋŋi ń nīn gà tɔb kàkaka.>.

eng: Many people are invited, but few people are chosen.
local: Di sá yii bi yíiń kunipaau, ama ní ki lútì fiii.».

eng: Then the Pharisees met together to find a way to trap Jesus in his words.
local: Niinee Fariisab cūtì ki sòon̄ ki bɔ́ntì bi nín làá dī min kí dū tibɔti kí píĺ Yeesu ú sòoǹ bí ŋmā kí cúú-uee.

eng: They sent their disciples, along with the supporters of Herod, to him. Teacher, they said, we know that you are genuine and that you teach Gods way as it really is. We know that you are not swayed by peoples opinions, because you dont show favoritism.
local: Ní bi dū bi-yab biba nì Eeroodi yab ki tú bí dāá bàlifì-u yii: «Ukpil, ti nyí yii sii di sòoǹ ibaamɔn ní ki dàkà min ní Unimbɔti là unil ń nīn bée, kaa fàŋkí binib nín dàkafī puee, kaa fàtīí tɔ̄ báà udaan yaa sá uŋyee.

eng: So tell us what you think: Does the Law allow people to pay taxes to Caesar or not.
local: Mimmee á tùkù-ti sii nín dàkafī tibɔti nee pu miŋyee. Ti-marau pú-ti nsan tí pà lampoo kí tīī Room gɔminau Seesaaraaa, an kaa pú?».

eng: Knowing their evil motives, Jesus replied, Why do you test me, you hypocrites?.
local: Ama ní Yeesu béè bi-landɔkɔkpitii, ní ki bí-bi yii: «Ipoonnyoondambi, ba pu ní ni píĺ-mi tibɔti kí kpèè m-nyɔkɔn míǹ?.

eng: Show me the coin used to pay the tax. And they brought him a denarion.
local: Dàkàmaan-mi animbil ŋin bi cáa pà lampooyee.» Ní bi lēē animbil ki dàkā-u.

eng: The kingdom of heaven is like a king who prepared a wedding party for his son.
local: «Unimbɔti Beel nín náań nì kuŋyee sɔ̀. Ubɔti ubɔ di cáá unimpu ki tīī u-jipɔɔn ní ki ŋá tican kí tīī binib.

eng: Whose image and inscription is this? he asked.
local: Ní u bālifì-bi yii: «Ŋma yil nì u-yindi di náká animbil pu na?».

eng: Caesars, they replied. Then he said, Give to Caesar what belongs to Caesar and to God what belongs to God.
local: Ní bi kíí-u yii: «An sá Room gɔminau Seesaar.» Ní Yeesu bí-bi yii: «Dūmaan tin gɔmina yèe kí tīī gɔmina, kí dū Unimbɔti mun yati kí tīī Unimbɔti!».

eng: When they heard this they were astonished, and they departed.
local: Bi nín gbìl̄ an bɔtee ní bi bākatì sèeee, ní ki yāntì-u ki búntì.

eng: That same day Sadducees, who deny that there is a resurrection, came to Jesus.
local: Ŋwimbaanti gbantee, Sadusab biba mun dɔ́miń Yeesu cee. Bíǹ di sá dinikitil din lì yii difìkitil kaa bée. Bi bālifì Yeesu yii.

eng: They asked, Teacher, Moses said, If a man who doesnt have children dies, his brother must marry his wife and produce children for his brother.
local: «Ukpil, Mooyiisi bí yii: <Ubɔ yaa kpíì, kaa màl̄ mbiyaamu, u-nabiki ń fōō ukpiipu kí màal̀ kí téeǹ u-nabiki kin kpíìyee yil.>.

eng: Now there were seven brothers among us. The first one married, then died. Because he had no children he left his widow to his brother.
local: Kun pu ti bàlifēe di sá nnabiyaamu mululi nìn bí ti-kansikin. Upeepeekaa cáá unimpu ní ki kpíì ki yāntì unimpuee, kaa màl̄ kibiki, ní u-naal fōō ukpiipu.

eng: The same thing happened with the second brother and the third, and in fact with all seven brothers.
local: Míǹ mbaantiim uliliitee kpíì, kaa cáá kibiki, ní utataati mun fōō ukpiipu ní ki mun kpíì. Bi pée fòol̄ ukpiipuee míǹ ní ki tin sāā ululuúti ní u mun kpíì.

eng: Finally, the woman died.
local: Boonee ní unimpuee mun dāa dūu kpíì.

eng: At the resurrection, which of the seven brothers will be her husband? They were all married to her.
local: Difìkitil dalee, nnabiyaamu mululee kansikinee, ŋma di dāa yì unimpu dee? Kun puee bi-kɔkɔ pée cáá-u ki ká.».

eng: Jesus responded, You are wrong because you dont know either the scriptures or Gods power.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-bi yii: «Ni bí diyìntil poon ní nee, kun puee naa nyí Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon bɔtaaa, kaa nyí Unimbɔti pɔŋŋu mun.

eng: He sent his servants to call those invited to the wedding party. But they didnt want to come.
local: ní ki dū u-tɔntɔnliibi ki tú bí cù kí tùkù binib bin u yíińee yii bí dāan̄, ama ní bi yíì kaa dɔ́miń.

eng: At the resurrection people wont marry nor will they be given in marriage. Instead, they will be like angels from God.
local: Di sá yii, binib yaa láa fīkitèe, dinimpucaal nì dicamɔndi dāa gíti kpá, báà ŋma dāa bí kii Unimbɔti tuuŋyi nín bí pu yilpuee ní.

eng: As for the resurrection of the dead, havent you read what God told you.
local: An yaa kíǹ bitaŋkpiibi fìkititamee, naa láá pée kàan̄ ki ká Unimbɔti nín sòon̄ nì ni pueeee? U bí yii.

eng: Im the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob? He isnt the God of the dead but of the living.
local: <Man di sá Abraam Nimbɔtiu, ki sá Isaaki Nimbɔtiu, ki sá Jaakɔb mun Nimbɔtiu.> Unimbɔti yaa bí yii u sá bin kpíìyee Nimbɔtiuee, mimmee an dàkà yii úǹ ceeyee, bíǹ kɔkɔ kaa kpíì dee.».

eng: Now when the crowd heard this, they were astonished at his teaching.
local: Kunipaau kun nìn bíi pílifíǹ-uee nìn bākatì nì u-dàkatam.

eng: When the Pharisees heard that Jesus had left the Sadducees speechless, they met together.
local: Fariisab nín gbìl̄ yii Yeesu sòon̄ ki líì Sadusabee ní bíǹ kpáfì.

eng: One of them, a legal expert, tested him.
local: ní bi-kansikin umaradakal ubɔ là kí píĺ Yeesu tibɔti kí kpèè u-nyɔkɔnee ní ki bālifì-u yii.

eng: Teacher, what is the greatest commandment in the Law.
local: «Ukpil, marab kɔkɔ kansikinee, ulɔu di sá ukpaan ní?».

eng: He replied, You must love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your being, and with all your mind.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-u yii: «Á nīn néeń a-Dindaan a-Nimbɔtiu nì a-pɔbil kɔkɔ, nì a-naŋki kɔkɔ, nì a-landɔkɔŋu kɔkɔ.».

eng: This is the first and greatest commandment.
local: Mara un sá ukpaan ki līīyee dee.

eng: And the second is like it: You must love your neighbor as you love yourself .
local: Ní maraliliiti un páb́ kii upeepeekaayee sèé: <Á nīn néeń a-juti kii sisi a-bee.>.

eng: Again he sent other servants and said to them, Tell those who have been invited,Look, the meal is all prepared. Ive butchered the oxen and the fattened cattle. Now everythings ready. Come to the wedding party.
local: Ní u gíti dū u-tɔntɔnliibi biba mun ki tú ki bí-bi yii: <Cùú tùkùmaan bin m yíińee yii, tijikaal bɔ́ntì ki dóò, m kūū inajaa, ki kūū tiwaŋkuti tin cáá ŋkpamee, an kɔkɔ bɔ́ntì ki sí! Bí dāan̄ tí jī jiŋaal.>.

eng: All the Law and the Prophets depend on these two commands.
local: Marab bili nee pu ní Mooyiisi marab nì Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi dàkatam kɔkɔ tɔ́kɔ́.».

eng: Now as the Pharisees were gathering, Jesus asked them.
local: Fariisab nín nìn dáá gbíntìi bí dicilpuee, ní Yeesu bālifì-bi dinyɔɔbundi nee yii.

eng: What do you think about the Christ? Whose son is he? Davids son, they replied.
local: «Ni dàkafī Meesiya pu yii ba ní? Ŋma naantiiu ní u sá ní?» Ní bi kíí-u yii: «Daafiidi naantiiu.».

eng: He said, Then how is it that David, inspired by the Holy Spirit, called him Lord when he said.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-bi yii: «Kin, ba pu ní Unimbɔti Fam yāntì Daafiidi u-ba yíiń-u yii u-Dindaan ní? Di sá u bí yii.

eng: The Lord said to my lord, Sit at my right side until I turn your enemies into your footstool?.
local: «<Ti-Dindaan tùkū m-Dindaan yii: Kàl̄ m-ŋaŋgiil pú kí nīn cí ŋyunti ŋun m làá yàntī á nīn kíǹ ki tɔ́kɔ́ a-nannanliibi puee.>.

eng: If David calls him Lord, how can he be Davids son.
local: «Mimmee, Daafiidi yaa yíì Meesiya yii u-Dindaanee, mana ní u gíti ŋūǹ kí nīn sá u-naantiiu mun?».

eng: Nobody was able to answer him. And from that day forward nobody dared to ask him anything.
local: Bi-kansikin ubɔ kaa ŋúń ki kíí-u báà dinyɔɔbumbaantiil. Kí yòon̄ ŋyunti gbantee ubɔ kaa ti mākaǹ kí bàlifì-u tiba kí kpèè u-nyɔkɔn.

eng: But they paid no attention and went away—some to their fields, others to their businesses.
local: Ama bi-kansikin báà ubɔ kaa fātìi tɔ́, ní báà ŋma búntì u-laŋki: ubɔ búntì u-saaku, ubɔ mun búntì u-bantil.

eng: The rest of them grabbed his servants, abused them, and killed them.
local: ní bin kíǹee cúú bitɔntɔnliibee ki sāaǹ ki kūū.

eng: The king was angry. He sent his soldiers to destroy those murderers and set their city on fire.
local: Ní ubɔtee fōō diŋuul ní ki dū u-soojab ki tú bi cūtìi kūū binikpɔliibi gbanti ki sēē bi-tiŋki mun ŋŋmi.

eng: Then he said to his servants, The wedding party is prepared, but those who were invited werent worthy.
local: Niinee ní u bí u-tɔntɔnliibi yii: <Tijikaal bɔ́ntì ki sí dee, ama ní bin m bàà yíińee kaa kítì.

eng: Therefore, go to the roads on the edge of town and invite everyone you find to the wedding party.
local: Míǹ puee nín̄ cámaan isampuyakati pu kí nīn yíìń bin kɔkɔ ní ni yaa pée kée bí dāan̄ kí jī.>.

eng: Then Jesus spoke to the crowds and his disciples.
local: Niinee, Yeesu bí kunipaauee nì u-boonnooliibi yii.

eng: Dont be called teacher , because Christ is your one teacher.
local: Ní taa yàntī bí nīn yíì-ni <Uyidaan>, kun puee ubaanti di sá ni-Yidaaŋwu, úǹ di sá Meesiya.

eng: But the one who is greatest among you will be your servant.
local: Un gíiǹ u-ba ni-tɔntɔnliuee di dāa sá ukpaan ni-kansikin.

eng: All who lift themselves up will be brought low. But all who make themselves low will be lifted up.
local: Un yaa yōoǹ u-ba yilpuee bi làá dāá sìntīǹ-u taapu, ní un yaa sīntiǹ u-ba taapuee, bi làá dāá yòon̄-u yilpu.».

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You shut people out of the kingdom of heaven. You dont enter yourselves, and you wont allow those who want to enter to do so.
local: Yeesu bí yii: «Mbusu dɔ̄ ki cí-ni, nimbi bimaradakaliibi nì Fariisab mpoonnyoondambi, kun puee ni pìintí binib Unimbɔti Beel punyɔkɔ. Naa làá kɔ́ ni-ba, ní kaa là bin bàà là kí kɔ́ee ń kɔ́.» .

eng: How terrible it will be for you, legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You travel over sea and land to make one convert. But when theyve been converted, they become twice the child of hell you are.
local: «Mbusu dɔ̄ ki cí-ni, nimbi bimaradakaliibi nì Fariisab mpoonnyoondambi, kun puee ni yìiǹ tiŋkub nì kitiŋ jaaŋŋi kɔkɔ pu ki nyàab̀ un làá kpántí ni-boonnooliuee, ní ni yaa ŋúń ki yāntì u kpántì u-bee, ní ní yàntī ú kpántí un làá dāá kɔ́ ditafadaaldinee mili pu kí jītíǹ nimbi.

eng: How terrible it will be for you blind guides who say, If people swear by the temple, its nothing. But if people swear by the gold in the temple, they are obligated to do what they swore.
local: «Mbusu dɔ̄ ki cí-ni, nimbi bijɔfub bin kpòotī binibee, kun puee ni lì yii: <Unil yaa pūtì kunimbɔtidiiyee, an kaa sá tiba, ama un yaa pūtì sindaariya un bí kunimbɔtidiinee, ú ŋá min u pūtèe.>.

eng: You foolish and blind people! Which is greater, the gold or the temple that makes the gold holy?.
local: Bigbaambi, bijɔfub! Ba di páb́ ní? Sindaariyaaa, kunimbɔtidii kun yāntì sindaariyee kpántì circiree?.

eng: You say, If people swear by the altar, its nothing. But if they swear by the gift on the altar, they are obligated to do what they swore.
local: Ni tí lì yii: <Unil yaa pūtì nsaraafanee, an kaa sá tiba, ama un di yaa pūtì saraa un pá nsaraafan puee, ú ŋá min u pūtèe.>.

eng: You blind people! Which is greater, the gift or the altar that makes the gift holy?.
local: Bijɔfub, tilati di páb́ ní? Saraayaaa, nsaraafan ŋun pu saraayee pá ki kpántì circiree ní?.

eng: The legal experts and the Pharisees sit on Moses seat.
local: «Bimaradakaliibi nì Fariisab di bí ki dàkà Mooyiisi marab.

eng: Therefore, those who swear by the altar swear by it and by everything thats on it.
local: Un di yaa pūtì nsaraafanee, pūtì ki kpáaǹ tin kɔkɔ pá nsaraafan puee dee.

eng: Those who swear by the temple swear by it and by everything thats part of it.
local: Un di yaa pūtì kunimbɔtidiiyee, pūtì Unimbɔti un kóò kudii gbantinee dee.

eng: Those who swear by heaven swear by Gods throne and by the one who sits on it.
local: Un yaa pūtì kutaayee, pūtì Unimbɔti beejal nì Unimbɔti un kā di-puee dee.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You give to God a tenth of mint, dill, and cumin, but you forget about the more important matters of the Law: justice, peace, and faith. You ought to give a tenth but without forgetting about those more important matters.
local: «Mbusu dɔ̄ ki cí-ni, nimbi bimaradakaliibi nì Fariisab mpoonnyoondambi, kun puee ni pà tikuŋfaal tin kɔkɔ ni kùee saalaa kamaa pu kubɔ kubɔ, ama ní ki yíití, kaa nóó marab bin bɔti páb́ee, an dàkà yii: á nīn cáá ibaamɔn, kí nīn sūǹ binib saai, kí nīn yú deedee. Tin bàà máaǹ ní ŋée dee, ní kí nín dū tin kíǹee kí páań boon.

eng: You blind guides! You filter out an ant but swallow a camel.
local: Bikpootiliibi bin jɔ̀b̄ee! Ni jákà ki lèekī isii ni-nyɔkaatin, ama ní ki nátí igutaamu.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You clean the outside of the cup and plate, but inside they are full of violence and pleasure seeking.
local: «Mbusu dɔ̄ ki cí-ni, nimbi bimaradakaliibi nì Fariisab mpoonnyoondambi, kun puee ni fíntí tiyuti nì asambil boon, ama ní ni-naayutuuŋŋi nì tiwan tin ni fìkēe gbí poon.

eng: Blind Pharisee! First clean the inside of the cup so that the outside of the cup will be clean too.
local: Fariisab bijɔfub! Ní péé kí fíntímaan kuyuu poon, ní boon mun ń kpáàa nyā.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You are like whitewashed tombs. They look beautiful on the outside. But inside they are full of dead bones and all kinds of filth.
local: «Mbusu dɔ̄ ki cí-ni, nimbi bimaradakaliibi nì Fariisab mpoonnyoondambi, kun puee ni náań nì akaakul ŋin bi píil̀ boonee ní. Ŋi ŋān̄ nnimbikpee, ama ní ŋi-poonee anikpil sūū ní akpab gbí.

eng: In the same way you look righteous to people. But inside you are full of pretense and rebellion.
local: Míǹ ní ni mun bí. Binib wāĺ-ni ŋgban puee yii ni sá deedeedambi, ama mpoonnyoon nì ikpiti di gbí ni ni.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You build tombs for the prophets and decorate the graves of the righteous.
local: «Mbusu dɔ̄ ki cí-ni, nimbi bimaradakaliibi nì Fariisab mpoonnyoondambi, kun puee ni māā Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi akaakumɔnti ní ki nyāantí bin yúl̀ deedeeyee kaakuliŋ.

eng: Therefore, you must take care to do everything they say. But dont do what they do.
local: Míǹ puee, ní nīn ŋáaḿmaan min bi dàkà-nee, ama ní taa fōō bíǹ bi-ba bimbim, kun puee baa ŋáań tin bi dàkà yii binib ń nīn ŋáańee.

eng: You say, If we had lived in our ancestors days, we wouldnt have joined them in killing the prophets.
local: ní ki lì yii: <Ti yaa bàà nìn bímaan ti-naanjab yuntiŋu, taa bàà nìn kútí bi-pu bí kpɔ̀ Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi gbanti.>.

eng: You testify against yourselves that you are children of those who murdered the prophets.
local: Míǹ ní ni bíi lèekī ni-ba pu seera yii ni sá bin kūū Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibee naantiibi.

eng: Go ahead, complete what your ancestors did.
local: Mimmee, púntímaan ni-naanjab nín nìn kíĺ ditundi diŋyee!.

eng: You snakes! You children of snakes! How will you be able to escape the judgment of hell?.
local: Nimbi bin tū kii ikoowaatee! Mana ní ni dàkafī yii ni ŋūǹ kí ŋmátí kí taa kɔ́ ditafadaaldi laŋki?.

eng: Therefore, look, Im sending you prophets, wise people, and legal experts. Some of them you will kill and crucify. And some you will beat in your synagogues and chase from city to city.
local: An puee, ní pīlifìmaan: M làá tɔ̄ń ni-cee Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi nì biciŋfitiibi nì bidakaliibi. Ni làá kpɔ̀ biba, kí kpáá biba idɔpuntikaa pu, kí gbá biba anaalab ni-kpafidiiŋŋin, kí jà biba bí póotí ntimu.

eng: Therefore, upon you will come all the righteous blood that has been poured out on the earth, from the blood of that righteous man Abel to the blood of Zechariah the son of Barachiah, whom you killed between the temple and the altar.
local: An puee nnikpaakoon tafadaaldi làá dāá lítí ni-pu, deedee nib bin kɔkɔ ni kūūyee pu, kí yòon̄ ŋyunti ŋun bi nìn kūū Abeelee kí tin sāā ŋyunti ŋun bi kūū Sakari Baaraasii jipɔɔn kunimbɔtidii nì nsaraafan kansikinee.

eng: I assure you that all these things will come upon this generation.
local: Ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-ni, nnikpaakoonee tafadaaldi làá dāá lítí nimbi waanee nib pu ní.».

eng: Jerusalem, Jerusalem! You who kill the prophets and stone those who were sent to you. How often I wanted to gather your people together, just as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you didnt want that.
local: «Jeerusaleem yab, Jeerusaleem yab, nimbi di kù Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi ní ki cáá ataŋkpee ki yāŋkí ki kù bin Unimbɔti túnní ni-ceeyee! Miŋa pu ní maa mākaǹ kí bàà kóoǹ ni-kɔkɔ kí kpāfì m-cee kii ukoonaati nín kōontí u-biyaamu u-taapu puee? Ama ní ni yíì.

eng: Look, your house is left to you deserted.
local: Too, kɔŋkɔnnee Unimbɔti làá fá ni nì ni-diiku.

eng: I tell you, you wont see me until you say, Blessings on the one who comes in the Lords name .
local: M tùkù-ni yii, kí yòon̄ díǹ kí nīn cée, naa làá ti ká-mi kí nīn cí ŋyunti ŋun ni làá dāá sòoǹ m-pu yii: <Unimbɔti ń bìin̄ un dòoń ti-Dindaan yindi puee.>».

eng: For they tie together heavy packs that are impossible to carry. They put them on the shoulders of others, but are unwilling to lift a finger to move them.
local: Bi nyāntí marab bin nyìm̀ kii awaŋgulee ní ki tūntí binib ní kaa là kí dū bi-ŋambiki kí dáb́ awaŋgulee kí tūtuǹ binib.

eng: Everything they do, they do to be noticed by others. They make extra-wide prayer bands for their arms and long tassels for their clothes.
local: Bi pée tūǹ bi-tuuŋŋi kɔkɔ binib ń nīn wāĺ-bi ní. Bi wàantí igban in pu bi ŋmà Unimbɔti bɔti ki cáa fìǹ bi-ŋalii nì bi-yigbeeŋee, ní ki fɔ̀kɔntí bi-bɔkutiŋ gbeeŋgbeeŋyi.

eng: They love to sit in places of honor at banquets and in the synagogues.
local: Bi néeń ki kàal̀ ikakaamɔnti diinabin nì nyɔkɔpu kakaai akpafidiinni.

eng: They love to be greeted with honor in the markets and to be addressed as Rabbi.
local: ki néeń bí nīn jáaḿ-bi binibin, ki néeń bí nīn yíì-bi <Ukpil, ukpil.>.

eng: But you shouldnt be called Rabbi , because you have one teacher, and all of you are brothers and sisters.
local: Ama nimbi ń taa yàntī bí nīn yíì-ni <Ukpil>, kun puee ni cáá ukpibaanti ní, ní ni-kɔkɔ sá nnabiyaamu.

eng: Dont call anybody on earth your father, because you have one Father, who is heavenly.
local: Ní taa yíiń ubɔ kitiŋ pu <ni-Baa>, kun puee ni-Baa pée sá ubambaanti ní, ki bí yilpu.

eng: Now Jesus left the temple and was going away. His disciples came to point out to him the temple buildings.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun Yeesu nyáń kunimbɔtidiin ki cée, ní u-boonnooliibi sútìń ki pìl̄-u ní ki bí yii ú kpèè bi nín baasii máń kunimbɔtidii puee.

eng: At that time many will fall away. They will betray each other and hate each other.
local: Ŋyunti gbantee, binib tikpil làá dāá fá bi-fookiiu un bi cáá man puee, kí nīn kíǹ ki nyáfí tɔb ki ná tɔb.

eng: Many false prophets will appear and deceive many people.
local: Bin yíì bi-ba yii Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi kin kaa sée làá dāá dāan̄ kí ŋmàtiǹ binib tikpil mun.

eng: Because disobedience will expand, the love of many will grow cold.
local: Tikpitil làá dāá wíiǹ, an puee, binib tikpil neendi làá dāá sɔ̀ŋkì.

eng: But the one who endures to the end will be delivered.
local: Ama un di yaa pɔ́ɔǹ mmɔntiimin ki tin kátì-kuee di dāa ŋmátí.

eng: This gospel of the kingdom will be proclaimed throughout the world as a testimony to all the nations. Then the end will come.
local: Bi dāa sòoǹ Unimbɔti Beel bɔmɔntiiti nee kitiŋ jaŋŋi kɔkɔ pái, án nīn sá seera kí tīī atimbun kɔkɔ kí wàatī ní duulinya ń nín kúntí.».

eng: When you see the disgusting and destructive thing that Daniel talked about standing in the holy place.
local: «Ni làá ká kuwaŋkpitiu kun cāabìń ntimbiilee, kun pu Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu Daniyeel nìn sòon̄ee sí kunimbɔtidiin. - Un kàaǹ tibɔti neeyee ń gbìl̄ ti-taapu!.

eng: then those in Judea must escape to the mountains.
local: Ŋyunti gbantee, Juudee tiŋki yab ń nīn sáń ki dàb̀ ajoon.

eng: Those on the roof shouldnt come down to grab things from their houses.
local: Un yaa láa bí dicindee ń taa láa lì yii u làá kpákatí kí kɔ́ kudiin kí yòon̄ tiba.

eng: Those in the field shouldnt come back to grab their clothes.
local: ní un yaa láa bí kusaauee ń taa láa lì yii u làá gítiń mmɔŋki kí yòon̄ u-bɔkutil.

eng: How terrible it will be at that time for women who are pregnant and for women who are nursing their children.
local: Ama falaa dɔ̄ ki cí bipɔdambi nì bimatib iwiin gbanti poon!.

eng: He responded, Do you see all these things? I assure that no stone will be left on another. Everything will be demolished.
local: Ní u bí-bi yii: «Kéè ni ká kɔkɔ neeyaa! Ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-ni, ditaŋkpal diba kaa làá dāá nīn gíti tɔ́kɔ́ di-juti pu, an kɔkɔ làá dāá wīī ní.».

eng: Pray that it doesnt happen in winter or on the Sabbath day.
local: Mèèmaan Unimbɔti an bɔtee ń taa bàń-ni kuseeun, kí taa nín̄ sá ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa dal.

eng: There will be great suffering such as the world has never before seen and will never again see.
local: kun puee doooo duulinya nín kíliń ki dāa sāā an yuntiŋuee, an nimbinaamandi juti kaa láá cúú binib, ki kpá kí gíti cúú-bi mun.

eng: If that time werent shortened, nobody would be rescued. But for the sake of the ones whom God chose, that time will be cut short.
local: Unimbɔti yaa kaa bàà bātì falaayee wiiŋyi pu, unibaanti kaa bàà ŋmátí. Ama binib bin u lútì bi sá u-yabee pu ní u bātì iwiin gbanti pu.

eng: Then if somebody says to you, Look, heres the Christ, or Hes over here, dont believe it.
local: «Ŋyunti gbantee, ubɔ yaa tùkū-ni yii: <Meesiya bí doo,> àá <U bíń lapu,> ní taa fōō kí kíí.

eng: False christs and false prophets will appear, and they will offer great signs and wonders in order to deceive, if possible, even those whom God has chosen.
local: Di sá yii, bin làá dāá mɔ̄ntì yii bíǹ di sá Meesiya nì Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibee, làá dāá nyā ki ŋáań tikpaŋkpantiil nì maamaaci tuuŋŋi ki nyàab̀ bi yaa ŋūǹ kí ŋmàtiǹ bin Unimbɔti lútèe gba.

eng: Look, Ive told you ahead of time.
local: M tùkū-ni ki yóoǹ dee.

eng: So if they say to you, Look, hes in the desert, dont go out. And if they say, Look, hes in the rooms deep inside the house, dont believe it.
local: «Míǹ puee bi yaa láa tùkū-ni yii, m dɔ́miń ki bí kuteeunee, taa cùmaan. Bi yaa láa tí bí-ni yii, m bí dilɔnlɔkɔlin kudiinaaa, taa fōō kí kíímaan.

eng: Just as the lightning flashes from the east to the west, so it will be with the coming of the Human One.
local: Kii utaa nín ŋmóò pu nyàlab báà ŋma wāĺee, kí kíliń ŋwìiń nyalaŋki pú kí cáá sāā ŋu-jeetilaŋki pú puee, míǹ ní man Kinibiki dāantam dāa bí.

eng: The vultures gather wherever theres a dead body.
local: Laakin diwaŋkpil yaa dɔ̄ee, niin ní kaajiikub kpáfí.».

eng: Now immediately after the suffering of that time the sun will become dark, and the moon wont give its light. The stars will fall from the sky and the planets and other heavenly bodies will be shaken.
local: Yeesu gíti lī yii: «Dinimbinaamandee yaa jítì kpalaayee, ŋwìiń dāa kpántí dibɔmbɔndi ní uŋmal mun kaa dāa gíti pīiǹ. Iŋmalbijaa dāa kìtīń kutaa pu ki lítiń, ní ipɔn in bí kutaa puee ń cìŋkì.

eng: Now while Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives, the disciples came to him privately and said, Tell us, when will these things happen? What will be the sign of your coming and the end of the age.
local: Yeesu nìn kā Ɔliifi sufii jool pu, ní u-boonnooliibi dɔ́miń ki ká-u bi-ŋmaniŋma ní ki bālifì-u yii: «Tùkù-ti ŋyunti ŋun tin a bàà lēe làá dāá bàńee, nì kudaaŋŋu kun làá dāá dàkà-ti ŋyunti ŋun a làá gítińee nì ŋyunti ŋun duulinya mun làá kúntée.».

eng: Then the sign of the Human One will appear in the sky. At that time all the tribes of the earth will be full of sadness, and they will see the Human One coming in the heavenly clouds with power and great splendor.
local: Niinee ní man Kinibiki daaŋku ń nyā kí páanní kutagbɔŋu pu, ní duulinya timbuliŋ kɔkɔ ń dāá fōō itiminsi, ní kí ká man Kinibiki ń páanní yilpu atagbanni nì mpɔn nì tinyool.

eng: He will send his angels with the sound of a great trumpet, and they will gather his chosen ones from the four corners of the earth, from one end of the sky to the other.
local: M làá tɔ̄ m-tuuŋyi kitiŋ jaŋŋi kɔkɔ kí tin sāā kutagbɔŋu nì kitiŋ kúntinyɔkɔ ní í búń dikantáŋkpaandi, kí kpāfì bin Unimbɔti lútèe.».

eng: Learn this parable from the fig tree. After its branch becomes tender and it sprouts new leaves, you know that summer is near.
local: «Pīlifìmaan fiigi subu nín dàkà-ti tiŋyee. Di sá yii, ni yaa ká fiigi subu tɔ́ apan ki bíi túkù tifaapɔnee, ni béetí yii kuseeu tóobiń dee.

eng: In the same way, when you see all these things, you know that the Human One is near, at the door.
local: Míǹ mbaantiim, ni yaa láa ká tin m máafì nee bíi jītée, béémaan yii m-dāantam tóobiń, m sútì ki pìlìń kipunyɔkɔ dee.

eng: I assure you that this generation wont pass away until all these things happen.
local: Ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-ni, waanee nib kɔkɔ kaa làá kpú kí dóó ní an bɔtee kɔkɔ ń bàń.

eng: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will certainly not pass away.
local: Kutagbɔŋu nì kitiŋ dāa bí kí kúntí, ama man bɔti kpá kí kúntí jaanjaan.».

eng: But nobody knows when that day or hour will come, not the heavenly angels and not the Son. Only the Father knows.
local: «Báà ubɔ kaa nyí ŋwiin nì kukúluu kun ni an bɔti làá bàńee. Unimbɔti tuuŋyi in bí yilpuee kaa nyí, man u-Jipɔɔn gba kaa nyí, see Ubaati Unimbɔti baba di nyí.

eng: As it was in the time of Noah, so it will be at the coming of the Human One.
local: Tin nìn jítì Noowa yuntiŋuee, man Kinibiki yaa làá dāá dāan̄ee, míǹ mbaantiim di làá tí jītí.

eng: In those days before the flood, people were eating and drinking, marrying and giving in marriage, until the day Noah entered the ark.
local: Kí wàatī nnyiŋgbeen ń bàńee, binib nìn jīǹ ki nyù, ní ki cāab̀ binimpoobi ki cáá bi-bisalib ki pūǹ ticati, áli Noowa dāa kpáàa kɔ́ meelan.

eng: They didnt know what was happening until the flood came and swept them all away. The coming of the Human One will be like that.
local: ní baa kpáàa béè kun dɔ̄ ki cí-bee ní nnyiŋgbeen dɔ́miń ki yōoǹ bi-kɔkɔ. Míǹ mbaantiim ní man Kinibiki yaa làá dāá dāan̄ee, án làá ŋá.

eng: Jesus replied, Watch out that no one deceives you.
local: Niinee ní Yeesu kíí-bi yii: «Ní nīn nyíǹ ni-ba ubɔ ń taa ŋmàtiǹ-ni.

eng: At that time there will be two men in the field. One will be taken and the other left.
local: Bininjab bili dāa gbāā kusaaun ki kù, ní ḿ yòon̄ ubɔ kí yàntī un kíǹee.

eng: Two women will be grinding at the mill. One will be taken and the other left.
local: Binimpoobi bili mun dāa gbāā kunaau pu ki nà, ní ḿ yòon̄ ubɔ kí yàntī un kíǹee.

eng: Therefore, stay alert! You dont know what day the Lord is coming.
local: An puee, ní taa nín̄ gèem̀maan, kun puee naa nyí ŋwiin ŋun ní ni-Dindaan làá fàtīí gítińee.

eng: But you understand that if the head of the house knew at what time the thief would come, he would keep alert and wouldnt allow the thief to break into his house.
local: Nín̄ nyímaan tiŋan yii, kudiidaan yaa bàà nyí kukúluu kun unaayuu làá dāan̄ kunyeeuee, u bàà kā ki cí ní, kí taa yàntī unaayuu ń cíĺ u-diiku.

eng: Therefore, you also should be prepared, because the Human One will come at a time you dont know.
local: Míǹ puee, ni mun bɔ́ntímaan kí nīn cí, kun puee man Kinibiki làá dāan̄ kukúluu kun naa dàkafī yii m làá dāan̄ee ní.».

eng: Who then are the faithful and wise servants whom their master puts in charge of giving food at the right time to those who live in his house?.
local: «Ŋma di sá utɔntɔnli un bí deedee ki cáá iciinee ní? Úǹ di sá un u-kpiliu dū u-tɔntɔnjutiibi ki ŋúbiǹ ú nīn tìì-bi tijin ŋyunti ŋun máaǹ ú nīn tìì-bee.

eng: Happy are those servants whom the master finds fulfilling their responsibilities when he comes.
local: An sá mmɔn ki tīī utɔntɔnli gbanti u-kpiliu yaa láa fātìi bàn̄ ki ká-u, u bíi tūǹ u-tundee.

eng: I assure you that he will put them in charge of all his possessions.
local: Ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-ni, ukpilee dù u-gaajawanti kɔkɔ ní kí kūtì kí ŋúbíǹ utɔntɔnlee.

eng: But suppose those bad servants should say to themselves, My master wont come until later.
local: Ama utɔntɔnlee yaa sá ukpitee, u làá dàkafì yii: <M-kpiliu kaa láá yàkań digitindi,>.

eng: And suppose they began to beat their fellow servants and to eat and drink with the drunks?.
local: ní ú kíĺ ki bíi gbáà u-tɔntɔnjutiibi ní u nì bidanyɔgbiliibi bí ki jīǹ ki nyù.

eng: Many will come in my name, saying, Im the Christ. They will deceive many people.
local: Di sá yii binib tikpil làá dāá dū man yindi kí kpántí bi-yaŋ kí lì yii bíǹ di sá <Meesiya> ní ki làá dāá kīil̀ binib tikpil.

eng: The master of those servants will come on a day when they are not expecting him, at a time they couldnt predict.
local: Niinee ní u-kpiliu ń dāá dūú túuǹ-u ŋwiin nì kukúluu kun pu waa nyí yii u làá bàńee.

eng: He will cut them in pieces and put them in a place with the hypocrites. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: ní kí dáaĺ u-tafal ŋkpitim pu, kí pà-u mpoonnyoondambi paati. Niin ní u làá sɔ̄ ní u-nyiŋŋi ń nīn gà tɔb kàkaka.».

eng: You will hear about wars and reports of wars. Dont be alarmed. These things must happen, but this isnt the end yet.
local: Ni làá dāá gbìl̄ bi sòoǹ tijáti tin bi bíi jáàyee nì tin gíti bí ki dòońee bɔti ama, ní nīn nyíǹ ni-ba ni-pɔbiliŋ ń taa bīil̀. An máaǹ tíǹ kɔkɔ ń bàń, ama niin kaa dá láá sá duulinya kúntitam.

eng: Nations and kingdoms will fight against each other, and there will be famines and earthquakes in all sorts of places.
local: Ditimbundi diba dāa yíkí kí jáǹ diba, ní dibeel diba yab mun ń jáǹ diba. Ŋkùm làá dāá nyā, ní kitiŋ ń dāá cìŋkì ajan kupaau pu.

eng: But all these things are just the beginning of the sufferings associated with the end.
local: Tíǹ kɔkɔee dāa sá kii imatiŋween nín kífí puee ní.

eng: They will arrest you, abuse you, and they will kill you. All nations will hate you on account of my name.
local: «Binib làá dāá cúú-ni kí tīī bí sàaǹ-ni ní kí kpɔ̀-ni. Atimbun kɔkɔ nib làá dāá nīn ná-ni ni nín fōō man ki kííyee pu.

eng: At that time the kingdom of heaven will be like ten young bridesmaids who took their lamps and went out to meet the groom.
local: «Unimbɔti Beel náań nì kii bisapɔmbi saalaa biba bin yōoǹ bi-fitilab ki búntì kí tūŋkìń unimpucaan calee ní.

eng: But while they were gone to buy oil, the groom came. Those who were ready went with him into the wedding. Then the door was shut.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun bi búntì kí dā ŋkpamee, ní unimpucaan cal bāań, bin bɔ́ntèe nóó-u ki kɔ́ kunimpucandiin, ní bi dū kipunyɔkɔ ki pīiǹ.

eng: Later the other bridesmaids came and said, Lord, lord, open the door for us.
local: Boonee, bisapɔmbi bin kíǹee mun dūu bāań ki bíi yíì yii: <Ukpil, ukpileeee, pìitī-ti!>.

eng: But he replied, I tell you the truth, I dont know you.
local: Ní u kíí-bi yii: <Ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-ni, maa nyí-ni.>».

eng: Therefore, keep alert, because you dont know the day or the hour.
local: Ní Yeesu gíti lī yii: «Taa nín̄ gèem̀maan, kun puee naa nyí ŋwiin, kaa nyí kukúluu kun m làá dāá gítińee.».

eng: The kingdom of heaven is like a man who was leaving on a trip. He called his servants and handed his possessions over to them.
local: «Unimbɔti beel dāa bí kii uninja ubɔ nín làá cù nsan ki yíiń u-tɔntɔnliibi ki dū u-gaajawanti ki ŋúbiǹ-bi puee ní.

eng: To one he gave five valuable coins, and to another he gave two, and to another he gave one. He gave to each servant according to that servants ability. Then he left on his journey.
local: U dū nnimbifɔmu muŋmoo ki tīī ubɔ, ní ki dū nnimbifɔmu muli ki tīī ubɔ, ní ki dū kinimbifɔŋ kiba ki tīī utataati. U pée tīī báà ŋma nì u-pɔŋŋu nyɔkɔm, ní ki búntì nsan.

eng: After the man left, the servant who had five valuable coins took them and went to work doing business with them. He gained five more.
local: Dicilpu niinee, un fōō nnimbifɔmu muŋmooyee kpáàa búntì dibàntil ní ki kánní nnimbifɔmu muŋmoo ki kútì muŋmoo mun bi tīī-uee pu.

eng: In the same way, the one who had two valuable coins gained two more.
local: Míǹ ní un fōō nnimbifɔmu mulee mun kánní muli ki kútì mun bi tīī-uee pu.

eng: But the servant who had received the one valuable coin dug a hole in the ground and buried his masters money.
local: Ama ní un fōō kiba jee cūtì ki gbìn̄ mbòó ki dū u-kpiliu nimbiliŋ ki bál̀.

eng: Now after a long time the master of those servants returned and settled accounts with them.
local: «Uninjee búntèe, ki yúntìń tikpil. U dāa fātìi bāańee ní ki yíiń u-tɔntɔnliibi bí tùkù-u bi nín dū u-nimbiliŋ ki ŋá kuŋyee.

eng: Now five of them were wise, and the other five were foolish.
local: Bi-kansikinee biŋmoo sá bigbaambi ní biŋmoo sá biciŋfitiibi.

eng: The one who had received five valuable coins came forward with five additional coins. He said, Master, you gave me five valuable coins. Look, Ive gained five more.
local: Ní un nìn fōō nnimbifɔmu muŋmooyee sútìń ki bí yii: <Ukpil, a nìn tīī-mi nnimbifɔmu muŋmoo, ní m dūu bāntì ki ká muŋmoo ki kútì, an sèé.>.

eng: His master replied, Excellent! You are a good and faithful servant! Youve been faithful over a little. Ill put you in charge of much. Come, celebrate with me.
local: Ní u-kpiliu bí-u yii: <An ŋān̄, a sá utɔntɔmmɔnti ki bí deedee! A nín ŋúb́ tin kaa náaǹee deedeeyee, m làá kàan̄-si á nīn kpèé tiwan tikpil. Kɔ́ man a-kpiliu mɔŋŋun!>.

eng: The second servant also came forward and said, Master, you gave me two valuable coins. Look, Ive gained two more.
local: Ní utɔntɔnli un mun fōō nnimbifɔmu mulee dɔ́miń ki bí yii: <Ukpil, a nìn tīī-mi nnimbifɔmu muli, ní m dūu bāntì ki ká nnimbifɔmu muli ki kútì, an sèé!>.

eng: His master replied, Well done! You are a good and faithful servant. Youve been faithful over a little. Ill put you in charge of much. Come, celebrate with me.
local: Ní u-kpiliu bí-u yii: <An ŋān̄, a sá utɔntɔmmɔnti ki bí deedee. A nín ŋúb́ tin kaa náaǹee deedeeyee, m làá kàan̄-si á nīn kpèé tiwan tikpil. Kɔ́ man a-kpiliu mɔŋŋun!>.

eng: Now the one who had received one valuable coin came and said, Master, I knew that you are a hard man. You harvest grain where you havent sown. You gather crops where you havent spread seed.
local: Ní un fōō kinimbifɔŋ kiba jee mun sútìń ki bí yii: <Ukpil, m nyí yii a sá unil un bɔti pɔ̄ɔ̄yee, laakin saa yāaǹ iwamboolee niin ní a dāa cēē, ní laakin saa gbáń ki bíl̀ee niin ní a fà.

eng: So I was afraid. And I hid my valuable coin in the ground. Here, you have whats yours.
local: An puee m fāŋkì, ní ki cáá a-nimbiliŋ ki gbìn̄ kitiŋ ki dūu bál̀, kpèè, a-wanti sèé!>.

eng: His master replied, You evil and lazy servant! You knew that I harvest grain where I havent sown and that I gather crops where I havent spread seed?.
local: Niinee ní u-kpiliu kíí-u yii: <Utɔntɔŋkpiti, uwutijoodaan ní a sá! A nín nyí yii laakin maa yāaǹee ní m cēē, ní laakin maa gbáń ki bíl̀ee ní m fèe.

eng: In that case, you should have turned my money over to the bankers so that when I returned, you could give me what belonged to me with interest.
local: ba pu ní saa dū m-nimbiliŋ ki ŋá-mi baŋkan? M bàà bāań nee ní kí fōō m-nimbijaliŋ nì ipaati,>.

eng: Therefore, take from him the valuable coin and give it to the one who has ten coins.
local: ní ki bí yii: <Fōōmaan u-nimbifɔŋki kí dūú kūtì un cáá nnimbifɔmu saalaayee.

eng: Those who have much will receive more, and they will have more than they need. But as for those who dont have much, even the little bit they have will be taken away from them.
local: Di sá yii un kamaa yaa ŋúb́ tin u cááyee tiŋanee bi dāa kútí-u, ní ú nīn bí digbilin. Ama un kamaa yaa kaa ŋúb́ tin u cááyee tiŋanee, bi dāa fòol̄ fiii un u cááyee ní.

eng: The foolish ones took their lamps but didnt bring oil for them.
local: Bigbaambee yōoǹ bi-fitilab, kaa ŋúb́ ŋkpam ansaŋkiya pu.

eng: Now take the worthless servant and throw him out into the farthest darkness. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: Ama an yaa kíǹ utɔntɔnli un kaa bí tiban nee, cúúmaan-u kí tɔ̄ mmɔŋki dibɔmbɔndin! Niin ní u làá nīn sūǹ, u-nyiŋŋi ń nīn gà tɔb kàkaka.>».

eng: Now when the Human One comes in his majesty and all his angels are with him, he will sit on his majestic throne.
local: «Man Kinibiki yaa láa dɔ́miń m-nyootinee nì Unimbɔti tuuŋyi kɔkɔee, m làá dāá kàl̄ m-beejal pu nì tinyool.

eng: All the nations will be gathered in front of him. He will separate them from each other, just as a shepherd separates the sheep from the goats.
local: Duulinya timbuliŋ kɔkɔ dāa kpáfí m-nimbiin ní ḿ gàntī-bi kii uwaŋkpaal nín gàntī ipii nì iŋoobu puee.

eng: He will put the sheep on his right side. But the goats he will put on his left.
local: U làá dū ipii kí yóoń m-ŋaŋgiil pú, ní kí dū iŋoobu kí yóoń m-ŋaŋgan pú.

eng: Then the king will say to those on his right, Come, you who will receive good things from my Father. Inherit the kingdom that was prepared for you before the world began.
local: Niinee ní man ubɔti ń bí bin bí m-ŋaŋgiil púee, yii: <Dāam̄maan, nimbi bin pu m-Baa bīiǹee, kí fōōmaan Dibeel din u bɔ́ntì ki yóoǹ-ni doooo duulinya kíltamee.

eng: I was hungry and you gave me food to eat. I was thirsty and you gave me a drink. I was a stranger and you welcomed me.
local: Di sá ŋkùm nìn cúú-mi ní ni tīī-mi tijin m jíń, nnyinnyoo nìn cáá-mi ní ni pú-mi nnyim m nyùn̄, m nìn sá unicaan ní ni fōō-mi.

eng: I was naked and you gave me clothes to wear. I was sick and you took care of me. I was in prison and you visited me.
local: Maa nìn cáá tiba kí pèen̄ ní ni pú-mi tiwan m pēeǹ, m nìn būǹ ní ni kpēē-mi, m nìn bí sarkan ní ni dɔ́miń ki māntì m-pu.>.

eng: Then those who are righteous will reply to him, Lord, when did we see you hungry and feed you, or thirsty and give you a drink?.
local: Niinee ní bin yúl̀ nì Unimbɔti deedeeyee ń dāá kíí-mi yii: <Ti-Dindaan, ba dal ní ti ká-si ŋkùm cáá-si ní ti tīī-si tijin a jíń, àá nnyinnyoo cáá-si ní ti tīī-si nnyim a nyùn̄ ní?.

eng: When did we see you as a stranger and welcome you, or naked and give you clothes to wear?.
local: Ba dal ní ti ká-si a sá unicaan ní ti fōō-si, àá aa cáá tiba kí pèen̄ ní ti tīī-si tiwan a pēeǹ ní?.

eng: When did we see you sick or in prison and visit you.
local: Ba dal ní ti ká-si a būǹ, àá a bí sarkan ní ti cūtì ki māntì a-pu?>.

eng: But the wise ones took their lamps and also brought containers of oil.
local: ama bin bin sá biciŋfitiibee yōoǹ bi-fitilab ní ki gíti kpíĺ ŋkpam miba ansaŋkiya pu.

eng: Then the king will reply to them, I assure you that when you have done it for one of the least of these brothers and sisters of mine, you have done it for me.
local: Ní man ubɔti ń dāá kíí-bi yii: <Ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-ni, ni nín pée ŋá min ki tīī bin sá ŋwaamu m-naalib nee kansikin ubɔee, man ní ni ŋá ki tīī.>».

eng: Then he will say to those on his left, Get away from me, you who will receive terrible things. Go into the unending fire that has been prepared for the devil and his angels.
local: «Míǹ boonee ní ḿ fàtī kí tùkù bin yú m-ŋaŋgan púee yii: <Sútímaan niin kí wàatī-mi, mbusudambi! Nín̄ cámaan ŋŋmi ŋun làá nīn wù jaanjaanee ni, ŋun bɔ́ntì ki yóoǹ Sitaan nì u-tuuŋyee!.

eng: I was hungry and you didnt give me food to eat. I was thirsty and you didnt give me anything to drink.
local: Di sá yii ŋkùm nìn cáá-mi, naa pú-mi tijin m jíń. Nnyinnyoo nìn cáá-mi, naa pú-mi nnyim m nyùn̄.

eng: I was a stranger and you didnt welcome me. I was naked and you didnt give me clothes to wear. I was sick and in prison, and you didnt visit me.
local: M nìn sá unicaan, naa fōō-mi. Maa nìn cáá tiba kí pèen̄, naa tīī-mi tiwan m pēeǹ. M nìn būǹ, naa kpēē-mi, m nìn bí sarkan, naa dɔ́miń ki māntì m-pu!>.

eng: Then they will reply, Lord, when did we see you hungry or thirsty or a stranger or naked or sick or in prison and didnt do anything to help you.
local: Niinee ní bí kíí-mi yii: <Ti-Dindaan, ba dal ní ti ká-si ŋkùm nì nnyinnyoo cáá-si, àá a sá unicaan, àá aa cáá tiba kí pèen̄, àá a būǹ, àá a bí sarkan, ní taa tútúǹ-si?>.

eng: Then he will answer, I assure you that when you havent done it for one of the least of these, you havent done it for me.
local: Ní man ubɔti ń dāá kíí-bi yii: <Ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-ni, ni nín kaa ŋá min ki tīī ŋwaamu nee kansikin kibee, man ní naa ŋá ki tīī.>.

eng: And they will go away into eternal punishment. But the righteous ones will go into eternal life.
local: Ní bí dāá dū-bi kí tɔ̄ ditafadaaldi din kaa cáá dikúntilee ni, ní bin yúl̀ nì Unimbɔti deedeeyee ń nīn cá dimaŋfal din kaa cáá dikúntilee ni.».

eng: When the groom was late in coming, they all became drowsy and went to sleep.
local: Unimpucaan cal nín kaa bíi bàń malaayee ní ŋgeem cúú bisapɔmbee kɔkɔ, ní bi gèen̄.

eng: But at midnight there was a cry, Look, the groom! Come out to meet him.
local: «Kunyataasiiu búmbumee, ní bi téeń yii: <Kpèèmaan unimpucaan cal dee! Cù kí tūŋkìmaan-u!>.

eng: Then all those bridesmaids got up and prepared their lamps.
local: Niinee ní bisapɔmbi saalaayee kɔkɔ fīntì ki bíi ŋáŋkí bi-fitilab.

eng: But the foolish bridesmaids said to the wise ones, Give us some of your oil, because our lamps have gone out.
local: Ní bisapɔŋgbaambee bí bin cáá iciinee yii: <Pɔ̄maan-ti ni-kpamaa! Timbi fitilab bíi kúù.>.

eng: But the wise bridesmaids replied, No, because if we share with you, there wont be enough for our lamps and yours. We have a better idea. You go to those who sell oil and buy some for yourselves.
local: Ní bin cáá iciinee bí-bi yii: <Aayee, min bí timbi cee nee kaa làá bà kí kpáaǹ ni mun. Pūtì nín̄ cámaan bin nyáfée cee kí dāmaan.>.

eng: When Jesus finished speaking all these words, he said to his disciples.
local: Yeesu sòon̄ an bɔti ki dóòyee, ní ki bí u-boonnooliibi yii.

eng: But Jesus knew what they were thinking. He said, Why do you make trouble for the woman? Shes done a good thing for me.
local: Yeesu gbìl̄ bi nín bí ki sòoǹ tiŋyee, ní u bíi lìǹ-bi yii: «Ba pu ní ni páb́ unimpu nee míǹ? An sá ditummɔntiil ní u ŋá ki tīī-mi.

eng: You always have the poor with you, but you wont always have me.
local: Kun puee ni nì biwɔfib di làá nīn pée bí baabadal, ama man kaa làá nīn bí ni-cee baabadal.

eng: By pouring this perfume over my body shes prepared me to be buried.
local: U nín yāaǹ m-wunti pu talaalu nee, u bɔ́ntì-tì ki síiǹ m-síbitam dee.

eng: I tell you the truth that wherever in the whole world this good news is announced, what shes done will also be told in memory of her.
local: Ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-ni, duulinya jaŋŋi kɔkɔ, laakin ní bi dāa sòoǹ tibɔmɔntiil neeyee, bi làá dāá sòoǹ unimpu nee nín ŋá min nee kí nīn téetí u-pu.».

eng: Then one of the Twelve, who was called Judas Iscariot, went to the chief priests.
local: Ní akpambal saalaa nì alee kansikin un bi yíì Juudasi Isikaariyɔtee cūtì ki ká bisaraaŋakpilib.

eng: and said, What will you give me if I turn Jesus over to you? They paid him thirty pieces of silver.
local: ní ki bí-bi yii: «Ni làá tīī-mi ba ní ḿ nyàab̀ nsan ŋun ni làá dī kí cúú Yeesuee?» Ní bi kàan̄ animbil jitɔmbi mmuŋku nì saalaa ki tīī-u.

eng: From that time on he was looking for an opportunity to turn him in.
local: Ŋyunti gbantee ní Juudasi kíĺ ki nyàab̀ nsan kí dū Yeesu kí ŋúbíǹ-bi.

eng: On the first day of the Festival of Unleavened Bread, the disciples came to Jesus and said, Where do you want us to prepare for you to eat the Passover meal.
local: Jiŋaal un bi ŋmóò kpɔnɔb bin baa ŋá ŋkpɔnɔnyɔkɔee wimpeepeekaaŋuee, Yeesu boonnooliibi dɔ́miń ki bālifì-u yii: «La ní a là tí bɔ́ntí-si Dilákatil jiŋaaliu jinti ní?».

eng: He replied, Go into the city, to a certain man, and say, The teacher says,My time is near. Im going to celebrate the Passover with my disciples at your house.
local: Niinee ní u bí-bi yii, cùmaan kitiŋin ŋmɔŋŋmɔkɔ dumpu kí tùkù-u yii: «Ukpil bí yii: <M-yuntiŋu bāań ní nee, ní sii cee ní man nì m-boonnooliibi làá jī Dilákatil jiŋaaliu.>».

eng: The disciples did just as Jesus instructed them. They prepared the Passover.
local: Ní bi ŋá kíí Yeesu nín tùkū-bi puee, ki bɔ́ntì jiŋaalee.

eng: You know that the Passover is two days from now. And the Human One will be handed over to be crucified.
local: «Kéè ni nyí yii an kíǹ iwiin ili dee tí jī Dilákatil jiŋaaliuaa! Niin ní bi làá dū man Kinibiki kí ŋúbiǹ m-nannanliibi bí kpáá-mi ndɔpuntikaa pu.».

eng: That evening he took his place at the table with the twelve disciples.
local: Kujoou jóotèe, bi nì u kāl̀ ki bíi jīǹ tijin.

eng: As they were eating he said, I assure you that one of you will betray me.
local: Bi bíi jīǹee, ní u bí-bi yii: «Ibaamɔn ní m sòoǹ, ni-kansikin ubɔ làá dū-mi kí tīī m-nannanliibi.».

eng: Deeply saddened, each one said to him, Im not the one, am I, Lord.
local: Niinee kunimbaau kɔ́-bi ní bi kíĺ ki bíi bàlifī-u ubɔ ubɔ yii: «Ukpil, kéè an kaa sá mamaa!».

eng: He replied, The one who will betray me is the one who dips his hand with me into this bowl.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-bi yii: «An sá man nì un jīǹ disambibaantiilinee di làá dū-mi kí ŋúbíǹ m-nannanliibi.

eng: The Human One goes to his death just as it is written about him. But how terrible it is for that person who betrays the Human One! It would have been better for him if he had never been born.
local: Man Kinibiki cá kí kpú ní nee, kii an nín ŋmàn̄ m-pu ki yóoǹ miŋyee, ama mbusu dɔ̄ ki cí un làá dū-mi kí nyāfiǹ m-nannanliibee! Bi yaa kaa bàà màl̄ udaan, an bàà sɔ́.».

eng: Now Judas, who would betray him, replied, Its not me, is it, Rabbi? Jesus answered, You said it.
local: Ní Juudasi un bíi cáá-u ki nyáfée bālifì-u yii: «Ukpil, man di làá dū-si kí tīīii?» Ní Yeesu kíí-u yii: «Iiin.».

eng: While they were eating, Jesus took bread, blessed it, broke it, and gave it to the disciples and said, Take and eat. This is my body.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun bi bí ki jīǹee, ní Yeesu yōoǹ kpɔnɔ ki pàkā Unimbɔti, ní ki cúu gífì ki yākatì ki tīī u-boonnooliibi, ní ki bí-bi yii: «Fōō kí ŋmɔ́maan! An di sá m-wunti.».

eng: He took a cup, gave thanks, and gave it to them, saying, Drink from this, all of you.
local: Ní ki tí yōoǹ ndamam ní ki jáam̀ Unimbɔti mi-pu, ní ki dūu tīī-bi, ní ki bí-bi yii: «Ni-kɔkɔ fōō kí nyɔ̀maan!.

eng: This is my blood of the covenant, which is poured out for many so that their sins may be forgiven.
local: An di sá m-fatikuu un nyáń ki cáań mpèé Unimbɔti nì binib tikpil kansikin bí ká bi-kpitii pɔ̄tamee.

eng: I tell you, I wont drink wine again until that day when I drink it in a new way with you in my Fathers kingdom.
local: Ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-ni, m pée gíti kpá kí nyɔ̀ ndaam nee kí nīn cí daliŋkin man nì ni làá dāá gíti kpāfì kí nyɔ̀ ndaam min sá mpɔmee m-Baa Beelinee.».

eng: Then the chief priests and elders of the people gathered in the courtyard of Caiaphas the high priest.
local: Ní bisaraaŋakpilib nì Juuda yab tindaŋkpilib kpáfì usaraaŋakpil un bi yíì Kaayiifee naakookun.

eng: Then, after singing songs of praise, they went to the Mount of Olives.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun bi gāā jiŋaal laŋyi ki dóòyee, ní bi nyáń ki búntì Ɔliifi sufii jool pu.

eng: Then Jesus said to his disciples, Tonight you will all fall away because of me. This is because it is written, I will hit the shepherd, and the sheep of the flock will go off in all directions .
local: Niinee, Yeesu bí u-boonnooliibi yii: «Bi nín làá ŋá-mi puee làá yàntī ni-kɔkɔ ń fá-mi kunyeeu nee gba, kii Unimbɔti gbɔŋku nín sòon̄ ki síiǹ puee yii: <M làá kpɔ̀ upikpaal ní ipii ń yàl̄.>.

eng: But after Im raised up, Ill go before you to Galilee.
