local: Ama m yaa láa fīkitèe, m làá lìntī kí cùú cīití-ni Galiilee».

eng: Peter replied, If everyone else stumbles because of you, Ill never stumble.
local: Ní Peetroo kíí-u yii: «Báà bi-kɔkɔ yaa féeń-si, man kpá kí fá-si jaanjaan!».

eng: Jesus said to him, I assure you that, before the rooster crows tonight, you will deny me three times.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-u yii: «Ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-si, díǹ nyeeku gba, kí wàatī ukooja ń bāakèe, a làá néé mita ní yii saa nyí-mi.».

eng: Peter said, Even if I must die alongside you, I wont deny you. All the disciples said the same thing.
local: Ní Peetroo kíí-u yii: «Báà an yaa sá-mi ŋkúm, man nì si làá kpú, man kpá kí néé yii maa nyí-si!» Ní u-boonnooliibi bin kɔkɔ kíǹee mun lī míǹ mbaantiim.

eng: Then Jesus went with his disciples to a place called Gethsemane. He said to the disciples, Stay here while I go and pray over there.
local: Niinee ní Yeesu nì u-boonnooliibi bàn̄ laakin bi yíì Geetiseemaneeyee, ní u bí-bi yii: «Kàl̄maan doo, man làá cù nyɔkɔpu doo kí mèè.».

eng: When he took Peter and Zebedees two sons, he began to feel sad and anxious.
local: Ní u kpáaǹ Peetroo nì Seebeedee jipɔmbi liitil. Niinee, u kíĺ ki kpáàa bíi gbìl̀ dinimbinaamandi nì kunimbaau pu.

eng: Then he said to them, Im very sad. Its as if Im dying. Stay here and keep alert with me.
local: ní ki bí-bi yii: «Kunimbaau kɔ́-mi tikpil, ki cáǹ-mi ŋkúmin! Nín̄ kāmaan doo kí nīn wāĺ kí taa gèeǹ!».

eng: Then he went a short distance farther and fell on his face and prayed, My Father, if its possible, take this cup of suffering away from me. However—not what I want but what you want.
local: Ní Yeesu sútì nyɔkɔpu fiii ní ki tātìi dōoǹ dicincibil ní ki bíi mèé yii: «M-Baa, falaanyɔkaa nee yaan ŋūǹ kí kàkatī kí wàatī-mi! Ama, min sii lèe nín ŋá, án taa ŋá min man lèe.».

eng: They were plotting to arrest Jesus by cunning tricks and to kill him.
local: ki sòon̄ ki bɔ́ntì kí cúú Yeesu dibalin kí kpɔ̀-u.

eng: He came back to the disciples and found them sleeping. He said to Peter, Couldnt you stay alert one hour with me?.
local: Ní u fātìi gítìń u-boonnooliibi bitee cee ki ká bi dɔ̄ ki gèeǹ, ní u bí Peetroo yii: «Naa kpáàa ŋūǹ man nì ni ń kàl̄ kukúbambaantiiu kpaŋkpam ní taa gèeǹaaa?.

eng: Stay alert and pray so that you wont give in to temptation. The spirit is eager, but the flesh is weak.
local: Fìntīmaan kí nīn mèé, án nín làá ŋá pu ni yaa kɔ́ icɔŋkinee ní taa lítée. Unil bàà cáá nlandɔkɔmɔnti, ama ní ki bí iwɔfin.».

eng: A second time he went away and prayed, My Father, if its not possible that this cup be taken away unless I drink it, then let it be what you want.
local: Ní Yeesu fātìi tí nín gítí nliliitiim ki tí mēē yii: «M-Baa, an yaa sá yii m yaa kaa nyùn̄ falaa nee waa ŋūǹ kí jītée, a nín là puee ní ń ŋá!».

eng: Again he came and found them sleeping. Their eyes were heavy with sleep.
local: Ní u fātìi tí nín gítiń ki bāańee, bi tí dɔ̄ ki gèeǹ, kun puee ŋgeem nìn cáá-bi tikpil.

eng: But he left them and again went and prayed the same words for the third time.
local: Ní u gíti tí yāntì-bi ki fātìi nín gítí ntataatiim, ki tí mēē ki būsì tibɔbaantiil.

eng: Then he came to his disciples and said to them, Will you sleep and rest all night? Look, the time has come for the Human One to be betrayed into the hands of sinners.
local: Míǹ boonee u fātìi nín gítiń u-boonnooliibi cee, ní ki bí-bi yii: «Ni dɔ̄ ki gèeǹ ki ŋùŋfāa! Kpèè, kukúluu kun bi làá dū man Kinibiki kí nyāfiǹ binikpitibee bāań ní nee.

eng: Get up. Lets go. Look, here comes my betrayer.
local: Yīkì, tí nīn cámaan! Un làá dū-mi kí nyāfèe di bāań nee!».

eng: While Jesus was still speaking, Judas, one of the Twelve, came. With him was a large crowd carrying swords and clubs. They had been sent by the chief priests and elders of the people.
local: Yeesu kaa láá sòon̄ ki ŋmíl̀, ní u-kpambaliŋ saalaa nì alee kansikin un bi yíì Juudasee bāań u nì kunipaau nóoń ki ŋúbiń njimu nì atambɔn. Bisaraaŋakpilib nì Juuda yab tindaŋkpilib di nìn túnní-bi.

eng: His betrayer had given them a sign: Arrest the man I kiss.
local: Úǹ un làá dū Yeesu kí nyāfèe nìn tīiń-bi kudaaŋŋu yii: «Un m yaa tin jáam̀ ki kpàl̄ee, un ni nyàab̀ee dee, ní cúú-u!».

eng: Just then he came to Jesus and said, Hello, Rabbi. Then he kissed him.
local: Dicilpu niinee, u sútì ki pìl̄ Yeesu, ki bí-u yii: «Ukpil, a-jooku!» ní ki kpàl̄-u.

eng: But they agreed that it shouldnt happen during the feast so there wouldnt be an uproar among the people.
local: Ama ní ki lī yii: «Án taa nín̄ sá jiŋaalin, án nín làá ŋá pu samaa ń taa láa yīkì kí jáǹ-tee.».

eng: But Jesus said to him, Friend, do what you came to do. Then they came and grabbed Jesus and arrested him.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-u yii: «M-bɔɔ, ŋá tin pú a dɔ́mińee!» Niinee ní binibee dɔ́miń ki cúú Yeesu.

eng: One of those with Jesus reached for his sword. Striking the high priests slave, he cut off his ear.
local: Bin nìn bí Yeesu ceeyee kansikin ubɔ fɔ́fì u-tàataajiki ní ki gāā ki wúkutì usaraaŋakpil tɔntɔnliu tafal.

eng: Then Jesus said to him, Put the sword back into its place. All those who use the sword will die by the sword.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-u yii: «Gíiǹ a-tàataajiki kí fúuń kujitooun, kun puee bin kɔkɔ yaa yōoǹ njimuee, njimu di làá dāá kpɔ̀ bidambi.

eng: Or do you think that Im not able to ask my Father and he will send to me more than twelve battle groups of angels right away?.
local: A nyí yii maan ŋūǹ kí yíiń m-Baa ń jòoń-mi, ní ú tīiń-mi niin niin ituun kutukub kutukubaaa?.

eng: But if I did that, how would the scriptures be fulfilled that say this must happen.
local: Ama, m yaa ŋá mimmee, mana ní Unimbɔti gbɔŋku nín sòon̄ ki síiǹ m-pu miŋyee làá ŋmā kí ŋá?».

eng: Then Jesus said to the crowds, Have you come with swords and clubs to arrest me, like a thief? Day after day, I sat in the temple teaching, but you didnt arrest me.
local: Niinee ní u fātìi bí kunipaauee yii: «M sá unikpiti naaa? Ba pu ní ni ŋúbiń ntàataajimu nì atambɔn kí dāá cúú-mi! Baabadal m kàal̀ kunimbɔtidiin ki dàkà, ba pu ní naa cúú-mi niin?.

eng: But all this has happened so that what the prophets said in the scriptures might be fulfilled. Then all the disciples left Jesus and ran away.
local: Ama tin bí nee kɔkɔ bāań, án ŋá kii Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi nín ŋmàn̄ ki síiǹ m-pu miŋyee.» Niinee ní u-boonnooliibi kɔkɔ sáǹ ki fá-u.

eng: Those who arrested Jesus led him to Caiaphas the high priest. The legal experts and the elders had gathered there.
local: Bin nìn cúú Yeesuee cáá-u ki cūnnì usaraaŋakpil Kaayiifi dumpu, niin ní bimaradakaliibi nì bitindaŋkpilib nìn kpáfì.

eng: Peter followed him from a distance until he came to the high priests courtyard. He entered that area and sat outside with the officers to see how it would turn out.
local: Ní Peetroo táá Yeesu aboon aboon ki tin bàn̄ usaraaŋakpil dumpu ki kāl̀ u-tɔntɔnliibi cee kí kpèè án nín làá kúntí miŋyee.

eng: The chief priests and the whole council were looking for false testimony against Jesus so that they could put him to death.
local: Bisaraaŋakpilib nì bibɔsoonliibi bin kɔkɔ kíǹee bíi nyàab̀ un làá ká nnyimɔn ŋubɔ kí sòoǹ u-pu bí ká nnaakpal kí kpɔ̀-uee.

eng: When Jesus was at Bethany visiting the house of Simon, who had a skin disease.
local: Yeesu nín nìn bí Beetanii, Siimɔn un nìn sá unyaŋgbantee dumpuee.

eng: They didnt find anything they could use from the many false witnesses who were willing to come forward. But finally they found two.
local: Ama, nì min kɔkɔ binib dɔ́miń kupaau ki cáań inyimɔn seerab ki túǹ u-puee, báà ubɔ yɔu kaa kāl̀. Boonee ní bili dɔ́miń ki bí yii.

eng: who said, This man said, I can destroy Gods temple and rebuild it in three days.
local: «Kijapaai nee bí yii, úǹ ŋūǹ kí wīī kunimbɔtidii kí fàtīí mā-ku iwiin ita.».

eng: Then the high priest stood and said to Jesus, Arent you going to respond to the testimony these people have brought against you.
local: Niinee ní usaraaŋakpil yíkì ki bí Yeesu yii: «Binib nee nín bí ki sòoǹ a-pu miŋyee, ba ŋá ní saa kíikí ní?».

eng: But Jesus was silent. The high priest said, By the living God, I demand that you tell us whether you are the Christ, Gods Son.
local: Ama ní Yeesu ŋmí-bi fɔ́ŋfɔŋ. Ní usaraaŋakpil gíti bí-u yii: «Nì Unimbɔti un bí dimaŋfalinee ní m pūtì ki tīī-si, á tùkù-ti a yaa sá Meesiya, ki sá Unimbɔti Jipɔɔn!».

eng: You said it, Jesus replied. But I say to you that from now on youll see the Human One sitting on the right side of the Almighty and coming on the heavenly clouds .
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-u yii: «Míǹ ní a lī nee. Ama m tùkù-ni yii, kí yòon̄ kɔŋkɔnnee, ni làá ká man Kinibiki kā Mpɔŋkɔdaan ŋaŋgiil pú, ki làá dāá ká m nyánní yilpu atagbanni ki dòoń.».

eng: Then the high priest tore his clothes and said, Hes insulting God! Why do we need any more witnesses? Look, youve heard his insult against God.
local: Niinee, usaraaŋakpil dáaĺ ki cátì tiwan tin u pēēyee, ní ki bí yii: «U ŋèeǹ nì Unimbɔti ní! Ti gíti nyàab̀ seeradambi kí ŋá ba? Nimbi ni-ba, ni gbìl̄ u nín ŋèeǹ nì Unimbɔtee.

eng: What do you think? And they answered, He deserves to die.
local: Ni ká yii ba?» Ní bi kíí-u yii: «U máaǹ ŋkúm ní!».

eng: Then they spit in his face and beat him. They hit him.
local: Niinee ní bi kíĺ ki tíiĺ timɔtan ki làbitī u-nimbiin ní ki gbáà-u aŋaŋkul, ní biba mun fátí-u itampa.

eng: and said, Prophesy for us, Christ! Who hit you.
local: ki lì-u yii: «Sii un bí yii a sá un Unimbɔti lēē ki túnnée, dàkà yii a sá Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu, tùkù-ti un di fáá-see!».

eng: Meanwhile, Peter was sitting outside in the courtyard. A servant woman came and said to him, You were also with Jesus the Galilean.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun Peetroo nyáń mmɔŋki ki kā dicindinee, usapɔɔn ubɔ un bí niin ki tūǹ ditundee sútìń ki pìl̄-u, ní ki bí-u yii: «A mun nìn bí Yeesu Galiilee yɔuee cee ní!».

eng: a woman came to him with a vase made of alabaster containing very expensive perfume. She poured it on Jesus head while he was sitting at dinner.
local: ní unimpu ubɔ sútìń ki pìl̄-u ki ŋúbiń talaalumɔnti un pɔ̄ɔ̄ kudaau tikpilee, an kā ditaŋkpal kpalbanyaaŋwun. Ŋyunti ŋun Yeesu kā ki jīǹ tijinee, ní u kpáatì talaaluee ki sītì Yeesu yil pu.

eng: But he denied it in front of all of them, saying, I dont know what you are talking about.
local: Ní Peetroo néé báà ŋma nimbiin yii: «Maa nyí tibɔti tin a sòoǹ nee.».

eng: When he went over to the gate, another woman saw him and said to those who were there, This man was with Jesus, the man from Nazareth.
local: U còom̄ ki cá kipunyɔkɔ watil púee, ní usapɔɔn ubɔ mun gíti ká-u, ní ki tùkū bin bí niinee yii: «Kijapaai nee mun nìn bí Yeesu Naasaareeti yɔuee cee ní.».

eng: With a solemn pledge, he denied it again, saying, I dont know the man.
local: Ní Peetroo gíti tí néé ki pūtì yii: «Maa nyí uninja gbanti!».

eng: A short time later those standing there came and said to Peter, You must be one of them. The way you talk gives you away.
local: An kútì fiiiyee, bin bí niinee mun sútìń u-cee ní ki bí-u yii: «A mun lafun sá binib bin nóó-uee kansikin ubɔ ní, a-sòontam dàkà míǹ.».

eng: Then he cursed and swore, I dont know the man! At that very moment the rooster crowed.
local: Niinee ní u kíǹ ki bíi pùtī ki tíntí yii: «Maa pée nyí kijapaai nee!» Kpalaayee, ní ukooja báakì.

eng: Peter remembered Jesus words, Before the rooster crows you will deny me three times.And Peter went out and cried uncontrollably.
local: Niinee ní Peetroo téetì dinyɔɔbundi din Yeesu bàà lī-uee, yii: «Kí wàatī ukooja ń bāakèe, a làá néé ŋfunyɔm mita yii saa nyí-mi.» Ní u nyáń ki súń ki yáliŋkì.

eng: Now when the disciples saw it they were angry and said, Why this waste?.
local: Yeesu boonnooliibi nín ká mimmee, an kaa mɔ́ɔkiǹ-bi, ní bi bí yii: «Ba pu ní bi bíil̀ talaalumɔnti nee míǹ?.

eng: This perfume could have been sold for a lot of money and given to the poor.
local: Bi bàà ŋūǹ kí nyāfì-u animbikpaan kí dūú yàkatī biwɔfibaa!».

eng: Early in the morning all the chief priests and the elders of the people reached the decision to have Jesus put to death.
local: Kutaafaau pɔ̀lpɔlee, bisaraaŋakpilib kɔkɔ nì Juuda yab tindaŋkpilib kpáfì ki sòon̄ bi nín làá dī nsan ŋun pu ní kí kpɔ̀ Yeesuee.

eng: and I gave them for the potters field, as the Lord commanded me .
local: ní ki dū an nimbiliŋ ki dá uyɔɔmaati kpaabu, kii ti-Dindaan nín nìn tùkū-mi puee.».

eng: Jesus was brought before the governor. The governor said, Are you the king of the Jews? Jesus replied, Thats what you say.
local: Bi cáá Yeesu gɔmina nimbiin, ní gɔmina bālifì-u yii: «Sii di sá Juuda yab bɔtiuaaa?» Ní Yeesu kíí-u yii: «Míǹ ní a lī nee.».

eng: But he didnt answer when the chief priests and elders accused him.
local: Ama, bisaraaŋakpilib nì Juuda yab tindaŋkpilib nín dū tibɔti tin ki túǹ u-puee, waa gātì báà tiba.

eng: Then Pilate said, Dont you hear the testimony they bring against you.
local: Ní gɔmina Piilati bí-u yii: «Aa gbìl̄ tin kɔkɔ bi sòoǹ ki tūntí a-puee níii?».

eng: But he didnt answer, not even a single word. So the governor was greatly amazed.
local: Ama Yeesu kaa pée gātì-u báà tiba an poon, ní an mɔ̄tìi bākatiǹ gɔmina tikpil.

eng: It was customary during the festival for the governor to release to the crowd one prisoner, whomever they might choose.
local: Dilákatil jiŋaaliu kamaayee, gɔmina nìn gīintí usarkadaan un samaa kɔkɔ yaa bālifèe ní kí líí.

eng: At that time there was a well-known prisoner named Jesus Barabbas.
local: Ŋyunti gbantee usarkadaan ubɔ nìn bí un pu báà ŋma pée nyée, bi yíì-u Baaraabaasi.

eng: When the crowd had come together, Pilate asked them, Whom would you like me to release to you, Jesus Barabbas or Jesus who is called Christ.
local: Ní Piilati bālifì samaa un nìn kpáfèe yii: «Ŋma ní ni là ḿ gíiǹ kí líí, Baaraabaasaaa, Yeesu un bi yíì yii Kriisitooyee?».

eng: He knew that the leaders of the people had handed him over because of jealousy.
local: Di sá yii u nìn nyí yii nnimbidiki ŋun bi cáá Yeesu puee di yì-tì ní bi cáań-u ki ŋúbiǹ-u.

eng: While he was serving as judge, his wife sent this message to him, Leave that righteous man alone. Ive suffered much today in a dream because of him.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun u kā ibɔsoon puee, ní u-nimpuu túnní bí tùkù-u yii: «Á taa pée cèeǹ deedeedaan nee bɔtin! Di sá yii m jíń u-pu falaa tikpil kudamintiiun díǹ.».

eng: They bound him, led him away, and turned him over to Pilate the governor.
local: Ní bi bóob́-u ŋŋmii ki cáá-u ki ŋúbiǹ gɔmina Piilati.

eng: But the chief priests and the elders persuaded the crowds to ask for Barabbas and kill Jesus.
local: Ní bisaraaŋakpilib nì Juuda yab tindaŋkpilib téé kunipaau ń lì yii bi là bí gíiǹ Baaraabaasi kí líí kí kpɔ̀ Yeesu.

eng: The governor said, Which of the two do you want me to release to you? Barabbas, they replied.
local: Ní gɔmina fātìi tí būsì ki bālifì-bi yii: «Binib bili nee kansikinee, ŋma ní ni là ḿ gíiǹ kí líí ní?» Ní bi kíí-u yii: «Baaraabaasi ní ti là á gíiǹ kí líí!».

eng: Pilate said, Then what should I do with Jesus who is called Christ? They all said, Crucify him.
local: Ní u fātìi bālifì-bi yii: «Kin, m làá ŋá Yeesu un bi yíì yii Kriisitooyee úǹ mana?» Ní bi-kɔkɔ kíí yii: «Bí kpáá-u ndɔpuntikaa pu!».

eng: But he said, Why? What wrong has he done? They shouted even louder, Crucify him.
local: Ní u bālifì-bi yii: «Mimmee, ba kpitii ní u ŋá?» Ama ní bi mɔ̀tīi kūuntí mpɔn yii: «Bí kpáá-u ndɔpuntikaa pu!».

eng: Pilate saw that he was getting nowhere and that a riot was starting. So he took water and washed his hands in front of the crowd. Im innocent of this mans blood, he said. Its your problem.
local: Gɔmina Piilati ká yii waa ti ŋūǹ kí ŋá tiba, fìkil fikil mɔ̀tīi bíi wīintée, ní u yōoǹ nnyim ki fíntì u-ŋalii samaa kɔkɔ nimbiin, ní ki bí yii: «Maa bí uninja nee kúm poon, ni nì ni-bɔti dee!».

eng: All the people replied, Let his blood be on us and on our children.
local: Ní kunipaauee kɔkɔ kíí yii: «U-kpuulool ń lúntí timbi nì ti-biyaamu pu!».

eng: Then he released Barabbas to them. He had Jesus whipped, then handed him over to be crucified.
local: Niinee ní gɔmina gíiǹ Baaraabaasi ki líì, ní ki yāntì bi gbáń Yeesu anaalab, ní ki dū-u ki tīī bí cáá-u kí kpáá ndɔpuntikaa pu.

eng: The governors soldiers took Jesus into the governors house, and they gathered the whole company of soldiers around him.
local: Niinee ní Piilati soojab cáá Yeesu ki kɔ́ǹ gɔmina cindi poon, ní ki yíiń disoojakitil kɔkɔ dɔ́miń ki kpáfì.

eng: They stripped him and put a red military coat on him.
local: Bi nìn pēetì u-wanti ní ki dū dibɔkutimandi cínciŋ ki pēeǹ-u.

eng: They twisted together a crown of thorns and put it on his head. They put a stick in his right hand. Then they bowed down in front of him and mocked him, saying, Hey! King of the Jews.
local: Bi nìn dū ikuŋkum ki lùn̄ kibeefuulii ki cíkiǹ-u, ki dū ŋkpandɔ ki ŋúbiǹ u-ŋaŋgiil, ní ki kíǹ ki gbàantí u-nimbiin ki bèel̄-u yii: «Ti jáaḿ-si, Juuda yab bɔtiu!».

eng: When Judas, who betrayed Jesus, saw that Jesus was condemned to die, he felt deep regret. He returned the thirty pieces of silver to the chief priests and elders, and.
local: Juudasi un nìn dū Yeesu ki nyáfèe nín ká yii bi làá kpɔ̀ Yeesuee, ní u-pɔbil bíil̀ tin u ŋée pu, ní u cáań animbil jitɔmbi mmuŋku nì saalaayee ki gíiǹ ki tīī bisaraaŋakpilib nì Juuda yab tindaŋkpilib.

eng: After they spit on him, they took the stick and struck his head again and again.
local: ní ki tíiĺ timɔtan ki làbitī-u, ki fòol̄ ŋkpandɔee ki cáa fátí u-yil.

eng: When they finished mocking him, they stripped him of the military coat and put his own clothes back on him. They led him away to crucify him.
local: Bi bēel̀-u míǹ ki dóòyee, ní ki cúú dibɔkutimandee ki pēetì-u, ní ki gíiǹ úǹ u-ba wanti ki pēeǹ-u, ní ki cáá-u ki búntì kí tin kpáá ndɔpuntikaa pu.

eng: As they were going out, they found Simon, a man from Cyrene. They forced him to carry his cross.
local: Bi nyáń ki cée, ki tú uninja ubɔ nsanni u sá Siireen tiŋkin, bi yíì-u Siimɔn. Bi nìn páb́-u ú fōō Yeesu dɔpuntikaaŋu kí bùkun̄.

eng: When they came to a place called Golgotha, which means Skull Place.
local: Bi nìn cáá Yeesu ki bānnì laakin bi yii Gɔlgɔtee, an taapu sá yii: «Kuyikpabiku laŋki.».

eng: they gave Jesus wine mixed with vinegar to drink. But after tasting it, he didnt want to drink it.
local: Bi nìn dū ŋkaam ki ŋmālim̀ ndamam poon ki tīī-u ú nyɔ̀. U dákée ní ki yíì, kaa nyùn̄.

eng: After they crucified him, they divided up his clothes among them by drawing lots.
local: Ní bi dū-u ki kpáá ndɔpuntikaa pu, ní ki tɔ́ kasi ki yākatì u-bɔkutiŋ.

eng: They sat there, guarding him.
local: ki kíǹ ki kā ki cí-u.

eng: They placed above his head the charge against him. It read, This is Jesus, the king of the Jews.
local: Bi nìn ŋmàn̄ tiŋmaŋmabiti ki nákáǹ u-yil pú kí dàkà kun pu ní bi kūū-uee, yii: «Un bí nee di sá Yeesu, Juuda yab bɔtiu.».

eng: They crucified with him two outlaws, one on his right side and one on his left.
local: Bi nìn kpáá digaŋgandambi bili idɔpuntikaa pu ki ŋàl̄-u, ubɔ bí u-ŋaŋgiil pú ní un kíǹee bí u-ŋaŋgan pú.

eng: Those who were walking by insulted Jesus, shaking their heads.
local: Bisanjitiliibi nìn bèel̄-u, ki gàatī bi-yiliŋ.

eng: said, I did wrong because I betrayed an innocent man. But they said, What is that to us? Thats your problem.
local: ní ki bí-bi yii: «M ŋá ikpiti ní ki dū un kaa ŋá tibee ki tīī ní kpɔ̀!» Ní bíǹ kíí-u yii: «An ká timbi la? Sii nì a-bɔti dee!».

eng: and saying, So you were going to destroy the temple and rebuild it in three days, were you? Save yourself! If you are Gods Son, come down from the cross.
local: ki lì yii: «Sii un gbáà kí wīī kunimbɔtidii kí fàtīí mā-ku iwiin itee, a yaa sá Unimbɔti biki, fīī a-ba kí kpákatiń ndɔpuntikaa pu!».

eng: In the same way, the chief priests, along with the legal experts and the elders, were making fun of him, saying.
local: Míǹ mbaantiim, bisaraaŋakpilib, nì bimaradakaliibi nì Juuda yab tindaŋkpilib mun bí ki bèel̄-u, ki lì yii.

eng: He saved others, but he cant save himself. Hes the king of Israel, so let him come down from the cross now. Then well believe in him.
local: «U fīī biba dee, kaa ŋūǹ kí fīī u-ba! U sá Isirayeel yab bɔtiu naa! Ú kpákatiń ndɔpuntikaa pu kɔŋkɔnnee! Mimmee, ti làá fōō-u kí kíí.

eng: He trusts in God, so let God deliver him now if he wants to. He said, Im Gods Son.
local: U dū u-mákal ki ŋá Unimbɔti pu dee, ki lì yii: <Man sá Unimbɔti Jipɔɔn>. Too, kɔŋkɔnnee Unimbɔti yaa néeń-u, ú fīī-uaa!».

eng: The outlaws who were crucified with him insulted him in the same way.
local: Digaŋgandambi bin bi nìn kpáá ki ŋàl̄-uee mun nìn sìí-u míǹ mbaantiim.

eng: From noon until three in the afternoon the whole earth was dark.
local: Kuwiŋkuluuee, dibɔmbɔndi jéetì kitiŋ kɔkɔ pu ki tin sāā tikúti tita.

eng: At about three Jesus cried out with a loud shout, Eli, Eli, lama sabachthani , which means, My God, my God, why have you left me.
local: Ki cá tikúti titee, Yeesu fáá mbiil mpɔn, yii: «Eelii, Eelii, lama sabakitanii?» Taapu sá yii, «M-Nimbɔtiu, m-Nimbɔtiu, ba ŋá ní a dū-mi ki fá?».

eng: After hearing him, some standing there said, Hes calling Elijah.
local: Yeesu fáá mbiil mimmee, ní bin bí niinee biba gbìl̄ u-biiliŋuee pu, ní ki lī yii: «U yíì Eelii dee!».

eng: One of them ran over, took a sponge full of vinegar, and put it on a pole. He offered it to Jesus to drink.
local: Ní bi-kansikin ubɔ sáǹ ki yōoǹ kuca ki múĺ ndaam min gàm̀ee ni, ki dūu túuń ŋkpandɔ pu ki tīī-u ú nyɔ̀.

eng: But the rest of them said, Lets see if Elijah will come and save him.
local: Ama ní bin kíǹee bí yii: «Yàntī! Tí kpèèmaan Eelii yaa làá dāá fīī-u!».

eng: Judas threw the silver pieces into the temple and left. Then he went and hanged himself.
local: Ní Juudasi dū animbilee ki yàl̄ kunimbɔtidiin, ní ki cūtìi lúntì u-ba ŋŋmii ki kpíì.

eng: Again Jesus cried out with a loud shout. Then he died.
local: Yeesu gíti fáá mbiil mpɔnee ní ki sīntì.

eng: Look, the curtain of the sanctuary was torn in two from top to bottom. The earth shook, the rocks split.
local: Niinee, sata un bi nìn dūu pītì kunimbɔtidii poon ki gāntì laakin bi yíì circir páaa laŋkee nì laan kíǹee cúu cátì yilpu ki tin kátì taapu, ní kitiŋ cīŋkì, ní titaŋkpanaakɔti cúu búkutì.

eng: and the bodies of many holy people who had died were raised.
local: Akaakul nìn cúu bīitì, ní bin nìn nóó Unimbɔti ki kpíìyee tikpil fīkitì.

eng: After Jesus resurrection they came out of their graves and went into the holy city where they appeared to many people.
local: ki nyáń bi-kaakuliŋin. Yeesu fìkititam boonee, an nibee kɔ́ Jeerusaleem tiŋkin, ní binib tikpil ká-bi.

eng: When the centurion and those with him who were guarding Jesus saw the earthquake and what had just happened, they were filled with awe and said, This was certainly Gods Son.
local: Room soojakpiliu nì u-yab bin nìn cí Yeesuee nín ká kitiŋ cìŋkitam nì tin kɔkɔ pée jítèe, ní tifaandi cúú-bi tikpil ní bi lī yii: «Tiŋman, kijapaai nee lafun sá Unimbɔti Jipɔɔn ní.».

eng: Many women were watching from a distance. They had followed Jesus from Galilee to serve him.
local: Binimpoobi kupaau mun nìn yú ndan ki kpèeń. Bi nìn céeǹní-u doooo Galiilee ní ki tútúǹ-u atuun.

eng: Among them were Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James and Joseph, and the mother of Zebedees sons.
local: Maarii un sá Magidalaa tiŋkinee nì Maarii un sá Yakubu nì Jooseefi nee, nì Seebeedee jipɔmbi na nìn bí bi-kansikin.

eng: That evening a man named Joseph came. He was a rich man from Arimathea who had become a disciple of Jesus.
local: Kujoou jóotèe, ní ugaaja ubɔ dɔ́miń laakin bi nìn kpáá Yeesuee, bi yíì-u Jooseefi, u sá Arimatee tiŋkin. U mun nìn sá Yeesu boonnooliu.

eng: He came to Pilate and asked for Jesus body. Pilate gave him permission to take it.
local: Ní u cūtì ki ká Piilati, ní ki mēē-u nsan kí yòon̄ Yeesu wunti, ní Piilati bí yii bí dūú tīī-u.

eng: Joseph took the body, wrapped it in a clean linen cloth.
local: Ní u cūtìi yōoǹ, ki nyàab̄ ŋŋaapiin mpɔɔn ki dūu fìn̄-u.

eng: The chief priests picked up the silver pieces and said, According to the Law its not right to put this money in the treasury. Since it was used to pay for someones life, its unclean.
local: Niinee bisaraaŋakpilib cūtì ki tōō animbilee, ní ki bí yii: «Ti-marau kaa pú-ti nsan tí dū an nimbiliŋee kí ŋá kunimbɔtidii nimbiliŋ dakaaun, kun puee an sá fatiku nimbiliŋ ní.».

eng: and laid it in his own new tomb, which he had carved out of the rock. After he rolled a large stone at the door of the tomb, he went away.
local: ní ki cáa ŋá u-kaakupɔndi din u nìn lùkū ditaŋkpalinee poon, ní ki bíntì ditaŋkpakpaandi diba ki pīiǹ dikaakul nyɔkɔpu, ní ki búntì.

eng: Mary Magdalene and the other Mary were there, sitting in front of the tomb.
local: Maarii un sá Magidalaa tiŋkinee nì Maarii un kíǹee mun nìn cūtì ki kā ki pìl̄ dikaakulee nyɔkɔpu.

eng: The next day, which was the day after Preparation Day, the chief priests and the Pharisees gathered before Pilate.
local: Bi bɔ́ntì ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa kutaa wúntèe, ní bisaraaŋakpilib nì Fariisab nóó dicilpu ki cūtìi ká Piilati.

eng: They said, Sir, we remember that while that deceiver was still alive he said, After three days I will arise.
local: ní ki bí-u yii: «Ukpil, ti téetí yii unyimɔndaan nee nín nìn dáá bí dimaŋfalinee u nìn bí yii, u yaa kpíìyee, winta dalee u làá fìkitī.

eng: Therefore, order the grave to be sealed until the third day. Otherwise, his disciples may come and steal the body and tell the people, Hes been raised from the dead. This last deception will be worse than the first.
local: Míǹ puee, yàntī soojab ń cù kí cīití u-kaakul áli iwiin ita, án nín làá ŋá pu u-boonnooliibi ń taa dāan̄ kí yɔ̀ u-wunti kí tùkù samaa yii u fīkitèe: mimmee, nnyimɔn gbanti ŋúǹ dāa pɔ́ɔkí kí jītíǹ mpeepeekaa.».

eng: Pilate replied, You have soldiers for guard duty. Go and make it as secure as you know how.
local: Ní Piilati kíí-bi yii «Ni cáá soojabaa! Cáámaan-bi bí cīití u-kaakul ni nín là puee.».

eng: Then they went and secured the tomb by sealing the stone and posting the guard.
local: Ní bi dū kudaaŋŋu ki nákáǹ ditaŋkpalee pu ubɔ yaa gbēēyee bí bée, ní ki cáá soojab ki cíitì.

eng: So they decided to use it to buy the potters field where strangers could be buried.
local: Ní bi sòon̄ ki gbìlīǹ tɔb, ní ki dū animbilee ki dá uyɔɔmaati ubɔ kpaabu ki dūu ŋá binicambi ceeku.

eng: Thats why that field is called Field of Blood to this very day.
local: An puee, bi yíì bukpaa gbanti yii «Fatiku kpaabu» áli nìn díǹ.

eng: This fulfilled the words of Jeremiah the prophet: And I took the thirty pieces of silver, the price for the one whose price had been set by some of the Israelites.
local: An ŋá kii Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu Yeereemii nín nìn sòon̄ puee dee yii: «Ní bi fōō animbil jitɔmbi mmuŋku nì saalaa ŋin Isirayeel yab nìn kíí yii bí pà u-puee.

eng: After the Sabbath, at dawn on the first day of the week, Mary Magdalene and the other Mary came to look at the tomb.
local: Ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa jítì ku wúntì kɔɔsil ntaafaabɔmbɔminee, Maarii un sá Magidalaa tiŋkinee, nì Maarii un kíǹee cūtì kí kpèè dikaakul.

eng: Then Jesus said to them, Dont be afraid. Go and tell my brothers that I am going into Galilee. They will see me there.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-bi yii: «Taa fàŋkìmaan! Cù kí tùkùmaan m-nabiyaamu yii bí nīn cá Galiilee. Niin ní bi làá tin ká-mi.».

eng: Now as the women were on their way, some of the guards came into the city and told the chief priests everything that had happened.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun binimpoobee dáá bí nsanni ki cée, ní soojab bin nìn cí dikaakulee biba cūtì kitiŋin ki máafì bisaraaŋakpilib tin jítèe kɔkɔ.

eng: They met with the elders and decided to give a large sum of money to the soldiers.
local: Ní bíǹ kpáfì Juuda yab tindaŋkpilib ki sòon̄ ki gbìlīǹ tɔb, ní ki nyàab̄ kunimbigbaau ki ŋéeb́ soojab nyɔmmu.

eng: They told them, Say that Jesus disciples came at night and stole his body while you were sleeping.
local: ní ki tùkū-bi yii: «Ní lì yii, kunyeeu timbi dɔ̄ ki gèeǹee, ní u-boonnooliibi dɔ́miń ki yùn̄ u-wunti.

eng: And if the governor hears about this, we will take care of it with him so you will have nothing to worry about.
local: Báà gɔmina yaa gbìl̄aaa, timbi làá sɔ̀kɔl̀-u kí nyāntí-ni tibɔtin.».

eng: So the soldiers took the money and did as they were told. And this report has spread throughout all Judea to this very day.
local: Ní soojab fōō animbil, ní ki ŋá bi nín bí bí ŋá puee. Ní an bɔtee gbītì Juuda yab kansikin ki dāa sāā díǹ.

eng: Now the eleven disciples went to Galilee, to the mountain where Jesus told them to go.
local: Yeesu boonnooliibi saalaa nì ubɔee búntì Galiilee gbantaŋki kiba pu, laakin Yeesu nìn bí bí kíntí-uee.

eng: When they saw him, they worshipped him, but some doubted.
local: Bi nín ká-uee, ní bi gbāaǹ ki jáam̀-u, ama nì míǹ kɔkɔee, bi-kansikin biba nìn dàkafī mili mili.

eng: Jesus came near and spoke to them, Ive received all authority in heaven and on earth.
local: Ní Yeesu sútìń ki sòon̄ nì bi, yii: «M fōō yilpu nì taapu kɔkɔ pu mpɔn.

eng: Therefore, go and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit.
local: Míǹ puee, nín̄ cámaan atimbun kɔkɔ nib cee kí dū-bi kí kpántí m-boonnooliibi, kí fàl̀-bi Unimbɔti nyim, Ubaati, nì man u-Jipɔɔn nì Circir Fam yindi pu.

eng: Look, there was a great earthquake, for an angel from the Lord came down from heaven. Coming to the stone, he rolled it away and sat on it.
local: Kpalaayee kitiŋ cīŋkì tikpil, ní Unimbɔti tuuŋŋu ŋubɔ nyánní yilpu ki dɔ́miń ki cúú ditaŋkpakpaandee ki bíntì ní ki kāl̀ di-pu.

eng: teaching them to obey everything that Ive commanded you. Look, I myself will be with you every day until the end of this present age.
local: Ní dàkà-bi bí nīn ŋúb́ tin kɔkɔ m tīī-ni ní ŋée. Ní nīn nyí yii m bí ni-cee baabadal áli duulinya kúntitam.».

eng: Now his face was like lightning and his clothes as white as snow.
local: U nìn jìl̀ kii ntaaŋmɔŋmɔee, ní u-wampeeŋkaati pīiǹ káŋkaŋ.

eng: The guards were so terrified of him that they shook with fear and became like dead men.
local: Soojab nín wál̀-uee, ní bi fāŋkì tikpil, bi-wunti bíi jìkì ní bi fìkī.

eng: But the angel said to the women, Dont be afraid. I know that you are looking for Jesus who was crucified.
local: Ní Unimbɔti tuuŋŋuee bí binimpoobee yii: «Nimbi taa fàŋkìmaan! M nyí ni nín nyàab̀ Yeesu un bi kpáá ndɔpuntikaa puee.

eng: He isnt here, because hes been raised from the dead, just as he said. Come, see the place where they laid him.
local: Waa bí doo, u fīkitì, kii u nín nìn pée lī puee. Dāan̄ kí kpèèmaan laakin u nìn dɔ̄ee.

eng: Now hurry, go and tell his disciples, Hes been raised from the dead. Hes going on ahead of you to Galilee. You will see him there. Ive given the message to you.
local: kí nīn cámaan malaa, kí tùkù u-boonnooliibi yii u fīkitì ditaŋkpiilin. U làá lìntī kí cù Galiilee, niin ní ni làá tin ká-u. Tin m cáá kí tùkù-nee dee.».

eng: With great fear and excitement, they hurried away from the tomb and ran to tell his disciples.
local: Niinee ní bíǹ búntì malaa, tifaandi nì dipɔɔpiŋkpaandi kɔkɔ ŋmàl̄, ki sáń ki cá kí cáá dinyɔɔbundee kí tùkù Yeesu boonnooliibi.

eng: But Jesus met them and greeted them. They came and grabbed his feet and worshipped him.
local: Niin kpalaa ní Yeesu tú-bi nsan ní ki jáam̀-bi, ní bi sútì ki pìl̄-u ní ki tātìi gbāaǹ u-taaŋi taapu ki jáam̀-u.

eng: In those days John the Baptist appeared in the desert of Judea announcing.
local: Ŋyunti gbantee ní Yoowaneesi un fùul̀ binib Unimbɔti nyimee dɔ́miń ki bíi báakí Unimbɔti bɔti Juudee teekun, yii.

eng: The ax is already at the root of the trees. Therefore, every tree that doesnt produce good fruit will be chopped down and tossed into the fire.
local: Unimbɔti bɔ́ntì kicɔkɔ ki bíl̀ isufi taapu kí kpìkitī-i. Busubu bun kamaa yaa kaa lòoǹ abimɔntee, bi làá gāā-bu kí tɔ̄ ŋŋmin ní.

eng: I baptize with water those of you who have changed your hearts and lives. The one who is coming after me is stronger than I am. Im not worthy to carry his sandals. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and with fire.
local: Man cáá nnyim ní ki fùul̀-ni ki dàkà yii ni bíi kpántí ni-bimbim, ama un làá dāan̄ man boonee làá dāá fàl̀-ni nì Unimbɔti Fam nì ŋŋmi ní. Úǹ cáá mpɔn ki jítiǹ man, maa sàafì kí nīn sá u-yumbuu kí yòon̄ u-naatakaŋ gba.

eng: The shovel he uses to sift the wheat from the husks is in his hands. He will clean out his threshing area and bring the wheat into his barn. But he will burn the husks with a fire that cant be put out.
local: Úǹ ŋúb́ u-yubakalkaaku, ní ki làá bàkal̀ kí gàntī tifin nì idi, ní kí dū idi kí ŋá kulɔŋŋun ní kí dū tifinee kí dūú tɔ̄ ŋŋmi ŋun kaa kúù jaanjaanee ni.».

eng: At that time Jesus came from Galilee to the Jordan River so that John would baptize him.
local: Niinee ní Yeesu nyánní Galiilee ki dɔ́miń Jɔɔdan buŋŋu caŋin Yoowaneesi cee ú fàl̀-u Unimbɔti nyim.

eng: John tried to stop him and said, I need to be baptized by you, yet you come to me.
local: Ní Yoowaneesi yíì, ki bí-u yii: «Man di bàà máaǹ kí dāan̄ sii cee sii ń fàl̀ man, ní sii gíti dòoń man ń fàl̀-saaa?».

eng: Jesus answered, Allow me to be baptized now. This is necessary to fulfill all righteousness. So John agreed to baptize Jesus.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-u yii: «Kíí tí ŋá míǹ kɔŋkɔnnee, kun puee an máaǹ tí ŋá míǹ kí nóó min kɔkɔ Unimbɔti bàlifēe.» Niinee ní Yoowaneesi kaa ti kpákáǹ-u, ní ki fùl̄-u.

eng: When Jesus was baptized, he immediately came up out of the water. Heaven was opened to him, and he saw the Spirit of God coming down like a dove and resting on him.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun Yoowaneesi fùl̄-u ki dóò, u bíi nyēeń nnyiminee, kpalaayee ní kutagbɔŋu cúu pīitì, ní u wál̀ Unimbɔti Fam kpákatìń u-pu ki bí kii dibeenanjilee.

eng: A voice from heaven said, This is my Son whom I dearly love; I find happiness in him.
local: Niinee ní Unimbɔti sòon̄ní kutaa pu yii: «Un bí nee di sá man Jipɔɔn un m néeń tikpilee, úǹ ní m-pɔbil fáatì.».

eng: Change your hearts and lives! Here comes the kingdom of heaven.
local: «Kpántímaan ni-bimbim kun puee Unimbɔti Beel sútì ki pìlīń-ni.».

eng: He was the one of whom Isaiah the prophet spoke when he said: The voice of one shouting in the wilderness, Prepare the way for the Lord; make his paths straight. .
local: Yoowaneesi pu ní Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu Yeesaya nìn sòon̄ ki bí yii: «Ubɔ báakí kuteeun yii: Bɔ́ntímaan ti-Dindaan saŋŋu kí tàntīmaan u-sàmbiyaamu kí cīití-u!».

eng: John wore clothes made of camels hair, with a leather belt around his waist. He ate locusts and wild honey.
local: Yoowaneesi nìn pēē ugutaamu kuti bɔkutil ní ki gbìn̄ ŋgban dambalau u-caŋin, ní ki ŋmóò inaakitoon nì tisiil ŋmaniŋma.

eng: People from Jerusalem, throughout Judea, and all around the Jordan River came to him.
local: Jeerusaleem yab nì Juudee kɔkɔ, nì bin kóò Jɔɔdan buŋŋu caŋinee kɔkɔ yab nìn dòoń Yoowaneesi cee.

eng: As they confessed their sins, he baptized them in the Jordan River.
local: ki bìitī bi-kpitii pu ní Yoowaneesi fùul̀-bi Unimbɔti nyim Jɔɔdan buŋŋun.

eng: Many Pharisees and Sadducees came to be baptized by John. He said to them, You children of snakes! Who warned you to escape from the angry judgment that is coming soon?.
local: Yoowaneesi nín ká yii Fariisab nì Sadusab dòoń ú fàl̀-bi Unimbɔti nyimee, ní u bí-bi yii: «Nimbi bin tū kii ikoowaatee! Ŋma tùkū-ni yii ní sāǹ kí nyā Unimbɔti ŋuul din dòońee ni?.

eng: Produce fruit that shows you have changed your hearts and lives.
local: Nín̄ tūm̀maan atuun ŋin dàkà yii ni fá ni-kpitiiyee.

eng: And dont even think about saying to yourselves, Abraham is our father. I tell you that God is able to raise up Abrahams children from these stones.
local: kí taa nín̄ kā ki dàkafī ki gà ni-biŋŋi ki lì yii: <Abraam sá ti-naanja>. Ibaamɔn ní m tùkū-ni, Unimbɔti ŋūǹ kí dū ataŋkpee nee kí kpántí Abraam naantiibi.

eng: Then the Spirit led Jesus up into the wilderness so that the devil might tempt him.
local: Boonee ní Unimbɔti Fam cáá Yeesu ki cūnnì kuteeun Sitaan ń cɔ̄ŋkì-u.

eng: Jesus responded, Go away, Satan, because its written, You will worship the Lord your God and serve only him .
local: Niinee Yeesu bí-u yii: «Sitaan, sútí niin kí wàatī-mi! Kun puee an ŋmàn̄ Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon yii: <Ti-Dindaan a-Nimbɔtiu baba ní an sá á jāam̀, kí nīn bí úǹ baba tundin.>».

eng: The devil left him, and angels came and took care of him.
local: Ní Sitaan cāntì-u ní Unimbɔti tuuŋyi dɔ́miń u-cee ki bíi kèetī-u.

eng: Now when Jesus heard that John was arrested, he went to Galilee.
local: Yeesu nín gbìl̄ yii bi cúú Yoowaneesi ki pīiǹ sarkee ní u yíkì ki nín cá Galiilee.

eng: He left Nazareth and settled in Capernaum, which lies alongside the sea in the area of Zebulun and Naphtali.
local: U bàn̄ee kaa kóò Naasaareeti ní ki tin kóòń Kapeernayum Galiilee mɔɔl caŋin, laakin sá Sabulɔn nì Neefitalii tiŋkinee.

eng: This fulfilled what Isaiah the prophet said.
local: An ŋá kii Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu Yeesaya nín nìn sòon̄ ki síiǹ puee dee, yii.

eng: Land of Zebulun and land of Naphtali, alongside the sea, across the Jordan, Galilee of the Gentiles.
local: «Sabulɔn nì Neefitalii timmu yab, tiŋku watil pú, Jɔɔdan buŋgbandi lapu, Galiilee tiŋki, laakin bin kaa sá Juuda yabee kóòyee!.

eng: the people who lived in the dark have seen a great light, and a light has come upon those who lived in the region and in shadow of death.
local: Niin ní inifunaan in kɔkɔ nìn kóò dibɔmbɔndinee ká ŋwalifikpaan! Bin nìn kā ŋkúm bɔmbɔndinee, ŋwalifi ŋmíntì bi-pu!».

eng: From that time Jesus began to announce, Change your hearts and lives! Here comes the kingdom of heaven.
local: Kí yòon̄ ŋyunti gbantee ní Yeesu kíĺ ki bíi sòoǹ Unimbɔti bɔti, yii: «Kpántímaan ni-bimbim, kun puee Unimbɔti beel sútì ki pìlīń-ni!».

eng: As Jesus walked alongside the Galilee Sea, he saw two brothers, Simon, who is called Peter, and Andrew, throwing fishing nets into the sea, because they were fishermen.
local: Yeesu nín bíi kpàkaǹ Galiilee mɔɔlee, ní u ká nnabiyaamu muli: an nìn sá Siimɔn un bi yíì Peetrooyee nì u-naal Andree. Bi nìn sá bijɔncuuliibi ní ki bíi tòó kulaaŋŋu.

eng: Come, follow me, he said, and Ill show you how to fish for people.
local: Ní Yeesu bí yii: «Dāan̄ kí nīn nóómaan-mi, ḿ dū-ni kí kpántí bin cāabìń binib m-ceeyee.».

eng: After Jesus had fasted for forty days and forty nights, he was starving.
local: Ní u bóob́ kinyɔkɔ iwiin imuŋku ili, ŋwiimpu nì kunyeeu, ní ŋkùm kíǹ ki cáá-u.

eng: Right away, they left their nets and followed him.
local: Dicilpu niin ní bi yāntì bi-laanti ki kíǹ ki táá-u.

eng: Continuing on, he saw another set of brothers, James the son of Zebedee and his brother John. They were in a boat with Zebedee their father repairing their nets. Jesus called them and.
local: U gíti cūtì nyɔkɔpuee, ní ki ká binib bili, bi mun tí sá unil nì u-naal. An sá Yakubu nì Yoowaneesi, Seebeedee jipɔmbi. Bi nìn kā buŋalimbun bi nì bi-baa ki ŋáŋkí bi-laanti. Ní Yeesu yíiń-bi.

eng: immediately they left the boat and their father and followed him.
local: ní bi yāntì bi-baa nì bi-ŋalimbu niin, ní ki táá Yeesu.

eng: Jesus traveled throughout Galilee, teaching in their synagogues. He announced the good news of the kingdom and healed every disease and sickness among the people.
local: Yeesu nìn yìiǹ Galiilee timmu kɔkɔn ki dàkà akpafidiinni ki sòoǹ tibɔmɔntiil tin sá Unimbɔti Beel bɔtee, ki cāantí bideembi nì bibunliibi kɔkɔ samaa kansikin.

eng: News about him spread throughout Syria. People brought to him all those who had various kinds of diseases, those in pain, those possessed by demons, those with epilepsy, and those who were paralyzed, and he healed them.
local: Siirii yab kɔkɔ nín gbìl̄ tin Yeesu ŋáańee ní bi cáań bin kɔkɔ būǹ igbaŋween nín pée bí pu iba ibee nì bin bí iyaliŋkin tikpilee, nì bin pu arasiniibi kēe nì bibimbidambi nì bifaliŋfaambi. Yeesu nìn cáaǹ bi-kɔkɔ.

eng: Large crowds followed him from Galilee, the Decapolis, Jerusalem, Judea, and from the areas beyond the Jordan River.
local: Dinikitikpaandi nìn kíǹ ki nóó-u, ki nìn nyánní Galiilee nì ntimu saalaa watil, nì Jeerusaleem nì Juudee tiŋkin nì kitiŋ kin bí Jɔɔdan buŋgbandi lapu puee.

eng: The tempter came to him and said, Since you are Gods Son, command these stones to become bread.
local: Ní Sitaan sútì ki pìl̄-u, ní ki bí-u yii: «A yaa lafun sá Unimbɔti Jipɔɔn, tīī ditaŋkpal nee mara dí kpántí kpɔnɔ.».

eng: Jesus replied, Its written, People wont live only by bread, but by every word spoken by God .
local: Ní Yeesu fātìi kíí-u yii: «Unimbɔti gbɔŋku lī yii: <An kaa sá tijin dijil ŋmaniŋma di yì unil maŋfal, ama, unil ń nīn tɔ́ Unimbɔti bɔtee di yì u-maŋfal.>».

eng: After that the devil brought him into the holy city and stood him at the highest point of the temple. He said to him.
local: Ní Sitaan cáá-u ki cūnnì Jeerusaleem ki tɔ́kɔǹ kunimbɔtidii yilpu kúnti kunti.

eng: Since you are Gods Son, throw yourself down; for it is written, I will command my angels concerning you, and they will take you up in their hands so that you wont hit your foot on a stone. .
local: ní ki bí-u yii: «A yaa sá Unimbɔti Jipɔɔn, yùkū kí tìin̄ taapu tiba kaa làá ŋá-si, kun puee an ŋmàn̄ Unimbɔti gbɔŋkun yii: <Unimbɔti làá yàntī u-tuuŋyi ń nīn tíkí-si i-ŋaaŋyin a-taal ń taa gbéetí ditaŋkpal.>».

eng: Jesus replied, Again its written, Dont test the Lord your God .
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-u yii: «An lī Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon yii: <Á taa cɔ̄ŋkì ti-Dindaan a-Nimbɔtiu.>».

eng: Then the devil brought him to a very high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms of the world and their glory.
local: Ní Sitaan tí cáá-u ki jɔ́m̀ dijoofɔŋfɔkɔl diba pu, ní ki yāntì u ká abee ŋin bí duulinyanee kɔkɔ nì ŋi-nyooti.

eng: He said, Ill give you all these if you bow down and worship me.
local: ní ki bí-u yii: «A yaa gbāaǹ ki jáam̀-mi, m làá dū an kɔkɔ kí tīī-si.».

eng: Now when Jesus saw the crowds, he went up a mountain. He sat down and his disciples came to him.
local: Yeesu nín ká dinikitikpaandi gbantee, ní u jɔ́m̀ ki kāl̀ dijool pu, ní u-boonnooliibi sútì ki pìl̄-u.

eng: Happy are people whose lives are harassed because they are righteous, because the kingdom of heaven is theirs.
local: Bin ní bi sàantí bi-deedeeu puee bí mmɔnneeee, kun puee bi sá Unimbɔti Beelin yab!.

eng: Happy are you when people insult you and harass you and speak all kinds of bad and false things about you, all because of me.
local: «Binib yaa bí ki sìí-ni ki sàantí-ni ki mɔ́ntí inyimɔn ki tūntí ni-pu ki sòoǹ tibɔkpitil kamaa ni-pu ni nín sá man yabee puee, ni bí mmɔnni.

eng: Be full of joy and be glad, because you have a great reward in heaven. In the same way, people harassed the prophets who came before you.
local: Nín̄ bímaan mmɔŋkpaan poon kí nīn fàal̀, kun puee tipaakpaandi dɔ̄ yilpu ki cí-ni. Míǹ mbaantiim ní bi nìn tí sāaǹ Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi bin nìn péé péé ki kāl̀ ní nimbi dāa nín páaǹní boonee.».

eng: You are the salt of the earth. But if salt loses its saltiness, how will it become salty again? Its good for nothing except to be thrown away and trampled under peoples feet.
local: Ní Yeesu kútì ki lī yii: «Nimbi di sá kii ŋyaam ki tīī duulinya. Ŋyaam yaa kpíìyee, mana ní bi gíti ŋūǹ kí ŋá ní mí fàtīí mɔ̄ɔkì? Mimmee maa ti bí tiban dee, see bí dū-mì kí fá mmɔŋki, ní binib jītí ki tà.

eng: You are the light of the world. A city on top of a hill cant be hidden.
local: «Ni sá kii ŋwalifee ní ki tīī duulinya. Kitiŋ kin bi máń ki tɔ́kɔ́ǹ kigbantaŋ puee kaa lìkintí ki-ba pu.

eng: Neither do people light a lamp and put it under a basket. Instead, they put it on top of a lampstand, and it shines on all who are in the house.
local: Baa tí tùutī fitila kí dū dibool kí cíkiń u-pu, asee bí dūú tɔ́kɔń dikɔl pu ní án nīn wāĺ bin kɔkɔ bí kudiinee.

eng: In the same way, let your light shine before people, so they can see the good things you do and praise your Father who is in heaven.
local: Míǹ mbaantiim ní ni mun walifiŋu ń nīn ŋmíntí binib, bí ká tiŋan tin ni ŋáańee, kí pàkà ni-Baa Unimbɔti un bí yilpuee.».

eng: Dont even begin to think that I have come to do away with the Law and the Prophets. I havent come to do away with them but to fulfill them.
local: «Ní taa dàkafì yii m dɔ́miń kí kpìtī Mooyiisi marab nì tin Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi nìn sòon̄ee. Maa dɔ́miń kí kpìtī, m dɔ́miń kí ŋá bi nín lī puee ní.

eng: I say to you very seriously that as long as heaven and earth exist, neither the smallest letter nor even the smallest stroke of a pen will be erased from the Law until everything there becomes a reality.
local: Ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-ni, ŋyunti ŋun ní yilpu nì taapu dáá bée, marabee ŋmaŋmabiti poon báà kubɔ kpá kí fɔ́táaa, kilɔmbiki kiba mun kpá kí kpìtī kí dāá sāā ŋyunti ŋun tin kɔkɔ pu an sòon̄ee ń ŋá.

eng: Therefore, whoever ignores one of the least of these commands and teaches others to do the same will be called the lowest in the kingdom of heaven. But whoever keeps these commands and teaches people to keep them will be called great in the kingdom of heaven.
local: An pu ní un di yaa kpītì kimarawaai kiba ní ki dàkà bin kíǹee yii bí nīn ŋáań mimmee, udaan dāa sá kiyaawaai ní Unimbɔti Beelin. Ama un di yaa ŋúb́ marab ní ki dàkà binib bí nīn ŋáań mimmee, udaan dāa sá ukpaan ní Unimbɔti Beelin.

eng: He taught them, saying.
local: U nìn kíĺ ki bíi dàkà-bi yii.

eng: I say to you that unless your righteousness is greater than the righteousness of the legal experts and the Pharisees, you will never enter the kingdom of heaven.
local: Ibaamɔn ní m sòoǹ nì ni, ni yaa kaa bí deedee ki jítiǹ bimaradakaliibi nì Fariisab, naa ŋūǹ kí kɔ́ Unimbɔti Beelin.».

eng: You have heard that it was said to those who lived long ago, Dont commit murder, and all who commit murder will be in danger of judgment.
local: «Ni gbìl̄ bi tùkū ti-naanjab yii: <Á taa kpɔ̀ unil, un kamaa yaa kūū unilee, see bí cáá udaan ibɔsoon kí dáaĺ u-tafal.>.

eng: But I say to you that everyone who is angry with their brother or sister will be in danger of judgment. If they say to their brother or sister, You idiot, they will be in danger of being condemned by the governing council. And if they say, You fool, they will be in danger of fiery hell.
local: Ama man tùkù-ni yii un kamaa di yaa fōō diŋuul u-nabiki puee, see bí cáá-u ibɔsoon kí dáaĺ u-tafal. Un yaa sīī u-nabiki yii <Kinaatuuyee,> see bí cáá-u Bibɔsoonliibi kitil cee, ní un yaa sīī u-nabiki yii <Kunifaŋuee,> udaan máaǹ bí cáá-u ditafadaaldi ŋmiŋun ní.

eng: Therefore, if you bring your gift to the altar and there remember that your brother or sister has something against you.
local: An puee a yaa cáań saraa nsaraafan pu kí tīī Unimbɔti ní ki téetì yii a-nabiki díkí a-pu tibee.

eng: leave your gift at the altar and go. First make things right with your brother or sister and then come back and offer your gift.
local: yàntī a-saraau nsaraafan cee kí cùú ŋāŋkì si nì a-nabikee kansikin, ní kí nín gítiń kí ŋá a-saraau kí tīī Unimbɔti.

eng: Be sure to make friends quickly with your opponents while you are with them on the way to court. Otherwise, they will haul you before the judge, the judge will turn you over to the officer of the court, and you will be thrown into prison.
local: «A yaa ŋá ubɔ tiba ní u cáǹ-si ibɔsoonee, ŋāŋkì sii nì u kansikin malaa, ŋyunti ŋun ni dáá bí nsannee, án nín làá ŋá pu ú taa cáá-si kí tīī ubɔsoonli ní ubɔsoonlee mun ń dū-si kí tīī pilisi, ní úǹ ń cáá-si kí pìin̄ sarkee.

eng: I say to you in all seriousness that you wont get out of there until youve paid the very last penny.
local: Ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-si, bi yaa cáá-si ki tɔ́ niinee, see á pà a-pɔndi nyíki nyiki, báà gboo kaa kīntí, ní bí nín líí-si.».

eng: You have heard that it was said, Dont commit adultery.
local: Yeesu bí-bi yii: «Kéè ni gbìl̄ bi síiǹ yii: <Á taa ŋá tilaakpaalaa!>.

eng: But I say to you that every man who looks at a woman lustfully has already committed adultery in his heart.
local: Ama man tùkù-ni yii un kamaa di yaa kpēē unimpu ki bàà là kí mɔ̀nnì-uee kpáàa ŋá tilaakpaal dee u-pɔbilin.

eng: And if your right eye causes you to fall into sin, tear it out and throw it away. Its better that you lose a part of your body than that your whole body be thrown into hell.
local: An puee a-nimbil ŋgiil yal di yaa làá yàntī á lítí ikpitinee, lúkutí-dì kí fá, kun puee an ŋān̄ ki tīī-si a-wunjandi diba ń nīn kpá, ki jítiǹ a-wunti ń nīn bí mmɔm á kɔ́ ŋŋmin.

eng: Happy are people who are hopeless, because the kingdom of heaven is theirs.
local: «Bin sīntiǹ bi-ba ki tīī Unimbɔtee bí mmɔnneeee, kun puee bi sá Unimbɔti Beelin yab!.

eng: And if your right hand causes you to fall into sin, chop it off and throw it away. Its better that you lose a part of your body than that your whole body go into hell.
local: A-ŋaŋgiil di yaa làá tí yàntī á lítí ikpitinee, gùutī-ŋu kí fá, di sá yii an sɔ́ a-wunjandi diba ń nīn kpá, ki jítiǹ a-wunti ń nīn bí mmɔm á kɔ́ ŋŋmin.».

eng: It was said, Whoever divorces his wife must give her a divorce certificate.
local: «An tí lī yii: <Sii un yaa là kí jà a-nimpuuee, á tīī-u kugbɔŋu kun dàkà kun pu ní a là kí jà-uee ní kí nín jà-u.>.

eng: But I say to you that whoever divorces his wife except for sexual unfaithfulness forces her to commit adultery. And whoever marries a divorced woman commits adultery.
local: Ama man tùkù-ni yii un kamaa yaa jìn̄ u-nimpuu ní an kaa sá yii bi-catee kòóyee, mimmee u dū u-nimpuuee ki síiǹ tilaakpaal saŋŋu pu dee. Ní un mun yaa cáá unimpu un bi jìn̄ee mun ŋá tilaakpaal.».

eng: Again you have heard that it was said to those who lived long ago: Dont make a false solemn pledge, but you should follow through on what you have pledged to the Lord.
local: «Ni gíti tí gbìl̄ bi nín tùkū ti-naanjab yii: <Á taa pùtī Unimbɔti yindi fám, a yaa pūtì-u ki bí yii a làá ŋá puee, ŋá míǹ kí tīī-u.>.

eng: But I say to you that you must not pledge at all. You must not pledge by heaven, because its Gods throne.
local: Ama man tùkū-ni yii ní taa pée pùtī kpataaa. Ní taa pùtī yilpu, kun puee niin di sá Unimbɔti beejal.

eng: You must not pledge by the earth, because its Gods footstool. You must not pledge by Jerusalem, because its the city of the great king.
local: kí taa pùtī kitiŋ, kun puee kitiŋ sá u-taaŋi tɔkɔnlaŋki ní, kí taa pùtī Jeerusaleem, kun puee Ubɔtikpaan tiŋki dee.

eng: And you must not pledge by your head, because you cant turn one hair white or black.
local: Á taa pùtī a-yil, kun puee aa ŋūǹ kí yàntī kuyikpibambaantiiu ń pīil̀aaa, kaa ŋūǹ kí yàntī kú bɔ̀ntī.

eng: Let your yes mean yes, and your no mean no. Anything more than this comes from the evil one.
local: An yaa sá á kíí iiiŋyee, kíí <iiin>, an yaa sá á kíí aayeeyee, kíí <aayee>. Tin a yaa gíti páaǹ ki lī boonee nyánní Sitaan cee ní.».

eng: You have heard that it was said, An eye for an eye and a tooth for a tooth .
local: «Ni gbìl̄ bi síiǹ yii: <Un yaa lēē a-nimbilee, a mun ń lēē u-yal kí téeǹ, ní un yaa lēē a-nyindee, a mun ń lēē u-yal kí téeǹ.>.

eng: But I say to you that you must not oppose those who want to hurt you. If people slap you on your right cheek, you must turn the left cheek to them as well.
local: Ama man tùkù-ni yii, a-juti yaa ŋá-si tikpitilee, á taa dū tikpitil kí tɔ̀-u ipɔntoon. Bi yaa fáá a-ŋaŋgiitaŋkpanjil ntampee, fàtī a-ŋaŋgan yal kí tīī bí fáá.

eng: Happy are people who grieve, because they will be made glad.
local: Bin bí kunimbaaunee bí mmɔnneeee, kun puee Unimbɔti làá dāá ŋméeǹ-bi!.

eng: When they wish to haul you to court and take your shirt, let them have your coat too.
local: Ubɔ yaa là kí cáá-si ibɔsoon kí fōō a-bɔkutilee, dū a-bɔkutikpaandi kí kūtì kí tīī-u.

eng: When they force you to go one mile, go with them two.
local: Ubɔ yaa páb́-si yii á túń diwaŋgul kí còom̀ ditaŋkpal dibee, á túń diwaŋgulee kí còom̀ ataŋkpee ali.

eng: Give to those who ask, and dont refuse those who wish to borrow from you.
local: Ubɔ yaa mēē-si tibee, pɔ̄-u, kí taa fàtī a-boon kí tīī un dɔ́miń a-cee kí pìnnì tibee.».

eng: You have heard that it was said, You must love your neighbor and hate your enemy.
local: «Ni gbìl̄ bi síiǹ yii: <Á nīn néeń a-bɔɔ, kí nīn ná a-nannanliu.>.

eng: But I say to you, love your enemies and pray for those who harass you.
local: Ama man tùkù-ni yii ní nīn néeń ni-nannanliibi kí nīn mèé Unimbɔti ki tìì bin sàantí-nee.

eng: so that you will be acting as children of your Father who is in heaven. He makes the sun rise on both the evil and the good and sends rain on both the righteous and the unrighteous.
local: Mimmee, ni sá ni-Baa un bí yilpuee biyaamu dee, kun puee úǹ yàntī u-wìiŋ́ŋu nyēē ki tìì binikpitib nì binimɔntiibi ní ki yàntī utaa nì ki tìì bin bí deedeeyee nì bin kaa bí deedeeyee.

eng: If you love only those who love you, what reward do you have? Dont even the tax collectors do the same?.
local: Ni yaa néeń bin néeń-nee ŋmaniŋmee, ba paatii ní ni gíti kā ki cí kí dāá fōō Unimbɔti cee? Kaa sá míǹ ní bifɔtiliibi bin ni kpèé yii bi sá bikpitibee mun ŋáańaaa?.

eng: And if you greet only your brothers and sisters, what more are you doing? Dont even the Gentiles do the same?.
local: Ni yaa tí jáaḿ ni-nabiyaamu ŋmaniŋmee, la di nyáń u-ba? Bin kaa nyí Unimbɔtee mun ŋáań míǹ mbaantiim naa!.

eng: Therefore, just as your heavenly Father is complete in showing love to everyone, so also you must be complete.
local: Míǹ puee ní nīn cáámaan mbimbimɔntiim tikɔkɔ poon kii ni-Baa un bí yilpuee nín cáá puee.».

eng: Happy are people who are humble, because they will inherit the earth.
local: Binib bin mɔ̄ee bí mmɔnneeee, kun puee Unimbɔti làá dāá tīī-bi kutindaŋu!.

eng: Happy are people who are hungry and thirsty for righteousness, because they will be fed until they are full.
local: Bin deedee kùm nì deedee nyinnyooŋu cááyee bí mmɔnneeee, kun puee Unimbɔti làá dāá tīī-bi tikɔkɔ!.

eng: Happy are people who show mercy, because they will receive mercy.
local: Bin sūǹ binib saayee bí mmɔnneeee, kun puee Unimbɔti làá dāá sɔ̄ bi mun saai!.

eng: Happy are people who have pure hearts, because they will see God.
local: Bin pɔbiliŋ bí diba nì Unimbɔtee bí mmɔnneeee, kun puee bi làá ká Unimbɔti!.

eng: Happy are people who make peace, because they will be called Gods children.
local: Bin cāabìń ŋgbansɔŋfi bi-jutiibi kansikinee bí mmɔnneeee, kun puee Unimbɔti làá dāá yíiń-bi u-biyaamu!.

eng: Be careful that you dont practice your religion in front of people to draw their attention. If you do, you will have no reward from your Father who is in heaven.
local: «Ní nīn nyí nì ni-ba kí taa nín̄ nyāntí ni-deedeebítam ki dàkà binib yii bí ká-ni. Ni yaa ŋáań tiŋan ki dàkà binibee, naa tin gíti kāntí tipaal tiba ni-Baa un bí yilpuee cee.

eng: Bring in your kingdom so that your will is done on earth as its done in heaven.
local: Yàntī a-Beel ń dāan̄. Bí ŋá a nín là puee taapu doo, kii bi nín ŋáań yilpu puee.

eng: Give us the bread we need for today.
local: Pɔ̄-ti díǹ jikaati.

eng: Forgive us for the ways we have wronged you, just as we also forgive those who have wronged us.
local: Dū ti-kpitii in ti yīntiǹ-see kí pɔ̄-ti, kii ti mun nín cáá bin yìntīǹ-tee kpitii ki pūǹ-bi puee.

eng: And dont lead us into temptation, but rescue us from the evil one.
local: Taa pɔ̄ mpaan Sitaan ń kīil̀-ti, ama á nyāntí-ti u-ŋaan. Kun puee sii di yì dibeel nì mpɔn nì tinyool kí nīn cá jaanjaan. Ameeni>.

eng: If you forgive others their sins, your heavenly Father will also forgive you.
local: Di sá yii ni yaa cáá tin ni-jutiibi yìntīǹ-nee ki pūǹ-bee, ni-Baa un bí yilpuee làá dū tin ni mun yīntiǹ-uee kí pɔ̄-ni.

eng: But if you dont forgive others, neither will your Father forgive your sins.
local: Ama ni yaa kaa cáá tin ni-jutiibi yìntīǹ-nee ki pūǹ-bee, ni-Baa un bí yilpuee kaa làá dū ni mun kpitii kí pɔ̄-ni.».

eng: And when you fast, dont put on a sad face like the hypocrites. They distort their faces so people will know they are fasting. I assure you that they have their reward.
local: «Ni yaa bóob́ kinyɔkɔee ní taa bīil̀ ni-nimbiiŋyi kii ipoonnyoondambi nín ŋáań puee. Bíǹ bíiĺ bi-nimbiiŋyi binib ń béé yii bi bóob́ kinyɔkɔ ní. Ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-ni, bi fōō bi-paati ki dóò.

eng: When you fast, brush your hair and wash your face.
local: Ama sii yaa bóob́ kinyɔkɔee, fíntí a-nimbiin kí càatī a-yikpiti.

eng: Then you wont look like you are fasting to people, but only to your Father who is present in that secret place. Your Father who sees in secret will reward you.
local: án nín làá ŋá pu báà ubɔ ń taa béé yii a bóob́ kinyɔkɔee. A-Baa un bí dibalinee baba ń nīn nyí, ní úǹ un wāĺ dibalinee ń tīī-si a-paati.».

eng: Stop collecting treasures for your own benefit on earth, where moth and rust eat them and where thieves break in and steal them.
local: «Ní taa nín̄ nyàab̀maan tigaajati ki sīintí ni-ba taapu doo laakin dikɔtuul ŋūǹ kí mɔ́, ní tì ŋūǹ kí tí dāmintì, ní binaayub mun ŋūǹ kí cíĺ kudii kí kɔ́ kí yɔ̀-tèe.

eng: Whenever you give to the poor, dont blow your trumpet as the hypocrites do in the synagogues and in the streets so that they may get praise from people. I assure you, thats the only reward theyll get.
local: «A yaa làá pɔ̄ uwɔfi ubɔ tibee, taa nín̄ báakí bí ká yii sii di bíi tìì tiba, kii ipoonnyoondambi nín báakí adan pu nì akpafidiinni puee. Bi ŋáań míǹ binib ń nīn pàkà-bi ní. Ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-ni, bi fōō bi-paati ki dóò.

eng: Instead, collect treasures for yourselves in heaven, where moth and rust dont eat them and where thieves dont break in and steal them.
local: Nín̄ ŋáaḿmaan min fáatì Unimbɔtee ní ú dū tipaal kí síiń-ni yilpu laakin dikɔtuul kpáaa, tiwan kaa dámintáaa, ní binaayub mun kaa tí ŋūǹ kí cíĺ kí kɔ́ kí yɔ̀ee.

eng: Where your treasure is, there your heart will be also.
local: Kun puee laakin a-gaajati bée, niin ní a-landɔkɔŋu mun bí.».

eng: The eye is the lamp of the body. Therefore, if your eye is healthy, your whole body will be full of light.
local: «Unil nimbiliŋ di sá u-wunti fitilau. A-nimbiliŋ yaa wāĺee, a-wunti kɔkɔ bí ŋwalifin ní.

eng: But if your eye is bad, your whole body will be full of darkness. If then the light in you is darkness, how terrible that darkness will be!.
local: Ama a-nimbiliŋ yaa kaa wāĺee, a-wunti kɔkɔ bí dibɔmbɔndin ní. Ŋwalifi ŋun bí a-nee yaa sá dibɔmbɔndee, mimmee dibɔmbɔndee kaa cáá juti.».

eng: No one can serve two masters. Either you will hate the one and love the other, or you will be loyal to the one and have contempt for the other. You cannot serve God and wealth.
local: «Unibaanti kaa ŋūǹ kí nīn sá bikpilib bili tɔntɔnliu: see ú nīn ná ubɔ kí nīn néeń un kíǹee, àá ú dū u-ba kí yā kí nákáǹ ubɔ ní kí nīn kpèé un béeyee fám. Naa ŋūǹ kí nīn sá Unimbɔti tɔntɔnliibi kí nīn sá animbil mun tɔntɔnliibi.».

eng: Therefore, I say to you, dont worry about your life, what youll eat or what youll drink, or about your body, what youll wear. Isnt life more than food and the body more than clothes?.
local: «An pu ní m tùkù-ni yii: Ní taa nín̄ yáliŋkí kun ni làá jī ki làá nyɔ̀ kí nīn bée, nì tin ni làá pèen̄ee pu. Di sá yii dimaŋfal jítiǹ tijin, ní tiwun mun jítiǹ abɔkuti, àá an kaa sá míǹ?.

eng: Look at the birds in the sky. They dont sow seed or harvest grain or gather crops into barns. Yet your heavenly Father feeds them. Arent you worth much more than they are?.
local: Kpèèmaan nnɔmbiyaamu mun ŋàaǹ kutaa puee, muaa būul̀aaa, kaa cēēyaaa, kaa cáá cakalaabi ki kpáafí tijikaal, ama ní ni-Baa un bí yilpuee kpīintí-mu. Kpèè, nimbi cáá kudapɔɔu ki jítiǹ-mu kpataaa!.

eng: Who among you by worrying can add a single moment to your life?.
local: Ni-kansikin ŋma di ŋūǹ kí nīn kā ki nìiǹ, kí gbéeǹ u-maŋfal wiiŋyi pu fiii?.

eng: And why do you worry about clothes? Notice how the lilies in the field grow. They dont wear themselves out with work, and they dont spin cloth.
local: «Kin, ba pu ní ni kā ki yáliŋkí kun ni làá pèen̄ee pu? Kpèèmaan, nsupumpukum min nyēē tisatinee: maa tūǹ ditundaaa, kaa lù satab.

eng: But I say to you that even Solomon in all of his splendor wasnt dressed like one of these.
local: ama m tùkù-ni yii ubɔti Saloomɔn, nì u-gaajati kɔkɔee, waa ŋúń ki ká tiwannyaandi ki pēeǹ an bàn̄ mpumpukum gbanti kansikin miba nyaaŋku.

eng: But when you give to the poor, dont let your left hand know what your right hand is doing.
local: Ama sii yaa làá pɔ̄ uwɔfi tibee, á tīī a-ŋaŋgan ń taa nín̄ nyí a-ŋaŋgiil nín ŋá tiŋyee.

eng: If God dresses grass in the field so beautifully, even though its alive today and tomorrow its thrown into the furnace, wont God do much more for you, you people of weak faith?.
local: Unimbɔti yaa nyāantí míǹ timool tin bí tisatin díǹ, fool ŋŋmi ń dāá wà-tèe, mimmee waa làá ŋá án jītíǹ míǹ ki tīī-naaa? Ba pu ní ni-fookiiu kaa náaǹ míǹ?.

eng: Therefore, dont worry and say, What are we going to eat? or What are we going to drink? or What are we going to wear.
local: Mimmee, taa nín̄ yáliŋkímaan ki lì yii: <Ti làá jī ba? Ti làá nyɔ̀ ba? Ti làá pèen̄ ba?>.

eng: Gentiles long for all these things. Your heavenly Father knows that you need them.
local: An sá bin kaa nyí Unimbɔtee di nyàab̀ tíǹ múlmul. Ama nimbi Baa un bí yilpuee nyí yii an wanti kɔkɔ máaǹ ní nīn cáá-tì.

eng: Instead, desire first and foremost Gods kingdom and Gods righteousness, and all these things will be given to you as well.
local: Péé kí nyàab̀maan Unimbɔti Beel nì deedee un u bàlifī-ni yii ní nīn bée, ní Unimbɔti ń dū tin kíǹee kí kūtì-ni.

eng: Therefore, stop worrying about tomorrow, because tomorrow will worry about itself. Each day has enough trouble of its own.
local: Míǹ puee ní kpèè díǹ yati, ní taa nín̄ yáliŋkímaan fool pu. Fool mun nì u-yati ní. Ŋwiin kamaa nì ŋu-gìńdi ní.».

eng: so that you may give to the poor in secret. Your Father who sees what you do in secret will reward you.
local: Á ŋá dibalin, ní a-Baa un wāĺ dibalinee ń téeǹ-si.».

eng: When you pray, dont be like hypocrites. They love to pray standing in the synagogues and on the street corners so that people will see them. I assure you, thats the only reward theyll get.
local: «Ni yaa bíi mèéyee, ní taa nín̄ mèémaan kii ipoonnyoondambi bin néeń ki yūlifí dicancaal akpafidiinni nì asaŋkaŋkpil ki mèé báà ŋma ń nīn wāĺ-bee. Ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-ni, bi fōō bi-paati ki dóò.

eng: But when you pray, go to your room, shut the door, and pray to your Father who is present in that secret place. Your Father who sees what you do in secret will reward you.
local: Ama, sii yaa làá mèèyee, á kɔ́ a-diikun, kí dūú pìin̄ kí mèè, a-Baa bí niin dibalin. A-Baa un wāĺ tin bí dibalinee làá pà-si.

eng: When you pray, dont pour out a flood of empty words, as the Gentiles do. They think that by saying many words theyll be heard.
local: «Ni yaa bíi mèéyee, taa nín̄ bùsīmaan anyɔɔbun kii bin kaa nyí Unimbɔtee nín ŋáań puee. Bíǹ dàkafī yii bi yaa bíi mèé ki bùsī anyɔɔbunee ní Unimbɔti làá pīlifiǹ-bi.

eng: Dont be like them, because your Father knows what you need before you ask.
local: Nimbi ń taa fōō bíǹ nín ŋáań puee, kun puee kí wàatī ní mèè ni-Baayee, u nyí kun páb́-nee.

eng: Pray like this: Our Father who is in heaven, uphold the holiness of your name.
local: Nín̄ mèémaan yii: <Ti-Baa un bí yilpuee, yàntī bí nyɔ̄ŋkì a-yindi.

eng: Dont judge, so that you wont be judged.
local: «Taa nín̄ sòom̀maan tɔb bɔti, ní Unimbɔti ń taa sòoǹ ni mun yati.

eng: Or give them a snake when they ask for fish?.
local: U yaa tí mēē-u ujɔm, u kíikí kí dū ukoobu kí tīī-uaaa?.

eng: If you who are evil know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your heavenly Father give good things to those who ask him.
local: Nimbi binikpitib nyí kí tīī ni-biyaamu tiwammɔntiil, kin ataa aŋa pu ní ni-Baa un bí yilpuee kaa nín ŋūǹ kí dū tiwammɔntiil kí tīī bin mèé-uee?.

eng: Therefore, you should treat people in the same way that you want people to treat you; this is the Law and the Prophets.
local: «Min ní ni yaa là bí ŋá kí tīī-nee, ŋámaan míǹ kí tīī bi mun. Mooyiisi marab nì Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi gbɔnti nín lì puee dee.».

eng: Go in through the narrow gate. The gate that leads to destruction is broad and the road wide, so many people enter through it.
local: «Dīmaan kipunyɔkɔwaai kí kɔ́. Kun puee kipunyɔkɔ nì nsan ŋun wāāyee di cáǹ binib diwaŋkilin, niin ní kunipaau nóó.

eng: But the gate that leads to life is narrow and the road difficult, so few people find it.
local: Ama kipunyɔkɔwaai kin pɔ̄ɔ̄ dijitilee nì nsan ŋun kaa wāāyee di cáǹ binib dimaŋfal laŋki, binib fiii di kāntí-ŋu kí dī.».

eng: Watch out for false prophets. They come to you dressed like sheep, but inside they are vicious wolves.
local: «Ní nīn nyím̀maan ni-ba nì bin yíì bi-ba yii Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi kin kaa sée. Bi yaa dòoń ni-ceeyee, bi bí soooo kii apibilee, ama ní bi-pɔbiliŋ sá isaakpan yaŋ ki cútí.

eng: You will know them by their fruit. Do people get bunches of grapes from thorny weeds, or do they get figs from thistles?.
local: Bi-tuuŋŋi ní ni làá dūú béé-bi, kun puee baa tòó atùun abɔjanaakun pu, kaa tí kìtī kɔŋkib bukuŋkumbu pu.

eng: In the same way, every good tree produces good fruit, and every rotten tree produces bad fruit.
local: Busumɔntiibu sá kamaa lòoǹ asubimɔnti ní, ní busukpitibu lòoǹ asubikpiti.

eng: A good tree cant produce bad fruit. And a rotten tree cant produce good fruit.
local: Busumɔntiibu kaa ŋūǹ kí lòoǹ asubikpiti, míǹ ní busukpitibu kaa tí ŋūǹ kí lòoǹ asubimɔnti.

eng: Every tree that doesnt produce good fruit is chopped down and thrown into the fire.
local: Isufi in kɔkɔ kaa lòoǹ asubimɔntee, bi làá gāā-i ní kí tɔ̄ ŋŋmin.

eng: Youll receive the same judgment you give. Whatever you deal out will be dealt out to you.
local: Min min ní a dì kí sòoǹ a-nabiki bɔtee, míǹ ní Unimbɔti làá dī kí sòoǹ si mun yati. A nín ŋmàntī min kí tīī a-jutee, míǹ ní u làá dāá ŋmàntī kí tīī si mun.

eng: Therefore, you will know them by their fruit.
local: An puee, bin yíì bi-ba yii Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi kin kaa sée tuuŋŋi ní ni làá dū kí béé-bi.».

eng: Not everybody who says to me, Lord, Lord, will get into the kingdom of heaven. Only those who do the will of my Father who is in heaven will enter.
local: «An kaa sá bin kɔkɔ yíì-mi yii: <M-Dindaan, m-Dindaanee> di làá dāá kɔ́ Unimbɔti Beelin, asee bin ŋáań min m-Baa un bí yilpuee lèe baba di dāa kóò.

eng: On the Judgment Day, many people will say to me, Lord, Lord, didnt we prophesy in your name and expel demons in your name and do lots of miracles in your name.
local: Ibɔsoon dalee binib tikpil làá dāá lì-mi yii: <Ti-Dindaan, ti-Dindaan, a-yindi puee, Unimbɔti kaa nìn sòon̄ nì ti, ti dūu nāatì binibaaa? Taa jìn̄ arasiniibi a-yindi puɔɔɔ? Taa ŋáaǹ maamaacib a-yindi puɔɔɔ?>.

eng: Then Ill tell them, Ive never known you. Get away from me, you people who do wrong.
local: Niinee ní ḿ dāá tùkù-bi yii: <Maa nyí-ni ki ká kpataaa. Sútímaan niin kí wàatī-mi, nimbi bikpitiŋaliibee dee!>».

eng: Everybody who hears these words of mine and puts them into practice is like a wise builder who built a house on bedrock.
local: «Míǹ ní un kamaa yaa gbìl̄ man bɔti tin m sòon̄ nee ní ki yaa kíǹ ki ŋáań mimmee, udaan náań nì uciŋfiti un máń u-diiku ki tɔ́kɔ́ǹ ditaŋkpal puee ní.

eng: The rain fell, the floods came, and the wind blew and beat against that house. It didnt fall because it was firmly set on bedrock.
local: Utaa dɔ́miń ki nìn̄, ní nnyiŋgbeen dɔ́miń ki léeǹ kudiiyee pu, ní kubuŋkpaaŋŋu jítì, ama kudiiyee kaa lítì, kun puee ku tɔ́kɔ́ ditaŋkpal pu.

eng: But everybody who hears these words of mine and doesnt put them into practice will be like a fool who built a house on sand.
local: Ama un kamaa di yaa gbìl̄ man bɔti tin m sòon̄ nee ní kaa ŋáań mimmee náań nì ugbaan un máń u-diiku ki tɔ́kɔ́ǹ dibul puee ní.

eng: The rain fell, the floods came, and the wind blew and beat against that house. It fell and was completely destroyed.
local: Utaa dɔ́miń ki nìn̄, ní nnyiŋgbeen dɔ́miń ki léeǹ kudiiyee pu, ní kubuŋkpaaŋŋu dɔ́miń ki jítì, ní ku lítì ki nàn̄ dúsi dusi.».

eng: When Jesus finished these words, the crowds were amazed at his teaching.
local: Yeesu nín sòon̄ ki dàkā samaayee ki dóòyee, ní u-dàkatam gbanti kɔ́-bi tikpil.

eng: because he was teaching them like someone with authority and not like their legal experts.
local: kun puee úǹ dàkatamee cáá mpɔn, kaa bí kii bimaradakaliibi yamee.

eng: Why do you see the splinter thats in your brothers or sisters eye, but dont notice the log in your own eye?.
local: Ba pu ní a wāĺ kimɔmbiki kin kā a-nabiki nimbilinee, ní kaa wāĺ didɔjal din kā sii a-ba yalinee?.

eng: How can you say to your brother or sister, Let me take the splinter out of your eye, when theres a log in your eye?.
local: Mana ní a ŋūǹ kí lì yii: <M-nabiki, yàntī ḿ lēē-si kimɔmbiki a-nimbilin,> ŋyunti ŋun didɔjal kā sii nimbilinee?.

eng: You deceive yourself! First take the log out of your eye, and then youll see clearly to take the splinter out of your brothers or sisters eye.
local: Mpoonnyoondaan, péé kí lēē didɔjal din kā sii a-ba nimbilinee kí wàatī, ní kí nín wāl̀ mmɔntiimin kí lēē kimɔmbiki kin kā a-nabiki yalinee.

eng: Dont give holy things to dogs, and dont throw your pearls in front of pigs. They will stomp on the pearls, then turn around and attack you.
local: «Ní taa dū tin bí circiree kí tīī iboo, yaa sá fám i làá fàtī ni-pu kí tɔ̀ntī-ni. Ní taa dū ni-beedimbiliŋ kí lúkú igbeeti, yaa sá fám bi làá tāā kí bīil̀.».

eng: Ask, and you will receive. Search, and you will find. Knock, and the door will be opened to you.
local: «Mèèmaan Unimbɔti, u làá tīī-ni. Nyàab̀maan, u làá yàntī ní ká. Fáámaan, u làá pìitī-ni kipunyɔkɔ.

eng: For everyone who asks, receives. Whoever seeks, finds. And to everyone who knocks, the door is opened.
local: Kun puee sii un yaa mēē Unimbɔti tibee, see ú tīī-si kudɔŋu, ní sii un yaa nyàab̀ tibee, see á ká kudɔŋu, sii un yaa tí yú ki fátée, see bí pìitī-si.

eng: Who among you will give your children a stone when they ask for bread?.
local: Ni-kansikin ubɔ jipɔɔn di yaa mēē-u kpɔnɔ, u kíikí kí dū ditaŋkpal kí tīī-uaaa?.

eng: Now when Jesus had come down from the mountain, large crowds followed him.
local: Yeesu kpákatìń dijool puee, ní kunipaau nóó-u.

eng: When Jesus heard this, he was impressed and said to the people following him, I say to you with all seriousness that even in Israel I havent found faith like this.
local: Yeesu nín gbìl̄ an nyɔɔbuŋŋee, ní u bākatì ki bí bin nóó-uee yii: «Ibaamɔn ní m sòoǹ nì ni, maa láá pée ká Isirayeel yab kansikin ubɔ cáá fookii kii Uroomja nee.

eng: I say to you that there are many who will come from east and west and sit down to eat with Abraham and Isaac and Jacob in the kingdom of heaven.
local: Míǹ ní m tùkù-ni yii binib tikpil làá dāá nyānní ŋwìiń nyalaŋki pú nì ŋu-jeetilaŋki pú kí kɔ́ Unimbɔti Beelin, bi nì Abraam nì Isaaki nì Jaakɔb ń kpāfì kí jī nnaam!.

eng: But the children of the kingdom will be thrown outside into the darkness. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: Ama ní Isirayeel yab bin bàà máaǹ kí nīn sá Unimbɔti Beelin yabee, bí dāá nyāntiń-bi mmɔŋki kí tɔ̄ dibɔmbɔndin, laakin ní bi làá nīn sūǹ bi-nyiŋŋi gà tɔb kàkakee.».

eng: Jesus said to the centurion, Go; it will be done for you just as you have believed. And his servant was healed that very moment.
local: Ní Yeesu tùkū usoojakpilee yii: «Nín̄ kùǹ a-dumpu, a nín fōō ki kíí puee, án ŋá míǹ kí tīī-si.» Kukúluu gbanti poon ní utɔntɔnlee kpáàa pɔ́ɔkì.

eng: Jesus went home with Peter and saw Peters mother-in-law lying in bed with a fever.
local: Yeesu kɔ́ Peetroo dumpuee ní ki ká Peetroo nimpuu na būǹ ki dɔ̄ u-wunti píĺ kpaŋkpam.

eng: He touched her hand, and the fever left her. Then she got up and served them.
local: Niinee ní Yeesu gbēē u-ŋal, ní u pɔ́ɔkì tiwun sɔ̄ŋkì ní u yíkì ki ŋá-u tijin u jíń.

eng: That evening people brought to Jesus many who were demon-possessed. He threw the spirits out with just a word. He healed everyone who was sick.
local: Kujoou jóotèe ní bi cáań-u binib bin pu arasiniibi kēe tikpil, ní u sòon̄ ki jìn̄ arasiniibee. U nìn cáaǹ bibunliibi kɔkɔ.

eng: This happened so that what Isaiah the prophet said would be fulfilled: He is the one who took our illnesses and carried away our diseases.
local: U ŋá míǹ ki nóó nì Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu Yeesaya nín nìn lī puee, yii: «U fōō ti-wɔfii nì ti-weeŋyi.».

eng: Now when Jesus saw the crowd, he ordered his disciples to go over to the other side of the lake.
local: Yeesu nín ká yii kunipaau māntì ki tú-uee, ní u pú u-boonnooliibi kinyɔkɔ yii bí kɔ́ buŋalimbu kí dùtī kí cù lapu pu mɔɔgbandi.

eng: A legal expert came and said to him, Teacher, Ill follow you wherever you go.
local: Niinee ní umaradakal ubɔ sútìń ki pìl̄-u ní ki bí yii: «Ukpil, m làá nīn nóó-si báà laakin ní a yaa cée.».

eng: A man with a skin disease came, kneeled before him, and said, Lord, if you want, you can make me clean.
local: Ní unyaŋgbanti ubɔ sútìń ki pìl̄-u, ki gbāaǹ u-nimbiin ki bí-u yii: «Ukpil, cáaǹ-mi kun puee m nyí yii a yaa là, a ŋūǹ kí cáaǹ-mi m-wunti ń ŋāŋkì.».

eng: Jesus replied, Foxes have dens, and the birds in the sky have nests, but the Human One has no place to lay his head.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-u yii: «Jiiŋkpaaibi cáá bi-kɔŋkooŋyi ki dɔ̀, ní nnɔmbiyaamu mun cáá mu-diiŋŋi, ama man Kinibiki kaa cáá laakin m làá bíĺ m-yil kí ŋùŋfèe.».

eng: Another man, one of his disciples, said to him, Lord, first let me go and bury my father.
local: Ní bin nìn nóó-uee kansikin ubɔ bí-u yii: «Ukpil, pɔ̄-mi nsan ḿ síb́ m-baa.».

eng: But Jesus said to him, Follow me, and let the dead bury their own dead.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-u yii: «Sii dee, nóó man, kí yàntī bitaŋkpiibi ń nīn síb̀ bi-taŋkpiibi.».

eng: When Jesus got into a boat, his disciples followed him.
local: Niinee ní Yeesu kɔ́ buŋalimbun ní u-boonnooliibi mun nóó-u ki búntì.

eng: A huge storm arose on the lake so that waves were sloshing over the boat. But Jesus was asleep.
local: Kpalaayee kubuŋkpaaŋŋu kubɔ kíĺ ki bíi jītí dimɔɔl pu ní buŋalimbuee bíi gbéè nnyim, ama ní Yeesu dɔ̄ ki gèeǹ.

eng: They came and woke him, saying, Lord, rescue us! Were going to drown.
local: Ní u-boonnooliibi sútì ki pìl̄-u ní ki fīntì-u ki bí-u yii: «Ti-Dindaan, fīī-ti, ti kpíì ní!».

eng: He said to them, Why are you afraid, you people of weak faith? Then he got up and gave orders to the winds and the lake, and there was a great calm.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-bi yii: «Ba ŋá ní ni fàŋkí míǹ? Ni-fookiiu kaa náaǹ báà fiii!» U lī míǹ ki dóòyee, ní ki yíkì ki yúl̀ ki kpātì kubuŋu nì nnyim pu, ní an ŋmíl̀ fɔ́ŋfɔŋ díkí diki.

eng: The people were amazed and said, What kind of person is this? Even the winds and the lake obey him.
local: Ní binibee bākatì sèeee, ní ki bíi lì yii: «Kin, u nín sá ŋma dee ní ki kpáaǹ kubuŋu nì dimɔɔl tɔ́-u nan?».

eng: When Jesus arrived on the other side of the lake in the country of the Gadarenes, two men who were demon-possessed came from among the tombs to meet him. They were so violent that nobody could travel on that road.
local: Yeesu nín dūtì lapu mɔɔgbandi Gadaraa yab tiŋkinee, ní binib bili bin pu arasiniibi kā bi wáatèe nyánní kuceeun ki túŋkiń-u. Biwaatiibee nìn cáá ntoom tikpil, an puee ubɔ kaa nìn màkantí kí dī niin.

eng: They cried out, What are you going to do with us, Son of God? Have you come to torture us before the time of judgment.
local: Bi nìn bíi kūuntí yii: «Unimbɔti Jipɔɔn, a nyàab̀-ti nì tilati poon ní? Ibɔsoon yuntiŋu kaa láá bāań ní a dɔ́miń doo kí sàaǹ-taaa?».

eng: Jesus reached out his hand and touched him, saying, I do want to. Become clean. Instantly his skin disease was cleansed.
local: Niinee ní Yeesu tāntì u-ŋal ki gbēē-u, ní ki bí yii: «M là. Pɔ̄ɔkìm̄!» Niin niin ní uninjee pɔ́ɔkì ní u-wunti ŋáŋkì.

eng: Far off in the distance a large herd of pigs was feeding.
local: Digbeetikitikpaandi diba nìn bí ŋkpaanceeti niin ki jīǹ.

eng: The demons pleaded with him, If you throw us out, send us into the herd of pigs.
local: Ní arasiniibee gbāam̀-u yii: «A yaa làá jà-tee, á jà-ti kí kɔ́ǹ digbeetikitil nee ni».

eng: Then he said to the demons, Go away, and they came out and went into the pigs. The whole herd rushed down the cliff into the lake and drowned.
local: Ní u bí yii: «Nín̄ cámaan!» Ní bi nyáń biwaatiibin ki kɔ́ digbeetikitilee ni, ní igbeetee sáǹ ki wúntì nsitimin ki jéetì dimɔɔlin ki gbíntì nnyimin ki kpíì.

eng: Those who tended the pigs ran into the city and told everything that had happened to the demon-possessed men.
local: Ní bin kpàá igbeetee sáǹ ki cūtì ki tùkū kitiŋin tin kɔkɔ jítèe ki kpáaǹ bin pu arasiniibi nìn kēe mun yati.

eng: Then the whole city came out and met Jesus. When they saw him, they pleaded with him to leave their region.
local: Ní kitiŋin nib kɔkɔ nyáń ki nín túŋkí Yeesu. Bi nín ká-uee, ní bi gbāam̀-u yii ú yīkì bi-tiŋki pu.

eng: Jesus said to him, Dont say anything to anyone. Instead, go and show yourself to the priest and offer the gift that Moses commanded. This will be a testimony to them.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-u yii: «Pīlifì tiŋan, á taa tùkù báà ubɔ tibɔti nee, ama á cáá a-ba kí dàkà usaraaŋal ú bée yii a-wunti ŋáŋkì, ní kí ŋá saraa kii Mooyiisi marab nín lī puee ní usaraaŋal ń tùkù báà ŋma ń gbìl̄ yii a pɔ́ɔkì.».

eng: When Jesus went to Capernaum, a centurion approached.
local: Yeesu kɔ́ Kapeernayum tiŋkinee, ní Room soojakpiliu ubɔ sútìń ki pìl̄-u ki gbāam̀-u yii.

eng: pleading with him, Lord, my servant is flat on his back at home, paralyzed, and his suffering is awful.
local: «Ukpil, m-tɔntɔnliu dɔ̄ kudiin ki būǹ, ki fàl̄ ki yáliŋkí tikpil.».

eng: Jesus responded, Ill come and heal him.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-u yii: «M làá cù kí cáaǹ-u.».

eng: But the centurion replied, Lord, I dont deserve to have you come under my roof. Just say the word and my servant will be healed.
local: Ní usoojakpilee bí-u yii: «Ukpil, maa máaǹ á kɔ́ m-dumpu. Pée lì dinyɔɔbundi dibaba m-tɔntɔnliu làá pɔ̄ɔkì.

eng: Im a man under authority, with soldiers under me. I say to one, Go, and he goes, and to another, Come, and he comes. I say to my servant, Do this, and the servant does it.
local: Di sá yii man m-ba, m cáá bikpilib bin taapu m bée, ní ki mun cáá soojab bin mun bí man taapuee. M ŋūǹ kí tùkù ubɔ yii: <Cù doo!> ní ú cù, ní kí bí ubɔ mun yii: <Dāan̄ doo!> ní ú dāan̄, ní kí bí m-tɔntɔnliu una yii: <Ŋá yéè!> ní ú ŋá.».

eng: Boarding a boat, Jesus crossed to the other side of the lake and went to his own city.
local: Niinee ní Yeesu kɔ́ buŋalimbun ki fātìi dūtì dimɔɔl ki nín gítiń u-tiŋkin.

eng: As Jesus sat down to eat in Matthews house, many tax collectors and sinners joined Jesus and his disciples at the table.
local: Yeesu kɔ́ ki kāl̀ Maatiiu dumpu ki bíi jīǹ tijinee ní bifɔtiliibi tikpil nì binikpitib bin kɔ kíǹee dɔ́miń ki kútì u nì u-boonnooliibi pu ki mun bíi jīǹ.

eng: But when the Pharisees saw this, they said to his disciples, Why does your teacher eat with tax collectors and sinners.
local: Fariisab ká mimmee, ní ki bí u-boonnooliibi yii: «Ba pu ní ni-kpiliu nì bifɔtiliibi nì binikpitib bin kɔ kíǹee kpáfì ki kā ki jīǹ tijin míǹ?».

eng: When Jesus heard it, he said, Healthy people dont need a doctor, but sick people do.
local: Yeesu gbìl̄ an bɔtee, ní ki bí-bi yii: «Bin pɔ̄ɔ̄yee kaa nyàab̀ unyɔɔdaan, see bin būǹee.

eng: Go and learn what this means: I want mercy and not sacrifice . I didnt come to call righteous people, but sinners.
local: Cùú bīfìmaan Unimbɔti nyɔɔbundi nee taapu, yii: <Maa là saraabi, m là ní nīn sūǹ tɔb saai ní.> Maa dɔ́miń kí yíiń deedeedambi, m dɔ́miń kí yíiń binikpitib ní.».

eng: At that time Johns disciples came and asked Jesus, Why do we and the Pharisees frequently fast, but your disciples never fast.
local: Niinee ní Yoowaneesi boonnooliibi sútìń ki pìl̄ Yeesu, ní ki bālifì-u yii: «Ba pu ní timbi nì Fariisab bōob̀ kinyɔkɔ múlmul, ní sii boonnooliibi kaa bōob̀ ní?».

eng: Jesus responded, The wedding guests cant mourn while the groom is still with them, can they? But the days will come when the groom will be taken away from them, and then theyll fast.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-bi yii: «Unimpucaan cal bɔɔbi ŋūǹ kí nīn bí kunimbaaun ŋyunti ŋun unimpucaan cal u-ba dáá béeee? Aayee, baa ŋūǹ! Ama ŋyunti ŋubɔ bí ki dòoń, bi làá dāá yòon̄-u bi-kansikin, an yuntiŋu ní bi dāa bōob̀ kinyɔkɔ.

eng: No one sews a piece of new, unshrunk cloth on old clothes because the patch tears away the cloth and makes a worse tear.
local: «Baa gífí ŋŋaapɔɔn kí dūú lìb̄ dibɔkutikpikil. Bi yaa ŋá mimmee, ŋŋaapɔɔn kpāantí dibɔkutikpikil ní kí cátí ní dibùndee ń mɔ̀tīí wàan̄.
