eng: No one pours new wine into old wineskins. If they did, the wineskins would burst, the wine would spill, and the wineskins would be ruined. Instead, people pour new wine into new wineskins so that both are kept safe.
local: Míǹ ní baa tí dù ndapɔm kí fúuń tilɔkpikilin. Bi yaa ŋá mimmee, tilɔti pòol̄ ní ndaam ń kpáatíí fá ní tilɔti mun ń bīil̀. Míǹ puee, bi dù ndapɔm ní kí sìtī tilɔpɔnni, ní an kɔkɔ ń nīn bí.».

eng: While Jesus was speaking to them, a ruler came and knelt in front of him, saying, My daughter has just died. But come and place your hand on her, and shell live.
local: Yeesu nìn bí ki sòoǹ nì bi an bɔtee, ní Juuda yab yidaaŋwu ubɔ bāań ki gbāaǹ u-nimbiin, ní ki bí yii: «M-sapɔmbiki dáá pée kúù dee, dāan̄ kí dū a-ŋal kí páań ki-pu, kì làá fìkitī!».

eng: So Jesus and his disciples got up and went with him.
local: Ní Yeesu yíkì ní u-boonnooliibi nóó-u.

eng: People brought to him a man who was paralyzed, lying on a cot. When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the man who was paralyzed, Be encouraged, my child, your sins are forgiven.
local: Binib biba nìn túǹní ufaliŋfaan ubɔ nlún pu ki cáań ki bíl̀-u. Yeesu nín ká binib gbanti fōō ki kíí yii u ŋūǹ kí cáaǹ ubunlee ní u bí ufaliŋfaanee yii: «M-biki, pɔ́ɔǹ a-ba, m dū a-kpitii ki pú-si!».

eng: Then a woman who had been bleeding for twelve years came up behind Jesus and touched the hem of his clothes.
local: Ní unimpu ubɔ nìn bí ki fùul̀ ŋkpin doooo abin saalaa nì ali an kaa gúǹ. Yeesu còom̄ ki cée, ní u dīń Yeesu boon pú ki dɔ́miń ki gbēē u-bɔkutijúti.

eng: She thought, If I only touch his robe Ill be healed.
local: Kun puee u nìn dākafì u-pɔbilin yii: «M yaa pée ŋúń ki gbēē u-bɔkutil kpaŋkpamee, m bàà pɔ́ɔkí.».

eng: When Jesus turned and saw her, he said, Be encouraged, daughter. Your faith has healed you. And the woman was healed from that time on.
local: Yeesu fātì ki ká-u ní ki bí yii: «M-bisal, pɔ́ɔǹ a-ba, a nín fōō ki kíí puee fīī-si.» Niin niin ní unimpuee pɔ́ɔkì.

eng: When Jesus went into the rulers house, he saw the flute players and the distressed crowd.
local: Yeesu dūu bàn̄ uyidaanee dumpuee ki ká bin búǹ awul ki gà ikpiisilanee nì kunipaau kun bíi gà dituweelee gbí niin kìlammm.

eng: He said, Go away, because the little girl isnt dead but is asleep; but they laughed at him.
local: Niinee ní u bí-bi yii: «Nyāmaan doo! Kisapɔmbiki kaa kpíì, kì gèeǹ ní.» Ní bi bíi bèel̄-u.

eng: After he had sent the crowd away, Jesus went in and touched her hand, and the little girl rose up.
local: Bi nìn nyántì binib kɔkɔ, ní Yeesu nín kóò kudii poon ní ki cúú kisapɔmbikee ŋal ní kì yíkì.

eng: News about this spread throughout that whole region.
local: An bɔtee nìn dūu gbītì kitiŋ gbanti jaŋŋi kɔkɔn.

eng: As Jesus departed, two blind men followed him, crying out, Show us mercy, Son of David.
local: Yeesu nyáń niin ki búntèe, ní bijɔfub bili páaǹ u-boon ki tátí yii: «Daafiidi naantiiu, sɔ̄ ti-saai!».

eng: When he came into the house, the blind men approached him. Jesus said to them, Do you believe I can do this? Yes, Lord, they replied.
local: Yeesu bàn̄ kudiinee, ní bijɔfubee sútì ki pìl̄-u, ní u bālifì-bi yii: «Ni fōō ki kíí yii m lafun ŋūǹ kí cáaǹ-naaa?» Ní bi kíí-u yii: «Iiin, Ukpil, a ŋūǹ kí cáaǹ-ti.».

eng: Then Jesus touched their eyes and said, It will happen for you just as you have believed.
local: Ní Yeesu gbēē bi-nimbiliŋ ki bí-bi yii: «Ni nín fōō ki kíí yii m ŋūǹ kí cáaǹ-nee, án ŋá míǹ kí tīī-ni.».

eng: Some legal experts said among themselves, This man is insulting God.
local: Niinee ní bimaradakaliibi biba bíi dàkafī bi-pɔbiliŋin yii: «Kijapaai nee ŋèeǹ nì Unimbɔti ní.».

eng: Their eyes were opened. Then Jesus sternly warned them, Make sure nobody knows about this.
local: Niinee ní bi-nimbiliŋ cúu ŋúbitì. Ní Yeesu kpáafì-bi yii: «Ní pīlifì tiŋan, ní taa pée yàntī ubɔ ń béé tibɔti nee!».

eng: But they went out and spread the word about him throughout that whole region.
local: Ama bi nyánnée ní ki kíĺ ki bíi sòoǹ Yeesu pu kitiŋ gbanti kɔkɔn.

eng: As they were leaving, people brought to him a man who was demon-possessed and unable to speak.
local: Bi nyáń niin ki cée ní bi cáań uninja ubɔ Yeesu cee, arasinii kā u-pu ní u sá umɔɔmiin.

eng: When Jesus had thrown out the demon, the man who couldnt speak began to talk. The crowds were amazed and said, Nothing like this has ever been seen in Israel.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun Yeesu jìn̄ arasiniiyee, ní umɔɔmiinee kíĺ ki bíi sòoǹ, ní kunipaau kun nìn bí niinee bākatì sèeee ní ki lī yii: «Doooo nee, taa láá pée ká yéè Isirayeel tiŋkin ki ká!».

eng: But the Pharisees said, He throws out demons with the authority of the ruler of demons.
local: Ama ní Fariisab bíǹ bí yii: «An sá arasiniibi bɔtiu di tīī-u an pɔŋŋu ní u cáa jì arasiniibi.».

eng: Jesus traveled among all the cities and villages, teaching in their synagogues, announcing the good news of the kingdom, and healing every disease and every sickness.
local: Yeesu nìn yìiǹ ntiŋkpaamu nì ntiŋwaamu poon ki dàkà binib akpafidiinni, ki sòoǹ tibɔmɔntiil tin sá Unimbɔti Beel bɔtee, ki cāantí iween kɔkɔ nì bin kɔkɔ wɔ̀b̄ee.

eng: Now when Jesus saw the crowds, he had compassion for them because they were troubled and helpless, like sheep without a shepherd.
local: U nín nìn ká kunipaau gbantee ní an kɔ́-u inimɔɔn kun puee binibee nìn gīī bi-wunti yɔ̄tì a lì kii ipii in kaa cáá ukpaalee.

eng: Then he said to his disciples, The size of the harvest is bigger than you can imagine, but there are few workers.
local: Niinee ní u bí u-boonnooliibi yii: «Kudiceeu wīikí tikpil, ama ní bicaliibi kaa náaǹ.

eng: Therefore, plead with the Lord of the harvest to send out workers for his harvest.
local: An puee, ní mèèmaan kusaaudaan ú tɔ̄ń bitɔntɔnliibi ń dāá cā!».

eng: But Jesus knew what they were thinking and said, Why do you fill your minds with evil things?.
local: Yeesu béè tin bi bíi dàkafēe, ní ki bālifì-bi yii: «Ba pu ní ni cáá ilandɔkɔkpiti míǹ?.

eng: Which is easier—to say, Your sins are forgiven, or to say, Get up and walk?.
local: Ba di pɔ̄ɔ̄ dilil ní: <A ká a-kpitii pɔ̄tam> di pɔ̄ɔ̄ dilil naaa, <Yīkì kí yūĺ kí nīn còom̄> di pɔ̄ɔ̄ ní?.

eng: But so you will know that the Human One has authority on the earth to forgive sins—he said to the man who was paralyzed—Get up, take your cot, and go home.
local: Ama ní béé yii, man Kinibiki cáá mpɔn kitiŋ pu kí dū unil kpitii kí pɔ̄-u.» Niinee ní u bí ufaliŋfaanee yii: «Yīkìm̄ kí yòon̄ a-lúŋŋu kí nīn kùǹ a-dumpu.».

eng: The man got up and went home.
local: Ní kijapaayee nyīī ki yíkì ki nín kùǹ.

eng: When the crowds saw what had happened, they were afraid and praised God, who had given such authority to human beings.
local: Kunipaauee nín ká mimmee, ní tifaandi kɔ́-bi, ní bi bíi pàkà Unimbɔti u nín pú binib mpɔn ŋuŋyee pu.

eng: As Jesus continued on from there, he saw a man named Matthew sitting at a kiosk for collecting taxes. He said to him, Follow me, and he got up and followed him.
local: Yeesu nyáń niin ki cée, ní ki ká uninja ubɔ bi yíì-u Maatiiu u kā fɔtiku diikun. Yeesu nìn bí-u yii: «Dāan̄ kí nīn nóó-mi!» Niinee ní u yíkì ki táá-u.

eng: The beginning of the good news about Jesus Christ, Gods Son.
local: Tibɔmɔntiil tin sòoǹ Yeesu Kriisitoo Unimbɔti Jipɔɔn puee kílilaŋki sèé.

eng: While he was coming up out of the water, Jesus saw heaven splitting open and the Spirit, like a dove, coming down on him.
local: Yoowaneesi nín fùl̄-u ki dóò u nyēeń nnyiminee, ní u wál̀ kutagbɔŋu pīitì ní Unimbɔti Fam kpákatìń kii dibeenanjilee ki tɔ́kɔǹ u-pu.

eng: And there was a voice from heaven: You are my Son, whom I dearly love; in you I find happiness.
local: ní u gbìl̄ Unimbɔti sòon̄ní kutaa pu ki lī yii: «Sii di sá m-Jipɔɔn un m néeń tikpilee! M-pɔbil fáatì-si.».

eng: At once the Spirit forced Jesus out into the wilderness.
local: Kpalaa niinee ní Unimbɔti Fam yāntì u cūtì kuteeun.

eng: He was in the wilderness for forty days, tempted by Satan. He was among the wild animals, and the angels took care of him.
local: U nìn ŋá kuteeun iwiin imuŋku ili ní Sitaan cɔ́ŋkì-u. U nì ipeel di nìn kíǹ ki bí, ní Unimbɔti tuuŋyi bí u-cee ki kèetī-u.

eng: After John was arrested, Jesus came into Galilee announcing Gods good news.
local: Bi nìn cúú Yoowaneesi ki pīiǹ kudii. Míǹ boonee ní Yeesu cūtì Galiilee ki kíĺ ki bíi sòoǹ Unimbɔti bɔmɔntiiti ki tùkù-bi yii.

eng: saying, Now is the time! Here comes Gods kingdom! Change your hearts and lives, and trust this good news.
local: «Ŋyunti bāań. Unimbɔti Beel sútì ki pìlīń-ni. Fámaan ni-kpitii kí fōōmaan tibɔmɔntiil nee kí kíí!».

eng: As Jesus passed alongside the Galilee Sea, he saw two brothers, Simon and Andrew, throwing fishing nets into the sea, for they were fishermen.
local: Ŋwiin ŋubɔ, Yeesu bíi kpàkaǹ Galiilee mɔɔlee ní ki ká bijɔncuuliibi bili, Siimɔn nì u-naal Andree bí ki tòó kulaaŋŋu dimɔɔlin.

eng: Come, follow me, he said, and Ill show you how to fish for people.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-bi yii: «Dāá nīn nóómaan-mi. Ni sá bi-jɔncuuliibi ní, ama m làá dū-ni kí kpántí bin nyàabìń binib ki cāabìń m-ceeyee.».

eng: Right away, they left their nets and followed him.
local: Ní bi yāntì bi-laanti dicilpu niin ki táá-u.

eng: After going a little farther, he saw James and John, Zebedees sons, in their boat repairing the fishing nets.
local: U cōom̀ ki cūtì ndan fiiiyee, ní ki gíti ká Seebeedee jipɔmbi Yakubu nì Yoowaneesi kā buŋalimbun ki ŋáŋkí tilaandi.

eng: happened just as it was written about in the prophecy of Isaiah: Look, I am sending my messenger before you . He will prepare your way.
local: Yeesaya Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu nìn ŋmàn̄ u-gbɔŋku poon ki síiǹ, yii: Unimbɔti bí yii: «M-tuuŋŋu sèé! M túnní-ŋu ŋú lìntīń a-nyɔkɔpu kí dāá ŋāŋkì-si nsan.

eng: At that very moment he called them. They followed him, leaving their father Zebedee in the boat with the hired workers.
local: Kpalaa niinee ní Yeesu yíiń-bi ní bi yāntì bi-baa Seebeedee nì bitɔntɔnliibi buŋalimbun ki táá-u.

eng: Jesus and his followers went into Capernaum. Immediately on the Sabbath Jesus entered the synagogue and started teaching.
local: Yeesu nì u-boonnooliibi cūtì Kapeernayum tiŋkin. Ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa bāańee, ní Yeesu kɔ́ kukpafidiin ki kíĺ ki bíi dàkà binib Unimbɔti bɔti.

eng: The people were amazed by his teaching, for he was teaching them with authority, not like the legal experts.
local: Binib bin nìn bíi pílifíǹ-uee nìn bākatì tikpil nì u-dàkatam. Kun puee u-dàkatam nìn cáá mpɔn kaa náań nì bimaradakaliibi yam.

eng: Suddenly, there in the synagogue, a person with an evil spirit screamed.
local: Kpalaa niinee, unil ubɔ un pu arasinii kēe téeń kukpafidiin niin yii.

eng: What have you to do with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are. You are the holy one from God.
local: «Yeesu Naasaareeti yɔu, a nyàab̀-ti nì tilati ní? A dɔ́miń kí kúntí-ti níii? M nyí un ní a sée! A sá circirdaan un nyánní Unimbɔti ceeyee ní!».

eng: Silence! Jesus said, speaking harshly to the demon. Come out of him.
local: Ní Yeesu kpātì arasiniiyee pu, yii: «Á ŋmīĺee kí nyā uninja nee ni!».

eng: The unclean spirit shook him and screamed, then it came out.
local: Ní arasinii yāntì uninjee wunti fáá dijinjikil ŋkpitim pu, ní u tīl̀ mbiil mpɔn, ní ki nyáń u-ni.

eng: Everyone was shaken and questioned among themselves, Whats this? A new teaching with authority! He even commands unclean spirits and they obey him.
local: Báà ŋma bākatì sèeee, ní ki bàlifī tɔb yii: «Ba bɔti ní ti nín ká na? Tibɔcan sèé! Nì mpɔn ní u pūǹ kinyɔkɔ ki kpáaǹ arasiniibi bí nyā, ní bi pílifíǹ-u!».

eng: Right away the news about him spread throughout the entire region of Galilee.
local: Ní Yeesu yindi gbītì kpalaa Galiilee jaŋŋi kɔkɔn.

eng: After leaving the synagogue, Jesus, James, and John went home with Simon and Andrew.
local: Bi nyáń kukpafidiin kpalaayee ní u nì Yakubu nì Yoowaneesi búntì Siimɔn nì Andree dumpu.

eng: a voice shouting in the wilderness: Prepare the way for the Lord; make his paths straight .
local: M-tuuŋŋuee di báakí kuteeun yii: Bɔ́ntímaan ti-Dindaan saŋŋu, kí tàntīmaan u-sàmbiyaamu kí cīití-u!».

eng: Simons mother-in-law was in bed, sick with a fever, and they told Jesus about her at once.
local: Siimɔn nimpuu na nìn būǹ ki dɔ̄ u-wunti píĺ kpaŋkpam. Bi bàn̄ kpalaayee ní bi tùkū Yeesu unimpuee bumbunti pu.

eng: He went to her, took her by the hand, and raised her up. The fever left her, and she served them.
local: ní u kɔ́ unimpuee cee ki cúú u-ŋal ki yáatì-u ki kāaǹ. Niin ní u-wunti sɔ̄ŋkì ní u pɔ́ɔkì, ní u yíkì ki kíĺ ki bíi ŋáań tijin kí tīī-bi.

eng: That evening, at sunset, people brought to Jesus those who were sick or demon-possessed.
local: Kujoou, ŋwìiń jéetèe, bi cūnnì Yeesu cee bibunliibi nì binib bin pu arasiniibi kēe kɔkɔ.

eng: The whole town gathered near the door.
local: ní kitiŋin yab kɔkɔ kóoǹ ki kpáfì kudii kun ni u nìn bée nyɔkɔpu.

eng: He healed many who were sick with all kinds of diseases, and he threw out many demons. But he didnt let the demons speak, because they recognized him.
local: Binib kupaau nìn būǹ igbaŋween nín bí pu i-ba i-bee ní u cáaǹ-bi, ní ki jìn̄ arasiniibi tikpil ki nyántì, kaa pú-bi kinyɔkɔ bí sòoǹ, kun puee bi nìn nyí un ní u sée.

eng: Early in the morning, well before sunrise, Jesus rose and went to a deserted place where he could be alone in prayer.
local: Kutaa wúntì ntaafaabɔmbɔminee Yeesu péé ki nyáń ki búntì ŋkpaanceeti kumooun kí mèè Unimbɔti.

eng: Simon and those with him tracked him down.
local: Siimɔn nì u-jutiibi nìn cūtìi bíi nyàab̀-u.

eng: When they found him, they told him, Everyones looking for you.
local: Bi ká-uee, ní ki bí-u yii: «Báà ŋma bíi nyàab̀-si.».

eng: He replied, Lets head in the other direction, to the nearby villages, so that I can preach there too. Thats why Ive come.
local: Ní u kíí-bi yii: «Tí cùmaan ntiŋgbamu mun kíǹee mun poon, ḿ sòoǹ nì bi mun Unimbɔti bɔti. Míǹ pu ní m dɔ́miń kitiŋ pu.».

eng: He traveled throughout Galilee, preaching in their synagogues and throwing out demons.
local: Ní u cūtì ki māntì Galiilee jaŋŋi kɔkɔ ki sòon̄ Unimbɔti bɔti akpafidiinni ní ki jìn̄ arasiniibi biwaatiibi pu.

eng: John the Baptist was in the wilderness calling for people to be baptized to show that they were changing their hearts and lives and wanted God to forgive their sins.
local: Ntuunee di sá un bi yíì Yoowaneesi u kóò kuteeun ki fùul̀ binib Unimbɔti nyimee. U nìn báakí ki tùkù binib yii: «Kpántímaan ni-bimbim kí yàntī bí fàl̀-ni nnyim, Unimbɔti ń dū ni-kpitii kí pɔ̄-ni.».

eng: A man with a skin disease approached Jesus, fell to his knees, and begged, If you want, you can make me clean.
local: Ŋwiin ŋubɔee, uninja ubɔ dɔ́miń Yeesu cee u-wunti píń dinyaŋgbandi. U gbāaǹ Yeesu nimbiin ní ki gbāam̀-u ki lī yii: «A yaa là, a ŋūǹ kí cáaǹ-mi m-wunti ń ŋāŋkì.».

eng: Incensed, Jesus reached out his hand, touched him, and said, I do want to. Be clean.
local: Ní Yeesu súń u-saai ki tāntì u-ŋal ki gbēē-u ní ki bí-u yii: «M là! Pɔ̄ɔkìm̄.».

eng: Instantly, the skin disease left him, and he was clean.
local: Niinee, dinyaŋgbandi kpáàa wáŋkì, ní u-wunti ŋáŋkì.

eng: Sternly, Jesus sent him away.
local: Yeesu ŋée, ní ki jìn̄-u ú fàtīí gítí malaa.

eng: saying, Dont say anything to anyone. Instead, go and show yourself to the priest and offer the sacrifice for your cleansing that Moses commanded. This will be a testimony to them.
local: ní ki kpáafì-u tiŋan ki lī yii: «Taa pée tùkù báà ubɔ tibɔti nee. Cáá a-ba kí dàkà usaraaŋal, kí ŋá saraa kii Mooyiisi marau nín lī puee kí dàkà yii a pɔ́ɔkì an nīn sá-bi seera.».

eng: Instead, he went out and started talking freely and spreading the news so that Jesus wasnt able to enter a town openly. He remained outside in deserted places, but people came to him from everywhere.
local: Nì min kɔkɔ bi kpáafì-uee, u nín pée búntèe, u kíĺ ki máafí báà ŋma tin jítì u-puee, ki yāntì tibɔti gbītì ilaan kɔkɔ. An puee, Yeesu kaa nìn gíti ŋūǹ kí kɔ́ kitiŋ poon binib kansikin. U nìn kíǹ ki bí ikpaanceeti ní binib nyēeń ajan kɔkɔ pu ki dòoń u-cee.

eng: Everyone in Judea and all the people of Jerusalem went out to the Jordan River and were being baptized by John as they confessed their sins.
local: Ní Juudee watil pú yab nì Jeerusaleem tiŋkin nib kɔkɔ nìn cá Yoowaneesi cee. Bi nìn bìitī bi-kpitii pu, ní u fùul̀-bi Unimbɔti nyim Jɔɔdan buŋŋun.

eng: John wore clothes made of camels hair, with a leather belt around his waist. He ate locusts and wild honey.
local: Yoowaneesi gbanti nìn pēē ugutaamu kuti bɔkutil ní ki gbìn̄ ŋgban dambalau. U nìn ŋmóò inaakitoon nì tisiil ní. U nìn báakí ki lì yii.

eng: He announced, One stronger than I am is coming after me. Im not even worthy to bend over and loosen the strap of his sandals.
local: «Ubɔ bí m-boon ki tóobiń ki cáá mpɔn ki jítiǹ man. Maa sàafì kí nīn sá u-yumbuu kí sɔ́ntí kí kútí u-naataaŋmiii gba.

eng: I baptize you with water, but he will baptize you with the Holy Spirit.
local: Nì nnyim ní m cáa fùul̀-ni, ama úǹ làá dāá dū Circir Fam ní kí fàl̀-ni.».

eng: About that time, Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee, and John baptized him in the Jordan River.
local: Ŋyunti gbantee, Yeesu nyánní Galiilee tiŋki kin bi yíì Naasaareetee, ki dɔ́miń Yoowaneesi fùl̄-u Jɔɔdan buŋŋun.

eng: Jesus left that place and went beyond the Jordan and into the region of Judea. Crowds gathered around him again and, as usual, he taught them.
local: Yeesu fātìi yíkì niin ki póotì Juudee watil ki dūtì Jɔɔdan buŋgbandi. Ní anikiti tí kóoǹní ki múkuǹ u-pu, ní u tí kíĺ ki bíi dàkà-bi Unimbɔti bɔti, kii u nín kpààa pée ŋáań puee.

eng: Inside the house, the disciples asked him again about this.
local: Yeesu nì u-boonnooliibi bàn̄ kudiinee, bi fātìi bālifì-u an bɔti pu.

eng: He said to them, Whoever divorces his wife and marries another commits adultery against her.
local: Ní u kíí-bi yii: «Un kamaa yaa jìn̄ u-nimpuu ki fātìi cáá upɔɔnee, udaan ŋá tilaakpaal Unimbɔti nimbiin ki yīntì nì u-nimpupeepeekaau.

eng: and if a wife divorces her husband and marries another, she commits adultery.
local: Míǹ mun mbaantiim, unimpu yaa yíkì u-cal cee ki cūtìi mɔ́ǹ uninjapɔɔnee, u mun ŋá tilaakpaal Unimbɔti nimbiin ki yīntì nì u-calkpiki.».

eng: People were bringing children to Jesus so that he would bless them. But the disciples scolded them.
local: Bi nìn cāabìń mbiyaamu Yeesu cee ú dū u-ŋalii kí páań mu-pu kí mèè kí tīī-mu, ní u-boonnooliibi bíi wòoǹ bin cāabìń-muee.

eng: When Jesus saw this, he grew angry and said to them, Allow the children to come to me. Dont forbid them, because Gods kingdom belongs to people like these children.
local: Yeesu ká mimmee, ní an kaa mɔ́ɔkiǹ-u, ní u bí-bi yii: «Yàntīmaan mbiyaamu ń dāan̄ m-cee kí taa pìim̄maan-mu nsan, kun puee Unimbɔti Beel sá ki tīī bin náań nì muee ní.

eng: I assure you that whoever doesnt welcome Gods kingdom like a child will never enter it.
local: Ibaamɔn ní m sòoǹ nì ni, un kamaa yaa kaa fōō Unimbɔti Beel bɔti kibiyaawaai yamee, kaa dāa kóò di-ni.».

eng: Then he hugged the children and blessed them.
local: Niinee ní u fōō mbiyaamuee ní ki dū u-ŋalii ki páaǹ mu-pu ní ki mēē Unimbɔti ú bìin̄ mu-boon.

eng: As Jesus continued down the road, a man ran up, knelt before him, and asked, Good Teacher, what must I do to obtain eternal life.
local: Yeesu yíkì ki tākaǹ nsan pu ki làá nīn cée, uninja ubɔ sáǹní ki dɔ́miń ki gbāaǹ u-nimbiin, ki bālifì-u yii: «Ukpimɔnti! M làá ŋá mana ní kí ká dimaŋfal din kaa cáá dikúntilee?».

eng: Jesus replied, Why do you call me good? No one is good except the one God.
local: Ní Yeesu fātìi kíí-u yii: «Ba pu ní a yíì-mi yii umɔnti? Unil báà ubɔ kaa bí ki sá umɔnti, see Unimbɔti baba.

eng: You know the commandments: Dont commit murder. Dont commit adultery. Dont steal. Dont give false testimony . Dont cheat. Honor your father and mother .
local: Sii a-ba nyí Unimbɔti marab, an lī yii: <Á taa kpɔ̀ unil, á taa ŋá tilaakpaal, á taa yɔ̀, á taa lēē nnyimɔn seerau, á taa ŋmàtiǹ ubɔ, á nīn tɔ́ a-baa nì a-na.>».

eng: Some Pharisees came and, trying to test him, they asked, Does the Law allow a man to divorce his wife.
local: Niinee, Fariisab sútìń ki pìl̄-u ki nyàab̀ kí bàkan̄-u tibɔti. Bi bālifì-u yii: «An pú nsan yii uninja ń jà u-nimpuuaaa?».

eng: Teacher, he responded, Ive kept all of these things since I was a boy.
local: Ní uninjee fātìi kíí-u yii: «Ukpil, doooo m-naacimpɔntin ní m pée ŋúb́ an marab.».

eng: Jesus looked at him carefully and loved him. He said, You are lacking one thing. Go, sell what you own, and give the money to the poor. Then you will have treasure in heaven. And come, follow me.
local: Niinee, Yeesu kpēē-u dímmm, ní uninjee bɔti fáatì-u, ní u lī-u yii: «Kuwambaantiiu di kíǹ-si á ŋá. Nín̄ cá kí nyāfì tin kɔkɔ a cááyee, kí dū animbil kí yàkatī bifalaadambi, ní kí tin ká tigaajati Unimbɔti cee. A yaa ŋá míǹ ki dóòyee, á dāan̄ kí nīn nóó-mi.».

eng: But the man was dismayed at this statement and went away saddened, because he had many possessions.
local: Kijapaayee gbìl̄ an nyɔɔbuŋŋee, u-nimbiin cúu bíil̀, ní u búntì nì kunimbaau, kun puee u nìn cáá tigaajawan tikpil.

eng: Looking around, Jesus said to his disciples, It will be very hard for the wealthy to enter Gods kingdom.
local: Niinee, Yeesu fātìi kpēē ki māntìi tú bin yú ki gɔ́ń-uee, ní ki bí u-boonnooliibi yii: «An dāa pɔ̄ɔ̄ ki tīī bigaajab bí kɔ́ Unimbɔti Beelineeee!».

eng: His words startled the disciples, so Jesus told them again, Children, its difficult to enter Gods kingdom!.
local: U-boonnooliibi bākatì nì an nyɔɔbuŋŋi gbanti. Ní Yeesu fātìi lī-bi yii: «M-biyaamu, an pɔ̄ɔ̄ ki tīī bin kɔ dū bi-mákal ki ŋá tigaajatinee ń kɔ́ Unimbɔti Beelineeee!.

eng: Its easier for a camel to squeeze through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter Gods kingdom.
local: An pɔ̀fì ki tīī ugutaamu ń jītí ujoo buŋŋun nì ugaaja ń kɔ́ Unimbɔti Beelin.».

eng: They were shocked even more and said to each other, Then who can be saved.
local: Niinee, u-boonnooliibi gíti bākatì tikpil, ki lì tɔb cee yii: «Kin, ŋma di nín ŋūǹ kí ŋmátí dee?».

eng: Jesus looked at them carefully and said, Its impossible with human beings, but not with God. All things are possible for God.
local: Ní Yeesu kpēē-bi ní ki bí yii: «Binib kaa ŋūǹ kí fīī bi-ba, asee Unimbɔti baba di ŋūǹ kí fīī-bi. Di sá yii Unimbɔti di ŋūǹ tikɔkɔ.».

eng: Peter said to him, Look, weve left everything and followed you.
local: Ní Peetroo bí-u yii: «Kin, timbi nín yíkì ki fá tin kɔkɔ ti cááyee ki táá-see?».

eng: Jesus said, I assure you that anyone who has left house, brothers, sisters, mother, father, children, or farms because of me and because of the good news.
local: Ní Yeesu fātìi kíí-u yii: «M sòoǹ nì ni ibaamɔn ní, un kamaa yaa yíkì ki cāntì u-diiku nì u-nabiyaamu nì u-na nì u-baa nì u-biyaamu nì u-kpaatanti man pu ní ki sòoǹ m-bɔmɔntiiti ki tìì binibee.

eng: Jesus answered, What did Moses command you.
local: Ní u kíí-bi yii: «Mara ulɔu ní Mooyiisi nìn tīī-ni ní?».

eng: will receive one hundred times as much now in this life—houses, brothers, sisters, mothers, children, and farms —and in the coming age, eternal life.
local: udaan làá fōō tipaal ŋyunti ŋun ti bí nee míǹ dilaataal pu. U làá fōō adiin nì nnabiyaamu nì nab, nì mbiyaamu nì tikpaatan, ní kí jī falaa mun. Ama ŋyunti ŋun bí ki dòońee poonee, udaan dāa fòol̄ dimaŋfal din kaa cáá dikúntilee.

eng: But many who are first will be last. And many who are last will be first.
local: Bisanlintiliibi tikpil dāa kpántí biboompaanliibi, ní biboompaanliibi mun tikpil ń kpántí bisanlintiliibi.».

eng: Jesus and his disciples were on the road, going up to Jerusalem, with Jesus in the lead. The disciples were amazed while the others following behind were afraid. Taking the Twelve aside again, he told them what was about to happen to him.
local: Yeesu nì u-boonnooliibi nìn tàkā nsan ki jɔ́ḿ Jeerusaleem, ní u līī bi-nyɔkɔpu. U-boonnooliibi pɔbiliŋ nìn bíil̀ ní tifaandi kɔ́ bin kɔkɔ nìn nóó-uee. Ní Yeesu gíti tí yíiń u-kpambaliŋ saalaa nì alee ki síiǹ u-cee, ní ki kíĺ ki bíi tùkù-bi tin di làá bàń u-puee, yii.

eng: Look! he said. Were going up to Jerusalem. The Human One will be handed over to the chief priests and the legal experts. They will condemn him to death and hand him over to the Gentiles.
local: «Ti jɔ́ḿ Jeerusaleem ní nee, ti yaa bàn̄ niinee, bi làá cúú man Kinibiki kí tīī bisaraaŋakpilib nì bimaradakaliibi ní bíǹ ń cáá-mi ibɔsoon kí lì yii m máaǹ bí kpɔ̀-mi, ní bí dū-mi kí ŋúbiǹ bin kaa nyí Unimbɔtee.

eng: They will ridicule him, spit on him, torture him, and kill him. After three days, he will rise up.
local: Bíǹ làá bèel̀-mi kí tíiĺ timɔtan kí sìtī m-pu, kí gbá-mi anaalab. Míǹ boonee ní bí kpɔ̀-mi. Bi yaa kūū-mee, ní ḿ fìkitī winta dal.».

eng: James and John, Zebedees sons, came to Jesus and said, Teacher, we want you to do for us whatever we ask.
local: Ŋyunti gbanti ní Seebeedee jipɔmbi Yakubu nì Yoowaneesi sútìń ki pìl̄ Yeesu ní ki bí-u yii: «Ukpil, ti là kí mèè-si tiba, á ŋá kí tīī-ti!».

eng: What do you want me to do for you? he asked.
local: Ní u fātìi kíí-bi yii: «Ni là ḿ ŋá ba ní kí tīī-ni?».

eng: They said, Allow one of us to sit on your right and the other on your left when you enter your glory.
local: Ní bi kíí-u yii: «Ti là yii, ŋyunti ŋun a yaa láa kpántì ubɔtee, ti-kansikin ubɔ ń dāá nīn kā a-ŋaŋgiil pú, un kíǹee a-ŋaŋgan pú.».

eng: Jesus replied, You dont know what youre asking! Can you drink the cup I drink or receive the baptism I receive.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-bi yii: «Naa nyí tin ni mèéyee. Ni ŋūǹ kí nyɔ̀ falaa un m làá nyɔ̀ naaa? Ni ŋūǹ kí fàl̀ falaa kii un man làá fàl̀ naaa?».

eng: We can, they answered. Jesus said, You will drink the cup I drink and receive the baptism I receive.
local: Ní bi kíí-u yii: «Iiin, ti ŋūǹ.» Ní Yeesu bí-bi yii: «Ibaamɔn, ni làá nyɔ̀ falaa un m làá nyɔ̀ee, ní kí fàl̀-u kii m nín làá fàl̀-u pu nee.

eng: They said, Moses allowed a man to write a divorce certificate and to divorce his wife.
local: Ní bi fātìi kíí-u yii: «Mooyiisi bí yii uninja ń ŋmà kugbɔŋu kun dàkà kun pu ní u jìn̄ u-nimpuuee kí ŋúbiǹ-u ní kí nín jà-u.».

eng: but to sit at my right or left hand isnt mine to give. It belongs to those for whom it has been prepared.
local: Ama an yaa kíǹ m-ŋaŋgiil nì m-ŋaŋgan kakaaŋuee, an kaa sá man di pūǹ an paaŋyi. Ipaan gbanti sá ki tīī bin pu Unimbɔti bɔ́ntì-i ki síiǹee ní.».

eng: Now when the other ten disciples heard about this, they became angry with James and John.
local: Akpambal saalaa ŋin kíǹee gbìl̄ mimmee, ní ki fōō diŋuul Yakubu nì Yoowaneesi pu.

eng: Jesus called them over and said, You know that the ones who are considered the rulers by the Gentiles show off their authority over them and their high-ranking officials order them around.
local: Ní Yeesu yíiń bi-kɔkɔ u-cee, ní ki bí-bi yii: «Ni nyí yii duulinya yidambi bin kpèé ntimuee tɔ́kɔ́ bin kíǹee pu, ní ki cáá bi-pɔŋyi ki fàantí-bi.

eng: But thats not the way it will be with you. Whoever wants to be great among you will be your servant.
local: An kaa máaǹ kí nīn bí míǹ nimbi kansikin, ama un yaa là kí nīn sá ukpaan ni-kansikinee, udaan ń gíiǹ u-ba ni-tɔntɔnliu.

eng: Whoever wants to be first among you will be the slave of all.
local: Míǹ mun ní un kamaa yaa là kí nīn sá ukpaan ni-kansikinee, udaan ń gíiǹ u-ba ni-kɔkɔ yumbuu.

eng: for the Human One didnt come to be served but rather to serve and to give his life to liberate many people.
local: Kun puee man Kinibiki kaa dɔ́miń bí ŋá tiba kí tīī-mi, m dɔ́miń kí tɔ̄ atuun kí tīī binib, ní kí dū m-maŋfal kí pà kí fōō binib tikpil kí líí.».

eng: Jesus and his followers came into Jericho. As Jesus was leaving Jericho, together with his disciples and a sizable crowd, a blind beggar named Bartimaeus, Timaeus son, was sitting beside the road.
local: Yeesu nì u-boonnooliibi nì kunipaau nìn bàn̄ Jeeriikoo. Bi yíkì Jeeriikoo kí nīn càatēe, ujɔfu ubɔ kā disaŋkaŋkpil ki mèé bisanjitiliibi animbil. Bi nìn yíì ujɔfu gbanti Baatiimee ní ki yíì u-baa Tiimee.

eng: When he heard that Jesus of Nazareth was there, he began to shout, Jesus, Son of David, show me mercy.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun u béè yii an sá Yeesu Naasaareeti yɔu di jītée, ní u kíĺ ki bíi tátí mpɔn yii: «Yeesu Daafiidi naantiiu, sɔ̄ m-saai!».

eng: Many scolded him, telling him to be quiet, but he shouted even louder, Son of David, show me mercy.
local: Binib tikpil nìn bí ki wòoǹ-u yii ú ŋmīĺ, ní u mɔ̀tīi tátí mpɔn yii: «Daafiidi naantiiu, sɔ̄ m-saai!».

eng: Jesus stopped and said, Call him forward. They called the blind man, Be encouraged! Get up! Hes calling you.
local: Yeesu sāā ki síl̀, ní ki bí yii: «Yíiḿmaan-u!» Ní bi yíiń ujɔfuee ní ki bí-u yii: «Pɔ́ɔǹ a-ba kí yīkì, u yíì-si!».

eng: Jesus said to them, He wrote this commandment for you because of your unyielding hearts.
local: Niinee ní Yeesu fātìi kíí-bi yii: «An sá ni-pɔɔgbifiŋŋu pu ní u síiǹ-ni an marau.

eng: Throwing his coat to the side, he jumped up and came to Jesus.
local: Niinee ní ujɔfuee lēē u-satau ki lúkú, ki nyīī ki yíkì kùrub ki síl̀ ní ki nín cá Yeesu cee.

eng: Jesus asked him, What do you want me to do for you? The blind man said, Teacher, I want to see.
local: Ní Yeesu bālifì-u yii: «Ba ní a là ḿ ŋá kí tīī-si ní?» Ní ujɔfuee fātìi kíí-u yii: «Ukpil, m là kí wāl̀ ní!».

eng: Jesus said, Go, your faith has healed you. At once he was able to see, and he began to follow Jesus on the way.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-u yii: «Nín̄ cá, a-fookiiu di fīī-si!» Kpalaa niinee, u-nimbiliŋ cúu ŋúbitì, ní u kíǹ ki táá Yeesu.

eng: At the beginning of creation, God made them male and female .
local: Ama ŋyunti ŋun Unimbɔti kíĺ ki náań tiwan kɔkɔee, u nìn náań uninja nì unimpu ní.

eng: Because of this, a man should leave his father and mother and be joined together with his wife.
local: Míǹ puee ní an sá uninja ń yīkì u-baa nì u-na cee ní u nì u-nimpuu ń nīn bí.

eng: and the two will be one flesh. So they are no longer two but one flesh.
local: ní bi-liitil ń kpántí unibaanti. Mimmee, baa ti sá binib bili, bi ŋá unibaanti dee.

eng: Therefore, humans must not pull apart what God has put together.
local: Míǹ puee, unil ń taa yàkatī tin Unimbɔti dūu kpáfèe.».

eng: When Jesus and his followers approached Jerusalem, they came to Bethphage and Bethany at the Mount of Olives. Jesus gave two disciples a task.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun bi cōom̀ ki tóob́ Jeerusaleem ki pìlīǹ Beetifagee nì Beetanii, Ɔliifi sufii jool caŋinee, ní Yeesu tú u-boonnooliibi kansikin bili.

eng: Blessings on the coming kingdom of our ancestor David! Hosanna in the highest.
local: Unimbɔti ń bìin̄ ti-naanja Daafiidi beel din dòońee pu! Oosaana! Dàntìmaan un bí yilpu yilpuee!».

eng: Jesus entered Jerusalem and went into the temple. After he looked around at everything, because it was already late in the evening, he returned to Bethany with the Twelve.
local: Yeesu bàn̄ Jeerusaleemee, ki kɔ́ kunimbɔtidiin. U kɔ́ee ní ki kpēē ki māntì ki tú. Ama kunyeeu nín nìn bíi móotée puee, ní u fātìi nyáń u nì u-kpambaliŋ saalaa nì alee nín cá Beetanii.

eng: The next day, after leaving Bethany, Jesus was hungry.
local: Kutaa wúntèe, Yeesu nì u-boonnooliibi nyáń Beetanii tiŋkin ki cée, ŋkùm cúú-u.

eng: From far away, he noticed a fig tree in leaf, so he went to see if he could find anything on it. When he came to it, he found nothing except leaves, since it wasnt the season for figs.
local: U kpēē ndanee, ki wál̀ busubu bun bi yíì fiigee sí ki cáá tifaal. Ní u cūtì bu-taapu kí kpèè u yaa bàà kāntí bu-biliŋ kí kìtī kí ŋmɔ́. U bàn̄ee, tifaal ŋmaniŋma di túkú. Kun puee an kaa nìn ŋmāntì ŋyunti ŋun busubuee lòoǹee.

eng: So he said to it, No one will ever again eat your fruit! His disciples heard this.
local: Niinee ní u lī busubuee yii: «Dalba dalba ubɔ ń taa láa gíti ŋmɔ́ sii biliŋ!» U nín sòon̄ mimmee, u-boonnooliibi gbìl̄.

eng: They came into Jerusalem. After entering the temple, he threw out those who were selling and buying there. He pushed over the tables used for currency exchange and the chairs of those who sold doves.
local: Bi búntèe, ki bàn̄ Jeerusaleem ki kɔ́ kunimbɔtidiin ní Yeesu kíĺ ki bíi jì biwannyafiliibi nì bidaliibi bin nìn bí niinee, ki lábití binimbikpantiliibi teebilib nì binanjinyafiliibi jaliŋ ki cìb̀.

eng: He didnt allow anyone to carry anything through the temple.
local: Waa pú mpaan ubɔ ń yòon̄ u-wanti kí còom̀ kí pùntī kunimbɔtidiin.

eng: He taught them, Hasnt it been written, My house will be called a house of prayer for all nations? But youve turned it into a hideout for crooks .
local: Ní u kíĺ ki bíi sòoǹ nì bi yii: «An kaa ŋmàn̄ ki síiǹ yii: M-diiku làá nīn sá ŋwimmeem diiku ní ki tīī atimbun kɔkɔɔɔ? Ama ní nimbi dū-ku ki kpántì kunaayudii.».

eng: The chief priests and legal experts heard this and tried to find a way to destroy him. They regarded him as dangerous because the whole crowd was enthralled at his teaching.
local: Bisaraaŋakpilib nì bimaradakaliibi gbìl̄ mimmee, ní ki bíi nyàab̀ nsan ŋun bi làá dī kí kpɔ̀-uee. Di sá yii bi nìn fàŋkí-u, kun puee tin u sòoǹee nìn kɔ́ samaa tikpil.

eng: When it was evening, Jesus and his disciples went outside the city.
local: Ku jóotèe ní Yeesu nì u-boonnooliibi nyáń kitiŋin.

eng: saying to them, Go into the village over there. As soon as you enter it, you will find tied up there a colt that no one has ridden. Untie it and bring it here.
local: ki tùkū-bi yii: «Cùmaan kitimbilii kin bíń ni-nyɔkɔpu nee. Ni làá ká dimampɔmbil din pu báà ubɔ kaa láá kāl̀ee. Ní gbìntīḿmaan-dì kí cáań-mi.

eng: Early in the morning, as Jesus and his disciples were walking along, they saw the fig tree withered from the root up.
local: Bi fàtīi gítiń kutaafaauee, ní ki ká fiigi subuee kūń ki kpáaǹ bu-nyaaŋyi.

eng: Peter remembered and said to Jesus, Rabbi, look how the fig tree you cursed has dried up.
local: Niinee, Peetroo téetì, ní ki bí Yeesu yii: «Ukpil, kpèè fiigi subu bun a fu tɔ́ mbusuee kūń!».

eng: Jesus responded to them, Have faith in God!.
local: Ní Yeesu fātìi kíí-bi yii: «Fōōmaan Unimbɔti kí kíí!.

eng: I assure you that whoever says to this mountain, Be lifted up and thrown into the sea—and doesnt waver but believes that what is said will really happen—it will happen.
local: Ibaamɔn ní m sòoǹ nì ni, ubɔ yaa lī dijool nee yii: Á yīkì laakin a yú nee kí cùú jēetí tiŋkun, ní ki yaa kaa dàkafī ki gùgufī u-pɔbilin, ama ní ki yaa máká yii u nín lī puee làá ŋá mimmee, asee án lafun ŋá míǹ kí tīī udaan.

eng: Therefore I say to you, whatever you pray and ask for, believe that you will receive it, and it will be so for you.
local: Míǹ pu ní m tùkù-ni yii, tin kɔkɔ ní ni yaa mēē Unimbɔtee, ní nīn máká yii ni fōō, ni làá lafun fōō kudɔŋu.

eng: And whenever you stand up to pray, if you have something against anyone, forgive so that your Father in heaven may forgive you your wrongdoings.
local: Ni yaa bíi mèé Unimbɔti ní ni nì ni-jutiibi kansikin kaa mɔ̄ee, dūú pɔ̄maan-bi, ní ni-Baa un bí yilpuee ń ŋmā kí dū ni mun yìntitam kí pɔ̄-ni.» .

eng: Jesus and his disciples entered Jerusalem again. As Jesus was walking around the temple, the chief priests, legal experts, and elders came to him.
local: Bi nìn tí fātìi gítì Jeerusaleem. Ŋyunti ŋun Yeesu kɔ́ ki bíi còom̄ kunimbɔtidiinee, bisaraaŋakpilib nì bimaradakaliibi nì bitindaŋkpilib dɔ́miń u-cee.

eng: They asked, What kind of authority do you have for doing these things? Who gave you this authority to do them.
local: ní ki bí-u yii: «Nì nsan ŋulɔŋu pu ní a fu jìn̄ binib kunimbɔtidiin ní? Ŋma di pú-si an saŋŋu ní?».

eng: Jesus said to them, I have a question for you. Give me an answer, then Ill tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Ní Yeesu fātìi kíí-bi yii: «Dinyɔɔbumbaantiil ní m làá bàlifì-ni, ní kíí-mi kí wàatī ní ḿ nín tùkù-ni nì nsan ŋun pu ní m ŋáań mimmee.

eng: If anyone says to you, Why are you doing this? say, Its master needs it, and he will send it back right away.
local: Ubɔ yaa bālifì-ni yii, Ba ní ni ŋáańee? Ní kíí-u yii, ti-Dindaan di nyàab̀ kimaŋ, u yaa dóòyee, u làá gíiǹní-kì doo an kaa làá yúntí.».

eng: Was Johns baptism of heavenly or of human origin? Answer me.
local: Yoowaneesi nín fùul̀ binib Unimbɔti nyimee, ŋma di tīī-u an pɔŋŋu ní? Unimbɔtaaa, binib? Kíímaan-mi!».

eng: They argued among themselves, If we say, Its of heavenly origin, hell say, Then why didnt you believe him.
local: Ní bi kíĺ ki bíi lɔ́kɔfí bi-ŋmaniŋma yii: «Ti yaa kíí-u yii: <Unimbɔti di tīī-u>, u làá bàlifì-ti yii: <Kin ba ŋá ní naa fōō u-bɔti ki kíí?>.

eng: But we cant say, Its of earthly origin. They said this because they were afraid of the crowd, because they all thought John was a prophet.
local: Mimmee, tí lì yii: <Binib di tīī-uaaa.>».

eng: They answered Jesus, We dont know. Jesus replied, Neither will I tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Niinee, ní bi kíí Yeesu yii: «Taa nyí.» Ní Yeesu mun fātìi kíí-bi yii: «Ma mun kaa làá tùkù-ni nì nsan ŋun pu ní m ŋáań mimmee!».

eng: They went and found a colt tied to a gate outside on the street, and they untied it.
local: Bi búntì ki bàn̄ ki ká dimampɔmbilee gbìn̄ ki nákáǹ kipunyɔkɔ, mmɔŋki didandi caŋin, ní bi bíi gbìntī-dì.

eng: Some people standing around said to them, What are you doing, untying the colt.
local: Bin nìn sí niinee biba bí-bi yii: «Ba di cáá-ni ní ni gbìntī kimaŋ?».

eng: They told them just what Jesus said, and they left them alone.
local: Ní bi kíí-bi kii Yeesu nín bàà tùkū-bi puee, ní binibee yāntì bi gbīntì-kì.

eng: They brought the colt to Jesus and threw their clothes upon it, and he sat on it.
local: Bi cáań dimampɔmbilee Yeesu cee ní ki pēetì bi-wampeeŋkaati ki páaǹ di-pu, ní Yeesu jɔ́m̀ ki kāl̀.

eng: Many people spread out their clothes on the road while others spread branches cut from the fields.
local: Binib tikpil nìn kpátí bi-wanti ki bīil̀ nsan poon. Ní biba mun gàń tifaal akpaanni ki bīil̀.

eng: Those in front of him and those following were shouting, Hosanna! Blessings on the one who comes in the name of the Lord!.
local: Bin nìn līī u-nyɔkɔpuee nì bin nìn pá u-boonee nìn kūuntí yii: «Oosaana! Unimbɔti ń bìin̄ un dòoń úǹ ti-Dindaan yindi puee!.

eng: Jesus spoke to them in parables. A man planted a vineyard, put a fence around it, dug a pit for the winepress, and built a tower. Then he rented it to tenant farmers and took a trip.
local: Niinee, Yeesu kíĺ ki cáá tibɔŋmantikaal ki sòoǹ, ní ki lī yii: «Uninja ubɔ di kùn̄ fiinyi sufii kpaabu. U cóoǹ kunaabakau ki māntì ki tú-bu, ní ki gbìn̄ mbòó ŋun ni u làá nīn cùkutī abil ki lèekī ndamamee, ní ki gāā isupaa, ki dū bukpaayee ki kpāaǹ bipaadambi, ní ki búntì nsan.

eng: Havent you read this scripture, The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone.
local: Ni pée máaǹ ki kàan̄ Unimbɔti gbɔŋkun dinyɔɔbundi din lì yii: <Ditaŋkpal din bidimaliibi nìn dūu fée, díǹ di dāa kpántì ditaŋkpal din yì kudii pɔŋŋuee.

eng: The Lord has done this, and its amazing in our eyes? .
local: Ti-Dindaan nín ŋá tiŋyee kaa gbí mmaam ti-ceeee?>».

eng: They wanted to arrest Jesus because they knew that he had told the parable against them. But they were afraid of the crowd, so they left him and went away.
local: Ŋyuntee, Yeesu nannanliibi bíi nyàab̀ kí cúú-u, ní ki gíti fàŋkí kunipaau. Di sá yii u-nannanliibee nìn béè yii bíǹ pu ní u nìn sòon̄ tibɔŋmantikaal gbanti. Ní bi yāntì-u ki búntì.

eng: They sent some of the Pharisees and supporters of Herod to trap him in his words.
local: Bi túnní Yeesu cee Fariisab nì Eeroodi naacimbi kansikin biba bí dāan̄ kí píĺ-u tibɔti.

eng: They came to him and said, Teacher, we know that youre genuine and you dont worry about what people think. You dont show favoritism but teach Gods way as it really is. Does the Law allow people to pay taxes to Caesar or not? Should we pay taxes or not.
local: Bi bāańee, ní ki bí-u yii: «Ukpil, ti nyí yii a sòoǹ ibaamɔn, kaa fàŋkí báà ubɔ, kaa fàtīi tɔ̄ báà udaan yaa bí puee, ki dàkà nnimbɔtisan nì ibaamɔn. Mimmee, ti-marau pú nsan yii tí pà lampoo kí tīī Room gɔminau Seesaaraaa, an kaa pú?».

eng: Since Jesus recognized their deceit, he said to them, Why are you testing me? Bring me a coin. Show it to me.
local: Yeesu béè bi-poonnyooŋŋu puee, ní ki bālifì-bi yii: «Ba pu ní ni píĺ-mi tibɔti míǹ kí kpèè m-nyɔkɔn? Cáaḿmaan-mi animbil ḿ kpèè!».

eng: And they brought one. He said to them, Whose image and inscription is this? Caesars, they replied.
local: Bi cáańee, ní u bālifì-bi yii: «Ŋma yindi nì u-yil di náká animbil pu na?» Ní bi kíí-u yii: «An sá Seesaar.».

eng: Jesus said to them, Give to Caesar what belongs to Caesar and to God what belongs to God. His reply left them overcome with wonder.
local: Niinee ní Yeesu fātìi kíí-bi yii: «Dūmaan tin Seesaar yèe kí tīī Seesaar kí dū tin Unimbɔti yèe kí tīī Unimbɔti!» Ní bi bākatì nì an nyɔɔbundi gbanti.

eng: Sadducees, who deny that there is a resurrection, came to Jesus and asked.
local: Ŋyuntee ní Sadusab dɔ́miń Yeesu cee. - Bíǹ di sá dikitil din lì yii difìkitil kpée. - Ní bi bí Yeesu yii.

eng: Teacher, Moses wrote for us that if a mans brother dies , leaving a widow but no children, the brother must marry the widow and raise up children for his brother.
local: «Ukpil, Mooyiisi ŋmàn̄ ki síiǹ-ti yii, unil yaa cáá kinabiki ní kinabikee yaa kpíì ki yāntì unimpu kaa cáá kibikee, ú fōō ukpiipu kí màal̀ kí téeǹ u-nabikee yil.

eng: When it was time, he sent a servant to collect from the tenants his share of the fruit of the vineyard.
local: Isufee biliŋ kìtitam bāańee, ní u túnní u-tɔntɔnliu ubɔ bipaadambee cee ú dāá fōō u-jandi.

eng: Now there were seven brothers. The first one married a woman; when he died, he left no children.
local: Kun pu ti bàlifēe di sá nnabiyaamu mululi di nìn bí. Upeepeekaa cáá unimpu ní ki kpíì ki yāntì unimpuee, kaa màl̄.

eng: The second married her and died without leaving any children. The third did the same.
local: Uliliiti mun fōō ukpiipuee ní ki mun kpíì, kaa màl̄. Utataati mun míǹ.

eng: None of the seven left any children. Finally, the woman died.
local: ki tin sāā bilulee kɔkɔ cáá-u ki kpíì, kaa ká kibiki. Bíǹ kɔkɔ kúm boonee, unimpuee mun dāa dūu kpíì.

eng: At the resurrection, when they all rise up, whose wife will she be? All seven were married to her.
local: Míǹ puee, difìkitil dalee, bi-kɔkɔ yaa láa fīkitèe, bi-kansikin ŋma di dāa yì unimpuee? Kun puee bilulee kɔkɔ cáá-u ki ká.».

eng: Jesus said to them, Isnt this the reason you are wrong, because you dont know either the scriptures or Gods power?.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-bi yii: «Kun pu ni bí diyìntil poonee di sá naa nyí Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon bɔti, kaa nyí Unimbɔti pɔŋŋu mun.

eng: When people rise from the dead, they wont marry nor will they be given in marriage. Instead, they will be like Gods angels.
local: Difìkitil yuntiŋuee, dinimpucaal nì dicamɔndi dāa kpá. Báà ŋma dāa bí kii Unimbɔti tuuŋyi nín bí pu yilpuee ní.

eng: As for the resurrection from the dead, havent you read in the scroll from Moses, in the passage about the burning bush, how God said to Moses, I am the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob?.
local: An yaa kíǹ bitaŋkpiibi fìkititamee, naa kàan̄ Mooyiisi nín ŋmàn̄ pu u-gbɔŋkuneeee? Laakin sòoǹ tin jítì kikuŋkumbiki caŋinee, Unimbɔti nìn lī-u yii: <Man di sá Abraam Nimbɔtiu, Isaaki Nimbɔtiu nì Jaakɔb Nimbɔtiu.>.

eng: He isnt the God of the dead but of the living. You are seriously mistaken.
local: Waa sá bitaŋkpiibi Nimbɔtiu, u sá bin wāĺee yɔu ní. Ni baasii bí diyintikpaandin ní!».

eng: One of the legal experts heard their dispute and saw how well Jesus answered them. He came over and asked him, Which commandment is the most important of all.
local: Bimaradakaliibee ubɔ nìn bí ki gbìl̄ tibɔti tin pu Yeesu nì Sadusab nìn kpákèe. U nín ká yii Yeesu kíí-bi mmɔntiimee ní u sútìń ki pìl̄-u ki bālifì-u yii: «Marab kɔkɔ kansikinee, ulɔu di sá ukpaan ní?».

eng: Jesus replied, The most important one is Israel, listen! Our God is the one Lord.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-u yii: «Mara un sá ukpaanee di sá un lì yii: <Isirayeel yab pīlifìmaan. Ti-Dindaan Unimbɔti sá ubaanti ní.

eng: But they grabbed the servant, beat him, and sent him away empty-handed.
local: Ní bi cúú utɔntɔnlee ki gbáń ní ki jìn̄-u ki gíiǹ nì iŋaŋkulim.

eng: and you must love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your being, with all your mind, and with all your strength.
local: Ni máaǹ kí nīn néeń ni-Dindaan ni-Nimbɔtiu nì ni-pɔbiliŋ kɔkɔ nì ni-naŋki kɔkɔ nì ni-landɔkɔŋu kɔkɔ nì ni-pɔŋŋu mun kɔkɔ.>.

eng: The second is this, You will love your neighbor as yourself . No other commandment is greater than these.
local: Maraliliiti di sá yii: <Á nīn néeń a-juti kii sii a-bee.> Mara ubɔ kaa ti bí ki jítiǹ bin bí nee.».

eng: The legal expert said to him, Well said, Teacher. You have truthfully said that God is one and there is no other besides him.
local: Ní umaradakalee lī-u yii: «An kā, ukpil. A lī nì ibaamɔn ní yii Unimbɔti sá ubaanti, ubɔ kaa ti bí asee úǹ baba.

eng: And to love God with all of the heart, a full understanding, and all of ones strength, and to love ones neighbor as oneself is much more important than all kinds of entirely burned offerings and sacrifices.
local: Unil máaǹ kí nīn néeń Unimbɔti nì u-pɔbil kɔkɔ nì u-ciiŋyi kɔkɔ nì u-pɔŋŋu kɔkɔ, ní ki máaǹ kí nīn néeń u-juti kii úǹ u-bee. Míǹ jítiǹ tiwaŋkuti saraabi bin bi dù kí wà ŋŋmi kí tīī Unimbɔtee nì saraabi bin kɔ kíǹee.».

eng: When Jesus saw that he had answered with wisdom, he said to him, You arent far from Gods kingdom. After that, no one dared to ask him any more questions.
local: Yeesu ká yii u kíí-u nì iciinee, ní u lī-u yii: «Aa dátiǹ Unimbɔti Beel.» Niinee báà ubɔ kaa ti mākaǹ kí bàlifì-u tiba.

eng: While Jesus was teaching in the temple, he said, Why do the legal experts say that the Christ is Davids son?.
local: Yeesu nìn bíi dàkà binib Unimbɔti bɔti kunimbɔtidiinee, u bālifì-bi tibɔti yii: «Nì nsan ŋulɔŋu poon ní bimaradakaliibi lì yii Meesiya sá Daafiidi naantiiu ní?.

eng: David himself, inspired by the Holy Spirit, said, The Lord said to my lord, Sit at my right side until I turn your enemies into your footstool.
local: Di sá Unimbɔti Fam yāntì Daafiidi u-ba sòon̄ yii: <Ti-Dindaan tùkū m-Dindaan yii: Kàl̄ m-ŋaŋgiil pú kí nīn cí ŋyunti ŋun m làá yàntī á nīn kíǹ ki tɔ́kɔ́ a-nannanliibi puee.>.

eng: David himself calls him Lord, so how can he be Davids son? The large crowd listened to him with delight.
local: Daafiidi u-ba yíì-u yii u-Dindaan, kin mana ní u ŋūǹ kí nīn sá u-naantiiu mun?» Kunipaau nìn pílifíǹ Yeesu ní an mɔ̄ǹ-bi.

eng: As he was teaching, he said, Watch out for the legal experts. They like to walk around in long robes. They want to be greeted with honor in the markets.
local: Yeesu dàkatam poonee, ní u gíti lī yii: «Nín̄ nyím̀maan ni-ba nì bimaradakaliibi! Bíǹ di néeń ki pèentí abɔkutifɔkɔ ki yìiǹ bi jáaḿ-bi ipaan in pu binib kpáfée.

eng: They long for places of honor in the synagogues and at banquets.
local: ki kàal̀ nyɔkɔpu jaliŋ akpafidiinni nì diinabin.

eng: Again the landowner sent another servant to them, but they struck him on the head and treated him disgracefully.
local: Ní bukpaadaanee gíti tí nyàab̄ u-tɔntɔnliu upɔɔn ubɔ ki tú bi-cee. Ní bi gíti cúú u mun ki gbáń ki cíl̀ diyil ki sāaǹ-u.

eng: They are the ones who cheat widows out of their homes, and to show off they say long prayers. They will be judged most harshly.
local: Bíǹ di fìkì bikpiipoobi wanti ki gbēentí ki mèé ŋwimmeem ki yúntí fɔ́rrr bí ká-bi. Bíǹ gbanti di dāa kóò ditafadaaldi din gíti lákatèe ni!».

eng: Jesus sat across from the collection box for the temple treasury and observed how the crowd gave their money. Many rich people were throwing in lots of money.
local: Yeesu cūtìi kāl̀ ki bíitiǹ laakin bi tòó saraa nimbiliŋee, ki kpèé kunipaau nín tòó animbil puee. Bigaajab tikpil nìn tòó animbikpaan.

eng: One poor widow came forward and put in two small copper coins worth a penny.
local: ní ukpiipusoodaan ubɔ mun cáań igboo ili ki tɔ́.

eng: Jesus called his disciples to him and said, I assure you that this poor widow has put in more than everyone whos been putting money in the treasury.
local: Niinee ní Yeesu yíiń u-boonnooliibi ki bí-bi yii: «Ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-ni, ukpiipusoodaanee tɔ́ ki jítiǹ bin kɔkɔ tɔ́ saraa dooyee.

eng: All of them are giving out of their spare change. But she from her hopeless poverty has given everything she had, even what she needed to live on.
local: Bin béeyee cáań bi-nimbiliŋ ŋin baa làá dūu ŋá nì tibee ní ki tɔ́, ama unimpu nee, nì u-sool poonee, tin kɔkɔ u bàà cáá kí jēe ní u kúntì ki tɔ́.».

eng: He sent another one; that one they killed. The landlord sent many other servants, but the tenants beat some and killed others.
local: Ní bukpaadaan gíti tí lēē unitataati ki túnní ní bi kūū úǹ. Míǹ mbaantiim ní bi nìn ŋáaǹ bitɔntɔnliibi kupaau, bi nìn gbáń biba ní ki kūū biba.

eng: Now the landowner had one son whom he loved dearly. He sent him last, thinking, They will respect my son.
local: Báà ubɔ kaa ti bí ú tɔ̄, see u-jipɔmbaabil un u néeńee. Ní u dū-u ki túnní bi-cee njoolkaam, ki dākafì yii: <Bi làá fàŋkì m-jipɔɔn.>.

eng: But those tenant farmers said to each other, This is the heir. Lets kill him, and the inheritance will be ours.
local: Ama bipaadambee nín wál̀ u dòońee, ní bi lī tɔb cee yii: <Kpèè, un làá dāá jī u-baa gaajawantee di dòońee! Tí kpɔ̀maan-u kí jī bukpaa.>.

eng: They grabbed him, killed him, and threw him out of the vineyard.
local: U bāańee ní bi cìb̄ ki cúú-u ki kūū, ní ki dū-u ki lúkú ŋkpaabuku.».

eng: So what will the owner of the vineyard do? He will come and destroy those tenants and give the vineyard to others.
local: Yeesu lī míǹ ki dóòyee, ní ki bālifì-bi yii: «Kɔŋkɔnnee, bukpaadaanee làá ŋá mana? Asee ú dāan̄ kí kpɔ̀ bipaadambee, ní kí dū bukpaayee kí ŋúbíǹ bipɔmbi.

eng: As Jesus left the temple, one of his disciples said to him, Teacher, look! What awesome stones and buildings.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun Yeesu nyēeń kunimbɔtidiinee, ní u-boonnooliibi kansikin ubɔ bí-u yii: «Ukpil, kpèè bi nín dū ataŋkpakpaan ŋin ki máń adikpaan an ŋān̄ miŋyee!».

eng: First, the good news must be proclaimed to all the nations.
local: Ama an máaǹ yii inibool kɔkɔ ń gbìl̄ m-bɔmɔntiiti kí wàatī ní duulinya ń nín kúntí.

eng: When they haul you in and hand you over, dont worry ahead of time about what to answer or say. Instead, say whatever is given to you at that moment, for you arent doing the speaking but the Holy Spirit is.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun bi yaa láa cúú-ni ki cáǹ ibɔsoonee, ní taa nín̄ yáliŋkí tin ni làá tin sòoǹee pu. Ni yaa láa bàn̄ee, ní sòoǹ tin Unimbɔti yaa tīī-ni niinee. Di sá yii, an kaa sá nimbi di làá sòoǹ, an sá Unimbɔti Fam di làá tīī-ni tin ni làá sòoǹee.

eng: Brothers and sisters will hand each other over to death. A father will turn in his children. Children will rise up against their parents and have them executed.
local: Unil làá dāá cúú u-nabiki ní kí tīī bí kpɔ̀, ní ubaati ń dū u-biki kí tīī bí kpɔ̀, ní kibiki ń jáǹ ki-baa nì ki-na ní kí yàntī bí kpɔ̀-bi.

eng: Everyone will hate you because of my name. But whoever stands firm until the end will be saved.
local: Báà ŋma dāa ná-ni kun puee ni nóó-mi. Ama un yaa pɔ́ɔǹ u-ba ki tin kátì-kuee di làá dāá ŋmátí.».

eng: When you see the disgusting and destructive thing standing where it shouldnt be , then those in Judea must escape to the mountains.
local: «Ni làá ká <kuwaŋkpitiu kun cāabìń ntimbiilee> kɔ́ ki kíǹ ki bí laakin kuaa bàà máaǹ kí kɔ́ee. - Un kàaǹ tibɔti nee máaǹ kí ŋá ŋká! - Ŋyunti gbantee, bin yaa láa bí Juudee tiŋkinee ń sāǹ kí dàb̄ ajoon.

eng: Those on the roof shouldnt come down or enter their houses to grab anything.
local: Un yaa láa bí dicindee ń taa lì yii u làá kpákatí kí kɔ́ kudiin kí yòon̄ tiba.

eng: Those in the field shouldnt come back to grab their clothes.
local: ní un yaa láa bí kusaauee ń taa lì yii u là kí fàtī mmɔŋki kí yòon̄ u-bɔkutil.

eng: How terrible it will be at that time for women who are pregnant and for women who are nursing their children.
local: Ama falaa dɔ̄ ki cí bipɔdambi nì bimatib yaadal wiiŋyi!.

eng: Pray that it doesnt happen in winter.
local: Mèèmaan Unimbɔti an bɔti ń taa bàń kuseeun.

eng: In those days there will be great suffering such as the world has never before seen and will never again see.
local: Doooo, Unimbɔti nín náań kitiŋ ki dāa sāā díǹ nee, an falaau juti kaa láá jéetì nnibiyaamu pu, míǹ ní falaa gbanti juti mun kaa ti bí kí dāan̄.

eng: Jesus responded, Do you see these enormous buildings? Not even one stone will be left upon another. All will be demolished.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-u yii: «Kéè a ká adikpaan neeyaa! Dalbee, ditaŋkpal diba kaa dāa gíti tɔ́kɔ́ di-juti pu. An kɔkɔ làá dāá wīī ní.».

eng: If the Lord hadnt shortened that time, no one would be rescued. But for the sake of the chosen ones, the ones whom God chose, he has cut short the time.
local: Ti-Dindaan yaa kaa bàà bātì falaayee wiiŋyi pu, báà unibaanti kaa bàà ŋmátí. Ama bin u lútì bí nīn sá u-yabee pu ní u bātì an wiiŋyi.

eng: Then if someone says to you, Look, heres the Christ, or There he is, dont believe it.
local: «Ŋyunti gbantee, ubɔ yaa tùkū-ni yii, Meesiya bí doo, àá u bíń lapu, ní taa fōō kí kíí.

eng: False christs and false prophets will appear, and they will offer signs and wonders in order to deceive, if possible, those whom God has chosen.
local: Di sá yii bin làá mɔ̄ntì yii bíǹ di sá meesiyabee nì Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibee làá dāá nyā ki ŋáań tikpaŋkpantiil nì maamaaci tuuŋŋi, kí bàà dūú ŋmàtiǹ bin Unimbɔti lútèe gba.

eng: But you, watch out! Ive told you everything ahead of time.
local: Míǹ puee, ní nīn nyíǹ ni-ba. M tùkū-ni tikɔkɔ ní nīn nyí ki yóoǹ ní án nín bàń.».

eng: In those days, after the suffering of that time, the sun will become dark, and the moon wont give its light.
local: «Falaayee yaa jítì boonee, ŋwìiń dāa kpántí dibɔmbɔndi, uŋmal mun kaa dāa gíti pīiǹ.

eng: The stars will fall from the sky, and the planets and other heavenly bodies will be shaken.
local: Iŋmalbijaa dāa kìtīń kutaa pu ki lítiń, ní ipɔn in bí kutaa puee ń cìŋkì.

eng: Then they will see the Human One coming in the clouds with great power and splendor.
local: Ŋyunti gbantee ní bi làá ká man Kinibiki dòoń atagbanni ki cáá mpɔn tikpil nì tinyool.

eng: Then he will send the angels and gather together his chosen people from the four corners of the earth, from the end of the earth to the end of heaven.
local: M làá dāá tɔ̄ń Unimbɔti tuuŋyi ń cù duulinya kɔkɔn, kí tin sāā kutagbɔŋu nì kitiŋkuntinyɔkɔ, kí kóoǹní bin bin Unimbɔti lútèe.».

eng: Learn this parable from the fig tree. After its branch becomes tender and it sprouts new leaves, you know that summer is near.
local: «Ní pīlifìmaan fiigi subu nín dàkà-ti tibɔti tiŋyee. Di sá yii ni yaa ká fiigi subu tɔ́ apan ki bíi túkù tifaapɔnee, ni béetí yii kuseeu tóobiń dee.

eng: In the same way, when you see these things happening, you know that hes near, at the door.
local: Míǹ mbaantiim, ni yaa ká tin kɔkɔ m máafì-ni nee bíi jītée, béémaan yii m-dāantam tóobiń dee.

eng: Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives across from the temple. Peter, James, John, and Andrew asked him privately.
local: Yeesu bàn̄ ki kāl̀ Ɔliifi sufii jool pu, u-nimbiin túliń kunimbɔtidiiyee watil, ní Peetroo nì Yakubu nì Yoowaneesi nì Andree bālifì-u tibɔti nee bi-ŋmaniŋma yii.

eng: I assure you that this generation wont pass away until all these things happen.
local: Ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-ni, waanee nib kaa làá kpú kí kúntí ní an bɔti kɔkɔ ń bàń.

eng: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will certainly not pass away.
local: Kutagbɔŋu nì kitiŋ dāa bí kí kúntí, ama man bɔti kaa bí kí kúntí jaanjaan.

eng: But nobody knows when that day or hour will come, not the angels in heaven and not the Son. Only the Father knows.
local: Ubɔ kaa nyí ŋwiin nì kukúluu kun ní an bɔtee làá bàńee. Unimbɔti tuuŋyi in bí yilpuee kaa nyí, man u-Jipɔɔn gba kaa nyí, asee ti-Baa Unimbɔti baba di nyí.

eng: Watch out! Stay alert! You dont know when the time is coming.
local: Ní nīn nyíǹ ni-ba kí taa gèeǹ. Di sá yii naa nyí an yuntiŋu.

eng: It is as if someone took a trip, left the household behind, and put the servants in charge, giving each one a job to do, and told the doorkeeper to stay alert.
local: An dāa bí kii unil ubɔ nín cá nsan pu ki yāntì u-tɔntɔnliibi u-cindee ní. U yākatì báà ŋma nì u-tundi, ní ki kpáafì un cí kipunyɔkɔee ń taa nín̄ gèeǹ, ní ki búntì.

eng: Therefore, stay alert! You dont know when the head of the household will come, whether in the evening or at midnight, or when the rooster crows in the early morning or at daybreak.
local: Taa gèem̀maan! Di sá yii naa nyí ŋyunti ŋun kudiidaan làá bàńee, an yaa sá kujooueeee, kunyataasiiueeee, ŋkoosiimeeee, kutaafaaueeee, naa nyí.

eng: Dont let him show up when you werent expecting and find you sleeping.
local: Ní taa yàntī ú bàń kí túuǹ-ni ni dɔ̄ ki gèeǹ.

eng: What I say to you, I say to all: Stay alert.
local: Tin m lì nee, m lì ki tùkù báà ŋma ní: taa gèem̀maan!».

eng: Tell us, when will these things happen? What sign will show that all these things are about to come to an end.
local: «Tùkù-ti ŋyunti ŋun ní tin kɔkɔ a bàà lēe làá dāá bàńee, nì kudaaŋŋu kun ti làá dū kí béé yii ŋyunti bāańee.».

eng: Jesus said, Watch out that no one deceives you.
local: Niinee, Yeesu kíĺ ki bíi sòoǹ nì bi yii: «Ní nīn nyíǹ ni-ba ubɔ ń taa ŋmàtiǹ-ni.

eng: Many people will come in my name, saying, Im the one! They will deceive many people.
local: kun puee binib tikpil làá dāá dū man yindi kí kpántí bi-yaŋ ní kí lì yii bíǹ di sá Meesiya ní kí ŋmàtiǹ binib tikpil.

eng: When you hear of wars and reports of wars, dont be alarmed. These things must happen, but this isnt the end yet.
local: Ni làá dāá gbìl̄ bi sòoǹ tijáti tin pìlīǹ-nee nì tin dátiǹ-nee bɔti. Ama ni-pɔbiliŋ ń taa bīil̀. An máaǹ tíǹ kɔkɔ ń bàń kí wàatī, ama duulinya kúntitam kaa dá láá yá.

eng: Nations and kingdoms will fight against each other, and there will be earthquakes and famines in all sorts of places. These things are just the beginning of the sufferings associated with the end.
local: Ditimbundi diba dāa yíkí kí jáǹ diba, ní dibeel diba mun ń jáǹ diba. Kitiŋ làá dāá cìŋkì ajan kupaau pu, ŋkùm làá dāá nyā. Tíǹ kɔkɔee dāa sá kii imatiŋween nín kífí puee ní.

eng: Watch out for yourselves. People will hand you over to the councils. You will be beaten in the synagogues. You will stand before governors and kings because of me so that you can testify before them.
local: «Ní nīn nyíǹ ni-ba! Man puee bi làá dāá cùnnì-ni ibɔsoon, kí gbá-ni anaalab akpafidiinni, kí cáá-ni gɔminab nì bibɔtiibi nimbiin, mimmee ni làá ká mpaan kí sòoǹ nì bi man pu.

eng: It was two days before Passover and the Festival of Unleavened Bread. The chief priests and legal experts through cunning tricks were searching for a way to arrest Jesus and kill him.
local: An nìn kíǹ iwiin ili, bí jī Dilákatil jiŋaaliu nì jiŋaal un bi ŋmóò kpɔnɔb bin kaa cáá ŋkpɔnɔnyɔkɔee. Bisaraaŋakpilib nì bimaradakaliibi nìn nyàab̀ nì nciin poon kisàmbiki kin pu bi làá dī kí cúú Yeesu kí kpɔ̀-uee.

eng: Judas Iscariot, one of the Twelve, went to the chief priests to give Jesus up to them.
local: Ŋyunti gbantee ní akpambal saalaa nì alee kansikin un bi yíì Juudasi Isikaariyɔtee cūtì ki ká bisaraaŋakpilibee kí sòoǹ nì bi min ní u làá ŋá kí dū Yeesu kí ŋúbíǹ-bee.

eng: When they heard it, they were delighted and promised to give him money. So he started looking for an opportunity to turn him in.
local: Bi nín gbìl̄ mimmee, an mɔ́ɔkiǹ-bi, ní bi pōoǹ-u yii bi làá tīī-u animbil. Juudasi nìn kíĺ ki bíi nyàab̀ ŋyunti ŋun di kítì ú dū Yeesu kí ŋúbíǹ-bee.

eng: On the first day of the Festival of Unleavened Bread, when the Passover lamb was sacrificed, the disciples said to Jesus, Where do you want us to prepare for you to eat the Passover meal.
local: Jiŋaal un bi ŋmóò kpɔnɔb bin kaa cáá ŋkpɔnɔnyɔkɔee wimpeekaaŋu dal ŋun bi làntī apibil kí jī Dilákatil jiŋaaliuee, ní Yeesu boonnooliibi bí-u yii: «La ní a là tí cù kí bɔ́ntí-si Dilákatil jiŋaaliu jinti?».

eng: He sent two of his disciples and said to them, Go into the city. A man carrying a water jar will meet you. Follow him.
local: Ní u tú u-boonnooliibi bili, ní ki tùkū-bi yii: «Nín̄ cámaan kitiŋin, ni làá tū uninja ubɔ tú dinyimbool. Ní nīn táá-u.

eng: Wherever he enters, say to the owner of the house, The teacher asks,Where is my guest room where I can eat the Passover meal with my disciples.
local: Laakin u yaa tin kɔ́ee, ni mun ń kɔ́ niin kí bàlifì kudiidaan yii: <Ukpil bàlifī-si yii, m-diiku kun poon man nì m-boonnooliibi làá jī Dilákatil jiŋaaliu jintee bí la?>.

eng: He will show you a large room upstairs already furnished. Prepare for us there.
local: Niinee, u làá dàkà-ni diditɔntɔkɔl yilpu, kudii kun poon wāā, bi bɔ́ntì tikɔkɔ sée. Niin ní ni làá bɔ́ntí-ti tijin.».

eng: The disciples left, came into the city, found everything just as he had told them, and they prepared the Passover meal.
local: U-boonnooliibi búntì ki bàn̄ kitiŋinee, ki ká an jítì kii u nín bàà tùkū-bi pu ki síiǹee deedee, ní bi bɔ́ntì Dilákatil jiŋaaliu jinti.

eng: That evening, Jesus arrived with the Twelve.
local: Kujoou jóotèe, Yeesu nì u-kpambaliŋ saalaa nì alee bāań niin.

eng: During the meal, Jesus said, I assure you that one of you will betray me—someone eating with me.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun bi kāl̀ ki bíi jīǹ tijinee, ní Yeesu bí yii: «Ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-ni, man nì nimbi bin kā ki jīǹee kansikin ubɔ làá dū-mi kí ŋúbíǹ m-nannanliibi.».

eng: Deeply saddened, they asked him, one by one, Its not me, is it.
local: Niinee, kunimbaau kɔ́-bi, ní bi kíĺ ki bíi bàlifī-u ubɔ ubɔ yii: «Kéè an kaa sá mamaa!» «Kéè an kaa sá mamaa!».

eng: But they agreed that it shouldnt happen during the festival; otherwise, there would be an uproar among the people.
local: Ní bi lī yii: «Án taa nín̄ sá jiŋaal poon, fìkil fikil ń taa kɔ́ samaa kansikin.».

eng: Jesus answered, Its one of the Twelve, one who is dipping bread with me into this bowl.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-bi yii: «An sá nimbi saalaa nì bili nee kansikin ubɔ man nì un sèetī disambibaantiilinee.

eng: The Human One goes to his death just as it is written about him. But how terrible it is for that person who betrays the Human One! It would have been better for him if he had never been born.
local: Man Kinibiki cá kí kpú ní nee, kii an nín ŋmàn̄ ki síiǹ m-pu miŋyee. Ama mbusu dɔ̄ ki cí un làá dū-mi kí ŋúbíǹ m-nannanliibee. Bi yaa kaa bàà màl̄ udaan, an bàà sɔ́.».

eng: While they were eating, Jesus took bread, blessed it, broke it, and gave it to them, and said, Take; this is my body.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun bi bíi jīǹee, ní Yeesu yōoǹ kpɔnɔ ki pàkā Unimbɔti, ki cúu gífì gífì, ki yākatì ki tīī u-boonnooliibi kɔkɔ, ní ki bí yii: «Fōōmaan! Doo di sá m-wunti.».

eng: He took a cup, gave thanks, and gave it to them, and they all drank from it.
local: Ní ki yōoǹ ndaam, ki jáam̀ Unimbɔti ki dóòyee, ní ki dūu tīī-bi, ní bi-kɔkɔ nyùn̄.

eng: He said to them, This is my blood of the covenant, which is poured out for many.
local: Ní u tùkū-bi yii: «M-fatikuu dee, fatiku un cāabìń mpèé binib nì Unimbɔti kansikinee dee. Fatikuee di nyáń kí fīī binib kupaau.

eng: I assure you that I wont drink wine again until that day when I drink it in a new way in Gods kingdom.
local: Ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-ni, maa ti bí kí nyɔ̀ ndamam nee kí nīn cí daliŋkin m làá gíti nyɔ̀-mì mpɔm Unimbɔti Beelinee.».

eng: After singing songs of praise, they went out to the Mount of Olives.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun bi gāā jiŋaal laŋyi ki dóòyee, ní bi nyáń ki búntì Ɔliifi sufii jool pu.

eng: Jesus said to them, You will all falter in your faithfulness to me. It is written, I will hit the shepherd, and the sheep will go off in all directions .
local: Yeesu bí u-boonnooliibi yii: «Ni-kɔkɔ làá fá-mi, kii an nín ŋmàn̄ Unimbɔti gbɔŋkun puee yii: <M làá dāá fáá kí kpɔ̀ upikpaal, ní ipii ń yàl̄.>».

eng: But after Im raised up, I will go before you to Galilee.
local: Yeesu gíti lī yii: «Ama m yaa láa fīkitèe, m làá lìntī kí cīití-ni Galiilee.».

eng: Peter said to him, Even if everyone else stumbles, I wont.
local: Ní Peetroo lī-u yii: «Bi-kɔkɔ yaa féeń-saaa, man kpá kí fá-si.».

eng: Jesus was at Bethany visiting the house of Simon, who had a skin disease. During dinner, a woman came in with a vase made of alabaster and containing very expensive perfume of pure nard. She broke open the vase and poured the perfume on his head.
local: Yeesu nìn bí Beetanii, Siimɔn un nìn sá unyaŋgbantee dumpu. Ŋyunti ŋun u kā ki jīǹ tijinee, unimpu ubɔ kóoń ki ŋúbiń talaalumɔnti un bi yíì náaree. U nìn pɔ̄ɔ̄ kudaau tikpil ki kā ditaŋkpal kpalbanyaaŋwun. Ní unimpuee fáá ki wīī kpalbee nyɔkɔ ki kpáatì talaaluee ki sītì Yeesu yil pu.

eng: But Jesus said to him, I assure you that on this very night, before the rooster crows twice, you will deny me three times.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-u yii: «Ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-si, díǹ nyeeku gba, kí wàatī ukooja ń bāakì milee, a làá néé diba dibee mita pu yii saa nyí-mi!».

eng: But Peter insisted, If I must die alongside you, I wont deny you. And they all said the same thing.
local: Ní Peetroo kíí-u yii: «Báà an yaa sá-mi ŋkúm, man nì si làá kpú, man kpá kí néé yii maa nyí-si!» Ní u-boonnooliibi bin kɔkɔ kíǹee mun lī míǹ mbaantiim!.

eng: Jesus and his disciples came to a place called Gethsemane. Jesus said to them, Sit here while I pray.
local: Yeesu nì u-boonnooliibi búntì laakin bi yíì Geetiseemaneeyee, ní Yeesu tùkū-bi yii: «Kàl̄maan doo, man làá cù kí mèè.».

eng: He took Peter, James, and John along with him. He began to feel despair and was anxious.
local: U nìn kpáaǹ Peetroo nì Yakubu nì Yoowaneesi. Tifaandi nì dinimbinaamandi kíĺ ki bíi bàń-uee, ní u tùkū-bi yii.

eng: He said to them, Im very sad. Its as if Im dying. Stay here and keep alert.
local: «Kunimbaau kɔ́-mi tikpil ki cáǹ-mi ŋkúmin. Kàl̄maan doo kí nīn cí kí taa gèeǹ!».

eng: Then he went a short distance farther and fell to the ground. He prayed that, if possible, he might be spared the time of suffering.
local: U lī mimmee, ní ki sútì nyɔkɔpu fiii ní ki tin dōoǹ dicincibil, ní ki mēē yii an yaa bàà kítì, Unimbɔti ń kàkatī falaa yuntiŋu gbanti kí wàatī-u.

eng: He said, Abba, Father, for you all things are possible. Take this cup of suffering away from me. However—not what I want but what you want.
local: U nìn lī yii: «M-Baa, tiba kaa pée pɔ̄ɔ̄ a-cee! Kàkatī falaanyɔkaa nee kí wàatī-mi! Ama nì míǹ kɔkɔee, m nyàab̀ a-làtam ní, kaa sá mama m-ba nín là puee.».

eng: He came and found them sleeping. He said to Peter, Simon, are you asleep? Couldnt you stay alert for one hour?.
local: Yeesu fātìi gítìń u-kpambaliŋ atee cee ki ká bi dɔ̄ ki gèeǹ, ní u fīntì-bi, ní kí bí Peetroo yii: «Siimɔn, a gèeǹ níii? Aa kpáàa ŋúń ki kāl̀ ki cíitì kukúbaantiiu ń jītí kí taa gèeǹaaa?.

eng: Stay alert and pray so that you wont give in to temptation. The spirit is eager, but the flesh is weak.
local: Fìntīmaan kí nīn mèé, án nín làá ŋá pu ni yaa kɔ́ icɔŋkinee ní taa lítée. Unil bàà cáá nlandɔkɔmɔnti, ama ní ki bí iwɔfin.».

eng: Again, he left them and prayed, repeating the same words.
local: Yeesu fātìi sútì nyɔkɔpu ki mēē ki sòon̄ tibɔbaantiil.

eng: Some grew angry. They said to each other, Why waste the perfume?.
local: Ní biba fōō diŋuul ki bí yii: «Ba pu ní bi fá talaalumɔnti nee míǹ?.

eng: And, again, when he came back, he found them sleeping, for they couldnt keep their eyes open, and they didnt know how to respond to him.
local: ní ki fātìi tí gítìń ní ki ká bi dɔ̄ ki gèeǹ, kun puee ŋgeem nìn cáá-bi tikpil. Bi fīntèe, baa nyí bi nín làá kíí-u puee.

eng: He came a third time and said to them, Will you sleep and rest all night? Thats enough! The time has come for the Human One to be betrayed into the hands of sinners.
local: U búntì ki gíti tí gítìń ntataatiim, ní ki lī-bi yii: «Ni dáá gbíntìi dɔ̄ ki gèeǹ ki ŋùŋfāa! Too, an dóò. Kɔŋkɔnnee an ŋmāntì bí dū man Kinibiki kí ŋúbíǹ binikpitib dee.

eng: Get up! Lets go! Look, here comes my betrayer.
local: Un làá dū-mi kí nyāfèe di bàan̄ nee. Yīkìmaan tí cù.».

eng: Suddenly, while Jesus was still speaking, Judas, one of the Twelve, came with a mob carrying swords and clubs. They had been sent by the chief priests, legal experts, and elders.
local: Yeesu kaa láá sòon̄ ki ŋmíl̀, kpalaayee ní u-kpambaliŋ saalaa nì alee kansikin un bi yíì Juudasee cílìń, u nì kunipaau kubɔ, kúǹ ŋúbiń ntàataajimu nì atambɔn. Bisaraaŋakpilib nì bimaradakaliibi nì bitindaŋkpilib di nìn túnní-bi.

eng: His betrayer had given them a sign: Arrest the man I kiss, and take him away under guard.
local: Juudasi un làá nìn dū Yeesu kí ŋúbíǹ-bee nìn tīī-bi kudaaŋŋu yii: «Un m yaa kpàl̄ ki jáam̀ee, un ni nyàab̀ee dee, ní cúúmaan-u tiŋan kí nīn cáá-u ki cá!».

eng: As soon as he got there, Judas said to Jesus, Rabbi! Then he kissed him.
local: Juudasi bāań kpalaayee ki sútì ki pìl̄ Yeesu, ní ki bí yii: «Ukpil,» ní ki kpàl̄-u.

eng: Then they came and grabbed Jesus and arrested him.
local: Niinee bin táań Juudasee cìb̄ ki cúú Yeesu.

eng: One of the bystanders drew a sword and struck the high priests slave and cut off his ear.
local: Bin nìn bí Yeesu ceeyee kansikin ubɔ fɔ́fì u-tàataajiki ní ki gāā ki wúkutì usaraaŋakpil tɔntɔnliu tafal.

eng: Jesus responded, Have you come with swords and clubs to arrest me, like an outlaw?.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-bi yii: «M sá unikpiti naaa? Ba pu ní ni ŋúbiń ntàataajimu nì atambɔn kí dāá cúú-mi?.

eng: Day after day, I was with you, teaching in the temple, but you didnt arrest me. But let the scriptures be fulfilled.
local: Baabadal m bí kunimbɔtidiin ni-kansikin ki dàkà binib, ba pu ní naa cúú-mi niin? Ama kɔŋkɔnnee, Unimbɔti gbɔŋku nín sòon̄ ki síiǹ m-pu miŋyee ń ŋá!».

eng: This perfume could have been sold for almost a years pay and the money given to the poor. And they scolded her.
local: Bi bàà ŋūǹ kí nyāfì-u animbikpaan kí dūú yàkatī biwɔfib.» Ní bi yíkìi bíi wòoǹ unimpuee.

eng: And all his disciples left him and ran away.
local: Niinee, u-boonnooliibi kɔkɔ sáǹ ki wāatì-u.

eng: One young man, a disciple, was wearing nothing but a linen cloth. They grabbed him.
local: Unaacimpɔɔn ubɔ nìn táá Yeesu ki pēē sata. Bi cìb̄ kí cúú-uee.

eng: but he left the linen cloth behind and ran away naked.
local: ní u yāntì sata bi-ŋaan ki sáǹ dicancaŋgbeel.

eng: They led Jesus away to the high priest, and all the chief priests, elders, and legal experts gathered.
local: Bi cáá Yeesu ki búntì usaraaŋakpil dumpu, ní bisaraaŋakpilib nì bitindaŋkpilib nì bimaradakaliibi kɔkɔ kóoǹní ki kpáfì niin.

eng: Peter followed him from a distance, right into the high priests courtyard. He was sitting with the guards, warming himself by the fire.
local: Peetroo nìn táá Yeesu aboon aboon ki tin bàn̄ usaraaŋakpil cindi ní ki kāl̀ soojab kansikin ki wùl̀ ŋŋmi.

eng: The chief priests and the whole Sanhedrin were looking for testimony against Jesus in order to put him to death, but they couldnt find any.
local: Bisaraaŋakpilib nì bibɔsoonliibi kitil kɔkɔ nyàab̄ bin làá lēē seera Yeesu pu bí ŋmā kí kpɔ̀-uee, ní kaa ká.

eng: Many brought false testimony against him, but they contradicted each other.
local: Di sá yii binib tikpil nìn mɔ́ntì u-nyɔkɔpu, ní bi-nyɔmmu kaa pēē.

eng: Some stood to offer false witness against him, saying.
local: Biba yíkìi mɔ́ntì Yeesu pu ki lī yii.

eng: We heard him saying, I will destroy this temple, constructed by humans, and within three days I will build another, one not made by humans.
local: «Ti gbìl̄ u sòon̄ yii: <M làá gbá kí wīī kunimbɔtidii kun binib máń nee, kí dū iwiin ita kí mā kupɔŋu kun binib kaa dū bi-ŋalii ki máńee.>».

eng: But their testimonies didnt agree even on this point.
local: Nì míǹ kɔkɔee, bin lēekì an seerau gbantee nyɔmmu kaa pēē.

eng: Jesus said, Leave her alone. Why do you make trouble for her? She has done a good thing for me.
local: Ní Yeesu bíi lìǹ-bi yii: «Yàntīmaan-u! Ba pu ní ni páb́-u míǹ? Unimpu nee ŋá ditummɔntiil ní ki tīī-mi.

eng: Then the high priest stood up in the middle of the gathering and examined Jesus. Arent you going to respond to the testimony these people have brought against you.
local: Niinee, usaraaŋakpil yíkìi síl̀ bi-kɔkɔ nimbiin ki bālifì Yeesu yii: «Binib nee nín bí ki sòoǹ a-pu miŋyee, ba ŋá ní saa kíikí ní?».

eng: But Jesus was silent and didnt answer. Again, the high priest asked, Are you the Christ, the Son of the blessed one.
local: Ní u ŋmí, kaa gātì tiba. Ní usaraaŋakpil gíti bālifì-u mpɔm yii: «Sii di sá Unimbɔti tinyooldaan Jipɔɔn un u túnní ú nīn sá ubɔteeee?».

eng: Jesus said, I am. And you will see the Human One sitting on the right side of the Almighty and coming on the heavenly clouds.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-u yii: «M lafun sá-u. Ni làá dāá ká man Kinibiki kā Unimbɔti Mpɔŋkɔdaan ŋaŋgiil pú, ní ki làá ká m nyánní yilpu atagbanni ki dòoń.».

eng: Then the high priest tore his clothes and said, Why do we need any more witnesses?.
local: Usaraaŋakpil gbìl̄ mimmee, ní ki dáaĺ tiwan tin u pēēyee ki cátì, ní ki bí yii: «Ti gíti nyàab̀ seeradambi kí ŋá ba?.

eng: Youve heard his insult against God. What do you think? They all condemned him. He deserves to die.
local: Nimbi ni-ba gbìl̄ u nín ŋèeǹ nì Unimbɔtee, ni dàkafī yii ba?» Ní bi-kɔkɔ bí yii u-bɔti kaa ŋān̄, u máaǹ ŋkúm.

eng: Some began to spit on him. Some covered his face and hit him, saying, Prophesy! Then the guards took him and beat him.
local: Ní biba kíĺ ki tíiĺ timɔtan ki làbitī Yeesu pu. Bi dū ŋŋaabu ki bīiǹ u-nimbiin ki fátí-u, ní ki bàlifī-u yii: «Sii un wāĺee, tùkù-ti un di fáá-see!» Ní pilisib mun fōō-u ki bíi fátí-u itampa.

eng: Meanwhile, Peter was below in the courtyard. A woman, one of the high priests servants, approached.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun Peetroo nìn dáá bí taapu cindinee, bisapɔmbi bin bí usaraaŋakpil cee ki tūǹ ditundee ubɔ dɔ́miń.

eng: and saw Peter warming himself by the fire. She stared at him and said, You were also with the Nazarene, Jesus.
local: ki ká Peetroo bíi wùl̀ ŋŋmi. Ní u kpēē u-nimbiin, ní ki bí yii: «Si mun nì Yeesu Naasaareeti yɔuee di nìn tāatí!».

eng: But he denied it, saying, I dont know what youre talking about. I dont understand what youre saying. And he went outside into the outer courtyard. A rooster crowed.
local: Ní Peetroo néè ki bí yii: «Maa nyí tin a sòoǹee, kaa gbìl̀ ti-taapu.» Niinee, Peetroo nyáń ki nín kóò kunaakooun ní ukooja súń.

eng: The female servant saw him and began a second time to say to those standing around, This man is one of them.
local: Ní usapɔɔnee gíti tí ká-u niin, ní ki bíi tùkù bin bí niinee yii: «Binibee kansikin ubɔ sèé!».

eng: You always have the poor with you; and whenever you want, you can do something good for them. But you wont always have me.
local: Ni nì biwɔfib di làá nīn pée bí baabadal. Ni ŋūǹ kí nīn ŋáań-bi tiŋan ŋyunti ŋun ni yaa lèe. An yaa kíǹ man, maa làá nīn bí ni-cee baabadal.

eng: But he denied it again. A short time later, those standing around again said to Peter, You must be one of them, because you are also a Galilean.
local: Ní u gíti néé. An kútì fiiiyee, bin nìn bí niinee bí Peetroo yii: «Tiŋman, si mun ŋmàl̄ binibee ni. Ti wāĺ yii a sá Galiilee watil pú niliu ní.».

eng: But he cursed and swore, I dont know this man youre talking about.
local: Niinee, Peetroo kíĺ ki bíi pùtī ki tíntí yii: «Maa nyí uninja un bɔti ni sòoǹee!».

eng: At that very moment, a rooster crowed a second time. Peter remembered what Jesus told him, Before a rooster crows twice, you will deny me three times. And he broke down, sobbing.
local: Kpalaayee, ukooja báakì nliliitiim, ní Peetroo téetì dinyɔɔbundi din Yeesu bàà lī-uee, yii: «Kí wàatī ukooja ń bāakì milee, a làá néé diba dibee mita pu yii saa nyí-mi.» Niinee ní u kíĺ ki bíi sūǹ ki yáliŋkí.

eng: She has done what she could. She has anointed my body ahead of time for burial.
local: Min u ŋúńee ní u ŋée: u péé ki dɔ́miń ki yāaǹ-mi talaalu kí bɔ́ntí m-wunti kí cīití ŋu-síbitam dal dee.

eng: I tell you the truth that, wherever in the whole world the good news is announced, what shes done will also be told in memory of her.
local: Ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-ni, duulinya kɔkɔwoooo laakin kamaa ní bi yaa láa sòon̄ m-bɔmɔntiitee, bi dāa téetí unimpu nee pu ki máafí min u ŋée.».

eng: At daybreak, the chief priests—with the elders, legal experts, and the whole Sanhedrin—formed a plan. They bound Jesus, led him away, and turned him over to Pilate.
local: Kutaafaau pɔ̀lpɔlee, bisaraaŋakpilib péé ki kpáfì, bi nì bitindaŋkpilib nì bimaradakaliibi, bíǹ bibɔsoonliibi kitilee kɔkɔ, kí sòoǹ Yeesu bɔti, ní ki bóob́-u ŋŋmii ki cáa ŋúbiǹ gɔmina Piilati.

eng: He knew that the chief priests had handed him over because of jealousy.
local: Di sá yii u nìn béè yii kináŋ kin kin bisaraaŋakpilib nìn cáá Yeesu puee di yāntì bi cáań-u ki ŋúbíǹ-u.

eng: But the chief priests stirred up the crowd to have him release Barabbas to them instead.
local: Niinee, bisaraaŋakpilibee téé kunipaau ń lì yii bi là gɔmina ń gíiǹ Baaraabaasi ní kí líí.

eng: Pilate replied, Then what do you want me to do with the one you call king of the Jews.
local: Ní Piilati fātìi bālifì-bi yii: «Kin, m làá ŋá un ní ni yíì Juuda yab bɔtiuee mana?».

eng: They shouted back, Crucify him.
local: Ní bi fātìi kíí-u ki téeń mpɔn yii: «Bí kpáá-u ndɔpuntikaa pu!».

eng: Pilate said to them, Why? What wrong has he done? They shouted even louder, Crucify him.
local: Ní gɔmina Piilati fātìi bālifì-bi yii: «Tikpitil tin u ŋée di sá tilati?» Niinee ní bi mɔ̄tìi kúuǹ mpɔn yii: «Bí kpáá-u ndɔpuntikaa pu!».

eng: Pilate wanted to satisfy the crowd, so he released Barabbas to them. He had Jesus whipped, then handed him over to be crucified.
local: Piilati là kí sɔ̀ŋkì bi-pɔbiliŋee, ní ki gíiǹ Baaraabaasi ki líì-bi, ní ki yāntì bi gbáń Yeesu anaalab, ní ki dū-u ki tīī-bi bí cáá-u kí kpáá ndɔpuntikaa pu.

eng: The soldiers led Jesus away into the courtyard of the palace known as the governors headquarters, and they called together the whole company of soldiers.
local: Niinee, soojab cáá Yeesu ki kɔ́ǹ gɔmina cindi poon ní ki yíiń disoojakitil din kíǹee kɔkɔ.

eng: They dressed him up in a purple robe and twisted together a crown of thorns and put it on him.
local: Ní bi dū dibɔkutimandi cínciŋ ki pēeǹ Yeesu, ní ki dū ikuŋkum ki lùn̄ kibeefuulii ki cíkiǹ-u.

eng: They saluted him, Hey! King of the Jews.
local: Bi nìn kíĺ ki jáaḿ-u yii: «Ti jáaḿ-si Juuda yab bɔtiu!».

eng: Again and again, they struck his head with a stick. They spit on him and knelt before him to honor him.
local: Bi cáá ŋkpandɔ ki fátí u-yil ki tíiĺ timɔtan ki làbitī-u, ní ki dòoń ki gbàantí u-nimbiin.

eng: Pilate questioned him, Are you the king of the Jews? Jesus replied, Thats what you say.
local: Ní Piilati bālifì-u yii: «Sii di sá Juuda yab bɔtiuaaa?» Ní Yeesu kíí-u yii: «Míǹ ní a lī nee.».

eng: When they finished mocking him, they stripped him of the purple robe and put his own clothes back on him. Then they led him out to crucify him.
local: Bi bēel̀-u míǹ ki dóòyee, ní ki cúú dibɔkutimandee ki pēetì-u ní ki gíiǹ úǹ u-ba wampeeŋkaati ki pēeǹ-u, ki nyántì-u kí cáá tin kpáá ndɔpuntikaa pu.

eng: Simon, a man from Cyrene, Alexander and Rufus father, was coming in from the countryside. They forced him to carry his cross.
local: Bi cée, ki ká usanjitil ubɔ nyánní kusaau, bi yíì-u Siimɔn, u sá Siireen tiŋki, Aleekisandir nì Rufusi baa dee. Bi nìn páb́-u yii ú fōō Yeesu dɔpuntikaaŋu kí bùkun̄.

eng: They brought Jesus to the place called Golgotha, which means Skull Place.
local: ní ki cáá Yeesu ki cūnnì laakin bi yíì Gɔlgɔtee .

eng: They tried to give him wine mixed with myrrh, but he didnt take it.
local: Bi tīī-u ndamam min ni nnyɔkɔ ŋun bi yíì miiree ŋmàl̄ee ú nyɔ̀, ama u yíì, kaa nyùn̄.

eng: They crucified him. They divided up his clothes, drawing lots for them to determine who would take what.
local: Ní bi dū-u ki kpáá ndɔpuntikaa pu, ní ki yākatì u-wampeeŋkaati, ki tɔ́ kasi kí kpèè báà ŋma nín làá jī kuŋyee.

eng: It was nine in the morning when they crucified him.
local: An nìn sá ntaafaawìiń tikúti tiwa ní bi kpáá-u ndɔpuntikaa pu ki dūu cóoǹ.

eng: The notice of the formal charge against him was written, The king of the Jews.
local: Bi nìn ŋmàn̄ tiŋmaŋmabiti ki nákáǹ u-yilpu kí dàkà kun pu ní bi kūū-uee, yii: «Juuda yab bɔtiu sèé.».

eng: They crucified two outlaws with him, one on his right and one on his left.
