local: Bi nìn kpáá digaŋgandambi bili mun idɔpuntikaa pu ki dūu cóoǹ ki ŋàl̄-u, ubɔ u-ŋaŋgiil pú, un kíǹee u-ŋaŋgan pú. .

eng: People walking by insulted him, shaking their heads and saying, Ha! So you were going to destroy the temple and rebuild it in three days, were you?.
local: Bisanjitiliibi nìn bèel̄-u ní ki gàatī diyil ki lì yii: «Iiin, sii un lī yii a làá gbá kí wīī kunimbɔtidii kí fàtīí mā-ku iwiin ita dalee.

eng: The chief priests were accusing him of many things.
local: Bisaraaŋakpilibee dɔ́miń ki sòon̄ tibɔti tikpil ki túǹ u-pu.

eng: Save yourself and come down from that cross.
local: fīī a-ba kí kpákatiń ndɔpuntikaa pu!».

eng: In the same way, the chief priests were making fun of him among themselves, together with the legal experts. He saved others, they said, but he cant save himself.
local: Bisaraaŋakpilib nì bimaradakaliibi mun nìn bí ki bèel̄-u ki lì tɔb cee yii: «U fīī bin kíǹee ní kaa ŋūǹ kí fīī u-ba!.

eng: Let the Christ, the king of Israel, come down from the cross. Then well see and believe. Even those who had been crucified with Jesus insulted him.
local: Isirayeel yab bɔtiu Meesiya ń kpákatiń kɔŋkɔnnee, tí kpèè kí nín fōō-u kí kíí!» Bin bi nìn kpáá idɔpuntikaa pu ki cóoǹ ki ŋàl̄-uee mun nìn bí ki sìí-u.

eng: From noon until three in the afternoon the whole earth was dark.
local: Kuwiŋkuluu gbáńee dibɔmbɔndi jéetì kitiŋ kɔkɔ pu ki tin sāā tikúti tita.

eng: At three, Jesus cried out with a loud shout, Eloi, eloi, lama sabachthani, which means, My God, my God, why have you left me.
local: Tikúti titee, Yeesu fáá mbiil mpɔn, yii: « Eelooi, Eelooi, lama sabakitanii? » - An taapu sá yii «M-Nimbɔtiu, m-Nimbɔtiu, ba ŋá ní a dū-mi ki fá?».

eng: After hearing him, some standing there said, Look! Hes calling Elijah.
local: Bin bí niinee biba nín gbìl̄ u fáá mbiil mimmee, ní bi bí yii: «Pīlifìmaan! U yíì Eelii dee!».

eng: Someone ran, filled a sponge with sour wine, and put it on a pole. He offered it to Jesus to drink, saying, Lets see if Elijah will come to take him down.
local: Ní bi-kansikin ubɔ sáǹ ki yōoǹ kuca ki múĺ ndaam min gàm̀ee ni, ní ki dūu túuń ŋkpandɔ pu ki tīī Yeesu ń nyɔ̀, ní ki lī yii: «Tí yàntīmaan kí kpèè Eelii yaa làá dāan̄ kí kpákatiń-u!».

eng: But Jesus let out a loud cry and died.
local: Yeesu gíti fáá mbiil mpɔnee, ní ki sīntì.

eng: The curtain of the sanctuary was torn in two from top to bottom.
local: Sata un nìn pītì kunimbɔtidii poon ki gāntì laakin bi yíì circir páaa laŋkee nì laan kíǹee cúu cátì abuku ali ki kíliń yilpu ki dāa kátì taapu.

eng: When the centurion, who stood facing Jesus, saw how he died, he said, This man was certainly Gods Son.
local: Room yab soojakpiliu un nìn sí ki bíitìń Yeesuee wál̀ u nín sīntì puee, ní ki lī yii: «Tiŋman kijapaai nee lafun sá Unimbɔti Jipɔɔn ní.».

eng: Pilate asked him again, Arent you going to answer? What about all these accusations.
local: Míǹ puee, ní Piilati fātìi gíti bālifì-u yii: «A ŋmí níii? Aa gbìl̀ tin kɔkɔ bi sòoǹ ki tūntí a-pueeee?».

eng: Some women were watching from a distance, including Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of James and Joses, and Salome.
local: Binimpoobi biba mun nìn sí kudandaŋu ki kpèé. Maarii un nyánní Magidalaa tiŋkinee, nì Saloomee nì Maarii un sá Yakubuwaai nì Joosee nee, mun nìn bí bi-kansikin.

eng: When Jesus was in Galilee, these women had followed and supported him, along with many other women who had come to Jerusalem with him.
local: Bíǹ di nìn táá Yeesu ki tútúǹ-u ŋyunti ŋun u nìn bí Galiileeyee. Binimpoobi biba mun tikpil nìn céeǹní-u ki jɔ́m̀ Jeerusaleem.

eng: Since it was late in the afternoon on Preparation Day, just before the Sabbath.
local: Bibɔsoonliibi kitil kansikin ukpil ubɔ nìn bí, binib nìn tɔ́-u tiŋan pu, bi yíì-u Jooseefi u sá Arimatee tiŋkin, u mun nìn bɔ́ntì u-ba ki cí Unimbɔti Beel. Ŋwìiń nín tóob́ kí jēetí ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa ń kíĺee, ní u ká dipɔbil ki cūtì Gɔmina Piilati cee ki mēē-u nsan kí yòon̄ Yeesu wunti.

eng: Joseph from Arimathea dared to approach Pilate and ask for Jesus body.
local: Bibɔsoonliibi kitil kansikin ukpil ubɔ nìn bí, binib nìn tɔ́-u tiŋan pu, bi yíì-u Jooseefi u sá Arimatee tiŋkin, u mun nìn bɔ́ntì u-ba ki cí Unimbɔti Beel. Ŋwìiń nín tóob́ kí jēetí ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa ń kíĺee, ní u ká dipɔbil ki cūtì Gɔmina Piilati cee ki mēē-u nsan kí yòon̄ Yeesu wunti.

eng: Pilate wondered if Jesus was already dead. He called the centurion and asked him whether Jesus had already died.
local: Gɔmina Piilati nín gbìl̄ yii Yeesu pūtì kpáàa kpíìyee, ní u bākatì, ní ki yíiń usoojakpil ki bālifì-u yii Yeesu nín kpíìyee an yúntàaa.

eng: When he learned from the centurion that Jesus was dead, Pilate gave the dead body to Joseph.
local: Usoojakpilee nín tùkū-u yii Yeesu lafun kpíì an yúntèe, ní Piilati pú Jooseefi kinyɔkɔ yii ú cùú yòon̄ Yeesu wunti.

eng: He bought a linen cloth, took Jesus down from the cross, wrapped him in the cloth, and laid him in a tomb that had been carved out of rock. He rolled a stone against the entrance to the tomb.
local: Ní Jooseefi cūtì ki dá ŋŋaapiin ki dɔ́miń ki kpáatì Yeesu wunti, ki dūu tākaǹ ŋŋaapiinee ni, ní ki cáá-u ki bíl̀ dikaakul din bi nìn lùkū ditaŋkpalinee. U dū-u ki ŋá dikaakulee ni ki dóòyee, ní ki bíntì ditaŋkpakpaandi diba ki dūu pīiǹ dikaakul nyɔkɔ.

eng: Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of Joses saw where he was buried.
local: Maarii un nyánní Magidalaa tiŋkinee nì Maarii Joosee na mun nìn sí ki kpèé laakin bi dū-u ki bíl̀ee.

eng: But Jesus gave no more answers, so that Pilate marveled.
local: Yeesu kaa ti pée gātì tiba, ní Piilati bākatì sèeee.

eng: During the festival, Pilate released one prisoner to them, whomever they requested.
local: Dilákatil jiŋaaliu kamaayee, gɔmina Piilati nìn gīintí usarkadaan ubɔ, un samaa yaa mēē-uee ní kí líí.

eng: A man named Barabbas was locked up with the rebels who had committed murder during an uprising.
local: Kijapaai kiba nìn bí, bi yíì-kì Baaraabaasi. Bi nìn cúú u nì u-jutiibi biba ki tɔ́ sarkan, kun puee bi nìn kūū unil daliŋkin kunipaau kubɔ nìn yíì yii baa là kí pīlifiǹ gɔminee.

eng: The crowd pushed forward and asked Pilate to release someone, as he regularly did.
local: Ní kunipaau jɔ́m̀ ki bālifī Piilati ń gíiǹ un bi lèe kí líí, kii u nín kpààa ŋáań-bi puee.

eng: Pilate answered them, Do you want me to release to you the king of the Jews.
local: Ní u bālifì-bi yii: «Ni là ḿ gíiǹ Juuda yab bɔtiu kí líí-naaa?».

eng: When the Sabbath was over, Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James, and Salome bought spices so that they could go and anoint Jesus dead body.
local: Ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa jítèe, Maarii un nyánní Magidalaa tiŋkinee, nì Maarii Yakubu na, nì Saloomee cūtì ki dáń talaalub bin bi cáa ŋáŋkí bitaŋkpiibi wuntee, kí cáá ŋmìtī Yeesu wunti.

eng: She went and reported to the ones who had been with him, who were mourning and weeping.
local: Unimpuee cūtì ki máafì bin nì Yeesu nìn tāatée. U bàn̄ee bíǹ kā nì dinimbinaamandi ki sūǹ.

eng: But even after they heard the news, they didnt believe that Jesus was alive and that Mary had seen him.
local: Ama bi nín gbìl̄ unimpuee cee yii Yeesu bí dimaŋfalin u ká-u muee, baa fōō ki kíí.

eng: After that he appeared in a different form to two of them who were walking along in the countryside.
local: Míǹ boonee, Yeesu gíti tí lēē u-ba mbamin nsanni ki dàkā u-boonnooliibi kansikin bili bin nìn yíkì ki cá kitimbilii kibanee.

eng: When they returned, they reported it to the others, but they didnt believe them.
local: Bi nìn fātìi gítìń ki máafì bin kíǹee, ní baa tí fōō bi mun bɔti ki kíí.

eng: Finally he appeared to the eleven while they were eating. Jesus criticized their unbelief and stubbornness because they didnt believe those who saw him after he was raised up.
local: Boonee ní u nyántì u-ba ki dàkā u-kpambaliŋ saalaa nì dibee ŋyunti ŋun bi kā ki jīǹ tijinee. Yeesu wòon̄-bi bi-pɔbiliŋ nín gbìfì baa fōō-u ki kííyee pu, kun puee baa nìn fōō bin ká u nín fīkitèe bɔti ki kíí.

eng: He said to them, Go into the whole world and proclaim the good news to every creature.
local: Ní u tùkū-bi yii: «Cùmaan kitiŋ jaŋŋi kɔkɔwoooo kí sòoǹ tibɔmɔntiil nee kí tīī kunibuluu kamaa!.

eng: Whoever believes and is baptized will be saved, but whoever doesnt believe will be condemned.
local: Un yaa fōō ki kíí ní bi fùl̄-u Unimbɔti nyimee kāntí difiil. Ama un yaa kaa fōō ki kííyee dāa kóò ditafadaaldin ní.

eng: These signs will be associated with those who believe: they will throw out demons in my name. They will speak in new languages.
local: Maamaaci tuuŋŋi ŋin bin yaa fōō-mi ki kííyee làá nīn ŋáańee sɔ̀. M-yindi puee bi làá jà arasiniibi, ki làá sòoǹ isooncaan.

eng: They will pick up snakes with their hands. If they drink anything poisonous, it will not hurt them. They will place their hands on the sick, and they will get well.
local: Bi làá cūtì ikoobu, kí nyɔ̀ ndabiim, tiba kaa làá ŋá-bi. Bi dāa pāantí bi-ŋalii bibunliibi pu ní bí ká dicáaǹdi.».

eng: After the Lord Jesus spoke to them, he was lifted up into heaven and sat down on the right side of God.
local: Ti-Dindaan Yeesu sòon̄ nì bi míǹ ki dóòyee, ní ki yíkìi nín jɔ́ḿ yilpu ki tin kāl̀ Unimbɔti ŋaŋgiil pú.

eng: Very early on the first day of the week, just after sunrise, they came to the tomb.
local: Ku wúntì ŋwimpeekaa dal kutaafaau pɔ̀lpɔlee ní bi búntì dikaakul laŋki.

eng: But they went out and proclaimed the message everywhere. The Lord worked with them, confirming the word by the signs associated with them.
local: Ní u-boonnooliibi búntì ki sòoǹ tibɔmɔntiilee ajan kɔkɔ, ní ti-Dindaan bí bi-cee ki tūǹ maamaaci tuuŋŋi ki dàkà yii tibɔti tin bi sòoǹee sá ibaamɔn.

eng: They were saying to each other, Whos going to roll the stone away from the entrance for us.
local: Bi cée ní ki bàlifī tɔb yii: «Ŋma di làá tin bìkitī-ti ditaŋkpal kí wàatī dikaakul nyɔkɔ dee?».

eng: When they looked up, they saw that the stone had been rolled away.
local: Bi wáaǹ ki kpēēyee, ki ká ditaŋkpakpaandee bíntì ki wāatì dikaakul nyɔkɔ.

eng: Going into the tomb, they saw a young man in a white robe seated on the right side; and they were startled.
local: Binimpoobee bāań ki kɔ́ dikaakulinee, ní ki wál̀ unaacimpɔɔn ubɔ kā bi-ŋaŋgiil pú ki pēē jibapiin, ní tifaandi kɔ́-bi.

eng: But he said to them, Dont be alarmed! You are looking for Jesus of Nazareth, who was crucified. He has been raised. He isnt here. Look, heres the place where they laid him.
local: Ama ní unaacimpɔɔnee bí-bi yii: «Taa fàŋkìmaan! M nyí ni nín nyàab̀ Yeesu Naasaareeti yɔu un bi kpáá ndɔpuntikaa puee. U fīkitì, waa bí doo. Kpèèmaan, laakin bi nìn bíl̀-uee sèé.

eng: Go, tell his disciples, especially Peter, that he is going ahead of you into Galilee. You will see him there, just as he told you.
local: kí nín̄ cámaan kí tùkù u-boonnooliibi nì Peetroo yii u làá lìntī, ni tin kāntí-u Galiilee. Niin ní ni làá tin ká-u, kii u nín nìn pée tùkū-ni puee.».

eng: Overcome with terror and dread, they fled from the tomb. They said nothing to anyone, because they were afraid. .
local: Bi nyáń dikaakulinee bi-pɔbiliŋ bíil̀ ní bi sáń bi-wunti gbáà. Bi nín fàŋkée puee, baa pée máafì báà ubɔ tiba. .

eng: They promptly reported all of the young mans instructions to those who were with Peter. Afterward, through the work of his disciples, Jesus sent out, from the east to the west, the sacred and undying message of eternal salvation. Amen. .
local: Yeesu nín fīkitì kinyɔŋ wimpeepeekaaŋuee, Maarii un nyánní Magidalaa tiŋkin, un ni u nìn jìn̄ arasiniibi bilulee ní u yāntì u péé ki ká-u.

eng: After a few days, Jesus went back to Capernaum, and people heard that he was at home.
local: Iwiin iba boonee, Yeesu nìn gítì Kapeernayum, ní binib gbìl̄ yii u bí mmɔŋki.

eng: But so you will know that the Human One has authority on the earth to forgive sins—he said to the man who was paralyzed.
local: Ama ní béé yii man Kinibiki cáá mpɔn kitiŋ pu kí dū unil kpitii kí pɔ̄-u.» Niinee ní u tùkū ufaliŋfaanee yii.

eng: Get up, take your mat, and go home.
local: «Sii yīkìm̄ kí yòon̄ a-lúŋŋu kí nīn kùǹ a-dumpu!».

eng: Jesus raised him up, and right away he picked up his mat and walked out in front of everybody. They were all amazed and praised God, saying, Weve never seen anything like this.
local: Ní u nyīī ki yíkì dicilpu niin ki yōoǹ u-lúŋŋu ki nyáń báà ŋma nimbiin, ní an bākatiǹ-bi sèeee, ní bi bíi pàkà Unimbɔti ki lì yii: «Taa láá pée ká míǹ dalba dalba!».

eng: Jesus went out beside the lake again. The whole crowd came to him, and he began to teach them.
local: Yeesu nyáń ki gítì Galiilee mɔɔl caŋin. Kunipaau nìn dɔ́miń u-cee ní u dàkā-bi Unimbɔti bɔti.

eng: As he continued along, he saw Levi, Alphaeus son, sitting at a kiosk for collecting taxes. Jesus said to him, Follow me. Levi got up and followed him.
local: U jītée, ki ká Leefii Alfee jipɔɔn kā fɔtiku diikun. Ní u bí-u yii: «Dāan̄ kí nīn nóó-mi!» Ní Leefii lafun yíkì ki táá-u.

eng: Jesus sat down to eat at Levis house. Many tax collectors and sinners were eating with Jesus and his disciples. Indeed, many of them had become his followers.
local: Yeesu nìn kɔ́ Leefii dumpu ki bíi jīǹ tijinee, bifɔtiliibi tikpil nì binikpitib bin kɔkɔ kíǹee dɔ́miń ki kútì u nì u-boonnooliibi pu: di sá yii an nikpitibee nìn nóó-u ki wīikí.

eng: When some of the legal experts from among the Pharisees saw that he was eating with sinners and tax collectors, they asked his disciples, Why is he eating with sinners and tax collectors.
local: Bimaradakaliibi bin sá Fariisab kitilinee nín ká u nì binikpitib kpáfí ki jīǹee, ní bi bālifì u-boonnooliibi yii: «Ba pu ní u nì binib nee kpáfí ki jīǹ míǹ?».

eng: When Jesus heard it, he said to them, Healthy people dont need a doctor, but sick people do. I didnt come to call righteous people, but sinners.
local: Yeesu gbìl̄ bi nín lī puee, ní ki gāā-bi dilaŋkpandi yii: «Bin cáá laafiyee kaa nyàab̀ unyɔɔdaan, see bin būǹee di nyàab̀ unyɔɔdaan. Di sá yii maa dɔ́miń kí yíiń deedeedambi, m dɔ́miń kí yíiń binikpitib ní.».

eng: Johns disciples and the Pharisees had a habit of fasting. Some people asked Jesus, Why do Johns disciples and the Pharisees disciples fast, but yours dont.
local: Yoowaneesi boonnooliibi nì Fariisab nìn bōob̀ kinyɔkɔ. Dalbee ní biba dɔ́miń ki bālifì Yeesu yii: «Ba pu ní Yoowaneesi boonnooliibi nì Fariisab boonnooliibi bōob̀ kinyɔkɔ ní sii yab kaa bōob̀ ní?».

eng: Jesus said, The wedding guests cant fast while the groom is with them, can they? As long as they have the groom with them, they cant fast.
local: Ní Yeesu fātìi bālifì-bi yii: «Ubɔ yaa cáá unimpucaan ki yaa yíiń u-bɔɔbee, bi jīǹ ki nyù naaa, kinyɔkɔ ní bi bōob̀ ní? Unimpucaan cal yaa bí bi-kansikinee, baa kàal̀ ŋkùm.

eng: So many gathered that there was no longer space, not even near the door. Jesus was speaking the word to them.
local: Kunipaau nìn dɔ́miń ki kpáfì u-cee kudiin, ní mpaan kaa ti kpáàa bí kipunyɔkɔpu gba. U nìn bíi sòoǹ nì bi Unimbɔti bɔti ní.

eng: But the days will come when the groom will be taken away from them, and then they will fast.
local: Ama ŋwiin ŋubɔ bí ki dòoń bi làá dāa yòon̄ unimpucaan cal bi-kansikin, an yuntiŋu ní u-bɔɔbi dāa bōob̀ kinyɔkɔ.

eng: No one sews a piece of new, unshrunk cloth on old clothes; otherwise, the patch tears away from it, the new from the old, and makes a worse tear.
local: «Bi yaa dū ŋŋaapɔɔn ki lìb̄ satakpiki bundi, ki yaa fíntèe, ŋŋaapɔɔn kōontí ní kí kpáaǹ satakpiki kí cátí, ní dibùndee ń mɔ̀tīí wàan̄.

eng: No one pours new wine into old leather wineskins; otherwise, the wine would burst the wineskins and the wine would be lost and the wineskins destroyed. But new wine is for new wineskins.
local: Míǹ mbaantiim ní baa tí dù ndapɔm kí fúuń tilɔkpikilin. Bi yaa dūu fúuń míǹ, tilɔkpikil pòol̄ ní ndaam ń fá, ní tilɔti mun ń bīil̀. Bi dù ndapɔm kí fúuń tilɔpɔn ni ní.».

eng: Jesus went through the wheat fields on the Sabbath. As the disciples made their way, they were picking the heads of wheat.
local: Ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa ŋubɔ dalee, Yeesu nì u-boonnooliibi bíi jītí bilee kpaaŋŋin ní u-boonnooliibi bíi gífí bilee fiŋyi ki ŋmèem̄ abil ki ŋmóò.

eng: The Pharisees said to Jesus, Look! Why are they breaking the Sabbath law.
local: Fariisab ká mimmee, ní ki bí-u yii: «Ba pu ní a-boonnooliibi ŋáań min kòó ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa dalee?».

eng: He said to them, Havent you ever read what David did when he was in need, when he and those with him were hungry?.
local: Ní u fātìi kíí-bi yii: «Naa láá pée kàan̄ kugbɔŋu poon ki ká tijin takaasiu nín nìn cúú ubɔti Daafiidi nì u-naacimbi bi ŋá pueeee? Ŋyunti ŋun Abiyataar nìn sá usaraaŋakpilee.

eng: During the time when Abiathar was high priest, David went into Gods house and ate the bread of the presence, which only the priests were allowed to eat. He also gave bread to those who were with him.
local: Daafiidi nìn kɔ́ kunimbɔtidiin ki ŋmɔ́ saraa kpɔnɔb ki kíǹ ki tīī u-yab. Abiyataar di nìn sá usaraaŋakpil ŋyunti gbanti. Ti-koobiliŋ poonee, usaraaŋal baba di cáá nsan kí ŋmɔ́ kpɔnɔb gbanti. Ama Daafiidi nìn ŋmɔ́ ki kíǹ ki tīī u-naacimbi gba.».

eng: Then he said, The Sabbath was created for humans; humans werent created for the Sabbath.
local: Yeesu gíti lī-bi yii: «Unimbɔti náań ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa binib pu ní, waa náań binib ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa pu.

eng: This is why the Human One is Lord even over the Sabbath.
local: Man Kinibiki di sá ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa daan.».

eng: Some people arrived, and four of them were bringing to him a man who was paralyzed.
local: Ní bininjab binaa túǹní ufaliŋfaan ubɔ ki cáań u-cee.

eng: They couldnt carry him through the crowd, so they tore off part of the roof above where Jesus was. When they had made an opening, they lowered the mat on which the paralyzed man was lying.
local: Nniwíin puee, baa nìn ŋúń kí pìl̄ Yeesu. Míǹ puee, bi nìn jɔ́m̀ ki cíl̀ kudii yilpu ki bíitì laakin Yeesu bée, ní ki dū ufaliŋfaan nì nlún ki dīń dibùndee ni ki sīntìń ki bíl̀.

eng: When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralytic, Child, your sins are forgiven.
local: Yeesu nín ká binib gbanti fōō ki kíí yii u ŋūǹ kí cáaǹ ubunlee, ní u tùkū ufaliŋfaanee yii: «M-biki, m dū a-kpitii ki pú-si.».

eng: Some legal experts were sitting there, muttering among themselves.
local: Bimaradakaliibi biba nìn kā niin ki dàkafī bi-pɔbiliŋin yii.

eng: Why does he speak this way? Hes insulting God. Only the one God can forgive sins.
local: «Ba pu ní uninja nee lì míǹ? U ŋèeǹ nì Unimbɔti ní. Ubɔ kaa ŋūǹ kí dū ikpiti kí pɔ̄, asee Unimbɔti baba.».

eng: Jesus immediately recognized what they were discussing, and he said to them, Why do you fill your minds with these questions?.
local: Niinee Yeesu béè bi-landɔkɔŋu ní ki bālifì-bi yii: «Ba pu ní ni dàkafī míǹ ni-pɔbiliŋin?.

eng: Which is easier—to say to a paralyzed person, Your sins are forgiven, or to say, Get up, take up your bed, and walk?.
local: Ba di kaa pɔ̄ɔ̄ dilil ní? Á tùkù ufaliŋfaan yii: <A ká a-kpitii pɔ̄tam> di kaa pɔ̄ɔ̄ dilil naaa, <Yòon̄ a-lúŋŋu kí nīn còom̄> di kaa pɔ̄ɔ̄ ní?.

eng: Jesus returned to the synagogue. A man with a withered hand was there.
local: Yeesu nìn fātìi kɔ́ kukpafidiin. Uninja ubɔ nìn bí niin u-ŋal fáká.

eng: He had healed so many people that everyone who was sick pushed forward so that they could touch him.
local: Di sá yii u nìn cáaǹ binib tikpil, an yāntì bibunliibi kɔkɔ kpákà kí gbēē-u.

eng: Whenever the evil spirits saw him, they fell down at his feet and shouted, You are Gods Son.
local: Arasiniibi bin kā biwaatiibi puee yaa nìn ká-uee, bi nìn gbàantí u-nimbiin, ní bi kūuntí ki lì yii: «Sii di sá Unimbɔti Jipɔɔn!».

eng: But he strictly ordered them not to reveal who he was.
local: U nìn kpàtī bi-pu ki lì-bi yii bí taa yàntī bí béé-u.

eng: Jesus went up on a mountain and called those he wanted, and they came to him.
local: Boonee Yeesu jɔ́m̀ kigbantaŋ pu ní ki yíiń bin u lèe ní bi dɔ́miń u-cee.

eng: He appointed twelve and called them apostles. He appointed them to be with him, to be sent out to preach.
local: Niinee ní u lútì saalaa nì bili bí nīn sá u-kpambaliŋ u nì bi ń nīn kíǹ ki bí, ú nīn tūǹ-bi bí nīn sòoǹ u-bɔti.

eng: and to have authority to throw out demons.
local: kí nīn cáá mpɔn ki jì arasiniibi.

eng: He appointed twelve: Peter, a name he gave Simon.
local: Binib saalaa nì bili bin u lútèe yiŋŋi sèé: Siimɔn - un u nìn páaǹ diyindi yii Peetrooyee -.

eng: James and John, Zebedees sons, whom he nicknamed Boanerges, which means sons of Thunder.
local: nì Seebeedee jipɔmbi Yakubu nì Yoowaneesi - bin u pú diyindi yii Bɔwaneegeesee an taapu sá yii «Binib bin náań nì ataadunee» -.

eng: and Andrew; Philip; Bartholomew; Matthew; Thomas; James, Alphaeus son; Thaddaeus; Simon the Cananaean.
local: Bin kíǹee di sá Andree nì Fiiliipi nì Baateeleemii nì Maatiiu nì Toomasi, nì Yakubu Alfee jipɔɔn, nì Tadee nì Siimɔn un nìn là kí já nì Room yabee.

eng: and Judas Iscariot, who betrayed Jesus.
local: nì Juudasi Isikaariyɔti, un dāa dū Yeesu ki tīī u-nannanliibee.

eng: Wanting to bring charges against Jesus, they were watching Jesus closely to see if he would heal on the Sabbath.
local: Fariisab nìn bíi cɔ́ŋkí Yeesu kí kpèè u yaa làá cáaǹ-u ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa dal, bí ŋmā kí ká nnaakpal kí lì yii u yīntì.

eng: Jesus entered a house. A crowd gathered again so that it was impossible for him and his followers even to eat.
local: Míǹ boonee, ní bi nín gítí mmɔŋki, ní kunipaau tí kóoǹní ki múkuǹ. An yāntì Yeesu nì u-boonnooliibi kaa ti kpáàa cáá mpaan kí jī tijin.

eng: When his family heard what was happening, they came to take control of him. They were saying, Hes out of his mind.
local: Yeesu yab gbìl̄ tin kɔkɔ jītée, ní bi yíkìi kíliń nsan kí dāá cúú-u, kun puee biba nìn lì yii: «U-yil lábitì ní.».

eng: The legal experts came down from Jerusalem. Over and over they charged, Hes possessed by Beelzebul. He throws out demons with the authority of the ruler of demons.
local: Bimaradakaliibi biba nìn nyánní Jeerusaleem ki lì yii: «Arasiniibi kpiliu Beelseebuul di kā u-pu. Úǹ pɔŋŋu ní u cáa jì arasiniibi.».

eng: When Jesus called them together he spoke to them in a parable: How can Satan throw Satan out?.
local: Ní Yeesu yíiń-bi u-cee, ki gāā-bi dilaŋkpandi ki lī yii: «Mana ní Sitaan ŋūǹ kí jà Sitaan?.

eng: A kingdom involved in civil war will collapse.
local: Dibeel din nib yaa cúú di-ba ki yākatì mili ki jáaǹ tɔbee kaa ŋūǹ kí nīn bí kí cù ndan.

eng: And a house torn apart by divisions will collapse.
local: Míǹ mun ní kudicaŋu kun di yaa cúú ku-ba ki yākatì mili ki jáaǹ tɔbee kaa tí ŋūǹ kí nīn bí ki cá.

eng: If Satan rebels against himself and is divided, then he cant endure. Hes done for.
local: An yaa sá yii Sitaan yíkìi jáaǹ u-ba ki cúú u-ba ki yākatèe, mimmee, waa ŋūǹ kí nīn bí ki cá, u-pɔŋŋu kúntí ní.

eng: No one gets into the house of a strong person and steals anything without first tying up the strong person. Only then can the house be burglarized.
local: «Bi yaa kaa bóob́ upɔɔl ŋŋmii ki wāatì, ŋma di ŋūǹ kí kɔ́ u-dumpu kí kóoǹ u-wanti? See bí bóob́-u kí wàatī ní kí nín ŋmā kí kɔ́ kí kóoǹ u-wanti.

eng: I assure you that human beings will be forgiven for everything, for all sins and insults of every kind.
local: «M sòoǹ nì ni ibaamɔn ní, nnibiyaamu ŋūǹ kí ká bi-kpitii kɔkɔ pɔ̄tam ki kpáaǹ tibɔfan tin bi sòon̄ Unimbɔtee.

eng: But whoever insults the Holy Spirit will never be forgiven. That person is guilty of a sin with consequences that last forever.
local: Ama un yaa sòon̄ Unimbɔti Fam tibɔfanee, úǹ kpá kí ká u-kpitii pɔ̄tam jaanjaan, di sá yii u kɔ́ diyìntil din kaa cáá dikúntilee ni!».

eng: He said to the man with the withered hand, Step up where people can see you.
local: Ní Yeesu bí unil un ŋal fákée yii: «Yīkì kí sīĺ ti-kansikin báà ŋma nimbiin.».

eng: He said this because the legal experts were saying, Hes possessed by an evil spirit.
local: Yeesu nìn lī míǹ, kun puee bi nìn lì yii: «U cáá arasinii u-ni!».

eng: His mother and brothers arrived. They stood outside and sent word to him, calling for him.
local: Yeesu na nì u-nabiyaamu bāań ki sí mmɔŋki ki tú bí yíiń-u.

eng: A crowd was seated around him, and those sent to him said, Look, your mother, brothers, and sisters are outside looking for you.
local: Binib tikpil nìn kā ki gɔ́ń-u ní bi tùkū-u yii: «A-na nì a-nabiyaamu sí mmɔŋki ki nyàab̀-si.».

eng: He replied, Who is my mother? Who are my brothers.
local: Ní u kíí-bi yii: «Ŋma di sá m-na nì m-nabiyaamu?».

eng: Looking around at those seated around him in a circle, he said, Look, here are my mother and my brothers.
local: U kpēē ki māntì ki tú bin nìn kā ki gɔ́ń-uee, ní ki lī yii: «Kpèèmaan, m-na nì m-nabiyaamu sèé nee!.

eng: Whoever does Gods will is my brother, sister, and mother.
local: Un kamaa yaa ŋáań Unimbɔti nín là puee, udaan gbanti di sá m-naal, m-niisal nì m-na.».

eng: Then he said to them, Is it legal on the Sabbath to do good or to do evil, to save life or to kill? But they said nothing.
local: Ní u bālifì-bi yii: «Ti-marau pú nsan tí ŋá tiŋan ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa dalaaa, tí ŋá tikpitil ní? An pú nsan yii tí fīī unil naaa, tí kpɔ̀-u ní?» Ní bi ŋmí.

eng: Looking around at them with anger, deeply grieved at their unyielding hearts, he said to the man, Stretch out your hand. So he did, and his hand was made healthy.
local: U nìn kpēē-bi dinimbil ki māntì, ki fōō bi-pu diŋuul, ama ní bi-pɔɔgbifiŋŋu mun nìn kɔ́ǹ-u kunimbaau. Ní u bí un ŋal fákée yii: «Tàntī a-ŋal!» Ní u tāntì-ŋu, ní u-ŋal ŋáŋkì.

eng: At that, the Pharisees got together with the supporters of Herod to plan how to destroy Jesus.
local: Niin niin ní Fariisab nyáń ki nyàab̄ ubɔti Eeroodi naacimbi bí kpāfì kí nyàab̀ nsan ŋun bi làá dī kí kpɔ̀ Yeesuee.

eng: Jesus left with his disciples and went to the lake. A large crowd followed him because they had heard what he was doing. They were from Galilee.
local: Niinee ní Yeesu nì u-boonnooliibi nyáń ki cūtì dimɔɔl caŋin. Kunipaau nìn táá-u ki nyánní Galiilee nì Juudee.

eng: Judea, Jerusalem, Idumea, beyond the Jordan, and the area surrounding Tyre and Sidon.
local: nì Jeerusaleem nì Idumee, nì Jɔɔdan buŋgbandi lapu pu, nì Tiir nì Siidɔn tiŋgbammun. Kunipaauee kɔkɔ nìn gbìl̄ tin kɔkɔ u ŋáańee ní ki dɔ́miń u-cee.

eng: Jesus told his disciples to get a small boat ready for him so the crowd wouldnt crush him.
local: Ní u bí u-boonnooliibi yii bí bɔ́ntí-u kiŋaliŋwaai kiba nnyim pu ú kɔ́ kunipaauee ń taa láa nyīī kí mátí-u.

eng: Jesus began to teach beside the lake again. Such a large crowd gathered that he climbed into a boat there on the lake. He sat in the boat while the whole crowd was nearby on the shore.
local: Yeesu tí kíĺ ki bíi dàkà Unimbɔti bɔti Galiilee mɔɔl caŋin, ní kunipaau kóoǹní ki kpáfì u-cee. Bi-wíiŋŋu puee, ní u jɔ́m̀ ki kāl̀ buŋalimbun nnyim pu, ní kunipaauee kɔkɔ sí dimɔɔgbandi ki pìlīǹ-u.

eng: When they were alone, the people around Jesus, along with the Twelve, asked him about the parables.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun u kíǹ u-babee ní bin nìn cēentí-uee nì u-boonnooliibi saalaa nì bilee bālifì-u tibɔŋmantikaalee taapu.

eng: He said to them, The secret of Gods kingdom has been given to you, but to those who are outside everything comes in parables.
local: Ní u kíí-bi yii: «Unimbɔti yāntì nimbi béè u-Beel bɔti tin nìn bí dibalinee taapu. An yaa kíǹ bifantantiibee, an sá tibɔti ní bí nīn cáa ŋmàntī-bi.

eng: This is so that they can look and see but have no insight, and they can hear but not understand. Otherwise, they might turn their lives around and be forgiven.
local: án nín làá ŋá pu ní: <Bi-nimbiliŋ ń nīn kpítì bí taa nín̄ wāĺ, bí nīn pílifí mun tikpil kí taa nín̄ gbìl̀ee. An yaa yíì, bi yaa kpēē ki wál̀, ki yaa pílifì mun ki gbìl̄ee, bi féeń bi-saŋkpitii, Unimbɔti ń dū bi-kpitii kí pɔ̄-bi!>».

eng: Dont you understand this parable? Then how will you understand all the parables?.
local: Míǹ boonee ní Yeesu gíti lī-bi yii: «Naa gbìl̀ tibɔŋmantikaal nee taapuɔɔ! Kin ni làá dāá ŋá mana kí gbìl̄ tin kíǹee taapu?.

eng: The farmer scatters the word.
local: An sá Unimbɔti nyɔɔbundi ní uwaŋyaanlee bí ki yàantí.

eng: This is the meaning of the seed that fell on the path: When the word is scattered and people hear it, right away Satan comes and steals the word that was planted in them.
local: Binib biba náań nì iwambool in lítì nsan puee ní. Binibee yaa gbìl̄ dinyɔɔbundee, Sitaan dòoń ki lèekī dicilpu tibɔti tin bi búĺ bi-pɔbiliŋinee.

eng: Heres the meaning of the seed that fell on rocky ground: When people hear the word, they immediately receive it joyfully.
local: Míǹ mbaantiim ní binib biba náań nì kuteeu, laakin iwambool lítèe. Binibee yaa gbìl̄ dinyɔɔbundee, bi fòol̄-dì nì dipɔɔpindi.

eng: Because they have no roots, they last for only a little while. When they experience distress or abuse because of the word, they immediately fall away.
local: Nì min kɔkɔ bi fòol̄ mimmee, baa cáá inyaan kí sīĺ kí báań. Bi nín fōō dinyɔɔbundee puee, an yaa sá digìńdi kii disàandi di yaa jéetì bi-puee ní bí dū dimákal din bi cáá Unimbɔti puee kí fá malaa.

eng: Others are like the seed scattered among the thorny plants. These are the ones who have heard the word.
local: Biba mun náań nì iwambool in lítì ikuŋkuminee ní. Bíǹ di sá bin gbìl̀ dinyɔɔbundee.

eng: but the worries of this life, the false appeal of wealth, and the desire for more things break in and choke the word, and it bears no fruit.
local: ní duulinya landɔkɔi páb́-bi, tigaajati ŋmàtíǹ-bi, ní nnimbitaataam mun cáá-bi. An wantee kɔkɔ mūkuntí ki bìintí dinyɔɔbundee pu, ní dàa ŋūǹ kí lòoǹ abil.

eng: He said many things to them in parables. While teaching them, he said.
local: U nìn cáá tibɔŋmantikaal tikpil ki sòoǹ nì bi tibɔti ní ki dàkà-bi ki bí-bi yii.

eng: The seed scattered on good soil are those who hear the word and embrace it. They bear fruit, in one case a yield of thirty to one, in another case sixty to one, and in another case one hundred to one.
local: Biba mun tí náań nì laakin kitiŋ ŋān̄ iwamboolee lítèe ní. Bíǹ di sá bin gbìl̄ dinyɔɔbundee ki fōō-dì, ní ki lòoǹ abil. Ŋfin ŋubɔ pu lòon̄ mmuŋku nì saalaa, ŋubɔ imuŋku ita, ŋubɔ mun dilaataal.».

eng: Jesus said to them, Does anyone bring in a lamp in order to put it under a basket or a bed? Shouldnt it be placed on a lampstand?.
local: Ní u gíti bí-bi yii: «Baa tùutī fitila kí dūú tàkan̄ kulɔŋŋun, kaa tí dù-u kí síiń kilambaŋ taapu. Bi dù fitila kí tɔ́kɔń kifaŋkɔlii pu ní.

eng: Everything hidden will be revealed, and everything secret will come out into the open.
local: Míǹ ní tin kɔkɔ pu binib bìintée làá dāá bìitī. Tiba kaa bí dibalin kaa làá nyā mpaampiin pu.

eng: Whoever has ears to listen should pay attention.
local: Sii un cáá ditafalee ń gbìl̄!».

eng: He said to them, Listen carefully! God will evaluate you with the same standard you use to evaluate others. Indeed, you will receive even more.
local: Ní u tí bí-bi yii: «Ní nīn pílifí tibɔti nee tiŋan. Ni nín ŋmāntì pu ki tīīyee, Unimbɔti dāa ŋmàntī míǹ ní kí tīī-ni, kí gbéeǹ-ni gba án yàaǹ.

eng: Those who have will receive more, but as for those who dont have, even what they dont have will be taken away from them.
local: Di sá yii Unimbɔti dāa kútí un cááyee ní kí fōō un kaa cááyee cee fiii un u bàà cááyee.».

eng: Then Jesus said, This is what Gods kingdom is like. Its as though someone scatters seed on the ground.
local: U gíti lī yii: «Unimbɔti Beel náań nì kii unil nín yàantí iwambool kitiŋ puee ní.

eng: then sleeps and wakes night and day. The seed sprouts and grows, but the farmer doesnt know how.
local: U yaa gèeǹeeee, u yaa wāĺeeee, kunyeeueeee, ŋwiimpueeee, iwamboolee nyēē ki wīintí, waa kpāantí kí béé an nín dì pu ní kí dāá ŋá mimmee.

eng: The earth produces crops all by itself, first the stalk, then the head, then the full head of grain.
local: Kitiŋ ki-ba càntī iwambool ń nyā kí cáá apoo kí màtī, ní kí lòoǹ abil ifinni.

eng: Whenever the crop is ready, the farmer goes out to cut the grain because its harvesttime.
local: Abil yaa bīń ki dóòyee, mimmee, kudiceeu bāań dee ní bí yòon̄ kijiki kí kɔ́ kí cā.».

eng: Listen to this! A farmer went out to scatter seed.
local: «Pīlifìmaan! Uwaŋyaanli ubɔ nyáń kí yàaǹ u-wamboolii.

eng: He continued, Whats a good image for Gods kingdom? What parable can I use to explain it?.
local: Yeesu gíti lī yii: «Ti làá dū Unimbɔti Beel kí nántíǹ ba? Àá tibɔŋmantikaal tilati ní ti làá dūú ŋmàntī kí dàkà Dibeelee nín bí puee?.

eng: Consider a mustard seed. When scattered on the ground, its the smallest of all the seeds on the earth.
local: Dì náań nì kii tikuŋfaal subiki kin bi yíì muutaadee bil ní. Bi yaa búĺ-dèe díǹ di sá kiwaai kitiŋ pu subiliŋ kɔkɔ kansikin.

eng: but when its planted, it grows and becomes the largest of all vegetable plants. It produces such large branches that the birds in the sky are able to nest in its shade.
local: Ama bi nín búĺ-dèe ní dí nyā kí wíiǹ kí tɔ̄ apan kí kpántí busukpaambu kí jītíǹ tikuŋfaal tin kɔ kíǹee subiyaamu, áli nnɔmbiyaamu gba ŋūǹ kí dāá páań mu-diiŋŋi kí báĺ busubuee faatin.».

eng: With many such parables he continued to give them the word, as much as they were able to hear.
local: Tibɔŋmantikaal nee jutiibi tikpil ní Yeesu nìn cáa sòoǹ nì bi u-bɔti, min ní bi nìn ŋūǹ kí gbìl̄ taapuee.

eng: He spoke to them only in parables, then explained everything to his disciples when he was alone with them.
local: Waa nìn sòoǹ nì bi fám an yaa kaa sá nì tibɔŋmantikaal. Boonee, u nì u-boonnooliibi yaa kíǹ bi-ŋmaniŋmee ní u cántí tibɔŋmantikaalee taapu ki tùkù-bi.

eng: Later that day, when evening came, Jesus said to them, Lets cross over to the other side of the lake.
local: Ŋwimbaanti gbanti jookuee, Yeesu bí u-boonnooliibi yii: «Tí dùtīmaan lapu buŋgbandi!».

eng: They left the crowd and took him in the boat just as he was. Other boats followed along.
local: U-boonnooliibi yāntì kunipaau yàl̄ee, ní bi kɔ́ buŋalimbu bun ni u nìn kēe ki kpáaǹ-u ki búntì. Iŋaliŋfi iba mun nìn táá-u.

eng: Gale-force winds arose, and waves crashed against the boat so that the boat was swamped.
local: Bi cée ní kubuŋkpaaŋŋu kubɔ kíĺ ki bíi jītí ki yòontí nnyim ki tòó buŋalimbun, ki yāntì buŋalimbuee bíi gbéè nnyim kpalaa.

eng: But Jesus was in the rear of the boat, sleeping on a pillow. They woke him up and said, Teacher, dont you care that were drowning.
local: Ama Yeesu úǹ nìn dɔ̄ buŋalimbu boon pú ki kúĺ diyikuŋkul ki gèeǹ, ní u-boonnooliibi fīntì-u, ní ki bí-u yii: «Ukpil, ti nín làá kpúee, an kaa ká-saaa?».

eng: He got up and gave orders to the wind, and he said to the lake, Silence! Be still! The wind settled down and there was a great calm.
local: U fīntèe, ní ki kpātì kubuŋu pu ní ki tùkū dimɔɔl yii: «Ŋmīĺ kí dòon̄ soooo!» Niinee ní kubuŋu kpáàa gbēeǹ, ní an ŋmíl̀ fɔ́ŋfɔŋ díkí diki.

eng: As he was scattering seed, some fell on the path; and the birds came and ate it.
local: U bíi yàantée ní iba lítì nsan pu, ní ilunlukuti dɔ́miń ki dūu jíń.

eng: Jesus asked them, Why are you frightened? Dont you have faith yet.
local: Míǹ boonee ní Yeesu bālifì u-boonnooliibi yii: «Ba ŋá ní ni fāŋkì mimmee? Ba pu ní naa máká m-pu?».

eng: Overcome with awe, they said to each other, Who then is this? Even the wind and the sea obey him.
local: Tifaandi nìn baasii kɔ́-bi tikpil, ní bi bàlifī tɔb yii: «Kijapaai nee nín baasii sá ŋma dee, ní ki sòoǹ kubuŋu nì dimɔɔl gbìliǹ-u míǹ nan dee?».

eng: Other seed fell on rocky ground where the soil was shallow. They sprouted immediately because the soil wasnt deep.
local: Iba mun lítì kuteeu pu, laakin kitiŋ kaa nyóòyee ní ki pūtì kpáàa nyáń.

eng: When the sun came up, it scorched the plants; and they dried up because they had no roots.
local: Ŋwìiń píĺee ní i kpíì kun puee kitiŋ kaa nyóò inyaan ń kɔ́.

eng: Other seed fell among thorny plants. The thorny plants grew and choked the seeds, and they produced nothing.
local: Iba mun lítì ikuŋkumin ki nyáń, ní ikuŋkum wíiǹ ki múkuǹ i-pu, ní iaa ŋúń ki lòon̄.

eng: Other seed fell into good soil and bore fruit. Upon growing and increasing, the seed produced in one case a yield of thirty to one, in another case a yield of sixty to one, and in another case a yield of one hundred to one.
local: In kíǹee mun lítì laakin kitiŋ ŋān̄ee, ki nyáń ki wíiǹ ki cáá apoo, ní ki lòon̄ ifin. Iba cáá abil mmuŋku nì saalaa, iba mun imuŋku ita, in kíǹee mun dilaataal.».

eng: He said, Whoever has ears to listen should pay attention.
local: Ní u gíti kútì ki lī yii: «Sii un yaa cáá ditafalee ń gbìl̄!».

eng: Jesus and his disciples came to the other side of the lake, to the region of the Gerasenes.
local: Bi nìn dūtì lapu mɔɔgbandi Geeraseen yab tiŋkin.

eng: They pleaded with Jesus not to send them out of that region.
local: Ní u gbāam̀ Yeesu tikpil ki lī yii: «Taa jà-ti kitiŋ nee pu!».

eng: A large herd of pigs was feeding on the hillside.
local: Dijool diba nìn bí niin, di-caŋinee digbeetikitikpaandi diba nìn sí ki jīǹ.

eng: Send us into the pigs! they begged. Let us go into the pigs.
local: Arasiniibee gbāam̀-u yii: «Càntī tí cùú kɔ́ igbeeti nee ni.».

eng: Jesus gave them permission, so the unclean spirits left the man and went into the pigs. Then the herd of about two thousand pigs rushed down the cliff into the lake and drowned.
local: Ní u pú-bi mpaan, ní bi nyáń uwaatee ni ki kɔ́ igbeetin, ní digbeetikitilee sáǹ ki wúntì nsitimin ki jéetì dimɔɔlin ki múĺ ki kpíì. Igbeetee nìn bàn̄ kutukub bili nyɔkɔm.

eng: Those who tended the pigs ran away and told the story in the city and in the countryside. People came to see what had happened.
local: Ní bin nìn kpàá igbeetee sáǹ ki cūtì kitiŋin nì ikaan ki máafì an bɔti. Binib nyánní kí dāá kpèè tin di ŋée.

eng: They came to Jesus and saw the man who used to be demon-possessed. They saw the very man who had been filled with many demons sitting there fully dressed and completely sane, and they were filled with awe.
local: ki bāań Yeesu ceeyee ki ká uwaati un pu kurasiniipaau nìn kēe kā ki pēē tiwan ki dàkafī nnimɔntiyaamin. Bi ká-uee, ní tifaandi kɔ́-bi.

eng: Those who had actually seen what had happened to the demon-possessed man told the others about the pigs.
local: Bin nìn ká tin jítèe máafì bin kíǹee an nín ŋá pu ní uwaatee pɔ́ɔkì ní igbeeti kpíìyee.

eng: Then they pleaded with Jesus to leave their region.
local: Ŋyunti gbantee ní bi kíĺ ki bíi gbàam̄ Yeesu yii ú yīkì bi-tiŋki pu.

eng: While he was climbing into the boat, the one who had been demon-possessed pleaded with Jesus to let him come along as one of his disciples.
local: Yeesu bíi kóò buŋalimbunee, ní uninja un nìn wáatèe gbāam̀-u yii ú yàntī ú táá-u kí nīn kíǹ ki bí u-cee.

eng: But Jesus wouldnt allow it. Go home to your own people, Jesus said, and tell them what the Lord has done for you and how he has shown you mercy.
local: Ama ní Yeesu kaa pú-u nsan, ní ki bí-u yii: «Nín̄ kùǹ a-mɔŋkiŋu a-yab cee kí māafì-bi min ní ti-Dindaan súń a-saayee nì tin kɔkɔ u ŋá ki tīī-see.».

eng: As soon as Jesus got out of the boat, a man possessed by an evil spirit came out of the tombs.
local: Yeesu kpákatì buŋalimbunee, kpalaayee, kijapaai kiba kin pu arasinii kēe nyánní kuceeun ki túŋkiń-u.

eng: The man went away and began to proclaim in the Ten Cities all that Jesus had done for him, and everyone was amazed.
local: Ní kijapaayee búntì ki kíĺ ki máafí ntimu saalaa jaaŋŋi kɔkɔn tin kɔkɔ Yeesu ŋá ki tīī-uee, ní báà ŋma bākatì sèeee.

eng: Jesus crossed the lake again, and on the other side a large crowd gathered around him on the shore.
local: Yeesu kɔ́ buŋalimbun ki fātìi dūtì dimɔɔgbandi. U dáá bí dimɔɔgbandee ní dinikitikpaandi dɔ́miń ki kpáfì u-cee.

eng: Jairus, one of the synagogue leaders, came forward. When he saw Jesus, he fell at his feet.
local: Kukpafidii kpiliu ubɔ nìn dɔ́miń, bi yíì-u Jayirusi. U ká Yeesuee, ní ki gbāaǹ u-nimbiin.

eng: and pleaded with him, My daughter is about to die. Please, come and place your hands on her so that she can be healed and live.
local: ki mēē-u nì igbaam poon ki bí yii: «M-sapɔmbiki būǹ ki làá kpú. Á jī kimɔtii kí pée dāan̄ kí dū a-ŋal kí páań ki-pu, kí cáaǹ-kì kí ŋmátí.».

eng: So Jesus went with him. A swarm of people were following Jesus, crowding in on him.
local: Ní u nì Yeesu búntì, ní dinikitilee fāā ki bīiǹ u-boon bɔ̀mmm.

eng: A woman was there who had been bleeding for twelve years.
local: Unimpu ubɔ nìn ŋmàl̄ niin ki fùul̀ ŋkpin an kaa gúǹ áli abin saalaa nì ali.

eng: She had suffered a lot under the care of many doctors, and had spent everything she had without getting any better. In fact, she had gotten worse.
local: U nìn jíń falaa tikpil ki nìn bíil̀ u-nimbiliŋ mun kɔkɔ binyɔɔdambi tikpil cee, kaa kpáàa ká dicáaǹdi, ní an mɔ̄tìi púntì-u.

eng: Because she had heard about Jesus, she came up behind him in the crowd and touched his clothes.
local: Unimpuee nìn gbìl̄ bi sòon̄ Yeesu pu, ní u dɔ́miń kunipaaun ki dīń Yeesu boon pú ki gbēē u-bɔkutil.

eng: She was thinking, If I can just touch his clothes, Ill be healed.
local: Di sá u nìn lī u-pɔbilin yii: «M yaan pée ŋúń ki gbēē u-bɔkutil kpaŋkpam, m bàà kāntí dicáaǹdi.».

eng: Her bleeding stopped immediately, and she sensed in her body that her illness had been healed.
local: Niinee, fatiku un nyēē-uee kpáàa gúń, ní u gbìl̄ u-wuntin yii u pɔ́ɔkì.

eng: This man lived among the tombs, and no one was ever strong enough to restrain him, even with a chain.
local: U nìn kóò akaakulin ní. Báà ubɔ kaa nìn ŋūǹ kí bóob́-u, báà bi yaa dū ikusaka gba.

eng: At that very moment, Jesus recognized that power had gone out from him. He turned around in the crowd and said, Who touched my clothes.
local: Kpalaayee Yeesu gbìl̄ u-ban yii mpɔn ŋubɔ nyáń u-ni ní u fātì kunipaauee pu ki bí yii: «Ŋma di gbēē m-bɔkutil?».

eng: His disciples said to him, Dont you see the crowd pressing against you? Yet you ask, Who touched me.
local: Ní u-boonnooliibi kíí-u yii: «Sii wāĺ kunipaau téekí-si yéè, ní a bàlifī yii: <Ŋma gbēē-maaa?>».

eng: But Jesus looked around carefully to see who had done it.
local: Yeesu kpēē ki māntì ki tú ki nyàab̀ kí béé un di ŋá mimmee.

eng: The woman, full of fear and trembling, came forward. Knowing what had happened to her, she fell down in front of Jesus and told him the whole truth.
local: Niinee, tifaandi cúú unimpuee, u-wunti kíĺ ki bíi jìkì, kun puee u nyí tin di jítì u-puee. Ní u dɔ́miń ki tātìi gbāaǹ Yeesu taaŋi taapu ní ki kàan̄-u tikɔkɔ nì ibaamɔn.

eng: He responded, Daughter, your faith has healed you; go in peace, healed from your disease.
local: Niinee ní Yeesu bí-u yii: «M-bisal, a nín fōō ki kíí puee fīī-si. Nín̄ cá nì ŋgbansɔŋfi, a-weeŋyi ń yàntī-si.».

eng: While Jesus was still speaking with her, messengers came from the synagogue leaders house, saying to Jairus, Your daughter has died. Why bother the teacher any longer.
local: Waa láá ŋmíl̀, ní kukpafidii kpiliuee yab biba nyánní u-dumpu ki bāań ki bí yii: «A-bisal kpíì. Taa ti páb́ ukpil yii ú dāan̄.».

eng: But Jesus overheard their report and said to the synagogue leader, Dont be afraid; just keep trusting.
local: Yeesu gbìl̄ dinyɔɔbundi din bi lēe, ní ki tùkū kukpafidii kpiliuee yii: «Taa yàntī a-pɔbil ń bīil̀! Sii dee fōō man kí kíí.».

eng: He didnt allow anyone to follow him except Peter, James, and John, James brother.
local: Waa yāntì báà ubɔ céeǹ-u ki kútì Peetroo nì Yakubu nì Yoowaneesi, Yakubu naal pu.

eng: They came to the synagogue leaders house, and he saw a commotion, with people crying and wailing loudly.
local: Bi nín bàn̄ kukpafidii kpiliuee dumpuee, Yeesu ká binib gà dituweel an tùm̀ dàiiyee.

eng: He went in and said to them, Whats all this commotion and crying about? The child isnt dead. Shes only sleeping.
local: ní u kɔ́ ki bí-bi yii: «Ba pu ní ni sūǹ ki ŋáań kuwoou míǹ? Kibikee kaa kpíì, ki gèeǹ ní.».

eng: He had been secured many times with leg irons and chains, but he broke the chains and smashed the leg irons. No one was tough enough to control him.
local: Di sá bi nìn bāā-u abaa ki gbìǹ-u ikusaka mun, ní ú tìfī ikusakee ní kí wìil̀ abaa mun. Ubɔ kaa nìn gíti ŋūǹ kí pèefì-u.

eng: They laughed at him, but he threw them all out. Then, taking the childs parents and his disciples with him, he went to the room where the child was.
local: Ní bi bíi bèel̄-u, ní u jìn̄ bi-kɔkɔ ki nyántì, ní ki yōoǹ kibiki baa nì ki-na nì u-boonnooliibi bita bin nìn céeǹní-uee, ki kɔ́ǹ laakin kibikee dɔ̄ee.

eng: Taking her hand, he said to her, Talitha koum , which means, Young woman, get up.
local: ní u cúú kibikee ŋal ki bí-kì yii: « Taliita, kumii .» An taapu di sá yii: «Kisapɔmbiki, yīkì, sii ní m lì!».

eng: Suddenly the young woman got up and began to walk around. She was years old. They were shocked!.
local: Kpalaayee ní kibikee nyīī yíkì ki bíi còom̄. Kì nìn cáá abin saalaa nì ali ní. Ní bin nìn bí niinee bākatì sèeee.

eng: He gave them strict orders that no one should know what had happened. Then he told them to give her something to eat.
local: Ama ní Yeesu kpáafì-bi tiŋan bí taa yàntī báà ubɔ ń béé, ní ki bí-bi yii, «Tīīmaan-kì tijin.».

eng: Night and day in the tombs and the hills, he would howl and cut himself with stones.
local: U nìn bí ní ki yìiǹ ŋwiimpu nì kunyeeu kɔkɔ akaakulin nì ajoon pu ki kūuntí ní ki cáá ataŋkpee ki gífí u-ba.

eng: When he saw Jesus from far away, he ran and knelt before him.
local: U wál̀ Yeesu ndanee, ní ki sáǹní ki dɔ́miń ki tātìi gbāaǹ u-nimbiin.

eng: shouting, What have you to do with me, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? Swear to God that you wont torture me.
local: ní ki fáá mbiil mpɔn yii: «Yeesu Unimbɔti un bí yilpuee Jipɔɔn, man nì si yii ba ní? M dū Unimbɔti ki gbāam̀-si, taa sàaǹ-mi!».

eng: He said this because Jesus had already commanded him, Unclean spirit, come out of the man.
local: U nìn lī míǹ kun puee Yeesu nìn lī-u yii: «Arasinii, nyā uninja nee ni!».

eng: Jesus asked him, What is your name? He responded, Legion is my name, because we are many.
local: Ní Yeesu bālifì-u yii: «Bi yíì-si yii ba?» Ní uwaatee kíí-u yii: «M-yindi di sá yii: <Kusoojapaau,> kun puee ti wīikí tikpil.».

eng: Jesus left that place and came to his hometown. His disciples followed him.
local: Yeesu nyáń niinee, ki nín kùǹ u-tiŋkin laakin u kāl̀ ki wíiǹee, ní u-boonnooliibi táá-u.

eng: He said, Whatever house you enter, remain there until you leave that place.
local: U tùkū-bi yii: «Ni yaa kɔ́ kudii kun nee, nín̄ bímaan niin kí nīn cí daliŋkin ni làá yīkèe.

eng: If a place doesnt welcome you or listen to you, as you leave, shake the dust off your feet as a witness against them.
local: Ni yaa bàn̄ laakin binib yaa yíì, kaa là kí fōō-ni, kaa tí là kí pīlifiǹ-nee, nyāmaan niin kí gbāfì ni-taaŋi kpɔŋkpɔm, kí dàkà yii ni sòon̄ ki kpáafì-bi.».

eng: So they went out and proclaimed that people should change their hearts and lives.
local: Ní bi búntì ki cūtìi bíi sòoǹ nì binib yii bí kpántí bi-bimbim.

eng: They cast out many demons, and they anointed many sick people with olive oil and healed them.
local: Bi nìn jì arasiniibi tikpil biwaatiibi pu, ní ki nìn bàtī ŋkpam ki jòotī bibunliibi tikpil pu ki cāantí-bi.

eng: Herod the king heard about these things, because the name of Jesus had become well-known. Some were saying, John the Baptist has been raised from the dead, and this is why miraculous powers are at work through him.
local: Ubɔti Eeroodi nìn gbìl̄ bi sòoǹ Yeesu pu, kun puee u-yindi nìn gbītì ajan kɔkɔwoooo. Binib biba nìn sòoǹ Yeesu pu yii: «An sá Yoowaneesi un nìn fùul̀ binib Unimbɔti nyimee di fīkitì ditaŋkpiilin, an pu ní u ŋūǹ ki ŋáań maamaacib.».

eng: Others were saying, He is Elijah. Still others were saying, He is a prophet like one of the ancient prophets.
local: Biba lì yii: «An sá Eelii.» Ní bin kíǹee mun lì yii: «U sá Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu kii bitiŋkpikib bɔnaatiliibi ubɔee ní.».

eng: But when Herod heard these rumors, he said, John, whom I beheaded, has been raised to life.
local: Eeroodi, úǹ nín gbìl̄ee, ní u lī yii: «Yoowaneesi un man yāntì bi tīl̀ u-yilee, di fīkitì.».

eng: He said this because Herod himself had arranged to have John arrested and put in prison because of Herodias, the wife of Herods brother Philip. Herod had married her.
local: Di sá yii Eeroodi nìn tīī mara bí cúú Yoowaneesi kí bóob́-u kí cáá tɔ̄ sarka. U-maan Fiiliipi nimpuu Eeroodiyadi un u nìn cááyee pu ní u nìn ŋá míǹ.

eng: but John told Herod, Its against the law for you to marry your brothers wife.
local: Di sá yii Yoowaneesi bí Eeroodi yii: «An kaa máaǹ á cáá a-maan nimpuu.».

eng: So Herodias had it in for John. She wanted to kill him, but she couldnt.
local: Eeroodiyadi nìn fōō Yoowaneesi pu kudɔ̀ŋu ki nyàab̀ kí kpɔ̀-u, ama waa nìn ŋūǹ.

eng: On the Sabbath, he began to teach in the synagogue. Many who heard him were surprised. Where did this man get all this? Whats this wisdom hes been given? What about the powerful acts accomplished through him?.
local: Ŋwiŋŋuŋfikaa bāańee, ní u kíĺ ki bíi dàkà binib Unimbɔti bɔti kukpafidiin. Kunipaau kun nìn bí ki pílifíǹ-uee bākatì ki bíi lì yii: «U nín kánní la tin u sòoǹ nan dee? U nín kánní la iciin nee bulifi dee? An dī mana ní u ŋūǹ ki ŋáań maamaacib nee?.

eng: This was because Herod respected John. He regarded him as a righteous and holy person, so he protected him. Johns words greatly confused Herod, yet he enjoyed listening to him.
local: Di sá yii Eeroodi, úǹ nìn fàŋkí Yoowaneesi ki nyí yii Yoowaneesi sá ibaamɔndaan ki sá Unimbɔti tɔntɔnliu. An puee Eeroodi nìn mɔ́tí u-pu. Yoowaneesi yaa nìn sòoǹee, an nìn páb̀ Eeroodi, ama u nìn néeń ki pílifíǹ-u.

eng: Finally, the time was right. It was on one of Herods birthdays, when he had prepared a feast for his high-ranking officials and military officers and Galilees leading residents.
local: Dalbee, Eeroodiyadi ká nsan ŋun u làá dī kí kpɔ̀ Yoowaneesee. Di sá yii Eeroodi nìn bíi jīǹ u-maaliwiiŋŋu jiŋaaliu. U nìn yíiń u-kpilib nì bisoojakpilib nì Galiilee yidambi kɔkɔ.

eng: Herods daughter Herodias came in and danced, thrilling Herod and his dinner guests. The king said to the young woman, Ask me whatever you wish, and I will give it to you.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun bi kpáfèe, Eeroodiyadi bisal kóoń bi-kansikin ki ŋɔ́ń ní u-ŋoolee fáatì ubɔti Eeroodi nì bin kɔkɔ u yíińee, ní u bí usapɔɔnee yii: «Mèè-mi tin kɔkɔ a lèe, m làá tīī-si!».

eng: Then he swore to her, Whatever you ask I will give to you, even as much as half of my kingdom.
local: Ní u pūtì ki lī yii: «Tin kamaa a yaa bālifì-mee, m làá tīī-si, báà an yaa sá m-beel dijandi, m làá tīī-si!».

eng: She left the banquet hall and said to her mother, What should I ask for? John the Baptists head, Herodias replied.
local: Niinee ní usapɔɔnee nyáń ki cūtì ki bālifì u-na yii: «M-na, ḿ mèè ba?» Ní u-na kíí-u yii: «Lì yii a là Yoowaneesi un fùul̀ binib Unimbɔti nyimee yil.».

eng: Hurrying back to the ruler, she made her request: I want you to give me John the Baptists head on a plate, right this minute.
local: Ní u fātìi tóotì ki gítì ubɔti cee ki bí-u yii: «M là yii á tùkutī Yoowaneesi un fùul̀ binib Unimbɔti nyimee yil kí tàkan̄-mi kpaatan kɔŋkɔnnee.».

eng: Although the king was upset, because of his solemn pledge and his guests, he didnt want to refuse her.
local: Niinee, kunimbaau kɔ́ ubɔti. U mun gíti dáá pūtì u-nicambi bin u yíińee nimbiin, waa ti ŋūǹ kí yíí.

eng: So he ordered a guard to bring Johns head. The guard went to the prison, cut off Johns head.
local: Ní u tú dicilpu niin u-soojau ubɔ ki tùkū-u yii ú cùú cáań Yoowaneesi yil. Ní soojee cūtì ki kɔ́ kusarkadiin ki lafun tūkutìń Yoowaneesi yil.

eng: brought his head on a plate, and gave it to the young woman, and she gave it to her mother.
local: ki tākaǹní kpaatan, ki cáań ki ŋúbíǹ usapɔɔnee, ní usapɔɔnee mun cáa tīī u-na.

eng: When Johns disciples heard what had happened, they came and took his dead body and laid it in a tomb.
local: Yoowaneesi boonnooliibi nín gbìl̄ an bɔtee, ní bi dɔ́miń ki yōoǹ-u ki cáa síb́.

eng: Isnt this the carpenter? Isnt he Marys son and the brother of James, Joses, Judas, and Simon? Arent his sisters here with us? They were repulsed by him and fell into sin.
local: Ti nyí-uaa! An sá Maarii jipɔɔn kapintaa! U-naalib mun di sá Yakubu nì Joosee nì Juudi nì Siimɔn. U-niisalib mun di kóò ti-kansikin neeyaa!» Ní an yāntì baa ti ŋūǹ kí fōō-u kí kíí.

eng: The apostles returned to Jesus and told him everything they had done and taught.
local: Yeesu kpambaliŋ ŋin u nìn túee kpáfì u-cee ki máafì-u bi nín cūtì ki ŋá tiŋyee nì tin bi dàkēe.

eng: Many people were coming and going, so there was no time to eat. He said to the apostles, Come by yourselves to a secluded place and rest for a while.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-bi yii: «Dāam̄maan tí kàkatī ŋkpaanceeti, laakin binib kpée ní ŋùŋfì fiii.» Kun puee binib nìn cáá ní lìbiti libiti. Biba kùǹ ní biba bàń, an yāntì Yeesu nì u-kpambaliŋ kaa nìn cáá mpaan kí jī tijin.

eng: They departed in a boat by themselves for a deserted place.
local: Ní bi kɔ́ buŋalimbun ki kākatì laakin binib kaa bée.

eng: Many people saw them leaving and recognized them, so they ran ahead from all the cities and arrived before them.
local: Binib tikpil ká bi nín cée ní ki béè-bi, ní ki sáǹ ki nyáń ntimun ki dū ataa ki péeǹ-bi ki bàn̄ laakin bi cée.

eng: When Jesus arrived and saw a large crowd, he had compassion on them because they were like sheep without a shepherd. Then he began to teach them many things.
local: Yeesu kpákatì buŋalimbunee, ki ká kunipaau sí kii ipii in kaa cáá ukpaalee. An nìn kɔ́-u falaa ní u kíĺ ki bíi dàkà-bi tibɔti tikpil.

eng: Late in the day, his disciples came to him and said, This is an isolated place, and its already late in the day.
local: Kujoou bíi jóotée, ní u-boonnooliibi sútìń ki pìl̄-u ki tùkū-u yii: «Binib kaa kóò laakin ti sí nee, kí wàatēe, kujoou mun bíi jóotí.

eng: Send them away so that they can go to the surrounding countryside and villages and buy something to eat for themselves.
local: Yàntī binib nee ń cù ikaan nì ntimbilimu mun pìlīǹ dooyee kí dā tijin kí jī.».

eng: He replied, You give them something to eat. But they said to him, Should we go off and buy bread worth almost eight months pay and give it to them to eat.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-bi yii: «Nimbi ni-ba, tīīmaan-bi tijin bí jī!» Ní bi fātìi bālifì-u yii: «Mimmee, ti dù animbil jitɔmbi alaataa ali ní kí dā tijin kí tīī bí jī deeyaaa?».

eng: He said to them, How much bread do you have? Take a look. After checking, they said, Five loaves of bread and two fish.
local: Ní Yeesu mun fātìi bālifì-bi yii: «Kpɔnɔb biŋa ní ni cáá? Cùú kpèèmaan!» Bi cūtìi bālifì ki béèyee, ní ki bí-u yii: «Ti cáá kpɔnɔb biŋmoo nì ijɔm ili.».

eng: He directed the disciples to seat all the people in groups as though they were having a banquet on the green grass.
local: Niinee ní u tùkū u-boonnooliibi bí tùkù binib ń kàl̄ akiti akiti kumɔsɔŋu pu.

eng: Jesus said to them, Prophets are honored everywhere except in their own hometowns, among their relatives, and in their own households.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-bi yii: «Laaba yab kaa kpèé Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu fám, see u-tiŋkin yab nì u nì bin pēēyee nì u-cindin yab.».

eng: They sat down in groups of hundreds and fifties.
local: Ní bi kāl̀ kitiŋ. Akiti aba cáá binib dilaataal, aba mun cáá imuŋku ili nì saalaa.

eng: He took the five loaves and the two fish, looked up to heaven, blessed them, broke the loaves into pieces, and gave them to his disciples to set before the people. He also divided the two fish among them all.
local: Yeesu fōō kpɔnɔb biŋmoo nì ijɔm ilee ki wáaǹ ki kpēē kutaa pu ki jáam̀ Unimbɔti tijinee pu, ní ki gífì gífì kpɔnɔb ki tīī u-boonnooliibi bí yàkatī binib. U yākatì ijɔm ilee mun ki tīī bi-kɔkɔ.

eng: Everyone ate until they were full.
local: ní bi-kɔkɔ jíń ki díkí.

eng: They filled twelve baskets with the leftover pieces of bread and fish.
local: Ní u-boonnooliibi tōō akpɔnɔkati nì ajɔŋgin ŋin kíǹee ki gbéeǹ tikabiti saalaa nì tili.

eng: About five thousand had eaten.
local: Bin nìn jíń tijin gbantee nìn nyáń bininjab kutukub biŋmoo ní.

eng: Right then, Jesus made his disciples get into a boat and go ahead to the other side of the lake, toward Bethsaida, while he dismissed the crowd.
local: Niinee ní Yeesu bí u-boonnooliibi yii bíǹ ń kɔ́ buŋalimbu kí lìntī malaa kí dùtī dimɔɔl Beetisayida watil pú, ŋyunti gbantee úǹ ń yàntī samaayee ń kpì.

eng: After saying good-bye to them, Jesus went up onto a mountain to pray.
local: U yāntì samaayee nín kùǹee ní u jɔ́m̀ dijool pu kí mèè.

eng: Evening came and the boat was in the middle of the lake, but he was alone on the land.
local: Kunyeeu bíi móotée u-boonnooliibi bàn̄ dimɔɔl kansikin, Yeesu úǹ dáá bíń dimɔɔgbandi u-baba.

eng: He saw his disciples struggling. They were trying to row forward, but the wind was blowing against them. Very early in the morning, he came to them, walking on the lake. He intended to pass by them.
local: U nìn ká yii u-boonnooliibi bí ki ŋàal̀, an kaa cá, kun puee kubuŋu nìn túŋkiń-bi. Ki cá kutaawuntuŋuee ní u tɔ́kɔǹ nnyim pu ki còom̄ ki cá bi-cee ki làá jītí bi-pu.

eng: When they saw him walking on the lake, they thought he was a ghost and they screamed.
local: Bi nín ká u còomìń nnyim pu ki dòońee, ní bi dākafì yii an sá ufaŋfanti, ní bi kíĺ ki bíi gbáà ibiil.

eng: He was unable to do any miracles there, except that he placed his hands on a few sick people and healed them.
local: Waa nìn gíti ŋúń ki ŋá maamaaci tuuŋŋi niin, see bibunliibi ŋwaam pu ní u nìn dū u-ŋalii ki páaǹ ki cáaǹ-bi.

eng: Seeing him was terrifying to all of them. Just then he spoke to them, Be encouraged! Its me. Dont be afraid.
local: kun puee bi-kɔkɔ nìn wál̀-u ní bi-pɔbiliŋ bíil̀. Kpalaayee, Yeesu sòon̄ nì bi yii: «Cúúmaan ni-pɔbiliŋ, an sá man! Taa fàŋkìmaan!».

eng: He got into the boat, and the wind settled down. His disciples were so baffled they were beside themselves.
local: Niinee ní u jɔ́m̀ bi-cee buŋalimbun, ní kubuŋu mun ŋmíl̀. Tifaandi nìn kɔ́-bi, ní bi kāl̀ ki bākatì.

eng: Thats because they hadnt understood about the loaves. Their minds had been closed so that they resisted Gods ways.
local: Di sá yii baa nìn gbìl̄ Yeesu nín nìn dū kpɔnɔb fiii ki būtì ki kpíiǹ kunipaauee taapu, kun puee an nìn pɔ̄ɔ̄ ki tīī-bi bí gbìl̄ tibɔti taapu.

eng: When Jesus and his disciples had crossed the lake, they landed at Gennesaret, anchored the boat.
local: Bi dūtì dimɔɔl ki dóòyee, ki bàn̄ Geeneesareeti tiŋkin ki síl̀ dimɔɔgbandi.

eng: and came ashore. People immediately recognized Jesus.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun bi kpákatì buŋalimbunee, binib kpáàa béè Yeesu.

eng: and ran around that whole region bringing sick people on their mats to wherever they heard he was.
local: ní ki sáǹ ki cūtì ntimun ki máafì ní binib yòontiń bibunliibi ki tūntiń ilún pu ki cāabìń laakin Yeesu bée.

eng: Wherever he went—villages, cities, or farming communities—they would place the sick in the marketplaces and beg him to allow them to touch even the hem of his clothing. Everyone who touched him was healed.
local: Míǹ ní báà laakin ní u yaa nìn bàn̄ee, ntiŋgbamuneeee, ntiŋkpaamuneeee, ikaaneeee, binib nìn cāabìń bibunliibi ki bīil̀ imɔŋkijaa ní ki gbàam̄-u yii ú pée yàntī bibunliibee ń dáb́ u-bɔkutijúti. Bin kɔkɔ yaa nìn gbēē-uee pɔ́ɔkí ní.

eng: He was appalled by their disbelief. Then Jesus traveled through the surrounding villages teaching.
local: Ní u bākatì bi nín kaa fōō-u ki kííyee. Ŋyunti gbantee, Yeesu nìn yìiǹ ntimbilimu nì ikpaanceeti ki dàkà binib Unimbɔti bɔti.

eng: He called for the Twelve and sent them out in pairs. He gave them authority over unclean spirits.
local: Ní u kpáfì u-kpambaliŋ saalaa nì alee, ní ki tú-bi bili bili, ní ki tīī-bi mpɔn bí nīn jì arasiniibi.

eng: He instructed them to take nothing for the journey except a walking stick—no bread, no bags, and no money in their belts.
local: ní ki kpáafì-bi yii: «Ni yaa cée, ní taa nín̄ ŋúb́ tiba see ŋkpandɔ baba. Ní taa nín̄ ŋúb́ tijin kí taa yòon̄ atɔkɔl kí taa nín̄ fúuń animbil mun.

eng: He told them to wear sandals but not to put on two shirts.
local: kí taa pèen̄ abɔkuti ali ali, ama kí nīn táká anaataka.».

eng: The Pharisees and some legal experts from Jerusalem gathered around Jesus.
local: Fariisab nì bimaradakaliibi biba nìn nyánní Jeerusaleem ki dɔ́miń ki kpáfì Yeesu cee.

eng: Moses said, Honor your father and your mother, and The person who speaks against father or mother will certainly be put to death .
local: Mooyiisi nìn lī u-marabin yii: <Á nīn tɔ́ a-baa nì a-na,> ní ki gíti lī yii: <Un yaa sòon̄ ki bíil̀ u-baa kii u-nee, see bí kpɔ̀-u.>.

eng: But you say, If you tell your father or mother,Everything Im expected to contribute to you is corban .
local: Ama nimbi lì yii, unil yaa cáá tiba ki bàà ŋūǹ kí tīī u-na kii u-baayee, u ŋūǹ kí lì yii: <An wanti sá Kɔɔban >, .

eng: then you are no longer required to care for your father or mother.
local: U yaa lī mimmee, naa ti yàntī ú ŋá tiba kí tīī u-baa kii u-na.

eng: In this way you do away with Gods word in favor of the rules handed down to you, which you pass on to others. And you do a lot of other things just like that.
local: Míǹ ní ni cáá nimbi ni-ba koobiliŋ ŋin ni-baabi dàkā-nee ki cáa kpìtī Unimbɔti bɔti. Tiwan tikpil bí ni ŋáań míǹ mbaantiim.».

eng: Then Jesus called the crowd again and said, Listen to me, all of you, and understand.
local: Míǹ boonee, Yeesu gíti yíiń kunipaau u-cee, ní ki tùkū-bi yii: «Ni-kɔkɔ ń pīlifìmaan kí gbìl̄ m nín làá lì puee taapu!.

eng: Nothing outside of a person can enter and contaminate a person in Gods sight; rather, the things that come out of a person contaminate the person.
local: Tiba kaa nyēeń dipaal pu kí kɔ́ unilin kí kɔ́ǹ-u tijɔkɔndi Unimbɔti nimbiin. An sá tin nyēeń unilinee di kɔ̄ntí-u tijɔkɔndi Unimbɔti nimbiin. .

eng: After leaving the crowd, he entered a house where his disciples asked him about that riddle.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun u yíkì kunipaau cee ki kɔ́ kudiinee, ní u-boonnooliibi bālifì-u dilaŋkpandi din u bàà gāāyee taapu.

eng: He said to them, Dont you understand either? Dont you know that nothing from the outside that enters a person has the power to contaminate?.
local: Ní u kíí-bi yii: «Ni mun kaa cáá iciin níii? Naa gbìl̀ yii tin kɔkɔ nyēeń dipaal pu ki kóò unilinee kaa ŋūǹ kí kɔ́ǹ-u tijɔkɔndi Unimbɔti nimbiinaaa?.

eng: Thats because it doesnt enter into the heart but into the stomach, and it goes out into the sewer. By saying this, Jesus declared that no food could contaminate a person in Gods sight.
local: Di sá yii tijikaal kaa kóò unil pɔbilin, an kóò u-poon ní, ní ú cù ataa pu.» Yeesu lī míǹ ki dàkā yii tijikaal kɔkɔ ŋān̄ ki tīī unil ń jī.

eng: They saw some of his disciples eating food with unclean hands. They were eating without first ritually purifying their hands through washing.
local: Bi bí niinee, ní ki ká Yeesu boonnooliibi biba jīǹ, kaa fíntì bi-ŋalii kii bi-yaajakoobiliŋ nín sá puee.

eng: Its what comes out of a person that contaminates someone in Gods sight, he said.
local: U gíti lī yii: «Tin nyēeń unilinee di sá tin kɔ̄ntí-u tijɔkɔndi Unimbɔti nimbiinee.

eng: Its from the inside, from the human heart, that evil thoughts come: sexual sins, thefts, murders.
local: Di sá yii binib pɔbiliŋin ní ilandɔkɔkpiti kɔkɔ nyēeń: binaacimpɔmbi nì bisapɔmbi jɔkɔnti, bunaayubu, nnikpaakoon.

eng: adultery, greed, evil actions, deceit, unrestrained immorality, envy, insults, arrogance, and foolishness.
local: binimpoobi nì bininjab laakpaati, ikummunti, digaŋgam, diŋmatindi, bimaramɔntiibi fátam, nnimbidiki, tibɔfan, timɔn nì ŋyipɔn.

eng: All these evil things come from the inside and contaminate a person in Gods sight.
local: Tibɔkpitil gbanti kɔkɔwoooo nyēeń binib pɔbiliŋin ní ki kɔ̄ntí-bi tijɔkɔndi.».

eng: Jesus left that place and went into the region of Tyre. He didnt want anyone to know that he had entered a house, but he couldnt hide.
local: Yeesu yíkì niinee, ki póotì Tiir nì Siidɔn jaaŋŋu pú. U bàn̄ ki kɔ́ kudii kubɔn, kaa bàà là yii báà ubɔ ń béé u-pu. Nì míǹ kɔkɔee, waa ŋúń ki bál̀.

eng: In fact, a woman whose young daughter was possessed by an unclean spirit heard about him right away. She came and fell at his feet.
local: Unimpu ubɔ nìn bí, ki cáá kisapɔmbiki arasinii kā ki-pu. U nín gbìl̄ bi sòon̄ Yeesu puee, waa ti kāl̀. U yíkìń ní malaa, ki dɔ́miń ki tātìi gbāaǹ u-taaŋi taapu.

eng: The woman was Greek, Syrophoenician by birth. She begged Jesus to throw the demon out of her daughter.
local: Unimpu gbanti kaa nìn sá Ujuudanimpu, u sá Feenisii, Siirii tiŋkin ní. U mēē Yeesu ń jà arasiniiyee u-bisal pu.

eng: He responded, The children have to be fed first. It isnt right to take the childrens bread and toss it to the dogs.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-u yii: «Yàntī mbiyaamu ń díkí kí wàatī. Kun puee an kaa máaǹ bí dū mbiyaamu jinti kí tīī mbɔbiyaamu!».

eng: But she answered, Lord, even the dogs under the table eat the childrens crumbs.
local: Ní unimpuee fātì ki kíí-u yii: «Tiŋman, an sá míǹ ní, m-Dindaan. Ama mbiyaamu yaa bíi jīǹ an gbátée, mbɔbiyaamu mun tòó igbasigbasi ní.».

eng: Good answer! he said. Go on home. The demon has already left your daughter.
local: Niinee, ní Yeesu bí-u yii: «A kíí tiŋan! Míǹ puee, nín̄ kùǹ. Arasiniiyee nyáń a-bisalin.».

eng: The Pharisees and all the Jews dont eat without first washing their hands carefully. This is a way of observing the rules handed down by the elders.
local: Di sá yii Fariisab nì Juuda yab bin kíǹee kɔkɔ yaa kaa fíntì bi-ŋalii tiŋan, kii bi-naanjab nín nìn ŋáań puee, baa jīǹ.

eng: When she returned to her house, she found the child lying on the bed and the demon gone.
local: Unimpuee fātìi gítìń u-dumpuee, arasiniiyee nyáń u-bisalin u pɔ́ɔkì ki dɔ̄ u-dooŋu pu.

eng: After leaving the region of Tyre, Jesus went through Sidon toward the Galilee Sea through the region of the Ten Cities.
local: Yeesu yíkì Tiir tiŋkin ki fātìi dīń Siidɔn, ki jítìń ntimu saalaa pu ki nín dòoń Galiilee mɔɔl watil pú.

eng: Some people brought to him a man who was deaf and could hardly speak, and they begged him to place his hand on the man for healing.
local: U bàn̄ niinee, bi cáań-u umɔɔmiin ubɔ ní ki mēē-u yii ú dū u-ŋalii kí páań u-pu.

eng: Jesus took him away from the crowd by himself and put his fingers in the mans ears. Then he spit and touched the mans tongue.
local: Ní Yeesu cáá-u ki kākatì ŋkpaanceeti u nì u liitil, ki dū u-ŋambiyaamu ki bɔ́ umɔɔmiinee tafaliŋin, ní ki tíil̀ timɔtan ki dūu gbēē umɔɔmiinee limbil.

eng: Looking into heaven, Jesus sighed deeply and said, Ephphatha, which means, Open up.
local: ki wáaǹ ki kpēē kutaa ki fáá diniindi, ní ki lī yii: « Eefata !» An taapu sá yii: «Cúú ŋátí!».

eng: At once, his ears opened, his twisted tongue was released, and he began to speak clearly.
local: Niin niin ní umɔɔmiinee tafaliŋ cúu ŋátì, ní u-limbil mun bútì, ní u kíǹ ki sòoǹ tiŋan.

eng: Jesus gave the people strict orders not to tell anyone. But the more he tried to silence them, the more eagerly they shared the news.
local: Ní Yeesu kpáafì-bi bí taa pée māafì báà ubɔ tin jítèe. Ama Yeesu yaa nìn mɔ́tì yii bí taa lèe, míǹ ní bíǹ làá nín mɔ̀tīí lì.

eng: People were overcome with wonder, saying, He does everything well! He even makes the deaf to hear and gives speech to those who cant speak.
local: Bi nìn bākatì tikpil, ní ki lì yii: «Tin kɔkɔ u ŋáańee ŋān̄ ki lákatì, áli ki yàntī bitafakpaafub gbìl̀, bimɔɔmiimbi sòoǹ!».

eng: Upon returning from the marketplace, they dont eat without first immersing themselves. They observe many other rules that have been handed down, such as the washing of cups, jugs, pans, and sleeping mats.
local: Bi yaa tí nyánní kitiŋin ki bāańee, baa tí jīǹ asee bí fíntí bi-ba nì nnyim kí wàatī, ní kí nín jī. Bi cáá akoobil tikpil ki kútì ŋin bí nee pu: ŋiba sá bí fíntí kɔŋkɔmbi, kí fíntí aboon nì asambil.

eng: So the Pharisees and legal experts asked Jesus, Why are your disciples not living according to the rules handed down by the elders but instead eat food with ritually unclean hands.
local: Tíǹ pu ní Fariisab nì bimaradakaliibee nìn bālifì Yeesu yii: «Ba pu ní a-boonnooliibi kaa ŋúb̀ ti-yaajakoobiliŋ, kaa fíntí ki jīǹ?».

eng: He replied, Isaiah really knew what he was talking about when he prophesied about you hypocrites. He wrote, This people honors me with their lips, but their hearts are far away from me.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-bi yii: «Mpoonnyoondambi, an pu ní Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu Yeesaya ŋmàn̄ u-gbɔŋkun ki sòon̄ ni-pu yii Unimbɔti bí yii: <Binib nee nyɔ́ŋkí-mi nì bi-nyɔmmun fám ní. Ama bi-pɔbiliŋ dátiǹ-mi.

eng: Their worship of me is empty since they teach instructions that are human words.
local: Bi jáaḿ-mi fám ní, kun puee tin binib náańee ní bi dàkà, ní ki cáa yíì man marab.>».

eng: You ignore Gods commandment while holding on to rules created by humans and handed down to you.
local: Ní Yeesu nìkī ki lī-bi yii: «Ni féeń Unimbɔti marab ki fòol̄ nnibiyaamu koobiliŋ.».

eng: Jesus continued, Clearly, you are experts at rejecting Gods commandment in order to establish these rules.
local: Ní u gíti kútì yii: «Ni féeń Unimbɔti marab tiŋan pu kí ŋmā kí ŋúb́ nimbi ni-ba koobiliŋ.

eng: In those days there was another large crowd with nothing to eat. Jesus called his disciples and told them.
local: Iwiin gbanti poonee, kunipaau kubɔ tí fātìi kpáfì u-cee, ní kaa nìn cáá tiba kí jī. An puee Yeesu yíiń u-boonnooliibi ki bí-bi yii.

eng: then got into a boat with his disciples and went over to the region of Dalmanutha.
local: ní u nì u-boonnooliibi kɔ́ buŋalimbun ki nín cá Dalmanuta watil.

eng: The Pharisees showed up and began to argue with Jesus. To test him, they asked for a sign from heaven.
local: Fariisab nìn jīnní Yeesu boon ki bāań ki bíi kpākantí-u kí píĺ-u tibɔti. Bi nìn bālifì-u yii ú ŋá maamaaci ubɔ un nyánní Unimbɔti ceeyee.

eng: With an impatient sigh, Jesus said, Why does this generation look for a sign? I assure you that no sign will be given to it.
local: Ní Yeesu fáá diniindi ní ki lī yii: «Ba pu ní waanee nib nee bàlifī kí ká maamaaci án nīn sá-bi kudaaŋŋu? Ibaamɔn ní m sòoǹ nì ni, ni kpá kí ká maamaaci báà ubɔ!».

eng: Leaving them, he got back in the boat and crossed to the other side of the lake.
local: U lī míǹ ki dóòyee, ní ki fātìi tí kɔ́ buŋalimbun ki dūtì lapu pu mɔɔgbandi ki wāatì-bi.

eng: Jesus disciples had forgotten to bring any bread, so they had only one loaf with them in the boat.
local: Yeesu boonnooliibi nìn súntì kaa kpáaǹ tijin, asee kpɔnɔbaanti di nìn tàkā bi-cee buŋalimbun.

eng: He gave them strict orders: Watch out and be on your guard for the yeast of the Pharisees as well as the yeast of Herod.
local: Ní Yeesu kpáafì-bi ki bí-bi yii: «Nín̄ nyím̀maan ni-ba nì Fariisab nì Eeroodi kpɔnɔnyɔkɔŋu!».

eng: The disciples discussed this among themselves, He said this because we have no bread.
local: U-boonnooliibi dākafì ki lī bi-ŋmaniŋma kansikin yii: «Ti nín kaa ŋúb́ tijinee pu ní u kalin sòoǹ mimmaaa?».

eng: Jesus knew what they were discussing and said, Why are you talking about the fact that you dont have any bread? Dont you grasp what has happened? Dont you understand? Are your hearts so resistant to what God is doing?.
local: Yeesu béè bi-landɔkɔyee ní ki bí-bi yii: «Ba pu ní ni sòoǹ ni nín kaa ŋúbiń kpɔnɔee pu? Nìn díǹ naa láá gbìl̀ tibɔti taapuɔɔɔ? Naa láá béè-maaa?.

eng: Dont you have eyes? Why cant you see? Dont you have ears? Why cant you hear? Dont you remember?.
local: Ni pɔbiliŋ tīlifì níii? Ni cáá animbil fám ní kaa wāĺaaa? Ni cáá atafal ki pílifí ní kaa gbìl̀aaa? Naa cáá nlandɔkɔ.

eng: When I broke five loaves of bread for those five thousand people, how many baskets full of leftovers did you gather? They answered, Twelve.
local: kí téetí ŋyunti ŋun m dū kpɔnɔb biŋmoo ki gífì gífì ki tīī binib kutukub biŋmoo bi ŋmɔ́ ki díkéeee? Tikabiti tiŋa ní ni tōō akati ki gbéeǹ ní?» Ní bi kíí-u yii: «Saalaa nì tili.».

eng: I feel sorry for the crowd because they have been with me for three days and have nothing to eat.
local: «Nnibiim nee gbí-mi falaa, di sá yii iwiin ita sèé bi nín bí m-cee, kaa ti cáá tiba kí jēe.

eng: And when I broke seven loaves of bread for those four thousand people, how many baskets full of leftovers did you gather? They answered, Seven.
local: «Kin, m nín gífì gífì kpɔnɔb biluli ki tīī binib kutukub binaayee, tikabiti tiŋa ní ni tí kóoǹ akati ki gbéeǹ ní?» Ní bi tí kíí-u yii: «Tiluli.».

eng: Jesus said to them, And you still dont understand.
local: Ní u lī-bi yii: «Ní áli nìn díǹ, naa láá gbìl̄aaa?».

eng: Jesus and his disciples came to Bethsaida. Some people brought a blind man to Jesus and begged him to touch and heal him.
local: Bi bàn̄ Beetisayidee, binib biba cáań ujɔfu ubɔ Yeesu cee ki gbāam̀-u yii ú gbēē ujɔfuee.

eng: Taking the blind mans hand, Jesus led him out of the village. After spitting on his eyes and laying his hands on the man, he asked him, Do you see anything.
local: Yeesu cúú ujɔfuee ŋal ki kpáaǹ-u ki kpōotì kitimbiliiyee kpaanceetiŋu, ní ki tíil̀ timɔtan ki ŋá ujɔfuee nimbilin, ki dū u-ŋalii ki gbēē u-nimbiliŋ, ní ki bālifì-u yii: «A wāĺ tibaaa?».

eng: The man looked up and said, I see people. They look like trees, only they are walking around.
local: Ujɔfuee kpēē, ní ki bí yii: «M wāĺ binib, ní bi bí kii isufi di còom̄ee.».

eng: Then Jesus placed his hands on the mans eyes again. He looked with his eyes wide open, his sight was restored, and he could see everything clearly.
local: Niinee ní Yeesu gíti tí dū u-ŋalii ki gbēē u-nimbiliŋ. Ŋyunti gbanti ní ujɔfuee kpítì u-nimbiliŋ ki kpēē ní ki pɔ́ɔkì ki kíǹ ki wāĺ bɔ́mbɔŋ.

eng: Then Jesus sent him home, saying, Dont go into the village.
local: Ní Yeesu tùkū-u yii: «Nín̄ kùǹ a-dumpu kí taa dī kitiŋ poon.».

eng: Jesus and his disciples went into the villages near Caesarea Philippi. On the way he asked his disciples, Who do people say that I am.
local: Yeesu nì u-boonnooliibi yíkìi búntì Seesaaree-Fiiliipi timbilimmu watil pú. Bi bàn̄ nsannee, ní u bālifì u-boonnooliibi yii: «Binib lì yii m sá ŋma ní?».

eng: They told him, Some say John the Baptist, others Elijah, and still others one of the prophets.
local: Ní bi kíí-u yii: «Biba lì yii a sá Yoowaneesi un fùul̀ binib Unimbɔti nyimee, ní biba mun lì yii a sá Eelii. Biba bíǹ ceeyee, a sá Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi kansikin ubɔ ní.» Ní u bālifì-bi yii.

eng: He asked them, And what about you? Who do you say that I am? Peter answered, You are the Christ.
local: «Kin nimbi fáà, ŋma ní nimbi nyí yii m sá ní?» Ní Peetroo kíí-u yii: «Sii di sá Meesiya!».

eng: If I send them away hungry to their homes, they wont have enough strength to travel, for some have come a long distance.
local: M yaa jìn̄-bi nì ŋkùm nee bí nīn kùǹ bi-dumpuyee, bi làá yɔ̀l̄ nsanni, kun puee bi-kansikin tikpil nyánní ndan.».

eng: Jesus ordered them not to tell anyone about him.
local: Niinee ní Yeesu kpáafì-bi tiŋan bí taa sòoǹ u-pu míǹ báà ubɔ cee.

eng: Then Jesus began to teach his disciples: The Human One must suffer many things and be rejected by the elders, chief priests, and the legal experts, and be killed, and then, after three days, rise from the dead.
local: Ŋyunti gbantee, u kíĺ ki bíi tùkù u-boonnooliibi yii: «An sá man Kinibiki ń jī falaa tikpil. Bitindaŋkpilib nì bisaraaŋakpilib nì bimaradakaliibi kɔkɔ làá dāá lì yii baa là m-bɔti, bi làá dāá kpɔ̀-mi, ní ḿ fàtīí fìkitī winta dal.».

eng: He said this plainly. But Peter took hold of Jesus and, scolding him, began to correct him.
local: U nìn pée tùkù-bi an bɔtee ki cántí-bi ní. Ní Peetroo cáá-u ki kākatì ŋkpaanceeti ki kíĺ ki bíi wòoǹ-u.

eng: Jesus turned and looked at his disciples, then sternly corrected Peter: Get behind me, Satan. You are not thinking Gods thoughts but human thoughts.
local: Niinee ní Yeesu fātìi kpēē u-boonnooliibi ní ki kpātì Peetroo pu ki bí yii: «Sii un cáá Sitaan landɔkɔŋuee, sútí kí wàatī-mi! Aa dàkafī Unimbɔti yam, a-landɔkɔi sá binib landɔkɔi ní.».

eng: After calling the crowd together with his disciples, Jesus said to them, All who want to come after me must say no to themselves, take up their cross, and follow me.
local: U lī míǹ ki dóòyee, ní ki yíiń kunipaau nì u-boonnooliibi u-cee, ní ki bí-bi yii: «Un yaa là kí nóó-mee ń fá úǹ u-ba nín là puee kí nóó min ní man lèe, kí yòon̄ u-dɔpuntikaaŋu kí nīn nóó-mi.

eng: All who want to save their lives will lose them. But all who lose their lives because of me and because of the good news will save them.
local: Kun puee un di yaa là kí fīī u-maŋfalee làá fá-dì, ama un di yaa dū u-maŋfal ki fá kí nóó-mi kí sòoǹ m-bɔmɔntiiti kí tīī binibee, udaan di làá fīī u-maŋfal.

eng: Why would people gain the whole world but lose their lives?.
local: Unil, a yaa jíń duulinya nee kɔkɔ ní sisi a-ba wáŋkèe, a páatì ba?.

eng: What will people give in exchange for their lives?.
local: Ba ní a ŋūǹ kí dūú pà kí fīī a-ba?.

eng: Whoever is ashamed of me and my words in this unfaithful and sinful generation, the Human One will be ashamed of that person when he comes in the Fathers glory with the holy angels.
local: Un kamaa yaa cáá icoo man nì m-bɔti pu waanee nib bin nyēē Unimbɔti boon ki ŋáań ikpitee kansikinee, ŋyunti ŋun m yaa láa gítìń m-baa nyootin nì u-tuuŋyee, man Kinibiki làá dāá kɔ́ǹ udaan icoo.».

eng: His disciples responded, How can anyone get enough food in this wilderness to satisfy these people.
local: Niinee ní u-boonnooliibi kíí-u yii: «La ní ti làá kánní tijin kuteeu nee ni kí kpíiǹ binib nee?».

eng: Jesus asked, How much bread do you have? They said, Seven loaves.
local: Ní Yeesu fātìi bālifì-bi yii: «Kpɔnɔb biŋa ní ni cáá?» Ní bi kíí-u yii: «Biluli.».

eng: He told the crowd to sit on the ground. He took the seven loaves, gave thanks, broke them apart, and gave them to his disciples to distribute; and they gave the bread to the crowd.
local: U yāntì kunipaau kāl̀ taapu ki dóòyee, ní u yōoǹ kpɔnɔb bilulee ki jáam̀ Unimbɔti tijin gbanti pu, ní ki gífì gífì ki tīī u-boonnooliibi ń yàkatī binib. Ní bi dūu yākatì kunipaauee.

eng: They also had a few fish. He said a blessing over them, then gave them to the disciples to hand out also.
local: U-boonnooliibi nìn cáá njɔmbiyaamu mubɔ mun. U jáam̀ Unimbɔti mu mun pu ki dóòyee ní ki tùkū-bi yii bí dū-mu kí yàkatī.

eng: They ate until they were full. They collected seven baskets full of leftovers.
local: Kunipaauee jíń ki díkí, ní bi kóoǹ ajɔŋgin nì akpɔnɔkati ŋin kíǹee ki gbéeǹ tikabiti tiluli.

eng: This was a crowd of about four thousand people! Jesus sent them away.
local: Bin nìn jíń an jikaatee ŋūǹ kí bà binib kutukub binaa. Bi jíń ki dóòyee, ní u yāntì bi nín kùǹ.

eng: Jesus continued, I assure you that some standing here wont die before they see Gods kingdom arrive in power.
local: Ní Yeesu gíti kútì ki lī yii: «Ibaamɔn ní m tùkù-ni, ni-kansikin biba kaa làá kpú ní kí ká Unimbɔti Beel dɔ́miń nì mpɔn.».

eng: So they kept it to themselves, wondering, Whats this rising from the dead.
local: Bi kpáafì dinyɔɔbundee bi-ni, nì míǹ kɔkɔee bi gíti bālifì tɔb yii: «Ba di sá yii ú fìkitī ditaŋkpiilinee?».

eng: They asked Jesus, Why do the legal experts say that Elijah must come first.
local: Ní bi bālifì-u dinyɔɔbundi nee yii: «Ba pu ní bimaradakaliibi bí yii Eelii di máaǹ kí péé kí dāan̄ kí wàatī ní?».

eng: He answered, Elijah does come first to restore all things. Why was it written that the Human One would suffer many things and be rejected?.
local: Ní Yeesu fātìi kíí-bi yii: «Ibaamɔn, Eelii máaǹ kí péé kí dāan̄ kí fàtīí bɔ́ntí tikɔkɔ. Too, an nín ŋmàn̄ man Kinibiki pu yii m làá jī falaa tikpil ní bí kpèè kí sìŋkīǹ-mee, ni dàkafī yii ba?.

eng: In fact, I tell you that Elijah has come, but they did to him whatever they wanted, just as it was written about him.
local: Càntī ḿ tùkùmaan-ni, Eelii pée dɔ́miń dooooyee, ní bi sāaǹ-u bi nín là puee, kii an nín ŋmàn̄ Unimbɔti gbɔŋkun ki síiǹ u-pu miŋyee.».

eng: When Jesus, Peter, James, and John approached the other disciples, they saw a large crowd surrounding them and legal experts arguing with them.
local: Bi bāań ki ŋmāntiǹ u-boonnooliibi bin kíǹeeyee, ní ki ká yii kunipaau sí ki māntì ki tú-bi. Bimaradakaliibi biba mun nìn bí niin, bi nì u-boonnooliibi sí ki kpákà.

eng: Suddenly the whole crowd caught sight of Jesus. They ran to greet him, overcome with excitement.
local: Kunipaauee wál̀ Yeesu kpalaayee ní an túuǹ-bi ní bi sáǹní kí dāá jāam̀-u.

eng: Jesus asked them, What are you arguing about.
local: Ní Yeesu bālifì-bi yii: «Ni nì bi kpákà ba pu ní?».

eng: Someone from the crowd responded, Teacher, I brought my son to you, since he has a spirit that doesnt allow him to speak.
