local: Ní kunipaauee kansikin ubɔ kíí-u yii: «Ukpil, arasinii di kā m-jipɔɔn pu ní ki yāntì waa ŋūǹ kí sòoǹ ní m bàà cáań-u a-cee.

eng: Wherever it overpowers him, it throws him into a fit. He foams at the mouth, grinds his teeth, and stiffens up. So I spoke to your disciples to see if they could throw it out, but they couldnt.
local: Arasiniiyee yaa yíkì u-puee, báà laakiŋyee u líikí-u kitiŋ, ní u ŋmóò u-nyiŋŋi ní kupumpukuu nyēē u-nyɔkɔn, ní u-wunti ń pɔ̄ɔkì kpàninini. Ní m gbāam̀ a-boonnooliibi ń jà arasinii gbanti ní baa ŋúń.».

eng: Jesus answered them, You faithless generation, how long will I be with you? How long will I put up with you? Bring him to me.
local: Ní Yeesu lī-bi yii: «Óo, nimbi waanee nib bin kaa cáá fookii nee! Ni là ḿ gíti kàl̄ ni-cee kí tin sāā ŋyunti ŋulɔŋu ní? M làá nīn jīǹ-ni kimɔtii kí tin sāā ŋyunti ŋulɔŋu ní? Cáaḿmaan-kì m-cee!».

eng: Six days later Jesus took Peter, James, and John, and brought them to the top of a very high mountain where they were alone. He was transformed in front of them.
local: Iwiin iluu boonee, Yeesu gāntì Peetroo nì Yakubu nì Yoowaneesi ki cáá-bi ki jɔ́m̀ dijoofɔkɔl diba pu. Niinee ní u cúú u-ba ki kpántì.

eng: They brought him. When the spirit saw Jesus, it immediately threw the boy into a fit. He fell on the ground and rolled around, foaming at the mouth.
local: Ní bi cáań kibikee u-cee. Arasiniiyee nín wál̀ Yeesu kpalaayee ní u cīŋkì kibikee ŋkpitim pu, ní kì lítì kitiŋ ki bíi bíntí, ní kupumpukuu bíi nyēē ki-nyɔkɔn.

eng: Jesus asked his father, How long has this been going on? He said, Since he was a child.
local: Ní Yeesu bālifì ki-baa yii: «Ŋyunti ŋulɔŋu ní an kíĺ-u?» Ní u kíí-u yii: «Doooo u-biyatin ní an kíĺ-u.

eng: It has often thrown him into a fire or into water trying to kill him. If you can do anything, help us! Show us compassion.
local: Arasiniiyee líikí-u iyunti iba ŋŋmin, iyunti iba mun nnyimin, ki nyàab̀ kí kpɔ̀-u. A yaa ŋūǹ kí ŋá tiba, sɔ̄ ti-saai kí jòoń-ti!».

eng: Jesus said to him, If you can do anything? All things are possible for the one who has faith.
local: Ní Yeesu bí yii: «A yaa ŋūǹaa! An kɔ pɔ̀fì ki tīī un un fōō ki kííyee.».

eng: At that the boys father cried out, I have faith; help my lack of faith.
local: Niinee, kibikee baa téeń mpɔn yii: «M fōō-si ki kíí! Jòoń-mi, m-fookiiu kaa náaǹ!».

eng: Noticing that the crowd had surged together, Jesus spoke harshly to the unclean spirit, Mute and deaf spirit, I command you to come out of him and never enter him again.
local: Yeesu wál̀ kunipaau sánní ki gíti dòoń kí kūtèe, ní u kpātì arasinii pu ki lī yii: «Sii arasinii un yì kibiki nee mɔɔmiindi nì ki-tafakpaalee, nyā ki-ni kí taa ti gítiń, sii ní m lì!».

eng: After screaming and shaking the boy horribly, the spirit came out. The boy seemed to be dead; in fact, several people said that he had died.
local: Ní arasiniiyee fáá mbiil ki cīŋkì kibikee ŋkpitim pu, ní ki nyáń. Kibikee dōoǹ kii kì kpíìyee, ki yāntì binib tikpil bí yii: «Kì kpíì ní!».

eng: But Jesus took his hand, lifted him up, and he arose.
local: Ama ní Yeesu cúú ki-ŋal ki yáatì-kì ki síiǹ, ní kì síl̀.

eng: After Jesus went into a house, his disciples asked him privately, Why couldnt we throw this spirit out.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun Yeesu kɔ́ kudiinee, ní u-boonnooliibi bālifì-u bi-ŋmaniŋma niin, yii: «Ba pu ní timbi kaa ŋúń ki jìn̄ arasinii gbanti?».

eng: Jesus answered, Throwing this kind of spirit out requires prayer.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-bi yii: «An yaa kaa sá nì ŋwimmeem ubɔ kaa ŋūǹ kí jà arasiniibi nee jutiibi.».

eng: and his clothes were amazingly bright, brighter than if they had been bleached white.
local: ní u-wampeeŋkaati píil̀ káŋkaŋ ki kíǹ ki jìl̀ pàlpal. Unil báà ubɔ kitiŋ nee pu kaa ŋūǹ kí fíntí tiwan tí pīil̀ kii u-wanti nín nìn pīiǹ puee.

eng: From there Jesus and his followers went through Galilee, but he didnt want anyone to know it.
local: Bi yíkì niinee ní ki cūtìi jítì Galiilee. Yeesu kaa nìn là báà ubɔ ń béé u-pu.

eng: This was because he was teaching his disciples, The Human One will be delivered into human hands. They will kill him. Three days after he is killed he will rise up.
local: Di sá u nìn dàkà u-boonnooliibi ki tùkù-bi yii: «Bi làá cúú man Kinibiki kí ŋúbiǹ binib bí kpɔ̀-mi. Bi yaa kūū-mi, ŋwintataati dal m làá fìkitī.».

eng: But they didnt understand this kind of talk, and they were afraid to ask him.
local: U-boonnooliibi kaa nìn gbìl̄ tibɔtee taapu ní ki fāŋkì kaa bālifì-u ti-taapu.

eng: They entered Capernaum. When they had come into a house, he asked them, What were you arguing about during the journey.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun bi bàn̄ Kapeernayum ki kɔ́ kudiinee, ní Yeesu bālifì u-boonnooliibi yii: «Ba pu ní ni bàà kpákà nsannee?».

eng: They didnt respond, since on the way they had been debating with each other about who was the greatest.
local: Ní bi ŋmí: di sá yii tikpiti pu ní bi bàà kpákà.

eng: He sat down, called the Twelve, and said to them, Whoever wants to be first must be least of all and the servant of all.
local: Niinee ní u kāl̀ ní ki yíiń u-boonnooliibi saalaa nì bilee ní ki tùkū-bi yii: «Un yaa là kí nīn sá usanliitee, udaan ń páań báà ŋma boon kí nīn sá báà ŋma tɔntɔnliu.».

eng: Jesus reached for a little child, placed him among the Twelve, and embraced him. Then he said.
local: U lī míǹ ki dóòyee, ní ki cūtì ki cúuń kibiyaawaai kiba ŋal, ki cáań-kì ki síiǹ bi-kansikin, ní ki dū u-ŋalii ki kpàl̄-kì, ní ki bí-bi yii.

eng: Whoever welcomes one of these children in my name welcomes me; and whoever welcomes me isnt actually welcoming me but rather the one who sent me.
local: «Un kamaa yaa fōō kibiyaawaai kiba kii kíǹee juti man puee, udaan fōō man dee. Un kamaa yaa tí fōō mamee, kaa sá man baba ní u fōō, udaan fōō un túnní-mee mun dee.».

eng: John said to Jesus, Teacher, we saw someone throwing demons out in your name, and we tried to stop him because he wasnt following us.
local: Ní Yoowaneesi bí-u yii: «Ukpil, ti ká uninja ubɔ un kaa sá ti-yɔuee cáá a-yindi ki jì arasiniibi, ní ti bí yii ú taa ti ŋá míǹ, kun puee waa bí ti-kansikin.».

eng: Jesus replied, Dont stop him. No one who does powerful acts in my name can quickly turn around and curse me.
local: Ní Yeesu kíí-u yii: «Taa nín̄ pìintímaan-u nsan! Di sá yii unil kaa ŋáań maamaaci man yindi pu, kí pūtì kpáàa bíi sòoǹ m-pu tikpitil.

eng: Elijah and Moses appeared and were talking with Jesus.
local: Niinee ní akpambal atee kpēēyee, Eelii nì Mooyiisi di sí bi nì Yeesu sòoǹee.

eng: Whoever isnt against us is for us.
local: Un kaa jáà nì tee sá ti-yɔu ní.

eng: I assure you that whoever gives you a cup of water to drink because you belong to Christ will certainly be rewarded.
local: M tùkù-ni ibaamɔn yii un kamaa di yaa pú-ni kinyinnyɔyukubiki ni nyùn̄ yii ni nín sá Kriisitoo yabee puee, udaan kaa làá lántí u-paati.».

eng: As for whoever causes these little ones who believe in me to trip and fall into sin, it would be better for them to have a huge stone hung around their necks and to be thrown into the lake.
local: «Ama un di yaa yāntì mbiyaawaamu mun fōō-mi ki kíí nee kansikin kiba ŋá ikpitee, an sɔ́ bí dū kunakpaaŋŋu kí kúĺ udaan neen kí cáá-u kí tɔ̄ tiŋkun.

eng: If your hand causes you to fall into sin, chop it off. Its better for you to enter into life crippled than to go away with two hands into the fire of hell, which cant be put out.
local: A-ŋal yaa làá yàntī á ŋá ikpitee, á tùkutī-ŋu kí fá. An sɔ́ á kɔ́ dimaŋfal din kaa cáá dikúntilee ni nì diŋaŋgindi ki jítiǹ á nīn cáá iŋal ili kí tin kɔ́ ŋŋmi ŋun kaa kúù jaanjaanee ni. .

eng: If your foot causes you to fall into sin, chop it off. Its better for you to enter life lame than to be thrown into hell with two feet.
local: A-taal di yaa làá tí yàntī á kɔ́ ikpitinee, gùutī-dì kí fá. An sɔ́ á kɔ́ dimaŋfal din kaa cáá dikúntilee ni nì ditaagindi ki jítiǹ á nīn cáá ataa ali bí tin dū-si kí tɔ̄ ŋŋmi ŋun kaa cáá digbíntilee ni. .

eng: If your eye causes you to fall into sin, tear it out. Its better for you to enter Gods kingdom with one eye than to be thrown into hell with two.
local: An yaa sá a-nimbil nín kpèé kuŋyee di yaa yàntī a ŋáań ikpitee, á lúkutí-dì kí fá. An sɔ́ á kpáaǹ diniŋkpundi kí kɔ́ Unimbɔti Beelin ki jítiǹ á nīn cáá animbil ali bí tin dū-si kí tɔ̄ ŋŋmin.

eng: Thats a place where worms dont die and the fire never goes out .
local: Niin ní akoosibil ŋin kaa kúùyee bí ki ŋmóò binib. Niin di sá ŋŋmi ŋun bí kaa cáá digbíntilee.».

eng: Everyone will be salted with fire.
local: Ŋŋmi dāa sá kii ŋyaamee ní bí dūú pùkū báà ŋma pu.

eng: Peter reacted to all of this by saying to Jesus, Rabbi, its good that were here. Lets make three shrines—one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.
local: Ní Peetroo tùkū Yeesu yii: «Ukpil, an ŋān̄ tí nīn bí doo, tí gāā tibìń tita: sii kubɔ, Mooyiisi kubɔ, Eelii kubɔ!».

eng: Salt is good; but if salt loses its saltiness, how will it become salty again? Maintain salt among yourselves and keep peace with each other.
local: «Ŋyaam sá tiwammɔntiil: ama ŋyaam yaa fá mi-mɔŋŋuee, mana ní ni làá gíti ŋá ní mí mɔ̄ɔkì? Ŋyaam ń nīn bí ni-ni, ŋgbansɔŋfi ń nīn bí ni-kansikin.».

eng: He said this because he didnt know how to respond, for the three of them were terrified.
local: Di sá yii waa ti pée nyí tin u làá sòoǹee, kun puee tifaandi nìn kɔ́-bi tikpil.

eng: Then a cloud overshadowed them, and a voice spoke from the cloud, This is my Son, whom I dearly love. Listen to him.
local: Niinee, ditagbandi diba dɔ́miń ki bīiǹ bi-pu, ní bi gbìl̄ Unimbɔti sòon̄ní ditagbandee ni yii: «Un bí nee di sá m-Jipɔnneeŋkaau. Ní pīlifim̀maan-u!».

eng: Suddenly, looking around, they no longer saw anyone with them except Jesus.
local: Kpalaayee, bi fātìi kpēē ki māntì ki tú, kaa ti ká báà ubɔ asee Yeesu baba di kíǹ bi-cee.

eng: As they were coming down the mountain, he ordered them not to tell anyone what they had seen until after the Human One had risen from the dead.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun bi fàtīi kpákatiń dijool puee ní Yeesu kpáafì-bi bí taa māafì báà ubɔ tin bi kée kí nīn cí daliŋkin úǹ Kinibiki làá dāá fìkitī ditaŋkpiilinee.

eng: From Paul, who is a prisoner for the cause of Christ Jesus, and our brother Timothy. To Philemon our dearly loved coworker.
local: Man Pɔɔl un sá usarkadaan Yeesu Kriisitoo puee nì ti-nabiki Tiimootee di ŋmàń kugbɔŋu nee. Ti ŋmàn̄-ku kí tīī sii Fiileemɔn ti-tɔntɔnjuti un ti néeńee.

eng: appeal to you for my child Onesimus. I became his father in the faith during my time in prison.
local: M gbàam̄-si m-biki Ɔneesiim pu. Sarkan dooyee, man di kpántì u-baa nnimbɔtisan poon.

eng: He was useless to you before, but now he is useful to both of us.
local: Dooooyee, waa nìn kèetī-si, ama kɔŋkɔnnee u kèetī man, ki làá kèetī si mun.

eng: Im sending him back to you, which is like sending you my own heart.
local: Kɔŋkɔnnee, u kpántì kii man m-ba wunti jandi dibee ní, ama m dū-u ki gíiǹ ki tīiń-si ú nīn bí a-cee.

eng: I considered keeping him with me so that he might serve me in your place during my time in prison because of the gospel.
local: Kii Tibɔmɔntiil sòontam nín yāntì m bí sarkan puee, m bàà là yii ú fōō a-yil kí nīn bí m-cee kí tūtuǹ-mi kii sii nín bàà làá nīn tútúǹ-mi puee.

eng: However, I didnt want to do anything without your consent so that your act of kindness would occur willingly and not under pressure.
local: Ama maa là kí ŋá míǹ kaa bālifì-si, kaa tí là kí páb́-si yii á ŋá tiŋan nee kí tīī-mi. M là yii á ŋá min a-pɔbil lèe ní.

eng: Maybe this is the reason that Onesimus was separated from you for a while so that you might have him back forever.
local: An yaa yíì, Ɔneesiim yíkì a-cee ŋyunti ŋubɔ fiii, án nín làá ŋá pu, u yaa láa fātì ki gítìńee, si nì u ń ŋmā kí nīn kíǹ ki bí jaanjaanee.

eng: no longer as a slave but more than a slave—that is, as a dearly loved brother. He is especially a dearly loved brother to me. How much more can he become a brother to you, personally and spiritually in the Lord!.
local: Kun puee waa ti sá uyumbufantanti. Kɔŋkɔnnee u jítì uyumbu pu, u kíǹ ki sá kinabineeŋkaai ní. Man néeń-u tiŋan, ama sii làá nīn gíti néeń-u tikpil, kun puee u sá sii yumbuu ní, ní ki sá a-nabiki mun ti-Dindaan pu.

eng: So, if you really consider me a partner, welcome Onesimus as if you were welcoming me.
local: Míǹ puee, a yaa nyí yii m sá a-bɔɔyee, á fōō-u kii a nín bàà làá fōō man puee.

eng: If he has harmed you in any way or owes you money, charge it to my account.
local: U yaa bíil̀-si tibaaa, u yaa tí jíń-si dipɔndaaa, man fōō ki ŋá m-ba.

eng: I, Paul, will pay it back to you . Of course, I wont mention that you owe me your life.
local: Man Pɔɔl m-ba di dū m-ŋal ki ŋmàn̄ní-si tibɔti nee yii, man di làá pà-si u-pɔndi din u yaa jíń-see. An taa nín̄ sá yii a nín sá m-pɔnliu, kun puee a ká difiil man puee pu ní m tɔ̀n̄-si.

eng: Apphia our sister, Archippus our fellow soldier, and the church that meets in your house.
local: nì ti-niisal Apiya nì Arisiipi ti-janjajuti, nì Kriisitoo nikitil din kpáfí a-diikunee ní.

eng: Yes, brother, I want this favor from you in the Lord! Refresh my heart in Christ.
local: Míǹ puee, m-nabiki, m cíkitì-si kifuulii. Ti-Dindaan puee á fōō m-gbaamii gbanti kí yàntī m-pɔbil ń sɔ̀ŋkì, ti nín ŋá ubaanti nì Kriisitooyee pu.

eng: Im writing to you, confident of your obedience and knowing that you will do more than what I ask.
local: M nyí m-pɔbilin yii tin m bíi ŋmà-si yii á ŋá kí tīī-mi nee, a làá ŋá án jītí míǹ pu gba.

eng: Also, one more thing—prepare a guest room for me. I hope that I will be released from prison to be with you because of your prayers.
local: Á kpáàa bɔ́ntí-mi kudii kubɔ, kun puee m nyí yii Unimbɔti làá fōō ni-wimmeem ḿ ŋmā kí fàtīí gítiń ni-cee.

eng: Epaphras, who is in prison with me for the cause of Christ Jesus, greets you.
local: Eepafrasi man nì un bí sarkan Yeesu Kriisitoo puee jáaḿ-si.

eng: as well as my coworkers Mark, Aristarchus, Demas, and Luke.
local: M-tɔntɔnjutiibi Maarki nì Ariistaarki nì Deemasi nì Luuki mun jáamiń-si.

eng: May the grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit.
local: Ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo ŋanti ń nīn bí ni-pu.

eng: May the grace and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
local: Ti-Baa Unimbɔti nì ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo ń ŋá-ni tiŋan kí tīī-ni ŋgbansɔŋfi.

eng: Philemon, I thank my God every time I mention you in my prayers.
local: Baabadal, m yaa bí ki mèé m-Nimbɔtiuee, m téetí a-pu ki jáaḿ-u a-pu.

eng: because Ive heard of your love and faithfulness, which you have both for the Lord Jesus and for all Gods people.
local: kun puee m gbìl̀ bi sòoǹ a-fookiiu un a cáá ti-Dindaan Yeesu puee nì dineendi din a cáá Unimbɔti yab kɔkɔ mun puee pu.

eng: I pray that your partnership in the faith might become effective by an understanding of all that is good among us in Christ.
local: M mèé-u yii fookii un yāntì ti nì si ŋá ubaantee ń mɔ̀tīí yàntī á béé tiŋan tin kɔkɔ Unimbɔti ŋáań-ti Kriisitoo puee.

eng: I have great joy and encouragement because of your love, since the hearts of Gods people are refreshed by your actions, my brother.
local: M-nabiki, a-neendi din a cáá nnabiyaamu puee mɔ́ɔkiǹ-mi ki tīī-mi kunyannyaau tikpil, kun puee a tīī Unimbɔti yab kunyannyaau.

eng: Therefore, though I have enough confidence in Christ to command you to do the right thing.
local: Míǹ puee, nì min kɔkɔ ti nì Kriisitoo ŋá ubaantee, m bàà ŋūǹ kí dàkà-si min sá á ŋée.

eng: I would rather appeal to you through love. I, Paul—an old man, and now also a prisoner for Christ Jesus.
local: ama man kaa làá dī míǹ pu. Nì dineendi puee, man Pɔɔl un sá utikpil ki gíti tí sá usarkadaan Yeesu Kriisitoo puee làá gbàam̀-si ní.

eng: From Paul and Timothy, slaves of Christ Jesus. To all those in Philippi who are Gods people in Christ Jesus, along with your supervisors and servants.
local: Man Pɔɔl nì Tiimootee, timbi bin sá Yeesu Kriisitoo tɔntɔnliibee, di ŋmàn̄ kugbɔŋu nee. Ti ŋmàn̄-ku kí tīī nimbi bin sá Unimbɔti yab ki kóò Fiiliipi tiŋkin ki fōō Yeesu Kriisitoo ki kííyee, ki kpáaǹ bin kpòotī Kriisitoo nikitilee nì bin tútúǹ ditundi Kriisitoo nikitil kansikinee.

eng: I pray this so that you will be able to decide what really matters and so you will be sincere and blameless on the day of Christ.
local: án nín làá ŋá pu ní ŋmā kí lēē tin kítèe. Ní Kriisitoo gítintam wiiŋŋu dalee, ní dāá nīn bí circir, bí taa ká ni-pu tiba.

eng: I pray that you will then be filled with the fruit of righteousness, which comes from Jesus Christ, in order to give glory and praise to God.
local: Yeesu Kriisitoo puee, ni làá dāá lòoǹ abil tikpil ŋin sá deedee tuuŋŋi ŋin tìì Unimbɔti tinyool nì inyɔŋkee.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I want you to know that the things that have happened to me have actually advanced the gospel.
local: Nnabiyaamu, m là ní béé yii sarka un bi cúú-mi nee di yāntì ní Yeesu bɔti sòontam cūtì nyɔkɔpu malaa.

eng: The whole Praetorian Guard and everyone else knows that Im in prison for Christ.
local: Di sá yii gɔminakpaan kàliŋkpilib kɔkɔ nì bin kɔ kíǹee béè yii an sá Kriisitoo un m nóóyee pu ní m bí sarkan.

eng: Most of the brothers and sisters have had more confidence through the Lord to speak the word boldly and bravely because of my jail time.
local: Áli gba, man sarkau nee pu ní ti-nabiyaamu tikpil mɔ̀tīi dù bi-mákal ki ŋáań ti-Dindaan ni, ki kāntí kunyannyaau ki sòoǹ Unimbɔti bɔti, kaa fàŋkí.

eng: Some certainly preach Christ with jealous and competitive motives, but others preach with good motives.
local: Ama bi-kansikin bibee, an sá kináŋ kin bi cáá m-puee di yì-tì ní bi sòoǹ Kriisitoo bɔti ki kpākantí-mi, ní bin kíǹee, bíǹ baasii sòoǹ Unimbɔti bɔti nì nlandɔkɔmɔnti.

eng: They are motivated by love, because they know that Im put here to give a defense of the gospel.
local: Bin sòoǹ Unimbɔti bɔti nì nlandɔkɔmɔntee, bíǹ néeń-mi ní ki nyí yii Unimbɔti lēē-mi ḿ nīn dàkà yii Kriisitoo bɔti sá ibaamɔn.

eng: the others preach Christ because of their selfish ambition. They are insincere, hoping to cause me more pain while Im in prison.
local: Ama bin béeyee, bíǹ sòoǹ Kriisitoo bɔti nì ilandɔkɔkpiti ki là kí kpákáǹ-mi, ki nyàab̀ kí gíti kɔ́ǹ-mi digìńdi kí kūtì sarka un poon m bí nee.

eng: What do I think about this? Just this: since Christ is proclaimed in every possible way, whether from dishonest or true motives, Im glad and Ill continue to be glad.
local: Bi yaa sòoǹ Unimbɔti bɔti ki là kí kpákáǹ-maaa, an yaa tí sá nì dipɔɔmɔntiilaaa, tíǹ kaa wù-mi. An sá man dipɔɔpindi ní ki làá nīn pée mɔ̄ǹ-mi ki cá, kun puee Kriisitoo bɔti ní bi-kɔkɔ sòoǹ.

eng: Im glad because I know that this will result in my release through your prayers and the help of the Spirit of Jesus Christ.
local: Di sá m nyí yii, nì ni-wimmeem pu, nì Yeesu Kriisitoo Fam tūtuntam puee, m làá ká difiil.

eng: May the grace and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
local: Ti-Baa Unimbɔti nì ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo ń ŋá-ni tiŋan kí tīī-ni ŋgbansɔŋfi.

eng: It is my expectation and hope that I wont be put to shame in anything. Rather, I hope with daring courage that Christs greatness will be seen in my body, now as always, whether I live or die.
local: M nín là tiwan tin ki cí tikpil ki cɔ́tí ti-puee di sá icoo ń taa cúú-mi tiba pu. Ama kɔŋkɔnnee kí nīn cée, m yaa làá kpúɔɔɔ, m yaa làá nīn tí báaa, ḿ mɔ̀tīí nīn cáá dipɔbil, m-wunti ń tīī Kriisitoo tinyool.

eng: Because for me, living serves Christ and dying is even better.
local: Ama mamee, Kriisitoo di sá man maŋfal, ní ŋkúm cāabìń-mi ipaati.

eng: If I continue to live in this world, I get results from my work.
local: An yaa tí sá ḿ nīn bí dimaŋfal poon kí ŋmā kí tɔ̄ atummɔnti abaaa, maa nyí laakin m làá lēēyee.

eng: But I dont know what I prefer. Im torn between the two because I want to leave this life and be with Christ, which is far better.
local: Ajan gbanti liitil bí ki dátí-mi, m bàà là kí kpú kí cùú nīn kā Kriisitoo cee, míǹ di bàà ŋān̄ tikpil.

eng: However, its more important for me to stay in this world for your sake.
local: Ama, nimbi puee, an gíti páb́ ki lákatì yii ḿ nīn dáá bí dimaŋfalin.

eng: Im sure of this: I will stay alive and remain with all of you to help your progress and the joy of your faith.
local: M nín béè yii an sá mimmee, ní m nyí yii m làá nīn bí dimaŋfalin, man nì ni kɔkɔ làá nīn bí, ḿ tūtuǹ-ni ní ŋmā kí cù nyɔkɔpu, kí nīn bí dipɔɔpindin Unimbɔti saŋŋu poon.

eng: and to increase your pride in Christ Jesus through my presence when I visit you again.
local: Mimmee m yaa fātìi tí gítìń ni-kansikinee, man puee, án làá dāá nīn mɔ̄ǹ-ni ki gíti lákatì ni nì Yeesu Kriisitoo nín ŋá ubaantee pu.

eng: Most important, live together in a manner worthy of Christs gospel. Do this, whether I come and see you or Im absent and hear about you. Do this so that you stand firm, united in one spirit and mind as you struggle together to remain faithful to the gospel.
local: Tin páb́ee di sá ni-bimbim ń nīn táá nì Kriisitoo Bɔmɔntiiti, ní báà m yaa dɔ́miń ni-ceeyaaa, m yaa kaa dɔ́mińaaa, ḿ nīn gbìl̀ bi sòoǹ ni nín pēē kinyɔkɔ pu ki báaǹ inyaan, ki jáà nì dipɔbil diba Tibɔmɔntiilee ń cù nyɔkɔpuee.

eng: That way, you wont be afraid of anything your enemies do. Your faithfulness and courage are a sign of their coming destruction and your salvation, which is from God.
local: Ní taa yàntī ni-nannanliibi ń fàaǹ-ni tiba poon. Ni nín bí miŋyee di làá dàkà bíǹ yii bi wáŋkì, ní kí dàkà nimbi yii ni yú difiil saŋŋu pu. Unimbɔti di làá yàntī bí béé míǹ.

eng: God has generously granted you the privilege, not only of believing in Christ but also of suffering for Christs sake.
local: Di sá yii u pú-ni diyimɔɔl ní nīn nóó Kriisitoo kí taa fōō-u kí kíí ŋmaniŋma, ama ní jī falaa mun u-pu.

eng: I thank my God every time I mention you in my prayers.
local: Ŋyunti sá kamaa, m yaa bíi dàkafī ni-bɔtee, m jáaḿ Unimbɔti ni-pu.

eng: You are having the same struggle that you saw me face and now hear that Im still facing.
local: Kɔŋkɔnnee, man nì ni kɔkɔ di kpáfì ki bí dimool poon. Dooooyee ní ni nìn ká yii m bí dimool gbantin, ní ni gbìl̄ yii m dáá gbíntìi bí ki mɔ̀ áli nìn díǹ.

eng: Im thankful for all of you every time I pray, and its always a prayer full of joy.
local: Ŋyunti kamaa m yaa bíi mèé ni-kɔkɔ puee, m mèé nì dipɔɔpindi ní.

eng: Im glad because of the way you have been my partners in the ministry of the gospel from the time you first believed it until now.
local: Di sá ni nìn tútuǹ-ti, kí yòon̄ ŋwimpeepeekaa ŋun ni fōō Yeesu ki kííyee ki dāa dūu sāā díǹ, tí ŋmā kí sòoǹ Tibɔmɔntiil ajan kɔkɔ pu.

eng: Im sure about this: the one who started a good work in you will stay with you to complete the job by the day of Christ Jesus.
local: M nyí yii Unimbɔti un kíĺ ki bíi tūǹ ditummɔntiil nee ni-pɔbiliŋ poonee kaa làá gbèen̄. U làá nīn pée gbíntìi tūǹ míǹ ní kí nīn pée cí Yeesu Kriisitoo gítintam wiiŋŋu.

eng: I have good reason to think this way about all of you because I keep you in my heart. You are all my partners in Gods grace, both during my time in prison and in the defense and support of the gospel.
local: An kítì ḿ nīn dàkafī ni-kɔkɔ pu míǹ ní, kun puee ni-bɔti kɔ́-mi tiŋan. Di sá yii, m yaa bí sarkanaaa, m yaa tí bí ki dàkà ki cántí Tibɔmɔntiilee baamɔŋyi nín sá pueeyaaa, ni-kɔkɔ kútí m-pu ki fòol̄ diyimɔɔbaantiil din Unimbɔti tīī-mee.

eng: God is my witness that I feel affection for all of you with the compassion of Christ Jesus.
local: Unimbɔti u-ba nyí yii m néeń ni-kɔkɔ nì dineembaamɔndi din nyánní Yeesu Kriisitoo ceeyee ní.

eng: This is my prayer: that your love might become even more and more rich with knowledge and all kinds of insight.
local: M nín mèé ni-pu tiŋyee di sá yii Unimbɔti ń yàntī ni-neendi ń nīn wīintí ki cá baabadal, ní nīn mɔ̀tīi béetí ibaamɔn ki gàntī tiŋan nì tikpitil.

eng: Therefore, if there is any encouragement in Christ, any comfort in love, any sharing in the Spirit, any sympathy.
local: Yeesu Kriisitoo cee ní ni ká kunyannyaau ní u-neendi tīī-ni dipɔɔsɔŋkil, ni nì Unimbɔti Fam ŋá ubaanti, ni néeń tɔb ki sūǹ tɔb saai, kaa sá mimmaaa?.

eng: so that at the name of Jesus everyone in heaven, on earth, and under the earth might bow.
local: án nín làá ŋá pu bin bí yilpuee nì bin bí kitiŋ puee nì bin gíti bí kitiŋ taapuee, báà ŋma ń gbàan̄ u-tabiŋyiliŋ pu kí nīn tɔ́ Yeesu yindi.

eng: and every tongue confess that Jesus Christ is Lord, to the glory of God the Father.
local: ní báà ŋma ń lì u-nyɔkɔn yii Yeesu Kriisitoo di sá ti-Dindaan, ní ti-baa Unimbɔti ń ká tinyool.

eng: Therefore, my loved ones, just as you always obey me, not just when I am present but now even more while I am away, carry out your own salvation with fear and trembling.
local: An puee, m-nabineeŋkaamu, kii m nín bí ni-cee ki ká ni tɔ́ Unimbɔti ŋyunti kamaa miŋyee, kɔŋkɔnnee m nín kpá ni-cee pu nee, ní nīn gbíntìi tɔ́-u míǹ gbīŋgbim. Kii Unimbɔti nín fīī-ni puee, ní nīn fàŋkí-u, kí nīn tūm̀maan ditundi.

eng: God is the one who enables you both to want and to actually live out his good purposes.
local: Kun puee Unimbɔti di bíi tūǹ ni-ni ditundi ki yàntī ni néeń u nín là miŋyee ki ŋáań míǹ.

eng: Do everything without grumbling and arguing.
local: Tin kɔkɔ ni yaa bíi ŋáańee, ní taa nín̄ gbùkum̄ kí taa nín̄ cáá kunikpɔŋkpɔkɔu.

eng: so that you may be blameless and pure, innocent children of God surrounded by people who are crooked and corrupt. Among these people you shine like stars in the world.
local: Mimmee baa làá ká ni-pu tiba, ní ní nīn bí circir ki sá Unimbɔti biyaamu mun kaa cáá ikpiti iba duulinya nib binikpitib nì bikiililiibi kansikinee. Ní nīn wāĺ duulinyan kii iŋmalbijaayee.

eng: because you hold on to the word of life. This will allow me to say on the day of Christ that I havent run for nothing or worked for nothing.
local: kí nīn báakí Unimbɔti bɔti tin sá dimaŋfalee. Ni yaa ŋá mimmee, daliŋkin Kriisitoo làá gítińee, m dāa mɔ̀ɔǹ ní, an di dāa dàkà yii m-tundi nì m-pɔ́ɔǹtam kaa ŋá fám.

eng: But even if I am poured out like a drink offering upon the altar of service for your faith, I am glad. Im glad with all of you.
local: Ni-fookiiu sá kii saraa un ni tìì Unimbɔtee ní. An yaa yíì, bi yaa kūū-mee, m-fatikuu di dāa kútí ni-saraau gbanti pu. An yaa lafun sá mimmee, an dāa mɔ́ɔkíǹ-mi, ní ni mun ń dāá kūtì m-pu tí fàal̀.

eng: You should be glad about this in the same way. Be glad with me!.
local: Míǹ mbaantiim, ni mun dāa bí dipɔɔpindi poon ní ma mun ń kūtì ni-pu tí fàal̀.

eng: I hope in the Lord Jesus to send Timothy to see you soon so that I may be encouraged by hearing about you.
local: Ti-Dindaan Yeesu yaa lèe, m máká yii an kaa làá yúntí m làá tɔ̄ń Tiimootee ni-cee, án nín làá ŋá pu, u yaa láa fātìi gítìń ki máafì-mi ni nín bí lapu miŋyee, ḿ ká kunyannyaauee.

eng: complete my joy by thinking the same way, having the same love, being united, and agreeing with each other.
local: An puee, m mèé-ni yii ní ŋámaan ubaanti kí nīn cáá tɔb pu dineendi, ni-pɔbiliŋ ń ŋá dibaantiil, ni-landɔkɔŋu mun ń nīn sá mbaanti. Ŋámaan míǹ, man pɔbil ń pīil̀ tiŋan.

eng: I have no one like him. He is a person who genuinely cares about your well-being.
local: Kun puee ubɔ kaa ti bí. Úǹ baba di kútì m-pu ti cáá nlandɔkɔbaanti ki dàkafī ni-pu tikpil.

eng: All the others put their own business ahead of Jesus Christs business.
local: Bin kɔkɔ kíǹee dàkafī bíǹ bi-ba bɔti pu ní kaa fātìi tɔ́ Yeesu Kriisitoo yati.

eng: You know his character, how he labors with me for the gospel like a son works with his father.
local: Nimbi ni-ba nyí min ní Tiimootee gbanti nìn dàkā u-bimbimɔntiim, nì man nì u nín túń ditundi ki sòoǹ Unimbɔti bɔti kii kibiki nì ki-baayee.

eng: So he is the one that I hope to send as soon as I find out how things turn out here for me.
local: M yaa láa béè tin poon m bée, m làá tɔ̄ń-u ni-cee malaa.

eng: I trust in the Lord that I also will visit you soon.
local: M máká ti-Dindaan pu yii, án kaa làá ti yúntí, mama m-ba làá dāan̄ kí màntì ni-pu.

eng: I think it is also necessary to send Epaphroditus to you. He is my brother, coworker, and fellow soldier; and he is your representative who serves my needs.
local: M ká yii an máaǹ ḿ tɔ̄ń ni-cee m-nabiki Eepafroodiiti m-tɔntɔnjuti man nì un bōō dikɔ̀ndi puee. Úǹ ní ni nìn túnní m-cee, u cáań-mi tin nìn lóò-mi ni tútúǹní-mee.

eng: He misses you all, and he was upset because you heard he was sick.
local: U nyàab̀ kí dāan̄ kí fàtīí ká ni-kɔkɔ ní. U nín béè yii ni gbìl̄ yii u búŋkèe, an páb́-u tikpil.

eng: In fact, he was so sick that he nearly died. But God had mercy on him—and not just on him but also on me, because his death would have caused me great sorrow.
local: U lafun búŋkì tikpil, áli ki tóob́ ŋkúm, ama ní Unimbɔti súń u-saai. An kaa sá úǹ baba saai, u súń ki kpáaǹ ma mun saai, kunimbaau ń taa dāan̄ kí gíti kūtì kunimbaau pu.

eng: Therefore, I am sending him immediately so that when you see him again you can be glad and I wont worry.
local: An puee m jìkì kí dū-u kí tɔ̄ń ni-cee malaa, án nín làá ŋá pu, ni yaa gíti fātìi ká-uee, ni-pɔbiliŋ ń fàtīí tí sɔ̀ŋkì, ní ma mun nimbaaku ń bàtēe.

eng: So welcome him in the Lord with great joy and show great respect for people like him.
local: Míǹ puee, u yaa bāańee, ní fōō-u nì dipɔɔpiŋkpaandi ti-Dindaan pu. Binib bin bí kii úŋ̀wuee, ni máaǹ kí nīn tɔ́-bi ní.

eng: Dont do anything for selfish purposes, but with humility think of others as better than yourselves.
local: Ní taa nín̄ ŋáań tiba ki là ni-ba jandi pu ŋmaniŋma, kí taa nín̄ nyàab̀ kupampakakuliŋŋu. Ní nīn sìntīǹ ni-ba taapu ki tìì tɔb, kí nīn kpèé ni-jutiibi yii bi jítiǹ-ni.

eng: He risked his life and almost died for the work of Christ, and he did this to make up for the help you couldnt give me.
local: kun puee Kriisitoo tundi puee, fiii di pée kpá ú bàà kpú. Waa fāŋkì ŋkúm ki tútúǹ-mi ditutundi din, ni yaa bàà bí doo, an bàà sá ní tūtuǹ-mee.

eng: Instead of each person watching out for their own good, watch out for what is better for others.
local: Báà ŋma ń nīn nyàab̀ min ŋān̄ee ki tìì u-juti kí taa nín̄ dàkafī u-baba ŋmaniŋma ŋanti pu.

eng: Adopt the attitude that was in Christ Jesus.
local: Ní nīn cáá Yeesu Kriisitoo landɔkɔŋu.

eng: Though he was in the form of God, he did not consider being equal with God something to exploit.
local: Úǹ un sá Unimbɔti u-bee, waa nyàab̄ yii u nì Unimbɔti ń nīn ŋmà.

eng: But he emptied himself by taking the form of a slave and by becoming like human beings. When he found himself in the form of a human.
local: Ama u yāntì tin kɔkɔ u nìn cááyee, ní ki fōō uyumbu bimbim. U dū u-ba ki nántiǹ binib, ki dɔ́miń nniyaamin.

eng: he humbled himself by becoming obedient to the point of death, even death on a cross.
local: ní ki sīntì u-ba taapu, ní ki pílifiǹ Unimbɔti áli ki tin kpíì ŋkúm min sá ndɔpuntikaa pu kúmee.

eng: Therefore, God highly honored him and gave him a name above all names.
local: An puee ní Unimbɔti yōoǹ-u ki kāaǹ mpaan ŋun bí yilpu ki jítiǹ ipaan kɔkɔee, ní ki pú-u diyindi din jítiǹ ayin kɔkɔee.

eng: So then, my brothers and sisters, be glad in the Lord. Its no trouble for me to repeat the same things to you because they will help keep you on track.
local: Kɔŋkɔnnee m-nabiyaamu, tin kíǹee di sá yii ní nīn fàal̀maan ti-Dindaan pu. An kaa sá-mi digìńdi ḿ fàtīí kùbitī kí lì tin m nìn pée ŋmàn̄-ni dooooyee, an sá tiŋan ní ki tīī-ni.

eng: The righteousness that I have comes from knowing Christ, the power of his resurrection, and the participation in his sufferings. It includes being conformed to his death.
local: Tin m nyàab̀ee di sá ḿ béé Kriisitoo nì u-fìkititam pɔŋŋu. Man nì u ń kpāfì kí jī u-falaau, m-bimbim ń kpú, ḿ nántíǹ Yeesu nín nìn kpíì puee.

eng: so that I may perhaps reach the goal of the resurrection of the dead.
local: ki cɔ́tí yii Unimbɔti làá dāá fìkitī ma mun ditaŋkpiilin.

eng: Its not that I have already reached this goal or have already been perfected, but I pursue it, so that I may grab hold of it because Christ grabbed hold of me for just this purpose.
local: An kaa sá yii m fōō tipaal, maa láá kpántì umɔnti ki dóò. Ama Yeesu Kriisitoo u-ba nín cúú-mi puee, m bíi pɔ̄ɔntí kí sāǹ kí cúú m-taatandi paati.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I myself dont think Ive reached it, but I do this one thing: I forget about the things behind me and reach out for the things ahead of me.
local: Nnabiyaamu, m nyí yii maa láá bàn̄ kí cúú-tì. Tiba ní m ŋáań: di sá yii m súntí tin pu m jítèe, ní ki pɔ̄ɔntí kí bà tin bí nyɔkɔpuee.

eng: The goal I pursue is the prize of Gods upward call in Christ Jesus.
local: An puee m sáń kí tin bà kigbèenlaŋ, ní kí tin fōō tipaal tin pu Unimbɔti yíiń-ti yilpu Yeesu Kriisitoo puee.

eng: So all of us who are spiritually mature should think this way, and if anyone thinks differently, God will reveal it to him or her.
local: Timbi bin kɔkɔ bīń nnimbɔtisan poonee, tí nīn cáámaan nlandɔkɔ gbanti. Ama ní ni-kansikin biba landɔkɔi yaa tí nyáń i-baaa, Unimbɔti làá dàkà-ni ní béé an nín sá miŋyee.

eng: Only lets live in a way that is consistent with whatever level we have reached.
local: Tin páb́ee di sá yii, nsan ŋun ti pée kíĺ ki nóóyee, tí nīn gbíntì ki nóó-ŋu ki cá.

eng: Brothers and sisters, become imitators of me and watch those who live this way—you can use us as models.
local: Nnabiyaamu, nín̄ nóómaan m-tabɔbiliŋ kí dū ni-nimbikpeeŋu kí báań bin còom̄ kii ni nín ká timbi còom̄ miŋyee pu.

eng: As I have told you many times and now say with deep sadness, many people live as enemies of the cross.
local: M nìn tùkū-ni, ki làá gíti bùsì kí tùkù-ni nì nsiim, yii binib biba bí, bi-saŋkindi dàkà yii bi ná Kriisitoo un kpíì ndɔpuntikaa puee.

eng: Their lives end with destruction. Their god is their stomach, and they take pride in their disgrace because their thoughts focus on earthly things.
local: Tin dɔ̄ ki cí-bee di sá ditafadaaldi, kun puee bi nóó tin bi-pooŋŋi lèe, kaa nóó Unimbɔti. Tin kɔ̄ntí-bi icooyee di mɔ̄ǹ-bi, ní taapu doo wanti pu ní bi dàkafī.

eng: Watch out for the dogs. Watch out for people who do evil things. Watch out for those who insist on circumcision, which is really mutilation.
local: Nín̄ nyím̀maan ni-ba nì bitɔntɔŋkpitib bin bimbim bí kii ibooyee. Bíǹ ceeyee, unil ń gíí dikpancandee di pée sá.

eng: Our citizenship is in heaven. We look forward to a savior that comes from there—the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Ama timbi sá yilpu tindambiyaamu ní. Ti kā ki cí ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo ń nyānní niin kí dāan̄ kí fīī-ti.

eng: He will transform our humble bodies so that they are like his glorious body, by the power that also makes him able to subject all things to himself.
local: U làá dāá dū mpɔn ŋun u cáá kí sìntīǹ tiwan kɔkɔ u-taapuee kí cúú ti-wunti tin bí iwɔfinee kí kpántí yilpu wunti kii u-yɔŋu nín bí puee.

eng: We are the circumcision. We are the ones who serve by Gods Spirit and who boast in Christ Jesus. We dont put our confidence in rituals performed on the body.
local: Timbi di sá bin gíí digiil tiŋman pu yalee, kun puee Unimbɔti Fam di kpòotī timbi ní ti nóó-u. Yeesu Kriisitoo pu ní timbi fàal̀, kaa máká nisaal tundi pu.

eng: though I have good reason to have this kind of confidence. If anyone else has reason to put their confidence in physical advantages, I have even more.
local: Ama m bàà ŋūǹ kí nīn máká nisaal tundi pu. Ubɔ yaa dàkafī yii u ŋūǹ kí dū u-mákal kí páań nisaal tundi puee, man bàà cáá nsan kí dàkafì míǹ kí jītíǹ udaan pu.

eng: I was circumcised on the eighth day. I am from the people of Israel and the tribe of Benjamin. I am a Hebrew of the Hebrews. With respect to observing the Law, Im a Pharisee.
local: Bi nín màl̄-mee, iwiin inii dal ní bi gíí-mi dikpancandi. M baasii sá Isirayeel nibul ní, ki sá Beenjameen naakookun, ki sá ujuudajabaamɔn. Juuda yab marab nóotamee, m nìn sá Fariisab kitilin ní.

eng: With respect to devotion to the faith, I harassed the church. With respect to righteousness under the Law, Im blameless.
local: ní ki cáá difaŋgiil áli ki sàantí Kriisitoo nikitil. A yaa kpēē m nín ŋúb́ Juuda yab marab puee, m nìn bí deedee, kaa yīntì báà tiba.

eng: These things were my assets, but I wrote them off as a loss for the sake of Christ.
local: M bàà nìn nyí yii tíǹ gbanti sá-mi tiŋan ní, ama kɔŋkɔnnee, Kriisitoo puee, m kpèé-tì kii tiwan tin kaa cáá ipaatee ní.

eng: But even beyond that, I consider everything a loss in comparison with the superior value of knowing Christ Jesus my Lord. I have lost everything for him, but what I lost I think of as sewer trash, so that I might gain Christ.
local: M nín béè yii m-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo sá-mi tiŋan tin jítiǹ tikɔkɔee, an puee, tin kɔkɔ m nìn pée kpèé yii an sá-mi tiwammɔntiilee, m kíǹ ki kpèé-tì kii tiwan tin kaa cáá ipaatee ní. U-puee m fá tikɔkɔ, ní ki kíǹ ki kpèé-tì kii titampummoolee kí ŋmā kí ká Kriisitoo.

eng: and be found in him. In Christ I have a righteousness that is not my own and that does not come from the Law but rather from the faithfulness of Christ. It is the righteousness of God that is based on faith.
local: man nì u ń ŋá ubaanti. Maa ti máká yii m nín ŋúb́ marabee pu ní m kpántì deedeedaan, ama m nín fōō Kriisitoo ki kííyee pu ní m kpántì deedeedaan, kii Unimbɔti nín yāntì bin fōō-u ki kííyee kpántì deedeedambi puee.

eng: Therefore, my brothers and sisters whom I love and miss, who are my joy and crown, stand firm in the Lord. Loved ones.
local: An puee, m-nabineeŋkaamu mun m mɔ̀ ki bàà là kí gíti kée, nimbi bin tìì-mi dipɔɔpindi ki sá-mi tinyoolee, m-bɔɔbi, ni nín sá ti-Dindaan yabee puee, ní nīn gbíntìi bá ki pɔ̄ɔ̄ míǹ.

eng: I was very glad in the Lord because now at last you have shown concern for me again.
local: M jáaḿ ti-Dindaan, an mɔ́ɔkiǹ-mi tikpil kii m nín ká ni fātìi tí bíi dàkafī m-pu ki tútúǹní-mi miŋyee. Ni nìn pée cáá nlandɔkɔ gbanti m-pu an yúntì, ní mpaan kaa nìn bí ní ŋá ni nín dàkafī m-pu miŋyee.

eng: Im not saying this because I need anything, for I have learned how to be content in any circumstance.
local: M nín bí ki sòoǹ nì ni pu nee, an kaa sá tiba di lóò-mi. M yaa cááyaaa, m yaa kaa tí cááyaaa, an kɔkɔ mɔ̄ǹ-mi ní.

eng: I know the experience of being in need and of having more than enough; I have learned the secret to being content in any and every circumstance, whether full or hungry or whether having plenty or being poor.
local: M bífì kí nīn bí disoolin, ki tí bífì kí nīn bí digbíl mun poon. M yaa jíń ki díkáaa, m yaa tí dɔ̄ ŋkùmaaa, m yaa cáá ki lákatàaa, m yaa kaa tí cááyaaa, m bífì kí ŋmèè tin kamaa di yaa pée bāań-mee.

eng: I can endure all these things through the power of the one who gives me strength.
local: M ŋūǹ tikɔkɔ Kriisitoo un tìì-mi mpɔnee pu.

eng: Still, you have done well to share my distress.
local: Ama ni nín kútì m-pu m jíń falaayee, ni túń ditundi.

eng: You Philippians know from the time of my first mission work in Macedonia how no church shared in supporting my ministry except you.
local: Fiiliipi tiŋki yab, nimbi ni-ba nyí, m nín kíĺ Unimbɔti bɔti sòontam Maseeduwaan tiŋkin ki dāa yíkèe, Kriisitoo nikitil báà diba kaa ti tútuǹ-mi, see nimbi ŋmaniŋma di kíí ki tīiń-mi tin m bàà làá lóóyee.

eng: You sent contributions repeatedly to take care of my needs even while I was in Thessalonica.
local: Di sá yii, doooo, min m nìn bí Teesalooniikee, nimbi di nìn tútúǹní-mi ŋfunyɔm kupaau pu.

eng: Im not hoping for a gift, but I am hoping for a profit that accumulates in your account.
local: M nín sòoǹ tin nee, kaa sá m nyàab̀ ní pɔ̄ń-mi tiba. M nyàab̀ ní nīn tūǹ atummɔnti kí ká ipaati tikpil ní.

eng: I now have plenty and it is more than enough. I am full to overflowing because I received the gifts that you sent from Epaphroditus. Those gifts give off a fragrant aroma, an acceptable sacrifice that pleases God.
local: Tin kɔkɔ ni tīiń-mee, m fōō, m kíǹ ki bí digbíl poon ní. Ni nín tīiń ni-tūtuntam puee, kɔŋkɔnnee m cáá tikɔkɔ nì tikɔkɔ. Ni-tūtuntam min Eepafroodiiti cáań-mee sɔ́fì fíŋfiŋ kii talaaluee, ní ki bí kii saraa un fáatì Unimbɔti u fòol̄ee.

eng: My God will meet your every need out of his riches in the glory that is found in Christ Jesus.
local: Kii m-Nimbɔtiu nín cáá tigaajawan tikpil nì tinyool puee, u làá tīī-ni tin kɔkɔ yaa lóò-nee Yeesu Kriisitoo pu.

eng: I urge Euodia and I urge Syntyche to come to an agreement in the Lord.
local: M mèé ki gbàam̄ Eefoodii nì Sintiikee yii bí ŋá ubaanti mmɔntiim ti-Dindaan pu.

eng: Let glory be given to God our Father forever and always. Amen.
local: Ti-Baa Unimbɔti ń ká tinyool kí nīn pée cá jaanjaan! Ameeni.

eng: Greet all Gods people in Christ Jesus. The brothers and sisters with me send you their greeting.
local: Ní jāam̀ bin kɔkɔ pée sá Yeesu Kriisitoo yabee. Ti-nabiyaamu mun bí man cee dooyee mun jáaḿ-ni.

eng: All Gods people here, especially those in Caesars household, send you their greeting.
local: Bin sá Unimbɔti yabee kɔkɔ jáaḿ-ni, kí bántée, gɔminakpaan Seesaar dicaŋkun yab jáaḿ-ni.

eng: The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirits.
local: Ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo ŋanti ń nīn bí ni-cee.

eng: Yes, and Im also asking you, loyal friend, to help these women who have struggled together with me in the ministry of the gospel, along with Clement and the rest of my coworkers whose names are in the scroll of life.
local: Míǹ puee, sii m-tɔntɔnjutimɔnti, m mèé-si yii á tūtuǹ-mi binimpoobi gbanti liitil, kun puee dikɔ̀ndi din poon m bí ki sòoǹ Unimbɔti bɔtee, binimpoobee mun tútúǹ-mi, bi nì Kleeman nì m-tɔntɔnjutiibi bin kíǹee. Unimbɔti ŋmàn̄ bi-yiŋŋi dimaŋfal gbɔŋkun.

eng: Be glad in the Lord always! Again I say, be glad!.
local: Ni nín bí ti-Dindaan ŋaan puee, nín̄ fàal̀maan ŋyunti sá kamaa. M gíti bùsī ki tùkù-ni yii, nín̄ fàal̀maan.

eng: Let your gentleness show in your treatment of all people. The Lord is near.
local: Ní nīn mɔ̄ báà ŋma cee, ti-Dindaan tóobiń.

eng: Dont be anxious about anything; rather, bring up all of your requests to God in your prayers and petitions, along with giving thanks.
local: Ní taa nín̄ yáliŋkí tiba nì tiba pu, ama tin kamaa di yaa bāań-nee, ní dū ŋwimmeem nì igbaam kí mèè Unimbɔti tin lóò-nee pu, kí nīn jáaḿ-u u-ŋanti pu.

eng: Then the peace of God that exceeds all understanding will keep your hearts and minds safe in Christ Jesus.
local: Unimbɔti gbansɔŋfiŋu ŋun wīikí ki jítiǹ unil nyítamee, làá ŋúb́ ni-pɔbiliŋ nì ni-landɔkɔi ni nín sá Yeesu Kriisitoo yabee pu.

eng: From now on, brothers and sisters, if anything is excellent and if anything is admirable, focus your thoughts on these things: all that is true, all that is holy, all that is just, all that is pure, all that is lovely, and all that is worthy of praise.
local: Nnabiyaamu, m-bɔjoolkaati sèé: ní nīn dàkafī tin máaǹ tinyoolee pu, an dàkà yii tin kɔkɔ sá tiŋmanee, tin kɔkɔ sá bí nyɔ̄ŋkèe, tin kɔkɔ bí deedeeyee, tin kɔkɔ bí circiree, tin kɔkɔ kítì dineendee, nì tin kɔkɔ bi pàkèe.

eng: Practice these things: whatever you learned, received, heard, or saw in us. The God of peace will be with you.
local: Ní nīn ŋáaḿmaan tin ni bífì ki fōō m-ceeyee nì tin ni gbìl̄ m sòon̄ee nì tin ni ká m ŋáańee, ní Unimbɔti ŋgbansɔŋfi daan ń nīn bí ni-cee.

eng: A revelation of Jesus Christ, which God gave him to show his servants what must soon take place. Christ made it known by sending it through his angel to his servant John.
local: Yeesu Kriisitoo nín lēē tibɔti tin ki dàkā Yoowaneesee sèé. Unimbɔti di bí yii ú lēē tin bí ki làá ŋá kɔŋkɔnneeyee kí dàkà u-tɔntɔnliibi, ní Kriisitoo túnní u-tuuŋŋu dɔ́miń ki tùkū u-tɔntɔnliu Yoowaneesi tibɔti tin u lēē ki dàkā-uee kɔkɔ taapu.

eng: I was in a Spirit-inspired trance on the Lords day, and I heard behind me a loud voice that sounded like a trumpet.
local: An nìn sá Kɔɔsil dal, ní Unimbɔti Fam dɔ́miń m-ni. Niinee ní m gbìl̄ m-boon nneen ŋubɔ sòoǹ ki pɔ̄ɔ̄ kpaŋkpam kii dikantándee.

eng: It said, Write down on a scroll whatever you see, and send it to the seven churches: to Ephesus, Smyrna, Pergamum, Thyatira, Sardis, Philadelphia, and Laodicea.
local: yii: «Á ŋmà tin a wāĺ nee kí ŋá kugbɔŋun kí tīī Kriisitoo nikitiŋ aluli ŋin yiŋŋi m làá yíiń nee: Eefeesi nikitil nì Simiirni nikitil nì Peergaam nikitil nì Tiyatiir nikitil nì Saardi nikitil nì Fiiladeelfii nikitil nì Lawoodiisee nikitil.».

eng: I turned to see who was speaking to me, and when I turned, I saw seven oil lamps burning on top of seven gold stands.
local: M fātì kí kpèè yii ŋma di sòoǹ m-boonee, m kpēēyee ki ká fitilab tɔ́kɔŋkaati tiluli yú ki sá sindaariya.

eng: In the middle of the lampstands I saw someone who looked like the Human One. He wore a robe that stretched down to his feet, and he had a gold sash around his chest.
local: Ní m ká ubɔ náań nì unil ki yú fitilab tɔ́kɔŋkaati tin sá sindaariyee kansikin, ki pēē dibɔkutifɔkɔl diba ní ki cúú sindaariya dambalau u-bindi.

eng: His head and hair were white as white wool—like snow—and his eyes were like a fiery flame.
local: Ní u-yikpiti pīiǹ káŋkaŋ kii gulfee, ní u-nimbiliŋ ŋmóò làka laka kii ŋŋmee.

eng: His feet were like fine brass that has been purified in a furnace, and his voice sounded like rushing water.
local: Ní u-taaŋi jìl̀ pàlpal kii tasa ní bi fíntèe, ní u-neen bí kii mbuŋkpaan nín wòoǹ puee.

eng: He held seven stars in his right hand, and from his mouth came a sharp, two-edged sword. His appearance was like the sun shining with all its power.
local: Ní u ŋúb́ u-ŋaŋgiilin iŋmalbijaa iluli, ní kitàataajiki kin jītí ajan liitilee nyēē u-nyɔkɔn, ní u-nimbiin jìl̀ kii ŋwìiń yaa bíitì ki píĺ ŋu nín jìliŋkī puee.

eng: When I saw him, I fell at his feet like a dead man. But he put his right hand on me and said, Dont be afraid. Im the first and the last.
local: M nín ká mimmee ní m lítì ki kíǹ ki dɔ̄ u-taaŋi taapu kii m kpíìyee, ní u dū u-ŋaŋgiil ki páaǹ m-pu ní ki bí-mi yii: «Taa fàŋkì! Man di sá upeepeekaa ní ki sá ujoolkaa.

eng: and the living one. I was dead, but look! Now Im alive forever and always. I have the keys of Death and the Grave.
local: Man di sá un bí dimaŋfalinee. M nìn kpíì, ama kɔŋkɔnnee m kíǹ ki bí dimaŋfalin kí nīn cá jaanjaan. Man di ŋúb́ ŋkúm nì ditaŋkpiil pɔŋŋu.

eng: So write down what you have seen, both the scene now before you and the things that are about to unfold after this.
local: An puee á ŋmà tin a ká nee, nì tin bí ki jītí nee nì tin bí boon ki dòońee.

eng: who bore witness to the word of God and to the witness of Jesus Christ, including all that John saw.
local: Yoowaneesi di ŋmàn̄ Unimbɔti nín sòon̄ puee nì ibaamɔn in Yeesu Kriisitoo lēē ki dàkā-uee, nì tin kɔkɔ u kée.

eng: As for the mystery of the seven stars that you saw in my right hand and the seven gold lampstands, here is what they mean: the seven stars are the angels of the seven churches, and the seven lampstands are the seven churches.
local: Iŋmalbijaa iluli in a ká m ŋúb́ m-ŋaŋgiilinee, nì fitilab tɔ́kɔŋkaati tiluli tin sá sindaariya a ká kaa nyí an nín sá puee, taapu sèé: iŋmalbijaa ilulee di sá Kriisitoo nikitiŋ alulee tuuŋyi, ní fitilab tɔ́kɔŋkaati tilulee sá Kriisitoo nikitiŋ alulee.».

eng: Favored is the one who reads the words of this prophecy out loud, and favored are those who listen to it being read, and keep what is written in it, for the time is near.
local: Unimbɔti ń yàntī án nīn mɔ̄ǹ un di kàaǹ tibɔti tin Unimbɔti tīiń an kā kugbɔŋu nee poonee, kí nīn mɔ̄ǹ un di pílifí-tì ki ŋáań tì nín lī puee. Kun puee an nín lī yii tin kɔkɔ làá dāá jītée, ŋyunti tóobiń án ŋá míǹ.

eng: John, to the seven churches that are in Asia: Grace and peace to you from the one who is and was and is coming, and from the seven spirits that are before Gods throne.
local: An sá man Yoowaneesi di ŋmàn̄ kugbɔŋu nee ki tīī Kriisitoo nikitiŋ aluli ŋin bí Asii tiŋkinee. Unimbɔti un bí, ki nìn pée bí dooooyee ki làá dāá dāan̄ee ń ŋá-ni tiŋan kí tīī-ni ŋgbansɔŋfi nì ŋfam miluli min bí u-beejal nimbiinee.

eng: and from Jesus Christ—the faithful witness, the firstborn from among the dead, and the ruler of the kings of the earth. To the one who loves us and freed us from our sins by his blood.
local: Yeesu Kriisitoo un sòoǹ ibaamɔn, ki sá Ujipɔŋkpil, ki sá upeepeekaa un Unimbɔti fīkitì ditaŋkpiilinee, ki sá duulinya bɔtiibi kpiliuee ń tīī-ni tíǹ kɔkɔ. Úǹ di néeń-ti ní ki nyántì u-fatikuu ki kpíì ki fīī-ti ti-kpitii poon.

eng: who made us a kingdom, priests to his God and Father—to him be glory and power forever and always. Amen.
local: Úǹ di kóoǹ-ti ki kpáfì u-Beelin ní ki dū-ti ki ŋá u-Baa Unimbɔti saraaŋaliibi. Úǹ Yeesu Kriisitoo di yì tinyool nì mpɔn kí nīn pée cáa cá jaanjaan! Ameeni.

eng: Look, he is coming with the clouds! Every eye will see him, including those who pierced him, and all the tribes of the earth will mourn because of him. This is so. Amen.
local: Kpèèmaan, u làá dāá dāan̄ atagban māntì ki tú-u. Báà ŋma làá dāá ká-u, ki kpáaǹ bin nìn sāā ki cíl̀ u-takakalee. Míǹ ní duulinya timbuŋŋi kɔkɔ làá dāá sɔ̄ kí yāliŋkì u-dāantam gbanti pu. Tiŋman, án ŋá míǹ!.

eng: I am the Alpha and the Omega, says the Lord God, the one who is and was and is coming, the Almighty.
local: Ti-Dindaan Unimbɔti Mpɔŋkɔdaan, un bí, ki nìn pée bí dooooyee ki làá dāá dāan̄ee bí yii: «Man di sá Alfa nì Oomeega, upeepeekaa nì ujoolkaa.».

eng: I, John, your brother who shares with you in the hardship, kingdom, and endurance that we have in Jesus, was on the island called Patmos because of the word of God and my witness about Jesus.
local: An sá man Yoowaneesi ni-nabiki, ni nì un kpáfì ki jīǹ Yeesu pu falaa nì kimɔtii u-Beel puee. M nìn bí Patimoosi tiŋki kin dimɔɔl māntì ki túee ni ní. Kun pu bi nìn cáá-mi ki cūnnì niinee di sá m sòon̄ Unimbɔti bɔti ki lēekì Yeesu pu seera.

eng: Then I saw another powerful angel coming down from heaven. He was robed with a cloud, with a rainbow over his head. His face was like the sun, and his feet were like fiery pillars.
local: Boonee ní m ká ntuun ŋubɔ ŋun cáá mpɔnee nyánní yilpu ki kpákatiń, ní ditagbandi bīī ŋu-pu ní dikuntinyindi māntì ki tú ŋu-yil, ní ŋu-nimbiin jìliŋkī kii ŋwìińee, ní ŋu-taaŋi bí kii ŋŋmipiinee.

eng: So I took the scroll from the angels hand and ate it. And it was sweet as honey in my mouth, but when I swallowed it, it made my stomach churn.
local: Ní m fōō kigbambikee ntuun cee ki dūu ŋmɔ́. M nín bíi ŋmóòyee, an mɔ̄ m-nyɔkɔn kii tisiilee ní, ama m nín dūu náĺee ní an túŋkì m-poon.

eng: I was told, You must prophesy again about many peoples, nations, languages, and kings.
local: Ní ŋu bí-mi yii: «An sá á gíti sòoǹ tin bí ki dòońee kí tùkù binib tikpil, nì atimbun nì isoon kɔkɔ nib nì bibɔtiibi ní.».

eng: He held an open scroll in his hand. He put his right foot on the sea and his left foot on the land.
local: ní ŋu ŋúb́ kigbambiki kiba, kì kpátì ki kā ŋu-ŋaan. U nìn dū u-ŋaŋgiitaal ki tɔ́kɔ́ǹ tiŋku pu, ní ki dū u-ŋaŋgantaal ki tɔ́kɔ́ǹ kitiŋ pu.

eng: He called out with a loud voice like a lion roaring, and when he called out, the seven thunders raised their voices.
local: Ní ŋu téeń mpɔn a lì kii gbikinti di súń ní. Niinee ní itaatantati iluli bíi tátí.

eng: When the seven thunders spoke, I was about to write, but I heard a voice from heaven say, Seal up what the seven thunders have said, and dont write it down.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun m bàà là kí ŋmà itaatantatee nín sòon̄ puee ní m gbìl̄ nneen ŋubɔ sòon̄ní yilpu yii: «Á dū itaatantati ilulee bɔti kí dūú kpāafì, á taa dūú ŋmà.».

eng: Then the angel I saw standing on the sea and on the land raised his right hand to heaven.
local: Ní ntuun ŋun m bàà ká ŋu-taal diba tɔ́kɔ́ tiŋku pu, din kíǹee kitiŋ puee, yōoǹ ŋu-ŋaŋgiil ki túĺ kutaa.

eng: He swore by the one who lives forever and always, who created heaven and what is in it, the earth and what is in it, and the sea and what is in it, and said, The time is up.
local: ní ki pūtì un bí kí nīn cá jaanjaan ki náań yilpu nì tin kɔkɔ bí yilpuee, ki náań kitiŋ nì tin kɔkɔ bí kitiŋ puee, ki náań tiŋku nì tin kɔkɔ bí tiŋkunee yii: «Unimbɔti kaa làá ti yóoń ŋyunti ŋubɔ!.

eng: In the days when the seventh angel blows his trumpet, Gods mysterious purpose will be accomplished, fulfilling the good news he gave to his servants the prophets.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun ní ntuun nluluútee làá dāá búń dikantándee, ŋyunti gbantee ní tin nìn bí dibalin Unimbɔti yāntì u-tɔntɔnliibi bin sá u-bɔnaatiliibee sòon̄ee làá ŋá.».

eng: Then the voice I heard from heaven spoke to me again and said, Go, take the opened scroll from the hand of the angel who stands on the sea and on the land.
local: Ní nneen ŋun bàà nyánní yilpu ki sòon̄ nì mee gíti bí-mi yii: «Cù ntuun ŋun yú ŋu-taal diba tɔ́kɔ́ tiŋku pu, din kíǹee tɔ́kɔ́ kitiŋ puee cee kí fōō kigbambiki kin kpátì ki kā ŋu-ŋaanee.».

eng: So I went to the angel and told him to give me the scroll. He said to me, Take it and eat it. It will make you sick to your stomach, but sweet as honey in your mouth.
local: Ní m cūtì ntuunee cee ki mēē-ŋu ŋú dū kigbambikee kí tīī-mi. Ní ŋu bí-mi yii: «Fōō kí dūú ŋmɔ́, kì làá nīn mɔ̄ a-nyɔkɔn kii tisiilee ní, ama ní kí tin nín̄ tū a-poon.».

eng: Then I was given a measuring rod, which was like a pole. And I was told, Get up and measure Gods temple, the altar, and those who worship there.
local: Ní bi dū ŋkpaŋkpaan ŋubɔ ki tīī-mi, ŋu náań nì ndɔ ŋun bidimaliibi cáa ŋmàntī ki māā adiin an tèen̄ee, ní ki bí-mi yii: «Yīkìí ŋmàntī kunimbɔtidii nì nsaraafan, kí kàaǹ bin kɔkɔ bí ku-poon ki jáaḿ Unimbɔtee.».

eng: Those who live on earth will rejoice over them. They will celebrate and give each other gifts, because these two prophets had brought such pain to those who live on earth.
local: Bi-kúm gbanti puee, an dāa sá duulinya yab kɔkɔ jiŋaal ní, ní bi fàal̀ ki pūǹ tɔb tiwan. Kun pu an dāa mɔ̄ǹ-bi mimmee di sá Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi bili gbantee nìn kɔ́ǹ-bi dinimbinaamandin.

eng: But after three and a half days, the breath of life from God entered them, and they stood on their feet. Great fear came over those who saw them.
local: Ama iwiin ita nì dijandee boonee, Unimbɔti yāntì kufoou kubɔ dɔ́miń ki kɔ́ bi-ni, ní bi fīkitì, ní tifaandi cúú bin yú ki kpèé-bee.

eng: Then they heard a loud voice from heaven say to them, Come up here. And they went up to heaven in a cloud, while their enemies watched them.
local: Bi yáatìi yúl̀ee ní ki gbìl̄ nneen ŋubɔ sòon̄ní yilpu mpɔn yii: «Jɔ̄mìḿmaan doo!» Bi-nannanliibi yú ki kpèé-bi míǹ ní bi yíkìi nín jɔ́ḿ yilpu ki tin kɔ́ atagbanni.

eng: At that hour there was a great earthquake, and a tenth of the city fell. Seven thousand people were killed by the earthquake, and the rest were afraid and gave glory to the God of heaven.
local: Dicilpu niinee, kitiŋ cīŋkì ŋkpitim pu. Bi yaa yākatì kitiŋ gbanti diiŋŋi saalaa puee, diba pu lēē ki lítì ní binib kutukub biluli kpíì. Ní tifaandi kɔ́ bin kíǹ kaa kpíìyee, ní bi bí ki nyɔ́ŋkí Unimbɔti un yì yilpuee.

eng: The second horror is over. The third horror is coming soon.
local: Mbusuliliiti jítì, utataati di làá bàń kɔŋkɔnnee.

eng: Then the seventh angel blew his trumpet, and there were loud voices in heaven saying, The kingdom of the world has become the kingdom of our Lord and his Christ, and he will rule forever and always.
local: Ntuun nluluútee búń ŋu-kantándee, ní ineen kupaau sòon̄ní yilpu mpɔn yii: «Duulinya beel kpántì ti-Dindaan nì u-Meesiyau Beel ní kɔŋkɔnnee. Úǹ di làá nīn pée kā di-pu kí nīn cá jaanjaan.».

eng: Then the twenty-four elders, who were seated on their thrones before God, fell on their faces and worshipped God.
local: Ní bikpilib mmuŋku nì binaa bin kā bi-beejaliŋ pu Unimbɔti ceeyee tātìi dōoǹ dicincibil u-nimbiin ki bí ki nyɔ́ŋkí-u.

eng: They said, We give thanks to you, Lord God Almighty, who is and was, for you have taken your great power and enforced your rule.
local: ki lì yii: «Ti-Dindaan Unimbɔti Mpɔŋkɔdaan, sii un nìn bí ki gbíntì ki bée, ti jáam̀-si a nín dàkā a-pɔŋkpaaŋŋu ki jíń dibeelee pu.

eng: The nations were enraged, but your wrath came. The time came for the dead to be judged. The time came to reward your servants, the prophets and saints, and those who fear your name, both small and great, and to destroy those who destroy the earth.
local: Atimbun nìn gíí a-pu diŋuul, ama ŋyunti bāań á dàkà-ŋi a-ŋuul nín sá puee, kí sòoǹ bitaŋkpiibi bɔti. Ŋyunti bāań dee á pà a-tɔntɔnliibi bin sá a-bɔnaatiliibee paati, nì bin kɔkɔ sá a-yab ki fàŋkí-see, bikpaambi nì ŋwaamu. Ŋyunti bāań dee á kúntí bin bí ki nàantí duulinyee.».

eng: Then Gods temple in heaven was opened, and the chest containing his covenant appeared in his temple. There were lightning, voices, thunder, an earthquake, and large hail.
local: Ní bi cúú yilpu Nimbɔtidiiku ki pīitì ní dipoondi dakaau nyáń ki yú kunimbɔtidiin. Niinee ní an bíi ŋmóò ki tátí kii utaayee, ní m gbìl̄ dibintiŋkpaandi pu, ní kitiŋ cīŋkì ŋkpitim pu, ní ataanyiŋkumbil bíi lítí.

eng: But dont measure the court outside the temple. Leave that out, because it has been given to the nations, and they will trample the holy city underfoot for forty-two months.
local: Ní bi gíti bí-mi yii: «Yàntī, taa ŋmàntī kunimbɔtidii cindi din bí mmɔŋki púee, kun puee bi dū niin ki tīī atimbun ŋin kaa nyí Unimbɔtee ní. Bíǹ di làá tāā circir tiŋki pùb kí tin sāā abin ata nì dijandi.

eng: And I will allow my two witnesses to prophesy for one thousand two hundred sixty days, wearing mourning clothes.
local: M-seeradambi bin m làá dāá tɔ̄ńee dāa pēē kutukub ní ki sòoǹ m-bɔti kí tin sāā abin ata nì dijandi.».

eng: These are the two olive trees and the two lampstands that stand before the Lord of the earth.
local: M-seeraleeliibi gbantee di sá ɔliifi sufii ili nì fitilab bili bin yú ti-Dindaan un yì kitiŋee nimbiinee.

eng: If anyone wants to hurt them, fire comes out of their mouth and burns up their enemies. So if anyone wants to hurt them, they have to be killed in this way.
local: Ubɔ yaa là kí bīil̀-bee, ŋŋmi ŋubɔ làá nyānní bi-nyɔmmun ní kí wà bi-nannanliibi. Un nyàab̀ kí bīil̀-bee nín làá dāá kpú miŋyee dee.

eng: They have the power to close up the sky so that no rain will fall for as long as they prophesy. They also have power over the waters, to turn them into blood, and to strike the earth with any plague, as often as they wish.
local: Bi cáá mpɔn kí pìin̄ kutagbɔŋu iwiin in bi làá dūú sòoǹ Unimbɔti bɔtee utaa ń taa jōō kitiŋ pu, ki cáá mpɔn kí yàntī nnyim ń kpántí fatiku, ki tí cáá mpɔn kí yàntī animbinaaman nín bí tɔbin puee ń jēetí kitiŋ pu ŋyunti ŋun kamaa bi yaa lèe.

eng: When they have finished their witnessing, the beast that comes up from the abyss will make war on them, gain victory over them, and kill them.
local: Bi yaa láa sòon̄ Unimbɔti bɔti ki dóòyee, upeel un bí mbookpaannee làá dāá nyānní kí jáǹ-bi ní kí pɔ̄ɔkiǹ-bi ní kí kpɔ̀-bi.

eng: Their dead bodies will lie on the street of the great city that is spiritually called Sodom and Egypt, where also their Lord was crucified.
local: U yaa kūū-bee, anikpilee làá gbíntí ki dɔ̄ kitiŋkpaaŋŋi gbanti daŋŋi pu. Kitiŋkpaaŋŋee ní bi dū ki nántiǹ Soodoom nì Eejipiti. Kitiŋ gbanti kibaantii poon ní bi kpáá bi-Dindaan ndɔpuntikaa pu.

eng: And for three and a half days, members of the peoples, tribes, languages, and nations will look at their dead bodies, but they wont let their dead bodies be put in a tomb.
local: Anibul kɔkɔ nì atimbun kɔkɔ nì isoon kɔkɔ nib làá dāá dāan̄ kí kpèè anikpil gbanti kí tin sāā iwiin ita nì dijandi. Baa làá dāá pɔ̄ nsan bí síb́-bi.

eng: Then a great sign appeared in heaven: a woman clothed with the sun, with the moon under her feet and a crown of twelve stars on her head.
local: Boonee ní maamaacikpaŋkpaan ubɔ nyáń ki páaǹ kutagbɔŋu pu: unimpu ubɔ pēē ŋwìiń ní ki tɔ́kɔ́ uŋmal pu ní ki cíkí kibeefuulii iŋmalbijaa saalaa nì ili náká ki-pu.

eng: Then I heard a loud voice in heaven say, Now the salvation and power and kingdom of our God, and the authority of his Christ have come. The accuser of our brothers and sisters, who accuses them day and night before our God, has been thrown down.
local: Ní m gbìl̄ nneen ŋubɔ téeń mpɔn yilpu yii: «Kɔŋkɔnnee Unimbɔti fiil bāań dee! Min bí nee ní ti-Nimbɔtiu nyántì u-beel pɔŋŋu ki dàkā! Kɔŋkɔnnee u-Meesiyau dàkā mpɔn ŋun u cááyee, kun puee ukuŋfil un yú Unimbɔti nimbiin ki kúŋfí ti-nabiyaamu boon ŋwiimpu nì kunyeeuee, bi téé-u ki nyántì.

eng: They gained the victory over him on account of the blood of the Lamb and the word of their witness. Love for their own lives didnt make them afraid to die.
local: Dipibil fatikuu nì Unimbɔti bɔti tin ti-nabiyaamu sòon̄ee pu ní bi jáń ki pɔ́ɔkiǹ-u, kaa fāŋkì kí dū bi-ba kí tīī ŋkúm.

eng: Therefore, rejoice, you heavens and you who dwell in them. But oh! The horror for the earth and sea! The devil has come down to you with great rage, for he knows that he only has a short time.
local: An puee án nīn mɔ̄ǹ yilpu yab. Án nīn mɔ̄ǹ nimbi bin kóò kutagbɔŋu puee, ama an sá mbusu ní ki tīī-ni tiŋku nì kitiŋ pu yab, kun puee nì diŋuukpaandi ní Sitaan dūu kpákatìń ni-cee, ki nyí mun yii ŋyunti fiii di kíǹ-u.».

eng: When the dragon saw that he had been thrown down to the earth, he chased the woman who had given birth to the male child.
local: Kupeebiliŋŋuee nín ká yii bi téeń-ku ki lúkuń taapuee, ní ku nín jì unimpu un nìn màl̄ kibininjabikee boon.

eng: But the woman was given the two wings of the great eagle so that she could fly to her place in the desert. There she would be taken care of—out of the snakes reach—for a time and times and half a time.
local: Ní bi tīī unimpuee ifeen ili kii ukaalun feeŋyee, ú ŋàaǹ kí nīn cá kuteeun laakin bi bɔ́ntì ki yóoǹ-u ú cùú kàl̄ bí kpíiǹ-u abin ata nì dijandee, ukoobu ń taa ŋmā kí ká-u.

eng: Then from his mouth the snake poured a river of water after the woman so that the river would sweep her away.
local: Ukoobuee sáń ki jì-uee ní ki wūliŋkì nnyim ki tɔ́ unimpuee boon pú án kpántí dimɔɔl kí yòon̄-u.

eng: But the earth helped the woman. The earth opened its mouth and swallowed the river that the dragon poured out of his mouth.
local: Ama ní kitiŋ fīī unimpuee. Kitiŋ wāaǹ ki dū nnyim min min kupeebiliŋŋuee bàà wūliŋkì ki tɔ́ń u-boon án bàà yòon̄-uee ki náĺ.

eng: So the dragon was furious with the woman, and he went off to make war on the rest of her children, on those who keep Gods commandments and hold firmly to the witness of Jesus.
local: Niinee ní kupeebiliŋŋuee fōō unimpuee pu diŋuul ní ki cūtì ki bíi jáà nì unimpuee biyaamu mun kíǹee, bin kɔkɔ tɔ́ Unimbɔti marab ki ŋúb́ Yeesu bɔti deedeeyee.

eng: Then the dragon stood on the seashore.
local: Ní kupeebiliŋŋuee kíǹ ki yú tiŋku gbandi.

eng: She was pregnant, and she cried out because she was in labor, in pain from giving birth.
local: Unimpuee làá màal̀ ní ki yáliŋkí imatiŋween ki gbáà ibiil.

eng: Then another sign appeared in heaven: it was a great fiery red dragon, with seven heads and ten horns, and seven royal crowns on his heads.
local: Ní maamaaci ubɔ gíti nyáń ki páaǹ kutagbɔŋu pu: an sá kupeebiliŋŋu kubɔ, ku sá kumaŋu ki cáá ayil aluli nì iyin saalaa, ní kibeefuulii cíkí diyil sá kamaa pu.

eng: His tail swept down a third of heavens stars and threw them to the earth. The dragon stood in front of the woman who was about to give birth so that when she gave birth, he might devour her child.
local: U-juul ní u dūu fùkū iŋmalbijaa yaa yākatì mitee diba pu ki lúkufìń kitiŋ pu, ní ki kíǹ ki yú ki kɔ́ĺ unimpu un làá màal̀ee, u yaa màl̄ee ú cúú kibikee kí ŋmɔ́.

eng: She gave birth to a son, a male child who is to rule all the nations with an iron rod. Her child was snatched up to God and his throne.
local: Ní unimpuee dūu màl̄ kibininjabiki. Kíǹ di làá nīn cáá mpɔn kii tikútee kí dūú kpòotī atimbun kɔkɔ, ní bi yōoǹ u-bikee malaa ki jɔ́m̀ Unimbɔti cee, u-beejal laŋki.

eng: Then the woman fled into the desert, where God has prepared a place for her. There she will be taken care of for one thousand two hundred sixty days.
local: Ní unaati sáǹ ki búntì kuteeun laakin binib kpée. Niin ní Unimbɔti bɔ́ntì ki yóoǹ-u ú cùú kàl̄ bí nīn kpīintí-u kí tin sāā abin ata nì dijandi.

eng: Then there was war in heaven: Michael and his angels fought the dragon. The dragon and his angels fought back.
local: Niinee ní kujaau fōō ki píĺ kutagbɔŋu pu, Unimbɔti tuuŋyi kpiliu Mikayeel nì u-yab bí ki jáà nì kupeebiliŋŋuee nì u-yab.

eng: but they did not prevail, and there was no longer any place for them in heaven.
local: ní Mikayeel nì u yab jáń ki pɔ́ɔkiǹ-bi, ní ki jìn̄-bi bí taa ti kàl̄ kutagbɔŋun.

eng: So the great dragon was thrown down. The old snake, who is called the devil and Satan, the deceiver of the whole world, was thrown down to the earth; and his angels were thrown down with him.
local: Ní bi téeń kupeebiliŋŋuee, ukookpiki un bi yíì Sitaan u kíil̀ duulinya kɔkɔee nì u-tuuŋyi ki tɔ́ń duulinyan.

eng: and I saw a beast coming up out of the sea. It had ten horns and seven heads. Each of its horns was decorated with a royal crown, and on its heads were blasphemous names.
local: M kpēēyee, ní ki ká upeel ubɔ nyánní tiŋkun, ki cáá ayil aluli nì iyin saalaa ní ki cíkí u-yiŋŋu kamaa pu kibeefuulii, ní diyil sá kamaa puee disiibil náká ki sìí Unimbɔti.

eng: If any are to be taken captive, then into captivity they will go. If any are to be killed by the sword, then by the sword they will be killed. This calls for endurance and faithfulness on the part of the saints.
local: Un Unimbɔti lī yii bi làá cúú kuyuŋuee, see bí cúú-u kuyuŋu. Un Unimbɔti lī yii bi làá kpɔ̀-u nì kitàataajikee, see bí kpɔ̀ udaan nì kitàataajiki. An sá Unimbɔti yab ń dàkà bi-pɔ́ɔǹtam nì bi-fookiiu un bi cáá Unimbɔti puee ní.».

eng: Then I saw another beast coming up from the earth. It had two horns like a lamb, but it was speaking like a dragon.
local: Boonee ní m ká upeel ubɔ mun púń ki nyáń kitiŋ ki cáá iyin ili an bí kii dipibil yiŋyee, ní ki sòoǹ kii kupeebiliŋŋuee.

eng: It exercises all the authority of the first beast in its presence. It also makes the earth and those who live in it worship the first beast, whose fatal wound was healed.
local: Upeel upeepeekaayee nimbiin ní u ŋáań maamaacib nì mpɔn ŋun u fōō u-ceeyee, ní ki yàntī binib bin kɔkɔ bí kitiŋ puee jáaḿ upeel upeepeekaa un nìn fōō ŋkúm weeŋyi ki dāa pɔ́ɔkèe.

eng: It does great signs so that it even makes fire come down from heaven to earth in the presence of the people.
local: Upeel uliliitee nìn ŋáań maamaacikpaambi, ki yàntī ŋŋmi nyēeń yilpu ki lítí kitiŋ binib kɔkɔ nimbiin.

eng: It deceives those who live on earth by the signs that it was allowed to do in the presence of the beast. It told those who live on earth to make an image for the beast who had been wounded by the sword and yet came to life again.
local: Mpɔn ŋun bi nìn tīī-u u cáa ŋáań maamaacib upeel upeepeekaa nimbiinee ní u nìn cáa kíiĺ binib ní ki páb́ binib bí náań upeel un bi nìn gāā kitàataajiki ki tīī tiweendi waa kpíìyee naŋku kí yóoń.

eng: It was allowed to give breath to the beasts image so that the beasts image would even speak and cause anyone who didnt worship the beasts image to be put to death.
local: Ní u fōō mpɔn kí ŋmā kí tīī upeelee naŋku kun bi máńee kufoou, án nín làá ŋá pu kú ŋmā kí nīn sòoǹee, ní bin yaa kaa gbāaǹ ki jáam̀-kuee ú kpɔ̀ bidambee.

eng: It forces everyone—the small and great, the rich and poor, the free and slaves—to have a mark put on their right hand or on their forehead.
local: Mbiyaamueeee, bikpaambeeee, bigaajabeeee, bisoodambeeee, bin tɔ́ bi-beeyeeee, iyumbueeee, u cāntì bi nákáǹ bi-kɔkɔ ŋaŋgiilii nì bi-yigbeeŋi pu tidaandi.

eng: It will not allow anyone to make a purchase or sell anything unless the person has the mark with the beasts name or the number of its name.
local: Ubɔ kaa ŋūǹ kí dā tiba, kaa tí ŋūǹ kí nyāfì tiba, upeelee yindi nì u-yindi nambau yaa kaa náká udaan pu.

eng: This calls for wisdom. Let the one who understands calculate the beasts number, for its a human beings number. Its number is six hundred sixty-six.
local: An sá mpɔ́ɔn ní, un cáá iciinee ń tùkù taapu. Kun puee tita nì ita nì biluuyee dàkà unil ubɔ yindi ní.

eng: The beast I saw was like a leopard. Its feet were like a bears, and its mouth was like a lions mouth. The dragon gave it his power, throne, and great authority.
local: Upeel un m kée náań nì ugbii ní, ní u-taaŋi náań nì digbiŋgbil, ní u-nyɔkɔ náań nì gbikinti nyɔkɔ, ní kupeebiliŋŋuee dū ku-pɔŋyi kɔkɔ nì ku-beel ki tīī-u.

eng: One of its heads appeared to have been slain and killed, but its deadly wound was healed. So the whole earth was amazed and followed the beast.
local: U-yiliŋee diba nìn fōō tiweendi ki cá ŋkúm, ama ní iweenee dāa dóò, ní an mɔ́ɔkiǹ duulinya yab kɔkɔ ní bi nóó upeelee.

eng: They worshipped the dragon because it had given the beast its authority. They worshipped the beast and said, Who is like the beast, and who can fight against it.
local: Ní binib kɔkɔ bí ki nyɔ́ŋkí kupeebiliŋŋuee ku nín dū ku-pɔŋyi kɔkɔ nì ku-beel ki dūu tīī upeel un nyánní tiŋkunee pu, ní bi bí ki nyɔ́ŋkí upeelee ki lì yii: «Ŋma di ŋūǹ kí dū u-ba kí ŋmàntīǹ upeel nee? Ŋma di ŋūǹ kí já nì u?».

eng: The beast was given a mouth that spoke boastful and blasphemous things, and it was given authority to act for forty-two months.
local: Ní bi pú upeelee kinyɔkɔ ú nīn sòoǹ tibɔfan ki bíiĺ Unimbɔti, ní ki tīī-u mpɔn ú nīn ŋáań míǹ kí tin sāā abin ata nì dijandi.

eng: It opened its mouth to speak blasphemies against God. It blasphemed Gods name and his dwelling place .
local: U nìn lafun kíĺ ki bíi sòoǹ tibɔfan ki bíiĺ Unimbɔti yindi, nì u-kɔŋkooŋŋu nì bin kɔkɔ kóò yilpuee.

eng: It was also allowed to make war on the saints and to gain victory over them. It was given authority over every tribe, people, language, and nation.
local: Bi nìn pú upeelee nsan ú já nì Unimbɔti yab kí pɔ̄ɔkiǹ-bi, ní ki tīī-u tinaakɔti kɔkɔ nì anibul kɔkɔ, nì isoon kɔkɔ nib nì atimbun kɔkɔ pu mpɔn.

eng: All who live on earth worshipped it, all whose names hadnt been written—from the time the earth was made—in the scroll of life of the Lamb who was slain.
local: Kitiŋ pu nib bin yiŋŋi kaa ŋmàn̄ doooo duulinya kíltam dimaŋfal gbɔŋku kun Dipibil din kpíìyee yèe poonee, an nibee kɔkɔ dāa jáaḿ upeelee ní.

eng: Whoever has ears must listen.
local: «Un yaa cáá ditafalee ń pīlifì tiŋan.

eng: Then I looked, and there was the Lamb, standing on Mount Zion. With him were one hundred forty-four thousand who had his name and his Fathers name written on their foreheads.
local: Niinee ní m ká Dipibil yú Siiyɔn jool pu, u nì binib kutukub dilaataal nì imuŋku ili nì binaa bin yigbeeŋi pu bi ŋmàn̄ u nì u-Baa yiŋŋee.

eng: they themselves will also drink the wine of Gods passionate anger, poured full strength into the cup of his wrath. They will suffer the pain of fire and sulfur in the presence of the holy angels and the Lamb.
local: Unimbɔti làá dāá nyùntī udaan ndaam min sá u-ŋuulee. U làá dāá fōō bidambi pu diŋuukpaandi din kaa cáá jutee kí dū-bi kí tɔ̄ farwata ŋmiŋun bí jī falaakpaan, u-tuuŋyi circirdambi nì Dipibil nimbiin.

eng: The smoke of their painful suffering goes up forever and always. There is no rest day or night for those who worship the beast and its image, and those who receive the mark of its name.
local: Ŋŋmi ŋun poon bi làá nīn bí ki jīǹ falaayee làá nīn pée dùkù ní kaa cáá dikúntil. Baa dāa cáá diŋuŋfil ŋwiimpu nì kunyeeu, bíǹ bin kɔkɔ gbāaǹ ki jáam̀ upeel nì u-naŋkuee, nì bin yāntì bi dū upeelee yindi ki dáań-bee.».

eng: This calls for the endurance of the saints, who keep Gods commandments and keep faith with Jesus.
local: Ŋyunti nee di máaǹ Unimbɔti yab bin ŋúb́ u-marab tiŋan ki máká Yeesu puee ń pɔ́ɔǹ bi-ba, kí nīn cáá kimɔtii.

eng: And I heard a voice from heaven say, Write this: Favored are the dead who die in the Lord from now on. Yes, says the Spirit, so they can rest from their labors, because their deeds follow them.
local: Ní m gbìl̄ nneen ŋubɔ sòon̄ní yilpu yii: «Ŋmà yii: <Bin nóó ti-Dindaan ki kúù kɔŋkɔnneeyee bí mmɔnneeee!> Unimbɔti Fam lī yii: <An sá mbaamɔn, bi làá cùú kàl̄ kí ŋùŋfì, kun puee atunnyaan ŋin bi túńee dī-bi!>».

eng: Then I looked, and there was a white cloud. On the cloud was seated someone who looked like the Human One. He had a gold crown on his head and a sharp sickle in his hand.
local: M kpēēyee, ní ki ká ditagbampindi diba, ní ubɔ kā di-pu ki náań nì unil, ní ki cíkí sindaariya beefuuliiki ki ŋúb́ ugɔti un jītée u-ŋaan.

eng: Another angel came out of the temple, calling in a loud voice to the one seated on the cloud: Use your sickle to reap the harvest, for the time to harvest has come, and the harvest of the earth is ripe.
local: Ní ntuun ŋubɔ nyánní kunimbɔtidiin ki téeń mpɔn ki tùkū un kā ditagbampindi puee yii: «Dū a-gɔtiuee kí cā tijikaal, kun puee kudiceeu ŋmāntì. Duulinya bīń ki ŋmāntì bí cā!».

eng: So the one seated on the cloud swung his sickle over the earth, and the earth was harvested.
local: Niinee ní un kā ditagbandi puee dū u-gɔtiu ki bɔ́ duulinyan ní ki cáń duulinya.

eng: Then another angel came out of the temple in heaven, and he also had a sharp sickle.
local: Ní ntuun ŋubɔ mun gíti nyánní yilpu Nimbɔtidiikun ki mun ŋúb́ ugɔti un jītée.

eng: Still another angel, who has power over fire, came out from the altar. He said in a loud voice to the one who had the sharp sickle, Use your sharp sickle to cut the clusters in the vineyard of the earth, because its grapes are ripe.
local: Ní ntuun ŋubɔ gíti tí nyánní nsaraafanni, ŋúǹ di ŋúb́ ŋŋmi pɔŋŋu, ki téeń mpɔn ki tùkū ŋun ŋúb́ ugɔti an jītée yii: «Dū a-gɔtiu un jītée kí bɔ̄ fiinyi sufii in sí kitiŋ puee sakaŋi poon kí kìtī, kun puee abil bīń.».

eng: So the angel swung his sickle into the earth, and cut the vineyard of the earth, and he put what he reaped into the great winepress of Gods passionate anger.
local: Ní ntuunee dū ŋu-gɔtiu ki bɔ́ fiinyi sufiin ní ki kītì duulinya fiinyi biliŋ ki gbéeǹ mbookpaan ŋun ni bi ŋmāam̀ ki lèekī ndaamee, an dàkà yii bi cáa kɔ́ǹ Unimbɔti ŋuulin dee.

eng: I heard a sound from heaven that was like the sound of rushing water and loud thunder. The sound I heard was like that of harpists playing their harps.
local: M gbìl̄ nneen ŋubɔ nyánní yilpu a lì kii mbuŋkpaan nyim nín sìtī puee, ní ki gíti bí kii utaa nín tátí pu mpɔn mpɔnee, ní nneen ŋun pu m gbìl̄ee bí kii bibeebɔɔliibi di bɔ̀ɔ́ bi-bati ki gà ilanee.

eng: Then the winepress was trampled outside the city, and the blood came out of the winepress as high as the horses bridles for almost two hundred miles.
local: Ní bi ŋmáaḿ fiinyi biliŋee mbookpaanee ni kitiŋ kpaanceetiŋu nnyim ń nyā. Kun nyáńee di sá fatiku, ki pùub̀ ki tin sāā ataŋkpee alaataa ata. Ní fatikuee nín dɔ̄ ki jɔ́m̀ puee tin sāā itaamu nyɔɔkpaaŋi.

eng: They sing a new song in front of the throne, the four living creatures, and the elders. And no one could learn the song except the one hundred forty-four thousand who had been purchased from the earth.
local: Binib gbantee nìn yú dibeejal nì tiwan tinaa tin cáá kufoouee nì bikpilibee nimbiin ki gà nlampɔɔn, ubɔ kaa ŋūǹ kí bīfì-ŋu, asee binib kutukub dilaataal nì imuŋku ili nì binaa bin bi dá ki nyántìń duulinyanee baba di ŋūǹ kí gāā an laŋŋu.

eng: They werent defiled with women, for these people who follow the Lamb wherever he goes are virgins. They were purchased from among humankind as early produce for God and the Lamb.
local: An nibee kaa dōoǹ nì binimpoobi ki kɔ́ǹ bi-ba tijɔkɔndi, bi ŋúb́ bi-ba circir ní. Bi dī Dipibil boon ki cá laakin dì yaa cée. Unimbɔti dá-bi ki nyántì binib kansikin bí nīn sá bin bi làá péé péé kí dūú sú Unimbɔti nì Dipibilee.

eng: No lie came from their mouths; they are blameless.
local: Baa gbìl̄ an nibee mɔ́ntì nnyimɔn dalba ki ká, kaa ti kāntí ikpiti iba bi-pu kí lì.

eng: Then I saw another angel flying high overhead with eternal good news to proclaim to those who live on earth, and to every nation, tribe, language, and people.
local: Boonee ní m ká ntuun ŋubɔ ŋàaǹ kutagbɔŋu pu yilpu yilpu ki cáá Tibɔmɔntiil tin kaa cáá dikúntilee ki tùkù binib bin kóò duulinyanee, ntimu kɔkɔ nib nì atimbun kɔkɔ nì isoon kɔkɔ nib nì anibul kɔkɔ.

eng: He said in a loud voice, Fear God and give him glory, for the hour of his judgment has come. Worship the one who made heaven and earth, the sea and springs of water.
local: Ŋu nìn tátí mpɔn yii: «Fàŋkìmaan Unimbɔti kí tīī-u tinyool, kun puee ŋyunti bāań ú sòoǹ nnibiyaamu bɔti. Nyɔ̄ŋkìmaan Unimbɔti un náań yilpu nì taapuee, nì tiŋku nì inyimbul kɔkɔee.».

eng: Another angel, a second one, followed and said, Fallen, fallen is Babylon the great! She made all the nations drink the wine of her lustful passion.
local: Ní ntuun nliliiti páaǹní ní ki lī yii: «Babilooŋkpaan lítì! Kitiŋkpaaŋŋi lítì! U nyūntì atimbun kɔkɔ u-daam min sá u-laakpaati tin u ŋáań an lákatèe!».

eng: Then another angel, a third one, followed them and said in a loud voice, If any worship the beast and its image, and receive a mark on their foreheads or their hands.
local: Ní ntuun ntataati páaǹní ki lī mpɔn yii: «Un di yaa gbāaǹ ki jáam̀ upeelee nì u-naŋku, ní ki yāntì bi dáań u-yigbeel nì u-ŋalee.

eng: Then I saw another great and awe-inspiring sign in heaven. There were seven angels with seven plagues—and these are the last, for with them Gods anger is brought to an end.
local: Ní m gíti ká kudaaŋŋu kubɔ yilpu, ku náaǹ ki cáá maamaaci. M ká ituun iluli ŋúb́ falaabi biluli. Bíǹ di sá bifalaajoolkaabi bin Unimbɔti làá dūú tɔ̄ binib pu ní u-ŋuul ń nín sɔ̀ŋkèe.

eng: Then I saw what appeared to be a sea of glass mixed with fire. Those who gained victory over the beast, its image, and the number of its name were standing by the glass sea, holding harps from God.
local: Ní m ká tiba an náań nì tiŋku ní ki wāĺ kílkil kii digitilee ní ki ŋmàl̄ nì ŋŋmi, ní m ká bin jáń ki pɔ́ɔkiǹ upeelee nì u-naŋku nì namba un sá u-yindee yú tiŋku un wāĺ kílkil kii digitilee cee ki ŋúb́ tibati tin Unimbɔti tīī-bee.

eng: They sing the song of Moses, Gods servant, and the song of the Lamb, saying, Great and awe-inspiring are your works, Lord God Almighty. Just and true are your ways, king of the nations.
local: ní bi bí ki gà Mooyiisi Unimbɔti tɔntɔnliu nì Dipibil laŋyi yii: «Ti-Dindaan Unimbɔti Mpɔŋkɔdaan, a-tuuŋŋi wīikí ki sá amɔnti, sii di sá atimbun bɔtiu. A nín lēē nsàmbiyaamu muŋyee bí deedee ní ki sá mbaamɔn.

eng: Who wont fear you, Lord, and glorify your name? You alone are holy. All nations will come and fall down in worship before you, for your acts of justice have been revealed.
local: Ti-Dindaan, ŋma di kaa làá fàŋkì-si? Ŋma di làá yíí, kaa làá tīī-si tinyool? Kun puee sii baba di sá circirdaan, atimbun kɔkɔ làá dāan̄ ní kí gbàan̄ a-nimbiin kí nyɔ̄ŋkì-si, kun puee báà ŋma ká a-tuuŋŋi, ŋi bí deedee.».

eng: After this I looked, and the temple in heaven—that is, the tent of witness—was opened.
local: Míǹ boonee m ká kunimbɔtidii kun bí yilpuee cúu pīitì, ní Unimbɔti tantiu un dàkà u-bítamee yú poon.

eng: The seven angels, who have the seven plagues, came out of the temple. They were clothed in pure bright linen and had gold sashes around their waists.
local: ní ituun iluli in ŋúb́ falaabi bilulee nyánní kunimbɔtidiiyee poon ki pēē tiwampin káŋkaŋ ki jìl̀, ní ki dū sindaariya dambalau ki cúú i-biŋŋi.

eng: Then one of the four living creatures gave the seven angels seven gold bowls full of the anger of the God who lives forever and always.
local: Tiwan tinaa tin cáá kufoouee kubɔ tīī ituun ilulee sindaariya sambiyaamu mululi, Unimbɔti un bí jaanjaanee ŋuul gbí poon.

eng: The temple was filled with smoke from Gods glory and power, and no one could go into the temple until the seven plagues of the seven last angels were brought to an end.
local: Niinee ní Unimbɔti nyooti nì u-pɔŋŋu yāntì nnyiim yíkì ki gbí kunimbɔtidiin, ní ubɔ kaa ti ŋūǹ kí kɔ́ niin, see falaabi bin bi tīī ituun ilulee nín dāa jítèe.

eng: Then I heard a loud voice from the temple say to the seven angels, Go and pour out the seven bowls of Gods anger on the earth.
local: Ní m gbìl̄ nneen ŋubɔ nyánní kunimbɔtidiin ki sòoǹ mpɔn ki tùkù ituun ilulee yii: «Nín̄ cámaan kí kpáatí Unimbɔti ŋuul din kā nsambiyaamu mululinee kí sìtīmaan duulinyan.».

eng: The fifth angel poured his bowl over the beasts throne, and darkness covered its kingdom. People bit their tongues because of their pain.
local: Ní ntuun ŋŋmooŋmooti kpáatì diŋuul din gbí ŋu-sambikinee ki sītì upeelee beejal pu, ní u-beelin nib jéetì dibɔmbɔndin, ní tifaandi yāntì binib júntí bi-limbiliŋ, kun puee iween nìn gbìl̄-bi.
