eng: and they cursed the God of heaven because of their pains and sores; but they didnt turn away from what they had done.
local: Bi-bunsooŋyi nì bi-falaau un bi bí ki jīǹee pu ní bi nìn bí ki bíiĺ Unimbɔti un yì yilpuee. Ama bi yíì, kaa fá bi-tuŋkpitiŋ.

eng: Then the sixth angel poured his bowl on the great river Euphrates. Its water was dried up so that the way was ready for the kings from the east.
local: Ní ntuun nluluuti kpáatì diŋuul din kā ŋu-sambikinee ki sītì Eefiraati mɔɔkpaandin, ní dimɔɔl fóotì ki kūń ní nsan dōoǹ ní ŋwìiń nyalaŋki pú bɔtiibi kíǹ ki dì.

eng: Then I saw three unclean spirits, like frogs, come from the dragons mouth, the beasts mouth, and the mouth of the false prophet.
local: Ní m ká arasiniibi bita, bi náań nì kpaadalib ki nyánní kupeebiliŋŋuee nì upeel un kíǹee nì u-bɔnaatiliu unyimɔndaan nyɔmmun.

eng: These are demonic spirits that do signs. They go out to the kings of the whole world, to gather them for battle on the great day of God the Almighty. .
local: Arasiniibi bitee ŋáań maamaacib, ní ki cūtì ki ká duulinya kɔkɔ bɔtiibi ki kpáfì-bi bí dāá já nì Unimbɔti Mpɔŋkɔdaan u-wiŋkpaaŋŋu dal.

eng: Look! Im coming like a thief! Favored are those who stay awake and clothed so that they dont go around naked and exposed to shame.
local: «Ní pīlifì, m làá dāan̄ kii unaayuu yamee ní! Un kaa gèen̄ ki kā ki cí u-bɔkutil dɔ̄ ki kɔ́ĺ-uee bí mmɔnni. Udaan kaa dāa nyēē dicancaŋgbeel bí ká u-cooi.».

eng: The spirits gathered them at the place that is called in Hebrew, Harmagedon.
local: Ní arasiniibee kóoǹ bibɔtiibi ki cáa kpáfì laakin Eebru yab yíì yii Arimageedɔŋyee.

eng: Then the seventh angel poured his bowl into the air, and a loud voice came out from the temple, from the throne, saying, It is done.
local: Ní ntuun nluluúti kpáatì diŋuul din kā ŋu-sambikinee ki yàl̄ kutaa pu, ní m gbìl̄ nneen ŋubɔ nyánní kunimbɔtidiin dibeejal cee ki sòon̄ mpɔn yii: «An ŋá ki dóò!».

eng: There were lightning strikes, voices, and thunder, and a great earthquake occurred. The earthquake was greater than any that have occurred since there have been people on earth.
local: Niinee an bíi ŋmóò nyàlnyal ki tátí kii utaayee, ní m gbìl̄ dibintiŋkpaandi pu ní kitiŋ cīŋkì ŋkpitim pu. Kí yòon̄ ŋwiin ŋun binib kíĺ ki kóò kitiŋ pu ki dāa sāā díǹ nee, kitiŋ kaa láá cīŋkì míǹ bi ká.

eng: The great city split into three parts, and the cities of the nations fell. God remembered Babylon the great so that he gave her the wine cup of his furious anger.
local: Ní kitiŋkpaaŋŋi lítì ki nābitì agin ata, ní atimbun tiŋkpaammu kɔkɔ wīil̀. Niinee Unimbɔti téetì Babiloon kitiŋkpaaŋŋi pu, ní ki dū u-ŋuul din pɔ̄ɔ̄ kpaŋkpamee ki nyūntì-kì kii ndaamee.

eng: So the first angel poured his bowl on the earth, and a nasty and terrible sore appeared on the people who had the beasts mark and worshipped its image.
local: Ní ntuun mpeepeekaa kpáatì diŋuul din gbí ŋu-sambikinee ki sītì duulinyan, ní ibunsoon dɔ́miń ki jéetì bin kɔkɔ cáá upeelee daaŋku ki jáaḿ u-naŋkuee pu.

eng: Every island fled, and the mountains disappeared.
local: Ní ntimu mun bí amɔɔ kansikinee nì ajoon wáŋkì.

eng: Huge hailstones weighing about one hundred pounds came down from heaven on the people. They cursed God for the plague of hail, because the plague was so terrible.
local: Ní ataanyiŋkumbil ŋin nyìm̀ kii kiiloobi imuŋku ilee lítí ki tàbitī binib, ní binib bí ki sìí Unimbɔti ditafadaaldi gbanti pu, kun puee ditafadaaldee nìn pɔ̄ɔ̄ ki lákatì.

eng: The second angel poured his bowl into the sea, and the sea turned into blood, like the blood of a corpse, and every living thing in the sea died.
local: Ní ntuun nliliiti kpáatì diŋuul din gbí ŋu-sambikinee ki sītì tiŋkun, ní tiŋku nyim kpántì fatiku a lì kii unil un bi kūūyee fatikuuee, ní tin kɔkɔ bí tiŋkun ki cáá kufoouee kpíì.

eng: The third angel poured his bowl into the rivers and springs of water, and they turned into blood.
local: Ní ntuun ntataati kpáatì diŋuul din gbí ŋu-sambikinee ki sītì amɔɔ nì ikpin poon, ní inyiŋfi kpántì fatiku.

eng: Then I heard the angel of the waters say, You are just, holy one, who is and was, because you have given these judgments.
local: Niinee m gbìl̄ ntuun ŋun yì inyiŋfi pɔŋŋuee bí yii: «A sá deedeedaan ní a nín ŋá min nee pu, sii un bí ki nìn pée bí dooooyee, a sá circirdaan ní.

eng: They poured out the blood of saints and prophets, and you have given them blood to drink. They deserve it.
local: Di sá bi nìn kūū a-nib nì a-bɔnaatiliibi bi-fatikuu nyáń ki fá, míǹ pu ní a yāntì inyiŋfi kpántì fatiku bí nyɔ̀, bi nín máaǹ puee ní a ŋá-bi nee!».

eng: And I heard the altar say, Yes, Lord God Almighty, your judgments are true and just.
local: Ní m gbìl̄ nneen ŋubɔ nyánní nsaraafanni ki lī yii: «Tiŋman, ti-Dindaan Unimbɔti Mpɔŋkɔdaan, a nín sòon̄ binib bɔti miŋyee sá mbaamɔn ní ki sá deedee.».

eng: The fourth angel poured his bowl on the sun, and it was allowed to burn people with fire.
local: Ní ntuun nnanaati kpáatì diŋuul din kā ŋu-sambikinee ki sītì ŋwìiń pu yii ŋwìiń ń píĺ binib tikpil.

eng: The people were burned by intense heat, and they cursed the name of the God who had power over these plagues. But they didnt change their hearts and lives and give him glory.
local: Ní kutuntuŋu píĺ binib tikpil ní bi bí ki bíiĺ Unimbɔti un cáá mpɔn falaabi gbanti puee yindi, ki yíì kaa fá bi-kpitii kí tīī Unimbɔti tinyool.

eng: Then one of the seven angels who had the seven bowls spoke with me. Come, he said, I will show you the judgment upon the great prostitute, who is seated on deep waters.
local: Boonee ní ituun iluli in kpáatì diŋuul ki sītì duulinya puee ŋubɔ sútìń m-cee ní ki bí-mi yii: «Dāan̄ tí cù ḿ dàkà-si bi nín làá dáaĺ ulaakpaadaan un pée sá ukpaan ki kā ikpin puee tafal miŋyee.

eng: Five kings have fallen, the one is, and the other hasnt yet come. When that king comes, he must remain for only a short time.
local: Bibɔtiibi bilulee kansikin biŋmoo lítì, ubɔ kā dibeejal pu, ní un kíǹee kaa láá bāań, ama u mun yaa láa bāańee, waa làá dāá kàl̄ kí yúntí.

eng: As for the beast that was and is not, it is itself an eighth king that belongs to the seven, and it is going to destruction.
local: Upeel un nìn bí ní kaa ti bée u-ba kútì ki sá ubɔtininiiti, u mun sá bibɔtiibi bilulee kansikin ubɔ ní. Úǹ ní bi làá nàan̄.

eng: The ten horns that you saw are ten kings, who havent yet received royal power. But they will receive royal authority for an hour, along with the beast.
local: «Iyin saalaa in a kée di sá bibɔtiibi saalaa bin kaa láá kāl̀ dibeel puee, ama bi làá tīī-bi mpɔn bi nì upeelee ń jī dibeel kukúluu kubɔ poon.

eng: These kings will be of one mind, and they will give their power and authority to the beast.
local: Bibɔtiibee kɔkɔ dāa pēē kinyɔkɔ ní kí dū bi-pɔŋyi kí kūtì upeelee.

eng: They will make war on the Lamb, but the Lamb will emerge victorious, for he is Lord of lords and King of kings. Those with him are called, chosen, and faithful.
local: Ní bí jáǹ Dipibil, ní Dipibil ń dāá pɔ̄ɔkiǹ-bi, u nì bin kɔkɔ sá u-yab u lútì-bi bi yú deedeeyee, kun puee úǹ di sá bidindambi kɔkɔ Dindaan, nì bibɔtiibi kɔkɔ Bɔtiu.».

eng: Then he said to me, The waters that you saw, where the prostitute is seated, are peoples, crowds, nations, and languages.
local: Ní ntuunee gíti bí-mi yii: «Inyiŋfi in pu a ká kilaakpaayee kēe di sá atimbun nibiim nì isoon kamaa nib.

eng: As for the ten horns that you saw, they and the beast will hate the prostitute. They will destroy her and strip her bare. They will devour her flesh and burn her with fire.
local: Iyin saalaa in a kée nì upeelee làá nīn ná kilaakpaayee ní. Bi làá dāá gbākal̀ tiwan tin kɔkɔ kā ki-wunti puee ní kí kíń dicancaŋgbeel, ní kí dāá ŋmɔ́ ki-nanti ní kí dū-kì kí wà ŋŋmi.

eng: because God moved them to carry out his purposes. That is why they will be of one mind and give their royal power to the beast, until Gods words have been accomplished.
local: Di sá Unimbɔti di dū min u lèe ki ŋá bi-pɔbiliŋin bí ŋá ubaanti kí dū bi-pɔŋyi kí kūtì upeelee pu, kí nīn cí ŋyunti ŋun Unimbɔti làá dāá ŋá min u nìn lī yii u làá ŋée.

eng: The woman whom you saw is the great city that rules over the kings of the earth.
local: «Unimpu un a kée di sá kitiŋkpaaŋŋi kin tɔ́kɔ́ duulinya bɔtiibi puee.».

eng: The kings of the earth have committed sexual immorality with her, and those who live on earth have become drunk with the wine of her whoring.
local: Kitiŋ pu bɔtiibi dōoǹ nì u ní kitiŋ pu nib mɔ̄nnì-u ní u-laakpaati gbí-bi kii ndaamee.».

eng: Then he brought me in a Spirit-inspired trance to a desert. There I saw a woman seated on a scarlet beast that was covered with blasphemous names. It had seven heads and ten horns.
local: Ní Unimbɔti Fam dɔ́miń m-ni ní ntuun yōoǹ-mi ti búntì kuteeun, ní m tin ká unimpu ubɔ kā upeel pu. Upeelee sá uman, ní u-wunti kɔkɔ náká asiibil ki sìí Unimbɔti, ki cáá ayil aluli nì iyin saalaa.

eng: The woman wore purple and scarlet clothing, and she glittered with gold and jewels and pearls. In her hand she held a gold cup full of the vile and impure things that came from her activity as a prostitute.
local: Unimpu gbanti pēē dibɔkutinyaandi diba dì máǹ, ní u-falfatib nì u-bɔmbiliŋ nì u-beedimbiliŋ sá sindaariya nì ataŋkpee ŋin pɔ̄ɔ̄ kudaau ki jìl̀ee, ní ki ŋúb́ sindaariya sambiki ikpiti nì tilaakpaal tin u ŋáańee jɔkɔnti gbí poon.

eng: A name—a mystery—was written on her forehead: Babylon the great, the mother of prostitutes and the vile things of the earth.
local: Ní dimaamaaciyindi diba ŋmàn̄ ki náká u-yigbeel pu yii: «Babiloon kitiŋkpaaŋŋi, tilaakpaal dambi nì bitiŋwiiliibi na.».

eng: I saw that the woman was drunk on the blood of the saints and the blood of Jesus witnesses. I was completely stunned when I saw her.
local: Ní m ká yii unimpuee nyùn̄ Unimbɔti nib nì bin nóó Yeesu deedee bi kūū-bee fatikuu ki gbí. M nín ká mimmee ní m bākatì sèeee.

eng: Then the angel said to me, Why are you amazed? I will tell you the mystery of the woman and the seven-headed, ten-horned beast that carries her.
local: Ní Unimbɔti tuuŋŋu bālifì-mi yii: «Ba ŋá ní a bākatì? M làá bìitī unimpuee nì upeel un pu u kā u cáá ayil aluli nì iyin saalaayee bɔɔŋŋi pu kí tùkù-si.

eng: The beast that you saw was and is not, and is about to come up out of the abyss and go to destruction. Those who live on earth, whose names havent been written in the scroll of life from the time the earth was made, will be amazed when they see the beast, because it was and is not and will again be present.
local: Upeel un a ká nee nìn bí dooooyee, ama kɔŋkɔnnee waa ti bí. U làá nyānní mbookpaan ŋun ni u nìn bée ní kí nīn cá laakin bi làá nàan̄-uee. An làá dāá ŋá bin doooo duulinya kíltam baa ŋmàn̄ bi-yiŋŋi dimaŋfal gbɔŋkunee maamaaci, bi yaa láa fātìi gíti ká upeel gbantee. Di sá upeelee nìn pée bí dooooyee ní ki dāa wáŋkì, ní ki dāa gíti fātìi nyánní.

eng: This calls for an understanding mind. The seven heads are seven mountains on which the woman is seated. They are also seven kings.
local: «Tibɔti nee pu ní unil máaǹ kí dàkà u-landɔkɔŋu nì u-ciiŋŋu. Upeelee yiliŋ alulee di sá ajoon aluli ŋin pu unimpuee kēe, ní ki sá bibɔtiibi biluli mun.

eng: After this I saw another angel coming down from heaven. He had great authority, and the earth was filled with light because of his glory.
local: Boonee ní m ká ntuun ŋubɔ nyánní yilpu ki cáá mpɔŋkpaan, ní ŋu nín jìl̀ puee ŋmíntì duulinya kɔkɔ.

eng: They will stand a long way off because they are afraid of the pain she suffers, and they will say, Oh, the horror! Babylon, you great city, you powerful city! In a single hour your judgment has come.
local: Dinimbinaamandi din poon kitiŋee làá dāá kɔ́ee làá cúú-bi tifaandi, ní bi dāa yú kudandaŋu ki lì yii: «Ba busuu di jéetì Babiloon kitiŋkpaaŋŋi kin cáá mpɔnee pu nan dee! Kukúbambaantiiu poon bi kpáàa pée dáaĺ sii tafal sɔkɔmiii?».

eng: The merchants of the earth will weep and mourn over her, for no one buys their cargoes anymore.
local: Ní duulinya bantiliibi sūǹ ki yáliŋkí ki-pu, kun puee baa làá ti ká ubɔ ń dā bi-nyampuwanti.

eng: cargoes of gold, silver, jewels, and pearls; fine linen, purple, silk, and scarlet; all those things made of scented wood, ivory, fine wood, bronze, iron, and marble.
local: bi-nyampuwanti tin sá sindaariyab nì animbil kúti, nì ataŋkpee ŋin pɔ̄ɔ̄ kudaauee, nì abeedimbil nì iŋaamɔnti nì iŋaabɔn nì iman, nì in bí kii sirci ki pɔ̄ɔ̄ kudaauee, nì idɔ in cáá kudapɔɔu tikpilee kɔkɔ, nì tin kɔkɔ bi cáá akuntinyin ki ŋáańee, nì bi nín cáá idɔ ki kpítí tiwammɔntiil tin an pɔ̄ɔ̄ kudaauee, nì tasa kúti nì tikúti wanti nì ataŋkpee ŋin jìl̀ee.

eng: cinnamon, incense, fragrant ointment, and frankincense; wine, oil, fine flour, and wheat; cattle, sheep, horses, and carriages; and slaves, even human lives.
local: nì talaalub nín bí tɔbin puee nì tifaal tin sɔ́fèe nì ŋkpaŋŋmitikaam, nì tikaliŋkpun tin bi tùutī an sɔ́fèe, nì idafi nì ikpafi nì ŋkpɔnɔyim, nì bilee biliŋ nì inaa nì ipii nì itaamu nì tɔrɔkub nì iyumbu, ki tin kpáaǹ binib nammu.

eng: The fruit your whole being craved has gone from you. All your glitter and glamour are lost to you, never ever to be found again.
local: Ní binyampoobee kíǹ ki lì yii: «Tiwan tin pu a-nimbiliŋ máǹee kaa ti dàbiǹ-si, a-gaajati nì a-wannyaanti tin jìl̀ pàlpalee wáŋkì a-ŋaan, a pée gíti kpá kí ká-tì jaanjaan!».

eng: The merchants who sold these things, and got so rich by her, will stand a long way off because they fear the pain she suffers. They will weep and mourn, and say.
local: Binyampoobi bin di bāntì kitiŋ gbanti poon ki ká animbilee, làá dāá fàŋkì falaa un kitiŋee bíi jīǹee, an pu ní bi dāa yú kudandaŋu ki sūǹ ki yáliŋkí.

eng: Oh, the horror! The great city that wore fine linen, purple, and scarlet, who glittered with gold, jewels, and pearls.
local: ki lì yii: «Mbusu, mbusu kɔ́ kitiŋkpaaŋŋi, kíǹ kin nìn pēē iŋaamɔnti, ibɔn nì iman bɔkutinyaaŋŋee. Kì nìn túuń sindaariya falfatib nì abeedimbil nì idiŋfi in pɔ̄ɔ̄ kudaau ki jìl̀ee.

eng: in just one hour such great wealth was destroyed. Every sea captain, every seafarer, sailors, and all who make their living on the sea stood a long way off.
local: kukúbambaantiiu ja poon ní bi dūu kúntì ki-gaajati kɔkɔ!» Bimeelaŋaalkpilib kɔkɔ nì bin kā meelabinee nì bimeelaŋaaliliibi nì bin kɔkɔ tūǹ ditundi tiŋkunee yúń kudandaŋu.

eng: They cried out as they saw the smoke from her burning and said, What city was ever like the great city.
local: ki wāĺ kitiŋee nín fōō ŋŋmi mi-nyiim yíkí jòoooyee, ní bi gbáà ibiil ki lì yii: «Kitiŋ kiba kpá bí dūú ŋmàntīǹ Babiloon tiŋki».

eng: They threw dust on their heads, and they cried out, weeping and mourning. They said, Oh, the horror! The great city, where all who have ships at sea became so rich by her prosperity—in just one hour she was destroyed.
local: Bi nìn jáfí ntam ki sìtī bi-yiliŋ pu ki sūǹ ki yáliŋkí ní ki lì yii: «Mbusu ulɔu di nín kɔ́ kitiŋkpaaŋŋi míǹ nan dee! Ki-gaajati pu ní meeladambi kpántì bigaajab. Ní kukúbambaantiiu ja poon ní bi kpáàa kúntì sii nì a-wanti kɔkɔɔɔ!».

eng: He called out with a loud voice, saying, Fallen, fallen is Babylon the great! She has become a home for demons and a lair for every unclean spirit. She is a lair for every unclean bird, and a lair for every unclean and disgusting beast.
local: Ní ntuunee téeń mpɔn yii: «Kì lítì, Babiloon tiŋkpaaŋkee lítì ki kpántì kɔŋkɔnnee arasiniibi kɔŋkooŋŋu, arasiniibi nín bí tɔbin puee balilaŋki, nì inoon in kɔkɔ kaa sàafì kpataaayee balilaŋki.

eng: Rejoice over her, heaven—you saints, apostles, and prophets—because God has condemned her as she condemned you.
local: Nimbi bin kóò yilpu ki sá Unimbɔti yab nì Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi nì Yeesu kpambaliŋee, nín̄ fàal̀maan tin bāań kitiŋkpaaŋŋi gbantee pu, kun puee ikpiti in kì ŋá-nee pu ní Unimbɔti dáaĺ ki-tafal kí pɔ̀-ni.

eng: Then a powerful angel picked up a stone that was like a huge millstone and threw it into the sea, saying, With such violent force the great city of Babylon will be thrown down, and it wont be found anymore.
local: Niinee ní ntuun mpɔndaan ŋubɔ yōoǹ ditaŋkpal diba dì gbìfì kii kunaauee ki tɔ́ tiŋkun, ní ki bí yii: «Míǹ ní bi làá dāá téé Babiloon kitiŋkpaaŋŋi kìlkil kí tɔ̄ taapu, ubɔ kaa dāa gíti líkití ki-pu jaanjaan.

eng: The sound of harpists and musicians, of pipers and trumpeters, will never be heard among you again. No craftsman of any kind will ever be found among you again. The sound of the hand mill will never be heard among you again.
local: Baa làá ti gbìl̄ kitiŋ gbanti poon bi bɔ̀ɔ́ kubeeu, kaa làá ti gbìl̄ bi búǹ diwul àá dikantándi, kaa làá ti gbìl̄ bigbiŋgbiililiibi baa ubɔ pu, kaa làá ti wāl̀ utɔntɔnli báà uŋyee kitiŋ gbanti poon, kaa làá ti gbìl̄ bi nà kunaau.

eng: The light of a lamp will never shine among you again. The sound of a bridegroom and bride will never be heard among you again because your merchants ran the world, because all the nations were deceived by the spell you cast, and because.
local: Fitila walifiŋu kaa làá ti ŋmíntí kitiŋ gbanti pu. Baa làá ti gbìl̄ unimpucaan nì u-cal pu. Kun puee bibantiliibi sá bikpikpaambi ní kitiŋ gbanti poon, ní ki cáá bi-bɔti ki kíiĺ binib kɔkɔ.».

eng: the blood of prophets, of saints, and of all who have been slaughtered on the earth was found among you.
local: Kitiŋ gbanti fōō ditafadaaldi, kun puee bi kūū Unimbɔti yab nì u-bɔnaatiliibi nì bin kɔkɔ bi kūū kitiŋ puee.

eng: because all the nations have fallen due to the wine of her lustful passion. The kings of the earth committed sexual immorality with her, and the merchants of the earth became rich from the power of her loose and extravagant ways.
local: Di sá yii kì nyūntì atimbun kɔkɔ ki-daam min sá ki-jɔkɔmbiikuee. Duulinya bɔtiibi kɔkɔ dōoǹ nì kì ki kpántì bijɔkɔndambi, ní duulinya bantiliibi kūliŋkì ki-wannyaanti tikpil tikpil.».

eng: Then I heard another voice from heaven say, Come out of her, my people, so that you dont take part in her sins and dont receive any of her plagues.
local: Ní m gíti gbìl̄ nneen ŋubɔ nyánní yilpu ki lī yii: «M-samaau, nyāmaan kitiŋ nee poon, án nín làá ŋá pu ní taa ŋá ki-kpitii, kí dāá fōō ki-tafadaaldi din bi bɔ́ntì ki yóoǹ-kèe.

eng: Her sins have piled up as high as heaven, and God remembered her unjust acts.
local: Kun puee kitiŋ gbanti yab kpitii kpúkú ní ki tin sāā kutagbɔŋu, Unimbɔti kaa súntì ki-tuŋkpitiŋ gbanti pu.

eng: Give her what she has given to others. Give her back twice as much for what she has done. In the cup that she has poured, pour her twice as much.
local: Ŋámaan-kì kii kì nín ŋá-ni puee, téem̀maan-kì ikpiti in kì ŋá-nee mili pu. Kì nín dáaĺ ni-tafal miŋyee, nimbi ń dáaĺ-kì míǹ mili pu.

eng: To the extent that she glorified herself and indulged her loose and extravagant ways, give her pain and grief. In her heart she says, I sit like a queen! Im not a widow. Ill never see grief.
local: Kì nín cáá tiwannyaandi ki gà ki-bindi miŋyee, ní yàntī falaa nì dinimbinaamandi ń kɔ́-kì míǹ, kun puee kì sòoǹ ki pɔ̀ ki-ba yii kíǹ sá unimpubɔti ní, kaa sá ukpiipu, dinimbinaamandi kpá kí kɔ́ kíǹ dalba dalba.

eng: This is why her plagues will come in a single day—deadly disease, grief, and hunger. She will be consumed by fire because the Lord God who judges her is powerful.
local: An pu ní ditafadaaldi din dɔ̄ ki cí-kèe kɔkɔ làá bàń ki-pu ŋwimbaanti, ŋkúm nì dinimbinaamandi nì ŋkùm, ní ŋŋmi ń dāá púntí-kì kí wà, kun puee ti-Dindaan Unimbɔti un dáaĺ ki-tafalee sá mpɔndaan ní.».

eng: The kings of the earth, who committed sexual immorality with her and shared her loose and extravagant ways, will weep and mourn over her when they see the smoke from her burning.
local: Niin ní duulinya bɔtiibi bin nì u kpáfì ki ŋá tijɔkɔndi ki yākatì ki-wannyaantee làá sɔ̄ kí yāliŋkì bi yaa láa ká ŋŋmi ŋun bíi wù kitiŋ gbantee ŋminyiim yíkì jòoooyee.

eng: After this I heard what sounded like a huge crowd in heaven. They said, Hallelujah! The salvation and glory and power of our God!.
local: Boonee ní m gbìl̄ yilpu an bí kii kunipaau neenee, ki lī mpɔn yii: «Aleeluya! Ti-Nimbɔtiu di yì difiil nì tinyool nì mpɔn.

eng: Then I fell at his feet to worship him. But he said, Dont do that! Im a servant just like you and your brothers and sisters who hold firmly to the witness of Jesus. Worship God! The witness of Jesus is the spirit of prophecy.
local: U lī mimmee ní m gbāaǹ kí jāam̀-u, ní u bí-mi yii: «Taa ŋée! Ma mun pée sá utɔntɔnli kii si nì a-nabiyaamu mun bí ki sòoǹ Yeesu bɔti tin u nyántì ki dàkā-nee ní. Unimbɔti ní a máaǹ kí jāam̀.» Kun puee Unimbɔti Fam nín yāntì Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi sòon̄ tiŋyee sòoǹ Yeesu pu ní.

eng: Then I saw heaven opened, and there was a white horse. Its rider was called Faithful and True, and he judges and makes war justly.
local: Niinee ní m ká yilpu pīitì, ní utampiin ubɔ nyánní, ní unil ubɔ kā u-pu, bi yíì udaan yii Deedee nì Mbaamɔn, u sòoǹ tibɔti deedee ki jáà kujaau nì deedee.

eng: His eyes were like a fiery flame, and on his head were many royal crowns. He has a name written on him that no one knows but he himself.
local: U-nimbiliŋ ŋmóò làka laka kii ŋŋmee, ní u cíkí mbeefuulimu tikpil u-yil pu. Diyindi diba náká u-pu, báà ubɔ kaa nyí-dì, see úǹ baba.

eng: He wore a robe dyed with blood, and his name was called the Word of God.
local: Ní u-bɔkutil din u pēēyee táká fatiku, ní bi yíì-u yii: «Unimbɔti nyɔɔbundi.».

eng: Heavens armies, wearing fine linen that was white and pure, were following him on white horses.
local: Ní yilpu janjaliibi nóó-u ki kā itampiin pu ki pēē abɔkutimɔnti apiin káŋkaŋ.

eng: From his mouth comes a sharp sword that he will use to strike down the nations. He is the one who will rule them with an iron rod. And he is the one who will trample the winepress of the Almighty Gods passionate anger.
local: U-nyɔkɔnee kitàataajiki kā ki jītí. An ní u làá dūú jáǹ atimbun. Mpɔn ŋun u làá dāá nīn cáa kpòotī-bee dāa pɔ̄ɔ̄ kii tikútee ní, ní Unimbɔti Mpɔŋkɔdaan nín díkí bi-pu diŋuul diŋyee puee, u làá dāá nà-bi dúsi dusi kii bi nín nà asubil pu ki ŋáań ndaamee ní.

eng: He has a name written on his robe and on his thigh: King of kings and Lord of lords.
local: Bi ŋmàn̄ diyindi diba u-bɔkutil nì u-tapandi pu yii: «Bibɔtiibi kɔkɔ Bɔtiu, bidindambi kɔkɔ Dindaan.».

eng: Then I saw an angel standing in the sun, and he called out with a loud voice and said to all the birds flying high overhead, Come and gather for Gods great supper.
local: Ní m ká Unimbɔti tuuŋŋu ŋubɔ yú ŋwìiń poon, ní ŋu téeń mpɔn ki tùkū inoon in kɔkɔ ŋàaǹ kutaa puee yii: «Dāá kpāfìmaan kí jī Unimbɔti jiŋaakpaaŋwu!.

eng: Come and eat the flesh of kings, the flesh of generals, the flesh of the powerful, and the flesh of horses and their riders. Come and eat the flesh of all, both free and slave, both small and great.
local: Ní dāan̄ kí jī bibɔtiibi nì bisoojakpilib nì bipɔndambi wunnanti nì itaamu nì bin kā-iee wunnanti, nì binib kɔkɔ wunnanti, bin yì bi-bee nì iyumbu, binikpaambi nì biwaabi.».

eng: Then I saw that the beast and the kings of the earth and their armies had gathered to make war against the rider on the horse and his army.
local: Niinee ní m ká upeelee nì duulinya bɔtiibi nì bi-soojab kpáfì kí jáǹ un kā utaamu puee nì u-janjaliibi.

eng: His judgments are true and just, because he judged the great prostitute, who ruined the earth by her whoring, and he exacted the penalty for the blood of his servants from her hand.
local: U-bɔsòontam sá mbaamɔn ní ki bí deedee. Di sá yii u sòon̄ ki kūū kinimpulaakpaai bɔti kì nín dū ki-laakpaati ki tīntì kitiŋ pu kɔkɔ nib ki yāntì bi kpántì bikpitibee, ní ki dáaĺ ki-tafal ki tɔ̀n̄-kì ipɔntoon kì nín kūū u-tɔntɔnliibi puee.».

eng: But the beast was seized, along with the false prophet who had done signs in the beasts presence. The two of them were thrown alive into the fiery lake that burns with sulfur.
local: Ní un kā utaamu puee nì u-janjaliibi cúú upeelee nì u-nyimɔmbɔnaatiliu un nìn ŋáań maamaacib upeelee nimbiin ki kíiĺ binib bin kɔkɔ bi nìn dáań upeelee daaŋku bi gbàantí ki jáaḿ upeelee naŋkuee. Ní bi dū bi-liitil nì bi-nimbiliŋ ki tɔ́ farwata ŋmibookun.

eng: The rest were killed by the sword that comes from the mouth of the rider on the horse, and all the birds ate their fill of their flesh.
local: Ní kitàataajiki kin nyēē un kā utampiinee nyɔkɔnee kūū bin kíǹee kɔkɔ, ní inoon dɔ́miń ki jíń bi-nanti ki bál̀.

eng: Then they said a second time, Hallelujah! Smoke goes up from her forever and always.
local: Ní ki gíti bí yii: «Aleeluya! Kun puee kitiŋ gbanti làá nīn pée wù ŋŋmi ní ki-ŋminyiim ń nīn yíkí míǹ kí nīn cá jaanjaan.».

eng: The twenty-four elders and the four living creatures fell down and worshipped God, who is seated on the throne, and they said, Amen. Hallelujah.
local: Ní bikpilib mmuŋku nì binaayee, nì tiwan tinaa tin cáá kufoouee, lítì Unimbɔti un kā dibeejal puee nimbiin ní ki bí yii: «Ameeni! Aleeluya!».

eng: Then a voice went out from the throne and said, Praise our God, all you his servants, and you who fear him, both small and great.
local: Ní nneen ŋubɔ nyánní dibeejal cee ki lī yii: «Nimbi bin kɔkɔ sá Unimbɔti tɔntɔnliibee, nyɔ̄ŋkìmaan-u! Bikpaambi nì mbiyaamu, bin kɔkɔ fàŋkí-uee, nyɔ̄ŋkìmaan-u!».

eng: And I heard something that sounded like a huge crowd, like rushing water and powerful thunder. They said, Hallelujah! The Lord our God, the Almighty, exercised his royal power!.
local: Ní m gbìl̄ an bí kii binib kupaau di sòon̄ee, ní ki bí kii dimɔɔl nyim nín sìtī nsitimin puee, ní ki gíti bí kii utaa nín tátí mpɔn puee ki lì yii: «Aleeluya! Ti-Dindaan Unimbɔti Mpɔŋkɔdaan di sá ubɔti ki kā dibeel pu.

eng: Let us rejoice and celebrate, and give him the glory, for the wedding day of the Lamb has come, and his bride has made herself ready.
local: Tí nīn fàal̀maan án nīn mɔ̄ǹ-ti tí nīn nyɔ́ŋkí Unimbɔti, kun puee Dipibil nimpucáatam bāań, u-bɔɔn bɔ́ntì ki dóò.

eng: She was given fine, pure white linen to wear, for the fine linen is the saints acts of justice.
local: Unimbɔti tīī-u dibɔkutipindi káŋkaŋ din jìl̀ee ú pèen̄.».

eng: Then the angel said to me, Write this: Favored are those who have been invited to the wedding banquet of the Lamb. He said to me, These are the true words of God.
local: Ní Unimbɔti tuuŋŋu bí yii ḿ ŋmà yii: «Bin bi yíiń bí dāá jī Dipibil nimpucáatam jintee bí mmɔnneeee!» Ní ki gíti kútì yii: «Unimbɔti u-ba di lī míǹ.».

eng: Write this to the angel of the church in Ephesus: These are the words of the one who holds the seven stars in his right hand and walks among the seven gold lampstands.
local: «Á ŋmà kugbɔŋu kí tīī m-nikitil din bí Eefeesee tuuŋŋu yii: «Un ŋúb́ iŋmalbijaa iluli u-ŋaŋgiilin ki còom̄ fitilab tɔ́kɔŋkaati tiluli tin sá sindaariyee kansikinee di lī yii.

eng: Dont be afraid of what you are going to suffer. Look! The devil is going to throw some of you into prison in order to test you. You will suffer hardship for ten days. Be faithful even to the point of death, and I will give you the crown of life.
local: Á taa fàŋkì falaa un dɔ̄ ki cí-si a làá jēe pu. Di sá yii Sitaan làá cɔ̄ŋkì-ni kí yàntī bí cūtì ni-kansikin biba kí tɔ̄ sarka, ní ní dāá jī falaa iwiin saalaa. Nín̄ yú deedee áli a-kúm dal, ní ḿ dāá tīī-si dijajil beefuuliiki, an dàkà yii dimaŋfal din kaa kúntée.

eng: If you can hear, listen to what the Spirit is saying to the churches. Those who emerge victorious wont be hurt by the second death.
local: «Un yaa cáá ditafalee ń pīlifì Unimbɔti Fam nín sòoǹ nì Kriisitoo nikitiŋ puee. «Un yaa jáń ki jíńee, ŋkúnliliitiim kaa dāa gíti gbèetī udaan.».

eng: Write this to the angel of the church in Pergamum: These are the words of the one who has the sharp, two-edged sword.
local: «Ŋmà kí tīī m-nikitil din bí Peergaamee tuuŋŋu yii: «Un ŋúb́ kitàataajiki kin jītí ajan liitilee bí yii.

eng: I know that you are living right where Satans throne is. You are holding on to my name, and you didnt break faith with me even at the time that Antipas, my faithful witness, was killed among you, where Satan lives.
local: M nyí yii kitiŋ kin poon Sitaan jíń dibeelee ní a kóò. Ama ní a ŋúb́ m-bɔti deedee kaa páatì a-fookiiu m-pu. A gbíntì ki ŋúb́ an píkí ki kpáaǹ ŋyunti ŋun bi nìn kūū m-tɔntɔmmɔntiu Antiipa ni-tiŋki kin sá Sitaan kɔŋkooŋŋuee nee.

eng: But I have a few things against you, because you have some there who follow Balaams teaching. Balaam had taught Balak to trip up the Israelites so that they would eat food sacrificed to idols and commit sexual immorality.
local: Ama nì míǹ kɔkɔee, m làá wòoǹ-si tiba pu. Di sá binib biba bí a-cee ki ŋáań kii Balaam nín nìn sáŋkì Balaaki ú ŋmàtiǹ Isirayeel biyaamu bí ŋmɔ́ tiwaatootinan kí ŋá tilaakpaal kí lítí ikpitinee.

eng: In the same way, you have some who follow the Nicolaitans teaching.
local: Míǹ ní si mun a-ba cáá binib bin tūǹ atuŋkpiti kii Niikoolaati yabee.

eng: So change your hearts and lives. If you dont, I am coming to you soon, and I will make war on them with the sword that comes from my mouth.
local: An puee á kpántí a-bimbim. A yaa yíì, m làá dāan̄ kí dū m-nyɔɔbundi din sá kitàataajikee kí já nì an nibee kɔŋkɔnnee.

eng: If you can hear, listen to what the Spirit is saying to the churches. I will give those who emerge victorious some of the hidden manna to eat. I will also give to each of them a white stone with a new name written on it, which no one knows except the one who receives it.
local: «Un yaa cáá ditafalee ń pīlifì Unimbɔti Fam nín bíi sòoǹ nì Kriisitoo nikitiŋ puee. «Un yaa jáń ki jíńee, m làá tīī-u maana un bí dibalinee, nì ditaŋkpapindi din pu bi ŋmàn̄ diyimpɔndi diba din báà ubɔ kaa nyée, see un fōō ditaŋkpalee.».

eng: Write this to the angel of the church in Thyatira: These are the words of Gods Son, whose eyes are like a fiery flame, and whose feet are like fine brass.
local: «Ŋmà kugbɔŋu kí tīī m-nikitil din bí Tiyatiiree tuuŋŋu yii: «Unimbɔti Jipɔɔn un nimbiliŋ ŋmóò làka laka kii ŋŋmi, u-taaŋi jìl̀ pàlpal kii tasa ní bi fíntèe di lī yii.

eng: I know your works, your love and faithfulness, your service and endurance. I also know that the works you have done most recently are even greater than those you did at first.
local: M nyí tin kɔkɔ a ŋáańee, m ká dineendi din a cááyee, nì a-fookiiu, nì a nín tūǹ atuun ŋin ki tútúǹ binibee nì dipɔ́ɔǹdi din a cááyee. M ká yii atummɔnti ŋin a bíi tūǹ kɔŋkɔnnee jítiǹ apeepeekaa.

eng: I know your works, your labor, and your endurance. I also know that you dont put up with those who are evil. You have tested those who say they are apostles but are not, and you have found them to be liars.
local: M nyí a-tuuŋŋi ŋin a túńee kɔkɔ pu, m nyí falaa un jíń-see, ki nyí a-pɔ́ɔǹtam. M nyí a-pu yii aa là binikpitib bɔti. A cɔ́ŋkì ki béè bin bí yii bi sá Yeesu kpambaliŋ, kin kaa sée, ní ki bīitì binyimɔndambi pu.

eng: But I have this against you: you put up with that woman, Jezebel, who calls herself a prophet. You allow her to teach and to mislead my servants into committing sexual immorality and eating food sacrificed to idols.
local: Ama nì míǹ kɔkɔee, m làá wòoǹ-si tiba pu, di sá a yāntì unimpu Jeesabeel un yíì u-ba yii u sá Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliuee cáá u-dàkatam ki kíiĺ m-tɔntɔnliibi yii bi yaa ŋáań tilaakpaalee, an kaa cáá tiba, ki yàntī bi ŋmóò tiwaatootinan.

eng: I gave her time to change her heart and life, but she refuses to change her life of prostitution.
local: M pú-u ŋyunti ú bàà kpántí u-ba kí fá u-bimbikpitim ní u yíì.

eng: Look! Im throwing her onto a sickbed. I am casting those who have committed adultery with her into terrible hardship—if they dont change their hearts from following her practices.
local: An puee m làá yàntī ú lítí iween pu kí jī falaa ki kpáaǹ u nì bin pēē kinyɔkɔ ki ŋáań tilaakpaalee. Asee bi yaa kpántì bi-ba ki fá bi-bimbikpitim baba.

eng: and I will even put her children to death with disease. Then all the churches will know that Im the one who examines minds and hearts, and that I will give to each of you what your actions deserve.
local: M làá fáá kí kpɔ̀ u-yab bin nóó-uee ní m-nikitiŋ kɔkɔ ń béé yii man di sá un nyí binib landɔkɔi nì bi-làtamee. M làá dāá pà báà ŋma tuuŋŋi paati ní.

eng: As for the rest of you in Thyatira—those of you who dont follow this teaching and havent learned the so-called deep secrets of Satan—I wont burden you with anything else.
local: «Ama nimbi bin kíǹ Tiyatiir nikitil kansikin kaa bí bi-tuŋkpitiŋ gbanti poon, kaa bífì kun bíǹ yíì yii Sitaan bɔti tin bí dibalin ki nyóòyee, nimbee, maa pée cáá tiba kí lì ni-pu.

eng: Just hold on to what you have until I come.
local: Ama tin baba m làá lèe di sá ní nīn ŋúb́ tin ni cááyee gbīŋgbim áli ḿ dāá gítiń.

eng: To those who emerge victorious, keeping my practices until the end, I will give authority over the nations.
local: «Un di yaa jáń ki jíń ki gbíntì ki nìkì ki ŋáań min fáatì-mee ki tin kátì-kuee, m làá yàntī ú jī dibeel atimbun pu.

eng: to rule the nations with an iron rod and smash them like pottery.
local: Mpɔn ŋun m làá tīī udaan ú dūú kpàà-bee dāa pɔ̄ɔ̄ kii tikútee ní. U dāa ŋūǹ kí dūú wīī binib sɔkɔmii kii atansambilee.

eng: just as I received authority from my Father. I will also give them the morning star.
local: Míǹ ní mama m-ba fōō mpɔn gbanti m-Baa cee. Ní ḿ dāá tīī un jáń ki jíńee utaafaaŋmalbija.

eng: If you can hear, listen to what the Spirit is saying to the churches.
local: «Un yaa cáá ditafalee ń pīlifì Unimbɔti Fam nín sòoǹ nì Kriisitoo nikitiŋ puee.».

eng: You have shown endurance and put up with a lot for my names sake, and you havent gotten tired.
local: A cáá dipɔ́ɔǹdi, ki jíń falaa m-pu kaa yɔ̄tì a-wunti.

eng: But I have this against you: you have let go of the love you had at first.
local: Ama tiba ní m cáá kí wòoǹ-si, di sá yii a nín nìn néeń-mi ŋkilkaamin puee, a dūu fá, aa ti néeń-mi míǹ.

eng: So remember the high point from which you have fallen. Change your hearts and lives and do the things you did at first. If you dont, Im coming to you. I will move your lampstand from its place if you dont change your hearts and lives.
local: An puee á téetí laakin ní a lítèe. Á kpántí a-bimbim, kí fàtīí nīn tūǹ atummɔnti ŋin a nìn tūǹee. A yaa yíìyee, m làá dāan̄ a-cee ní kí yòon̄ a-fitilau nì u-tɔkɔŋkaati ti-paaŋŋu.

eng: But you have this in your favor: you hate what the Nicolaitans are doing, which I also hate.
local: Ama nì míǹ kɔkɔee, a cáá tiba tì ŋān̄. Tíǹ di sá a nín ná atuun ŋin Niikoolaati yab tūǹee, kii ma mun nín ná-ŋi puee.

eng: If you can hear, listen to what the Spirit is saying to the churches. I will allow those who emerge victorious to eat from the tree of life, which is in Gods paradise.
local: «Un yaa cáá ditafalee ń pīlifì Circir Fam nín bíi sòoǹ nì Kriisitoo nikitiŋ puee. «Un yaa jáń ki jíńee, m làá tīī-u dimaŋfal subu bun yú Unimbɔti kpaabunee bil ú ŋmɔ́.».

eng: Write this to the angel of the church in Smyrna: These are the words of the one who is the first and the last, who died and came back to life.
local: «Á ŋmà kugbɔŋu kí tīī m-nikitil din bí Simiirnee tuuŋŋu yii: «Un sá upeepeekaa ki sá uluŋkikaayee, man un nìn kpíì ki fīkitèe di lī yii.

eng: I know your hardship and poverty . I also know the hurtful things that have been spoken about you by those who say they are Jews .
local: M nyí a-falaau nì a-sool, ama nì míǹ kɔkɔee, a sá ugaaja ní. M nyí bin kúŋfí a-boon ki yíì bi-ba yii Juuda yab kin kaa sée. An nibee sá Sitaan nikitil ní.

eng: Then I saw an angel coming down from heaven, holding in his hand the key to the abyss and a huge chain.
local: Boonee ní m ká ntuun ŋubɔ nyánní yilpu ki ŋúbiń mbookpaan saafiiu nì ikusaka iba i nyìm̀ na!.

eng: Then the devil, who had deceived them, was thrown into the lake of fire and sulfur, where the beast and the false prophet also were. There painful suffering will be inflicted upon them day and night, forever and always.
local: Ní bí dāá téé Sitaan un kíiĺ-bee kí tɔ̄ farwata nì ŋŋmi bookun, laakin upeelee nì u-nyimɔmbɔnaatiliu bée. Niin ní bi làá nīn bí falaa poon ki yáliŋkíǹ bi-ba ŋwiimpu nì kunyeeu kí nīn pée cáa cá jaanjaan.

eng: Then I saw a great white throne and the one who is seated on it. Before his face both earth and heaven fled away, and no place was found for them.
local: Boonee ní m ká dibeejapindi dikpaandi diba, ní kitiŋ nì kutagbɔŋu sáǹ un kā dibeejapindi gbanti puee nimbiin, baa ti ká-bi.

eng: I saw the dead, the great and the small, standing before the throne, and scrolls were opened. Another scroll was opened too; this is the scroll of life. And the dead were judged on the basis of what was written in the scrolls about what they had done.
local: Ní m ká bitaŋkpiibi, bikpaambi nì biwaabi yú dibeejalee nimbiin, ní bi cúú tigbɔn ki kpátì, ní ki gíti cúú kugbɔŋu kubɔ mun ki kpátì, kúǹ di sá dimaŋfal gbɔŋku. Ní bi sòon̄ bitaŋkpiibi bɔti ki táá nì bi nín túń atuun ŋin bi ŋmàn̄ ki ŋá kugbɔŋu poonee.

eng: The sea gave up the dead that were in it, and Death and the Grave gave up the dead that were in them, and people were judged by what they had done.
local: Ní tiŋku gíiǹní binib bin u nìn yōoǹee, ní ŋkúm nì ditaŋkpiil gíiǹní bitaŋkpiibi bin kɔkɔ bi ŋúb́ee, ní bi sòon̄ báà ŋma bɔti tin u ŋée.

eng: Then Death and the Grave were thrown into the fiery lake. This, the fiery lake, is the second death.
local: Ní bi téé ŋkúm nì ditaŋkpiil ki tɔ́ ŋŋmi bookun, ŋŋmi booku gbanti di sá ŋkúnliliitiimee.

eng: Then anyone whose name wasnt found written in the scroll of life was thrown into the fiery lake.
local: Un kamaa yindi ní baa ká dimaŋfal gbɔŋku poonee, bi dù-u kí tɔ̄ ŋŋmi bookun ní.

eng: He seized the dragon, the old snake, who is the devil and Satan, and bound him for a thousand years.
local: Ní ntuunee cúú kupeebiliŋŋu ukookpikee, an dàkà yii Sitaan, ki dū ikusakee ki bóob́-u ú nīn bí míǹ kí nīn cí abin kutuku.

eng: He threw him into the abyss, then locked and sealed it over him. This was to keep him from continuing to deceive the nations until the thousand years were over. After this he must be released for a little while.
local: Ŋu téé-u ki tɔ́ mbookpaanee ni ní ki pīiǹ ki kpàl̄ ki ŋéeb́, án nín làá ŋá pu ú taa ti kīil̀ atimbun nib kí tin cáá sāā abin kutukuee. Boonee bi làá fàtīí gíiǹ-u kí líí ŋyunti ŋubɔ poon fiii.

eng: Then I saw thrones, and people took their seats on them, and judgment was given in their favor. They were the ones who had been beheaded for their witness to Jesus and Gods word, and those who hadnt worshipped the beast or its image, who hadnt received the mark on their forehead or hand. They came to life and ruled with Christ for one thousand years.
local: Ní m ká abeejal aba, ní bi tīī bin kā ŋi-puee mpɔn bí nīn sá bibɔsoonliibi, ní m gíti ká bin nìn sòoǹ Unimbɔti bɔti ki lèekī Yeesu pu seera bi kūū-bee nammu, nì bin kaa nìn gbāaǹ ki jáam̀ upeelee nì u-naŋkuee, kaa yāntì bi dáań bi-yigbeeŋi nì bi-ŋaliiyee, bi-kɔkɔ fātìi fīkitì bi nì Kriisitoo kāl̀ dibeel abin kutuku.

eng: The rest of the dead didnt come to life until the thousand years were over. This is the first resurrection.
local: Bitaŋkpiibi bin kíǹee kaa làá fìkitī, see abin kutukuee yaa láa jítì, difìkitipeekaal bɔti dee.

eng: Favored and holy are those who have a share in the first resurrection. The second death has no power over them, but they will be priests of God and of Christ, and will rule with him for one thousand years.
local: Bin yaa láa bí difìkitipeekaal poonee bí mmɔnni nì circir poon. Ŋkúnliliitiim kaa dāa gíti cáá bíǹ pu mpɔn. Bíǹ di dāa sá Unimbɔti nì Kriisitoo saraaŋaliibi, ní bi nì Kriisitoo ń kàl̄ u-beelin abin kutuku.

eng: When the thousand years are over, Satan will be released from his prison.
local: Abin kutukuee yaa láa jítèe, bi làá dāá gíiǹ Sitaan kí líí, ú nyā sarkan.

eng: He will go out to deceive the nations that are at the four corners of the earth—Gog and Magog. He will gather them for battle. Their number is like the sand of the sea.
local: U yaa nyáńee, u làá dāá nīn yìiǹ ki mɔ́ntí duulinya jannaatiliŋ timbuliŋ, an dàkà yii Gɔɔgi nì Maagoogi, kí kpāfì-bi bí bɔ́ntí kujaau. Bi làá dāá nīn wīikí tikpil kii tiŋku gbandi bulee ní.

eng: They came up across the whole earth and surrounded the saints camp, the city that God loves. But fire came down from heaven and consumed them.
local: Bi làá dāá kpátí kí gbí kitiŋ kɔkɔ, ní kí dāá léeń Unimbɔti nibul nì kitiŋ kin u néeńee pu, ama ŋŋmi ŋubɔ làá nyānní yilpu kí dāá wà bi-kɔkɔ.

eng: Then I saw a new heaven and a new earth, for the former heaven and the former earth had passed away, and the sea was no more.
local: Boonee ní m ká kutagbɔmpɔŋu nì kitimpɔŋ. Kutagbɔŋkpikuu nì kitiŋkpikii wáŋkì ní tiŋku kaa ti bí.

eng: He took me in a Spirit-inspired trance to a great, high mountain, and he showed me the holy city, Jerusalem, coming down out of heaven from God.
local: Ní Unimbɔti Fam dɔ́miń m-ni, ní ntuun yōoǹ-mi ki jɔ́m̀ dijoofɔkɔl diba pu, ní ki dàkā-mi Jeerusaleem circir tiŋki kin nyánní Unimbɔti cee ki sìntīńee.

eng: The city had Gods glory. Its brilliance was like a priceless jewel, like jasper that was as clear as crystal.
local: Kì nìn jìlìń Unimbɔti nyaaŋku ní. Kitiŋee jìl̀ kii ataŋkpanyaanee, a lì kii ataŋkpee ŋin jɔ̀m̀ pɔ́lpɔl ki wāĺ kílkilee.

eng: It had a great high wall with twelve gates. By the gates were twelve angels, and on the gates were written the names of the twelve tribes of Israels sons.
local: Ní digundi din bi máń ki māntì ki tú kitiŋee gbìfì ki fɔ̀kɔ̀ mun, ní ki cáá mpunyɔmu saalaa nì muli, ní ituun saalaa nì ili yú mpunyɔmu pu ki cí, ní bi ŋmàn̄ Isirayeel yab naakɔti saalaa nì tilee yiŋŋi ki nákáǹ mpunyɔmuee pu.

eng: There were three gates on the east, three gates on the north, three gates on the south, and three gates on the west.
local: Ní mpunyɔmu muta túĺ ŋwìiń nyalaŋki pú, ní muta túĺ ŋwìiń jeetilaŋki pú, ní muta túĺ ŋgan pú, ní muta túĺ ŋgiil pú.

eng: The city wall had twelve foundations, and on them were the twelve names of the Lambs twelve apostles.
local: Ataŋkpee saalaa nì ali pu ní bi nìn máń kitiŋee gundi ki tɔ́kɔ́ǹ, ní ki ŋmàn̄ Dipibil kpambaliŋ saalaa nì alee yiŋŋi ataŋkpeeyee pu.

eng: The angel who spoke to me had a gold measuring rod with which to measure the city, its gates, and its wall.
local: Ní ntuun ŋun yú ki sòoǹ nì mee ŋúb́ tiwaŋŋmantikaal, sindaariya kaluuku, kí ŋmàntī kitiŋee nì ki-punyɔmmu nì ki-guŋŋi.

eng: Now the city was laid out as a square. Its length was the same as its width. He measured the city with the rod, and it was fifteen hundred miles. Its length and width and height were equal.
local: Kitiŋee púuŋŋu nì ki-waaŋŋu ŋmà ní. Ntuunee dū sindaariya kaluukuee ki ŋmāntì kitiŋee púuŋŋu, ní ki ká ataŋkpee kutukub bili nì kulaafaau, míǹ ní ki-waaŋŋu nì ki-fɔkɔŋŋu mun nìn sá.

eng: He also measured the thickness of its wall. It was two hundred sixteen feet thick, as a person—or rather, an angel—measures things.
local: Ní ŋu ŋmāntì digundi mun ní ki ká di-gbifiŋŋu meetab imuŋku ita nì biŋmoo, kii binib nín pée ŋmàntī puee ní ŋu ŋmāntì.

eng: The wall was built of jasper, and the city was pure gold, like pure glass.
local: Ataŋkpee ŋin jɔ̀m̀ pɔ́lpɔlee ní bi dū ki máń digundi, ní kitiŋ ki-ba kɔkɔ sá sindaariyamɔnti ki wāĺ kílkil kii digitilee.

eng: The city walls foundations were decorated with every kind of jewel. The first foundation was jasper, the second was sapphire, the third was chalcedony, and the fourth was emerald.
local: Ataŋkpee ŋin bi dūu kíĺ taapu ki nín máń digundee ki tɔ́kɔ́ǹee puee, bi dū ataŋkpanyaan ŋin bí tɔbinee ní ki nákáǹ. Bi nìn dū ataŋkpee saalaa nì ali ní ki kíĺ taapu. Ntantukupeekaa taŋkpeeŋi ní bi yíì yii jaaspi, ní ki yíì dililiitiil yii safiir, ní ki yíì ditataatiil yii kalseeduwana, ní ki yíì dinanaatiil yii eemeeroodi.

eng: I saw the holy city, New Jerusalem, coming down out of heaven from God, made ready as a bride beautifully dressed for her husband.
local: Ní m ká circir tiŋki Jeerusaleempɔɔn nyánní Unimbɔti cee yilpu ki kpákatìń ki ŋān̄ kii unimpucaan di fùl̄ nnyim ki cí u-calee.

eng: The fifth was sardonyx, the sixth was carnelian, the seventh was chrysolite, and the eighth was beryl. The ninth was topaz, the tenth was chrysoprase, the eleventh was jacinth, and the twelfth was amethyst.
local: ní ki yíì diŋmooŋmootiil yii saarduwaan, ní ki yíì diluluutiil yii kɔɔrnaliini, ní ki yíì diluluútiil yii kriistooliiti, ní ki yíì dininiitiil yii beeriil, ní ki yíì diwawaatiil yii toopaasi, ní ki yíì disaalaatiil yii kriisooprasi, ní ki yíì saalaa nì dipeepeekaal yii iyasinti, ní ki yíì dijoolkaalee yii ameetiisti.

eng: The twelve gates were twelve pearls; each one of the gates was made from a single pearl. And the citys main street was pure gold, as transparent as glass.
local: Mpunyɔmu saalaa nì mulee sá ataŋkpanyaan ní. Kipunyɔkɔ kamaa nì ditaŋkpapiiŋkaal dimɔndi. Ní kitiŋ ki-ba daŋŋi sá sindaariyamɔnti ki wāĺ kílkil kii digitilee.

eng: I didnt see a temple in the city, because its temple is the Lord God Almighty and the Lamb.
local: Ama maa ká kunimbɔtidii kitiŋ gbanti poon, kun puee ti-Dindaan Unimbɔti Mpɔŋkɔdaan nì Dipibil di sá kunimbɔtidii.

eng: The city doesnt need the sun or the moon to shine on it, because Gods glory is its light, and its lamp is the Lamb.
local: Kitiŋ gbanti kaa nyàab̀ ŋwìiń nì uŋmal ń ŋmíntí-kì i-walfiŋu, Unimbɔti nyooti di dāa sá-kì ŋwalifi, ní Dipibil dāa sá ki-fitilau.

eng: The nations will walk by its light, and the kings of the earth will bring their glory into it.
local: Atimbun kɔkɔ dāa còom̄ ki-walifiŋun ní, ní duulinya bɔtiibi ń dāá cáań bi-gaajati niin.

eng: Its gates will never be shut by day, and there will be no night there.
local: Mpunyɔmu dāa pée wāā ní ŋwiimpu, baa pìintí, kun puee kunyeeu dāa kpá niin.

eng: They will bring the glory and honor of the nations into it.
local: Bi dāa cāabìń atimbun jilmau nì ŋi-nyooti niin.

eng: Nothing unclean will ever enter it, nor anyone who does what is vile and deceitful, but only those who are registered in the Lambs scroll of life.
local: Ikpiti iba kaa dāa kóò niin, nì bitiŋwiiliibi nì binyimɔndambi, see bin baba yiŋŋi bi ŋmàn̄ Dipibil gbɔŋku kun sá dimaŋfal gbɔŋkuee nee.

eng: I heard a loud voice from the throne say, Look! Gods dwelling is here with humankind. He will dwell with them, and they will be his peoples. God himself will be with them as their God.
local: Ní m gbìl̄ nneen ŋubɔ nyánní dibeejal cee ki téeń mpɔn yii: «Kɔŋkɔnnee Unimbɔti ŋá u-kɔŋkooŋŋu binib cee. U nì bi di làá nīn kíǹ ki kóò, ní bí nīn sá u-nibul, ní úǹ ń nīn sá bi-Nimbɔtiu un làá nīn bí bi-ceeyee.

eng: He will wipe away every tear from their eyes. Death will be no more. There will be no mourning, crying, or pain anymore, for the former things have passed away.
local: Úǹ di làá dāá pítí bi-nyinnyim kɔkɔ, ŋkúm dāa gíti kpá, dinimbinaamandi nì ibiil nì iyaliŋki dāa gíti kpá, kun puee duulinyakpiki kpáaǹ tíǹ kɔkɔ ki jítì.».

eng: Then the one seated on the throne said, Look! Im making all things new. He also said, Write this down, for these words are trustworthy and true.
local: Ní un kā dibeejal puee bí yii: «Kpèèmaan, m fātìi ŋá tiwan kɔkɔ mpɔm,» ní ki bí-mi yii: «Á ŋmà-tì, kun puee tibɔti gbanti sá mbaamɔn ní ki máaǹ bí fōō-tì.».

eng: Then he said to me, All is done. I am the Alpha and the Omega, the beginning and the end. To the thirsty I will freely give water from the life-giving spring.
local: Ní u gíti bí-mi yii: «An kɔkɔ bɔ́ntì ki dóò! Man di sá Alfa nì Oomeega, ukílkaa nì ukúntikaa. Un ní nnyinnyoo yaa cááyee ń dāan̄ m-cee, m làá tīī-u nnyim min nyēeń dimaŋfal nyimbuliŋunee faalaa.

eng: Those who emerge victorious will inherit these things. I will be their God, and they will be my sons and daughters.
local: Un yaa jáń ki jíńee ní m làá tīī tiwammɔntiil gbanti, ní ki dāa sá u-Nimbɔtiu, ní úǹ dāa sá man biki.

eng: But for the cowardly, the faithless, the vile, the murderers, those who commit sexual immorality, those who use drugs and cast spells, the idolaters and all liars—their share will be in the lake that burns with fire and sulfur. This is the second death.
local: Ama an yaa kíǹ bifaantidambee nì bin kaa yú nì Unimbɔti deedeeyee, nì bijɔkɔndambee, nì binikpɔliibee, nì bin ŋáań tilaakpaalee, nì bikpaŋkpantiibee nì biwaatootiliibee nì binyimɔndambee kɔkɔee, bíǹ laŋki di sá farwata ŋmibookun, niin di sá ŋkúnliliitiimee.».

eng: Then one of the seven angels who had the seven bowls full of the seven last plagues spoke with me. Come, he said, I will show you the bride, the Lambs wife.
local: Ní ituun iluli ijoolkaa in bàà ŋúb́ nsambiyaamu falaabi gbí poonee kansikin ŋubɔ dɔ́miń ki bí-mi yii: «Dāan̄ ḿ dàkà-si unimpucaan, Dipibil nimpuu.».

eng: Then the angel showed me the river of life-giving water, shining like crystal, flowing from the throne of God and the Lamb.
local: Ní ntuun dàkā-mi mbun ŋun pùub̀ nnyim min tìì dimaŋfalee. Nnyim gbanti wāĺ ní kílkil ní ki jìl̀ pàlpal, ní ki nyēeń Unimbɔti nì Dipibil beejal laŋki.

eng: Then he said to me, Dont seal up the words of the prophecy contained in this scroll, because the time is near.
local: Ní u bí-mi yii: «Á taa báĺ tibɔti tin bi nyántì ki dàkā-si tì kā kugbɔŋu nee poonee, kun puee ŋyunti ŋun an bɔtee kɔkɔ làá bàńee tóobiń.

eng: Let those who do wrong keep doing what is wrong. Let the filthy still be filthy. Let those who are righteous keep doing what is right. Let those who are holy still be holy.
local: Unikpiti ń mɔ̀tīí nīn ŋáań u-kpitii, ujɔkɔndaan ń mɔ̀tīí nīn ŋáań u-jɔkɔnti, deedeedaan ń mɔ̀tīí nīn ŋáań deedee tuuŋŋi, ní circirdaan ń mɔ̀tīí nīn bí circir.».

eng: Look! Im coming soon. My reward is with me, to repay all people as their actions deserve.
local: Ní Yeesu bí-mi yii: «Pīlifì! M làá dāan̄ kɔŋkɔnnee kí nīn ŋúbiń tipaal, kí pà báà ŋma u-tundi nín sá puee.

eng: I am the alpha and the omega, the first and the last, the beginning and the end.
local: Man di sá Alfa nì Oomeega, an dàkà yii upeepeekaa nì ujoolkaa, ukílkaa nì ukúntikaa.».

eng: Favored are those who wash their robes so that they may have the right of access to the tree of life and may enter the city by the gates.
local: Bin fíntí bi-bɔkutiŋ ŋaa cáá tijɔkɔndi, ki máaǹ kí ŋmɔ́ dimaŋfal subil, ki làá dī mpunyɔmu kí kɔ́ kitiŋinee bí mmɔnneeee!.

eng: Outside are the dogs, the drug users and spell-casters, those who commit sexual immorality, the murderers, the idolaters, and all who love and practice deception.
local: Ní bijɔkɔndambi nì bikpaŋkpantiibi nì bin ŋáań tilaakpaalee nì binikpɔliibi nì biwaatootiliibi nì binyimɔndambee dāa yú kitiŋ kpaanceetiŋu.

eng: I, Jesus, have sent my angel to bear witness to all of you about these things for the churches. Im the root and descendant of David, the bright morning star.
local: «Man Yeesu di túnní m-tuuŋŋu ŋú sòoǹ an bɔti m-nikitiŋ kansikin kí tùkù-ni. M sá Daafiidi naakookun ní, ki sá utaafaaŋmalbija un jìl̀ee.».

eng: The Spirit and the bride say, Come! Let the one who hears say, Come! And let the one who is thirsty come! Let the one who wishes receive life-giving water as a gift.
local: Unimbɔti Fam nì Unimpucaan bí yii: «Dāan̄!» Bin mun gbìl̄ an bɔtee ń lì yii: «Dāan̄!» Un nnyinnyoo yaa cááyee ń dāan̄ kí fōō nnyim min tìì dimaŋfalee faalaa.

eng: Now I bear witness to everyone who hears the words of the prophecy contained in this scroll: If anyone adds to them, God will add to that person the plagues that are written in this scroll.
local: Man Yoowaneesi di kpáafí bin kɔkɔ gbìl̄ tibɔti tin Unimbɔti sòon̄ an kā kugbɔŋu nee poonee yii: Un yaa gbéeǹ tibɔti nee poon tibee, Unimbɔti làá dāá gbéeǹ udaan ditafadaaldi din u ŋmàn̄ ki ŋá kugbɔŋu nee poonee.

eng: If anyone takes away from the words of this scroll of prophecy, God will take away that persons share in the tree of life and the holy city, which are described in this scroll.
local: Un yaa tí bātì tibɔti gbanti poon tiba ki fée, Unimbɔti làá dāá fōō u-jandi din u cáá disubil din tìì dimaŋfalee poonee, ú taa kɔ́ circir tiŋki kin bɔti bi ŋmàn̄ kugbɔŋu nee poonee.

eng: through the middle of the citys main street. On each side of the river is the tree of life, which produces twelve crops of fruit, bearing its fruit each month. The trees leaves are for the healing of the nations.
local: Mì pùub̀ kitiŋ kansikin didandi pu. Ní dimaŋfal subu yú abuŋgbán liitil pu ki lòoǹ dibindi poonee saalaa nì mili, an dàkà yii uŋmal kamaa poonee bu lòoǹ diba. Bu-faati di cāantí atimbun weeŋyi.

eng: The one who bears witness to these things says, Yes, Im coming soon. Amen. Come, Lord Jesus!.
local: Úǹ Yeesu un bí ki sòoǹ nì ti an bɔtee di lī yii: «Iiin! M làá dāan̄ kɔŋkɔnnee.» Án lafun ŋá míǹ! Dāan̄, ti-Dindaan Yeesu!.

eng: The grace of the Lord Jesus be with all.
local: Ti-Dindaan Yeesu ŋanti ń nīn bí ni-pu!.

eng: There will no longer be any curse. The throne of God and the Lamb will be in it, and his servants will worship him.
local: Mbusu dāa gíti kpá. Unimbɔti nì Dipibil beejal làá dāá nīn bí kitiŋ poon ní, ní u-tɔntɔnliibi ń nīn nyɔ́ŋkí-u.

eng: They will see his face, and his name will be on their foreheads.
local: Bi làá dāá wāl̀ u-nimbiin, ní bi dāa ŋmà u-yindi bi-yigbeeŋi pu.

eng: Night will be no more. They wont need the light of a lamp or the light of the sun, for the Lord God will shine on them, and they will rule forever and always.
local: Kunyeeu kaa dāa gíti bí. Ubɔ kaa dāa gíti nyàab̀ fitila kí tūū án nīn wāĺ-u, kaa dāa gíti nyàab̀ ŋwìiń ń nyā bí nīn wāĺ, kun puee ti-Dindaan Unimbɔti di dāa sá-bi ŋwalifi, ní bí dāá nīn bí ki jīǹ dibeel kí nīn pée cáa cá jaanjaan.

eng: Then he said to me, These words are trustworthy and true. The Lord, the God of the spirits of the prophets, sent his angel to show his servants what must soon take place.
local: Ní ntuunee bí-mi yii: «Tibɔti nee sá ibaamɔn ní, ki máaǹ bí fōō-tì. Ní ti-Dindaan Unimbɔti un tìì u-Fam u-bɔnaatiliibee túnní u-tuuŋŋu ń dāá dàkà u-tɔntɔnliibi tin làá bàń kɔŋkɔnneeyee.».

eng: Look! Im coming soon. Favored is the one who keeps the words of the prophecy contained in this scroll.
local: Ní Yeesu bí yii: «Pīlifì! M làá dāan̄ kɔŋkɔnnee. Un ŋúb́ tibɔti tin m sòon̄ bi ŋmàn̄ an kā kugbɔŋu nee poonee bí mmɔnni.».

eng: I, John, am the one who heard and saw these things. When I heard and saw them, I fell down to worship at the feet of the angel who had shown them to me.
local: Man Yoowaneesi di gbìl̄ an bɔtee kɔkɔ ki wál̀-tì mun. M nín gbìl̄ ki wál̀-tì mimmee ní m lítì dicincibil ntuun ŋun dàkā-mi tibɔti gbanti kɔkɔ taapuee nimbiin kí jāam̀-ŋu.

eng: But he said to me, Dont do that! Im a servant just like you and your brothers and sisters, the prophets, and those who keep the words of this scroll. Worship God.
local: Ní ŋu bí-mi yii: «Taa ŋée, kun puee ma mun sá utɔntɔnli kii sisee ní, kii a-nabiyaamu mun nàatī Unimbɔti bɔtee, nì bin kɔkɔ tɔ́ tibɔti tin bi ŋmàn̄ kugbɔŋu nee poonee ní. Unimbɔti ní an sá á jāam̀.».

eng: Write this to the angel of the church in Sardis: These are the words of the one who holds Gods seven spirits and the seven stars: I know your works. You have the reputation of being alive, and you are in fact dead.
local: «Ŋmà kugbɔŋu kí tīī m-nikitil din bí Saardee tuuŋŋu yii: «Un cáá Unimbɔti Fam miluli nì iŋmalbijaa ilulee di bí-u yii: M nyí a-tuuŋŋi, ki nyí yii a-yindi yíkì yilpu. Bi nyí a-pu yii a sá dinikitil din cáá dimaŋfalee, ama ní a kpíì.

eng: Because you kept my command to endure, I will keep you safe through the time of testing that is about to come over the whole world, to test those who live on earth.
local: Kun puee a nín pɔ́ɔǹ ki ŋúb́ m-bɔtee, ma mun làá ŋúb́-si ŋyunti ŋun dinimbinaamandi làá dāá jēetí duulinya nib kɔkɔ pu kí cɔ̄ŋkì-bee.

eng: Im coming soon. Hold on to what you have so that no one takes your crown.
local: M dòoń kɔŋkɔnnee. Á ŋúb́ tin a cááyee gbīŋgbim ubɔ ń taa fōō a-cee kibeefuulii kin bi tìì bin jáń ki jíńee.

eng: As for those who emerge victorious, I will make them pillars in the temple of my God, and they will never leave it. I will write on them the name of my God and the name of the city of my God, the New Jerusalem that comes down out of heaven from my God. I will also write on them my own new name.
local: «Un yaa jáń ki jíńee, m làá dū-u kí kpántí m-Nimbɔtiu diiku jaantalkuu ní ú báań kí nīn kíǹ ki bí niin kí taa ti nyā, ní ḿ dāá dū m-Nimbɔtiu yindi nì m-Nimbɔtiu tiŋki, Jeerusaleempɔɔn un nyánní yilpu m-Nimbɔtiu ceeyee yindi nì man yimpɔndi kí ŋmà udaan pu.

eng: If you can hear, listen to what the Spirit is saying to the churches.
local: «Un yaa cáá ditafalee ń pīlifì Unimbɔti Fam nín bí ki sòoǹ nì Kriisitoo nikitiŋ puee.».

eng: Write this to the angel of the church in Laodicea: These are the words of the Amen, the faithful and true witness, the ruler of Gods creation.
local: «Ŋmà kugbɔŋu kí tīī m-nikitil din bí Lawoodiiseeyee tuuŋŋu yii: «Un bi yíì Ameeni u sá un lèekī seera deedee nì ibaamɔn, ki sá Unimbɔti wannaaŋkaati kɔkɔ nyeelee.

eng: I know your works. You are neither cold nor hot. I wish that you were either cold or hot.
local: di bí-u yii: M nyí atuun ŋin a túńee kɔkɔ. Aa sɔ̀m̀aaa, kaa tùm̀ mun. A yaa là kí nīn sɔ̀m̀aaa, á nīn sɔ̀m̀. A yaa là kí nīn tí tùm̀maaa, á nīn tùm̀.

eng: So because you are lukewarm, and neither hot nor cold, Im about to spit you out of my mouth.
local: Kii a nín bí gbìmmm kaa sɔ̀m̀aaa, kaa tùm̀ muee, m làá wùliŋkì-si kí fá.

eng: After all, you say, Im rich, and Ive grown wealthy, and I dont need a thing. You dont realize that you are miserable, pathetic, poor, blind, and naked.
local: Di sá a lì yii sii sá ugaaja ní. A-gaajati tin a ká nee puee, aa ti là tiba nì tiba. Di sá kun pu a lì mimmee di sá yii aa nyí yii a sá ufalaadaan, ki sá unandaan, ki sá usoodaan, ki sá ujɔfu, ki sá ucancaŋgbeefu.

eng: My advice is that you buy gold from me that has been purified by fire so that you may be rich, and white clothing to wear so that your nakedness wont be shamefully exposed, and ointment to put on your eyes so that you may see.
local: An puee m sáŋkí-si yii á dāan̄ m-cee kí dā sindaariya un bi dū ŋŋmi ki cɔ́ŋkì ki ká yii u sá umɔntee, ní kí nín ŋmā kí kpántí ugaaja, kí dā m-cee abɔkutipiin kí pèen̄ kí bìin̄ a-cooi pu, kí dā nnyɔkɔ m-cee kí jōō a-nimbiliŋ kí wāl̀.

eng: I correct and discipline those whom I love. So be earnest and change your hearts and lives.
local: Man dátí bin m néeńee tafal ní ki sáŋkí-bi. An puee á ká kunyannyaau kí kpántí a-ba.

eng: Wake up and strengthen whatever you have left, teetering on the brink of death, for Ive found that your works are far from complete in the eyes of my God.
local: Fìntī kí tíkiń tin dáá bí a-ni kaa kpíì ama ki làá kpúee, kun puee maa ká a-tummɔntiŋ aba m-Nimbɔtiu cee.

eng: Look! Im standing at the door and knocking. If any hear my voice and open the door, I will come in to be with them, and will have dinner with them, and they will have dinner with me.
local: Á pīlifì, m yú kipunyɔkɔ pu ki ŋáań gaafalaa. Un yaa gbìl̄ m-nyɔkɔ pu ní ki pīitì-mee, m làá kɔ́ udaan cee ní man nì u ń kpāfì kí jī.

eng: As for those who emerge victorious, I will allow them to sit with me on my throne, just as I emerged victorious and sat down with my Father on his throne.
local: «Un yaa jáń ki jíńee, m làá yàntī ú dāan̄ kí kàl̄ kí kɔ́ĺ-mi m-beelin, kii ma mun nín jáń ki jíń pu ki dɔ́miń ki kāl̀ m-Baa beelin ki kɔ́ĺ-u puee.

eng: If you can hear, listen to what the Spirit is saying to the churches.
local: «Un yaa cáá ditafalee ń pīlifì Unimbɔti Fam nín bí ki sòoǹ nì Kriisitoo nikitiŋ puee.».

eng: So remember what you received and heard. Hold on to it and change your hearts and lives. If you dont wake up, I will come like a thief, and you wont know what time I will come upon you.
local: Á téetí bi nín nìn dàkā-si Unimbɔti bɔti a fōō-tì ki kíí puee. Á nīn gbíntì ki ŋúb́-tì kí kpántí a-ba. A yaa kaa fīntèe, m làá dāan̄ ŋyunti ŋun saa nyí yii m làá dāan̄ee ní kí túuǹ-si kii unaayuu nín tūuntí unil puee.

eng: But you do have a few people in Sardis who havent stained their clothing. They will walk with me clothed in white because they are worthy.
local: Ama nì míǹ kɔkɔee, biba bí Saardi nikitil kansikin kaa ŋá ikpiti bi-bɔkutiŋ pīiǹ. Binib gbanti dāa pēē abɔkutipiin káŋkaŋ ní man nì bi ń nīn táá ki còom̄, kun puee bi máaǹ míǹ.

eng: Those who emerge victorious will wear white clothing like this. I wont scratch out their names from the scroll of life, but will declare their names in the presence of my Father and his angels.
local: «Míǹ ní un yaa jáń ki jíńee dāa pèentí abɔkutipiin káŋkaŋ. Maa làá dāá kpìtī udaan yindi dimaŋfal gbɔŋku poon, ama m làá dāá lì m-Baa nì u-tuuŋyi kɔkɔ nimbiin yii man di yì-u.

eng: If you can hear, listen to what the Spirit is saying to the churches.
local: «Un yaa cáá ditafalee ń pīlifì Unimbɔti Fam nín bí ki sòoǹ nì Kriisitoo nikitiŋ puee.».

eng: Write this to the angel of the church in Philadelphia: These are the words of the one who is holy and true, who has the key of David. Whatever he opens, no one will shut; and whatever he shuts, no one opens.
local: «Ŋmà kugbɔŋu kí tīī m-nikitil din bí Fiiladeelfiiyee tuuŋŋu yii: «Un sá circir ki sá ubaamɔnee, un di ŋúb́ Daafiidi saafiiu ki yaa pīitì, ubɔ kaa ŋūǹ kí dūú pìin̄, ki yaa tí dūu pīiǹ, ubɔ kaa ŋūǹ kí pìitēe.

eng: I know your works. Look! I have set in front of you an open door that no one can shut. You have so little power, and yet you have kept my word and havent denied my name.
local: di bí-u yii: M nyí atuun ŋin kɔkɔ a túńee. M pīitì kipunyɔkɔ ki wāaǹ a-nimbiin, ní ubɔ kaa ŋūǹ kí dūú pìin̄. Kun puee m nyí a-pɔŋŋu kaa náaǹ, ama a ŋúń ki ŋúb́ m-bɔti, kaa dū-mi ki fá.

eng: Because of this I will make the people from Satans synagogue —I will make them come and bow down at your feet and realize that I have loved you.
local: Á kpèè tin m làá ŋá kí tīī-see: Binib bin kpáfì ki sá Sitaan yab ki mɔ́ntí yii bi sá Juuda yab kin kaa sée, m làá dū-bi kí ŋá a-ŋaan. M làá yàntī bí dāá gbàan̄ a-taaŋi taapu ní kí béé yii m néeń-si.

eng: After this I looked and there was a door that had been opened in heaven. The first voice that I had heard, which sounded like a trumpet, said to me, Come up here, and I will show you what must take place after this.
local: U lī míǹ ki dóòyee, boonee m kpēē kutagbɔŋu puee, kipunyɔkɔ kiba cúu pīitì. Niinee nneen ŋun m bàà péé péé ki gbìl̄ ŋu pɔ̄ɔ̄ kpaŋkpam kii dikantándee bí-mi yii: «Jɔ̄mìń doo ḿ dàkà-si tin bí ki dòoń boonee.».

eng: the twenty-four elders fall before the one seated on the throne. They worship the one who lives forever and always. They throw down their crowns before the throne and say.
local: ní bikpilib mmuŋku nì binaayee lítí un kā dibeejal pu ki làá nīn bí jaanjaanee taapu ní kí nyɔ̄ŋkì-u, ní ki cíkití bi-fuulimmu ki lúkufí u-beejal taapu ní ki gà ilan ki pàkà-u yii.

eng: You are worthy, our Lord and God, to receive glory and honor and power, because you created all things. It is by your will that they existed and were created.
local: «Sii ti-Dindaan ti-Nimbɔtiu, a máaǹ jilma nì tinyool nì mpɔn. Di sá yii sii di náań tikɔkɔ nì tikɔkɔ, ní ki là ní ki tīī tikɔkɔ dimaŋfal tí nīn bí.».

eng: At once I was in a Spirit-inspired trance and I saw a throne in heaven, and someone was seated on the throne.
local: Kpalaa niinee ní Circir Fam yōoǹ-mi ki jɔ́m̀ yilpu ní m ká dibeejal ní ubɔ kā dibeejalee pu.

eng: The one seated there looked like jasper and carnelian, and surrounding the throne was a rainbow that looked like an emerald.
local: Udaan nimbiin jìl̀ ki ŋmóò làka laka kii ataŋkpamɔnti ŋin bi yíì jaspee nì ŋin bi yíì saarduwaanee, ní dikuntinyindi māntì ki tú dibeejalee ní ki jìl̀ pàlpal kii ditaŋkpal din bi yíì eemeeroodee.

eng: Twenty-four thrones, with twenty-four elders seated upon them, surrounded the throne. The elders were dressed in white clothing and had gold crowns on their heads.
local: Ní abeejal mmuŋku nì anaa aba mun māntì ki tú dibeejalee, ní bikpilib bin kā an beejaliŋ gbanti puee pēē tiwampin ní ki cíkí sindaariya beefuulimmu.

eng: From the throne came lightning, voices, and thunder. In front of the throne were seven flaming torches, which are the seven spirits of God.
local: Dibeejalee ni ní itaaŋmɔŋmɔ nì ataadun nì dibintiŋkpaandi nyēeń ní iŋminaapool iluli tūū ki yú dibeejalee nimbiin ki tùm̀ kpaŋkpam, an di sá Unimbɔti Fam milulee.

eng: Something like a glass sea, like crystal, was in front of the throne. In the center, by the throne, were four living creatures encircling the throne. These creatures were covered with eyes on the front and on the back.
local: Tiŋku ubɔ dɔ̄ dibeejalee nimbiin ki wāĺ kílkil kii digitil nín wāĺ puee, kii ataŋkpee ŋin bi yíì kriistaalee nín jìl̀ puee. Dibeejalee jannaatiliŋ puee, tiwan tinaa tin cáá kufoouee yú kansikin ki māntì ki tú, ní ti-boon nì ti-nimbiinee kɔkɔ sá animbil ŋma.

eng: The first living creature was like a lion. The second living creature was like an ox. The third living creature had a face like a human being. And the fourth living creature was like an eagle in flight.
local: Upeepeekaayee náań nì gbikinti, ní uliliitee náań nì unaja, ní utataatee nimbiin nì unil yɔŋu náań, ní unanaatee náań nì ukaalun yaa bíi ŋàaǹ u nín bí puee.

eng: Each of the four living creatures had six wings, and each was covered all around and on the inside with eyes. They never rest day or night, but keep on saying, Holy, holy, holy is the Lord God Almighty, who was and is and is coming.
local: Tiwan tinaa tin cáá kufoouee gbanti kɔkɔ cáá ifeen iluu iluu in pu animbil píńee poon nì boon. Ní bi lì ŋwiimpu nì kunyeeu kaa gbèentí yii: «Circir, circir, ti-Dindaan Unimbɔti di sá circir, úǹ di sá Mpɔŋkɔdaan, un nìn bí, ki gbíntì ki bí, ki làá dāan̄ee.».

eng: Whenever the living creatures give glory, honor, and thanks to the one seated on the throne, who lives forever and always.
local: Ŋyunti ŋun ní tiwan tinaa tin cáá kufoouee yaa bí ki tìì jilma nì tinyool ki pàkà un kā dibeejal pu ki làá nīn bí kí nīn cá jaanjaanee.

eng: Then I saw a scroll in the right hand of the one seated on the throne. It had writing on the front and the back, and it was sealed with seven seals.
local: Ní m ká un kā dibeejal puee ŋúb́ kugbɔŋu kubɔ u-ŋaŋgiilin bi kpáb́-ku, ní an ŋmàn̄ poon nì boon ní bi dū tiwampiiŋkaal tiluli ki bóob́ kugbɔŋuee.

eng: You made them a kingdom and priests to our God, and they will rule on earth.
local: ki dū-bi ki ŋá dibeel poon nib, bí nīn sá ti-Nimbɔtiu saraaŋaliibi kí jī dibeel duulinya poon.».

eng: Then I looked, and I heard the sound of many angels surrounding the throne, the living creatures, and the elders. They numbered in the millions—thousands upon thousands.
local: M bí ki kpèéyee, ní ki gbìl̄ ituun kucikukpaaŋŋu kutukub kutukub wooku māntì ki tú dibeejalee nì tiwan tin cáá kufoouee nì bikpilibee.

eng: They said in a loud voice, Worthy is the slaughtered Lamb to receive power, wealth, wisdom, and might, and honor, glory, and blessing.
local: Ní bi gà ilan mpɔn yii: «Dipibil din bi kūūyee máaǹ bí tīī-dì mpɔn nì tigaajati nì iciin nì jilma nì tinyool nì ipaka.».

eng: And I heard every creature in heaven and on earth and under the earth and in the sea—I heard everything everywhere say, Blessing, honor, glory, and power belong to the one seated on the throne and to the Lamb forever and always.
local: Niinee ní m gbìl̄ tiwannáaŋkaal tin kɔkɔ bí yilpuee nì tin kɔkɔ bí taapuee nì tin gíti bí kitiŋ taapu taapuee nì tin bí tiŋkunee, tiwannáaŋkaal kɔkɔ bí ki gà ilan yii: «Un kā dibeejal puee nì dipibilee di máaǹ inyɔŋki nì ipaka nì tinyool nì mpɔn kí nīn pée cáa cá jaanjaan.».

eng: Then the four living creatures said, Amen, and the elders fell down and worshipped.
local: Ní tiwan tinaa tin cáá kufoouee kíí yii: «Ameeni!» Ní bikpilibee tātìi gbāaǹ ki nyɔ́ŋkì.

eng: I saw a powerful angel, who proclaimed in a loud voice, Who is worthy to open the scroll and break its seals.
local: Ní ntuun ŋun cáá mpɔnee bíi báakí mpɔn yii: «Ŋma di cáá mpɔn kí bútí tiwampiiŋkaal nee kí kpátí kugbɔŋu nee dee?».

eng: But no one in heaven or on earth or under the earth could open the scroll or look inside it.
local: Báà ubɔ nìn kpá yilpuɔɔɔ, ki kpá taapuɔɔɔ, ki kpá ditaŋkpiilin kí ŋmā kí kpátí kugbɔŋuee kí kàaǹ poon.

eng: So I began to weep and weep, because no one was found worthy to open the scroll or to look inside it.
local: Niinee ní m bíi sūǹ tikpil, kun puee ubɔ kaa nìn bí laaba ki ŋūǹ kí kpátí kugbɔŋuee kí kàaǹ tin bí poonee.

eng: Then one of the elders said to me, Dont weep. Look! The Lion of the tribe of Judah, the Root of David, has emerged victorious so that he can open the scroll and its seven seals.
local: Ní bikpilib mmuŋku nì binaayee kansikin ubɔ bí-mi yii: «Taa ti sɔ̄. Kpèè! Juuda naakookun gbikintiu un sá ubɔti Daafiidi naantiiuee jáń ki jíń. Úǹ di làá ŋmā kí bútí tiwampiiŋkaal tiluli tin bi dū ki bóob́ kugbɔŋuee kí kpátí-ku kí kàaǹ.».

eng: Then, in between the throne and the four living creatures and among the elders, I saw a Lamb, standing as if it had been slain. It had seven horns and seven eyes, which are Gods seven spirits, sent out into the whole earth.
local: Ní m kpēēyee, dipibil di yú bikpilibee nimbiin, dibeejal nì tiwan tinaa tin cáá kufoouee kansikinee, ki bí kii bi nìn kūū-dèe, ní dì cáá iyin iluli, animbil aluli. Animbil alulee di sá Unimbɔti Fam miluli min u tú duulinya kɔkɔnee.

eng: He came forward and took the scroll from the right hand of the one seated on the throne.
local: Dipibilee di dɔ́miń ki fōō un kā dibeejal puee ŋaŋgiiliŋun kugbɔŋu.

eng: When he took the scroll, the four living creatures and the twenty-four elders fell down before the Lamb. Each held a harp and gold bowls full of incense, which are the prayers of the saints.
local: U fōō kugbɔŋuee ki dóòyee ní tiwan tinaa tin cáá kufoouee nì bikpilib mmuŋku nì binaayee dɔ́miń ki lítì u-nimbiin, báà ŋma ŋúb́ kubeeu nì sindaariya sambiyaamu, ní talaalu gbí poon. Talaaluee di sá Unimbɔti nibul wimmeem.

eng: They took up a new song, saying, You are worthy to take the scroll and open its seals, because you were slain, and by your blood you purchased for God persons from every tribe, language, people, and nation.
local: Ní bi bí ki gà nlampɔɔn ki nyɔ́ŋkí-u yii: «A máaǹ kí fōō kugbɔŋuee kí bútí tiwampiiŋkaal tin bi dūu bóob́ee. Kun puee bi kūū-si ní a-fatikuu dá anibul kɔkɔ nì isoon kɔkɔ nib, nì atimbun kɔkɔ nib nì ntimu kɔkɔ nib ki tīī Unimbɔti.

eng: Then I looked on as the Lamb opened one of the seven seals. I heard one of the four living creatures say in a voice like thunder, Come.
local: M yú ki kpèéyee, ní Dipibil cúú tiwampiiŋkaal tiluli tin bóob́ kugbɔŋuee kubɔ ki bútì, ní m gbìl̄ tiwan tinaa tin cáá kufoouee kansikin kubɔ téeń mpɔn mpɔn kii ntaateenteenee yii: «Dāan̄!».

eng: They cried out with a loud voice, Holy and true Master, how long will you wait before you pass judgment? How long before you require justice for our blood, which was shed by those who live on earth.
local: ní bi bí ki tátí mpɔn yii: «Ti-Dindaan un sá circir ki sá mbaamɔndaanee, a làá cīití kí tin sāā ŋyunti ŋulɔŋu ní kí sòoǹ duulinya yab bin kūū-tee bɔti kí dáaĺ bi-tafal ní?».

eng: Each of them was given a white robe, and they were told to rest a little longer, until their fellow servants and brothers and sisters—who were about to be killed as they were—were finished.
local: Niinee ní bi cáań abɔkutipiin ki pēeǹ bi-kɔkɔ ní ki bí-bi yii bí dá láá kàl̄ kí ŋùŋfì fiii, kí nīn cí ŋyunti ŋun ní bi yaa láa kūū bi-tɔntɔnjutiibi nì bi-nabiyaamu mun kíǹ duulinyan bi mun dɔ́miń ki kútì bi-puee.

eng: I looked on as he opened the sixth seal, and there was a great earthquake. The sun became black as funeral clothing, and the entire moon turned red as blood.
local: Ní Dipibilee tí cúú tiwampiiŋkaal kululuutiiu ki bútì, ní kitiŋ cīŋkì ŋkpitim pu, ní ŋwìiń fōō ki bɔ̄ntì kii satabɔnee, ní uŋmal mántì cínciŋ kii fatikuee.

eng: The stars of the sky fell to the earth as a fig tree drops its fruit when shaken by a strong wind.
local: ní iŋmalbijaa tìfī yilpu ki lítí a lì kii kubuŋkpaaŋŋu nín tìfī asubigaan ki lúkufí puee.

eng: The sky disappeared like a scroll being rolled up, and every mountain and island was moved from its place.
local: Ní kutagbɔŋu wáŋkì a lì kii kukampeeu ní bi kpáb́ ki yōoǹee, ní ajoon nì ntimu mun nnyim māntì ki túee kpīkitì.

eng: Then the kings of the earth, the officials and the generals, the rich and the powerful, and everyone, slave and free, hid themselves in caves and in the rocks of the mountains.
local: Ní duulinya bɔtiibi nì biyidambi nì bisoojakpilib nì bigaajab nì ipɔndambi nì iyumbu nì bin tɔ́ bi-bee, báà ŋma sáń ki bāal̀ afaginni nì titaŋkpanaakɔtin ajoon pu.

eng: They called to the mountains and the rocks, Fall on us and hide us from the face of the one seated on the throne and from the Lambs wrath!.
local: ní binibee lì ajoon nì ataŋkpee yii: «Lítímaan kí bìin̄ ti-pu, un kā dibeejal puee ń taa ká-ti, Dipibil mun ŋuul ń taa bàń-ti.

eng: The great day of their wrath has come, and who is able to stand.
local: Kun puee ŋwiin ŋun sá diŋuukpaandi wiiŋŋuee bāań. Ŋma di sá-u kí yūĺ?».

eng: So I looked, and there was a white horse. Its rider held a bow and was given a crown. And he went forth from victory to victory.
local: M kpēēyee, ki ká utampiin, un kā-uee ŋúb́ butɔbu, ní bi cíkiǹ-u kibeefuulii, ní u búntì nì dijajil poon kí já kí jīń.

eng: When the Lamb opened the second seal, I heard the second living creature say, Come.
local: Ní Dipibilee bútì tiwampiiŋkaal kuliliitiiu, ní m gbìl̄ kuwaŋu kun cáá kufoouee kuliliitiiu téeń yii: «Dāan̄!».

eng: Out came another horse, fiery red. Its rider was allowed to take peace from the earth so that people would kill each other. He was given a large sword.
local: Niinee ní utaamu ubɔ mun nyánní ki sá uman ní bi pú un kā u-puee mpɔn ú lēē ŋgbansɔŋfi kitiŋ pu binib ń nīn kù tɔb, ní bi dū kitaataajikpaaŋŋi ki tīī-u.

eng: When he opened the third seal, I heard the third living creature say, Come! So I looked, and there was a black horse. Its rider held a balance for weighing in his hand.
local: Ní Dipibilee bútì tiwampiiŋkaal kutataatiiu, ní m gbìl̄ kuwaŋu kun cáá kufoouee kutataatiiu téeń yii: «Dāan̄!» M kpēēyee, ki ká utambɔn, un kā-uee ŋúb́ saniya u-ŋaan.

eng: I heard what sounded like a voice from among the four living creatures. It said, A quart of wheat for a denarion, and three quarts of barley for a denarion, but dont damage the olive oil and the wine.
local: Ní m gbìl̄ nneen ŋubɔ sòon̄ní tiwan tinaa tin cáá kufoouee kansikin ki lī yii: «Dū saniya kí ŋmàntī bilee kiiloo ubɔ ŋwiin ŋubɔ tundi paati, kí ŋmàntī ɔɔji kiiloo bita ŋwiin ŋubɔ tundi paati. Ama á taa gbēē ŋkpam nì ndaam pu.».

eng: When he opened the fourth seal, I heard the voice of the fourth living creature say, Come.
local: Ní Dipibilee tí bútì tiwampiiŋkaal kunanaatiiu ní m gbìl̄ kuwaŋu kun cáá kufoouee kunanaatiiu téeń yii: «Dāan̄!».

eng: So I looked, and there was a pale green horse. Its riders name was Death, and the Grave was following right behind. They were given authority over a fourth of the earth, to kill by sword, famine, disease, and the wild animals of the earth.
local: M kpēēyee, ki ká utaamu un pīiǹ fùuuyee, ní bi yíì un kā-uee yii Ŋkúm ní Ditaŋkpiil nóó-u. Bi nìn tīī-bi mpɔn yii bí dū ntàataajimu nì ŋkùm nì bìlbil kúm kí kpɔ̀ kitiŋ pu nib kɔkɔ yaa yākatì minaayee dijandi diba pu, ní tipeecuncuti tin bí kitiŋ puee ń cūtì binib.

eng: When he opened the fifth seal, I saw under the altar those who had been slaughtered on account of the word of God and the witness they had given.
local: Ní u cúú tiwampiiŋkaal kuŋmooŋmootiiu ki bútì. M kpēēyee, ki ká nsaraafan ceeyee bin bin nìn sòoǹ Unimbɔti bɔti ki lèekī seerab bi kūū-bee nammu.

eng: After this I saw four angels standing at the four corners of the earth. They held back the earths four winds so that no wind would blow against the earth, the sea, or any tree.
local: Míǹ boonee ní m ká ituun inaa yú duulinya jaŋŋi naatil kɔkɔ pu, ní ki ŋúb́ duulinya buŋku naatil, án nín làá ŋá pu kubuŋu ń taa ti jītí tiŋku puɔɔɔ, kí taa ti jītí kitiŋ puɔɔɔ, kí taa ti jītí busubu báà bubɔ puee.

eng: They cried out with a loud voice: Victory belongs to our God who sits on the throne, and to the Lamb.
local: Bi nìn tátí mpɔn yii: «Difiil nyēeń ti-Nimbɔtiu un kā dibeejal puee nì Dipibil cee ní.».

eng: All the angels stood in a circle around the throne, and around the elders and the four living creatures. They fell facedown before the throne and worshipped God.
local: Ní ituun in māntì ki tú dibeejalee, nì bikpilibee nì tiwan tinaa tin cáá kufoouee, tātìi dōoǹ dicincibil dibeejal taapu ní ki bíi nyɔ́ŋkí Unimbɔti.

eng: saying, Amen! Blessing and glory and wisdom and thanksgiving and honor and power and might be to our God forever and always. Amen.
local: ní ki lì yii: «Ameeni! Ti-Nimbɔtiu di yì inyɔŋki nì jilma nì iciin nì ijaam nì tinyool nì mpɔn kí nīn pée cáa cá jaanjaan! Ameeni.».

eng: Then one of the elders said to me, Who are these people wearing white robes, and where did they come from.
local: Ní bikpilibee kansikin ubɔ bālifì-mi yii: «Binib bin pēē abɔkutipiin nee sá bilab ní? Bi nyánní la ní?».

eng: I said to him, Sir, you know. Then he said to me, These people have come out of great hardship. They have washed their robes and made them white in the Lambs blood.
local: Ní m kíí-u yii: «M-Dindaan, sii di nyí-bi.» Ní u fātì ki bí-mi yii: «Bin nyánní falaakpaŋkpaan poonee dee. Bíǹ di fíntì bi-bɔkutiŋ ki dū-ŋi ki ŋá Dipibil fatikuun ŋi píil̀ káŋkaŋ.

eng: This is the reason they are before Gods throne. They worship him day and night in his temple, and the one seated on the throne will shelter them.
local: An pu ní bi yú kunimbɔtidiin Unimbɔti beejal nimbiin ki jáaḿ-u ŋwiimpu nì kunyeeu, ní un kā dibeejal puee làá nīn bí bi-cee kí mɔ̄tì bi-pu.

eng: They wont hunger or thirst anymore. No sun or scorching heat will beat down on them.
local: Ŋkùm kaa dāa gíti cútí-bi, nnyinnyoo kaa dāa gíti cútí-bi, diwiŋkaŋkambil kaa dāa wù-bi, ní kutuntuŋu kubɔ kaa dāa bí niin.

eng: because the Lamb who is in the midst of the throne will shepherd them. He will lead them to the springs of life-giving water, and God will wipe away every tear from their eyes.
local: Kun puee Dipibil din yú dibeejal kansikinee di dāa sá bi-kpàaliu, ní kí cáá-bi kí cùnnì nnyim min tìì dimaŋfalee laŋki, ní Unimbɔti ń pítí bi-nyinnyim kɔkɔ.».

eng: I saw another angel coming up from the east, holding the seal of the living God. He cried out with a loud voice to the four angels who had been given the power to damage the earth and sea.
local: M gíti kpēēyee, ki ká ntuun ŋubɔ nyánní ŋwìiń nyalaŋki pú ki ŋúb́ Unimbɔti un bí dimaŋfalinee daaŋku, ní ki téeń ki tùkū ituun inaa in fōō mpɔn kí dāá bīil̀ kitiŋ nì tiŋkuee yii.

eng: He said, Dont damage the earth, the sea, or the trees until we have put a seal on the foreheads of those who serve our God.
local: «Ní taa láá ŋá kitiŋ nì tiŋku nì isufi tiba kí nīn cí ŋyunti ŋun ti yaa láa dū tidaandi ki nákáǹ ti-Nimbɔtiu tɔntɔnliibi yigbeeŋi pu ki dóòyee.».

eng: Then I heard the number of those who were sealed: one hundred forty-four thousand, sealed from every tribe of the Israelites.
local: Ní m gbìl̄ bi lī bin bi nákáǹ Unimbɔti daaŋku bi-yigbeeŋi puee wíiŋŋu nyɔkɔm, yii bi sá binib kutukub dilaataal nì imuŋku ili nì binaa, ní ki nyánní Isirayeel naakɔti kɔkɔ poon.

eng: From the tribe of Judah, twelve thousand were sealed; from the tribe of Reuben, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Gad, twelve thousand.
local: Bi dáań Juuda naakookun yab binib kutukub saalaa nì bili, ki dáań Rubeen naakookun yab binib kutukub saalaa nì bili, ki dáań Gaadi naakookun yab binib kutukub saalaa nì bili.

eng: from the tribe of Asher, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Naphtali, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Manasseh, twelve thousand.
local: ki dáań Aseer naakookun yab binib kutukub saalaa nì bili, ki dáań Neefitalii naakookun yab binib kutukub saalaa nì bili, ki dáań Manasee naakookun yab binib kutukub saalaa nì bili.

eng: from the tribe of Simeon, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Levi, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Issachar, twelve thousand.
local: ki dáań Simeeyɔn naakookun yab binib kutukub saalaa nì bili, ki dáań Leefii naakookun yab binib kutukub saalaa nì bili, ki dáań Isakaar naakookun yab binib kutukub saalaa nì bili.

eng: from the tribe of Zebulun, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Joseph, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Benjamin, twelve thousand were sealed.
