local: ki dáań Sabulɔn naakookun yab binib kutukub saalaa nì bili, ki dáań Jooseefi naakookun yab binib kutukub saalaa nì bili, ki dáań Beenjameen naakookun yab binib kutukub saalaa nì bili.

eng: After this I looked, and there was a great crowd that no one could number. They were from every nation, tribe, people, and language. They were standing before the throne and before the Lamb. They wore white robes and held palm branches in their hands.
local: Míǹ boonee, m kpēēyee, ní ki ká nnibiim tikpil tikpil, ubɔ kaa ŋūǹ kí kàaǹ-mì. An nìn sá anibul kɔkɔ nì atimbun kɔkɔ nib nì ntimu kɔkɔ nib nì isoon kɔkɔ nib. Bi nìn yú dibeejal nì Dipibil nimbiin ki pēē abɔkutipiin káŋkaŋ ki ŋúb́ tikpakpati bi-ŋaan.

eng: Then, when the Lamb opened the seventh seal, there was silence in heaven for about half an hour.
local: Dipibil bútì tiwampiiŋkaal kululuútiiuee, ní yilpu ŋmíl̀ fɔ́ŋfɔŋ díkí diki ki tin cáa sāā kukúluu dijandi pu.

eng: Then the third angel blew his trumpet, and a great star, burning like a torch, fell from heaven. It fell on a third of the rivers and springs of water.
local: Ní ntuun ntataatee búń ŋu-kantándi, ní uŋmalbija ubɔ nyánní yilpu ki wù kii ŋŋminaapoolee ki dɔ́miń ki lítì amɔɔ nì inyimbul yaa yākatì mitee dijandi diba pu.

eng: The stars name is Wormwood, and a third of the waters became wormwood, and many people died from the water, because it became so bitter.
local: Inyiŋfi jandi din pu uŋmalbijee lítèe túŋkì ní binib tikpil nyùn̄-mì ní ki kpíì, kun puee mì nìn tū.

eng: Then the fourth angel blew his trumpet, and a third of the sun was struck, and a third of the moon, and a third of the stars so that a third of them became dark. The day lost a third of its light, and the night lost a third of its light too.
local: Ní ntuun nnanaatee búń ŋu-kantándi, ní ŋwìiń yaa yākatì mitee, dijandi fōō ki bɔ̄ntì, ní uŋmal yaa yākatì mitee, dijandi fōō ki bɔ̄ntì, ní iŋmalbijaa yaa yākatì mitee, dijandi fōō ki bɔ̄ntì, ní dibɔmbɔndi jéetì kitiŋ pu ŋwiin yaa yākatì mitee dijandi pu, ní uŋmal nì iŋmalbijaa kaa ti ŋmíntí kunyeeu yaa yākatì mitee, dijandi diba pu.

eng: Then I looked and I heard an eagle flying high overhead. It said with a loud voice, Horror, horror, oh! The horror for those who live on earth because of the blasts of the remaining trumpets that the three angels are about to blow.
local: M bí ki kpèéyee, ní ki gbìl̄ ukaalun ubɔ nín ŋàaǹ yilpu yilpu ki tátí mpɔn yii: «Mbusu, mbusu, mbusu gíti dɔ̄ ki cí kitiŋ pu nib ŋyunti ŋun ituun ita in kíǹee yaa láa búń i-kantáŋŋee.».

eng: Then I saw the seven angels who stand before God, and seven trumpets were given to them.
local: Ní m ká ituun iluli in yú Unimbɔti nimbiinee, ní bi tīī-i akantán aluli.

eng: Another angel came and stood at the altar, and he held a gold bowl for burning incense. He was given a large amount of incense, in order to offer it on behalf of the prayers of all the saints on the gold altar in front of the throne.
local: Ní ntuun ŋubɔ mun dɔ́miń ki yúl̀ nsaraafan cee ki ŋúb́ sindaariya sambiki kin poon bi ŋáań talaalu ki tùutēe. Ní bi tīī-ŋu talaalu tikpil yii ŋú tūū kí kpáaǹ Unimbɔti nib wimmeem kí ŋá saraa kí tīī Unimbɔti, sindaariya saraafaŋŋu ŋun yú dibeejal nimbiinee pu.

eng: The smoke of the incense offered for the prayers of the saints rose up before God from the angels hand.
local: Ní talaalu un ntuunee tūūyee nyiim yíkí ŋu-ŋaan ki jɔ́ḿ Unimbɔti cee ki kpáaǹ Unimbɔti nib wimmeem.

eng: Then the angel took the incense container and filled it with fire from the altar. He threw it down to the earth, and there were thunder, voices, lightning, and an earthquake.
local: Ní ntuunee yōoǹ kisambiki kin poon bi ŋáań talaalu ki tùutēe ní ki pātì ŋŋmi nsaraafan pu ki gbéeǹ ki-poon, ní ki dūu lúkuń duulinyan. Niinee ní m gbìl̄ dibintiŋkpaandi nì ataadun pu, ki ká an bíi ŋmóò kii utaayee ní kitiŋ cīŋkì.

eng: Then the seven angels who held the seven trumpets got ready to blow them.
local: Ní ituun iluli in ŋúb́ akantánee bɔ́ntì kí búń i-kantáŋŋi.

eng: The first angel blew his trumpet, and hail and fire mixed with blood appeared, and was thrown down to the earth. A third of the earth was burned up. A third of the trees were burned up. All the green grass was burned up.
local: Ntuun mpeepeekaa búń ŋu-kantándi ní ataanyiŋkumbil nì ŋŋmi ŋmàl̄ fatiku ki nyánní yilpu ki lítí kitiŋ, ní kitiŋ nín dɔ̄ ki wīikí puee yaa yākatì mitee dijandi lēē ki wùn̄, ní isufi nì timool nín dɔ̄ ki wīikí puee yaa yākatì mitee, an lēē dijandi ki wùn̄.

eng: Then the second angel blew his trumpet, and something like a huge mountain burning with fire was thrown down into the sea. A third of the sea became blood.
local: Ní ntuun nliliiti búń ŋu-kantándi, ní an bí kii dijookpaandi di fōō ŋŋmee, ní bi dūu lúkuń tiŋkun, ní tiŋku nín dɔ̄ ki wīikí puee yaa yākatì mitee dijandi kpántì fatiku.

eng: a third of the creatures living in the sea died, and a third of the ships were destroyed.
local: Ní tiwan tin cáá kufoou ki bí tiŋkunee nì ti-nyɔkɔmee yaa yākatì mitee, dijandi lēē ki kpíì poon, ní meelab nì bi-nyɔkɔm bin bí tiŋkunee yaa yākatì mitee, dijandi lēē poon ki nāaǹ.

eng: Then the fifth angel blew his trumpet, and I saw a star that had fallen from heaven to earth, and he was given the key to the shaft of the abyss.
local: Ní ntuun ŋŋmooŋmootee búń ŋu-kantándi, ní m ká uŋmalbija ubɔ nyánní yilpu ki lítì kitiŋ ní bi dū mbookpaan saafiiu ki tīī-u.

eng: They also have tails with stingers, just like scorpions; and in their tails is their power to hurt people for five months.
local: I-juuŋi nì i-looti bí kii inoomee ní i cáa júntí binib kí tin cáá sāā iŋmal iŋmoo.

eng: Their king is an angel from the abyss, whose Hebrew name is Abaddon, and whose Greek name is Apollyon.
local: Inaakitoon gbanti bɔtiu di sá ntuun ŋun kā kubookpaaŋŋun bi yíì-ŋu eebru sooŋyin yii «Abadɔn», ní greeki sooŋyin yii «Apooliyɔn», an taapu sá yii «Un nàantée».

eng: The first horror has passed. Look! Two horrors are still coming after this.
local: Mbusupeepeekaa dɔ́miń ki ŋá ki jítì, an kíǹ mbusub bili mun ń dāan̄ dee.

eng: Then the sixth angel blew his trumpet, and I heard a voice from the four horns of the gold altar that is before God.
local: Ní ntuun nluluuti búń ŋu-kantándi, ní m gbìl̄ nneen ŋubɔ nyánní iyin inaa in nìn cɔ́ sindaariya saraafaŋŋu ŋun yú Unimbɔti nimbiinee ni.

eng: It said to the sixth angel, who had the trumpet, Release the four angels who are bound at the great river Euphrates.
local: Ní nneenee lī ntuun nluluuti ŋun ŋúb́ dikantándee yii: «Cùú gbìntī ituun inaa in bi bóob́ i kā dimɔɔl din bi yíì Eefiraatee kí líí.».

eng: Then the four angels who had been made ready for that hour, day, month, and year were released to kill a third of humankind.
local: Ní bi gíiǹ ituun inaa in bɔ́ntì ki cí i-kúluku nì i-wiiŋŋu nì i-ŋmaliu nì i-bindi din bi làá líí-i í lēē duulinya poon binib yaa yākatì mitee diba pu kí kpɔ̀ee ki líì ŋyunti ŋun bi yóoǹee deedee.

eng: The number of cavalry troops was two hundred million. I heard their number.
local: Ní m gbìl̄ bi lī yii i-soojab bin kā itaamu puee sá miiliyɔmbi kulaafaau ní.

eng: And this is the way I saw the horses and their riders in the vision: they had breastplates that were fiery red, dark blue, and yellow as sulfur. The horses heads were like lions heads, and out of their mouths came fire, smoke, and sulfur.
local: M nín ká i-soojab bin kā itaamu puee nín bí puee sèé: bi-janjabɔkutiŋ máǹ kii ŋŋmee, ní iba yaŋ jìl̀ kii ataŋkpamɔnti ŋin bi yíì iyasintee, ní iba yaŋ bɔ̀ǹ kii kunaaluŋuee, ní iba yaŋ bí kii kpalkuee. Itaamu yiliŋ bí kii gbikinti yilee, ní ŋŋmi nì nnyiim nì farwata nyēē i-nyɔmmun.

eng: By these three plagues a third of humankind was killed: by the fire, smoke, and sulfur coming out of their mouths.
local: Ŋŋmi nì nnyiim nì farwata un nyēē i-nyɔmmunee di sá ditafadaaldi din bi dūu kūū duulinya nib yaa yākatì mitee dijandi dibee.

eng: The horses power is in their mouths and their tails, for their tails are like snakes with heads that inflict injuries.
local: Di sá itaamuee pɔŋŋu kā i-nyɔmmun nì i-juuŋin ní, ní i-juuŋi náań nì ikoobu, ní i cáá ayil. An ní i cáa ŋáań binib asaluu.

eng: He opened the shaft of the abyss; and smoke rose up from the shaft, like smoke from a huge furnace. The sun and air were darkened by the smoke from the shaft.
local: Ní u cúú mbookpaanee ki pīitì, ní ŋŋminyiim nyēeń poon tikpil tikpil a lì kii kipaai kikpaŋkpaaŋŋinee, ní ki dūu bīiǹ ŋwìiń pu ki kpáaǹ kubuŋu mun pu.

eng: The rest of humankind, who werent killed by these plagues, didnt change their hearts and lives and turn from their handiwork. They didnt stop worshipping demons and idols made of gold, silver, bronze, stone, and wood—idols that cant see or hear or walk.
local: Binib bin kíǹ, ditafadaaldee kaa kūūyee, baa dū bíǹ bi-ba nín ŋá tiŋyee ki fá, bi gbíntì ki jáaḿ arasiniibi nì awaa ŋin bi dū sindaariya nì animbil kúti nì tasa kúti ki mɔ́ɔ́yee, awaa ŋin sá ataŋkpee nì isufee. Tiwan gbanti kaa wāĺ, kaa gbìl̄, kaa ŋūǹ kí còom̀ mun.

eng: They didnt turn away from their murders, their spells and drugs, their sexual immorality, or their stealing.
local: Baa dū bi-nikpaakooŋu, nì bi-nyɔɔjil, nì bi-laakpaati nì bi-naayufii ki fá.

eng: Then locusts came forth from the smoke and onto the earth. They were given power like the power that scorpions have on the earth.
local: Ní inaakitoon nyánní ŋŋminyiimee poon ki kpátìi gbí kitiŋ pu, ní Unimbɔti tīī-i mpɔn í nīn júntí binib án nīn wàlifī kii inoomee.

eng: They were told not to hurt the grass of the earth or any green plant or any tree. They could only hurt the people who didnt have the seal of God on their foreheads.
local: U nìn tùkū-i yii í taa jú kumoou, kí taa jú nsuwaamu, kí taa jú isukpaan, ama í nīn pée júntí binib bin kaa cáá Unimbɔti daaŋku bi-yigbeeŋi puee.

eng: The locusts werent allowed to kill them, but only to make them suffer for five months—and the suffering they inflict is like that of a scorpion when it strikes a person.
local: ní ki bí-i yii í taa nín̄ júntí ki kù binib, ama í nīn júntí-bi bi yáliŋkí áli iŋmal iŋmoo. I-looti weeŋyi sá kii unoom yaa jú unil an nín wù u yáliŋkí puee ní.

eng: In those days people will seek death, but they wont find it. They will want to die, but death will run away from them.
local: Iwiin gbantee, binib dāa nyàab̀ ŋkúm ń dāá kpɔ̀-bi, ama baa làá dāá ká-mì. Ŋkúm ní bi dāa néeń, ama ní ŋkúm ń dāá sāǹ kí wàatī-bi.

eng: The locusts looked like horses ready for battle. On their heads were what seemed to be gold crowns. Their faces were like human faces.
local: Inaakitoonee bimbim náań nì kii itaamu in bi bɔ́ntì i cá kujaauee ní, ní i cáá tiba i-yiliŋ pu an náań nì sindaariya beefuuliiki, ní i-nimbiin náań nì binib nimbiin.

eng: their hair was like womens hair, and their teeth were like lions teeth.
local: ní i-yikpiti fɔ̀kɔ̀ kii binimpoobi yikpitee ní i-nyiŋŋi náań nì gbikinti nyiŋŋi.

eng: In front they had what seemed to be iron armor upon their chests, and the sound of their wings was like the sound of many chariots and horses racing into battle.
local: Ní i cáá ajanjabɔkuti i-biŋŋi pu an náań nì tikúti. I yaa ŋàaǹee, i-feeŋyi bintindi bí kii itaamu kupaau nín dátí tɔrɔkub ki sáń ki jáà kujaau puee bintindi ní.

eng: From Paul, a slave of Christ Jesus, called to be an apostle and set apart for Gods good news.
local: Man Pɔɔl un sá Yeesu Kriisitoo tɔntɔnliu, Unimbɔti yíiń-mi ki gāntì ḿ nīn sá Yeesu kpambal kí sòoǹ u-bɔmɔntiitee di jáaḿ-ni.

eng: in all my prayers. Im always asking that somehow, by Gods will, I might succeed in visiting you at last.
local: Baabadal m-wimmeem poonee, m bàlifī Unimbɔti yii an yaa sá u-lammi, ú pɔ̄-mi nsan ŋyunti nee ḿ dāan̄ kí màntì ni-pu.

eng: I really want to see you to pass along some spiritual gift to you so that you can be strengthened.
local: Di sá yii m-nimbiliŋ máǹ ḿ dāan̄ ni-cee kí cáań-ni Circir Fam pɔ̄kaati tiba tin ŋūǹ kí kūtì-ni mpɔnee.

eng: What I mean is that we can mutually encourage each other while I am with you. We can be encouraged by the faithfulness we find in each other, both your faithfulness and mine.
local: M nín làá lì puee di sá tí ŋmā kí dū ti-fookiiu un ti-kɔkɔ cáá Unimbɔti puee kí tīī tɔb kunyannyaau.

eng: I want you to know, brothers and sisters, that I planned to visit you many times, although I have been prevented from coming until now. I want to harvest some fruit among you, just as I have done among the other Gentiles.
local: Nnabiyaamu, m là ní béé yii m mākafì miŋa nì miŋa kí bàà dāan̄ ni-cee, ama nìn díǹ maa ká nsan. M bàà là yii m-tundi ń lòoǹ abil ni-cee kii dì nín lòon̄ atimbun ŋin kíǹee cee puee.

eng: I have a responsibility both to Greeks and to those who dont speak Greek, both to the wise and to the foolish.
local: An sá-mi dipɔndi ní ḿ sòoǹ Yeesu bɔti kí tùkù báà ŋma, bin nimbiliŋ ŋúbitèe nì bin nimbiliŋ kaa ŋúbitèe, bin nyí iciinee nì bin kaa nyí tibee.

eng: Thats why Im ready to preach the gospel also to you who are in Rome.
local: Míǹ pu ní m-nimbiliŋ máǹ ḿ dāan̄ kí sòoǹ Tibɔmɔntiil gbanti kí tùkù nimbi bin bí Roomee mun.

eng: Im not ashamed of the gospel: it is Gods own power for salvation to all who have faith in God, to the Jew first and also to the Greek.
local: An kaa cáá-mi icoo ḿ sòoǹ Tibɔmɔntiil gbanti, kun puee tì sá Unimbɔti pɔŋŋu ki fìil̄ bin fòol̄-tì ki kíikée. Mpeepeekaamee, Juuda yab, ní boonee ki dāa nín kpáaǹ bin kaa sá Juuda yabee mun.

eng: Gods righteousness is being revealed in the gospel, from faithfulness for faith, as it is written, The righteous person will live by faith. .
local: Tibɔtee dàkà-ti nsan ŋun Unimbɔti dì ki kpántí unil deedeedaan u-nimbiinee: an sá unil ń fōō kí kíí ní. An kífí nì fookii ní kí tin kúntí nì fookii, kii an nín ŋmàn̄ Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon puee yii: «Deedeedaan kāntí dimaŋfal u-fookiiu pu ní.».

eng: Gods wrath is being revealed from heaven against all the ungodly behavior and the injustice of human beings who silence the truth with injustice.
local: Di sá yii doooo yilpu ní Unimbɔti dàkà u-ŋuul nnibiyaamu bimbikpitim pu. Baa tɔ́ úǹ Unimbɔti ní ki ŋáań ikpiti. Bi-tuŋkpitiŋ kaa yàntī ibaamɔn ń tɔ̄ i-tundi bi-ni.

eng: This is because what is known about God should be plain to them because God made it plain to them.
local: Kun pu ní Unimbɔti làá dáaĺ bi-tafalee di sá bi wál̀ kun unil ŋūǹ kí ká kí béé Unimbɔtee. An sá Unimbɔti di nyántì ki dàkā-bi.

eng: God promised this good news about his Son ahead of time through his prophets in the holy scriptures. His Son was descended from David.
local: Dooooyee ní Unimbɔti nìn dī u-bɔnaatiliibi pu ki pōoǹ-ti u-bɔmɔntiiti gbanti ki ŋá u-gbɔŋku poon ki síiǹ.

eng: Ever since the creation of the world, Gods invisible qualities—Gods eternal power and divine nature—have been clearly seen, because they are understood through the things God has made. So humans are without excuse.
local: Di sá yii doooo Unimbɔti nín náań yilpu nì taapuee, atuun ŋin u túńee ŋūǹ kí yàntī unil ń béé u-ban Unimbɔti bimbim min baa wāĺee, an dàkà yii u-pɔŋŋu ŋun kaa kúntée nì u-nimbɔtiiti. Mimmee unil kaa ŋūǹ kí ká nnaakpal yii waa nyí-u.

eng: Although they knew God, they didnt honor God as God or thank him. Instead, their reasoning became pointless, and their foolish hearts were darkened.
local: Bi nyí Unimbɔti, ní kaa pàkà-u, kaa jáaḿ-u u-ŋanti pu min máaǹ bí jāam̀-uee. Bi fá-u, ki nóó bi-landɔkɔi in kaa bí deedeeyee, ki kpántì bigbaambi, ní bi-pɔbiliŋ kɔ́ dibɔmbɔndin.

eng: While they were claiming to be wise, they made fools of themselves.
local: Bi bí ki gà bi-biŋŋi yii bi cáá iciin, kaa nyí yii bi wáatì ní.

eng: They exchanged the glory of the immortal God for images that look like mortal humans: birds, animals, and reptiles.
local: An nín bàà sá bí nyɔ̄ŋkì Unimbɔti tinyooldaan un kaa kúùyee puee, ní bi nyɔ́ŋkí tiwan tin bi kpítì ki nántiǹ unil un kúùyee nì inoon nì tiwaŋkuti nì tin fù kitiŋee mun.

eng: So God abandoned them to their hearts desires, which led to the moral corruption of degrading their own bodies with each other.
local: Kii bi-pɔbiliŋ poon bi nín lēē kí nīn ŋáań tijɔkɔndee puee, Unimbɔti dū-bi ki líiǹ tijɔkɔndi, bí nīn bíiĺ bi-ba.

eng: They traded Gods truth for a lie, and they worshipped and served the creation instead of the creator, who is blessed forever. Amen.
local: Bi dū Unimbɔti bɔti tin sá ibaamɔnee ki fá, ki yōoǹ inyimɔn, ní ki nóó ki tòotī tiwan tin Unimbɔti náańee, ní ki fá unáanli u-ba, úǹ un máaǹ bí nīn nyɔ́ŋkí-u baabadal kí nīn cée! Ameeni.

eng: Thats why God abandoned them to degrading lust. Their females traded natural sexual relations for unnatural sexual relations.
local: Míǹ pu ní Unimbɔti dū-bi ki líiǹ kukpeeu kun kɔ̄ntí-bi icooyee. Di sá yii an nín bàà sá binimpoobi ń mɔ̄ǹ bininjabee, ní bi fàtīi cáa mɔ́ń bi-nimpujutiibi.

eng: Also, in the same way, the males traded natural sexual relations with females, and burned with lust for each other. Males performed shameful actions with males, and they were paid back with the penalty they deserved for their mistake in their own bodies.
local: Míǹ mbaantiim, bininjab kaa ti là binimpoobi, ní ki kíǹ ki là tɔb ŋmaniŋma tikpil ki dɔ̀ nì tɔb. Bi-tuuŋŋi gbanti cáań ditafadaaldi din sá bi-paati tin máaǹ-bee ki tɔ́ bi-pu.

eng: Since they didnt think it was worthwhile to acknowledge God, God abandoned them to a defective mind to do inappropriate things.
local: Kii bi-landɔkɔi nín sá yii Unimbɔti kaa máaǹ bí béé-uee puee, Unimbɔti dū-bi ki líì, ní bi-landɔkɔi gátì, ní bi kíǹ ki ŋáań min kaa máaǹ bí ŋée.

eng: So they were filled with all injustice, wicked behavior, greed, and evil behavior. They are full of jealousy, murder, fighting, deception, and malice. They are gossips.
local: Bi kpántì binikpitib mbaamɔm, ki ŋáań min kaa ŋān̄ee, ki cáá nnimbitaataam nì ntoom, ki cáá kináŋ nì nnikpaakoon tikpil, ki néeń kujaau, ki ŋmàtíǹ tɔb, ki kíiĺ tɔb, ki sòoǹ ki bíiĺ tɔb.

eng: God promised this good news about his Son ahead of time through his prophets in the holy scriptures. His Son was descended from David.
local: U-Jipɔɔn, ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo pu ní tibɔtee sòoǹ. Ti yaa làá kpèè tinitanee, u nyánní ubɔti Daafiidi maaliŋu poon ní.

eng: they slander people, and they hate God. They are rude and proud, and they brag. They invent ways to be evil, and they are disobedient to their parents.
local: ki sìí tɔb. Bi sá Unimbɔti nannanliibi, ki kpèé ki sɔ́kɔ̀ binib, ki yòontí bi-ba yilpu, ki dàkà bi-ba, ki nāantí isampɔɔn in pu bi dì ki ŋáań ikpitee, ní kaa tɔ́ bi-nab nì bi-baabi.

eng: They are without understanding, disloyal, without affection, and without mercy.
local: Baa cáá iciin. Bi yaa pōoǹee, baa ŋáań tin bi pōoǹee. Baa cáá dineendi, kaa sūǹ bin kíǹee saai.

eng: Though they know Gods decision that those who persist in such practices deserve death, they not only keep doing these things but also approve others who practice them.
local: Nì min kamaa bi baasii nyí tiŋan yii Unimbɔti bí yii bin bin ŋáań ikpiti gbantee máaǹ ŋkúmee, bi gbíntì ki ŋáań ikpiti gbanti. Tin gíti lákatèe di sá bi bí yii bin ŋáań ikpiti gbantee di ŋáań min kítèe.

eng: He was publicly identified as Gods Son with power through his resurrection from the dead, which was based on the Spirit of holiness. This Son is Jesus Christ our Lord.
local: Ti yaa làá tí kpèè u-Fam min sá circiree jandi púee, Unimbɔti nín fīkitì-u ditaŋkpiilinee, u dàkā nì mpɔn yii u sá u-Jipɔɔn.

eng: Through him we have received Gods grace and our appointment to be apostles. This was to bring all Gentiles to faithful obedience for his names sake.
local: U-pu ní m ká diyimɔɔl kí nīn sá u-kpambal kí tīī Kriisitoo tinyool, án nín làá ŋá pu atimbun kɔkɔ nib ń fōō-u kí kíí kí nīn tɔ́-uee.

eng: You who are called by Jesus Christ are also included among these Gentiles.
local: Nimbi Room yab bin Unimbɔti yíiń ní nīn kíǹ ki sá Yeesu Kriisitoo yabee mun kútì poon.

eng: To those in Rome who are dearly loved by God and called to be Gods people. Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Nimbi ní m ŋmàn̄ kugbɔŋu nee, nimbi bin kɔkɔ Unimbɔti néeń ki yíiń ní nīn sá u-yabee. Ti-Baa Unimbɔti nì ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo ń ŋá-ni tiŋan kí tīī-ni ŋgbansɔŋfi!.

eng: First of all, I thank my God through Jesus Christ for all of you, because the news about your faithfulness is being spread throughout the whole world.
local: Mpeekaaminee, m dī Yeesu Kriisitoo pu ki jáaḿ m-Nimbɔtiu ni-kɔkɔ pu, kun puee bi sòoǹ ni nín fōō Unimbɔti bɔti ki kíí puee ilaan kɔkɔ.

eng: I serve God in my spirit by preaching the good news about Gods Son, and God is my witness that I continually mention you.
local: M téetí ni-pu múlmul m-wimmeem poon. Unimbɔti un m nóó nì m-pɔbil kɔkɔ ki sòoǹ u-Jipɔɔn bɔmɔntiitee nyí yii tin m lèe sá ibaamɔn.

eng: Brothers and sisters, my hearts desire is for Israels salvation. Thats my prayer to God for them.
local: Nnabiyaamu, m-pɔbil poon nín là miŋyee nì tin tin m mèé Unimbɔti Isirayeel yab puee di sá bí ká difiil.

eng: Trusting with the heart leads to righteousness, and confessing with the mouth leads to salvation.
local: Di sá yii un kamaa yaa fōō Yeesu ki kíí u-pɔbil poonee kpántí deedeedaan u-nimbiin, ní un yaa lī u-nyɔkɔn yii u fōō-u ki kííyee, udaan kāntí difiil.

eng: The scripture says, All who have faith in him wont be put to shame .
local: kii Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon nín lī puee yii: «Un kamaa yaa fōō ti-Dindaan ki kííyee, icoo kaa jīǹ udaan.».

eng: There is no distinction between Jew and Greek, because the same Lord is Lord of all, who gives richly to all who call on him.
local: Mimmee, digàntil kaa bí un sá Juuda yɔuee nì un kaa sá Juuda yɔuee kansikin. Bi-kɔkɔ Dindaan sá ubaanti ní. Úǹ ŋáań bin kɔkɔ yíì u-yindi ú fīī-bee tiŋan tikpil.

eng: All who call on the Lords name will be saved. .
local: kii an nín ŋmàn̄ puee yii: «Un kamaa yaa yíiń ti-Dindaan yindee kāntí difiil.».

eng: So how can they call on someone they dont have faith in? And how can they have faith in someone they havent heard of? And how can they hear without a preacher?.
local: Ama, bi yaa kaa fōō-u ki kíí, bi làá ŋá mana kí yíiń-u? Bi yaa kaa gbìl̄ u-pu, mana ní bi làá fōō-u kí kíí? Ubɔ yaa kaa bíi sòoǹ u-pu, mana ní bi làá gbìl̄?.

eng: And how can they preach unless they are sent? As it is written, How beautiful are the feet of those who announce the good news. .
local: Unimbɔti yaa kaa túnní binib, mana ní bi làá sòoǹ-tì? Kii an nín ŋmàn̄ puee yii: «Bin sòoǹ Tibɔmɔntiilee nín dòoń bi-taaŋi ŋānní puee.».

eng: But everyone hasnt obeyed the good news. As Isaiah says, Lord, who has had faith in our message? .
local: Ama bi-kɔkɔ kaa fōō tibɔmɔntiilee ki kíí, kii Yeesaya nín lī puee yii: «Ti-Dindaan, an kaa sá binib fiii di fōō ti-bɔti ki kííii?».

eng: So, faith comes from listening, but its listening by means of Christs message.
local: Mimmee bi gbìl̀ ní ki nín fōō ki kíí, ní tin bi gbìl̄ ki fōō ki kííyee sá Kriisitoo bɔti.

eng: But I ask you, didnt they hear it? Definitely! Their voice has gone out into the entire earth, and their message has gone out to the corners of the inhabited world. .
local: Míǹ puee, Isirayeel yab kaa gbìl̄ tibɔmɔntiilee naaa? Bi gbìl̄-tì. An nóó nì min an ŋmàn̄ee dee, yii: «Kitiŋ kɔkɔ gbìl̄ bi-neeŋyi pu, ní tibɔti tin bi sòoǹee bàn̄ kitiŋgbeennyɔkɔ.».

eng: But I ask you again, didnt Israel understand? First, Moses says, I will make you jealous of those who arent a people, of a people without understanding. .
local: Mimmee, Isirayeel yab kaa gbìl̄ tibɔtee taapu naaa? Dooooyee ní Mooyiisi nìn pée sòon̄ bi-pu yii: «Man Unimbɔti làá yàntī binib bin kaa bí tibanee ń nīn jīǹ mmɔn, ní án nīn wù nimbi, kí yàntī ní fōō diŋuul binib bin kaa nyí iciinee pu.».

eng: I can vouch for them: they are enthusiastic about God. However, it isnt informed by knowledge.
local: M ká bi-pɔ́ɔǹtam dinimbɔtinoolin, ama, bi-pɔ́ɔǹtam gbantee, baa béè ki dóò.

eng: And Isaiah even dares to say, I was found by those who didnt look for me; I revealed myself to those who didnt ask for me. .
local: Ní Yeesaya mun sòon̄ ki cántì yii: «Bin bin kaa nìn nyàab̀ man Unimbɔtee ká-mi, ní m cáá m-ba ki dàkā bin kaa bàlifī m-puee.».

eng: But he says about Israel, All day long I stretched out my hands to a disobedient and contrary people. .
local: Ama tin u sòon̄ Isirayeel yab puee sèé: «M tāntì m-ŋalii kutaafaau áli kujoou ki yíìń bin kaa tɔ́-mi, bi-yiliŋ pɔ̄ɔ̄yee.».

eng: They dont submit to Gods righteousness because they dont understand his righteousness, and they try to establish their own righteousness.
local: Di sá yii baa béè min ní Unimbɔti dì ki kpántí binib deedeedambi u-nimbiinee, ní ki bíi nyàab̀ bi-ba saŋŋu ŋun bi làá dī kí kpántí deedeedambee. Bi yíì, kaa sīntì bi-ba taapu Unimbɔti ń kpántí-bi deedeedambi.

eng: Christ is the goal of the Law, which leads to righteousness for all who have faith in God.
local: Kriisitoo di dɔ́miń ki kúntì marab dītam. Kɔŋkɔnnee, bin kpántì deedeedambee di sá bin kɔkɔ fōō Kriisitoo ki kííyee.

eng: Moses writes about the righteousness that comes from the Law: The person who does these things will live by them. .
local: Mooyiisi nìn ŋmàn̄ marab dītam nín cāabìń deedee uŋyee yii: «Un yaa nóó marab deedeeyee kāntí difiil bi-pu.».

eng: But the righteousness that comes from faith talks like this: Dont say in your heart , Who will go up into heaven? .
local: Ama deedee un nyēeń fookiinee nín sá puee sɔ̀. Unimbɔti gbɔŋku sòoǹ yii: «A-pɔbil poonee, taa lì yii, ŋma di ŋūǹ kí jɔ̄m̀ yilpu?».

eng: or Who will go down into the region below? .
local: kí taa tí lì yii: «Ŋma di ŋūǹ kí sìntī taapu ditaŋkpiilin?».

eng: But what does it say? The word is near you, in your mouth and in your heart .
local: Mimmee, tí lì yii ba? Unimbɔti gbɔŋku lī yii: «Dinyɔɔbundi gbanti bí ki kɔ́ĺ-si, ki bí a-nyɔkɔn nì a-pɔbilin.» Dinyɔɔbundi gbanti di sá fookii bɔti tin ti sòoǹee, yii.

eng: Because if you confess with your mouth Jesus is Lord and in your heart you have faith that God raised him from the dead, you will be saved.
local: A yaa lī binib cee yii Yeesu sá a-Dindaan ki yaa fōō ki kíí a-pɔbilin yii Unimbɔti fīkitì-u ditaŋkpiilinee, a làá ká difiil.

eng: So I ask you, has God rejected his people? Absolutely not! Im an Israelite, a descendant of Abraham, from the tribe of Benjamin.
local: Mimmee, Unimbɔti fá Isirayeel yab naaa? Aayee, kpataaa! Kun puee man m-ba sá Isirayeel ní, ki sá Abraam naantiiu, ní ki sá Beenjameen naakookun.

eng: Their eyes should be darkened so they cant see, and their backs always bent. .
local: bi-nimbiliŋ ń jɔ̀b̄ kí taa nín̄ wāĺ! Yàntī bí nīn pée bōō baabadal.».

eng: So Im asking you: They havent stumbled so that theyve fallen permanently, have they? Absolutely not! But salvation has come to the Gentiles by their failure, in order to make Israel jealous.
local: Ḿ bàlifì tibɔti nee: Mimmee, Juuda yab nín gbéetèe, bi lítì kɔkɔɔɔ? Aayee, kpataaa! Ama bi-lítitamee di yāntì atimbun ŋin kɔkɔ kíǹee ká difiil, án nín làá ŋá pu bi yaa ká bin béeyee, bi mun ń nyàab̀ee.

eng: But if their failure brings riches to the world, and their defeat brings riches to the Gentiles, how much more will come from the completion of their number!.
local: Bi-yìntitam yaa cáań kitiŋ kɔkɔ nib tiwammɔntiilee, ní bi nín wáŋkì puee yaa cáań bin kaa sá Juuda yabee tiwammɔntiilee, kin Juuda yab kɔkɔ yaa fātìń Unimbɔti ceeyee, kun an làá cáańee làá nīn bí mana dee?.

eng: Im speaking to you Gentiles. Considering that Im an apostle to the Gentiles, I publicize my own ministry.
local: Kɔŋkɔnnee, m sòoǹ nì nimbi bin kaa sá Juuda yabee ní. Man sá Yeesu kpambal din u tú bin kaa sá Juuda yabee ceeyee ní, ní an mɔ̄ǹ-mi ditundi din u tīī-mee pu.

eng: in the hope that somehow I might make my own people jealous and save some of them.
local: M-landɔkɔŋu sá yii ḿ téé m-nabiyaamu Juuda yab bí nyàab̀ kí ká kun bin béeyee cááyee, ḿ ŋmā kí fīī bi-kansikin biba.

eng: If their rejection has brought about a close relationship between God and the world, how can their acceptance mean anything less than life from the dead?.
local: Unimbɔti nín yíì Juuda yab bí taa nín̄ gíti sá u-yabee di ŋáŋkì binib bin kɔkɔ kíǹee nì Unimbɔti kansikin. Mimmee, u yaa láa fātìi fōō Juuda yabee, án làá dāá nīn bí mana dee? An dāa sá bin nìn kpíìyee dimaŋfal ní.

eng: But if part of a batch of dough is offered to God as holy, the whole batch of dough is holy too. If a root is holy, the branches will be holy too.
local: Bi yaa nìn cáań tijincɔ̀fikaal ki tīī Unimbɔtee, tin kíǹee muee, úǹ di yì. Unimbɔti di yaa yì busubu nyaaŋyee, úǹ di yì asupan mun.

eng: If some of the branches were broken off, and you were a wild olive branch, and you were grafted in among the other branches and shared the root that produces the rich oil of the olive tree.
local: Bi yaa gāā ɔliifi bumɔŋkisubu paŋŋi aba ní ki gāań sii kumooun subu pandi ki cáań ki túkuǹ ŋin béeyee paaŋyi pu, ní a kíǹ ki fòol̄ busumɔntiibuee nyaaŋyi kpiimee.

eng: then dont brag like youre better than the other branches. If you do brag, be careful: its not you that sustains the root, but its the root that sustains you.
local: sii ń taa nín̄ lámintí ki dàkà apan ŋin bi gāā ki fée. A yaa bíi lámintée, á béé yii an kaa sá sii di tú inyaan, inyaan di tú sii.

eng: You will say then, Branches were broken off so that I could be grafted in.
local: A ŋūǹ kí lì yii: «Bi gāā apanee ki fá ní ki cáań man ki túkuǹ.».

eng: God hasnt rejected his people, whom he knew in advance. Or dont you know what the scripture says in the case of Elijah, when he pleads with God against Israel?.
local: Unimbɔti kaa fá u-nikitil din pu u pée nyí dooooyee. Naa kalin nyí Unimbɔti gbɔŋku nín sòon̄ pu dijandi din sòoǹ Eelii nín nìn bíi kɔ́kɔĺ Isirayeel yab Unimbɔti nimbiin pueeee? U bí yii.

eng: Fine. They were broken off because they werent faithful, but you stand only by your faithfulness. So dont think in a proud way; instead be afraid.
local: Tiŋman bi lafun gāā-ŋi ki fá, kun puee baa cáá fookii ní sii fookiiu puee ní a túkú niin. Míǹ puee, á fàŋkì kí taa nín̄ lámintí.

eng: If God didnt spare the natural branches, he wont spare you either.
local: Di sá yii Unimbɔti yaa kaa tāaǹ bumɔŋkisubu paŋŋee, á nīn nyí yii waa làá dāá tàan̄ si mun.

eng: So look at Gods kindness and harshness. Its harshness toward those who fell, but its Gods kindness for you, provided you continue in his kindness; otherwise, you could be cut off too.
local: Nín̄ nyí Unimbɔti ŋanti nì u-tafadaaldi pu! U dáaĺ bin bin lítèe tafal, ní ki ŋá sii tiŋan. Á nīn gbíntìi bí u-ŋanti gbantin, an yaa kaa sá míǹ, see ú dāá gāā-si kí fá.

eng: And even those who were cut off will be grafted back in if they dont continue to be unfaithful, because God is able to graft them in again.
local: Bíǹ yaa kpántì bi-bimbim ki fōō Yeesu ki kííyee, u ŋūǹ kí fàtīí gíiǹní-bi kí túkuń laakin u nìn gāā-bee. Di sá yii Unimbɔti cáá mpɔn kí gíiǹ-bi kí túkuń mpɔm.

eng: If you were naturally part of a wild olive tree and you were cut off from it, and then, contrary to nature, you were grafted into the cultivated olive tree, wont these natural branches stand an even better chance of being grafted back onto their own olive tree?.
local: Kpèè, sii kumooun supandi din bi gāāyee, bi cáań-si ki túkuǹ bumɔŋkisubu bun kaa sá a-suyilee pu. Mimmee, an kaa pɔ̄ɔ̄ bí gíiǹní asupan ŋin bi gāāyee kí túkuń disuyil din pu ŋi nìn bí, bi gāā-ŋee.

eng: I dont want you to be unaware of this secret, brothers and sisters. That way you wont think too highly of yourselves. A part of Israel has become resistant until the full number of the Gentiles comes in.
local: M-nabiyaamu, m là ní béé ibaamɔn iba i bí dibalin, án nín làá ŋá pu ní taa nín̄ yíì ni-ba yii biciŋfitiibee. Di sá yii Isirayeel yab dijandi kaa ŋūǹ kí gbìl̄ tibɔti nee taapu, kí dāá sāā ŋyunti ŋun ntimu mun kɔkɔ kíǹee yab ń dāá kɔ́ difiilinee.

eng: In this way, all Israel will be saved, as it is written: The deliverer will come from Zion. He will remove ungodly behavior from Jacob. .
local: Min ní Isirayeel yab kɔkɔ làá ká difiilee dee, kii an nín pée ŋmàn̄ puee yii: «Ufiil làá nyānní Siiyɔn ní kí lēē ikpiti in bí Jaakɔb yab kansikinee kí fá.

eng: This is my covenant with them, when I take away their sins .
local: Man Unimbɔti yaa láa lēē bi-kpitii ki fée, an di dāa sá dipoondi din m pōoǹ-bee.».

eng: According to the gospel, they are enemies for your sake, but according to Gods choice, they are loved for the sake of their ancestors.
local: A yaa kpēē bi nín fá Tibɔmɔntiil puee, bi kpántì Unimbɔti nannanliibi nimbi ń ká difiil. A yaa kpēē Unimbɔti nín lútì-bi puee, bi sá bin u néeńee bi-naanjab pu.

eng: Gods gifts and calling cant be taken back.
local: Di sá yii Unimbɔti yaa pú ubɔ diyimɔɔl diba, àá u yaa tí yíiń ubɔee, waa kpántí u-landɔkɔŋu.

eng: Lord, they have killed your prophets, and they have torn down your altars. Im the only one left, and they are trying to take my life. .
local: «Ti-Dindaan, bi kūū a-bɔnaatiliibi ki wīī a-saraafaŋyi, an pée kíǹ man baba ní, ní bi nyàab̀ kí kpɔ̀ ma mun!».

eng: Once you were disobedient to God, but now you have mercy because they were disobedient.
local: Dooooyee, naa nìn tɔ́ Unimbɔti, ama, Juuda yab nín nìn yíì, kaa fōō u-bɔti puee ní u súń ni-saai kɔŋkɔnnee ki fīī-ni.

eng: In the same way, they have also been disobedient because of the mercy that you received, so now they can receive mercy too.
local: Míǹ mbaantiim, Juuda yab kaa tɔ́ Unimbɔti kɔŋkɔnnee ní Unimbɔti súń ni-saai, ama an sá bi mun ń ŋmā kí ká kɔŋkɔnnee Unimbɔti saasɔl ní.

eng: God has locked up all people in disobedience, in order to have mercy on all of them.
local: Mimmee, Unimbɔti yāntì binib kɔkɔ gbíntì bi-yìntitam poon, ú ŋmā kí dàkà bi-kɔkɔ u nín sūǹ binib saai puee dee.

eng: Gods riches, wisdom, and knowledge are so deep! They are as mysterious as his judgments, and they are as hard to track as his paths!.
local: Óo, Unimbɔti ciiŋyi nì u-nyítam nì u-ŋanti wīikéeee, an kaa cáá bi lì! U nín ŋáań u-bɔti miŋyee, unil kaa ŋūǹ kí béé, ní min min u lēē ki làá ŋée, unil kaa ŋūǹ kí gbìl̄ taapu.

eng: Who has known the Lords mind? Or who has been his mentor? .
local: kun puee, kii Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon nín lī puee yii: «Ŋma di ŋūǹ kí béé ti-Dindaan landɔkɔŋu? Ŋma di ŋūǹ kí sāŋkì-u mun?.

eng: Or who has given him a gift and has been paid back by him? .
local: Ŋma di péé ki tīī-u tiba ní an sá yii ú fàtīí téeǹ udaan?».

eng: All things are from him and through him and for him. May the glory be to him forever. Amen.
local: Úǹ di náań tikɔkɔ, úǹ di ŋúb́ tikɔkɔ, ní an kɔkɔ bí ki tīī-u. Unimbɔti gbanti ń ká tinyool kí nīn cá jaanjaan! Ameeni.

eng: But what is Gods reply to him? I have kept for myself seven thousand people who havent bowed their knees to Baal. .
local: Unimbɔti nìn kíí-u yii ba ní? U nìn bí yii: «M kpáafì binib kutukub biluli, bi sá m-yab. Bidambi tabiŋyiliŋ kaa gbāaǹ diwaal Baal nimbiin.».

eng: So also in the present time there is a remaining group by the choice of Gods grace.
local: Míǹ mbaantiim, ti-yuntiŋu nee, an kíǹ binib fiii bin Unimbɔti ŋá tiŋan ki lútì-bee.

eng: But if it is by grace, it isnt by whats done anymore. If it were, Gods grace wouldnt be grace.
local: U ŋá bidambi tiŋan ní ki lútì-bi, an kaa sá bi-tunnyaaŋku pu. An yaan sá bi-tunnyaaŋku puee, mimmee Unimbɔti kaa ti ŋá-bi tiŋan dee.

eng: So what? Israel didnt find what it was looking for. Those who were chosen found it, but the others were resistant.
local: Tibɔti nee taapu sá yii ba dee? Isirayeel yab nín nyàab̀ kuŋyee, baa ká kudɔŋu, see bin Unimbɔti lútèe baba di ká kudɔŋu, ní u yāntì bin kíǹee kaa ŋūǹ kí gbìl̄ taapu.

eng: As it is written, God gave them a dull spirit, so that their eyes would not see and their ears not hear, right up until the present day. .
local: kii an nín ŋmàn̄ puee yii: «Unimbɔti tātì bi-lammi, ki yāntì bi-nimbiliŋ kaa wāĺ, bi-tafaliŋ kpáá, áli ki dāa sāā díǹ.».

eng: And David says, Their table should become a pitfall and a trap, a stumbling block and payback to them for what they have done. .
local: Daafiidi mun lī yii: «Bi-jinti ń kpántí-bi kutaliŋŋu, kí nīn sá-bi kulaaŋŋu, bí lítí ikpitin kí ká ditafadaaldi din máaǹee.

eng: So, brothers and sisters, because of Gods mercies, I encourage you to present your bodies as a living sacrifice that is holy and pleasing to God. This is your appropriate priestly service.
local: Nnabiyaamu, kii Unimbɔti nín dàkā-ti u-saasɔl puee, m mèé-ni yii ní dū ni-wunti kí tīī Unimbɔti kii saraa un bí circir kaa kpíì, ki fáatì-uee. An di làá nīn sá-ni dinimbɔtinoobaamɔndi din máaǹ ní nóóyee.

eng: Love each other like the members of your family. Be the best at showing honor to each other.
local: Nín̄ néeḿmaan tɔb tiŋan kii nnabiyaamu nín néeń tɔb puee, kí nīn sá bipeekaabi ki tɔ́ bin kíǹee.

eng: Dont hesitate to be enthusiastic—be on fire in the Spirit as you serve the Lord!.
local: Ní nīn tūm̀maan ditundi nì difaŋgiil kí taa nín̄ sá biwutijoodambi, kí nīn pɔ̄ɔntí ki tūm̀maan ti-Dindaan tundi nì ni-pɔbiliŋ kɔkɔ.

eng: Be happy in your hope, stand your ground when youre in trouble, and devote yourselves to prayer.
local: Án nīn mɔ̄ǹ-ni dimákal din ni cááyee pu, kí jīmaan kimɔtii falaa poon, kí nīn mèé múlmul.

eng: Contribute to the needs of Gods people, and welcome strangers into your home.
local: Tūtum̀maan biwɔfib bin nóó Unimbɔtee, kí nīn fòol̄ binicambi bin kóò ni-puee nì mmɔn.

eng: Bless people who harass you—bless and dont curse them.
local: Mèèmaan yii Unimbɔti ń ŋá bin sàantí-nee tiŋan, ní nīn nyàab̀ bi-ŋanti kí taa nín̄ nyàab̀-bi tikpitil.

eng: Be happy with those who are happy, and cry with those who are crying.
local: Bin yaa bí dipɔɔpindinee, ní kūtì bi-pu, ni-kɔkɔ ń fàal̀maan. Bin yaa tí bíi sūǹee, ní kūtì bi mun pu, ni-kɔkɔ ń sɔ̄.

eng: Consider everyone as equal, and dont think that youre better than anyone else. Instead, associate with people who have no status. Dont think that youre so smart.
local: Nín̄ mɔ̄maan tɔb cee. Ní taa nín̄ sá bin néeń timɔnee. Nín̄ sìntīmaan ni-ba taapu, kí taa dàkafìmaan yii ni sá biciŋfitiibi.

eng: Dont pay back anyone for their evil actions with evil actions, but show respect for what everyone else believes is good.
local: Ubɔ yaa ŋá-ni tikpitilee, ní taa dū tikpitil kí téeǹ-u. Pɔ́ɔm̀maan kí ŋá min kítèe binib kɔkɔ nimbiin.

eng: If possible, to the best of your ability, live at peace with all people.
local: Ni yaa ŋūǹee, ní yàntī ŋgbansɔŋfi ń nīn bí ni nì báà ŋma kansikin, tiba ń taa nyānní ni-cee.

eng: Dont try to get revenge for yourselves, my dear friends, but leave room for Gods wrath. It is written, Revenge belongs to me; I will pay it back, says the Lord .
local: M-nabineeŋkaamu, ní taa tɔ̀maan ni-ba ipɔntoon. Yàntīmaan Unimbɔti ŋuul ń tɔ̄ di-tundi, kun puee an ŋmàn̄ yii: «Ti-Dindaan bí yii: Man di làá dāá tɔ̀ ipɔntoon, man di làá dāá pà bi-tuuŋŋi paati.

eng: Dont be conformed to the patterns of this world, but be transformed by the renewing of your minds so that you can figure out what Gods will is—what is good and pleasing and mature.
local: Taa dūmaan ni-ba kí nántíǹ waanee bimbim, ama ní yàntī Unimbɔti ń pɔ̄-ni ilandɔkɔpɔɔn kí kpántí ni-bimbim. Míǹ ní ni ŋūǹ kí béé min ní Unimbɔti lèe, min ŋān̄ ki fáatì-u ki sá mmɔntiimee.

eng: Instead, If your enemy is hungry, feed him; if he is thirsty, give him a drink. By doing this, you will pile burning coals of fire upon his head .
local: Ama, ŋkùm yaa cáá a-nannanliu, á tīī-u tijin ú jī. Nnyinnyoo yaa tí cáá-u, á tīī-u nnyim ú nyɔ̀. A yaa bíi ŋáań mimmee, an bí kii amɔsiŋkoon ní a cáa pāantí u-yil puee.».

eng: Dont be defeated by evil, but defeat evil with good.
local: Ní taa yàntī tikpitil ń pɔ̄ɔkiǹ-ni, nimbi dūmaan tiŋan kí pɔ̄ɔkiǹ tikpitil.

eng: Because of the grace that God gave me, I can say to each one of you: dont think of yourself more highly than you ought to think. Instead, be reasonable since God has measured out a portion of faith to each one of you.
local: Diyimɔɔl din Unimbɔti pú-mee puee ní m ŋūǹ kí tùkù ni-kɔkɔ yii ní taa nín̄ yòontí ni-ba yilpu min kaa máaǹee, ama nín̄ cáámaan ilandɔkɔ in kítì ki nóó nì fookii un Unimbɔti yākatì ki pú báà ŋmee.

eng: We have many parts in one body, but the parts dont all have the same function.
local: Kii tiwun nín sá tibaantiil ki cáá ajan tikpil, ŋi-tuuŋŋi kaa pēē puee.

eng: In the same way, though there are many of us, we are one body in Christ, and individually we belong to each other.
local: míǹ mbaantiim, ti nín wīikí min nee, Kriisitoo di kpáfì-ti tiwumbaantiil, ní báà ŋma túkú u-laŋki u-laŋki.

eng: We have different gifts that are consistent with Gods grace that has been given to us. If your gift is prophecy, you should prophesy in proportion to your faith.
local: Unimbɔti pú-ti diyimɔɔl ki yākatì báà ŋma u-pɔl, tí nīn cáa tūǹ atuun ŋin bí aba abee. Míǹ puee, sii un u-Fam tìì tibɔti a nàatēe, nín̄ nàatī án nīn nóó nì u nín tīī-si fookii uŋyee.

eng: If your gift is service, devote yourself to serving. If your gift is teaching, devote yourself to teaching.
local: Sii un u yaa tīī á nīn sá bin kíǹee tɔntɔnliuee, á nīn tūǹ ki tìì-bi. Sii un dàkèe, á dū a-ba kí tīī a-dàkatam.

eng: If your gift is encouragement, devote yourself to encouraging. The one giving should do it with no strings attached. The leader should lead with passion. The one showing mercy should be cheerful.
local: Sii un tìì binib kunyannyaauee, a mun ń dū a-ba kí tīiǹ kunyannyaau ditiil. Sii un pūǹ tiwanee, á nīn pūǹ nì dipɔbil diba. Sii un kpòotēe, á nīn kpòotī nì difaŋgiil. Sii un tútúǹ bifalaadambee, á tūtuǹ-bi nì dipɔɔpindi.

eng: Love should be shown without pretending. Hate evil, and hold on to what is good.
local: Ni-neendi ń taa nín̄ cáá mpoonnyoon. Ní nīn ná ikpiti, kí dū ni-ba kí nákáǹ tiŋan mmɔntiim.

eng: Every person should place themselves under the authority of the government. There isnt any authority unless it comes from God, and the authorities that are there have been put in place by God.
local: Báà ŋma ń nīn tɔ́ biyidambi bin cáá mpɔn ki kpòotī kitiŋee, kun puee uyidaan ubɔ kaa bí Unimbɔti kaa kāaǹ-u. Unimbɔti di yāntì ní biyidambi kɔkɔ bí.

eng: Love doesnt do anything wrong to a neighbor; therefore, love is what fulfills the Law.
local: A yaa néeń a-jutee, aa ŋáań-u tikpitil. A yaa néeń binibee, a bíi ŋáań min mara lēe kɔkɔ dee.

eng: As you do all this, you know what time it is. The hour has already come for you to wake up from your sleep. Now our salvation is nearer than when we first had faith.
local: Míǹ puee, ní nīn néeḿmaan tɔb míǹ, kun puee ni nyí ŋyunti ŋun poon ti bí nee nín sá ŋuŋyee. Ŋyunti ŋun ti máaǹ kí fìntī ŋgeeminee bāań, di sá yii Yeesu gítintam sútì ki pìlīń-ti ki jítiǹ min min ti nìn péé péé ki fōō ki kííyee.

eng: The night is almost over, and the day is near. So lets get rid of the actions that belong to the darkness and put on the weapons of light.
local: Kunyeeu mɔ́ ki síitì, kutaa tóob́ diwuntil. Mimmee, tí fá dibɔmbɔndi poon tuuŋŋi ní kí dū ŋwalifin janjawanti kí pèen̄.

eng: Lets behave appropriately as people who live in the day, not in partying and getting drunk, not in sleeping around and obscene behavior, not in fighting and obsession.
local: Tí còom̀maan min kítèe, kii bi nín còom̄ ŋwiimpu puee, kí taa jī kí lákatí ti-ba pu, kí taa gbí ndaam, kí taa ŋá tilaakpaal, kí taa ŋá tijɔkɔndi, kí taa nín̄ jáà, kí taa nín̄ cáá tɔb pu kudɔ̀ŋu.

eng: Instead, dress yourself with the Lord Jesus Christ, and dont plan to indulge your selfish desires.
local: Ní dūmaan ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo bimbim kí pèen̄ kii tijanjawanee, kí taa fàtīí tɔ̄ ni-bimbikpitim kí ŋá mì nín là puee.

eng: So anyone who opposes the authority is standing against what God has established. People who take this kind of stand will get punished.
local: Míǹ puee, un yaa yíì biyidambee jáaǹ mara un Unimbɔti yóoǹee dee. Bin yíití biyidambee dátiń ditafadaaldi ki tòó bi-ba pu ní.

eng: The authorities dont frighten people who are doing the right thing. Rather, they frighten people who are doing wrong. Would you rather not be afraid of authority? Do whats right, and you will receive its approval.
local: Di sá yii bin bin ŋáań tiŋanee kaa fàŋkí biyidambi, bin ŋáań tikpitilee di fàŋkí-bi. A yaa là biyidambi ń taa nín̄ cáá-si tifaandee, nín̄ ŋáań tiŋan, bi làá pàkà-si.

eng: It is Gods servant given for your benefit. But if you do whats wrong, be afraid because it doesnt have weapons to enforce the law for nothing. It is Gods servant put in place to carry out his punishment on those who do what is wrong.
local: Bi bíi tūǹ Unimbɔti tundi ní, ki téekí-si á ŋá tiŋan. Ama a yaa ŋá digaŋgam, see á fàŋkì, kun puee an kaa sá fám ní bi cáá mpɔn ki dátí binib tafal. Bi yaa bíi dátí ubɔ tafalee, bi bíi tūǹ Unimbɔti tundi ki dàkà u-ŋuul din u cáá digaŋgam dambi puee dee.

eng: That is why it is necessary to place yourself under the governments authority, not only to avoid Gods punishment but also for the sake of your conscience.
local: Míǹ pu ní an máaǹ yii tí nīn tɔ́ biyidambi. Tí taa fàŋkì ditafadaaldi ŋmaniŋma pu, tí nīn nyímaan yii míǹ di ŋān̄.

eng: You should also pay taxes for the same reason, because the authorities are Gods assistants, concerned with this very thing.
local: An pu ní ni pà lampoobee, bí ŋmā kí tɔ̄ ditundi gbanti mbaamɔmin, kun puee bi sá Unimbɔti tɔntɔnliibi ní.

eng: So pay everyone what you owe them. Pay the taxes you owe, pay the duties you are charged, give respect to those you should respect, and honor those you should honor.
local: Pàmaan un sá kamaa kun ni máaǹ kí pà-uee: pàmaan lampoo kí tīī un an sá ní pà lampoo kí tīīyee, kí tīī bin gífí patanti bi pèe bi-nimbiliŋ, kí fàŋkì bin bin sá ní fàŋkèe, kí nīn tɔ́ bin bin sá ní nīn tɔ́ee.

eng: Dont be in debt to anyone, except for the obligation to love each other. Whoever loves another person has fulfilled the Law.
local: Ní taa nín̄ jīǹ báà ubɔ nì ubɔ apɔɔn. Dipɔndi din ni cááyee di sá ní nīn néeń tɔb. Sii un néeń a-jutiibee, sii ŋá min mara lèe kɔkɔ dee.

eng: The commandments, Dont commit adultery, dont murder, dont steal, dont desire what others have, and any other commandments, are all summed up in one word: You must love your neighbor as yourself. .
local: Di sá yii marab bin lì yii: «Á taa ŋá tilaakpaal, á taa kpɔ̀ unil, á taa yɔ̀, á taa nín̄ cáá nnimbitaataamee» nì marab bin kíǹee kɔkɔ taapu sá marabaanti nee ní yii: «Á nīn néeń a-juti kii sii a-bee.».

eng: Welcome the person who is weak in faith—but not in order to argue about differences of opinion.
local: Ní fōōmaan ni-kansikin bin kaa láá ká mpɔn Unimbɔti saŋŋu poonee, kí taa nín̄ kpākantí bi-landɔkɔi.

eng: But why do you judge your brother or sister? Or why do you look down on your brother or sister? We all will stand in front of the judgment seat of God.
local: Kin sii, ba pu ní a sòoǹ ki bíiĺ a-nabiki? Ní si mun, ba pu ní a kpèé a-nabiki fám? Ti-kɔkɔ làá dāá cù Unimbɔti nimbiin ibɔsoon.

eng: Because it is written, As I live, says the Lord, every knee will bow to me, and every tongue will give praise to God. .
local: kii an nín ŋmàn̄ puee, yii: «Ti-Dindaan bí yii: Man Unimbɔti bí. Báà ŋma làá dāá gbàan̄ man nimbiin, dilimbil kamaa làá dāá nyɔ̄ŋkì man Unimbɔti.».

eng: So then, each of us will give an account of ourselves to God.
local: Míǹ ní ti-kɔkɔ làá dāá sòoǹ ti-ba yil pu bɔti Unimbɔti nimbiin.

eng: So stop judging each other. Instead, this is what you should decide: never put a stumbling block or obstacle in the way of your brother or sister.
local: Tí gbèen̄ kí taa ti nín̄ sòom̀maan tɔb bɔti, ama á dàkafì min ní a làá ŋá a-nabiki ń taa gbéetáaa, kí taa lítí ikpitinee.

eng: I know and Im convinced in the Lord Jesus that nothing is wrong to eat in itself. But if someone thinks something is wrong to eat, it becomes wrong for that person.
local: Ti-Dindaan Yeesu yāntì m béè ki nyí ibaamɔn yii tijin tiba kaa bí ki kɔ̄ntí unil tijɔkɔndi Unimbɔti nimbiin. Sii ubɔ yaa dàkafī yii tiba kaa ŋān̄ee, tì lafun kpántì tin kaa ŋān̄ee sii cee ní.

eng: If your brother or sister is upset by your food, you are no longer walking in love. Dont let your food destroy someone for whom Christ died.
local: Tijin tiba pu ní a yaa yāntì an páb́ a-nabikee, mimmee, aa ti yàntī dineendi kpòotī-si dee. Tijikaal tin a jīǹee puee, taa yàntī un pu Kriisitoo kpíìyee ń wāŋkì.

eng: And dont let something you consider to be good be criticized as wrong.
local: Tin ŋān̄ a-ceeyee ń taa gíti pɔ̄ nsan bí nīn sòoǹ tikpitil.

eng: Gods kingdom isnt about eating food and drinking but about righteousness, peace, and joy in the Holy Spirit.
local: Di sá yii Unimbɔti Beel kaa sá tijikaal nì tinyɔkaal bɔti, an sá deedee nì nsɔŋfi nì dipɔɔpindi din u-Fam tììyee ní.

eng: Whoever serves Christ this way pleases God and gets human approval.
local: Un yaa nóó Kriisitoo mimmee fáatí Unimbɔti, ní binib mun lèekī u-mɔ́ɔŋŋu.

eng: So lets strive for the things that bring peace and the things that build each other up.
local: Mimmee, tí nyàab̀maan tin làá cáań dipɔɔsɔŋkil ti-kansikinee nì tin làá tūtuǹ ti-nabiyaamu bí báań inyaan nnimbɔtisan poonee.

eng: One person believes in eating everything, while the weak person eats only vegetables.
local: Ubɔ máká yii u yaa jīǹ tikɔkɔ an kaa ŋáań-u tiba, ama un kaa láá ká mpɔn nnimbɔtisan poonee kaa là, ní ki jīǹ nnyim ŋma.

eng: Dont destroy what God has done because of food. All food is acceptable, but its a bad thing if it trips someone else.
local: Tijin puee, taa gbá kí kpìtī Unimbɔti tundi. Tikɔkɔ ŋān̄ ki tīī bí jī, ama an kaa ŋān̄ yii tijikaal tin a jīǹee puee, á yàntī a-nabiki ń lítí.

eng: Its a good thing not to eat meat or drink wine or to do anything that trips your brother or sister.
local: Tin ŋān̄ee di sá á taa ŋmɔ́ tinan, kí taa nyɔ̀ ndaam, kí taa ŋá tiba min ŋūǹ kí yàntī a-nabiki ń lítée.

eng: Keep the belief that you have to yourself—its between you and God. People are blessed who dont convict themselves by the things they approve.
local: Sii nín fōō ki kíí ki nyí tiŋyee, dūú kpāafì a-ban Unimbɔti nimbiin. Un yaa lēē min ŋān̄ u-cee u-pɔbil kaa máfée bí mmɔnni.

eng: But those who have doubts are convicted if they go ahead and eat, because they arent acting on the basis of faith. Everything that isnt based on faith is sin.
local: Ama un yaa bíi jīǹ tiba ní u-pɔbil máfée kɔ́ tibɔtin, kun puee u-pɔbil tùkù-u yii min u ŋáańee kaa máaǹ ú ŋá. A yaa ŋá tiba ní a-pɔbil máfée, an sá ikpiti ní.

eng: Those who eat must not look down on the ones who dont, and the ones who dont eat must not judge the ones who do, because God has accepted them.
local: Sii un jīǹ tikɔkɔ nì tikɔkɔee ń taa kpèè un gàntī ki jīǹee fám, ní sii un gàntēe ń taa bīil̀ un jīǹ tikɔkɔ nì tikɔkɔee, kun puee Unimbɔti fōō-u.

eng: Who are you to judge someone elses servants? They stand or fall before their own Lord .
local: Sii di sá ŋma ní ki sòoǹ ubɔ tɔntɔnliu bɔti ní? U yaa yúɔɔɔ, u yaa tí lítàaa, an sá u-kpiliu bɔti, an kaa sá a-yati. Ama u làá nīn yú, kun puee ti-Dindaan cáá mpɔn kí tíkiń-u.

eng: One person considers some days to be more sacred than others, while another person considers all days to be the same. Each person must have their own convictions.
local: Biba dàkafī yii iwiin iba sá ikpaan ki jítiǹ iba, ní biba mun dàkafī yii iwiin kɔkɔ sá ibaanti ní. Báà ŋma ń nīn nyí u-ban yii min u ŋáańee di máaǹ.

eng: Someone who thinks that a day is sacred, thinks that way for the Lord. Those who eat, eat for the Lord, because they thank God. And those who dont eat, dont eat for the Lord, and they thank the Lord too.
local: Un gàntī iwiin iba yii i sá ikpaanee ŋáań míǹ ti-Dindaan pu ní, ní un jīǹ tikɔkɔ nì tikɔkɔee mun jīǹ míǹ ti-Dindaan pu, kun puee u jáaḿ Unimbɔti tijin gbanti pu ní kí nín jī. Un mun jīǹ nnyimee, ti-Dindaan pu ní u jīǹ míǹ, ní ki jáaḿ Unimbɔti.

eng: We dont live for ourselves and we dont die for ourselves.
local: Di sá yii ubɔ kaa bí dimaŋfalin ki tīī u-ba, ubɔ kaa tí kúù ki tīī u-ba mun.

eng: If we live, we live for the Lord, and if we die, we die for the Lord. Therefore, whether we live or die, we belong to God.
local: Ti yaa báaa, ti bí ki tīī ti-Dindaan ní, ti yaa tí kpíìyaaa, ti kpíì ki tīī ti-Dindaan ní. Ti yaa báaa, ti yaa tí kpíìyaaa, ti sá ti-Dindaan yab ní.

eng: This is why Christ died and lived: so that he might be Lord of both the dead and the living.
local: Di sá yii Kriisitoo kpíì ki fīkitì kí ŋmā kí nīn sá bin kpíìyee nì bin bée kɔkɔ Dindaan ní.

eng: We who are powerful need to be patient with the weakness of those who dont have power, and not please ourselves.
local: Timbi bin ká mpɔn Unimbɔti saŋŋu poonee, ti máaǹ kí tūtuǹ bin kaa láá ká mpɔnee kí jī kimɔtii bi-pu, kí taa nín̄ nyàab̀ min làá nīn fáatiǹ-tee.

eng: And again, it says, Rejoice, Gentiles, with his people .
local: An gíti ŋmàn̄ yii: «Atimbun, fàal̀maan nì dinibul din Unimbɔti lútèe!».

eng: And again, Praise the Lord, all you Gentiles, and all the people should sing his praises .
local: An gíti tí ŋmàn̄ yii: «Nimbi atimbun kɔkɔ ń nyɔ̄ŋkì ti-Dindaan, ní anibul kɔkɔ ń pàkà-u!».

eng: And again, Isaiah says, There will be a root of Jesse, who will also rise to rule the Gentiles. The Gentiles will place their hope in him .
local: Yeesaya mun nìn lī yii: «Jeesee naantiiu ubɔ làá dāan̄, u làá dāá yīkì kí jī atimbun kɔkɔ pu dibeel, ní atimbunee kɔkɔ làá dāá nīn máká u-pu.».

eng: May the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace in faith so that you overflow with hope by the power of the Holy Spirit.
local: Unimbɔti un tìì dimákalee ń gbéeǹ-ni dipɔɔpindi kí tīī-ni ŋgbansɔŋfi mmɔntiimin ni nín fōō u-bɔti ki kíí puee, án nín làá ŋá pu u-Fam pɔŋŋu ń yàntī ní nīn cɔ́tí u-pu tikpil tikpilee.

eng: My brothers and sisters, I myself am convinced that you yourselves are full of goodness, filled with all knowledge, and are able to teach each other.
local: M-nabiyaamu, man m-ba nyí yii ni sá binib bin tiŋan gbí ni-pɔbiliŋ, ki nyí tin kɔkɔ ni máaǹ kí nīn nyée, ki ŋūǹ kí nīn sáŋkí tɔb muee.

eng: But Ive written to you in a sort of daring way, partly to remind you of what you already know. Im writing to you in this way because of the grace that was given to me by God.
local: Nì míǹ kɔkɔee, m ŋmàn̄ ki cántì ajan aba, kaa fāŋkì kí fàtīí téetí-ni tin ni nìn nyí dooooyee. M yaa ŋmàn̄-ni mimmee, an sá diyimɔɔl din Unimbɔti pú-mi.

eng: It helps me to be a minister of Christ Jesus to the Gentiles. Im working as a priest of Gods gospel so that the offering of the Gentiles can be acceptable and made holy by the Holy Spirit.
local: ḿ nīn sá Yeesu Kriisitoo tɔntɔnliu bin kaa sá Juuda yabee ceeyee pu. Unimbɔti dū Tibɔmɔntiil disoondi ki ŋúbiǹ-mi, an ŋman kii bisaraaŋaliibi tundi ní m bíi tūǹee, ḿ ŋmā kí dū bin kaa sá Juuda yabee kí tīī Unimbɔti, u-Circir Fam ń yàntī bí nīn sá saraamɔnti un làá fáatí-uee.

eng: So in Christ Jesus I brag about things that have to do with God.
local: Man nì ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo nín ŋá ubaantee puee ní m ŋūǹ kí nīn gàafī nì ditundi din m tūǹ ki tìì Unimbɔtee pu.

eng: I dont dare speak about anything except what Christ has done through me to bring about the obedience of the Gentiles. He did it by what Ive said and what Ive done.
local: Di sá yii maa làá wàan̄ kí lì tiba an yaa kaa sá tin Kriisitoo dī m-pu ki ŋá, ki cáań binib bin kaa sá Juuda yabee ń nīn tɔ́ Unimbɔtee. Kriisitooyee di yāntì m sòon̄ anyɔɔbun ki túń atuun.

eng: by the power of signs and wonders, and by the power of Gods Spirit. So Ive completed the circuit of preaching Christs gospel from Jerusalem all the way around to Illyricum.
local: ki ŋá tidaandi nì maamaacib, nì Unimbɔti Fam pɔŋŋu. Míǹ ní m sòon̄ Tibɔmɔntiil tin sá Kriisitoo yatee an kɔkɔ, ki yōoǹ Jeerusaleem ki dāa sāā Iliirii tiŋki.

eng: Each of us should please our neighbors for their good in order to build them up.
local: Báà ŋma ń nyàab̀ min làá tūtuǹ u-jutee mmɔntiim ú báań inyaan.

eng: In this way, I have a goal to preach the gospel where they havent heard of Christ yet, so that I wont be building on someone elses foundation.
local: M pɔ̄ɔntí m-ba kí sòoǹ Tibɔmɔntiilee laakin binib kaa láá gbìl̄ Kriisitoo puee, án nín làá ŋá pu ḿ taa mā kí tɔ́kɔń laakin biba nìn kíĺee.

eng: Instead, as its written, Those who hadnt been told about him will see, and those who hadnt heard will understand .
local: Míǹ ní m ŋá kii an nín ŋmàn̄ puee yii: «Binib bin cee baa sòon̄ u-puee làá dāá ká-u, bin bin kaa nìn gbìl̄ u-puee làá dāá gbìl̄ taapu.».

eng: Thats why Ive been stopped so many times from coming to see you.
local: Ditundi gbanti pu ní m mākafì miŋa nì miŋa kí dāan̄ ni-cee ní kaa ŋúń.

eng: But now, since I dont have any place to work in these regions anymore, and since Ive wanted to come to see you for many years.
local: Kɔŋkɔnnee, maa ti cáá ditundi diba ntimu gbanti poon. M baasii nyàab̄ kí dāan̄ ni-cee abin kupaau sèé.

eng: Ill visit you when I go to Spain. I hope to see you while Im passing through. And I hope you will send me on my way there, after I have first been reenergized by some time in your company.
local: M yaa làá cù Eespaanyee, see ḿ dīń ni-cee, man nì ni ń kàl̄, ní kpáàa tūtuǹ-mi ḿ cù kitiŋ gbantin. Mimmee m làá ŋmā kí kàl̄ ni-cee ŋyunti ŋubɔ fiii an mɔ̄ɔkiǹ-mi.

eng: But now Im going to Jerusalem, to serve Gods people.
local: Kɔŋkɔnnee, m dá láá cá Jeerusaleem kí tɔ̄ ditundi diba kí tīī Unimbɔti yab bin bí niinee.

eng: Macedonia and Achaia have been happy to make a contribution for the poor among Gods people in Jerusalem.
local: Kun puee Kriisitoo yab bin bí Maseeduwaan nì Akayee kpáfì ki tɔ́ animbil kí tūtuǹ Kriisitoo yab bin bí Jeerusaleem ki sá bisoodambee.

eng: They were happy to do this, and they are actually in debt to Gods people in Jerusalem. If the Gentiles got a share of the Jewish peoples spiritual resources, they ought to minister to them with material resources.
local: Bíǹ bi-ba di lēē yii bi làá ŋá míǹ, ní an lafun máaǹ bí ŋá-bi míǹ, kun puee Kriisitoo yab bin bí Jeerusaleemee dū Tibɔmɔntiil ki yākatì nì bi. Míǹ puee, an máaǹ bi mun ń dū bi-wanti kí tūtuǹ-bi.

eng: So then after I have finished this job and have safely delivered the final amount of the Gentiles offering to them, I will leave for Spain, visiting you on the way.
local: M yaa dū inannakan in bi nākaǹní-mee ki dūu tīī-bi, ki kpēē tibɔti gbanti ki dóòyee, m làá dīń ni-cee, ní kí nín nìkī Eespaanyi.

eng: And I know that when I come to you I will come with the fullest blessing of Christ.
local: M nyí yii Kriisitoo làá bìin̄ m-dāantam ni-cee pu án nīn mɔ̄ tikpil.

eng: Christ didnt please himself, but, as it is written, The insults of those who insulted you fell on me. .
local: Di sá yii Kriisitoo kaa nìn nyàab̀ kí ŋá min fáatì-uee, ama, kii an nín ŋmàn̄ puee yii: «Asiibil ŋin bi nìn sìí sii Unimbɔtee, lítì man pu.».

eng: Brothers and sisters, I urge you, through our Lord Jesus Christ and through the love of the Spirit, to join me in my struggles in your prayers to God for me.
local: Ama, nnabiyaamu, m mèé-ni ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo nì dineendi din Circir Fam tììyee pu, ni-nimbiliŋ ń nīn máǹ ŋwimmeem pu. Ní nīn mèé Unimbɔti ki tútúǹ-mi.

eng: Pray that I will be rescued from the people in Judea who dont believe. Also, pray that my service for Jerusalem will be acceptable to Gods people there.
local: ḿ ŋmā kí nyā Juudee yab bin kaa fōō ki kííyee ŋaan, ní Unimbɔti yab bin bí Jeerusaleemee mun ń fōō nì dipɔbil diba ditutundi din m cáǹ kí tīī-bee.

eng: so that I can come to you with joy by Gods will and be reenergized with your company.
local: Míǹ ní, Unimbɔti yaa kíí, m làá ŋmā kí bàń ni-cee nì dipɔɔpindi man nì ni ń kàl̄ ḿ ŋùŋfì.

eng: May the God of peace be with you all. Amen.
local: Unimbɔti un tìì ŋgbansɔŋfee ń nīn bí ni-kɔkɔ cee. Ameeni.

eng: Whatever was written in the past was written for our instruction so that we could have hope through endurance and through the encouragement of the scriptures.
local: Di sá yii tin kɔkɔ bi nìn ŋmàn̄ Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon dooooyee sá kí dàkà-ti tí nīn cáá kimɔtii nì kunyannyaau, kí nīn máká Unimbɔti pu.

eng: May the God of endurance and encouragement give you the same attitude toward each other, similar to Christ Jesus attitude.
local: Unimbɔti un di tìì kimɔtii nì kunyannyaauee ń tūtuǹ-ni ní ŋá ubaanti kii Yeesu Kriisitoo nín là puee.

eng: That way you can glorify the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ together with one voice.
local: Ni-kɔkɔ ń ŋmā kí kpāfì kinyɔɔbaantii kí nyɔ̄ŋkì Unimbɔti un sá ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo Baayee.

eng: So welcome each other, in the same way that Christ also welcomed you, for Gods glory.
local: Míǹ puee, kii Kriisitoo nín fōō-ni puee, ní nīn fòol̄maan tɔb án tīī Unimbɔti tinyool.

eng: Im saying that Christ became a servant of those who are circumcised for the sake of Gods truth, in order to confirm the promises given to the ancestors.
local: An taapu sá yii Kriisitoo dɔ́miń Juuda yab kansikin ki túń ditundi ki ŋá min Unimbɔti nìn pōoǹ ki tīī bi-naanjabee ki dàkā yii Unimbɔti sá deedeedaan.

eng: and so that the Gentiles could glorify God for his mercy. As it is written, Because of this I will confess you among the Gentiles, and I will sing praises to your name .
local: ní ki dɔ́miń bin kaa sá Juuda yabee mun ń nyɔ̄ŋkì Unimbɔti u-saasɔl pu, kii an nín ŋmàn̄ puee, yii: «An pu ní m làá nyɔ̄ŋkì-si atimbun kansikin, kí gāā ilan kí pàkà a-yindi.».

eng: Im introducing our sister Phoebe to you, who is a servant of the church in Cenchreae.
local: M dū ti-niisal Fooyibee un tūǹ ditundi Kriisitoo nikitil din bí Saŋkireeyee kansikinee ki ŋá ni-ŋaan.

eng: Say hello to Apelles, who is tried and true in Christ. Say hello to the members of the household of Aristobulus.
local: Ní jāam̀ Apeeleesi, un jíń falaa Kriisitoo pu, kaa lítèe. Ní jāam̀ Ariistoobul dicaŋkun yab.

eng: Say hello to my relative Herodion. Say hello to the members of the household of Narcissus who are in the Lord.
local: Ní jāam̀ Eeroodiyoon, man nì un pēēyee. Ní jāam̀ Naasiisi dicaŋkun yab bin sá ti-Dindaan yabee.

eng: Say hello to Tryphaena and Tryphosa, who are workers for the Lord. Say hello to my dear friend Persis, who has worked hard in the Lord.
local: Ní jāam̀ Triifeeni nì Triifoosi, bin tūǹ ditummɔntiil ki tìì ti-Dindaanee, kí jāam̀maan ti-niisamɔnti Peersiidi un túń atummɔnti tikpil ki tīī ti-Dindaanee.

eng: Say hello to Rufus, who is an outstanding believer, along with his mother and mine.
local: Ní jāam̀ Rufusi un ti-Dindaan lútì ú tɔ̄ u-tundee, kí kpáàa jāam̀ u-na un kpántì ma mun nee.

eng: Say hello to Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermes, Patrobas, Hermas, and the brothers and sisters who are with them.
local: Ní jāam̀ Asiŋkiltu nì Fileegɔn nì Eermeesi nì Patrooba nì Eermaasi, nì ti-nabiyaamu mun bí bi-ceeyee.

eng: Say hello to Philologus and Julia, Nereus and his sister, and Olympas, and all the saints who are with them.
local: Ní jāam̀ Filooloogu nì Julii nì Neeree nì u-niisal Oolimpa nì Unimbɔti yab bin kɔkɔ bí bi-ceeyee.

eng: Say hello to each other with a holy kiss. All the churches of Christ say hello to you.
local: Ni-kɔkɔ ń jāam̀maan tɔb tiŋan kii nnabiyaamuee. Kriisitoo nikitiŋ kɔkɔ jáamiń-ni.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I urge you to watch out for people who create divisions and problems against the teaching that you learned. Keep away from them.
local: Nnabiyaamu, m kpáafí-ni ní nīn nyí nì ni-ba nì bin fá tibɔmɔntiil tin bi dàkā-nee ki cāabìń diyakatil, ki yàntī binib lítée. Sútí kí wàatīmaan bidambi.

eng: People like that arent serving the Lord. They are serving their own feelings. They deceive the hearts of innocent people with smooth talk and flattery.
local: An nibee kaa nóó ti-Dindaan Kriisitoo. Bi nyàab̀ bi-ba jim ní, ní ki sòoǹ anyɔɔbummɔɔn ki kíiĺ binib, ki yàntī bin nyil kaa náaǹee lítí.

eng: The news of your obedience has reached everybody, so Im happy for you. But I want you to be wise about whats good, and innocent about whats evil.
local: Báà ŋma nyí ni nín tɔ́ ti-Dindaanee, an puee, m-pɔbil sɔ̀m̀ ni-pu. Ama m là ní nīn sá bin nyí ki ŋáań tiŋanee, kí nīn sá bin bí circir, kaa là ikpitee.

eng: Welcome her in the Lord in a way that is worthy of Gods people, and give her whatever she needs from you, because she herself has been a sponsor of many people, myself included.
local: Fōōmaan-u ti-Dindaan pu kii Unimbɔti nib nín máaǹ kí nīn fòol̄ tɔb puee. Ní ajan ŋin kɔkɔ poon an bàlifī yii ní tūtuǹ-uee, ní tūtum̀maan-u. Míǹ ní u mun jòoń binib tikpil, ki kpáaǹ man m-ba.

eng: The God of peace will soon crush Satan under your feet. The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
local: An kaa làá yúntí, Unimbɔti un tìì ŋgbansɔŋfee làá yàntī ní tāā kí mátí Sitaan. Ti-Dindaan Yeesu ŋanti ń nīn bí ni-pu!.

eng: Timothy my coworker says hello to you, and Lucius, Jason, and Sosipater, my relatives.
local: M-tɔntɔnjuti Tiimootee jáamiń-ni. Lusiyusi nì Jasɔn nì Soospatee, man nì bin pēēyee, mun jáamiń-ni.

eng: Im Tertius, and Im writing this letter to you in the Lord—hello!.
local: Man Teertiyusi un ŋmàn̄ kugbɔŋu neeyee mun jáamiń-ni ti-Dindaan pu.

eng: Gaius, who is host to me and to the whole church, says hello to you. Erastus the city treasurer says hello to you, along with our brother Quartus.
local: Gaayusi un dumpu m kóòyee, nì un dumpu Kriisitoo nikitil kɔkɔ kpáfée, jáamiń-ni. Eelasitu un ŋúb́ bi-tiŋki nimbiliŋee, nì ti-nabiki Kaartusi jáamiń-ni. .

eng: May the glory be to God who can strengthen you with my good news and the message that I preach about Jesus Christ. He can strengthen you with the announcement of the secret that was kept quiet for a long time.
local: Tí nyɔ̄ŋkìmaan Unimbɔti! Nì tibɔmɔntiil tin m sòoǹ Yeesu Kriisitoo puee, Unimbɔti cáá mpɔn kí báań-ni inyaan nnimbɔtisan poon. Doooo ŋyuntikpikee, tibɔtee nìn bí dibalin, Unimbɔti kaa nìn dáá bīitì ti-pu.

eng: Now that secret is revealed through what the prophets wrote. It is made known to the Gentiles in order to lead to their faithful obedience based on the command of the eternal God.
local: ama kɔŋkɔnnee u nyántì-tì mpaampiin pu. U dī tigbɔn tin u-bɔnaatiliibi ŋmàn̄ ki nāatì u-bɔtee pu ki yāntì atimbun kamaa nib béè ti-pu án nín làá ŋá pu bí fōō kí kíí kí nīn kíǹ ki tɔ́ Unimbɔtee. Jaanjaan Nimbɔtiu nín là puee dee.

eng: May the glory be to God, who alone is wise! May the glory be to him through Jesus Christ forever! Amen.
local: Míǹ puee, Unimbɔti un baba sá uciŋfitee ń ká tinyool Yeesu Kriisitoo pu kí nīn pée cá jaanjaan! Ameeni.

eng: Say hello to Prisca and Aquila, my coworkers in Christ Jesus.
local: Ní jāam̀ Priisiil nì Akiilasi, m-tɔntɔnjutiibi Yeesu Kriisitoo tundin.

eng: who risked their own necks for my life. Im not the only one who thanks God for them, but all the churches of the Gentiles do the same.
local: Bi kíí kí dū bi-ba kí tīī ŋkúm man ŋmátí. An kaa sá man baba di làá jāam̀-bi an pu, Kriisitoo nikitiŋ ŋin kaa sá Juuda yabee kɔkɔ mun làá jāam̀-bi.

eng: Also say hello to the church that meets in their house. Say hello to Epaenetus, my dear friend, who was the first convert in Asia for Christ.
local: Ní jāam̀ Kriisitoo nikitil din kpáfí bi-dumpuee mun. Ní jāam̀ Eepayineeti, m-bɔɔ un m néeńee, un péé péé ki fōō ki kíí Asii tiŋkinee.

eng: Say hello to Mary, who has worked very hard for you.
local: Ní jāam̀ Maarii un túń ditundi tikpil ki tīī-nee.

eng: Say hello to Andronicus and Junia, my relatives and my fellow prisoners. They are prominent among the apostles, and they were in Christ before me.
local: Ní jāam̀ Androonikuusi nì Juniyaasi, man nì bin pēē kubaabinaatoou ki pēē sarka muee. Yeesu kpambaliŋ kansikinee, bi tɔ́-bi tikpil. Bíǹ péeǹ man ki fōō Kriisitoo ki kíí.

eng: Say hello to Ampliatus, my dear friend in the Lord.
local: Ní jāam̀ Ampiliyatuu, m-bɔɔ un pu maa mɔ̄ ti-Dindaan puee.

eng: Say hello to Urbanus, our coworker in Christ, and my dear friend Stachys.
local: Ní jāam̀ Urbeen, ti nì un kpáfí ki tūǹ ditundi Kriisitoo puee, kí jāam̀ m-bɔɔmɔnti Sitaku mun.

eng: So every single one of you who judge others is without any excuse. You condemn yourself when you judge another person because the one who is judging is doing the same things.
local: Ŋma ní a sá, sii un sòoǹ bin béeyee bɔti yii bi nín ŋáań puee kaa ŋān̄ee. Aa cáá nnaakpal ŋubɔ kí dāá kpáĺ. Di sá yii sii un bíi sòoǹ bin béeyee bɔti yii bi nín ŋáań puee kaa ŋān̄, ní ki ŋáań míǹ a-bee, a bíi kù a-ba bɔti dee.

eng: But there will be glory, honor, and peace for everyone who does what is good, for the Jew first and also for the Greek.
local: Ama, tinyool nì ipampaka nì ŋgbansɔŋfi dɔ̄ ki cí bin kɔkɔwoooo ŋáań tiŋanee. Juuda yab ní bi làá dāá péé kí tīī kí wàatī, ní kí nín tīī bin kaa sá Juuda yabee.

eng: God does not have favorites.
local: Di sá yii Unimbɔti kaa gàntī binib.

eng: Those who have sinned outside the Law will also die outside the Law, and those who have sinned under the Law will be judged by the Law.
local: Bin kaa béè marab bin Unimbɔti tīī Mooyiisee yaa ŋá ikpitee, bi làá wāŋkì, ama marabee ní baa làá dūú sòoǹ bi-bɔti. Ní bin kɔkɔ nyí marab gbanti ní ki ŋáań ikpitee, marabee ní bi làá dūú sòoǹ bidambi bɔti.

eng: It isnt the ones who hear the Law who are righteous in Gods eyes. It is the ones who do what the Law says who will be treated as righteous.
local: Kaa sá bin pílifí marabee di sá deedeedambi Unimbɔti nimbiin. Ama bin bin ŋáań marabee nín lī puee, bíǹ ní bi dāa yíì deedeedambi.

eng: Gentiles dont have the Law. But when they instinctively do what the Law requires they are a Law in themselves, though they dont have the Law.
local: Mimmee, bin kaa sá Juuda yab, kaa nyí Mooyiisi marab puee, bi-pɔbiliŋ yaa yāntì bí ŋmā kí ŋá min nóó nì marabee, báà bi yaa kaa nyí marab, bi nyí tiŋan diŋál kii marab nín lī puee.

eng: They show the proof of the Law written on their hearts, and their consciences affirm it. Their conflicting thoughts will accuse them, or even make a defense for them.
local: An dàkà yii marab tuuŋŋi taapu ŋmàn̄ bi-pɔbiliŋ poon. Min máaǹee dòoń bi-neeŋyin, ní bi-landɔkɔi tùkù-bi ŋyunti ŋubɔee, tin bi yaa ŋá an ŋān̄ee, nì tin bi yaa ŋá an kaa ŋān̄ee.

eng: on the day when, according to my gospel, God will judge the hidden truth about human beings through Christ Jesus.
local: Ŋwiin ŋun Unimbɔti làá yàntī Yeesu Kriisitoo ń bìitī nnibiyaamu tuuŋŋi ŋin bí dibalinee pu kí sòoǹee, tin tin báà ŋma làá dāá kée dee, kii Tibɔmɔntiil tin man Pɔɔl sòoǹee nín lì puee.

eng: But, if you call yourself a Jew; if you rely on the Law; if you brag about your relationship to God.
local: Ama sii un bi yíì Juuda yɔu, a máká Mooyiisi marab pu, ki dàkà a-ba yii a nyí Unimbɔti.

eng: if you know the will of God; if you are taught by the Law so that you can figure out the things that really matter.
local: ki nyí u nín là puee, marab dàkà-si á lēē laakin ŋān̄ee.

eng: if you have persuaded yourself that you are: a guide for the blind; a light to those who are in darkness.
local: A nyí a-ba yii a ŋūǹ kí kpòotī bijɔfub kí nīn sá ŋwalifi kí tīī bin bí dibɔmbɔndinee.

eng: We know that Gods judgment agrees with the truth, and his judgment is against those who do these kinds of things.
local: Ti nyí yii Unimbɔti nín sòoǹ ki kù bin ŋáań an tuuŋŋee bɔtee, u cáá ibaamɔn.

eng: an educator of the foolish; a teacher of infants.
local: ki là kí nīn dàkà bin nimbiliŋ kaa ŋúbitèe nì bin kaa nyí tiba nnimbɔtisan poonee, kun puee a béè marab ní marabee dàkā-si iciin nì ibaamɔn kɔkɔ.

eng: then why dont you who are teaching others teach yourself? If you preach, No stealing, do you steal?.
local: Too, ba ŋá ní sii dàkà bin kíǹee, ní kaa dàkà a-ba ní? Ba ŋá ní sii un sòoǹ ki tùkù bin kíǹee yii bí taa yɔ̀ee, yù ní?.

eng: If you say, No adultery, do you commit adultery? If you hate idols, do you rob temples?.
local: Sii un lì yii an kaa máaǹ bí ŋá tilaakpaalee, ba ŋá ní sii ŋáań tilaakpaal ní? Sii un ná awaayee, ba ŋá ní a kóò awaadiinni ki yù tiwan ní?.

eng: If you brag about the Law, do you shame God by breaking the Law?.
local: Sii un gà a-bindi yii a nyí marabee, ŋyunti ŋun a yìntīǹ marab gbantee, Unimbɔti yindi ní a bíi bíiĺ.

eng: As it is written: The name of God is discredited by the Gentiles because of you .
local: kii Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon nín lī puee yii: «Nimbi Juuda yab pu ní bin kaa sá Juuda yabee sòoǹ ki bíiĺ Unimbɔti yindi.».

eng: Circumcision is an advantage if you do what the Law says. But if you are a person who breaks the Law, your status of being circumcised has changed into not being circumcised.
local: A yaa tɔ́ marabee, ní digiil din a gííyee ŋān̄ ki tīī-si, ama a yaa yìntī marabee, nì min kɔkɔ bi gíí-see, an sá fám ní.

eng: So if the person who isnt circumcised keeps the Law, wont his status of not being circumcised be counted as if he were circumcised?.
local: Unil un kaa gííyee yaa kpáafí marabee, nì min kɔkɔ baa gíí-uee, Unimbɔti kpèé-u kii u gííyee ní.

eng: The one who isnt physically circumcised but keeps the Law will judge you. You became a lawbreaker after you had the written Law and circumcision.
local: Un baa gíí, u nóó marabee làá dāá jīǹ sii un gíí ki cáá marab gbɔŋku ki gíti yìntī marabee ibaamɔn.

eng: It isnt the Jew who maintains outward appearances who will receive praise from God, and it isnt people who are outwardly circumcised on their bodies.
local: An kaa sá un bi wāĺ yii u sá Juuda yɔu, bi gíí-u, u cáá kudaaŋŋu u-wunti puee di sá Juuda yɔu ubaamɔn.

eng: Instead, it is the person who is a Jew inside, who is circumcised in spirit, not literally. That persons praise doesnt come from people but from God.
local: un sá Juuda yɔu ubaamɔnee di sá un sá Juuda yɔu u-pɔbil poonee. Bi gíí-u nì digiil din sá Unimbɔti Fam gíitamee, kaa sá marab gbɔŋku nín lī puee gíitam. Unimbɔti di pàkà an niliuee, an kaa sá binib di pàkà-u.

eng: If you judge those who do these kinds of things while you do the same things yourself, think about this: Do you believe that you will escape Gods judgment?.
local: Mimmee, sii un kù bin béeyee bɔti ki mun ŋáań míǹ mbaantiimee, a dàkafī yii a làá nyā Unimbɔti bɔsooŋyinaaa?.

eng: Or do you have contempt for the riches of Gods generosity, tolerance, and patience? Dont you realize that Gods kindness is supposed to lead you to change your heart and life?.
local: Unimbɔti ŋanti tin u cáá tikpilee, nì u-mɔtiiki nì u-niti ní a cáa kpèé fámaaa? Aa nyí yii Unimbɔti nín ŋáań-si tiŋan puee, u là kí tūtuǹ-si á kpántí a-bimbim níii?.

eng: You are storing up wrath for yourself because of your stubbornness and your heart that refuses to change. Gods just judgment will be revealed on the day of wrath.
local: Di sá yii a nín pɔ́ɔkí a-pɔbil pu ki yíití, kaa là kí kpántí a-bimbimee, a mɔ̀tīi kútí a-ba ditafadaaldi din a làá fōō Unimbɔti ŋuul dalee, ŋwiin ŋun Unimbɔti làá dāá dū ibaamɔn kí sòoǹ binib bɔtee ní.

eng: God will repay everyone based on their works .
local: Yaadal ní u làá pà báà ŋma u-tundi paati.

eng: On the one hand, he will give eternal life to those who look for glory, honor, and immortality based on their patient good work.
local: U làá dū dimaŋfal din kaa cáá dikúntilee kí tīī bin pɔ̄ɔntí ki ŋáań tiŋan ki nyàab̀ tinyool nì ipampaka nì dimaŋfal din bí kaa kúntée.

eng: But on the other hand, there will be wrath and anger for those who obey wickedness instead of the truth because they are acting out of selfishness and disobedience.
local: Bin yíì, kaa là kí nóó u-baamɔŋyi ki là kí nīn nóó tin kaa ŋān̄ee, u làá dāá fōō diŋuul bi-pu kí dáaĺ bi-tafal.

eng: There will be trouble and distress for every human being who does evil, for the Jew first and also for the Greek.
local: Falaa nì dinimbinaamandi dɔ̄ ki cí bin kɔkɔwoooo ŋáań tin kaa ŋān̄ee. Juuda yab tafal ní Unimbɔti làá péé péé kí dáaĺ kí wàatī, ní kí dāá nín dáaĺ bin kaa sá Juuda yabee tafal.

eng: So whats the advantage of being a Jew? Or whats the benefit of circumcision?.
local: A yaa sá Juuda yɔuee, a gíti cáá diyimɔɔl dibaaa? Àá digiil sá tiwammɔntiilaaa?.

eng: As it is written, There is no righteous person, not even one. .
local: Kii Unimbɔti gbɔŋku nín lī puee, yii: «Unil báà ubɔ kaa bí ki bí deedee, báà ubaanti, an kpá.

eng: There is no one who understands. There is no one who looks for God. .
local: Ubɔ kaa bí ki cáá iciin. Ubɔ kaa bí ki nyàab̀ Unimbɔti.

eng: They all turned away. They have become worthless together. There is no one who shows kindness. There is not even one. .
local: Bi-kɔkɔ yīntiǹ-u ki wáŋkì, báà ubɔ kaa bí ki ŋáań tiŋan, báà uniseen, an kpá.

eng: Their throat is a grave that has been opened. They are deceitful with their tongues, and the poison of vipers is under their lips. .
local: Dikaakul din wāā ki núùyee nín bí puee, míǹ ní tibɔti tin bí bi-neeŋyinee bí. Bi cáá bi-limbiliŋ ki kíiĺ binib, bi-nyunculiŋ nín tábití ki sòoǹ tiŋyee sá tilool kii ukoowaati lootee ní.

eng: Their mouths are full of cursing and bitterness. .
local: Bi-nyɔmmu poon gbí mbusu nì ntuntun.

eng: Their feet are quick to shed blood; .
local: Bi yáb̀ nnikpaakoon pu.

eng: destruction and misery are in their ways; .
local: Laakin kamaa bi yaa cūtèe, see bí cáá bi-timbiilii nì falaa niin.

eng: and they dont know the way of peace. .
local: Baa nyí ŋgbansɔŋfi saŋŋu.

eng: There is no fear of God in their view of the world. .
local: Baa fàŋkí Unimbɔti báà fiii.».

eng: Now we know that whatever the Law says, it speaks to those who are under the Law, in order to shut every mouth and make it so the whole world has to answer to God.
local: Ti nyí yii marab nín sòoǹ tiŋyee kɔkɔee, bi sòoǹ ki tìì bin bin Unimbɔti nìn tīī u-marabee ní. Míǹ puee, ubɔ kaa dāa cáá kinyɔkɔ kí wàan̄ kí tīī nnaakpal. Kitiŋ pu yab kɔkɔ dāa béetí yii bi sá binikpitib Unimbɔti nimbiin.

eng: Plenty in every way. First of all, the Jews were trusted with Gods revelations.
local: Tiŋman, bi-yimɔɔl jítiǹ bin béeyee yal kpataaa. Diyimɔɔpeepeekaal di sá Unimbɔti nín dū u-bɔti ki ŋúbiǹ Juuda yabee.

eng: It follows that no human being will be treated as righteous in his presence by doing what the Law says, because the knowledge of sin comes through the Law.
local: Di sá yii unil nín ŋúb̀ marab mmɔntiimin puee kaa ŋūǹ kí yàntī ú nīn bí deedee Unimbɔti nimbiin, kun puee marab tundi di pée sá bí dàkà binib kun di sá ikpitee.

eng: But now Gods righteousness has been revealed apart from the Law, which is confirmed by the Law and the Prophets.
local: Ama kɔŋkɔnnee, Unimbɔti dàkā min ní unil ŋūǹ kí dī kí kpántí deedeedaan u-nimbiin kí taa dī marab puee: Mooyiisi gbɔnti nì Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliibi mun nìn sòon̄ an bɔti pu.

eng: Gods righteousness comes through the faithfulness of Jesus Christ for all who have faith in him. Theres no distinction.
local: An sá ti nín fōō Yeesu Kriisitoo ki kííyee pu ní Unimbɔti kpántì-ti deedeedambi u-nimbiin. Unimbɔti kaa gàntī binib, an sá ki tīī bin kɔkɔ fōō Yeesu ki kííyee.

eng: All have sinned and fall short of Gods glory.
local: kun puee báà ŋma ŋá ikpiti ki dátiǹ Unimbɔti nyooti.

eng: but all are treated as righteous freely by his grace because of a ransom that was paid by Christ Jesus.
local: Unimbɔti ŋáań-ti tiŋan ki yàntī ti kpántí deedee nib u-nimbiin faalaa, Yeesu Kriisitoo nín dū u-ba ki fīī-ti ki líìyee pu.

eng: Through his faithfulness, God displayed Jesus as the place of sacrifice where mercy is found by means of his blood. He did this to demonstrate his righteousness in passing over sins that happened before.
local: Úǹ ní Unimbɔti yōoǹ ki lēē-ti saraa, u-fatikuu ń pítí bin kɔkɔwoooo fōō-u ki kííyee kpitii, bi nì Unimbɔti kansikin ń ŋāŋkì. Unimbɔti ŋá míǹ kí dàkà u nín sá deedeedaan puee ní. Di sá yii dooooyee, u nìn jíń kimɔtii kaa nìn dátí binikpitib tafal bi-kpitii pu.

eng: during the time of Gods patient tolerance. He also did this to demonstrate that he is righteous in the present time, and to treat the one who has faith in Jesus as righteous.
local: Ama kɔŋkɔnnee, u dàkā binib u-deedeeu: u-deedeeuee di sá u dū Yeesu ki pàn̄ ti-kpitii pɔndi, ní ki yíì bin kɔkɔ fòol̄ Yeesu ki kíikée deedeedambi.

eng: What happens to our bragging? Its thrown out. With which law? With what we have accomplished under the Law?.
local: Mimmee, unil cáá nsan kí dàkà u-baaa? Aayee, nsan kaa bí báà fiii. Ba pu ní a ká difiil? A nín nóó Mooyiisi marabee puɔɔɔ? Aayee, an sá a nín fōō ki kííyee pu.

eng: No, not at all, but through the law of faith. We consider that a person is treated as righteous by faith, apart from what is accomplished under the Law.
local: Ti nyí yii unil un fōō Yeesu ki kííyee di kpántí deedeedaan Unimbɔti nimbiin, an kaa sá marab bin u nóóyee pu.
