eng: Or is God the God of Jews only? Isnt God the God of Gentiles also? Yes, God is also the God of Gentiles.
local: An yaan sá marab puee, mimmee, Unimbɔti bàà sá Juuda yab ŋmaniŋma Nimbɔtiu naa! Waa bàà sá bin kaa sá Juuda yabee mun Nimbɔtiu. Ama u sá báà ŋma Nimbɔtiu ní.

eng: What does it matter, then, if some werent faithful? Their lack of faith wont cancel Gods faithfulness, will it?.
local: Ama bi-kansikin biba kaa ŋúń ki yúl̀ deedee. Mimmee, bi nín kaa yúl̀ deedeeyee puee di làá yàntī Unimbɔti deedeeu ń ŋá fámaaa?.

eng: Since God is one, then the one who makes the circumcised righteous by faith will also make the one who isnt circumcised righteous through faith.
local: Di sá yii u pée sá Unimbɔtibaanti ní, ki làá yàntī Juuda yab bin gíí dikpancandi ki fōō Yeesu ki kííyee nì bin kaa sá Juuda yab, kaa gíí, ki mun fōō Yeesu ki kííyee kɔkɔ ń kpántí deedeedambi u-nimbiin.

eng: Do we then cancel the Law through this faith? Absolutely not! Instead, we confirm the Law.
local: Mimmee, an dàkà yii ti yaa fōō Yeesu ki kííyee, taa ti dù marab kí yíiń tiba deeyaaa? Aayee, an kaa sá míǹ. Ŋyunti gbanti ní ti nín tìì marab mpɔn.

eng: Absolutely not! God must be true, even if every human being is a liar, as it is written: So that it can show that you are right in your words; and you will triumph when you are judged. .
local: Aayee, kpataaa! Unimbɔti di làá nīn pée sá deedeedaan, ní binib kɔkɔ ń nīn sá binyimɔndambi, kii Unimbɔti gbɔŋku nín lī puee yii: «Binib máaǹ kí ká a nín sá deedee puee tin a sòoǹee poon, ní bi yaa sòon̄ a-bɔtee, bí ká yii a cáá ibaamɔn.».

eng: But if our lack of righteousness confirms Gods justice, what will we say? That God, who brings wrath upon us, isnt just ?.
local: Ti nín ŋáań ikpiti iŋyee yaa dàkà yii Unimbɔti sá umɔntee, mimmee u nín dátí ti-tafal ti-kpitii puee, waa bí deedee deeyaa! .

eng: Absolutely not! If God werent just, how could he judge the world?.
local: Aayee kpataaa! Unimbɔti yaa kaa bàà bí deedee, waan ŋūǹ kí sòoǹ duulinya bɔti.

eng: But if Gods truth is demonstrated by my lie and it increases his glory, why am I still judged as a sinner?.
local: Mimmee, m-nyimɔŋyi yaa ŋūǹ kí yàntī Unimbɔti baamɔŋyi ń nyā mpaampiin pu bí wāl̀ kí tīī-u tinyoolee, ba pu ní bi làá gíti sòoǹ kí kpɔ̀ m-bɔti yii m sá unikpiti?.

eng: Why not say, Lets do evil things so that good things will come out of it?.
local: Míǹ puee, tí lì yii: «Tí nīn ŋáań ikpiti, án dāá kpántí tiŋanaa!» Míǹ ní bin mɔ́ntí nnyimɔn ki tūntí ti-puee lì. Bin sòoǹ ti-pu mimmee máaǹ ditafadaaldi.

eng: So what are we saying? Are we better off? Not at all. We have already stated the charge: both Jews and Greeks are all under the power of sin.
local: Mimmee, timbi Juuda yab jítiǹ binib bin kíǹeeee? Aayee, kpataaa! Kii m nín bàà pée lī puee, Juuda yab nì bin kaa sá Juuda yabee kɔkɔ bí ikpitin.

eng: So what are we going to say? Are we going to find that Abraham is our ancestor on the basis of genealogy?.
local: Mimmee, ti làá sòoǹ diyimɔɔl din timbi Juuda yab naanja Abraam nìn kée pu yii ba?.

eng: So how was it credited? When he was circumcised, or when he wasnt circumcised? In fact, it was credited while he still wasnt circumcised, not after he was circumcised.
local: Ŋyunti ŋulɔŋu ní Unimbɔti yíiń-u deedeedaan ní? U nín gíí ki dóòyeeyaaa, u nín kaa láá gííyee? An kaa sá u nín gíí ki dóòyee ní u lī, an sá min waa láá gííyee ní u nìn lī.

eng: He received the sign of circumcision as a seal of the righteousness that comes from the faith he had while he still wasnt circumcised. It happened this way so that Abraham could be the ancestor of all those people who arent circumcised, who have faith in God, and so are counted as righteous.
local: Boonee ní Unimbɔti bí Abraam ń gíí dikpancandi án nīn sá kudaaŋŋu yii u sá deedeedaan u-fookiiu pu. Míǹ puee, bin kɔkɔ fōō Unimbɔti ki kíí, kaa gífì ki sá deedeedambi Unimbɔti nimbiinee, bi-baa di sá Abraam.

eng: He could also be the ancestor of those circumcised people, who arent only circumcised but who also walk in the path of faith, like our ancestor Abraham did while he wasnt circumcised.
local: ní u sá bin gííyee mun baa. An kaa sá bi nín gííyee ŋmaniŋma pu, an sá bi nín fōō ki kííyee pu, kii ti-baa Abraam mun nín nìn fōō ki kíí pu, kaa láá gííyee.

eng: The promise to Abraham and to his descendants, that he would inherit the world, didnt come through the Law but through the righteousness that comes from faith.
local: Unimbɔti nìn pōoǹ Abraam nì u-naantiibi yii u làá dāá dū duulinya kí tīī-bi. An kaa sá Abraam nín nìn nóó marabee pu ní u nìn pōoǹ-u míǹ, ama an sá u nín nìn fōō Unimbɔti ki kíí Unimbɔti yíì-u deedeedaanee pu.

eng: If they inherit because of the Law, then faith has no effect and the promise has been canceled.
local: An yaa sá tí nīn tɔ́ marab ní kí fōō tin Unimbɔti pōoǹ-tee, mimmee fookii ŋá fám dee, ní Unimbɔti poondi kaa ti bí tiban.

eng: The Law brings about wrath. But when there isnt any law, there isnt any violation of the law.
local: Di sá yii marab cāabìń ditafadaaldi ní, ní laakin marab kpée, marab yìntitam mun kpá.

eng: Thats why the inheritance comes through faith, so that it will be on the basis of Gods grace. In that way, the promise is secure for all of Abrahams descendants, not just for those who are related by Law but also for those who are related by the faith of Abraham, who is the father of all of us.
local: Mimmee, ti ká yii ti nín fōō Unimbɔti ki kííyee pu ní ti fòol̄ tin u pòontí-tee. An sá Unimbɔti ŋanti pu ní an bí míǹ ki tīī Abraam maaliŋun yab kɔkɔ. An kaa sá ki tīī bin tɔ́ Mooyiisi marabee ŋmaniŋma, an sá ki kpáaǹ bin kɔkɔ fōō Unimbɔti ki kíí kii Abraam nín nìn fōō ki kíí puee. Timbi bin kɔkɔ fōō ki kííyee baa di sá Abraam.

eng: As it is written: I have appointed you to be the father of many nations. So Abraham is our father in the eyes of God in whom he had faith, the God who gives life to the dead and calls things that dont exist into existence.
local: Unimbɔti lī u-gbɔŋku poon míǹ ní yii: «M dū-si ki kpántì atimbun tikpil baa.» Unimbɔti un fìkitī bitaŋkpiibi ki wāĺ tin kaa láá bée kii tì bée, Unimbɔti un pu Abraam nìn mákée yāntì u sá ti-baa.

eng: When it was beyond hope, he had faith in the hope that he would become the father of many nations, in keeping with the promise God spoke to him: Thats how many descendants you will have. .
local: Abraam nìn fōō ki kíí ní ki máká, nì min kɔkɔ dimákal diba nìn pée kpée, ní ki lafun dūu kpántì atimbun tikpil baa. Kii Unimbɔti nín nìn pée tùkū-u puee yii: «A-maaliŋun yab nín làá nīn wīikí min nyɔkɔmee dee.».

eng: Without losing faith, Abraham, who was nearly years old, took into account his own body, which was as good as dead, and Sarahs womb, which was dead.
local: U nìn púĺ ki bàn̄ abin dilaataal. U yaa kpēē u-bee, u-wunti pēefì, ní u yaa tí kpēē u-nimpuu Saarayee, u mun kpántì unimpunaati. Bi-liitil nìn jítì mmaal pu, ama ní u-mákal dáá gbíntìi pɔ̄ɔ̄.

eng: Because if Abraham was made righteous because of his actions, he would have had a reason to brag, but not in front of God.
local: An yaa sá Abraam tunnyaaŋŋi pu ní Unimbɔti yíiń-u deedeedaanee, u bàà ŋūǹ kí nīn dàkà u-ba. Ama waa ŋūǹ kí dàkà u-ba Unimbɔti nimbiin.

eng: He didnt hesitate with a lack of faith in Gods promise, but he grew strong in faith and gave glory to God.
local: Waa fá u-mákal kí dàkafì yii Unimbɔti kaa ŋūǹ kí ŋá tin u pōoǹee, ama u-mákal tīī-u mpɔn ní u nyɔ́ŋkì Unimbɔti.

eng: He was fully convinced that God was able to do what he promised.
local: ki béè u-pɔbilin yii, ibaamɔn, Unimbɔti cáá mpɔn ki làá ŋá min u pōoǹee.

eng: Therefore, it was credited to him as righteousness.
local: An pu ní Unimbɔti yíiń-u deedeedaan.

eng: But the scripture that says it was credited to him wasnt written only for Abrahams sake.
local: An kaa sá úǹ baba pu ní an ŋmàn̄ yii Unimbɔti yíiń-u deedeedaan.

eng: It was written also for our sake, because it is going to be credited to us too. It will be credited to those of us who have faith in the one who raised Jesus our Lord from the dead.
local: An sá ti mun pu, kun puee Unimbɔti kpèé-ti yii ti mun sá deedeedambi dimákal din ti cáá úǹ Unimbɔti un fīkitì ti-Dindaan Yeesu ditaŋkpiilinee pu.

eng: He was handed over because of our mistakes, and he was raised to meet the requirements of righteousness for us.
local: Ti-kpitii pu ní Yeesu kpíì, ní ki fīkitì, ti mun ń ŋmā kí kpántí deedeedambi Unimbɔti nimbiin.

eng: What does the scripture say? Abraham had faith in God, and it was credited to him as righteousness .
local: Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon lī yii ba ní? An lī yii: «Abraam nìn fōō Unimbɔti ki kíí, ní Unimbɔti ká u-fookiiu un u cáá u-puee ní ki yíiń-u deedeedaan.».

eng: Workers salaries arent credited to them on the basis of an employers grace but rather on the basis of what they deserve.
local: Unil un túń ditundee, bi yaa pàn̄-uee, baa lì yii bi pú-u fám, ama u-tundi paati ní u fōō.

eng: But faith is credited as righteousness to those who dont work, because they have faith in God who makes the ungodly righteous.
local: Un kaa ŋá diba, ama ní ki fōō Unimbɔti un di yàntī binikpitib kpántí deedeedambee ki kííyee, Unimbɔti yíì udaan deedeedaan u-fookiiu pu ní.

eng: In the same way, David also pronounces a blessing on the person to whom God credits righteousness apart from actions.
local: An di yāntì ní Daafiidi nìn lī yii un bí dipɔɔpindinee di sá unil un Unimbɔti yíiń deedeedaan an kaa sá u-tuuŋŋi puee.

eng: Happy are those whose actions outside the Law are forgiven, and whose sins are covered. .
local: «Bin bin Unimbɔti dū bi-yìntitam ki pú-bi ki pítì bi-kpitiiyee bí dipɔɔpindinaaa!.

eng: Happy are those whose sin isnt counted against them by the Lord .
local: Un ní ti-Dindaan kaa ti lì yii u ŋá ikpitee bí dipɔɔpindinaaa!».

eng: Is this state of happiness only for the circumcised or is it also for those who arent circumcised? We say, Faith was credited to Abraham as righteousness.
local: Mimmee, dipɔɔpindi gbanti sá ki tīī Juuda yab bin gífì dikpancandee ŋmaniŋma, kaa sá ki tīī bin kíǹeeee? Ti dáá ká yii: «Abraam nín fōō Unimbɔti ki kííyee pu ní Unimbɔti yíiń-u deedeedaan.».

eng: Therefore, since we have been made righteous through his faithfulness, we have peace with God through our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Mimmee, ti ká yii ti nín fōō-u ki kííyee pu ní ti kpántì deedeedambi Unimbɔti nimbiin, ní ti nì Unimbɔti kansikin ŋáŋkì ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo pu.

eng: If we were reconciled to God through the death of his Son while we were still enemies, now that we have been reconciled, how much more certain is it that we will be saved by his life?.
local: Dooooyee, ti nìn dáá sá Unimbɔti nannanliibi ní. U-Jipɔɔn kúm di ŋáŋkì ti-kansikin, ní ti mɔ́ɔkì. Kɔŋkɔnnee, ti-kansikin mɔ́ɔkì ki dóò, ní u-Jipɔɔn bí dimaŋfalin, kin, án làá gíti ŋá mana ní taa làá ká difiil?.

eng: And not only that: we even take pride in God through our Lord Jesus Christ, the one through whom we now have a restored relationship with God.
local: Tiba mun gíti sɔ̀: ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo yāntì ti nì Unimbɔti kansikin ŋáŋkèe, ní ti bí ki fàal̀ Unimbɔti pu.

eng: Just as through one human being sin came into the world, and death came through sin, so death has come to everyone, since everyone has sinned.
local: Unibaanti Adam pu ní ikpiti dī ki kɔ́nní duulinyan, ní ikpitee cáań ŋkúm, ní báà ŋma kúù, kun puee báà ŋma ŋá ikpiti.

eng: Although sin was in the world, since there was no Law, it wasnt taken into account until the Law came.
local: Dooooyee, kí wàatī Unimbɔti ń nìn tīī Mooyiisi marabee, ikpiti nìn pée bí kitiŋ pu dee. Míǹ ní marab yaa kpá, baa ŋūǹ kí lì yii unil ŋá ikpiti.

eng: But death ruled from Adam until Moses, even over those who didnt sin in the same way Adam did—Adam was a type of the one who was coming.
local: Nì míǹ kɔkɔee, kí yòon̄ Adam yuntiŋu kí dāá sāā Mooyiisi yuntiŋuee, ŋkúm di nìn jíń báà ŋma, ki kpáaǹ binib bin kaa ŋá ikpiti kii Adam nín ŋá puee. Adam nìn sá Yeesu un dāa dɔ́miń boonee naŋku ní.

eng: But the free gift of Christ isnt like Adams failure. If many people died through what one person did wrong, Gods grace is multiplied even more for many people with the gift—of the one person Jesus Christ—that comes through grace.
local: Ama Adam yìntitam nín cáań-ti tiŋyee kaa pēē nì tin Unimbɔti pūǹ-ti faalaayee. Tiŋman, binib kupaau kpíì unibaanti Adam yìntitam pu, ama Unimbɔti nín ŋáań binib tiŋan puee, ní u dū diyimɔɔl bulifi ki pú binib kupaau unibaanti Yeesu Kriisitoo pu.

eng: The gift isnt like the consequences of one persons sin. The judgment that came from one persons sin led to punishment, but the free gift that came out of many failures led to the verdict of acquittal.
local: Unimbɔti pɔl nín tūǹ ditundi diŋyee nyáń di-ba nì unibaantee kpitii tundi. Unibaantee kpitii nín cáań-ti kun kuŋyee di sá ŋkúm. Unimbɔti ŋanti tin u pú-ti faalaayee nín cáań-ti kuŋyee di sá, a-kpitii yaa yāntì ki wīikí min kamaa, u làá dūú pɔ̄-si.

eng: If death ruled because of one persons failure, those who receive the multiplied grace and the gift of righteousness will even more certainly rule in life through the one person Jesus Christ.
local: Tiŋman, unibaanti yìntitam pu ní ŋkúm jíń báà ŋma. Ama Yeesu Kriisitoo ubaanti pu ní binib fōō tiŋan tin bulifi Unimbɔti ŋée. U pú-bi bí nīn bí deedee u-nimbiin, bidambi ń nīn cáá dimaŋfal kí jī dibeel.

eng: So now the righteous requirements necessary for life are met for everyone through the righteous act of one person, just as judgment fell on everyone through the failure of one person.
local: Kii Adam baba kpitii nín cáań nnibiyaamu kɔkɔ ŋkúm puee, Yeesu Kriisitoo tundi din u túń nì deedeeyee yāntì nnibiyaamu kɔkɔ ŋūǹ kí kpántí deedeedambi kí ká dimaŋfal.

eng: Many people were made righteous through the obedience of one person, just as many people were made sinners through the disobedience of one person.
local: Kii unibaanti yìntitam nín kɔ́ǹ binib tikpil ikpitin puee, míǹ ní unibaanti nín tɔ́ Unimbɔti puee di cāntì binib tikpil làá kpántí deedeedambi.

eng: We have access by faith into this grace in which we stand through him, and we boast in the hope of Gods glory.
local: Yeesu Kriisitoo puee ní ti ŋúń ki jéetì diyimɔɔl din poon ti bí nee, ti nín fōō-u ki kííyee pu. Ní kun mɔ̄ǹ-tee di sá ti cɔ́tí yii ti làá kɔ́ Unimbɔti nyootin.

eng: The Law stepped in to amplify the failure, but where sin increased, grace multiplied even more.
local: Mara úǹ dɔ́miń ikpiti ń wíiǹ ní. Ama ikpiti yaa yāntì ki wīikí min kamaa, Unimbɔti ŋanti wīikí ki jítiǹ-i.

eng: The result is that grace will rule through Gods righteousness, leading to eternal life through Jesus Christ our Lord, just as sin ruled in death.
local: An di yì-tì ní, kii ikpiti nín nìn jíń báà ŋma ki cáań ŋkúm puee, Unimbɔti ŋanti kíǹ ki jīǹ mpaan binib ń kpántí deedeedambi kí fōō dimaŋfal din kaa cáá dikúntilee ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo pu.

eng: But not only that! We even take pride in our problems, because we know that trouble produces endurance.
local: Áli gba, ti nín jīǹ falaa puee, an mɔ̄ǹ-ti, kun puee ti nyí yii falaa di yàntī unil ń pɔ́ɔǹ u-ba.

eng: endurance produces character, and character produces hope.
local: ní dipɔ́ɔǹdi ń yàntī unil ń ŋmā kí já kí nyā dinimbinaamandin, ní ki kíǹ ki kā ki cɔ́tí yii Unimbɔti nín lī puee, u làá ŋá míǹ.

eng: This hope doesnt put us to shame, because the love of God has been poured out in our hearts through the Holy Spirit, who has been given to us.
local: Taa cɔ́tí fám, kun puee Unimbɔti pú-ti Circir Fam min di gbēentí ti-pɔbiliŋ u-neendee.

eng: While we were still weak, at the right moment, Christ died for ungodly people.
local: Ti nín nìn dáá bí iwɔfineee, ní ŋyunti ŋun Unimbɔti yóoǹee bāań, ní Kriisitoo kpíì binikpitib pu.

eng: It isnt often that someone will die for a righteous person, though maybe someone might dare to die for a good person.
local: An pɔ̄ɔ̄ unil ń kíí kí kpú deedeedaan pu. An yaa yíì ní ni kāntí ubɔ ń kíí kí kpú unimɔnti pu.

eng: But God shows his love for us, because while we were still sinners Christ died for us.
local: Ama Unimbɔti dàkà-ti u nín néeń-ti min bulifee di sá yii, ŋyunti ŋun ti dáá sá binikpitibee, Kriisitoo kpíì ti-pu.

eng: So, now that we have been made righteous by his blood, we can be even more certain that we will be saved from Gods wrath through him.
local: U nín kpíìyee, ní u-fatikuu yāntì ti kpántì deedeedambi Unimbɔti nimbiin. Kin, an làá ŋá mana ní kɔŋkɔnnee waa làá ŋmā kí fīī-ti tí taa kɔ́ Unimbɔti ŋuulin?.

eng: So what are we going to say? Should we continue sinning so grace will multiply?.
local: Mimmee, tí lì yii ba? Tí nīn gbíntìi ŋáań ikpiti ní Unimbɔti ŋanti ń wíiǹaaa?.

eng: He died to sin once and for all with his death, but he lives for God with his life.
local: U nín kpíìyee, u kpíì diba pu ki kúntì ikpiti, ní kaa ti làá kpú jaanjaan. Kɔŋkɔnnee, u nín bí dimaŋfalinee, u bí ki tīī Unimbɔti ní.

eng: In the same way, you also should consider yourselves dead to sin but alive for God in Christ Jesus.
local: Míǹ mbaantiim, ni nì Yeesu Kriisitoo nín ŋá ubaanti puee, ni mun ń nīn nyí yii ni kpíì ikpiti jandi pú, ki kíǹ ki bí ki tīī Unimbɔti.

eng: So then, dont let sin rule your body, so that you do what it wants.
local: Mimmee, ikpiti ń taa ti jī ni-wunti tin bí ki làá dāá kpúee ni mpaan, ní taa nín̄ nóó ni-landɔkɔkpitii nín là puee.

eng: Dont offer parts of your body to sin, to be used as weapons to do wrong. Instead, present yourselves to God as people who have been brought back to life from the dead, and offer all the parts of your body to God to be used as weapons to do right.
local: Ní taa ti dū ni-wunti jaŋŋi kí tīī ikpiti ń nīn cáá-ŋi ki ŋáań min kaa bí deedeeyee. Dūmaan ni-ba kɔkɔ kí tīī Unimbɔti, kii binib bin kpíì ki fīkitèe, ú nīn cáá ni-wunti jaŋŋi ki tūǹ atuun ŋin bí deedeeyee.

eng: Sin will have no power over you, because you arent under Law but under grace.
local: Di sá yii ikpiti kaa ti máaǹ kí nīn jīǹ ni-pu mpaan, kun puee marab kaa ti cí-ni, Unimbɔti ŋanti di kíǹ ki cí-ni.

eng: So what? Should we sin because we arent under Law but under grace? Absolutely not!.
local: Mimmee, tí lì yii ba? Tí nīn gbíntìi ŋáań ikpiti kun puee marab kaa ti cí-ti, Unimbɔti ŋanti di kíǹ ki cí-tee puɔɔɔ? Aayee, kpataaa!.

eng: Dont you know that if you offer yourselves to someone as obedient slaves, that you are slaves of the one whom you obey? Thats true whether you serve as slaves of sin, which leads to death, or as slaves of the kind of obedience that leads to righteousness.
local: Naa nyí yii a yaa sīntiǹ a-ba taapu ki tīī unil ki tūǹ u-tundee, a kpántì uyumbu ní ki tīī udaan un a sīntiǹ a-ba ki tīīyeeee? A ŋūǹ kí sìntīǹ a-ba kí tīī ikpiti in làá cáá-si ŋkúminee, ki tí ŋūǹ kí sìntīǹ a-ba kí nīn tɔ́ Unimbɔti kí kpántí deedeedaan.

eng: But thank God that although you used to be slaves of sin, you gave wholehearted obedience to the teaching that was handed down to you, which provides a pattern.
local: Ama tí jāam̀maan Unimbɔti kun puee ni nìn sá ikpiti yumbui dooooyee, ní kɔŋkɔnnee ni sīntiǹ ni-pɔbiliŋ kɔkɔ ki tīī ibaamɔn in bi dàkā-ni kii bi nín dàkà an kɔkɔ puee.

eng: Now that you have been set free from sin, you have become slaves of righteousness.
local: Ni nyáń ikpiti yuŋkun ki kɔ́ deedee tuuŋŋi yuŋkun dee.

eng: Once, you offered the parts of your body to be used as slaves to impurity and to lawless behavior that leads to still more lawless behavior. Now, you should present the parts of your body as slaves to righteousness, which makes your lives holy.
local: Ni nín kaa gbìl̀ tibɔti taapu malaayee pu ní m dū kuyuŋu bɔti tin tin binib nyée ki dūu ŋá tibɔŋmantikaal. Di sá yii, dooooyee, ni nìn dū ni-wunti jaŋŋi ní ki tīī tijɔkɔndi, ki ŋáań min kaa kítèe ní ki mɔ̄tìi kɔ́ diyìntilin. Kɔŋkɔnnee, ní fàtīí dūmaan ni-wunti jaŋŋi kí tīī deedee tuuŋŋi kí kpántí circir nib.

eng: Absolutely not! All of us died to sin. How can we still live in it?.
local: Aayee, kpataaa! Timbi bin kpíì ikpiti jandi púee, mana ní ti gíti ŋūǹ kí nīn bí ki ŋáań ikpiti?.

eng: When you were slaves of sin, you were free from the control of righteousness.
local: Dooooyee, ŋyunti ŋun ni nìn sá ikpiti yumbuyee, ni nìn tɔ́ ni-ba tiba kaa páb́-ni ní ŋá min Unimbɔti lèe.

eng: What consequences did you get from doing things that you are now ashamed of? The outcome of those things is death.
local: Ba biliŋ ní ni nìn lòoǹ ní? Atuun ŋin ni nìn tūǹee, díǹ pu nee ŋi cáá-ni icoo, atuunee di cāabìń ŋkúm.

eng: But now that you have been set free from sin and become slaves to God, you have the consequence of a holy life, and the outcome is eternal life.
local: Kɔŋkɔnnee, ni nyánní ikpitin ki kpántì Unimbɔti yumbui. Abil ŋin ni lòoǹ niinee di sá circir tuuŋŋi, ní boonee, dimaŋfal din kaa cáá dikúntilee dɔ̄ ki cí-ni.

eng: The wages that sin pays are death, but Gods gift is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord.
local: Tipaal tin ikpiti pèe di sá ŋkúm, ama tin tin Unimbɔti pūǹ faalaayee di sá á ŋá ubaanti nì ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo kí ká dimaŋfal din kaa cáá dikúntilee.

eng: Or dont you know that all who were baptized into Christ Jesus were baptized into his death?.
local: Naa nyí yii, timbi bin kɔkɔ bi fùl̄ Unimbɔti nyim ti ŋá ubaanti nì Yeesu Kriisitooyee, ti kpáfì ki kpíì u-kúm min u kpíìyee níii?.

eng: Therefore, we were buried together with him through baptism into his death, so that just as Christ was raised from the dead through the glory of the Father, we too can walk in newness of life.
local: Kii bi nín fùl̄-ti Unimbɔti nyim puee, bi síb́-ti tí nīn bí ŋkúmin nì Yeesu dee, ní kii ti-Baa Unimbɔti nín dū u-pɔŋkpaaŋŋu ki fīkitì Yeesu Kriisitoo ditaŋkpiilin ki fātìi gíiǹní-u dimaŋfalin puee, mimmee, ti nì u kɔkɔ di kíǹ ki bí dimaŋfapɔndin.

eng: If we were united together in a death like his, we will also be united together in a resurrection like his.
local: Di sá yii timbi nì u yaa kpáfì ki kpíì u-kúmee, míǹ mbaantiim ní ti nì u kpáfì ki fīkitì difìkitibaantiil.

eng: This is what we know: the person that we used to be was crucified with him in order to get rid of the corpse that had been controlled by sin. That way we wouldnt be slaves to sin anymore.
local: Tí béémaan yii, ti nín nìn bí min min dooooyee, ti-bimbim gbanti kpíì nì Yeesu ndɔpuntikaa pu. Mimmee ti-bimbikpitim kpíì dee. Tí taa ti nín̄ sá ikpiti yumbui.

eng: because a person who has died has been freed from sins power.
local: Di sá yii un yaa kpíìyee, úǹ nyáń ikpiti ŋaan dee.

eng: But if we died with Christ, we have faith that we will also live with him.
local: Ti nì Kriisitoo yaa kpáfì ki kpíì, ti nyí yii ti nì u làá kpāfì kí nīn tí bí dimaŋfalin.

eng: We know that Christ has been raised from the dead and he will never die again. Death no longer has power over him.
local: Ti nyí yii, Kriisitoo nín kpíì ki fātìi fīkitèe, waa ti bí kí kpú ŋwindal, ŋkúm kaa ti cáá u-pu mpɔn.

eng: Brothers and sisters, Im talking to you as people who know the Law. Dont you know that the Law has power over someone only as long as he or she lives?.
local: Nnabiyaamu, m pàkā yii m bíi sòoǹ nì binib bin nyí marabee ní. Ni nyí yii mara cáá mpɔn unil pu ŋyunti ŋun u dáá bí dimaŋfalinee ŋmaniŋma ní.

eng: and I died. So the commandment that was intended to give life brought death.
local: ní man kpíì. Mimmee, marab bin bàà sá ukpootil kí cáá-mi dimaŋfalinee fātìi téé-mi ki tɔ́ ŋkúmin.

eng: Sin seized the opportunity through the commandment, deceived me, and killed me.
local: Ní ikpiti dī marab pu ki ká nsan ki kíil̀-mi ki kūū-mi marab gbanti pu.

eng: So the Law itself is holy, and the commandment is holy, righteous, and good.
local: Marab bi-ba bí circir, ní isaŋki in bi sáŋkí-tee mun bí circir ki bí deedee ki ŋān̄.

eng: So did something good bring death to me? Absolutely not! But sin caused my death through something good so that sin would be exposed as sin. That way sin would become even more thoroughly sinful through the commandment.
local: Mimmee, tin sá tiwammɔntiilee di cáań-mi ŋkúmaaa? Aayee, kpataaa! An sá ikpiti di cáań míǹ, ki dūu dīń tiwammɔntiil pu ki kūū-mi, ki dàkā i nín sá ikpiti iŋyee mbaamɔmin. Marab puee, ti béè yii ikpiti kaa lafun sàafì.

eng: We know that the Law is spiritual, but Im made of flesh and blood, and Im sold as a slave to sin.
local: Ti nyí yii marab lafun sá Unimbɔti wanti. Ama ní man sá nniwuŋgban, ikpiti dá-mi kuyuŋu.

eng: I dont know what Im doing, because I dont do what I want to do. Instead, I do the thing that I hate.
local: Maa nyí tin m ŋáańee, kun puee maa ŋáań tin m bàà là kí ŋée, ní ki ŋáań tin tin m ná, kaa bàà là kí ŋée.

eng: But if Im doing the thing that I dont want to do, Im agreeing that the Law is right.
local: M yaa bíi ŋáań tin tin maa là kí ŋée, mimmee m béè yii marab ŋān̄ dee.

eng: But now Im not the one doing it anymore. Instead, its sin that lives in me.
local: Mimmee, an dàkà yii an kaa sá man m-ba di bíi ŋáań tikpitil, an sá ikpiti in bí m-nee di ŋáań.

eng: I know that good doesnt live in me—that is, in my body. The desire to do good is inside of me, but I cant do it.
local: M nyí tiŋman yii tiŋan kaa bí m-ni, an dàkà yii m-wuntin. Nì min kɔkɔ m bàà cáá nlandɔkɔmɔnti kí ŋá tiŋanee, mpɔn kpá ḿ ŋmā kí ŋá.

eng: I dont do the good that I want to do, but I do the evil that I dont want to do.
local: Tiŋan tin m bàà là kí ŋée, maa ŋáań tíǹ, tikpitil tin maa bàà là kí ŋée, tíǹ ní m ŋáań.

eng: A married woman is united with her husband under the Law while he is alive. But if her husband dies, she is released from the Law concerning her husband.
local: Míǹ ní unimpu yaa mɔ́ǹ ticatee, mara bóob́-u ki nákáǹ u-cal ŋyunti ŋun u dáá bí dimaŋfalinee. U-cal yaa kpíìyee, mara bútì-u ki nákatiǹ u-cal un u nìn nákée dee.

eng: But if I do the very thing that I dont want to do, then Im not the one doing it anymore. Instead, it is sin that lives in me that is doing it.
local: Mimmee, m yaa bíi ŋáań tin tin maa bàà là kí ŋée, an kaa ti sá mama m-ba di bíi ŋáań, an sá ikpiti in bí m-nee di bíi ŋáań.

eng: So I find that, as a rule, when I want to do what is good, evil is right there with me.
local: M dáań ki ká tiba: m yaa bàà bí yii ḿ ŋá tiŋanee, tikpitil ní m ŋáań.

eng: I gladly agree with the Law on the inside.
local: M-pɔbilinee, Unimbɔti marab di fáatì-mi.

eng: but I see a different law at work in my body. It wages a war against the law of my mind and takes me prisoner with the law of sin that is in my body.
local: ama m ká yii mpɔn ŋubɔ bí m-wunti nì ti-jaŋŋin ki jáaǹ kun kun m-landɔkɔŋu fáatèe, ki dū-mi ki nyáfiǹ ikpiti pɔŋŋu ŋun bí m-wuntinee.

eng: Im a miserable human being. Who will deliver me from this dead corpse?.
local: Man nimbinaamandi neeee! Tiwun nee dátí-mi ki cáǹ ŋkúmin, ŋma di làá fīī-mi kí líí?.

eng: Thank God through Jesus Christ our Lord! So then Im a slave to Gods Law in my mind, but Im a slave to sins law in my body.
local: Tí jāam̀maan Unimbɔti ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo un fìil̄-tee pu! Mimmee, m-landɔkɔŋu poonee, m bàà là kí nóó Unimbɔti marab, ama iwɔfi in bí m-wuntinee là kí nóó ikpiti.

eng: So then, if she lives with another man while her husband is alive, shes committing adultery. But if her husband dies, shes free from the Law, so she wont be committing adultery if she marries someone else.
local: Mimmee, unimpu cal yaa dáá bí, kaa kpíì, u yaa yíkìi mɔ́ǹ ubɔee, bi ŋūǹ kí yíiń-u kinimpulaakpaai. U-cal yaa kpíìyee, mara un bóob́-u ki nákáǹ u-calee cúu bútì dee, u ŋūǹ kí mɔ̄ǹ uninjapɔɔn ubɔ. Niinee, baa ti yíì-u kilaakpaai.

eng: Therefore, my brothers and sisters, you also died with respect to the Law through the body of Christ, so that you could be united with someone else. You are united with the one who was raised from the dead so that we can bear fruit for God.
local: M-nabiyaamu, míǹ mbaantiim, ni nín kpáfì ki kpíì nì Kriisitooyee, ni-kúmee yākatì ni nì marab kansikin. Kɔŋkɔnnee, ni kíǹ ki sá Kriisitoo un fīkitì ditaŋkpiilinee yab ní. Tí lòoǹ abimɔnti kí tīī Unimbɔti.

eng: When we were self-centered, the sinful passions aroused through the Law were at work in all the parts of our body, so that we bore fruit for death.
local: Dooooyee, ti nín nìn dáá bí ki ŋáań ti-ba lammee, marab nìn tútúǹ ti-landɔkɔkpitii in téekí-ti, ti tūǹ atuun ŋin cáǹ-ti ŋkúminee.

eng: But now we have been released from the Law. We have died with respect to the thing that controlled us, so that we can be slaves in the new life under the Spirit, not in the old life under the written Law.
local: Dooooyee, marab nìn cúú-ti kuyuŋu, ama kɔŋkɔnnee ti yākatì tɔbin, baa ti cáá ti-pu mpɔn, ti tɔ́ ti-ba. Míǹ puee, ti ŋūǹ kí tɔ̄ ditundi min ní Circir Fam kpòotī-tee, an kaa ti sá min min kugbɔŋu poon marab nìn kpòotī-ti dooooyee.

eng: So what are we going to say? That the Law is sin? Absolutely not! But I wouldnt have known sin except through the Law. I wouldnt have known the desire for what others have if the Law had not said, Dont desire to take what others have .
local: Mimmee, tí lì yii ba? Marab sá ikpiti deeyaaa? Aayee kpataaa! An yaa kaa sá marab pu, maa bàà nyí kun di sá ikpitee. Di sá yii marab yaa kaan lī yii: «Á taa nín̄ cáá nnimbitaataam», maa bàà nyí kun bi yíì nnimbitaataamee.

eng: But sin seized the opportunity and used this commandment to produce all kinds of desires in me. Sin is dead without the Law.
local: Marab lī mimmee, ní tikpitil ká nsan kí kɔ́ǹ-mi tiwan kɔkɔ nnimbitaataam. Di sá yii, marab yaa kpée, ikpiti kaa cáá mpɔn.

eng: I used to be alive without the Law, but when the commandment came, sin sprang to life.
local: Doooo min marab nìn kpée, m nìn bí dimaŋfalin. Ama marab nín bāańee, ikpiti dɔ́miń ki fōō mpaan.

eng: So now there isnt any condemnation for those who are in Christ Jesus.
local: Kɔŋkɔnnee, bin nì Yeesu Kriisitoo ŋá ubaantee, dibɔkpil diba kaa ti dɔ̄ ki cí-bi.

eng: If Christ is in you, the Spirit is your life because of Gods righteousness, but the body is dead because of sin.
local: Kriisitoo yaa bí a-ni, nì min kɔkɔ ikpiti puee a-wunti làá kpúee, Circir Fam làá tīī-si dimaŋfal, kun puee a bí deedee.

eng: If the Spirit of the one who raised Jesus from the dead lives in you, the one who raised Christ from the dead will give life to your human bodies also, through his Spirit that lives in you.
local: Unimbɔti un fīkitì Yeesu Kriisitoo ditaŋkpiilinee Fam yaa kóò a-nee, Unimbɔti gbanti un fīkitì Kriisitoo ditaŋkpiilinee làá dāá yàntī u-Fam min kóò a-nee ń tīī a-wunti tin kúùyee dimaŋfal.

eng: So then, brothers and sisters, we have an obligation, but it isnt an obligation to ourselves to live our lives on the basis of selfishness.
local: Mimmee, m-nabiyaamu, taa ti cáá ti-bimbikpitim dipɔndi diba, kí nīn ŋáań mì nín là puee.

eng: If you live on the basis of selfishness, you are going to die. But if by the Spirit you put to death the actions of the body, you will live.
local: Ni yaa gbíntì ki ŋáań min ni-bimbikpitim lèe, ni kúù ní. Ama Circir Fam pɔŋŋu puee, ni yaa kūū tin ni-bimbikpitim là ní ŋée, ni kāntí dimaŋfal.

eng: All who are led by Gods Spirit are Gods sons and daughters.
local: Di sá yii bin kɔkɔwoooo Unimbɔti Fam kpòotēe sá Unimbɔti biyaamu ní.

eng: You didnt receive a spirit of slavery to lead you back again into fear, but you received a Spirit that shows you are adopted as his children. With this Spirit, we cry, Abba, Father.
local: Mimmee, naa ti máaǹ kí nīn gbíntìi fàŋkí kii iyumbuee, kun puee ni fōō Unimbɔti Fam min yāntì Unimbɔti fōō-ti ti yíì-u yii ti-Baayee.

eng: The same Spirit agrees with our spirit, that we are Gods children.
local: ní Circir Fam mi-ba tùkù ti-pɔbiliŋ poon yii ti sá Unimbɔti biyaamu.

eng: But if we are children, we are also heirs. We are Gods heirs and fellow heirs with Christ, if we really suffer with him so that we can also be glorified with him.
local: Ti nín sá u-biyaamu puee, ti fòol̄ u-gaajawanti. Ti nì Kriisitoo di dāa fòol̄-tì. Ti yaa kpáfì ki jīǹ falaayee, boonee ti nì u di dāa tí fòol̄ tinyool.

eng: I believe that the present suffering is nothing compared to the coming glory that is going to be revealed to us.
local: M nyí yii falaa un ti bíi jīǹ ti-yuntiŋu nee kaa wīikí ki bàn̄ Unimbɔti nyooti tin u làá dāá tīī-tee.

eng: The whole creation waits breathless with anticipation for the revelation of Gods sons and daughters.
local: Unimbɔti nín náań tiwan tin kɔkɔee jìkì ki cí ŋwiin ŋun Unimbɔti làá dāá tīī u-biyaamu tinyool báà ŋma ń kée.

eng: The law of the Spirit of life in Christ Jesus has set you free from the law of sin and death.
local: Di sá yii Circir Fam min tīī-ti dimaŋfal Yeesu Kriisitoo puee marau nyántì-ti ikpiti nì ŋkúm marau ŋaan.

eng: Creation was subjected to frustration, not by its own choice—it was the choice of the one who subjected it—but in the hope.
local: Unimbɔti wannaaŋkaati gbanti kɔkɔ ŋá fám. Kaa sá yii tì là, Unimbɔti di yāntì an ŋá míǹ. Nì min kɔkɔ tì kpántì fámee, tì máká yii.

eng: that the creation itself will be set free from slavery to decay and brought into the glorious freedom of Gods children.
local: tì làá dāá nyā ntimbiil gbanti yuŋkun kí kpāfì nì Unimbɔti biyaamu kí tɔ̄ ti-ba ní kí fōō tinyool.

eng: We know that the whole creation is groaning together and suffering labor pains up until now.
local: Ti nyí yii Unimbɔti wannaaŋkaati gbanti kɔkɔ bí falaa ni áli ki dāa sāā díǹ kii upɔdaan nín yáliŋkí imatiŋween puee.

eng: And its not only the creation. We ourselves who have the Spirit as the first crop of the harvest also groan inside as we wait to be adopted and for our bodies to be set free.
local: Ama an kaa sá tiwannáaŋkaal gbanti baba, ki kpáaǹ timbi bin Unimbɔti tìì u-Fam min yāntì ti dáká tin bí ki dòońee. Ti mun bí ki yáliŋkíǹ ti-ba ki cí ŋyunti ŋun ti làá kpántí Unimbɔti biyaamu mbaamɔminee, an dàkà yii ŋyunti ŋun u làá dāá fīī ti-wunti mun kí lííyee.

eng: We were saved in hope. If we see what we hope for, that isnt hope. Who hopes for what they already see?.
local: Mimmee, ti ká difiil, ama ní an dáá nín bí dimákalin. A yaa ká kun a mákée, an kaa ti sá dimákal, kun puee tiwan tin a fōōyee, a gíti ŋūǹ kí nīn máká ti-puɔɔɔ?.

eng: But if we hope for what we dont see, we wait for it with patience.
local: Ti yaa máká tin taa láá kée puee, see tí jī kimɔtii kí nīn kpèé nsan pú.

eng: In the same way, the Spirit comes to help our weakness. We dont know what we should pray, but the Spirit himself pleads our case with unexpressed groans.
local: Míǹ mbaantiim ní Circir Fam bí ki tútúǹ-ti ti-wɔfii poon, kun puee taa nyí min máaǹ tí mèèyee, ama Ŋfamee mi-ba yáliŋkí ki jítiǹ anyɔɔbun nín ŋūǹ kí sòoǹ tibɔti tiŋyee ki mèé Unimbɔti ti-pu.

eng: The one who searches hearts knows how the Spirit thinks, because he pleads for the saints, consistent with Gods will.
local: Ní Unimbɔti un wāĺ binib pɔbiliŋ poonee béetí kun Ŋfam lèe, di sá yii kun mì mèéyee nóó nì kun Unimbɔti là kí tīī u-yabee ní.

eng: We know that God works all things together for good for the ones who love God, for those who are called according to his purpose.
local: Ní ti nyí yii tin kɔkɔ pée bàń bin néeń Unimbɔtee, Unimbɔti cáa kpántí tiŋan ní ki tìì-bi. Bíǹ ní u bɔ́ntì ki lēē ki síiǹ yii bí nīn sá u-yab.

eng: We know this because God knew them in advance, and he decided in advance that they would be conformed to the image of his Son. That way his Son would be the first of many brothers and sisters.
local: Bin u gāntì dooooyee ní u lēē ki yóoǹ bí kpántí kii u-Jipɔɔn nín bí puee, án nín làá ŋá pu u-Jipɔɔnee ń nīn sá ukpil u-nabiyaamu mun sá kunipaauee kansikinee.

eng: God has done what was impossible for the Law, since it was weak because of selfishness. God condemned sin in the body by sending his own Son to deal with sin in the same body as humans, who are controlled by sin.
local: Di sá yii Mooyiisi marab kaa nìn ŋúń ki ŋá míǹ, kun puee binib bí iwɔfin, kaa ŋūǹ kí nóó marabee. Ama ní Unimbɔti dū úǹ u-ba Jipɔɔn ki túnní, u dɔ́miń ki cáá tiwumbaantiil kii timbi bikpitibee, ki ŋúń ki lēē ikpiti. Unimbɔti ŋá míǹ ki kúntì mpɔn ŋun ikpiti cáá binib puee.

eng: Those who God decided in advance would be conformed to his Son, he also called. Those whom he called, he also made righteous. Those whom he made righteous, he also glorified.
local: Bin u lēē dooooyee ki yóoǹee, u yíiń-bi. Bin u yíińee, ní u yāntì bi kpántì deedeedambi Unimbɔti nimbiin, ní bin u yāntì bi kpántì deedeedambee, u kɔ́ǹ-bi u-nyootin.

eng: So what are we going to say about these things? If God is for us, who is against us?.
local: Ti làá gíti lì tibɔti nee pu yii ba dee? Unimbɔti yaa yú ti-boonee, ŋma di gíti ŋūǹ kí ŋá-ti tiba?.

eng: He didnt spare his own Son but gave him up for us all. Wont he also freely give us all things with him?.
local: Timbi pu ní Unimbɔti kaa tāaǹ úǹ u-ba Jipɔɔn ki dū-u ki ŋá saraa, mana ní u ŋūǹ kí yíí kí taa tīī-ti tikɔkɔ kí kūtì u-Jipɔɔnee pu?.

eng: Who will bring a charge against Gods elect people? It is God who acquits them.
local: Unimbɔti bí yii bin u lútèe sá deedeedambi, kin, ŋma di gíti ŋūǹ kí ká tikpitil tiba bi-pu kí sòoǹ?.

eng: Who is going to convict them? It is Christ Jesus who died, even more, who was raised, and who also is at Gods right side. It is Christ Jesus who also pleads our case for us.
local: Yeesu Kriisitoo un kpíì ki kpáàa fīkitì mun ki kā Unimbɔti ŋaŋgiil púee bí ki mèé ki tìì-ti. Kin ŋma di gíti ŋūǹ kí kpɔ̀ ti-bɔti?.

eng: Who will separate us from Christs love? Will we be separated by trouble, or distress, or harassment, or famine, or nakedness, or danger, or sword?.
local: Dineendi din Kriisitoo cáá ti-puee, tiba ŋūǹ kí yàkatī ti-kansikinaaa? Falaa di ŋūǹ kí yàkatī-taaa, dinimbinaamandaaa, disàandaaa, ŋkùmaaa, disoolaaa, mbusuaaa, ŋkúm?.

eng: As it is written, We are being put to death all day long for your sake. We are treated like sheep for slaughter. .
local: Kii Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poon nín lī puee yii: «Sii ti-Dindaan pu ní, kí yòon̄ kutaafaau kí dāá mɔ́-kuee, ti tūŋkí ŋkúm, ní bi ŋáań-ti kii ipii in bi cáǹ kí kpɔ̀ee.».

eng: But in all these things we win a sweeping victory through the one who loved us.
local: Tíǹ kɔkɔ poonee, un néeń-tee tútúǹ-ti ti jáà ki jīǹ ki kúntí tikɔkɔ.

eng: Im convinced that nothing can separate us from Gods love in Christ Jesus our Lord: not death or life, not angels or rulers, not present things or future things, not powers.
local: M nyí ibaamɔn poon yii tiba kaa ŋūǹ kí yàkatī dineendi din bí timbi nì ti-Dindaan kansikinee: ŋkúmaaa, dimaŋfalaaa, Unimbɔti tuuŋyaaa, an yaa tí sá abeedambi bin baa wāĺeeyaaa, tin tin poon ti bí kɔŋkɔnneeyaaa, tin bí ki dòońeeyaaa, mpɔndambaaa.

eng: or height or depth, or any other thing that is created.
local: an yaa sá tin bí yilpuee nì tin bí taapueeyaaa, an yaa tí sá tiwannáaŋkaal tin kɔkɔ kíǹeeyaaa, báà tiba nì tiba kaa ŋūǹ kí yàkatī timbi nì dineendi din Unimbɔti dī ti-Dindaan Yeesu Kriisitoo pu ki dàkā-tee.

eng: He did this so that the righteous requirement of the Law might be fulfilled in us. Now the way we live is based on the Spirit, not based on selfishness.
local: Mimmee, timbi bin kaa nóó mbimbikpitim ama ní ki nóó Circir Fam nín kpòotī-ti puee, ti kpántí deedeedambi kii marab nín là puee dee.

eng: People whose lives are based on selfishness think about selfish things, but people whose lives are based on the Spirit think about things that are related to the Spirit.
local: Bin bin nóó mbimbikpitimee nyàab̀ kí nīn ŋáań tin fáatì-bee ní, ní bin nóó Circir Famee mun nyàab̀ kí nīn ŋáań tin Circir Fam lèe.

eng: The attitude that comes from selfishness leads to death, but the attitude that comes from the Spirit leads to life and peace.
local: Mbimbikpitim nín là tiwan tiŋyee di cāabìń ŋkúm. Circir Fam nín là kun kuŋyee di cāabìń mmaŋfafɔkɔn nì ŋgbansɔŋfi.

eng: So the attitude that comes from selfishness is hostile to God. It doesnt submit to Gods Law, because it cant.
local: Bin nóó bi-bimbikpitim nín là puee sá Unimbɔti nannanliibi ní. Baa sìntīǹ bi-ba ki tìì Unimbɔti marab, kaa pée ŋūǹ gba.

eng: People who are self-centered arent able to please God.
local: Bin nóó bi-bimbikpitim nín là puee kaa ŋūǹ kí fáatíǹ Unimbɔti.

eng: But you arent self-centered. Instead you are in the Spirit, if in fact Gods Spirit lives in you. If anyone doesnt have the Spirit of Christ, they dont belong to him.
local: Ama nimbee, nimbi kaa nóó ni-bimbikpitim nín là puee. Min min Unimbɔti Fam lèe, míǹ ní ni nóó, kun puee u-Fam kóò ni-ni. Un yaa kaa cáá Kriisitoo Famee, kaa sá u-yɔu.

eng: Im speaking the truth in Christ—Im not lying, as my conscience assures me with the Holy Spirit.
local: M sòoǹ ibaamɔn kii Kriisitoo yɔu nín máaǹ kí sòoǹ puee ní. Maa mɔ́ntí, Circir Fam mun sòoǹ m-pɔbil poon yii an sá tiŋman.

eng: Not only that, but also Rebecca conceived children with one man, our ancestor Isaac.
local: Tiba mun gíti sɔ̀: Reebeeka mun bɔti bí. U màl̄ ti-naanja Isaaki mbininjabiyaamu muli ní.

eng: When they hadnt been born yet and when they hadnt yet done anything good or bad, it was shown that Gods purpose would continue because it was based on his choice.
local: Kí wàatī ú màal̀ mbiyaamu gbantee, muaa láá nyáń ki ŋá tiŋanaaa, kaa láá ŋá ikpiti, ní Unimbɔti tùkū Reebeeka yii: «Ukpil di làá dāá nīn bí kiwaai taapu,» án ŋmā kí nóó nì Unimbɔti nín lēē ki síiǹ u-ban puee. Míǹ ní u yíì binib, kaa kpèé bi-tūntɔtam pu.

eng: It wasnt because of what was done but because of Gods call. This was said to her: The older child will be a slave to the younger one .
local: Kí wàatī ú màal̀ mbiyaamu gbantee, muaa láá nyáń ki ŋá tiŋanaaa, kaa láá ŋá ikpiti, ní Unimbɔti tùkū Reebeeka yii: «Ukpil di làá dāá nīn bí kiwaai taapu,» án ŋmā kí nóó nì Unimbɔti nín lēē ki síiǹ u-ban puee. Míǹ ní u yíì binib, kaa kpèé bi-tūntɔtam pu.

eng: As it is written, I loved Jacob, but I hated Esau .
local: kii an nín ŋmàn̄ puee, yii: «M néeń Jaakɔb, ní kaa là Eesayuu.».

eng: So what are we going to say? Isnt this unfair on Gods part? Absolutely not!.
local: Ti yaa kpēē an bɔtee, ti làá lì yii Unimbɔti kaa sá ibaamɔndaanaaa? Aayee, kpataaa!.

eng: He says to Moses, Ill have mercy on whomever I choose to have mercy, and Ill show compassion to whomever I choose to show compassion .
local: Di sá u nìn tùkū Mooyiisi yii: «Un saai m yaa là kí sɔ̄ee, úǹ saai ní m sūǹ, ní un m yaa là kí ŋá tiŋanee, úǹ ní m ŋáań tiŋan.».

eng: So then, it doesnt depend on a persons desire or effort. It depends entirely on God, who shows mercy.
local: Mimmee, an kaa nyēeń unil nín là pueeyaaa, kaa nyēeń u-pɔ́ɔǹtam pu, Unimbɔti di sūǹ unil saai kí fīī udaan.

eng: Scripture says to Pharaoh, I have put you in this position for this very thing: so I can show my power in you and so that my name can be spread through the entire earth. .
local: Unimbɔti gbɔŋku poonee, ti kāntí yii Unimbɔti tùkū Eejipiti bɔtiu yii: «Kun pu m baasii kāaǹ-si dibeelee di sá ḿ dī a-pu kí dàkà m-pɔŋŋu kí yàntī ntimu nib kɔkɔ ń béé m-yindi.».

eng: So then, God has mercy on whomever he wants to, but he makes resistant whomever he wants to.
local: An dàkà yii Unimbɔti yaa là kí fīī binibaaa, u yaa tí là kí nyìŋfì bi-pɔbiliŋaaa, an bí úǹ cee ní.

eng: So you are going to say to me, Then why does he still blame people? Who has ever resisted his will.
local: An yaa yíì, ubɔ ŋūǹ kí bàlifì-mi yii: «Kin mimmee, ba pu ní Unimbɔti gíti wòoǹ nnibiyaamu? Di sá ubɔ kaa yíití min Unimbɔti lèe.».

eng: I have great sadness and constant pain in my heart.
local: yii m-pɔbil poon gbí kunimbaakpaaŋŋu ní m yáliŋkí baabadal.

eng: You are only a human being. Who do you think you are to talk back to God? Does the clay say to the potter, Why did you make me like this? .
local: M làá kíí udaan yii: «Unil, a sá ŋma ní ki kpākantí Unimbɔti? Tiwan tin bi máńee kaa ŋūǹ kí tùkù un máń-tèe yii: <Ba pu ní a máń-mi yéè?>».

eng: Doesnt the potter have the power over the clay to make one pot for special purposes and another for garbage from the same lump of clay?.
local: Uyɔɔmaati ŋūǹ kí dū kuyɔkɔu kí mā kun kun u yaa lèe: u ŋūǹ kí dū-ku kí mā dibɔmɔntiil, ki tí ŋūǹ kí dūú mā kinyinsaŋ.

eng: What if God very patiently puts up with pots made for wrath that were designed for destruction, because he wanted to show his wrath and to make his power known?.
local: Kin Unimbɔti fáà? U cáá nsan kí gíí diŋuul kí dàkà binib u-pɔŋŋu. U jíń kimɔtii tikpil bin bàà máaǹ ditafadaaldi ki ŋmāntì ú kúntí-bee pu.

eng: What if he did this to make the wealth of his glory known toward pots made for mercy, which he prepared in advance for glory?.
local: ní ki kpáàa là kí dàkà u-nyooti nín wīikí pu tikpil tikpil ki tīī bin u fīī ki bɔ́ntì ki yóoǹ bí kɔ́ u-nyootinee.

eng: We are the ones God has called. We dont come only from the Jews but we also come from the Gentiles.
local: Timbi bin u lútì ki yíiń Juuda yab nì bin kaa sá Juuda yabee kansikinee kɔkɔee, ti mun kútì poon.

eng: As it says also in Hosea, I will call my people those who arent my people, and the one who isnt well loved, I will call loved one. .
local: Míǹ ní u lī Ooseeya gbɔŋku poon yii: «Bin kaa nìn sá m-nibulee làá dāá nīn kíǹ ki sá m-nibul, ní ditimbundi din maa nìn néeńee, m làá nīn kíǹ ki néeń-dì tikpil.

eng: And in the place where it was said to them, You arent my people, there they will be called the living Gods children .
local: Ní laakin bi nìn tùkū-bi yii: <Naa sá man nibulee>, niin ní bi làá yíiń-bi yii Unimbɔti un bí dimaŋfalinee jipɔmbi.».

eng: But Isaiah cries out for Israel, Though the number of Israels children will be like the sand of the sea, only a remaining part will be saved, .
local: Unimbɔti bɔnaatiliu Yeesaya mun báakì ki sòon̄ Isirayeel yab bɔti yii: «Báà Isirayeel naantiibi yaa wīikí kii dibul din bí tiŋku gbandee, bi-kansikin binib fiii di làá kíń kí ŋmátí.

eng: because the Lord does what he says completely and quickly. .
local: Di sá yii ti-Dindaan làá ŋá tin tin kɔkɔ u sòon̄ ki síiǹ yii u làá ŋá kitiŋ puee malaa.».

eng: As Isaiah prophesied, If the Lord of the heavenly forces had not left descendants for us, we would have been like Sodom, and we would have become like Gomorrah. .
local: Yeesaya gbanti di nìn gíti sòon̄ tibɔti nee ki yóoǹ dooooyee, yii: «Ti-Dindaan Mpɔŋkɔdaan yaa kaa bàà kíǹ ti-maaliŋu poon binib bibee, ti bàà kpántì kii Soodoom tiŋkee ní, ki bàà kíǹ ki náań nì Goomoora tiŋkee.».

eng: I wish I could be cursed, cut off from Christ if it helped my brothers and sisters, who are my flesh-and-blood relatives.
local: M yaan ŋūǹ Unimbɔti ń ŋá-mi fám, man nì Kriisitoo ń yàkatī tɔbin, m-nabiyaamu man nì bin pēē mmaalee ń ŋmā kí ká difiilee, m bàà là.

eng: So what are we going to say? Gentiles who werent striving for righteousness achieved righteousness, the righteousness that comes from faith.
local: Tibɔti tin ti sòon̄ee, ti làá kúntí-tì yii ba? Bin kaa sá Juuda yab, kaa nìn nyàab̀ kí nīn bí deedeeyee di kpántì deedeedambi Unimbɔti nimbiin, kun puee bi fōō-u ki kíí.

eng: But though Israel was striving for a Law of righteousness, they didnt arrive.
local: Juuda yab bin nìn nyàab̀ kí nóó marab kí ŋmā kí kpántí deedeedambee kaa ŋúń ki ŋúb́ marab gbanti.

eng: Why? Its because they didnt go for it by faith but they went for it as if it could be reached by doing something. They have tripped over a stumbling block.
local: Ba yì-tì? Kun puee bi dàkafī yii bi-tuuŋŋi pu ní bi ŋūǹ kí nīn bí deedee, kaa nyàab̀ kí fōō Unimbɔti kí kíí kí nīn bí deedee. Bi gbéetì ditaŋkpal.

eng: As it is written: Look! Im putting a stumbling block in Zion, which is a rock that offends people. And the one who has faith in him will not be put to shame. .
local: din pu Unimbɔti gbɔŋku nìn ŋmàn̄ee yii: «Kpèè, m bíl̀ ditaŋkpagbéetikaal Siiyɔn, ditaŋkpal din líikí binibee. Un yaa fōō-dì ki kííyee, icoo kaa làá jī udaan.».

eng: They are Israelites. The adoption as Gods children, the glory, the covenants, the giving of the Law, the worship, and the promises belong to them.
local: M-nabiyaamu gbanti di sá Isirayeel yab bin Unimbɔti lútì bí nīn sá u-biyaamuee. U nì u-walifiŋu nìn kóò bi-kansikin, ní u nì bi ŋá mpèé. U tīī-bi marab ki dàkā-bi min ní bi làá nīn jáaḿ-uee, ki pōoǹ-bi ipoon.

eng: The Jewish ancestors are theirs, and the Christ descended from those ancestors. He is the one who rules over all things, who is God, and who is blessed forever. Amen.
local: Bi-naanjab sá Jaakɔb jipɔmbi saalaa nì bilee ní. A yaa kpēē tinitanee, Kriisitoo mun sá bi-maaliŋu poon, Kriisitoo un di tɔ́kɔ́ tikɔkɔ nì tikɔkɔ pu ki sá Unimbɔti un máaǹ inyɔŋki baabadal kí nīn pée cá jaanjaanee! Ameeni.

eng: But its not as though Gods word has failed. Not all who are descended from Israel are part of Israel.
local: Isirayeel yab nín kaa fōō Yeesu ki kííyee, an kaa dàkà yii Unimbɔti poondi ŋá fám. Di sá yii an kaa sá bin kɔkɔ nyánní Isirayeel maaliŋunee di sá Isirayeel yab páaa.

eng: Not all of Abrahams children are called Abrahams descendants, but instead your descendants will be named through Isaac. .
local: Bin kɔkɔwoooo nyánní Abraam maaliŋunee kaa sá u-biyaamu páaa, kii Unimbɔti nín nìn sòon̄ nì Abraam puee yii: «See Isaaki naantiibi baba di làá fōō a-yindi.».

eng: That means it isnt the natural children who are Gods children, but it is the children from the promise who are counted as descendants.
local: An dàkà yii an kaa sá mmaal ŋun ti nyí nee di màal̀ Unimbɔti biyaamu. Abraam naantiibi bin Unimbɔti nìn pōoǹ ki tīīyee baba di sá bimɔntiibi.

eng: The words in the promise were: A year from now I will return, and Sarah will have a son .
local: kii Unimbɔti nín nìn pōoǹ-u puee yii: «Ŋyunti ŋun m yóoǹee yaa bāańee, m làá dāan̄, ní a-nimpuu Saara ń màal̀ kibininjabiki.».

eng: From Paul, a slave of God and an apostle of Jesus Christ. Im sent to bring about the faith of Gods chosen people and a knowledge of the truth that agrees with godliness.
local: Man Pɔɔl un sá Unimbɔti tɔntɔnliu ki sá Yeesu Kriisitoo kpambalee di ŋmàn̄ kugbɔŋu nee. Yeesu Kriisitoo di túnní-mi ḿ yàntī bin Unimbɔti lútì bí nīn sá u-yabee ń fōō-u kí kíí, kí béé nnimbɔtisan mmɔntiimin.

eng: In fact, there are many who are rebellious people, loudmouths, and deceivers, especially some of those who are Jewish believers.
local: Di sá yii binib tikpil bí ki gáatì ki cáá tibɔfan ki kíiĺ binib. Bin pée yì diyilee di sá Juuda yab kansikin biba.

eng: They must be silenced because they upset entire households. They teach what they shouldnt to make money dishonestly.
local: Á pìin̄-bi nsan bí taa ti sòoǹ, kun puee bi dàkà tin kaa máaǹ bí dàkèe, ki bíiĺ adicammɔn, ki dì icoo saŋyi pu míǹ kí ŋmā kí ká animbil.

eng: Someone who is one of their own prophets said, People from Crete are always liars, wild animals, and lazy gluttons.
local: Bi-bɔnaatiliu ubɔ, u sá ukreetija. Úǹ di nìn lī yii: «Kreeti tiŋki yab pée sá binyimɔndambi ní, ki cáá ntoom kii ipeecuncutee, ki sá biwutijoodambi, ki sá bipoofub.».

eng: This statement is true. Because of this, correct them firmly, so that they can be healthy in their faith.
local: Kijapaayee nín lī puee, waa mɔ́ntì. An puee, á kpìin̄-bi tiŋan án nín làá ŋá pu bi-fookiiu ń nīn sá ibaamɔn yɔuee.

eng: They shouldnt pay attention to Jewish myths and commands from people who reject the truth.
local: Bí yàntī titiŋkpikibɔti tin Juuda yab cáá ki máafée, nì binib bin fá ibaamɔn saŋŋu ki yōoǹ bíǹ bi-ba marabee bɔti.

eng: Everything is clean to those who are clean, but nothing is clean to those who are corrupt and without faith. Instead, their mind and conscience are corrupted.
local: Binib bin bí circiree, tikɔkɔ sá circir ní ki tīī-bi. Ama bin kaa fōō Yeesu ki kíí ki kɔ́ tijɔkɔndee, tiba kpá ki bí bíǹ cee circir, kun puee bi-landɔkɔi nì bi-ciiŋyi kɔ́ tijɔkɔndi.

eng: They claim to know God, but they deny God by the things that they do. They are detestable, disobedient, and disqualified to do anything good.
local: Bi gà bi-biŋŋi yii bi nyí Unimbɔti, ní bi-tuuŋŋi ŋin bi tūǹee dàkà yii baa nyí-u. Bi sá bikpitib ní ki gáatì, kaa ŋūǹ kí tɔ̄ ditummɔntiil báà diba.

eng: Their faith and this knowledge are based on the hope of eternal life that God, who doesnt lie, promised before time began.
local: Bí ŋmā kí nīn kíǹ ki máká dimaŋfal din kaa cáá dikúntilee pu. Doooo jaanjaan ní Unimbɔti un kaa mɔ́ntée pōoǹ yii u làá tīī-ti dimaŋfal gbanti.

eng: God revealed his message at the appropriate time through preaching, and I was trusted with preaching this message by the command of God our savior.
local: Ŋyunti bāańee, ní ti-Fiiliu Unimbɔti dī u-nyɔɔbundi pu ki nyántì tin u nìn pōoǹee ki dàkā-ti. Dinyɔɔbundee di sá din u dūu ŋúbiǹ-mi ki bí yii ḿ nīn sòoǹee.

eng: To Titus, my true child in a common faith. Grace and peace from God the Father and Christ Jesus our savior.
local: Sii Tiiti un kpántì m-ba bindi pu jipɔɔn ti-kɔkɔ nín fōō Yeesu ki kííyee puee ní m ŋmàn̄ kugbɔŋu nee kí tīī. Ti-Baa Unimbɔti nì Yeesu Kriisitoo ti-Fiiliu ń ŋá-si tiŋan, kí tīī-si ŋgbansɔŋfi.

eng: The reason I left you behind in Crete was to organize whatever needs to be done and to appoint elders in each city, as I told you.
local: M yāntì-si Kreeti tiŋkin yii á púntí kí bɔ́ntí ditundi din kíǹ niinee, kí kàan̄ bikpilib Kriisitoo nikitiŋ kansikin kitiŋ sá kamaa poon, kii m nín nìn pée tùkū-si puee.

eng: Elders should be without fault. They should be faithful to their spouse, and have faithful children who cant be accused of self-indulgence or rebelliousness.
local: Kriisitoo nikitil kpiliu máaǹ bí taa ká u-pu ibɔkpiti iba, kí nīn cáá unimpubaanti, u-biyaamu ń nīn sá bin fōō Yeesu ki kíí ki sá mun pu binib kaa sòoǹ yii mu-bimbim kaa ŋān̄, muaa tɔ́ binibee.

eng: This is because supervisors should be without fault as Gods managers: they shouldnt be stubborn, irritable, addicted to alcohol, a bully, or greedy.
local: Di sá yii un kpòotī Kriisitoo nikitilee tundi di sá ú nīn kpèé Unimbɔti tundi ń còom̀. An puee waa máaǹ bí ká u-pu ikpiti iba. Ú taa nín̄ sá ulambidaan nì uŋuudaan nì udanyɔgbil, kí taa nín̄ sá kílkil niliu nì un dì isaŋkpiti pu ki kāntí animbilee.

eng: Instead, they should show hospitality, love what is good, and be reasonable, ethical, godly, and self-controlled.
local: Ama ú nīn fòol̄ binicambi tiŋan, kí nīn néeń tin ŋān̄ee, kí nīn cáá iciin, kí nīn bí deedee, ki bí circir, kí nīn ŋūǹ ki ŋúb̀ u-ba.

eng: They must pay attention to the reliable message as it has been taught to them so that they can encourage people with healthy instruction and refute those who speak against it.
local: U máaǹ kí nīn kpáafí tibɔŋmanee án píkí kii bi nín dàkā-tì puee, án nín làá ŋá pu ú ŋmā kí nīn cáá tibɔŋman gbantee ki tìì binib kunyannyaauee, kí kpáàa dàkà bin mun kaa là tibɔti gbantee bi-yìntitamee.

eng: But you should talk in a way that is consistent with sound teaching.
local: Ama sii ń dàkà tibɔŋman kii tì nín sá puee.

eng: or steal. Instead, they should show that they are completely reliable in everything so that they might make the teaching about God our savior attractive in every way.
local: Í taa yɔ̀ i-dindambi wanti báà tiba, ama i máaǹ kí nīn dàkà i-deedeebítam mmɔntiimin iyunti kɔkɔ, kí ŋmā kí tīī Unimbɔti ti-Fiiliu bɔti tin bi dàkèe tinyool an kɔkɔ pu.

eng: The grace of God has appeared, bringing salvation to all people.
local: Unimbɔti ŋanti tin cáań binib kɔkɔ difiilee nyáń mpaampiin pu.

eng: It educates us so that we can live sensible, ethical, and godly lives right now by rejecting ungodly lives and the desires of this world.
local: U-ŋantee di dàkà-ti tí fá ti-bimbikpitim nì duulinya doo nimbitaataam. Ŋyunti ŋun poon ti bí nee, ti máaǹ kí nīn ŋúb́ ti-ba, kí nīn bí deedee ki nóó Unimbɔti mmɔntiimin.

eng: At the same time we wait for the blessed hope and the glorious appearance of our great God and savior Jesus Christ.
local: kí nīn cí dipɔɔpindi wiiŋŋu ŋun pu ti cɔ́tée. Ŋwiin gbanti ní Yeesu Kriisitoo, ti-Nimbɔtiu un sá-ti ukpaan ki sá-ti ufiilee, làá dāá nyāntí u-nyootee.

eng: He gave himself for us in order to rescue us from every kind of lawless behavior, and cleanse a special people for himself who are eager to do good actions.
local: Úǹ di dū u-ba ki tīī ki dá-ti ki nyántì ikpiti kɔkɔ poon, ní ki dū-ti ki kpántì dinikitil din sá úǹ baba yal, ki bí circir, ki cáá difaŋgiil ki tūǹ atummɔntee.

eng: Talk about these things. Encourage and correct with complete authority. Dont let anyone disrespect you.
local: A nín máaǹ kí dàkà miŋyee dee, kí tīī-bi kunyannyaau, kí kpìin̄-bi nì mpɔn, kí taa yàntī ubɔ ń kpèè kí sìŋkīǹ-si.

eng: Tell the older men to be sober, dignified, sensible, and healthy in respect to their faith, love, and patience.
local: kí tùkù bijaŋkpilib bí ŋúb́ bi-ba binib ń nīn tɔ́-bi. Bí nīn cáá iciin, bi-neendi nì bi-mɔtiiki nì bi-fookiiu ń nīn pɔ̄ɔ̄.

eng: Likewise, tell the older women to be reverent in their behavior, teaching what is good, rather than being gossips or addicted to heavy drinking.
local: Míǹ ní kí tùkù binimpucikitikaabee yii bi-bimbim ń nīn sá binib bin bí circiree bimbim. Baa máaǹ kí nīn sòoǹ ki bíiĺ binib, kí taa nín̄ sá ndaam yumbui, ama bí nīn sá bin tìì isaŋkimɔntee.

eng: That way they can mentor young women to love their husbands and children.
local: Mimmee, bi ŋūǹ kí dàkà binimpupɔmbee bí béé bi nín máaǹ kí nīn néeń bi-calib nì bi-biyaamu miŋyee.

eng: and to be sensible, morally pure, working at home, kind and submissive to their own husbands, so that Gods word wont be ridiculed.
local: Bí nīn cáá iciin kí nīn ŋúb́ bi-ba, kí nīn nyí kudii poon tundi tiŋan, kí nīn sá bilaŋŋaŋfitiibi, kí nīn tɔ́ bi-calib, binib ń taa ká bi-pu ikpiti iba kí nīn cáa sòoǹ ki bíiĺ Unimbɔti bɔti.

eng: Likewise, encourage the younger men to be sensible.
local: Míǹ mbaantiim, á sāŋkì binaacimpɔmbi yii bí nīn cáá mbimbimɔntiim.

eng: in every way. Offer yourself as a role model of good actions. Show integrity, seriousness.
local: Tikɔkɔ poonee, sii a-ba ń tɔ̄ atummɔnti kí dàkà báà ŋma bi mun ń nīn ŋáań míǹ. A-dàkatam ń nīn sá mbaamɔn ki kítì.

eng: and a sound message that is above criticism when you teach, so that any opponent will be ashamed because they wont find anything bad to say about us.
local: Á nīn sòoǹ anyɔɔbummɔnti ŋin pu baa ŋūǹ kí kpákáǹ-see, ní icoo ń cúú ti-nannanliibi, kun puee baa làá ká ti-pu ikpiti iba kí sòoǹ.

eng: Tell slaves to submit to their own masters and please them in everything they do. They shouldnt talk back.
local: Á sāŋkì iyumbu í nīn tɔ́ i-dindambi tikɔkɔ poon, kí nīn ŋáań tin ŋān̄ i-dindambi ceeyee, kí taa nín̄ lì min kaa fáatì-bee.

eng: Remind them to submit to rulers and authorities. They should be obedient and ready to do every good thing.
local: Á téetí-bi yii bi máaǹ kí sìntīǹ bi-ba kí tīī bikpilib bin sá gɔmina nì biyidambee. Bí nīn tɔ́-bi, ki yáb̀ atummɔnti kɔkɔ pu.

eng: After a first and second warning, have nothing more to do with a person who causes conflict.
local: Á kpāafì un cāabìń ni-kansikin diyakatilee mili pu, boonee, u yaa kaa gbìl̄ee, á nyāntí-u.

eng: because you know that someone like this is twisted and sinful—so they condemn themselves.
local: Di sá yii a nyí yii an niliuee fá nsammɔnti ki ŋá unikpiti, ní u-kpitiiyee dàkà yii u gáatì.

eng: When I send Artemas or Tychicus to you, try to come to me in Nicopolis, because Ive decided to spend the winter there.
local: M yaa láa dū Ariteemasi àá Tiisiiki ki túnní a-cee u bāań-see, á pɔ́ɔǹ a-ba kí kíntiń-mi Niikoopoolii, kun puee niin ní m là kí dāá nīn bí kuseeun.

eng: Help Zenas the lawyer and Apollos on their journey with enthusiasm so that they wont need anything.
local: Á pɔ́ɔǹ a-ba kí tūtuǹ Seenaa un sá umaranyilee nì Apooloosi, án nín làá ŋá pu tiba ń taa lóó-bi bi-saŋkindi poonee.

eng: But our people should also learn to devote themselves to doing good in order to meet pressing needs so they arent unproductive.
local: An máaǹ ti-yab mun ń dū bi-ba kí tīī atummɔnti tɔ̄tam, án nín làá ŋá pu tiba yaa bāań ki páb́ee, bí ŋmā kí tūtuǹee. Bí taa nín̄ kā, kaa kèetī diba.

eng: Everyone with me greets you; greet those who love us faithfully. Grace be with all of you.
local: Man nì bin kɔkɔ bí dooyee jáaḿ-si. Á jāam̀ bin fōō Yeesu ki kíí ki néeń-tee. Kriisitoo ŋanti ń nīn bí ni-kɔkɔ pu.

eng: They shouldnt speak disrespectfully about anyone, but they should be peaceful, kind, and show complete courtesy toward everyone.
local: Bí taa nín̄ sòoǹ ki bíiĺ binib, kí nīn sá ŋgbansɔŋfi nib, kí nīn ŋáań tiŋan, kí nīn mɔ̄ báà ŋma cee.

eng: We were once foolish, disobedient, deceived, and slaves to our desires and various pleasures too. We were spending our lives in evil behavior and jealousy. We were disgusting, and we hated other people.
local: Kun puee, dooooyee ti mun nìn sá bin kaa nyí tibee, ní kaa nìn tɔ́ Unimbɔti, ki yìntī, ní ti-nimbitaataam nì min ti fáatèe nìn cúú-ti kuyuŋu. Ti nìn cáá ntoom nì inimbidiki, ki ná ti-jutiibi ní bi mun ná-ti.

eng: But when God our saviors kindness and love appeared.
local: Ama Unimbɔti ti-Fiiliu dàkā-ti tiŋan nì dineendi din u cáá timbi binib puee.

eng: he saved us because of his mercy, not because of righteous things we had done. He did it through the washing of new birth and the renewing by the Holy Spirit.
local: ki fīī-ti. An kaa sá yii ti túń atunnyaan aba, an sá isaa ní u súń ti-pu. Bi nín fùul̀-ti Unimbɔti nyim puee di dàkà min Circir Fam fātìi màl̄-ti mpɔm ki tīī-ti dimaŋfapɔndi diŋyee.

eng: which God poured out upon us generously through Jesus Christ our savior.
local: Yeesu Kriisitoo ti-Fiiliu puee, Unimbɔti tīiń-ti u-Fam tikpil tikpil.

eng: So, since we have been made righteous by his grace, we can inherit the hope for eternal life.
local: tí ŋmā kí kpántí deedeedambi Unimbɔti nimbiin u-ŋanti pu, kí fōō dimaŋfal din pu ti máká, dàa cáá dikúntilee.

eng: This saying is reliable. And I want you to insist on these things, so that those who have come to believe in God might give careful attention to doing good. These things are good and useful for everyone.
local: Tibɔti tin m sòoǹ nee sá ibaamɔn ní. M là á nīn cɔ́ tibɔtee pu tikpil, án nín làá ŋá pu bin fōō Unimbɔti ki kííyee ń dū bi-ba kí tīī atummɔnti tɔ̄tamee. Tin ŋān̄ ki kèetī binibee dee.

eng: Avoid stupid controversies, genealogies, and fights about the Law, because they are useless and worthless.
local: Ama bí taa nín̄ kpákà tibɔfan pu ki kàaǹ bi-naanjab yiŋŋi an kaa kúntí, kí taa nín̄ kpákà ki jáà Juuda yab marab pu. Tíǹ sá tibɔfan ní kaa kèetī.
